INDIA OFFICE LIBRARY.

The Library was founded in 1801 by the East India Company. It contains about 230,000 printed books, principally relating to the East, and 15,000 manuscripts and xylographs.

There are the following catalogues, and others are in preparation:—

ORIENTAL MANUSCRIPTS AND XYLOGRAPHS.IN PRINT.

Sanskrit.—Catalogue of the Sanskrit and Prâkrit manuscripts in the Library of the India Office.
Vol. I. J. Eggeling. London, 1888-1904.
Vol. II. A. B. Keith and F. W. Thomas. Oxford, 1934.
(This catalogue includes descriptions of all the Sanskrit MSS belonging to the Special Collections, excepting those of the Royal Society Collection).

„ Catalogues of the Special Collections:—

„ Mackenzie Collection. H. H. Wilson. Calcutta, 1828, 2 vols.; 2nd. Ed. in one ol. Madras, 1882.

„ Burnell Collection (Vedic MSS.). A. C. Burnell. London, 1869.

„ Hodgson Collection Sir W. W. Hunter. (Life of B. H. Hodgson, London, 1896, p. 344.)

„ Tagore Collection. S. Mohun Tagore. Calcutta, 1890.

„ „ T. Aufrecht. (Zeitsch. der Deutsch. Morgenlând. Gesellschaft, lviii, pp. 525-38, 1904.)

„ Böhler Collection. G. Böhler. (Zeitsch. der Deutsch. Morgenlând. Gesellschaft, xlii, pp. 530-52, 1888.)

„ Royal Society Collection. C. H. Tawney and F. W. Thomas. London, 1903.

„ Aufrecht Collection (MSS., Indices, Pratika-lists, &c.). F. W. Thomas. (Journal of the Royal Asiatic Society, London, 1908, pp. 1020-63.)

Arabic.—Vol. I. O. Loth. London, 1877. Vol. II, Pt. I (Qur'anic Literature). C. A. Storey, London, 1930. Pt. II. (Sûfism and Ethics). A. J. Arberry, London, 1936. Pt. III. R. Levy (in the Press)

Arabic and Persian.—Royal Society Collection. E. Denison Ross and E. G. Browne. London, 1902

Persian.—H. Ethé. Vol. I. Oxford, 1903. Vol. II. Index, etc., revised and completed by E. Edwards. Oxford, 1936.

Bengali and Assamese.—J. F. Blumhardt. Oxford, 1924.

Chinese.—F. W. Summers. London, 1872.

„ (Tripitaka). S. Beal. Devonport, 1876.

„ Bunyiu Nanjio. Oxford, 1883.

Hindustani.—J. F. Blumhardt. Oxford, 1926.

Marathi.—J. F. Blumhardt and S. G. Kanhere (in the Press).

Javanese.—S. Keyser. (Bijdragen tot de Taal-, Land- en Volkenkunde van Neêrlandsch Indië. The Hague, 1854, part II, pp. 339-42.)

Malay.—H. van der Tuuk. (Tijdschr. voor Nederlandsch Indië, 1849 (1), pp. 385-400.)

Oriya.—J. F. Blumhardt. Oxford, 1924.

Pali.—H. Oldenberg. (Journ. Pali Text Soc., London, 1882, pp. 59-128.)

„ (Mandalay).—V. Fausböll. (Journ. Pali Text Soc., Woking, 1897, pp. 1-52.)

Zend and Pahlavi.—M. N. Dhalla (Iranian MSS. in the Library of the India Office, Journal of the R. Asiatic Society, 1912, pp. 387-98).

IN MANUSCRIPT.

Sanskrit.—Mackenzie Collections (R. Rost : Chinese.—Royal Society Collection. By H. A. incomplete). Giles.

Arabic.—Delhi Collection. Burmese.—E. Chevilion.

Persian.—Delhi Collection. „ (Mandalay), by Taw Sein Ko (Notes).

Ethiopic and Syriac.—By W. Wright. Siamese.—H. Alabaster.

Chinese.—S. Beal. Batta.—H. van der Tuuk.

Tibetan.—L. de la Vallée Poussin.

(Others in progress.)

EUROPEAN MANUSCRIPTS.Catalogue of MSS. in European Languages.—

Vol. I. The Mackenzie Collections. Pt. I: The 1822 Collection and the Private Collection. By C. O. Blagden, London, 1916. Pt. II. The General Collection. By J. Charpentier and E. H. Johnston (in the Press).

Vol. II, Pt. I: The Orme Collection. By S. C. Hill, London, 1916. Pt. II. Minor Collections and Miscellaneous MSS. By G. R. Kaye and E. H. Johnston. Section I. (Nos. 1-538), by G. R. Kaye. London, 1936. Section II. (Nos. 539-842), by E. H. Johnston (in the press).

PRINTED BOOKS AND LITHOGRAPHS.

Catalogue, London, 1845, and Supplement, 1851 (superseded).

  • „ Vol. I and Index London, 1888.
  • „ „ Supplement. London, 1895.
  • „ „ Supplement 2: 1895-1908. London, 1909.
  • „ Accessions: 1. 1911 (1); 2. 1911 (2); 3. 1912 (1); 4. 1912 (2); 5. 1913 (1); 6. 1913 (2)
    7. 1918; 8. 1919; 9. 1920; 10. 1921; 11. 1923; 12. 1924; 13. 1926; 14. 1928;
    15. 1931; 16. 1934; 17. 1936; 18. 1936.
  • „ Vol. II, Pt. 1. Sanskrit Books. R. Rost. London, 1897. Revised edition to 1932.
    Pran Nath and J. B. Chaudhuri. Section I. (A-G) (in the Press).
  • „ „ Pt. 2. Hindustani Books. J. F. Blumhardt. London, 1900.
  • „ „ Pt. 3. Hindi, Panjabi, Pushtu, and Sindhi Books. J. F. Blumhardt. Lond., 1902.
  • „ „ Pt. 4. Bengali, Oriya, and Assamese Books. J. F. Blumhardt. London, 1905.
  • „ „ Pt. 5. Supplement, 1906-20. Bengali Books. J. F. Blumhardt. London, 1923.
  • „ „ Pt. 5. Marathi and Gujarati Books. J. F. Blumhardt. London, 1907.
  • „ „ Pt. 6. Persian Books. A. J. Arberry. London, 1937.

(Others in manuscript.)

EXTRACT FROM THE RULES FOR THE USE OF THE LIBRARY.
  1. 1 The use of the Library (including the Reading Room) is restricted to (a) members of the establishment of the India Office and of the Office of the High Commissioner for India, (b) persons named in the India Office List, (c) gazetted officers of the Indian Army or of the British Army in India, and (d) such other persons as have previously obtained tickets for that purpose.
  2. 2 Subject to the following provisions, volumes may be taken away on loan:—(a) The Librarian shall require from each person an acknowledgment for every volume borrowed. (b) No person shall have on loan at one time more than eight volumes. But the Librarian may exempt scholars engaged in special researches from this limitation. (c) Manuscripts, original drawings, bound volumes of tracts, rare publications and works of reference shall not be lent to individuals. But the Librarian shall have power to authorise such loans in exceptional circumstances. If such a loan is made, the Librarian may require the borrower to sign a bond. (d) The period of a loan shall be one month, but a volume may be borrowed again by the same reader unless it is required by another reader or for official purposes, provided that the borrower shall either produce the volume or certify in writing that it is in his possession and in good condition. The Librarian may, however, exempt from this provision scholars engaged in special investigations. A current periodical shall not be kept for more than one week. Any volume may be at any time recalled at the discretion of the Librarian.
  3. 3 The hours during which the Library is open shall be from 10 a.m. to 5 p.m., except on Saturdays when it shall close at 1 p.m. It shall be closed on Sundays, Good Friday and the day following, Christmas Day, the Saturday before Whitsun, and the Bank Holidays; also during the first two weeks in September for cleaning purposes.
  4. 4 No book or manuscript will be issued later than half-an-hour before the time of closing.

OFFICE OF THE SECRETARY OF STATE
FOR BURMA,

WHITEHALL, S.W.1.

of office.		 Remarks.

HEADS OF ADMINISTRATION IN BURMA AND AT HOME—contd.

CHIEF COMMISSIONERS OF BURMA.

Name. Assumed charge of office. Remarks
Sir C. E. Bernard, K.C.S.I. ... 25 Sept., 1886
C. H. T. Crosthwaite, C.S.I. ... 12 March, 1887 (Aft. K.C.S.I.)
A. P. MacDonnell, C.S.I.(a) ... 8 Sept., 1889 Officiating until 8 December, 1889.
Alexander Mackenzie, C.S.I. ... 10 Dec., 1890 (Aft. K.C.S.I.)
D. M. Smeaton ... 2 May, 1892 (Aft. C.S.I.) Officiating until 23 May, 1892, and from 25 April to 9 August, 1896.
Sir F. W. R. Fryer, K.C.S.I., C.I.E. 3 April, 1895 And officiating 23 May, 1892, to 3 May, 1894. Lieutenant-Governor, 1 May, 1897.

(a) Afterwards (by creation) Baron MacDonnell.

LIEUTENANT-GOVERNORS OF BURMA.

Name. Assumed charge of office. Remarks.
Sir F. W. R. Fryer, K.C.S.I., C.I.E. 1 May, 1897 Formerly Chief Commissioner.
Sir H. S. Barnes, K.C.S.I., K.C.V.O. 4 April, 1903
Sir H. T. White, K.C.I.E. ... 9 May, 1905
Sir Harvey Adamson, Kt., K.C.S.I., LL.D. 19 May, 1910
Sir G. W. Shaw, Kt., C.S.I. ... 15 May, 1913 Officiating.
Sir Harvey Adamson, Kt., K.C.S.I., LL.D. 1 Nov., 1913
Sir Spencer Harcourt Butler, K.C.S.I., C.I.E. 28 Oct., 1915 (Aft. G.C.S.I., G.C.I.E.) Died 2 Mar., 1938.
W. F. Rice, C.S.I. ... 22 Sept., 1917 Officiating.
Sir R. H. Craddock, K.C.S.I. ... 15 Feb., 1918 (Aft. G.C.I.E.) Died 10 Feb., 1937.
Sir Spencer Harcourt Butler, K.C.S.I., C.I.E. 21 Dec., 1922 (Aft. G.C.S.I., G.C.I.E.) Died 2 Mar., 1938.

GOVERNORS OF BURMA.

Name. Assumed charge of office. Remarks.
Sir Spencer Harcourt Butler, K.C.S.I., C.I.E. 2 Jan., 1923 (Aft. G.C.S.I., G.C.I.E.) Died 2 Mar., 1938. Sir William J. Keith, C.I.E., acted as Governor, 26 March to 4 Aug., 1925.
Sir Charles Alexander Innes, K.C.S.I., C.I.E. 20 Dec., 1927
Sir Joseph Augustus Maung Gyi ... 12 Aug., 1930 Officiating to 13 Jan., 1931.
Sir Hugh Lansdown Stephenson, K.C.S.I., K.C.I.E. 20 Dec., 1932 (Aft. G.C.I.E.)
T. Couper, C.S.I. ... 22 Apr., 1935 (Aft. Kt.) Officiating to 19 Aug., 1935.
The Hon. Sir Archibald Douglas Cochrane, K.C.S.I., D.S.O. 8 May, 1936 (Aft. G.C.M.G. Became first Governor of separated Burma on 1st April, 1937.)

MEMBERS OF THE COUNCIL OF THE GOVERNOR OF BURMA.

NOTE—The Executive Council was abolished on 1st April, 1937.

W. J. Keith, C.I.E. ... 2 Jan., 1923 (Aft. K.C.S.I., Kt.; acted as Governor, 26 March to 4 Aug., 1925.) Died 22 Jan., 1937.
Maung Kin ... 2 Jan., 1923 (Aft. K.C.I.E.)
May Oung ... 28 Nov., 1924 (Temp. from 12 Nov., 1924.) Died 5 June, 1926.

BURMA CIVIL SERVICE (CLASS I.).

The Burma Civil Service (Class I.) is the Service in Burma corresponding to the Indian Civil Service in India, and appointments to this Service are made by the Secretary of State for Burma. There are at present three methods by which appointments are made to the Burma Civil Service (Class I.) for which men only are eligible, namely,

  1. (1) By written competitive examination held in England;
  2. (2) By selection in England;
  3. (3) By written competitive examination held in Burma.

The written competitive examination is the same as that which takes place annually for appointment to the Indian Civil Service and the Administrative Class of the Home Civil Service, and is held at the same time. A candidate, on payment of a single fee, may enter for all or any of these Services at the same examination. Candidates must be between the ages of 21 and 24 on the 1st August of the year in which the examination is held, and must be British subjects either of European descent, or of Burman domicile or descent.

The qualifications for appointment by selection are identical with those required for the Indian Civil Service (see page 154). The selection of candidates is made by the same board as that which selects candidates for the Indian Civil Service, and a candidate may indicate on his application form that he also wishes to be considered for the Indian Civil Service.

Candidates for the written competitive examination held in Burma are required to be British subjects domiciled in Burma, to be between the ages of 21 and 24 on the 1st January of the year in which the examination is held and to hold a Degree of an approved University or the Senior Diploma of the Mayo College, Ajmer.

The terms of the probation of successful candidates are similar to those of the Indian Civil Service. The work and conditions of service generally of the Burma Civil Service (Class I.) are also the same as those of the Indian Civil Service (see memorandum on that Service on page 154), but in addition a Burma allowance is admissible at the following rates:—

Pay Amount of
Burma Allowance
admissible.
Rs. per mensem. Rs. per mensem.
200 to 299 .. .. 50
300 to 399 .. .. 60
400 to 499 .. .. 75
500 to 599 .. .. 90
600 to 799 .. .. 105
800 to 999 .. .. 120
1,000 to 1,499 .. .. 135
1,500 to 1,999 .. .. 150
2,000 and upwards .. .. 165

Further information and forms of application for appointment by competitive examination or selection in England may be obtained from the Under-Secretary of State, Burma Office, Whitehall, S.W.1.

Enquiries regarding the competitive examination in Burma should be addressed to the Secretary to the Government of Burma, Home Department.

CANDIDATES SELECTED AFTER OPEN COMPETITION HELD IN LONDON IN JULY, 1937.

  • G. L. Merrels.
  • R. F. Mole.

  • P. Murray.

  • Maung Ba Kyaw.
  • Maung Ba San.

BURMA POLICE (CLASS I.)

The Burma Police (Class I.) is the Service in Burma corresponding to the Indian Police in India, and appointments to this Service are made by the Secretary of State for Burma.

The method of entry is by a written competitive examination held concurrently with the Indian Police and the Army and Air Force entrance examinations.

The subjects of the examination, the conditions of eligibility, the fee payable and the method of application are the same as those laid down for the Indian Police. Payment of one fee will admit candidates to the examination for the Indian Police as well as for the Burma Police (Class I.).

Successful candidates are appointed on probation and attached to the Police Training School at Mandalay. In all other respects the

conditions of their probation are identical with those of the Indian Police.

The work and conditions of service generally of the Burma Police (Class I.) are also the same as for the Indian Police (see the memorandum on that Service on page 157), but in addition, a Burma allowance is admissible at the rates given in the memorandum on the Burma Civil Service (Class I.) on page 191. Further information and forms of application may be obtained from the Under-Secretary of State, Burma Office, Whitehall, S.W.1.

Entry into the Burma Police in Burma is confined to candidates domiciled in Burma. Forms of application, together with Rules, Regulations, and the syllabus of the examination may be obtained from the Secretary to the Government of Burma, Home Department.

THE BURMA MILITARY POLICE.

The Burma Military Police consists of the three battalions which before the separation of Burma from India were the garrison battalions of the Burma Military Police. They are, in essence, a civil force and are now under the

control of the Home Minister. Officers for the Burma Military Police are found by secondment for short tours of duty from British Service and Indian Army Units.

THE BURMA FRONTIER SERVICE.

The Burma Frontier Service is a service which is classed as "Superior," and is, as its name implies, a service whose members are appointed to posts in certain Frontier districts of Burma, notably in the Shan States, the Chin Hills and the Myitkyina District.

Officers are appointed, and their conditions of service regulated, by the Governor of Burma in his discretion. European officers are recruited by the Secretary of State on behalf of the Governor. Applications are invited by advertisement in the general Press as vacancies arise. Officers of Asiatic domicile are recruited locally from officers serving in the Burma Civil Service (Class II.).

A European candidate must be unmarried and between the ages of 21 and 25. He must be of sound bodily health, and must produce evidence of having received a good general education. The possession of a University degree is not essential, but is regarded as an additional qualification. A candidate's ability to ride is also taken into consideration. He must be prepared to serve for long periods in remote parts of Burma where there will be little society.

Candidates are required to attend, at their own expense, at the Burma Office, for a personal interview. Those selected are required to produce satisfactory evidence of their date of birth, and to appear before the Medical Board at the Burma Office for examination as to their physical fitness for appointment. There is no charge for this examination, but candidates must be prepared to pay their own travelling expenses.

Successful European candidates must serve for two years on probation, and remain unmarried for this period, under penalty of forfeiture of their appointment. An officer is not confirmed in his appointment until he

  • (a) has satisfactorily completed such course of practical training in the district as may from time to time be prescribed;
  • (b) has satisfactorily passed such departmental examinations as may from time to time be prescribed; and
  • (c) is considered by the Government to be fit for confirmation.

If an officer fails to be confirmed within a period of three years from the date of his appointment his services may be dispensed with summarily.

THE BURMA FRONTIER SERVICE—continued.PAY.

An officer's salary rises from £450 in his first year of service to £1,485 in his 23rd year. These figures include overseas pay, and are calculated on the exchange value of the rupee being 1s. 6d. The pay of the Service is not subject to the vote of the Burma Legislature.

LEAVE.

An officer earns for every year spent on duty rather more than 2\frac{1}{2} months leave on full pay, or five months on half-pay. He cannot, of course, take leave every year, but he can accumulate his leave.

PASSAGES

On proceeding to take up his appointment a European officer is provided with a free first-class passage to Burma, subject to the condition that its cost shall be refunded in the event of an officer resigning before the expiry of the period of probation on grounds other than ill-health, certified to the satisfaction of the Government. If on the expiry of the period of probation he is not confirmed in his appointment, or if he is compelled by certified ill-health to resign the service before such expiry, he will be provided with a similar free passage back to England, or at his option to any other country at a cost not exceeding that of such passage.

Every European officer after his period of probation is entitled to four free first-class passages to and from Burma, and a number not exceeding four for his wife, the number being calculated according to the officer's length of service at the date of his marriage. If he prefers to do so, he can travel at a cheaper rate, and so increase the number of times he is able to come home at Government expense.

PENSION.

The pension which an officer can earn is fixed according to a scale which rises from Rs.6,000 a year after 25 years' qualifying service to Rs.7,000 after 30 years or more.

PROVIDENT FUND.

Every officer contributes to a Provident Fund, and on retirement his contributions are repaid to him in a lump sum, to which compound interest is added.

Further information about the Burma Frontier Service may be obtained from the Under-Secretary of State, Burma Office, Whitehall, S.W.1.

BURMA ECCLESIASTICAL ESTABLISHMENT,
(CHURCH OF INDIA, BURMA AND CEYLON)

1. Appointments of chaplains to the Burma Ecclesiastical Establishment are made by nomination by the Secretary of State for Burma on the recommendation of the India and Burma Chaplaincies Board.

2. Candidates for these appointments, which are made as vacancies occur, must be Priests who have been at least three years in Holy Orders, and should ordinarily be between the ages of twenty-seven and thirty-four years.

3. Applications for nominations should be submitted to the India and Burma Chaplaincies

Board of the Church of England. A memorandum giving the conditions of service together with forms of application may be obtained from the Under-Secretary of State, Burma Office, Whitehall, S.W.1.

4. An Ecclesiastical Establishment is maintained in India as well as in Burma, but not in Ceylon, and a candidate may indicate on his application form if he also desires to be considered for appointment to the Indian Ecclesiastical Establishment.

BURMA RAILWAY SERVICES.

The Burma Railway Services are under the control of the Burma Railway Board, by whom appointments are made. Any recruitment of candidates undertaken in this country on behalf of the Board is conducted by the High Commissioner for India, India House, Aldwych,

London, W.C.2, to whose office enquiries should be addressed. Vacancies for which the High Commissioner is likely to be required to recruit occur only at irregular intervals and applications are ordinarily invited by advertisement in the press.

AGRICULTURAL, EDUCATIONAL, FOREST, ENGINEERING
AND VETERINARY SERVICES IN BURMA.

Recruitment for appointments to the above-mentioned service posts in Burma is not ordinarily made by the Secretary of State for Burma. Such appointments are now under the control of the Government of Burma and any recruitment undertaken for them in this country is conducted by the High Commissioner for India, India House, Aldwych, London, W.C.2. These appointments occur only at

irregular intervals. Applications for appointments for which the High Commissioner may be asked by the Government of Burma to recruit are ordinarily invited by advertisement in the general press. Particulars as to such Rules as may have been issued by the Government of Burma regulating these Services may be obtained from the High Commissioner for India.

GEOLOGICAL SURVEY, BURMA CUSTOMS SERVICE,
BURMA AUDIT DEPARTMENT.

Recruitment to these Services is normally undertaken in Burma. Enquiries concerning entry to the above-mentioned Departments

should be addressed to the High Commissioner for India, India House, Aldwych, London, W.C.2.

BURMA DEFENCE FORCE.

The Burma Defence Force is under the control of the Governor of Burma and consists of four parts:—

(a) The Burma Army (i.e., forces subject to the Burma Army Act) comprising the four existing battalions of the Burma Rifles which are shortly to be raised to five.

(b) The Imperial Forces in Burma, viz.:—
2 British Infantry battalions,
1 Indian Mountain Battery R.A.
1 Indian Company Sappers and Miners.

(c) The Burma Auxiliary Force and the Burma Territorial Force.

All the above, which form the Army in Burma, are under the immediate command of the General Officer Commanding who is

responsible to the Governor. The Army Headquarters are at Maymyo.

(d) The Burma Frontier Force, comprising five battalions which, before the separation of Burma from India, were the frontier battalions of the Burma Military Police. The Force is under the immediate command of an Inspector-General who in turn is responsible to the Governor. The Headquarters of the Force are at Rangoon.

The principal peace-time duties of the Force are watch and ward of the frontier, and the maintenance of order in the rough tribal areas in which its units are chiefly stationed.

Officers for the Burma Defence Force are found by secondment for short tours of duty from British Service and Indian Army Units.

MEDICAL SERVICES IN BURMA.

The Medical needs of the Army and of the European members of the Services in Burma

continue to be met by the secondment of officers from the Indian Medical Service.

Yasin Khan, Hon. Capt., Sardar Bahadur, C.I.E., 24 July, 1901; M.B.E., 1 Jan., 1920.

Young, Brig.-Gen. H. G., C.I.E., 7 Feb., 1921; D.S.O., 25 Aug., 1917.
Young, Lieut.-Col. H. N., C.I.E., 3 June, 1919; 1 Jan., 1917.
Young, Major James, M.B.E., 1 Jan., 1920.
Young, Sir J. D., Kt., 1 Jan., 1935.
Young, Dr. M. Y., C.I.E., 1 Jan., 1917; K-i-H. Medal, 2nd Class, 12 Dec., 1911.
Young, Mrs. R., M.B.E., 4 June, 1928; K-i-H. Medal, 1st Class, 23 June, 1936.
Younghusband, Lieut.-Col. Sir F. E., K.C.S.I., 1 Jan., 1917; K.C.I.E., 16 Dec., 1904; K-i-H. Medal, 1st Class, 1 Jan., 1901.
Younghusband, Maj.-Gen. Sir G. J., K.C.M.G., 29 Oct., 1915; K.C.I.E., 1 Jan., 1913; C.B., 29 Nov., 1900.
Younghusband, Maj.-Gen. L. N., C.B., 25 Aug., 1917; C.M.G., 3 June, 1918.

Zafullah Khan, Sir Muhammad, K.C.S.I., 11 May, 1937.
Zetland, the Most Hon. Marquess of, P.C., 1922; G.C.S.I., 20 April, 1932; G.C.I.E., 21 Feb., 1917.
Zia-ud-din, Ahmed Sir, Kt., 1 Jan., 1938; C.I.E., 3 June, 1915.
Zobel, Mrs. E., M.B.E., 30 Dec., 1919.
Zutshi, Rai Bahadur Pandit Brijmohan Nath, O.B.E., 1 Jan., 1923.

RECORD OF SERVICES.

The following List includes Officers—

(1) of the Establishments of :—

  • (a) the Secretary of State for India,
  • (b) the Secretary of State for Burma,
  • (c) the High Commissioner for India,
  • (d) the Auditor of Indian Home Accounts ;

(2) of the

  • (a) All-India Services,
  • (b) Indian Political Service,
  • (c) Indian Ecclesiastical Establishment,
  • (d) Burma Class I Services,
  • (e) Burma Ecclesiastical Establishment ;

(3) in other Civil employment in India or in Burma whose substantive pay (exclusive of overseas pay) is at least Rs.1000 a month ;

(4) of the above classes who have retired since April, 1928, in addition to a few officers who retired before 1928. It has not been found practicable to include more than a limited number of officers still surviving who retired before that year.

NOTE.—Particulars of officiating appointments to posts other than those shewn in pp. 97-127 and 186-187 have not been recorded unless they appeared in the 1937 edition of the India Office List.

EXPLANATION OF ABBREVIATIONS.

Acad.—Academy. F.C.H.—Fellow of Cooper's Hill College. Offd.—Officiated.
Acadl.—Academical. Fin.—Finance. Offg.—Officiating.
Acctnt.—Accountant. Finl.—Financial. Offr.—Officer.
Accts.—Accounts. For.—Foreign. Optg.—Operating.
Addl.—Additional. F.S.S. { Federated Shan States Parly.—Parliamentary.
Addn.—Addition. Fellow of the Royal Statistical Society. Photo.—Photographic.
A.G.G.—Agent to Governor General. P.M.O. — Principal Medical Officer.
Agric.—Agriculture. Gaz.—Gazetteer. Pol.—Political.
Agriclt.—Agricultural. Gen.—General. Postmr.—Postmaster.
Appr.—Apprentice. G.I.P. Rly.—Great Indian Peninsula Railway. Pres.—President.
Apptd.—Appointed. Govr.—Governor. Presdy.—Presidency.
A.R.C.A.—Associate of the Royal College of Art, South Kensington. Govt.—Government. Probr.—Probationer.
A.T.C.L.—Associate of Trinity Coll., London. Gr.—Grammar. Proby.—Probationary.
Asst.—Assistant. Hon.—Honorary. Profr.—Professor.
Barr.—Barrister-at-Law. Hosp.—Hospital. Prov.—Provinces.
Cantonmt.—Cantonment. I.A.R.O.—Indian Army Reserve of Officers. Provl.—Provincial.
Capt.—Captain. I.A.S.—Indian Agricultural Service. Publd.—Published.
Col.—Colonel. I.C.S.—Indian Civil Service. Publn.—Publication.
Coll.—College, Collegiate. I.E.S.—Indian Educational Service. P.W.D.—Public Works Department.
Collr.—Collector. I.F.S.—Indian Forest Service. R.C.—Royal Commission.
Comm.—Commission. Inspn.—Inspection. Registrn.—Registration.
Commr.—Commissioner. Inspr.—Inspector. Regr.—Registrar.
Comptr.—Comptroller. Inst.—Institute. Regt.—Regiment.
Conf.—Confirmed. Instn.—Institution. Rest.—Resident.
Consvr.—Conservator. Instrn.—Instruction. Retd.—Retired.
Cov.—Covenanted. I.P.—Indian Police. Rev.—Revenue.
Corpn.—Corporation. I.P.S.—India Police Service. R.I.E. Coll.—Royal Indian Engineering College, Cooper's Hill.
Dep.—Deputy. Irrig.—Irrigation. Sch.—School.
Depd.—Deputed. I.S.E.—Indian Service of Engineers. Sec.—Secretary.
Depn.—Deputation. I.V.S.—Indian Veterinary Service. Sectt.—Secretariat.
Dept.—Department. Jud.—Judicial. Senr.—Senior.
Deptl.—Departmental. Junr.—Junior. Settlt.—Settlement.
Dir.—Director. K.H.P.—King's Hon. Physician. Subord.—Subordinate.
Divl.—Divisional. K.H.S.—King's Hon. Surgeon. S.p.t.—Substantive pro tempore.
Divn.—Division. K.-i-H. medal.—Kaisar-i-Hind medal for public service in India. Supt.—Superintendent.
D.M.O.—District Medical Officer. Legisl.—Legislative. Suptg.—Superintending.
E.B. Rly.—Eastern Bengal Railway. Lieut.—Lieutenant. Surgn.—Surgeon.
Ecclesl.—Ecclesiastical. Litho.—Lithographic. T.C.E. Coll.—Thomason Civil Engineering College, Roorkee.
Edn.—Edition. Mag.—Magistrate. Topogr.—Topographical.
Educ.—Educated. Mechl.—Mechanical. Transfd.—Transferred.
Educl.—Educational. Med.—Medical. Transld.—Translated.
Educn.—Education. Memo.—Memorandum. Transln.—Translation.
E.I. Rly.—East Indian Railway. Mil.—Military. Translr.—Translator.
Engr.—Engineer. N.W.F.P.—North West Frontier Province. Transpn.—Transportation.
Engrg.—Engineering. N.W. Rly.—North Western Railway. Trigonl.—Trigonometrical.
Establ.—Establishment. Uncov.—Uncovenanted.
Exam.—Examination. Univ.—University.
Examr.—Examiner. U.S. List—Unemployed Supernumerary List.
Exec.—Executive. Vaccn.—Vaccination.
Exhibn.—Exhibition. Vety.—Veterinary.
Exhibr.—Exhibitioner.
Expedn.—Expedition.
Expedny.—Expeditionary.

RECORD OF SERVICES.

A-ABABRELTON, ROBERT RELTON DE RELTON, M.A., F.S.S., India Office (supdt., telegraph and mails branch).—(b. 1st July, 1875).—Educ. at Downing Coll., Camb.; apptd. second div. clerk, War Office, April, 1900; 2nd class clerk, India Office, 15th Sept., 1903; transf. to central telegraph section, July, 1920; asst. editor of telegraph codes, April, 1921; higher clerical officer, April, 1922; actg. as officer in charge, central telegraph section, May to Oct., 1923; supdt., tel. and mails branch, Oct., 1931.

ABBAS ALI KHAN, SAHIB BAHADUR, M.A., LL.B., Barr. (ch. presdy. magte., Madras) (b. 19th April, 1891).—Joined the service as offg. ch. presdy. magte., 26th May, 1928; confd., Nov., 1928.

ABBAS, ASGHAR (see Asghar Abbas).

ABBASI, MUHAMMAD WAHAJUDDIN, Indian C.S. (jl. mag., United Provs.) (b. 4th July, 1905).—Educ. at Lucknow University and Jesus College, Cambridge; joined the service 11th Oct., 1930, arrived in India Nov., 1930, and served in the United Provs. as asst. collr. and mag.

ABBOTT, JOHN, B.A. (Oxon), late Indian C.S. (b. 28th Sept., 1884).—Educ. at Manchester Gr. Sch., and Corpus Christi Coll., Oxford; apptd. after exam. of 1908; arrived, 25th Nov., 1909, and served in Bombay as asst. collr. and asst. settlt. offr.; offg. collr. and dist. mag. and pol. agent, Oct., 1918, Feb., 1920, Dec., 1923, and Jan., 1928; confd. Jan., 1928; retd., Dec., 1932.

ABBOTT, STUART EVELYN, B.A., Indian C.S. (asst. commr., Punjab) (b. 21st Aug., 1910).—Educ. at Rugby and Balliol College, Oxford; apptd. after exam. of 1933; arrived in India, Nov., 1934, and served in Punjab as asst. commr.

ABDUL AHAD, SHAIKH, B.A., LL.B., Indian Police (b. 14th Jan., 1899).—Joined the service as asst. supt., Punjab, on 15th July, 1923; offg. supt., Feb., 1932.

ABDUL ALI, ABUL FAIZ MUHAMMAD KHAN BAHADUR, M.A., Bengal Civil Service (keeper of records of Govt. of India) (b. 1st Jan., 1884).—Joined the service, 24th August, 1906, as dep. mag. and dep. collr.; asst. sec., General dept., Bengal, Nov., 1915, to April, 1916; sub-divl. officer, April, 1916, to Sept., 1918; placed on special duty in the pol. dept., Bengal, as special Muhammadan press censor, Sept., 1918, to March, 1919; police mag. Alipore, Sept., 1921, to March, 1922; offg. keeper of the records of the govt. of India, April, 1922; confd. 1st Sept., 1923.

ABDUL ALI, HAMID MOIZUDIN, B.A., late Indian C.S. (b. 5th Jan., 1880).—Educ. at St. Xavier's High Sch., Bombay, Bombay Univ., and Emmanuel Coll., Cambridge; apptd. after exam. of 1903; arrived, 2nd Dec., 1904, and served in Sind as asst. collr.

and mag.; supdt. land records and registr., Sind, Dec., 1909; manager, Sind encumbered estates, June, 1913, to May, 1914; offg. collr. and dist. mag., Jan., 1919; ditto, Oct., 1923; confirmed, Feb., 1925; retd., Feb., 1937.

ABDUL ALI KHAN, Indian Forest Service (dep. consvr., Madras) (b. 7th Nov., 1904).—Joined the service, 14th Oct., 1927; dep. consvr., Nov., 1931.

ABDUL AZIZ, KHAN BAHADUR, SHAIKH, C.I.E., O.B.E., late Indian Police (b. 1st June, 1878).—Joined the service, 1st June, 1899; King's police medal, Jan., 1916; supdt., May, 1924; O.B.E., June, 1925; C.I.E., June, 1930; retd., June, 1934.

ABDUL AZIZ, KHAN BAHADUR, MIAN, C.B.E., M.A., late Punjab Civil Service (b. 27th Jan., 1881).—Joined the service, 1st Nov., 1902; dep. commr., Sept., 1923; C.B.E., June, 1930; jt. secty., Indian round table conf., London, Nov., 1930-Jan., 1931; offg. commr., Oct., 1933; offg. finl. commr. (rev.) and secty. to govt., Punjab, rev. dept., Oct., 1935; retd., May, 1936.

ABDUL AZIZ KHAN, Khan Bahadur, Indian State Rlys. (transpn. supt., E.I. rly.) (b. 4th Dec., 1891).—Joined the service, 1st Oct., 1913; traffic manager, Mysore Rlys., June, 1922; offg. agent and secty. to Govt. for rlys., Mysore, May, 1923; confd., Apl., 1927.

ABDUL AZIZ, MUHAMMAD, KHAN BAHADUR, C.I.E., B.A., Indian Service of Engineers (suptg. engr., United Provs.) (b. 13th Dec., 1884).—Joined the service, 14th Oct., 1908; asst. engr., Oct., 1909; exec. engr., Oct., 1917; offg. suptg. engr., Feb., 1931; confd., Dec., 1933; offg. ch. engr., and jt. secty., irrign. bch., U.P., Aug., 1936; suptg. engr., Apl., 1937; C.I.E., May, 1937.

ABDUL GHAFOOR KHAN, KHAN BAHADUR, B.A., M.Sc. (Delhi), M.Sc. (Tech.), Manch. M.I.Mech.E., M.I.E.E., M.I.E. (Ind.) (dep. dir. inspection) (b. 2nd Aug., 1893).—Joined service 14th Feb., 1921, as tempy. engr., P.W.D., Delhi; Mar., 1923, elec. inspr. (in addn.), asst. dir. of insp. Indian Stores Dept., June, 1923; contr. of insp., Apl., 1930; dep. dir. of insp., Apl., 1930.

ABDUL GHAFFUR, Indian Service of Engrs. (exec. engr., Punjab) (b. 1st Nov., 1899).—Joined the service as aspr. engr., 3rd Nov., 1921; asst. engr. (provl.), Nov., 1922; confd. Jan., 1923; apptd. to the I.S.E. as asst. exec. engr., Jan., 1930; exec. engr., Jan., 1937.

ABDUL HAFEEZ, MUHAMMAD, M.A., Indian Finance Dept. (b. 30th Sept., 1883).—Joined the service, 12th Feb., 1907; asst. acct. genl., Nov., 1907; dep. acct. genl., April, 1914; dep. auditor-genl., Jan., 1927; comptr., Assam, Mar., 1930; offg. acct. genl., C.P., Feb., 1931; dep. ch. auditor, E.I. Rly., Nov., 1931; offg. acct. genl., Central Provs., Jan., 1932; acct. genl., Bihar and Orissa, Nov., 1933; ch. auditor, N.W. Rly., June, 1936.

ABDUL HALIM, MUNSHI, late Judicial dept., United Provs. (b. Mar., 1877).—Joined the service as offg. munsi, 23rd Jan., 1906; confd., Sept., 1908; sub. judge (s.p.t.), July, 1919; confd., Sept., 1919; offg. dist. and sess. judge, Aprl., 1927; confd., Nov., 1930; ret'd., Mar., 1932.

ABDUL HAMID, KHAN BAHADUR, B.Sc., M.B., B.S.(Alld.), D.P.H.(Eng.), Public Health Dept., United Provs. (asst. dir. of Public Health) (b. Aprl., 1893).—Joined the service as health officer, 1 June, 1926.

ABDUL HAMID KHAN, Indian Forest Service (dep. consvr., Madras) (b. 28th Oct., 1906).—Joined the service as asst. consvr., 13th Nov., 1931; dep. consvr., Dec., 1936.

ABDUL HAMID, SHEIKH, M.A., Punjab Civil Service (b. 16th Oct., 1891).—Extra asst. commr., Punjab, Jan., 1916; dep. commr., criminal tribes, insp.-genl., reformatory schools and reclam. officer, Dec., 1935.

ABDUL KAIYUM KHAN, KHAN, Indian Police (supt., Punjab) (b. 5th June, 1898).—Joined the service as asst. supt. on 12th April, 1920; offg. supt., June, 1925; confd. Nov., 1926.

ABDUL KARIM, MAULAVI, B.Sc., Indian Service of Engrs. (exec. engr., Bihar) (b. 26th Aug., 1893).—Joined the service, 13th Oct., 1917; asst. engr., Oct., 1918; asst. exec. engr., June, 1921; exec. engr., Oct., 1927.

ABDUL MAJID KHAN, B.A., LL.B., Bombay Civil Service, Judl. Branch (asst. judge) (b. 12th Dec., 1884).—Held various judl. appts. from Oct., 1913; sub. judge, 16th April, 1918; offg. asst. judge, June, 1928; confd., Oct., 1931; offg. dist. and sess. judge, June, 1931.

ABDUL MAJID, KHAN BAHADUR, O.B.E., late Indian Police Service (b. 16th June, 1879).—Held non-gazetted appt. from 10th March, 1898, to 8th July, 1910; inspr., July, 1910; dep. supt., Feb., 1912; asst. dir., intell. bureau, home dept., govt. of India (prov.), July, 1918; confd. May, 1922; O.B.E., Jan., 1920; apptd. to I.P.S. as supt., Nov., 1925; ret'd., Dec., 1929.

ABDUL QADIR, Sir, Kt., KHAN BAHADUR, Barr. (adviser to the Sec. of State for India) Apptd. addl. judge, high ct., Lahore, 20th Feb., 1930, Feb., 1932; and Feb., 1934; member of Council of India, July, 1934; adviser to the sec. of state for India, Aprl., 1937.

ABDUL QAIYUM AHMAD KHAN, Khan, Bahadur, B.A., Indian Police (supt., Punjab) (b. 10th Oct., 1887).—Joined the service, 27th Sept. 1912, as inspr. of police; apptd. to I.P. as supt., Aprl., 1935; asst. to D.I.G., C.I.D., Feb., 1937.

ABDUL, QAIYUM KHAN, KHAN, BARA KHAN KHEL, Indian Police (supt.) (b. 5th July, 1898).—Apptd. asst. supt., Punjab, on probn., Aprl., 1920; Confd., Feb., 1922; supt., Nov., 1926.

ABDUL RAOOF, SIR MUHAMMAD, Kt., Khan Bahadur, Barr.; apptd. offg. temp. addl. judge, Punjab chief ct., Dec., 1918; judge, Punjab high ct., June, 1920 to Oct., 1925; Kt., Jan., 1926.

ABDUL RASHID, MIAN, M.A., Barr. Judl. Dept., Punjab (judge, high ct., Lahore).—Apptd. asst. legal remembr., 5th Mar., 1925; offg. judge, high ct., Lahore, May, 1933; addl. ditto, Oct., 1933; judge, ditto, Mar., 1937.

ABDUL WAHID, B.Sc., Indian Service of Engrs. (exec. engr., Central Provs.) (b. Aprl., 1894).—Joined the service, 15th Oct., 1917; asst. engr., Oct., 1928; asst. exec. engr., Nov., 1918; offg. exec. engr., Mar., 1926; confd., Oct., 1927; on service in Bhopal state, Aug., 1933.

ABDULLAH ABU SAIYID, KHAN BAHADUR, M.A., Indian Educl. Service (b. June, 1885).—Apptd. prinpl., govt. Coll., Madras, Sylhet and profir. Murarichand coll., Sylhet, 5th Feb., 1921; principl., Murarichand coll., July, 1933.

ABDULLAH IBN YUSUF ALI, C.B.E., Khan Bahadur, M.A., LL.M., Barr. (1896), late, Indian C.S.—Educ. at Bombay Univ., and St. John's Coll., Camb., and Lincoln's Inn, London; apptd. after exam. of 1894; arrived, 18th Jan., 1896, and served in N.W.P. and Oudh as asst. mag. and collr.; asst. sessions judge, March, 1902; joint mag., Dec., 1903; asst. commr., July, 1907; dep. commr., May, 1910; mag. and collr., Dec., 1911; offg. under secty. to govt. of India. finl. dept., June-Sept., 1907; on spl. duty in finl. dept., Aug., 1911 to Jan., 1912; ret'd., Sept., 1914; C.B.E., Dec., 1917; war service in Scandinavia under British Foreign Office and Ministry of Information, 1918; rev. minister, Hyderabad State, 1921-2; served on Indian delegation to League of Nations, 9th Assembly, 1928; author of a monograph on silk fabrics, and of 'Life and Labour of the People of India'; Mestrovic and Serbian sculpture; India and Europe; 'The Making of India'

ABDULLAH MUHAMMAD (see Muhammad A.)

ABDULLAH, MUHAMMAD ABOO (see Muhammad Aboo Abdullah.)

ABDUR RAHIM, Sir, K.C.S.I., M.A., Barr.—Apptd. judge, Madras High Court, 24th July, 1908; member, Royal commn. on public services in India, 1912; Kt., June, 1919; member of govrs. exec. council, Bengal, Jan., 1921, to Dec., 1925; K.C.S.I., June, 1925; presdt., Indian legis. assembly, 1935; author of 'Principles of Muhammadan Jurisprudence.'

ABDUR RAHIM KHAN, Capt. I.A. (Indian Political Service (b. 9th Nov., 1898).—First commn. dated 17th July, 1920; in milty. employ prior to 31st May, 1925; asst. collr., Jhansi, June, 1925; asst. mag., Moradabad, Nov., 1925; asst. commr., Bannu, Aprl., 1926; ditto, Thal, Nov., 1927; ditto, Kohat, Nov., 1928; asst. pol. agent, N. Waziristan, Feb., 1929; 1st asst. to resdt., Baroda, May, 1929; asst. pol. agent, S. Waziristan, Aprl., 1930; city mag., Peshawar, Aprl., 1931; addl. dist. and sess. judge, Peshawar and Hazara, Nov., 1933; dist. and sess. judge, Dera Ismail Khan, Mar., 1934; ditto, Peshawar, Oct., 1935; pol. agent, S. Waziristan, Nov., 1937.

ABDUR RAHIM SAHIB, KHAN BAHADUR, B.A., Indian Educl. Service (dep. dir. pub. instrn., Madras.) (b. 16th July, 1883).—Apptd. to the I.E.S. as inspect. of schools, 23rd Feb.,

1921; addl. dep. dir., public instrn., July, 1921; offg. dist. eduel. offr., Mar., 1926; confd., Apl., 1929; dep. dir. pub. instrn., Madras, Dec., 1935.

ABDURRAHMAN, KHAN BAHADUR KHWAJA, O.B.E., M.B., Ch.B., D.PH., late Dept. of Public Health, Punjab (b. 21st Aug., 1881).—Joined the service, 2nd March, 1910; dep. sany. commr., Nov., 1915; asst. dir., Jan., 1923; offg. dir., May, 1930; and again, July, 1932; confd., Dec., 1933; O.B.E., June, 1934; retd., Aug., 1936.

ABDUR RAHMAN, KHAN BAHADUR, SHEIKH, Indian Service of Engrs. (exec. engr., Punjab) (b. 13th Feb., 1884).—Joined the service, 20th Sept., 1905; asst. engr., Oct., 1906; exec. engr., Oct., 1914; offg. suptg.-engr. and seecty., rural sanitary board, Punjab, Apl., 1927.

ABDUS SALAM SAIYID, Income-tax Dept., Madras (asst. commr. of income-tax) (b. 15th Dec., 1890).—Joined the service, May, 1914; held non-gaz. appts.; inspector, 4th grade (Salt, etc., Dept.), Apl., 1920; income-tax offr., Apl., 1924; confd. as asst. commr. of income-tax, Dec., 1935.

ABDUSSAMAD KHAN, M.Sc., Indian Educl. Service (dep. dir. of public instrn., Bihar) (b. 1st Apl., 1891).—Joined the service, 4th Dec., 1919; inspr. of schools, May, 1922; proftr. of chemistry, science coll., Patna, Jan., 1928; offg. princpl., Greer Bhumihar Coll., Muzaffarpur, Jan., 1936. dep. dir. of public instrn., Aug., 1936.

ABELL, GEORGE EDMOND BRACKENBURY, Indian Civil Service (asst. commr. Punjab) (b. 22nd June, 1904).—Educated at Marlborough and Corpus Christi College, Oxford; joined the service, 10th Oct., 1928, arrived, Nov., 1928, and served in the Punjab as asst. commr.; offg. dep. regr., co-op. soc., Mar., 1932; confd., July, 1932; settlement offr., Oct., 1935.

ABERNETHY, JOHN, Indian Police (asst. supt.) (b. 12th June, 1910).—Joined the service as proby. asst. supt., Bombay, 25th Nov., 1930; confd., Sept., 1932; offg. dist. supt., June, 1933.

ABIGAIL, REGINALD PHILIP, Indian C.S. (dep. commr., Burma) (b. 11th Aug., 1892).—Educ. at Trent Coll., Derbyshire and Bedford Modern Sch.; on mil. service from May, 1918, to Mar., 1923; joined the service 29th Oct., 1923, and served in Burma as asst. commr.; offg. under secy. to govt., April, 1925; dep. commr., Jan., 1931.

ABRAHAM, EDGAR GARTON FURTADO, C.B., B.A., late Indian C.S. (Punjab) (b. 8th Apl., 1880).—Educ. at St. Paul's Sch. and Corpus Christi Coll., Oxford; apptd. after exam. of 1903; arrived 28th Nov., 1904, and served in the Punjab as asst. commr.; offd. as asst. collr. of Customs, Calcutta, from Nov., 1908, to March, 1910; on military service from March, 1915, to Dec., 1919; C.B., Jan., 1916; dep. commr., Mar., 1922; sec. to govt. of Punjab, home dept., March, 1924; dist. and sess. judge, Mar., 1927; retd., Mar., 1930.

ABRAHAM, KILAKETHOLOCAL MATHAI, B.A., Indian Finance dept. (b. 23rd Nov., 1885).—Joined the service 11th Feb., 1910; asst. acct.-genl., Nov., 1915; ditto and currency officer,

Burma, Aug., 1924; asst. acct.-genl., Burma, March, 1927; dep. ditto, Mar., 1928; exr., local fund accts., Bombay, Oct., 1929; asst. dir., army audit, W.C., Nov., 1930; with finl. commr., rlwys., Apl., 1931; dep. acct.-genl., posts and tels., Calcutta, Apl., 1932; offg. acct.-genl., Bengal, June, 1933; offg. govt. exr. of accts., M. and S.M. and S.I., rlwys., Feb., 1935; dep. ch. accts. officer, E.I. rly., Aug., 1937.

ABU ABDULLAH MUHAMMAD ZAKA-ULLAH KHAN, M.A., late United Provs. C.S. (b. 1879).—Joined the service as proby. dep. collr., 3rd June, 1902; confd. Mar., 1904; dep. registrar, co-operative societies, Aug., 1924; magte, and collr., Feb., 1929; retd., Sept., 1934.

ABU HAMID MUHAMMAD SHAMSOD-DOHA, Indian Police (supt.) (b. 1st Dec., 1901).—Joined the service 3rd Aug., 1924, apptd. supt Bengal, Mar., 1937, awarded King's Police Medal, 1937.

ABU MUHAMMAD ARSHAD ALI, Khan Bahadur, Maulvi, B.A., Dept. of co-op. soc., Bengal (regr.) (b. 16th Apl., 1885).—Joined the service, 24th Feb., 1909; asst. regr., May, 1924; regr., Oct., 1934.

ABUL FAEZ MUJIBOR RAHMAN, M.Sc. (Cal.), Indian C.S. (dist. and sess. judge, Bengal) (b. 23rd Sept., 1897).—Educ. at Calcutta Univ. and Trinity Coll., Cambridge; joined the service, 28th Oct., 1923; arrived 10th Nov., 1923, and served in Bengal as asst. mag. and collr.; jt. magte. & dep. collr., Nov., 1924; offg., dist. & sess. judge, Apl., 1929; addl. ditto, Oct., 1932; confd. as dist. and sess. judge, Nov., 1935.

ABUL FAZL ABDUL HAMID, O.B.E., Indian Police (supt., Bihar) (b. 17th Jan., 1897).—Joined the service as asst. supt. on 15th Jan., 1922; supt., Feby., 1929; O.B.E., June, 1933.

ABUL HASAN, B.A., late Indian Educl. Service (b. 11th Mar., 1878). Apptd. inspector of Muhammadan schools, United Provs., 1st April, 1916; retd., Mar., 1933.

ABUL MUZAFFAR AHMAD, B.L., B.C.L., Barr., Bengal Civil Service (dist. and sess. judge) (b. 21st June, 1883).—Joined the service, 7th Sept., 1903; dep. mag. and dep. collr., Nov., 1904; addl. dist. and sess. judge, April, 1920; confd., Jan., 1926.

ABUL QASIM, KHAN BAHADUR, Indian Police (supt., United Provinces) (b. 24th Aug., 1883).—Joined the service as dep. supt. on probn., 2nd Nov., 1908; confd., Nov., 1910; offg. supt., Sept., 1911; confd., July, 1931.

ACHARIYAR, C. R. TIRUVENKATA (see Tiruvenkata Achariyar, C.R.).

ACHARYA, ATUR R. V. (see Venkata Acharya, A. R.).

ACHARYA, BEJOY KRISHNA, B.A., Indian C.S. (asst. mag. and collr., Bengal) (b. 1st May 1912); educated at Hare School, Calcutta, Calcutta University, University College, London, and School of Oriental Studies, London.—Joined the service 21st Sept., 1936, arrived in India, Oct., 1936, and served in Bengal as asst. mag. and collr.

ACHARYA, K.S.S. (see Srinivasa A. K. S.).

ACHARYA, PRASANNA KUMAR (see Prasanna Kumar A.).

ACHARYA, SIR T. VIJAYARAGHAVA (see) Vijayaraghava Acharya, Sir T.).

ACHARYA, V. T. KRISHNAMA (see Krishnama Acharya, V. T.).

ACHESON, JAMES GLASGOW, C.I.E., B.A., Indian C.S. (Indian Political Service) (b. 24th Jan., 1889).—Educ. as St. Andrew's Coll., Dublin, and Trinity Coll., Dublin; apptd. after exam. of 1912; arrived 22nd Nov., 1913, and served in the United Prov. as asst. mag. and collr. and asst. commr.; temp. asst. pol. agent and asst. commr.; Quetta-Pishin, April, 1917; cantonment mag., Quetta, Nov., 1917; asst. pol. agent in Kalat, and pol. agent in charge of Bolan Pass, Oct., 1918; on special duty in connection with Indo-Afghan Conference, March, 1920; member of British Mission to Kabul, Dec., 1920; dep. commr.; Dera Ismail Khan, Oct., 1923; pol. agent, Tochi, Oct., 1924; pol. agent, North Waziristan, Oct., 1925; offg. dep. secty. to govt. of India, for and pol. dept., May, 1927; confd., Dec., 1927; C.I.E., June, 1929; dep. commr., Peshawar, Apl., 1932; rev. commr., N.W.F.P., Apl., 1934; offg. for secty. to govt. of India for. dept., July, 1935; offg. resdt., Waziristan, Nov., 1935; pol. resdt., N.W.F.P., Apl., 1937.

ACLAND, RT. REV. CANON RICHARD DYKE, M.A. (b. 3rd Oct., 1881).—Apptd. Bishop of Bombay, 13th Sept., 1929.

ACOCK, WHITFIELD EDGAR, late Indian Police Service (b. 11th Nov., 1880).—Joined the service, 23rd Nov., 1901, and posted to United Provs.; asst. supt., July, 1906; supt., Dec., 1914; ret'd., May, 1929.

ACOTT, ARTHUR SYDNEY VERNON, M.A., Indian C.S. (collector, Bombay) (b. 31st July, 1888).—Educ. at Magdalen Coll. Sch., Oxford, and Brasenose Coll., Oxford; apptd. after exam. of 1911; arrived, 21st Nov., 1912, and served in Bombay as asst. collr. and mag. and asst. pol. agent; persl. asst. to agent to govr., Kathiawar, March, 1916, to July, 1917; actg. dep. commr., April, 1923; dep. sec. to govt., rev. dept., April, 1926; offg. collr. and dist. mag., May, 1927; confd., Jan., 1931; member of Indian legisl. assembly; contr. of emigrant lab., Oct., 1936, to Nov., 1937.

ACTON, HAMPDEN ANTONY GREER, B.A., Indian C.S. (asst. collr., Bombay) (b. 30th July, 1914).—Educ. at Eton and Magdalene College, Cambridge; joined the service, 21st Sept., 1937; arrived in India, Oct., 1937, and served in Bombay as asst. collr.

ACTON, JAMES ROWLAND, F.C.S., F.I.C., Office of the High Commr. for India (dir. of inspection, store dept.) (b. 6th Dec., 1875).—Educ. at Ardingly Coll. and London Univ.; appt. viewer, India Store Depot, 1897; asst. inspr. of stores, 1898; chief chemist, 1909; dep. supt. India Store Depot, Aug., 1923; dep. dir. of inspection, Apr., 1927; dir. of inspection, July, 1936; on mil. service, 1914-19 (mentioned in despatches).

ACTON, THEODORE JOHN CHICHESTER, B.A., Indian C.S. (magte. and collr., United Provs.) (b. 18th Aug., 1886).—Educ. at Shrewsbury, Keble Coll., Oxford, and Trinity Coll., Dublin; apptd. after exam. of 1910; arrived in India, 25th Nov., 1911, and served in the United Provs. as asst. mag. and collr., and asst. commr.; 2nd asst. to agent to the govr.-gen. in Baluchistan, Jan., 1916; served as asst.

pol. agent, and pol. agent, Sibi, from Sept., 1916; ditto, Quetta, June, 1920; jt. mag., Nov. 1920; actg. dep. commr., March, 1921; actg. mag. and collr., April, 1925; confd., Nov., 1928.

ADAIR, ARTHUR ROBIN, B.A., Indian C.S. (asst. mag. and collr., B. and O.) (b. 10th Feb., 1913).—Educ. at Queen Elizabeth's Grammar Sch., Wimbourne, and Emmanuel College, Cambridge; joined the service, 16th Sept., 1937; arrived in India, Nov., 1937, and served in B. and O. as asst. mag. and collr.

ADALJA, MANEKCHAND TARACHAND, L.C.E., late Indian Service of Engrs. (b. 18th May, 1880).—Asst. engr., March, 1906; exec. engr., March, 1914; ret'd., May, 1935.

ADAM, JOHN BUCKNOLL MERCER, F.C.H., late Indian Forest Service (Burma) (b. 6th Mar., 1884).—Arrived, 29th Oct., 1906, and served as asst. consvr.; dep. consvr., Nov., 1911; ret'd., Apl., 1930.

ADAM, JOHN HUNTER, C.I.E., O.B.E., late Indian Police (b. 1st June, 1882).—Joined the service, 22nd Nov., 1902; supt., N.W. Frontier Prov., Nov., 1912; asst. to inspr.-gen. of police, N.W. Frontier Prov., Nov., 1914; O.B.E., June, 1918; on special duty in charge of Intelligence Bureau, Peshawar, Dec., 1919; on depn. to England from Feb., 1923; offg. dep. inspr.-genl., Dec. 1925; pro l. ditto, Jan., 1927; offg. inspr.-genl., N.W.F.P., April, 1928; confd., July, 1931; C.I.E., June, 1933; ret'd., June, 1937.

ADAMI, SIR LEONARD CHRISTIAN, Kt., B.A., late Indian C.S. (Bihar and Orissa) (b. 15th Mar., 1874).—Educ. at Cheltenham and Jesus Coll., Cambridge; apptd. after exam. of 1896; arrived, 9th Dec., 1897, and served in Bengal as asst. mag. and collr.; joint mag. and dep. collr., Jan., 1906; dist. and sess. judge, from March, 1911; transf. to Bihar and Orissa, April, 1912; supt. and remembrancer of legal affairs, and sec. to legisl. council, Sept., 1914; actg. judge, Patna high ct., May, 1919; confd. Nov., 1919; Kt., June, 1929; ret'd., Mar., 1931.

ADAMS, ARTHUR GEORGE, Indian Police (dist. supt., Burma) (b. 16th Oct., 1893).—Joined the service as asst. supt. on 29th Nov., 1913; dist. supt., May, 1918; offg. dep. inspr.-genl., Sept., 1935.

ADAMS, FREDERICK JAMES, Office of High Commr. for India (suptg. exec. officer, general dept.) (b. 18th Feb., 1885).—Apptd. 2nd class clerk, 14th April, 1905; transf. to Jud. and Public Dept., Feb., 1911; on mil. service from Aug., 1914, to Jan., 1919; minor staff clerk, Jan., 1919; staff clerk, Apl., 1921; administrative officer, April, 1922; transf. to Office of High Commr. for India, asst. principal, Apl., 1922; principal exec. officer, Jan., 1924; suptg. ditto, April, 1927.

ADAMS, HUGH BENEDICT, Indian State Rlys. (elec. engr., N.W. rly.) (b. 27th Feb., 1893).—Apptd. asst. elec. engr., N.W. rly., 1st Dec., 1922; elec. engr., Nov., 1931.

ADAMS, JOHN EUSTACE, Indian Police (b. 9th Feb., 1915).—Joined the service as asst. supt., Bengal, on probn., 16th Dec., 1934.

ADAMS, PHILIP FRANCIS, B.Com., Indian C.S. (asst. commr., Assam) (b. 18th July, 1914).—Educ. at Mitcham Secondary Sch. and London School of Economics; joined the

service, 15th Sept., 1937; arrived in India, Nov., 1937, and served in Assam as asst. commr.

ADAMS, RICHARD PERCIVAL, O.B.E., A.M.I.M.E. (b. 4th Mar., 1876).—Joined the service as offg. asst. inspr. of factories, Bengal, 24th Feb., 1908; supt. of jail manufactures, Dec., 1909; chief inspr. of factories for Bengal, Bihar and Orissa and Assam, March, 1914; O.B.E., June, 1918; ret'd. Sept., 1931.

ADAMSON, SIR HARVEY, Kt., K.C.S.I., M.A., late Indian C.S.—Educ. at the Gymnasium, Old Aberdeen, and King's Coll., Aberdeen; apptd. after exam. of 1875; arrived, 17th Dec., 1877, and served in Burma as asst. commr. and settlt. officer; dep. commr., April, 1888; commr., July, 1898; jud. commr., Upper Burma, April, 1902; C.S.I., Jan., 1903; addl. member of gov.-genl. slegisl. council, Nov., 1903; chief judge, chief court, Lower Burma, May, 1905; Kt., Jan., 1906; member of gov.-genl.'s council, Oct., 1906; lieut. gov. of Burma, May, 1910; K.C.S.I., Jan., 1910; ret'd., Oct., 1915.

ADDAMS - WILLIAMS, CHRISTOPHER, C.I.E., late Indian Service of Engrs. (b. 2nd April, 1877).—Trained at R.I.E. Coll.; joined the service, 1st Oct., 1897, as asst. engr.; exec. engr., Oct., 1907; supt. engr., Sept., 1914; C.I.E., June, 1916; chief engr., and sec. to gov. of Bengal, Sept., 1920; ret'd., May, 1932.

ADDIS, FRANK LESLIE, Office of High Commissioner for India (higher clerical officer, store dept.) (b. 25th Oct., 1889).—Educ. at Strand Sch., King's Coll.; apptd. second class clerk, India Office, 14th Oct., 1909, and att'd. to Store Dept.; on mil. service from Nov., 1915, to Dec., 1917; transf. to office of High Commissioner for India, Oct., 1920; exec. officer, April, 1922; higher clerical officer, 1st Jan., 1926.

ADDISON, SIR JAMES, Kt., M.A., B.Sc., Indian C.S. (judge, high ct., Lahore) (b. 13th Nov., 1879).—Educ. at Banff Burgh Sch. and Aberdeen Univ., apptd. after exam. of 1902; arrived, 30th Nov., 1903, and served in the Punjab as asst. commr.; sec., Delhi municipality, March to Nov., 1906; spl. land acquisition officer, Delhi, Oct., 1912, to July, 1915; judge, small cause ct., Simla, Jan., 1917; offg. dist. and sessions judge, Nov., 1920; dist. and sessions judge, June, 1923; addl. judge, high ct., Lahore, April, 1925; judge, ditto, Jan., 1927; knighted, June, 1935.

ADDISON, LEWIS PERCY, B.A., Indian C.S. (asst. commr., Punjab) (b. 16th February, 1910).—Educated at Bromsgrove and Worcester College, Oxford; apptd. after exam. of 1931; arrived in India, November, 1932, and served in the Punjab as asst. commr.; comdt., border mily. police, Dera Ghazi Khan, Nov., 1936.

ADIE, WALTER SIBBALD, M.A., late Indian C.S. (b. 17th Aug., 1872).—Educ. at Trin. Coll., Camb., senr. wrangler, 1894, 1st Smith's Prize, 1895, Fellow of Trinity Coll., 1896; apptd. after exam. of 1895; arrived, 7th Dec., 1896, and served in Bengal as asst. mag. and collr.; transf. to fin. dep. as asst. comptr., Assam, Sept., 1899; asst. acctnt.-gen., Burma, April, 1900; dep. acctnt.-gen., Madras, Dec., 1901; re-transf. to Bengal as asst. mag. and collr., Sept., 1903; joint mag. and

dep. collr., July, 1905; offg. acctnt.-genl., Bengal, April, 1907; dep. acctnt.-genl., Bengal, Oct., 1907; ditto, Punjab, Nov., 1908; offg. acctnt. gen., Burma, and commr. of paper currency, Nov., 1909; mag. and collr., Bengal, Aug., 1912; attached to Br. min. of muns., March to Sept., 1917; ret'd., Aug., 1929.

ADLARD, LEONARD STANLEY, B.Sc., Indian Service of Engrs. (exec. engr., Punjab) (b. 3rd Dec., 1893).—Joined the service, 1st Oct., 1919; asst. exec. engr., Mar., 1920; offg. exec. engr., Feb., 1926; confid., Oct., 1926; services placed at disposal of the Govt. of India, Nov., 1934.

ADVANI, KESSAMAL CHATTAMAL, L.C.E., A.M.I.E. (Ind.), late Indian Service of Engineers (b. 20th Sept., 1883).—Held non-gazetted appts. in P.W.D. from Aug., 1905, to Jan., 1920; asst. engr., 1st Feb., 1920; I.S.E. as asst. exec. engr., 1st Jan., 1931; offg. exec. engr., Feb., 1932; ret'd., Oct., 1932.

ADVANI, PRITAMDAS BHOJRAJ, M.Sc., Dept. of Industries, Bombay (dir.) (b. 21st May, 1891).—Appointed Director of Industries, 1st August, 1928.

ADVANI, RADHAKRISHNA TAHILRAM, M.B., B.S., Maj., I.M.S., Jail Dept., Bombay (supt.) (b. 26th Jan., 1896).—Date of first commn., 1st Mar., 1922; in mily. employ until 2nd Sept., 1928; supt., Jail Dept., Bombay, July, 1929.

AFZAL, LESLIE ZIA-UD-DIN (see Zia-ud-din Afzal, L.).

AFZAL-UR-RAHMAN, B.A., Khan Bahadur, late Bengal Civil Service (b. Jan., 1874).—Joined the service, 4th June, 1894; sub. dep. collr., July, 1899; dep. mag. and dep. collr., Nov., 1904; dep. mag., Oct., 1905; ret'd., Feb., 1929.

AGA, HILALY, Indian C.S. (asst. magte. and collr., Bengal) (b. 20th May, 1911).—Educ. at Madras and King's Coll., Cambridge, arrived in India, 18th Feb., 1937, and served in Bengal as asst. magte. and collr.

AGARWAL, GOVERDHANDAS, B.A., Indian Service of Engrs. (exec. engr., Central Provs.).—(b. 11th Mar., 1892).—Joined the service 18th Sept., 1915; asst. engr., Jan., 1918; asst. exec. engr., June, 1924; exec. engr., Oct., 1925.

AGARWAL, KEDAR NATH (see Kedar Nath Agarwal).

AGARWALA, CLIFFORD MONMOHAN, Barr. (judge, high ct., Patna).—Apptd. judge, high ct., Patna, 11th July, 1933.

AGARWALLA, ANANDA CHANDRA, RAI BAHADUR, late Indian Police Service (b. 30th Sept., 1874).—Joined the service, 24th May, 1894; asst. supt., Jan., 1920; supt., April, 1923; ret'd., Apl., 1930.

AGGARWAL, KUNDAN LAL (see Kundan Lal, A.).

AGHA HAIDER, SYED, Barr.-at-Law, late Judl. Dept., Punjab.—Joined the service as addl. judge, high ct., Lahore, 4th Oct., 1926; judge, ditto, Oct., 1931; ret'd., July, 1936.

AGHA, MUHAMMAD AKRAM (see Muhammad Akram, A.).

AGRAWAL, PITAM CHAND (see Pitam Chand A.).

AHAD, ABDUL (see Abdul Ahad).

AHMAD, A. M. (see Abu Muzaffar Ahmad).

AHMAD, ALI (see Ali Ahmad).

AHMAD, GHULAM (see Ghulam Ahmad).

AHMAD, IQBAL (see Iqbal Ahmad).

AHMAD, ISLAM (see Islam Ahmad.)

AHMAD, KAMALUDDIN (see Kamaluddin Ahmad).

AHMAD, MUHAMMAD BASHEER, M.A., Indian Civil Service (sess. and sub-judge, United Provinces) (b. 11th Mar., 1904).—Educ. at Univ. of Allahabad, Aligarh Muslim Univ. and Sch. of Oriental Studies, London; joined the service, 29th Sept., 1928; arrived, Oct., 1928, and served in the United Provinces as asst. collr. and mag.; asst. commr., Mar., 1930; offg. sess. and sub-judge, May, 1932; offg. dist. and sess. judge, Mar., 1934; author of 'Problem of Rural Uplift in India.'

AHMAD, MUZUDDIN, B.A., Indian C.S. (jt. mag. and dep. collr., Bengal) (b. 1st Oct., 1909).—Educ. at Aminabad High School, Lucknow, Government High School, Sitapur, Government Jubilee Collegiate School, Lucknow, University of Lucknow and Jesus College, Oxford; joined the service, 9th Oct., 1934; arrived in India, Nov., 1934, and served in Bengal as asst. mag. and collr.; jt. mag. and dep. collr., May, 1936.

AHMAD, MUSHTAQ (see Mushtaq Ahmad).

AHMAD, NASIR, Indian C.S. (asst. commr., Punjab) (b. 14th June, 1905).—Educ. at Punjab University and Wadham College, Oxford; joined the service, 11th Oct., 1930; arrived in India, Nov., 1930, and served in the Punjab as asst. commr.; offg. dep. commr., Aug., 1935.

AHMAD, RAHIMUDDIN (see Rahimuddin Ahmad).

AHMAD, RIAZ (see Riaz Ahmad).

AHMAD SAID KHAN, MUHAMMAD, SIR, NAWAB OF CHHITARI, K.C.I.E., M.B.E.—M.B.E., June, 1918; C.I.E. June, 1921; app'd. member of govr. 'sexec. council, United Provs.', 3rd Jan., 1926; K.C.I.E., June, 1928, delegate to Indian round table conf., London, Nov., 1930-Jan., 1931; 2nd. Session, Sept., Dec., 1931; temp. member govr. gen'l. council, Jan., 1932; govr., United Provs., Apl. to Nov., 1933; minister of home affairs, U.P., Apl. to July, 1937.

AHMAD, SAIYED AMIN, B.A., Indian Civil Service (jt. mag. and dep. coll., Bihar) (b. 1st Mar., 1904).—Educ. at University of Patna and Caius Coll., Cambridge; joined the service, 5th Oct., 1928; arrived, 23rd Nov., 1928, and served in Bihar and Orissa as asst. coll. and mag.; jt. mag., Dec., 1928.

AHMAD, SHAMSUDDIN, see SHAMSUDDIN AHMAD.

AHMAD, SIRAJUDDIN (see Sirajuddin Ahmad).

AHMAD SYED, KHAN SAHIB (see Syed Ahmad).

AHMAD, ZIA-UD-DIN, M.A., LL.B., late Indian Police (b. 5th Apl., 1881).—Held non-gazetted appt. from 31st July, 1904, to 17th Nov., 1906; offg. dep. supt., Nov., 1906; confd., Dec., 1907; joined the I.P.S. as supt. of police, May, 1921; commr. of police, Bhowalpur State, July, 1929; offg. dep. inspr. gen., May, 1933; ret'd., Apl., 1936.

AHMED, AMIN (see Amin Ahmed).

AHMED, AZIZ, B.Sc., Indian Civil Service (jt. mag. and dep. coll., Bengal) (b. 24th June, 1906).—Educ. at Punjab University and London School of Oriental Studies; joined the service, 10th Oct., 1930; arrived in India, Nov., 1930, and served in Bengal as asst. collr. and mag.; jt.

mag. and dep. coll. Dec., 1931; offg. depy. regr., co-op. soc., Jan., 1936.

AHMED, QUTUB-UD-DIN (see Qutub-ud-din Ahmed).

AHMED, SHEIKH FATEH, B.Sc., Indian State Rlys. (exec. engr. E.I. rly.) (b. 18th Aug., 1888).—Joined the service, 7th Jan., 1914; resdt. engr., E.I. rly., Jan., 1922; exec. engr., ditto, Sept., 1927.

AHMED, SIR SYED SULTAN, Kt., D.L., Bar.-at-Law (b. 24th Dec., 1880).—Apptd. Dep. Legal Remembrancer to the Govt. of Bihar and Orissa, 1913; Govt. Advocate, 1916-37; acted as judge Patna high court, 1919-20; vice-chancellor, Patna Univ., 1923-30; member Hartog Educ. Committee, 1928-29; delegate, Indian Round Table Conferences, 1930-31; acting member of the Exec. Council of the Govr. of Bihar and Orissa, 1932; acting member of the Exec. Council of the Governor-General in charge of railways and commerce, 1937.

AHMED, ZAFFRUDDIN ZIAUDDIN, Indian Police (b. 28th Nov., 1910).—Joined the service as proby. asst. supt., Bombay, 7th Feb., 1933.

AHSAN, ZAFARUL, M.A., Indian C.S. (asst. commr., Punjab) (b. 15th April, 1911).—Educated at St. John's College, Oxford; joined the service 21st Sept., 1936; arrived in India, Oct., 1936, and served in the Punjab as asst. commr.

AHSAN-UD-DIN, B.A., Indian C.S. (asst. commr., Punjab) (b. 7th Sept., 1909).—Educ. at Govt. High Sch., Ludhiana, Ismalia High Sch., Amritsar, Punjab Univ., and Queen's Coll., Cambridge; joined the service, 4th Oct., 1933; arrived in India, Nov., 1933, and served in the Punjab as asst. commr.; sub-divl. offr., Nov., 1936.

AHSAN-UL-HAQ, MIAN, BARR., late Punjab Civil Service (b. 16th July, 1878).—Joined the service, 1st Oct., 1921; offg. dist. and sess. judge, Oct., 1921; confd., May, 1926; ret'd., July, 1933.

AHSANULLAH, MAULVI KHAN BAHADUR, M.A., late Indian Educ. Service (Bengal) (b. Feb., 1875).—Joined the service, 1st Aug., 1896; inspr. of schools, May, 1919; ret'd., Feb., 1930.

AIKMAN, EDWARD HENRY, Indian State Rlys. (dep. traffic Manager Burma Rlys.) (b. 23rd Sept., 1884).—Joined the service as asst. traffic supt., 19th Nov., 1909; dist. traffic supt., Mar., 1928; offg. dep. traffic manager, transport., Aug., 1932; ditto, comml., Aug., 1932; dep. traffic manager, Burma rlys., Dec., 1932; offg. traffic manager, Feb., 1933.

AINSLEY, J. E., L.R.C.P. & S., L.D.S. (Edin.).—Lt.-Col., I.M.S., Medical Dept., Central Provs. (civil surgeon) (b. 7th Sept., 1893).—First commn., 15th Mar., 1920; civil surgeon, Aug., 1933.

AITCHISON, DAVID ALEXANDER DONALD, M.R.C.V.S., M.P.S., late Indian Veterinary Service.—Joined the service, April, 1906; apptd. princpl., Madras vety. coll., Feb., 1909; chief supt., civil vety. dept., Madras, Feb., 1926; ret'd., May, 1928.

AITCHISON, PATRICK EDWARD, C.I.E., late Indian Forest Service (b. 17th Mar., 1881).—Joined the service, 24th Nov., 1903, as asst. consvr. of forests; dep. consvr., Dec., 1908; on mil. service, 1917-18; consvr. (acting),

May, 1922; confd., Aug., 1927; offg. ch. consvr., Mar., 1932; confd., Aug., 1933; C.I.E., June, 1934; retd., May, 1935.

AITCHISON, WILLIAM, M.C., M.A., B.Sc., M.B., Ch.B., Major, I.M.S., Medical Dept., United Provs. (civil surg.) (b. 4th June, 1894).—Date of first commn., 28th Jan., 1925; civil surg., United Provs., Feb., 1932.

AITIZAZ-UD-DIN AHMAD KHAN, SAHIB-ZADA MIRZA, Indian Police (supt., Punjab) (b. 6th Sept., 1899).—Joined the service as asst. supt. on 10th April, 1920; offg. supt., Mar., 1926; confd., Oct., 1931.

AITKEN, CECIL EDWARD, B.Sc., Indian Service of Engrs. (exec. engr., Bombay) (b. 8th Oct., 1888).—Joined the service as asst. engr. on 1st Oct., 1911; exec. engr., Oct., 1920; offg. suptg. engr., Dec., 1935.

AIYAR (see also Ayyar).

AIYAR, N. K. K. (see Krishna Aiyar, N. K.).

AIYAR, S. K. (see Krishna Aiyar, S.).

AIYAR, SIR P. S. A., Sivaswami (see Sivaswami Aiyar, Sir P. S. A.).

AIYAR, T. SADASIVA (see Sadasiva Aiyar, T.).

AIYAR, V. (see Venkatarama Aiyar).

AJIT CHAND, MALHOTRA, LALA, Indian Service of Engrs. (exec. engr., Punjab) (b. 7th Oct., 1902).—Joined the service as apprentice engr., 30th Oct., 1925; asst. exec. engr., Oct., 1926; exec. engr., Oct., 1935.

AJIT SINGH, KALHA, SARDAR, Indian Service of Engrs. (exec. engr., Punjab) (b. 27th May, 1904).—Joined the service as apprentice engr., 29th Oct., 1926; asst. exec. engr., Oct., 1927; exec. engr., Oct., 1936.

AJREKAR, SHRIPAD LAKSHMAN, B.A. (Bombay and Cantab.) late Indian Educational Service (b. 29th Mar., 1883).—Joined the service as prof. of botany, Royal Institute of Science, Bombay, 16th May, 1924; ditto, Gujarat coll., Ahmedabad, June, 1928; retired.

AJUDHIA NATH, KHOSLA, RAISAHIB, LALA, B.A., A.M.I.E. (Ind.), Indian Service of Engrs. (exec. engr., Punjab) (b. 11th Dec., 1892).—Joined the service, 28th Sept., 1916; asst. engr., Sept., 1917; asst. exec. engr., Jan., 1920; exec. engr., Oct., 1926.

AKBAR HUSAIN, CHAUDHRI, B.A., Indian C.S. (dist. and sess. judge, United Provs.) (b. 7th Oct., 1896).—Educ. at Canning Coll., Lucknow, and Peterhouse, Cambridge; joined the service 12th Dec., 1921; served in United Provs. as asst. commr. from 14th Jan., 1922; asst. mag. and collr., July, 1924; offg. sess. and sub-judge, Oct., 1926; offg. dist. and sess. judge, May, 1928; confd., Sept., 1930.

AKHIL CHANDRA MITRA, B.Sc., Indian Service of Engrs. (exec. engr., United Provs.) (b. 16th Jan., 1900).—Joined the service, 30th Oct., 1923; asst. exec. engr., Oct., 1924; exec. engr., Oct., 1933.

AKHUND, ABDULLAH SHAFI MUHAMMAD, B.A., LL.B., Bombay Civil Service (judge and sess. judge) (b. 26th Dec., 1884).—Joined the service as sub. judge, 23rd Nov., 1911; offg. asst. judge, Feb., 1925; confd., Nov., 1928; offg. judge and sess. judge, Jan., 1930; confd. Oct., 1931.

AKRAM, MUHAMMAD (see Muhammad Akram).

AL HASAN MUHAMMAD, B.A., Indian Posts and Telegraphs (dep. P.M.G.) (b. 16th

Dec., 1885).—Joined the service as proby. supt., 1st June, 1909; confd., May, 1911; asst. dep. dir.-genl., Mar., 1932; on depn. to Univ. Postal Congr., Cairo, from Jan. to May, 1934; dep. P.M.G., Nov., 1935.

ALAM, SHAH NAZIR (see Nazir Alam, S.).

ALAY RAZA, SAIVID, Barr., late Judl. Dept., Central Provs. (b. Oct., 1879).—Joined the service as munisif, March, 1903; sub-judge, May, 1910; addl. dist. and sess. judge, June, 1931; retd., Nov., 1934.

ALBAN, REGINALD GEORGE EVELIN WILLIAM, Major, I.A., Indian Political Service (b. 21st Aug., 1899).—First commn., 1st Oct., 1918; in milly. employ. to 20th May, 1925; under-secy. to pol. resdt., Persian Gulf, Dec., 1930; pol. agent and Brit. consul at Muscat, May, 1931; under-secty. to A.G.G. in C.I., Oct., 1934; asst. to pol. agent, Kalat, Oct., 1935; pol. agent, Sibi, Dec., 1936.

ALCOCK, ALFRED SYDNEY, Indian State Rlys. (secty., G.I.P. Rly.) (b. 29th Oct., 1881).—Joined the service, 24th Feb., 1898, and was employed in loco. dept. to May, 1903, and in Agent's Office from June, 1903, to May, 1920; 3rd asst. secty., June, 1920; 2nd ditto, May, 1921; offg. secty., Apr., 1922; Jan., 1925 and Nov., 1927; asst. dep. agent, July, 1925; secty., Feb., 1930.

ALEXANDER, HAROLD GEORGE, F.R.C.S., L.R.C.P., Lt.-Col., I.M.S., Bihar Medical Dept. (prof. of surgery, medical coll. Palna) (b. 7th Feb., 1888).—First commn., 16th July, 1915; offg. prof. of surgery, medical coll., Patna, July, 1929; confd., Oct., 1932.

ALEXANDER, HEBER MAITLAND, D.S.O., O.B.E., *Lt.-Col., I.A. (retd.), late Indian Stores dept. (b. 1st Feb., 1881).—First commn., dated 11th Aug., 1900; O.B.E., June, 1919; in milty. employ. to 31st Dec., 1921; dir. of purchase and intelligence, Jan., 1922; retd., Oct., 1931.

ALEXANDER, HENRY RAYMOND, Indian Police (dist. supt., Burma) (b. 6th Aug., 1889).—Joined the service as asst. supt. on 15th Nov., 1909; dist. supt., Dec., 1917; awarded King's police medal, Jan., 1931; offg. dep. inspr.-genl., Oct., 1932, and again, Sept., 1936.

ALEXANDER, IAN URQUHART, M.A., Indian C.S. (asst. mag. and collr., U.P.) (b. 13th June, 1915).—Educ. at High School of Glasgow, and Glasgow Univ.; joined the service, 22nd Sept., 1937; arrived in India, Oct., 1937, and served in the U.P. as asst. mag. and collr.

ALEXANDER, ROBERT DONALD THAIN, D.S.O., O.B.E., T.D., A.K.C., M.I.C.E., M.I.E.E., late Indian State Rlys. (b. 29th Sept., 1878).—Educ. at Dulwich Coll. and King's Coll., London Univ.; apptd. asst. engr., public wks. dept., ry branch, 1st Oct., 1904; served on the survey and construction of the Nagda-Muttra State ry. to Nov., 1906; engaged on preparation of projects for proposed electric rys., Burma, and frontier, Nov., 1906, to Oct., 1907; persl. asst. to engr.-in-chief, Nagda-Muttra State ry., Oct., 1907, to Feb., 1909; also signal engr., Nagda-Muttra State ry., Sept., 1908, to Feb., 1909; offg. exec. engr., Sept., 1908; services lent to Burma Ry. Co. from Feb. to June, 1909; exec. engr., Lower Ganges Bridge. Sept., 1909, to Oct.,

1915 (three times deputed to England in connection with the project); on active service, Mesopotamia, Oct., 1915, to Aug., 1919 (temp. Lt.-Col., London Scottish, April, 1917; subst. Lt.-Col., T.F. Res., Nov., 1919; D.S.O., 1917; O.B.E., 1919; T.D., 1919; four times mentioned in despatches); offg. asst. sec., rly. board, April, 1920; services lent to Bengal Nagpur Rly. Co. as chief engr., Oct., 1921; ret'd., Sept., 1933.

ALEXANDER, ROBERT VEITCH, Indian Forest Engineering Service (b. 22nd Oct., 1891).—Joined the service, as forest engineer, Bombay, 24th Nov., 1921.

ALEXANDER, WILLIAM GORDON, M.A., Indian C.S. (asst. collr., Bombay) (b. 9th July, 1908).—Educated at George Watson's College, Edinburgh, Edinburgh University and Brasenose College, Oxford; apptd. after exam. of 1931; arrived in India, November, 1932, and served in Bombay as asst. collr.; under-secty., defence dept., Mar., 1937.

ALI AHMAD, B.A., Indian Service of Engrs. (exec. engr., Assam), (b. 30th July, 1889).—Joined the service, 26th Sept., 1912; asst. engr., Jan., 1920; exec. engr., Sept., 1922; asst. ch. engr., and under secty., p.w.d., Assam, Nov., 1935; offg. suptg. engr., Oct., 1936.

ALI, ASA MUHAMMAD, B.A., Indian State Rlys. (transptn. supt.) (b. 16th Feb., 1893).—Joined the service, 10th Nov., 1915; asst. supt., E.I. Rly., July, 1925; offg. secty., rly. bd., Apl., 1931; transptn. supt. (traffic comml) E.I. Rly., Feb., 1935.

ALI, A. (see Asghar Ali).

ALI, A. F. M. A. (see Abdul Ali, A. F. M.).

ALI, ALBAN HYDAR YUSUF, Indian Police (asst. supt., Assam), (b. 23rd Sept., 1904).—Joined the service, 5th Dec., 1924; in training to Sept., 1926; proby. asst. supt., Sept., 1926; confd., April, 1928; offg. supt., Apl., 1934.

ALI AUSAT, MUHAMMAD MUNSHI, B.A., LL.B., late United Provs. C.S. (b. Mar., 1875).—Joined the service, 22nd June, 1905; sub-judge, 22nd Feb., 1918; offg. dist. and sess. judge, Jan., 1926; ret'd., Mar., 1930.

ALI, BAKHT YAWAR (see Bakht Yawar, A.).

ALI, HAMID MOIZUDIN ABDUL (see Abdul Ali, H. M.).

ALI HASAN, KHAN BAHADUR, B.A., late Indian Police, (b. 16th Dec., 1879).—Held non-gazetted appt. from 1st July, 1909, to 25th Feb., 1912; dep. supt., Feb., 1912; promoted to I.P.S. as supt., 8th Dec., 1929; awarded Indian police medal, Nov., 1932; ret'd., Dec., 1934.

ALI KHAN, G. (see Ghazanfur Ali Khan).

ALI KHAN, S. (see Shamshad Ali Khan).

ALI, MAULVI SAIYID IJAZ (see Ijaz Ali, M.S.).

ALI, MUHAMMED BARKAT (see Barkat Ali, M.).

ALI, MUHAMMED HAMID (see Hamid Ali, M.).

ALI, MUHSIN (see Muhsin Ali).

ALI, NASIM (see Nasim Ali, Maulvi).

ALI, RAMZAN (see Ramzan Ali).

ALI, SAIYID FAZL (see Fazl Ali, S.).

ALI, SHUJAT OSMAN (see Osman Ali, S.).

ALI, SYED RAZA (see Raza Ali S.).

ALI, TORICK AMEER (see Ameer Ali T.).

ALI, WARIS AMEER (see Ameer Ali, W.).

ALI, ZAFAR (see Zafar Ali).

ALINGTON, NOEL STANLEY, M.C., Major I.A. Indian Political Service (b. 4th Jan., 1899).—First commn., dated 27th Oct., 1917; in milty. employ prior to 29th Mar., 1925; offg. pers. asst. to administr., Nabha, Mar., 1925; probnr. in pol. dept., Sept., 1925; pers. asst. to ch. commr. and agent to govr. genl. N.W.F.P., Jan., 1927; asst. commr., Bannu, Oct., 1928; under sec. to ch. commr., N.W.F.P., May, 1930; asst. pol. agent, Zhob, Apl., 1931; asst. pol. agent and asst. commr., Quetia-Pishin, Apl., 1932; pol. agent, Zhob, Mar., 1933; offg. under secty. to the Govt. of India, for. and pol. dept., Nov., 1935; confd., July, 1936; secty. to A.G.G. in C.I., Aug., 1936.

ALLAH BAKSH, KHAN SAHIB, B.A. (Oxon.), Indian Forest Service (dep. consvr., Punjab), (b. 1st June, 1895).—Joined the service as asst. consvr., Punjab, on 29th Nov., 1923; dep. consvr., Nov., 1924.

ALLAH YAR KHAN, KHAN SAHIB, MALIK, Indian Forest Service (dep. consvr., Punjab) (b. 28th June, 1887).—Apptd. asst. consvr., Punjab, from 19th July, 1922; dep. consvr., April, 1928.

ALLAN, DONALD ANDREW, late Indian Forest Service (b. 27th Sept., 1878).—Joined the service, 14th Sept., 1904; dep. consvr., June, 1923; ret'd., July, 1928.

ALLAN, ROBERT GEORGE, C.I.E., M.A. (Cantab.), late Indian Agricl. Service (b. 7th Nov., 1879).—Apptd. principl. agricl. coll., Nagpur, 23rd Nov., 1907; offg. dir. of agricl., Central Provs., March, 1926; employed as dir. of agricl., United Provs., Dec., 1931; confd., Oct., 1933; ret'd., Nov., 1935; C.I.E., Jan., 1936.

ALLANSON, HARRY LLEWELYN LYONS, C.I.E., B.A. (Oxon.), late Indian C.S. (Bihar and Orissa) (b. 16th April, 1876).—Educ. at Bedford Grammar School and Exeter Coll., Oxford; apptd. after exam. of 1899; arrived, 13th Dec., 1900, and served in Bengal as asst. mag. and collr. and settlmt. offr.; jt. mag. and dep. collr., Feb., 1910; transfed. to Bihar and Orissa, April, 1912; dist. and sess. judge, Mar., 1916; ditto, and regr., high ct., Patna, Feb., 1919; sec. to govt., judl. dept., June, 1921, to April, 1924; offg. dist. and sess. judge, March, 1926; C.I.E., July, 1926; acting judge, high ct., Patna, Jan., 1927; addl. judge, ditto, May, 1927; actg. judge, ditto, April, 1928; ret'd., April, 1932.

ALLEN, FRANCIS RICHARD WILLIAM KINKEAD, M.A., M.D., B.Ch., Major, I.M.S., Medical Dept., Central Provs. (civil surg.) (b. 1st May, 1890).—Date of first commn., 1st Aug., 1928; in Milty. employ to May, 1933; offg. civil surg., Central Provs., 17th May, 1933; civil surg. and medl. officer, dist. jail, May, 1935.

ALLEN, GUY OLDFIELD, M.A., late Indian C.S. (b. 7th Nov., 1883).—Educ. at Rugby, and New Coll., Oxford; apptd. after exam. of 1907; arrived, 23rd Nov., 1908, and served in the United Provs. as asst. mag. and collr.; asst. supdt., Dehra Dun, April, 1914; jt. mag., Dec., 1916; on mil. service, July, 1918, to April, 1919; sessions and sub-judge, Nov., 1922; offg. dist. and sess. judge, Oct., 1923; confd. July, 1924; offg. judge, Allahabad high ct., May-Aug., 1931; ret'd., Jan., 1934.

ALLEN, HARRY S., Indian State Ry's. (colliery manager, E.I. Ry.), (b. 14th Apl., 1887).—Joined the service as asst. colliery manager, 22nd Feb., 1914; colliery manager, Oct., 1920.

ALLIN, WILLIAM ROY, late Indian Service of Engrs. (Punjab).—Joined the service, 1st Oct., 1919; asst. exec. engr., Jan., 1920; exec. engr., Oct., 1926; ret'd., Oct., 1928.

ALLINSON, HENRY JOHN, A.M.I.E.E., Indian State Ry's. (dep. ch. mechl. engr. (elec.)) (b. 13th Mar., 1889).—Joined the service as dist. elec. engr., E.I. Ry., 8th Apl., 1927; offg. ch. elec. engr., variously from Mar., 1930; offg. dep. ch. mechl. engr. (elec.), E. B. rly., Oct., 1935; confd., Nov., 1936.

ALLSOP, FRED, M.Sc., B.A., Indian Forests, Service (dep. consvr., Burma) (b. 26th Sept., 1901).—Joined the service as asst. consvr., Burma, on 22nd Dec., 1925; dep. consvr., Apl., 1932; prof. of forestry, Univ. Coll., Rangoon, July, 1933.

ALLSOP, JAMES JOSEPH WHITTLESEA, Indian C.S. (judge, high ct., Allahabad), (b. 11th Feb., 1887).—Educ. at St. George's Coll., Weybridge, and University Coll., London; apptd. after exam. of 1909; arrived in India, 28th Nov., 1910, and served in the United Provs., as asst. mag. and collr.; jt. mag., Oct., 1916; on mil. duty, July, 1918, to Jan., 1919; sess. and sub-judge, July, 1924; dist. and sess. judge, Nov., 1925; offg. judl. secty. to govt., Apl., 1931; confd., Mar., 1933; offg. judge, ch. ct. of Oudh, May, 1933; addl. judge, high ct., Allahabad, Oct., 1934; judge, ditto, July, 1935.

ALLUM, FREDERICK WARNER, C.B.E., late Railway Dept.—Trained at T.C.E. Coll.; joined the service 4th June, 1890, as asst. engr.; exec. engr., June, 1902; offg. dep. agent, O and R. rly., Sept., 1914; engr. in-chief, Nushki Extn. rly., Nov., 1916; suptg. engr., and ditto, May, 1919; C.B.E., Jan. 1919; chief engr., March, 1924; dir., civil engineering, rly. board, April, 1924; ret'd.

ALMOND, JAMES, B.A., Indian C.S., Indian Political Service (b. 28th Sept., 1891).—Educ. at Bolton Grammar Sch. and Emmanuel Coll., Cambridge; apptd. after exam. of 1914; arrived 3rd Dec., 1915, and served in Bihar and Orissa as asst. mag. and collr.; on mil. service, Aug., 1916, to Feb., 1919; supy. asst. commr., Peshawar, Feb., 1919; ditto, and persl. asst. to chief commr., N.W. Frontier Prov., April, 1919; confd. in pol. dept. of govt. of India, July, 1923; asst. commr., Mardan, Oct., 1923; dep. commr., Hazara, Dec., 1924; dist. judge, Peshawar, June, 1927; divl. and sess. judge, ditto, Jan., 1928; presdt., enquiry comee., D. I. Khan, Sept., 1931; dist. and sess. judge, Peshawar, Oct., 1931; offg. resdt. and judl. commr., N.W.F.P., Mar., 1934; addl. judl. commr., N.W.F.P., Apl., 1935; judl. commr., N.W.F.P., Apl., 1937.

AMAR NATH, RAI SAHIB, LALA, B.Sc., A.M.I.C.E., Indian Service of Engrs. (exec. engr., Punjab), (b. 26th Mar., 1890).—Joined the service, 7th June, 1915; asst. exec. engr., Oct., 1922; exec. engr., Oct., 1931.

AMAR NATH, NANDA, RAI BAHADUR, LALA, B.A., late Public Works Dep.,

Punjab (b. 4th Dec., 1877).—Joined the service 10th Nov., 1899; exec. sanitary engr., Oct., 1923; sanitary engr. to govt. Nov., 1925 suptg. engr. Dec., 1928; ret'd. Dec., 1932.

AMAR NATH, SAVARA, LALA, B.A., Indian Service of Engrs. (exec. engr., Punjab), (b. 20th Nov., 1887).—Joined the service, 9th Sept., 1910; asst. engr., Sept., 1911; exec. engr., Oct., 1922.

AMBARNATH BHATTACHARYA, L. C. E., Indian Service of Engrs. (exec. engr., Bombay), (b. 1st Feb., 1884).—Joined the provl. service as apprentice engr., 20th Feb., 1909; asst. engr., Feb., 1910; exec. engr., Feb., 1918.

AMBEGAONKAR, KRISHNANATH GANESH, Indian C.S. (asst. commr., Central Provs.), (b. 12th Aug., 1902).—Educ. at Univ. of Bombay and Univ. Coll., London. —Joined the service, 22nd Oct., 1926; arrived, 28th Nov. 1926, and served in the Central Provs. as asst. commr.; under secty. to govt., Dec., 1928; offg. dep. commr., April, 1930; and again, Mar., 1933; on depn. under res. bank of India, Apl., 1935.

AMBIKA PRASAD, RAI BAHADUR LALA, late Indian Service of Engrs.—(b. 12th Nov., 1877).—Joined the service, 20th Nov., 1901; asst. engr., Nov., 1902; exec. engr., Dec., 1911; offg. suptg. engr., June, 1923; confd., Aug., 1927; offg. ch. engr., and secty. to govt., Punjab, irrigation brch., July, 1932; ret'd., Nov., 1932.

AMBLER, FRANCIS BARLOW, M.B., B.S., M.R.C.S., late Indian State Railways (b. 15th Feb., 1877).—Joined the service as dist. surg., 8th Mar., 1907; in milty. employ from 28th Mar., 1916, to 8th Mar., 1920; dist. medl. officer, 1st Apl., 1929; ret'd., Dec., 1932.

AMBRAVANESWAR, SIVARAMA KRISHNA, M.A., B.L., Indian Audit Dept. (b. 6th Feb., 1894).—Joined the service, 9th Mar., 1918; dep. acct. gen., central revs. (works), Feb., 1929; ditto (civil), Mar., 1929; dep. acct. genl., Punjab, July, 1929; asst. acct. genl., posts and tels., Feb., 1931; dep. acct. genl., ditto, Mar., 1931; offg. acct. genl., ditto, June, 1933; offg. ch. auditor, E.B. rly., June, 1934; dep. acct. genl., posts and tels., Sept., 1934; offg. dep. acct. genl. (works), Punjab, Apl., 1935.

AMEER ALI, TORICK, Barr., Judicial dept., Bengal.—Apptd. judge, high court, Calcutta, Nov., 1931.

AMEER ALI, WARIS, late Indian C.S. (b. 12th Oct., 1886).—Educ. at Wellington and Balliol Coll., Oxford; apptd. after exam. of 1910; arrived in India, 20th Nov., 1911, and served in the United Provs. as asst. mag. and collr. and asst. commr.; sub-judge, Oct. 1923; offg. dist. and sess. judge, March, 1924; confd. as sess. and sub-judge, Nov., 1925; dist and sess. judge, Dec., 1926; ret'd., June, 1929.
