29th Nov., 1901, and served in Bombay as asst. collr. and mag., and forest settlmt. offer.; asst. judge, and sess. judge, Aug., 1906; under-sec. to govt., pol., judl., etc., depts., Jan., 1911, to Aug., 1913; judl. asst. to agent to Govt., Kathiawar, Sept., 1919; judge and sess. judge and ditto, Dec., 1919; member of legisl. council, 1925; offg. addl. judicial commr. of Sind, June, 1926; offg. judge, Bombay high ct., June, 1930; confd., May, 1931; knighted, May, 1937; ret'd., Nov., 1937.

BARLOW, HAROLD EVERARD, Indian C.S. (mag. and collr., United Provs.) (b. 12th May, 1892).—Educ. at Haileybury and Institut Minerva, Zurich; joined the service, 26th Aug., 1920; arrived, 7th Dec., 1920, and served in United Provs. as asst. commr.; jt. mag., July, 1922; city mag., Nov., 1924; offg. mag. and collr., April, 1925; offg. dep. commr., March, 1926; offg. mag. and collr., Dec., 1926; confd., Mar., 1932.

BARLOW, HENRY ARTHUR NORTHEY, Indian C.S., Indian Political Service (b. 11th Aug., 1905).—Educ. at Marlborough and Corpus Christi Coll., Oxford; joined the service, 16th Oct., 1929; arrived, 18th Nov., 1929, and served in the United Provinces as asst. coll. and mag.; asst. commr., Mar., 1931; jt. mag., Mar., 1931; under secty. to A.G.G. in States of W.I., Dec., 1933; vice-consul, Kashgar, Oct., 1934; and again, Aug., 1936; city mag., Peshawar, Dec., 1936; secty. to Resident for Eastern States, Nov., 1937.

BARNARD, ALBERT ALFRED, O.B.E., B.E., Indian Service of Engrs. (exec. engr., Assam), (b. 5th Aug., 1891).—Joined the service, 1st Oct., 1914; asst. engr., Nov., 1914; O.B.E., June, 1919; exec. engr., Oct., 1923.

BARNARD, ALBERT WILLIAM STANDFIELD, Indian Police (supt., Bombay) (b. 1st Nov., 1898).—Joined the service as asst. supt., 5th Oct., 1920; offg. supt., Mar., 1923; confd. Apl., 1929.

BARNARDO, FREDERICK ADOLPHUS FLEMING, C.I.E., C.B.E., M.D., F.R.C.S.E., Lt.-Col. I.M.S. (ret'd.), (b. 4th June, 1874).—Joined the service, 26th July, 1902; on mil. duty till Aug., 1906, when he was apptd. civil surgeon, Bengal; on mil. service, Jan., 1915 to March, 1921; C.I.E., June, 1918; C.B.E., June, 1919; principal, medical coll. Calcutta, March, 1922; ret'd., June, 1929.

BARNE, Rev. GEORGE DUNSFORD, M.A., C.I.E., O.B.E., late Indian Eccles. Estab. (Bishop of Lahore) (b. 6th May, 1879).—Joined the service, 11th May, 1908; prinpl. and chaplain, The Lawrence Royal Military School, Sanawar, Oct., 1912; O.B.E., Jan., 1919; C.I.E., June, 1923; Bishop of Lahore, Apl., 1932; vice-chancellor of Punjab univ., Jan., 1936.

BARNES, Sir GEORGE STAPYLTON, K.C.B., K.C.S.I.—Educ. at Eton and Univ. Coll., Oxford; barr., Inner Temple, 1883; counsel to the Board of Trade in bankruptcy matters, 1886; official receiver in companies' liquidation, 1893; senr. official receiver, 1896; compt. of the Companies Dept., Board of Trade, 1904-11; compt. gen. of the Labour Dept., Board of Trade, 1911-13; member of the cttee. on the amendment of the

company laws, 1906; of royal commn. on sugar supplies during the war, 1914; of Treasury cttee. on new issues of capital during the war, 1915; K.C.B., 1915; member of council of govr.-gen., in charge of commerce and industry and railway depts., Mar., 1915; took his seat on the council, Apl., 1916; K.C.S.I., June, 1920; ret'd., Apl., 1921.

BARNES, HERBERT CHARLES, C.I.E., M.A., late Indian C.S. (Assam).—Educ. at Westminster Sch. and Christ Church, Oxford; apptd. after exam. of 1893; arrived, 13th Dec., 1894, and served in Assam as asst. commr., personal asst. to chief commr., and settlmt. offr.; dir. of land records and agric., E. Bengal and Assam, Oct., 1905; dy. commr. and ditto, Oct., 1905; mag. and collr., Nov., 1907; dep. commr., Assam, Apl., 1912; on mil. duty with I. A. R. O., Apl., 1917, to Aug., 1918 (commndt., Naga Labour Corps); C.I.E., June, 1919; temp. member of govr.'s exec. council, Assam, 11th Apl. to 5th Aug., 1925, and from 11th Aug. to 8th Dec., 1925; offg. commr., Dec., 1925; confd., July, 1926; ret'd., Oct., 1928.

BARNES, Sir HUGH SHAKESPEAR, K.C.S.I., K.C.V.O., late Indian C.S.—Educ. at Malvern Coll.; apptd. after exam. of 1872; arrived, 30th Oct., 1874, and served in the N.W. Prov. as asst. mag. and collr.; transfed. to finl. dept. of govt. of India, Dec., 1876, and apptd. asst. acctnt.-gen. in the N.W. Prov.; private sec. to the finl. member of the govr.-gen.'s council, Dec., 1876; acted as under sec. in the home dept. in 1878; on special duty under the foreign dept., Nov., 1879; asst. to rest., Kandahar, Nov., 1880; served in the Afghan campaign, 1881, as pol. officer with Gen. Wilkinson's brigade; asst. to the govr.-gen.'s agent in Baluchistan, 1881-83; pol. agent in Quetta and Pishin, June, 1883; in charge of arrangements, supplies, &c., for passage of Afghan boundary commn. across Baluch desert, Aug., 1884; under sec. to govt. of India, foreign dept., May, 1890; rev. commr., Baluchistan, Dec., 1890; offd. as govr.-gen.'s agent and chief commr. in 1891 and 1892; rest., Kashmir, Mar., 1895; offd. as sec. to govt. of India, Foreign Dept., Mar., 1896, and again Apl., 1899; rest. and agent for govr.-genl. in Baluchistan, June, 1896; C.S.I., June, 1897; sec. to govt. of India, Foreign dept., Dec., 1900; K.C.S.I., Jan., 1903; K.C.V.O., Mar., 1903; lieut.-govr. of Burma, Apl., 1903; ret'd., May, 1905; member of the council of India, May, 1905, to Nov., 1913.

BARNES, HUMPHREY ASTON, C.I.E., Capt., I.A., Indian Political Service (b. 27th Aug., 1900).—First commn., dated 15th Apl., 1919; in milty. employ prior to 23rd June, 1926; vice-consul, Ahwaz, May, 1927; under sec. to pol. restd. in Persian Gulf, Apl., 1928; asst. commr., Kohat, May, 1928; dep. commr. Hazara (temp.), May, 1930; asst. commr., Charsadda, Aug., 1930; addl. under-secty. to C.C. and A.G.G., N.W.F.P., May, 1931; under-secty., ditto, Sept., 1931; under-secty. to govt., N.W.F.P., Apl., 1932; asst. pol. agent, Malakand, Aug., 1933; ditto, North Waziristan, Sept., 1933; ditto Sth. Waziristan, Oct., 1934; C.I.E., Jan., 1938.

BARNES, Rev. JOHN, B.A., Indian Eccles. Estabt. (junr. chaplain, Bombay) (b. 7th Jan.,

1898.—Apptd. chaplain on probation, Bombay, 25th Feb., 1928; junr. chaplain, Feb., 1930.

BARNES, PAUL, Indian Police (b. 5th Jan., 1916).—Apptd. asst. supt., Bengal, on probn., 26th Nov., 1936.

BARNETT, CECIL GUY, C.I.E., late Indian Service of Engrs. (b. 7th Nov., 1881).—Trained at R.I.E. Coll.; joined the service, 1st Oct., 1902; arrived, Nov., 1902; asst. engr., Oct., 1902; exec. engr., Oct., 1910, services placed at disposal of chief commr., Delhi, from May, 1913; suptg. engr., Nov., 1921; C.I.E., June, 1923; offg. chief engr., July, 1926; ret'd., Mar., 1930.

BARNES, C. J., All India Radio (News Editor, Central News Orgn.) (b. Oct., 1902).—Joined the service 9th Sept., 1937.

BARON, CECIL GEORGE, late Indian State Railways (b. 12th Dec., 1881).—Joined the service, 19th June, 1912; dist. traffic supt., E.B. Rly., Mar., 1931; ret'd., Dec., 1936.

BARR, ARTHUR GUY, O.B.E., late Indian Finance Dept. (b. 25th July, 1877).—Apptd. to fin. dept., 16th Mar., 1901; asst. acctnt.-gen., Bombay, Apl., 1902; inspr. of local fund accounts, Burma, Nov., 1903; asst. acctnt.-gen., Burma, Dec., 1903; asst. acctnt.-gen., Bombay, Jan., 1907; acting asst. sec. to govt. of India, fin. dept., Nov., 1908; asst. acctnt.-gen., Bengal, Oct., 1909; asst. acctnt.-gen., Bombay, Feb., 1911; employed in Bikaner State from Oct., 1911; on mil. service under War Office from Sept., 1914; on deptn. as audit officer, South Persia Rifles, from Apl., 1918; O.B.E., July, 1920; dep. acctnt.-gen., Sept., 1921; dep. auditor-gen., Delhi, Sept., 1923; dir. of army audit and offg. acctnt.-gen., Sept., 1924; acct.-genl. (provl.), Nov., 1925; confd., Jan., 1927; offg. acct.-genl., Bombay, Oct., 1927; dir. of army audit, Jan., 1927; ret'd., July, 1932.

BARRACLOUGH, NORMAN, B.Sc., Dept. of Mines in India (inspr.) (b. 10th June, 1896).—Joined the service as junr. inspr., 31st Dec., 1923; inspr., Dec., 1926.

BARRETT, HAMILTON BERESFORD, Indian Forest Service (dep. consvr., Burma) (b. 4th June, 1895).—Joined the service as asst. consvr., on 23rd Dec., 1921; dep. consvr., Oct., 1924.

BARRETT, PAULIN FREDERICK, Indian C.S. (asst. magt. and collr., United Provs.) (b. 19th July, 1913).—Educ. at Ald. Newton's Boys' School, Leicester, Univ. Coll., Leicester, and Emmanuel Coll., Cambridge; joined the service, 16th Jan., 1937; arrived in India, Mar., 1937, and served in U.P. as asst. magt. and collr.

BARRETT, LEONARD MILLER FRANK, B.Sc., Indian Service of Engrs. (exec. engr., Orissa) (b. 2nd Mar., 1896).—Joined the service, 1st Oct., 1919; asst. exec. engr., Mar., 1920; offg. exec. engr., Apl., 1927; confd., Oct., 1928; on depn. to Orissa, Apl. to Oct., 1936.

BARRETTO, WILLIAM LOUIS, O.B.E., B.A., late Burma Commn. (b. 5th Aug., 1882).—Previous service from Aug., 1909; dep. commr., May, 1924; O.B.E., June, 1932; ret'd., Aug., 1937.

BARRINGTON, JAMES, B.Sc., Indian C.S. (asst. commr., Burma) (b. 15th Aug., 1911).—Educated at Keble College, Oxford; joined the service, 21st Sept., 1936; arrived in India, Nov., 1936, and served in Burma as asst. commr.

BARRON, CLAUD ALEXANDER, C.S.I., C.I.E., C.V.O., late Indian C.S.—Educ. at the Aberdeen Gr. Sch., Aberdeen Univ., and Clare Coll., Cambridg; apptd. after exam. of 1890; arrived, 12th Nov., 1892, and served in the Punjab as asst. commr.; under sec. to govt., rev. dept., May, 1896, to Jan., 1899; dep. commr., March, 1902; sec. to committee on the survey of India, Nov., 1904, to July, 1905; C.I.E., Dec., 1911; chief sec. to govt., Jan., 1912; finl. sec. to govt., June, 1915; chief sec. to govt., Nov., 1915; dist. and sess. judge, July, 1916; commr. under Defence of India Act., Feb. to March, 1917, and May to June, 1917; temp. chief commr., Delhi, Nov., 1918; confirmed Nov., 1919; C.S.I., Jan., 1921; finl. commr. and sec. to govt. of Punjab, development dept., March, 1924; ret'd., May, 1928.

BARRON, DOUGLAS SHIELD, Indian C.S. (jt. mag., United Provs.) (b. 18th Mar., 1904).—Educ. at Archbishop Holgate's Sch., Barnsley, and Corpus Christi Coll., Cambridge; joined the service, 22nd Oct., 1926; arrived, 20th Dec., 1926, and served in the United Provs. as asst. mag. and collr.; offg. mag. and collr., Feb., 1933.

BARRON, WILLIAM JAMES, Indian Police (dist. supt., Burma) (b. 16th Apr., 1896).—Joined the service as asst. dist. supt., on 16th Dec., 1921; offg. dist. supt., June, 1923; confd., Sept., 1927.

BARROW, JOHN ROTHNEY, M.A., late Indian Educl. Service (b. 25th Oct., 1881).—Joined the service, 9th June, 1905, and served as inspr. in Assam; profsr., Dacca Coll., Jan., 1911; prinpl. of Chittagong Coll., Nov., 1915; offg. prinpl., presdy. coll., Calcutta, Nov., 1917, July, 1921, and again, July, 1929; confd., July, 1930; inspr. of schools, June, 1925; ret'd., Sept., 1932.

BARRY, DENIS THURBURN, late Indian Forest Service (Madras) (b. 23rd Mar., 1880).—Apptd. asst. consvr., Madras, 18th March, 1905; dist. forest offr., Oct., 1908; forest research officer, Coimbatore, Aug., 1919; dep. consvr., May, 1921; actg. consvr., Oct., 1923; consvr., June, 1925; ret'd., Oct., 1930.

BARTELS, REV. REGINALD CAIN, B.A., Indian Eccles. Estabt. (senr. chaplain), (b. 18th Nov., 1883).—Apptd. temporary chaplain, 6th June, 1921; junior chaplain, April, 1924; senr. chaplain, Aprl., 1934.

BARTER, ELDER GAUL, Indian C.S. (dist. and sess. judge, Madras) (b. 9th Jan., 1892).—Educ. at Sidney Sussex Coll., Cambridge, and University Coll., London; apptd. after exam. of 1915; arrived 6th Dec., 1916, and served in Madras as asst. collr. and mag.; on mil. duty from Nov., 1917, to Feb., 1919; sub. collr. and jt. mag., Feb., 1922; offg. dist. & sess. judge, June, 1928; confd., Sept., 1930.

BARTLETT, FREDERICK ROBERT, late India Office (b. 8th Aug., 1871).—Apptd. clerical officer, 2nd Aug., 1921; higher clerical officer (acting), Aug., 1922; confd., April, 1928; ret'd., Aug., 1936.

BARTLEY, CHARLES, B.A., Barr., Indian C.S. (judge, high ct., Calcutta) (b. 18th Dec., 1882).—Educ. at St. Columba's Coll., Londonderry, Methodist Coll., Belfast, and Trinity Coll., Dublin; apptd. after exam. of 1906; arrived 10th Dec., 1907, and served in Bengal as asst. mag. and collr.; jt. mag., and dep. collr., Sept., 1914; dist. and sess. judge, Jan., 1921; transfid. to Assam as dist. and sess. judge, Oct., 1930; supt. and membr. of legal affairs and secty. to govt., legal dept., Bengal, Nov., 1931; offg. judge high ct., Calcutta, April, 1932; Apl. and Nov., 1933; confd., Mar., 1934.

BARTLEY, FREDERICK DOUGLAS C.B.E., late Indian Police (b. 1st June, 1889).—Joined the service as proby. asst. supt., 27th Nov., 1908; asst. supt., June, 1910; supt., May, 1916; dep. commr., Calcutta, Mar., 1921; awarded King's police medal, 1923; C.B.E., Jan., 1932; awarded bar to King's police medal, Jan., 1933; ret'd., Aug., 1936.

BARTLEY, HERBERT GWYTHER, Indian Police (supt., Assam) (b. 18th Feb., 1893).—Joined the service as asst. supt. on 6th Dec., 1912; supt., Dec., 1923; offg. dep. inspr.-genl., Assam, Jan., 1936.

BARTLEY, JOHN, C.I.E., M.A., LL.B., Barr. Indian C.S. (jt. secty. to the govt. of India, legisl. dept.) (b. 2nd Mar., 1886).—Educ. at Campbell Coll., Belfast, and Trinity Coll., Dublin; apptd. after exam. of 1909; arrived in India, 30th Nov., 1910, and served in Bengal as asst. mag. and collr.; pol. agent, Hill Tippera (temp.), July, 1915; jt. mag. and dep. collr., Jan., 1916; dist. and sess. judge, Jan., 1924; offg. supt. and rembr. of legal affairs, Nov., 1923; offg. sec., govt. of Bengal, legisl. dept., April, 1924; confd. and sec., legisl. council, Aug., 1925; offg. jt. sec. and draftsman to Govt. of India, legisl. dept., Aprl., 1932; addl. ditto, Nov., 1932; offg. dep. secty. to the govt. of India, legisl. dept., Aprl., 1933; secty., council of state, Aprl., 1933; jt. secty. to govt. of India, legisl. dept., Oct., 1933; C.I.E., Jan., 1936.

BARTON, JAMES CALVERT, Indian State Rlys. (divl. transptn. supt., G.I.P. rly.) (b. 6th Aprl., 1881).—Joined the service, 26th Sept., 1902; asst. traffic supt., Sept., 1903; dist. ditto, May, 1910; dep. traffic manager, Oct., 1921; divl. transportation supt., Dec., 1922; offg. dep. ditto, June, 1928; dep. traffic manager, G.I.P. Rly., Nov., 1933; offg. ch. traffic manager ditto, Aprl., 1934.

BARTON, JOHN EVERARD, Indian State Rly. (dist. contr. of stores) (b. 30th Jan., 1889).—Joined the service, 16th Mar., 1921; resdt. engr., Feb., 1922; dist. contr. of stores, G.I.P. Rly., Dec., 1932.

BARTON, LESLIE ERIC, Lt.-Col., I.A. Indian Political Service (b. 3rd Feb., 1880).—First commn., 8th Sept., 1909; in mil. employ to May, 1919; asst. pol. agent, Khyber, May, 1919; dep. commr., Dera Ismail Khan, July, 1921; asst. commr., Hangu, Oct., 1922; dep. commr., Kohat, Mar., 1925; asst. commr., Thal, Oct., 1925; dep. commr., Bannu, April, 1926; pol. agent, Haraoti and Tonk, Oct., 1928; secty. to A.G.G. Deccan states and resdt., Kolhapur, Oct., 1933; offg. adjutant Kolhapur infy., Nov., 1933; resdt., Jaipur, Aprl., 1934;

offg. pol. agent, E. Rajputana states in addn., Aug., 1935; pol. agent, Bundelkhand, Nov., 1935.

BARTON, Sir WILLIAM PELL, K.C.I.E., C.S.I., late Indian C.S. (Punjab) (b. 29th, May, 1871).—Educ. at Bedford Modern Sch., Worcester Coll., Oxford, and Univ. Coll., London; apptd. after exam. of 1893; arrived, 17th Dec., 1894, and served in the Punjab as asst. commr.; under sec. to govt., Oct. to Nov., 1898; agent on special duty, Kurram, Sept., 1899, to Dec., 1902; asst. commr., N.W. Frontier Prov., Jan., 1903; dep. commr., Dec., 1904; pol. agent, Feb., 1906; div. judge, Peshawar, April, 1907; dep. commr., Dera Ismail Khan, Oct., 1907; dep. commr., Kohat, Feb., 1908; pol. agent, Dir., Swat and Chitral, March, 1910; ditto and rev. commr., N.W.F. Prov., Feb., 1911; sec. to chief commr., Dec., 1911; offg. judl. commr., April, 1912, to Jan., 1914; C.I.E., Jan., 1914; dep. commr., Kohat, Nov., 1914; judl. commr., N.W.F. Prov., Jan., 1915; resdt., and ditto, Aug., 1916; resdt., Baroda, May, 1919; ditto in Waziristan, and chief pol. offr. with Waziristan Fd. Fce. (in addn.) June 1919; resdt., Baroda, Sept., 1919; C.S.I., Jan., 1920; resdt., Mysore, and chief commr. of Coorg, Dec., 1920; resdt., Hyderabad, July, 1925; K.C.I.E., Jan., 1927; ret'd., Apl., 1930.

BARTY, DERMONT CAMPBELL, M.A., Indian C.S. (asst. collr., Bombay) (b. 27th Feb., 1914).—Educ. at Fettes and St. Andrew's Univ.; joined the service, 20th Sept., 1937; arrived in India, Nov., 1937, and served in Bombay as asst. collr.

BARUA, GURU PRASAD, B.L., Indian Police (supt., Assam) (b. Mar., 1888).—Joined the service, 28th Jan., 1914; dep. supt., Oct., 1921; supt., Sept., 1931.

BARUA, HARI PRASAD, Indian Service of Engrs. (exec. engr., Assam) (b. 12th Apl., 1892).—Joined the service, 1st Nov., 1916; asst. engr., Jan., 1920; exec. engr., Sept., 1928; asst. ch. engr. and under secty. to Govt., P.W.D., Assam, Mar., 1934.

BARUA, KANAK LAL, RAI BAHADUR, C.I.E., B.A., B.L., late Assam Civil Service.—Joined the service as extra asst. commr., 4th Sept., 1900; dep. mag. and dep. collr., May, 1906; dep. commr. and regr. of co-operative societies and director of industries, Assam, May, 1922; dir. of agriculture, Assam, Oct., 1923; ret'd., June, 1928; C.I.E., May, 1937.

BARVE, SADASHIV GOVIND, B.A., Indian C.S. (asst. collr., Bombay) (b. 27th April, 1914).—Educated at New English School, Budhwar Peth, Poona, Bombay University and St. John's College, Cambridge; joined the service, 21st Sept., 1936; arrived in India, Nov., 1936, and served in Bombay as asst. collr.

BASAK, KANTI CHANDRA, B.Sc. (Cal.), B.A. (Cantab.), Indian C.S. (jt. magte. and dep. collr., Bengal) (b. 17th Sept., 1901).—Educ. at Univ. of Calcutta and Downing Coll., Cambridge; joined the service, 18th Oct., 1926; arrived, Nov., 1926; and served in Bengal as asst. mag. and collr.; offg. under-secty. to govt. fin., &c., depts., Bengal, Nov., 1930; confd., Feb., 1931; offg. mag. and collr., May, 1932, and

again Feb., 1935; addl. dist. and sess. judge, Oct., 1932; offg. dist. and sess. judge, May, 1933; offg. asst. commr. of income tax, Oct., 1935.

BASANT LAL, LALA, late Income tax dept., Punjab (b. 15th Feb., 1880).—Joined the service, 2nd Apl., 1900, and held non-gazetted appts. to 3rd Dec., 1919; extra asst. commr., Feb., 1921; asst. commr. of inc. tax, Nov., 1926; ret'd., Feb., 1936.

BASHEER, AHMAD MUHAMMAD (see Ahmad M. B.).

BASHI RAM, SAHGAL, M.A., Indian Service of Engrs. (exec. engr., Punjab) (b. 10th Jan., 1889).—Joined the service as asst. engr. on 1st Oct., 1912; exec. engr., Oct., 1921; under-secty. to govt. P.W.D., Punjab, Mar., 1931; offg. suptg. engr., Mar., 1935.

BASHIR-UD-DIN, SAIYID, Barr., Judicial dept., Bihar (b. 11th Dec., 1885).—Apptd. dist. and sess. judge, 1st June, 1930.

BASKERVILLE, HENRY DAVENPORT, B.A., late Indian C.S. (Bombay) (b. 30th Nov., 1886).—Educ. at Eltham Coll., Kent, and Worcester Coll., Oxford; apptd. after exam. of 1910; arrived, 20th Jan., 1912, and served in Bombay as asst. collr. and mag. and asst. pol. agent; under sec. to govt., judl., &c., depts., 1918; passport officer in addition from Dec., 1919; offg. collr., dist. mag. and pol. agent, May, 1923; collr., May, 1930; ret'd., Apl., 1933.

BASSETT, THOMAS RALPH, Indian Police (supt., Punjab) (b. 25th July, 1894).—Joined the service 31st May, 1920; asst. supt., April, 1923; King's police medal, Jan., 1924; supt., June, 1925; asst. insp.-genl., rly. police, Oct., 1936.

BASTIN, REGINALD WALTER, B.A., Indian C.S. (asst. mag. and collr., Bengal) (b. 2nd April, 1910).—Educated at Trowbridge High School, University College and School of Oriental Studies, London; apptd. after exam. of 1931; arrived in India, December, 1932, and served in Bengal as asst. mag. and collr.

BASU, ADYA NATH, B.E., Indian Service of Engrs. (suptg. engr., Bengal) (b. 3rd Oct., 1886).—Joined the service, 11th Jan., 1911; asst. exec. engr., Jan., 1912; exec. engr., Jan., 1921; offg. suptg. engr., Feb., 1930; confd., Nov., 1934.

BASU, ASWANI KUMAR, M.A., B.L., late Excise and Salt dept., Bengal (b. Mar., 1882).—Joined the service as proby. sub. dep. collr., 14th Feb., 1907; confd. Jan., 1908; supt. of excise and salt, April, 1915; offg. dep. commr., May, 1924, and again May, 1927; confd., Aug., 1929; offg. commr., Jan., 1932; ret'd., Mar., 1937.

BASU, BIMALA PRASANA, B.Sc., B.A., Indian Forest Service (asst. consvr., Orissa) (b. 1st July, 1898).—Joined the service as asst. consvr., Bihar and Orissa, on 9th Nov., 1923.

BASU, BIRENDRA KUMAR, M.A., Indian C.S. (dist. and sess. judge, Bengal) (b. 8th Aug., 1888).—Educ. at schools in India, Calcutta univ., and Emmanuel Coll., Camb.; apptd. after exam. of 1912; arrived 20th Oct., 1913, and served as asst. mag. and collr. in Bengal; offg. dist. and sess. judge, May, 1919; confd., June, 1925.

BASU, JATINDRA KUMAR, B.L., late Indian Police Service (Assam) (b. 22nd Sept., 1876).—Joined the service, 26th March, 1906; supt., May, 1923; ret'd., Sept., 1931.

BASU, JOTENDRA MOHAN, Rai Bahadur, Babu, B.A., LL.B., late United Provs. Civil Service (b. 25th July, 1874).—Joined the service, 5th July, 1899; sub-judge, April, 1913; dist. and sess. judge, Oct., 1924; ret'd., Nov., 1929.

BASU, MRIGANKA MAULI, B.A., Indian C.S., (jt. mag. and dep. collr., Bengal) (b. 1st Mar., 1912).—Educated at the Scottish Church Collegiate School Calcutta, University of Calcutta, the School of Economics, London, and the School of Oriental Studies, London; joined the service, 29th Oct., 1935; arrived in India, Nov., 1935, and served in Bengal as asst. mag. and collr.; jt. mag. and dep. collr., Nov., 1936.

BASU, NABA KUMAR, B.Sc., A.M.I.M.E., Indian State Rlys. (transpn. (power) supt., E.I. rly.) (b. 21st Sept., 1892).—Joined the service, 12th Feb., 1918, as asst. dist. loco. supt.; asst. supt. (junr.), E.I. rly., Oct., 1926; transpn. (power) supt., E.I. rly., May, 1934.

BASU, PASHUPATI, M.A., B.L., late Bengal Civil Service (Judicial) (b. 5th Apl., 1877).—Joined the service, 20th Apl., 1902; munsiif, May, 1904; sub-judge, Aug., 1922; addl. dist. and sess. judge, Sylhet and Cachar, Jan., 1928; confd., Jan., 1929; ret'd., Apl., 1932.

BASU, PHANENDRA NATH, M.B., M.R.C.P., Lt.-Col., I.M.S., Medical Dept., United Provs. (civil surgeon) (b. 31st Oct., 1889).—First commn., 1st Nov., 1920; civil surgeon, United Provs., Mar., 1930.

BASU, PRABODH CHANDRA, Indian Service of Engrs. (b. 28th Jan., 1883).—Apptd. exec. engr., Bengal, 1st Jan., 1931.

BASU, SATISH CHANDRA, M.A., B.L., late Bengal Judicial dept. (b. 4th Nov., 1875).—Joined the service, 24th April, 1902; munsiif, Jan., 1905; sub-judge, July, 1923; small cause ct. judge, Nov., 1929; ret'd., Nov., 1930.

BASU, SUBODH KUMAR, B.Sc., Indian Forest Service (dep. consvr., Madras), (b. 7th Apl., 1900).—Joined the service on 9th Nov., 1923; dep. consvr., Nov., 1927.

BASU, SUKUMAR, B.A., Indian C.S. (dep. secty., home dept., Bengal) (b. 30th Oct., 1900).—Educ. at Calcutta Univ. and Jesus Coll., Oxford; joined the service, 23rd Oct., 1915; arrived, 21st Dec., 1925, and served in the Central Provs. as asst. commr.; offg. dist. and sess. judge, April, 1931; offg. mag. and collr., Sept., 1933; offg. dep. secty. to govt., Bengal, fin., comm. and marine dept., Oct., 1934; ditto, pol. and appt. depts., Mar., 1935; confd., Aug., 1935; dep. secty., Home dept., Apl., 1937.

BASU, SURESH CHANDRA, RAI BAHADUR, late Bengal Civil Service (b. June, 1883).—Joined the service, 13th June, 1904; dep. magte., Apl., 1905; magte. and collr., Jan., 1932; ret'd., Nov., 1937.

BATALIA RAM, India Posts and Tels. (divl. engr., tels.) (b. 20th Nov., 1887).—Joined the service as apprentice asst. supt., 19th Sept., 1910; asst. supt., Feb., 1913; asst. divl. engr., Aug., 1920; divl. engr., Oct., 1926.

BATES, HARRY STUART, B.A., Indian Civil Service (mag. and collr., United Provs.) (b. 16th March, 1893).—Educated at St. Catherine's Coll., Cambridge; arrived 11th May, 1920, and served in the United Provs. as asst. mag. and collr.; asst. commr., April, 1921, to Nov., 1923; city magistrate, July, 1925; offg. mag. and collr., April, 1926; confd., Apl., 1932; dept. sec. to govt., U.P. rev. dept. (tempy.), Oct., 1933. spec. manager, Court of Wards, Balrampur estate, Jan., 1935.

BATHEJA, HASSANAND RADHAKRISHNA, M.A., Indian Educl. Service (proftr., Patna Coll.) (b. 11th July, 1891).—Joined the service, 6th Nov., 1918; proftr. of economics, Ravenshaw coll., Cuttack, Jan., 1925; ditto, Patna coll., Oct., 1927; member, tariff bd., Jan., 1933; offg. dep. dir. of public instn., Bihar and Orissa, May, 1933; member, tariff bd., Oct., 1934, offg. princpl., Ravenshaw Coll., Cuttack, Sept., 1935; offg. dep. dir. of public instn., Bihar, Mar., 1936.

BATRA, DINA NATH, RAI BAHADUR, Indian State Rlys. (dep. chief engr.) (b. 4th Feb., 1888).—Joined the service as asst. engr., 21st Sept., 1911; exec. engr., Oct., 1920; offg. govt. inspr., Oct., 1932; offg. dep. ch. engr., E.B. rly., Mar., 1935; provl. perm., Aug., 1937.

BATRA, GIRIDHARI LAL, M.B., D.P.H., Dept. of Public Health, Bengal (b. 16th Mar., 1882).—Apptd. asst. dir. of public health, Mar., 1921; awarded K-i-H. medal, cl. II, Jan., 1936.

BATRA, HARGOBIND LAL, M.C., Lt.-Col., I.M.S. (civil surg., Assam) (b. 14th June, 1885).—First commn. dated 30th July, 1910; in mil. employ to 13th Nov., 1921; civil surg., May, 1925.

BATRA, KANSHI RAM, M.R.C.S., L.R.C.P., Lt.-Col., I.M.S. (civil surg., Punjab) (b. 10th Aug., 1890).—First commn. dated 16th July, 1915; civil surg., Jan., 1927.

BATRA, RAM KANWAR, B.A., Indian Service of Engrs. (exec. engr., Central Provs.), (b. 8th Aug., 1898).—Joined the service, 14th Oct., 1922; apptd. to Punjab Engrg. Service, 14th Oct., 1923; asst. exec. engr., Central Provs., June, 1924; offg. exec. engr., Apl., 1929, and again, Jan., 1933; confd., June, 1933.

BATTEN, HENRY HALLET, Lt.-Col., I.A., ret'd., late Burma Commn.—Served in mil. dept., 8th May, 1901, to Nov., 1905; employed with mil. police, Nov., 1905, to May, 1908; asst. commr., May, 1908; on mil. service, Dec., 1914, to Aug., 1919; dep. commr. (provl. sub.), Feb., 1924; confd., Oct., 1925; offg. commr., May, 1929; confd., May, 1936; ret'd., Oct., 1936.

BATTEN, SIR JOHN KAYE, Kt., late Indian C.S.—Educ. at Haileybury Coll., and at Ariel Coll., Oxford; apptd. after exam. of 1884; arrived, 29th Nov., 1887, and served in Madras as asst. collr. and mag.; special asst., Malabar, Nov., 1893; head asst., Jan., 1895; dep. commr., Berar, April, 1899; civil and sess. judge, Feb., 1900; addl. judl. commr., Central Prov., Sept., 1905; 1st addl. judl. commr., ditto, Dec., 1906; judl. commr. Dec., 1921; Kt. Jan., 1924; ret'd., Sept., 1923.

BATTEN, KENNETH CHARLES, Office of High Commr. for India (exec. officer, trade dept.) (b. 5th Aug., 1890).—Appd. 2nd class clerk, 9th March, 1914; on mil. service from Jan., 1917 to Jan., 1919; transf. to office of high commr. for India, Oct., 1920; exec. officer, April, 1922; higher clerical officer (tempy.), May, 1926.

BATTERSBY, ERIC WORSLEY, Indian Police (b. 18th Apl., 1916).—Appd. asst. supt., Burma, on probn., 6th Dec., 1935.

BATTYE, RICHMOND KEITH MOLESWORTH, Capt. I.A., Indian Political Service (b. 30th Aug., 1905).—First commn., 3rd Sept., 1925; in military employ until 28th Jan., 1931; asst. pol. agent, Sibi, June, 1932; under sect. to A.G.G., Balu, Feb., 1933; asst. pol. agent, Loralai, May, 1933; ditto, Quetta-Pishin, May, 1934; Brit. trade agent, Gyantse, June, 1935; offg. pol. officer in Sikkim in addn., Nov., 1935; under secty. to A.G.G., Punjab States, Aug., 1936; asst. pol. agent, Gilgit, Oct., 1937.

BATTYE, WALTER ROTHNEY, D.S.O., M.B., B.Sc., F.R.C.S., Lt.-Col. I.M.S. (rettd.).—First commission, 27th July, 1888; offg. agency surgeon, Gwalior, Aug., 1903; agency surgeon and vice-consul, Meshed, Nov., 1904; residency surgeon, Mewar, May, 1910; on mil. duty, Nov., 1914, to Dec., 1916; administrative medical officer, Central India, Jan., 1921; residency surgeon, Indore, in addition from Feb., 1922; rettd., Jan., 1929.

BAUM, ERNEST FREDERICK, B.A., Indian C.S. (commr., Burma) (b. 4th Oct., 1881).—Educ. at Manchester Gr. Sch., and Keble Coll., Oxford; apptd. after exam. of 1904; arrived, 9th Dec., 1905, and served in Burma as asst. commr.; dep. commr., Dec., 1917; on mil. duty, May, 1918, to July, 1918; offg. commr. May, 1924; confd., Mar., 1932.

BAVDEKAR, RAJARAM SHRIPAD, B.A. (Cantab.), Indian C.S. (dist. and sess. judge, Bombay) (b. 16th Sept., 1898).—Educ. at Bombay and Cambridge (Sidney Sussex); joined the service, 2nd Nov., 1922; arrived 9th Force 1923, and served in Bombay as asst. collr. and mag.; offg. dist. and sess. judge, Oct., 1931; and again, Sept., 1932; confd., Oct., 1933; regr., high ct., Bombay, appellate side, June, 1934, to June, 1937.

BAVIN, LANCELOT NEVILLE, late Indian Police (b. 12th May, 1892).—Joined the service as proby. asst. supt. on 4th Dec., 1911; confd., Apl., 1912; supt., May, 1925; rettd., Apl., 1937.

BAW, MAUNG SHWE, M.A., Indian C.S. (asst. commr. Burma) (b. 28th April, 1904).—Educ. at University of Rangoon and Wadham Coll., Oxford; joined the service, 8th Oct., 1929; arrived, Nov., 1929, and served in Burma as asst. commr.

BAWA KARTAR SINGH, M.A., D.Sc., F.I.C., Indian Educl. Service (prof. of chemistry, science coll., Patna) (b. 17th Apl., 1886).—Joined the service, 19th July, 1911; prof. Cuttack, Nov., 1921; offg. prof., science coll., Patna, Jan., 1936; confd., Aug., 1936.

BAWA RAMNICK SINGH, Diwan Bahadur, M.I.S.E., late Indian State Railways (b. 18th Mar., 1880).—Trained at the R.I.E. Coll.; joined the service as asst. engr., state rys., 1st Oct., 1902; exec. engr., Oct., 1909; Rai

Bahadur, Jan., 1915; dep. chief engr., Feb., 1927; offg. ch. engr., April, 1929; confd., June, 1930; offg. agent, Mar., 1932; confd., June, 1934; rettd., Mar., 1935.

BAXTER, ERIC GORDON, late Indian Forest Engrg. Service (b. 8th May, 1884).—Joined the service as forest engr., Bihar and Orissa, 24th Nov., 1921; rettd., Mar., 1933.

BAXTER, GEORGE HERBERT, M.A., India Office (actg. asst. secty., finl. dept.) (b. 30th Nov., 1894).—Educ. at Manchester Gr. Sch. and New Coll., Oxford; war service: Roy. Fus., Sept., 1914, to Jan., 1915; R.G.A., Jan., 1915, to Armistice; apptd. jun. clerk, mil. dept., July, 1920; transf. to fin. dept., Jan., 1921; asst. principal, April, 1921; deputed on special mission to Baghdad, Oct. to Dec., 1921; actg. pte. sec. to perm. under sec. of state, Jan., 1924; appt. as pte. sec. confd., April, 1924; principal, Nov., 1924; sec., Royal Commn. on Indian Currency and Finance, Aug., 1925, to July, 1926; adviser to British delegation, Indian round table conf., London, 2nd session, Sept.-Dec., 1931; actg. asst. secty., finl. dept., Jan.-Apl., 1932; and again, Dec., 1933; secty., cte. on Indian Reserve Bank legislation, July-Aug., 1933.

BAXTER, GERALD DORRETT, A.M.I.C.E., Indian Service of Engrs. (exec. engr., Central Prov.) (b. 16th Nov., 1894).—Joined the service as asst. engr., 7th April, 1920; asst. exec. engr., March, 1921; exec. engr., Oct., 1927.

BAXTER, NORMAN BERNARD, Indian Service of Engrs. (suprg. engr., Bombay) (b. 12th June, 1883).—Trained at the R.I.E. College; joined, Oct., 1905, as asst. engr.; exec. engr., Oct., 1913; offg. suptg. engr., March, 1928, again Feb., 1930; ditto (prov. perm.), Nov., 1932; confd., Nov., 1934; offg. ch. engr., April, 1934.

BAILEY, ARCHIBALD STUART BUTTERWORTH, late Indian Audit and Accounts Service (b. 8th July, 1885).—Joined the service, 1st Oct., 1907; asst. acct.-genl., P.W. accts., July, 1910; ditto, Bombay, Oct., 1921; currency offr. Karachi, July, 1923; dep. acct.-genl., Bombay, Jan., 1925; asst. acct.-genl., ditto, Nov., 1925; divl. audit offr., N.W. Rly., Oct., 1927; dep. auditor E.B. Rly., Feb., 1928; auditor, workshop, Kanchrapara, March, 1928; govt. examr., M. & S.M. and S.I. Rlys., Apl., 1929; dep. govt. examr. of accts., S.I. Rly., Apl., 1930; rettd., Sept. 1934.

BAILEY, VERNON THOMAS, Indian Police (asst. supt., N.W.F.P.).—Joined the service as asst. supt., Punjab, 20th Nov., 1928; awarded King's police medal, Jan., 1933; asst. dir., intell. bureau, Home Dept., Mar., 1937.

BAILEY, VICTOR, C.I.E., C.B.E., A.M.I.C.E., late Indian State Railways (N. W. Rly.) (b. 30th May, 1880).—Joined the service, 15th Sept., 1905; asst. engr., Sept., 1908; exec. engr., Oct., 1914; asst. sec. rly. bd., Jan., 1914; C.I.E., Jan., 1917; on mil. service in Mesopotamia from Jan., 1918; offg. supt. of works, Khyber Rly. construction, March, 1924; C.B.E., June, 1926; contr. of stores, N.W. Rly., April, 1927; rettd., May, 1931.

BAYLIS, HERBERT THEOPHILUS, late Indian State Railways (E.B. Rly.) (b. 20th Mar., 1876).—Joined the service, 10th March,

1893; asst. traffic supt., Oct., 1907; dist. traffic supt., April, 1914; dep. traffic manager (provl.), Dec., 1927; retd., Mar., 1931.

BAYLIS, ROBERT FRANCIS SARJEANT, Indian C.S. (jt. mag. United Provs.) (b. 11th June, 1903).—Educ. at Christ's Hospital and Lincoln Coll., Oxford; joined the service, 20th Oct., 1927; arrived, Dec., 1927, and served in the United Provs. as asst. mag. and collr.; jt. mag., July, 1929; offg. sess. and sub-judge, Oct., 1932; addl. dist. and sess. judge, Apr., 1934.

BAYNES, FREDERIC WILLIAM WILBERFORCE, B.A., Indian C.S. (mag. and collr., United Provs.) (b. 4th Aug., 1889).—Educ. at Harrow and Caius Coll., Cambridge; apptd. after the exam. of 1912; arrd., 22nd Nov., 1913, and served in the United Prov. as asst. mag. and collr.; jt. mag., Nov., 1917; asst. supt., Dehra Dun, Nov., 1920; offg. mag. and collr., Nov., 1923; confd., Nov., 1929; dep. commr., Mar., 1930.

BAZALGETTE, JOHN EVELYN ARNOLD, Capt., I.A., Indian Political Service (b. 24th Jan., 1907).—First commn., 30th Aug., 1926 in military employ prior to 11th Oct., 1932; under training in Punjab, Oct., 1932; P.A. to Resident at Hyderabad, Jan., 1933; under secty. to the A.G.G., Punjab States, Aug., 1934; under secty. to the Pol. Resdt., Persian Gulf, June, 1935; H.M.'s Consul, Kerman, Nov., 1935; vice-consul, Khorramshahr, Apr., 1936.

BAZALGETTE, REGINALD LOUIS, Major I.A., Indian Political Service (b. 8th July, 1900).—First commn., dated 15th April, 1919; in milty. employ prior to 17th April, 1926; asst. mag. and collr., Meerut, April, 1926; and asst. to resdt., Kashmir, June, 1926; asst. mag., Moradabad, Nov., 1926; under sec. to pol. resdt. in Persian Gulf, April, 1927; under sec. to agent to govt. genl. in W.I. States, Apr., 1929; ditto and addl. dist. mag., Rajkot, Oct., 1929; pol. agent, Eastern Kathiawar agency, Aug., 1931; under secty. to A.G.G. in Western India, Nov., 1931; offg. under-secty., for. and pol. dept., Oct., 1932; secty. to A.G.G. and ch. commr. in Baluchistan, Mar., 1933; asst. pol. agent and asst. commr., Quetta-Pishin, Oct., 1933; asst. to pol. agent in Kalat, Apr., 1934; H.M.'s consul for Kerman and offg. vice-consul, Bandar, Abbas, Nov., 1935; secty. to A.G.G., Deccan States, Aug., 1936.

BEADNELL, CHRISTOPHER BEADNELL, I.S.O., late Excise Dept., Madras (b. 20th Jan., 1880).—Apptd. asst. commr., June, 1916; offg. dep. commr., May, 1930; I.S.O., June, 1932; retd., Jan., 1935.

BEADON, CORNWALLIS WILLIAM ROGER, Indian Police (b. 11th Oct., 1902).—Joined the service as asst. dist. supt., Burma, on 14th Nov., 1922; offg. dist. supt., Mar., 1932.

BEADON, ERIC HAMMETT FAIRFAX, Indian Police (b. 19th Mar., 1907).—Joined the service as asst. dist. supt., Burma, 10th Dec., 1926.

BEADON, VERNON, Lt.-Col., M.C., I.A., Burma commn. (dep. commr.) (b. 14th Apr., 1884).—Served in mil. dept., Aug., 1903, to Jan., 1910; asst. commr., Feb., 1910; on mil. service, Dec., 1914, to Oct. 1919; offg. dep. commr., May, 1920; dep. commr. (provl.), Nov., 1925; confd., July, 1927; offg. commr., Aug., 1933.

BEAL, HASTINGS EVELYN, late Indian C.S. (Bihar and Orissa) (b. 19th Sept., 1883).—Educ. at Charterhouse and Balliol Coll., Oxford; apptd. after exam. of 1906; arrived 28th Nov., 1907, and served in Bengal as asst. mag. and collr.; transf. to Bihar and Orissa, April, 1912; jt. mag. and dep. collr., Nov., 1913; temply. serving under govt. of India, army dept., from Sept., 1916, to March, 1919; dep. commr., May, 1922; mag. and collr., Sept., 1924; retd., Nov., 1929.

BEAMAN, GERALD WILLIAM DEVENISH, Indian State Rlys. (dist. traffic supt., Burma Rlys.) (b. 18th Nov., 1887).—Joined the service as asst. traffic supt., 24th Mar., 1909; dist. traffic supt., Sept., 1924; asst. traffic supt., Mar., 1928; dist. traffic supt. (tempy.), Sept., 1928; offg. dep. traffic manager (transptn.), Burma rlys., Nov., 1933.

BEAMAN, VALENTINE ARDEN HULME, M.B.E., Burma Excise Service (chief supt.) (b. 14th Feb., 1884).—Previous service from May, 1911; asst. supt. excise, June, 1912; supt., Sept., 1913; chief supt., May, 1933.

BEAN, BASIL CLARENCE LAHEY, O.B.E., B.A., Indian State Rlys. (divl. supt., N.W. Rly.) (b. 4th Sept., 1886).—Joined the service as asst. traffic supt., 5th Nov., 1909; dist. ditto, Oct., 1918; divl. trans. traffic officer, Oct., 1924; offg. divl. supt., Apr., 1930, confd., Dec., 1932; O.B.E., Nov., 1935.

BEAN, SALVADOR MICHAEL LAHEY, late Indian Audit and Accounts Service (b. 14th Oct., 1882).—Joined the service, 1st June, 1907; asst. acct. genl., P.W.D., July, 1910; dep. acct. genl., U.P., March, 1919; ditto, Bombay, Sept., 1921; audit offr., Bombay dev. scheme, March, 1922; audit and accounts offr., ditto, Oct., 1923; finl. advr., P.W.D., Punjab, Apr., 1929 (tempy.); offg. audit officer, Lloyd barrage and canals constrn., Mar., 1930; ch. auditor, E.B. Rly. and Burma Rlys., Nov., 1931; ditto, N.W. rly., Apr., 1932; retd., July, 1935.

BEASLEY, SIR HORACE OWEN COMPTON, Kt., B.A. (Cantab.), Barr., late Madras Judicial Dept. (b. 2nd July, 1877).—Apptd. judge, high ct., Rangoon, 21st Mar., 1923; judge, high ct., Madras, Aug., 1924; chief justice, ditto, June, 1929; Kt., June, 1930; retd., July, 1937.

BEATSON, ERNEST BRUCE, V.D., late Indian State Railways (b. 17th Aug., 1874).—Trained at the R.I.E. Coll.; joined the service, 1st March, 1901, as asst. engr., state rlys.; exec. engr., March, 1908; asst. manager, N.W. Rly., Sept., 1908; asst. agent, ditto, Jan. to July, 1913; offg. supt. engr., June, 1918; confd., April, 1924; offg. senr. govt. inspr. of rlys., Oct., 1925; retd., Aug., 1929.

BEATTY, HENRY, B.A., late Indian C.S. (b. 3rd June, 1891).—Educ. at Trinity Coll., Dublin; apptd. after exam. of 1914; arrived, 3rd Dec., 1915, and served in the United Prov. as asst. commr.; apptd. to the I.A.R.O., Oct., 1917; reverted from mil. duty, Jan., 1919; jt. mag., June, 1922; offg. dist. and sess. judge, March, 1923; sess. and sub. judge, Sept., 1927; retd., June, 1929.

BEAUMONT, HERBERT CHRISTOPHER, B.A., Indian C.S. (asst. commn., Punjab) (b. 2nd June,

1912).—Educated at Uppingham and Worcester College, Oxford; appointed after exam. of 1935; arrived in India, Dec., 1936, and served in the Punjab as asst. commr.

BEAUMONT, SIR JOHN WILLIAM FISHER Kt., K.C. (b. 4th Sept., 1877).—Apptd. chief justice, high ct., Bombay, June, 1930 Kt., Jan., 1931.

BEAZLEY, JOHN GODFREY, C.I.E., B.A. late Indian C.S. (Punjab) (b. 9th Feb., 1885).—Educ. at Rugby and Trinity Coll., Oxford; apptd. after exam. of 1907; arrived, 30th Nov., 1908, and served in the Punjab, as asst. commr.; sec., Delhi Municipality, Feb., 1914; under-sec. to govt., Punjab, rev. dept., Oct., 1915; asst. settl. offr., Nov., 1917; coln. offr., Lower Bari Doab Canal, Aug., 1918; addl. dep. sec. to govt. of India, commerce dept., Feb., 1920; offg. dir. of industries, Punjab, Oct., 1922; dep. commr. (provl.), Sept., 1923; sec. to govt. of Punjab, transf. depts., Oct., 1924; dep. commr. (perm.), Aug., 1925; secty. to govt., transferred depts., Jan., 1927; C.I.E., June, 1928; offg. ch. secty. to govt., Punjab, Nov., 1928; ret'd., Dec., 1931.

BECKETT, JAMES, Indian Police (supt., Madras) (b. 25th Mar., 1891).—Joined the service, 16th Dec., 1911; asst. supt., July, 1913; offg. supt., July, 1919; confd., Sept., 1926; asst. inspr.-genl., Mar., 1927; dep. commr., crime bch., Oct., 1932; awarded Indian Police medal, Jan., 1933.

BECKETT, RICHARD HENRY, C.S.I., C.I.E., B.Sc., (London), late Indian Educ. Service (b. 3rd Jan., 1882)—Apptd. prof. of chemistry and physics, Victoria Technical Inst., Nagpur, 1st July, 1906; principal, ditto, July, 1908; inspr. of European Schools and Science, Central Provs., Jan., 1915; principal, Victoria coll. of science, Nagpur, July, 1917; offg. dir. of public instrn. and sec. to govt. of Central Provs., educ. dept., Nov., 1924; C.I.E., June, 1928; offg. dir. of public instrn., Bombay, Jan., 1930; confd., May, 1930; member, legisl. council, Mar., 1932; C.S.I., June, 1934; ret'd., Aug., 1936.

BECKETT, RONALD BRYMER, B.A., Indian C.S. (secty. to govt. legis. dept., Punjab) (b. 17th Jan., 1891).—Educ. at Lincoln Coll., Oxford; apptd. after exam. of 1913; arrived, 30th Nov., 1914, and served in the Punjab as asst. commr.; guardian to H.H. Nawab of Mamdot, Oct., 1915, to Jan., 1917; sec., municipal cttee., Lahore, March, 1923; offg. dep. commr., Oct., 1925; offg. dist. and sess. judge, May, 1926; registrar, high ct., Lahore, and addl. dist. and sess. judge, April, 1927; services placed at disposal of Govt. of India, Mar., 1932; addl. judge, high ct., Lahore, May, 1934; secty. legis. dept., Feb., 1937.

BEDDY, EDWIN SIDNEY LANGDON, late Indian Service of Engrs. (b. 9th Sept., 1875).—Joined the service, 31st Dec., 1902, and held various temporary appts.; asst. engr., Feb., 1908; exec. engr., Jan., 1909; supt. engr., March, 1920; offg. ch. engr. and secty. to govt., P.W.D., Oct., 1925; ret'd., Sept., 1930.

BEDEKAR, GOPAL VAMAN, B.Sc., Indian C.S. (asst. collr., Bombay) (b. 10th Oct., 1908).—Educ. at the High School, Miraj, Bombay University, Battersea Polytechnic, London, and

the School of Oriental Studies, London; joined the service, 6th Oct., 1933; arrived in India, Nov., 1933, and served in Bombay as asst. collr.

BEDFORD, JAMES DOUGLAS HARDY, Indian Service of Engrs. (ch. engr., Punjab) (b. 1st June, 1884).—Trained at the R.I.E. Coll.; joined the service, 1st Oct., 1905; asst. engr., Nov., 1905; exec. engr., Oct., 1913; offg. supt. engr., Jan., 1926; confd., May, 1930; offg. ch. engr., and secty. to govt., Punjab, p.w.d., irrign. bch., May, 1933; confd., Jan., 1935.

BEDI, KUNWAR DAYA SINGH, Capt., I.A., Indian Political Service (b. 27th Jan., 1899).—First commn., 14th July, 1921; in milty. employ to Sept., 1928; asst. pol. agent in southern states and Central India, Sept., 1931; under secty. to agent to govt.-genl., in the states of Western India; Mar., 1932; under-secy. to A.G.G. in Rajputana, Oct., 1933; asst. commr., Thal, Sept., 1935; ditto, Kohat, Apl., 1937; under secty. to A.G.G. Resident and Chief Commr., Baluchistan, Dec., 1937.

BEDI, SHEO SINGH (see Sheo Singh Bedi).

BEDI, TARLOCHAN DAS, Indian C.S. (asst. commr., Punjab) (b. 25th Dec., 1905).—Educ. at Punjab University and London School of Oriental Studies; joined the service, 10th Oct., 1930; arrived in India, Nov., 1930; and served in the Punjab as asst. commr.; offg. dist. and sess. judge, Mar., 1936.

BEELEY, REV. BEN DARCY, late Indian Eccl. Estabt. (b. 8th Mar., 1875).—Joined the service, 25th Dec., 1908; junr. chaplain, Dec., 1911; on mil. duty, 6th Aug., 1916, to 30th April, 1917; senr. chaplain Dec., 1921; ret'd. June, 1928.

BEESON, CYRIL FREDERICK CHANINGTON, M.A., D.Sc., Indian Forest Service (forest entomologist, Dehra Dun) (b. 10th Feb., 1889).—Joined the service, 25th Nov., 1911; asst. consvr., Nov., 1912; dep. consvr., Nov., 1916; forest zoologist, July, 1920; forest entomologist, Dec., 1926; offg. consvr., variously from Mar., 1931; confd., Dec., 1936; offg. inspr.-genl. of forests and presdt., forest research inst., Dehra Dun, Aug., 1935.

BEEVOR, RALPH BRANTHWAYT, Indian C.S. (dist. and sess. judge, Bihar) (b. 29th April, 1895).—Educ. at Felsted and Emmanuel Coll., Cambridge; joined the service, 2nd Oct., 1919; arrived 1st Dec., 1919, and served in Bihar and Orissa as asst. mag. and collr.; offg. dep. commr., March, 1926; offg. dist. and sess. judge, Jan., 1927; confd., Nov., 1932.

BEHARI LAL, UPPAL, LALA, Indian Service of Engrs. (exec. engr., Punjab) (b. 19th Apl., 1888).—Joined the service, 15th Sept., 1910; asst. engr., Sept., 1911; exec. engr., Oct., 1920; offg. supt. engr., Apl., 1933.

BEIT, FRANCIS VICTOR OWEN, Lt.-Col., I.M.S. (ret'd.), M.D. (Lond.), late Burma Medical Dept. (b. 14th Aug., 1875).—Arrived 20th April, 1900, and served in mil. dept.; offg. civil surgn., June, 1902; civil surgn., May, 1907; on mil. service, Oct., 1914, to Dec., 1920; ret'd., Aug., 1929.

BELHAVEN AND STENTON, LORD, C.I.E., Lieut.-Col., I.A. (ret'd.).—First commn., dated 8th Nov., 1890; in mil. employ to June, 1898; pol. asst., March, 1901; asst. to resdt. in Mewar, Nov., 1901; asst. resdt. in Kashmir, May, 1905; asst. commr., Ajmer, Oct., 1908; Guardian to H.H. Maharajah of Indore, April, 1910; specl. offr. for Delhi Durbar work in Central India, Aug., 1911; special duty in Central India, Feb., 1912; asst. resdt., Mewar, Oct., 1912; pol. offr. with the Maharajah Holker of Indore, April, 1913; first asst. to agent to govr.-gen. in Rajputana, and chief commr. of Ajmer-Merwara, Oct., 1913; asst. resdt., Mewar, Nov., 1913; first asst. to agent to govr.-gen. in Rajputana, Dec., 1913; ditto, to pol. resdt., Persian Gulf, April, 1915; pol. agent, Koweit, June, 1916; C.I.E., Aug., 1918; 1st asst. to agent to govr.-gen. in Rajputana, Dec., 1918; finl. member, state council, Jodhpur, from March, 1919; ret'd., Mar., 1923.

BELL, SIR CHARLES ALFRED, K.C.I.E., C.M.G., late Indian C.S.—Educ. at Winchester, and New Coll., Oxford; apptd. after exam. of 1889; arrived, 3rd Nov., 1891, and served in Bengal as asst. mag. and collr. and asst. settl. officer; joint mag. and dep. collr., June, 1898; mag. and collr., Feb. 1904; asst. pol. officer, Sikkim, May, 1904, to Nov., 1905; mag. and collr. and offg. pol. officer, Sikkim, April, 1908; pol. offr., ditto, from Oct., 1908; transfed. to Bihar and Orissa cadre, April, 1912; specl. duty with Tibet conference, Sept., 1913, to Aug., 1914; C.M.G., Jan., 1915; C.I.E., June, 1919; ret'd., March, 1919; K.C.I.E., Jan. 1922; author of a 'Manual of Colloquial Tibetan.'

BELL, CYRIL FRANCIS, C.I.E., Indian Forest Service (ch. consvr., Central Provs.) (b. 23rd Feb., 1883).—Joined the service, 20th Nov., 1905, as asst. consvr., Central Provs.; dep. consvr., Dec., 1910; offg. consvr., March, 1921; transfed. to Burma as dep. consvr., Apl., 1923; consvr., Dec., 1925; re-transfd. dep. consvr., Central Provs., Nov., 1926; offg. consvr., April, 1927; confd., Nov., 1927; offg. ch. consvr., Apl., 1933; and again, Apl., 1935; confd., Aug., 1936; C.I.E., Jan., 1938.

BELL, SIR ERNEST ALBERT SEYMOUR, Kt., C.I.E., A.M.I.C.E., F.C.H., late Railway Dept.—Trained at R.I.E. Coll.; apptd. asst. engr., 1st Oct., 1888, and posted to state rlys.; lent to Assam-Bengal rly. co. from Jan., 1895, to Nov., 1902; exec. engr., July, 1898; asst. sec., public works dept., rly. branch, Dec., 1903, to Jan., 1905; dep. manager, E. B. state rly., March, 1908; suptg. engr., N.W. rly., March, 1913; agent, E. Bengal rly., June, 1915; C.I.E., Jan., 1919; agent, N.-W. ry., Jan., 1919; member, rly. board, Jan., 1920; Kt., June, 1923; govt. dir. of Indian rly. cos., India Office, Oct., 1923; ret'd., Oct., 1926; member, Ind. staty. rly. bd. ctee., June, 1933.

BELL, FRANK OWEN, B.A., Indian C.S. (jt. mag. and dep. collr., Bengal) (b. 2nd June, 1907).—Educ. at Christ's Hospital and Christ's College, Cambridge; joined the service, 16th Oct., 1930; arrived in India, Nov., 1930, and served in Bengal as asst. collr. and mag.; jt. mag. and dep. collr., Dec., 1931.

BELL, HERBERT JASPER, A.M.I.C.E., late Indian Service of Engineers (b. 4th May, 1886).—Joined the service as asst. engr. on 1st Oct., 1909; exec. engr., March, 1920; offg. suptg. engr., Delhi, May, 1926; ret'd., Jan., 1935.

BELL, JAMES LOGIE, late Indian Forest Service (b. 18th Dec., 1894).—Joined the service, 14th Feb., 1921, as asst. consvr.; dep. consvr., Feb., 1922; ret'd., Aug., 1932.

BELL, JOHN ALTON, Indian State Rlys. (agent, E.I. Rly.) (b. 27th July, 1887).—Joined the service as asst. engr., 29th Oct., 1910; in milty. employ from 5th Oct., 1915, to 18th Sept., 1919; services lent to Bengal-Nagpur Rly. Co. from 7th March, 1923; offg. exec. engr., E.I. Rly., April, 1927; offg. divl. supt., Feb., 1920; confd., June, 1933; offg. agent., E.I. rly., Oct., 1935; confd., Jan., 1936; dep. ch. engr. (provl. perm.), Dec., 1935.

BELL, JOHN MICHAEL GEOFFREY, B.A., Indian C.S. (asst. mag. and collr., Bengal) (b. 17th Apl., 1913).—Educ. at Winchester, Marlborough Coll., and Caius Coll., Cambridge; joined the service, 6th Jan., 1938; arrived in India, Feb., 1938, and served in Bengal as asst. mag. and collr.

BELL, SIR ROBERT DUNCAN, K.C.S.I., C.I.E., M.A., B.Sc., late Indian C.S. (b. 18th May, 1878).—Educ. at Heriot's Sch., Edinburgh, and Edinburgh Univ.; apptd. after exam. of 1901; arrived, 12th Dec., 1902, and served in Bombay as asst. collr. and mag.; under sec. to govt., rev. and fin. depts., June, 1910, to Oct., 1912; asst. settl. officer, Nov., 1913; sec. to the Indian Industrial Commn., June, 1916; controller of industrial intelligence, under Indian Munitions Board, March, 1917; temp. dir. of industries and controller of munitions, 1919; C.I.E., June, 1919; offg. sec. to govt. of Bombay, development dept., July, 1924; confd., June, 1925; offg. dir. of development, Apl., 1926; member of legisl. council, 1927, 1928, 1930, and 1931; offg. ch. secy. to govt., rev. dept., Mar., 1930; confd., July, 1930; C.S.I., Jan., 1932; commr., Jan., 1932; member, govr.'s exec. council, Bombay, Jan., 1933; K.C.S.I., June, 1935; actg. govr. of Bombay, May, 1936; ret'd., Dec., 1937.

BELL, ROBERT WALLACE, late Indian Police (b. 6th Nov., 1891).—Joined the service as proby. asst. supt. on 28th Nov., 1912; confd., Nov., 1914; supt., Jan., 1925; ret'd., Jan., 1935.

BELL, ROGER CECIL SUTHERLAND, Indian Civil Service (sub. collr., Madras) (b. 4th Feb., 1905).—Educ. at Fettes, Worcester Coll., Oxford, and Sch. of Oriental Studies, London; joined the service, 9th Oct., 1928; arrived, Nov., 1928, and served in Madras as asst. collr. and magte.; sub-collr., Mar., 1931; offg. under secty. to govt., Madras, public dept., June, 1934; served temp. with govt. of Orissa from Apl., 1936.

BELL, ROLAND STANLEY, late Indian Police (b. 8th Oct., 1882).—Joined the service, 19th Nov., 1903, and posted to United Provs.; asst. supt. of police, Sept., 1907; supt., Oct., 1915; on military service, July, 1918, to Apl., 1919;

offg. dep. inspr.-genl., May, 1928; confd., Nov., 1932; I.G. rly. police and police asst. to A.G.G. Rajputana; ret'd., Oct., 1937.

BELVALKAR, Dr. SHRIPAD KRISHNA, M.A., Ph.D. (Harvard), late Indian Educlt. Service (b. 11th Dec., 1881).—Held non-gazetted appts. in educl. dept., Bombay, from 5th Nov., 1906, to 10th Mar., 1915; prof., Deccan Coll., Poona, Mar., 1915; I.E.S. from 5th Feb., 1921; K-i-H medal, 2nd class, June, 1931; ret'd., Dec., 1936.

BEMROSE, WILLIAM HENRY, Indian Police (b. 21st June, 1906).—Joined the service as asst. supt., Bengal, on 23rd Nov., 1925; offg. addl. supt. Apl., 1932.

BENCE-JONES, PHILIP REGINALD, M.A., M.Inst.C.E., A.M.I.M.E. (b. 12th Jan., 1897).—Apptd. principl., Maclagan engrg. coll., 29th Mar., 1934.

BENEGAL, SANJIVA RAO (see Sanjiva Rao, R.)

BENEST, FRANCIS EDWARD, M.I.E.E., late Office of High Commr. for India (b. 12th July, 1871).—Educ. at Merchant Taylors' School; 1st asst., Bournemouth elec. works, Apl., 1895; station supt., Dover elec. works, July, 1895; sen. asst., Madrid elec. works, Sept., 1896 to 1898; chief asst., Bournemouth and Poole elec. works, Aug., 1899; apptd. asst. examr. of stores, India store depôt, 1st June, 1901; inspr. of scientific supplies, 1st March, 1905; dep. supt., India store depôt, Aug., 1917; transfed. to office of high commr. for India, Oct., 1920; supt., India store depôt, Aug., 1923; dir. of inspection, April, 1927; ret'd., July, 1936.

BENJAMIN, HERBERT DANIEL, B.A. (Oxon.), Indian C.S. (district and sessions judge) (b. 4th March, 1899).—Educ. at Monmouth Gr. Sch., and Oxford (Jesus); joined the service, 31st Oct., 1922; arrived, 13th Dec., 1922, and served in Bengal as asst. mag. and collr.; and sub. divl. officer, June, 1924; under-secty. to govt. of Bengal, finl., &c., dept., May, 1927; offg. dep. secty. to ditto, March, 1929; offg. dist. and sess. judge, Sept., 1930; confd. Mar., 1935; offg. secty. to govt., Bengal, legisl. dept., Mar., 1935; attd. legisl. dept., govt. of India, May, 1936; special officer, fin. dept., govt. of Bengal, Oct., 1936.

BENN, RT. HON. W. WEDGWOOD, D.S.O. D.F.C., secty. of state for India, June, 1929-Aug., 1931.

BENNETT, EDWARD, B.A., LL.D., Barr., Indian C.S. (judge, Allahabad high ct.) (b. 26th Oct., 1880).—Educ. at Campbell Coll., Belfast, and Trinity Coll., Dublin, apptd. after exam. of 1903; arrived, 30th Nov., 1904, and served in the United Provs. as asst. mag. and collr.; jt. mag., May, 1914; sess. and sub. judge, Jan., 1917; dist. and sessions judge, Oct., 1922; offg. judge, high ct., Allahabad, April, 1928; and again, Apl., 1929; addl. judge, ditto, Oct., 1929; and again March, 1931; judge, ditto, Dec., 1932.

BENNETT, WILLIAM RYLAND RAINSFORD MOORE, M.C., late Indian State Railways (b. 22nd May, 1881).—Joined the dept., Oct., 1905; signal engr., E.B. Rly., Jan., 1920; dep. ch. entr., signals, Oct., 1922; ret'd., May, 1936.

BENNETT, CHRISTOPHER MACAULAY, B.Sc. Indian Service of Engrs. (exec. engr., Madras) (b. 15th Feb., 1903).—Joined the service as exec. engr., Madras, 1st Oct., 1926; offg. exc. engr., Mar., 1933; confd., Oct., 1935.

BENNETT, ERIC, M.A., Indian C.S. (collr., Madras) (b. 20th Nov., 1892).—Educ. at Berkhamsted and Trinity Coll., Cambridge; joined the service, 7th March, 1921; arrived, 29th April, 1921, and served in Madras as asst. collr. and mag.; sub. collr. and jt. mag., Jan., 1923; spl. asst. settlt. offr., Oct., 1924; offg. collr. and dist. mag., May, 1928; ditto (subs.), Sept., 1935.

BENNETT, HERBERT JOHN, Office of High Commr. for India (higher exec. officer, store dept.) (b. 30th June, 1881).—Apptd. clerk, 2nd class, India Store Depôt, 6th April, 1901; transfed. to office of high commr. for India, Oct., 1920; supervisor, India Store Dept., April, 1921; higher exec. officer, April, 1922.

BENNETT, JOHN REGINALD WILLIAM, B.A. (Oxon.), Indian C.S. (dist. and sess. judge, United Provs.) (b. 18th Oct., 1888).—Educ. at King's Sch., Warwick, and Worcester Coll., Oxford; apptd. after exam. of 1911; arrived, 23rd Nov., 1912, and served in the United Provs. as asst. mag. and collr. and asst. commr.; jt. mag., July, 1917; under-sec. to govt., Jan., 1919, to March, 1921; offg. dep. sec. to govt., judl. dept., Oct., 1923; sess. and sub-judge, Nov., 1926; dist. and sess. judge, Aug., 1927; and again, Nov., 1933; judl. secty. to govt., U.P., July, 1931.

BENNETT, JOHN THORNE MASEY, C.B.E., M.C., Indian Police (supt., Punjab) (b. 7th Oct., 1894).—Joined the service, 27th Nov., 1914; asst. supt., Jan., 1919; supt., July, 1924; C.B.E., June, 1933; offg. dep. inspr.-genl. variously from Apl., 1934.

BENNETT, JOSEPH WILLIAM, B.E., B.Sc., late Indian State Rlys. (b. 17th July, 1890).—Joined the service as asst. engr., 1st Oct., 1913; asst. exec. engr., Jan., 1920; offg. exec. engr., June, 1923; confd., Sept., 1931; ret'd., Sept., 1935.

BENNISON, JOHN JENNINGS, B.A., Indian C.S. (dep. commr., Burma) (b. 15th June, 1892).—Educ. at Middlesbrough High School and Corpus Christi Coll., Cambridge; apptd. after exam. of 1915; arrived in India, Dec., 1916, and served in Burma as asst. commr.; on mil. duty from July, 1918, to July, 1919; offg. dep. commr., and jt. regr., co-op. societies, Nov., 1924; offg. dep. commr., Dec., 1925; confd., June, 1930; supt., census operations, Burma, Apl., 1930.

BENSKIN, ERNEST, M.A., late Indian Forest Service (b. 7th July, 1888).—Joined the service, 17th Dec., 1900, as asst. consvr.; dep. consvr., Jan., 1915; offg. consvr., Bengal, April, 1928, to Dec. 1928; ditto, Bihar and Orissa, Nov., 1929; ditto, Oct., 1932; ret'd., Apl., 1933.

BENSON, WILFRED JOHN, B.Sc., Indian Service of Engineers (exec. engr., Punjab) (b. 27th July, 1903).—Joined the service as asst. exec. engr., Punjab, 1st Oct., 1926; exec. engr., Oct., 1935.

BENTINCK, ARTHUR HAROLD WALTER, C.I.E., M.A., late Indian C.S. (b. 29th Dec., 1875).—Educ. at Harrow and Balliol Coll., Oxford; apptd. after exam. of 1897; arrived, 6th Dec., 1898, and served in Bengal as asst. mag. and collr.; transf. to E. Bengal and Assam, Oct., 1905; joint mag. and dep. collr., Jan., 1906; mag. and collr., May, 1911; dep. commr., Assam, April, 1912; offg. commr., April, 1924; mbr. Assam leg. council; offg. chief sec. to govt., March, 1926; C.I.E., July, 1926; offg. commr., Dec., 1926; confd., May, 1927; retd., Dec., 1933.

BENTLEY, CHARLES ALBERT, C.I.E., M.B., D.P.H., late Dept. of Public Health, Bengal (b. 25th April, 1873).—Joined the service, 8th May, 1911, as special dep. sanitary commr. E. Bengal and Assam, after special employment in connection with Bombay malaria epidemic; special offr. for malaria research, Bengal, March, 1913; K.-i.-H. medal, 1st class, Jan., 1915; san. commr., Bengal, May, 1917; dir. of public health, Bengal, Feb., 1921; C.I.E., June, 1929; retd., June, 1931.

BENTON, GORDON WILLIAM, Indian Police (dist. supt., Central Provs.) (b. 25th March, 1893).—Joined the service as asst. dist. supt., Nov., 1912; dist. supt., Sept., 1924; dep. dir. genl. police, Hyderabad State, Feb., 1929; and again, Nov., 1932; officer on spl. duty under for. and pol. dept., Aug., 1936; attd. for service with dir., intel. bureau, Oct., 1936; ditto with rly. bd., Oct., 1937.

BENTON, WILLIAM STEPHEN, Indian State Rlys. (exec. engr., N.W. Rly.) (b. 21st Jan., 1893).—Asst. engr., 27th Dec., 1919; exec. engr. (prov.), Nov., 1935.

BERG, CLEMENT LEONARD, Indian Service of Engrs. (exec. engr., Madras) (b. 1st Jan., 1898).—Joined the service as asst. exec. engr., 1st Oct., 1921; offg. exec. engr., Sept., 1927; confd., Oct., 1930; deputed to mil. engrg. serv., Karachi, Oct., 1937.

BERKELEY-HILL, OWEN ALFRED ROWLAND, M.D., Lt.-Col., I.M.S., retd., late Civil Medl. Dept., Bihar and Orissa (b. 22nd Dec., 1879).—First commn. dated 2nd Feb., 1907; in mil. employ up to 19th Oct., 1919; medl. supt., Ranchi European mental hosp., Nov., 1922; retd., Dec., 1934.

BERKELEY, GERALD EUSTACE, Indian State Rlys. (dep. traffic manager, G.I.P. Rly.) (b. 24th Jan., 1896).—Joined the service as traffic probnr., 7th Nov., 1914; asst. traffic supt., Jan., 1918; divl. traffic manager, Aug., 1924; goods supt., Oct. 1924; asst. traffic manager, May, 1926; offd. variously as dep. traffic manager from May, 1926; confd., Dec., 1929.

BERRIE, JOHN GEORGE, late India Posts and Tels. (b. 1st June, 1876). Trained at R.I.E. Coll.; joined the service, 1st Oct., 1898, as asst. supt., telegraphs; supt., April, 1910, ditto, and dep. postmr.-gen., traffic, Feb., 1916; offg. dep. dir.-gen. telegraph traffic, Jan., 1923; confd., July, 1924; dir., tele. engrg., eastern circle, Nov., 1925; ditto, southern circle, Nov., 1927; retd. June, 1931.

BERRILL, BERNARD MARIE, late Survey of India (b. 22nd Oct., 1874).—Joined the service as proby. sub-assst. supt., 5th Nov., 1897; confd., Nov., 1898; extra asst. supt., Feb., 1906; supt., Feb., 1927; retd., Oct., 1929.

BERRILL, REGINALD, late Indian Service of Engrs. (b. 20th Aug., 1878).—Joined the service, 11th Feb., 1902; asst. exec. engr., Oct., 1922; exec. engr., Oct., 1923; retd., Aug., 1933.

BERRY, WILLIAM, Public Works drpt., Punjab (b. 30th Mar., 1889).—Appt. prof. Maclagan Engrg. coll., Lahore, 10th Oct., 1924.

BERTHOUD, EDWARD HENRY, C.I.E., O.B.E., B.A., late Indian C.S. (b. 13th Sept., 1876).—Educ. at Uppingham Sch., and New Coll., Oxford; apptd. after exam. of 1899; arrived, 13th Dec., 1900, and served in Bengal as asst. mag. and collr. and asst. settlmt. offr.; jt. mag. and dep. collr., Feb., 1910; transf. to Bihar and Orissa, April, 1912; mag. and collr., Dec., 1912; ditto and dep. commr., June, 1917; O.B.E., June, 1919; commr. of excise and inspr.-gen. of registration, &c., Oct., 1921; C.I.E., Jan., 1928; commr., Nov., 1928; retd., May, 1932.

BERY, HARBANS LAL, B.Sc., Indian Police (b. 9th Dec., 1912).—Apptd. asst. supt., Bengal, on probn., 30th Jan., 1936.

BERY, KASH RAM, late Indian Service of Engrs. (b. 7th June, 1880).—Trained at the R.I.E. Coll.; apptd. asst. engr., 5th Dec., 1901; exec. engr., Oct., 1909; suptg. engr., Feb., 1922; retd., June, 1935.

BEST, KENNETH HENRY, Indian Police (b. 20th Mar., 1918).—Apptd. asst. supt., Bombay, on probn., Dec., 1937.

BESTALL, FREDERICK GEORGE, Indian Police (b. 22nd July, 1910).—Joined the service as asst. dist. supt., Burma, 3rd Dec., 1929.

BETHAM, GEOFFREY LAWRENCE, C.I.E., M.C., Lt.-Col., I.A., Indian Political Service (resdt., Mewar) (b. 8th Apr., 1889).—First commn., dated 20th Jan., 1909; asst. commr., May, 1919; asst. pol. agent, Mekran, Mar., 1924; pol. agent, Chagai, Jan., 1925; ditto, Sibi, July, 1927; ditto, Zhob, Mar., 1929; commr., Ajmer-Merwara, Apr., 1933; C.I.E., Jan., 1934; resdt., Mewar, Mar., 1935, H.B.M.'s., minister Nepal, Apr., 1938.

BETT, JOHN RAYMOND, Indian C.S. (b. 12th Nov., 1901).—Educ. at Wintringham Secondary School, Grimsby, Univ. Coll., London, and St. Edmunds Hall, Oxford; joined the service, 22nd Oct., 1926; arrived, Nov., 1926, and served in Madras as asst. collr. and magte.; sub. collr. and jt. magte., Nov., 1928; offg. mag. and collr., variously from Apr., 1936.

BETTERTON, FREDERICK ARCHIBALD, Indian Service of Engrs. (ch. engr. and secty. to govt., P.W.D. (irrigation bch.), Bihar (b. 29th Dec., 1882).—Trained at R.I.E. Coll.; joined the service, 1st Oct., 1906; asst. engr., 21st Oct., 1906; exec. engr. Oct., 1914; offg. suptg. engr., June, 1925; confd., June, 1925; offg. ch. engr. and sec. to govt. P.W.D., April, 1931; confd., Jan., 1933.

BETTS, WILLIAM JOHN, late Indian State Rlys. (E.I. Rly.) (b. 14th Aug., 1876).—Held non-gazetted appt. from 15th Dec., 1902, to

9th April, 1916; asst. dist. loco. supt. (temp.), Dec., 1916; confd., Oct., 1919; dist. loco. supt., April, 1924; supt., Feb., 1925; retd., Oct., 1929.

BEVAN, GILBERT JOHN BECKFORD, M.B.E., Indian State Rlys. (dep. agent, Burma Rlys.) (b. 19th Dec., 1882).—Joined the service as asst. engr. (temp.), 6th Jan., 1910; ditto (perm.), July, 1911; on milty. duty from 27th Sept., 1916, to 19th Sept., 1919; M.B.E., June, 1919; dist. engr., Feb., 1921; offg. dep. agent, Apl., 1933; confd., Mar., 1934.

BEVERIDGE, ERNEST JOHN, Indian Police (b. 14th Nov., 1909).—Joined the service as proby. asst. supt., Central Provs., 26th Nov., 1929; confd., Oct., 1931; asst. supt., Deoli deten. jail, Jan., 1935.

BEWERS, DUDLEY FRANCIS, Office of High Commr. for India (higher clerical officer, education dept.) (b. 28th Sept., 1893).—Apptd. clerk to local adviser to Indian Students, London, 25th Aug., 1919; transf. to Office of High Commr. for India, Oct., 1920; clerical officer, April, 1922; higher clerical officer, April, 1925.

BEWOOR, GURUNATH VENKATESH, C.I.E., B.A., Indian C.S. (dir.-genl., posts and tels.) (b. 20th Nov., 1888).—Educ. at schools in India, Bombay Univ., and Sidney Sussex Coll., Cambridge; apptd. after exam. of 1911; arrived, 27th Nov., 1912, and served in the Central Prov., as asst. commr.; under-sec. to chief commr., March, 1919; offg. postmr.-gen., Bihar and Orissa, July, 1922; confd. as postmr.-gen., July, 1924; dep. commr. (prov.), Nov., 1924; confd., June, 1925; dep. dir.-genl., posts and tels., Nov., 1927; delegate to internl. Univ. Postal Congress, London, Mar., 1929; P.M.G., Bombay, Oct., 1929; C.I.E., June, 1932; dir.-genl., posts and tels., Mar., 1934; member, Council of State, Mar., 1936.

BEYNON, ALBERT CYRIL, M.A., Barr. Indian C.S. (dist. and sess. judge, Burma) (b. 28th Aug., 1896).—Educ. at Sandhurst, and Jesus Coll., Oxford; joined the service, 28th Sept., 1921; arrived, 2nd Dec., 1921, and served in Burma as asst. commr.; offg. dist. and sess. judge, Nov., 1925, and again Jan., 1927; confd., Sept., 1930.

BHADKAMKAR, BALAKRISHNA VASUDEO, B.Sc., Indian C.S. (mag. and collr., United Provs.) (b. 28th Aug., 1893).—Educ. at Bombay and University Coll., London; arrived, 23rd March, 1919, and posted to Burma; transferred to United Provs. as asst. mag. and collr., July, 1920; jt. mag., July, 1920; offg. mag. and collr., March, 1926; confd., May, 1932; offg. dep. commr., Dec., 1932.

BHADRAN, CHAKAPANY AIY'NGAR RAMA, M.A., Indian Forest Service (b. 24th Sept., 1907).—Joined the service as asst. consvr., Assam, 15th Nov., 1932.

BHAGADE, SHRIDHAR MADHO RAO SAHIB, B.A., B.L., late Central Provs., C.S. (b. 1st Feb., 1879).—Joined the service as munsif, 14th Mar., 1903; sub-judge, Dec., 1914; dist. and sess. judge, July, 1932; retd., Apl., 1933.

BHAGAT, SURENDRA SINGH (see Surendra Singh, B.).

BHAGWAN DAS, RAI BAHADUR, LALA, M.V.O., late Indian Police (b. July, 1875).—Joined the service, 24th Dec., 1895; M.V.O., Mar., 1922; supy. supt., intell. bureau, govt. of India, home dept., May, 1924; awarded King's police medal, Jan., 1930; retd., July, 1930.

BHAGWATI PRASAD SAKSENA, B.Sc., Indian Service of Engrs. (exec. engr., United Provs.) (b. 17th July, 1899).—Joined the service 15th Oct., 1923; apprentice engr., Sept., 1924; asst. exec. engr., Nov., 1925; offg. exec. engr., Mar., 1929; confd., Oct., 1934.

BHAGWATI PRASAD VARMA, Rai Bahadur, B.A., M.R.S.I., M.I.M.E., late Indian Service of Engrs. (b. 17th Mar., 1877).—Joined the service, 8th Aug., 1898; asst. engr., Punjab, Aug., 1899; exec. engr., Aug., 1910; offg. suptg. engr., Oct., 1920; confd., Dec., 1921; offg. ch. engr. and sec. to govt., irrigation branch, April, 1928; member, public services commn., Oct., 1930 to May, 1935; retd., Oct., 1935.

BHALJA, GOVARDHAN SHANKERLAL, B.A., Indian C.S. (dep. commr., Central Provs.) (b. 8th Aug., 1895).—Educ. at Bombay Univ. and Caius Coll., Cambridge; joined the service, 2nd Nov., 1920; arrived, 8th Jan., 1921, and served in Central Provs. as asst. commr.; offg. dep. commr., March, 1925, and Nov., 1932; confd., Aug., 1933; dir. of industries and regr. of co-op. societies, Aug., 1933; secty. to local self govt. etc. deptts., Apl., 1937.

BHALLA, T. P. (see Tejasvi Prasad Bhalla).

BHAN, CHANDAR (see Chandar, B.).

BHANDARKAR, VAMAN PRABHAKAR, Indian State Rlys. (dist. traffic supt., E.B. Rly.) (b. 18th Apl., 1892).—Asst. traffic supt., Oct., 1914; dist. ditto, Dec., 1936.

BHANDARI, AMAR NATH, B.A., Indian C.S. (asst. commr., Punjab) (b. 21st Nov., 1899).—Educ. at Punjab Univ. and Oxford Univ. (non-collegiate); joined the service, 24th Oct., 1924; arrived, 9th Dec., 1924, and served in the Punjab as asst. commr.; offg. dep. commr., April, 1929; and again, Oct., 1930; offg. dist. and sess. judge, Nov., 1933; confd., July, 1934; attd. govt. of India, legisls. dept., Nov., 1935; dist. and sess. judge, Delhi, Sept., 1936.

BHANDARI, JAIGOPAL, M.A., India Finance Dept. (b. 14th Dec., 1882).—Joined the service, 9th Feb., 1906; asst. acct.-genl., Punjab, Sept., 1906; asst.-comptr., Central Provs., Jan., 1909; asst. acct.-genl., U.P., Nov., 1912; ditto, Bengal, Aug., 1914; addl. asst.-sec. to govt. of India, fin. dept., Nov., 1918; asst. acct.-genl., posts and tels., Calcutta, April, 1919; dep. acct.-genl., Bengal, Oct., 1922; ditto, central revenues, Jan., 1924, ditto, Punjab, Dec., 1924; offg. ditto, posts and tels., March, 1925; offg. acct.-genl., Punjab, March, 1927; dep. ditto, Nov., 1927; offg. acct.-genl., Bihar and Orissa, June, 1929; offg. acct.-genl., central revenues, Nov., 1929; ditto, Bengal, May, 1930; ditto, posts and tels., Mar., 1933; ditto, Punjab, Apl., 1934; acct.-genl., Punjab, Oct., 1934.

BHANDARI, MADAN GOPAL, M.B., Lt-Col., I.M.S., Jail Dept., Bombay (supt.) (b. 3rd Jan., 1892).—First commn. dated 1st Nov., 1920; in milty. employ. to 21st Oct., 1924; addl. supt., Oct., 1924; offg. supt., Feb., 1925; confd., Sept., 1927; supt., Borstal Institute, Lahore, April, 1928, to Dec., 1928; ditto, Central prison, Bombay, Apl., 1933; offg. inspr.-genl. of prisons, Bombay, Feb., 1936.

BHANDARI, RAM NATH, M.D., Ch.B., Major, I.M.S., Jail Dept., United Provinces (b. 15th Aug., 1882).—Apptd. supt., central prison, Bareilly, 5th Jan., 1929; ditto, Allahabad (Naini), Mar., 1937.

BHANDARKAR, GOPAL PRABHAKAR, M.B.E., B.A., late Indian Service of Engrs. (b. 23rd Oct., 1882).—Joined the service as asst. engr., (prov. service), 16th May, 1918; asst. exec. engr., Aug., 1919; offg. exec. engr., Oct., 1926; confd., May, 1927; M.B.E. June, 1935; ret'd., Oct., 1937.

BHANOI, HARNAM DAS, B.A., Indian C.S. (dep. commr., Punjab) (b. 6th Jan., 1897).—Educ. at Punjab Univ. and Sidney Sussex Coll., Cambridge; arrived, 9th Jan., 1922, and served in the Punjab as asst. commr.; offg. dep. commr., March, 1926; dep. commr. (prov.), May, 1928; ditto (subs. perm.), Dec., 1931; senr. secty. to finl. commrs., and dept. secty. to govt., Punjab, dev. dept., July, 1934; secty. ditto fin. dept., Sept., 1937.

BHANSALI, MANSEN DAMODAR, M.A., LL.B. Barr., Indian C.S. (coll. and mag., Bombay, (b. 7th Nov., 1898).—Educ. at Bombay and Dulwich Coll., and St. John's Coll., Camb.; apptd. after exam. in 1923; arrived in India and served in Bombay as asst. collr. and magte. Dec., 1923; offg. dep. sec. to govt., finance dept., Jan., 1931; confd., Nov., 1931; member, legisl. council, Bombay, 1932; offg. regt. of co-op societies, Bombay, July, 1936.

BHARGAV, LACHHMAN PRASAD (see Lachhman, P.B.).

BHARGAVA, KALKAR PRASAD, M.A., Indian C.S. (jt. mag., United Provinces) (b. 15th July, 1908).—Educated at Government High School, Orai, Allahabad University and Emmanuel College, Cambridge; joined the service 6th October, 1933; arrived in India November, 1933, and served in the United Provinces as asst. mag. and collr.; jt. magte., Mar., 1937.

BHARGAVA, MURARI LAL, B.Sc., LL.B. A.S.A.A., Office of High Commr. for India (exec. officer, store dept.) (b. 1st Aug., 1895).—Apptd. Sept., 1926.

BHARGAVA, RAMESHWAR PRASAD, M.A., Indian C.S. (jt. mag., United Provinces) (b. 27th Nov., 1906).—Educ. at Allahabad University and School of Oriental Studies, London. Joined the service, 12th Oct., 1931; arrived, in India, Nov., 1931, and served in the United Provinces as asst. mag. and collr.; jt. magte., Oct., 1934.

BHARGAVA, VASHISTHA, Indian C.S. (jt. mag., United Provinces) (b. 5th Feb., 1906).—Educ. at Allahabad University and London School of Oriental Studies; joined the service 10th Oct., 1930, arrived in India, Nov., 1930, and served in the United Provs. as asst. mag. and collr.; jt. magte., Oct., 1934.

BHARUCHA, JAMASJI CURSETJI, Lt. Col. I.M.S. (princpl., Medical sch., Agra) (b. 9th July 1887).—First comm., dated 27th Jan., 1912; in milty. employ until 3rd April, 1923; offg. dist. medl. officer, March, 1926; offg. surg., first dist., and supt., Rayapuram medl. school, &c., April, 1928; offg. supt., jail dept., Burma, Aug., 1929;

confd., July, 1931; offg. inspr.-genl. of prisons, Burma, Oct., 1933; ditto, Bihar and Orissa, May, 1934; princpl., medl. sch., Agra, Feb., 1935.

BHARUCHA, JEHANGIR SORAB, B.A., Indian Police (b. 13th Oct., 1897).—Joined the service as asst. supt., Bombay, on 6th Aug., 1923; offg. supt., Feb., 1932.

BHARUCHA, KAIKHUSRU BYRAMJI, Lt.-Col., I.M.S., Jail Dept., Bihar (supt.) (b. 19th July, 1889).—First commn., dated 1st Nov., 1920; in milty. employ up to 15th March, 1924; offg. supt., March, 1924; confd., Jan., 1928.

BHARUCHA, PHIROZSHAH BYRAMJI, D.S.O., O.B.E., M.D. (Lond.), F.R.C.S., L.R.C.P., Lt-Col., I.M.S. (medl. supt., Mayo hosp., Lahore) (b. 21st Feb., 1886).—First commn., 30th July, 1910; O.B.E., Jan., 1920; on mil. duty till April, 1921, when he was apptd. prof. of anatomy, King Edward Medical Coll., Lahore; offg. prof. of operative surgery, ditto, Oct., 1921; and again, March, 1926; medl. supt., Mayo hosp., Lahore, Nov., 1935; offg. princpl. King Edward Medl. Coll., Feb., 1936.

BHARUCHA, SORAB MANEKSHAH, B.A., late Bombay Civil Service (b. 28th Jan., 1875).—Apptd. dep. collr., Bombay; 5th April, 1897; ditto, and oriental translator to govt., July, 1913; collr., Thana, and pol. agent, Javhar, May, 1916; collr. and mag., Kolaba, and pol. agent, Janjira, Feb., 1918; collr. and mag. and pol. agent, Jawhar, Nov., 1921; collr. and Salsette dev. officer, May, 1925; offg. secty. to govt., dev. dept., &c., May, 1929; ret'd., May, 1930.

BHASHYAM AYYANGAR, VALAVARAM, late Madras Civil Service (b. Nov., 1876).—Joined the service 18th Aug., 1903; dist. munisif., Dec., 1904; sub. judge, Aug., 1920; dist. and sess. judge, June, 1928; offg. judge high ct., Madras, July, 1930; ret'd., Nov., 1931.

BHASKARA, RAO NAYUDU, TAMARAPAKAM, B.A., Indian C.S. (sub. collr., Madras) (b. 10th May, 1896).—Educ. at Presidency Coll. Madras; joined the service, 12th Dec., 1921; arrived, 2nd Jan., 1922, and served in Madras as asst. collr. and mag.; sub-coll. and jt. mag., Feb., 1925; offg. collr. & dist. magte., Feb., 1930.

BHASKARAIYA, CHATTA, M.A., Indian Audit Dept. (b. 27th Oct., 1894).—Joined the service, 6th Mar., 1920; asst. acct.-genl., Bombay, July, 1921; asst. dir. of comml. audit, Calcutta, Jan., 1930, dep. acct.-genl., central revs., Calcutta, Feb., 1933; ditto, Bengal, April, 1936.

BHASKARAN, PUDIYA VITTIL, Indian Police (asst. supt., Madras) (b. 18th Nov., 1908).—Apptd. proby. asst. supt., Madras, 23rd Jan., 1933; confd., Jan., 1935.

BHAT, JYESTHARAM NANABHAI, B.A., LL.B., late Judl. Dept., Bombay.—Joined the dept., 7th June, 1914, as sub-judge; offg. judge, small cause ct., Ahmedabad, July, 1927; confd., Sept., 1927; ret'd., Oct., 1928.

BHAT, MADHUSUDAN DAMODAR, M.A., Indian C.S. (collr. and magte., Sind) (b. 4th Nov., 1896).—Educ. at Elphinstone Coll., Bombay, and Trinity Hall, Camb.; joined the service, 9th Nov., 1921; arrived, 27th Jan., 1922, and served in Bombay as asst. collr.

offg. collr., dist. magte. and pol. agent, May, 1925, and again Aug., 1929; temp. under secty. to govt., fin. dept., Apl., 1929; offg. collr. and pol. agent, Jewhar; offg. collr. and pol. agent, Jath, Feb., 1933; Collr., Mar., 1936.

BHATHENA, SORABJI JAMASJI, L.R.C.P. (Edin.), L.F.F.S. (Glas.), L.M. (Dub.). Lieut.-Col. I.M.S., ret'd. (b. 24th Jan., 1879).—First commn. dated 2nd Feb., 1907; and served in mil. dept. prior to 24th Oct., 1921; civil surg., Oct., 1921; ret'd., Jan., 1934.

BHATIA, KISHINCHAND MULCHAND, B.E., Indian Service of Engrs. (b. 23rd Dec., 1907).—Apptd. asst. exec. engr., Bombay, 12th Aug., 1931.

BHATIA, KRISHNA BEHARI, Indian C.S. (jt. mag., United Provinces) (b. 15th July, 1907).—Educ. at University of Allahabad; Royal College of Science, London, and School of Oriental Studies, London; joined the service, 9th Oct., 1929; arrived 7th Dec., 1929, and served in the United Provinces as: stt. coll. and mag.; offg. mag. and collr., Jan., 1936; secty. tariff bd., Apl., 1937.

BHATIA, RAM NARAYAN, RAI BAHADUR, B.A., B.Sc., M.B., F.R.C.S., late United Provs. medl. service (b. 23rd July, 1884).—Joined the service as asst. surg., 18th Feb., 1910; civil surg., Aug., 1919; offg. prof. of surg., King George's medl. coll., Aug., 1924; ret'd., Nov., 1934.

BHATIA, SOHAN LAL, M.A., M.D., B.Ch. (Cantab.), L.R.C.P., M.R.C.S., F.C.P.S. (Bom.), M.C., Lt.-Col. I.M.S. (princl., G.M. Coll., Bombay, and supt. J.J. group hosp.) (b. 5th Aug., 1891).—First commn. dated 15th May, 1920; served in milty. dept. prior to 22nd Aug., 1920; offg. prof. of physiology, &c., G.M. Coll., Sept., 1920; confd., Sept., 1923; princpl. G.M. Coll. and supt. J.J. group hosp., Oct., 1937.

BHATNAGAR, AWADH NARAYAN, M.Sc., Indian Forest Service (b. 16th Oct., 1905).—Joined the service as asst. consvr., Assam, 8th Nov., 1929.

BHATTACHARJI, DHARMADAS, B.A., Indian Police. (asst. supt., Bengal) (b. 1st Mar., 1905). Joined the service as proby. asst. supt., 12th June, 1927; confd., June, 1929; offg. addl. supt., Apl., 1934; offg. supt., Jan., 1936.

BHATTACHARJI, DURGAGATI, B.Sc., Indian Police (b. 1st July, 1913).—Apptd. asst. supt., Bengal, on probn., 1st Feb., 1936.

BHATTACHARJI, MAHENDRA KUMAR, B.Sc., B.E., Indian Service of Engrs. (exec. engr., Bengal) (b. Apl., 1889).—Joined the service, 8th Jan., 1918; asst. exec. engr., Jan., 1919; offg. exec. engr., Oct., 1927; confd. Jan., 1928.

BHATTACHARJI, NAND LAL, B.A., late Indian Service of Engrs. (United Provs.) (b. 26th June, 1876).—Trained at T.C.E. Coll. and apptd. in India as apprentice engr., 30th June, 1899; asst. engr., June, 1900; exec. engr., Sept., 1908; ret'd., June, 1931.

BHATTACHARJI, PRAMATHA NATH, M.A., B.L., late Bihar and Orissa Civil Service (b. 16th Jan., 1876).—Joined the service, 13th June, 1904; offg. munisf., Oct., 1906; confd. Feb., 1907; sub-judge, Sept., 1918; dist. and sess. judge, Jan., 1931; ret'd., Oct., 1931.

BHATTACHARJI, TARAKESWAR, Rai Bahadur, B.A., Excise and Salt dept., Bengal (dep. commr.) (b. 25th Apl., 1885).—Joined the service, 10th Apl., 1906; sub-dep. collr., Dec., 1907; supt. of excise and salt, Apl., 1915; pers. asst. to commr. of excise, June, 1928; offg. dep. commr., Sept., 1928; confd., Mar., 1930.

BHATTACHARYA, AMBARNATH (see Ambarnath Bhattacharya).

BHAUMIK, HARIPADA, O.B.E., B.A., late India Posts and Tels. (b. 10th June, 1882).—Joined the service, 28th Sept., 1905; asst. supt., Dec., 1908; divl. engr., tels., Nov., 1921; senr. elect. engr., May, 1930; offg. as elect. engr.-in-ch., Mar., 1931; O.B.E., Jan. 1934; offg. p.m.g., May, 1934; dir. tels., Aug., 1934; elec. engr. in ch., Sept., 1934; offg. p.m.g., Madras, Dec., 1935; confd. as p.m.g., Oct., 1936; ret'd., June, 1937.

BHAVE, MAHADEV ANANT, B.A., LL.M., Judl. Dept., Bombay (asst. judge) (b. 14th Apl., 1884).—Joined the service, 4th Apl., 1914; asst. judge, Feb., 1934; offg. dist. and sess. judge variously from Apl., 1934.

BHIDE, MAHADEVA VISHNU, B.A., Indian C.S. (judge, high ct., Lahore) (b. 13th Feb., 1883).—Educ. at Poona, Bombay Univ., and St. John's Coll., Cambridge; apptd. after exam. of 1906; arrived, 1st Jan., 1908, and served in the Punjab as asst. commr.; asst. censor, Bombay, Jan., 1915, to Apl., 1919; postal censor, Lahore, May, 1919; offg. legal membr. and sec. to govt., legislative dept., May, 1925; confd., April, 1926; actg. judge, Lahore High Ct., June-July, 1927, and again, Mar., 1933; addl. judge, ditto, variously from Nov., 1927; dist. and sess. judge, Mar., 1934; judge, high ct., Lahore, July, 1936.

BHIDE, MANOHAR RAMCHANDRA, B.Sc., Indian C.S. (asst. commr., Punjab) (b. 6th Dec., 1907).—Educ. at Nagpur University, University College, London and Trinity College, Cambridge. Joined the service, 12th Oct., 1931; arrvd. in India, Nov., 1931, and served in the Punjab as asst. commr.

BHIDE, VITHAL SHIVARAM, B.A., LL.B., Barr.-at-Law, Indian C.S. (collr., Bombay) (b. 23rd Sept., 1890).—Educ. at Miraj High School, Bombay Univ., and Cambridge (non-collegiate); apptd. after exam. of 1914; arrived in India, Dec., 1915, and served in Burma as asst. commr.; transf. to Bombay as asst. collr., April, 1919; asst. collr. and personal asst. to collr., Feb., 1922; offg. collr. and dist. magte., April, 1926; confd., Apl., 1935; offg. regr. co-op. societies, Oct., 1927.

BHIKAJI RAGHUNATH MEHENDALE (see Mehendale, B. R.).

BHIM SEN, LALA, B.A., Indian Service of Engrs. (exec. engr., Punjab) (b. 26th Dec., 1894).—Joined the service, 8th Sept., 1918; asst. engr., Sept., 1919; asst. exec. engr., Jan., 1920; offg. exec. engr., Aug., 1927; confd. Apl., 1929; on depn. to Orissa, Apl., 1936.
