BHIMASENA RAU, HANUMANTA, B.A., late Indian Finance Dept. (b. 30th April, 1876).—Joined the service, 6th Sept., 1899; asst. accent.-gen., Sept., 1901; asst. compt. Post Office, June, 1905; dep. ditto, Jan., 1906; asst. accent.-gen., Madras, July, 1906; ditto and examr. of local fund accs., Jan., 1908; dep.

comptr., Ind. treasuries, Oct., 1901; dep. acctnt.-gen., Burma, Oct., 1912; ditto, Madras, April, 1914; asst. acctnt.-gen., Madras, Dec., 1915; dep. acctnt.-gen., ditto, March, 1916; offg. comptr., Assam, June, 1921; dep. acctnt.-gen., Central Prov., May, 1922; offg. acctnt.-gen., United Prov., Jan., 1924; confd., Oct., 1925; dir. of audit, United Provs., April, 1926; acct.-genl., Bihar and Orissa, Oct., 1927; ditto, Madras; ret'd., Apl., 1931.

BHOLA, MATHURA PRASHAD, Rai Bahadur, O.B.E., late Indian Forest Service (b. 21st Sept., 1881).—Joined the service, 1st July, 1902; dep. consvr., Aug., 1922; ret'd., Sept., 1936; O.B.E., Feb., 1937.

BHOOTHALINGAM, SUBRAHMANYA, B.A. Indian C.S. (jt. mag. and sub-collr., Madras) (b. 22nd Feb., 1909).—Educ. at Caius College, Cambridge; joined the service, 14th Oct., 1931; arrvd. in India, Nov., 1931, and served in Madras as asst. mag. and collr.; sub collr. and jt. mag., Apl., 1933.

BHORE, SIR JOSEPH WILLIAM, K.C.S.I., K.C.I.E., C.B.E., B.A., late Indian C.S. (b. 6th April, 1879).—Educ. at Bishop's High School, Poona, and Bombay Univ.; apptd. after exam. of 1901; arrived, 13th Dec., 1902; and served in Madras as asst. collr. and mag.; under-sec. to govt., rev. dept., May, 1910; sub-collr. and jt. mag., Oct., 1910; diwan of Cochin, March, 1914; dep. dir. of civil supplies, Bezwada, April, 1919; C.B.E., Dec., 1919; sec. to high commr. for India, 1st Oct., 1920, to 28th Nov., 1923; acting high commr. for India from 20th Oct., 1922, to 18th April, 1923; C.I.E., June, 1923; secy., govt. of India, dept. of education, health and lands, April, 1924; temp. member of gov. genl.'s council, Nov., 1926; secy., Indian Statutory Commission, Nov., 1927; member, govr.-genl.'s exec. council, April, 1930; vice-presd., ditto, Apl., 1935 to May, 1935; K.C.I.E., June, 1930; K.C.S.I., Jan., 1933; chairman Central Advisory Council for Railways, May, 1934; delegate of Govt. of India at Int. Lab. Conf., Geneva, June, 1935; ret'd., April, 1936.

BICKFORD, WILLIAM REGINALD, B.A., Indian C.S. (asst. commr., Burma) (b. 14th Sept., 1911).—Educated at Cheltenham College and Wadham College, Oxford; apptd. after exam. of 1934; arrived in India, Dec., 1935, and served in Burma as asst. commr.

BIGGIE, HENRY JAMES LESLIE, Indian Police (suplt.) (b. 12th Mar., 1904).—Joined the service as asst. supt., United Provs., on 20th Nov., 1923; offg. supt., Feb., 1927; confd., Nov., 1936; comdt. and dist. supt., Port Blair, Apl., 1924.

BIGGIE, LAWRENCE PATRICK, Indian Police (b. 24th Apl., 1917).—Apptd. asst. suplt., United Provs., on probn., Nov., 1936.

BIGG-WITHER, LIONEL, late Indian State Rlys. (b. 31st Dec., 1876).—Joined the service, 11th March, 1888; asst. loco. supt., July, 1902; dep. ditto, March, 1917; in milty. employ from 25th Oct., 1917; dep. ch. mech. engr., Dec., 1922; offg. ch. mech. engr., Feb., 1924; confd., Oct., 1930; ret'd., Dec., 1933.

BIGSBY, SYDNEY HERBERT C.I.E., A.M.I.C.E., Indian Service of Engrs. (suplg. engr., Punjab) (b. 26th July, 1885).—Trained at

R.I.E. Coll.; joined the service as asst. engr., Jan., 1907; exec. engr., Jan., 1915; on deputation to Jaipur State from 1 Aug., 1924; offg. supt. engr., Oct., 1926; confd., Mar., 1933; C.I.E., June, 1934; offg. ch. engr. and secty. to govt. p.w.d. irrig. bch., May, 1936.

BIJAWAT, MUL CHAND (see Mul Chand Bijawat).

BIJAY, SIR CHAND MAHTAB (see Burdwan).

BILARAM, RUPCHAND (see Rupchand, B.).

BILDERBECK, ALURED CHARLES LOWTHER O'SHEE, M.A., M.B., B.S., D.P.H., Lt.-Col., I.M.S. (port health officer, Bombay) (b. 22nd Aug., 1886).—First commn. dated 31st Jan., 1914; in mil. employ to 21st June, 1923; asst. dir. of public health, Burma, June, 1923; offg. health officer, Aden, Apl., 1929; ditto, Bombay, Jan., 1930; confd., July, 1930.

BILMORIA, RUSTORNE SORABIJ, M.A., LL.B., Barr., Bombay Judl. Dept. (offl. assignee)—Apptd. offl. assignee, high ct., Bombay, 1st Dec., 1935.

BILL, CHARLES ESTWICK SYDNEY, B.A., Indian C.S. (asst. mag. and collr., U.P.) (b. 15th Mar., 1914).—Educ. at Haileybury and Keble College, Oxford; joined the service, 21st Sept., 1937; arrived in India, Oct., 1937, and served in U.P. as asst. mag. and collr.

BILL, VEN. SYDNEY ALFRED, M.A., late Eccles. Dept., United Provs. (b. 21st Feb., 1884).—Apptd. a chaplain on probation, 15th Dec., 1911; junr. chaplain, Dec., 1914; senr. chaplain, Dec., 1924; archdeacon of Lucknow, Nov., 1925, to May, 1937.

BINDRA BAN B. A., Indian Audit Dept. (b. 7th July, 1892).—Joined the service, 1st May, 1913; asst. auditor genl., Jan., 1933; dept. acct.-genl., posts and tels., May, 1936; asst. auditor genl., Nov., 1936.

BINGHAM, Hon. HUGH TERENCE DE BURGH, A.M.I.C.E., Indian State Rlys. (dep. ch. engr., N.W. Rly.) (b. 31st Dec., 1885).—Joined the service as asst. engr. on 1st Oct., 1909; exec. engr., Oct., 1917; offg. dep. dir. projects, rly. bd., May, 1924; offg. supt. of works, Aug., 1928; offg. dep. ch. engr., June, 1929; and again, May, 1933; confd., June, 1935; offg. senr. govt. inspr. of rlys., June, 1934; chief engr. (prov.), Aug., 1937.

BINNEY, ANTHONY LOCKHART, Indian C.S. (commr., Central Provs.) (b. 18th July, 1890).—Educ. at Rugby; apptd. after exam. of 1913; arrived in India, 4th Dec., 1914, and served in the Central Provs. as asst. commr.; on mil. service from March, 1915, to Sept., 1919; dep. commr. (prov.), Aug., 1925; confd., May, 1926; with resident, Hyderabad, from 3rd Sept., 1928; dir.-genl. and secty., rev. dept., Nizam's govt., Aug., 1931; offg. commr., June, 1934; finl. secty. to govt., C.P., Oct., 1935; offg. ch. secty. to govt., Central Provs., Feb., 1936; commr. (prov.), Nov., 1936.

BINNS, BERNARD OTTWELL, B.A., Indian C.S. (commr. of settlements, etc., Burma) (b. 16th July, 1898).—Educ. at Grammar Sch., Bury, and Brasenose Coll., Oxford; joined the service, 24th Dec., 1923, and served in Burma as asst. commr.; under-secty. to govt., fin. dept., May, 1927; offg. dep. commr., June, 1930; dep. secty., fin. dept., Dec., 1935; commr. of settlements and land records, Burma, Dec., 1936.

BION, ARNOLD EUSTACE, Indian Police (inspr.-genl., Orissa) (b. 2nd Feb., 1890).—Joined the service as asst. supt. on 1st Dec., 1910; supt., June, 1922; offg. dep. inspr.-genl., Apl., 1934; and again, Apl., 1936.

BION, CLARENCE RAYMOND FIELD, late Indian Police. (b. 31st, July, 1884).—Joined the service, 23rd Dec., 1903, as asst. supt., Bihar and Orissa; transf. to Assam, Oct., 1906; supt. (provl.), Nov., 1910; confd. as supt., April, 1911; on mil. duty, Sept., 1918; to Feb., 1919; offg. inspr.-genl., Assam, June, 1932; retd., Mar., 1935.

BIRCH, Rev. Canon ORMONDE WINSTANLEY, M.C., late Indian Eccles. Estabt. (b. 14th May, 1879).—Apptd. chaplain on prob. 7th Nov., 1911; juvr. chaplain, Nov., 1914; senr. chaplain, March, 1925; retd., Sept., 1936.

BIRD, JOHN BEMROSE, B.A. (Cantab.), Indian Civil Service (asst. collr. of customs, Bombay) (b. 22nd Feb., 1905).—Educ. at Nottingham High School and Emmanuel Coll., Cambridge; joined the service, 10th Oct., 1928; arrived, Nov., 1928, and served in Bengal as asst. mag. and collr.; offg. under-sec. Finance Comm. and Marine depts., Bengal, July, 1932; offg. retrenchment officer, finance dept., Apl., 1933; asst. collr. of customs, Calcutta, Feb., 1935; attd. res. bank of India, July, 1935; pte. secty. to finance member, govt. of India, Oct., 1935; asst. collr. of customs, Bombay, Nov., 1937.

BIRDWOOD, Baron (Sir WILLIAM RIDDELL BIRLWOOD), G.C.B., G.C.S.I., G.C.M.G., G.C.V.O., C.I.E., D.S.O., LL.D., Field Marshal, I.A. (b. 13th Sept., 1865).—First commn., dated 9th May, 1885; div. prov. marshal, Tirah expedy. force, Dec., 1897, to Jan., 1898; special service, South Africa, from Nov., 1899; D.A.A.G., South Africa, Oct., 1900, to June, 1902; mil. sec. to C.-in-C. in India, June, 1902; specially employed at Hqrs. of Army, July to Oct., 1902; A.M.S., and interpreter to C.-in-C., India, Nov., 1902; A.A.G., Hqrs., India, Sept., 1904; mil. sec. to C.-in-C., India, Dec., 1905; A.D.C. to the King, Feb., 1906, to Oct., 1911; C.I.E., Jan., 1908; brig. commr., India, June, 1909; Q.M.G. in India, May, 1912; sec. to govt., Army Dept., Nov., 1912; war services: Hazara expdn., 1891, medal with clasp; Isazai expdn., 1892; N.W. Frontier of India, 1897-98, medal with two clasps; Tirah, 1897-98, clasp; S. African War, 1899-1902, Queen's medal with six clasps, King's medal with two clasps; N.W. Frontier of India, 1908; medal with clasp, D.S.O.; European War, 1914 (commanded Australian and New Zealand Army Corps, 1914-15; ditto, Mediterranean Expedy. Force, 1915-16; ditto, 5th Army, France, 1918-19); K.C.M.G., June, 1915; K.C.B., June, 1917; G.C.M.G., Jan., 1919; G.C.B., Jan., 1923; temp. member of govr.-gen.'s exec. council, Aug. to Nov., 1924; C.-in-C. in India and member, govr.-gen.'s exec. council, Aug. 1925, to Nov., 1930; G.C.S.I., Jan., 1930; Col.-Commandant, 13 F.F. Rifles, Sept., 1930; apptd. master of Peterhouse, Cambridge, Mar., 1931; G.C.V.O., May, 1937; created Baron, Jan., 1938.

BIRLEY, LEONARD, C.S.I., C.I.E., B.A., late Indian C.S.—Educ. at Uppingham and New Coll., Oxford; apptd. after exam. of 1897;

arrived, 5th Dec., 1898, and served in Bengal as asst. mag. and collr.; joint mag. and dep. collr., July, 1907; mag. and collr., Oct., 1911; C.I.E., June, 1914; offg. sec. to govt. of Bengal, rev. dept., May, 1915; ditto (s.p.t.), April, 1916; confd., March, 1918; employed as sec. to govt. to Dec., 1918; mag. and collr., Nov., 1920; chief sec. to govt. of Bengal, Nov., 1922; temp. member of exec. council, Bengal, June, 1926, and again, Oct., 1926; C.S.I., July, 1926; retd., March, 1929.

BIRT, A. G., B.Sc., late Dept. of Agriculture, Assam.—Apptd. dir. of agriculture, 1st Apl., 1931; retd., Feb., 1933.

BISCOE, WALTER TREWEEK, Indian State Ry's. (divl. comml. manager, N.W. Rly.) (b. 8th Jan., 1892).—Joined the service as asst. traffic supt., 11th July, 1910; on milty. duty from 6th Oct., 1914, to 28th Oct., 1918; asst. dist. traffic supt., Jan., 1922; dist. traffic supt., Dec., 1925; offg. dep. ch. operating supt., June, 1928; resgd. manager, Indian rly. publicity bureau, New York, Dec., 1930; divl. comml. manager, N.W. Rly., Feb., 1936; offg. dep. ch. comml. manager May, 1936.

BISHUMBHAR DAYAL, M.A., Indian Police (b. 29th July, 1907).—Joined the service as prob. asst. supt., United Provs., 10th Jan., 1930; confd., Jan., 1932.

BISHEN CHAND, RAI BAHADUR, B.A., Indian Audit Dept. (b. 1st Oct., 1884).—Joined the service, 21st Sept., 1904; Dep. chief auditor, N.W. Rly., Apl., 1933.

BISHNATH PRASAD, RAI SAHIB BABU, B.A., late Income Tax Dept., United Provs. (b. 13th Mar., 1875).—Joined the service, 15th March, 1901; dep. collr., May, 1913; asst. commr., March, 1923; retd., March, 1930.

BISHOP, JOHN EADIE, Indian Police (b. 15th Dec., 1917).—Apptd. asst. supt., Bihar, on probn., Dec., 1937.

BISHOP, LAURENCE ARTHUR, Indian Police (dist. supt., Madras) (b. 3rd Feb., 1895).—Joined the service, 30th Nov., 1914; asst. supt., Jan., 1920; on milty. duty from 4th Aug., 1916, to 2nd Jan., 1919; offg. supt., Nov., 1924; confd., Feb., 1930; on foreign service as commr. of police, Travancore, Aug., 1928; reverted to Brit. service, Nov., 1932.

BISHOP, ROBERT WILSON, late govt. printing and staty. dept., Burma (b. 24th Sept., 1876).—Rendered previous services in non-gazetted appts. from 24th April, 1902; dep. supt. govt. printing, India, 8th Dec., 1904; supt., ditto, Burma, Aug., 1905; retd. Jan., 1932.

BISHOP, THEODORE BENDYSH WATSON, B.A., Indian C.S. (mag. and collr., United Provs.) (b. 13th Dec., 1886).—Educ. at Rugby, and Emmanuel Coll., Cambridge; apptd. after exam. of 1910; arrived in India, 25th Nov., 1911, and served in the United Provs. as asst. mag. and collr., and asst. commr.; pte. sec. to the lieut.-govr., United Provs., May to July, 1916; jt. mag., Dec., 1916; offg. mag. and collr., April, 1924; mag. and collr. (prov. sub.), Jan., 1927; confd., July, 1927; dep. commr., Nov., 1927; magte. and collr. again, Nov., 1932.

BISHWA NATH LAHIRI, M.A., Indian Police (supt.) (b. 13th Jan., 1898).—Joined the service as asst. supt., United Provs., 9th Aug., 1922; offg. supt., Dec., 1925; confd., Dec., 1932.

BISSET, ERNEST, M.B., D.P.H., Lt.-Col., L.M.S. (ret'd.) (b. 1st Dec., 1876).—First commn. dated 29th Jan., 1922; in mil. employ to 28th May, 1911; on plague duty in U.P., June, 1911; dep. sany. commr. U.P., July, 1912; offg. dir. of public health, Burma, Jan., 1913; confd., July, 1923; ret'd., Apl., 1931.

BISSETT, ALLAN, India Audit Office (suptg. exec. officer) (b. 4th Mar., 1895).—Apptd. 2nd class clerk, 5th Aug., 1914; on mil. service from May, 1915, to Feb., 1919; exec officer, April, 1922; higher exec. officer, April, 1926; suptg. exec. officer, Oct., 1934.

BISWAS, KSHITISH CHANDRA, M.A., late Indian Finance Dept. (b. 11th Nov., 1886).—joined the service, 4th April, 1909; asst. acct.-genl., Sept., 1910; dep. ditto, Punjab, April, 1925; ditto, Burma, Feb., 1926; asst. acct.-genl., Punjab, April, 1927; dep. ditto, March, 1928; ditto, Bihar and Orissa, Feb., 1929; asst. auditor-genl., Mar., 1931; dep. acct.-genl., Bihar and Orissa, Aug., 1931; ret'd., Aug., 1934.

BISWAS, PRAMATHA NARAYAN, B.A., A.M.I.E.E. (late India Posts and Telegraphs) (b. 18th Feb., 1880).—Apptd., 22nd Sept., 1902; asst. supt., April, 1904; supt., Feb., 1916; divnl. engr., Aug., 1920; ret'd., Mar., 1929.

BISWAS, SATISH CHANDRA, M.R.C.S., L.R.C.P., D.P.H., Indian State Ry's, (dist. medl. officer, G.I.P. Ry.) (b. 12th Nov., 1889).—Joined the service as dist. surg., 13th April, 1918; dist. medl. officer, Apl., 1929.

BISWAS, SATISH CHANDRA, Rai Bahadur, Indian Political Service (b. 23rd Oct., 1884).—Joined the service, 4th July, 1904; supt. F. and P. Dept., Nov., 1927; asst. secty., ditto, May, 1935; asst. secty., political dept., Apl., 1937.

BIVAR, HUGH GODFREY STUART, Indian C.S. (dist. and sess. judge, Bengal) (b. 28th Nov., 1897).—Educ. at Cheltenham Coll., and Keble Coll., Oxford (Classical Exhibitioner); joined the service, 28th Oct., 1921; arrived, 2nd Dec., 1921, and served in Bengal as asst. mag. and collr.; and sub-divl. officer, Aug., 1923; addl. presdy. mag., April, 1926; addl. dist. and sess. judge, May, 1928; offg. ditto, Nov., 1931; confd., Feb., 1934.

BLACK, D. G. LYALL, Indian Police (supt., United Provs.) (b. 4th Oct., 1894).—Joined the service as asst. supt. on 9th July, 1920; offg. supt., April, 1923; confd., Aug., 1927.

BLACKBURN, JOHN INGAMELLS, B.A., Indian C.S. (jt. mag. and dep. collr., Bihar) (b. 24th Nov., 1910). Educ. at County High School and Trinity College, Cambridge; apptd. after exam. of 1933; arrived in India, Nov., 1934, and served in Bihar and Orissa as asst. mag. and collr.

BLACKER, HAROLD ALFRED CECIL, B.A., Indian C.S. (judge, high ct., Lahore) (b. 15th Aug., 1889).—Educ. at Charterhouse, Bedford Grammar Sch., and Queen's Coll., Cambridge; apptd. after exam. of 1913; arrived, 30th Nov., 1914, and served in the Punjab as asst. commr.; on mil. service from Aug., 1917, to Dec., 1918; registrar, high ct., Punjab, Nov., 1922; dist. and sess. judge (provl.), July, 1925; ditto (perm.), Oct., 1925; addl. judge, high ct.,

Lahore, Oct., 1934; regr., high ct., Lahore, Oct. 1935; actg. judge, ditto, June, 1936; and again, Apl., 1937; judge, ditto, Dec., 1937.

BLACKSTONE, THOMAS WEST, late Indian Police (b. 3rd July, 1881).—Joined the service, 10th Dec. 1901, as prob.; asst. supt., Jan., 1903; supt., Jan., 1910; asst. inspr.-gen., Oct., 1923; offg. dep. inspr.-genl., March, 1924; confd., Oct., 1926; commr. of police, Madras, June, 1931; ret'd., July, 1936.

BLACKWELL, SIR CECIL PATRICK, M.B.E. (milty. dn.), B.A., Barr. (judge, high ct., Bombay) (b. 8th Nov., 1881).—Joined the service as judge, high ct., Bombay, Nov., 1926; offg. ch. justice ditto, Apl., 1935; and again, Mar., 1937; knighted, Jan., 1938.

BLACKWOOD, JAMES FULTON, A.M.I.C.E., late Indian State Ry's. (b. 20th Nov., 1883).—Joined the service as asst. engr., 1st Oct., 1907; exec. engr., Oct., 1915; offg. suptg. engr., April, 1925; offg. dep. dir. programme, rly. board, Nov., 1926; secty., rly. bd., May, 1931; ret'd., June, 1935.

BLAIR, FRANCIS RITCHIE, M.A., Indian Educl. Service (princl., Patna training coll.) (b. 10th Apl., 1883).—Joined the service, 27th Dec., 1913; inspr. of schools, April, 1914; and again Oct., 1921; asst. dir. of public instrn., June, 1915; dep. ditto, Oct., 1927; special officer in charge, primary and girls' educ., Aug., 1930; prinpl., Patna training coll., July, 1931.

BLAIR, JAMES RICHARD, C.I.E., M.A., Indian C.S. (mag. and collr., Bengal) (b. 20th Aug., 1890).—Educ. at Daniel Stewart's Coll., Edinburgh, and Edinburgh Univ.; apptd. after exam. of 1913; arrived 18th Dec., 1914, and served in Bengal as asst. mag. and collr.; on mil. service from March, 1915, to Feb., 1919; offg. asst. collr. of customs, Feb., 1920; confd., March, 1921; jt. mag. and dep. collr., March, 1922; mag. and collr., Nov., 1926; dep. secty., pol. and appt. dept., April, 1927; dep. secty. to govt. of India, army dept., June, 1930; offg. addl. secty., pol. dept., Bengal, Feb., 1936; C.I.E., May, 1937.

BLAKE, ALEXANDER GRANVILLE, late Indian C.S. (b. 28th Aug., 1895).—Educ. at Rossall and King's Coll., Cambridge; joined the service, 6th Oct., 1920; arrived, 3rd Dec., 1920, and served in Madras as asst. collr. and mag.; sub. collr. and jt. mag., Oct., 1922; offg. coll., Nov., 1926; offg. commr. of Coorg, May to Nov., 1927; offg. collr. and dist. magte., May, 1930; ret'd., Nov., 1937.

BLAKE, EDGAR VALENTINE MYLES, Indian Service of Engrs. (exec. engr.) (b. 2nd Dec., 1903).—Joined the service as asst. exec. engr., Burma, 1st Oct., 1927; exec. engr., Oct., 1936.

BLAKE, REGINALD GILBERT, India Office (executive officer, acct.-gen.'s dept.) (b. 20th Feb., 1898).—Apptd. asst. clerk, 3rd Dec., 1919, after previous service from 22nd Oct., 1915; second class clerk, 29th April, 1920; exec. officer, April, 1922.

BLAKER, CHARLES EDWARD, O.B.E., late Indian Service of Engrs. (b. 12th June, 1881).—Joined the service, 18th Aug., 1905; asst. engr., Jan., 1907; exec. engr., Jan., 1915;

prinpl., govt. school of engrg., Rasul, April, 1918; offg. supt. engr., May, 1932; O.B.E., June, 1935; retd., June, 1936.

BLAKISTON, JOHN FRANCIS, C.I.E., late Archaeological Survey Dept. (b. 21st Mar., 1882).—Joined the service as asst. supt., 17th Mar., 1911; I.A.R.O., Oct., 1915; supt., Jan., 1916; offg. dep. dir.-genl., Jan., 1925; offg. dir.-genl., Jan., 1933, and again, Dec., 1934; confd., June, 1935; C.I.E., May, 1937; retd., Sept., 1937.

BLANDY, EDMOND NICOLAS, C.I.E., Indian C.S. (commr., Bengal) (b. 31st July, 1886).—Educ. at Clifton Sch., and Balliol Coll. Oxford; apptd. after exam. of 1909; arrived in India, 30th Nov., 1910, and served in E. Bengal and Assam as asst. collr. and mag.; transf. to Bengal, April, 1912; offg. under-sec. to govt. of Bengal, finl. dept., Sept., 1914; confd., Oct., 1914; jt. mag. and dep. collr., June, 1917; sec. provl. recruiting board, Sept., 1917; under-sec. to govt. of India, fin. dept., April, 1919; offg. commr. of income tax, Feb., 1922; mag. and collr., Oct., 1924; dep. commr., Dec., 1928; offg. secty., govt. of Bengal, fin., commerce and marine depts., Apl., 1930; confd., Aug., 1930; C.I.E., Jan., 1933; commr., Jan., 1934; offg. ch. secty. to govt., Bengal, Nov., 1934; offg. secty. to govt., Bengal, fin. comm. and marine depts., Mar., 1936; offg. ch. secty. to govt., Bengal, Aug., 1936.

BLANEY, BRYAN EDWARD, Indian Service of Engrs. (exec. engr., Burma) (b. 17th Dec., 1895).—Joined the service, 17th Oct., 1917; asst. engr., Oct., 1918; asst. exec. engr. and offg. exec. engr., Oct., 1919; confd., Aug., 1929.

BLANFORD, HARRY RICHARD, O.B.E., Indian Forest Service (ch. consvr., Burma) (b. 15th June, 1884).—Joined, 15th Nov., 1905, and served as asst. consvr.; dep. consvr., Dec., 1910; O.B.E., Dec., 1919; consvr., Sept., 1925; ch. consvr., June, 1935.

BLANK, ABRAHAM LEWIS, M.A., Indian C.S. (secty. to govt., legisl. dept., Assam) (b. 19th May, 1891).—Educ. at City of London School and Wadham Coll., Oxford; apptd. after exam. of 1914; arrived in India, Nov., 1915, and served in Bengal as asst. mag. and collr.; apptd. to the I.A.R.O., Aug., 1916, to Nov., 1919; jt. mag. and dy. collr., Oct., 1920; offg. mag. and collr., April, 1923; offg. dist. and sess. judge, Assam, Nov., 1925; confd., Nov., 1926; commr. for workmen's compn., March, 1931; offg. secty. to govt., Assam, legisl. dept., Mar., 1935; confd., Nov., 1935.

BLASHECK, ARTHUR DAVID, F.C.H., Oec. D., late Indian Forest Service (b. 16th Jan., 1879).—Joined the service 30th Nov., 1900, as asst. consr. of forests; dep. consr., Jan., 1907; instructor, Impl. Forest Coll., Dehra Dun, April, 1911, to April, 1914; on spl. duty in rev. and agric. dept., govt. of India, from June to Oct., 1915; consr., Dec., 1921; offg. chief consr., Punjab, Jan., 1924, and again, May, 1926; confd., Mar., 1929; inspr.-genl. of forests, Oct., 1930; retd., July, 1934.

BLEASE, Rev. RUPERT GORDON, B.Sc., Indian Eccles. Estabt. (junr. chaplain, Punjab) (b. 12th Oct., 1896).—Apptd. chaplain on probation, 11th Dec., 1927; junr. chaplain, Oct., 1929.

BLENCH, THOMAS, A.M.I.C.E., Indian Service of Engrs. (b. 4th Nov., 1905).—Joined the service as asst. exec. engr., Punjab, 15th Oct., 1927; exec. engr., Oct., 1936.

BLENKINSOP, BERNARD ARTHUR, late Indian Service of Engrs. (Assam) (b. 22nd Mar., 1879).—Trained at R.I.E. Coll.; joined the service, 1st Oct., 1902, as asst. engr., Bengal; exec. engr., Oct., 1910; ditto, Assam, April, 1912; offg. suptg. engr., May, 1922; offg. chief engr., Aug., 1925; suptg. engr., Oct., 1926; offg. secty. P.W. dept., Oct., 1927; chief engr., Sept., 1928; retd., Oct., 1932.

BLEWITT, ALBERT ERNEST, Indian Police (suptg., Bihar) (b. 2nd Nov., 1898).—Joined the service as asst. supt., Bihar and Orissa, on 15th June, 1920; offg. supt., June, 1924; and again, Feb., 1930; confd., Aug., 1932.

BLOMFIELD, DOUGLAS JOHN, A.C.G.I., Indian Service of Engrs. (suptg. engr., Bengal) (b. 20th Dec., 1885).—Joined the service, 1st Oct., 1908, as asst. engr.; on mil. service, Aug., 1916, to Feb., 1919; exec. engr., Oct., 1916; offg. suptg. engr., Feb., 1927; confd., Feb., 1931; offg. ch. engr., Mar., 1934.

BLOOD, SIR BINDON, G.C.B., G.C.V.O., Genl., R.E., late Mil. Works Dept., Govt. of India.—Apptd. to Bombay public works dept., 13th March, 1871; transf. to Bengal sappers and miners, Sept., 1871; on tamine duty, 1874; exec. engr., March, 1874; took part in Jowaki expdn., 1877-78 (medal with clasp); on special duty, Calcutta torpedo defence, April, 1878; served in S. African war (medal with clasp) and Afghan war, 1879-80 (medal); rejoined Indian establt., Feb., 1880, as offg. commandant, Bengal sappers and miners; took part in Egyptian expdn., 1882 (medal, star, and 4th class Osmanieh); supernumerary exec. engr., mil. works dept., Simla, April, 1885; second in command, Bengal sappers and miners, Aug., 1885; commandant, 1886-93; apptd. to mil. works dept. as suptg. engr., Sept., 1893; col. on staff, Rawal Pindi, Oct., 1894; chief staff officer, Chitral relief force, 1895; K.C.B., Jan., 1896; cmdg. Bandelkhand dist., Jan., 1896; commanded the Malakand Field Force, July, 1897; cmdg. Meerut divn., Sept., 1898; served in South African war, 1899-1901; cmdg. Northern Army Corps, Nov., 1904; retd., Nov., 1907; G.C.B., June, 1909; G.C.V.O., June, 1932.

BLUMENTHAL, SIMON, India Audit Office (suptg. executive officer), (b. 2nd Dec., 1881).—Apptd. clerk, 2nd class, India Audit Office, 25th April, 1901; minor staff clerk, Oct., 1919; staff clerk, Feb., 1922; higher exec. officer, April, 1922; suptg. ditto, Aug., 1933.

BLUNDEN, ALFRED CHARLES, Indian Police (supt., United Provs.) (b. 18th Oct., 1891).—Joined the service as asst. supt., Dec., 1912; supt., Aug., 1924; addl. asst. to D.I.G., C.I.D., Jan., 1931; ditto, spec. beh., Apl., 1936; cent. intell. officer, U.P., Nov., 1937.

BLUNT, ALFRED DICKINSON, Indian Service of Engrs. (exec. engr., United Provs.) (b. 29th Nov., 1883).—Joined the service, 8th Aug., 1903; in mil. employ from 26th May, 1916, to 31st Jan., 1920 asst. engr., U.P.

engrg. service, Feb., 1920; I.S.E., 1st Oct., 1923; asst. exec. engr., Oct., 1923; exec. engr., Oct., 1927

BLUNT, BRIAN GAIRE, late Indian C.S. (b. 18th July, 1897).—Educ. at Christ's Coll., Christchurch, New Zealand, and Christ Church, Oxford; joined the service, 28th Oct., 1920; arrived, 3rd Dec., 1920, and served in Bihar and Orissa as asst. mag.; under-sec. to govt., pol. and dept., appt. uly, 1924; actg. pte. sec. to govr. of Bihar and Orissa, April, 1925; settlement officer, June, 1927; retd., May, 1933.

BLUNT, SIR EDWARD ARTHUR HENRY, K.C.I.E., O.B.E., B.A., late Indian C.S. (b. 14th March, 1877).—Educ. at Marlborough Coll., and Corpus Christi Coll., Oxford; apptd. after exam. of 1900; arrived, 2nd Dec., 1901, and served in the United Prov. as asst. commr. and asst. mag. and collr.; under-sec. to govt., Jan., 1906, to Oct., 1907; jt. mag., Oct., 1911; supt., census operations, from April, 1910; spl. duty, Nov. to Dec., 1912; spl. duty in fin. dept., govt. of India, Dec., 1912, to April, 1913; dep. commr. (provl. subs.), and settlt. offr., Oct., 1915; dir. of civil supplies, Sept., 1918; O.B.E., June, 1919; dir. of industries, United Provs., Oct., 1919; offg. fin. sec. to govt., Jan., 1920; confd., Nov., 1920; C.I.E., Jan., 1922; commr., Apl., 1931; member, govr.'s exec. council, United Provs., Dec., 1931 to Apl., 1935; K.C.I.E., June, 1934; retd., Dec., 1936; lecturer on "Indian Social Welfare" to I.C.S. probationers at Cambridge Univ., and Sch. of Oriental Studies, Nov., 1937.

BO, U, Indian Police (b. 13th Aug., 1911).—Apptd. asst. supt., Burma, on probn., 21st Jan., 1936.

BOAG, GEORGE TOWNSEND, C.S.I., C.I.E., Indian C.S. (second secty. to govt., Madras) (b. 12th Nov., 1884).—Educ. at Westminster and Trinity Coll., Camb.; apptd. after exam. of 1907; arrived 24th Nov., 1908 and served in Madras as asst. collr. and mag.; spl. settlt. officer, Sept., 1912; spl. duty as asst. to controller of tan stuffs, Madras, April, 1918; sub-collr. and jt. mag., March, 1919; offg. secty. to govt., fin. dept., Oct., 1923; supt. of census, Madras, April, 1920, to July, 1922; commr., corpn. of Madras, Nov., 1923, to April, 1925; on special duty at Bangalore under govt. of India, for. and pol. dept., April to June, 1925; offg. collr. and dist. magte. and sec. to govt. of Madras, fin. dept., June, 1925; confd., Aug., 1926; C.I.E., Jan., 1928; on spl. duty in ch. secretariat, June, 1930; curator, Madras record office, Nov., 1930; member, Indian tariff board, Apl., 1931; offg. addl. secty. to govt., Madras, public dept., Oct., 1933; secty. to govt., law and educn. dept., Madras, Apl., 1935; actg. second secty. to govt., Madras, Apl., 1935; ditto (provl. subs.), Mar., 1936; C.S.I., June, 1936; offg. ch. secty. to govt., Madras, July, 1936; secty. to the Governor, Apl., 1937.

BOCQUET, GUY SUTTON, C.I.E., V.D., late Indian State Railways (b. 14th May, 1882).—Apptd. traffic inspr., 19th Jan., 1901; asst. traffic supt., June, 1902; dist. traffic supt., April, 1909; on mil. duty from May, 1917, to July, 1918; dep. traffic manager, April 1918; C.I.E., Aug., 1918; offg.

traffic manager, Aug. 1925; traffic manager (provl.), Nov., 1928; confd., Feb., 1933; retd., Aug., 1936.

BODY, CHARLES STUART, late India Audit Office (b. 29th Dec., 1875).—Educ. at Beccles and Ashford Gr. Sch., clerk, 2nd class, 29th Jan., 1895; staff clerk, April, 1907; junior clerk, Feb., 1915; temply. transf. to mily. dept., May, 1917; senior clerk, Feb., 1922; suptg. exec. officer, April, 1922; retd., Mar., 1933.

BOEDDICKER, FRANCIS LEONARD FITZMAURICE, Indian State Rlys. (dist. loco. supt., Burma Rlys.) (b. 18th Feb. 1884).—Joined the service as asst. loco. supt. 7th July, 1910; dist. loco. supt., Mar., 1926; offg. dep. loco. supt., Apl., 1929.

BOLAM, JOHN WALLACE, Indian Police (b. 30th May, 1906).—Joined the service as asst. supt., United Provs., 17th Nov., 1926; asst. to A.G.G. in C. India in criminal bch., Mar., 1933; supt. of police, Benares State, Feb., 1934.

BOLDEY, TIGRANE DAVID, P.W. Dept., Burma (div. elec. engr.) (b. 16th June, 1896).—Joined the service as temp. engr., Mar., 1927; div. elec. engr., Apl., 1937.

BOLTON, SIR HORATIO NORMAN, K.C.I.E. C.S.I., B.A., late Indian C.S. (Punjab) (b. 1st Feb., 1875).—Educ. at Rossall and Corpus Christi Coll., Oxford; apptd. after exam. of 1897; arrived, 9th Dec., 1898, and served in the Punjab and N.W. Frontier Prov. as asst. commr.; asst. sec. to chief commr., Sept., 1903; to May, 1904; pol. asst., Feb., 1906; pol. asst. and divl. judge, April, 1907; pol. asst. and dep. commr., March, 1909; pol. asst. and divl. and sess. judge, Dec., 1909; pol. agent and sec. to chief commr., N.W.F. Prov., May, to Dec., 1910; pol. agent, Dir, Swat and Chitral, Feb., 1911; dep. commr., Peshawar, Feb., 1912; offg. dep. sec. to govt. of India, foreign dept., Oct., 1914; dep. commr., Peshawar, Jan., 1915; C.I.E., Jan., 1916; C.S.I., Jan., 1918; rev. commr., N.W. Frontier Prov., Decr., 1920; offg. chief commr., and agent to govr.-gen., N.W.F. Prov., July, 1921; confd. Dec., 1924; K.C.I.E., Jan., 1926; retd., Oct., 1931.

BOLTON, JOSEPH CLEMENT, B.A., Indian C.S. (dep. secty. fin. dept., Bombay) (b. 20th Mar., 1906).—Educ. at Bradford Grammar School and Queen's Coll., Oxford; joined the service, 12th Oct., 1928; arrived, Nov., 1928, and served in Sind as asst. collr.; asst. rev. offcr., Lloyd Barrage and Can. Constr., 1930-1; under-secty. to govt., home, etc., depts., Feb., 1933 to 1936; confd., July, 1933; offg. collr., Sept., 1936; dep. secty., fin. dept., May, 1937.

BOLTON, REGINALD RAINSBURY, M.C., B.A., Indian Service of Engrs. (b. 2nd Nov., 1896).—(exec. engr., Burma).—Joined the service as asst. exec. engr., on 1st Oct., 1922; offg. exec. engr., Apl., 1927; confd. Oct., 1929.

BOMFORD, GUY, CAPT. R.E., Survey of India, (supt.) (b. 28th June, 1899). First commn. dated 28th Feb., 1917; in mily. employ to 24th Aug., 1921; proby. asst. supt. Aug. 1921; confd. Oct., 1925; supt., Aug., 1929

BOMFORD, HUGH, C.I.E., B.A., Indian C.S. (acting govr. C.P. and Berar.) (b. 12th Aug., 1882).—Educ. at Marlborough and Balliol Coll., Oxford; apptd. after exam. of 1905; arrived, 26th Nov., 1906, and served in the United Provs. as asst. commr. and asst. settlt. offr.; served in Datia State from Aug., 1913, to April, 1916; joint mag., Dec., 1914; joint sec., board of revenue, March, 1920; mag. and collr., March, 1920; settlt. commr., Rewah State, Sept., 1921; C.I.E., Jan., 1931; offg. commr., Mar., 1932; and again, Feb., 1933; offg. ch. secty. to govt., Sept., 1932, and again Mar., 1934; commr., Dec., 1934; ch. secty. to govt., U.P., Oct. 1935; presdt., bd. of public health, U.P., Oct., 1936; offg. member, bd. of rev., U.P., Oct., 1936; confd., Dec., 1936; apptd. actg. govr. C.P. and Berar, Mar., 1938.

BONAR, ROBERT, A.M.I.M.E., Indian State Rlys. (works manager, E.I. Rly.) (b. 10th Apl., 1889).—Joined the service as asst. works manager, 1st April, 1927; production engr., Sept., 1928; works manager, Oct., 1930; offg. divl. supt. mechl., E.I. rly., Sept., 1935.

BONARJEE, NEIL BRUNIAT, Indian C.S. (jt. magte., United Provs.) (b. 10th March, 1901).—Educ. at Dulwich and Hertford Coll., Oxford; joined the service, 23rd Oct., 1925; arrived, 9th Dec., 1925; and served in the United Provinces as asst. commr.; jt. magte., Mar., 1927; offg. dep. commr., Mar., 1932; offg. mag. and collr., Jan., 1934; offer on spec. duty Imp. Council Agric. Research, Nov., 1936.

BOND, FRANCIS SELWYN, late Indian State Rlys. (N.W. Rly.) (b. 18th Feb., 1883).—Joined the service as asst. engr., 1st Oct. 1905; exec. engr., Oct., 1913; I.A.R.O. from 16th Aug., 1918; offg. suptg. engr., Sept., 1923; offg. dep. ch. engr., Oct., 1925; offg. ch. engr., Dec., 1927; retd., Dec., 1930.

BONINGTON, MAX CARL CHRISTIAN, O.B.E., late Indian Forest Service (Burma) (b. 26th Jan., 1874).—Joined the service, 23rd Aug., 1905; dep. consvr., March, 1920; retd., Dec., 1930; O.B.E., Jan., 1934.

BONNAUD, REGINALD CHARLES, A.M.I.C.E., late Indian Service of Engrs. (b. 8th Oct., 1894).—Joined the service as asst. engr. on 1st Oct., 1916; asst. exec. engr., 1st Jan., 1920; exec. engr., Oct., 1925; retd., July, 1933.

BONTOLT, REV. REGINALD ARTHUR, M.A., late Indian Eccles. Estabt.—Apptd. chaplain on probation, N.W.F.P., 9th Oct., 1930; junr. chaplain, Oct., 1932; retd., Oct., 1936.

BOON, GEORGE ALAN JAMES, Indian Police (b. 3rd Oct., 1912).—Joined the service as proby. asst. supt. of police, United Provinces, Nov., 1933; confd., Nov., 1935.

BOOTH, EDGAR HICKMAN, Indian Service of Engrs. (asst. exec. engr., United Provs.).—Joined the service, 1st Oct., 1929; asst. exec. engr., 31st Oct., 1929.

BOOTH, JOHN REGINALD TREVOR, C.I.E., Indian C.S. (b. 14th August, 1883).—Educ. at Bromsgrove School and Worcester Coll., Oxford; appointed after exam. of 1906; arrived, 25th November, 1907, and served in United Provs. as asst. mag. and collr.; jt. mag., Jan., 1916; spl. duty in posts and telegraphs dept., July, 1918; postmr.-gen., Bengal and Assam, Jan., 1919; ditto. central circle, Feb.,

1920; offg. dep. dir.-gen. of post office, May, 1924; postmr.-gen., Punjab and N.W.F. circle, Feb., 1925; dep. dir.-genl., posts and tels., May, 1928; P.M.G., Bengal and Assam, and offg. senr. dep. dir.-genl., Mar., 1930; confd., Feb., 1931; C.I.E., Jan., 1934; P.M.G., Bombay circle, Oct., 1936.

BOOTH, JOSEPH, B.A., Indian C.S. (asst. collr., Bombay) (b. 14th Nov., 1906).—Educ. at Clongowes Wood College, Co. Kildare, Balliol College, Oxford, University College, London, and London School of Oriental Studies; joined the service, 13th Oct., 1930; arrived in India, Nov., 1930, and served in Bombay as asst. collr.; offg. minister, Khairpur state, May, 1935.

BOOTH-GRAVELY, WALTER, C.S.I., C.I.E., M.A., Indian C.S. (counsellor to the govr., Burma) (b. 22nd May, 1882).—Educ. at Daniel Stewart's Coll., and the Univ., Edinburgh, and Trinity Coll., Oxford; apptd. after exam. of 1904; arrived, 2nd April, 1906, and served in Burma as asst. commr., asst. sec. to govt., March, 1909; offg. under-sec., May, 1910; asst. sec., rev. dept., Nov., 1910, to Nov., 1911; pte. sec. to Lt.-govr., Aug., 1912; under-sec. to govt. of India, home dept., Dec., 1913; dep. sec. to ditto, ditto (s.p.t.), Oct., 1916, to April, 1918; dep. commr., April, 1918; offg. sec. to govt. of Burma, April, 1920; member of Burma legisl. council, June, 1920; rev. sec. to govt. of Burma, Jan., 1921; sec. to govt., fin. and rev. dept., Feb., 1924; offg. commr., Jan., 1928; ditto (prov. subs.), Nov., 1931; confd., Aug., 1933; C.I.E., Jan., 1931; ch. secty. to govt., Burma, Aug., 1932; C.S.I., Jan., 1936; temp. member, govr.'s exec. council, Burma, May, 1936; counsellor to the govr., Apl., 1937.

BOR, NORMAN LOFTUS, B.A., B.Sc., Indian Forest Service (forest botanist) (b. 4th May, 1893).—Joined the service as asst. consvr. on 20th Dec., 1921; dep. consvr., Aug., 1924; pol. offer., Balipara frontier tract, Dec., 1931; bot. for. offer. and silvicul., Assam, Nov., 1936; for. botanist, Dehra Dun, Oct., 1937.

BORLASE, REV. JOHN JENNINGS DINGLE, LL.D., M.B.E., late Indian Eccl. Estabt. (Madras) (b. 10th Feb., 1878).—Apptd. chaplain, 15th Dec., 1916; rendered service during the war; M.B.E., June, 1919; senior chaplain, Dec., 1919; retd., Sept., 1931.

BOSE (see also Basu).

BOSE, AMBUJI NATH, M.B.E., Lt.-Col., I.M.S., Bihar civil Medl. dept. (prof. Patna medl. coll.) (b. 21st Aug., 1890).—First commd. 13th Mar., 1916; in milty. employ prior to 1st Apl., 1926; offg. prof., Prince of Wales medl. coll., Patna, Apl., 1926; prof., medl. coll., Patna, Sept., 1927; bacteriologist to govt., Bihar and Orissa, Feb., 1928.

BOSE, ARUN KUMAR, RAI BAHADUR, B.A., M.B.E., late Bihar and Orissa Civil Service (b. 27th Nov., 1880).—Joined the service as offg. dep. magte. and dep. collr., 4th Sept., 1903; confd., May, 1904; M.B.E., June, 1918; offg. magte. and collr., Apl., 1930, and again, Apl., 1933; confd., Jan., 1934; retd., Aug., 1935.

BOSE, BABU JATINDRA NATH, late Indian Forest Service (b. 12th Nov., 1881). Apptd. to I.F.S. as dep. consvr., 1st April, 1924; retd., Jan., 1934.

BOSE, BIRENDRA KUMAR, L.C.E., late Indian Service of Engrs. (b. 16th Sept., 1882).—Joined the service as apprentice engr. (prov. service), 20th Feb., 1908; asst. engr., Aug., 1914; exec. engr., Feb., 1917; ret'd., Sept., 1937.

BOSE, CONSTANCE HOPE, Office of High Commr. for India (educ. officer, exec. grade, educn. dept.).—Apptd. educ. officer, 14th April, 1930.

BOSE, FANINDRA NATH, B.Sc., M.R.S.I., A.M.I.C.E., A.M.I.E.E., Indian State Rlys. (exec. engr., E.I. rly.) (b. 10th May, 1887).—Joined the service, 7th Nov., 1914 as junr. engr.; asst. engr., Jan., 1919; asst. exec. engr., E.I. Rly., Nov., 1927; exec. engr. (junr.), Mar., 1928; ditto (senr.), Oct., 1933.

BOSE, HEMENDRA KUMAR, M.A., Finance dept., govt. of India (b. 10th Aug., 1892).—Joined the service as probnr., 11th Feb., 1916; asst. acct.-genl., Feb., 1918; dep. acct.-genl., Burma, May, 1929; ditto, Bengal, Sept., 1929; examr., outside audit dept., Bengal, Aug., 1930; dep. acct.-genl., June, 1932; offg. dep. ch. auditor, Burma rlys., Feb., 1933; offg. ch. auditor, rly. clearing accts., Apl., 1934; dep. acct.-genl., United Provs., Jan., 1935; offg. dep. acct.-genl., posts and tels., Jan., 1935.

BOSE, PRAMOTHA NATH, Rai Bahadur, M.A., late India Posts and Tels.—Joined the service, 5th Aug., 1895, and held appts. in Ordnance dept. to 10th April, 1897; proby. supt., April, 1897; confd., July, 1900; dep. postmaster-genl., June, 1918; offg. postmaster-genl., Bengal and Assam, Jan., 1926; ditto, U.P., March, 1927; ret'd., Oct., 1928.

BOSE, PROBHASH CHUNDER, Rai Bahadur, Indian State Rlys. (dist. traffic supt., E.B. Rly.) (b. 12th Dec., 1890).—Asst. traffic supt., Apl., 1922; asst. dist. traff. supt., junior scale, May, 1927; dist. traff. supt. (prov.), June, 1937.

BOSE, SUDHANSU KUMAR, B.Sc., A.R.S.M., Indian School of Mines (b. 7th Oct., 1900).—Apptd. prof. of mining and surveying, 7th Dec., 1927; offg. vice-princl., Indian sch. of mines, May, 1935.

BOSE, SUDHANSU MOHAN, M.A., LL.B. (Cantab). Bar.-at.-Law, apptd. Member, Public Service Commission, Legal, 13th Apl., 1937.

BOSE, SUKUMAR, B.Sc., Indian C.S. (asst. commr., Central Provinces) (b. 11th Jan., 1909); educ. at University College, London, and School of Oriental Studies, London.—Joined the service, 12th Oct., 1931; arrived in India, Nov., 1931, and served in the Central Provinces as asst. commr.

BOSE, VIVIAN, Barr. Judl. Dept., Central Provs. (Judge high ct., Nagpur).—Apptd. Judge, high ct., Nagpur, Jan., 1936.

BOSTOCK, THOMAS MAURICE, late Indian Service of Engrs. (b. 29th Aug., 1877).—Trained at the R.I.E. Coll., apptd. asst. engr., 1st Oct., 1900; attached to Punjab, Dec., 1900; exec. engr., Oct., 1908; offg. suptg. engr., Feb., 1921; confd., Feb., 1922; offg. ch. engr., and secty. to govt., Punjab irrigation bch., July, 1931; confd., June, 1932; ret'd., Aug., 1932.

BOTHAM, ARTHUR WILLIAM, C.S.I., C.I.E., B.A., late Indian C.S. (b. 10th Dec., 1874).—Educ. at Newcastle-under-Lyme and Magdalene Coll., Cambridge; apptd. after exam. of 1897; arrived, 21st Dec., 1898, and served in Bengal as asst. mag. and collr.; transf. to E. Bengal and Assam, Oct., 1905; joint mag. and dep. collr., Feb., 1906; mag. and collr., April, 1911; dep. commr., commr. of excise, &c., Assam, April, 1912; second sec. to chief commr., Assam, Nov., 1913; dep. commr., and ditto, March, 1916; C.I.E., Jan., 1918; chief sec. to govt., Assam, Dec., 1919; member of govr.'s exec. council, Assam, Dec., 1925; C.S.I., July, 1926; ret'd., Apl., 1933.

BOTTOMLEY, JOHN EDWARD, Indian State Rlys. (works manager, G.I.P. rly.) (b. 13th July, 1894).—Joined the service, 13th Jan., 1920; asst. works manager, G.I.P. rly., Jan., 1925; works manager, Oct., 1932.

BOTTOMLEY, JOHN MELLOR, C.I.E., M.A. (Oxon.), Indian Educl. Service (dir. of public instrn., Bengal) (b. 21st Mar., 1888).—Educ. at Merchant Taylor's sch., Crosby and Christ Church, Oxford; joined the service as prof'r., Dacca Coll., 12th Feb., 1911; insp. of schools, Nov., 1925; asst. dir. of public instrn., Aug., 1926; prinpl., Chittagong Coll., Dec., 1926; asst. dir. of public instrn. for Muhammadan educn., Bengal, June, 1929; prinpl., presy. coll., Calcutta, Aug., 1931; dir. of public instrn., Bengal, May, 1933; offg. secty. to govt., Bengal, educn. dept., July, 1934; C.I.E., May, 1937.

BOTTOMS, ALVAN DOUGLAS, India Audit Office (higher executive officer) (b. 22nd Sept., 1899).—Apptd. asst. clerk, 8th Aug., 1919, after previous service from 25th April, 1917; second class clerk, 29th April, 1920; transf. to Office of High Commr. for India, Oct., 1920; transf. to India Audit Office, March, 1922; exec. officer, Apl., 1922; higher exec. officer, Oct., 1934.

BOUCHIER, EDWARD WALLACE, Indian C.S. (under secty. to govt., pub. dept., Madras) (b. 1st Jan., 1906).—Educ. at St. Olave's Grammar School, and University Coll., Oxford; joined the service, 14th Oct., 1929; arrived Nov., 1929, and served in Madras as asst. coll. and mag.; sub-collr., Sept., 1932; under secty. to govt., rev. dept., Madras, June, 1934; ditto, home dept., Apl., 1936; ditto public dept., Sept., 1937.

BOUCHIEB, OWEN PLATT, Office of High Commr. for India (exec. officer, accts. dept.) (b. 2nd Mar., 1900).—Apptd. clerical officer, India Office, 14th April, 1920; transf. to Office of High Commr. for India, 1st Oct., 1920; exec. officer, April, 1925.

BOUGHEY, SIR GEORGE MENTETH, Bart., O.B.E.; B.A., late Indian C.S.—Educ. at Wellington Sch. and Trinity Coll., Cambridge; apptd. after exam. of 1901; arrived, 1st Dec., 1902, and served in the Punjab as asst. commr. and asst. settlmt. offr.; under-sec. to govt., Feb., 1910, to Feb., 1911; settlmt. offr., Dec., 1913; ret'd., Dec., 1916; succeeded baronetcy, 1927.

BOULD, REGINALD PERRY, Indian State Rlys. (dep. transpn. supt. (tels.), G.I.P. Rly.) (b. 6th Jan., 1884).—Joined the service on N.W. Rly. on 16th Nov., 1905; services transferred to G.I.P. Rly. from 6th April, 1912; asst. tel. supt., April, 1912; dep. transpn. supt. (tels.), Oct., 1926.

BOULTON, Rev. WALTER, B.A., Indian Eccles. Estabt. (junr. chaplain) (b. 11th July, 1901).—Apptd. junr. chaplain, Bengal, Jan., 1927.

BOURDILLON, Sir BERNARD HENRY, K.C.M.G., K.B.E., late Indian C.S. (United Provs.) (b. 3rd Dec., 1883).—Educ. at Tonbridge, and St. John's Coll., Oxford; apptd. after exam. of 1907; arrived, 23rd Nov., 1908, and served in the United Provs. as asst. mag.; under-sec. to govt., March, 1913; regr., high court, Allahabad, May, 1915; apptd. to I.A.R.O. Sept., 1917; jt. mag. (seconded), March, 1918; judl. asst., Persian Gulf, June, 1918; land acquisition offr., Baghdad, March, 1919; dep. sec., Iraq High Comm., Dec., 1921; sec., ditto, Jan., 1922; mag. and collr., April, 1923, and sec. to high commr., Iraq; councillor to ditto, Nov., 1924; C.M.G., June, 1924; transferred to Colonial Service as Colonial Secty., Ceylon, 27th Nov., 1929; K.B.E., June, 1931; govr. of Uganda, Nov., 1932; K.C.M.G., Jan., 1934.

BOURNE, FREDERICK CHALMERS, B.A. (Oxon), Indian C.S. (dep. commr.) (b. 12th Aug., 1891).—Educ. at Rugby and Christ Church, Oxford; joined the service, 10th Aug., 1920; arrived, 4th Dec., 1920, and served in the Punjab as asst. commr.; offg. dep. commr., Mar., 1924; (dep. commr. (provl.)) Nov., 1926; (ditto perm.), Oct., 1929; senr. secty. to finl., commrs. and dy. secty. to govt., Punjab development dept., Feb., 1932; secty. to govt., Punjab, elec. and industries dept., July, 1934 to Mar., 1937.

BOURNE, GERALD COOKE, Indian C.S. (dist. and sess. judge, Burma) (b. 23rd Feb., 1905).—Educ. at Gresham's School, Holt, Norfolk, and King's Coll., Cambridge; joined the service, 19th Oct., 1927; arrived, Dec., 1927, and served in Burma as asst. commr.; dep. registrar, high ct., Rangoon, Aug., 1931; dist. and sess. judge, Apl., 1934; regr., high ct., Rangoon, Mar., 1936.

BOWEN, ROWLAND, Indian Police (b. 27th Feb., 1916).—Apptd. asst. supt., Punjab, on probn., Nov., 1936.

BOWER, EDWARD HENRY MITCHELL, I.S.O., B.A., LL.B. (Lon.), late Registration Dept., Madras (b. 17th Jan., 1876).—Apptd. inspr.-gen. of registration, Madras, July, 1927; I.S.O., June, 1929; ret'd., Jan., 1931.

BOWER, GEORGE NOEL, B.A., Imperial Customs Service (collr.) (b. 25th Dec., 1884).—Joined the service, 23rd Oct., 1907; asst. collr. of customs, Dec., 1908; ditto, Rangoon, and dep. foodstuffs commr. in addn.; rice commr., Rangoon, Jan., 1920, to March, 1921; asst. collr., Calcutta, Dec., 1922; ditto, Bombay, Nov., 1924; ditto, Calcutta, Oct., 1927; offg. collr., Karachi, Mar., 1930; confd., Nov., 1930; collr., Calcutta, Mar., 1933; ditto Madras, Apl., 1934; ditto, Calcutta, Apl., 1936.

BOWERS, PERCY LLOYD, C.I.E., M.C., A.M.I.C.E., late Indian Service of Engrs. (b. 19th Mar., 1879).—Apptd. temp. engr.,

Bombay, 3rd Jan., 1903; asst. engr., Jan., 1903; exec. engr., Feb., 1912; on mil. duty from July, 1916 to Oct., 1919; C.I.E., March, 1919; army rank, Col. I.A.R.O.; offg. suptg. engr., April, 1926; offg. ch. engr. and jt. sec. to govt., P.W.D., Mar., 1930; confd., Mar., 1931; member, legisl. council, Bombay, 1932; ret'd., Mar., 1934.

BOWLER, VERNON ROBERT, Office of High Commr. for India (higher clerical officer, store dept.) (b. 8th Aug., 1889).—Apptd. 2nd class clerk, 23rd Jan., 1911, and att'd. to record and registry dept.; on mil. service from Dec., 1915, to March, 1919; transf'd. to office of high commr. for India, Oct., 1920; exec. officer, April, 1922; higher clerical officer, 1st Jan., 1926.

BOWMAN, ARCHIBALD IAN, M.A., Indian C.S. (asst. mag. and collr., U.P.) (b. 11th May, 1915).—Educ. at Glasgow High School and Glasgow Univ.; joined the service, 30th Sept., 1937; arrived in India, Oct., 1937, and served in the U.P. as asst. mag. and collr.

BOWN, ARTHUR MERVYN, B.A., M.C., Indian C.S. (commr. of inc.-tax, Burma) (b. 12th Aug., 1894).—Educ. at Gainsborough Gr. Sch., and Trinity Coll., Oxford; on mil. service from Aug., 1914; apptd., 3rd Feb., 1920; arrived, 25th March, 1920, and served in Burma; supt. Southern Shan States, Dec., 1923; offg. dep. commr., July, 1924; offg. commr. of inc.-tax, Bengal, Mar., 1933; ditto, Burma, Nov., 1933; ditto, N.W.F.P., Mar., 1934; ditto, N.W.F.P., Jan., 1935; confd., Jan., 1935; ditto, Burma, Apl., 1937.

BOWN, JOSEPH EDGAR, Indian Police (dep. inspr.-genl., Bihar) (b. 16th Feb., 1883).—Joined the service, 27th Nov., 1902, as asst. supt. of police; supt., April, 1912; on mil. service, Oct., 1918, to April, 1919; offg. dep. inspr.-genl., Nov., 1925, March, 1928, and again, Feb., 1932; confd., Oct., 1932.

BOWSTEAD, JOHN, M.C., B.A., Indian C.S. (mag. and collr., Bihar) (b. 16th Feb., 1897).—Educ. at King's Sch., Worcester, and Corpus Christi, Cambridge; joined the service, 11th Nov., 1921; arrived, 13th Jan., 1922, and served in Bihar and Orissa as asst. mag. and collr.; offg. under-sec. pol. and appt. dept., April, 1925; confd., July, 1926; offg. asst. pol. agent, Orissa Feudatory States, April, 1928; confd., Oct., 1931; offg. agent, ditto, Feb., 1932; offg. secty. to A.G.G. Eastern States and pol. agent, Sambalpur, Apl., 1933; offg. mag. and collr., Sept., 1935; confd., Mar., 1936.

BOYCE, WILLIAM HENRY, B.A., late Indian C.S. (Bihar and Orissa) (b. 25th July, 1885).—Educ. at Lurgan Coll., and Trinity Coll., Dublin; apptd. after exam. of 1907; arrived, 5th Dec., 1908, and served in Bengal as asst. mag. and collr.; transf'd. to Bihar and Orissa, April, 1912; asst. settl., offr., Oct., 1913; ditto and jt. mag. and dep. collr., July, 1914; dist. and sess. judge, Nov., 1921; ret'd. Sept., 1929.

BOYD, Sir DONALD JAMES, K.C.I.E., M.A. late Indian C.S. (b. 23rd Dec., 1877).—Educ. at George Watson's Coll., Edinburgh, and Edinburgh Univ.; apptd. after exam. of 1899; arrived, 3rd Dec., 1900, and served in the Punjab as asst.

commr., under-sec. to Punjab govt., Nov., 1907, to May, 1910; settlt. offr., Oct., 1911; dir. of land records, &c., May, 1916, to Oct., 1917; dep. commr., Oct., 1917; sec. to govt. of Punjab, home dept., Dec., 1922; elections of commr., and jt. sec. to govt., transferred depts., Dec., 1925; offg. commr., April, 1927; C.I.E., Jan., 1930; offg. ch. secty. to govt., Punjab, Apl., 1930; confd., Oct., 1930; commr., Dec., 1930; offg. finl. commr. (dev.), and sec. to govt., Punjab (dev. dept.), May, 1932; ty. member, govt.'s exec. council, Punjab, July, 1932; offg. finl. commr. (rev.), Nov., 1932; ditto (development), Mar., 1933; member, govt.'s exec. council, Punjab, May, 1934; K.C.I.E., Jan., 1936; ret'd., Apl., 1937.

BOYD, THOMAS CRAWFORD, Lt-Col. I.M.S. (prin. medl. coll., Calcutta) (b. 26th August, 1886).—Joined the service, 1st Aug., 1908; in mil. employ up to 7th Aug., 1921; chemical examr. and prof'r. of chemistry, Aug., 1921; prin. medl. coll., Calcutta, Apl., 1932; offg. surg.-genl., Bengal, Dec., 1934; and again, Oct., 1936.

BOYS, GUY PONSONBY, Barr., late Judicial Dept., United Provs. (b. 10th Aug., 1871).—Joined the service on 7th April, 1924, as offg. judge, high ct., Allahabad; judge, ditto, Mar., 1926; ret'd., Aug., 1932.

BOZMAN, GEOFFREY STEPHEN, Indian C.S. (addl. dep.-secty. to the Govt. of India, dept. of educn., health and lands) (b. 26th Nov., 1896).—Educ. at Whitgift Sch., and Brasenose Coll., Oxford; joined the service, 1st Nov., 1922; arrived, 13th Dec., 1922, and served in Madras as asst. collr. and mag.; spl. asst. settlt. offr., Feb., 1925; under secty. to govt., law dept., Madras, May, 1927; offg. secty., ditto, May, 1928; secty. tariff bd., June, 1930; secty. to agent of govt. of India in South Africa, Oct., 1932; commr. of Coorg, Mar., 1935; addl. dep.-secty. to the Govt. of India, dept. of educn., health and lands, Mar., 1936; on spec. duty from Nov., 1936.

BRABOURNE, Baron (Sir MICHAEL HERBERT RUDOLPH KNATCHBULL), G.C.S.I., G.C.I.E., M.C. (Governor of Bengal) (b. 8th May, 1875).—Educated at Wellington and Royal Military Academy. Served in Great War, 1915-18. (despatches thrice, M.C.) Staff officer, R.A.F., 1919-20; retired with rank of captain, 1920; J.P., Kent, 1927; M.P. for Ashford Division of Kent, 1931-33; Parliamentary Private Secretary to Secretary of State, 1932-33; succeeded to barony, Feb., 1933; G.C.I.E., Nov., 1933; appointed Governor of Bombay, 9th Dec., 1933; govt. of Bengal, Nov., 1937; G.C.S.I., Nov., 1937.

BRACKEN, SIR GEOFFREY THOMAS HIRST, K.C.I.E., C.S.I., Indian C.S. (member, govr.'s exec. council, Madras) (b. 11th April, 1879).—Educ. at Malvern Sch. and Oriel Coll., Oxford; apptd. after exam. of 1902; arrived, 30th Nov., 1903, and served in Madras as asst. collr. and mag.; supt. for Pudukkottai, March, 1909; sub.-collr. and jt. mag., Aug., 1914; sec. to commrs. of land rev., Dec., 1915; on mily. duty from June, 1916, to Aug., 1919; sub.-collr. and joint mag., Sept., 1919; collr. and dist. mag. and sec. to govt. of Madras, development dept., Feb., 1925; C.I.E., June, 1930; offg. 3rd member, bd. of rev., May, 1930;

confd., Sept., 1932; ch. secty. to govt., Madras (prov. subs.), Sept., 1931; confd., June, 1933; C.S.I., June, 1934; offg. member, govt.'s exec. council, Madras, May, 1934; confd., Mar., 1935; K.C.I.E., June, 1936; presdtt. tariff bd., Apl., 1937.

BRACKENBURY, CECIL FABIAN, C.S.I., Indian C.S. (ch. secty. to govt., Madras) (b. 13th March, 1881).—Educ. at Uppingham Sch. and Selwyn Coll., Cambridge; apptd. after exam. of 1904; arrived, 11th Dec., 1905, and served in Madras as asst. collr. and mag., and spl. settlt. offr.; sub.-collr. and jt. mag., Jan., 1916; offg. collr. and dist. mag., Dec., 1918; ditto, Dec., 1919; ditto, Nov., 1921; ditto, Nov., 1924; confd., April, 1925; offg. ch. secty. to govt., Madras, July, 1933; 3rd member, bd. of rev., Madras (prov. subs.), Aug., 1933; offg. 2nd member, ditto, and commr. of excise, Nov., 1933; 3rd member, bd. of rev. and 2nd member, ditto (prov.), Jan., 1935; offg. ch. secty. to Govt., Madras, Jan., 1935; confd., Mar., 1935; C.S.I., Jan., 1936.

BRADBURY, CECIL HOWARD, A.M.I.C.E., late Indian Service of Engrs. (b. 13th May, 1891).—Joined the service as asst. engr., Oct., 1914; asst. exec. engr., Nov., 1920; exec. engr., Oct., 1923; ret'd., Mar., 1935.

BRADBURY, Rev. JOHN HENRY, A.K.C., Indian Eccles. Estabt. senr. chaplain, Punjab) (b. 8th Oct., 1891).—Apptd. chaplain on probation, 23rd Oct., 1925; junr. chaplain, Oct., 1927. senr. ditto, Apl., 1937.

BRADFIELD, ERNEST WILLIAM CHARLES, C.I.E., O.B.E., Major-Genl., I.M.S. (dir.-genl., I.M.S.) (b. 28th May, 1880).—Joined the service, 31st Aug., 1903; prof'r. of surg., medl. coll., Jan., 1924; offg. supt., gen. hosp., Madras, May, 1926; C.I.E., June, 1928; reverted to milty. employ, Apl., 1931; offg. surg.-genl. with govt. of Bombay, June, 1935; confd., Aug., 1935; hon. surg. to the King, Dec., 1935; presdtt. medl. council of India, Feb., 1937; dir.-genl., I.M.S., Mar., 1937; member, Council of State, Feb., 1937.

BRADFORD, WALTER GLYNNE, Indian C.S. (secty. to govt., medl. and local govt. depts., Punjab) (b. 15th Oct., 1890).—Educ. at Sherborne and Corpus Christi Coll., Oxford; apptd. after exam. of 1914; arrived, 25th May, 1916, and posted to the Punjab as asst. commr.; on mily. duty, May, 1915; to Aug., 1919; offg. dep. commr., May, 1923; provl. ditto, Oct., 1926; perm. ditto, Aug., 1928; secty. to govt., transfid. depts., Punjab, Oct., 1935; ditto medl. and local govt. depts., Apl., 1937.

BRADLEY, CHARLES ALFRED KEMBLE, Indian State Rlys. (dep. ch. mechl. engr., E.B. Rly.) (b. 19th Dec., 1888).—Joined the service as asst. loco. supt., and Nov., 1913; dist. loco. supt., Nov., 1922; offg. dep. loco. supt., Mar., 1930; offg. dep. ch. mechl. engr., E.B. rly., May, 1934; confd., Nov., 1936; offg. ch. mechl. engr., Oct., 1934; and again, Apl., 1937.

BRADLEY, JOHN BERTRAM GRANVILLE, B.A., Indian C.S. (dist. and sess. judge, Burma) (b. 12th March, 1899).—Educ. at Wellington Coll., and University Coll., Oxford; joined the service 30th Oct., 1922; arrived, 26th Dec., 1922, and served in Burma as asst. commr.; sub-divnl. officer, June, 1923; offg. under-sec., to

govt., fin. and rev. dept., June, 1926; under-secy. to govt., home and pol. dept., April, 1927; dep. registrar, high ct., Rangoon, Sept., 1930; addl. dist. and sess. judge, Aug., 1931; offg. dist. and sess. judge, July, 1932; confd., Nov., 1933.

BRADLEY, JOHN WILLIAM, late Indian Forest Service (Burma) (b. 5th May, 1887).—Arrived, 28th Dec., 1910, and served as asst. consvr.; dep. consvr., Dec., 1915; retd., June, 1929.

BRADLEY-BIRT, FRANCIS BRADLEY, B.A., late Indian C.S. (Bengal).—Educ. at Brasenose Coll., Oxford; apptd. after exam. of 1897; arrived 28th Nov., 1898, and served in Bengal as asst. mag. and collr.; on spl. duty editing the Bengal Administration Reports for 1904-5 and 1905-6; ditto examining old records, 1906-7; ditto in connection with frontier trade statistics, 1907; joint mag. and dep. collr., Aug., 1907; mag. and collr., April, 1912; on mil. service from April, 1918, to Feb., 1919; depy. collr. of land rev. and collr. of excise, and collr. of stamp rev., Calcutta, Oct., 1921; mag. and collr., Aug., 1923; member, legislative assembly, 1921-1923; retd., July, 1928; author of "Story of an Indian Upland," "Chota Nagpur," "Twelve Men of Bengal," "Through Persia," &c.

BRADSHAW, ERIC JEAN, B.A., B.A.I., M.Sc., Geological Survey Dept. (geologist) (b. 30th Sept., 1897).—Joined the service as asst. supt. on probn., 8th Jan., 1923; confd., Jan., 1925; offg. supt., May, 1934.

BRADSHAW, JOHN RICHARD LANDON, Major, I.A., Indian Political Service (b. 8th Nov., 1896).—First commn., dated 16th Dec., 1914; joined the pol. dept., 23rd Oct., 1922; asst. commr., Bannu, March, 1925; asst. pol. agent, Chitral, May, 1926; dep. commr., Bannu, Nov., 1928; asst. commr., Charsadda, Jan., 1929; 3rd asst. resdt., Aden, Feb., 1930; secty. to pol. resdt. in Persian Gulf, Dec., 1930; dep. commr., Dera Ismail Khan, Jan., 1933; secty. to govt., N.W.F.P., transf. depts., Jan., 1936; ditto, devel. depts., Apl., 1937.

BRADSHAW-SMITH, ROBERT ALFRED, B.Sc., late Indian Service of Engrs. (b. 5th May, 1891).—Joined the service as asst. engr. on 1st Oct., 1913; asst. exec. engr., Oct., 1920; exec. engr., Oct., 1922; prof., civil engr. coll., Roorkee, Jan., 1925; to Oct., 1928; offg. suptg. engr., Apl., 1934 and again, Apl., 1936; retd., Dec., 1937.

BRADY, H. W., late Factory Inspection Dept., Bihar and Orissa (b. 3rd July, 1879).—Joined the service as inspr. of factories, 7th Feb., 1921; ch. ditto, June, 1923; retd., July, 1935.

BRAGANZA, LEOPOLD SCHOLASTICUS, Indian State Rlys. (exec. engr.) (b. 10th Feb., 1886).—Joined the service, 19th Apl., 1913; temp. engr., G.I.P. Rly., Dec., 1921; resdt. engr., Apl., 1924; exec. engr., May, 1935.

BRAGANZA, PAUL, L.C.E., A.M.I.E. (Ind.), late Indian Service of Engrs. (b. 1st July, 1879). Held non-gazetted appts. from 1st April, 1905, to 19th Dec., 1919; sub-engr., Dec., 1919; asst. engr., Feb., 1920; asst. exec. engr., Oct., 1923; offg. under-sec. to govt., P.W.D. (rds. and bldgs.), June, 1927; exec. engr., Jan., 1928; retd., July, 1934.

BRAHAM, HARRY VINCENT, C.I.E., M.A., late Indian C.S. (b. 6th April, 1886).—Educ. at Uppingham Sch. and University Coll., Oxford; apptd. after exam. of 1909; arrived in India, 24th Nov., 1910, and served in Bombay as asst. collr.; temp. dep. sec. to govt., rev. and fin. depts., July, 1920; actg. collr. and dist. mag. and pol. agent, July, 1922, April, 1925, and Jan., 1926; confd., April, 1928; C.I.E., Jan., 1931; retd., Mar., 1936.

BRAHAMACHARI, BIPIN BIHARI, L.M.S., D.P.H., Dept. of Public Health, Bengal (dir. of public health laboratory) (b. 10th Sept., 1876).—Joined the service as offg. dep. sany. commr., 15th March, 1916; asst. dir. of public health, May, 1918; offg. dir. of public health laboratory, June, 1929.

BRAHAWAR, RAJ NARAYAN (see Raj, N. B.)
BRAHMAMAR, RAJ NARAYAN (see Raj Narayan, B.).

BRAIDWOOD, HAROLD LITHGOW, C.S.I., B.A., late Indian C.S. (Madras) (b. 12th Aug., 1872).—Educ. at St. Paul's Sch., Corpus Christi Coll., Oxford, and Univ. Coll., London; apptd. after exam. of 1895; arrived, 3rd Dec., 1896, and served in Madras as asst. collr. and mag.; head asst., Oct., 1906; sub-collr. and joint mag., May, 1910; collr. and dist. mag., April, 1915; resdt. in Travancore and Cochin, Feb., 1917, to Dec., 1920; C.S.I., June, 1929; retd., Feb., 1930.

BRAILSFORB, REV. CYRIL, A.K.C., Indian Eccles. Estabt. (senr. chaplain, Punjab) (b. 1st Feb., 1887).—Apptd. chaplain on probation, 1st Feb., 1925; junr. chaplain, Feb., 1927; senr. chaplain, July, 1935.

BRAJ BHUSAN LAL, B.A., late Indian Posts and Tels. (b. 3rd June, 1880).—Joined the service as proxy. supt., 1st Apl., 1903; confd., July, 1907; dep. P.M.G., Bihar and Orissa, July, 1930; ditto, United Provs., May, 1932; retd., June, 1935.

BRAJ BHUSAN, SINGH, Indian C.S. (asst. jt. mag., United Provinces) (b. 21st August, 1904).—Educ. at University of Allahabad and Christ Church Coll., Oxford; joined the service, 10th Oct., 1929; arrived Nov., 1929, and served in the United Provinces as asst. coll. and mag.; offg. mag. and collr., Mar., 1936.

BRAMWELL, ERNEST CHARLES, Office of the High Commr. for India (asst. engr., mechanical transport) (b. 16th Jan., 1889).—Apptd. asst. inspr. of stores, March, 1926; asst. engr., mechanical transport, Aug., 1926.

BRANDER, GEORGE MACONACHIE, Indian C.S. (asst. commr., Punjab) (b. 12th Oct., 1906).—Educ. at Edinburgh Academy and Exeter College, Oxford; joined the service 14th Oct., 1930; arrived in India, Nov., 1930, and served in Punjab as asst. commr.; colonization offer., Dec., 1936.

BRANDER, JAMES PATERSON, M.A., late Indian C.S. (b. 30th July, 1876).—Educ. at Kelvinside Acad., Glasgow, Glasgow Univ. and Christ Church, Oxford; apptd. after exam. of 1899; arrived, 16th Nov., 1900, and served in Bombay as asst. collr., and mag. and asst. settlt. officer; junr. collr., July, 1918; offg. dir. of civ. supplies, May, 1919; collr. and mag., Dec., 1919; supdt. of stamps Jan., 1923; offg. land acquisition offr. for city

of Bombay, May, 1923; member of legisl. council, 1925 and 1932; pol. agent, Phaltan and Aundh, Jan., 1927; commr. of excise, Jan., 1928; retd., Nov., 1935.

BRANDER, WILLIAM BROWNE, C.I.E., C.B.E., M.A., late Indian C.S. (b. 1st Aug., 1880).—Educ. at George Watson's Coll. and the Univ., Edinburgh; apptd. after exam. of 1903; arrived, 28th Nov., 1904, and served in Burma as asst. commr.; dep. commr., April, 1917; C.B.E., June, 1918; offg. commr. of excise, Jan., 1920; confd., May, 1920; addl. sec., home and pol. dept., Jan., 1925; chief sec. to govt. (actg.), April, 1925; confd., Feb., 1926; C.I.E., Jan., 1927; chairman, development trust, Rangoon, April, 1928; retd., Jan., 1931.

BRANDOM, HAROLD FRANK, Office of High Commissioner for India (exec. officer, accts. dept.) (b. 28th July, 1898).—Apptd. clerical officer, acctnt.-gen.'s dept., India Office, 13th Oct., 1919; transf. to Office of High Commr. for India, 1st Oct., 1920; exec. officer, April, 1922; actg. hr. exec. offcr., Nov., 1936, to Jan., 1937, ditto, Nov., 1937, to Jan., 1938.

BRANDON, EDWIN HAROLD, M.B.E. (admin. officer, Central P.W.D.) (b. 6th Sept., 1885).—Held non-gazetted appts. from 13th May, 1903; supt., leg. dept., s.p.t., 2nd Nov., 1915; confd., June, 1916; offg. asst. sec., govt. of India, home dept., April, 1922; confd., June, 1923; M.B.E., June, 1928; transferred tempy. to dept. of industries and labour, P.W. branch, from June, 1930; admin. officer, central p.w.d., Mar., 1932.

BRANFORD, RICHARD, M.R.C.V.S., late Indian Vety. Service (Punjab) (b. 6th May, 1882).—Joined the service, 23rd Nov., 1907; supt., govt. cattle farm, Hissar; offg. chief supt., civil vety. dept., Punjab, April, 1924; retd., Nov., 1930.

BRAUND, HENRY BENEDICT LINTHWAITE, Barr., Judl. Dept., Burma (Judge, high ct., Rangoon).—Apptd. judge, high ct., Rangoon, June, 1934.

BRAY, SIR DENYS DE SAUMAREZ, K.C.S.I., K.C.I.E., C.B.E., B.A., late Indian C.S. (b. 28th Nov., 1875). Educ. at Blundell's Sch., Tiverton, and Balliol Coll., Oxford; apptd. after exam. of 1898; arrived, 21st Nov., 1899, and served in the Punjab as asst. commr.; transf. to N.W. Frontier Prov., Jan., 1902; acting asst. to agent to the govr.-gen. in Baluchistan, Aug., 1903, to July, 1906; pol. asst., July, 1906; ditto and asst. to agent to govr.-gen. in Baluchistan, Dec., 1906; census supt., Baluchistan, Jan., 1910; K.-i-H. medal, 1st class, Dec., 1911; asst. to agent to govr.-gen. in Baluchistan, April, 1912; actg. dep. sec. (foreign), for. and pol. dept., govt. of India, April, 1916; confirmed, May, 1916; C.I.E., June, 1917; offg. sec., for. and pol. dept., Sept. to Oct., 1917; offg. pte. sec. to the Viceroy, July to Oct., 1918; offg. sec., for. and pol. dept., Nov., 1918; temp. jt. foreign sec., ditto, June, 1919; C.B.E., June, 1919; offg. foreign sec., ditto, Dec., 1920; C.S.I., Jan., 1922; confd. as foreign sec. to govt. of India, May, 1923; K.C.I.E., June, 1925; retd. from I.C.S., May, 1930; member of Council of India, May, 1930; vice-presdt., ditto, Aug., 1934; adviser to sec. of state for India, Apl. to July, 1937; K.C.S.I., June, 1930; delegate of India at Assembly of League of Nations, Sept.,

1932, 1933, 1934, 1935, and 1937; author of "The Brahui Language," "Baluchistan Census Report, 1909," "The Life History of a Brahui," &c.

BRAYBROOKE, AUBREY TREVOR, late Indian Service of Engrs. (b. 7th Nov., 1878).—Joined the service, 21st Oct., 1901, as asst. engr.; exec. engr., Oct., 1913; offg. supt. engr., Sept., 1922; confd., Oct., 1925; retd. Nov., 1933.

BRAYDEN, DESMOND ANTHONY, Indian Police (supt., Bengal) (b. 28th Oct., 1901).—Joined the service as asst. supt., Bengal, on 29th Nov., 1921; offg. supt., April, 1926; confd., Mar., 1932.

BRAYNE, ALBERT FREDERIC LUCAS, C.I.E., M.A., late Indian C.S. (b. 1st April, 1884).—Educ. at the Royal Academy, Irvine, Glasgow Univ., and Trinity Coll., Oxford; apptd. after exam. of 1907; arrived, 20th Nov., 1908, and served in Bombay as asst. collr.; compiled genl. admtn. report, 1909-10; supt., land records and regn., June, 1913; under-sec. to govt., rev. and finl. depts., Bombay, Aug., 1916; dep. sec. to govt., ditto, July, 1919; offg. dep. sec. to govt. of India, fin. dept., Oct., 1920; offg. finl. adviser, posts and tels., finl. dept., govt. of India, April, 1923; C.I.E., June, 1923; on spl. duty in the finance dept., govt. of India, May, 1922, and again, May, 1931; financial adviser, posts and tels. (tempy.), April, 1923; offg. financial adviser, mily. finance, April, 1924; confd., Jan., 1925; offg. sec. to govt. of India, finance dept., Sept., 1926; offg. sec. to govt. of India, army dept., May, 1928; ditto, inance dept., Dec., 1931; secty. to Indian delegation, monetary and economic conf., London, June, 1933; retd., May, 1935.

BRAYNE, FRANK LUGARD, C.I.E., M.C., B.A., Indian C.S. (commr. rural reconstr., Punjab) (b. 6th Jan., 1882).—Educ. at Monkton Combe Sch. and Pembroke, Coll. Cambridge; apptd. after exam. of 1924; arrived, 27th Nov., 1905, and served in the Punjab as asst. commr.; sec. to Delhi munpl. cttee., June, 1910, to Sept., 1912; on military service from Dec., 1915 (M.C., March, 1919), to Nov., 1919; dep. commr., Jan., 1922, offg. commr., April, 1931; offg. commr., Oct., 1932; confd., July, 1935; commr. rural reconstruction Oct., 1934; C.I.E., Feb., 1937.

BRAYNE, WILLIAM FREDERIC, M.B., Lt. Col. I.M.S. (retd.), late Burma medl. dept. (b. 4th Mar., 1876).—First commn. dated 30th Jan., 1904; in mil. employ to 7th April, 1909; medl. offr., genl. plague duty, July, 1910; offg. dep. sany. commr., Burma, Aug., 1913; offg. civil surg., June, 1914; on mil. serv. from 26th Aug., 1914, to 28th April, 1919; civil surg., March, 1921; retd., Mar., 1931.

BRAYSHAY, SIR MAURICE WILLIAM, Kt., M.Sc., A.M.I.C.E., late Indian State Railways (agent, B.B. and C.I. rly.) (b. 7th March, 1883).—Joined the service, 1st Oct., 1905, as asst. engr.; exec. engr., Oct., 1913; dep. contr., timber supplies, Sept., 1917, to July, 1918; offg. asst. sec., rly. board, Oct., 1919; confd., Jan., 1920; services lent to B.B. & C.I. Rly. Co. from 30th March, 1924; offg. member, rly. bd., Mar., 1929; retd., Oct., 1932; offg. ch. commr. of rlys., May, 1933; member, council of state, Aug., 1933; knighted, June, 1934; offg. ch. commr. of rlys., July, 1935.

BREAKEV, ARTHUR WILLIAM HUMPHREY, B.A., Indian C.S. (asst. commr., Burma) (b. 23rd March, 1909).—Educated at the High School, Dublin, and Trinity College, Dublin; appointed after exam. of 1932; arrived in India, December, 1933, and served in Burma as asst. commr.

BREBNER, SIR ALEXANDER, C.I.E., B.Sc., Indian Service of Engrs. (chief engr. central P.W.D.) (b. 19th Aug., 1883).—Joined the service, 1st Oct., 1906, as asst. engr.; exec. engr., Oct., 1916; C.I.E., Jan., 1920; under-sec. to govt. of India, public works dept., June, 1920; suptg. engr., Feb., 1926; with govt. of India, P.W.D., from Dec., 1919, and employed as suptg. engr., Simla impl. circle; offg. ch. engr., ditto, Apl., 1931; confd., Aug., 1933; knighted, Jan., 1938.

BREE, PHILIP GEORGE, Indian C.S. (dep. commr., Central Provs.) (b. 1st Dec., 1894).—Educ. at Victoria Coll., Jersey, and Exeter Coll., Oxford; joined the service 13th Sept., 1923; arrived, 13th Nov., 1923, and served in Central Provs. as asst. commr.; offg. dep. commr., June, 1926; confd., Nov., 1932; excise and opium commr. in C.I., Nov., 1934.

BREMNER, CLAUDE EDWARD URQUHART, M.C., Lt.-Col., I.A., Indian Political Service) (b. 30th Aug., 1891).—First commn. dated 4th Mar., 1911; in milty. employ to July, 1919; asst. pol. agent, July, 1919; asst. commr., Dec., 1924; on foreign service in Baroda State from 17th May, 1927; pol. agent, South Waziristan, April, 1928; vice-consul, Ahwaz, May, 1932; pol. agent and consul, Muscat, Nov., 1932; pol. agent, Sabar Kantha agency, Apl., 1936; ditto, Quetta-Pishin, Feb., 1937; offg. rev. and judl. commr., Baluchistan, June, 1937.

BRENDON, PATRICK, B.A., Indian C.S. (asst. commr., Punjab) (b. 17th Mar., 1913).—Educ. at Tonbridge School, and Exeter College, Oxford; joined the service, 25th Sept., 1937; arrived in India, Nov., 1937, and served in the Punjab as asst. commr.

BRERETON, CHARLES CECIL TRELAWNY, M.B.E., India State Rlys. (divl. supt., N.W. kly.) (b. 14th Dec., 1889).—Joined the service as asst. traffic supt., Jan., 1909; M.B.E., June, 1919; offg. dep. traffic manager, June, 1920; dist. traffic supt., Jan., 1923; dist. comml. officer, Oct., 1926; offg. divl. supt., June, 1927; confd., Nov., 1929; offg. ch. operating supt., June, 1936.

BRETT, SIR CECIL MICHAEL WILFORD, Kt., C.S.I., Barr. (1884), late Indian C.S.—Educ. at Guildford Gr. Sch. and Cheltenham Coll.; apptd. after exam. of 1872; arrived, 30th Nov., 1874, and served in Bengal as asst. mag. and collr.; joint mag. and dep. collr., April, 1885; registrar, high court (appellate side), April, 1886; district and sessions judge, Oct., 1890; offg. jud. commr., Chota Nagpur, April-Sept., 1891; judge, Calcutta high court, June, 1900, C.S.I., Jan., 1906; knighted, Dec., 1911; ret'd., Jan., 1913.

BRETT, CYRIL TEMPLETON, C.I.E., Indian Police (dep. inspr.-gen., Bihar) (b. 23rd July, 1885).—Joined the service, 22nd Nov., 1906; asst. supt., May, 1909; supt., Oct., 1915; prinl. police training coll., Hazaribagh,

March, 1923; offg. dep. inspr.-genl., Mar., 1930; confd., Mar., 1931; C.I.E., June, 1931; offg. inspr.-genl., Mar., 1936.

BRETT, JOHN ALOYSIUS, C.I.E., Lt.-Col., I.A. (ret'd.) (b. 20th June, 1879).—First commn. dated 26th May, 1900; in mil. employ until Aug., 1906, when apptd. asst. to agent to govr.-gen. in Baluchistan; asst. pol. agent and asst. commr., Sibi, Feb., 1907; asst. commr., Peshawar, Jan., 1908; asst. pol. agent, Dir and Swat, Nov., 1908; asst. commr., Thal, Feb., 1909; ditto, Kohat, March, 1909; ditto, Thal, Aug., 1909; ditto, Bannu, and commdt. B.M. police, Dec., 1910; asst. commr., Thal, July, 1911; ditto, Nowshera, Jan., 1913; ditto, Hazara, March, 1913; city mag., Peshawar, Nov., 1914; dist. judge, ditto, Jan., 1915; asst. commr., Mardan, May, 1915; dep. commr., Dera Ismail Khan, May, 1917; asst. commr., Mardan, Aug., 1917; dep. commr., Hazara, Sept., 1918; asst. commr., Mardan, Oct., 1918; pol. offr., Dir, Swat and Chitral, Jan., 1919; jt. dep. commr., Peshawar, May, 1919; dep. commr., Kohat, June, 1919; pol. agent, Khyber, March, 1922; C.I.E., June, 1925; offg. rev. commr., N.W.F.P., Dec., 1926; pol. agent and dep. commr. in Quetta Pishin, Jan., 1927; pol. agent in Kalat and pol. agent in charge of Bolan Pass, March, 1928; offg. rev. and judl. commr., Baluchistan, Feb., 1929, and again, Mar., 1931; offg. rev. commr., N.W.F.P., Nov., 1930; offg. resdt., and A.G.G. and ch. commr., Baluchistan, May, 1932; ret'd., June, 1934.

BRETT, WILLIAM BAILIE, C.S.I., C.I.E., B.A., Indian C.S. (ch. secty. to govt., Bihar) (b. 27th Nov., 1889).—Educ. at Liverpool Coll. and Hertford Coll., Oxford; pptd. after exam. of 1912; arrived, 23rd Nov., 1913, and served in Bihar and Orissa as asst. mag. and collr.; under-sec. to govt., pol., appt., and educ. depts., Oct., 1917; ditto and pte. sec. to govr. of Bihar and Orissa, Dec., 1920; sec., Indian taxation enquiry cttee., Oct., 1924; offg. dep. commr., Oct., 1926; mag. and collr., Nov., 1928; secty., fin. dept., Bihar and Orissa, Apl., 1929; C.I.E., June, 1933; relief commr., Feb., 1934; ch. secty. to govt., Bihar and Orissa, Oct., 1935; C.S.I., May, 1937.

BREWITT, CHARLES PATRICK, M.B.E., Indian State Rlys. (dist. traffic supt.) (b. 17th Mar., 1899).—Joined the service as asst. traffic supt., 2nd Jan., 1920; dist. ditto, Burma Rly., Mar., 1931; offg. dep. agent, Burma rlys., Apl., 1935.

BRIDGE, MAURICE FREDERICK, M.B.E., B.A., Indian Forest Service (dep. consvr., Madras) (b. 14th Mar., 1893).—Joined the service as asst. consvr., Dec., 1921; dep. consvr., Dec., 1922; forest economist, Oct., 1925; M.B.E., May, 1937.

BRIDGES, Rev. FRANCIS LLEWELLYN, M.A., late Eccles. Dept., Central Provs. (b. 27th Mar., 1879).—Apptd. chaplain on probation, 21st Oct., 1910; junr. chaplain, Oct., 1913; senr. chaplain, April, 1925; ret'd., Aug., 1932.

BRIDGMAN, FRANCIS HARRY GEORGE, Indian Police (asst. supt.) (b. 14th Nov., 1912).—Joined the service as proby. asst. supt., Punjab, Nov., 1932; confd., Nov., 1934; offg. supt., June, 1935.

BRIDLE, HENRY LEWIS, India Office (higher clerical officer, acct.-genl.'s dept.). (b. 1st May, 1892).—Apptd. clerical offr., 21st Oct., 1921; higher clerical officer, April, 1926.

BRIERLEY, Sir CHARLES ISHERWOOD, Kt., C.I.E., Col., I.M.S., ret'd. (b. 24th Mar., 1879).—First commn. dated 31st Aug., 1903; in milty. employ to 18th April, 1907; offg. agency surg., Wana, April, 1907; ditto, Khyber agency, May, 1908; civil surg., Peshawar, May, 1914; chief medl. officer, N.W.F.P., Oct., 1927; C.I.E., Mar., 1929; inspr.-genl. of civilhosp., N.W.F.P., Feb., 1933; ret'd., Mar., 1936; Kt., Jan., 1936.

BRIGGS, HERBERT SAMUEL, Office of High Commr. for India (higher exec. officer, store dept.) (b. 22nd Dec., 1881).—Apptd. clerk, second class, 25th June, 1900; minor staff clerk, May, 1920; transfd. to office of high commr. for India, Oct., 1920; actg. higher exec. officer, Dec., 1921; confd., Apl., 1922.

BRIGGS, NORMAN, Lt.-Col., I.M.S., Jail dept., Sind (dir.) (b. 18th Nov., 1891).—First commn. dated 8th Aug., 1917; in milty. employ to 24th Mar., 1925; supt., borstal institution, Lahore, April, 1925; offg. dep. inspr.-genl. of prisons, Punjab, Mar., 1932; dir. of health and prisons, Sind, Apl., 1936.

BRIGGS, ROBERT CHAPMAN, late Indian State Rlys. (E.I. Rly.) (b. 4th Sept., 1882).—Trained at R.I.E. Coll.; joined the service 1st Oct., 1905; asst. engr., Nov., 1905; exec. engr., Oct., 1913; offg. suptg. engr., May, 1926; offg. divl. supt., E.I. rly., July, 1928; ret'd., Mar., 1932.

BRIGSTOCKE, FRANK LEWIS, late Indian C.S. (Madras) (b. 4th Sept., 1885).—Educ. at Llandovery Coll., and Lincoln Coll., Oxford; apptd. after exam. of 1909; arrived in India, Nov., 1910, and served in Madras as asst. collr., and mag.; under-sec. to govt., March, 1915; 1st asst. commr. and dist. mag., Coorg, Nov., 1915, to Nov., 1920; sub. collr. and jt. mag., Oct., 1921; offg. collr. and dist. mag., Nov., 1924; confd., Feb., 1930; ret'd., Feb., 1932.

BRIJ BHUSAN SARAN JETLY, M.A., L.L.B., Indian Police (suplt.) (b. 10th July, 1900).—Joined the service as asst. supt., United Provs., 21st July, 1924; offg. supt., Nov., 1932; confd., Jan., 1934.

BRIJ BHUSHAN SARAN VARMA, B.A., L.L.B., Indian Police (b. 10th June, 1908).—Joined the service as proby. asst. supt., United Provs., 25th Jan., 1932.

BRIJ LAL, RAI BAHADUR, Babu, B.A., late United Provs. Civil Service (b. 25th Nov., 1875).—Joined the service, 17th July, 1899, as dep. collr.; mag. and collr., May, 1927; offg. dep. dir. of land records and inspr.-gen. of regist., United Provs.; ret'd., Nov., 1930.

BRIJ MOHAN LAL, LALA, Indian Service of Engrs. (exec. engr., Punjab) (b. 11th Sept., 1898).—Joined the service, 31 Nov., 1921; asst. exec. engr., Nov., 1922; offg. exec. engr., May, 1928; confd., Nov., 1931.

BRIJ NARAYAN, B.A., Indian Service of Engrs. (exec. engr., Bihar) (b. 10th Oct., 1893).—Joined the service, 13th Oct., 1916; asst. engr., Oct., 1917; offg. exec. engr., April, 1925; confd., Oct., 1926.

BRISCOE, HUGH KYNASTON, C.S.I., C.I.E., M.A., late Indian C.S. (b. 29th Sept., 1879).—Educ. at Bedford Gr. Sch., and Pembroke Coll., Oxford; apptd. after exam. of 1902; arrived 27th Nov., 1903, and served in Bengal as asst. mag. and collr.; transfd. to E. Bengal and Assam, Oct., 1905; settlt. officer, Oct., 1906; under-sec. to govt., jud. and gen. dep., Feb., 1909; jt. mag. and dep. collr., Jan., 1912; transfd. to Bihar, April, 1912; on duty as asst. to dir. of temp. works, Delhi, Feb., 1912; spl. offr., temp. capital, Delhi, and sec., notified area cttee., April to May, 1913; sec., board of rev., Bihar and Orissa, March, 1914; mag. and collr., and ditto. March, 1916; offg. dep. sec. to govt. of India, fin. dept., May, 1919; C.I.E., June, 1921; sec. to govt. of Bihar and Orissa, fin. dept., April, 1924; offg. chief sec. to govt., April, 1926; confd., Nov., 1927; temp. member, govr.'s exec. council, June, 1930; C.S.I., Jan., 1931; ret'd., Jan., 1933.

BRISLEE, FRANK RICHARD, B.A., Indian C.S. (sub. collr. and jt. mag., Madras) (b. 3rd Dec., 1895).—Educ. at King's Coll. and Sch. of Oriental Studies, London; joined the service, 28th Oct., 1921; arrived, 12th Dec., 1921, and served in Madras as asst. collr. and mag.; sub. collr. and jt. mag., July, 1923; offg. collr. and dist. mag., March, 1928; spl. rev. offr., Cauvery-Mettur project, May, 1930; offg. collr., Bangalore, Apl., 1935; secty., bd. of rev., Madras, Mar., 1936.

BRISTOW, CHARLES HOLDITCH, C.I.E., B.A., Indian C.S. (collr. and mag., Bombay) (b. 28th Dec., 1887).—Educ. at Bedford Gr. Sch., and Christ's Coll., Cambridge; apptd. after exam. of 1910; arrived in India, 23rd Nov., 1911, and served in Bombay as asst. collr., and supt., land records, &c.; on mil. duty, May, 1915, to April, 1919; actg. collr., dist. mag. and pol. agt., May, 1920; settlement officer, Dec., 1923; dep. sec. to govt., genl. edl. and marine depts. (temp.), Nov., 1925; offg. collr., dist. mag. and pol. agent, Dec., 1926, and again, April, 1928; on special duty, Deccan canals, June, 1927; collr., Sept., 1929; offg. secty. to govt., Bombay, home and eccl. depts., Jan., 1935; secty. to govr. of Bombay, Apl., 1935; C.I.E., May, 1937.

BROADBENT, ARTHUR HENRY, B.Sc., A.I.C., India Audit Office (exec. officer) (b. 30th May, 1913).—Educ. at Whitgift Sch. and Birkbeck Coll., London Univ.; apptd. exec. officer, 21st Aug., 1933, after previous service at Home Office.

BROADBENT, ROBERT EVELYN CECIL, B.A., Indian C.S. (asst. commr., Punjab) (b. 12th Dec., 1909).—Educated at Charterhouse and Magdalen College, Cambridge; apptd. after exam. of 1932; arrived in India, Nov., 1933, and served in Punjab as asst. commr.

BROADFOOT, ROBERT CAMPBELL, N.D.A., Indian Agric. Service (princl., agricl. coll., Coimbatore) (b. 5th Feb., 1887).—Apptd. dep. dir. of agric., June, 1919; offg. princl. Agricl. College, Coimbatore, Jan., 1934; confd., June, 1935.

BROADWAY, Sir ALAN BRICE, Kt., Barr., late Judl. Dept., Punjab (b. 2nd Nov., 1873).—Apptd. asst. legal remembrancer, Punjab, 13th June, 1905; public prosecutor, ditto, Dec.,

1905; asst. legal remembr., ditto, April, 1909; addl. govt. advocate, ditto, March, 1915; again asst. legal remembr., Aug., 1915; addl. govt. advocate and offl. trustee, Punjab, Oct., 1915, to March, 1916; temp. addl. judge, chief ct., Punjab, May, 1916; administr.-gen. and official trustee, Punjab, Aug., 1916; temp. addl. judge, chief ct., Punjab, from Oct., 1916, to Aug., 1917, and from Oct., 1917; judge, high ct., Punjab, April, 1919; acting chief justice, June, 1927, and Oct., 1932; Kt., June, 1928; ret'd., Oct., 1933.

BROCK, HENRY NORMAN, Indian State Ry's. (transportation supt., G.I.P. Rly.) (b. 17th Dec., 1890).—Joined the service as asst. loco. supt., 13th March, 1914; asst. transportation supt., Nov., 1922; offg. div., ditto, variously from Mar., 1927.

BROCK, LESLIE EVELYN, Indian State Ry's. (dep. ch. mechl. engr., N.W. Rly.)
