(b. 13th Jan., 1885).—Joined the service, 14th Oct., 1908; asst. loco. supt., Nov., 1908; dist. ditto, April, 1915; dist. loco. officer, Dec., 1926; divl. operating officer, Dec., 1926; offg. divl. supt., April, 1928; confd., Feb., 1931; ditto (senr.), Apl., 1934; dep. ch. operating supt., N.W. Rly. (prov'l.), May, 1934; dep. ch. mechl. engr., Oct., 1936.

BROCKLEHURST, WALTER HARRISON, late Indian State Ry's. (b. 7th Sept., 1880).—Joined the service as asst. engr. (temp.), 21st April, 1909; resdt. engr., Sept., 1912; divl. engr., Nov., 1926; offg. dep. ch. engr., Aug., 1933; ret'd., Apl., 1936.

BRODIE, NEAL, M.Sc., F.C.S., A.I.C. (b. 11th July, 1893).—Joined the service, 27th Dec., 1913, as asst. chemist, Govt. Test House, Alipore; chemist, Dec., 1918; supt., Govt. Test House, Aug., 1925; dir., Industrial Research Bureau (temp.), Nov., 1934.

BROKENSHAW, ARTHUR, India Posts and Telegraphs (p.m.g.) (b. 8th Nov., 1885).—Trained at R.I.E. Coll.; joined the service as asst. supt., 1st Oct., 1906; supt., April, 1920; contr., telegraph office, March, 1927; supt., Calcutta tel. office, May, 1929; ch. supt., Bombay, tele. office, May, 1929; divl. engr., tels., and offg. dir. of engrg., Mar., 1931; confd., Sept., 1932; offg. p.m.g., Madras, Apl., 1934; dir. tel. engrg., Bombay, Sept., 1934; offg. p.m.g., Bombay, Mar., 1935; p.m.g., Madras, June, 1935; dep. dir.-gen., tels., Apl., 1936.

BROMAGE, JOHN ALDHELM RAIKES, Public Works Dept., Punjab (exec. sany. engr.) (b. 7th May, 1891).—Joined the service as asst. sany. engr., 25th Dec., 1920; exec. sany. engr., June, 1921; offg. suptg. engr., Apl., 1933; suptg. engr., health (temp.), Aug., 1935.

BROMAGE, JAMES VINCENT BENNETT RAIKES, late Indian Police Service (b. 22nd Jan., 1883).—Joined the service, 17th Dec., 1903, as asst. supt.; supt., July, 1912; on mil. service, Sept., 1916, to April, 1919; offg. dep. inspr.-gen., Dec., 1924, and again, April, 1926; confd., July, 1927; ret'd., Jan., 1931.

BROMMAGE, JOSEPH CHARLES, M.B.E., Indian Finance Dept.—Dep. fin. advr., mil. finance, May, 1933; controller of mil. accounts, Burma, Apl., 1937.

BROMLEY, RALPH EDWARD, Indian Police (dist. supt., Central Provs.) (b. 12th Jan., 1893).—Joined the service as asst. dist. supt., Nov., 1912; dist. supt., Sept., 1924.

BROOKES, ALBERT, M.A. (Cantab.), Indian Educl. Service (lecturer, Univ. Coll., Rangoon) (b. 22nd Aug., 1885).—Joined the service as a lecturer in history, Univ. Coll., Rangoon, on 6th Sept., 1922; prinpl. inter. coll., Mandalay, Nov., 1925; lecturer, Univ. Coll., Rangoon, April, 1927.

BROOKS, JOHN BIRTHWHISTLE TYRILL, B.A., Indian Forest Service (consvr., Bombay) (b. 13th Dec., 1889).—Joined the service, 5th Dec., 1912, as asst. consvr.; on milty. duty from 8th Nov., 1917, to 24th Nov., 1918; dep. consvr., Dec., 1917; offg. consvr., Feb., 1934; and again, Nov., 1936; confd., Apl., 1937.

BROOME, HAROLD HOLKAR, C.I.E., M.B., F.R.C.S., Lt.-Col., I.M.S. (ret'd.) (b. 6th March, 1875).—First commn. dated 31st Jan., 1903; in mil. employ until Oct., 1907, when he was apptd. asst. plague med. officer, Rawalpindi; profr. of anatomy, Lahore Med. Coll., July, 1903; on mil. duty from Aug., 1915, to Nov., 1918; medl. supt., Mayo Hospital, Lahore, June, 1922; prinpl., ditto, April, 1923; principal, King Edward Medical Coll., Lahore, June, 1926; C.I.E., Mar., 1929; ret'd., Mar., 1930.

BROOME, WILLIAM, Indian C.S. (jt. mag., United Provs.) (b. 18th March, 1910).—Educated at Latymer Upper School, Hammersmith, and Caius College, Cambridge; apptd. after exam. of 1931; arrived in India, November, 1932, and served in the United Provinces as asst. mag. and collr.; jt. magte., Nov., 1936.

BROOMFIELD, ROBERT STONEHOUSE, B.A., Indian C.S. (judge, high ct. Bombay) (b. 1st Dec., 1882).—Educ. at City of London Sch. and Christ's Coll., Cambridge; apptd. after exam. of 1904; arrived, 25th Nov., 1905, and served in Bombay as asst. collr. and mag., and chief inspr. of factories; asst. judge and sess. judge, Nov., 1909; jt. judge and addl. sess. judge, June, 1913; offg. judge and sess. judge, Jan., 1919; confirmed, March, 1925; judge, high ct., Bombay, Nov., 1929.

BROUGHTON, Rev. ARTHUR HARDWICKE, M.A., late Indian Eccles. Estabt. (b. 18th Oct., 1881).—Apptd. chaplain on probation, 16th April, 1917; junr. chaplain, April, 1919; senr. chaplain, Apl., 1929; ret'd., Oct., 1936.

BROWN, ALAN SUNLEY, Office of the High Commr. for India (exec. officer, store dept.) (b. 15th Feb., 1893).—Apptd. to store dept., Dec., 1921; clerical officer, Feb., 1924; exec. officer, Jan., 1926.

BROWN, ARTHUR EDWARD, M.A., LL.B., Barr., late Indian Educl. Service (b. 5th Apl., 1884).—Apptd. profr. Cotton Coll., Gauhati, Assam, 13th March, 1914; on spl. duty in legisl. dept. of govt. of India from 25th Feb., 1920, to 30th March, 1923; ret'd., Apl., 1935.

BROWN, ARTHUR FREDERICK ROBERT, M.C., B.A., Indian Forest Service (dep. consvr., Burma) (b. 9th March, 1894).—Joined the service as asst. consvr. on 18th Dec., 1922; dep. consvr., Aug., 1925.

BROWN, CHARLES HILTON, M.A., late Indian C.S. (b. 31st March, 1890).—Educ. at St. Andrew's Univ., and Univ. Coll.,

London; apptd. after exam. of 1912; arrived, 2nd Dec., 1913, and served in Madras as asst. collr. and mag.; sub. collr. and jt. mag., Dec., 1917; under-sec. to govt., home dept., Oct., 1918; first asst. commr. and dist. mag., Coorg, May, 1920; offg. commr. of Coorg, May, 1921, to Jan., 1922; offg. spl. offr., chief secretariat, May, 1924; offg. collr. and dist. mag., Oct., 1924; ditto, June, 1925; offg. collr., June, 1926; offg. secty. to govt., local self-govt. dept., Dec., 1928; collr., June, 1933; ret'd., Jan., 1935.

BROWN, GILBERT ALEXANDER MURRAY, O.B.E., Indian Service of Engrs (exec. engr., Punjab) (b. 24th Jan., 1893).—Joined the service 1st Oct., 1919; O.B.E., June, 1919; asst. exec. engr., May, 1920; exec. engr., Oct., 1925; on spl. duty in Waziristan survey circle, May, 1929.

BROWN, HAROLD ARROWSMITH, B.A., Barr., late Indian C.S. (b. 30th June, 1879).—Educ. at Plymouth and Mannamead Coll., and Caius Coll., Cambridge; apptd. after exam. of 1902; arrived 28th Nov., 1903, and served in Burma as asst. commr.; asst. sec. to govt., Dec., 1906; under-sec., Oct., 1908, to Feb., 1910; dep. commr., Aug., 1916; dist. judge, Aug., 1916; offg. divl. judge, Oct., 1917; addl. judge, chief court of Upper Burma, July, 1920; actg. judge, high ct., Rangoon, April, 1924, and again, March, 1926; confd., Jan., 1927; ret'd., Nov., 1933.

BROWN, HUBERT ARNOLD, late Indian State Rlys. (b. 12th May, 1884).—Joined the service as asst. loco. supt., 14th March, 1910; dist. ditto, Sept., 1915; divl. rolling stock officer, Nov., 1923; ret'd., June, 1934.

BROWN, JAMES BIRCH, C.I.E., B.A., Indian C.S. (collr., Madras) (b. 18th Nov., 1888).—Educ. at Trinity Coll., Dublin; apptd. after exam. of 1912; arrived, 25th Nov., 1913, and served in Madras as asst. collr. and mag.; on mil. duty, Jan., 1918, to March, 1919; sub-collr. and jt. mag. (seconded), Feb., 1918; temp. under-sec. to govt., June, 1919; offg. sec., board of rev. (separate rev.), July, 1922; dep. sec. to govt. finance dept., Aug., 1923; offg. sec. to ditto, April, 1925; dep. sec. to ditto, June, 1925; offg. collr. of salt rev., March, 1927; C.I.E., Jan., 1931; offg. collr. and dist. mag., June, 1932; confd., July, 1933; offg. commr. of lab., Madras, May, 1934.

BROWN, JOHN, late Indian State Rlys. (b. 4th Feb., 1884).—Joined the service as asst. colliery manager, 7th Feb., 1912; colliery manager, April, 1915; asst. supt., April, 1923; ret'd., Jan., 1933.

BROWN, JOHN COGGIN, O.B.E., D.Sc., F.G.S., M.I.M.E., late Indian Geological Survey (b. 24th Sept., 1884).—Joined the service, 19th Nov., 1905, as asst. supt.; O.B.E., June, 1918; supt., June, 1921; ret'd., July, 1934.

BROWN, LESLIE NANSCAWEN, B.A., late Indian C.S. (b. 19th Nov., 1886).—Educ. at Plymouth Coll., and Worcester Coll., Oxford; apptd. after exam. of 1910; arrived in India, 17th Nov., 1911, and served in Bombay as asst. collr.; persl. asst. to agent to govt., Kathiawar, Dec., 1914; on mil. duty from May, 1915; attd. to civil admin., Mesopotamia, from May, 1917; pol. offr., Qurnah divn., April to Sept., 1919; offg. collr.

and dist. mag., March, 1923; ditto and pol. agent, March, 1925; collr., Aug., 1929; pol. agent, Surgana, Oct., 1931; ret'd., Nov., 1936.

BROWN, RICHARD HAROLD, India Office (higher clerical officer, milty. dept.) (b. 27th Apr., 1882).—Appointed clerical officer, 10th Mar., 1922; his clerical officer, Sept., 1932.

BROWN, RALPH ROBERTS, B.A., late Indian C.S. (b. 8th July, 1881).—Educ. at Plymouth Coll., and Exeter Coll., Oxford; apptd. after exam. of 1904; arrived, 9th Dec., 1905, and served in Burma as asst. commr.; offg. registrar, chf. court, Lower Burma, from March, 1914, to Jan., 1917; dep. commr., Dec., 1917; offg. commr., July, 1926; confd., Aug., 1930; ret'd., Jan., 1934.

BROWN, ROBERT SIDNEY, India Office (principal, finl. dept.) (b. 10th Feb., 1889).—Apptd. second-class clerk, India Office, 1st March, 1911, and attd. to the registry and record dept.; transfed. to mil. dept., Jan., 1917; on mil. service from Dec., 1915, to Jan., 1919; minor staff clerk, Jan., 1920; asst. principal, April, 1921; temp. reading clerk to council, June, 1926—Aug., 1927; jt. editor, India Office List, Aug., 1927 to Oct., 1932; actg. princpl., Oct., 1932; confd., Dec., 1934.

BROWN, STUART KELSON, C.B., C.V.O., India Office (asst. under-secty. of state) (b. 24th Dec., 1885).—Educ. at Dulwich Coll., and King's Coll., Cambridge (Porson Prize, 1906); apptd. junr. clerk, store dept., India Office, 11th Oct., 1909; transfed. to judl. and public dept., March, 1910; asst. pte. sec. to sec. of state, Oct., 1911; pte. sec. to parl. under-sec. of state, July, 1913; senr. clerk, March, 1918; on mil. service from Nov., 1915, to Feb., 1919 (Italian Silver Medal for military valour, March, 1917; mentioned in despatches July, 1917); asst. sec. to Lord Crewe's commee. on the India Office, March, 1919; actg. pte. sec. to sec. of state, June, 1919; pte. sec. to sec. of state, June, 1919, to Sept., 1924; principal, April, 1921; joint sec., mil. dept., Sept., 1924; C.V.O., Jan., 1925; personally attached to Prime Minister for Indian Round Table Conf., London, Nov., 1930 to Jan., 1931; sec.-genl. to Indian del. to Disarmament conf., Jan.-July, 1932; and again Jan., 1933; C.B., June, 1932; actg. asst. under-secty. of state, Sept., 1934; confd., Dec., 1934.

BROWN, WILLIAM SCOTT, C.I.E., M.A., Indian C.S. (collr. and magte., Madras) (b. 30th Sept., 1890).—Educ. at Robert Gordon's Coll., Aberdeen, and University, Aberdeen; apptd. after exam. of 1913; arrived, 12th Dec., 1914; and served in Madras as asst. mag. and collr.; under-sec. to govt., home dept., April, 1920; offg. sec., board of rev., Madras, March, 1924; offg. collr. of Madras, etc., Aug., 1927; offg. collr. and dist. magte., Nov., 1928; collr. and magte. (provl. subs.), Sept., 1932; confd., Dec., 1933; secty. to govt., p.w. and lab. dept., Madras, Jan., 1935, to Oct. 1937; C.I.E., June, 1936.

BROWNE, CECIL-ARTHUR, Indian Service of Engrs. (exec. engr., Bihar) (b. 17th Dec., 1904).—Joined the service as asst. exec. engr., Bihar and Orissa, 1st Nov., 1927; offg. exec. engr., Aug., 1934; confd., Oct., 1936.

BROWNE, DENIS ROBERT HOWE, O.B.E., A.K.C. (Lon.), M.I.E. (Ind.), late Indian Service of Engrs. (b. 19th Dec., 1879).—Apptd. asst. engr., 1st Oct., 1903; exec. engr., Oct., 1911; under-sec. to govt., p.w.d. (rds. and bds.), Bombay, Feb., 1915; on mil. duty from Aug., 1916, to July, 1919; O.B.E., June, 1919; Army rank, Lt.-Col. I.A.R.O.; offg. supfg. engr., Jan., 1926; offg. ch. engr. and jt. sec. to govt., P.W.D., Bombay, Dec., 1926; ch. engr. (provl. perm.), Nov., 1928; confd., July, 1931; ret'd., Mar., 1934.

BROWNE, ERNEST GARDINER, Opium Dept., United Provs. (dist. opium officer) (b. 8th May, 1884).—Joined the service as proby. asst. opium agent, 23rd April, 1906; confd., Oct., 1907; dist. opium officer, April, 1925.

BROWNE, HORACE FAIRFAX MORESBY, Lieut.-Col., I.A. (ret'd.), late Burma Commn. (b. 14th Dec., 1876).—Arrived in India, 17th March, 1897; served in mil. dept. to July, 1908; asst. commr., Jan., 1911; on mil. service, Dec., 1914, to March, 1919; offg. dep. commr., March, 1920; dep. commr. (provl. sub.), March, 1926; offg. commr., July, 1928; ret'd., Feb., 1930.

BROWNE, ROBERT STEEL, Indian Forest Service (dep. consvr., Madras) (b. 31st July, 1899).—Joined the service as asst. consvr. on 11th Dec., 1922; dep. consvr., Nov., 1926.

BROWNE, WALTER FITZGERALD, B.A., Indian C.S. (mag. and collr., United Provs. (b. 3rd Mar., 1897).—Educ. at Trinity Coll., Dublin; joined the service, 31st Oct., 1921; arrived 3rd Dec., 1921; asst. mag. and collr., United Provs., 3rd Dec., 1921; asst. commr. from Oct., 1923, to May, 1924; jt. mag., Nov., 1927; offg. dep. commr., Sept., 1930; offg. magte. and collr., Feb., 1935; confd., Apl., 1935.

BROWNLOW, ANDREW L'ESTRANGE, Indian Police (dist. supt., Burma) (b. 22nd Jan., 1894).—Joined the service as asst. dist. supt., 19th Feb., 1921; dist. supt., Jan., 1924.

BRUCE, CHARLES EDWARD, C.S.I., C.I.E., C.B.E., Lt.-Col., I.A. (ret'd.) (b. 23rd Mar., 1876).—First commn. dated 5th Sept., 1896; in mil. employ. to Nov., 1901, when he was attached to for. dept.; pol. asst., Oct., 1902; city mag., Peshawar, Sept., 1911; dist. judge, Peshawar, April to Oct., 1912; asst. commr., Oct., 1913; asst. pol. agent, Sibi, Oct., 1914; ditto, Loralai, Oct., 1915; pol. agent, Zhor, Dec., 1916; asst. pol. agent, Loralai, Oct., 1917; pol. agent, ditto, Jan., 1918; spl. duty as pol. offir. with Marri Punitive Force, March to June, 1918; O.B.E., Jan., 1919; C.I.E., Jan., 1920; dep. commr., Khat, May, 1921; ditto, Dera Ismail Khan, Oct., 1924; C.B.E., June, 1925; offg. resident in Waziristan, Sept., 1926; C.S.I., March, 1929; rev. and judl. commr., Baluchistan, Sept., 1929; offg. ch. commr., Baluchistan, Oct., 1930 to March, 1931; ret'd., Mar., 1931.

BRUCE, ERNEST HENRY, late Indian Police Service (United Provs.) (b. 17th Oct., 1881).—Joined the service, 27th Nov., 1901, and posted to United Provs.; asst. supt. of police, Oct., 1906; supt., May, 1914; offg. dep. inspr.-gen., May, 1925; confd., Sept., 1930; offg., inspr.-genl. of rly. police, etc., Rajputana, Mar., 1930; offg. inspr.-genl. Mar., 1931; ret'd., Oct., 1932.

BRUCE, HAROLD EASTON, M.C., Indian Police (supt., Bihar) (b. 18th Sept., 1897).—Joined the service as asst. supt. on 20th Mar., 1921; offg. supt., Oct., 1923; confd., Mar., 1931; supt., police training coll., Feb., 1931.

BRUCE-JOY, ARTHUR WILLIAM, Indian State Rlys. (transportation supt., G.I.P. Rly.) (b. 23rd Mar., 1891).—Joined the service as dist. loco. supt., 22nd May, 1920; asst. transportation supt., Nov., 1922; supt., May, 1926; offg. divl. ditto, variously from March, 1927.

BRUCE-KERR, Rev. GEORGE HENRY, B.A., late Eccles. Estabt. (b. 1st Mar., 1880).—Apptd. chaplain on probation, 9th Oct., 1910; junr. chaplain, Oct., 1913; resigned on appt. to I.A.R.O. on 18th Sept., 1915; reappointed and arrived at Rangoon on 8th Dec., 1919; senr. chaplain, Aug., 1924; ret'd., Sept., 1934.

BRUEN, EDWARD JOHN, Indian Agric. Service (live stock expert to govt. of Bombay) (b. 16th July, 1886).—Held non-gazetted appts. from 14th July, 1914, to 15th Sept., 1919; dep. dir. of agric. for animal breeding, Sept., 1919; live stock expert, Aug., 1924.

BRUNYATE, Sir JAMES BENNETT, K.C.S.I., C.I.E., Indian C.S. (ret'd.).—Educ. at St. Paul's Sch., and Trinity Coll., Cambridge; apptd. after exam. of 1889; arrived, 9th Dec., 1891, and served in the N.W. Prov. and Oudh as asst. mag. and collr., asst. settlt. officer, and asst. commr.; under sec. to govt., June, 1896; under sec. to govt. of India, fin. and commerce dept., Nov., 1897; asst. commr., March, 1901; joint mag., Nov., 1901; dep. commr., March, 1904; dept. sec. to govt. of India, fin. dept., July, 1905; temp. joint sec., mil. fin. branch, April, 1907; specl. duty with International Opium Cttee., Oct., 1908, to Feb., 1909; reverted to employment of govt. of United Prov., Jan., 1909; joint sec. to govt. of India, fin. dept., mil. fin. branch, Nov., 1909; C.I.E., Jan., 1910; mag. and collr., Nov., 1912; offg. sec. to govt. of India, fin. dept., May, 1913; addl. member, govr.-gen.'s council, May, 1913; sec. to govt. of India fin. dept. April, 1914; C.S.I., June, 1915; ret'd. from Indian C.S., May, 1917; member of the Council of India, from May, 1917, to Sept., 1924; K.C.S.I., July, 1918; member, Indian constitutional relations commee., 1919; member, commee. on Indian exchange and currency, 1919.

BRYAN, ALAN FRANCIS, late Opium Dept., United Provs. (b. 26th Apl., 1882).—Joined the service, 10th Apl., 1902; dist. opium officer, Apl., 1925; ret'd., July, 1934.

BRYAN, DENZIL ARNOLD, B.A., Indian C.S. (asst. commr., Punjab) (b. 15th Oct., 1909).—Educated at the Lawrence College, Ghora Galli, University of the Punjab and Selwyn College, Cambridge; apptd. after exam. of 1932; arrived in India, Nov., 1933, and served in Punjab as asst. commr.

BRYANT, STANLEY CLARE, Indian State Rlys. (dist. traffic supt.) (b. 13th Mar., 1898).—Joined the service as asst. traffic supt., 11th May, 1920; dist. ditto, Burma Rlys., Apl., 1931.

BRYANT, WALTER TELHAM, Indian C.S. (sub-collr., Madras) (b. 12th Feb., 1906).—Educ. at Christ's Hospital and Keble Coll.,

Oxford; joined the service, 12th Oct., 1929; arrived Nov., 1929, and served in Madras as asst. coll. and mag.; sub-collr., Apl., 1931.

BRYSON, CHRISTOPHER LENWOOD, B.A., Indian C.S. (jt. mag. and dep. collr., Bihar) (b. 9th Nov., 1908).—Educated at Dulwich College and Corpus Christi College, Oxford; apptd. after exam. of 1932; arrived in India, Nov., 1933, and served in Bihar and Orissa as asst. mag. and collr.

BU, Sir MYA, Barr., Judl. Dept., Burma (ch. justice, high ct., Rangoon) (b. 10th Mar., 1885).—Apptd. asst. gov. advocate, March, 1924; addl. judge, high ct., June, 1926; judge, ditto, Dec., 1926; ch. justice, Nov., 1935; knighted, Feb., 1937.

BU, U. KLA (A.I.), late Burma Civil Service (b. 21st June, 1876).—Held non-gazetted appts. from 18th Feb., 1903; extra asst. commr., Feb., 1917; offg. dep. commr., June, 1929; confd., July, 1931; ret'd., Dec., 1932.

BUCH, Nilkanthrai Mohanlal, B.A., Indian C.S. (asst. commr., Punjab) (b. 9th July, 1908).—Educ. at School of Economics and School of Oriental Studies, London; joined the service 12th Oct., 1931; arrvd. in India, Nov., 1931, and served in the Punjab as asst. commr.; offg. dep. commr., July, 1935.

BUCHANAN, Rev. GEORGE, M.A., Indian Eccles. Estabt. (junr. chaplain) (b. 11th July, 1906).—Apptd. chaplain on probn., Church of Scotland, July, 1934; junr. chaplain, Nov., 1936.

BUCKLAND, Sir PHILIP LINDSAY, Kt., Barr. (b. 12th Sept., 1874).—Apptd. judge of the High Court, Calcutta, 10th Nov., 1919; Kt., July, 1926; actg. ch. justice Feb., 1934; ret'd., Sept., 1934.

BUCKLEY, HUGH CLIVE, M.D., F.R.C.S.E., Major-Genl., I.M.S. (surg.-genl. with the Govt. of Bombay) (b. 31st Oct., 1880).—First commn., 1st Feb., 1905; in mil. employ till Nov., 1907, and from April, 1909, to Aug., 1911; plague medl. officer, Punjab, Sept., 1911; on mil. service, Aug., 1914 to Apl., 1921; civil surgeon, United Prov., April, 1921; offg. inspr.-genl. of civil hosp., United Provs., Mar., 1933; confd., Dec., 1933, ditto, Bihar and Orissa, Dec., 1933; ditto, United Provs., Apl., 1935; surg.-genl. with the Govt. of Bombay, Mar., 1937.

BUCKNER, CEDRIC STANLEY, late Indian Police Service (b. 26th Jan., 1899).—Joined the service as asst. supt., Bengal, on 7th Sept., 1921; addl. supt., Feb., 1930; ret'd., June, 1931.

BUCKNER, JOHNSTONE STANLEY, M.B.E., late India Posts and Tels. (b. 1st June, 1873).—Joined the service, 15th Nov., 1893; on deputation with I.E.F., Mesopotamia, from 11th Sept., 1916; presdy. postmaster, Bombay, Aug., 1922; ret'd., Aug., 1929.

BUCKNEY, FRANK, B.A., Imperial Customs Service (asst. collr.) (b. 1st Jan., 1883).—Joined the service as probr., 17th Feb., 1908; asst. collr., Dec., 1911; offg. collr., Madras, Feb., 1930; ditto, Karachi, Apr., 1931; ditto, Rangoon, Oct., 1932; ditto, Karachi, Mar., 1933.

BUCKWELL, Rev. Canon FREDERICK CHARLES, B.A., late Indian Eccles. Estabt.—

Apptd. chaplain on probation, 11th Jan., 1904; junr. chaplain, Jan., 1907; senr. chaplain, Jan., 1917; ret'd., Jan., 1929.

BUDD, BERNARD WILFRED, B.A., Indian C.S. (asst. collr., Bombay) (b. 18th Dec., 1912).—Educated at West Leeds High School and Pembroke College, Cambridge; appointed after exam. of 1934; arrived in India, Nov., 1935, and served in Bombay as asst. commr.

BUDDEN, FRANCIS HENRY, M.C., Lieut.-Col., R.E., late Indian State Rlys. (b. 4th Mar., 1887).—First commn. dated 24th July, 1907; joined State Rlys. on 15th April, 1912; on milty. duty from 22nd Oct., 1914; asst. traffic supt., April, 1915; asst. agent, N.W. Rly., Sept., 1919; dep. dir., statistics, April, 1924; supt., E.I. Rly., Nov., 1926; on spl. duty with Rly. bd., April, 1928; offg. ch. publicity officer, Sept., 1928; confd., Nov., 1930; divl. supt., July, 1932; ret'd., Nov., 1933.

BUDDH SINGH, SARDAR, Indian Police (b. 20th Jan., 1905).—Joined the service as proby. asst. supt., Punjab, 27th Jan., 1929; confd., Jan., 1931.

BUDHI SINGH MIAN, RAI SAHIB, late Indian Forest Service (Punjab) (b. 20th Nov., 1876).—Joined the service 12th June, 1904, and held non-gazetted appts. to 12th May, 1913; extra asst. consvr., Nov., 1914; apptd. to I.F.S. as asst. consvr., from 23rd May, 1921; dep. consvr., May, 1922; ret'd., Nov., 1931.

BULKELEY, JOHN PIERSON, C.I.E., M.A., late Indian Educl. Service. (b. 17th Jan., 1879).—Arrived in India, Feb., 1907, and apptd. prin. Rangoon Collegiate and Normal Schools; inspr. of schools, Oct., 1911; offg. asst. dir. of public instrn., Burma, Feb., 1923; offg. dir., ditto, April, 1924; C.I.E., Jan., 1932; ret'd., Jan., 1934.

BULKLEY, ALFRED FRANCIS, Indian Police (dep. inspr.-genl., Madras) (b. 9th Mar., 1888).—Joined the service, 24th Nov., 1908; asst. supt., Jan., 1911; supt., Nov., 1921; awarded King's police medal, Feb., 1917; offg. dep. inspr.-genl., Apl., 1935; confd., July, 1936; awarded Indian police medal, Jan., 1936.

BULL, Rev. FRANCIS FAULKENER, late Indian Eccl. Estab. (Madras) (b. 9th Mar., 1878).—Joined the service 11th Oct., 1909; junr. chaplain, Oct., 1922; senr. chaplain, Oct., 1922; ret'd., Mar., 1932.

BULL, HERBERT GEORGE, India Office (suptg. executive officer, mil. dept.) (b. 13th Feb., 1882).—Educ. at Owen's Gr. Sch., Islington; served as sec. divn. clerk, War Office, from April, 1900; apptd. 2nd class clerk, accent.-genl.'s dept., 17th April, 1902; transfed. to registry and record dept., July, 1906; temply. attached to mily. dept., Jan., 1917; minor stat. clerk, April, 1918; staff clerk (temp.), Oct., 1910; higher exec. officer, April, 1922; actg. suptg. exec. officer, Mar., 1933; confd., Dec., 1934; jt. editor, India Office List, May to Nov., 1934.

BULL, LOUIS ALBERT, late Punjab Civil Service. (b. 21st Oct., 1875).—Joined the service 1st Dec., 1896; extra asst. commr., Dec., 1908; offg. dep. commr., Dec., 1926; dep. commr., Oct., 1927; ret'd. July, 1931.

BULL, WALTER, late Indian State Ry. (b. 9th Feb., 1882).—Apptd. asst. elec. engr., N.W. rly., 1st May, 1921; elec. engr., Mar., 1931; offg. ch. elec. engr., N.W. rly., Nov., 1933; retd., Feb., 1937.

BUNN, ARTHUR GEORGE, B.A., Indian C.S. (asst. mag. avl collr., Bihar) (b. 29th Jan., 1911).—Educated at Rutlish School, Merton, and St. Catherine's College, Cambridge; appointed after exam. of 1934; arrived in India, Nov., 1935 and served in B. & O. as asst. mag. and collr.

BUNYAD HUSSAIN, KHAN BAHADUR SAYAD, B.A., Punjab C.S. (dep. commr.) (b. 2nd Feb., 1886).—Joined the service, 13th May, 1912; extra asst. commr., Nov., 1913; dep. commr., confd., July, 1934.

BURBIDGE, HERBERT FREDERICK GEORGE, Indian Police (supt., Assam) (b. 25th July, 1904).—Joined the service, Dec., 1923; and deputed to police training coll., Sardah, Bengal; proby. asst. supt., Oct., 1925; confd., Nov., 1925; offg. supt., Dec., 1932; confd., Apr., 1935.

BURDON, Sir ERNEST, K.C.I.E., C.S.I., B.A., Indian C.S. (auditor-genl. in India b. 27th Jan., 1881).—Educ. at Edinburgh Acad., and University Coll., Oxford; apptd. after exam. of 1904; arrived, 27th Nov., 1905; and served in the Punjab as asst. commr., asst. acctnt.-gen., Punjab, July, 1912; dep. acctnt.-gen., ditto, Oct., 1913; under sec., fin. dept., govt. of India, July, 1914; addl. dep. finl. adviser, mil. fin. branch, June, 1916; finl. adviser, Mesopotamia expedy. force, Dec., 1918, to May, 1919; temp. finl. adviser, mil. fin. branch, and member, Indian munitions bd., July, 1919; C.I.E., June, 1921; sec. to govt. of India, army dept., Jan., 1923; C.S.I., Jan., 1926; sec. to govt. of India, fin. dept., Aug. 1927; auditor-genl. in India, Nov., 1929; Kt., June, 1931; K.C.I.E., June, 1934; Kt. of Grace of Order of St. John of Jerusalem, June, 1936.

BURDWAN, Sir BIJAY CHAND MAHTUB; Maharajadhiraja Bahadur of G.C.I.E., K.C.S.I., I.O.M.—Member of Imperial legisl. council, 1909-1912; K.C.S.I., Dec., 1911; Bengal legisl. council, 1907-1918; temp. member of Bengal exec. council, May, 1918; confd., Jan., 1919; resigned, April, 1924; G.C.I.E., Jan., 1924. Literary works:—'Vijaya Gitika,' and other Bengal poetical works and dramas; 'Impressions' (the diary of a European tour); 'Meditations,' &c.

BURFORD, WALTER BECKWITH, Indian State Railways (exec. engr., G.I.P. rly.) (b. 8th Oct., 1888).—Joined the service, 29th Dec., 1911; resdt. engr., G.I.P. rly., Apr., 1920; offg. supt. of statistics, July, 1929; exec. engr., May, 1932; offg. dep. ch. engr., bridges, G.I.P. rly., Apr., 1935.

BURGESS, FREDERICK GEORGE, Indian Forest Service (dep. cons r., Burma) (b. 1st Oct., 1895).—Joined the service as asst. consvr., on 13th Dec., 1923; dep. consvr., Nov., 1928.

BURGESS, GEOFFREY, Indian C.S. (sub-judge, Central Provs.) (b. 29th April, 1906).—Educ. at Hanley High Sch., Stoke-on-Trent, and Emmanuel Coll., Cambridge; joined the service, 8th Oct., 1928; arrived, 12th Nov., 1928, and served in the Central Provs. as asst. commr.; offg. sub-judge, Dec., 1931; regr.

judl. commr.'s ct., Apr., 1934; offg. dist. and sess. judge, Sept., 1935; confd. as sub-judge, Jan., 1935.

BURKE, EDMUND PLUNKETT, C.I.E., late Indian Service of Engrs. (b. 13th June, 1885).—Trained at R.I.E. Coll.; joined the service as asst. engr. on 1st Jan., 1907; exec. engr., Jan., 1915; offg. suptg. engr., May, 1928; confd., Oct., 1932; offg. secty., P.W. dept., Assam; offg. ch. engr., Nov., 1931; confd., Dec., 1932; C.I.E., Jan., 1936; retd., July, 1938.

BURKE, GERALD TYLER, C.I.E., M.D., Lt.-Col., I.M.S., retd. (b. 31st May, 1882).—First commn. dated 31st July, 1909; in mil. employ to 15th Feb., 1924; profir., King George's medl. coll., Lucknow, Feb., 1924; civil surg., Nov., 1925; employed under Lucknow Univ., Apr., 1929; secty., India Medl. Council, May, 1935; retd., May, 1937; C.I.E., Jan., 1938.

BURKE, Sir RICHARD JOHN CHARLES, Kt., Lieut.-Col., I.A., retd. (b. 5th May, 1878).—First commn., dated 22nd Jan., 1898; in mil. employ to Oct., 1900; asst. pol. agent, Bombay, Oct., 1902; admr. Sangli State, Feb., 1905; June, 1910; special duty in connection with visit of Prince of Wales, Oct. to Nov., 1905, and with visit of the Amir, Jan. to Feb., 1907; adviser to H.H. the Raja of Devas (junior branch) from Oct., 1911; on mil. duty from March, 1915; temp. asst. sec. to govt. of India, Army dept., April to Aug., 1915; vice-pres. council of administration, Bhavnagar, from 1st Nov., 1920, to 4th March, 1927; offg. resident, Baroda, March, 1927; ditto, Mysore and ch. commr., Coorg, June, 1930; confd., Aug., 1931; Kt., Jan., 1933; retd., Sept., 1933.

BURKE, SAMUEL MARTIN, M.A., Indian C.S. (asst. commr., Punjab) (b. 3rd July, 1906).—Educ. at Punjab University and School of Oriental Studies, London; joined the service 12th Oct., 1931; arrvd. in India, Nov., 1934, and served in the Punjab as asst. commr.; offg. dep. commr., Sept., 1936.

BURKITT, FRANCIS HOLY, C.I.E., O.B.E., late Indian Service of Engrs. (b. 5th July, 1880).—Trained at the R.I.E. Coll.; joined the service, 1st Oct., 1903; asst. engr., Punjab, Jan., 1904; exec. engr., Oct., 1911; O.B.E., June, 1918; offg. suptg. engr., Dec., 1923; confd., Sept., 1926; secty., irrigation bch., N.W.F.P., Nov., 1930; C.I.E., Jan., 1931; offg. ch. engr., April, 1931; retd., July, 1935.

BURLINGHAM, GEORGE, late India Posts and Telegraphs (b. 9th Sept., 1879).—Joined the service as asst. supt., telegraphs, 1st Oct., 1903; deputed to the N.-W. Rly. from Sept., 1914; divl. engr., Shillong, Dec., 1920; ditto, Delhi, July, 1922; divl. engr., tels., and dep. postmr.-gen., Sind and Baluchistan circle, June, 1925; retd., Jan., 1930.

BURMAN, HAROLD PERCY, Indian State Ry. (dist. elec. engr., E.B. rly.) (b. 25th June, 1896).—Joined the service, 3rd Nov., 1923; offg. dist. elec. engr., E.B. rly., Jan., 1929; confd., Mar., 1933.

BURMAN, RAM PRASAD (see Ram Prasad B.).

BURN, DUGALD STUART, C.I.E., late Indian State Ry. (b. 21st July, 1877).—Joined the service as asst. traffic supt., 14th Nov., 1901; dist. ditto, Jan., 1907; dep. traffic manager, May, 1918; genl. traffic manager, Oct., 1921;

ch. traffic manager, Nov., 1922; offg. agent, Aug., 1928; confd., Dec. 1929; C.I.E., Jan., 1932; ret'd., Sept., 1933.

BURN, Rev. JOHN HUMPHREY, B.A., Indian Eccles. Estabt. (sewr. chaplain) (b. 6th May, 1891).—Apptd. chaplain on probation, 29th Sept., 1924; junr. chaplain, Sept., 1926; sewr. chaplain, Aug., 1933.

BURN, Sir RICHARD, Kt., C.S.I., late Indian C.S.—Educ. at the Liverpool Inst., and Christ Church, Oxford; apptd. after exam. of 1889; arrived, 2nd Dec., 1891; and served in the N.W. Prov. and Oudh as asst. mag. and collr.; joint mag., June, 1896; under-sec. to govt., Nov., 1897; supt. of census operations, April, 1900; supt. of revision of Imperial Gaz., Oct., 1902; dep. commr., May, 1904, and ditto; editor of Imperial Gaz. for India, Feb., 1905; mag. and coll., July, 1907; K-i-H. medal, 1st class, Jan., 1909; sec. to govt., United Prov., Oct., 1910; chief sec., April, 1912, to Feb., 1918; C.S.I., Jan., 1917; temply. employed in finl. dept., India Office, from March to May, 1918; commr., Nov., 1918; member board of revenue, May, 1923; temp. member govt.'s exec. council, United Prov., 5th Dec., 1925; ditto, Aug., 1926; Kt., Jan., 1927; ret'd., Jan., 1927; author of 'United Prov. Census Report, 1901,' 'United Prov. Gazetteer,' and part editor of 3rd edn., 'Imperial Gazetteer.'

BURN, ROBERT NATHANIEL, O.B.E., Col. R.E., ret'd., late Indian State Railways (b. 10th July, 1881).—First commn. dated 2nd May, 1901; garn. engr., mil. wks. services, Punjab, May, 1902; asst. engr., state rys., July, 1904; exec. engr., Dec., 1911; on mil. duty from Sept., 1914 to 1918; offg. supt. engr., Nov., 1921; divl. supt. (provl.), Oct., 1924; suptg. engr., March, 1928; offg. ch. engr., March, 1928; reverted to milty. employ. Nov., 1929.

BURN, SIDNEY, Indian C.S. (judge, high ct., Madras) (b. 19th June, 1881).—Educ. at Wakefield Gr. Sch., and Queen's Coll., Oxford; apptd. after exam. of 1904; arrived 11th Dec., 1905, and served in Madras as asst. collr. and mag.; sub-collr. and jt. mag., March, 1913; supt., Pudukkottai State, from Oct., 1915, to Oct., 1922; dist. and sess. judge, Jan., 1925; offg. judge, high ct., Madras, July, 1932; Oct., 1932; Jan., 1933, and again July, 1933; confd., June, 1934.

BURN, WILLIAM GEORGE, O.B.E., V.D., B.Sc., late Indian State Rlys. (b. 8th May, 1878).—Apptd. 2nd asst. contr. of stores, 11th Jan., 1908; storekeeper, govt. of India, marine dept., Aug., 1916; dep. asst. dir., inland water transport (stores), Jan., 1918; 1st asst. contr. of stores, Sept., 1919; spl. stores officer, rly. bd., Feb., 1925; dist. contr. of stores, E.I. Rly., July, 1925; offg. dep. ditto, N.W. Rly., Nov., 1928; and again, Apl., 1932; ret'd., June, 1934.

BURNAND, WILLIAM HENRY, V.D., late Indian State Rlys. (b. 19th Aug., 1880).—Held non-gazetted appt. from 19th Sept., 1897 to 14th Sept., 1910; and again from 19th Nov., 1910, to 31st March, 1912; asst. supt., March, 1912; dist. supt., April, 1919; coal manager, Feb., 1923; dep. ch. operating supt., Feb., 1925; offg. divl. supt., March 1926; confd., Oct., 1927; offg. ch. operatg. supt., Jan., 1931; confd., June, 1933; ret'd., Apl., 1937.

BURNE, LINDSAY ELIOTT LUMLEY, C.I.E., C.B.E., Lieut.-Col. I.A. (ret'd.) late Burma commn. (b. 1st July, 1877).—Arrived in India, Dec., 1897, and rendered services in the Mily. Dept.; asst. commdt., Burma Mounted Police, July, 1902; asst. commr., June, 1905; dep. commr., Dec., 1917; commdt., Burma mily. police, May, 1918, to May, 1919; C.B.E., June, 1919; offg. dep. commr., Nov., 1920; confd., July, 1921; C.I.E., Mar., 1929; ret'd., Dec., 1937.

BURNETT, ROBERT RICHARDSON, O.B.E., Major, I.A., Indian Political Service (b. 22nd Oct., 1897).—First commn. dated 3rd July, 1915; in milty. employ. prior to 16th Mar., 1922; adjutant, Zhow levy. Corps, March, 1922; probnr. in pol. dept., Feb., 1925; asst. pol. agent, Mekran, May, 1926; asst. p. l. agent and asst. commr., Quetta-Pishin, Nov., 1927; asst. to pol. agent in Kalat, Sept., 1929; pol. agent, Zhow, Oct., 1929; secty. to agent to G.G. and C.C., Baluchistan, Oct., 1930; O.B.E., June, 1931; offg. dep. secty., for. and pol. dept., Oct., 1932; and again, May, 1933; offg. under secty., ditto, Feb., 1933; princpl. officer and adviser, Tonk state, Apl., 1934; pol. agent, Punjab Hill States, Oct., 1936.

BURNS, FRANCIS GILBERT, late Indian Service of Engrs. (b. 9th Feb., 1886).—Joined the service, 25th July, 1912; asst. exec. engr. and offg. exec. engr., July, 1923; confd., Oct., 1928; ret'd., Mar., 1935.

BURNS, HENRY GEORGE, Indian Service of Engrs. (exec. engr., United Provs.) (b. 29th Apl., 1903).—Joined the service as apprentice engr., 6th Nov., 1924; asst. exec. engr., 5th Sept., 1926; offg. exec. engr., Mar., 1931; confd., Oct., 1934.

BURNS, HERBERT PERCY, Lieut., I.M.D. Civil Surgeon, United Provs. (b. 9th Mar., 1887)—Civil surg., Nov., 1920.

BURNS, WILLIAM, D.Sc., Indian Agricul. service (dir. of agric., Bombay) (b. 6th July, 1884).—Apptd. economic botanist, Poona, 11th Oct., 1908; deputed to mil. dept., Nov., 1917; princpl., Agric. Coll., Poona, May, 1922; jt. dir. of agric. (temp.), April, 1926; offg. dir. of agric., Oct., 1932; confd., July, 1933; offg. agricul. expert, Aug., 1936.

BURRARD, Sir SIDNEY GERALD, Bart., K.C.S.I., F.R.S., Col., R.E., ret'd., late Survey of India.—First commn., 6th April, 1879; asst. sec. ord. committee for gunnery exports, April, 1881; apptd. to Indian estabt., and attached to B. Sappers and Miners, Jan., 1883; attached to mil. works dept., Baluchistan, March, 1883; asst. supt., trigonl. survey, Sept., 1884; dep. supt., June, 1889; spl. duty in connection with longitude operations between Greenwich and India, 1895; supt., Oct., 1899; offg. surveyor-gen. of India, Feb., 1908; confd., Oct., 1911; C.S.I., Dec., 1911; awarded Victoria medal of R.G.S., 1913; K.C.S.I., June, 1914; ret'd., Sept., 1919; author of 'An Account of the Scientific Work of the Survey of India' and 'Levelling of Precision in India.'

BURRELL, OSCAR LAWRENCE, Indian Police (asst. supt., Madras) (b. 13th Mar., 1908).—Joined the service as asst. supt., Madras, 14th Nov., 1927; confd., Aug., 1934.

BURRELL, ROY HERBERT ADAMS, Indian Police (b. 14th June, 1906).—Joined the service as asst. dist. supt., Central Provs., 16th Nov., 1925; offg. dist. supt., March, 1928.

BURROWS, LIONEL BURTON, C.B.E., B.A., Bengal Civil Service (commr.) (b. 12th Mar., 1883).—Joined the service, 7th May, 1903; dep. mag. and dep. collr., Oct., 1907; mag. and collr., Oct., 1924; C.B.E., Jan., 1931; offg. commr., Oct., 1932; confd., Apl., 1936.

BURT, SIR BRYCE CHUDLEIGH, Kt., C.I.E., M.B.E., B.Sc., F.C.S., Indian Agricultural Service (agric. expert, imp. council of agric. research) (b. 29th Apl., 1881).—Joined the service, 14th Jan., 1908; apptd. dep. dir. of agri., United Provs., Feb., 1908; K-i-H. medal, 2nd class, Dec., 1911; M.B.E., Jan., 1919; sec., central cotton cttee., April, 1921; dir. of agric., Bihar and Orissa, Apl., 1928; C.I.E., Jan., 1930; agric. expert, imp. council of agric. research, July, 1929; offg. vice-chairman, ditto, Oct., 1935; knighted, Jan., 1936; offl. adviser to Central Govt's delegate to Indo-British Trade negotiations, May, 1937.

BURTON, BERTRAM HAND, B.A., late Indian Police Service (b. 5th Sept., 1875).—Joined the service 2nd April, 1897, and posted to United Prov.; supt. of police, Sept., 1907; dep. inspr.-gen., Nov., 1925; retd., Sept., 1930.

BURTON, EDWARD, late Indian State Railways (b. 28th Jan., 1877).—Joined the service, 7th June, 1900, as asst. loco. supt.; dist. loco. supt., April, 1907; dep. ch. mechl. engr., May, 1922; retd. May, 1931.

BURTON, EDWARD STANLEY VINER, B.Sc. (Edin.) Indian Forest Service (b. 25th Nov., 1908).—Joined the service as asst. consvr., Punjab, 20th Oct., 1930.

BURTON, FRANCIS HELY, B.A., late Indian C.S. (Punjab).—Educ. at Cheltenham and Clare Coll., Cambridge; apptd. after exam. of 1895; arrived, 21st Dec., 1896, and served in the Punjab as asst. commr.; under-sect. to govt., Punjab, Nov., 1900, to May, 1903; dep. commr., Dec., 1906; offg. commr., March, 1922; commr. (sub-provl.); retd. Aug., 1928.

BURTON, FRANK, India Office (hr. exec. officer, acctnt.-gen's dept.) (b. 4th Aug., 1889). Apptd. second divn. clerk, Board of Trade, Sept., 1908; transfd. to Board of Educn., Sept., 1909, and to Census Office, April, 1910; apptd. second cl ss clerk, India Office, 8th May, 1913, and attd. to registry and record dept.; temporarily transfd. to mil. dept., Sept., 1914; higher clerical officer, July, 1922; exec. officer, Sept., 1932; actg. hr. exec. officer, Apl., 1936; confd., Nov., 1937.

BURTON, SIR GEOFFREY POWNALL, K.C.I.E., M.A. (Oxon.) Indian C.S. (fin. commr., Central Provs.) (b. 19th Nov., 1884).—Educ. at Bradford Gr. Sch. and Queen's Coll., Oxford; apptd. after exam. of 1908; arrived, 22nd Nov., 1909, and served in the Central Provs. as asst. commr.; under sec. to chief commr., Sept., 1913; spl. duty in sectt., Nov., 1916, to Jan., 1918; dep. commr. (provl.), Nov., 1920; confd. as dep. commr., Feb., 1921; ex-sec. commr., March, 1924; rev. secty. to govt., C.P., Jan., 1929; off. commr., Feb., 1932, and again, Mar., 1933; offg.

commr. of settlements and dir. of records, etc., Sept., 1932; commr., May, 1934; C.I.E., Jan., 1935; member, govt's exec. council, Central Provs., Apl., 1936; fin. commr., Mar., 1937; K.C.I.E., May, 1937.

BURTON, LIONEL HAND, late Indian Police (b. 19th Aug., 1883).—Joined the service as proby. asst. supt., 3rd Feb., 1903; asst. supt., May, 1904; supt., Dec., 1909; offg. dep. inspr.-genl., Jan., 1927; confd., May, 1929; retd., Mar., 1937.

BURTT, THEODORE, Indian Service of Engrs. (exec. engr., Punjab) (b. 2nd July, 1896).—Joined the service as asst. exec. engr. on 1st Oct., 1921; exec. engr., April, 1927; under-secty. (estab.), Punjab, Mar., 1932.

BURY, RONALD EDWARD, late Indian State Rys. (b. 14th July, 1880).—Joined the service as works manager, E.I. Rly., 1st Nov., 1927; retd., July, 1935.

BUSH, SYDNEY POYNTZ, A.R.I.B.A. (b. 6th Sept., 1889).—Apptd. consulting architect to govt. of Burma, 27th April, 1925.

BUSHBY, WALTER EDWIN, A.C.G.I., A.M.I.C.E., Indian Service of Engrs. (exec. engr., Sind) (b. 28th Dec., 1890).—Joined the service, 1st Oct., 1913; asst. engr., Nov., 1913; in milty. employ from 31st July, 1916, to 21st Jan., 1920; asst. exec. engr., Jan., 1920; exec. engr., Oct., 1922.

BUTCHER, GEORGE WILFRED, late India Posts and Tels. (b. 4th Aug., 1879).—Held non-gazetted appts. from 6th Aug., 1896; tele. master, central circle, Nov., 1917; proby. supt., Apl., 1921; confd., Apl., 1925; asst. dir.-genl., tels., Mar., 1929; retd., Sept., 1933.

BUTCHER, WALTER VINCENT, late Indian State Rlys. (b. 2nd Feb., 1878).—Joined the service as asst. engr., 6th Sept., 1905; exec. engr., Sept., 1914; offg. suptg. engr., Feb., 1925; offg. dep. ch. engr., Oct., 1926; retd., Aug., 1931.

BUTCHER, WILLIAM HENRY, M.C., Indian State Rlys. (exec. engr.) (b. 15th Dec., 1888).—Joined the service as temp. asst. engr., Burma rlys., 26th Jan., 1921; asst. engr., Jan., 1926; exec. engr., Burma rlys., Mar., 1935.

BUTLER, CHARLES WILLIAM, Office of High Commr. for India (higher exec. officer, trade dept.) (b. 3rd Nov., 1876).—Apptd. to office of High Commr. 18th Oct., 1920; temp. admin. officer, Dec., 1922; high exec. officer, Oct., 1929.

BUTLER, FRANK GREGORY, Barr., late Indian C.S. (b. 11th July, 1887).—Educ. at Malvern, and Peterhouse Coll., Cambridge; apptd. after exam. of 1910, arrived in India, 2nd Dec., 1911, and served in Madras as asst. collr. and mag.; sub-collr. and jt. mag., Dec., 1916; regr., high ct., Madras, Feb., 1921; dist. and sess. judge, March, 1926; actg. judge, high ct., Madras, Jan., 1934; retd., Nov., 1937.

BUTLER, G. S., B.A. (Oxon), F.I.C., Indian Ordnance Dept. (supt. cordite factory, Aruvankadu).—Apptd. chemist, cordite factory, Aruvankadu, 2nd Sept., 1910; manager and chemist, ditto, Dec., 1921; offg. supt., ditto, May, 1928; confd., Feb., 1930.

BUTLER, HAROLD EDWIN, O.B.E., Indian Police (supt., Bombay) (b. 1st Apr., 1893).—Joined the service, 14th Nov., 1913, as asst. supt.; on milty. duty from Oct., 1917, to May, 1919; supt. of police, Sept., 1925; awarded King's police medal, Jan., 1933; O.B.E., May, 1937.

BUTLER, SIR MONTAGU SHERARD DAWES, K.C.S.I., C.B., C.I.E., C.V.O., C.B.E., M.A., late Indian C.S. (b. 19th May, 1873).—Educ. at Haileybury and Pembroke Coll., Cambridge; apptd. after exam. of 1895; arrived, 26th Nov., 1896, and served in the Punjab as asst. commr.; junr. sec. to finl. commr., Nov., 1900; asst. settl. offr., May, 1902; regr. of co-operative credit societies, Sept., 1904; settlmt. offr., Kotah State, Oct., 1904; special duty under for. dept., July, 1908; ditto finl. dept., Jan. to April, 1909; C.I.E., June, 1909; dep. commr., Nov., 1909; dep. sec. to govt. of India, home dept., May, 1911; C.V.O., Dec., 1911; special duty as jt. sec. to Royal Commn. on the public service in India, from Oct., 1912, to Aug., 1915; C.B., Jan., 1916; C.B.E., Dec., 1919; presdt., Punjab legisl. council, Jan., 1921; sec. to govt. of India, dept. of educn., health and lands, July, 1922; presdt., Council of State, April, 1924; Kt., June, 1924; K.C.S.I., Dec., 1924; govr. of Central Provs., Jan., 1925, to Nov., 1929; and again, Mar., 1930 to Sept., 1933; govr. of the Isle of Man, Oct., 1933.

BUTLER, RICHARD AUSTEN, M.P. (b. 9th Dec., 1902).—Educ. at Marlborough and Pembroke Coll., Cambridge; President Union Soc., 1924; Fellow. Corpus Christi Coll., Cambridge, 1925-1929; M.P. for Saffron Walden, 1929; member, Indian Franchise ctee., Jan. to June, 1932; parly. under-sec. of state for India, Sept., 1932 to May, 1937; parly. under secty. of state for Burma, Aprl.-May, 1937; delegate to Indian round-table conf., London, Nov.-Dec., 1932.

BUTLER, STANLEY GORDON, late Indian Service of Engrs. (b. 15th Nov., 1885).—Joined the service as asst. engr., 1st Oct., 1909; exec. engr., Oct., 1917; ret'd., Aug., 1936.

BUTTERFIELD, EDWARD, D.S.O., Lt.-Col., I.A., ret'd., late Burma Commission. (b. 9th Aprl., 1880).—Arrived in India, April, 1900, and rendered services in milty. dept.; asst. commr., May, 1906; on milty. duty from Dec., 1914, to Feb., 1917; comdt., milty. police, Feb., 1917; dep. commr., May, 1918; development officer in addn., Nov., 1923; ditto and land officer, Jan., 1924; sec., &c., to Rangoon dev. trust board, March, 1924; chairman, dev. trust, Rangoon, March, 1926; commr., town lands, Burma, Feb., 1927; offg. commr. variously since Feb., 1930; ret'd., June, 1935.

BUTTERFIELD, PHILIP HENRY, late Indian Police Service (Bihar and Orissa).—Joined the service, 2nd Dec., 1907; asst. supt., Oct., 1910; supt., Aug., 1919; ret'd., June, 1928.

BUYERS, WILLIAM ALEXANDER, V.D., late Indian State Rlys. (b. 13th Aprl., 1876).—Joined the service as junr. asst. engr., 20th Nov., 1896; asst. engr., March, 1899; junr. engr., June,

1903; resdt. engr., July, 1907; dist. engr., Oct., 1914; in milty. employ from 25th May, 1918, to 8th Jan., 1919; dep. ch. engr., April, 1923; senr. govt. inspr., circle No. 5, Oct., 1926; ret'd., Sept., 1931.

BYERS, JOSEPH AUSTEN, M.A., Indian C.S. (dist. and sess. judge, Madras) (b. 1st Oct., 1895).—Educ. at King's Sch., Chester, and Balliol Coll., Oxford; joined the service, 1st Nov., 1921; arrived, 12th Dec., 1921, and served in Madras as asst. collr. and mag. sub. collr. and mag., Jan., 1924; addl. sessions judge, June, 1930; offg. dist. and sess. judge, Feb., 1932; confd., June, 1934; served temp. with govt. of Orissa, from Dec., 1936.

BYRNES, WILLIAM FRANCIS, late Indian Service of Engrs. (b. 10th Oct., 1883).—Trained at R.I.E. Coll.; joined the service as asst. engr. on 1st Jan., 1907; exec. engr., Jan., 1915; offg. dep. ch. engr., Feb., 1928; ret'd., Oct., 1935.

BYRON, FRANCIS HARVEY, Salt Rev. Dept., Burma (collr.) (b. 21st Sept., 1884).—Joined the service, 1st June, 1912; supt., Jan., 1912; offg. collr., June, 1929; confd., Nov., 1934; addl. commr., salt rev., Mar., 1937.

BYRON, RICHARD ROCHFORT, late Indian State Rlys. (E.I. Rly.) (b. 28th Mar., 1876).—Joined the service, 6th Sept., 1905; asst. engr., Aug., 1908; exec. engr., Sept., 1914; offg. suptg. engr., Jan., 1926; ret'd., Aprl., 1931.

BYU, UTUN, Barr., Burma Judl. dept. (asst. govt. advocate) (b. 22nd Aug., 1895).—Apptd. offg. asst. govt. advocate, 1st March, 1924; confd., Dec., 1926; offg. govt. advocate, Burma, May, 1936.

CABLE, WILLIAM ARTHUR, Eng. Lt. Comdr., R.I.N., ret'd. (late Marine Survey dept., Bombay) (b. 15th Jan., 1883).—Apptd. engr. and ship surveyor, Karachi, Feb., 1928; offg. princl. ditto, Bombay, Jan., 1933; ret'd., Sept., 1935.

CADELL, SIR PATRICK ROBERT, Kt., C.S.I., C.I.E., late Indian C.S.—Educ. at Haileybury Coll. and Balliol Coll., Oxford; apptd. after exam. of 1889; arrived, 7th Dec., 1891, and served in Bombay as asst. collr. and mag., and administrator of Sachin state; 2nd asst., May, 1897; chairman, Karachi plague commn., July, 1898; under-sec. to govt., rev., &c., depts., Sept., 1900, to April, 1901; 1st asst., April, 1902; collr. of customs, Bombay (imperial customs service), March, 1906; pte. sec. to govr., July, 1907; collr. of customs, Calcutta, March, 1908; senr. collr., Nov., 1912; munpl. commr., Bombay City, May, 1910; C.I.E., June, 1913; offg. commr. in Sind, July to Oct., 1913; chief sec. to govt., rev., finl. and septe. depts., Bombay, April, 1916; chief sec. in charge war purposes and septe. depts., and controller of prices (in addn.), July, 1918; C.S.I., June, 1919; offg. commr. in Sind and contr. of prices, Jan., 1920; offg. commr., Oct., 1920; confd., Mar., 1921; chairman, Bombay Port Trust, Mar., 1922; and again, Aug., 1924; temp. member of govr. exec. council, Bombay, Jan. to Aug., 1924; commr. in Sind, Sept., 1925; ret'd., Dec., 1926 knighted, Jan., 1935.

CADMAN, SAMUEL PARKES HUBERT, B.A., Indian C.S. (jt. mag. and dep. collr., Bengal) (b. 15th Nov., 1906).—Educ. at Adams' Grammar School, Newport, Shropshire, and St. John's College, Cambridge; joined the service, 15th Oct., 1930; arrived in India, Nov., 1930, and served in Bengal as asst. collr. and mag.; jt. mag. and dep. collr., Nov., 1932.

CAFFIN, ALBERT EDWARD, Indian Police (supt., Bombay) (b. 16th June, 1902).—Joined the service as asst. supt., Bombay, on 25th Nov., 1922; offg. supt., Mar., 1927, and again July, 1933; confd., Sept., 1933.

CAIRNS, JAMES, C.I.E., O.B.E., M.A., M.B., Ch.B., D.P.H., Indian State Rlys. (ch. medl. officer, N.W. Rly.) (b. 12th July, 1885).—Joined the service as principal medl. and health officer, G.I.P. Rly., 12th March, 1920; O.B.E., Sept., 1919; C.M.O., Aug., 1927; C.I.E., Jan., 1936.

CALDECOTT, CHARLES D'ARCY, M.A., Indian Forest Service (dep. consvr., Central Provs.) (b. 18th Oct., 1893).—Joined the service as asst. consvr. on 18th Feb., 1921; dep. consvr., Oct., 1922.

CALDER, CHARLES CUMMING, B.Sc., F.L.S. (Supt. Royal Botanic Garden, Calcutta, and cinchona cultivation in Bengal) (b. 3rd Dec., 1884).—Joined the service, 23rd March, 1912, as curator of the herbarium at Royal Botanic Garden, Calcutta; offg. supt. Royal Botanic Garden, Calcutta, and cinchona cultivation in Bengal, May, 1925.

CAIDER, NORMAN DOUGLAS, V.D., late Indian State Rlys. (b. 13th Jan., 1882).—Joined the service, 9th June, 1900; asst. traffic supt., June, 1905; dist. traffic supt., April, 1912; dep. traffic mgr., April, 1920; offg. dir. of estabt., rly. bd., Oct., 1929; on spl. duty, rly. bd., Oct., 1930; offg. dir. of traffic, rly. bd., Dec., 1932; offg. traffic manager, E.B. rly., Oct., 1934; confd., Aug., 1936; retd., Jan., 1937.

CALDER, WILLIAM BUCHANAN, B.Sc., Indian Service of Engrs. (exec. engr.) (b. 18th Jan., 1906).—Joined the service as asst. exec. engr., Bombay, 1st Oct., 1927; offg. exec. engr., Apr., 1931; confd., Oct., 1936.

CALDWELL, KENNETH SOMERVILLE, M.A., B.Sc., Ph.D., F.I.C., F.C.S., late Indian Educl. Service (b. 19th Feb., 1880).—Joined the service, 17th Jan., 1910, as prof. of chemistry; offg. prinpl., science coll., Patna, July, 1927; confd., Jan., 1928; retd., Feb., 1935.

CALVERT, HUBERT, C.S.I., C.I.E., B.Sc., late Indian C.S. (b. 30th Nov., 1875).—Educ. at Univ. Coll., London and King's Coll., Cambridge; apptd. after exam. of 1897; arrived, 28th Nov., 1898, and served in the Punjab as asst. commr., dep. commr., Oct., 1915; ditto, and registrar, co-operative credit societies, &c., Punjab, April, 1916; C.I.E., Jan., 1925; offg. commr., April, 1925; member, Royal Commn. on Agric. in India, 1926-27; commr., Oct., 1928; finl. commr., Mar., 1929; finl. commr.

(dev.) and secty. to govt., Punjab, dev. dept., Mar., 1932; temp. member of gov.'s exec. council, Punjab, May, 1932; and again, Sept., 1932; C.S.I., June, 1933; retd., Nov., 1933.

CALVERT, WILLIAM HANDLEY, Indian Police (supt., Assam) (b. 27th Jan., 1896).—Joined the service as proby. asst. supt., 15th March, 1921; confd., Mar., 1923; supt., Apl., 1930; pol. officer. Sadiya Frontier tract, Mar., 1935.

CAME, WILLIAM GERALD, B.Sc., Indian Service of Engrs. (suptg. engr., Bihar) (b. 8th Dec., 1889).—Joined the service as asst. engr. on 1st Oct., 1913; exec. engr., Oct., 1922; suptg. engr. (temp.), Mar., 1934; confd., Apl., 1936.

CAMERON, BRUCE LACHLAN, Indian State Rlys. (dep. agent, personnel, N.W. Rly.) (b. 13th Jan., 1893).—Joined the service, 21st Mar., 1913; on milty. service from May, 1918, to Nov., 1920; offg. dist. traffic supt., Nov., 1920; asst. ditto, Jan., 1922; divl. transpn. supt., July, 1927; dep. agent, personnel, Jan., 1933.

CAMERON, DUNCAN CHARLES THOMAS, Indian Police (dist. supt., Madras) (b. 6th May, 1903).—Joined the service as asst. supt., Madras, on 2nd Dec., 1922; offg. dist. supt., Mar., 1929, and again June, 1933; confd., Aug., 1935.

CAMERON, GILBERT STUART, Dept. of Mines in India (inspr.) (b. 22nd April, 1891).—Joined the service as junr. inspr., 25th April, 1921; offg. inspr., June, 1934; confd., Sept., 1936.

CAMERON, JOHN GORDON PATRICK, C.I.E., late Indian Posts and Tels. (b. 31st Jan., 1885).—Trained at R.I.E. Coll.; joined the service, 1st Oct., 1906; asst. supt., Oct., 1906; on mil. service from Nov., 1915, to Feb., 1919; C.I.E., Jan., 1918; divl. engr., tels., 1920; elec. engr. in-chief, April, 1926; retd., March, 1932.

CAMERON, JOHN PHILIP, C.S.I., C.I.E., Col., I.M.S., retd., late Jail Dept., Assam (b. 22nd May, 1879).—Joined the service, 26th July, 1901; apptd. supt. of jails, Madras, 1st Dec., 1905; reverted to mil. duty, Nov., 1914; inspr. gen. of prisons, Madras, April, 1919; C.I.E., Jan., 1923; transf. to Assam as inspr. genl. of civil hosps. and prisons, April, 1931; C.S.I., Jan., 1932; retd., Feb., 1935.

CAMERON KER, EDWARD, Indian Railway Accts. Service (dep. chief accts. officer, E.I. Rly.) (b. 31st Mar., 1888).—Joined the service as asst. auditor, E.I. rly., 29th Feb., 1908; served in I.A.R.O., from Oct., 1914, to Sept., 1916; accts. officer, gr. I., E.I.R., Dec., 1925; confd. as dep. chief accts. officer, E.I.R., Dec., 1926; dep. ditto, E.B. Rly., Apl., 1930, to Oct., 1932.

CAMMIADE, PAUL EUGENE, B.A., late Indian C.S. (Bengal) (b. 17th Jan., 1874).—Educ. at Madras Univ., Caisus Coll., Cambridge, and Univ. Coll., London; apptd. after exam. of 1896; arrived, 9th Dec., 1897, and

served in Bengal as asst. mag. and collr.; transf. to Assam as asst. commr., Dec., 1902; joint mag. and dep. collr., E. Bengal and Assam, Jan., 1906; mag. and collr., March, 1909; dist. and sess. judge, April, 1911; retransf. to Bengal, April, 1912; actg. judge, Calcutta high ct., June to Aug., 1926; Nov., 1926; and again, Feb. to Aug., 1927; retd., Jan., 1931.

CAMPBELL, ALEXANDER, M.C., M.A., Indian Edul. Service (asst. dir. of public instn., Burma) (b. 29th Jan., 1892).—Joined the service as prof. of English on 24th March, 1915; on mil. service from 31st Aug., 1916, to 14th Feb., 1919; registrar Rangoon Univ., Dec., 1921; inspr. of schools, Burma, June, 1933; offg. dir. of public instn., Aug., 1933; and again, June, 1936; asst. dir. of public instn., Feb., 1935.

CAMPBELL, ARCHIBALD, B.A., late Indian Civil Service (Punjab).—Educ. at Harrow Sch. and Pembroke Coll., Cambridge; apptd. after exam. of 1900; arrived, 9th Dec., 1901, and served in the Punjab as asst. commr.; regr., chief ct., April, 1912, to Jan., 1917; dep. commr., April, 1918; dist. and sess. judge, April, 1919; addl. judge, Punjab high ct., Oct., 1921; judge, high ct., Punjab, Oct., 1925; retd., Sept., 1928.

CAMPBELL, Sir ARCHIBALD YOUNG GIPPS, K.C.I.E., C.S.I., C.B.E., B.A., late Indian C.S. (b. 18th May, 1872).—Educ. at Westminster and Trinity Coll., Cambridge; apptd. after exam. of 1895; arrived, 3rd Dec., 1896, and served in Madras as asst. collr. and mag.; under sec. to govt., Nov., 1903; private sec. to govr. of Madras, May, 1906; C.I.E., Jan., 1911; sub. collr. and jt. mag., Jan., 1912; actg. presdt. of the Corporation of Madras, July to Oct., 1913; member, weights and measures cttee., govt. of India, Oct., 1913, to July, 1914; collr. and dist. mag., Madras, Oct., 1914; emplyd. in British Red Cross Society, June to Dec., 1916; dir. of industries, Madras, Nov., 1919, to Aug., 1921; on depn. to England, Sept., 1921, to Jan., 1922; emplyd. under govt. of India in connection with an enquiry into cost of administration of Coorg, April to June, 1922, and in connection with similar enquiry in respect of Central India, Rajputana, &c., Oct., 1923, to Jan., 1924; sec. to govt., rev. dept., July, 1914; C.B.E., June, 1921; chief sec. to govt. of Madras, March, 1925; temp. member of govr.'s exec. council, Aug., 1926; Aug., 1927; Mar., 1928; and again, June, 1929; confd., Jan., 1930; C.S.I., Jan., 1927; K.C.I.E., Jan., 1932; retd., Jan., 1935.

CAMPBELL, CHARLES ROSS, D.S.O., M.V.O., Captain, R.I.M. (r td.) (b. 19th Nov., 1876).—Apptd. depy. conservator of the Port of Madras, May, 1922; M.V.O., Jan., 1922; presdy. port officer, Madras, May, 1924; retd., Feb., 1930.

CAMPBELL, DOUGLAS COLIN, Indian Railway Accts. Service (dir., rly. clrg. accts. office) (b. 28th May, 1891).—Joined the service as dep. chief accts. offcr., Burma Rly., 20th Mar., 1932, on abolition of appt. as dir. of comml. audit; confd. as chief accts. offcr., July, 1933; dir. rly. clearing accts. office, Oct., 1934.

CAMPBELL, JAMES DONALD, D.S.O., Col., R.E., Survey of India (dir.) (b. 3rd May, 1884).—First commn. dated 21st Dec. 1901;

joined the survey of India dept. as asst. supt., 5th Dec., 1906; temp. reverted to mil. duty, Oct., 1914, to Oct., 1919; dep. supt., Feb., 1921; supt., May, 1928; offg. dir., Feb., 1929; confd., Aug., 1931.

CAMPBELL, Sir JOHN, K.C.M.G., C.S.I., O.B.E., late Indian C.S.—Educ. at Glasgow Univ. and Christ Church, Oxford; apptd. after exam. of 1896; arrived, 22nd Nov., 1897, and served in the N.W. Provs. as asst. mag. and collr.; under-sec. to govt., March, 1902; under-sec. to govt. of India, fin. and comm. dept., April, 1904; ditto, dept. of commerce and industry, Feb., 1905; sec., factory commn., Oct., 1907, to Jan. 1908; jt. mag. and dep. collr., July, 1908; chairman, Cawnpore Municipal Bd., Dec., 1908, to March, 1912; dep. commr., May, 1914; mag. and collr., April, 1915; O.B.E., June, 1918; temp. employed in rev. and stats. dept., India Office, from May, 1919, to July, 1921; retd., Oct., 1922; vice-chairman, Greek Refugee Settlt. Commn., 1923; C.S.I., June, 1923; Kt., June, 1925; economic and finl. adviser, C.O., Nov., 1929; K.C.M.G., June, 1933.

CAMPBELL, JOHN HERBERT, Indian Service of Engineers (asst. exec. engr.), (b. 23rd May, 1908).—Joined the service as asst. exec. engr., Bihar and Orissa, 20th Oct., 1930; offg. exec. engr., May, 1936.

CAMPBELL, WALTER FENDALL, Major I.A., Indian Political Service (b. 20th May, 1894).—First commn., dated 14th Jan., 1914; joined the pol. dept., 21st Jan., 1921; asst. pol. agent and asst. commr., Sibi, June, 1921; asst. pol. agent, Loralai, May, 1926; asst. commr., Hangu, Nov., 1926; pol. agent, Kurram, N.W., 1927; dep. commr., D. I. Khan, Oct., 1928; ditto, Kohat, Oct., 1928; asst. commr., Thal, Nov., 1928; dep. ditto, Bannu, Nov., 1929; secty. to A.G.G., in the states of W.I., Oct., 1933; prime minister, Alwar, Mar., 1935, to Sept., 1936; pol. agent, Bundelkhan, Oct., 1937.

CANDY, RONALD HERBERT, C.I.E., M.B., B.S. (Lond.), M.R.C.S. (Eng.), L.R.C.P. (Lond.) Lt.-Col. I.M.S. (civil surg.) (b. 13th June, 1886).—First commn. dated 27th Jan., 1912; offg. civil surg., April, 1923; confd., Aug., 1927; offg. surg.-genl. with the Govt. of Bombay, July, 1936; C.I.E., May, 1937.

CANNING, FREDERICK, C.I.E., late Indian Forest Service (b. 31st Dec., 1882).—Joined the service, 24th Nov., 1903, as asst. consvr.; dep. consvr., Dec., 1908; consvr., Apl., 1924; offg. ch. consvr., May, 1928; confd., Nov., 1929; C.I.E., June, 1934; offg. inspr.-genl. of forests, Apl., 1936; confd., Nov., 1936; retd., Dec., 1937.

CANTIN, PIERRE ANTOINE LEON, late Indian Service of Engrs. (b. 17th Jan., 1879).—Trained at the R.I.E. Coll.; joined the service, 1st Oct., 1902, as asst. engr., United Provs.; exec. engr., Oct., 1910; suptg. engr., May, 1923; dep. ch. engr., Feb., 1928; ch. engr., Nov., 1930; retd., Jan., 1934.

CANTLIE, KEITH, M.A., Indian C.S. (dep. commr., Assam) (b. 6th Feb., 1886).—Educ. at Robert Gordon's Coll. (Aberdeen), Aberdeen Univ., and Christ Church, Oxford; apptd. after exam. of 1909; arrived in India, 13th Dec., 1910, and served in E. Bengal and Assam as asst. collr.

and mag.; asst. commr., June, 1911; ditto, Assam, April, 1912; on mil. duty (I.A.R.O.), May, 1915, to April, 1919; presdt., Manipur State Darbar, Sept., 1922; offg. dep. commr., Apl., 1923; confd., May, 1927; offg. commr., Mar., 1937.

CAPOOR, SHYAM BEHARI, B.A., Indian C.S. (asst. commr., Punjab) (b. 15th Jan., 1907).—Educ. at Allahabad University and Jesus College, Oxford; joined the service 10th Oct., 1931; arrvd. in India, Nov., 1931, and served in the Punjab as asst. commr.

CARBERY, MURLOUGH, M.A., B.Sc., M.C., Indian Agricultural Service (asst. dir. of agric., Bengal) (b. 24th July, 1890).—Joined the service as agric. chemist on 1st Dec., 1920; offg. asst. dir. of agric., Bengal, June, 1933; and again, Sept., 1933; confd., Oct., 1935.

CARDEN, Ven. HENRY CRAVEN, M.A., Indian Eccles. Estabt. (archdeacon, Lahore) (b. 23rd Dec., 1882).—Apptd. chaplain on probation, 23rd Feb., 1913; junr. chaplain, Feb., 1916; on mil. duty during 1918-19; senr. chaplain, Feb., 1926; archdeacon, Lahore, Oct., 1929.

CARDEW, DAVID, V.D., Indian State Railways (ch. mechl. engr., N.W. rly.) (b. 28th Jan., 1883).—Joined the service as asst. loco. supt., 12th Aug., 1905; dist. loco. supt., April, 1913; divl. rolling stock officer and offg. supt., mech. workshops, May, 1929; offg. dep. loco. supt., May, 1931; offg. supt., mech. workshops, N.W. rly., Apl., 1932; confd., Dec., 1932; offg. ch. mechl. engr., N.W. rly., Jan., 1933; confd., May, 1934.

CARDEW, JOHN HORACE, Indian State Ryis. (dep. loco. supt., Burma Ryis.) (b. 31st July, 1889).—Joined the service as asst. loco. supt., 3rd July, 1913; on milty. duty from 13th Dec., 1914, to 8th May, 1920; offg. variously as dist. loco. supt. from July, 1920; offg. dep. loco. supt., Feb., 1930; confd., May, 1931.

CAREY-EVANS, Sir THOMAS JOHN, Kt., M.C., F.R.C.S., Lieut.-Col. I.M.S., ret'd., late Office of High Commr. for India (b. 26th June, 1884).—Entered I.M.S., 1907; served in Egypt and Mesopotamia during European War, 1914-18; civil medl. officer, Baghdad; resdt. medl. officer, Mysore State; surgeon to H.E. the Viceroy of India; ret'd., July, 1926; apptd. examr. of surgical instruments, India store dept., 13th June, 1925.

CARGILL, WILLIAM M., A.M.I.C.E., late Indian State Ryis. (b. 12th Aug., 1879).—Joined the service as asst. signal engr., 1st Aug., 1909; senr. asst. signal engr., March, 1914; signal engr., March, 1924; dep. ch. engr., signals, Oct., 1930; ret'd., Aug., 1934.

CARLESS, HENRY ALFRED, M.A., LL.B., Indian Police (supt., United Provs.) (b. 26th Sept., 1894).—Joined the service as asst. supt. on 20th Nov., 1914; offg. supt., Feb., 1921; confd., Jan., 1926; princpl. police trng. school, Moradabad, Sept., 1930, to July, 1931.

CARLESTON, HADDEN HAMILTON, Indian C.S. (sub. coll. and jt. mag., Madras) (b. 25th July, 1904).—Educ. at St. Olave's Grammar School, and Cambridge (Trinity Hall); joined the service, 25th Oct., 1927; arrived, Dec., 1927, and served in Madras as asst. coll. and mag.; sub.-collr. and jt. magte., Jan., 1929.

CARLIN, DANIEL, late Office of High Commr. for India (b. 10th July, 1882).—Educ. at Glasgow Athenæum; apptd. clerk, 2nd class, India store depot, 22nd Nov., 1901; transf. to office of high commr. for India, Oct., 1920; higher clerical officer, April, 1922; higher exec. officer, May, 1922; ret'd., July, 1932.

CARMAN, FRANK, Indian Police (b. 15th May, 1917).—Apptd. asst. supt., Bengal, on probn. 26th Nov., 1936.

CARNAGHAN, DOUGLAS WILLIAM BERESFORD, Indian Police (asst. supt., Bombay) (b. 21st Dec., 1912).—Joined the service as prob. asst. supt., Bombay, Nov., 1932; confd., Apl., 1935.

CAROE, OLAF KIRKPATRICK, C.I.E., M.A. (Oxon.), Indian C.S. Indian Political Service (b. 15th Nov., 1892).—Educ. at Winchester and Magdalen Coll., Oxford; joined the service, 31st Aug., 1920; arrived, 4th Dec., 1920, and served in the Punjab as asst. commr.; city mag., Peshawar, Nov., 1923; dep. commr., Peshawar, April, 1926; asst. dep. commr., Mardan, Oct., 1926; dep. commr., Hazara, Aug., 1927; ditto, Kohat, Nov., 1928; jt. dep. commr., Peshawar, April, 1930; dep. commr., ditto, July, 1930; C.I.E., Jan., 1932; ch. secty. to govt., N.W.F.P., Jan., 1933; dep. secty. to govt. of India, pol. dept., May, 1934; offg. resdt., Persian Gulf, Aug., 1937; offg. A.G.G., Resident and C.C. in Baluchistan, Oct., 1937.

CARPENTER, JOHN HOWARD, Indian State Ryis. (publicity officer, G.I.P. Ryis.) (b. 23rd Nov., 1886).—Joined the service as 2nd asst. secty. to agent, 5th July, 1912; 1st asst. ditto, July, 1913; I.A.R.O. from 12th Nov., 1914, to 9th July, 1919; secty., June, 1920; offg. publicity officer, Nov., 1928; confd., Feb., 1930.

CARR, JOSEPH, late Indian Forest Service (b. 17th Oct., 1885).—Joined the service, 17th Dec., 1909, and posted to Central Provs. as asst. consvr.; dep. consvr., Dec., 1914; offg. consvr., July, 1926, and again, Mar., 1927; confd., June, 1930; ret'd., Dec., 1936.

CARR, Sir WILLIAM, Kt., late Indian C.S. (b. 9th Feb., 1872).—Educ. at Lancaster Gr. Sch., and Christ's Coll., Cambridge; apptd. after exam. of 1891; arrived, 14th Nov., 1893, and served in Burma as asst. commr.; judge, small cause court, Mandalay, Feb., 1905; also dist. judge, March, 1905; divl. judge, 1909; addl. judge, high ct., Rangoon, Jan., 1923; judge, high ct., Rangoon, July, 1924; offg. ch. justice, July, 1930; Kt., June, 1931; ret'd., Feb., 1932.

CARREY, JOHN, M.B., B.Ch., B.A.O., B.Sc., Major I.M.S., Medl. Dept., Central Provs. (civil surgeon) (b. 23rd July, 1894).—First commn., 20th Jan., 1923; offg. civil surgeon, Central Provs., Mar., 1932; confd., Mar., 1934.

CARRIER, PHILIP LESLIE CARO, M.R.C.S., L.R.C.P., Indian State Ryis. (C.M.O., Burma ryis.) (b. 14th Oct., 1893).—Joined the service as dist. medl. officer, Burma Ryis., 14th Jan., 1927; offg. C.M.O., ditto, Nov., 1933; and again, Mar., 1936; confd., May, 1936.

CARRITT, MICHAEL JOHN, B.A., Indian C.S. under secty. to govt., pol. and appts. depts., Bengal (b. 3rd Jan., 1906).—Educ. at Sedbergh and Queen's College, Oxford; joined the service, 13th Oct., 1930, arrived in India, Nov., 1930, and served in Bengal as asst. mag. and collr.; jt. mag. and dep. collr., May, 1932; offg. under-secty., pol. and appts. depts., Bengal; confd., Mar., 1936.

CARROLL, EDWARD WILLIAM, B.A., Indian Forest Service (consvr., Burma) (b. 21st July, 1890).—Joined the service as asst. consvr. on 20th Dec., 1913; dep. consvr., Dec., 1918; offg. consvr., May, 1934; confd., June, 1937.

CARSE, ROBERT, Indian Police (supt., Assam) (b. 4th Nov., 1902).—Joined the service, Nov., 1923, and deputed to police training coll., Sardah, Bengal; proby. asst. supt., Oct., 1925; confd., May, 1926; offg. supt., Feb., 1934; confd., Feb., 1935.

CARSON, CHARLES FRED, M.C., Lieut.-Col. R.E. Indian State Rlys. (dep. ch. engr.) (b. 10th Feb., 1886).—First commn. dated 26th June, 1908; asst. engr., July, 1913; on mil. duty from 23rd Oct., 1914, to 14th Sept., 1919; exec. engr., Dec., 1918; offg. divl. supt., June, 1930; offg. dep. agt., organisation, Apr., 1933; offg. divl. supt. (senr.), June, 1935; offg. dep. agent (organisation), N.W. rly., Sept., 1935; offg. agent, Mar., 1936; dep. ch. engr. (provl. perm.), Apr., 1936.

CARSON, CHARLES WILLIAM CHARTERIS, C.I.E., O.B.E., late Fin. Dept., Govt. of India (b. 21st July, 1874).—Joined the service, 9th May, 1893, and served in acctnt.-gen.'s office, Bombay and Hyderabad; asst. compr., Hyderabad, April, 1898; asst. acctnt.-gen., Bombay, Aug., 1898; ditto, Bengal, April, 1901; ditto, Burma, and inspr. of local fund acts., June, 1901; ditto, ditto, United Prov., Jan., 1903; asst. compr., Central Prov., Nov., 1903; asst. acctnt.-gen., Bengal, Nov., 1904; ditto and examr. of local fund acts., Jan., 1905; on deputation as acctnt.-gen., Jammu and Kashmir State, March, 1905, to June, 1912; offg. dep. compr.-gen., India, Nov., 1913; confirmed, Nov., 1915; offg. acctnt.-gen., Bombay, from May, 1918; O.B.E., June, 1919; dep. contr. of currency, Bombay, Dec., 1919; acctnt.-gen., United Prov., May, 1922; ditto, Punjab, Nov., 1923; offg. contr. of civil acts., March, 1927; C.I.E., June, 1927; ret'd., July, 1929.

CARTER, ALFRED COLDRICK, Indian Police (b. 6th Jan., 1896).—Joined the service as asst. dist. supt., Burma, on 28th Oct., 1923; offg. dist. supt., Feb., 1931; asst. commr., Nov., 1933.

CARTER, ARTHUR CHARLES, Indian Police (supt., Bihar) (b. 27th May, 1897).—Joined the service as asst. supt. on 24th March, 1922; offg. supt., Feb., 1925; confd., Feb., 1933.

CARTER, Rev. BRYAN BERNARD, M.A., late Eccles. Dept., Central Provs. (b. 15th Dec., 1876).—Apptd. chaplain on probation, 16th Oct., 1909; junr. chaplain, 27th Oct., 1909; senr. chaplain, Oct., 1919; ret'd., Aug., 1930.

CARTER, HORACE LEONARD, M.I.R.S.E., Indian State Rlys. (dist. signal engr., E.I. Rly.) (b. 17th Dec., 1898).—Asst. signal engr., 15th Dec., 1922; dist. ditto, 5th Feb., 1935.

CARTER, MALCOLM OGILVY, Indian C.S. (jt. mag. and dep. collr., Bengal) (b. 2nd July, 1898).—Educ. at Bedford Sch., and Balliol Coll., Oxford; joined the service, 1st Nov., 1921; arrived, 15th Dec., 1921, and served in Bengal as asst. mag. and collr.; jt. magte. and dep. collr., May, 1923; offg. magte. and collr., variously from Feb., 1929; offg. secty., bd. of rev., Bengal, Sept., 1934.

CARTER, REGINALD ROBERT LANGHAM, Indian C.S. (asst. commr., Burma) (b. 16th Nov., 1903).—Educ. at Bradfield and University College, Oxford; joined the service, 26th Oct., 1927; arrived, Dec., 1927, and served in Burma as asst. commr.; offg. under-secty. to govt., home and pol. dept., Mar., 1930; offg. dep. commr., Aug., 1932.

CARTER, SIR RICHARD HENRY ARCHIBALD, K.C.B., K.C.I.E., M.A., late India Office (b. 31st Mar., 1887).—Educ. at Eton and Trinity Coll., Camb.; apptd. to surveying staff, post office, April, 1911; apptd. junr. clerk, mil. dept., India Office, 1st Aug., 1911; acting reading clerk to Council, July, 1913; reading clerk to Council, April, 1914, to May, 1917; pte. sec. to permit. under-sec. of state, and acting pte. sec. to parly. under-sec., May, 1917; senr. clerk, and pte. sec. to under-sec. of state, Nov., 1919; senr. clerk, mily. dept., Nov., 1919; principal, April, 1921; sec. to Indian delegation to 3rd Assembly of League of Nations, 1922; pte. sec. to sec. of state, Sept., 1924; asst. secty., Indian Statutory Commission, Nov., 1927; C.B., June, 1930; asst. secty., pol. (internal) dept., July, 1930; secty.-genl., Indian round table conf., London, Nov., 1930—Jan., 1931; 2nd session, Sept., Dec., 1931; 3rd session, Nov., 1932; asst. secty., finl. dept., Dec., 1933; K.C.I.E., Jan., 1935; asst. under-secty. of state, June, 1936; apptd. perm. secty. of the Admiralty, July, 1936; K.C.B., Jan., 1938.

CARTER, THOMAS, M.B.E., Dept. of Printing and Stationery (manager G. of I. Press) (b. 22nd Nov., 1891).—Joined the service, 27th Mar., 1920; asst. manager, G. of I. press, Feb., 1927; offg. manager, ditto, Mar., 1927; confd., Apr., 1931; M.B.E., June, 1934.

CARTLAND, MURRAY A., late Opium Dept., United Provs. (b. 18th Apr., 1876).—Joined the service, 4th April, 1889; dist. opium officer, March, 1926; managing dir. opium factory, Ghazipur, Mar., 1927; ret'd., Oct., 1931.

CARTLAND, WALTER CUTHBERT, late Indian State Rlys. (b. 14th Nov., 1879).—Joined the service in subordinate estabts., 3rd Jan., 1907; block signalling works manager, July, 1919; asst. engr., Jan., 1922; dist. signal engr., June, 1927; offg. dep. ch. engr. (signals), Mar., 1933; confd., Aug., 1933; ret'd., Aug., 1936.

CARTWRIGHT, ALBERT, Indian Police (b. 27th Dec., 1911).—Joined the service as proby. asst. supt., Central Provs., 21st Nov., 1931; confd., Apr., 1934.

CARVEY, EUSTACE MALTRAVERS, Indian State Rlys. (dist. contr. of stores, E.B. rly.) (b. 22nd July, 1893).—Joined the service, 10th Dec., 1915; asst. contr. of stores, Aug., 1919; offg. dist. contr. of stores, E.B. rly. variously from Feb., 1921; confd., Dec., 1932.

CASE, ROBERT CHARLES, A.M.I.C.E., Indian State Rlys. (dep. ch. contr. of standardisation (mech.) (b. 19th Apl., 1893).—Joined the service, 27th Nov., 1919, as asst. loco. supt.; offg. dist. ditto, E.B. rly., Dec., 1925; offg. dep. dir., rly. bd., Nov., 1927; dep. ch. mech. engr., G.I.P. rly., Nov., 1931; offg. dir., rly. bd., Sept., 1936; dep. ch. contr., standardisation (mechl.), Oct., 1936.

CASEMENT, EDGAR REGINALD, Indian State Rlys. (divl. supt., E.I. Rly.) (b. 13th Oct., 1886).—Joined the service as asst. engr., 29th Oct., 1910; on milty. duty from June, 1915, to Oct., 1918; exec. engr., Oct., 1918; offg. dep. ch. engr., Feb., 1930; offg. divl. supt., E.I. rly., March, 1934; confd., Nov., 1935; dep. ch. engr., Oct., 1935.

CASSELLS, ALEXANDER, C.I.E., M.A., late Indian C.S. (b. 19th Dec., 1883).—Educ. at Kirkcaldy High Sch., St. Andrews Univ., and Lincoln Coll., Oxford; apptd. after exam. of 1907; arrived, 23rd Nov., 1908, and served in Bengal as asst. mag. and collr.; under sec. to govt., judl. poll. and appt. depts., Aug., 1913; jt. mag. and dep. collr., June, 1916; mag. and collr., March, 1920; dep. sec., pol. and appt. depts., Feb., 1921; chief sec., govt. of Bengal, March, 1922, to Sept., 1923; offg. secty. to govt., finance, &c., depts., Nov., 1927; confd., Feb., 1928; offg. commr., Nov., 1930; confd., Nov., 1931; C.I.E., Jan., 1931; retd., Dec., 1933.

CASSELLS, SIR ROBERT ARCHIBALD, G.C.B., C.S.I., D.S.O., p.s.c., General, Indian Army (commander-in-chief in India) (b. 15th Mar., 1876).—First commr. dated 22nd Jan., 1896; A.D.C. to Div. Comdr. India, 1906-07; brig.-maj., India, 1909-11; G.S.O.2 India, 1911-13; D.A.A.G. India, 1914-15; D.A.A.G., I.E.F. "D", April-Dec., 1915; G.S.O.I, 14th Div. and Cav. Div., I.E.F. "D" and Mes. E.F., 1915-17; Brig.-Genl. Gen. Staff, Mes. E.F., Aug.-Nov., 1917; Brig.-Comdr. and Div. Comdr., Mes. E.F. (18th Div. and Central Kurdistan Field Force), 1917-19; Div. Comdr., Egypt, 1919-20; Maj.-Gen. Cav., India, 1920-23; Dist. Comdr., Peshawar, India, 1923-27; A.G. in India, 1928-30; A.D.C. Gen. to the King, 1929-33; G.O.C. in C., N. Comd., India, 1930-34; C. in-C. in India and member Gov. Genl.'s exec. council, 29th Nov., 1935; G.C.B., 1933. War services, Iraq, 1915-19; Egypt, 1919-20. Mentione in despatches nine times. Legion of Honour, 4th class; Order of the White Eagle, 4th class, with swords.

CASTENS, HERBERT ERNEST, Indian Forest Service (dep. consvr., Burma) (b. 10th May, 1902).—Joined the service as asst. consvr., Burma, on 22nd Dec., 1925; dep. consvr., Nov., 1930.

CATER, SIR ALEXANDER NORMAN LEY, K.C.I.E., Indian C.S., Indian Political Service (b. 15th June, 1880).—Educ. at Wellington Coll. and Christ's Coll., Cambridge; apptd. after exam. of 1903; arrived, 5th Dec., 1904, and served in the Punjab as asst. commr.; personal asst. to pol. agent, Phulkian States and Bahawalpur, April, 1907; pol. asst., Baluchistan, Jan., 1909; asst. pol. agent, Sibi, Nov., 1909; asst. agent to govr.-gen., Baluchistan, Oct., 1910; asst. pol. agent, Zlob, Sept., 1911; asst. to agent to govr.-gen., Baluchistan, Sept., 1914; asst. pol. agent, Kalat, Oct., 1914; second asst. to

agent to govr.-gen., in Baluchistan, Oct., 1915; first asst. to ditto, Jan., 1916; first asst. resdt., Hyderabad, March, 1917; offg. dep. sec. (foreign), for. and pol. dept., govt. of India, April, 1919; confd., Oct., 1919; sec. to resdt. in Mysore, Feb., 1923; resident at Jaipur, Nov., 1925; also addl. charge of office of pol. agent, Haraoi and Tonk from April, 1926; resdt., Jaipur, Sept., 1928; offg. agent to govr.-genl. in Rajputana and C.C., Ajmer, Merwara, Apl., 1929; ditto, Madras States, Dec., 1929; C.I.E., Jan., 1930; judl. commr., Baluchistan, Nov., 1930; offg. A.G. & C.C., Baluchistan, Mar., 1931; confd., Dec., 1931; K.C.I.E., Jan., 1934.

CAUTY, EDWIN LIONEL, late Indian Police Service (Bombay).—Joined the service, 1st March, 1898; awarded King's police medal, Jan., 1921; supt. of police and dep. commr. of police, Sept., 1923; retd. Oct., 1928.

CAVE, LESLIE STORRER, Indian State Rlys. (divl. mech. engr., N.W. Rly.) (b. 3rd Mar., 1894).—Asst. loco. supt., junr. scale, 28th Feb., 1922; divl. mech. engr., senr. scale, Feb., 1937.

CHADHA, CHARANJIT LAL, B.Sc., late Indian Forest Service (b. 21st Dec., 1899).—Joined the service as asst. consvr., 19th Jan., 1923; dep. consvr., Jan., 1927; retd., May, 1935.

CHADWICK, SIR DAVID THOMAS, K.C.M.G., C.S.I., C.I.E., late Indian C.S. (Madras) (b. 7th April, 1876).—Educ. at De Aston Sch., Market Rasen, and Sidney Sussex Coll., Cambridge; apptd. after exam. of 1899; arrived, 3rd Dec., 1900, and served in Madras as asst. collr. and mag.; spcl. asst. settlmnt. offr., May, 1903, to Feb., 1907; specl. settlmnt. offr. and specl. asst. collr., March, 1908; sub-collr. and jt. mag., Jan., 1912; collr. and mag., Oct., 1912; dir. of agric., Oct., 1912; on deputn. to Russia, France and Italy, June, 1916; Indian trade commr. in England, Oct., 1917, to July, 1922; C.I.E., Jan., 1921; sec. to govt. of India, dept. of commerce, Oct., 1922; temp. member, govr.-genl.'s council, July-Dec., 1923; C.S.I., Jan., 1925; Kt., Jan., 1927; retd., March, 1929; K.C.M.G., May, 1937.

CHADWICK, FREDERICK JOHN, Indian Police (supt., Madras) (b. 19th June, 1894).—Joined the service, 17th Oct., 1921; asst. supt., Sep., 1925; offg. dist. supt., July, 1932; confd., July, 1933.

CHAIN, U BA MAUNG, B.Sc., Indian Service of Engrs. (exec. engr., Burma) (b. 24th Oct., 1901).—Joined the service as asst. exec. engr., Burma, 1st Oct., 1924; exec. engr., Oct., 1933.

CHAINANI, HASHMATRAI KHUBCHAND, Indian C.S. (judge and sess judge, Bombay) (b. 29th Feb., 1904).—Educ. at Univ. of Bombay and Magdalene Coll., Cambridge; joined the service, 18th Oct., 1927; arrived, Dec., 1927, and served in Bombay as asst. coll. and magte; asst. judge and addl. sess. judge, Aug., 1933; offg. membr. of legal affairs, June, 1935; judge and sess. judge, Aug., 1935; offg. secty., legisl. council, Bombay, Mar., 1936.

CHAKKO, K. C., B.A., B.Sc., Indian Educl. Service (princl., coll. of engrg.) (b. 24th Aug., 1892).—Apptd. profir. of civil engrg., coll. of engrg., Madras, 18th Nov., 1921; princpl., coll. of engrg., Guindy May, 1936.

CHAKRABATTI, JATINDRA NATH, B.A., late Indian Agricultural Service (b. 12th Jan., 1882).—Joined the service as agricl. supervisor, 4th Jan., 1908; offg. dep. dir. of agric., May, 1919; confd. Dec., 1920; offg. dir., Mar., 1933; ret'd., Sept., 1937.

CHAKRABATTI, REBATI MOHAN, Rai Bahadur, M.A., late Bengal Civil Service (b. 2nd July, 1879).—Joined the service as proby. dep. collr., 18th July, 1901; dep. magte. and dep. collr., May, 1902; magte. and collr., Mar., 1929; ret'd., June, 1932.

CHAKRA DHAR JAYAL, RAI BAHADUR, B.A. (late Indian Police Service) b. 17th Jan., 1879).—Held non-gazetted appts. from 14th Aug., 1904, to 24th Dec., 1908; dep. supt., Dec., 1908; awarded King's police medal, Jan., 1921; transferred to Tehri State from June, 1925; supt., Jan., 1926; ret'd., Sept., 1932.

CHAKRAVARTHI, SUBRAHMANYAM, B.A., Indian C.S. (sub. collr., Madras) (b. 30th Aug., 1910).—Educated at the Hindu High School, Triplicane, University of Madras, The School of Economics, London, and Trinity College, Cambridge; joined the service, 6th Sept., 1935; arrived in India, Oct., 1935, and served in Madras as asst. mag. and collr.; sub. collr., Jan., 1937.

CHAKRAVARTI, A., M.A., Indian Educational Service (principal, arts coll., Rajahmundry) (b. 1st July, 1883).—Apptd. profr., presdy. coll., Madras, 23rd Feb., 1921; offg. principal, arts coll., Rajahmundry, 3rd July, 1929; confd., Feb., 1930.

CHAKRAVARTI, PHANI BHUSAN, O.B.E., Rai Bahadur, Indian Audit Dept. (b. 16th June, 1884).—Joined the service, 1st Mar., 1906; asst. auditor genl., Dec., 1934; O.B.E., May, 1937; offer on spec. duty in the fin. dept., govt. of India, Nov., 1937.

CHAKRAVARTI, SURENDRA NATH, M.B.E., Indian Service of Engrs. (exec. engr., United Provs.) (b. 10th June, 1892).—Joined the service, 20th Sept., 1915; asst. engr., Sept., 1916; asst. exec. engr., Jan., 1922; exec. engr., Oct., 1925; municipal engr., Delhi, Dec., 1933; M.B.E., Feb., 1937.

CHAKRAVARTI, TULSI DAS, B.A., Indian State Rly. (transpn. supt.) (b. 21st Aug., 1887).—Joined the service 24th Mar., 1913; asst. supt., Jan., 1916; transpn. supt. (traffic comm.), E.I. Rly., Nov., 1934.

CHAKRAVARTY, BIRENDRA NARAYAN, Indian C.S. (jt. mag. and dep. coll., Bengal) (b. 20th Dec., 1904).—Educ. at University of Calcutta, University C. ll., London, and School of Oriental Studies, London; joined the service, 9th October, 1929; arrived November, 1929, and served as asst. coll. and mag., Bengal; jt. mag. and dep. collr., July, 1930; offg. addl. dist. and sess. judge, June, 1935; offg. mag. and collr., Feb., 1936.

CHAKRAVARTY, NARENDRA NARAIN, M.A. Income Tax dept., Bengal (asst. commr.) (b. Aug., 1888).—Joined the service, 30th Nov., 1912; sub dep. collr., Dec., 1913; inc. tax officer, Aug., 1923; asst. commr., July, 1929.

CHALAM, BALAJIPAT SESHA, Indian State Ry's. (maliariologist, E.B. rly.) (b. 7th Nov., 1886).—Apptd. maliariologist, E.B. rly., on probn., 23rd June, 1928; confd., Sept., 1932.

CHALK, WALTER OLIVER, late Indian State Ry's.
