(b. 29th Nov., 1881).—Joined the service as asst. loco. supt., 4th Dec., 1907; dist. ditto, June, 1914; divl. rolling stock officer, Oct., 1924; pers. asst. to ch. mechl. engr., Oct., 1927; offg. dep. ch. opertg. supt., Nov., 1928; offg. supt., mech. workshops, Oct., 1920; and again, Oct., 1933; confd., May, 1934; dep. ch. operatg. supt., N.W. rly. (prov'l), Mar., 1932; confd., Sept., 1934; offg. ch. mechl. engr., N.W. rly., Sept., 1935; ret'd., Nov., 1936.

CHALMERS, DAVID FERGUSON, late Indian C.S. (Burma) (b. 8th Feb., 1877).—Educ. at Edinburgh Univ.; apptd. after exam. of 1900; arrived, 10th Dec., 1901, and served in Burma as asst. commr., asst. supt., N. Shan states, and asst. settlmt. offr.; offg. dep. dir., land records dept., Nov., 1913; confd., Oct., 1914; dep. commr., April, 1915; offg. commr. of settlts. and land records, July to Oct., 1916; dir. of agric., Oct., 1918; commr. (s.p.t.), Oct., 1923; confd., 1925; ret'd., May, 1930.

CHAMAN LAL, RAI BAHADUR, B.A., late Indian Audit Dept. (b. 6th Dec., 1879).—Joined the service, Dec., 1901; asst. acct.-genl., Central Revs., Oct., 1925; dept. acct.-genl., Punjab, Mar., 1930; dep. acct.-genl., United Provs., Nov., 1931; offg. acct.-genl., ditto, Mar., 1933; ret'd., Nov., 1934.

CHAMBERS, DAVID GOSTLING, B.A., Indian C.S. (asst. collr., Bombay) (b. 19th Oct., 1907); educ. at Dulwich College and Caius College, Cambridge; apptd. after examination of 1930; arrvd. in India, Nov., 1931, and served in Bombay as asst. collr.

CHAMBERS, HENRY ANGRAVE, A.M.I.C.E., late Indian Service of Engrs. (b. 16th Jan., 1877).—Joined the service, 12th Jan., 1904; asst. engr., Nov., 1909; exec. engr., Jan., 1913; offg. suptg. engr., March, 1925; confd., Jan., 1929; ret'd., Jan., 1932.

CHAMBERS, JOHN, O.B.E., M.C., A.M.I.C.E. Indian Service of Engrs. (exec. engr., Bengal) (b. 17th Mar., 1892).—Joined the service as asst. exec. engr., on 1st Oct., 1919; exec. engr., Apl., 1926; O.B.E., June, 1935.

CHAMBERS, LESLIE RAPER, B.A., Indian C.S. (jt. mag. and dep. collr., Bengal) (b. 30th Apl., 1910).—Educated at Bradford Grammar School and Pembroke College, Cambridge; apptd. after exam. of 1932; arrived in India, Nov., 1933, and served in Bengal as asst. mag. and collr.

CHAMIER, SIR EDWARD MAYNARD DESCHAMPS, K.C.S.I., K.C.I.E., Barr. (1887), late India Office (b. 4th June, 1866).—Entered the service as govt. advocate in the high court, N.W.P., 2nd Jan., 1896; 2nd addl. judl. commr. Oudh, Nov., 1901; 1st ditto, Nov., 1904; judl. commr., July, 1907; judge, high court, N.W.P., April, 1910; knighted, Jan., 1916; chief justice, high court, Patna, March, 1916, to Oct., 1917; legal adviser and solicitor to the sec. of state, Nov., 1917; K.C.I.E., June, 1924; ret'd., June, 1933; K.C.S.I., Jan., 1934.

CHAMIER, Miss Ethel, M.A., Indian Educ. Service (inspectress of schools, Central Provs.) (b. 19th Jan., 1885).—Joined the service, 24th Sept., 1909; asst. inspectress, May, 1910; inspectress, Oct., 1915.

CHAMPA LAL, LALA, B.A., Indian Service of Engrs. (exec. engr.) (b. 1st Jan., 1897).—Joined the service, 23rd Nov., 1920; asst. exec. engr., Nov., 1921; offg. exec. engr., Mar., 1928; confd., Oct., 1932.

CHAMPHEKAR, MAHADEO LAXMAN, M.C.E., Indian Service of Engrs. (exec. engr., Sind) (b. 6th Jan., 1899).—Joined the service as apprentice engr., 7th Oct., 1921; asst. exec. engr., Oct., 1922; offg. exec. engr., June, 1925, March, 1926, March, 1927, and Nov., 1927; confd., Oct., 1931.

CHAMPION, FREDERICK WALTER, B.A., Indian Forest Service (dep. consvr., United Provs.) (b. 24th Aug., 1893).—Joined the service as asst. consvr., on 13th Dec., 1921; dep. consvr., Nov., 1927.

CHAMPION, HARRY GEORGE, B.A., Indian Forest Service (dep. consvr., United Provs.) (b. 17th Aug., 1891).—Joined the service as asst. consvr. on 9th Dec., 1915; dep. consvr., July, 1919; asst. silviculturist, Dehra Dun, Oct., 1925; silviculturist, Jan., 1926.

CHAMPION, REGINALD STUART, O.B.E. (b. 21st Mar., 1895).—On military service, 1914-1917. Served in Occupied Enemy Territories Administration; appointed Deputy Governor Grade III, Hebron, Palestine, July, 1920; transferred to Beersheba, April, 1924; title changed to Administrative Officer, Jan., 1925; transferred to Jerusalem Division as asst. dist. commissioner, July, 1927; seconded for service as Protectorate Secretary, Aden, Oct., 1928; first asst. and protectorate secretary (now designated political sec.), Aden, March, 1932; offg. ch. commr., Aden, Apl., 1933; O.B.E., Jan., 1934.

CHAN TOON, JOHN, B.Sc., Indian Service of Engrs. (exec. engr., Burma) (b. 4th Jan., 1890).—Joined the service, 11th Feb., 1920; asst. exec. engr., Oct., 1922; exec. engr., Nov., 1931.

CHANCE, PERCIVAL VINCENT, B.A., Indian Service of Engrs. (suptg. engr., Central Provs.) (b. 12th June, 1888).—Joined the service as asst. engr. on 1st Oct., 1911; exec. engr., Oct., 1920; offg. suptg. engr., Apl., 1929; confd., Sept., 1930; offg. ch. engr. and secty. to govt., Bombay, buildings and roads and irrig. beh., Apl., 1935.

CHANCE, WILLIAM HENRY, Indian State Railways (ch. accts. officer) (b. 28th Feb., 1888).—Joined the service 15th Feb., 1912; senr. asst. auditor, Nov., 1926; dep. ch. accts. officer, Nov., 1930; dep. agent, Burma rly., Mar., 1931; ch. accts. officer, Mar., 1934.

CHAND, AJIT (see Ajit Chand).

CHAND, BISHEN (see Bishen Chand).

CHAND, AMIR (see Amir Chand).

CHAND, HARI (see Hari Chand).

CHAND, HUKAM (see Hukam Chand).

CHAND, KARM (see Karm Chand).

CHAND, KHUB, B.A., Indian C.S. (jt. mag. and collr., U.P.) (b. 16th Dec., 1911).—Educated at Government High Schools, Delhi and Simla, University of Delhi and Oriel College, Oxford; joined the service, 4th Sept., 1935; arrived in India, Oct., 1935, and served in the United Provinces as asst. mag. and collr; jt. magte., Mar., 1937.

CHAND MAL, B.Sc., Indian C.S. (jt. mag. and collr., U.P.) (b. 7th April, 1911).—Educated at Govt. High School, Delhi, Delhi University, Thomason C.E. College, Roorkee, and Balliol College, Oxford; joined the service, 5th Sept., 1935; arrived in India, Oct., 1935, and served in the United Provinces as asst. mag. and collr.; jt. magte., Jan., 1937.

CHAND, MUNISHWAR (see Munishwar Chand).

CHAND, NIHAL (see Nihal Chand).

CHAND, PREM (see Prem Chand).

CHAND, SAKHI (see Sakhi Chand).

CHAND, SUMER (see Sumer Chand).

CHAND, TEK (see Tek Chand).

CHANDA, APURVA KUMAR, Indian Educl. Service (princl., David Hare trng. coll.) (b. 12th Feb., 1893).—Joined the service, 5th July, 1920; vice-prin., Dacca Coll., June, 1922; prof., presdy. coll., Calcutta, Oct., 1926; offg. prin., Krishnagore Coll., Mar., 1930; confd., June, 1930; offg. asst. dir. of public instrn., Bengal, May, 1934; confd., Oct., 1934; princl. David Hare trng. coll., Nov., 1936.

CHANDAR BHAN, Rai Bahadur, A.M.I.E. (Ind.), Indian Service of Engrs. (exec. engr., Punjab) (b. 20th Sept., 1885).—Joined the service, 19th Oct., 1908; as t. engr., Oct., 1909; exec. engr., Oct., 1918; offg. suptg. engr., Apl., 1933.

CHANDAVARKAR, PROBHAKAR NARAYAN, B.A., LL.B. Impl. Customs Service (asst. collr., Madras) (b. 20th Aug., 1889).—Joined the service as probr., 20th Jan., 1914; asst. collr., July, 1915; offg. collr., Sept., 1933.

CHANDIRAM KOTUMAL (see Kotumal, C.).

CHANDIRAMANI, SANTDAS BULCHAND, B.A., Indian C.S. (jt. mag. United Provs.) (b. 1st Oct., 1902).—Educ. at Univ. of Bombay, King's Coll., London; joined the service, 18th Oct., 1927; arrived, Dec., 1927, and served in the United Provinces as asst. mag. and coll.; jt. mag., Mar., 1930; tempy. sessions and sub-judge, Oct., 1932; offg. dist. and sess. judge, Sept., 1935.

CHANDRA, CHAUDHURI NANAK, M.A., Barr., Indian C.S. (dep. commr., Punjab) (b. 10th Oct., 1896).—Educ. at Govt. High Sch., Mooltan, and Sidney Sussex Coll., Camb.; joined the service, 24th Nov., 1921; arrived 9th March, 1922, and served in Bengal as asst. mag. and collr.; transfd. to Punjab as asst. commr., April, 1923; dep. commr. (provl.), Oct., 1929; confd., Jan., 1934; junr. secty. to finl. commrs., Dec., 1931; dep. secy. to govt., rev. dept., Feb., 1934; to Oct., 1935.

CHANDRA, HARISH (see Harish Chandra)

CHANDRA KISHOR, B.A., Indian Service of Engrs. (exec. engr., United Provs.) (b. 9th Nov., 1886).—Joined the service, 13th Sept., 1910; asst. engr., Sept., 1911; exec. engr., Jan., 1922.

CHANDRA, RAM (see Ram Chandra).

CHANDRA, ROMESH (see Romesh Chandra)

CHANDRASEKHARA AYYAR, NAGAPUDI, Judl. Dept., Madras (dist. and sess. judge) (b. 25th Jan., 1888).—Joined the service as offg. city civil judge, Madras, 18th July, 1927; dist. and sess. judge, Sept., 1935.

CHANDRASEKHARAN, VALIA VERTIL, Indian Police (asst. supt., Madras) (b. 10th May, 1910).—Joined the service as asst. supt., Madras, on probn., 5th Feb., 1934; confd., Mar., 1936.

CHANDU LAL, DONALD, Indian Police (b. 28th June, 1903).—Joined the service as asst. supt., Punjab, 29th Apl., 1927; confd., Apl., 1929; asst. I.G. rly. police, Apl., 1937.

CHANING PEARCE, WILLIAM ALAN, Indian Police (asst. supt.) (b. 3rd Feb., 1911).—Joined the service as proby. asst. supt., United Provinces, 1st Nov., 1931; confd., Apl., 1934.

CHANNER, FREDERICK FRANCIS RALPH, C.I.E., O.B.E., late Indian Forest Service (United Provs.) (b. 20th June, 1875).—Joined the service, 16th Nov., 1896 as asst. consr. of forests; dep. consr., Jan., 1906; O.B.E., June, 1919; consr., Jan., 1921; offg. chief consr., United Provs., April, 1922; confd., Nov., 1926; C.I.E., June, 1930; ret'd., June, 1930.

CHAPMAN, ARTHUR FREDERIC, B.A., A.M.I.C.E., late Indian Service of Engrs., (b. 18th Sept., 1886).—Joined the service as asst. engr., on 1st Oct., 1910; exec. engr., Apl., 1922; offg. suptg. engr., Apl., 1927; ret'd., Apl., 1937.

CHAPMAN, EDWARD FREDERIC GILBERT, Indian Police (b. 2nd Aug., 1895).—(supt., United Provs.) Joined the service as asst. supt., 17th Aug., 1921; offg. supt., March, 1924; confd., July, 1930.

CHAPMAN, HAROLD VICTOR, B.Sc., Indian Service of Engrs. (exec. engr., Burma) (b. 15th Apl., 1900).—Joined the service on 1st Oct., 1923; exec. engr., Oct., 1932.

CHAPMAN, HENRY, Indian State Ryls. (supt. (Power), senr. scale, E.I. Rly.) (b. 19th Oct., 1883).—Asst. supt., junior scale, 1st Oct., 1928; supt. (Power), senr. scale, Jan., 1937.

CHAPMAN, JOHN ALEXANDER (b. 2nd Dec., 1875).—Joined the service, 1st July, 1905; prinpl. govt. comm. institute, Calcutta, Jan., 1911; librarian, impl. library, Aug., 1911; ret'd., Dec., 1930.

CHAPMAN, LEONARD ALFRED, Indian C.S. (under secty. to govt., fin., comm. and marine depts., Bengal) (b. 29th Nov., 1905).—Educ. at Chigwell School, Essex, and Oxford (non-collegiate); joined the service, 14th Oct., 1929; arrived, Nov., 1929, and served in Bengal as asst. coll. and mag.; jt. mag. and dep.-collr., Nov., 1931; under secty. to govt., fin., comm. and marine depts., Bengal, Apl., 1935; offg. dep. secty., ditto, May, 1936; placed on spec. duty fin. dept., govt. of India, Apl., 1937.

CHAPMAN, R. C. G., Capt. R.E. (deputy master, Security Printing) (b. 18th May, 1899).—Under mil. dept. up to 31st Aug., 1934; deputy master, Security Printing, Sept., 1934.

CHAPPLE, FRANCIS GEORGE, Indian C.S. (under secty., home dept., Madras) (b. 21st Dec., 1906).—Educ. at Queen Elizabeth's Grammar School, Wakefield, and University College, Oxford; joined the service, 16th Oct., 1930; arrived in India, December, 1930, and served in Madras as asst. mag. and collr.; sub.-collr., Dec., 1932; under secty., home dept., Madras, Sept., 1937.

CHARAGH, MAHBUB ILAHI (see Mahbub Ilaahi Charagh).

CHARAN, ATMA, Indian C.S. (jt. mag., United Provs.) (b. 15th Sept., 1904).—Educ. at University of Allahabad and Sidney Sussex Coll., Cambridge; joined the service, 11th Oct., 1929; arrived, 7th Dec., 1929, and

served in the United Provinces as asst. mag. and coll.; temp. sess. and sub-judge, Nov., 1934; addl. dist. and sess. judge, Oct., 1936.

CHARI, PERUNGAVUR VARADA, M.B.E., A.C.G.I., M.I.E.E., A.M.I.Mech.E., A.A.I.E.E. (elec. engr., Electricity Dept., Madras) (b. 11th Dec., 1887).—Joined the service as asst. elec. engr., 1st Dec., 1918; offg. elec. inspr. to govt., Feb., 1930; elec. engr., Feb., 1931.

CHARLTON, HENRY JOHNSON, late Indian State Ryls. (N. W. Rly.) (b. 22nd Sept., 1879).—Joined the service as chief draughtsman on 7th Dec., 1910; dist. carr. and wagon supt., April, 1914; dep. ditto, Dec., 1917; dep. mech. engr., May, 1924; ret'd., Oct., 1929.

CHARLTON, JAMES, M.Sc., F.I.C., Indian Agricl. Service (dir. of agric., Burma) (b. 11th June, 1894).—Joined the service as agricl. chemist, agricl. coll., Mandalay, 26th Jan., 1921; princl., ditto in addn., Dec., 1922; offg. dir. of agricl., Burma, Sept., 1935; confd., Mar., 1936.

CHARSLEY, CLAUDE ROBERT, Indian Police (dist. supt., Madras) (b. 11th Feb., 1894).—Joined the service, 4th Nov., 1921, as asst. supt., on probn.; confd., July, 1924; offg. dist. supt., Oct., 1928; confd., Mar., 1932; awarded King's police medal, Jan., 1924.

CHASE, ALFRED JAMES, O.B.E., late Indian Railway Board (b. 15th Sept., 1875).—Joined the service, 8th Sept., 1898, and apptd. asst. loco. supt.; dist. loco. supt., April, 1904; dep. loco. supt., April, 1911; on depn. as member of Mesopotamia Transport Commn., Nov., 1917, to March, 1918; on spl. duty, office of rly. bd., March, 1918; O.B.E., Jan., 1919; loco. and carriage supt., O. and R. rly., April, 1919; dir. mechanical engrg., April, 1924; ret'd., Sept., 1930.

CHATARJI, ABANIBHUSHAN, B.A., Indian C.S. (jt. mag. and dep. collr., Bengal) (b. 13th Jan., 1907).—Educated at Patna University and King's College, Cambridge; joined the service, 10th Oct., 1932; arrived in India, Nov., 1932, and served in Bengal as asst. mag. and collr.; jt. mag. and dep. collr., May, 1934.

CHATARJI, ANIL CHANDRA, M.B., Lt.-Col. I.M.S., (dir. of pub. health, Bengal) (b. 21st Dec., 1891).—Date of commn., 17th April, 1919; in mil. employ up to 18th May, 1934; asst. dir. of public health, Delhi Prov., to April, 1936; dir. ditto, Bengal, June, 1936.

CHATARJI, GANESH CHANDRA, M.A., Indian Educl. Service (profr., govt. coll., Lahore) (b. 4th Mar., 1894).—Joined the service as profr., C.T. coll., Lahore, on 1st Dec., 1919; profr., govt. coll., Lahore, Sept., 1921.

CHATARJI, GOPENDRA BHUSHAN, BABU, M.A., LL.B., late United Provs. C.S. (b. 18th June, 1882).—Joined the service, 2nd May, 1911; sub-judge, May, 1925; dist. and sess. judge, July, 1935; ret'd., June, 1937.

CHATARJI, SIR NALINI RANJAN, Kt., M.A., B.L.—Apptd. acting judge, Calcutta high ct., Oct., 1910; confd., Dec., 1910; Kt., Jan., 1920; acting chief justice, high ct., Calcutta, 31st July to 27th Aug., 1925, 8th Jan. to 27th Feb., 1926, and from 2nd July to 26th Aug., 1926; ret'd., Nov., 1926.

CHATARJI, SUKUMAR, M.B.E., M.A., Bengal C.S. (inspr.-genl. of regn.) (b. 16th Dec., 1886).—Joined the service, 5th Dec., 1908; dep. mag. and dep. collr., Jan., 1911; inspr.-genl. of regn., Bengal, May, 1936.

CHATARJI, SURENDRA NATH, Indian Police (b. 31st Oct., 1901).—Joined the service as asst. supt., Bengal, 29th June, 1925; offg. supt., Mar., 1932; King's Police Medal, 1937.

CHATARJI, SURESH CHANDRA, B.E., Indian Service of Engrs. (exec. engr., Bengal) (b. 5th June, 1892).—Joined the service, 24th Jan., 1916; asst. exec. engr., Jan., 1917; offg. exec. engr., Dec., 1925; confd., Jan., 1928.

CHATARJI, HARI DAS, Rai Bahadur, B.A., late Income Tax Dept., Bihar (b. 8th Aug., 1880).—Joined the service as proby. dep. collr., Dec., 1903; dep. magte. and dep. collr., April, 1905; supt. of excise and salt, Oct., 1916; supt. of inc. tax, Dec., 1921; asst. commr., Apl., 1924; commr., May, 1932; retd., Sept., 1936.

CHATTARJI, RANGA LAL, M.A., B.L., Bihar C.S. (dist. and sess. judge) (b. 9th Sept., 1883).—Joined the service, 9th May, 1911; sub-judge, Aug., 1931; offg. dist. and sess. judge, June, 1934; confd., Oct., 1934.

CHATTARJI, SHIBA PRIYA, Rai Bahadur, B.L., Bihar C.S. (dist. and sess. judge) (b. 1st Jan., 1882).—Joined the service, 7th Nov., 1910; asst. regr., high ct., Patna, July, 1921; sub-judge, July, 1931; offg. dist. and sess. judge, May, 1932; confd., Sept., 1933.

CHATTERJEE, SIR ATUL CHANDRA, G.C.I.E., K.C.S.I., late Indian C.S. (b. 24th Nov., 1874).—Educ. at Calcutta Univ. and King's Coll., Cambridge; apptd. after exam. of 1896; arrived, 10th Dec., 1897, and served in the N.W.P. as asst. mag. and collr.; joint mag., Jan., 1906; asst. commr., Nov., 1909, to Oct., 1910; regr., co-op. credit soc., June, 1912; ditto and dep. commr., May, 1914; ditto, and mag. and collr., March, 1915; temp. sec. to govt., rev. dept., Sept., 1918; offg. sec. to govt., April, 1919; C.I.E., June, 1919; chief sec. to govt., July, 1919; delegate of govt. of India to internat. labour conference, Washington, Oct.-Nov., 1919; and Geneva, 1921, 1924, 1925, 1926, 1927, 1928, 1929 and 1933; member, Indian munitions bd., Feb., 1920; sec. to govt. of India, dept. of industries, Feb., 1921; member of govr.-gen.'s council, and Jan., 1923; K.C.I.E., Jan., 1925; retd. from I.C.S., Jan., 1925; high commr. for India, 5th Jan., 1925 to 30th June, 1931; K.C.S.I., July, 1930; member of council of India, July, 1931 to July, 1936; G.C.I.E., Jan., 1933.

CHATTERJEE, BASANTA KUMAR, M.A., Indian Finance Dept. (b. 14th Dec., 1890).—Joined the service, 11th June, 1913; asst. acct.-gen., Bengal, May, 1914; ditto, Punjab, Jan., 1918; ditto, Bihar and Orissa, May, 1923; ditto, Calcutta, Jan., 1924; ditto, Punjab, Mar., 1925; ditto, Calcutta, April, 1926; dep. acct.-gen., Bengal, Jan., 1927; offg. dep. auditor-genl., June, 1930; dep. ch. auditor, E.I. rly., Feb., 1932; offg. acct.-genl., Bihar and Orissa, Mar., 1933; comptr., Assam, Nov., 1933; and again, Sept., 1935; offg. acct.-genl., Burma, Mar., 1935; ditto, Central Provs., Mar., 1936.

CHATTERJEE, GOURI PATI, M.Sc. (Calcutta), Meteorological Dept. (meteorologist) (b. 25th Jan., 1894).—Joined the service as scientific asst. (temp.), 15th Apl., 1921; meteorologist, Feb., 1922.

CHATTERJEE, HRISHI KESH, Dept. of Mines (junr. inspr.) (b. 12th June, 1893).—Joined the service as junr. inspr. on probn., 29th Nov., 1925; confd., Aug., 1930.

CHATTERJEE, NRITYA GOPAL, Dept. of Mines (junior inspr.) (b. 15th Jan., 1896).—Apptd. junior inspr. of mines on probn., 14th July, 1923; confd., July, 1925.

CHATTERJEE, SANTOSH KUMAR, B.A. (Cal. and Cantab.), Indian C.S. (jt. mag. and dep. coll., Bengal) (b. 12th Sept., 1904).—Educ. at University of Calcutta and Emmanuel Coll., Cambridge; joined the service, 8th Oct., 1928; arrived, Dec., 1928, and served in Bengal as asst. mag. and coll.; jt. mag. and dep. collr., Dec., 1929; addl. dist. and sess. judge, Feb., 1935.

CHATTERJEE, SISIR CHANDRA, M.D., M.R.C.S., D.P.H., Indian State Rlys. (C.M.O., E.B. rly.) (b. 4th Dec., 1886).—Joined the service as dist. surg., G.I.P. Rly., 28th March, 1918; offg. dep. C.M.O., May, 1929; ditto, G.I.P. Rly., May, 1931; offg. C.M.O., E.B. rly., Apl., 1932; ditto, N.W. rly., Mar., 1933; ditto, G.I.P. rly., Sept., 1933; C.M.O., E.B. rly., Nov., 1934.

CHATTERJEE, SURENDRA NATH, M.A., Indian Audit Dept. (b. Nov., 1888).—Joined the service, 10th Mar., 1910; dep. acct.-genl., posts and tels., Nagpur, Sept., 1930; asst. acct.-genl., Central Provs., Nov., 1931; dep. acct.-genl., Central Provs., Dec., 1932; ditto, Bengal, Jan., 1934; exr., local audit dept., Bengal, Dec., 1935.

CHATTERJI (see entries under Chatarji).

CHATTERTON, SIR ALFRED, C.I.E., B.Sc., A.M.I.C.E., M.I.M.E., late Indian Educl. Service.—Apptd. prof. of engrg., Mad. Engrg. Coll., 6th Aug., 1888; K.-i-H. medal, 1st class, May, 1900; dir. of industrial and technical inquiries, Madras, Aug., 1906; dir. of industries, Oct., 1908; on service under the Mysore Durbar, from July, 1912; C.I.E., June, 1912; retd., Aug., 1918; Kt., June, 1919; member of the Indian industrial commn.; author of "Lift Irrigation."

CHATTERTON, RIGHT REV. EYRE, D.D., F.R.G.S.—Educ. at Haileybury Coll. and Dublin Univ.; ordained in 1887, and apptd. to curacy at Stockton-on-Tees; head of Dublin univ. mission to Chota Nagpur, 1891-1900; curate of parish church, Richmond, Surrey, 1901; apptd. bishop of Nagpur, 3rd April, 1903; K.-i-H. medal, Jan., 1926; retd., Oct., 1926.

CHATURVEDI, MANOHAR DAS (see Manohar, D. C.).

CHAUDHRI AKBAR HUSAIN (see Akbar Husain, Chaudhri).

CHAUDHURI, CHANDRA MOHAN, Indian Forest Service (asst. consvr., Bihar) (b. 26th Sept., 1897).—Joined the service as asst. consvr., Bihar and Orissa, on 19th Dec., 1922; divl. forest officer, Nov., 1929.

CHAUDHURI, HARI SADHAN GHOSH (see Ghosh Chaudhuri, H. S.).

CHAUDHURI, KUMUD RANJAN, M.A., Indian Police (asst. supt., Assam) b. (2nd Oct., 1901).—Joined the service, 11th May, 1925; in training to April, 1927; asst. supt., May, 1927; offg. supt., Mar., 1932, and again, Jan., 1933; confid., 1937.

CHAUDHURI, MUHAMMAD, KHAN BAHADUR, B.A., Assam C.S. (commr. of excise, Assam) (b. 10th Nov., 1886).—Joined the service, 1st Mar., 1909; dep. mag. and dep. collr., June, 1910; extra asst. commr., Apl., 1912; dep. commr., Jan., 1933; commr. of excise, Assam, Mar., 1935; offg. secty., transf. depts., Assam, in addn., Sept., 1936.

CHAUDHURI, NIRMALKANTI RAY, see Ray Chaudhuri, N.)

CHAUDHURI, PRABHAT CHANDRA, Indian Agricultural Service (dep. dir. of sericulture, Bengal) (b. 9th Dec., 1887).—Joined the service, 10th Oct., 1913; dep. dir. of sericulture, Oct., 1922.

CHAUDHURI, PRAPHULLA CHANDRA, M.A. B.L., Indian Audit Dept. (b. 14th Feb., 1892).—Joined the service, 7th Feb., 1916; asst. acct.-genl., Punjab, Apl., 1918, ditto, Bengal, Feb., 1924; asst. auditor, E.B. rly., Dec., 1925; ditto, N.W. rly., June, 1927; dep. auditor (constn. bch.), N.W. rly., July, 1927; audit and accts. officer, Bombay dev. scheme, Apl., 1929; accts. officer, G.I.P. rly., Mar., 1930; ditto, E.B. rly., Apl., 1931; offg. dep. dir., rly. clearing accts. office, Apl., 1933; offg. dep. ch. accts. officer, E. B. rly., Jan., 1935.

CHAUDHURI, PRASANTA CHANDRA, M.A. (Punjab), Indian C.S. (jt. mag. and dep. collr., Bihar) (b. 21st Oct., 1903).—Educ. at University of the Punjab, Sch. of Oriental Studies, London, and Corpus Christi Coll., Oxford; joined the service, 1st Oct., 1928; arrived, 9th Nov., 1928, and served in Bihar and Orissa as asst. collr. and mag.; jt. mag., Nov., 1928; addl. dist. and sess. judge, Apl., 1935.

CHAUDHURI, RAMCHANDRA, Pandit, M.A., B.L., late Bihar Civil Service (b. 21st Aug., 1879).—Joined the service, 15th Nov., 1909; munsy, Aug., 1910; sub-judge, Dec., 1922; dist. and sess. judge, Oct., 1931; retd., July, 1936.

CHAUDHURI, SUKUMAR, Indian Forest Service (dep. consvr., Bengal) (b. 2nd Oct., 1898).—Joined the service as asst. consvr. on 14th Nov., 1923; dep. consvr., Nov., 1927.

CHAUDHURI, SURATH NATH (see Surath Nath, C.)

CHAUDHURI, TAJAL ISLAM MUHAMMAD NURANNABI (see Tajal I. M. N. Chaudhuri).

CHAUDHURI, TAPENDRA KUMAR GHOSH (see Ghosh Chaudhuri, T. K.).

CHAUNCY, FREDERICK CHARLES LESLIE, Capt., I.A., Indian Political Service (b. 22nd Dec., 1904).—First commn., 27th Aug., 1924; in milty. employ until 12th Feb., 1930; under secty. to A.G.G. in C.I., Feb., 1931; under secty. to pol. resdt. in the Persian Gulf; vice-consul, Mohammedah, Nov., 1932; asst. pol. agent, Zhob, May, 1937.

CHAVE, ELMER HARGREAVES, B.Sc., Indian Service of Engrs. (exec. engr., Madras) (b. 10th

Oct., 1891).—Joined the service as asst. exec. engr., on 14th April, 1920; exec. engr., Oct., 1924.

CHAVES, HENRY JOSEPH, Indian Posts and Telegraphs (chief supt.) (b. 8th Oct., 1883).—Joined the service, 20th Sept., 1901; supt. tel. traffic, Feb., 1929; chief supt., Apl., 1937.

CHEEMA, SARDAR GANDASING (see Gandasing, C. S.).

CHEESMAN, GEORGE MAURICE, Office of High Commr. for India (higher clerical officer, accts. dept.) (b. 16th May, 1876).—Apptd. to acctnt.-gen.'s dept., India Office, 3th Nov., 1914; on mil. service, Sept., 1916, to April, 1918; transf. to Office of High Commr. for India, April, 1921; higher clerical officer, April, 1922.

CHEIN, U. AUNG, B.A., Indian Police (asst. supt.) (b. 3rd Aug., 1904).—Joined the service as asst. supt. police, Burma, 21st Apl., 1928; confid., Apl., 1930; offg. dist. supt., Aug., 1934.

CHENEVIX-TRENCH, SIR RICHARD HENRY, Kt., C.I.E., O.B.E., Lieut.-Col. I.A. (ret'd.) (b. 4th April, 1876).—First commn., dated 2nd Jan., 1896; apptd. to for. dept., Nov., 1901; pol. asst., Oct., 1902; first asst. to resdt., Kashmir, Feb., 1910; under sec. to govt. of India, for. dept., Dec., 1912; asst. sec., March, 1913; under sec., Nov., 1913; asst. sec., for. and pol. dept., March, 1915; dep. ditto, sec., Sept., 1915; asst. sec., ditto, April, 1916; first asst. and sec. to chief commr., Baluchistan, Feb., 1917; O.B.E., Jan., 1919; pol. agent and dep. commr., Sibi, Oct., 1918; 1st asst. to agent to govr.-gen. in Baluchistan, Sept., 1919; pol. agent, Zhob, July, 1920; C.I.E., June, 1923; offg. rev. and judl. commr., Baluchistan, April, 1925; pol. agent and dep. commr., Quetta, Pishin, Oct., 1926; on foreign service in Hyderabad State, Jan., 1927; Kt., Jan., 1930; adviser to delegate for Hyderabad, Indian round table conf., Nov., 1930-Jan., 1931; 2nd Session, Sept.-Dec., 1931; 3rd Session, Nov.-Dec., 1932; ret'd., Apl., 1931.

CHENGAPPAH, KODANDERA APPIAH, late Indian Forest Service (b. 10th Feb., 1879).—Joined the service, 31st Mar., 1903; dep. consvr., June, 1922; ret'd. Feb., 1934.

CHERRY, HERBERT CECIL EDGAR, B.Sc., A.M.I.C.E., Indian Service of Engrs. (exec. engr., Burma) (b. 16th June, 1892).—Joined the service, 1st Oct., 1919; asst. exec. engr., Feb., 1920; exec. engr., Oct., 1924.

CHESHIRE, FRANCIS MORETON, M.A., late Indian Educl. Service (b. 14th Aug., 1887).—Joined the service as profr., Nagpur Coll., 3rd Feb., 1914; offg. princpl., July, 1926; confid., May, 1930; ret'd., June, 1933.

CHETPAT, PATTABHERAMA RAMASWAMI AYYAR (see Ramaswami Ayyar, Sir C. P.).

CHETTI, K.C.S. (see Sundaram Chetti, K.C.).

CHETTLE, GEORGE, Indian Police (b. 18th June, 1895) (dist. supt., Burma).—Joined the service as asst. dist. supt., on 19th Feb., 1921; offg. dist. supt.; June, 1926; confid., July, 1927; awarded King's police medal, June, 1932.

CHETTUR MADHAVAN NAYAR (see Nayar, C. Madhavan).

CHETTUR, SANKARA KRISHNA, Indian C.S. (sub-collr., Madras) b. 20th May, 1905.—Educ. at University of Madras and University Coll., Oxford; joined the service, 10th October, 1929; arrived, November, 1929, and served in Madras as asst. coll. and mag; sub-collr., Sept., 1931.

CHETWODE, SIR PHILIP WALHOUSE, Bt., G.C.B., O.M., G.C.S.I., K.C.M.G., D.S.O., D.C.L., Field-Marshal (b. 21st Sept., 1869).—First commn. dated 20th Nov., 1889; asst. milty. secty. to G.O.C.-in-C., Aldershot, 1906-1908; brig.-comdr., Lond. dist., 1912-14; ditto, N. Comd., May-Aug., 1914; ditto, France, 1914-15; div. comdr., France, 1915-16; comdr., desert col. and sect. canal defences, E.E.F., 1916-17; comdr., eastern force, Egypt, Aprl.-Aug., 1917; army corps comdr., E.E.F., 1917-1919; milty. secty., War Office, 1919-1920; adjt.-genl. to forces, W.O., 1922-1923; G.O.C.-in-C., Aldershot Comd., 1923-1927; A.D.C. Gen. to King, July, 1927; ch., genl. staff, India, Oct., 1928; C.-in-C. in India and member govr.-genl.'s exec. council, Nov., 1930 to Nov., 1935; G.C.S.I., June, 1934; O.M., Jan., 1936; War services, Burma, 1892-3; Chin Hills, S.A. War, 1899-1902; France and Belgium, 1914-16; E.E.F., 1916-18. Order of the Nile, 2nd class; Legion of Honour, 3rd class; French War Cross; Order of the Sacred Treasure, 1st class.

CHEVIS, SIR WILLIAM, Kt., late Indian C.S.—Educ. at Lancing Coll., and Balliol Coll., Oxford; apptd. after exam. of 1883; arrived, 4th Dec., 1885, and served in the Punjab as asst. commr.; dist. judge, April, 1893; dep. commr., Jan., 1899; divl. judge, Nov., 1900; temp. addl. judge, chief court, Punjab, Feb., 1910; judge, ditto, Jan. 1914; judge, high ct., Punjab, April, 1919; offg. chief justice, high ct., Punjab, June, 1920; knighted, Jan., 1922; ret'd., June, 1923.

CHEYNE, GEORGE COLLIE, M.B.E., M.A., B.Sc., Public Works Dept., Burma (river training expert) (b. 1st Jan., 1887).—Joined the Indian Forest Service in Burma as asst. consvr., 16th Dec., 1911; dep. consvr., Dec., 1916; M.B.E., June, 1924; transferred to public wks. dep. as river training expert, 24th Oct., 1927.

CHHUTTAN LAL, RAI BAHADUR, late Indian Service of Engrs. (b. 11th Feb., 1882).—Joined the service, 29th Sept., 1904; asst. engr., Sept., 1905; exec. engr., Sept., 1913; offg. suptg. engr., July, 1922; confd., Oct., 1926; ch. engr., Aprl., 1933; ret'd., Feb., 1937.

CHICK, ANDREW WILBERT, Legisl. Dept., Govt. of India (asst. secty.) (b. 20th Aug., 1888).—Joined legis. dept., 1908; supt., Mar., 1932; asst. secty., Oct., 1932.

CHIDELL, JOHN WALLACE PHILIP, B.A., Indian Office (pte. secty. to permit. under-secty. of state for India and for Burma) (b. 26th Oct., 1909).—Educ. at Charterhouse and Trinity Coll., Cambridge; apptd. asst. princpl., 21st Dec., 1933; resdt. clk. Oct., 1935; pte. secty. to permit. under-secty. of state for India and for Burma, Oct., 1937.

CHILTON, ALFRED HENRY, A.M.I.E.E., A.M.I.M.E., Indian State Rlys. (b. 5th Nov., 1889).—Joined the service as elec. engr., E.I. Rly., 1st Mar., 1927; ditto, N.W. Rly. and offg. as senr. elec. engr., Feb., 1928.

CHISHOLM, DONALD MALCOLM FRANCIS, V.D., Indian State Rlys. (transpn. supt., E.I. Rly.) (b. 23rd Oct., 1894).—Joined the service, 17th Sept., 1914; offg. supt., Feb., 1925; confd., Mar., 1930.

CHIT, U.BA, B.A., B.L., Burma Judl. Service (dist. and sess. judge) (b. 23rd Aug., 1890).—Joined the service, 1st June, 1912; extra asst. commr., Jan., 1924; dist. and sess. judge, Mar., 1935.

CHITALE, P. K. (see Padmakar Krishan Chitale).

CHITHAM, SIR CHARLES CARTER, Kt., C.I.E., Indian Police (inspr.-genl., Central Provs.) (b. 13th Sept., 1886).—Joined the service, Nov., 1906; asst. dist. supt., Dec., 1908; dist. supt., Jan., 1917; offg. dep. inspr.-gen., March, 1926; confd., Aug., 1927; awarded King's police medal, Jan., 1931; offg. inspr.-gen., Feb., 1931; confd., June, 1933; C.I.E., Jan., 1934; knighted, June, 1936; offg. member, Public Service Commn., Aprl., 1937, to Sept., 1937.

CHITHAM, JOHN ROWLAND, Indian Police (b. 21st Nov., 1914).—Apptd. asst. supt., Bengal on probn., 24th Nov., 1935.

CHITRE, ATMARAM ANANT, B.A., LL.B., late Bombay Judl. Dept. (b. 17th May, 1877).—Apptd. judge, small cause court, Bombay, 1st Aprl., 1918; chief judge, ditto, Dec., 1928; offg. judge, high ct., Bombay, Aprl., 1935; ret'd., May, 1937.

CHOPRA, AMAR NATH, B.A., A.M.I.E., Indian Service of Engrs. (exec. engr., Burma) (b. 15th June, 1893).—Joined the service, 4th Oct., 1915; asst. engr., Oct., 1916; on mil. duty with Indian D.F., 1st Aug. to 18th Oct., 1918; asst. exec. engr., Jan., 1920; exec. engr., Oct., 1925.

CHOPRA, GYAN CHAND, late Indian Service of Engrs. (b. 30th Sept., 1881).—Joined the service, 4th Oct., 1904; asst. engr., Feb., 1909; exec. engr., Nov., 1915; offg. suptg. engr., Aprl., 1926; ret'd. Feb., 1934.

CHOPRA, PINDI DAS, M.B., Lt.-Col. I.M.S., (supt., central jail, Punjab) (b. 1st June, 1888).—First commn. dated 16th July, 1915; supt., central jail, May, 1925; offg. dep. inspr.-genl. of prisons, Punjab, Feb., 1935.

CHOPRA, RAM NATH, C.I.E., M.B., Bt.-Col., I.M.S. (proftr., sch. of tropical medicine, Calcutta) (b. 17th Aug., 1882).—Joined the service, 1st Aug., 1908; in mil. employ up to 12th July, 1921; proftr., sch. of tropical medicine, Calcutta, Aug., 1921; C.I.E., Jan., 1934; Hon. physician to the King, Dec., 1935.

CHOPRA WAZIR CHAND (see Wazir Chand Chopra).

CHOTZNER, ALFRED JAMES, B.A., late Indian C.S. (Bengal).—Educ. at Harrow and St. John's College, Cambridge; apptd. after exam. of 1895; arrived, 7th Dec., 1896, and served in Bengal as asst. mag. and collr.; asst. collr. of customs, May, 1904; joint mag. and dep. collr., E. Bengal and Assam, Aprl., 1906; dist. and sess. judge, March, 1909; re-transfd. to Bengal, Aprl., 1912; actg. judge, high ct., Calcutta, June, 1923; apptd. judge, high ct., Calcutta, Nov., 1924; ret'd., July, 1928; M.P. for Upton division of West Ham, Oct., 1931.

CHOWDHRY, KHEM CHANDRA, M.A., Indian C.S. (asst. commr., Punjab) (b. 25th Aug., 1912).—Educ. at D.A.V. High Sch., Lahore, Punjab Univ., and London School of Economics; joined the service, 15th Sept., 1937; arrived in India, Oct., 1937, and served in the Punjab as asst. commt.

CHRISTIAN, HERSCHEL DEAR, Bihar C.S. (supt., Gangpur State) (b. 10th Mar., 1883).—Joined the service, 3rd Nov., 1904; dep. mag. and collr., Nov., 1906; diwan, Gangpur State, Feb., 1922.

CHRISTIAN, JOHN BERESFORD, Lt.-Col. I.M.S. (ret'd.).—Joined the service, 27th June, 1901; in mil. employ till Aug., 1908; civil surg., May, 1909; on mil. service, Nov., 1914, to April, 1918; reverted to mil. employ, 1st Dec., 1924; ret'd., June, 1928.

CHRISTIE, WALTER HENRY JOHN, B.A. (Cantab), Indian C.S. (under-secty., govt. of India, dept. of communications) (b. 17th Dec., 1905).—Educ. at Eton and King's Coll., Cambridge; joined the service, 8th Oct., 1928; arrived, Dec., 1928, and served in Bengal as asst. mag. and collr.; offg. under secty. to govt. of Bengal, poll. and appts. depts., Aug, 1931; confd., Feb., 1932; offg. mag. and collr., Apl., 1936; under-secty., govt of India, dept. of communications, Dec., 1937.

CHRISTIE, WILLIAM, M.C., Indian C.S. (mag. and collr., United Provs.) (b. 29th Feb., 1896).—Educ. at St. Andrews Univ. and Clare Coll., Cambridge; joined the service, 28th Oct., 1920; arrived, 29th Dec., 1920, and served in United Provs. as asst. mag. and collr.; offg. dep. commr., May, 1924; asst. commr., March, 1925; offg. mag. and collr., April, 1926; offg. dep. commr., Delhi, April to Novr., 1928; secty. bd. of rev., U.P., Nov., 1929; offg. addl. dep. secty., govt. of India, dept. of educn., health and lands, Oct., 1932; dep. secty. to the Govt. of India, finance dept., Apl., 1933; mag. and collr. (prov. subs.), Apl., 1933; confd., Dec., 1934; offg. commr., N.I. salt rev., Apl., 1936, to Oct., 1937.

CHRISTIE, WILLIAM ALEXANDER KYNOCH, B.Sc., Ph.D., late Geological Survey of India (b. 2nd Oct., 1882).—Apptd. chemist, geological survey of India, 18th Nov., 1906; on mil. service (I.A.R.O.), March, 1915, to Aug., 1917; transf. temply. to fin. dept., govt. of India, as dep. assay master, Calcutta mint, April, 1918; ret'd., Oct., 1932.

CHRISTOPHERS, SIR SAMUEL RICHARD, Kt., C.I.E., O.B.E., Bt.-Col. I.M.S. (ret'd.) (b. 27th Nov., 1873).—First commn. dated 1st Sept., 1902; on spl. duty under dir.-gen., I.M.S., July, 1903, to May, 1904; supt. of the King Inst. of preventive medicine, and the King Inst. of hygiene and bacteriology, Medl. Coll., Madras, May, 1904; on spl. duty under sany. commr. with govt. of India, for enquiry into blackwater fever in the Dooars, Dec., 1907, to Jan., 1909; asst. to dir., Central Research Inst., Kasauli, Jan., 1909; dir., Central Research Inst., Kasauli (s.p.t.), Dec., 1914; C.I.E., Jan., 1915; on mily. duty, from Jan., 1916, to Oct., 1919; O.B.E., June, 1918; dir., Kala-azar Commission, Jan., 1923; dir., central research inst., ret'd. and re-employed from 27th Nov., 1930, Kasauli, June, 1925; K.H.P., Feb., 1927; ret'd., Nov., 1930; Kt., June, 1931.

CHUDASAMA, MANSINHJI MERUJI, LL.B., Indian Police (b. 25th Apl., 1905).—Joined the service as asst. supt., Bombay, 24th Dec., 1929.

CHUNDER (see Chandra).

CHUNDER, KAMAL CHUNDER, B.A. (Calc.). B.A. (Oxon), Barr., Indian C.S. (dist. and sess. judge, Assam) (b. 4th Jan., 1895).—Educ. at Calcutta Univ. and Pembroke Coll., Oxford; joined the service, 20th Oct., 1919; arrived, 3rd Dec., 1919, and posted to Bengal; as asst. mag. and collr., services placed temp. under govt. of Assam, Dec., 1924; addl. dist. and sess. judge, Assam, Dec., 1924; offg. ditto, Bengal, April, 1927; confd., July, 1931.

CHUNI LAL, RAI BAHADUR, LALA, M.A., LL.B., Punjab Civil Service, Judicial Bch. (dist. and sess. judge) (b. 22nd June, 1883).—Joined the service as proby. extra asst. commr., 24th Jan., 1906; confd., Sept., 1910; sub-judge, Oct., 1912; offg. dist. and sess. judge, Aug., 1921; confd., Oct., 1932.

CHUNI LAL, RAI BAHADUR, LALA, Indian Police (supt.) (b. 8th Apl., 1888).—Joined the service, 24th May, 1909; dep. supt., Dec., 1913; apptd. to I.P. as supt., April, 1933.

CHUNI LAL, HANDA, Indian Service of Engrs. (asst. exec. engr., Punjab) (b. 2nd Feb., 1907).—Joined the service as apprentice engr., 21st Jan., 1929; proby. asst. exec. engr., Jan., 1930; confd., Jan., 1933.

CHURCHER, JOHN EDWARD GEORGE, Indian Police (asst. supt., Bihar) (b. 29th Jan., 1905).—Joined the service as asst. supt., Bihar and Orissa, on 18th Nov., 1925; offg. supt., Feb., 1933.

CHURCHILL, EDWARD ARCHIBALD HICKLING, B.Sc., Indian Agricl. Service (dep. dir. of agric. Centr. Provs.) (b. 15th Dec., 1889).—Apptd. asst. dir. of agric., 31st Jan., 1922; dep. dir. of agric., Jan., 1930; offg. prin., agricl. coll., Nagpur, Nov., 1935.

CLAGUE, JOHN, C.I.E., B.A., late Indian C.S., (adviser to sec. of state for Burma), (b. 29th Jan., 1882).—Educ. at King William's Coll., Isle of Man and St. Peter's Coll., Cambridge; apptd. after exam. of 1905; arrived, 26th Nov., 1906, and served in Burma as asst. commr., and as settl. offr.; offg. dep. dir., land records, Nov., 1916; dep. commr., Dec., 1917; on mil. duty, May, 1918, to Feb., 1919; dir. of agric., Burma, Nov., 1920; dep. commr., July, 1921; addl. sec. to govt., F.R. Dept. (in addn.), Nov., 1923; confd., July, 1924; offg. ch. secty. to govt., home and pol. dept., April, 1928; C.I.E., Jan., 1930; offg. commr., May, 1931; confd., Aug., 1933; ret'd., Mar., 1937; adviser to sec. of state for Burma, Apl., 1937.

CLARE, REV. HEBER, B.A., Indian Eccles. Estab. (junn. chaplain).—Apptd. junn. chaplain, 16th Feb., 1933.

CLARK, ALLAN McDONALD, M.A., Indian C.S. (magte. and collr., Bengal) (b. 16th Oct., 1895).—Educ. at Keith Gr. Sch. and Aberdeen, and Oxford (Non-Coll.) Universities; apptd. after exam. of 1920; arrived, 4th Dec., 1920, and served in the Prov. of Bengal as asst. mag. and collr., and sub-divl. officer, Nov., 1923; addl. mag. and collr., March, 1925; on spl. duty,

rev. dept., Nov., 1925, to March, 1926; offg. dist. and sess. judge, Dec., 1926; offg. mag. and collr., Mar., 1927; confd., Nov., 1932; dep. sec. pol. and apptt. depts., Bengal, Mar., 1932.

CLARK, ARTHUR STIRLING, M.A., Office of High Commr. for India (controller, store dept.). (b. 3rd Nov., 1894).—Educ. at Daniel Stewart's Coll., Edinburgh, and at Edinburgh Univ.; apptd. junior clerk, store dept., 19th Feb., 1920; war service—European War, Oct., 1914, to April, 1919; transfed. to office of high commr. for India, Oct., 1920; services lent to Colonial Office, April to Dec., 1921; principal exec. officer, April, 1922; contr. (temp.), April, 1927.

CLARK, FRANK KYNOC, M.A. (Cantab.). Indian Educl. Service (b. 19th Mar., 1885).—Apptd. headmaster, Patwardhan high school, Nagpur, June, 1917; offg. inspr. of schools, Jubbulpore Circle, Jan., 1927; confd., Nov., 1927; dep. dir., public instrn. and sec. to high school educn. board, Apr., 1931; educl. commr. with govt. of India (temp.), Mar., 1932 to June, 1933; offg. principl., Spencer training coll., Jubbulpore, June, 1933.

CLARK, GEORGE EDWARD, B.A., Indian C.S. (asst. commr., Burma) (b. 6th Aug., 1912).—Educated at Sandown (I. of W.) Secondary School and St. John's College, Cambridge; appointed after exam. of 1935; arrived in India, Nov., 1936, and served in Burma as asst. commr.

CLARK, THOMAS JOHN, late Indian Police (b. 26th Feb., 1895).—Joined the service as asst. supt., Bengal, on 10th Oct., 1921; offg. supt., July, 1927; confd., Mar., 1928.

CLARK, THOMAS VINSON, Indian Police (b. 6th May, 1913).—Joined the service as proby. asst. supt., Burma, Nov., 1932.

CLARK, Sir WILLIAM HENRY, G.C.M.G., K.C.S.I.—Educ. at Eton and Trinity Coll., Cambridge; clerk, Board of Trade, 1899; sec. to special mission to Shanghai to negotiate a commercial treaty with China, 1901; acting second sec. in diplomatic service, 1902; sec. to royal commn. on supply of food, &c., in time of war, 1903-05; C.M.G., 1903; private sec. to presdt. of board of trade, 1906-08; ditto to chancellor of exchequer, 1908-10; member of council of govr.-gen. of India, commerce and industry dept., Nov., 1910, to April, 1916; C.S.I., Dec. 1911; K.C.S.I., June, 1915; compt.-gen., commercial intelligence, Board of Trade, 1916-17; compt.-gen., dept. of overseas trade, Sept., 1917; high commr. in Canada for H.M. Govt. since 1928; K.C.M.G., June, 1930; G.C.M.G., May, 1937.

CLARK, WILLIAM MORRISON, B.Sc., M.B.E., Indian Agricl. Service (b. 23rd May, 1895).—Joined the service as dep. dir. of agric., Burma, on 3rd April, 1923; M.B.E., June, 1918.

CLARKE, CHARLES JAMES, D.S.O., Col., R.E. (ret'd.), late Indian State Railways (b. 3rd June, 1875).—First commn., dated 27th Feb., 1894; joined the Public Works Dept. as asst. engr., state rlys., 1st June, 1896; exec. engr., June, 1907; on mil. duty from Oct., 1914, to May, 1919; suptg. engr., Dec., 1921; chief engr., Sept., 1924; ret'd., June, 1930.

CLARKE, E. W., Income Tax Dept. (commr., inc. tax, Madras) (b. 12th June, 1888).—Apptd. to income tax dept., central board of

revenue, 7th March, 1919; asst. commr., Western range, Madras, March, 1924; offg. commr. variously from Dec., 1928; confd., Mar., 1937.

CLARKE, GEOFFREY, A.M.I.C.E., Indian Service of Engrs. (suptg. engr., Burma) (b. 23rd Oct., 1882).—Trained at R.I.E. Coll.; joined the service as asst. engr., on 1st Oct., 1904; exec. engr., Oct., 1912, temp. suptg. engr., Nov., 1923; perm. ditto, Feb., 1927; offg. ch. engr., Sept., 1931.

CLARKE, Sir GEOFFREY ROTHE, Kt., C.S.I., O.B.E., late Indian C.S.—Educ. at Dublin Univ., apptd. after exam. of 1894; arrived, 5th Dec., 1895, and served in N.W. Prov. and Oudh as asst. mag. and collr.; offd. as postmr.-genl., Madras, from Jan., 1904; confd., April, 1906; dep. dir.-gen., post office, April, 1907; postmaster-gen., United Prov., Nov., 1909; dep. dir.-gen., post office, March, 1912; postmaster-gen., Punjab, June, 1915; ditto, Bengal and Assam, April, 1917; O.B.E., Jan., 1918; offg. dir.-gen., posts and telegraphs, March, 1918; confd., Sept., 1918; C.S.I., Jan., 1921; on depn. as Indian delegate to Internl. Postal Congress, Stockholm, May, 1924; Kt., Jan., 1925; ret'd., Jan., 1926.

CLARKE, GEORGE, C.I.E., F.I.C., F.C.S., late Indian Agric. Service (b. 23rd Oct., 1878).—Apptd. agricl. chemist to govt., United Provs., Feb., 1907; offg. dir. of agric., United Provs., Oct., 1923; confd., Oct., 1926; C.I.E., July, 1926; ret'd., Oct., 1933.

CLARKE, JOHN ARTHUR, Indian Forest Service (b. 4th Dec., 1897) (dep. consvr. Burma).—Joined the service as asst. consvr., on 27th Dec., 1924; dep. consvr., Nov., 1928.

CLARKE, MATTHEW JOHN, Capt., C.I.E., I.A. (asst. commr., Burma) (b. 19th July, 1895).—First commn. dated 1st Dec., 1914; asst. commr., July, 1921; sec. to finl. commr., 11th Nov., 1926; offg. dep. commr., May, 1927; C.I.E., June, 1932; chairman, bd. of trustees for development of City of Rangoon, Aug., 1934.

CLARKE, Rev. MAURICE, M.A., Indian Eccl. Estab. (b. 21st July, 1893) (junr. chaplain, Madras).—Apptd. chaplain on probation, Madras, 15th Oct., 1927; junr. chaplain, Oct., 1929.

CLARKE, MAURICE NOBLE, Indian Civil Service (dist. and sess. judge, Central Provs.) (b. 13th Sept., 1891).—Educ. at Uppingham School and Trinity Coll., Dublin; on mil. service from Jan., 1915; joined the service, 13th Feb., 1920, and served in the Central Provs. as asst. commr. from 9th May, 1920; sub-judge, Dec., 1924; offg. dist. and sess. judge, March, 1926; confd., May, 1929.

CLARKE, Sir REGINALD, Kt., C.I.E., late Indian Police Service.—Joined the service, Jan., 1900, and served as proby. asst. supt.; confd., Dec., 1905; supt., April, 1906; offg. persnl. asst. to inspr.-genl., March, 1911; confd., Jan., 1912; dep. commr. of police, Calcutta, and Burma mily. police, April, 1912; offg. commr. of police, Calcutta, March, 1915; confd., May, 1916; C.I.E., June, 1919 knighted, June, 1922; ret'd., June, 1925.

CLARKE, Rev. RICHARD CHARLES BOLINGBROKE, M.A., late Eccles. dept., Central Provs. (b. 13th July, 1879).—Apptd. chaplain on probation, 2nd March, 1912; junr. chaplain, Nov., 1912; senr. chaplain, Nov., 1922; ret'd., July, 1932.

CLARKE, ROBERT LUCAS HYRAPIET, late Indian C.S.—Educ. at St. Paul's Sch., and New Coll., Oxford; apptd. after exam. of 1892; arrived, 25th Dec., 1893, and served in the N.W. Prov. and Oudh as asst. mag. and collr., and asst. commr.; joint mag., July, 1898; dep. commr., Sept., 1907; mag. and collr., March, 1910; commr., March, 1922; offg. member, board of rev., Aug., 1926; ret'd., Dec., 1928.

CLARKE, WILLIAM GEORGE, M.B.E., late Indian Police Service.—Joined the service, 7th June, 1898; M.B.E., Jan., 1921; supt., Aug., 1923; awarded King's Police Medal, Jan., 1924; ret'd.

CLARKSON, WILLIAM, India Office (hr. clerical officer, econ. and overseas dept.) (b. 10th May, 1899).—Apptd. clerical officer, 1st Jan., 1920 after previous service from Apl., 1917; hr. clerical officer, Sep., 1936.

CLAUSON, MILES JOHN, B.A., India Office (pte. secty. to secty. of state for India and for Burma) (b. 29th Mar., 1902).—Educ. at Eton and Corpus Christi Coll., Oxford; secty.'s office, G.P.O., Oct., 1925; to Dec., 1926; asst. prinpl., India Office, Jan., 1927; resdt. clerk, 1st Oct., 1927; asst. pte. secty. to secty. of state, Dec., 1928; actg. prinpl., Jan., 1932; confd., Dec., 1933; pte. secty. to secty. of state for India and for Burma, Oct., 1937.

CLAY, Sir JOSEPH MILES, K.C.I.E., C.S.I., O.B.E., B.A., Indian C.S. (adviser to secty. of state for India) (b. 6th Sept., 1881).—Educ. at Winchester and New Coll., Oxford; apptd. after exam. of 1904; arrived, 2nd Dec., 1905, and served in the United Provs., as asst. mag. and collr.; under-sec. to govt., July, 1911; dep. commr., Garhwal, Apl., 1913; asst. mag. and collr., and forest settlt. offr. (in addn.), March, 1916; O.B.E., June, 1918; mag. and collr., Nov., 1921; C.I.E., June, 1925; offg. commr., March, 1925; offg. sec. to govt., local self-govt. dept., April, 1927, and again, Oct., 1929; dep. commr., Oct., 1927; offg. ch. secty. to govt., Nov., 1931; confd., June, 1932; ty. member, govr.'s exec. council, United Provs., Sept.-Dec., 1932; commr., Apl., 1933; C.S.I., Jan., 1934; member, govr.'s exec. council, United Provs., Apl., 1935; K.C.I.E., June, 1936; adviser to sec. of state for India, Aug., 1937.

CLAYDON, Rev. EVAN, M.A., Indian Eccles. Estabt. (b. 15th May, 1898) (junr. chaplain, Punjab).—Apptd. chaplain on probation, 27th Mar., 1928; junr. chaplain, Mar., 1929.

CLAYTON, ALFRED HARE, B.A., late Indian C.S. (Bengal) (b. 22nd Nov., 1873).—Educ. at Charterhouse and Emmanuel Coll., Cambridge; apptd. after exam. of 1895; arrived, 7th Dec., 1896, and served in Bengal as asst. mag. and collr.; joint mag. and dep. collr., Feb., 1904; transf. to E. Bengal and Assam, Dec., 1905;

mag. and collr., Jan., 1908; re-transf. to Bengal, April, 1912; chairman, Chittagong Port Trust, July, 1915; commr., Oct., 1924; ret'd., Dec., 1931.

CLAYTON, HUGH BYARD, C.I.E., M.A., late Indian C.S. (b. 24th Dec., 1877).—Educ. at Wadham Coll., Oxford; apptd. after exam. of 1900; arrived, 16th Dec., 1901, and served in Bombay as asst. collr. and mag. and railway mag.; asst. pol. agent, Nov., 1909; attached to mil. intelligence directorate, War Office, Sept., 1914, to Dec., 1918; offg. munpl. commr., city of Bombay, Dec., 1919; collr., Nov., 1920; munpl. commr., city of Bombay, Oct., 1922; C.I.E., Jan., 1924; on spl. duty with govt. of India, dept. of educn., etc., Nov., 1930, to Mar., 1931; commr., Jan., 1931; ret'd., Dec., 1936.

CLAYTON, RICHARD HENRY MICHAEL, B.A., I.C.S. (asst. collr., Bombay) (b. 11th Aug., 1907).—Educ. at Lancing College, Christ Church, Oxford, and the School of Oriental Studies, London; apptd. after the exam. of 1931; arrived in India, Jan., 1933, and served in Bombay as asst. collr.

CLAYTON, ROBERT VICKERS, M.B., B.S., D.P.H., Indian State Ry's. (P.M.O. G.I.P. rly.) (b. 18th Dec., 1887).—Apptd. dist. medl. officer, N.W. rly. (temp.), 26th Jan., 1928; ditto (perm.), B.B. & C.I. rly., May, 1929; offg. dep. C.M.O., N.W. rly., Nov., 1929; offg. C.M.O., N.W. rly., Dec., 1929; P.M.O., G.I.P. rly., July, 1932.

CLEAR, THOMAS, M.A., Indian Forest Service (consr., Madras) (b. 17th June, 1886).—Joined the service, 21st Dec., 1908, as asst. consvr.,; dist. forest officer, Sept., 1910; dep. consvr., Jan., 1914; on mil. service from Nov., 1917, to July, 1919; actg. consvr., Mar., 1924; confd., June, 1926.

CLEAR, THOMAS, late Indian Police Service (Bengal) (b. 25th Aug., 1876).—Held non-gazetted appts. from 25th March, 1896, to 14th July, 1909; asst. supt., July, 1909; supt., July, 1917; ret'd., Aug., 1931.

CLEARY, DENIS MACKROW, M.A., India Office (actg. principal mil. dept.) (b. 20th Dec., 1907).—Educ. at St. Ignatius Coll., Stamford Hill and St. Olave's and St. Saviour's Grammar Sch.; St. John's Coll., Cambridge; apptd. asst. principal, 19th Oct., 1931; resdt. clerk, Sept., 1934; pte. secty. to perm. under-secy. of state, Aug., 1936, to Oct., 1937; actg. principal, Oct., 1937.

CLEARY, MICHAEL FRANCIS, Indian Police (supt., Bengal) (b. 4th Oct., 1894).—Joined the service as proby. asst. supt., on 4th Dec., 1914; confd., Nov., 1916; addl. supt., June, 1922; pers. assistant to dir. intell. bureau, govt. of India, Nov., 1923; supt., Dec., 1925; awarded King's police medal, Jan., 1930; cent. intell. offcr, Bengal and Assam, Nov., 1935; on depn. to Imp. Defence Coll., Jan., 1938.

CLEBURNE, JEFFREY ST. HILL, late Indian Police (b. 16th Nov., 1891).—Joined the service as asst. supt. on 4th Dec., 1911; dist. supt., Dec., 1917; ret'd., May, 1933.

CLEE, CHARLES BEAUPRE BELL, Indian C.S. (secty. fin. dept., Govt. of Sind) (b. 5th Feb., 1893).—Educ. at Fitzwilliam Hall, Cambridge; joined the service, 4th Oct.; 1919; arrived, 28th Nov., 1919, and served in Bombay as asst. collr. and magte.; actg. dir. of information, March, 1924; sec., tariff bd., 1st June, 1925; offg. dep. secty. to govt., finance dept., Bombay, Apl., 1928; confd., Dec., 1928; offg. secty. to govt. of Bombay, home and eccl. depts., May, 1932; member, legisl. council, Oct., 1932; offg. secty. to govt., of Bombay, fin. dept., June, 1933, again Jan. 1936; collr. and mag., June, 1935; secty., fin. dept., Sind, Apl., 1937.

CLEGG, EDWARD LESLIE GILBERT, B.Sc., Geological Survey of India (suprg. geologist) (b. 24th Feb., 1894).—Joined the service as asst. supt., 5th Dec., 1920; offg. supt., Apl., 1927; confd., June, 1932.

CLEMENTS, ERNEST, late Indian C.S. (Bombay).—Educ. at Dulwich Coll., and Christ Church, Oxford; apptd. after exam. of 1891; arrived, 22nd Nov., 1893, and served in Bombay as asst. collr. and mag. and forest settl. officer; 2nd asst., Aug., 1900; asst. agent for sardars in Deccan, Oct., 1902, to Sept., 1903; 1st asst., Aug., 1905; judge and sess. judge, Feb., 1907; ret'd., Nov., 1928; author of an "Introduction to the Study of Indian Music," and "The Ragas of Tanjore"; joint author of "The Ragas of Hindustan."

CLEMENTS, JOHN EDMUND, M.B., Lt.-Col. I.M.S. (ret'd.), late Jail Dept., United Provs. (b. 25th Feb., 1875).—1st commn., 29th Jan., 1902; in mil. employ till April, 1904, when he was apptd. jail supt., Lucknow; on mil. duty, Oct 1914, to May, 1919; supt., central prison, Lucknow, Oct., 1916; offg. inspr.-gen. of prisons, United Provs., March, 1923; confd., July, 1925; ret'd., Aug., 1930.

CLERICI, CHARLES JOHN EMILE, C.I.E., O.B.E., late Indian Posts and Telegraphs Dept. (b. 22nd Mar., 1877).—Joined the service, 1st Aug., 1900; asst. dir.-gen., posts and tels., Oct., 1918; offg. dep. dir.-gen., posts and tels., Calcutta; C.I.E., Aug., 1916; O.B.E., June, 1918; asst. dir.-gen., Calcutta, March, 1926; offg. postmaster-genl., Central Circle, July, 1927; confd., Sept., 1928; P.M.G., U.P., Oct., 1928; ditto, Bengal and Assam, Apl., 1930; offg. senr. dep. dir. genl., Mar., 1931; ret'd., May, 1932.

CLERICI, MARIO CLAUDE, Indian Police (b. 8th Nov., 1914).—Apptd. asst. supt., Bihar, on probn., 11th Nov., 1935.

CLERK, FREDERIC VIVIAN, late Burma Frontier Service (b. 20th April, 1879).—Joined the service as extra asst. commr., 1st Apl., 1906; dep. commr., Dec., 1927; ret'd., Aug., 1932.

CLIFF, ARNOLD PEARSE, B.A., Indian Agric. Service (dep. dir. of agric., Bihar) (b. 3rd Aug., 1891).—Joined the service as dep. dir. of agric., 5th Oct., 1920; secty., Indian Central Jute Ctee., Dec., 1936.

CLIFFORD, REGINALD CHARLES, D.S.O., M.C., F.R.C.S.E., M.R.C.P., Lt.-Col., I.M.S., Medical Dept., United Provs. (civil surgeon) (b. 17th Sept., 1884).—First commn., 30th July, 1910; civil surgeon, United Provs., July, 1930; ditto, Simla W., and New Delhi, Oct., 1935.

CLIFFORD, WILLIAM WALTER, Indian Police (Supt., Bombay) (b. 26th Sept., 1887).—Joined the service, 5th July, 1908; dep. supt., Aug., 1912; apptd. to I.P. as supt., Bombay, Nov., 1933.

CLIFFORD-HURST, LESLIE ERNEST, Indian State Rlys. (dist. supt., Central Provs.) (b. 6th Mar., 1881).—Apptd. asst. supt., watch and ward, E.I. rly., 1st Sept., 1925; offg. dep. supt., Apl., 1929; dist. supt., Nov., 1933.

CLIFT, FRANK CHARLES LECHMERE, A.C.H., late Indian Service of Engrs. (b. 6th Jan., 1882).—Joined the service, 1st Oct., 1905, as asst. engr.; exec. engr., Oct., 1916; on mil. duty, Nov., 1917, to Mar., 1918; offg. suptg. engr., Apl., 1929; confd., Oct., 1931; ret'd., Jan., 1937.

CLIFT, GEORGE, B.Sc., Indian Police (b. 9th May, 1902).—Joined the service as asst. dist. supt., Burma, on 19th Aug., 1924; offg. dist. supt., Apl., 1933.

CLIFT, LAWRENCE, late Indian Police Service (b. 17th Aug., 1890).—Joined the service, 24th Nov., 1910; asst. supt., Nov., 1911; supt. (provl.), Jan., 1926; ret'd., May, 1930.

CLINTON-THOMAS, ROBERT ANTONY, B.A., Indian C.S. (asst. commr., Punjab) (b. 5th Apl., 1913).—Educ. at Haileybury and Peterhouse Coll., Cambridge; joined the service, 28th Sept., 1937; arrived in India, Oct., 1937, and served in the Punjab as asst. commr.

CLOSE, ERNEST REGINALD WARD, Indian Police (supt., Madras) (b. 4th Feb., 1896).—Joined the service, 16th March, 1921; asst. supt., July, 1923; offg. dist. supt., May, 1928; confd., July, 1930.

CLOUGH, ARTHUR ROBERT, A.M.I.C.E., Indian Service of Engrs. (exec. engr., United Provs.) (b. 28th Oct., 1891).—Joined the service, 1st Oct., 1919; asst. exec. engr., Dec., 1919; exec. engr., Oct., 1924.

CLOUGH, Rev. Canon EDMUND RICHARD, late Indian Eccles. Estabt. (Central Provs.).—Joined the service as chaplain on probn., 22nd Nov., 1903; junr. chaplain, Nov., 1906; senr. chaplain, Jan., 1917; ret'd., July, 1928.

CLOUGH, JOHN WILLIAM late India Office (b. 8th Jan., 1873).—Clerk, 2nd class, 25th July, 1892; staff clerk, Nov., 1914; cn. deputn. to India in connection with the accounts of British troops, April, 1918, to April, 1919; junior clerk, Oct., 1919; senior clerk, April, 1921; dep. chief acctn. (acting), April, 1922; appt. confirmed from 1st Nov., 1922, designation changed to asst. acctn.-gen., Jan., 1926; dep. acct.-gen., and clerk to Commrs. of Income Tax, March, 1927; ret'd., Nov., 1934.

CLOUSTON, DAVID, C.I.E., M.A., D.Sc., late Indian Agricl. Service (b. 13th Apl., 1872).—Educ. at Edinburgh Univ.; apptd. first dep. dir. of agric., Central Prov., 25th Oct., 1905; C.I.E., June, 1919; dir. of agric., Nov., 1919; offg. agric. adviser to Govt. of India, June, 1923; confd., Sept., 1924; apptd. as liaison officer between the Royal Commn. on Agric. and the govt. of India and the local govt. from 4th May, 1926; ret'd., Oct., 1929.

CLOW, ANDREW GOURLAY, C.S.I., C.I.E., M.A., F.S.S., Indian C.S. (secty. to govt. of India, dept. of labour) (b. 29th April 1890).—Educ. at Merchiston Castle School and St. John's Coll., Cambridge; apptd., after exam. of 1913; arrived in India, 29th Nov., 1914, and served in the United Provs. as asst. mag. and collr.; asst. record offr. and asst. settl. offr., Oct., 1917; dep. commr. (prov.) and settl. offr., Sept., 1918, to June, 1919; sec. to Disorders Enquiry Cttee, Feb., 1920; controller, labour bureau, May, 1920; under-sec. to Govt. of India, dept. of industries, April, 1923; dep. sec., ditto, Aug., 1924; C.I.E., Jan., 1928; sec. ditto (temp.), April, 1928; adviser and substitute delegate, internl. labour conference, Geneva, May-June, 1929; member of R.C. on labour in India, July, 1929; on spl. duty in dept. of indus. and labour, Oct., 1931; jt. secty., ditto, Nov., 1931; offg. secty., ditto, June, 1935; and again, Jan., 1936; C.S.I., June, 1935; confd. as secty. to govt. of India, indus. and lab. dept., July, 1936; member, council of state, Feb., 1936; secty. dept. of lab., Nov., 1937.

CLUTTERBUCK, Sir PETER HENRY, Kt., C.I.E., C.B.E., F.Z.S., F.R.G.S., V.D., late Indian Forest Service.—Joined the service, 27th Dec., 1889, as asst. consr., Central Prov., transf. to N.W.P. and Oudh, Jan., 1896; dep. consr., July, 1896; re-transf. to Central Prov., July, 1905; K.-i.-H. medal, 2nd class, Dec., 1911; consr., Nov., 1913; offg. chief consr., Central Prov., March to Oct., 1914; chief consr., United Prov., Oct., 1915; C.I.E., Jan., 1918; offg. inspr.-gen. of forests, govt. of India, May, 1919; confd., Oct., 1921; C.B.E., Dec., 1919; Kt., June, 1924; ret'd., Oct., 1926; timber adviser to High Commr. for India, Jan., 1927 to May, 1929.

CLYDE, DAVID, M.B., D.P.H., Lt.-Col., I.M.S. (civil surg., United Provs.) (b. 30th Mar., 1894).—First commn., dated 1st Nov., 1920; in milty. employ to 12th Feb., 1924; asst. dir. of public health, Feb., 1924; civil surg., Nov., 1929.

COATES, CHARLES LESLIE, Indian C.S. (asst. commr., Punjab) (b. 1st Oct., 1906).—Educ. at Manchester Grammar School and Wadham College, Oxford; apptd. after examination of 1930; arrvd. in India, Nov., 1931, and served in the Punjab as asst. commr.; dep. regr. co-op. societies, Mar.-May, 1937.

COATES, ERIC THOMAS, Indian C.S. (jt. secty., fin. dept., Govt. of India) (b. 1st Oct., 1897).—Educ. at Heath Gr. Sch., Halifax, and Queen's Coll., Oxford; joined the service, 28th Oct., 1921; arrived, 3rd Dec., 1921, and posted to Bengal as asst. magte. and collr., and sub. divl. officer, April, 1923; on spl. duty in finl., commerce and marine depts., Nov., 1925, to March, 1926; offg. under-sec. in ditto, March, 1926; dep. acct.-genl., Bombay, Nov., 1928; ditto, central revenues, Apl., 1929; offg. acct. genl., Bombay, July, 1930; accts. officer, N.W.F.P., Oct., 1930; dep. acct.-genl., Punjab, Nov., 1931; offg. dep. contr. of currency, Calcutta, Feb., 1932; offg. acct.-gen., Central Provs., Oct., 1933; ditto, Bombay, Apl., 1934; ditto, posts and tels., May, 1934; on spl. duty, fin. dept., govt. of India, Oct., 1935; jt. secty., fin. dept., Govt. of India, Apl., 1936.

COATMAN, JOHN, C.I.E., B.A., late Indian Police service (b. 5th Nov., 1889).—Joined the service, 21st Jan., 1911; asst. supt., May, 1912; awarded King's police medal, Jan., 1916; supt., Sept., 1922; dir. of public information, Feb., 1926; C.I.E., June, 1929; secty. to Liberal delegation, Indian round table conf., London, Nov., 1930 to Jan., 1931; 2nd session, Sept.-Dec., 1931.

COBB, EVELYN HEY, O.B.E., Capt., I.A. Indian Political Service (b. 15th Sept., 1899).—First commn., dated 15th Apl., 1919; in milty. employ prior to 15th June, 1921; pers. asst. to ch. commr., Peshawar, Nov., 1924; asst. collr., Agra, Dec., 1925; asst. pol. agent, Chitral, Nov., 1927; supy. asst. commr., Peshawar, June, 1929; city mag., ditto, May, 1930; asst. commr., Charsadda, Dec., 1931; ditto, Mardan, 1933; O.B.E., June, 1935; dep. commr., Bannu, Nov., 1935; pol. agent, Dir, Swat and Chitral, Mar., 1937.

COBBALD, ERNEST BARHAM, late Indian C.S. (b. 5th Aug., 1891).—Educ. at Ipswich Grammar School and Queen's Coll., Oxford; apptd. after exam. of 1914; arrived, 10th Dec., 1915, and served in Madras as asst. collr. and mag.; sub-collr. and jt. mag., Dec., 1922; offg. collr., May, 1925; ditto and dist. mgte., Nov., 1925; ret'd., Oct., 1934.

COBURN, MARMADUKE ROBERT, O.B.E., India Posts and Telegraphs (b. 9th Mar., 1885).—Joined the service as milty. acennt., 11th March, 1910; O.B.E., Jan., 1919; dep. finl. adviser, May, 1920; offr. finl. adviser, July, 1925; offg. dir. of establishment, Nov., 1916; finl. advr., April, 1928; reverted to milty. accts. dept., Apl., 1933; dep. fin. advr. mil. fin., Oct., 1935; mil. acct. genl., Apl., 1937.

COCHRANE, ARCHER WILLIAM ROSS, M.B., F.R.C.S., Col., I.M.S. (ret'd.)—First commn., 29th Jan. 1896; civil surgn., Bengal, Dec., 1899; temp. reverted to mil. dept., Aug., 1900; supt., lunatic asylum, Lahore, Feb., 1901; resdt. med. offr., med. coll. hosp., Calcutta, Jan. to March, 1902; civil surgn., May, 1902; supt., lunatic asylum, Agra, April, 1905; supt., King Edward VII Memorial Sanatorium, Bhowali, April, 1912; supt., lunatic asylum, Moradabad, Jan., 1918; prof. of medicine, King George's med. coll., Lucknow, Oct., 1918; inspr.-gen. of civil hosp., United Provs. Nov., 1922; ret'd. Feb. 1929.

COCHRANE, Hon. Sir ARCHIBALD DOUGLAS, G.C.M.G., K.C.S.I., D.S.O., Commander, R.N., ret'd. (govr. of Burma) (b. 8th Jan., 1885).—European War, 1914-1918 (desp. thrice, D.S.O. and bar); M.P. for East Fife, 1924-1929; M.P., Dumbartonshire, 1932-1936; K.C.S.I., Mar., 1936; govr. of Burma, May, 1936; G.C.M.G., Apl., 1937.

COCHRANE, GEORGE ALFRED DAVIES, B.Sc., A.M.I.Struct.E., Indian service of Engrs. (exec. engr., Central Provs.) (b. 10th Aug., 1901).—Joined the service as asst. exec. engr., Central Provs., 26th Sept., 1924; exec. engr. and under-sec. to govt. p.w.d., Sept., 1933.

COCK, CHARLES MATTHEW, Indian State Rlys. (transpn. (traction) engr., G.I.P. rly.) (b. 27th Jan., 1895).—Apptd. traction engr., distribn., G.I.P. rly., 21st Oct., 1929.

COCKBURN, ARTHUR BALCOMBE, Indian Finance Dept. (b. 23rd Aug., 1885).—Joined the service, 6th Apl., 1905; asst. acct.-gen., May, 1914; examr. of local fund accts. Punjab, June, 1918; dep. acct.-gen., posts and tels., March, 1925; dep. acct.-gen., Bengal, Nov., 1926; dep. dir. of audit, United Provs., Jan., 1927; offg. chief pay and accts. officer, April, 1927; dep. ch. accounting officer, United Provs., April, 1928, and again, Mar., 1930; ch. pay and accts. officer, U.P., Mar., 1929; dep. acct.-genl., U.P., Nov., 1931; audit officer, Lloyd barrage and canals constn., Mar., 1932; offg. comptr., N.W.F.P., Mar., 1933; offg. acct.-genl., Bihar and Orissa, Oct., 1935.

COCKBURN, GEORGE WILLIAM MACLOU, M.A., Burma C.S. (Cl. I.) (asst. commr.) (b. 27th May, 1913).—Educ. at Fettes and St. Andrew's Univ.; joined the service, 30th Sept., 1937; arrived in Burma, Nov., 1937, and served as asst. commr.

COCKBURN, EDWIN DUNDAS, late Indian Service of Engrs. (b. 14th Feb., 1875).—Trained at R.I.E., Coll.; joined the service, 1st Oct., 1897, as asst. engr.; exec. engr., Nov., 1906; temp. suptg. engr., July, 1916; confd., Oct., 1918; ret'd., Feb., 1930.

COEN, TERENCE BERNARD CREAGH, Indian C.S., Indian Political Service (b. 31st March, 1903).—Educ. at Uppingham and Queen's Coll., Oxford; joined the service, 25th Oct., 1927; arrived, Dec., 1927, and served in the Punjab as asst. commr.; under-secty., local self-govt. dept., Dec., 1932; reg., high ct., Lahore, Nov., 1933; under-secty. to A.G.G. in C.I., Oct., 1935; pol. agent, W. Kathiawar Agency, Mar., 1936; secty. to the Resident at Hyderabad, Oct., 1937.

COFFEY, THOMAS MALO, Indian Forest Service (dep. consvr., Bengal) (b. 4th May, 1894).—Joined the service as asst. consvr. on 15th March, 1921; dep. consvr., Mar., 1922.

COGHILL, DUNCAN JOSEPH KNIGHT, Indian C.S. (dep. commr., United Provs.) (b. 25th April, 1903).—Educ. at Cheltenham and New Coll., Oxford; joined the service, 21st Oct., 1927; arrived, Dec., 1927, and served in the United Provs., as asst. commr.; jt. mag., June, 1929; asst. resdt., Kolhapur, Nov., 1930; asst. pol. agent, Zhob, Nov., 1932; offg. under-secty. to govt. of India, for and pol. dept., Mar., 1934; offg. dep. secty., ditto, May, 1934, to Apl., 1935; offg. dep. commr., Apl., 1935.

COGHLAN, JOHN ALOYSIUS, B.A., M.C., Indian Police Service (supt., Bombay) (b. 16th Jan., 1896).—Joined the service as asst. supt., on 27th Dec., 1919; offg. supt., Oct., 1924; confd., Nov., 1927.

COHEN, SAMUEL, India Audit Office (suptg. exec. officer) (b. 4th Dec., 1881).—Apptd. clerk, 2nd class, 12th March, 1900; minor staff clerk, Oct. 1919; staff clerk, Dec., 1921; higher exec. officer, April, 1922; suptg. exec. officer, April, 1933.

COHEN, WILLIAM CHARLES HENRY, India Office (higher clerical officer, acctnt.-gen.'s dept.); (b. 28th Oct., 1890).—Educ. at Christ's Hospital; apptd. second class clerk, 21st Jan., 1909, and attd. to acctnt.-gen.'s dept.; higher clerical officer, April, 1922.

COHU, REV. CLIFFORD JOHN, late Indian Eccles. Estabt. (b. 30th Dec., 1883).—Apptd. chaplain on probn., 26th Feb., 1912; junr. chaplain, Feb., 1915; senr. chaplain, Feb., 1925; ret'd., Feb., 1937.

COLDMAN, REV. ALFRED THEODORE, B.D., Indian Eccles. Estabt. (senr. chaplain) (b. 3rd July, 1884).—Apptd. junr. chaplain, Madras, 5th Oct., 1926; senr. chaplain, July, 1933.

COLDSTREAM, SIR JOHN, B.A., late Indian C.S. (b. 23rd Dec., 1877).—Educ. at Edinburgh Acad. and Worcester Coll., Oxford; apptd. after exam. of 1900; arrived, 19th Nov., 1901, and served in the Punjab as asst. commr.; under-sec. to govt., Oct., 1906, to Feb., 1910; asst. commr. and settlt. offr., April, 1910; temply. employed under War Office from Oct., 1914, to July, 1916; dep. commr., Oct., 1917; dist. and sess. judge, Aug., 1918; legal remembrancer and sec. to govt. of Punjab, legisl. dept., Oct., 1923; actg. judge, high ct., Lahore, May to July, 1925; ditto, April to July, 1926; addl. judge, ditto, Oct., 1926; judge, ditto, Sept., 1928; ret'd., Dec., 1937; Kt., Jan., 1938.

COLE, FRANCIS NORMAN, Indian C.S. (asst. commr., Central Provs.) (b. 29th July, 1906).—Educ. at Dulwich College, Trinity College, Oxford, and London School of Oriental Studies; joined the service, 14th Oct., 1930; arrived in India, Nov., 1930, and served in the Central Prov. as asst. commr.; pte. secty. to govr., Central Provs., Apl., 1934.

COLE, FREDERICK EUSTACE, V.D., late Indian State Railways (N.W. Rly.) (b. 29th July, 1875).—Trained at T.E.C. Coll.; joined the service, 27th Oct., 1898; asst. engr., Oct., 1899; senr. asst. signal engr., June, 1909; signal engr., March, 1912; on mil. duty, Aug., 1917, to March, 1919; offg. dep. ch. engr., Oct., 1925; offg. technical officer, rly. bd., Nov., 1927; ret'd., Oct., 1930.

COLE, GEORGE ASHMOND, Capt., I.A., Indian Political Service (b. 1st Feb., 1902).—First commn., 23rd Dec., 1921; ass), commr., Kohat, 8 Apl., 1931; asst. commrt. Peshawar, Nov., 1932; asst. pol. agent. Bahrain, Mar., 1935; asst. to pol. agent, Kalat, Nov., 1936.

COLE, JOHN SAMUEL, late Office of High Commr. for India (b. 21st July, 1872).—Educ. at City of Westminster Sch., and City and Guilds' Technical Coll.; apptd. clerk for railway stores, 21st May, 1907; transf. to office of high commr. for India, Oct., 1920; ret'd., July, 1932.

COLE, LESLIE PRICHARD, B.A., Indian Service of Engrs. (b. 4th Nov., 1907).—Joined the service as asst. exec. engr., Madras, 20th Oct., 1929.

COLEBROOK, EDWARD HILDER, M.C., Indian Police (dist. supt., Madras) (b. 16th Oct., 1898).—Joined the service, 16th Oct., 1921, as asst. supt. on probn.; confd. Apl., 1925; offg. spl. supt., Malabar spl. police, Mar., 1928; offg. dist. supt., Aug., 1929; confd., Nov., 1930; awarded King's police medal; Indian police medal, Jan., 1936.

COLERIDGE, SIR JOHN FRANCIS STANHOPE DUKE, K.C.B., C.M.G., D.S.O., A.D.C., Lieut.-General, I.A. (b. 25th April, 1878).—First commn., 20th July, 1898; 8th Gurkha Rifles, 1900; staff captain, France,

Nov., 1914-Feb., 1915; G.S.O.3, Home forces and Med. E.F., Feb., 1915-Sept., 1915; G.S.O.2, Med. E.F., Sept., 1915-Apr., 1915; G.S.O.1, E.E.F., and France, Apr., 1916-Oct., 1917; Brig. Commdr., France, Oct., 1917-Nov., 1918; D.D. of Staff Duties, India, Nov., 1919-Oct., 1923; asst. cmdt., staff coll., Quetta, Oct., 1923-Nov., 1925; milty. secty., A.H.Q., India, Feb., 1926-Jan., 1930; dist. cmdr., India, Jan., 1930-Oct., 1933; K.C.B., June, 1933; milty. secty., India Office, Nov., 1933 to May, 1936; A.D.C. Gen. to the King, June, 1936; G.O.C. in C., Nthn. Cmd., June, 1936; colonel, 2/1st Punjab Regt., and 8th Gurkha Rifles; war services, Tibet, 1903-1904-Niani, Gyantsi, march to Lhasa, medal and clasp; Abor, 1911-1912, despatches; European War, 1914-1918, brevets of Lieut.-Col. and Colonel, D.S.O., with clasp., C.M.G., C.B., eight times mentioned in despatches; N.W. Frontier of India, 1930-1931, despatches; Chitral reliefs, 1932, despatches.

COLLET, ARTHUR LOWE, Judl. Dept., Bengal (regr., etc., high ct., Calcutta) (b. 9th Mar., 1888).—Apptd. off. regr. in insolvency, high ct., Calcutta, 23rd Nov., 1931; confd. (prov.), May, 1932; off. regr., original side, Sept., 1932.

COLLETT, ARTHUR PRESTON, late Indian C.S. (b. 10th Sept., 1880).—Educ. at Malvern Coll., and Queen's Coll., Cambridge; apptd. after exam. of 1903; arrived, 28th Nov., 1904, and served in the United Provs. as asst. mag. and collr.; dep. commr., Nov., 1911; jt. mag., May, 1914; pte. sec. to lieut.-govr., Sept., 1915; on milv. service from May, 1916, to Jan., 1919; mag. and collr., Nov., 1920; offg. opium agent and commr. of inc. tax, U.P., Mar., 1928; retd., Feb., 1932.

COLLETT, HENRY ASHWOOD, V.D., late Indian State Rlys. (b. 1st Jan., 1880).—Held non-gazetted appts. from 10th Aug., 1896, to 15th April, 1914; offg. asst. supt., April, 1914; confd., Oct., 1914; offg. dist. supt., May, 1921; dist. supt. (s.p.t.), July, 1923; supt., Feb., 1925; statistical officer, Nov., 1929, and again Apr., 1931; offg. dep. ch. optg. supt., Jan., 1931; Mar., 1932, and again, Oct., 1932; retd., Aug., 1936.

COLLIER, Rev. CHARLES BERNARD GREY, M.A., late Indian Eccl. Estabt. (b. 17th July, 1877).—Joined, Feb., 1908; junr. chaplain, Feb., 1911; senr. chaplain, Feb., 1921; retd., Aug., 1933.

COLLIER, Sir GEORGE HERMAN, Kt., C.I.E., late Office of High Commr. for India. Educ. at Uppingham Sch.; junr. clerk, India Office, 17th Feb., 1876; senr. clerk, Aug., 1896; dep. dir.-gen. of stores, July, 1912; dir.-gen. of stores, Nov., 1915; C.I.E., June, 1918; on deputation to India to attend Stores Cttee., Nov., 1919; knighted, Jan., 1921; retd., Aug., 1921.

COLLINGS, FRANCIS WILLIAM, late Indian Forest Service (b. 14th March, 1881).—Arrived, 28th Nov., 1903, and served as asst. consvr.; dep. consvr., Nov., 1917; on mil. service, May, 1918, to June, 1919; consvr., Sept., 1924; retd., Jan., 1932.

COLLINS, BERNARD ABDY, C.I.E., late Indian C.S. (b. 17th Feb., 1880).—Educ. at Malvern Coll., and Brasenose Coll., Oxford;

apptd. after exam. of 1903; arrived, 14th Nov., 1904, and served in Bengal as asst. mag. and collr., and asst. settlt. offr.; priv. sec. to lieut.-govr. of Bengal, March to July, 1908; under-sec. to govt., rev., pol. and appt. depts., July, 1909, to March, 1910; jt. mag. and dep. collr., March, 1911; transfd. to Bihar and Orissa, April, 1912; under-sec. to govt., pol., appt. and educ. depts., April, 1912; regr., co-op. credit soc., May, 1913; temp. employed in rev. and stat. dept., India Office, from April, 1916, to May, 1917; mag. and collr., April, 1917; regr., co-operative credit societies, Sept., 1917; on spl. duty under Indian munitions board, Delhi, April, 1918; controller (industrial intell.), Simla, July, 1918, to Feb., 1919; offg. sec. to govt., Bihar and Orissa, finl. and munpl. depts., April, 1919; dir. of industries, April, 1920; also sec. to govt., educ. dept., in addition, from Dec., 1922; C.I.E., Jan., 1926; on deputation as dir. of com. and industry, Hyderabad State, March, 1927; retd., Oct., 1933.

COLLINS, FREDERICK MICHAEL, M.A., M.B.M.Chir. (Camb.), F.R.C.S. (Eng.), L.R.C.P. (Lond.), Major I.M.S. (D.M.O., Madras) (b. 5th Sept., 1900).—First commn., 24th Jan., 1933; in mil. employ until 29th Oct., 1934; dist. med. offr., Oct., 1934.

COLLINS, GODFREY FERDINANDO STRATFORD, C.I.E., O.B.E., M.A., Indian C.S. (collr., Bombay) (b. 3rd Nov., 1888).—Educ. at Charterhouse, and Christ Church Coll., Oxford; apptd. after exam. of 1911; arrived, 21st Nov., 1912, and served in Bombay as asst. collr. and mag.; on milv. duty from July, 1916, to Jan., 1919; O.B.E., Jan., 1919; extra dep. dir. of civil supplies, Bombay, 1919; forest settlement officer (temp.), Sept., 1920; offg. collr. and dist. mag., Jan., 1923; settlement officer, Oct., 1924; addl. dy. sec. to govt., fin. dept., Sept., 1925; offg. collr. and dist. magte., April, 1926; confd., June, 1930; actg. regr. to co-op. societies, June, 1926; secty. to govt. of Bombay, home and eccl. depts., Aug., 1929; member legisl. council, Bombay, 1929 and 1931; C.I.E., Jan., 1931; offg. pte. secty. to govr. of Bombay, Aug., 1934; confd., Jan., 1935; offg. commr., Sind, Apr., 1935.

COLLINS, JOHN LAVILLE, late Indian Police (b. 10th March, 1881).—Joined the service, 26th Aug., 1901; on mil. duty from 28th Sept., 1918, to 7th Feb., 1920; asst. supt., July, 1922; offg. supt., Nov., 1927; confd., June, 1931; retd., Mar., 1936.

COLLINS, TERENCE MOSTYN, Indian Police (dist. supt., Central Provs.) (b. 21st Mar., 1894).—Joined the service as asst. supt., Dec., 1913; on mil. duty from 10th Nov., 1917, to 21st Oct., 1919; dist. supt., Jan., 1925; prinpl. training school, Saugor, March, 1925; central intel. offr., Central Provs., Nov., 1936.

COLLIS, MAURICE STEWART, B.A., late Indian C.S. (b. 10th Jan., 1889).—Educ. at Rugby, and Corpus Christi Coll., Oxford; apptd. after exam. of 1911; arrived, 2nd Dec., 1912, and served in Burma as asst. commr.; on mil. duty, Dec., 1917, to May, 1919; services placed at disposal of Govt. of India, rev. and agric. dept., Jan. to Feb., 1920; offg. dep.

sec. to govt., H. & P. dept., April, 1925; offg. dep. commr., Mar., 1928; confd., Dec., 1930; ret'd., Jan., 1936.

COLLISTER, HAROLD JAMES, Indian C.S. (judge, high ct., Allahabad) (b. 14th Jan., 1885).—Educ. at South-Eastern Coll., Ramsgate, and Sidney Sussex Coll., Cambridge; apptd. after exam. of 1908; arrived, 29th Nov., 1909, and served in the United Provs. as asst. mag.; addl. sess. and sub-judge, Sept., 1917; jt. mag., Feb., 1918; on mil. duty (I.A.R.O.), July, 1918, to March, 1919; dist. and sessions judge, March, 1922; addl. judge, high ct., Allahabad, July, 1933, and July, 1934; judge, ditto, July, 1935.

COLLISTER, JOHN ARTHUR, V.D., late Indian State Rlys. (b. 8th May, 1882).—Joined the service as asst. dist. loco. supt., 8th Dec., 1905; dist. ditto, July, 1919; supt., Feb., 1926; offg. dep. ch. operating supt., May, 1929, and again, Nov., 1932; ret'd., Jan., 1934.

COLQUHOUN, SAMUEL, Survey of India (manager, litho. bch.) (b. 23rd Aug., 1883).—Joined the service, 18th Aug., 1905; manager, litho. bch., Nov., 1930.

COLSON, LIONEL HEWITT, C.I.E., Indian Police (commr. of police, Calcutta) (b. 24th May, 1887).—Joined the service as proby. asst. supt., 5th Dec., 1906; asst. supt., April, 1908; supt., Aug., 1913; awarded King's police medal, 1916; offg. dep. inspr.-genl., March, 1928; offg. commr. of police, Calcutta, June, 1929; confd., Dec., 1931; dep. inspr.-genl., Apl., 1930; C.I.E., Jan., 1934.

COLTHUP, SIDNEY ROBERT, India Office (hr. executive officer, acctnt.-gen.'s dept.) (b. 11th Feb., 1893).—Educ. at Rochester Math. Sch.; served as sec. div. clerk, Local Govt. Bd., from Feb. to Sept., 1912; apptd. second class clerk, India Office, 23rd Sept., 1912, and att'd. to acctnt.-gen.'s dept.; on mil. service from Aug., 1915, to Feb., 1919; exec. officer, April, 1922; hr. exec. officer, Sept., 1932; assessor of income tax, from Oct., 1935, to Dec., 1937.

COLVILLE, WILLIAM, Indian Police (b. 12th April, 1905).—Joined the service as asst. supt., United Provs., 8th Dec., 1924; offg. supt., Mar., 1928; and again, Nov., 1933; services placed at disposal of govt. of India, Home dept., Apl., 1932; pers. asst. to dir., intelligence bureau, Mar., 1933; asst. to D.I.G., C.I.D., Apl., 1936, to Apl., 1937.

COLVIN, SIR CLEMENT PRESTON, Kt., O.B.E., V.D., late Indian State Rlys. (b. 12th July, 1879).—Joined the service, 21st March, 1900; asst. traffic supt., April, 1903; dist. ditto, Jan., 1905; O.B.E., Dec., 1919; dep. traffic manager, May, 1921; offg. dir. of traffic, rly. bd., Nov., 1928; confd., Apl., 1931; offg. member, rly. bd., Apl., 1931, May, 1932; and again, Nov., 1932; confd., Jan., 1933; knighted, June, 1935; ret'd., Aug., 1936.

COLVIN, SIR ELLIOT GRAHAM, K.C.S.I., late Indian C.S.—Educ. at Charterhouse, and King's Coll., Cambridge; apptd. after exam. of 1880; arrived, 4th Jan., 1883, and served in Bengal as asst. mag. and collr.; transf. to Assam as personal asst. to chief commr., May, 1884; asst. pol. agent, Rajputana, Jan., 1885; private sec. to lieut.-govr. of Bengal, April, 1887; 1st asst. to govr.-gen.'s agent in Baluchistan, April, 1889; offg. postmr.-gen., Bengal, July,

1890; joint mag. and dep. collr., May, 1891; mag. and collr., March, 1895; pol. agent, Ulwar, Nov., 1896; pol. agent, Eastern Rajputana States, Sept., 1897; rev. and jud. commr., Baluchistan, April, 1900; resident in Kashmir, March, 1903; agent to govr.-genl., Rajputana, and chief commr., Ajmer-Merwara, from Oct., 1905; C.S.I., June, 1906; K.C.S.I., Dec., 1911; transf. to Bihar and Orissa cadre, April, 1912; ret'd., Jan., 1918.

COLVIN, ELLIOT JAMES DOWELL, C.I.E., Lt.-Col., I.A., Indian Political Service (b. 27th July, 1885).—First commn., 9th Jan., 1904; in mil. employ to 11th July, 1908, when he was apptd. personal asst. to resdt., Hyderabad; pol. officer on probation, 12th Feb., 1912; appt. to pol. dept. confirmed, July, 1914; agent to govr.-gen. in Central India, June, 1915; pol. agent, Baghelkhand, March, 1921; under-sec. to agent to govr.-gen. in Central India, April, 1922; on foreign service as adviser to H.H. the Maharaja of Rewa, Feb., 1924; offg. resdt. Gwalior, Apl., 1930; offg. pol. agent, E.R. States, April, 1930; confd., Sept., 1930; prime minister, Kashmir state, Mar., 1932, and again, Mar., 1935; C.I.E., June, 1933.

COLVIN, SIR GEORGE LETHBRIDGE, Kt., C.B., C.M.G., D.S.O., V.D., late Indian State Rlys. (b. 1st April, 1878).—Agent, E.I. Rly., 30th Sept., 1921 to Mar., 1933; C.M.G., 1918; C.B., 1919; Kt., Jan., 1933.

COLYER, CECIL ALFRED, A.M.I.C.E., M.I.E. (Ind.), late Indian Service of Engrs. (b. 29th April, 1879).—Joined the service, 30th Jan., 1904; exec. engr., Feb., 1916; offg. suptg. engr., June, 1928; ret'd., Apl., 1934.

COLYER, JOHN ALFRED, Indian State Rlys. (divl. mech. engr., N.W. Rly.) (b. 23rd Oct., 1884).—Asst. works manager, junr. scale, 28th, July, 1927; ditto, senr. scale (provl.), July, 1937.

COMMISSARIAT, MANEKSHAH SORABSHAH, M.A., late Indian Educl. Service (b. 11th Dec., 1881).—Apptd. prof. of history and pol. economy, Gujarat Coll., Ahmedabad, Feb., 1921; offg. prim., ditto, June, 1933; ret'd., Dec., 1936.

COMRIE, MARTIN CAMERON, M.A., late Indian C.S. (b. 6 Nov., 1886).—Educ. at George Watson's Coll., Edinburgh, Edinburgh Univ., and Christ Church Coll., Oxford; apptd. after exam. of 1910; arrived in India, 18th Nov., 1911, and served in the Central Provs. as asst. commr.; dep. commr. (provl.), July, 1922; confd., Nov. 1924; ret'd., Oct., 1937.

CONDER, JOHN MARMADUKE, late Indian C.S. (Burma) (b. 10th May, 1889).—Educ. at Windermere Grammar School, and at St. John's Coll., Cambridge; apptd. after exam. of 1912; arrived 10th Jan., 1914, and served in Burma as asst. commr.; on mil. service, Jan., 1918, to Jan., 1920; offg. dist. and sess. judge, Dec., 1922; confd., Dec., 1924; ret'd., Nov., 1931.

CONDER, STANLEY JOHN, India Office (higher executive officer, acctnt.-gen.'s dept.)
