(b. 28th June, 1892).—Apptd. second class clerk, India Office, 10th June, 1913, and att'd. to acctnt.-gen.'s dept.; on mil. service from July, 1915, to Dec., 1918; exec. officer, April, 1922; higher exec. officer, March, 1927; assessor of income tax, Oct., 1936.

CONDON, CHARLES WILLIAM, M.B.E., Indian Police (supt., Bombay) (b. 22nd Feb., 1886).—Joined the service 8th April, 1910; King's Police Medal, Jan., 1925; promoted to I.P. as supt., April, 1936.

CONES, HERBERT ALFRED MICHAEL, late Indian Police (b. 11th April, 1888).—Joined the service as asst. supt., on 7th Dec., 1908; supt., Jan., 1923; ret'd., Dec., 1933.

CONLIN, JOHN FRANCIS, M.C., Indian Police (supt., Ajmer-Merwara) (b. 30th Oct., 1895).—Joined the service as asst. supt., United Provs., 14th Oct., 1923; offg. supt., May, 1926, and again, Nov., 1932; asst. commr. in Andamans, Oct., 1926; supt., May, 1936; ditto, Ajmer-Merwara, Oct., 1936.

CONNAUGHT and STRATHEARN (DUKE OF), H.R.H. ARTHUR WILLIAM PATRICK ALBERT, K.G., K.T., K.P., P.C., G.C.B., G.C.S.I., G.C.M.G., G.C.I.E., G.C.V.O., G.B.E., D.C.L., L.L.D., Field-Marshal.—First commn., 19th June, 1868; asst. adjutant-gen., Gibraltar, Oct., 1875; brigadier-gen., Aldershot, Sept., 1880; accompanied the expedny. force to Egypt, Aug., 1882 (medal with clasp, 2nd class Medjidie, and C.B.); commander-in-chief, and 2nd member of council, Bombay, Dec., 1886, to March, 1890; on return to England was apptd. to command of southern divn., Aug., 1890; commanding at Aldershot, Oct., 1893; commanding the forces in Ireland, Jan., 1900; and the third Army Corps, Oct., 1901; presdt. of Council of R. Patriotic Fund Corpn., 1903; inspr.-genl. of the forces, May, 1904; field-marshal commanding-in-chief, and high commr. in the Mediterranean, 1907, to July, 1909; govr.-gen. and C.-in-C., domn. of Canada, 1911-16.

CONNELL, CECIL BOURKE, A.M.I.C.E., late Indian Service of Engrs. (Madras) (b. 13th Feb., 1886).—Joined the service, 1st Jan., 1907, as asst. engr.; exec. engr., Jan., 1915; ret'd., Jan., 1930.

CONNELL, EDMOND COLEMAN, late Indian State Rlys. (E.B. Rly.) (b. 11th Nov., 1876).—Joined the service, 1st July, 1896; storekeeper, March, 1916; dist. contr. of stores, July, 1918; contr. of stores, C.I.C. Rly. construction, Aug., 1924; offg. contr. of stores, Feb., 1926; confid., Apl., 1929; ret'd., Nov., 1931.

CONNOLLY, JOHN STANLAY D'ARCY, late Indian Forest Service (Madras) (b. 19th Jan., 1895).—Joined the service, 11th Dec., 1921, as asst. consvr.; dep. consvr., Dec., 1922; ret'd., Feb., 1930.

CONNOLLY, VINCENT, B.A., late Indian C.S. (Punjab) (b. 17th Oct., 1878).—Educ. at Belvedere Coll., Dublin, Trinity Coll., Dublin, and Balliol Coll., Oxford; apptd. after exam. of 1901; arrived, 2nd Dec., 1902, and served in the Punjab as asst. commr.; addl. dist. mag., Delhi, Nov., 1913; senr. sec. to finl. commrs., Punjab, March, 1917; dep. commr., Dec., 1917; sec., home dept., Punjab, April, 1921; offg. commr., April, 1927; ret'd., Sept., 1930.

CONNOR, FRANK ARTHUR, late Punjab Civil Service (b. 17th Nov., 1877).—Joined the service 21st June, 1896; extra asst. commr., Nov., 1910; offg. dep. commr., Mar., 1928; confid., May, 1931; ret'd., Nov., 1932.

CONNOR, Sir FRANK POWELL, Kt., D.S.O., F.R.C.S., Major-Gen., I.M.S. (surg.-genl. with the Govt. of Madras.) (b. 16th Sept., 1877).—Joined the service, 1st Sept., 1902; arrived, 5th March, 1904; on milty. duty to Nov., 1906, and Aug., 1914, to May, 1919; profir. of clinical and operative surgery and surgeon to med. coll. hosp., Calcutta, March, 1920; Kt., Jan., 1926; surg.-genl. with the Govt. of Madras, Nov., 1933; offg. dir.-genl., I.M.S., Oct., 1935; presdt., medl. council of India, Oct., 1935.

CONNOR, GEORGE POWELL, late Indian Service of Engrs. (b. 12th Oct., 1884).—Trained at R.I.E. Coll.; joined the service as asst. engr. on 1st Oct., 1906; on milty. service from 15th Nov., 1917, to 29th Jan., 1920; asst. exec. engr., Feb., 1920; exec. engr., Dec., 1921; ret'd., July, 1934.

CONNOR, JAMES POWELL, late Land Records Dept., Burma (b. 13th Oct., 1875).—Joined the service 1st April, 1899; supt., March, 1901; addl. asst. dir., Dec., 1925; ret'd., Aug., 1930.

CONNOR, WILLIAM CASEY, B.Sc., Indian Police (supt., Punjab) (b. 29th Nov., 1884).—Joined the service as proby. dep. supt., 24th May, 1909; confid., May, 1910; offg. supt., Oct., 1911; confid., May, 1931.

CONSTABLE, ERNEST HENRY, India Office (higher clerical officer, acctnt.-gen.'s dept.) (b. 8th July, 1883).—Apptd. clerical officer, 1st March, 1922, after previous temporary service from Feb., 1920; higher clerical officer, 1st October, 1925.

CONSTANTINE, GEORGE BAXANDALL, B.A., Indian C.S. (judge and sess. judge, Bombay) (b. 22nd June, 1902).—Educ. at Bradford Grammar School, and Balliol Coll., Oxford; joined the service, 25th Oct., 1926; arrived, Dec., 1926, and served in Bombay as asst. collr.; asst. judge and asst. sess. judge, Feb., 1932; dist. and sess. judge, Aden, Feb., 1936; judge and sess. judge, Aug., 1936.

CONTRACTOR, KHAN BAHADUR SHERLARIJ DADABHAI, B.A., late Bombay Educl. Service (b. 5th Sept., 1878).—Joined the service 19th Nov., 1903; educ. inspr., Sind, Feb., 1922; asst. dir. of public instn., Bombay educ. service, Jan., 1931; ret'd., Jan., 1936.

CONTRACTOR, SORAB CURSHEDJI, M.B., Lt.-Col. I.M.S., Jail dept., Madras (supt.) (b. 25th Jan., 1887).—First commn. dated 1st Nov., 1920; in milty. employ. to 25th Sept., 1925; offg. supt., Jan., 1926; confid., June, 1928.

COODE, JOHN, late Indian Forest Service (b. 7th Jan., 1900).—Joined the service, 5th Dec., 1924; dep. consvr., Madras, May, 1931; ret'd., June, 1932.

COOK, BERNARD CHRISTOPHER ALLEN, Indian C.S. (jt. mag., United Provinces) (b. 20th July, 1906).—Educ. at Radley, and Brasenose Coll., Oxford; joined the service, 15th Oct., 1929; arrived, 2nd Dec., 1929, and served in the United Provinces as asst. mag. and coll.; under secty. to govt., U.P., genl. bch. (tempy.), Apl., 1934; offg. mag. and collr., Feb., 1935.

COOK, Sir EDWARD MITCHENER, Kt., C.S.I., C.I.E., B.A., late Indian C.S.—Educ. at Uppingham Sch., and Clare Coll., Cambridge; apptd.

after exam. of 1903; arrived, 28th Nov., 1904, and served in the United Prov. as asst. mag. and collr., and asst. commr.; under sec. to govt., Nov., 1909; under sec. to govt. of India, home dept., Oct., 1911; under sec. to govt. of India, fin. dept., Jan., 1912; temp. employed in finl. dept., India Office, June to Nov., 1915; joined finance dept., govt. of India, as dep. acct.-gen., Bengal, Jan., 1916; addl. dep. sec. to govt. of India, fin. dept., June, 1916; addl. dep. acct.-gen., Bombay, July, 1916; offg. controller of currency, May, 1917; acct.-gen., Bombay, Nov., 1917; offg. controller of currency, Calcutta, Sept., 1918; C.I.E., Jan., 1919; sec. to govt. of India, fin. dept., June, 1919; C.S.I., Jan., 1923; Kt., June, 1923; sec. to high commr. for India, 29th Nov., 1923, to 7th Sept., 1924; apptd. finl. adviser to govt. of Siam, 1925; ret'd., June, 1926.

COOK, HENRY PATULLO, M.B., F.R.C.S., Lt.-Col., I.M.S. (ret'd.) (b. 13th Nov., 1882).—First commn., dated 1st Feb., 1906; in mil. employ to 11th Sept., 1913, and again from 9th Aug., 1914, to 30th Sept., 1918; civil surg. Oct. 1918; confd., April, 1921; ret'd., Feb., 1933.

COOK, JACK MERVYN GERARD, Indian Police (asst. supt.) (b. 22nd Mar., 1910).—Apptd. proby. asst. supt., Bengal, 24th Nov., 1930; confd., Jan., 1933.

COOK, JAMES HIGH ERNEST, Indian Posts and Telegraphs (dep. P.M.G.) (b. 8th June, 1896).—Joined service as proby. supt., 26th April, 1907; confd., Jan., 1908; on depn. to I.E.F., Aug., 1914, to May, 1915; dep. P.M.G., July, 1932.

COOK, LEWIS, C.I.E., M.B., B.S., F.R.C.S., Col., I.M.S., ret'd., late Medical Dept., Bihar and Orissa (b. 24th Oct., 1876).—First commission, 26th July, 1902; in mil. employ till Aug., 1906, when he was apptd. offg. civil surgeon; on mil. service, Aug., 1914, to Jan., 1921; civil surgeon, Jan., 1921; C.I.E., June, 1928; offg. inspr. genl., civil hosp., Bihar and Orissa, May, 1929; again Dec., 1930; confd., Aug., 1931; ret'd., Oct., 1933.

COOK, WILLIAM LOUIS CHARLES GERARD, Indian Police (addl. supt., Bengal) (b. 21st Feb., 1904).—Joined the service as asst. supt., Bengal, on 13th Nov., 1923; offg. addl. supt., Apl., 1929; confd., Feb., 1936.

COOKE, CHRISTOPHER HERBERT, B.A., Indian C.S. (joint mag., United Provs.) (b. 29th May, 1899).—Educ. at Clifton Coll. and Christ Church Coll., Oxford; joined the service, 31st Oct., 1922; arrived, 14th Dec., 1922, and served in United Prov. as asst. mag. and collr.; joint mag., March, 1925; offg. mag. and collr., Dec., 1925; offg. dep. commr., March, 1927; offg. dep. secty. to govt., United Provs. Jan. 1934; offg. secty., bd. of rev., United Provs., Oct., 1934; settlement officer., Oct., 1936.

COOKE, EDWARD BARNARD, late Indian State Ryls. (b. 17th Nov., 1877).—Joined the service, 7th Feb., 1903; asst. traffic supt., Nov., 1904; dist. ditto, Jan., 1911; divl. traffic manager, Nov., 1922; ret'd., Nov., 1934.

COOKE, GEORGE HENRY, M.C., B.A., Indian C.S. (sub-collr., Madras) (b. 24th June, 1895).—Educ. at Trinity Coll., Dublin; joined the service 26th Oct., 1920; arrived

3rd Dec., 1920, and served in Madras as asst. collr. and mag.; sub-collr. and jt. mag., July, 1922; offg. under sec. to govt., dev. dept., June, 1924; offg. collr. and dist. magte., March, 1928; collr., C. & M Station, Bangalore Oct., 1928; addl. jt. regr. of co-op. soc. (temp.), Nov., 1935.

COOKE, GEORGE HUMPHREY, Capt., I.A., Indian Political Service (b. 23rd Jan., 1903).—First commn., 1st Feb., 1923; in milty. employ to 19th Jan., 1930; asst. commr., Kohat, Oct., 1931; senr. sub-judge, Hazara, Nov., 1933; under-secty. to govt., N.W.F.P., Oct., 1934; asst. pol. agent, Gilgit, Oct., 1935.

COOKE, ROBERT FRANCIS RICKARD, V.D., late Indian State Ryls. (G.I.P. Rly.) (b. 27th Dec., 1874).—Joined the service, 2nd Oct., 1897; asst. traffic supt., July, 1898; dist. ditto, Jan., 1906; asst. transportation supt., Nov., 1922; ret'd., Sept., 1931.

COOKE, THOMAS FOTHERGILL, O.B.E., late Indian Police (b. 15th May, 1889).—Joined the service, 1st Dec., 1909; asst. supt., April, 1911; O.B.E., Jan., 1919; supt., Sept., 1921; ret'd., June, 1937.

COOKSEY, WILFRID, Indian State Ryls. (colliery manager, E.I. Rly.) (b. 9th Dec., 1882).—Joined the service as asst. colliery manager, 1st Jan., 1913; colliery manager, April, 1921; offg. and asst. colliery supt., Mar., 1932.

COOKSON, DOUGLAS MACGREGOR, late Indian State Ryls. (b. 20th Nov., 1882).—Joined the service as asst. engr., 1st Oct., 1905; exec. engr., Oct., 1915; services lent to Jaipur Durbar as suptg. engr. from Dec., 1913, to Dec., 1915, and again from Nov., 1922, to Aug., 1924; offg. suptg. engr., Oct., 1924; offg. dep. ch. engr., Aug., 1926; ret'd., Feb., 1931.

COOMBS, ERNEST EDWIN, O.B.E., late Printing and Stationery Dept. (b. 17th Dec., 1875).—Joined the service as supt., govt. central press., 1st Nov., 1902; supt., govt. printing, Bombay, July, 1914; O.B.E., June, 1925; contr., printing, stationery and stamps, July, 1925; ret'd., Dec., 1930.

COOMBS, GEORGE OSWALD, Indian Forest Service (United Provs.) (b. 16th Nov., 1876).—Held non-gazetted appts. from 29th May, 1898; extra asst. consvr., May, 1905; awarded Kaisar-i-Hind medal, 2nd Class, June, 1915; dep. consvr., Mar., 1920; ret'd., Nov., 1931.

COOPER, ALLAN, late Indian State Railways (b. 12th Dec., 1882).—Joined the service, Oct., 1903; dist. traffic supt., April, 1914; divl. comml. officer, Nov., 1928; offg. dep. ch. operatg. supt., Apl., 1929; ditto (provl.), Sept., 1929; confd., Aug., 1933; dep. ch. comml. manager, Oct., 1932; offg. ch. ditto, Nov., 1934; ret'd., Dec., 1937.

COOPER, CHARLES ROBERT PLANT, B.A., Indian C.S. (commr., Burma) (b. 26th April, 1882).—Educ. at Bath Coll., and Caius Coll., Cambridge; apptd. after exam. of 1905; arrived, 26th Nov., 1906, and served in Burma as asst. commr.; asst. sec. to finl. commr., July, 1913, to March, 1914; on spl. duty in Co-operative Credit Societies Dept.; dep. commr., Dec., 1917; on mil. duty, June, 1918, to Feb., 1919; offg. regr., co-operative societies, Burma, Oct., 1919; also dir. of agric. from May to Sept., 1920; dep. commr., jt. regr. co-op. soc., Dec., 1921; offg. sec. to govt. (agri.,

excise and forest dept.), Sept., 1924; confd. Oct., 1925; secty. to govt., rev. dept., May, 1927; offg. commr., Aug., 1930; confd. Jan., 1934.

COOPER, Sir DHANJISHAH BOMANJEE, Khan Bahadur.—Appd. member, govr's. exec. council, Bombay, 23rd June, 1934; knighted, Feb., 1937.

COOPER, DHANJISHAH DOSABHAI, B.A., LL.B., late Bombay C.S., Judl. Branch (b. 19th Sept., 1874).—Joined the service, 15th June, 1904, as sub-judge; asst. judge and addl. sess. judge, Nov., 1924; offg. judge and sess. judge, Sept., 1927; retd., Mar., 1930.

COOPER, HAROLD HINTON, Indian State Rlys. (divl. mechl. engr., N.W. Rly.) (b. 20th May, 1891).—Joined the service as asst. loco. supt., 13th Dec., 1914; offg. dist. loco. supt. Oct., 1919; offg. works manager, loco. and carr., Nov., 1926; pers. asst. to ch. mechl. engr., Feb., 1927; works manager, loco., Sept., 1927; offg. supt., mechl. workshops, Aug., 1932; and again, Sept., 1935; offg. dep. ch. mechl. engr., Oct., 1936; and again, Mar., 1937.

COOPER, HARRY LYSTER, late Indian Forest Service (b. 4th Aug., 1883).—Joined the service, 7th Nov., 1906, as asst. consr., Assam; dep. consr., Nov., 1915; served in United Provs., from April, 1924, to March, 1925; offg. consr., Punjab, Oct., 1925; ditto, United Provs., Nov., 1927; confd., Oct., 1928; retd., Oct., 1933.

COOPER, JOHN WALTER, Office of High Commr. for India (principal exec. officer, store dept.) (b. 22nd Dec., 1879).—Appd. 2nd class clerk, India Store Depot, 9th June, 1888; supervisor, ditto, Aug., 1919; transf. to office of high commr. for India, Oct., 1920; higher exec. officer, April, 1922; principal ditto (temp.), April, 1927; confd., Apl., 1929.

COOPER, ROBERT FRANCIS, M.A. (inspr. schools) Indian Educl. Service (b. 12th May, 1886).—Joined the service as prinl., G.B.B. Coll. Muzaffarpur, on 1st July, 1915; hd. master, Ranchi Zila School, Aug., 1923; inspr. of schools, Oct., 1927; profir., Patna training coll., Oct., 1932; headmaster, Zila sch., Ranchi, Mar., 1933; inspr. of schools, Chota Nagpur div., Nov., 1935.

COOREY, CLARENCE OSWOLD, B.A., B.Sc., Indian C.S. (sub. collr., Madras) (b. 28th Feb., 1910).—Educ. at Royal Coll., Colombo, Univ. of Ceylon and Emmanuel Coll., Cambridge; joined the service, 9th Oct., 1934; arrived in India, Nov., 1934, and served in Madras as asst. mag. and collr.; sub. collr., July, 1936.

COPELAND, THEODORE BENFEY, C.I.E., M.A., late Indian C.S. (b. 7th Oct., 1878).—Educ. at Watson's Coll., Edinburgh, and Edinburgh Univ.; apptd. after exam. of 1900; arrived, 26th Nov., 1901, and served in the Punjab and N.W. Frontier Prov. as asst. commr.; cmdt., border mounted police, Oct., 1905; pol. asst., Feb., 1906; asst. sec. to chief commr., July, 1907; dist. judge, Nov., 1907, to July, 1908; sec. to chief commr., Coorg, Oct., 1908; collr. of Bangalore, Jan., 1910; dep. commr., Dera Ismail Khan, April, 1911; ditto, Bannu, Nov., 1911; dist. judge, Peshawar, March, 1912; dep. commr., Dehra Ismail Khan, April, 1912; pol. agent, Wana, April, 1914; K.-i.-H. medal, 1st class, Jan., 1916; dep.

commr., D.I. Khan, Nov., 1917; ditto, Kohat, Feb., 1918; divl. and sess. judge, Peshawar, Jan., 1919; judl. commr., N.W. Frontier Prov., May, 1919; divl. and sess. judge, ditto, June, 1919; ditto, and under-secy. to commr., N.W.F. Prov., June, 1923; offg. rev. commr., N.W.F. Prov., Dec., 1924; confd., May, 1927; C.I.E., June, 1928; retd., Sept., 1929.

COPPINGER, WALTER VALENTINE, C.I.E., D.S.O., M.D., B.Sc., F.R.C.S.I. (Dublin), Maj.-Genl., I.M.S., retd. (b. 1st Mar., 1875).—Joined the Service, 28th June, 1900; on mil. employ. to May, 1903; offg. dep. sanitary commr., Calcutta, Aug., 1903; offg. civil surg., March, 1904; resdt. surgn., med. coll. hosp., Manbuhm, May 1908; resdt. surgn., med. coll., Calcutta, March, 1913; profir. med. coll., Calcutta, April, 1914; services placed at disposal of govt. of Burma, Oct., 1918, to Feb., 1919; inspr.-genl., civil hosps., Cent. Provs., Mar., 1929; C.I.E., Jan., 1930; surg.-genl. with govt. of Bengal, Nov., 1930; officer of Order of St. John of Jerusalem, Dec., 1931; retd., Nov., 1933.

CORBAN-LUCAS, ARTHUR JOHN LIONEL, late Indian Police (b. 23rd Aug., 1887).—Joined the service as asst. supt. on 21st Nov., 1908; dist. supt., Dec., 1917; offg. dep. commr., Rangoon, Sept., 1926; offg. dep. inspr.-genl., Apl., 1932; confd., June, 1934; retd., Dec., 1936.

CORFIELD, CONRAD LAURENCE, C.I.E., M.C., M.A. (Cantab.), Indian C.S. (Indian political service) (b. 15th Aug., 1893).—Educ. at St. Catherine's Coll., Cambridge; joined the service, 16th Feb., 1920, and served in the Punjab as asst. commr. from 5th May, 1920; asst. pte. sec. to the Viceroy, May, 1921, to Dec., 1922; sec. to the agt. to the govr.-genl. in the states of Western India, Jan., 1925; asst. to pol. agent, Kalat, Nov., 1926; secty. to agent to govr.-genl., Rajputana, Nov., 1928; pol. agent in Southern States of C.I. and in Malwa, June, 1931; secty. to resdt., Hyderabad, Oct., 1931; vice-presdt., Rewa State council, Mar., 1932; adviser to delegate for Rewa, Indian round-table conf., 3rd session, Nov.-Dec., 1932; jt. secty. to govt. of India, for. and pol. dept., June, 1934; offg. addl. ditto, Apl., 1935; confd., Aug., 1936; jt. secty., F. and P. Dept., Apl., 1935; C.I.E., Feb., 1937; jt. secty., Pol. dept., Apl., 1937; offg. secty. to H.E. the Crown Representative, May, 1937; jt. secty., Pol. dept., Oct., 1937.

CORFIELD, WILLIAM FRANCIS, Office of the High Commissioner for India (hr. clerical officer, accs. dept.) (b. 24th April, 1882).—Appd. clerical officer, 1st June, 1923; actg. hr. ditto, April, 1927; confd., Mar., 1936.

CORIN, JOHN MADAN, B.A., Indian C.S. (asst. collr., Bombay) (b. 9th May, 1907).—Educ. at St. Paul's, and Magdalene College, Cambridge; joined the service, 10th Oct., 1930, arrived in India, Nov., 1930, and served in Bombay as asst. collr.; offg. under-secty., home, pol. etc. depts., Bombay, Aug., 1935; passport officer, Bombay, Nov., 1936; secty. to govr., Sind.

CORMACK, FREDERICK EUSON, B.Sc., Indian Service of Engrs. (exec. engr., Assam) (b. 18th Jan., 1892).—Joined the service, Oct., 1913; asst. engr., Feb., 1914; I.A.R.O. from 27th June, 1916, to 7th June, 1919; exec. engr. Oct., 1922.

CORMACK, HENRY SLATER, M.C., M.B., F.R.C.S.E., Lt.-Col., I.M.S., Burma Medl. Dept., (civil surg.). (b. 6th May, 1886).—First commn. dated 28th Jan., 1911; in mil. employ to 6th Nov., 1922; offg. ophthalmic surg., Rangoon, Nov., 1922; March, 1926, and again, May, 1929; civil surg., Nov., 1923.

CORNELIUS, ALVIN ROBERT, Indian C.S. (dist. and sess. judge, Punjab) (b. 1st May, 1903).—Educ. at Allahabad Univ., and Selwyn Coll., Cambridge; joined the service, 22nd Oct., 1926; arrived, Nov., 1926, and served in the Punjab as asst. commr.; offg. dist. and sess. judge, Feb., and Oct., 1932; and again, May, 1933; confd., Nov., 1936.

CORNELIUS, EUSTACE HERBERT, late Indian Service of Engrs. (b. 28th May, 1879).—Trained at the R.I.E. Coll.; joined the service as asst. engr.; 1st Oct., 1902, and posted to United Provs.; exec. engr., Oct., 1912; offg. suptg. engr., May, 1922; confd. Oct., 1926; ret'd., May 1934.

CORNFORTH, ERNEST, late Indian Finance Dept. (b. 4th May, 1931).—Joined the service, 29th Mar., 1909; asst. auditor, N.W. Rly., June, 1918; ditto, E.B. Rly., Jan., 1923; govt. examr. of accts., Burma Rlys., April, 1924; dep. auditor, N.W. Rly., March, 1927; govt. examr., B.B. & C.I. Rly., Apl., 1929; ret'd., Aug., 1934.

CORNISH, ATHELSTAN, Indian State Rlys. (dep. ch. mechl. engr., N.W. Rly.) (b. 22nd July, 1888).—Joined the service as asst. loco. supt., 7th Oct., 1913; divl. rolling stock officer, Oct., 1926; offg. dep. ch. mechl. engr., N.W. rly., Mar., 1935; and again, Mar., 1936; offg. dep. ch. opertg. supt., N.W. rly., Nov., 1935; dep. ch. mechl. engr. (provl. perm.), Feb., 1937.

CORNISH, HENRY DAUNCEY, Barr., late Judl. Dept., Madras (b. 7th Aug., 1877).—Apptd. admin. genl. and offl. trustee, Madras, Jan., 1921; actg. judge, high cr., July, 1927, and again, July, 1929; confd., Jan., 1930; ret'd., Aug., 1937.

CORNWELL, RICHARD BARRY, Indian Forest Service (dep. consvr., Madras) (b. 20th Feb., 1890).—Joined the service, 5th Jan., 1909; dep. consvr., Oct., 1922; on deputation to Forest Research Institute, Sept., 1924; dist. forest officer, Oct., 1933.

CORRY, MATTHEW, M.D., C.M., Lieut.-Col., I.M.S. (ret'd.).—First commission, 27th Jan., 1900; in mil. employ till May, 1902; offg. civil surgeon, Punjab, June, 1902; dist. plague medical officer, Sept., 1902; civil surgeon, July, 1910; on mil. duty, May, 1917, to Aug., 1920; lecturer on midwifery and forensic medicine, medical sch., Amritsar, Oct., 1920; ret'd., Nov., 1928.

CORY, EDGAR MYRIE, A.M.I.C.E., Indian State Rlys. (dep. ch. engr., G.I.P. Rly.) (b. 6th Jan., 1885).—Joined the service as asst. engr., 14th Sept., 1906; resdt. engr., Sept., 1912; in milty. employ from 22nd May, 1916, to 12th Feb., 1920; dist. engr., Nov., 1925; offg. divl. transpn. supt., Nov., 1930; dep. ch. engr., Sept., 1933; offg. ch. engr., variously from May, 1936.

COSGRAVE, WILLIAM ALEXANDER, C.I.E., B.A., Indian C.S. (Commr., Assam) (b. 6th April, 1879).—Educ. at Shrewsbury Sch.

and Trinity Coll., Dublin; apptd. after exam. of 1902; arrived, 30th Nov., 1903, and served in Bengal as asst. mag. and collr.; transfld. to E. Bengal and Assam, Oct., 1905; vice-president, Manipur Darbar, May, 1907, to March, 1910; jt. mag. and dep. collr., Nov., 1910; asst. commr., Assam, April, 1912; dep. commr., May, 1917; ditto, and pol. agent, Manipur, Dec., 1917, to March, 1920; offg. ch. secy. to govt. of Assam, April, 1930; confd., Oct. 1930; C.I.E., Jan., 1931; member, legisl. council, Assam, Nov., 1932; commr., Dec., 1933; offg. member, public service commn., Apl., 1934; ch. commr., Andaman and Nicobar Islands, Feb., 1935, to Feb., 1938.

COSTELLO, JOHN FRANCIS, M.A. late Indian State Rlys. (b. 28th Mar., 1879).—Joined the service as asst. engr. (temp.), 28th May, 1909; resdt. engr., July, 1914; dist. engr., July, 1925; offg. principal, Bina Transportation School, Nov., 1926, and again, Dec., 1929; offg. dep. agent, May, 1929; Apl., 1930, and again May, 1931; confd. (provl.), July, 1932; offg. dep. ch. engr., Oct., 1932; ret'd., Mar., 1936.

COSTELLO, SIR LEONARD WILFRED JAMES, Kt., M.A., LL.B., Barr. (b. 25th Aug., 1881).—Apptd. judge, high ct., Calcutta, 30th Dec., 1926; knighted, June, 1935.

COSTER, GILBERT WALLACE, O.B.E. (b. 20th Apl., 1880).—Held non-gazetted appts. from 21st May, 1909; asst. contr. of printing, staty. and stamps, Calcutta, Sept., 1919; dep. ditto, May, 1924; dep. contr., central stamp stores, Bombay, Feb., 1928; dep. contr. of stamps, Apl., 1928; ret'd. Apl., 1935; O.B.E., June, 1935.

COTTAM, WILLIAM JOHN, Indian Police (b. 23rd Mar., 1904).—Joined the service as asst. supt., Bengal, on 5th Dec., 1924; addl. supt., June, 1930; offg. supt., Mar., 1931.

COTTER, EDWARD, M.B., B.Ch., D.P.H., Lt.-Col., I.M.S. (dep. public health commr. with the Govt. of India) (b. 6th Nov., 1892).—First commn., dated 8th Dec., 1915; in mily. employ prior to 1st July, 1930; asst. dir. pub. health, Burma, July, 1930; offg. dir., May, 1933; dep. public health commr. with the govt. of India, July, 1937.

COTTER, GERALD DE PURCELL, B.A., D.Sc., F.G.S., late Geological Survey of India (b. 8th Apl., 1881).—Joined the service 15th April, 1905, as asst. supt.; supt., Jan., 1920; ret'd., Aug., 1933.

COTTERELL, CECIL BERNARD, C.S.I., C.I.E., late Indian C.S. (b. 27th Dec., 1875).—Educ. at St. Peter's Sch., York, and Balliol Coll., Oxford; apptd. after exam. of 1898; arrived, 16th Nov., 1899, and served in Madras as asst. collr. and agent to govr., Vizagapatam; dep. commr., salt, abkari, and customs dept., Feb., 1906; sub-collr. and joint mag., May, 1910; pte. sec. to govr., Madras, Jan., 1912, to Feb., 1915; C.I.E., June, 1915; collr. and dist. mag., Dec., 1917; sec. to govt., local self-govt. dept., March, 1925; offg. third member, bd. of rev., Apr., 1928; offg. 2nd member, ditto, June, 1929; offg. ch. secty. to govt., Madras, May, 1930; 2nd member, bd. of revenue, Madras, Oct., 1930; 1st member, ditto, Sept., 1932; C.S.I., June, 1933; ret'd., Aug., 1933.

COTTON, Rev. BEN, M.A., late Indian Eccles. Estabt. (United Provs.) (b. 13th June, 1878).—Apptd. chaplain on probation, 15th Feb., 1910; junr. chaplain, Feb., 1913; senr. chaplain, Feb., 1923; ret'd., Oct., 1931.

COTTON, Rev. HENRY WILMOT STAPLETON (b. M.A., Indian Eccles. Estabt. (senr. chaplain) (b. 22nd Feb., 1894).—Apptd. chaplain on probation, 22nd Oct., 1925; junr. chaplain, Oct., 1927; offg. archdeacon of Lucknow, April, 1936; and again, May, 1937; senr. chaplain, Oct., 1937.

COTTON, WILLIAM BENSLEY, Barr. (1894), late Indian C.S. (United Provs.) (b. 7th Feb., 1872).—Educ. at Wellington Sch. and Wadham Coll., Oxford; apptd. after exam. of 1893; arrived, 12th Dec., 1894, and served in the N.W. Prov. and Oudh as asst. mag. and collr.; joint mag., Oct., 1903; dep. commr., Oct., 1909; mag. and collr., July, 1911; on mil. duty, March, 1915, to April, 1919; ret'd., Dec., 1929.

COUCHMAN, Sir HAROLD JOHN, D.S.O., M.C., Brig., R.E. (ret'd.), late Survey of India (b. 29th July, 1882).—First commn. dated 18th Aug., 1900; joined the Survey of India dept. as asst. supt. 30th Jan., 1906; temp. reverted to mily. duty, Oct., 1914, to Nov., 1919; dep. supt., Sept., 1916; temp. dep. master, security printing, India, April, 1926; offg. master, security printing, Feb., 1929; dir. (temp.) May, 1929; confd., Aug., 1929; offg. surveyor-genl., Apl., 1933; confd., Oct., 1933; knighted, May, 1937; ret'd., Sept., 1937.

COUESLANT, D. C., B.Sc. (Lond.), Industries Dept., Bihar (b. 23rd July, 1877).—Apptd. princpl. Bihar Coll. of engrg., 3rd Apl., 1928; offg. dir. of industries, Feb., 1931, and again, Apl., 1931.

COULSON, ARTHUR LENNOX, M.Sc., D.I.C., F.G.S., Geological Survey of India (supig. geologist) (b. 25th Jan., 1898).—Joined the service as asst. supt. on probn., 25th Nov., 1922; confd., Nov., 1924; offg. supt., Aug., 1930; confd., April, 1936.

COULTON, GORDON FRANCIS, late Indian Police Service.—Joined the service as asst. supt., United Provs., Nov., 1912; ret'd., Feb., 1929.

COUPER, Sir THOMAS, Kt., C.S.I., M.A., late Indian C.S. (b. 15th March, 1878).—Educ. at Fettes Coll. and Worcester Coll., Oxford; apptd. after exam. of 1901; arrived 1st Dec., 1902, and served in Burma as asst. commr. and settl. offr.; dep. dir., land records from Nov., 1911, to Oct., 1914; dep. commr., April, 1916; prov. subst. director of agric., April, 1917, to May, 1918; on mil. duty, May, 1918, to Jan., 1920; on spl. duty Ctee. Land rev. system from Dec., 1920; offg. commr. from March, 1923; offg. fin. commr., Oct., 1925; confd., May, 1928; temp. member of govr.'s exec. council, Aug., 1929; and again, Aug., 1930; confd., Oct., 1930; C.S.I., Jan., 1931; actg. govr. of Burma, Apl., 1935; knighted, June, 1935; ret'd., Dec., 1937.

COUPLAND, RICHARD ERNEST, late Indian Police Service (b. 1st Oct., 1875).—Educ. at New Coll., Oxford; joined the service, 25th Nov., 1926, and posted to United Provs.; supt. of police, July, 1906; empd. in Ajmer-Merwara from

March, 1907; supt., govt. rly. police, Rajputana, April, 1914; spl. duty under dir. criminal intell., India, May, 1917, to March, 1918; spl. duty, criminal investign., dept., United Provs., April, 1918; offg. dep. inspr.-gen., United Provs., March, 1923; confd., Aug., 1924; awarded King's police medal, Jan., 1928; ret'd., Oct., 1930.

COURTENAY, REGINALD HERBERT, late Indian C.S. (b. 12th Sept., 1885).—Educ. at Dover Coll., Emmanuel Coll., Cambridge, and University Coll., London; appointed after exam. of 1909; arrived in India, 15th Dec., 1910, and served in Madras as asst. collr. and mag.; under-sec. to govt., Nov., 1915; addl. under-sec. to govt. of India, home dept., Nov., 1917, to Oct., 1918; regr., high ct., Madras, March, 1919; offg. dist. and sess. judge, July, 1922; confd., Dec., 1925; ret'd., Sept., 1932.

COUSENS, HENRY JAMES MORRISON, B.Sc., A.M.I.C.E., Indian Service of Engrs. (supig. engr., Bombay) (b. 4th May, 1883).—Joined the service, 1st Oct., 1905, as asst. engr.; exec. engr., Oct., 1913; offg. suptg. engr., Oct., 1930; confd., Nov., 1934.

COUSINS, EDMUND RICHARD JOHN RATCLIFFE, B.A., Indian C.S. (magte. and collr., Bihar) (b. 22nd April, 1888).—Educ. at Westminster and Christ Church, Oxford; apptd. after exam. of 1910; arrived in India, 25th Nov., 1911, and served in Bengal as asst. mag. and collr.; transfed. to Bihar and Orissa, April, 1912; jt. mag. and dep. collr., Dec., 1918; offg. dep. commr., March, 1922; offg. mag. and collr., Oct., 1922; confd., July, 1925; dep. commr., Feb., 1926; offg. commr., Apl., 1934.

COUTTS, DUNCAN, M.B., Lt.-Col. I.M.S., ret'd., late Civil Med. Dept., Bihar (b. 7th Nov., 1882).—First commn. dated 30th July, 1906; in mily. employ up to 21st Nov., 1922; civil surg., Dec., 1922; princpl. medical coll., Patna, April, 1930; ret'd., Sept., 1936.

COVENTRY, BERNARD OKES, F.C.H., late Indian Forest Service.—Joined the service, 9th Dec., 1894, as asst. consr.; dep. consr., Feb., 1900; pol. asst. to supt., Hill States, Simla, April to July, 1906; impl. forest chemist, research institute, Oct., 1909, to Nov., 1910; personal asst. to consr. of forests, Punjab, March, 1911; offg. consvr., Berar, April, 1915; on deputn. as consvr. of forests, Jammu and Kashmir State, Oct., 1915; consr., Feb., 1910; ret'd., May, 1928.

COVENTRY, JOHN PHILIP CHETWYND, Indian Police (b. 14th Dec., 1917).—Apptd. asst. supt., Bombay, on probn., Dec., 1937.

COWAN, JOHN, M.B.E.—Joined the service as temporary asst. inspr. of factories, Bengal, March, 1909; inspr., July, 1912; offg. chf. inspr., April, 1914; M.B.E., Jan., 1919; offg. ch. inspr. of boilers, Feb., 1920; confd., Jan., 1924; ret'd., Mar., 1929.

COWARD, DUNCAN CECIL, Indian State Rlys. (dist. traffic supt.) (b. 16th Aug., 1894).—Joined the service as asst. traffic supt., 25th June, 1920; dist. ditto, Burma Rlys., Feb., 1932.

COWBURN, Rev. FRANK, B.A., Indian Eccl. Estabt. (junr. chaplain, Bombay) (b. 11th, May, 1896).—Apptd. chaplain on probation, 10th April, 1926; confd., Apl., 1928.

COWGILL, JOHN FELIX, O.B.E., Indian Police (supt., Bengal) (b. 11th June, 1903).—Joined the service as asst. supt., Bengal, on 25th Nov., 1923 ; pers. asst. to dir., intelligence bureau, home dept., Mar., 1930 ; confd. as supt. July, 1936 ; O.B.E., June, 1935.

COWGILL, JOHN HARRY CHRISTOPHER, Indian Police (b. 22nd Dec., 1907).—Joined the service as asst. supt., Bengal, 14th Nov., 1927 ; offg. addl. supt., Mar., 1934.

COWHAM, REV. ARTHUR GERARD, M.A., Indian Eccl. Estabt. (junr. chaplain) (b. 27th Oct., 1895).—Apptd. chaplain on probation, 25th Feb., 1929 ; junr. chaplain, Feb., 1931.

COWIE, JOHN GRANDISON, Dept. of Industries, Punjab (inspr. of industrial schools) (b. 12th Dec., 1888).—Joined the service as vice-princl., Mayo sch. of arts, Sept., 1924 ; inspr. of industrial schools, May, 1928.

COX, ALEXANDER RANKEN, late Indian C.S. (b. 16th July, 1878).—Educ. at Clifton and Emmanuel Coll., Cambridge ; apptd. after exam. of 1901 ; arrived, 31st Dec., 1902, and served in Madras as asst. collr. and mag. ; on police duty from April, 1905, to March, 1908 ; sub-collr. and jt. mag., Oct., 1910 ; dist. mag. and collr., Bangalore, and asst. to resdt. in Mysore, Nov., 1912, to Nov., 1917 ; on mil. duty from May to Oct., 1918 ; collr. and dist. mag., July, 1924 ; offg. 2nd member, bd. of rev., Madras, Aug., 1931 ; ret'd., Oct., 1936.

COX, ALFRED INNES, M.R.C.S., L.R.C.P., Major, I.M.S. Medical Dept., Madras (dist. medl. officer) (b. 1st Nov., 1894).—First commn., 27th Sept., 1919 ; in milty. employ until 25th Jan., 1932 ; dist. medl. officer, Madras, May, 1934.

COX, AUBREY PAUL, B.A. (Cantab.), M.A. (New Zealand), late Indian Educl. Service.—Joined the service as profir., Muir Central Coll., Allahabad, on 21st April, 1913 ; inspr. of schools, United Provs., Sept., 1916 ; ret'd., Aug., 1928.

COX, CUTHBERT EUSTACE CONNOP, Indian Forest Service (consr., Central Provs.) (b. 9th Mar., 1835).—Joined the service, 8th Nov., 1907, as asst. consr. ; dep. consr., Dec., 1912 ; offg. consvr. June, 1924, July, 1925, and again, May, 1928 ; confd., Nov., 1928.

COX, EDMUND LEONARD, Indian Police (asst. comdt., Malabar Special Police) (b. 29th Jan., 1913).—Joined the service as proby. asst. supt., Madras, Nov., 1932 ; confd., Nov., 1934 ; asst. comdt., Malabar spec. police, Oct., 1936.

COX, EDWARD ORAM, B.A.I., M.B.E., Indian Service of Engrs. (suptg. engr., Punjab) (b. 30th May, 1885).—Joined the service as asst. engr. on 1st Oct., 1908 ; exec. engr., Oct., 1916 ; M.B.E., June, 1919 ; offg. suptg. engr., Apl., 1932 ; confd., Jan., 1935.

COX, GEORGE CECIL ARTHUR, M.C., V.D., late Indian State Rlys. (E.I. Rly.) (b. 24th May, 1877).—Joined the service as junr. engr., (tempy.), 2nd Jan., 1903 ; ditto (perm.), Oct., 1906 ; in milty. employ, Oct., 1914, to June, 1919 ; resdt. engr., Jan., 1919 ; supt. (way and works), July, 1925 ; ret'd., June, 1929.

COX, HUBERT EUSTACE, Indian State Railways (dep. ch. engr., signals, G.I.P. rly.) (b. 14th Nov., 1892).—Joined the service, 2nd Jan.,

1915 ; signal and interlocking engr., G.I.P. rly., Apl., 1921 ; offg. dep. ch. engr., signals, July, 1935 ; confd., Dec., 1935.

COX, VERNON MAURICE HERVALWIL, Major, I.A., Indian Political Service (b. 28th Aug., 1898).—First commn., 12th Sept., 1917 ; in milty. employ to 1st Nov., 1927 ; asst. for Mekran to pol. agent in Kalat, Jan., 1930 ; asst. to pol. agent in Kalat and of Chagai, May, 1932 ; asst. commr., Peshawar, Feb., 1935 ; dep. commr., Peshawar, May, 1936 ; offcr. on spec. duty, N. Waziristan, Dec., 1936 ; pol. agent, ditto, Mar., 1937.

COYAJEE, SIR JEHANGIR COOVERJEE, Kt., B.A. (Cantab.), LL.B. (Bom.), late Indian Educl. Service (b. 11th Sept., 1875).—Joined the service as profir., Presdy. Coll., on 17th March, 1911 ; member of Royal Commn. on Indian Tariff and Indian Currency ; Kt., Jan., 1928 ; offg. prinpl. presdy. coll., Oct., 1930 ; ret'd., Nov., 1931 ; sub-delegate of India at Assembly of League of Nations, Sept., 1932.

CRACKNELL, ARTHUR JOHN, Indian State Rlys. (dep. ch. mech. engr., G.I.P. Rly.) (b. 17th May, 1885).—Joined the service, 2nd Aug., 1912 ; asst. loco. supt., July, 1914 ; in milty., employ from 6th July, 1915, to 21st April, 1916 ; asst. mechanl. engr., Nov., 1922 ; offg. asst. works manager, May, 1925 ; confd., Dec., 1927 ; offg. dep. ch. mechanl. engr., May, 1928, June, 1930, and again, Apl., 1931 ; confd. (provl.), Mar., 1932 ; offg. works manager, Nov., 1929.

CRACKNELL, FREDERICK GRAHAM, Indian C.S. (jt. mag., United Provinces) (b. 12th Feb., 1910).—Educated at Leeds Grammar School and Trinity Hall, Cambridge ; apptd. after exam. of 1931 ; arrived in India, Nov., 1932, and served in the United Provinces as asst. mag. and collr. ; jt. magte., June, 1934.

CRAIG, JOHN, late Indian Service of Engrs. (Burma) (b. 14th July, 1876).—Joined the service, 6th July, 1899 ; asst. engr., July, 1900 ; exec. engr., July, 1908 ; suptg. engr., Sept., 1923 ; ret'd., July, 1929.

CRAIG, MALCOLM THOMAS, B.A., A.M.I.C.E., Indian Service of Engrs. (exec. engr., Central Provs.) (b. 22nd Jan., 1894).—Joined the service as asst. engr., 6th April, 1920 ; asst. exec. engr., May, 1921 ; exec. engr., Oct., 1926.

CRAIG, THOMAS JOSEPH ALEXANDER, C.I.E., late Indian Police (b. 3rd Dec., 1881).—Joined the service, 27th Nov., 1902 ; supt., Feb., 1911 ; prin. police training coll., July to Sept., 1913 ; offg. dep. inspr.-genl., Feb., 1924 ; confd., Sept., 1925 ; inspr.-genl., Sept., 1930 ; C.I.E., June, 1932 ; ret'd., Dec., 1936.

CRAIK, SIR HENRY DUFFIELD, Bart., K.C.S.I., B.A., Indian C.S. (member, govr.-gen'l's. exec. council) (b. 2nd Jan., 1876).—Educ. at Eton Coll., Pembroke Coll., Oxford, and Univ. Coll., London ; apptd. after exam. of 1898 ; arrived, 21st Nov., 1899, and served in the Punjab as asst. commr. ; regr. of the chief court, March, 1905 ; asst. settlt. offr., Oct., 1908 ; settlt. offr., Oct. 1910 ; dist. and sess. judge, Jan., 1915 ; dep. commr., and rev. sec. to govt., Feb., 1916 ; dep. sec. to govt. of India, home dept., Feb., 1919 ; offg. addl. sec. to govt. of India,

home dept., Oct., 1919; offg. chiet sec. to Punjab Govt., April, 1922; confd., July, 1922; C.S.I., Jan., 1924; offg. finl. commr., Nov., 1927; commr. (prov.), Oct., 1929; temp. member, govr.'s exec. council, Punjab, Apl., 1930; confd., Aug., 1930; K.C.S.I., Jan., 1933; member, govr.-genl.'s exec. council, July, 1934.

CRAW, HENRY HEWAT, C.I.E., M.A., Indian C.S. (ch. secty. to govt., Burma) (b. 29th Oct., 1882).—Educ. at George Watson's Coll., and Jena Coll., Edinburgh; apptd. after exam. of 1906; arrived, 23rd Dec., 1907, and served in Burma as asst. commr.; on mil. duty, Oct., 1917, to Jan., 1919; offg. depy. commr., May, 1921; dep. commr., Jan., 1923; offg. commr., May, 1931; confd., June, 1935; ch. secty. to govt., Burma, Sept., 1935; C.I.E., Feb., 1937.

CRAWFORD, CHARLES ANTHONY, Indian State Rlys. (dep. chief comml. mgr., E.I. Rly.) (b. 14th April, 1900).—Joined the service 14th Jan., 1916, and held non-gazetted appts.; asst. supt., Feb., 1923; asst. comml. manager, Mar., 1926; offg. dep. ch. comml. manager, June, 1929; and again, Nov., 1935; confd. Dec., 1936.

CRAWFORD, COLIN GRANT, late Indian C.S. (Assam) (b. 3rd June, 1890).—Educ. at Clifton and Trinity Hall, Cambridge; apptd. after exam. of 1913; arrived, 7th Dec., 1914, and served in the Punjab as asst. commr.; on mil. service, March, 1915, to Dec., 1919; transf. to Assam, Dec., 1919; presdt., Manipur State Durbar from Sept., 1922; ret'd. Jan., 1930.

CRAWFORD, MAURICE NEWENHAM, A.C.G.I., A.M.I.C.E., late India Posts and Telegraphs (b. 2nd Sept., 1879).—Apptd. asst. supt., 1st Oct., 1902; ditto, and asst. postmr.-gen., traffic (temp.), Apl., 1914; supt., engrg., Feb. 1917; asst. chief engr., April, 1917; on field service (Afghan War), from May, 1919; dep. postmr.-gen., Sind and Baluchistan, July, 1922; dir., posts and tels., ditto, May, 1928; offg. dir., tel. engrg., Aug., 1929; offg. P.M.G., B. & O., 1929; offg. P.M.G., Burma, Mar., 1930; dir., tel. engrg., Aug., 1930; addl. dep. dir.-genl., tels. (temp.), Oct., 1933; offg. P.M.G., central circle, Jan., 1934; ditto Bengal and Assam, Apl., 1933; confd. as P.M.G., May, 1934; ret'd., Sept., 1934; War decorations:—South African medal, 2 clasps, Tibet medal with clasp.

CRAWFORD, WILLIAM GLASGOW, B.A., Indian Forest Service (dep. consvr., Burma) (b. 28th April, 1902).—Joined the service as asst. consvr., Burma, on 22nd Dec., 1925; dep. consvr., May, 1931.

CRAWLEY, GEORGE RICHARD, Indian Police (asst. supt., Central Provs.) (b. 22nd Apl., 1910).—Joined the service as proby. asst. supt., Central Provs., 26th Nov., 1930; confd., Apl., 1933; A.D.C. to govt., Oct., 1936.

CREAGH-COEN (see Coen, T. B. C.).

CREANER, THOMAS AMBROSE, B.E., B.Sc., Indian Service of Engineers (b. 24th Nov., 1906).—Joined the service as asst. exec. engr., Punjab, 20th Oct., 1930.

CREED, CLARENCE JAMES, M.C. Indian Police (supt., Bihar) (b. 19th July, 1894).—Joined the service as asst. supt. on 29th March, 1920; supt., Sept., 1924; awarded King's police medal, Jan., 1933; offg. dep. inspr.-genl., Mar., 1936.

CREEDY, HAROLD DOUGLAS, B.Sc., late Indian State Rlys. (b. 15th June, 1885).—Joined the service as asst. engr., 1st Oct. 1908; exec. engr., Oct., 1916; offg. dep. agent, Mar., 1929, and again, Feb., 1932; ret'd., Mar., 1935.

CREEK, ERNEST GEORGE, B.A., Indian C.S. (jt. mag. and dep. collr., Bengal) (b. 11th May, 1910).—Educated at the East Anglian School, Bury St. Edmunds, and St. John's College, Cambridge; apptd. after exam. of 1932; arrived in India, Nov., 1933, and served in Bengal as asst. mag. and collr.

CREIGHTON, THOMAS GORDON, B.Sc., A.M.I.M.E., M.I.L.E., Indian State Rlys. (divl. mech. engr., N.W. Rly.) (b. 29th Dec., 1898), asst. loco. supt., junr. scale, 11th Dec., 1922; divl. mech. engr., senr. scale, July, 1937.

CRERAR, SIR JAMES, K.C.S.I., C.I.E., M.A., late Indian C.S. (b. 11th Dec., 1877).—Educ. at Geo. Watson's Coll., Edinburgh, Edin. Univ., and Balliol Coll., Oxford; apptd. after exam. of 1900; arrived 15th Nov., 1901, and served in Sind as asst. collr. and mag.; asst. collr. and manager, encumbered estates, May, 1906; asst. commr. in Sind and Sindhi translator to govt., Sept., 1907, to Jan., 1911; dep. munpl. commr., Bombay, Oct., 1911; asst. pol. agent, Kathiawar, Jan., 1912; special duty in connection with Royal commn., 1912; munpl. commr., Bombay, July, 1913; offg. dep. sec. to govt., pol., judl. and spl. depts., Aug., 1914; pte. sec. to govr., Bombay, Oct., 1914; C.I.E., June, 1917; addl. sec. to govt., poll., judl. and spl. depts., July, 1918; sec. to govt. pol., &c., depts., Sept., 1919; C.S.I., Jan., 1922; offg. sec. to govt. of India, home dept., Dec., 1922; confd., Jan., 1923; temp. member of Gov.-Gen.'s Council April, 1926; member of ditto, July, 1927, to Mar., 1932; K.C.S.I., June, 1929; ret'd., Aug., 1934.

CREWE (MARQUESS OF), MOST HON. ROBERT OFFLEY ASHURTON CREWE-MILNES, K.G., P.C., M.A., F.S.A., Earl of Madeley, in the county of Stafford, and Marquess of Crewe (cr. 1911). Sec. of State for India, 7th Nov., 1910, to March, 1911, and from May, 1911, to May, 1915; chairman, Ind. constit'n'l relations ctee., 1919.

CRICHTON, GERALD CHARLES LAWRENCE, Capt., I.A. Indian Political Service (b. 30th July, 1900).—First commn. dated 14th July, 1921; prob. in pol. dept., May, 1926; settlement officer, Peshawar, Jan., 1927; asst. commr., Bannu, July, 1927; secty. to A.G.G. in Rajputana and C.C. Ajmer Merwara, June, 1928; pol. asst. to C.C. and A.G.G., N.W.F.P., Oct., 1928; asst. commr., Tank, Nov., 1930; offg. asst. resdt., Kolhapur, Oct., 1931; under-secty. to govt., N.W.F.P., Apl., 1933; offg. secty. to govt., N.W.F.P., transf. depts., Dec., 1933; pol. agent, N. Waziristan, Oct., 1934; home secty. to govt., N.W.F.P., Dec., 1937.

CRICHTON, WALTER HUGH, Major, I.M.S. (b. 24th July, 1896).—First commn., 8th Dec., 1924; agency surg., Kurram, Feb., 1933; health officer, Simla, Oct., 1934; asst. dir. pub. health, Delhi, Oct., 1936.

CRICHTON, The Ven. WALTER RICHARD, M.A., late Indian Eccles. Estabt. (b. 4th Jan., 1884).—Apptd. chaplain on probation, 31st

Oct., 1917; junr. chaplain, Oct., 1919; senr. chaplain, Oct., 1929; archdeacon of Madras, Mar., 1929; retd. Nov., 1937.

CRICKMAY, JOSEPH GEORGE, India Office (supgt. executive officer, milly. dept.) (b. 23rd April, 1883).—Apptd. clerk, 2nd class, registry and record dept., 17th Jan., 1902; temporarily attached to mil. dept., from Oct., 1914; minor staff clerk, March, 1917; staff clerk (actg.), Nov., 1919; higher exec. officer, April, 1922; representative of the India Office on the Navy, Army and Air Force Insurance Fund Advisory Ctee., Apl., 1922; actg. suptg. exec. officer, Mar., 1935; and again, Apl., 1936; confd., Nov., 1937.

CRITCHLY, VERNON FAIRBANK (dep. chief engr. Punjab Electricity) (b. 22nd Sept., 1893).—Joined the service 13th Oct., 1927, served as exec. and elec. engr. variously up to Dec., 1934; dep. chief engr., Jan., 1936.

CROAD, ALBERT, A.M.I.C.E., Indian Service of Engrs. (exec. engr.) (b. 3rd Dec., 1885).—Trained at R.I.E. Coll.; joined the service as asst. engr. on 1st Jan., 1907; exec. engr., Jan., 1915; offg. suptg. engr., Sept., 1923; secty. to ch. commr., Delhi, Dec., 1932.

CROCKETT, WILLIAM GORDON, M.B.E., Office of High Commr. for India (controller, store dept.) (b. 18th Dec., 1880).—Apptd. clerk, 2nd class, store dept., 23rd Nov., 1900; junior clerk, Nov., 1914; M.B.E., Jan., 1915; transfed. to office of high commr. for India, Oct., 1920; personal asst. to high commr., Oct., 1920; senr. clerk, April, 1921; contr., store dept., April, 1922.

CROFT, WILLIAM DAWSON, C.I.E., C.V.O., B.A., India Office (asst. secty., econ. and overseas dept.) (b. 16th Aug., 1892).—Educ. at Winchester Coll., and Trinity Coll., Oxford; apptd. junr. clerk, military dept., 28th May, 1919, after previous service as temporary junr. clerk since Nov., 1915; apptd. pte. sec. to the perm. under sec. of state, Jan., 1920; principal, April, 1921; establishment officer, July, 1921, to Jan., 1922; jt. secty., Indian round table conf., London, Nov., 1930-Jan., 1931; pte. secty. to secty. of state, Apl., 1931 to Oct., 1937; C.I.E., Jan., 1934; asst. secty., Aug., 1936; posted to econ. and overseas dept., Oct., 1937; C.V.O., Jan., 1938.

CROFTON, CHARLES D'ARCY, late Indian C.S. (Madras) (b. 19th April, 1879).—Educ. at Wellington Coll. and Pembroke Coll., Cambridge; apptd. after exam. of 1902; arrived, 1st Dec., 1903, and served in Madras as asst. collr. and mag.; asst. acctnt.-gen., Bengal, May, 1909; ditto, Punjab, Nov., 1909; dep. acctnt.-gen., June, 1910; acctnt.-gen., Bihar and Orissa, May to December, 1913; sub-collr., and jt. mag., Madras, March, 1914; asst. censor, Bombay, Jan., 1919; reverted from mil. duty, July, 1919; collr. and dist. mag., March, 1925; retd., Aug., 1929.

CROFTON, DENIS HAYES, Indian C.S. (jt. mag. and dep. collr., Bihar) (b. 14th Dec., 1908).—Educated at Tonbridge School and Corpus Christi College, Oxford; apptd. after exam. of 1931; arrived in India Dec., 1932, and served in Bihar and Orissa as asst. mag. and collr.; under secty. to govt., pol. dept., Bihar, May, 1936.

CROFTON, RICHARD MARSH, B.A., Indian C.S. (dir.-genl., rev. dept., Hyderabad) (b. 6th April, 1891).—Educ. at Trinity Coll., Dublin; apptd. after exam. of 1914; arrived 3rd Dec., 1915, and served in the Central Provs. as asst. commr.; on mil. duty from Oct., 1917, to March, 1919; offg. dep. commr., July, 1922; dep. commr. (prov.) and finl. sec to govt., May, 1926; dep. commr., Dec., 1926; offg. rev. secty. to govt., Dec., 1927; services placed at disposal of govt. of India, for. and pol. dept., Oct., 1931; excise and opium commr. in C. India, Nov., 1931; dir.-genl., rev. dept., Hyderabad, Feb., 1935.

CROFTS, FRANK NORMAN, Indian C.S. (jt. mag., United Provs.) (b. 23rd July, 1906).—Educ. at Rugby and Caius Coll., Cambridge; joined the service, 16th Oct., 1929; arrived 1st Dec., 1929, and served in the United Provinces as asst. collr. and mag.; temp. sess. and sub-judge, May, 1935; offg. dist. and sess. judge, Mar., 1936.

CROLY, ROBERT GEORGE GIBBON, M.B., Lt.-Col., I.M.S., retd. (b. 16th Sept., 1881).—Joined the service, 1st Feb., 1906; D.M.O., Kistna, Jan., 1924; offg. surg. 4th dist., and supt., Royapetta hosp., Feb., 1926; offg. prof., genl. hosp., Madras, Sept., 1930; offg. surg.-genl. with govt. of Madras, July, 1933; retd., Feb., 1935.

CROMBIE, ALAN DOUGLAS, C.I.E., Indian C.S. (collr., Madras) (b. 20th Jan., 1894).—Educ. at Wolverhampton Grammar Sch.; joined the service, 25th Aug., 1920; arrived 3rd Dec., 1920, and served in Madras as asst. collr. and mag.; sub-collr. and jt. mag., Nov., 1923; under-sec. to govt., rev. dept., Nov., 1925; on foreign service as agent under Rajah of Parlakimedi, Nov., 1926; dep. secty. to govt., local self-govt. dept., Madras, Dec., 1929; pte. secty. to govr., Madras, June, 1931; C.I.E., Jan., 1935; collr. (prov. subs.), Sept., 1935; confd., Feb., 1936.

CROMBIE, GEORGE EDMOND, M.A., India Office (actg. principal, Pol. dept.) (b. 14th June, 1908).—Educ. at Aberdeen Grammar Sch. and Fettes Coll.; Aberdeen Univ.; apptd. asst. principal, 19th Oct., 1931; actg. resdt. clk. July to Aug., 1932; resdt. clk., July, 1934 to Oct., 1934; pte. secty. to perm. under-secty. of State, Oct., 1935; pte. secty. to parly. under-secty. of State, Aug., 1936; actg. princpl. April, 1937.

CROMBIE, PETER, M.A., Indian C.S. (asst. mag. and collr., Madras) (b. 2nd Nov., 1911);—Educ. at Fettes College, University of Aberdeen and Caius College, Cambridge; appointed after exam. of 1934; arrived in India, Nov., 1935, and served in Madras as asst. mag. and collr.

CROOKSHANK, HENRY, B.A., B.A.I. (Dub.) Geological Survey of India (supvg. geologist) (b. 13th Feb., 1893).—Joined the service as asst. supt., 5th Dec., 1920; offg. supt., Sept., 1929; and again Mar. and Oct., 1933; confd., Nov., 1935.

CROOKSHANK, SIR SYDNEY D'AGUILAR, K.C.M.G., C.B., C.I.E., D.S.O., M.V.O., A.I.C.E., Col., late R.E., India Public Works Dept. (retd.).—First commn., dated 27th July, 1889; joined the Public Works Dept., Jan.,

1892, and served on the state rlys.; in charge of Gohna Lake operations, 1894, and famine relief wks., 1896-97; under-sec. to govt., United Prov., 1905-07; in charge of construction of King George's Hospital, Lucknow, 1908-10; supt. of wks., Delhi Durbar, 1911; suptg. engr., impl. city wks., Delhi, 1913-14; offg. sec. to the govt. of India, public wks. dept., Nov., 1919; chief engr. (provl.), Nov., 1919; confd., Nov., 1920; reverted to Impl. estab., June, 1925; retd., June, 1927. War services—Relief of Chitral, 1895 (medal and clasp); British Expedy. Force, 1914-19 (dir.-gen. of transportation, France, 1914-15 Star, British War and Victory medals); Honours—M.V.O., Dec., 1911; C.I.E., June, 1912; D.S.O., June, 1916; Bt.-Col., Jan., 1918; C.B., June, 1918; K.C.M.G., June, 1919; Commdr., Légion d'Honneur; ditto, Ordre de Leopold; Grande Croix, Ordre d'Aviz; Croix de Guerre Française (avec palme); Croix de Guerre Belge.

CROSBY, CEDERIC CONWAY, Burma Civil Service (dep. commr. and collr., Rangoon Town) (b. 17th Oct., 1884).—Held ministerial appts. from 26th Sept., 1907; offg. extra asst. commr., May, 1912; confd., Oct., 1912; offg. dep. commr., July, 1926; confd., Jan., 1932.

CROSSLEY, DONALD, Indian Police (supt., Madras) (b. 22nd July, 1903).—Joined the service as asst. supt., Madras, on 2nd Dec., 1922; offg. dist. supt., Apl., 1929; confd., Mar., 1934; awarded Indian Police Medal, Mar., 1937.

CROSSLEY, THOMAS BIGHAM, B.A., Indian C.S. (asst. mag. and collr., United Provs.) (b. 9th Feb., 1910).—Educated at Blackburn Grammar School and King's College, Cambridge; apptd. after exam. of 1933; arrived in India, Nov., 1934, and served in the United Provinces as asst. mag. and collr.

CROSTHWAIT, LELAND GEORGE, Lt.-Col., I.A. (retd.), late Survey of India (b. 29th Aug., 1881).—First commn., 23rd July, 1900; asst. supt. on probn., trigon. branch, Oct., 1905; confd., Oct., 1906; on mil. service in Mesopotamia, Dec., 1914, to June, 1919; dept. supt., May, 1916; supt., Jan., 1926; offg. dir., eastern circle, Oct., 1926; ditto, Burma circle, May, 1927; ditto, Southern circle, Oct., 1929; retd., Dec., 1933.

CROSTHWAIT, SIR BERTRAM MAITLAND, Kt., V.D., late Indian State Rlys. (b. 9th Oct., 1880).—Joined the service as asst. traffic supt., 14th Dec., 1898; dist. traffic supt., Jan., 1905; dep. traffic manager, April, 1915; offg. traffic manager, June, 1924; confd., Sept., 1924; offg. agent, Dec., 1926; member of rly. bd., May-Nov., 1927; actg. agent, July, 1929; confd., Aug., 1932; retd., Dec., 1935; knighted, June, 1935.

CROSTHWAIT, CHARLES GILBERT, C.B.E., Lt.-Col., I.A. (retd.) (b. 3rd June, 1878).—First commn. dated 4th Aug., 1897; in mil. emp. to Jan., 1903; pol. asst., Dec., 1903; asst. to agent to govr.-gen. in Central India, Jan., 1904; asst. commr., N.W.F. Prov., Feb., 1904; dep. commr., July, 1911; dist. judge, Peshawar, Oct., 1912; dep. commr., Kohat, Jan., 1915; addl. divl. and sess. judge, Peshawar, Nov., 1915; dep. commr., Bannu, April, 1916; pol. agent, Wana, April, 1919; O.B.E., June, 1919; dep. commr. Peshawar, May, 1921; C.B.E., June, 1924; offg. pol. resdt., Persian Gulf, June, 1925; pol. agent,

Eastern Rajputana States, March, 1926; offg. resident at Gwalior, June, 1926; offg. agent to govr. genl., Madras States, April, 1928; offg. resdt., Baroda, Mar., 1931; confd., June, 1931; retd., June, 1933.

CROSTHWAIT, HUGH STUART, C.I.E., B.A., Indian C.S. (member, fed. public service commn.) (b. 20th Oct., 1879).—Educ. at Rugby Sch. and New Coll., Oxford; apptd. after exam. of 1902; arrived, 30th Nov., 1903, and served in the United Prov. as asst. mag. and collr., and asst. commr.; under sec. to govt., Feb., 1910-1912; chairman, Allahabad mump. bd., Apl., 1913, to Oct., 1916; supt. Dehra Dun, from Mar., 1917; mag. and collr., Jan., 1921; sec. to govt. of United Prov., industries dept., April, 1922; C.I.E., Jan., 1926; offg. finance secty. to govt., April, 1927; dep. commr., Nov., 1927; offg. commr., Mar., 1929; confd., May, 1932; offg. ch. commr., Delhi, Apl., 1934; member fed. public service commn., Feb., 1935.

CROTHERS, EDWARD MICHAEL, late Indian Forest Service (b. 9th Feb. 1880).—Joined the service, 19th Nov., 1901; dep. consvr., March, 1920; retd. Sept., 1933.

CROXFORD, ERNEST WILLIAM, India Audit Office (exec. officer) (b. 18th Jan., 1915).—Apptd. exec. officer, 2nd Oct., 1933.

CRUICKSHANK, FLETCHER CECIL MACDONALD, B.A., late Indian C.S. (b. 17th Aug., 1882).—Educ. at Rugby and Magdalen Coll., Oxford; apptd. after exam. of 1906; arrived, 25th Nov., 1907, and served in United Provs. as asst. mag. and collr.; special manager. Awah estate, Oct., 1912; jt. mag., Jan., 1916; on mil. duty (I.A.R.O.), July, 1915, to May, 1919; asst. supt., Dehra Dun, May, 1919; mag. and collr., Nov., 1923; offg. dep. commr., March, 1926; confd., June, 1928; retd., July, 1934.

CRUICKSHANK, MARTIN MELVIN, M.D., B.Sc., Ch.M., D.O.M.S., Lt.-Col. I.M.S., Medical Dept., Madras (dist. medl. officer) (b. 22nd Mar., 1888).—First commn., 17th Mar., 1919; in milty. employ until 25th Feb., 1931; dist. medl. officer, Madras, May, 1934; prof. of surgery, Madras medl. coll., and surgeon, genl. hospital, Madras, Sept., 1934.

CRUMP, EDWIN SAMUEL, C.I.E., A.M.I.C.E., Indian Service of Engrs. (suptg. engr. Punjab) (b. 6th July, 1882).—Joined the service as asst. engr., on 1st Oct., 1906; exec. engr., Nov., 1914; offg. suptg. engr., Feb., 1926; confd., Feb., 1933; C.I.E., June, 1936.

CRUMP, SIR HENRY ASHBROOKE, K.C.I.E., C.S.I., B.A., late Indian C.S. (Central Provs.).—Educ. at Balliol Coll., Oxford; apptd. after exam. of 1882; arrived, 10th Nov., 1885, and served in the Central Provs. as asst. commr.; commr. of excise, June, 1893; dep. commr., Aug., 1894; second sec. to chief commr., Nov., 1894, to Feb., 1895; chief sec. to ditto, April, 1901, to May, 1902, and from June, 1906; commr., Oct., 1907; offg. chief commr., Jan. to Feb., 1912; offg. finl. commr., Feb. to Aug., 1912; finl. commr., March, 1913; offg. chief commr., Jan. to April, 1914; C.S.I., June, 1914; retd., Nov., 1920; K.C.I.E., Jan. 1921.

CRUMP, SIR LOUIS CHARLES, Kt., late Indian C.S.—Educ. under private tuition and at Balliol Coll., Oxford; apptd. after exam. of 1888;

arrived, 10th Dec., 1890, and served in Bombay as asst. collr. and mag., and asst. judge; 2nd asst., March, 1897; ditto, and asst. judge, Feb., 1898; ditto, and asst. to the legal remembrancer, March, 1899; under-sec. to govt. pol., judl., and legis. depts., March, 1899, to Jan., 1900; 1st asst., April, 1902; judge and sess. judge, Sept., 1904; remembrancer of legal affairs and sec. to govt., legal dept., Jan., 1909, to July, 1911; addl. jud. commr., Sind, Nov., 1918; temp. member govr.'s exec. council, June, 1919, to Oct., 1919; offg. judge, Bombay High Ct., from Nov., 1919; judl. commr., Sind, April, 1920; judge, Bombay High Ct., Jan., 1921; Kt., Jan., 1928; retd., Jan., 1929.

CUERDON, THOMAS, late Indian Service of Engrs. (Bengal) (b. 28th Feb., 1876).—Held non-gazetted appt. from 9th Aug., 1899, to 31st Jan., 1920; asst. engr., Feb., 1920; asst. engr., March, 1922; exec. engr., June, 1923; retd., Feb., 1931.

CULHANE, ALBERT JOSEPH CHARLES, M.B., Ch.B., Major, I.M.S., Medical Dept., United Provs. (civil surgeon) (b. 24th Sept., 1894).—Date of first commn., 29th Apr., 1927; civil surg., United Provs., Jan., 1932.

CUMING, SIR ARTHUR HERBERT, Kt., late Indian C.S. (Bengal) (b. 24th Nov., 1871).—Educ. at Westminster Sch., London Univ., and Oriel Coll., Oxford; apptd. after exam. of 1893; arrived, 3rd Jan., 1895, and served in Bengal as asst. mag. and collr.,; asst. commr., Assam, Jan., 1899; mag. and collr., E. Bengal and Assam, Jan., 1906; dist. and sessions judge, March, 1908; re-transfd. to Bengal, April, 1912; offg. supt. and remembrancer of legal affairs, Bengal, April, 1914; acting judge, Calcutta high ct., June to Sept., 1916, March to April, 1917, and from April, 1919, to Sept., 1920, and from Feb., 1921; permanently apptd. as judge, high ct., Calcutta, 10th Nov., 1921; Kt., Jan., 1928; retd., Nov., 1931.

CUMMING, DONALD BRODBY, D.F.C., late Indian Police Service (Bihar and Orissa) (b. 8th Nov., 1891).—Joined the service as asst. supt. on 6th Dec., 1911; supt., Sept., 1924; retd., Oct., 1932.

CUMMING, SIR JOHN GHEST, K.C.I.E., C.S.I., M.A., late Indian C.S.—Educ. at High Sch., Glasgow, Glasgow Univ., and Balliol Coll., Oxford; apptd. after exam. of 1887; arrived, 24th Oct., 1890, and served in Bengal as asst. mag. and collr. and settl. officer; offg. under sec. to govt., July to Oct., 1894; joint mag. and dep. collr., April, 1895; mag. and collr. June, 1900; sec. to govt., judl. and genl. depts., Jan., 1909; ditto, rev. and genl. depts., Sept., 1909; C.I.E., Dec., 1911; dir., land records, Dec., 1911; special duty, Bengal secretariat, Dec., 1911; offg. commr., May, 1912; offg. chief sec. to govt., May, 1913; confd., Jan., 1914; addl. member, govr.-genl.'s council, Dec., 1915; C.S.I., June, 1916; temps. member of govr.'s council, Bengal, Sept., 1917, to March, 1918; member, govr.'s exec. council, Bengal, April, 1918, to May, 1920; K.C.I.E., 1920; retd., Oct., 1921.

CUMMING, RALPH FLETCHER EDWARD, late Bombay C.S. (b. 10th Dec., 1878).—Held non-gazetted appts. from 17th Dec., 1895

dep. collr., May, 1911; offg. asst. commr. of salt, etc., Aprl., 1914; asst. supt. of stamps, etc., Dec., 1919; retd., Dec., 1933.

CUMMING, ROUALEYN CHARLES ROSSITER, Indian Police (inspr.-genl. police, Assam) (b. 2nd Nov., 1891).—Joined the service as asst. supt. on 7th Dec., 1911; supt., Aprl., 1922; offg. dep. inspr.-genl., Aug., 1935; inspr.-genl., Assam, Dec., 1937.

CUNLIFFE, SIR JOHN ROBERT ELLIS, Kt., Barr. (b. 4th Mar., 1886).—Apptd. judge, high ct., Rangoon, 12th Nov., 1924; Kt., Jan., 1932; judge, high ct., Calcutta, Sept., 1934; retd., Nov., 1937.

CUNNINGHAM, SIR CHARLES BANKS, Kt., C.S.I., late Indian Police (b. 8th May, 1884).—Apptd. asst. supt. of police, Madras, 24th Dec., 1904; supt. and dep. commr. of police, Madras, Feb., 1913; commr. of police, Travancore, from Aug., 1915, to March, 1921; offg. dep. inspr. genl., Jan., 1928; actg. commr. of police, Madras, May, 1928; confd., Aprl., 1930; awarded King's police medal, Mar., 1929; offg. inspr.-genl., May, 1930; confd., Oct., 1930; C.S.I., Jan., 1931; knighted, June, 1933; awarded Indian police medal, Mar., 1937; retd., Jan., 1938.

CUNNINGHAM, SIR GEORGE, K.C.S.I., K.C.I.E., O.B.E., B.A., Indian C.S. (govr., N.W.F.P.) Indian Political Service (b. 23rd Mar., 1888).—Educ. at Fettes Coll., Edinburgh, and Magdalen Coll., Oxford; apptd. after exam. of 1910; arrived in India, 26th Nov., 1911, and served in the Punjab as asst. commr.; asst. commr., Kohat, Nov., 1914; personal asst. to chief commr., N.W. Frontier Prov., Oct., 1915; apptd. to pol. dept., govt. of India, Jan., 1917; asst. commr., Tank, D.C., 1918; pol. asst. to chief commr., N.W. Frontier Prov., Feb., 1919; subdiv. offr., Tank, April, 1919; pte. sec. to chief commr., N.W. Frontier Prov., May, 1919; spl. duty on staff of chief pol. offr. with N.W. Frontier Field Force, May, 1919; O.B.E., Aug., 1920; asst. commr., Thal, Nov., 1920; pol. agent, Tochi, April, 1922; C.I.E., Jan., 1925; counsellor, British Legation, Kabul, Nov., 1925; pte. sec. to the Viceroy, July, 1926; C.S.I., June, 1931; member. exec. council, govr. N.W.F.P., June, 1932, to May, 1936; actg. govr. N.W.F.P., Nov., 1932; offg. agent to govr.-genl., N.W.F.P., Nov., 1932; K.C.I.E., June, 1935; K.C.S.I., Feb., 1937; govr., N.W.F.P., Mar., 1937.

CUNNINGHAM, JOHN, C.I.E., M.D., Lt.-Col., I.M.S. (ret'd.) late Medical Research Dept. (b. 9th July, 1881).—In mil. employ from 1st Feb., 1905 to 14th Oct. 1908, when apptd. to officiate as asst. to dir., Bombay bacteriological laboratory; asst. dir., central research inst., Kasauli, Jan., 1921; on spl. duty for dysentery enquiry under the Indian research fund assocn., from July, 1913; reverted to mil. employ, April, 1915; ctng. dir., central research inst., Kasauli, Jan., 1916; asst. dir., ditto, May, 1917; dir., King inst. of prev. medicine, Madras, March, 1919; dir., Pasteur institute, Kasauli, Nov., 1925; offg. dir., central research institute, Kasauli, April, 1928; C.I.E., June, 1928; retd., June, 1931.

CUNNINGHAM, JOHN RICHARD, C.I.E., M.A., late Indian Educl. Service (Assam) (b. 18th Feb., 1876).—Joined the service,

18th Nov., 1907, and officiated as profrr., Presdy. Coll., Calcutta, and asst. dir. of public instn., Bengal; inspr. of schools, Bengal, Jan., 1912; dir. of public instn., Assam, April, 1912; C.I.E., June, 1921; offg. member, public services commn., June, 1928; ret'd., July, 1931.

CURGENVEN, SIR ARTHUR JOSEPH, B.Sc., late Indian C.S. (b. 24th July, 1876).—Educ. at St. Paul's Sch., and New Coll., Oxford; apptd. after exam. of 1898; arrived, 16th Nov., 1899, and served in Madras as asst. collr. and mag.; 1st asst. commr. and dist. mag., Coorg, Sept., 1905; sub. collr. and jt. mag., April, 1911; dist. and sess. judge, April, 1919; offg. judge, high ct., June, 1926, Oct., 1926, July, 1927, and again July, 1928; confd., July, 1929; ret'd., July, 1936; knighted, Feb., 1937.

CURRIE, ALGERNON JAMES, late Finance Dept., Govt. of India (b. 18th Dec., 1883).—Joined the service, 14th June, 1905; offg. comptroller, Assam, Sept., 1925; confd., Oct., 1926; offg. acct.-gen., Bengal, Mar., 1929; confd., Oct., 1929; acct. genl., Bombay, Nov., 1930; ditto, Central Provs., Dec., 1930; ditto, Central Provs., Nov., 1931; offg. dir. of army audit, Feb., 1932; acct. genl., Bihar and Orissa, Oct., 1932; ditto, Central Provs., Oct., 1934; ret'd., Sept., 1936.

CURRIE, DONALD McDONALD, B.Sc. (dep. consvr., Madras) (b. 11th Dec., 1894).—Joined the service as asst. consvr., 11th Dec., 1921; dep. consvr., Dec., 1922.

CURRIE, MARK MAINWARING LEE, B.A., Indian C.S. (dist. and sess. judge, Punjab) (b. 14th April, 1882).—Educ. at Cheltenham Coll., and Pembroke Coll., Oxford; apptd. after exam. of 1904; arrived, 27th Nov., 1905, and served in the Punjab as asst. commr. and settlt. offr.; addl. dist. mag., Delhi, Feb., 1917; dep. commr., Nov., 1921; dist. and sess. judge, Oct., 1924; legal remembr. and secty., legisl. dept., Nov., 1927; addl. judge, high ct., Lahore, variously from Mar., 1930; actg. judge, ditto, variously from May, 1932; offg. secty. to govt., legal dept., Punjab, Oct., 1932.

CURRY, JOHN COURT, late Indian Police Service (b. 31st Dec., 1887).—Joined the service, 18th Nov., 1907, as asst. supt.; supt. of police, Dec., 1920; dy. commr; awarded King's police medal, Jan., 1923; ret'd., Jan., 1933.

CURRY, JOHN ELLIOT PRINGLE, late Marine Dept., Bombay (b. 25th July, 1876).—Joined the dept., 1st April, 1906; dep. shipping master, May, 1907; shipping master, July, 1918; ret'd., March, 1932.

CURRY, THOMAS ARCHIBALD, A.M.I.C.E., Indian Service of Engrs. (ch. engr., Bengal) (b. 2nd Dec., 1884).—Trained at R.I.E. Coll.; joined the service, 1st Oct., 1906, as asst. engr.; exec. engr., Oct., 1914; suptg. engr., Dec., 1927; offg. ch. engr., May, 1933; confd., Sept., 1935.

CURSETJI, MANEKJI JEHANGIR MANEKJI, B.Sc. (Lond.), A.K.C., A.M.I.C.E., Indian Service of Engrs. (exec. engr., Sind) (b. 18th Sept., 1890).—Joined the provl. service as asst. engr., June, 1915; asst. engr. (impl. service), Jan., 1917; asst. exec. engr., Aug., 1919; exec. engr., June, 1924.

CURTIS, GERALD COLVILLE SEYMOUR, B.A., Indian C.S., Indian Political Service, (b. 4th Sept., 1904).—Educ. at Westminster, Christchurch Coll., Oxford, and Trinity Coll., Dublin; joined the service, 8th October, 1929; arrived Dec., 1929, and served as asst. commr. in the Punjab; pol. asst. and commandant, Border Mily. police, May, 1932; colonisation officer and asst. pol. agent, Nasirabad, May, 1933; and again, Dec., 1934; pol. agent, Sibi, Nov., 1934; asst. pol. agent, Punjab Hill States, Oct., 1936.

CURTIS, SYDNEY JOSEPH, M.C., Indian Forest Service (dep. consvr., Bengal) (b. 1st Oct., 1894).—Joined the service as asst. consvr., 15th March, 1921; dep. consvr., Mar., 1922.

DABHOLKAR, ANANT YASHWANT, M.C., M.B., B.Sc. (Bom.), D.P.H., Lt.-Col., I.M.S., Public health dept., Bombay (dir.) (b. 30th June, 1888).—First commn., 17th Mar., 1919; in milty. employ prior to 11th Dec., 1929; asst. dir. of public health, Jan., 1930; dir., 1933.

DADABHOY, SIR MANECKJI BYRAMJI, K.C.S.I., K.C.I.E.—Apptd. pres., council of state, 21st Dec., 1932; K.C.S.I., Jan., 1936.

DADACHANJI, RATANJI SORABJI, B.A., LL.B., Barr., late Bombay Judicial Dept. (b. 27th Nov., 1875).—Apptd. judge, small cause courts, 25th Oct., 1919; master and regrtr. in equity, Apl., 1929; ret'd., Apl., 1935.

DAFF, THOMAS ALLEN, late Indian Police Service (b. 5th Aug., 1889).—Joined the service, 11th Feb., 1910; asst. supt., April, 1912; supt., Aug., 1919; awarded King's police medal, Jan., 1930; ret'd., Apl., 1932.

DAFTARY, GAVRISHANKAR DULLABJI, B.E. (Civil), Indian Service of Engrs. (exec. engr., Bombay) (b. 23rd Jan., 1889).—Joined the provl. service as apprentice engr., March, 1913; asst. engr., March, 1914; asst. exec. engr., Aug., 1919; exec. engr., March, 1923.

DAGLISH, JULIAN, Burma Police (Class 1) (b. 19th Sept., 1916).—Apptd. asst. supt., Burma, on probn., Dec., 1937.

DAIN, JOHN RUTHERFORD, C.I.E., B.A., Indian C.S. (rev. commr., Orissa) (b. 13th Jan., 1883).—Educ. at Tonbridge, and Hertford Coll., Oxford; apptd. after exam. of 1906; arrived, 25th Nov., 1907, and served in Bengal as asst. mag. and collr.; transfed. to Bihar and Orissa, April, 1912; asst. settlt. offr., Oct., 1914; jt. mag. and ditto, Nov., 1915; mag. and collr., Oct., 1922; sec. to govt. of Bihar and Orissa, rev. dept., Oct., 1923; dep. commr., Oct., 1926; C.I.E., Mar., 1929; offg. commr., Apl., 1929; confd., May, 1932; offg. member, bd. of rev., Bihar and Orissa, Aug., 1933; rev. commr., Orissa, Dec., 1936.

DAINTITH, TERENCE JOSEPH, M.C., India Posts and Tels. (dep. p.m.g.) (b. 23rd Jan., 1885).—Joined the service, 1st Aug., 1907; supt., July, 1908; offg. dep. p.m.g., Madras, Dec., 1933; dep. p.m.g., United Provs., Mar., 1934; ditto p. and t. dept., Nov., 1936.

DALAL, ARDESHIR RUSTAMJI, M.A., late Indian C.S. (Bombay) (b. 24th Apl., 1884).—Educ. at Fort High Sch., Bombay, Bombay Univ., and St. John's Coll., Camb.; apptd. after exam. of 1907; arrived 14th Nov., 1908, and served in Bombay as asst. collr. and supt. of land records; asst. collr. and dep. sec. to govt.,

rev. dept. (temp.) Nov., 1921; dep. sec. to govt. fin. dept., Aug., 1925; offg. sec. to govt., fin. dept., March, 1926; member of legisl. council, 1926; offg. secty. to govt. of India, educn., health and lands dept., July-Nov., 1927; collr. and munpl. commr. for city of Bombay, Dec., 1928; retd., July, 1931.

DALAL, Sir DADIBA MERWANJEE, Kt., C.I.E.—Member of comtee. on Indian exchange and currency, 1919; chairman, govt. securities rehabilitation comtee., Bombay, 1921; apptd. member of council of sec. of state for India, 19th Nov., 1921; delegate for India at Genoa Economic Conference, and representative for India at The Hague Economic Conference, 1922; member of Indian retrenchment comtee., 1922; high commr. for India, 19th April, 1923, to 26th Sept., 1924; delegate for India at Impl. Economic Conference, 1923; C.I.E. Jan., 1918; Kt., Jan., 1924.

DALAL, RATANJI DINSHAH, C.I.E., M.L.A., L.M. & S. (Bom.), M.R.C.S. (Eng.), L.R.C.P. (Lond.), D.P.H. (Lond.).—Apptd. dep. sany. commr., Southern Regtn. Dist., Bombay, 14th June, 1913; asst. dir. of public health, Southern Regtn. District, Oct., 1921; recd. K.-I.-H. medal (2nd class), June, 1923; dir. vaccine institute, Belgaum, Oct., 1923; retd., Oct., 1925; member, Indian legisl. assembly, since 1930; C.I.E., June, 1936; author of "A Manual of Vaccination."

DALIP SINGH, KANWAR, B.A., Barr., Judl. Dept., Punjab (judge, high ct., Lahore) (b. 2nd June, 1885).—Apptd. asst. legal remembrancer, Nov., 1922; govt. advocate, Nov., 1924; addl. judge, high ct., Lahore, Oct., 1925; judge, ditto, Feb., 1930.

DALIP SINGH, M.A., Income-tax Dept., Punjab (asst. commr.) (b. 29th Nov., 1896).—Joined the service, Aug., 1920; held non-gaz. appts., Aug., 1920-1921; collr. of Income-tax, Sept., 1921; on special duty, Punjab Secretariat, May, 1930; Income-tax officer, June, 1930; cnfd. as asst. commr. of income-tax, June, 1936.

DALJIT SINGH, RAJA, Sir, K.B.E., C.S.I.—Member of the legislative council, Punjab, 1913-1915; member govr.-gen.'s legislative council, 1913-1915; C.S.I., June, 1914; member of council of Sec. of State for India, March, 1915, to June, 1917; K.B.E., June, 1919; chief minister, Kashmir, to 1921.

DALLEY, RICHARD POMEROY, B.A., Indian Forest Service (dep., consvr., Bombay) (b. 10th Aug., 1888).—Joined the service, 24th Nov., 1913, as asst. consvr.; on milty duty from 3rd Aug., 1916, to 13th Nov., 1918; dep. consvr., Mar., 1919; offg. consvr., Mar., 1935.

D'ALMEIDA, WILLIAM, M.B.E. (asst. secty., home dept., Govt. of India) (b. 21st Oct., 1888).—Joined the service, 1st Dec., 1909; supt., home dept., June, 1922; supt., Indian staty. commn., Mar., 1928; offg. asst. secty., home dept., Govt. of India, June, 1930; confd., Mar., 1933; offg. under-secty., home dept., Govt. of India, Sept., 1934; asst. secty., ditto, Sept., 1934.

DALY, CLIVE KIRKPATRICK, C.I.E., Lt.-Col., I.A., Indian Political Service (b. 3rd Apr., 1888).—First commn., 18th Dec., 1907; C.I.E.,

Jan., 1925; pol. agent, Bahrain, Nov., 1925; H.M. consul, Seistan and Kain, Mar., 1929; offg. consul-genl., Khorasan, June, 1932; confd., Nov., 1934.

DALY, Sir HUGH, K.C.S.I., K.C.I.E., Lt.-Col. Indian Army, retd.—Arrived 12th Feb., 1882, and served in the mil. dept.; from Nov., 1884, served in the pol. dept. as pol. asst., Rajputana; on special duty in Upper Burma, June, 1886, and with the N. Shan column in Nov., 1887; supt., N. Shan states, May, 1888; on special duty at Mandalay, 1891; C.I.E., May, 1892; asst. sec. to govt. of India, foreign dept., July, 1892; dep. sec., June, 1896; C.S.I., Jan., 1903; offg. agent to gov.-gen., Central India, and gen. supt. of thagi and dakaiti operations, Nov., 1903, to May, 1904; confd., Feb., 1905; resdt. in Mysore and chief commr. of Coorg, Aug., 1910; K.C.I.E., Dec., 1911; retd., April, 1916; K.C.S.I., June, 1916; author of 'Memoirs of Gen. Sir H. M. Daly.'

DALZIEL, WALTER WATSON, B.A. (Oxon), Indian C.S. (dist. and sess. judge, Bihar) (b. 5th Oct., 1900).—Educ. at Edinburgh Academy and Corpus Christi, Oxford; joined the service, 24th Oct., 1924; arrived, 7th Dec., 1924, and served in Bihar and Orissa as asst. mag. and collr.; asst. settlement offr., Nov., 1927; offg. judl. commr., Apr., 1932; offg. dist. and sess. judge, Dec., 1932; confd., Apr., 1934; services placed at disposal of Govt. of India, legisl. dept., Apr. to Oct., 1935.

DAM, SARAT CHANDRA, B.Sc., B.E., Indian Service of Engrs. (exec. engr., Bengal) (b. 13th Aug., 1894).—Joined the service, 19th April, 1922; asst. exec. engr., April, 1923; offg. exec. engr., June, 1929; confd., Apr., 1932.

DAMLE, KASHINATH RAGHUNATH, Indian C.S. (jt. mag., United Provs.) (b. 3rd March, 1906).—Educ. at Allahabad University, King's College, London, and London School of Oriental Studies; joined the service, 10th Oct., 1930; arrived in India, Nov., 1930, and served in the United Provs., as asst. mag. and collr.

DANDEKER, NARAYAN, B.Sc., Indian C.S. (asst. collr. of customs, Madras) (b. 24th June, 1908).—Educ. at School of Oriental Studies Lond.; joined the service 13th Oct., 1931; arrived in India Nov., 1931, and served in Madras as asst. mag. and collr.; sub-collr. and jt. mag., Apr., 1933; asst. collr. of customs, Madras, Jan., 1937.

DANE, Sir Louis WILLIAM, G.C.I.E., C.S.I., F.R.G.S., late Indian C.S.—Apptd. after exam. of 1874; arrived, 14th Nov., 1876, and served in the Punjab as asst. commr.; private sec. to lieut.-govr., Oct., 1879, to April, 1882; on special duty, Lahore Rakhs, 1884-85; offg. registrar, chief court, June, 1886; settl. officer, Gurdaspur, Oct., 1887; asst. commr., 1st grade, May, 1889, dep. commr. and settl. collr., Peshawar, Dec., 1892; chief sec. to govt., Punjab, Nov., 1898; resident in Kashmir, Nov., 1901; sec. to govt. of India, foreign dept., March, 1903; C.S.I., June, 1904; on spl. duty with the Kabul mission from Oct., 1904, to April, 1905; K.C.I.E., May, 1905; lieut.-govr. of the Punjab, May, 1908, to May, 1913; G.C.I.E., Dec., 1911; retd., May, 1913.

DANE, Sir RICHARD MORRIS, K.C.I.E., late Indian C.S.—Educ. at Kingstown Sch., Dublin; apptd. after exam. of 1872; arrived, 20th Nov., 1874, and served in the Punjab as asst. commr. and settlt. officer; served under the foreign dept. of the govt. of India, 1878-79; offg. sec. to finl. commr., Punjab, Nov., 1880, to Jan., 1881; offg. under sec. to govt., Punjab, March to Aug., 1882; boundary settlt. officer, Central India, 1883-84; asst. commr., Ajmir, Jan., 1885; served subsequently as offg. dep. commr., Punjab; settlt. officer, Gurdaspur, Nov., 1885; offg. commr. of excise, inspr.-gen. of registrn., &c., 1890-92; acted as commr., N. India salt rev., May, 1892; on special duty in connection with opium commn., Sept., 1893; dep. sec. to govt. of India, fin. and commerce dept., March, 1895; C.I.E., Jan., 1896; commr., N. India salt rev., Dec., 1898; inspr.-gen., excise and salt, Sept. 1907; K.C.I.E., Jan., 1900; retd., Oct., 1909.

DANIELS, MARK, O.B.E., B.A., Office of High Commr. for India (controller, store dept.) (b. 22nd Feb., 1892).—Educ. at Central Foundation School, London, and Sidney Sussex Coll., Cambridge (wrangler; scholar, 1910-14); apptd. temp. junr. clerk, finl. dept., 16th March, 1915; resdt. clerk (temp.), Sept., 1915, to Dec., 1916; junr. clerk, store dept., 1st Nov., 1915; on mil. service from Dec., 1916, to Feb., 1919; transfed. to office of high commr. for India, Oct., 1920, as asst. controller, store dept.; senr. clerk, Dec., 1921; contr., April, 1922; O.B.E., June, 1934.

DAR, JEEWAN NATH, RAI SAHIB, B.A., Indian Posts and Telegraphs (dep. P.M.G.) (b. 31st Jan., 1884).—Joined the service, 8th Oct., 1906; asst. supt., Apl., 1910; supt., Dec., 1912; dep. P.M.G., Oct., 1937.

DANTYAGI, VISHWESWAR DAYAL, M.A., LL.B., Indian Audit Dept. (b. 14th Jan., 1899). Joined the service, 13th July, 1922; dep. acct. genl., central revenues, Apl., 1936; dep. acct. genl., P. and T., Delhi, Apl., 1937.

DAR, SHYAM SUNDAR LAL, M.A. (Cantab), Barr, Indian C.S. (mag. and collr., United Provs.) (b. 17th Dec., 1895).—Educ. at Punjab Univ., and Queen's Coll., Cambridge; joined the service, 30th Oct., 1920; arrived, 16th Mar., 1921, and served in United Provs. as asst. mag. and collr. and jt. mag.; offg. mag. and collr., June, 1925; confd., Nov., 1932; offg. excise commr., July, 1931; inspr.-genl., regns., United Provs., Apl., 1932; magte. and collr., Nov., 1933.

DARBY, GEORGE SCOTT, V.D., Indian State Ryis. (traffic manager, Burma Ryis.) (b. 16th April, 1885).—Held non-gazetted appts. from 17th Mar., 1904; asst. traffic supt., May, 1905; on milty. duty from 30th Mar., 1915, to 20th Feb., 1919; dist. traffic supt., Sept., 1921; offg. dep. traffic manager, Apl., 1928, and again April, 1929; confd., Apl., 1931; offg. traffic manager, June, 1932; confd., Aug., 1932.

DARGAN, PATRICK ARTHUR, M.R.C.S., L.R.C.P., Lt.-Col., I.M.S. (civil surgeon, Punjab) (b. 1st Aug., 1888).—First commn., 1st Nov., 1920; civil surgeon, Punjab, Apl., 1931.

DARLEY, Sir BERNARD D'OLIER, Kt., C.I.E., A.M.I.C.E., late Indian Service of Engrs. (b. 24th Aug., 1880).—Trained at the R.I.E. Coll.;

joined the service, 1st Oct., 1903, as asst. engr.; exec. engr., Oct., 1911; C.I.E., Jan., 1919; supt. engr., March, 1921; offg. chief engr. and joint sec. to govt., March, 1924; confd., Oct., 1925; Kt., Dec., 1928; on spl. duty in P.W.D., Nov., 1931; retd., Mar., 1934; apptd. commr. for Superior Services (India) Family Pensions Fund, Oct., 1937.

DARLING, GEORGE KENNETH, C.I.E., B.A., Indian C.S. (member, bd. of rev., United Provs.) (b. 10th May, 1879).—Educ. at Eton and King's Coll., Cambridge; apptd. after exam. of 1902; arrived, 1st Dec., 1903, and served in the United Provs. as asst. mag. and collr.; jt. mag., June, 1913; mag. and collr., Feb., 1921; offg. commr., Jan., 1927; confd., July, 1932; C.I.E., June, 1936; member, bd. of rev., United Provs., Oct., 1936.

DARLING, HENRY JOHN, Indian State Ryis. (ch. electrical engr., E.I. Rly.) (b. 24th Jan., 1885).—Joined the service as asst. elec. engr., 13th Oct., 1926; dist. elec. engr., E.I. Rly., Feb., 1928; offg. dep. loco. supt., E.B. Rly., Aug., 1928; confd., Feb., 1930; ch. electrical engr., N.W. Rly., Nov., 1931; offg. ditto, E. I. rly., Oct., 1935; confd., Nov., 1936.

DARLING, MALCOLM LYALL, C.I.E., B.A., Indian C.S. (finl. commr., Punjab) (b. 10th Dec., 1880).—Educ. at Eton and King's Coll., Cambridge; apptd. after exam. of 1903; arrived, 30th Nov., 1904, and served in the Punjab as asst. commr.; tutor and guardian to H.H. the Raja of Devas, Jan., 1907; under sec. to govt. of Punjab Feb., 1911, to July, 1913; dep. commr., and jt. registr., co-operative socs., Punjab, Jan., 1920; commr. of income tax, Oct., 1921; regr. co-op. societies, Oct., 1927; commr., July, 1931. C.I.E., June, 1934; offg. finl. commr. (dev.) and secty. to govt., dev. dept., Jan., 1936; finl. commr., Dec., 1936.

DARRINGTON, VICTOR GEORGE, B.A., Indian Forest Service (dep. consvr., Madras) (b. 25th Nov., 1896).—Joined the service as asst. consvr. on 12th Dec., 1921; dep. consvr., Dec., 1924; senr. instructor, Madras Forest Coll., Dec., 1925; pers. asst. to ch. consvr., Mar., 1931, to Mar., 1934.

DARSHAN SINGH, SARDAR, F.R.H.S., Barr., Indian Agricl. Service (dep. dir. of agric., Punjab) (b. Oct., 1891).—Joined the service as dep. dir. of agric. on 23rd. Dec. 1913.

DART, Rev. JOHN LOVERING CAMPBELL, M.A. (Oxon.), late Indian Eccles. Estabt. (b. 30th May, 1882).—Apptd. chaplain on probation, 30th Sept., 1911; junr. chaplain, Sept., 1914; senr. chaplain, Sept., 1924; offg. Archdeacon of Bombay, June, 1931; retd., Mar., 1936.

DARVELL, HAROLD F., late Marine dept., Bengal (b. 8th Apl., 1881).—Apptd. shipping master, Calcutta, 2nd Dec., 1924; retd., Apl., 1936.

DARWIN, JOHN HENRY, C.I.E., late Indian C.S. (b. 27th Oct., 1884).—Educ. at Charterhouse Sch., and St. John's Coll., Cambridge; apptd. after exam. of 1908; arrived, 25th Nov., 1909, and served in the United Provs. as asst. mag. and collr.; jt. mag., Sept., 1917; on mil. service (I.A.R.O.) Sept., 1918, to Jan., 1919; dep. dir. of civil supplies, United Prov., Jan.,

1919; acting mag. and collr., May, 1921; confd. Feb., 1925; dep. secty. to govt., genl. branch, Sept., 1927; C.I.E., June, 1932; reforms commr., Oct., 1936; retd., Nov., 1937.

DARWOOD, ARTHUR JOHN, Barr., late Burma Commission.—Apptd. dist. judge, March, 1921; dist. and sess. judge, Dec., 1922; retd., April, 1928.

DAS, AMARENDRA NATH, Rai Bahadur, M.A., Bihar C.S. (secty. bd. of rev.) (b. 16th July, 1885).—Joined the service, 28th Nov., 1905; dep. mag. and dep. collr., May, 1907; offg. mag. and collr., Jan., 1928; confd., July, 1935; offg. secty., rev. bd., June, 1933; secty. bd. of rev., Dec., 1935.

DAS, AMRITA LAL, B.E., Indian Service of Engrs. (asst. exec. engr., Sind) (b. 1st Feb., 1906).—Joined the service as student, 9th May, 1928; asst. exec. engr., May, 1929; offg. exec. engr., May, 1933.
