Univ.; apptd. after exam. of 1914; arrived in India, Dec., 1915, and served in Bengal as asst. mag. and collr.; I.A.R.O., Aug., 1916, to March, 1919; offg. mag. and collr., July, 1922; offg. dist. and sess. judge, Assam, Feb., 1923; magte. and collr., Sept., 1928; offg. secty. educ. dept., Bengal, Feb., 1937; confd., May, 1937.

DOWLEY, FRANCIS MICHAEL, A.M.I.C.E., Indian Service of Engrs. (chief engr., Madras) (b. 29th Sept., 1885).—Joined the service, 1st Jan., 1907, as asst. engr.; exec. engr., Jan., 1915; on mil. duty from June, 1915, to July, 1920; offg. engr.-in-ch., Cauvery (Mettur) project, July, 1929; supt. engr., Nov., 1934; chief engr., Sept., 1936.

DOWLING, LIONEL EDWARD, Indian State Railways (exec. engr., G.I.P. rly.) (b. 30th Aug., 1885).—Joined the service, 4th June, 1915; resdt. engr., G.I.P. rly., Sept., 1919; exec. engr., May, 1932.

DOWNING, FRANCIS WILLIAM HALL, Indian Service of Engrs. (exec. engr.) (b. 26th Mar., 1901).—Joined the service as asst. exec. engr., N.W.F.P., on 30th Sept., 1924; offg. exec. engr., Feb., 1932; confd., Sept., 1933.

DRACUP, ATHELSTANE HAMLEIGH, B.A. (Cantab.), Bombay C.S. (collr. and dist. magte.) (b. 26th Oct., 1887).—Joined the service, Dec., 1913; dep. collr., Dec., 1915; offg. under-secty. to Govt., Bombay, genl., educ., marine and eccl. depts., Mar., 1925; offg. collr. and dist. magte., Mar., 1933; confd., Dec., 1934.

DRAKE, ANTONY ELLIOT, Indian C.S. (b. 15th June, 1907).—Educ. at Aldenham School and New College, Oxford; apptd. after exam. of 1930; arrived in India, Nov., 1931, and served in Bihar and Orissa as asst. magte. and collr.; under-secty. to resdt. Rajputana, Mar., 1937.

DRAKE, DONALD HENRY CHARLES, C.I.E., late Indian C.S. (b. 23rd Aug., 1887).—Educ. at Blundell's School, Tiverton, and Christ Church Coll., Oxford; apptd. after exam. of 1911; arrived, 22nd Nov., 1912, and served in the Central Provs. as asst. commr.; on deputn. as addl. asst. master, Mayo Coll., Ajmer, from Feb., 1915; guardian to the Ticca Sahib of Poona, Dec., 1918, to Nov., 1919; dep. commr. (provl.), Nov., 1924; confd., June, 1925; pol. agent, C.P. Feudatory States, March, 1928; on special duty under A.G.G., Eastern States, Apl., 1933; C.I.E., June, 1933; offg. commr., Feb., 1934; retd., Aug., 1937.

DRAKE, JOHN COLLARD BERNARD, C.S.I., C.I.E., C.B.E., B.A., late Indian C.S. (b. 7th Mar., 1884).—Educ. at Blundell's Sch., Tiverton, and Balliol Coll., Oxford; apptd. after exam. of 1907; arrived, 24th Nov., 1908, and served in Bengal as asst. mag. and collr.; transf. to Bihar and Orissa, April, 1912; under-sec. to govt., rev. dept., Nov., 1912; ditto, pol., app. and educn. depts., April, 1915; pte. sec. to lieut.-govr., Nov., 1915; jt. mag. and dep. collr., and ditto, March, 1916; on mil. duty from Sept., 1918 (2nd lt., 20 Deccan Horse); spl. duty, for. and pol. dept., govt. of India, Dec., 1918, to Jan., 1919; under-sec. to govt. of India, rev. and agri. dept., 1919; O.B.E., Jan., 1919; dep. sec. to govt. of India, dept. of industries, Feb., 1921; sec. to high commr. for India, 8th Sept., 1924 to 7th Nov., 1929; actg. high commr. for India, 26th Sept. to 5th Nov., 1924, and 11th

Dec., 1924, to Jan., 1925; C.I.E., July, 1926; jt. secty., govt. of India, dept. of commerce; C.B.E., July, 1930; secty. govt. of India, dept. of commerce, Mar., 1932; C.S.I., Jan., 1933; retd., July, 1935.

DRAKE-BROCKMAN, Sir DIGBY LIVINGSTONE, C.S.I., C.I.E., B.A., late Indian C.S. (chairman, United Provs. public serv. commr.) (b. 21st Dec., 1877).—Educ. at Dulwich Coll., and Christ Church, Oxford; apptd. after exam. of 1900; arrived, 5th Dec., 1901, and served in the United Provs. as asst. mag. and collr., and asst. settlmt. offr.; jt. mag., Dec., 1911; settlt. offr. Oct., 1912; jt. regr., co-operative credit societies, Nov., 1916; dep. commr. (temp.), Sept., 1917; ditto, and settlt. offr., Oct., 1917; employed in Jodhpur State, from Nov., 1920; revenue member, Nov., 1922; C.I.E., June, 1927; offg. commr., Nov., 1929; commr. (provl. sub.), Aug., 1930; C.S.I. Jan., 1933; offg. member, bd. of rev., Mar., 1933; confd., Dec., 1934; presdt., bd. of health, United Provs., Oct., 1935; retd., Dec., 1936; knighted, Feb., 1937; chairman, United Provs. public service commn., Apl., 1937.

DRIVID, NILKANTH KRISHNA, B.A., B.Sc., Indian C.S. (asst. collr., Bombay) (b. 24th Sept., 1908).—Educ. at London School of Economics and School of Oriental Studies, London; joined the Service, 12th Oct., 1931; arrived in India, Nov., 1931, and served in Bombay as asst. collr.

DRAYSON, GEORGE FREDERICK, Office of High Commr. for India (higher executive officer, store dept.) (b. 11th Dec., 1884).—Apptd. 2nd class clerk, 8th Aug., 1904; on milv. service from Dec., 1915, to Sept., 1919; transf. to office of high commr. for India, Oct., 1920; exec. officer, April, 1922; pers. asst. to high commr., July, 1927; actg. higher exec. officer, Oct., 1928; confd., June, 1929.

DREDGE, ALLAN LANCELOT AUSTIN, Capt., I.A., Indian Political Service (b. 14th Nov., 1907).—First commn., 1st Sept., 1927; in mil. employ up to Oct., 1933; under-secty. to A.G.G., Baluchistan, Oct., 1933; officer on special duty; Peshawar, Dec., 1935; under-secty. to govt., N.W.F.P., Jan., 1936; officer on special duty, civil secretariat, Peshawar, Feb., 1936; asst. pol. agent N. Waziristan, Mar., 1936; vice-consul, Zahidan, Nov., 1937.

DREWE, GEOFFREY GRABHAM, B.A., Indian C.S. (asst. collr., Bombay) (b. 3rd May, 1904).—Educ. at Cheltenham, Pembroke Coll., Oxford, and School of Oriental Studies, London. Joined the service, 12th Oct., 1928; arrived, Nov., 1928, and served in Bombay as asst. collr.; offg. collr., Mar., 1932; offg. dep. commr., Feb., 1935.

DREYFUS, BERTRAM EDWARD, late Indian C.S. (United Provs.) (b. 8th July, 1890).—Educ. at City of London School, and Queen's Coll., Cambridge; apptd. after exam. of 1913; arrived in India, 30th Nov., 1914, and served in the United Prov. as asst. mag. and collr.; on mil. duty (I.A.R.O.), Sept., 1916, to Jan., 1919; jt. mag., May, 1918; offg. dep. commr., April, 1923; offg. mag. and collr., April, 1927; offg. dep. commr. Dec., 1927; retd., Oct., 1931.

DRING, ARTHUR JOHN, Capt., I.A. Indian Political Service (b. 4th Nov., 1902).—First commn., 1st Feb., 1923; in milv. employ to June, 1927; asst. pte. secty. to govt.

genl., 16th July, 1931; asst. commr., Tank, Feb., 1934; ditto. Dira Ismail Khan, Feb., 1935; dep. secty. to the govr.-genl's. exec. council (temp.), May, 1936; pte. secty. to govr., N.W.F.P., Mar., 1937; secty., ditto, Apl., 1937.

D'ROZARIO, WILFRED ALBERT VICTOR, late India Posts and Tels. (b. 7th Feb., 1875).—Joined the service, 25th Nov., 1893; asst. supt., Oct., 1917; supt., Calcutta central tel. office, April, 1925; retd., Sept., 1929.

DRUCQUER, SETH, B.A., Indian C.S. (jt. mag. and dep. collr., Bengal) (b. 31st May, 1911).—Educ. at Winchester Coll., Trinity Hall, Cambridge, and the Sch. of Oriental Studies, London; apptd. after exam. of 1933; arrived in India, Nov., 1934, and served in Bengal as asst. mag. and collr.

DRUMMOND, JOHN GRAHAM, late Indian C.S. (b. 28th Oct., 1884).—Educ. at Loretto and Christ's Coll., Cambridge; apptd. after exam. of 1907; arrived, 5th Dec., 1908, and served in Bengal as asst. mag. and collr.; on special duty in connection with Arms Act Manual, May to June, 1914; jt. mag. and dep. collr., Dec., 1914; I.A.R.O., Aug., 1916 to Feb. 1919; mag. and collr., Jan., 1922; offg. sec., govt. of Bengal, local self-govt. dept., Feb., 1926; confd., June, 1926; offg. commr., Mar., 1929; retd., Feb., 1936.

DRUMMOND, ROBERT OLIVE, B.Sc., Indian Forest Service (dep. consvr.).—Joined the service as asst. consvr., United Provs., 11th Oct., 1929; dep. consvr., Nov., 1934.

D'SOUZA, FRANK, Indian State Rlys. (dir., rly. bd.) (b. 6th Dec., 1883).—Held non-gazetted appt. from 28th Nov., 1902 to 15th Nov., 1918; offg. asst. traffic supt., Nov., 1918; confd., Jan., 1922; offg. dep. dir. of traffic and statistics, rly. bd., Mar., 1929; confd., May, 1932; offg. dir., ditto, Apl., 1931; May, 1932; confd., June, 1934.

D'SOUZA, MICHAEL, India Posts and Tels. (chief supt.) (b. 8th Jan., 1887).—Held non-gazetted posts from 3rd Aug., 1903; supt. tel. traffic, July, 1925; ch. supt., Jan., 1936.

DUBE, BHAGWATI CHARAN, Indian Service of Engrs. (suptg. engr., Central Provs.) (b. 2nd Jan., 1881).—Trained at T.C.E. Coll.; joined the service, 28th Oct., 1903, and posted to Central Provs.; exec. engr., Oct., 1912; under sec. to the chief commr., public wks. dept., June, 1916, to July, 1918; offg. suptg. engr., March, 1919; confd. as suptg. engr., Feb., 1923.

DUBE, KAMLAKAR (see Kamlakar Dube).

DUBE, SHAMBHU NATH (see Shambhu Nath Dube).

DUBOIS, BASIL ALLEN, Indian Police (supt., Bombay) (b. 5th Mar., 1887).—Joined the service, 5th July, 1908; dep. supt., June, 1910; promoted to I.P.S. as supt., April, 1928.

DU BOULAY, Sir JAMES HOUSSEMYNE, K.C.I.E., C.S.I., late Indian C.S.—Educ. at Winchester and Balliol Coll., Oxford; apptd. after exam. of 1887; arrived, 1st Dec., 1889, and served in Bombay as asst. collr., and mag., and forest settlt. officer; 2nd asst., May, 1896; 1st asst., April, 1899; pte. sec. to the governor, Dec., 1903; C.I.E., June, 1906; junr. collr., March, 1907; sec. to govt., pol., spl. and judl. depts., April, 1909; priv. sec. to H.E. the

Viceroy, Nov., 1910; K.C.I.E., Dec., 1911; commr., and pte. sec. to Viceroy, Dec., 1915; C.S.I., June, 1916; sec. to govt. of India, home dept., and addl. member, govr.-genl.'s legisl. council, from Oct., 1916; temp. member, govr.-genl.'s exec. council, Oct., 1917, to Feb., 1918; temp. member of exec. council, Bombay, March, 1918, to Sept., 1918; on deptn. as member of jail cttee. from April, 1919; ret'd., July, 1922.

DUCKFIELD, WILLIAM EDMUND, Indian Police (supt., Bengal) (b. 28th June, 1895).—Joined the service as asst. supt. on 2nd Oct., 1920; addl. supt., March, 1924; offg. supt., April, 1928; confd., Aug., 1928; dep. commr., Calcutta, Oct., 1935.

DUCKWORTH, SIR EDWARD DYCE, Bart., B.A., late Indian C.S.—Educ. at Marlborough Coll. and Pembroke Coll., Oxford; apptd. after exam. of 1898; arrived, 20th Dec., 1899, and served in Burma as asst. commr.; dist. judge, Sept., 1910; divl. judge, Sept., 1921; temp. addl. judge, chief ct., Lower Burma, Jan., 1922; actg. judge, high ct., Rangoon, March, 1923; judge, high ct., Rangoon, Nov., 1924; ret'd., Jan., 1927; succeeded to baronetcy, 1928.

DUGGAN, SIR JAMSHEDJI NASARVANJI, Kt., C.I.E., O.B.E., L.M. & S., D.O. (Oxon.), F.C.P.S. (prof. of ophthalmic medicine and surgery. G.M. Coll., and supt., C.J. Ophthalmic Hosp., Bombay) (b. 8th April, 1884).—Apptd. prof. of ophthalmic medicine and surgery, C.J. ophthalmic hosp., 1st Jan., 1925; O.B.E., June, 1929; C.I.E., June, 1933; knighted, Jan., 1935.

DU HEAUME, FRANCIS HERBERT, O.B.E., Indian Police (supt., N.W.F.P.) (b. 27th May, 1897).—Joined the service, 27th Jan., 1920; asst. dist. officer, frontier constabulary, April, 1922; asst. supt., Dec., 1925; officer in charge, intell. branch, N.W.F.P., May, 1926; supt., Feb., 1927; dist. officer, frontier constabulary, May, 1929; O.B.E., Jan., 1932; princpl. trng. school Phillaur, Apl. 1934.

DUKE CHARLES BERESFORD, Indian C.S. Indian Political Service (b. 19th Dec., 1905).—Educ. at Charterhouse and Lincoln Coll., Oxford; joined the service, 11th Oct., 1929; arrived November, 1929, and served in the United Provinces as asst. mag. and collr.; under-secty. to A.G.G. in C.I., Feb., 1934; asst. pte. secty. to Viceroy, Nov., 1934, to Jan., 1938.

DUKE, JAMES ALEXANDER, Indian Police (dep. inspr. genl., Central Provs.) (b. 26th Aug., 1887).—Joined the service, Nov., 1907; asst. dist. supt., June, 1909; dist. supt., Oct., 1918; offg. dep. inspr.-genl., Feb., 1930; confd., June, 1933.

DUKE, JOSEPH ALGERNON HARE. (b. 27th Mar., 1881).—Apptd. oil expert, technological institute, Cawnpore, 30th Oct., 1922; offg. princpl., ditto, Apl., 1926; offg. dir. of industries, U.P., July, 1932; ret'd., Mar., 1936.

DUKE, WILLIAM VICTOR, M.A., late Indian Educt. Service (b. 14th Feb., 1886).—Joined the service, 19th August, 1912, as prof., Ravenshaw Coll., Cuttack; inspr. of schools, June, 1915; asst. dir. of public instrn., Bihar and Orissa, Dec., 1921; princpl., Greer Bhumihar Brahman Coll., Muzaffarpur, Oct., 1924; prinpl., Ravenshaw Coll., July, 1931; ret'd., Aug., 1936.

DULAT, SHAMSHER SINGH, M.A., Indian C.S. (asst. commr., Punjab) (b. 3rd April, 1904).—Educ. at University of the Punjab and King's Coll., Cambridge; joined the service, 8th Oct., 1928; arrived, Nov., 1928, and served in the Punjab as asst. commr.; offg. dep. commr., April, 1933; offg. dist. and sess. judge, May, 1934; addl. ditto, July, 1934.

DUMBELL, PERCY HAROLD, M.A., late India Office.—Educ. at Wolverhampton Gr. Sch. and University Coll., Oxford; apptd. junr. clerk, rev. and stat. dept., 9th Feb., 1903; transfed. to mil. dept., May, 1903; transfed. to pol. dept., Oct., 1903; reading clerk to Council, Oct., 1903; private sec. to parly. under-sec. of State, Mar., 1906; asst. private sec. to sec. of state, and précis writer, March, 1907; senior clerk, March, 1910; sec. to cttee. on state technical scholarships, April to Sept., 1912; on mily. service from Jan., 1917, to Dec., 1918; asst. sec., Nov., 1919; ditto, judl. and public dept., Dec., 1919; dep. clerk of council, April, 1921; sec., services and general dept. and establishment officer, April, 1924; ret'd., June, 1929.

DUN, U. BA, Barr., Burma Judl Dept. (b. 21st June, 1884).—Apptd. dep. govt. advocate and secty. to Burma legisl. council, 28th Jan., 1926.

DUNBAR, ARCHIBALD, M.A., late Indian Police (b. 3rd Oct., 1886).—Joined the service as asst. supt. on 4th Dec., 1906; dist. supt., Aug., 1917; offg. dep. inspr.-genl., Dec., 1926; confd., June, 1933; awarded King's police medal, Jan., 1932; ret'd., Feb., 1937.

DUNCAN, ARCHIBALD WALLACE, Lt.-Col., I.M.S., Civil Medl. Dept., Bihar (civil surg.) (b. 11th July, 1884).—First commn. dated 29th July, 1911; in mil. employ up to 1st April, 1923; offg. civil surg., April, 1923 confd., Nov., 1924.

DUNCAN, JOHN ELDER, B.Sc., A.M.I.C.E., Indian Service of Engrs. (exec. engr., Burma) (b. 30th Jan., 1898).—Joined the service as asst. exec. engr., 1st Oct., 1921; exec. engr., Oct., 1930.

DUNCAN, JOHN SAMUEL, B.Sc., F.G.S. late Indian Service of Engrs. (b. 20th April, 1894).—Joined the service as asst. exec. engr., on 25th Feb., 1921; offg. exec. engr., Jan., 1928; confd., Feb., 1929; ret'd., Sept., 1932.

DUNCAN, PERCY RICHARD, B.Sc., Indian Forest Service (dep. consvr., United Provs.) (b. 15th Oct., 1889).—Joined the service as forest engr., United Provs., on 1st Dec., 1921; dep. consvr., Aug., 1930.

DUNCAN, ROBERT SYMON, Indian Service of Engrs. (exec. engr.) (b. 27th Sept., 1901).—Joined the service as asst. exec. engr., Punjab, on 25th Sept., 1925; exec. engr., Sept., 1934.

DUNDAS, AMBROSE DUNDAS FLUX, C.I.E., Indian C.S., Indian Political Service (b. 14th Apl., 1899).—Educ. at Harrow and Christ Church Coll., Oxford; joined the service, 30th Oct., 1922; arrived, 14th Dec., 1922, and served in United Prov. as asst. mag. and collr.; asst. commr., March, 1924; jt. mag., Oct., 1924; offg. mag. and collr., May, 1925; asst. commr., Dera Ismail Khan, Sept., 1925; pers. asst. to C.-C. and A.G.G., N.W.F. Prov., Dec., 1925; asst. commr., Charsadda, Nov., 1927; pol. agent, N. Waziristan, Dec., 1928; ditto, Dir, Swat and

Chitral, Apl., 1932; secty. to govt., N.W.F.P. (transfd. depts.), Nov. 1932; ch. secty. to govt., N.W.F.P., Dec., 1933; dep. commr., Peshawar, Apl., 1934; C.I.E., June, 1936; employed on spl. duty under For. and Pol. dept., Nov., 1936; secty. to resdt. for Punjab states, Mar., 1937; chief secty. to govt., N.W.F.P., Oct., 1937.

DUNDON, GEORGE HEWITT, M.C., Indian Service of Engrs. (exec. engr., Punjab) (b. 11th May, 1893).—Joined the service, 1st Oct., 1919; asst. exec. engr., Jan., 1920; exec. engr., Oct., 1925; under-secty., public wks. dept. (irrign. branch), Mar., 1929; under-secty., Western admin., irrign. bch., June, 1937.

DUNKLEY, HERBERT FRANCIS, M.A., Barr., Indian C.S. (judge, high ct., Rangoon) (b. 2nd July, 1886).—Educ. at Wellington Grammar Sch., and St. John's Coll., Cambridge; apptd. after exam. of 1909; arrived in India, 6th Dec., 1910, and served in Burma as asst. commr.; regr., chief court, Lower Burma, Jan., 1919, to Nov., 1921; dist. and sessions judge, Dec., 1922; offg. regr., high ct., Rangoon, Sept., 1926; acting judge, high ct., Rangoon, Nov., 1932; confd., Nov., 1933.

DUNLOP, Rev. Canon DOUGLAS LYALL CHANDLEE, late Indian Eccles. Estabt. (b. 5th Aug., 1881).—Apptd. chaplain on probation, 22nd Oct., 1914; junr. chaplain, Oct., 1917; in milty. employ from 2nd Oct., 1918, to 16th June, 1919; senr. chaplain, Oct., 1927; ret'd., July, 1934.

DUNLOP, Sir R. W. L., Kt., C.I.E., D.S.O., late Indian Legisl. Dept.—Apptd. solicitor to govt. of India, Jan., 1920; Kt., June, 1925; ret'd., July, 1927.

DUNLOP, SIDNEY WILLIAM CECIL, B.A., Indian C.S. (asst. mag. and collr., Madras) (b. 9th May, 1914).—Educ. at Epsom Coll. and Downing Coll., Cambridge; joined the service, 20th Sept., 1937; arrived in India, Oct., 1937, and served in Madras as asst. mag. and collr.

DUNLOP, WALTER NIGEL USHER, B.A. (Oxon.), late Indian C.S. (Bomb y) (b. 5th Dec., 1892).—Educ. at Fettes Coll., and Christ Ch., Oxford; joined the service, 24th Oct., 1919; arrived, 29th Dec., 1919, and served in Bombay as asst. collr.; asst. judge, April, 1923; offg. judge and sess. judge March, 1925; offg. under-secty. to govt., home and eccl. depts., March, 1928; ret'd., March, 1929.

DUNN, ARTHUR GEORGE, Indian State Rlys. (dist. loco. supt., Burma rlys.) (b. 2nd Jan., 1898).—Joined the service, 25th Nov., 1922, as asst. loco. supt.; dist. ditto, Burma rlys., May, 1929; offg. transptn. officer (power), Jan., 1931; offg. dep. carr. and wagon supt., Burma rlys., Mar., 1934.

DUNN, CHARLES WILLIAM, C.I.E., B.A., late Indian C.S. (b. 26th March, 1877).—Educ. at Bath Coll. and Trinity Coll., Cambridge; apptd. after exam. of 1899; arrived, 3rd Dec., 1900, and served in Burma as asst. commr. and settlt. offr.; under sec. to govt., Dec., 1904, to Nov., 1906; dep. dir. of land records, Nov., 1909; offg. registrar under the Co-operative Societies Act, from May, 1914; dir. of agric., and asst. to regr., co-operative societies dept., July, 1915; dep. commr., and ditto, Sept., 1914; regr. co-op. societies, Oct.,

1920, to Nov., 1923; C.I.E., Jan., 1924; offg. development commr., June, 1927; offg. finl. commr., July, 1927; confd., Dec., 1927; finl. commr. (transfd. subjects) and offg. ditto (reserved subjects), Oct., 1931; ret'd., Apl., 1935.

DUNN, CUTHBERT LINDSAY, C.I.E., D.P.H., Lt.-Col. I.M.S. (ret'd.) (b. 15th May, 1875).—First commn., 1st Sept., 1902; in mil. employ to Dec., 1904; asst. plague medl. officer, United Prov., Dec., 1904; dep. sanitary commr., May, 1912; on mil. duty, Oct., 1914, to April, 1919; sanitary commr., July, 1919; C.I.E., Jan., 1928; ret'd., May, 1930; re-employed after retirement as dir. of public health, United Provs., for two years from 15th Nov. 1930.

DUNN, JOHN ALEXANDER, D.Sc., D.I.C., F.G.S., Geological Survey Dept. (geologist) (b. 27th June, 1899).—Apptd. asst. supt., 17th Dec., 1921, curator, geol. museum, Calcutta, May, 1929; offg. supt., Apl., 1931.

DUNN, STANLEY GERALD, M.A. (Oxon.), late Indian Educl. Service (United Provs.) (b. 7th Oct., 1879).—Joined the service, 23rd Nov., 1908, and apptd. prof'r., Muir Central Coll., Allahabad; empld. under govt. of India, army dept., from July to Sept., 1918, and from Oct., 1918, to March, 1919; prof'r., Allahabad univ., Oct., 1923; ret'd., Sept., 1931; author of 'The Treasure of the Sea and other Poems,' 'In England,' 'The Indian Treasury of English Verse,' &c.

DUNN, SYDNEY ERNEST, Office of High Commr. for India (exec. officer, store dept.) (b. 26th Nov., 1899).—Apptd. asst. clerk, 22nd Oct., 1919, after previous service from 25th April, 1917; second class clerk, 29th April, 1920; transfd. to office of high commr. for India, Oct., 1920; exec. officer, April, 1922.

DUNNETT, Sir JAMES MACDONALD, K.C.I.E., M.A., late Indian C.S. (b. 11th April, 1877).—Educ. at Kilmarnock Acad., and Edinburgh Univ.; apptd. after exam. of 1900; arrived, 2nd Dec., 1901, and served in the Punjab as asst. commr. and asst. settlmt. offr.; junior sec. to fin. commrs., Nov., 1913, to Dec., 1916; dep. commr., Oct., 1917; C.I.E., June, 1922; sec. to govt. of Punjab, home dept., Oct., 1924; offg. chief sec., Dec., 1925; offg. jt. sec., home dept., govt. of India, from Oct. to Dec., 1926; confd., Dec., 1926; on spl. duty, home dept., July, 1927; addl. secty., ditto, Nov., 1929; reforms commr., May, 1930; Kt., June, 1932; on depn. in connection with Indian round table conf., Nov.-Dec., 1932; K.C.I.E., June, 1934; ret'd., Nov., 1936.

DUNNICLIFF, HORACE BARRATT, M.A., D.Sc., F.I.C., Indian Educl. Service (princpl., govt. coll., Lahore) (b. 23rd Sept., 1885).—Joined the service as prof'r., Khalsa Coll., Amritsar, on 7th March, 1914; prof'r., govt. coll., Lahore, Aug., 1917; chemist, cordite factory, Aruvankadu, Nov., 1918; prof'r., govt. coll., Lahore, April, 1921; spl. chemical adviser for customs, Calcutta, Oct., 1928; princpl., govt. coll., Lahore, May, 1936.

DUNSDON, ARTHUR CULHAM, Indian State Railways (exec. engr., E.I. rly.) (b. 28th June, 1887).—Joined the service, 9th Oct., 1913; resdt. engr., E.I. rly., Oct., 1921; exec. engr., ditto, Aug., 1927; offg. dep. ch. engr., Oct., 1934.

DURAIRAJA AYYAR, NARAYANASWAMI AYYAR, M.A., B.E., Indian Service of Engrs. (exec. engr., Madras) (b. 17th Mar., 1891).—Joined the service as appr. engr., 13th Oct., 1915; asst. engr., Oct., 1916; asst. exec. engr., Sept., 1920; exec. engr., Oct., 1925.

DURGA PRASAD ROHATGI, Indian Service of Engrs. (exec. engr., United Provs.) (b. 15th Feb., 1899).—Joined the service, 12th Oct., 1922; apprentice engr., June, 1923; asst. exec. engr., Oct., 1923; offg. exec. engr., Apl., 1928; confd., Oct., 1932.

DURGA PRASHAD, M.A., Rai Bahadur, late Bihar and Orissa Civil Service (b. 16th Aug., 1878).—Joined the service, 28th July, 1902; dep. mag. and dep. collr., Nov., 1903; mag. and collr., Feb., 1926; offg. registrar, co-operative societies, July, 1926; confd., Nov., 1926; retd., Aug., 1933.

DURNO, LESLIE GEORGE, Indian C.S. (dep. commr., Bengal) (b. 27th July, 1892).—Educ. at Rugby and Univ. Coll., Oxford; joined the service, 30th Sept., 1921; arrived, 15th Nov., 1921, and served in Bengal as asst. mag. and collr.; addl. mag. and collr., Oct., 1925; offg. mag. and collr., Feb., 1927; confd., June, 1928; offg. dep. sec. finance, commerce and marine depts., Bengal, May, 1932; confd., July, 1933; dep. commr., Mar., 1937.

DUROISSELLE, CHARLES, M.A., late Archaeological Survey dept. (b. 25th June, 1871).—Joined the service, 1st Jan., 1904; apptd. supt., archaeological survey, Burma circle, 7th Dec., 1919; retd., June, 1931; offg. as supt. archae. survey, Mar., 1936.

DURRANT, GEORGE, Indian Police (supt. Punjab) (b. 9th Jan., 1898).—Joined the service, 12th Oct., 1914; in mil. employ from 12th Oct., 1914, to 5th Oct., 1922; asst. supt., Oct., 1922; offg. supt., Nov., 1925; supt. (prov. subs.), Jan., 1935; confd., Feb., 1936.

DURRELL, WILLIAM HENRY, Indian State Ryls. (elec. engr., Burma rlys.) (b. 6th Sept., 1890).—Joined the service, 26th Jan., 1914; offg. asst. elec. engr., Sept., 1919; confd., Jan., 1920; dist. elec. engr., Dec., 1927; offg. dep. loco. supt. (elec.), Oct., 1930.

DURST, THOMAS ORAM, India Office (higher executive officer, acctnt.-gen.'s dept.) (b. 25th Nov., 1889).—Educ. at Emanuel Sch., Wandsworth Comm.; second div. clerk, bd. of edn., April, 1909, to Nov., 1911; apptd. second class clerk, India Office, 27th Nov., 1911, and attd. to acctnt.-gen.'s dept.; on mil. service from May, 1915, to Jan., 1919; higher clerical officer, April, 1922; higher executive officer, Sept., 1926; assessor of inc. tax, Jan., 1928.

DUTCH, ROBERT AUSTEN, B.A. (Cantab.), Indian C.S. (jt. mag. and dy. collr., Bengal) (b. 22nd Oct., 1905).—Educ. at St. Olave's Grammar School and Christ's Coll., Cambridge; joined the service, 11th Oct., 1929; arrived, Dec., 1929, and served in Bengal as asst. coll. and mag.; jt. mag. and dep. collr., Aug., 1931; offg. mag. and collr., Feb., 1936.

DUTT (see also Datta).

DUTT, BIRENDRA CHANDRA, M.A., Finance dept., govt. of India (b. 21st Nov., 1895).—Joined the service as probnr., 28th Feb., 1920; asst. acct. genl., Bengal, Nov., 1922; ditto, Madras, Feb., 1924; ditto central

revs., Mar., 1926; currency officer, Lahore, Apl., 1927; ditto, Calcutta, Jan., 1931; exr., local fund acct., Bengal, Oct., 1933; exr., outside audit, Bengal, Apl., 1935.

DUTT, DEBPATI (b. 1st Apl., 1884).—Joined the service, 1905. Asst. secy., Legis. Assembly Dept., 10th Jan., 1929.

DUTT, DIVAKAR, B.A., Indian State Ryls. (exec. engr., N.W. Rly.) (b. 3rd Dec., 1886).—Joined the service as asst. engr., 1st June, 1915; asst. exec. engr., Jan., 1921; exec. engr., June, 1924; offg. dep. agent, N.W. rly., May, 1935.

DUTT, GURU SADAY, Indian C.S., Barr. (secy. to govt., local self-govt. dept., Bengal) (b. 10th May, 1882).—Educ. at Govt. High Sch., Sylhet, and Calcutta Univ.; apptd. after exam. of 1904; arrived, 11th Dec., 1905; and served in Bengal as asst. mag. and collr.; jt. mag. and dep. collr., Oct., 1911; mag. and collr., Oct., 1917; sec. to govt. of Bengal, agric. and industry depts., June, 1924; offg. commr., Aug., 1927; offg. dir. of industries, Bengal, June, 1933; secy. to govt., Bengal, local self-govt., dept., Feb., 1934; K-i-H. Medal, 1st class, Jan., 1938.

DUTT, NARESH CHANDRA, B.A., Indian Posts and Tels. (dep. P.M.G.) (b. 7th Feb., 1884).—Joined the service, 26th Jan., 1905; supt. of post offices, Jan., 1909; dep. P.M.G., Dec., 1935.

DUTT, PAVITRA KUMAR, Office of High Commr. for India (educ. officer, suptg. exec. grade, educn. dept.) (b. 25th Mar., 1888).—Apptd. educ. officer, 8th Feb., 1929; jt. social secy. to Indian delegation to round table conf., London, Oct., 1930; and again, Sept., 1931.

DUTT, PRATAP CHANDRA, late Indian C.S. (b. 4th Feb., 1876).—Educ. at Calcutta Univ. and New Coll., Oxford; apptd. after exam. of 1898; arrived, 25th Dec., 1899, and served in Madras as asst. collr. and mag.; sub-collr. and joint mag., May, 1910; collr. and dist. mag., Aug., 1919; on foreign service under Travancore durbar, Mar., 1930; retd., Dec., 1934.

DUTT, RABINDRA CHANDRA, B.A., Indian C.S. (asst. mag. and collr., Bengal) (b. 5th Nov., 1912).—Educ. at Hare Sch., Calcutta, Calcutta Univ., and Corpus Christi Coll., Cambridge; joined the service, 20th Sept., 1937; arrived in India, Nov., 1937, and served in Bengal as asst. mag. and collr.

DUTT, SUBIMAL, B.Sc.(Cal.), Indian C.S. (jt. magte. and dep. collr., Bengal) (b. 13th March, 1905).—Educ. at University of Calcutta and School of Oriental Studies, London; joined the service, 28th Sept., 1928; arrived, Oct., 1928, and served in Bengal as asst. mag. and collr.; jt. mag. and dep. collr., Aug., 1929; offg. mag. and collr., Feb., 1935; addl. dist. and sess. judge, Jan., 1936; offg. dep. commr., Apl., 1936.

DUTTON, HUGH REGINALD, C.I.E., F.R.C.P., Lt.-Col., I.M.S. (retd.), late Medical Dept., Bihar and Orissa (member, medl. bd., India Office) (b. 21st Nov., 1875).—First commn., 1st Sept., 1902; in mil. employ till March, 1920; supt., central jail, Bhagalpur, May, 1920; civil surgeon, Patna, March, 1922; prinpl., Prince of Wales medical coll., Patna, March 1925; C.I.E., June, 1930; retd., Nov.,

1930; addl. member, medl. bd., India Office, Jan., 1931; examr. of surgical instruments, India store dept., Apl., 1932, to May, 1934; member, medl. bd., India Office, May, 1934.

DYER, REV. BASIL SAUNDERS, late Indian Eccles. Estabt. (b. 28th Feb., 1880).—Joined the service, 14th Oct., 1909; junr. chaplain, Aug., 1910; senr. chaplain, Jan., 1922; retd., July, 1933.

DYER, JAMES FERGUSON, C.I.E., M.A., late Indian C.S. (b. 2nd July, 1880).—Educ. at the High Sch. and Univ., Glasgow; apptd. after exam. of 1902; arrived, 30th Nov., 1903, and served in the Central Provs., as asst. commr., and as regr., judl. commr.'s court; settlt. offr., Feb., 1915; 3rd sec. to the chief commr., Dec., 1916; dep. commr., and ditto, July, 1917; ditto and commr. of settlt. and dir. of land records, &c., Nov., 1922; offg. commr., Jan., 1926; C.I.E., March, 1929; commr., July, 1929; retd., Nov., 1932.

DYSON, WILLIAM GEOFFREY, M.C., B.Sc., Indian Forest Service (dep. consvr., Madras) (b. 7th Sept., 1892).—Joined the service, 6th Dec., 1915, as asst. consvr.; on mil. duty from 6th Dec., 1915, to 3rd Feb., 1919; dep. consvr., Dec., 1920; inspr., Madras forest coll., May, 1923; prinpl., ditto, Aug., 1923; dist. forest officer, July, 1924; actg. consvr., variously since Sept., 1929; provl. sylviculturist, July, 1931.

E, U BA, B.A., late Burma Civil Service (b. 15th May, 1876).—Apptd. extra asst. commr., 1st July, 1916; offg. dep. commr., Feb., 1928; confid., Feb., 1931; retd., May, 1932.

EADES, GERALD JOHN, Indian State Rlys. (dep. ch. engr., signals, N.W. Rly.) (b. 20th July, 1887).—Joined the service as asst. signal engr., 18th Dec., 1912; offg. dist. signal engr., Jan., 1925; confid., Mar., 1931; offg. dep. ch. engr., signals, N.W. Rly., Nov., 1933; confid., Aug., 1934.

EADES, JOHN HENRY P.W. Dept., Burma (elec. inspr.) (b. 21st Sept., 1893).—Joined the service as elec. engr., Nov., 1926; elec. insp., Dec., 1930.

EAGEN, THOMAS GEORGE ROYAL, B.Sc., A.M.I.C.E., Indian State Rlys. (asst. exec. engr., N.W. Rly.) (b. 12th June, 1892).—Joined the service as asst. engr., 1st. Oct., 1919; asst. exec. engr., Nov., 1921; offg. exec. engr., Jan., 1926.

EASSON, GEORGE ALEXANDER, B.Sc., late Indian Service of Engrs. (b. 3rd Dec., 1883).—Joined the service, 1st Oct., 1904; served as asst. engr.; exec. engr., Oct., 1912; and under-sec. to govt. of Bengal, P.W.D., March, 1914; offg. suptg. engr., April, 1921; confd. as suptg. engr., Dec., 1923; offg. ch. engr., June, 1929; confd. Sept., 1929; retd., Aug., 1933.

EASTMAN NAGLE, JAMES NICHOLAS, Indian State Rlys. (dist. traffic supt.) E.B. Rly.) (b. 7th Oct., 1891).—Joined the service as asst. traffic supt., 25th March, 1913; dist. traffic supt., July, 1926; publicity officer, Nov., 1928.

EASTMOND, ARCHIBALD, M.C., M.Inst.C.E., Indian Service of Engrs. (exec. engr., United Provs.) (b. 11th Oct., 1889).—Joined the service as asst. engr. on 1st Oct., 1913; in mil. employ from 16th May, 1915, to 16th Feb., 1919; exec. engr., Oct., 1922.

EASTON, THOMAS WILLIAM ROBERTSON, Indian Service of Engrs. (under secty. to govt. (constrn.), Punjab) (b. 29th Nov., 1896).—Joined the service as asst. exec. engr. 20th Oct., 1922; offg. exec. engr., Dec., 1926; confid., Oct., 1929; under secty. to govt. (constrn.), Burma, Feb., 1936.

EASTWELL, WILFRED HAROLD, Office of High Commr. for India (exec. officer) (b. 1st Mar., 1898).—Apptd. to India Office, store dept., as clerical officer, 1st Dec., 1919, after previous service in General Post Office from 22nd Oct., 1915; on mil. service, Aug., 1916, to Oct., 1919; transf. to office of high commr. for India, 1st Oct., 1920; exec. officer, June, 1922; actg. hr. exec. officer, Sept.-Nov., 1936.

EASTWICK, Rev. ROWLAND, B.A., Indian Eccles. Estab. (junr. chaplain) (b. 16th Aug., 1892).—Apptd. chaplain on probn., 6th Oct., 1929; confid., Oct., 1931.

EAYRES, VERNON JOHN GEORGE, Office of High Commr. for India (higher exec. officer, store dept.) (b. 17th Sept., 1890).—Educ. at Strand Sch., King's Coll.; apptd. 2nd class clerk, 4th Sept., 1908; on mil. service from Aug., 1914, to April, 1917; transf. to office of high commr. for India, Oct., 1920; exec. officer, April, 1922; personal asst. to high commr., Oct., 1928, to Nov., 1936; hr. exec. officer, Nov., 1936.

EBDEN, JAMES WYLDE, B.A. (Cantab.), late Indian Finance Dept. (b. 6th Feb., 1882).—Joined the service, 9th Feb., 1906, as probr.; asst. acctnt.-gen., April, 1909; on mil. service (I.A.R.O.), from April, 1915, to May, 1920; auditor of govt. of India sanctions (civil), Aug., 1921; dep. acctnt.-gen., United Prov., Nov., 1923; offg. dep. auditor-gen., Nov., 1925; offg. dep. dir. of army audit, E.C., Nov., 1926; off. dir. of army audit, June, 1927; dep. dir., army audit, Nov., 1927; offg. ch. auditor, rly. clearing accts., Apl., 1929; offg. acct.-genl., B. & O., Apl., 1930; acct.-genl., Burma, Nov., 1932; dir. of audit, defence services, Oct., 1936; retd., Apl., 1937.

EBDEN, JOHN ALFRED WYLDE, M.D., M.S., F.R.C.S., Major, I.M.S., Medical Dept., Madras (dist. medl. officer) (b. 20th Feb., 1891).—First commn., 6th Dec., 1929; dist. medl. officer, May, 1934.

EDGAR, SAMUEL GARTHWAITE, B.Sc., Indian Service of Engrs. (exec. engr., Burma) (b. 8th July, 1887).—Joined the service as asst. engr., United Provs., on 1st Oct., 1911; exec. engr., Oct., 1919; on foreign service in Jodhpur, Nov., 1929.

EDGCUMBE, GEORGE AUBREY VALLETORT, B.E., Indian Service of Engrs.—Joined the service as asst. exec. engr., Burma, 9th Feb., 1931.

EDGECOMBE, ARTHUR RALPH BROOKS, B.Sc., Indian Service of Engrs. (exec. engr.) (b. 14th Sept., 1902).—Joined the service as asst. exec. engr., United Provs., 1st Oct., 1924; offg. exec. engr., Jan., 1930; confid., Oct., 1933.

EDGLEY, NORMAN GEORGE ARMSTRONG, M.A., Barr., Indian C.S. (dist. and sess. judge, Bengal) (b. 19th June, 1888).—Educ. at schools in Switzerland, and New Coll., Oxford; apptd. after exam. of 1910; arrived in India, 5th Dec., 1911, and served in Eastern Bengal and Assam

as asst. mag. and collr.; transf. to Bengal, April, 1912; under-sec., judl., poll. and appt. depts., Bengal, June, 1916; regr., high court, Calcutta, appellate side, April, 1918; dist. and sessions judge, Jan., 1924; services placed at disposal of govt. of Assam, Oct., 1927; Kaisar-i-Hind medal, 1st class, Jan., 1930; offg. secty. to govt., judl. dept., Bengal, and supt., and remembrancer, legal affairs, Mar., 1933; confd., July, 1933; offg. judge high ct., Calcutta, variously from June, 1934.

EDMONDS, Lbert Charles, B.A., Indian Agric. Servi (b. 2nd Nov., 1895).—Apptd. dep. dir. of agric., Madras, 5th Dec., 1920.

EDMONDS, Ven. Canon Herbert James, M.A., Indian Eccles. Estabt. (Archdeacon of Madras) (b. 20th Mar., 1886).—Apptd. chaplain on probn., July, 1917; junr. chaplain, July, 1918; senr. chaplain, July, 1928; canon, 1932; archdeacon, 1937.

EDMUNDS, Percy James, M.A., B.Sc., India Posts and Tels. (dir. of wireless) (b. 1st Feb., 1890).—Joined the service as physician and research officer, wireless branch, 5th Dec., 1919; divl. engr., Oct., 1921; offg. dir. of wireless, Apl., 1916; confd., Apl., 1927; contr. of broadcasting in addn., Mar., 1935; dep. dir.-genl., wireless, Apl., 1936.

EDWARDS, Arthur John Charles, F.I.A., Office of High Commr. for India (chief accounting officer) (b. 24th July, 1883).—Educ. at Aske's Hatcham Sch.; entered Natl. Debt. Office, May, 1903; pte. sec. to contr., Oct., 1911, to July, 1913; apptd. junior clerk, India Office, acctnt.-gen. s dept., 5th July, 1913; senr. clerk, Feb., 1920; services lent to high commr. for India from April, 1921; clerk to commrs. of income tax, April, 1921, to April, 1922, and from Oct., 1922; asst. chief accounting officer, 1st April, 1922; deputy ditto, 21st April, 1922; apptd. Indian income tax officer, June, 1924; chief account. officer, and commnr. of inc. tax., Nov., 1934.

EDWARDS, C. A. H., late Indian State Railways (b. 10th June, 1879).—Trained at the R.I.E. Coll.; joined the service as asst. engr., Oct., 1903; exec. engr., Jan., 1913; offg. dep. ch. engr., Nov., 1925; offg. engr. in chief, E.B. Rly., Feb., 1927; dep. ch. engr. (provl.), Sept., 1927; offg. ch. engr., Oct., 1927; ret'd., Mar., 1934.

EDWARDS, Charles Cyril Rogers, D.S.C., B.Sc., A.M.I.C.E., Indian State Rlys. (exec. engr.) (b. 15th July, 1893).—Joined the service as asst. engr., 6th Jan., 1921; exec. engr., Burma rlys., Mar., 1934.

EDWARDS, Cosmo Grant Niven, Major, I.A., Indian Political Service (b. 13th Aug., 1896).—First commn., dated 19th June, 1915; joined the pol. dept., 8th March, 1921; asst. commr., Kohat, April, 1922; ditto, Mardan, April, 1926; pol. agent, N. Waziristan, Nov., 1926; dist. judge, Peshawar, Dec., 1928; dep. commr., D.I. Khan, Apl., 1929; offg. under-secty. to govt. of India, for. and pol. dept., April, 1930; and again, Mar., 1931; offg. dep. sec. for. and pol. dept., June, 1930; and again, Sept., 1932; secty. to resdt. in Mysore, Feb., 1934; offg. judl. commr., Coorg, May, 1936.

EDWARDS, Edwin Norman, India Police (b. 1st Oct., 1915).—Apptd. asst. supt., Punjab, on probn., 10th Nov., 1935.

EDWARDS, Harold Mills, late India Office (b. 13th Oct., 1895).—Apptd. surveyor and clerk of the works, June, 1926; transf. to Office of Works, Feb., 1936.

EDWARDS, John Ashworth, Indian Police (asst. supt.) (b. 17th Feb., 1912).—Joined the service as proby. asst. supt., Burma, 23rd Dec., 1931; confd., Jan., 1934.

EDWARDS, Osborne George, V.D., Indian State Rlys. (contr. of stores, G.I.P., Rly.) (b. 17th June, 1884).—Joined the service as asst. engr., 24th Nov., 1905; resdt. engr., Dec., 1911; in milty. employ from 6th Nov., 1916, to 24th Nov., 1920; dist. engr., Aug., 1924; offg. contr. of stores, May, 1932; confd., Aug., 1932.

EDWARDS, Percy Ashworth, V.D., late Indian State Rlys. (b. 31st July, 1877).—Joined the service as asst. loco. supt., 21st March, 1902; dist. ditto, Jan., 1914; divl. transportation supt., Nov., 1922; offg. contr. of stores Apl., 1930; confd., Nov., 1931; ret'd., May, 1934.

EDWARDS, William Cecil, late Indian Police (b. 19th Sept., 1888).—Joined the service, 1st Dec., 1909, as asst. supt., Bombay; on milty. duty from Dec., 1914, to Apl., 1917; supt. of police, Sept., 1922; awarded King's police medal, Jan., 1930.

EDYE, Ernest Henry Huish, M.B.E., M.A., Indian C.S. (mag. and collr., United Provs.) (b. 2nd June, 1884).—Educ. at Harrow, and Balliol College, Oxford; apptd. after exam. of 1907; arrd., 23rd Nov., 1908, and served in the United Provs. as asst. mag.; jt. mag., July, 1917; M.B.E., Jan., 1919; supt., census operations, United Provs., April, 1920; dep. commr., April, 1922; jt. mag., August, 1922; settlt. officer, Nov., 1923; mag. and collr., Sept., 1924; offg. commr., Apl., 1935.

EGAN, Thomas Jarlette, M.R.C.V.S., Indian Vety. Service (dir. of vety. services, United Provs.) (b. 5th Oct., 1894).—Joined the service as asst. supt., March, 1922; supt., April, 1925; profrr., Punjab vety. coll., Nov., 1933 dir. of vety. services, United Provs., July, 1934.

EGERTON, William Le Belward, Indian C.S., Indian Political Service (b. 1st Dec., 1901).—Educ. at Eton and Trinity Coll., Cambridge; joined the service, 24th Oct., 1924; arrived, 22nd Dec., 1924, and served in Madras as asst. collr. and mag.; asst. pte. secty. to Viceroy, March, 1927; asst. pol. agent, Kalat, Dec., 1929; under-secty. to agent to govr.-genl. in C.I., Nov., 1930; secty., ditto, Feb., 1931; offg. pol. agent, Bhopal, in addn. Aug., 1935; offer. on spec. duty, pol. dept., Apl., 1937; dep. secty., pol. dept., May, 1937; pol. agent, W. Kathiawar Agency, Oct., 1937.

EGGAR, Sir Arthur, M.A., Barr., Burma Judl. Dept. (advocate genl., Burma) (b. 22nd May, 1877).—Apptd. acting asst. govt. advocate, 7th May, 1911; law officer to R.A.F., Middle-East, 1918-19; apptd. dep. govt. advocate and sec. to Burma legisl. council, Dec., 1922; offg. govt. advocate, Mar., 1924; confd., Dec., 1925; adv.-genl., Apl., 1937; knighted, Jan., 1938.

EKAMBARAM, Kolipakkam Venkatisa, Indian Service of Engineers (asst. exec. engr., Madras) (b. 2nd June, 1907).—Apptd. asst. exec. engr., Madras, 13th Aug., 1932.

EKANATHA RAJARAM, RAO, B.A. (Hons.), Imperial Customs Service (asst. collr.) (b. 2nd Mar., 1901).—Joined the service, Mar., 1926; asst. coll. of customs, July, 1927.

ELATTI VALIAGATH AMU SAHIB (see Amu Sahib, E. V.).

ELCOCK, ROWLAND EDWARD, V.C., M.M. Indian Posts and Tels. (divl. engr.) (b. 5th June 1899).—Joined the service, 23rd Nov., 1923, as asst. div. engr.; divl. engr., Oct., 1932.

ELLIOT, HENRY HAWES, M.B.E., M.C., M.B., F.R.C.S.E., Lt.-Col., I.M.S. (surg. to Viceroy) (b. 22nd May, 1891).—First commn., 3rd Dec., 1921; offg. civil surg., New Delhi, Apl., 1929; agency surg., Apl., 1932; legn. surg., Kabul, Aug., 1933; surg. to Viceroy, Apl., 1936.

ELLIOT, WALTER, late Indian Police Service (United Provs.) (b. 21st Aug., 1889).—Joined the service, 28th Jan., 1911, as asst. supt.; in mil. employ from 28th May, 1918 to 23rd Aug., 1919; supt., R.M. Rly. Police, Ajmer, Oct., 1925, to April, 1927; offg. supt., April, 1927; ret'd., Sept., 1930.

ELLIOTT, Sir Ivo D'Oyly, Bart., late Indian C.S. (United Provs.) (b. 7th March, 1882).—Educ. at Harrow, and Balliol Coll., Oxford; apptd. after exam. of 1905; arrived, 26th Nov., 1906, and served in the United Provs. as asst. commr.; under-sec. to govt., Aug., 1912; jt. mag., Nov., 1914; on spl. duty in govt. sectt., April to May, 1915; under-sec. to govt. of India, com. and industry dept., Jan., 1916; temp. dep. sec., ditto, Nov., 1917, to Jan., 1919; dep. commr., Nov., 1919; mag. and collr., Sept., 1922; offg. rev. and judl. sec. to govt., United Provs., April, 1925; pub. health sec. to govt., April, 1926; secty. to govt., local self-govt. and public health dept., Oct., 1927; ret'd., Mar., 1932.

ELLIOTT, JACK, Indian Police (dist. supt., Madras) (b. 5th Jan., 1891).—Joined the service, 24th Nov., 1910; asst. supt., July, 1912; awarded King's police medal, Jan., 1916; awarded bar to ditto, Jan., 1923; supt. (prov.), April, 1925; supt., govt. rlwy., police, Oct., 1926; pers. asst. to dep. inspr.-genl., C.I.D., Sept., 1927; asst. inspr.-genl., May, 1929; and again, Apl., 1933; dist. supt., Sept., 1930; offg. commr. of police, Madras, Mar., 1936; offg. dep. inspr.-genl., Sept., 1936.

ELLIOTT, Rev. THOMAS REGINALD HATCHARD, M.A., Indian Eccles. Estabt. (senr. chaplain, Bombay) (b. 5th Aug., 1894).—Apptd. chaplain on probation, Bombay, 27th Oct., 1927; junr. chaplain, Oct. 1927; senr. ditto, Oct., 1937.

ELLIS, EDWARD EARDLEY, late Indian State Rlys. (b. 26th July, 1880).—Joined the service, 1st Jan., 1897; asst. traffic manager, July, 1910; offg. asst. supt., March, 1913; confd., March, 1914; offg. dist. supt., Feb., 1919; confd., Jan., 1922; asst. comml. manager, Oct., 1926; offg. dep. ch. comml. manager, April, 1927, and again, Feb., 1934; confd., June, 1934; ret'd., Aug., 1936.

ELLIS, SAMUEL FRANCIS, late Indian Police (b. 8th Jan., 1882).—Joined the service, 22nd Nov., 1902, and posted to Bombay; supt. of police, Feb., 1912; ret'd., Feb., 1934.

ELLIS, THOMAS HOBART, Indian C.S. (dist. and sess. judge, Bengal) (b. 11th Oct., 1894).—Educ. at Queen's Coll., Oxford; joined the service, 31st Jan., 1919; arrived, 31st March, 1919, and posted to Bengal; addl. dist. and sess. judge, Oct., 1924; offg. ditto, Feb., 1930; confd., July, 1930.

ELMES, ARCHIBALD JAMES, India Audit Office (higher exec. officer) (b. 8th Jan., 1893).—Apptd. 2nd class clerk, 6th Aug., 1914; on mil. service, Jan., 1917, to March, 1919; transf. to office of high commr. for India, Oct., 1920; transf. to India Audit Office, Feb., 1922; exec. officer, April, 1922; higher exec. officer, Apl., 1933.

ELMES, JULIUS PAUL JONATHAN, B.A., Indian C.S. (mag. and collr., Bihar) (b. 1st Nov., 1887).—Educ. at St. Andrew's Coll., Dublin, and Trinity Coll., Dublin; apptd. after exam. of 1911; arrived, 30th Nov., 1912, and served in Bihar and Orissa as asst. mag. and collr.; temp. to mil. duty, Oct., 1915, to Feb., 1920; offg. mag. and collr. and dep. commr., March, 1920; offg. dep. commr., April, 1924; offg. mag. and collr., Nov., 1925; confd., July, 1927; manager, Bettiah wards estate, Jan., 1933.

ELMORE, ALBERT EDWARD, Indian Forest Service (dep. consvr., Burma) (b. 24th Jan., 1883).—Joined the service, 1st Sept. 1912; dep. consvr., Nov., 1923.

ELMSLIE, Cyril STEWART, Indian Police (supt., United Provs.) (b. 10th Apl., 1893).—Joined the service as asst. supt. Dec., 1913; supt., Aug., 1925.

ELWIN, DONALD HAMILTON, Indian C.S. (sub. collr., Madras) (b. 15th April, 1904).—Educ. at Bradford and Hertford Coll., Oxford; joined the service, 12th Oct., 1928; arrived, Nov., 1928, and served in Madras as asst. collr. and mag.; under-secty. public dept., Madras, May, 1932; offg. under-secty. to govt. of India, home dept., Apl., 1934; offg. dep. secty., ditto, Sept., 1934; pte. secty. to the Gov. of Madras, Sept., 1935.

ELWIN, REGINALD BARNES, Indian C.S. (asst. commr., Punjab) (b. 12th June, 1908).—Educ. at Bradford and Corpus Christi Coll., Cambridge; apptd. after examination of 1930; arrived in India, Nov. 1931, and served in the Punjab as asst. commr.; offg. under-secty. to govt., Punjab, pol. and home dept., May, 1933; offg. judge, small cause ct., Simla, Mar., 1934; attd., home dept., govt. of India, Oct., 1937.

EMERSON, DONALD BOMFORD, B.A., B.A.I., late Indian Service of Engrs. (b. 5th Feb., 1885).—Joined the service as asst. engr. on 1st Oct., 1907; in mil. employ from 16th May, 1915, to 29th Feb., 1920; exec. engr., Oct., 1915; offg. suptg. engr., June, 1926; confd., Apl., 1930; ret'd., July, 1936.

EMERSON, GERALD HERBERT, Indian C.S. (b. 3rd Dec., 1907).—Educ. at Rugby and Magdalene Coll., Cambridge; joined the service, 15th Oct., 1930; arrived in India, Nov., 1930, and served in the Punjab as asst. commr.; offg. dep. commr., Mar., 1935; offg. asst. pol. agent, Loralai, Nov., 1935; secty. to A.G.G., Eastern states, Mar., 1936; asst. commr., Nowshera sub-div., Nov., 1937.

EMERSON, SIR HERBERT WILLIAM, G.C.I.E., K.C.S.I., C.B.E., B.A., Indian C.S. (Gouv. of Punjab) (b. 1st June, 1881).—Educ. at Calday Grange Gr. Sch. and Magdalen Coll., Cambridge; apptd. after exam. of 1904; arrived, 27th Nov., 1905, and served in the Punjab as asst. commr., manager, Bashahr State, June, 1911, to March, 1914; supt. and settlt. offr., Mandi State, March, 1915; offg. under-sec. to govt. of India, rev. and agri. dept., Oct., 1915; again supt. and settlement offr., Mandi State, Jan., 1916; asst. commr. and settlt. offr., Punjab, Oct., 1917; dep. commr., Jan., 1922; C.B.E., Jan., 1923; C.I.E., Jan., 1926; sec. to govt., fin. dept., April, 1926; ch. sec., Nov., 1927; secty. to govt. of India, home dept., Apl., 1930; C.S.I., June, 1931; K.C.S.I., Jan., 1933; govr. of Punjab, Apl., 1933; G.C.I.E., May, 1937.

EMINSON, BASIL FRANKLIN, M.B., B.S. (Lon.), Lt.-Col., I.M.S. (civil surg.) (b. 24th Sept., 1887).—First commn., dated 27th July, 1912; in milty. employ prior to 27th Nov., 1923; offg. civil surg., May, 1925; civil surg., Hyderabad, May, 1933.

EMMER, ANDREW FRANK, I.S.O., late For. and Pol. dept. (b. 10th May, 1880).—Held non-gazetted appts. from 1st May, 1896; treasury officer, Hyderabad resdy., Oct., 1918; asst. secty., ditto, Jan., 1927; asst. secty., for. and pol. dept., Nov., 1929; offg. under secty., ditto, Sept., 1932, and May, 1934; retd., Apl., 1935.

ENGLAND, FREDERICK SAMUEL EDWARD, B.Sc., Indian Service of Engrs. (exec. engr., Burma) (b. 2nd July, 1905).—Joined the service as asst. exec. engr., Burma, 24th Oct., 1926; exec. engr., Oct., 1935.

ENGLAND, Rev. CANON HERBERT GEORGE, M.A., late Indian Eccles. Estabt. (b. 7th Jan., 1880).—Joined the service as junior chaplain, 4th Nov., 1912; senr. chaplain, Nov., 1922; retd., May, 1934.

ENGLAND, WALTER BRASSINGTON, M.A., Office of High Commr. for India (higher clerical officer, public dept.) (b. 4th April, 1893).—Educ. at Tonbridge Sch. and Christ's Coll., Cambridge; apptd. to India Office, 4th April, 1921; transfld. to Office of High Commr. for India, April, 1922; exec. officer, Jan., 1924; higher clerical officer, June, 1927; actg. higher exec. officer, Sept., 1929, to Aug., 1930.

ENGLEDUE, GUY, Comdr., R.I.M. (retd.).—Apptd. port officer, Akyab, 1st March, 1926; retd., Dec., 1928.

ENGLEFIELD, ARTHUR GUY, Indian State Rlys. (divl. mechl. engr., N.W. rly.) (b. 13th July, 1893).—Joined the service, asst. loco. supt., Dec., 1919; asst. rolling stock officer, July, 1926; asst. works manager, N.W. rly., Dec., 1932; divl. mechl. engr., N.W. rly. (provl.), Aug., 1934; confd., Sept., 1936.

ENGLISH, ERIC ERLE, Indian Police (supt., Bengal) (b. 24th Jan., 1895).—Joined the service as asst. supt., 6th Sept., 1921; offg. supt., March, 1925; confd., Feb., 1930.

ENGLISH, WALTER, late Opium Dept., United Provs. (b. 1st Aug., 1877).—Joined the service, 31st March, 1898; dist. opium officer, July, 1924; retd., July, 1932.

ENSOR, ERIC LOVELL, Indian State Rlys. (asst. telegraph supt., G.I.P. Rly.) (b. 25th Aug., 1891).—Joined the service as asst. telg. supt., 4th Oct., 1921; asst. transpn. supt. (tels.), 1st Nov., 1922; offg. dep. ditto, Apl., 1932, and again, Sept., 1935.

ERACH MANEKSHAH NANA VATI (see Nanavati, E. M.).

ERSKINE, LORD, JOHN FRANCIS ASHLEY, G.C.I.E. (Gouv. of Madras) (b. 26th Apl., 1895).—Educ. at Eton and Christ Church, Oxford; asst. pte. secty. to First Lord of Admy., 1920-1921; parly. pte. secty. to P.M.G., 1923; princpl. pte. secty. to Home Secretary, 1924; M.P. for Weston-super-Mare Divn. of Somerset, 1922-1923 and again 1924; asst. govt. whip in Natl. Govt., 1932; G.C.I.E., Aug., 1934; Govr. of Madras, 15th Nov., 1934.

EUSTACE, EDWARD ARTHUR RAWLINS, Indian C.S. (dep. commr., Punjab) (b. 18th Nov., 1899).—Educ. at Wellington Coll. and R.M. Coll., Sandhurst; joined the service, 4th Sept., 1923; arrived, 14th Nov., 1923, and served in the Punjab as asst. commr.; offg. under-sec. to govt., March, 1925; addl. under-sec. to govt. of India, dept. of educn., &c., Dec., 1927; offg. regr., high ct., Lahore, Jan., 1930; offg. dep. commr., Feby., 1931; dep. commr. (subs. provl.), Nov., 1931; confd., July, 1935.

EUSTACE, FRED ARTHUR, A.M.I.C.E., A.M.I.M.E., late Indian Service of Engrs. (b. 15th Feb., 1892).—Joined the service as asst. exec. engr., 8th Oct., 1920; exec. engr., April, 1928; retd., Nov., 1932.

EUSTACE, FREDERICK ROBERT HARRINGTON, D.S.O., Col., R.E. (retd.), late Indian State Railways (b. 14th Aug., 1875).—First commn. dated 8th Aug., 1895; joined the Public Works Dept. as asst. engr., Feb., 1898; exec. engr., Oct., 1908; on mily. duty from Sept., 1914, to Sept., 1919; supt. engr., Dec., 1922; offg. dir., civil engrg., May, 1925; ch. engr., March, 1925; offg. senr. govt. inspr. of rlys., May, 1927; retd., Oct., 1929.

EUSTACE, JOHN CURTIS WEINHER, Indian C.S. (assh. commr., Punjab) (b. 22nd Nov., 1906).—Educ. at Wellington; Brentwood Coll.; Victoria, British Columbia and Exeter Coll., Oxford; joined the service 9th Oct., 1929; arrived Nov., 1929, and served in the Punjab as asst. commr.; offg. dep. commr., July, 1933; dep. regr., co-op. societies, Punjab, Apl., 1935; offg. regr., ditto, Mar., 1936, and again Oct., 1936.

EVANS, AIDAN OSWALD, B.A., Indian State Rlys. (divl. supt., E.I. Rly.) (b. 26th Aug., 1887).—Joined the service as asst. traffic supt., 30th Oct., 1909; offg. dist. ditto, Feb., 1915; offg. dep. traffic manager, Aug., 1921; with Indian Rly. conference assen., April, 1922; supt., July, 1925; ditto, operating dept., Oct., 1926; offg. secty. to agent, E.I. Rly., March, 1928; offg. divl. supt., March, 1929; offg. dep. agent, E.I. Rly., Dec., 1929; offg. divl. supt., Oct., 1930; and again, Nov., 1932; confd., Jan., 1936.

EVANS, ARTHUR GARBETT, Indian Police (asst. supt.) (b. 6th May, 1912).—Joined the service as proby. asst. supt., United Provs., 1st Nov., 1931; confd., Nov., 1933.

EVANS, ARTHUR LAUREN FANE, late Indian Police (b. 17th Jan., 1886).—Joined the service 31st May, 1907; asst. supt., Nov., 1910; supt., July, 1920; asst. inspr.-genl., govt. rly. police, April, 1927; ret'd., Nov., 1933.

EVANS, ERNEST SYDNEY, late India Audit Office (b. 16th Feb., 1876).—Clerk, 2nd class, 11th March, 1895; staff clerk, Feb., 1915; junior clerk, Dec., 1921; suptg. exec. officer, April, 1922; ret'd., Mar., 1936.

EVANS, FREDERICK BUISSON, C.S.I., late Indian C.S.—Educ. at St. Paul's Sch., and Trin. Coll., Oxford; apptd. after exam. of 1897; arrived, 5th Dec., 1898, and served in Madras as asst. collr. and mag.; spl. asst. settlt. officer, March, 1901; asst. sec. to govt., Jan., 1904; under-sec. to govt., rev. dept., Nov., 1904; ditto, in depts. under chief sec., May, 1906; head asst. collr. and mag., March, 1909; sub-collr. and jt. mag., May, 1910; collr. and dist. mag., Dec., 1916; C.S.I., Jan., 1922; on spl. duty in chief secretariat, March, 1925; temp. sec. to govt., pub. wks. dept., May, 1925; sec. to govt., pub. wks. dept., Oct., 1925; offg. 2nd sec. to govt., Dec., 1925; confd., Aug., 1926; ret'd., Oct., 1928.

EVANS, SIR GEOFFREY, Kt., C.I.E., M.A. (Cantab.), late Indian Agricl. Service.—Apptd. principal, agric. coll., Nagpur, 24th Oct., 1906; second dep. dir. of agric., Central Prov., Nov., 1907; sec., Central Prov. war board, Aug., 1917; on duty under govt. of India, army dept., from May, 1918, to May, 1919 (dir. of agric., Mesopotamia); C.I.E., June, 1919; on deputn. to Burma, June, 1919; dir. of agric., Bengal, July, 1920; ret'd., Feb., 1925; knighted, Jan., 1934.

EVANS, GEORGE EMLYN THOMAS HUISE, M.C., Indian Police (supl., Bengal) (b. 18th June, 1899).—Joined the service as asst. supt., Bengal, on 15th March, 1921; offg. supt., March, 1925; confd. Jan., 1932.

EVANS, HUBERT JOHN, Indian C.S. (jt. mag., United Provinces) (b. 21st Nov., 1904).—Educ. at City of London School, Jesus Coll., Cambridge, and School of Oriental Studies, London; joined the service 14th Oct., 1929; arrived 27th Dec., 1929, and served in the United Provinces as asst. mag. and collr.; jt. mag., Mar., 1932; offg. mag. and collr., Mar., 1936.

EVANS, LEONARD GLYDE LAVINGTON, C.I.E., B.A., late Indian C.S., and Indian Political Service (b. 9th Nov., 1888).—Educ. at Harrow, New Coll., Oxford, and Univ. Coll., London; apptd. after exam. of 1911; arrived, 26th Nov., 1912, and served in Bihar and Orissa as asst. mag. and collr.; on temp. service under govt. of India, for. and pol. dept., as addl. asst. master, Mayo Coll., March, 1915; on spl. duty under pol. resdt., Persian Gulf, April, 1918; offr. of pol. dept., govt. of India, from Jan., 1919; under-sec. to agent to govr.-gen. in Rajputana, Aug., 1922; on foreign service in Jodhpur State from April, 1923; sec. to resdt. in Mysore and sec. to ch. commr. of Coorg, Feb., 1925; sec. to agent to govr.-genl. in Rajputana and ch. commr., Ajmer-Merwara, April, 1927; pol. agent, Baghelkhand, Nov., 1928; pol. agent, Bhopal, March, 1929; ditto, Southern States of C.I. and Malwa, Oct., 1931; secty. to agent to govr.-genl. in Rajputana

and ch. commr. in Ajmer-Merwara, Mar., 1932; pol. agent, E. Rajputana states and presdt., council of state, Bharatpur, June, 1933; C.I.E., Jan., 1935; A.G.G., E. states, Feb., 1935; judl. commr., Coorg, Nov., 1935; offg. resdt., Mysore, Nov., 1935; ret'd., Mar., 1938.

EVANS, SIR T. J. CAREY (see Carey-Evans, Sir T. J.).

EVANS, VICTOR DONALD CHAPPELL, Indian Police (b. 8th Jan., 1915).—Apptd. asst. supt., Bombay, on probn., 11th Nov., 1935.

EVANS, WILLIAM HUGH, B.A., Indian C.S. (dist. and sess. judge, Burma) (b. 24th June, 1892).—Educ. at County Sch., Pwlheli, and Univ. Coll., Bangor; joined the service, 30th Sept., 1921; arrived, 30th Nov., 1921, and served in Burma as asst. commr.; offg. dist. and sess. judge, Dec., 1926; confd., Sept., 1927.

EVANS-GORDON, KENMURE ALICK GARTH, Lt.-Col., I.A., Indian Political Service (b. 20th Aug., 1885).—First commn. dated 24th Jan., 1906; in mil. employ to 11th May, 1913, and again from 13th Oct., 1914; services lent to Rewa Durbar from 1st Jan., 1919; asst. commr. Ajmer-Merwara, Oct., 1924; sec. to resdt. at Mysore and sec. to ch. commr., Coorg, Feb., 1925; pol. agent, Baghelkhand, March, 1929; ditto, E. Kathiawar agency, Nov., 1930; vice-presdt., council of regency, Cooch Behar State, Mar., 1931; pol. agent, E. Kathiawar Agency, Nov., 1934; offg. resdt., Baroda, May, 1935; resdt., Jaipur, Nov., 1935; offg. A.G.G., Deccan states, June, 1936; resdt. for Kolhapur and Deccan States, Apl., 1937.

EVERALL, WILLIAM TEAGUE, O.B.E., A.M.I.C.E., late Indian State Rlys. (b. 28th Aug., 1880).—Joined the service as asst. bridge engr., 7th Dec., 1908; O.B.E., June, 1924; bridge engr. (s.p.t.), Oct., 1924; confd., Nov., 1926; dep. ch. engr. (bridges), Nov., 1930; ret'd., Aug., 1935.

EVERETT, CECIL HOWARD, Indian Police (supl.) (b. 9th Sept., 1900).—Joined the service as asst. supt., Punjab, on 6th Jan., 1923; offg. supt., Feb., 1930; supt. (subs. provl.), Mar., 1936; confd., Oct., 1936.

EVERETT, GEORGE ELIOT, late Indian State Rlys. (b. 10th Oct., 1878).—Joined the service as asst. engr., 9th Sept., 1904; resdt. engr., June, 1910; senr. resdt. engr., July, 1919; dist. engr., Oct., 1921; offg. suptg. engr., Feb., 1928; ret'd., June, 1934.

EVERS, Rev. MERVYN SAXELBYE, M.A., M.C., Indian Eccles. Estabt. (senr. chaplain) (b. 8th Sept., 1887).—Apptd. chaplain on probation, 18th May, 1924; junr. chaplain, May, 1926; senr. ditto, May, 1932.

EVERSHED, WALLIS ALBURY, B.Sc., (Lond.), A.M.I.C.E., late Indian Service of Engrs. (b. 2nd July, 1889).—Joined the service, 1st Oct., 1913; asst. engr., Nov., 1913; in milty. employ from 9th Aug., 1916, to 5th Aug., 1919; asst. exec. engr., March, 1920; exec. engr., Oct., 1922; offg. suptg. engr., Feb., 1929; ret'd., July, 1937.

EWART, SIR JOHN MURRAY, Kt., C.I.E., Indian Police (dir. intel. bureau, home dept.) (b. 23rd Sept., 1884).—Joined the service, 17th Nov., 1905; supt. of police, March, 1913; dist. offr., frontier constabulary, Hazara, May, 1919; granted King's police medal, Jan., 1919; C.I.E., June, 1924; on foreign service with Sudan

govt. for 1 yr. from 12th Nov., 1924; offg. dep. inspr.-gen., April, 1926; dep. inspr.-genl. (provl.), Dec., 1928; confd., Aug., 1930; offg. inspr.-genl. of police and under-sec., home (police) dept., Punjab, Apl., 1932; confd., Aug., 1932; knighted, Jan., 1936; dir., intel. bureau, home dept., Mar., 1936.

EWBANK, ROBERT BENSON, C.S.I., C.I.E., B.A., late Indian C.S. (b. 22nd Oct., 1883).—Educ. at Carlisle Sch., and Queen's Coll., Oxford; apptd., after exam. of 1906; arrived, 22nd Nov., 1907, and served in Bombay as asst. collr. and mag.; regr., co-op. credit soc., Bombay Presy., from May, 1913; on deputn. as sec., commee. on co-operation in India, Oct., 1914, to June, 1915; offg. dep. sec., commerce dept., govt. of India, 1910; confd., April., 1921; dep. sec. to govt. of India, dept. of rev. and agri., March, 1922; C.I.E., Jan., 1924; on depn. as sec., Colonies Cttee., March to July, 1924; on depn. in connection with Southborough Cttee., Jan., 1925; dep. sec. to govt. of India, dept. of educn., &c., Aug., 1925; offg. sec. to ditto, Sept., 1925, and again, Dec., 1926; sec., Back Bay enquiry commee., July, 1926; collr., dist. mag. and pol. agent, Feb., 1927; member of East African deputation, Oct., 1927, to April, 1928; offg. secty. to govt., genl., edl. and marine depts., Mar., 1929; confd., June, 1929; member legis. council, 1929 and 1932; C.S.I., Jan., 1934; retd., July, 1936; author of "Manual for Co-operative Societies in the Bombay Presidency."

EWING, ROBERT ROSS, Lt.-Col., I.A. (dep. commr., Burma).—Arrived in India 3rd Oct., 1912; served in mil. dept. until June, 1916, when he was apptd. asst. commdt.; asst. adj., July, 1917; asst. commr., Nov., 1920; and sub-divnl. officer Aug., 1921; warden of oilfields, Dec., 1923; offg. dep. commr., March, 1924, Oct., 1926, and again Feb., 1931; confd., May, 1932.

EWING, STEPHEN ROBERT, late Indian Finance Dept.—Joined the service, 6th April, 1892; dep. examr., June, 1910; asst. auditor, O. & R. Rly., Oct., 1919; dep. auditor, E.B. Rly., Nov., 1921; offg. ditto, E.I. Rly., June, 1926; retd., Sept., 1928.

EYERS, CYRIL, V.D., late Indian State Rlys. (b. 27th Sept., 1878).—Joined the service as asst. dist. loco. supt., 4th Nov., 1905; dist. ditto, May, 1915; divl. supt. (provl.), Feb., 1923; supt., March, 1926; offg. dep. ch. operating supt., April, 1927; confd., Apl., 1931; dep. ch. mechl. engr. (running), July, 1933; retd., July, 1935.

EYRE, MONTAGUE HENRY, late Indian Police Service (United Provs.) (b. 11th Sept., 1882).—Joined the service, 24th Nov., 1902, and posted to United Provs.; asst. supt. of police, Feb., 1908; supt., June, 1914; on mil. duty (I.A.R.O.), May, 1918, to April, 1919; retd., July, 1930.

EZECHIEL, CHARLES ERNEST, late Indian Police Service (Bengal) (b. 5th Nov., 1874).—Joined the service, 4th Dec., 1893, and served as asst. supt.; dist. supt., Nov., 1903; (s.p.t.) supt., Feb., 1914; offg. dep. inspr.-gen., April, 1924; confd., July, 1925; retd., Nov., 1929.

FABRE-TONNERRE, JOSEPH, Indian Police (supt., Bihar) (b. 20th Apl., 1882).—Joined the service as asst. supt. on 26th July, 1909; supt., Nov., 1919.

FAGAN, Sir PATRICK JAMES, K.C.I.E., C.S.I., late Indian C.S.—Educ. at Blundell's Sch., Tiverton, and St. John's Coll., Cambridge; apptd. after exam. of 1885; arrived, 26th Nov., 1886, and served in the Punjab as asst. commr. and settl. officer; dep. commr., Nov., 1896; commr., Dec., 1910; offg. finl. commr., Jan., 1916; confirmed, Nov., 1916; C.S.I., June, 1917; K.C.I.E., Jan., 1922; retd., 1923.

FAIRLEY, FRANCIS NOEL, Office of High Commr. for India (tempy. suptg. exec. officer, accounts dept.) (b. 11th Dec., 1883).—Apptd. 2nd class clerk, India store depôt, 17th March, 1903; transf. to office of high commr. for India, Oct., 1920; exec. officer, April, 1922; higher exec. officer, Apl., 1923; temp. suptg. exec. officer, Apl., 1938.

FAIRLEY, JOHN, B.Sc., late India Posts and Telegraphs (b. 7th June, 1880).—Joined the telegraph service, 1st Oct., 1902, as asst. supt.; supt. and asst. to chief engr., March, 1914; suptg. engr., April, 1917; divl. engr., tels., Nov., 1921; offg. dir. tel. engrg., Punjab and N.W.F. circle, Feb., 1930; confd., Mar., 1931; ditto, Burma, June, 1932; offg. P.M.G., Burma, June, 1932, and again, Mar., 1934; dir. tel. engrg., Bengal and Assam, Oct., 1932; retd., June, 1935.

FAIRWEATHER, CHARLES EDWARD STUART, C.I.E., M.A., Indian Police (dep. inspr.-genl., Bengal) (b. 16th Jan., 1889).—Joined the service as asst. supt. on 26th Nov., 1909; supt., April, 1923; dep. commr. of police, Calcutta, June, 1924; awarded King's police medal, Jan., 1932; offg. dep. inspr.-genl., Apl., 1932; confd., Mar., 1937; C.I.E., Jan., 1934.

FAIRWEATHER, JAMES COURTENAY THORPE, late I.P.S. (b. 5th July, 1876).—Joined the service, 26th Nov., 1896, and posted to Central Provs.; dist. supt., July, 1899; retd., Jan., 1930.

FAIZ MUHAMMAD KHAN, KHAN, Punjab Civil Service (b. 16th Dec., 1899).—Extra asst. commr., Punjab, Jan., 1914.

FAKHAR - UD - DIN, MUHAMMAD (see Muhammad F.).

FALCONER, GEORGE ARTHUR, Capt. I.A., Indian Political Service (b. 3rd June, 1894).—First commn. dated 28th Aug., 1917; joined pol. dept., 18th June, 1923; under-sec. to pol. resident in Persian Gulf, May, 1924; asst. resident, Aden, Aug., 1927; 2nd asst. to resdt., Kashmir, Mar., 1929; secty. to A.G.G., Deccan States, Apl., 1933; ditto, Gujerat States, Oct., 1933; offg. A.G.G., ditto, Apl., 1935; consul for Kerman, Nov., 1937.

FALSHAW, DONALD, Indian C.S. (dist. and sess. judge, Punjab) (b. 22nd Jan., 1905).—Educ. at Lancaster Royal Grammar Sch., and Sidney Sussex Coll., Cambridge; joined the service, 13th October, 1928; arrived, Nov., 1928, and served in the Punjab as asst. commr.; addl. dist. and sess. judge, Apl., 1932; offg. ditto, Nov., 1933; sub. perm., June, 1937.

FALVEY, CORNELIUS VINCENT, L.R.C.P. and S., Major, I.M.S. Medical Dept., United Provs. (civil surgeon) (b. 8th Apl., 1898).—Date of first commn., 17th Oct., 1922; civil surg., United Provs., Jan., 1932.

FANE, ARTHUR GEORGE CECIL, M.C., late Indian Service of Engrs. (b. 29th Dec., 1880).—Trained at the R.I.E. Coll.; joined the service, 1st Oct., 1902, as asst. engr., Punjab; exec. engr., Oct., 1910; on mil. service from Feb., 1915, to April, 1910; offg. suptg. engr., April, 1922; confd., July, 1926; ret'd., Mar., 1933.

FANN, JOHN MONTAGU GROSSE, B.A., Indian Civil Service (asst. commr., Burma) (b. 31st Dec., 1904).—Educ. at Latymer Upper School, Hammersmith, and Pembroke Coll., Cambridge; joined the service, 18th Oct., 1927; arrived, Dec., 1927, and served in Burma as asst. commr.; asst. warden, oilfields, Apl., 1930; offg. dep. commr., Aug., 1932.

FANSHAW, JOCELYN HERBERT, V.D., late Indian State Rlys. (b. 21st Nov., 1880).—Joined the service as asst. engr., 9th Oct., 1903; resdt. engr., Oct., 1906; in milty. employ from 26th Feb., 1917, to 31st Oct., 1919; dist. engr., Apl., 1921; offg. dep. ch. engr., G.I.P. rly., Jan., 1935; ret'd., Nov., 1937.

FAQIR MUHAMMAD KHAN, KHAN SAHIB, B.A., Indian Service of Engrs. (exec. engr., Punjab) (b. 19th Dec., 1886).—Joined the service, 15th Nov., 1909; asst. engr., July, 1915; asst. exec. engr., Jan., 1920; exec. engr., July, 1925.

FARBROTHER, ERNEST SAMUEL, M.R.C.V.S., Indian Veterinary Service (dir. vety. services, Bombay) (b. 16th July, 1890).—Joined the service, Oct., 1913; on milty. duty from July, 1915, to April, 1919; offg. supt., Sind, July, 1919; ditto, Bombay Presdy., Feb., 1922; confd., Nov., 1923; dir. vety. services, June, 1932.

FARGUE, DUDLEY GORDON HERIOT DE LA, Major I.A., Indian Political Service (b. 20th Sept., 1897).—First commn. dated 21st Sept., 1916; joined pol. dept., 11th April, 1923; under-sec. to agent to G.G. in C.I., April, 1925; asst. pol. agent, Loralai, Nov., 1926; asst. to pol. agent in Kalat, Jan., 1928; offg. under-sec. for. and pol. dept., July, 1930; on foreign service in Gwalior State, Mar., 1931; asst. commr., Ajmer-Merwara, Mar., 1932; secty. to A.G.G. in Rajputana, and ch. commr., Ajmer-Merwara, Jan., 1933; commr., Ajmer-Merwara, Oct., 1934; pol. agent, Zho, Nov. 1934.

FARIDUNJI, R. (see Rustomji Faridunji).

FARMBROUGH, ALFRED, A.I.C., Office of High Commr. for India (asst. chemist, store dept.) (b. 30th Sept., 1889).—Apptd. junior asst., chemist, India Store depot, 1909; asst. chemist, 1920.

FARMER, JOHN COTTON, C.I.E., Indian Police (inspr. genl., Bengal) (b. 18th May, 1886).—Joined the service as proby. asst. supt., 5th Dec., 1906; asst. supt., Oct., 1908; supt., Nov., 1915; offg. dep. inspr. genl., June, 1928, and Nov., 1929; confd., Jany., 1931; awarded King's police medal, Jan., 1931; offg., inspr. genl., Apl., 1932; and again, Apl., 1935; confd., Dec., 1936; Indian Police Medal, 1937; C.I.E., Jan., 1938.

FARMER, PATRICK SCOTT LETHBRIDGE, Indian Police (b. 18th July, 1913).—Joined the service as proby. asst. supt., Punjab, Nov., 1932.

FARNON, JOSEPH ASHLEY ST. JOHN, M.A., Opium dept., United Provs. (opium agent) (b. 23rd July, 1882).—Joined the service as proby. asst. opium agent, 5th April, 1904; confd., March, 1906; dist. opium agent (s.p.t.), Aug., 1924; confd., Oct., 1924.

FARQUHAR, ARTHUR GRAHAM FRANCIS, Indian C.S. (asst. commr., Central Provs.) (b. 21st Feb., 1905).—Educ. at Glenalmond and Sch. of Economics, London; joined the service, 20th Oct., 1927; arrived, 22nd Nov., 1927, and served in the Central Provinces as asst. commr.; under-sec. to govt., Oct., 1929; offg. dep. commr. variously from Mar., 1931.

FARQUHARSON, FRANCIS ARCHIBALD, M.C., Indian Service of Engrs. (suptg. engr., Punjab) (b. 20th Jan., 1886).—Trained at R.I.E. Coll.; joined the service as asst. engr. on 1st Jan., 1907; on milty. duty from 11th March, 1915, to 26th May, 1919; exec. engr., Jan., 1915; offg. suptg. engr., April, 1928; under secty., public wks. dept., irrign. branch (revd.), May, 1929; suptg. engr., Apl., 1933.

FARRAN, ARTHUR COURTNEY, B.A., Indian Educl. Service (prinpl., Karnatak Coll., Dharwar) (b. 15th June, 1890).—Joined the service, 5th Jan., 1915; profir. of history and pol. economy, Elph. Coll., Bombay, Jan., 1917; profir. of history and economy, Deccan Coll., Poona, July, 1926; offg. prinpl. and profir., Elph. Coll., Bombay, June, 1929 and again June, 1932; prinpl., Karnatak Coll., Dharwar, Nov., 1930.

FARRANT, GEORGE YORKE SILVERTHORNE, M.C., Indian Police (supt., Bombay) (b. 23rd June, 1894).—Joined the service, 28th Dec., 1919, as asst. supt.; offg. supt., Feb. 1924; confd., Sept., 1927.

FARRELL, NELSON NORBERT, A.C.G.I., A.M.I.C.E., late Indian Service of Engrs.—Joined, 1st Oct., 1915, as asst. engr.; exec. engr., Oct., 1924; ret'd., Dec., 1928.

FARRINGTON, SIR HENRY ANTHONY, Bart., late Indian Forest Service.—Joined the service 29th Nov., 1894, and served as asst. consvr. of forests; dep. consvr., June, 1901; apptd. to Port Blair in Feb., 1908, with charge of Andamans Forest Dept.; dir. of forest school, Nov., 1911; dep. consvr., Bengal, March, 1912; transf. to Central Prov. as offg. consvr., April, 1915; offg. consvr. of forests, Bengal, Jan., 1916; consvr., Sept., 1919; offg. chief consvr., Central Prov., May, 1922; confd., July, 1923; ret'd., Oct., 1926.

FARUQI, GHUFAN AHMAD, B.A., Indian C.S. (jt. mag., United Provinces) (b. 1st Aug., 1909).—Educ. at Government High School, Sitapur, Allahabad University and King's College, Cambridge; joined the service, 6th Oct., 1933; arrived in India, Nov., 1933, and served in the United Provinces as asst. mag. and collr.; jt. magte., Apl., 1935.

FARUQI, MUHAMMAD ZAHURUDDIN AHMAD (see Zahuruddin Ahmad, F. M.).

FARUQI, NASIR AHMAD, B.A., Indian C.S. (unde rsecty., home and pol. depts., Bombay) (b. 15th Dec., 1906).—Educ. at Punjab Univ. and

Trinity Coll., Cambridge; joined the service, 14th Oct., 1931, and served in Bombay as asst. collr.; offg. under-secty., rev. dept., Bombay, Aug., 1935; confd., Apl., 1936; under-secty., home and pol. depts., and passport officer, Nov., 1937; attd. home dept., govt. of India, Dec., 1937.

FATEH, BAHADUR VARMA, BABU, B.A., LL.B. (b. 24th Nov., 1876).—Joined the service, 2nd July, 1903; sub. judge, Dec., 1915; offg. dist. and sess. judge, Oct., 1925; confd., Oct., 1926; ret'd., Nov., 1931.

FATEH-UD-DIN, KHAN BAHADUR MAULVI, M.B.E., B.A., M.R.A.S., A.R.H.S., Indian Agricl. Service (dep. dir. of agric., Punjab) (b. 13th Apl., 1887).—Joined the service as asst. dir. of agric. on 10th Aug., 1909; dep. dir. of agric., Dec., 1921; M.B.E., June, 1935.

FAWCETT, Sir CHARLES GORDON HILL, Kt., late Indian C.S. (Bombay) (b. 28th June, 1869).—Educ. at Harrow and Pembroke Coll., Cambridge; apptd. after exam. of 1888; arrived, 9th Nov., 1890, and served in Bombay as asst. collr. and mag.; 2nd asst. and asst. judge, Feb., 1897; under-sec. to govt., pol., &c., depts., June, 1898; acting remembrancer of legal affairs, March, 1899, and April, 1901; 1st asst. and ditto, Nov., 1901; judge and ditto, Sept., 1903; remembrancer of legal affairs, Dec., 1904, to March, 1905; agt. for Sardars in Deccan, April, 1912; addl. judl. commr., Sind, May, 1918; judl. commr. of Sind, Nov., 1918; judge, high ct., Bombay, April, 1920; Kt., June, 1927; offg. chief justice, June, 1928; ret'd., June, 1929, author of "The First Century of British Justice in India."

FAWCETT, EDWARD PINDER, C.B.E., B.A., late Indian C.S. (United Provs.) (b. 17th March, 1874).—Educ. at Dean Close Sch., Cheltenham, Ley's Sch., and Caius Coll., Cambridge; appointed after exam. of 1897; arrived, 7th Dec., 1898; and served in the N.W. Prov. as asst. mag. and collr., and asst. commr.; joint mag., Nov., 1907; dep. commr., Nov., 1914; C.B.E., June, 1918; mag. and collr., July, 1918; dep. commr., March, 1921; addl. commr., Dec., 1924; offg. commr., April, 1925; commr. (prov. sub); Aug., 1926; confd., July, 1927; ret'd., Aug., 1930.

FAWCUS, GEORGE ERNEST, C.I.E., O.B.E., V.D., M.A. (Oxon), Indian Educ. Service (dir. of public instrn., Bihar) (b. 12th Mar., 1885).—Joined the service, 4th Oct., 1909; inspr. of schools, Dec., 1909; asst. dir. of public instrn., Bihar and Orissa, April, 1915; inspr. of schools, Chota Nagpur div., July, 1915; temp. dir. of public instrn., Bihar and Orissa, Oct., 1917; confd., June, 1921; O.B.E., Jan., 1923; C.I.E., Jan., 1927.

FAWCUS, LOUIS REGINALD, Indian C.S. (mag. and collr., Bengal) (b. 7th Nov., 1887).—Educ. at Uppingham, Trinity Coll., Cambridge, and Univ. Coll., London; apptd. after exam. of 1910; arrived in India, 4th Dec., 1911, and served in Eastern Bengal and Assam as asst. mag. and collr. and asst. settlt. offr.; transf. to Bengal, April, 1912; jt. mag. and dep. collr., Oct., 1917; I.A.R.O., Aug., 1916, to March, 1919; mag. and collr., Sept., 1925; offg. secty., bd. of

rev., Bengal, Oct., 1927; offg. dir., land records, Bengal, Mar., 1929; offg. secy. to govt., Bengal agric. and ind. depts., Jan., 1931; confd., July, 1931; offg. secty., rev. dept., Bengal, June, 1935; offg. commr., Feb., 1936.

FAV, ARCHIBALD THOMAS, Indian Police (dist. supt.) (b. 22nd Aug., 1883).—Joined the service, 1st Apl., 1901; dep. supt., Mar., 1922; apptd. to I.P. as dist. supt., Burma, 20th Mar., 1935.

FAZL, A. H. A. (see Abul Fazl, A. H.).

FAZL ALI, SAIVID, Barr. Judl. dept., Bihar (b. 19th Sept., 1886).—Apptd. judge, high ct., Patna, Feb., 1929.

FAZL MUHAMMAD KHAN, KHAN, B.A., Indian Forest Service (dep. consvr., Punjab) (b. Jan., 1888).—Apptd. asst. consvr., from 19th July, 1922; dep. consvr., July, 1926.

FAZLUR RAHMAN KHONDKAR, B.A., Indian Police (asst. supt.) (b. 1st Feb., 1905).—Joined the service as proby. asst. supt., Bengal, 22nd April, 1929; confd., Oct., 1931.

FEARNLEY-WHITTINGSTALL, FRANCIS HERBERT, C.I.E., late Indian Police (b. 8th June, 1894).—Joined the service as asst. supt. on 15th Nov., 1914; dist. supt., May, 1918; awarded King's police medal, Jan., 1921; offg. asst. commr., Rangoon, Sept., 1925; pte. sec. to govr., Burma, March, 1926; C.I.E., March, 1929; dep. commr. of police, Rangoon, Apl., 1929; asst. inspr.-genl. of police, Burma, May, 1931; ret'd., June, 1936.

FEARON, LEONARD KEARY, Salt Dept., Madras (dep. commr.) (b. 10th Nov., 1881).—Held non-gazetted appt. from 11th Dec., 1901; asst. inspr., April, 1905; inspr., April, 1910; asst. commr., Oct., 1920; dep. commr., Mar., 1932.

FELL, DOUGLAS YATES, B.A., Indian C.S. (asst. collr., Bombay) (b. 18th Sept., 1910).—Educ. at Charterhouse and St. John's Coll., Oxford; apptd. after exam. of 1933; arrived in India, Nov., 1934, and served in Bombay as asst. collr.

FELL, Sir GODFREY BUTLER HUNTER, K.C.I.E., C.S.I., B.A., late Indian C.S.—Educ. at Eton, and Magdalen Coll., Oxford; apptd. after exam. of 1894; arrived, 7th Dec., 1895, and served in Burma as asst. commr.; personal asst. to chief commr., April, 1897; private sec. to lieut.-govr., May, 1897; acting asst. sec. to govt. of Burma, March, 1899; confd., Oct., 1899; under sec. to govt., Oct., 1900; private sec. to lieut.-gov., April, 1903, to Dec., 1904; offg. under sec. to govt. of India, home dept., April, 1906; confd., July, 1906; dep. sec., home dept., Oct., 1906, to July, 1909; dep. commr., Burma, March, 1910; C.I.E., June, 1909; offg. sec. to govt., July, 1912; addl. member, gov.-genl.'s council, July, 1913; dep. sec. to govt. of India, fin. dept., Nov., 1914; offg. finl. advr., mil. fin. dept., govt. of India, April, 1915; confirmed, July, 1915; member, Indian munitions bd., in addition, from Feb., 1917; C.S.I., Jan., 1917; K.C.I.E., June, 1918; member of commee. to enquire into Indian Army administration and organization, 1919; commr., Jan. 1921; ret'd., Sept., 1924.

FENTON, SIR MICHAEL WILLIAM, K.C.S.I., B.A., late Indian C.S.—Educ. at the Royal Sch., Armagh, and Trinity Coll., Dublin; apptd. after exam. of 1881; arrived, 18th Nov., 1883, and served in the Punjab as asst. commr.; under sec. to govt., rev. dept., Oct., 1887, to Nov., 1891; senr. sec. to finl. commrs., July, 1892; rev. sec. to govt., Oct., 1896; dep. commr., Oct., 1899; commr., Dec., 1909; chief sec. to govt., Oct., 1910; C.S.I., Jan., 1911; second finl. commr., Jan., 1912; K.C.S.I., June, 1914; ret'd., July, 1916; author of 'Punjab Municipal Manual.'

FENWICK, EDGAR NORMAN, Indian Service of Engrs. (exec. engr., Punjab) (b. 30th Aug., 1892).—Joined the service as asst. engr., Oct., 1919; asst. exec. engr., Jan., 1920; exec. engr., Oct., 1925.

FERARD, JOHN EDWARD, C.B.E., late India Office.—Educ. at Eton, and Magdalen Coll., Oxford; upper divn. clerk, local govt. board, 2nd Feb., 1892; junr. clerk, India Office, 7th April, 1893; private sec. to parly. under sec. of state, Feb., 1894; private sec. to permanent under sec. of state, July, 1898, to Feb., 1901; senior clerk, public works dept., March, 1901; transf'd. to mil. dept., Oct., 1903, to judl. and public dept., April, 1904; asst. sec. pol. dept., April, 1907; ditto, judl. and public dept., Oct., 1912; acting sec., judl. and public dept., Oct., 1917, to May, 1918, and from Dec., 1918; C.B.E., Jan., 1918; sec. judl. and public dept., Dec., 1919; designation of dept. changed to public and judl., April, 1924; ret'd., July, 1929.

FERGUSON, DONALD HARRY, Indian State Rlys. (transpn. supt., G.I.P. rly.) (b. 8th Feb., 1890).—Joined the service as asst. transpn. supt., G.I.P. Rly., 16th June, 1924; supt., July, 1930.

FERGUSON, JAMES, M.C., Indian Police (supt., United Provs.) (b. 22nd Mar., 1896).—Joined the service as asst. supt., United Provs., 24th June, 1920; offg. supt., Sept., 1923, and again, April, 1925; supt. (prov'l. sup.), Sept., 1930; confd., Aug., 1932.

FERGUSON, ROLAND EDWARD STUART, Indian Police (supt., Bihar) (b. 22nd Aug., 1899).—Joined the service on 16th Dec., 1921; offg. supt., Apl., 1928; confd., Oct., 1932, princpl., training coll., Hazaribagh, Mar., 1936.

FERGUSSON, JOHN CARLYLE, B.A., late Indian C.S. (b. 17th June, 1872).—Educ. at St. Paul's Sch., and Trinity Coll., Oxford; apptd. after exam. of 1895; arrived 8th Dec., 1896, and served in the N.W. Prov. and Oudh as asst. mag. and collr.; asst. commr., May, 1901; settlt. offr., July, 1902; under sec. to govt., June, 1903; offg. under sec. to govt. of India, home dept., Jan., 1904; confd., Dec., 1904; re-transf'd. to United Provs., March, 1908; asst. commr., May, 1908; dep. commr., March, 1913; mag. and collr., Sept., 1914; commr., N.I. salt rev., March, 1920; ret'd., Aug., 1930.

FERMIE, OTTO WILLIAM PATRICK, B.Sc., B.E., India Posts and Tels. (dir. tels.) (b. 16th July, 1891).—Joined the service, 21st Oct., 1918; asst. divl. engr., July, 1920; divl. engr., tels., July, 1928; supt. of tels., N.W. rly., Jan., 1932; offg. dir., tels., United Provs., Mar., 1936; confd., Aug., 1936.

FERMOR, SIR LEWIS LEIGH, Kt., O.B.E., D.Sc., A.R.S.M., F.G.S., F.A.S.B., M.Inst.M.M., late Geological Survey of India. (b. 18th Sept., 1880).—Entered the service, 18th July, 1902; supt., Dec., 1910; transf'd. to service under ry. bd., Jan., 1916; on deputn. to Indian munitions bd., April, 1917, to Aug., 1918; O.B.E., June, 1919; offg. dir., Jan., 1930; confd., June, 1932; knighted, June, 1935; ret'd., Nov., 1935.

FERRAR, MICHAEL LLOYD, C.S.I., C.I.E., O.B.E., Lt.-Col. I.A. (ret'd.) (b. 16th Apl., 1876).—First commn. 22nd Jan., 1896; in mil. employ to Sept., 1901, when he was apptd. asst. commr., Punjab; asst. censor, Bombay, Dec., 1914; postal censor, Bombay, Oct., 1916; O.B.E., Jan., 1919; dep. commr., Nov., 1919; C.I.E., June, 1922; chief commr., Andaman and Nicobar Islands, April, 1923; C.S.I., June, 1927; ret'd., Apl., 1931.

FERRAR, WALTER & INGLIS, V.D., M.I.E. (Ind.), late Indian State Rlys. (b. 12th Feb., 1875).—Joined the service as proby. asst. engr., 6th Dec., 1895; confd., March, 1899; junr. engr., July, 1903; resdt. engr., Oct., 1906; dist. engr., June, 1917; dep. ch. engr., May, 1926; ret'd., Aug., 1930.

FERRERS, VIVYAN MACLEOD, M.A., late Indian C.S. (b. 20th Jan., 1877).—Educ. at Bath Sch. and Trinity Coll., Cambridge; apptd. after exam. of 1899; arrived, 10th Dec., 1900, and served in Sind as asst. collr. and mag. and asst. judge; dist. and sess. judge, Nov., 1918; offg. judl. commr. of Sind, Dec., 1931; confd., Nov., 1933; ret'd., Dec., 1935.
