FERRIE, ALLAN, Indian Service of Engrs. (exec. engr., Punjab) (b. 12th Apl., 1892).—Joined the service, 1st Oct., 1919; asst. exec. engr., May, 1920; exec. engr., Oct., 1924; under secty., P.W.D., irrgn. br., Punjab, Oct., 1929.

FFORDE, SIR CECIL ROBERT, Kt., K.C., late Judicial Dept., Punjab (b. 24th June, 1876).—Addl. judge, high ct., Lahore, 1922; apptd. judge, high ct., Lahore, Oct., 1925; Kt., June, 1930; ret'd., Mar., 1931.

FFORDE, THEOBALD, late Indian Police (b. 29th Mar., 1883).—Held non-gazetted appts. from 17th Oct., 1902; dep. supt. (prov'l. service), May, 1914; apptd. to the I.P.S. as dist. supt., 11th Feb., 1927; ret'd., June, 1937.

FFORDE, THEOBALD PHILIP FEATHERSTONEHAUGH, Indian Police (asst. supt.) (b. 25th Jan., 1909).—Joined the service as proby. asst. supt., Burma, 27th Jan., 1932; confd., Jan., 1934.

FFOULKES, GEORGE ROBERT GWYNNE, Indian Police (b. 9th June, 1915).—Apptd. asst. supt., Bihar, on probn., 11th Nov., 1935.

FIELD, SIR DONALD MOYLE, C.I.E., Lieut.-Col. I.A., ret'd. (b. 19th Nov., 1881).—First commn. dated 28th July, 1900; in mil. employ until 13th Nov., 1906; on spl. duty in connection with Amir's visit to India, Nov., 1906, to March, 1907; apptd. supy. asst. commr., Punjab, Mar., 1907; asst. commr., N.W.F. Prov., May, 1908; pol. asst. and asst. to resdt., Kashmir, Aug., 1908; asst. pol. agent, Kalat, Mar., 1914; temply. reverted to mil. duty, Oct., 1914; asst. resdt., Baroda, Jan., 1917; asst. to resdt. in Kashmir

for Leh and Ladakh, May, 1917; under-sec. to the govt. of India, for. and pol. dept., Oct., 1917; pol. agent, S. Rajputana States, Dec., 1918; commr., Ajmer-Merwara, March, 1920; pol. agent, S. Rajputana States, Dec., 1920; on foreign service in Chamba State, Oct., 1923 to Feb., 1925; resdt. at Jaipur, Feb., 1925; pol. agent in Southern States of C.I. and in Malwa, Nov., 1925; resident in Mewar, March, 1928; offg. agent to govr. genl. in Rajputana and ch. commr. of Ajmer-Merwara, Oct., 1930; resdt. in Mewar and pol. agent, S. Rajputana States, Feb., 1931; agent to govr.-genl., Madras States, Nov., 1932; C.I.E., Jan., 1935; ret'd., July, 1935; knighted, Feb., 1937.

FIELD, GEOFFREY GLENNIE, Indian Police (supt., United Provs.) (b. 3rd June, 1895).—Joined the service as asst. supt. on 22nd Nov., 1915; I.A.R.O. from 8th July, 1918, to 8th Aug., 1919; offg. supt., Oct., 1924; confd., Dec., 1926; awarded King's police medal, Jan., 1932.

FIELD, JOHN BERTRAM FINCH, Indian Police (asst. supt.) (b. 15th July, 1910).—Joined the service as proby. asst. supt., Punjab, 24th Nov., 1930; confd., Nov., 1932; offg. supt., Nov., 1933.

FIELD, ROBERT EDWIN, O.B.E. India Office (principal, services and genl. dept.) (b. 27th Dec., 1888).—Apptd. second class clerk, India Office, 22nd April, 1910, and attached to the pol. dept.; transf. to acct.-gen's dept., Dec., 1911; asst. sec. to cttee on State technical scholarships, April to Sept., 1912; transf. to dept. of sec. for Indian students, Sept., 1912; sub-warden, 21, Cromwell Road, S.W., April to Oct., 1913; actg. warden, ditto, Nov., 1914, to Feb., 1919; sec. to London advisory cttee for Indian students, Aug., 1913; minor staff clerk, Feb., 1916; asst. to educt. adviser for Indian students, Feb., 1919; transf. to office of high commr. for India, Oct., 1920; asst. to jt. sec. for Indian students, Nov., 1920; re-transf. to India Office as asst. principal, April, 1921; jt. sec. to cttee. on Indian students, 1921-2; reading clerk to council, Nov., 1924; pte. sec. to perm. under sec. of state, June, 1926; actg. principal, 1st Dec., 1928; confd., Apl., 1930; O.B.E., June, 1936.

FIELDEN, LIONEL, All-India Radio (Controller of Broadcasting) (b. 15th May, 1896).—Joined the service, as Controller of Broadcasting on 30th Aug., 1935.

FIELDING, CHARLES HENRY, M.B., Lt.-Col., I.M.S. (ret'd.), late Jail Dept., Burma (b. 8th Nov., 1880).—First commn. dated 1st Feb., 1908; arrived, 8th Oct., 1908; offg. supt., central jail, Insein, June, 1912; reverted to milty. service from 21st Sept., 1914, to 20th March, 1921; supt., March, 1921; offg. inspr.-genl. of prisons, May, 1927; ret'd., May, 1930.

FIELDING, STUART SKENE McA'FEE, late Survey of India (b. 21st Jan., 1877).—Joined the service as sub-assst. supt. (proby.), 21st Nov., 1898; confd., Nov., 1899; extra asst. supt., Oct., 1906; supt., June, 1928; ret'd., Jan., 1932.

FIELDING-HALL, JOHN GRAHAM, Indian C.S. (under secty., fin. dept., Burma) (b. 1st May, 1908).—Educ. at Rugby and New Coll., Oxford;

apptd. after exam. of 1930; arrived in India, Nov., 1931, and served in Burma as asst. commr.; under secty., fin. dept., Burma, Aug., 1936.

FIELDS-CLARKE, VICTOR HERBERT TOM, late Indian Forest Service (b. 11th Feb., 1888).—Arrived, 18th Dec., 1909, and served as asst. consvr.; dep. consvr., Dec., 1914; offg. consvr., Nov., 1926; confd., Sept., 1930; ret'd., Feb., 1935.

FIFE, JOHN EDWARD, late Indian Police Service (b. 25th Apl., 1880).—Joined the service, 7th Jan., 1901, and posted to United Provs.; asst. supt. of police, April, 1906; supt., Oct., 1913; awarded King's police medal, Jan., 1922; offg. dep. inspr.-genl., March, 1927; ret'd., July, 1932.

FILSON, JAMES THOMAS WARNOCK, late Indian Police Service (Madras) (b. 23rd Apl., 1880).—Joined the service, 30th Nov., 1900, as probr.; asst. supt., July, 1903; supt., June, 1910; asst. inspr.-gen., Madras, Sept., 1915; offg. commr. of police, Madras, April to Nov., 1923; offg. dep. inspr. of police, Feb., 1925; confd., Jan., 1927; ret'd., Mar., 1932.

FINDLAY, SIR CHARLES STEWART, Kt., M.A., LL.B., late Indian C.S. (Central Provs.) (b. 28th Aug., 1874).—Educ. at Aberdeen and Edinburgh Univs. and at Univ. Coll., London; apptd. after exam. of 1897; arrived, 5th Dec., 1898, and served in the Cent. Provs. as asst. commr.; employed in legislative dept., govt. of India, from June to Dec., 1912; and in sectt., Central Provs., from Dec., 1912, to March, 1913; legal membr., sec. to legisl. council, and sec. to chief commr., Central Provs., April, 1913; dep. commr., and ditto, Sept., 1914; dist. and sess. judge, and ditto, May, 1917, to March, 1919; addl. judl. commr., March, 1919; dist. and sess. judge, Jan., 1921, to March, 1925; offg. judl. commr., March, 1925; confd., March, 1926; Kt., June, 1929; ret'd., Jan., 1932.

FINDLAY, JAMES, M.A., M.B., Ch.B., Lt.-Col., I.M.S. Jail Dept., Burma (inspr.-genl.) (b. 19th Aug., 1886).—Date of first commn., 26th July, 1913; supt., jail dept., Burma (temp.), Mar., 1924; ditto (perm.) Feb., 1925; inspr.-genl. of prisons, Mar., 1934.

FINLAY, WILLIAM WATERS, M.A. (Edin.), Indian C.S. (dep. commr., United Provs.) (b. 8th May, 1899).—Educ. at Edinburgh Univ., and Christ Church, Oxford; joined the service, 27th Oct., 1924; arrived, 8th Dec., 1924, and served in the United Provs. as asst. mag. and collr.; jt. mag., Nov., 1928; offg. mag. and collr., Dec., 1931, and again, Sept., 1933; offg. secty., bd. of rev., United Provs., Jan., 1934; dep. commr., Nov., 1934.

FINLAYSON, ANDREW, Indian Service of Engrs. (exec. engr., Bengal) (b. 11th Aug., 1900).—Joined the service as asst. exec. engr., Bengal, on 17th Oct., 1923; exec. engr., Oct., 1932.

FINLOW, ROBERT STEEL, C.I.E., B.Sc., F.I.C., late Indian Agricl. Service (b. 17th Aug., 1877).—Joined the service, 1st April, 1905 (prevy. nominally employed in service of Bihar Indigo Planters' Assocn., but mainly in research work for govt. of Bengal); jute expert, April, 1905; fibre expert, E. Bengal and Assam, July, 1906; employed as 2nd dep. dir. of agric.,

July, 1910, to July, 1911; fibre expert, Bengal, April, 1912; offg. dir. of agric., Bengal and dir. of fisheries, Oct., 1922; dir. of agric., Bengal, Feb., 1925; C.I.E., March, 1929; retd., Aug., 1932.

FINNEY, PHILIP EDMUND STANLEY, O.B.E., Indian Police Service (b. 11th Dec., 1904).—Joined the service as asst. supt., Bengal, on 5th Dec., 1924; offg. supt., temp. employed as supt., Deoli jail, Dec., 1931; confd. as supt. July, 1937; O.B.E., June, 1934.

FIRTH, JOHN RUPERT, M.A., late Indian Educl. Service.—Joined the service as master-in-charge, training class for European schools, Sanawar, 4th March, 1915; on mil. duty from 23rd April, 1916, to 17th Nov., 1919; prof. govt. coll., Lahore, Nov., 1919; retd., Feb., 1929.

FIRTH, LESLIE GEORGE BECKWITH, late Revenue survey dept., Madras (b. 24th Sept., 1879).—Joined the service as asst. dir., 25th March, 1911; offg. dir., April, 1927; confd., May, 1928; retd., Sept., 1934.

FISH, Rev. FREDERIC JOHN, B.A., M.C., Indian Eccl. Estab. (junr. chaplain) (b. 13th April, 1894).—Apptd. chaplain-on-probation, Punjab, 5th Feb., 1930; junr. chaplain, Feb., 1932.

FISHER, D., M.B.E., late Indian State Rlys. (b. 2nd July, 1882).—Joined the service, 30th July, 1908; offg. supt., watch and ward, E.B. rly., July, 1929; confd., July, 1931; retd., July, 1937.

FISHER, FRANK, B.A., LL.B., late Indian C.S. (Burma) (b. 6th March, 1886).—Educ. at St. Paul's Sch., Clare Coll., Cambridge, and University Coll., London; apptd. after exam. of 1909; arrived in India, 30th Nov., 1910, and served in Burma as asst. commr.; apptd. temp. to pol. dept. as asst. commr., N.-W. Frontier Prov., Dec., 1916; offg. dep. commr., Jan., 1922; dep. commr. (provl. sub.), Feb., 1926; retd., Feb., 1931.

FISHER, GERALD THOMAS, C.I.E., B.A., Lt.-Col., I.A., Indian Political Service (b. 27th Aug., 1887).—First commn., 9th Sept., 1908; apptd. to pol. dept., govt. of India, as asst. commr., Kohat, 13th May, 1919; under-sec. to agent to gov.-gen. in Central India, March, 1921; confd. as pol. officer, June, 1922; pol. agent, Chagai, March, 1924; H. M. Consul for Sistan and Kain, Jan., 1925; asst. pol. resdt., Aden, Dec., 1927; pol. agent, Bundelkhand, Nov., 1928; secty. to resdt., Hyderabad, Mar., 1932; ch. minister, Kapurthala state, Jan., 1935; resdt. at Gwalior and pol. agent for States of Rampur and Benares, Apl., 1937; C.I.E., Jan., 1938.

FISHWICK, DAVID, B.A., Indian C.S. (asst. commr., Burma) (b. 29th April, 1906).—Educ. at Bradford Grammar School and Peterhouse Coll., Cambridge; joined the service, 14th Oct., 1929; arrived, Dec., 1929, and served in Burma as asst. commr.; under-secty. to govt., local govt. dept., Burma, Mar., 1933; asst. collr., Impl. customs service, Rangoon, Mar., 1934.

FITT, WATKIN, Office of High Commr. for India (higher exec. officer, accts. dept.) (b. 22nd June, 1898).—Apptd. asst. clerk, 10th Oct., 1919, after previous service from 22nd Oct.,

1915; on mil. service from Feb., 1916 to Aug., 1919; second class clerk, 29th April, 1920; services lent to high commr. for India from April, 1921; exec. officer, April, 1922; higher exec. officer, Nov., 1934.

FITZE, KENNETH SAMUEL, C.I.E., B.A., Indian C.S., Indian Political Service (b. 6th Jan., 1887).—Educ. at Marlborough, Corpus Christi Coll., Oxford, and Univ. Coll., London; apptd. after exam. of 1910; arrived in India, 25th Nov., 1911, and served in the Central Provs. as asst. commr.; apptd. templ. to pol. dept. as asst. to resdt., Kashmir, April, 1915; first asst. to resdt., Baroda, May, 1917; under-sec. to govt. of India, for. and pol. dept., Nov., 1918; apptd., substantively to pol. dept., govt. of India, Jan., 1919; census supt., Central India, Oct., 1921, to Jan., 1922; sec. to agent to gov.-gen. in Central India, Jan., 1922; dep. sec. to govt. of India, for. and pol. dept., Nov., 1924; pol. agent, Baghelkhand, Nov., 1926; jt. secty. to govt. of India, for. and pol. dept. (temp.), July, 1930; jt. secty. to British delegation, Indian round table conf., London, Nov., 1930-Jan., 1931; 2nd Session, Sept.-Dec., 1931; C.I.E., Jan., 1932; jt. secty., Indian States enquiry comee., Jan., 1932; pol. agent. southern states of C.I., Nov., 1932; offg. and opium commr. in C.I., Apl., 1933; offg. resdt. A.G.G., in C.I., Jan., 1935; confd., Aug., 1935; resdt. for Central India, Apl., 1937.

FITZGERALD, DESMOND, late Indian Police (b. 20th June, 1879).—Joined the service, 14th Aug., 1903; supt. of police, May, 1912; retd., June, 1934.

FITZGERALD, SEYMOUR GONNE VESEY, M.A., LL.D., Barr., late Indian C.S.—Educ. at Charterhouse and Keble Coll., Oxford; apptd. after exam. of 1906; arrived, 5th Dec., 1907 and served in the Central Prov. as asst. commr.; asst. commr. and sub-judge, Feb., 1910; ditto and dist. judge, Sept., 1910; regr., judl. commr.'s ct., Feb., 1913; addl. dist. and sess. judge, Oct., 1913; dist. and sess. judge, May, 1917; legal membr., sec. to legisl. council and sec. to chief commr., in legal dept., Feb., 1918; dist. and sess. judge, May, 1918; on mil. service (I.A.R.O.), June, 1918, to March, 1919; retd., Nov., 1923; lecturer to I.C.S. probationers under the Indian Civil Service delegacy from Oct., 1925, to June, 1932, and a lecturer in Muhammadan Law under the Colonial Office scheme for African Civil Service probationers from Oct., 1926, to June, 1933; lecturer, Inns of Ct. since 1927; lecturer, sch. of Oriental Studies, Oct., 1932; supervisor of I.C.S. probationers, ditto, Oct., 1937.

FITZHERBERT, RICHARD ANTHONY, A.C.G.I., A.M.I.C.E., Indian Service of Engrs. (exec. engr., Bombay) (b. 4th Mar., 1887).—Joined the service as asst. engr., 1st Oct., 1909; in milty. employ. from 12th Nov., 1915, to 16th Jan., 1919; exec. engr., Oct., 1917.

FITZMAURICE, DESMOND FITZ-JOHN, B.A., Major, R.E. (master, security printing) (b. 17th Aug., 1893).—Apptd. dep. mint master, on probn., Nov., 1929; ditto (temp.), Dec., 1932; offg. master, security printing, Sept., 1934; confd., Oct., 1934.

FLACK, ALAN WILLIAM, B.A., B.Sc., Indian C.S. (asst. mag. and collr., B. & O.) (b. 1st Nov., 1913).—Educ. at City of London School and St. John's College, Cambridge; joined the service, 16th Sept., 1937; arrived in India, Nov., 1937, and served in B. and O. as asst. mag. and collr.

FLATT, LESLIE NEEVE, B.Sc., V.D., A.M.I.C.E., A.M.I.M.E., Indian State Ryls. (ch. mechl. engr., E.B. Rly.) (b. 26th March, 1889).—Joined the service, 4th Jan., 1913; asst. loco. supt., E.I. Rly., June, 1913; in milty. employ from 22nd Feb., 1916, to 24th Feb., 1919; works manager, G.I.P. Rly., Aug., 1920; dep. ch. mech. engr., March, 1923; dep. carr. and wagon supt., Oct., 1923; dep. ch. mech. engr., Oct., 1924; offg. dep. loco. supt., April, 1927; offg. loco. and carr. supt., Dec., 1930; offg. ch. mechl. engr., E.B. rly., Feb., 1934; confd., July, 1935.

FLEETON, ERIC RALPH, Indian State Ryls. (supt.) (b. 24th Aug., 1896).—Joined the service as asst. carr. and wagon supt., 1st Sept., 1920; transptn. (power) supt., E.I. rly., Sept., 1929; offg. dep. ch. mechl. engr., E.I. rly., Mar., 1936.

FLETCHER, ALAN HENRY SAUL, Indian C.S. (asst. commr., Assam) (b. 12th Oct., 1903).—Educ. at Sherborne, and Trinity College, Dublin; joined the service, 5th Oct., 1928; arrived, Nov., 1928, and served in Assam as asst. commr.; offg. dep. commr., Mar., 1934.

FLETCHER, ANTONY LEOCADIA, M.Sc., Indian C.S. (asst. commr., Punjab) (b. 12th Dec., 1909).—Educated at St. Joseph's High School, Trivandrum, the Universities of Madras and Nagpur, and the School of Oriental Studies, London; joined the service, 10th October, 1933; arrived in India, November, 1933, and served in the Punjab as asst. commr.; sub-divl. offic., Nov., 1936.

FLETCHER, EDWARD WALTER, Major, I.A., Indian Political Service (b. 23rd July, 1899).—First commn., 23rd Jan., 1919; in milty. employ to Jan., 1928; British trade agent, Gyantse, and asst. to pol. officer in Sikkim, Nov., 1929; secty., Brit. legn., Kabul, Dec., 1932; pol. agent, Sabar Kantha agency, Mar., 1935; on depn. to Baltic States for 13 mths., from Dec., 1936.

FLETCHER, JOHN HINTON, M.C., Indian Service of Engrs. (under secty. to govt. (northern admin.), Punjab) (b. 23rd June, 1893).—Joined the service as asst. engr., Oct., 1919; asst. exec. engr., Jan., 1920; exec. engr., Oct., 1925; under secty. to govt., irrigation branch, Punjab, Jan., 1931; ditto (construction), Oct., 1934; offg. ditto (northern admin.), Feb., 1936.

FLETCHER, LEONARD RAWCLIFFE, late Indian Forest Engrg. Service (b. 9th March, 1891).—Joined the service as forest engr., Burma, on 26th Dec., 1921; ret'd., Mar., 1933.

FLETCHER, THOMAS BAINBRIDGE, F.L.S., F.E.S., late Indian Agricl. Service (b. 25th Mar., 1878).—Joined the service as supy. entomologist, 2nd March, 1910; confd., March, 1912; govt. entomologist, Coimbatore, April, 1912; impl. entomologist, Dec., 1913; ret'd., Mar., 1933.

FLEURY, WALFRID RONALD, Indian Service of Engrs. (asst. exec. engr., Orissa) (b. 7th Apr.,

1908).—Joined the service as asst. exec. engr., Bihar and Orissa, 25th Aug., 1930.

FLEWETT, WILLIAM EDWARD, B.A., Indian Forest Service (dep. consvr.) (b. 19th Aug., 1894).—Apptd. dep. consvr., Punjab, 16th Dec., 1921; div. forest officer, Andamans, Sept., 1931; offg. ch. forest officer, Andamans, Aug., 1931 to Aug., 1936.

FLINT, HARRY EWART, O.B.E., B.A., Indian Forest Service (dep. consvr., Burma) (b. 22nd Aug., 1893).—Joined the service as asst. consvr. on 18th Dec., 1922; dep. consvr., May, 1925; O.B.E., June, 1932.

FLOWERDEW, RICHARD EDWARD, C.I.E., M.B., Lt.-Col., I.M.S., Jail Dept. (inspr.-genl. of prisons, Bengal) (b. 26th Sept., 1886).—First commn. dated 30th Jan., 1909; services placed at disposal of govt. of Burma from 28th May, 1922; supt., central jail, Insein, May, 1922; ditto, Rangoon, Feb., 1925; offg. inspr.-genl. of prisons, Burma, June, 1930; inspr.-genl. of prisons, Bengal, Mar., 1931; C.I.E., Feb., 1937.

FLOWERDEW, SPENCER PELHAM, V.D., late Indian State Railways (b. 6th Oct., 1881).—Trained at the R.I.E. Coll.; apptd. asst. engr., state rys., 1st Oct., 1902; exec. engr., Nov., 1910; asst. agent, N.W. Ry., Oct., 1917; junior dep. agent, ditto, Oct., 1919; offg. divl. supt., March, 1925; suptg. engr., July, 1926; offg. ch. engr., E.I. Rly., March, 1927; confd., June, 1928; offg. dir. of civil engrg., rly. bd., Dec., 1929; ret'd., July, 1932.

FLUX, DUNDAS A. D. (see Dundas, A. D. F.).

FLUX, RONALD CECIL, B.Sc., Indian Service of Engrs. (exec. engr., Burma) (b. 2nd June, 1901).—Joined the service on 1st Oct., 1923; exec. engr., Oct., 1932.

FOGARTY, PHILIP CHRISTOPHER, B.A., Indian C.S. (dep. commr., Burma) (b. 20th Dec., 1884).—Educ. at St. Joseph's Seminary, Galway, and Queen's Coll., Galway, Royal University of Ireland; apptd. after exam. of 1908; arrived 13th Dec., 1909, and served in Burma as asst. commr.; asst. sec. to finl. commr., Burma, April, 1914; on mily. duty from Jan., 1917, to April, 1919; dep. commr. (prov. sub.), Oct., 1924; confd., April, 1926; jt. regr., co-op. societies, Dec., 1927; regr., ditto, Dec., 1928; secty., forest dept., Burma, Mar., 1932, to Feb., 1935.

FOLEY, BLANCHARD, C.S.I., M.A., late Indian C.S. (Bihar and Orissa).—Educ. at Highgate Sch., and Merton Coll., Oxford; apptd. after exam. of 1892; arrived, 14th Dec., 1893, and served in Bengal as asst. mag. and collr.; under sec. to govt., finl. dept., May, 1896; joint mag. and dep. collr., June, 1900; mag. and collr., July, 1905; acting commr. of excise and salt, Feb., 1908, to May, 1909, and from March to May, 1910; transf. to Bihar and Orissa as mag. and collr., April, 1912; commr. of a divn., April, 1917; offg. member, board of rev., April, 1917; offg. member, board of rev., April, 1924; confd., Oct., 1924; C.S.I., Jan., 1927; ret'd., Dec., 1928.

FOOKS, RAYMOND HATHERELL, B.A., late Indian Police (b. 23rd June, 1888).—Joined the service, 10th Dec., 1908; asst. supt., Oct., 1909; supt., March, 1919; offg. dep. inspr.-genl., Nov., 1931; dep. inspr.-genl. (prov. subs.), Aug., 1932; ret'd., June, 1935.

FORBES, ALISTER ESME BUCHAN, O.B.E., late Indian State Rlys., G.I.P. Rly. (b. 4th Apl., 1881).—Joined the service as asst. loco. supt., 30th Dec., 1904; dist. ditto, June, 1915; in milty. employ. from 3rd Dec., 1917, to 27th March, 1918; O.B.E., June, 1919; divl. transportation supt., Nov., 1922; contr. of stores, Aug., 1926; ret'd., Nov., 1931.

FORBES, GEORGE FREEMAN MURRAY, O.B.E., M.C., Indian Police (supt., Orissa) (b. 5th June, 1896).—Joined the service, 1st Feb., 1922; asst. supt. (proby.), Sept., 1922; confd., Jan., 1927; offg. supt. govt. rly. police, May, 1927; offg. dist. supt., Oct., 1927; confd., July, 1930; O.B.E., Jan., 1932.

FORBES, SIR GEORGE STUART, K.C.S.I., M.A., late Indian C.S.—Educ. at Aberdeen Gr. Sch. and Univ.; apptd. after exam. of 1870; arrived, 16th Oct., 1872, and served in Madras as asst. collr. and mag.; from Sept., 1880, was employed as acting under sec. in the rev. dept., asst. sec., jud. and legisl. dept., and under sec. in the depts. under the chief sec.; from Feb., 1883, served under the govt. of India in the home dept. as offg. under sec., also in the foreign dept., on special duty, Dec., 1884, and as junr. under sec., Jan., 1885; offg. under sec. to govt. of India, foreign dept., Dec., 1886; acting 1st asst. to the rest. at Hyderabad, and sec. for Berar, Dec., 1887, to April, 1889; sub-collr. and joint mag., Sept., 1888; district and sessions judge, April, 1890; collr. and mag., Jan., 1891; acted as rev. sec. in 1895, 1896, 1897 and 1898; sec. in local and municipal depts., Oct., 1897; addl. member of legisl. council, Dec., 1897, June, 1898, June, 1899, June, 1900, June, 1902, and Feb., 1904; sec. to govt., rev. dept., Sept., 1898; offg. chief sec. to govt., Nov., 1901, May, 1902, and March, 1903; confd., Nov., 1903; temp. member of council, Madras, May, 1904, and May, 1906; C.S.I., June, 1904; ordinary member of council, Madras, June, 1906; K.C.S.I., June, 1909; ret'd., July, 1909.

FORBES, HENRY FLAVELLE, C.I.E., B.A., late Indian C.S. (Punjab).—Educ. at Wesley Coll., Dublin, and Dublin Univ.; apptd. after exam. of 1900; arrived, 3rd Dec., 1901, and served in the Punjab as asst. commr.; judge, small cause ct., Simla, Nov., 1915; junr. judl. offr., Basra, Jan., 1917, to Dec., 1920; C.I.E., March, 1919; dist. and sessions judge, Punjab, Nov., 1921; ret'd., May, 1928.

FORD, WILLIAM ALAN WILBRAHAM, late Indian Finance Dept. (b. 25th Oct., 1885).—Trained at R.I.E. Coll.; joined the service as asst. examr. on 1st Oct., 1906; asst. acct. gen., Sept., 1910; dep. acct. gen., Central revenues, Dec., 1923; dep. acct. gen., United Provs., April, 1923; dep. dir. of audit, ditto, April, 1926; audit and accts. officer, Bombay development scheme, Nov., 1926; offg. dep. dir. army audit, E.C., Oct., 1928; offg. dep. ch. auditor, N.W. Rly., April, 1929; with finl. commr. (rlys.), Oct., 1929; ret'd., Dec., 1932.

FORDHAM, JAMES ALFRED, Indian Civil Service (jt. mag., United Provs.) (b. 27th Aug., 1902).—Educ. at Sloane School, Chelsea, and Downing Coll., Cambridge; joined the service, 22nd Oct., 1926; arrived 19th Dec.,

1926, and served in the United Provinces as asst. mag. and collr.; temp. addl. sess. and sub-judge, Nov., 1932; offg. mag. and collr. Mar., 1933; settlement officer, Sept., 1935.

FORREST, GEORGE HENRY, B.Sc., late Indian Service of Engrs. (b. 10th Aug., 1884).—Joined the service, 1st Oct., 1908, as asst. engr.; exec. engr., June, 1918; offg. supt. engr. (s.p.t.), Sept., 1918; ret'd., Aug., 1932.

FORREST, JOHN M.B., Lt.-Col., I.M.S. (ret'd.) (b. 22nd Oct., 1876).—Joined the service, 26th July, 1902; D.M.O. and medl. officer, central jail, Coimbatore, Dec., 1923; offg. D.M.O. and medl. officer, central jail, Bellary, April, 1926; ret'd., 4th Nov., 1929.

FORRESTER, CHARLES, A.H.W.C., F.I.C., Ph. D. (Edin.), F.R.S.E., A.M.I.Chem.E., Indian School of Mines (princpl., Indian Sch. of Mines) (b. 6th Mar., 1895).—Apptd. profr. of chemistry and assaying on probn., 3rd Nov., 1926; confd., 1929; offg. principal, Indian School of Mines, May, 1927, Nov., 1929, and June, 1934; Municipal, ditto, Nov., 1936.

FORSTER, WILLIAM HUGHAN CHARLES, C.I.E., M.B., C.M., D.Ph., Maj.-Genl., I.M.S., ret'd. (b. 15th June, 1874).—First commn. dated 27th July, 1899; in mil. employ until April, 1904, when he was appointed dep. sanitary commr., Punjab; profr. of pathology and hygiene, Lahore med. coll., April, 1909; dep. sany. commr., Punjab, March, 1912; sany. commr., Aug., 1918; dir. of public health, Punjab, Nov., 1921; inspr.-genl. of civil hosps., Burma, Nov., 1927; surg. genl. with govt. of Bombay, Jan., 1930; member of legisl. council, Sept., 1932; C.I.E., Jan., 1933; ret'd., July, 1933; author of various works on medical subjects.

FORSYTH, JAMES PETERS, B.A., Office of High Commr. for India (dir. of purchase, store dept.) (b. 22nd Apl., 1883).—Educ. at R. Grammar Sch., Lancaster, and Caius Coll., Cambridge (8th wrangler); apptd. junr. clerk, store dept., 19th Oct., 1906; senr. clerk, July, 1912; transf. to Office of High Commr. for India, Oct., 1920; dir. of purchase (temp.), April, 1927; confd., Nov., 1932.

FORTESQUE, Rev. CECIL FRANCIS, L.Th. Indian Eccles. Estabt. (senr. chaplain, Bombay) (b. 14th Oct., 1888).—Apptd. chaplain on probation, 18th Jan., 1922; junr. chaplain, Jan., 1924; senr. chaplain, Aug., 1930.

FORTY, FREDERICK CHARLES, Office of High Commr. for India (exec. officer, accts. dept.) (b. 17th Sept., 1894).—Apptd. to acct. gen.'s dept. India Office, 15th Dec., 1919; transf. to office of High Commr. for India, 8th April, 1921; clerical officer, June, 1921; exec. officer, April, 1922.

FOSTER, ESMOND LEWIS PEARCE, B.A., Indian Forest Service (dep. consvr., Central Provs.) (b. 28th Oct., 1898).—Joined the service as asst. consvr., 13th Dec., 1921; dep. consvr., Nov., 1924; divl. forest officer, Sth. Andamans, Aug., 1935; ch. forest officer, ditto, Oct., 1935.

FOSTER, RODNEY, Lt.-Col. I.A., ret'd., late Survey of India (b. 27th May, 1882).—First commn. dated 8th May, 1901; apptd. asst. supt., trigl. survey, 12th Dec., 1906; temp. reverted to mil. duty, Nov., 1914, to April, 1919; dep., June, 1921; supt., Jan., 1926; offg. dir., Oct., 1931; ret'd., Sept., 1934.

FOSTER, Sir WILLIAM, Kt., C.I.E., B.A., late India Office.—Clerk, 2nd class, 22nd Sept., 1882; staff clerk, Jan., 1901; asst. Registrar, Oct., 1901; Registrar and Supt. of Records, Oct., 1907; editor, India Office List, 1891-95; C.I.E., Jan., 1913; retd., Nov., 1923; apptd. to temporary post of Historiographer, Nov., 1923; resigned, 31st Dec., 1927; Kt., Jan., 1925; has published 'The Embassy of Sir T. Roe to India, 1615-19', 'The Letters received by the E. India Co., 1615-17' (4 vols.), 13 vols. on 'The English Factories in India', 'The Journal of John Jourdain, 1608-17', 'The Founding of Fort St. George', 'Early Travels in India, 1583-1619', 'A Guide to the India Office Records, 1600-1858', 'Catalogue of the India Office Pictures' (five editions), 'The History of the Indian Wars', 'The East India House', 'John Company', 'Supplementary Calendar of India Office Records, 1600-40', 'The Travels of Thomas Herbert in Persia', 'Hamilton's New Account of the East Indies', 'The Travels of John Sanderson 1584-1602', 'British Artists in India, 1760-1820', 'England's Quest of Eastern Trade', and 'The Voyage of Captain Best to the East Indies, 1612-14.'

FOWLE, FULWAR WILLIAM CRAVEN, Indian State Rlys. (exec. engr., G.I.P. Rly.) b. 4th Mar., 1887.—Joined the service as asst. eng., 27th Nov., 1908; resdt. engr., Sept., 1912; in milty. employ from 27th Sept., 1917, to 26th May, 1920; divl. engr., Feb., 1926.

FOWLE, Sir TRENCHARD CRAVEN WILLIAM, K.C.I.E., C.B.E., Lieut.-Col., I.A., Indian Political Service (b. 28th Nov., 1884).—First commn., 29th Nov., 1905; in mil. employ till April, 1914; pol. officer on probation, June, 1914; H.M. Consul for Sistan and Kain, Mar., 1921; asst. pol. agent, Loralai, Dec., 1921; ditto Zhob, Sept., 1922; pol. agent, Chagai, Dec., 1923; H. M. Consul for Kerman and Persian Baluchistan, March, 1924; first asst. resident, Aden, Sept., 1925; offg. pol. resdt., Aden, Sept., 1927; and again, April, 1928; C.B.E., Mar., 1929; pol. agent and H.M. Consul, Muscat, June, 1930; offg. pol. resdt., Persian Gulf, May to Nov., 1931; again pol. agent, Muscat, Nov., 1931, to July, 1932; pol. resdt., Persian Gulf, July, 1932; K.C.I.E., May, 1937.

FOWLER, GEORGE, L.R.C.P., D.P.H., Lt.-Col., I.M.S. (ret'd.) (b. 29th Apr., 1874).—First commn., 27th June, 1901; in mil. employ till May, 1914, when he was apptd. civil surgeon (Central Provs.); on mil. duty, Jan., 1915, to Oct., 1919; retd., Sept., 1929.

FOWLER, ROBERT WILLIAM DOUGHTY, B.A., Burma C.S. (Cl. I.) (asst. commr.) (b. 6th Mar., 1914).—Educ. at Queen Elizabeth's Grammar School, Mansfield, and Emmanuel College, Cambridge; joined the service, 23rd Sept., 1937; arrived in Burma, Dec., 1937, and served as asst. commr.

FOX, CYRIL SANKEY, D.Sc., Geological Survey of India (suptg. geologist) (b. 24th Feb., 1886).—Joined the service as asst. supt., 2nd Mar., 1911; supt., Dec., 1930; offg. dir., July, 1936.

FOY, THOMAS ARTHUR WYNES, Indian Service of Engrs. (exec. engr.) (b. 20th Feb., 1925).—Joined the service as asst. exec. engr., 1st Oct., 1920; exec. engr., Oct., 1927; under secty. to

govt., irrigation branch, Punjab, May, 1931; ditto (constn.), Punjab irrig. sect., May, 1933; chief engr., irrign., Bikaner State, from Oct., 1935.

FRAMJI, KAVSI KAIKHOSRU, B.A., B.Sc., Ind. Service of Engrs. (b. 21st Mar., 1908).—Apptd. asst. exec. engr., Bombay, 30th July, 1932.

FRAMJI, K. S. (see Kaikhosru Sorabji Framji).

FRAMPTON, HENRY JAMES, M.C., B.A. (Oxon.), Indian C.S. (mag. and collr., United Provs.) (b. 14th Aug., 1897).—Educ. at Christ's Hospital and St. John's Coll., Oxford; joined the service, 28th Oct., 1921; asst. mag. and collr., United Provs., 2nd Dec., 1921; jt. mag., Dec., 1923; offg. mag. and collr., Jan., 1926; dep. secty. to govt., United Provs., genl. bch., Oct., 1932; mag. and collr., Nov., 1936; dep. commr., Mar., 1937.

FRANCE, Rev. ALEXANDER, Indian Eccles. Estabt. (senr. chaplain) (b. 24th Oct., 1886).—Apptd. chaplain on probation, United Provs., 16th Mar., 1925; junr. chaplain, Mar., 1927; senr. chaplain, Madras, 1937.

FRANCE, ROBERT EDWARD, India Office (actg. hr. exec. officer) (b. 16th Aug., 1898).—Apptd. asst. clerk, 27th Oct., 1919, after previous service from 27th Apr., 1916; clerical offr., 1st Jan., 1920; exec. offr., May, 1926; actg. hr. exec. officer, Oct., 1936.

FRANKAU, GEORGE NEUBERG (b. 15th Feb., 1881).—Apptd. chief inspr. of boilers, Central Provs., Dec., 1925; retd., Aug., 1936.

FRANKLIN, ERIC ALEXANDER, B.A., Indian C.S. (asst. commr., Burma) (b. 3rd July, 1910).—Educated at Government English High School, Maymyo, Rangoon University and Emmanuel College, Cambridge; joined the service, 7th Sept., 1935; arrived in India, Oct., 1935, and served in Burma as asst. commr.

FRANKLIN, GEORGE DENNE, C.I.E., O.B.E., B.A., M.B., B.C., Bt.-Col., I.M.S. ret'd., (b. 28th July, 1877).—First commn., 26th July, 1902; agency surg., Gilgit, Sept., 1913; ditto, Southern States of C.I., Sept., 1914; in milty. employ from Nov., 1914, to Jan., 1919; O.B.E., June, 1919; civil surg., and C.M.O., Rajputana, March, 1923; C.M.O., N.W. Frontier Prov., Nov., 1923; C.M.O., Delhi, April, 1924; hony. surg. to King., Jan., 1928; civil surg., Ajmer Merwara and C.M.O., Rajputana, April, 1928; C.I.E., June, 1928; resdy. surg., Hyderabad, Dec., 1928; retd., July, 1934.

FRANKLIN, MURIEL CURRAN, LL.A., Indian Educl. Service (inspectress of schools, Burma) (b. 18th Nov., 1888).—Joined the service as head mistress, govt. normal school, Rangoon, on 3rd Jan., 1924; inspectress of schools, Feb., 1929.

FRANKLIN, ROGER WARWICK, late Indian Police Service (bl 2nd Sept., 1896).—Joined the service as asst. dist. supt., on 26th Jan., 1920; offg. dist. supt., May, 1923; retd., June, 1932.

FRASER, DENHOLME DE MONTALTE STUART, Lt.-Col., I.A., Indian Political Service (b. 5th Oct., 1889).—First commn. dated 20th Jan., 1909; first asst. to pol. resident, Persian Gulf, Nov., 1919; under-sec. to agent to gov.-gen. in Central India, Nov.,

1922; asst. mag. and collr., Moradabad, Dec., 1924; asst. commr., Kohat, Nov., 1925; dep. commr., Dera Ismail Khan, May, 1926; pol. agent, Eastern Kathiawar States, Dec., 1926; pol. agent in Southern States of C.I. and Malwa, Oct., 1928; on foreign service in Tonk State, Oct., 1930; pol. agent, Malwa, Apr., 1935; offg. secty. to A.G.G. in C.I. in addn., May, 1936; pol. agent, Malwa, Apr., 1937.

FRASER, DONALD MACDONALD, Major I.M.S., Civil Surgeon, United Provs.—Date of first commn., 18th Jan., 1925; civil surgeon, March, 1934.

FRASER, EBENEZER, A.M.I.C.E., late Indian State Rlys. (b. 28th Mar., 1878).—Joined the service, 12th Sept., 1905; asst. engr., N.W. Rly., Sept., 1908; services lent to Gondal-Porbandar Rly., June, 1914, to Jan., 1916; exec. engr., Oct., 1915; I.A.R.O., Jan., 1916, to April, 1919; services lent to B.N. Rly. from Sept., 1921, to July, 1922; dep. ch. engr., G.I.P. Rly., March, 1924; ch. engr., ditto, Feb., 1928; ret'd., Mar., 1933.

FRASER, FREDERICK CHARLES, Lt.-Col., I.M.S., ret'd. (b. 15th Feb., 1880).—First commn. dated 1st Sept., 1906; in mil. employ to 29th May, 1911; asst. supt., govt. maternity hosp., Madras, June, 1911; reverted to mil. duty from 12th Aug., 1914, to 16th March, 1921; civil surg., Coorg, April, 1923; profrr., King George hosp., Vizagapatam, Sept., 1926; prinpl. and prof. of surgery, medl. coll., Vizagapatam, April, 1927; dist. medl. officer, April, 1928; supt., central jail, Coimbatore, Jan., 1933; ret'd., Feb., 1935.

FRASER, IAN MARTIN, M.A., Indian C.S. (sub. collr., Madras) (b. 13th Sept., 1894).—Educ. at Edinburgh Univ.; joined the service, 7th March, 1921; arrived, 29th April, 1921, and served in Madras as asst. collr. and mag.; sub. collr. and jt. mag., Jan., 1924; offg. collr. and dist. magte., Oct., 1927; and again, Sept., 1932; offg. registrar, high ct., Madras, July, 1929; confd., Oct., 1929; commr. of Coorg, Mar., 1936.

FRASER, SIR JOHN HUGH RONALD, Kt., C.I.E., O.B.E., B.A., late Indian C.S. (b. 15th March, 1878).—Educ. at Haileybury, and Pembroke Coll., Cambridge; apptd. after exam. of 1901; arrived, 1st Dec., 1902, and served in the Punjab and N.W. Frontier Prov. as asst. commr.; pol. asst., Feb., 1906; asst. commr., Thal, Nov., 1909; dep. commr., Dera Ismail Khan, Jan., 1910; dist. judge, Peshawar, Sept., 1911, to April, 1912; addl. divnl. and sess. judge, Oct., 1913; asst. commr., Peshawar dist., April, 1914; dep. commr., April, 1914; O.B.E., June, 1919; judl. commr., N.W.F. Prov., Aug., 1922; C.I.E., June, 1927; Kt., June, 1932; ret'd., Aug., 1935.

FRASER, SIR STUART MITFORD, K.C.S.I., C.I.E., late Indian C.S., Political Dept.—Educ. at Blundell's Sch., Tiverton, and Balliol Coll., Oxford; apptd. after exam. of 1882; arrived, 25th Nov., 1884, and served in Bombay as asst. collr. and mag., and forest settl. officer; tutor and guardian to the Raja of Kholapur, the Chief of Kagal, and Kunvar Saheb of Bhaunagar, May, 1889, to April, 1894; 2nd asst., April, 1894; 1st asst., Sept., 1895; tutor and govr. to H.H. the

Maharaja of Mysore, May, 1896, and asst. to resdt., July, 1898; C.I.E., June, 1902; junr. collr., Aug., 1902; dep. sec. to govt. of India, foreign dept., Nov., 1903, and April, 1905; resdt., Dec., 1903; pol. agent, Jan., 1904; special duty in foreign dept., from Dec., 1903; offg. sec., foreign dept., Oct., 1904, to Oct., 1905; dep. sec., ditto, April, 1905, to Oct., 1905; resdt., Mysore, and chief and jud. commr. of Coorg, Nov., 1905; resdt., Kashmir, Dec., 1911; C.S.I., Jan., 1913; offg. resdt., Hyderabad, March to Oct., 1914; resdt., Hyderabad, July, 1916; K.C.S.I., Jan., 1918; ret'd., June, 1920.

FRASER, THOMAS, A.M.I.C.E., V.D., late Indian State Rlys. (G.I.P. Rly.) (b. 30th Oct., 1874).—Joined the service as asst. engr., 16th Dec., 1904; resdt. engr., Jan., 1908; in milty. employ from 19th Oct., 1918, to 12th April, 1920; dist. engr., April, 1920; offg. dep. ch. engr., Feb., 1928; ret'd., Oct., 1931.

FRASER, WILLIAM GORDON, M.A., late Indian Educl. Service (b. 14th Nov., 1890).—Joined the service as profrr., 5th June, 1915; on mil. service from 8th Aug., 1918, to 8th Aug., 1919; prinpl., govt. high school, Moulmein, Aug., 1919; lecturer in English, univ. coll., Rangoon, Jan., 1924; ret'd., Dec., 1935.

FRASER-TYTLER, WILLIAM KERR, C.M.G., M.C., Lt.-Col., I.A., Indian Political Service (b. 26th Dec., 1886).—First commn. dated 24th Aug., 1910; asst. sec., for. and pol. dept., Oct., 1921; sec., Kabul legation, April, 1923; asst. to agent, gov.-gen., Madras States, Dec., 1925; asst. commr., Mansehra, March, 1927; counsellor, British legation, Kabul, Dec., 1927; and again, Oct., 1930; dep. commr., D.I. Khan, June, 1928; counsellor, British legation, Kabul, Oct., 1930; charge d'affaires, ditto, Apr., 1932; offg. dep. secty. to govt. of India, for. and pol. dept., Feb., 1933; confd., Sept., 1933; C.M.G., June, 1933; offg. foreign secty. to Govt. of India, May, 1933; offg. pte. secty. to Viceroy, May, 1934; H.B.M.'s minister, Kabul, Mar., 1935.

FREAK, LESLIE ASHFIELD, B.Sc., A.M.I.C.E., Indian Service of Engrs. (exec. engr., Punjab) (b. 28th Dec., 1892).—Joined the service, 1st Oct., 1919; asst. exec. engr., Jan., 1920; exec. engr., Oct., 1925.

FREDERICK, AMBULLUR EZEKIEL DAVID, B.A., Indian Police (asst. supt., Madras) (b. 7th May, 1905).—Joined the service as proby. asst. supt. 6th Sept., 1928; confd., Sept., 1930; awarded King's police medal, Jan., 1932; offg. dist. supt., Jan., 1936.

FREDERICK, JESUBATHAM MOSES, B.A., B.E., Indian Service of Engrs. (exec. engr., Madras) (b. 19th Nov., 1901).—Joined the service as appr. engr., 8th July, 1925; asst. exec. engr., Oct., 1926; offg. exec. engr., Jan., 1933; confd., Oct., 1935.

FREELAND, SIR HENRY FRANCIS EDWARD, K.C.I.E., C.B., M.V.O., D.S.O., Major-Gen., R.E., late Railway Dept.—First commn. dated 13th Feb., 1891; apptd. asst. engr., mil. works services, Oct., 1896; transfed. to railway dept., June, 1898; dep. traffic manager, N.W. Ry., Jan., 1911; M.V.O., Dec., 1911; traffic manager,

April, 1916; addl. member, rly. board, 1918-19; agent, Bombay, Baroda and Central India Ry. Co., Feb., 1920; war service, Chitral, 1895, medal and clasp; European War 1914-18 (asst. dir. of rly. transport under W.O., Sept., 1914; dep. dir. of rly. transport, May, 1915; dir. of transportation (graded as brig.-gen.), Oct., 1916; dep. dir.-gen. of transportation, Feb., 1917 (temp. maj.-gen., Oct., 1917); mentd. in despatches; D.S.O.; C.B.; officer of Legion of Honour); K.C.I.E., Dec., 1919; ret'd., July, 1920.

FREEMAN, FREDERICK CHARLES, late Indian State Railways.—Apptd. to temp. engr. estabt., Sept., 1902; storekeeper, 3rd Sept., 1908; controller of stores, O. and R. Rly., March, 1919; ditto, E.I. Rly., July, 1925; ret'd., April, 1928.

FREEMAN, WILLIAM PITT, late Indian State Railways (b. 27th June, 1874).—Joined the service, Aug., 1905; asst. traffic supt., N.W. Rly., Aug., 1906; dist. traffic supt., April, 1909; offg. dep. traffic manager, May, 1919; dep. ch. operating supt. (provl.), Oct., 1924; offg. asst. acts. officer, E.I. Rly., Jan., 1928; ret'd., June, 1929.

FREKE, CECIL GEORGE, C.I.E., M.A., B.Sc., Indian C.S. (collr., Bombay) (b. 8th Oct., 1887).—Educ. at Merchant Taylors', London, St. John's Coll., Cambridge, and University Coll., London; apptd. after exam. of 1911; arrived, 21st Nov., 1912, and served in Bombay as asst. collr. and mag.; spl. duty as dep. contr. of munitions, Bombay circle, Sept., 1918; temp. under sec., comm. and ind. dept., govt. of India, April, 1919; asst. to dir.-gen. of comml. intellig., govt. of India, Oct., 1919; offg. dir.-gen. of comml. intellig., Sept., 1921; confd., Dec., 1922; on depn. as Indian trade commr., Aug., 1924; dy. sec. to govt., fin. dept., Feb., 1926; secty., ditto, Apl., 1932, to May, 1937; member legisl. council, 1927 to 1932; C.I.E., Feb., 1937.

FREMANTLE, SIR SELWYN HOWE, Kt., C.S.I., C.I.E., V.D., late Indian C.S.—Educ. at Eton and Magdalen Coll., Oxford; apptd. after exam. of 1888; arrived, 27th Nov., 1890, and served in the N.W. Prov. and Oudh as asst. mag. and collr., asst. settl. officer, and asst. commr.; joint mag., Jan., 1896; dep. commr., Oct., 1903; mag. and collr., May, 1905; regr., co-operative credit societies, June, 1907, to Jan., 1912; spl. duty in England under commerce, fin. and industry dept., Jan. to Nov., 1909; C.I.E., June, 1915; commr., April, 1918; controller of passages, govt. of India, Dec., 1918; commr. and offr. in charge of ex-Amir of Kabul, July, 1919; C.S.I., Jan., 1920; member of exec. council, United Provs., May, 1922; member, board of rev., Nov., 1922; temp. member of govr.'s exec. council, United Provs., April, 1925; Kt., June, 1925; ret'd., March, 1926; author of reports on Rae Bareli and Bareilly settlt., and labour supply in the United Provs. and Bengal, and 'A Policy on Rural Education.'

FRENCH, CHARLES EDWIN CLUGE, late Survey of India (b. 16th Mar., 1875).—Joined the service as proby. sub.-asst. supt., 14th Nov., 1896; confd., Nov., 1897; extra asst. supt., Sept., 1904; dep. supt., Nov., 1924; supt., Nov., 1926; ret'd., Mar., 1930.

FRENCH, JOSEPH CHARLES, B.A., late Indian C.S. (b. 15th Jan., 1883).—Educ. at Wimbledon Coll., Lincoln Coll., Oxford, and University Coll., London; apptd. after exam. of 1905; arrived, 26th Nov., 1906, and served in Bengal as asst. mag. and collr.; joint mag. and dep. collr., Sept., 1912; I.A.R.O., Jan. to March, 1918; mag. and collr., Oct., 1918; ret'd., Apl., 1932.

FRENCH, LESLIE HAROLD VINCENT, B.A., Indian C.S. (asst. commr., Punjab) (b. 11th Nov., 1912).—Educ. at Copthorne School, Sussex, Winchester Coll., and Magdalen Coll., Cambridge; joined the service, 23rd Dec., 1937; arrived in India, Jan., 1938, and served in the Punjab as asst. commr.

FRESTON, THOMAS ANTHONY, late Indian C.S. (b. 20th May, 1898).—Educ. at Wellington Coll., and Corpus Christi Coll., Oxford; joined the service, 16th Dec., 1922; served in Bihar and Orissa as asst. mag. and collr. from 16th Dec., 1922; offg. dep. commr., Mar., 1929; offg. magte. and collr., Apl., 1932; ret'd., Sept., 1937.

FRIEL, RALPH, B.A., late Indian C.S. (b. 25th June, 1884).—Educ. at Waterford and Trinity Coll., Dublin; apptd. after exam. of 1906; arrived, 10th Dec., 1907; and served in E. Bengal and Assam as asst. mag. and collr.; asst. commr., Assam, April, 1912; on mil. duty (I.A.R.O.), July, 1918, to March, 1919; dep. commr., Sept., 1921; offg. sec. to govt. of Assam, transferred depts., Jan., 1927; confd., Sept., 1927; ret'd., July, 1934.

FROME, NORMAN FREDERIC, B.Sc. (Eng.), A.M.I.E.E., D.F.C., Indian Posts and Telegraphs (senr. elec. engr.) (b. 23rd Sept., 1899).—Joined the service, 23rd Nov., 1923, as asst. div. engr.; div. engr., Oct., 1931; dir., Sind and Baluchistan Circle, Nov., 1934; senr. elec. engr., Mar., 1935.

FRY, ARTHUR BROWNFIELD, C.B., C.I.E., D.S.O., M.D., Col., I.M.S. (ret'd.) late Bengal Medical Dept.—Joined the service, 27th July, 1899; in mil. employ prior to 31st Aug., 1910; addl. dep. sanitary commr., malaria research, Dec., 1910; and offg. sanitary commr., April, 1914; on mil. service from Oct., 1914; D.S.O., Aug., 1917; C.I.E., June, 1919; prof'r. of hygiene, sch. of tropical medicine, etc., Calcutta, Feb., 1922; reverted to mil. employ, April, 1926; C.B., Jan., 1930; ret'd., Apl., 1930.

FRY, AUGASTINE SARGOOD, M.B., Ch.M., F.R.C.S.E., Lt.-Col., I.M.S. (civil surg., Punjab) (b. 4th Nov., 1890).—First commn., 13th Mar., 1916; civil surg., Punjab, Apl., 1933.

FRY, REV. ERNEST HENRY, Indian Eccles. Estab. (junr. chaplain).—Apptd. junr. chaplain, Madras, 16th Oct., 1931.

FRY, JOHN TIMOTHY, late Indian State Rlys. (G.I.P. Rly.) (b. 10th Feb. 1873).—Joined the service, 6th Jan., 1888; asst. storekeeper, March, 1903; 1st asst. storekeeper, June, 1906; services lent to Munitions Board from 5th July, 1917, to 30th Sept., 1919; asst. stores supt., Oct., 1917; dep. ditto, June, 1918; dep. contr. of stores, Aug., 1926; offg. contr. of stores, May, 1927; ret'd., July, 1929.

FRY, LESLIE ALFRED CHARLES, Capt. I.A., Indian Political Service (b. 17th Apl., 1908).—First commn. dated 2nd Feb., 1928; in mil. employ till Nov., 1933; asst. to A.G.G. in Rajputana, Nov., 1933; asst. commr., Luck-

now, June, 1934; under training in the Govt. of India Secretariat, May, 1935; asst. commr., Lucknow, July, 1935; under-secty. to the resdt. at Hyderabad, Oct., 1935; vice-consul, Zabul, Dec., 1936.

FRYER, ARTHUR CLARE, Indian Police (asst. supt., N.W.F.P.) (b. 2nd Oct., 1900).—Joined the service as asst. supt., Punjab, on 18th Nov., 1922; comdt., Baluch. Levy, April, 1926; asst. supt., govt. rly. police, March, 1927; services placed at disposal of N.W.F.P., Dec., 1932; supt. (subs. provl.), Feb., 1936.

FULLER, EDWIN GERALD, Burma Commn. (dep. commr.) (b. 16th Jan., 1893).—Held non-gazetted appts. from 20th Oct., 1914; extra asst. commr., Mar., 1921; addl. dist. magte., July, 1931; dep. commr., Aug., 1934.

FURLEY, HUGH DENYS, Indian State Rlys. (ch. operating supt., N.W. Rly.) (b. 7th Jan., 1883).—Joined the service, 16th March, 1906; as asst. loco supt.; dist. loco. supt., N.W. Rly., April, 1912; divl. supt. (provl.), Oct., 1924; offg. dep. chief operating supt., Oct., 1926; offg. divl. supt., Oct., 1927; confd., Sept., 1928; offg. ch. operating supt., N.W. rly., Apl., 1934; confd., Sept., 1934.

FURNESS, CECIL ARUNDEL, Office of High Commr. for India (exec. officer, accts. dept.) (b. 5th Mar., 1885).—Apptd. to acctnt.-gen.'s dept., India Office, 4th Sept., 1919; transfed. to office of high commr. for India, 1st April, 1921; clerical officer, Oct., 1921; exec. officer, April, 1922.

FURNESS, HORACE GEOFFREY, Indian Police (supt., Madras) (b. 11th Mar., 1890) Joined the service, 23rd Nov., 1909; asst. supt., July, 1912; I.A.R.O. from 6th Aug., 1916, to 28th Feb., 1919; offg. supt., Jan., 1920, and again, Jan., 1923; confd., Oct., 1926; princpl., police training sch., Vellore, June, 1936.

FYFFE, RIGHT REV. ROLLESTONE STERRITT, M.A., D.D., late Indian Eccles. Estab.—Educ. at Clifton Coll., and Emmanuel Coll., Cambridge; ordained in 1894 and apptd. as curate to Bishop Wearmouth and St. Agnes, Bristol, 1898-1900; vicar, 1901-04; S.P.G. missionary at Mandalay, 1904-10; Bishop of Rangoon, Jan., 1910; retd., Aug., 1928.

FYFE, WILLIAM, Dept. of Industries, Madras (b. 25th Jan., 1885).—Apptd. inspr. of industrial schools, 9th June, 1920.

FYSON, HUGH, O.B.E., B.A., late Indian C.S. (Punjab) (b. 16th May, 1880).—Educ. at Ressall Sch. and Jesus Coll., Cambridge; apptd. after exam. of 1903; arrived, 1st Dec. 1904; and served in the Punjab as asst. commr.; K.-i.-H. medal, 2nd class, Jan., 1915; supt., Mandi State, Sept., 1915, to April, 1917; O.B.E., Jan., 1921; dep. commr., Dec., 1919; retd., Oct., 1929.

FYSON, PHILIP FURLEY, M.A., F.L.S., late Indian Educ. Service (b. 17th April, 1877).—Apptd. prof. of biology, presdy. coll., Madras, 11th July, 1904; prof. of botany, ditto, ditto, Dec., 1914; dist. educl. officer, Feb., 1923; offg. princpl., presdy. coll., July, 1925; retd., Apl., 1932.

GABRIEL, CECIL HAMILTON, C.V.O., Lt.-Col., I.A. (retd.) (b. 16th Feb., 1879).—First commn. dated 7th Dec., 1901; was in mil. employ until 18th Sept., 1904, when he was

employed on special duty under govt. of India, for. dept.; spl. asst. to pol. resdt., Persian Gulf, March, 1905; pol. asst., and supy. asst. commr., Jan., 1906; asst. commr., Delhi, March, 1906; H.B.M.'s consul, and asst. to pol. resdt., Persian Gulf, July, 1906; second asst. to resdt., Hyderabad, Oct., 1908; asst. pol. agent, Chilas, Oct., 1909; first asst. to resdt. in Kashmir, Jan., 1913; ditto to pol. resdt., Persian Gulf, Jan., 1919; dist. judge, Kohat, Nov., 1920; offg. dep. sec. to govt. of India, for. and pol. dept., Feb., 1921; confd., Aug., 1921; C.V.O., Feb., 1922; pol. agent, Bhopal, April, 1925; resident, Mewar, Feb., 1928; commr., Ajmer-Merwara, March, 1928; resident, Jaipur, May-Sept., 1928; ditto, Rajputana states, May, 1928; offg. resdt., Gwalior, Apl., 1929; retd., Feb., 1932.

GABRIEL, SIR EDMUND VIVIAN, C.S.I., C.M.G., C.V.O., C.B.E., V.D., M.A., late Indian C.S.—Educ. at Emmanuel Coll., Cambridge; apptd. after exam. of 1897; arrived 7th Dec., 1898, and served in Bengal as asst. mag. and collr.; offd. as commr., Ajmer-Merwara, June-July, 1901; offd. as asst. to the agent to the govr.-gen. in Central India, Nov., 1901; asst. to pol. A.D.C. to S. of S., India Office, for H.M.'s coronation, June to July, 1902; on deputation as attache, govt. of India, Nov., 1902, to Jan., 1903; asst. sec. in the foreign dept., Feb., 1903; offg. under sec., March, 1905; confd., Nov., 1905; C.V.O., Jan., 1906; pol. offr. with the Prime Minister of Nepal, Sept., 1908; resdt. in W.R. States, Sept., 1908; sec. to chief commr., N.W.F. Prov., Jan., 1909; first asst. to agent to govr.-gen. in Central India, Oct., 1909; pol. agent and ditto, Feb., 1910; C.S.I., Dec., 1911; sec. to central cttee. for coronation Durbar, Delhi, Dec., 1910, to March, 1912; special duty, govt. of India for. dept., Jan., 1913, to March 1914; on deputation to Bikaner State, March 1914; on special duty under agent to the govr. gen. in the Rajputana, Sept., 1914; service replaced with govt. of Bengal, March, 1915 on mil. duty, from March, 1915; finl. adviser, Occupied Enemy Territory, 1918-19; Knight of Justice of the Order of St. John of Jerusalem; Order of Crown of Italy (Officer); Order of St. Maurice and St. Lazarus (Officer); Italian War Cross; C.M.G., June, 1918; C.B.E., June 1919; retd., Mar., 1920; knighted, May, 1937.

GADRE, NARAYAN BALKRISHNA, A.M.I.E. (Ind.), B.E., Indian Service of Engrs. (exec. engr., Bombay) (b. 3rd Dec., 1899).—Joined the service as apprentice engr., 8th Oct., 1923; asst. exec. engr., Oct., 1924; offg. exec. engr., Nov., 1931; confd., Oct., 1933.

GADSDEN, CECIL HOLROYD, C.I.E., Indian Police (dep. inspr.-genl., Madras) (b. 18th Aug., 1887).—Joined the service, 25th Nov., 1907; asst. supt., Sept., 1909; supt., Dec., 1917; asst. inspr.-genl., May, 1929; offg. dep. inspr.-genl., Apl., 1930, June, 1933, and again, Jan., 1935; confd., Dec., 1935; C.I.E., Jan., 1938.

GAGE, EDWARD TREGURTHA, M.A., Indian Educ. Service (inspr. of schools, Burma) (b. 6th April, 1888).—Joined the service as princpl., govt. high school, Rangoon, on 18th Nov., 1918; head master, Taunggyi, Nov., 1922; ditto, English high school, Maymyo, Apl., 1933; inspr. of schools, Burma, Apl., 1936.

GAINSFORD, DONALD, Indian Police (supt., Punjab) (b. 18th Feb., 1900).—Joined the service as asst. supt., on 12th June, 1920; offg. supt., April, 1926; confd., Oct., 1931.

GAISFORD, PHILIP, Lieut.-Col., I.A., Indian Political Service (b. 28th Nov., 1891).—First commn. dated 18th Jan., 1911; in milty. employ to 24th June, 1919; asst. commr., June, 1919; sec. to agent to govt.-genl. and ch. commr. in Baluchistan, April, 1921, offg. under-sec. to govt. of India, for, and pol. dept., March, 1927; confd., Oct., 1927; on foreign service as guardian to H.H. the Maharajah of Gwalior, Mar., 1929; secty. to A.G.G., Rajputana, Nov., 1934; offg. dep. secty. to govt. of India, for, and pol. dept., Mar., 1936; confd., Nov., 1936; dep. secty., pol. dept., Apl., 1937, and again, Sept., 1937; offg. jt. secty., ditto, May, 1937.

GAIT, Sir Edward Albert, K.C.S.I., C.I.E., late Indian C.S.—Educ. at Univ. Coll., London; apptd. after exam. of 1882; arrived, 11th Dec., 1884, and served as asst. commr. in Assam; provl. supt. of census, 1890, and wrote the provl. report; acted as sec. to the chief commr., 1891-92 and 1895-96; dep. commr., Nov., 1892; dir. of land records and agric., Nov., 1893; mag. and collr., Nov., 1897; supt. of census operations, Bengal, April, 1900; offg. census commr. of India, Jan., 1903; dep. commr., Dec., 1904; member of legisl. council, Bengal, June, 1905; chief sec. to govt. of Bengal, pol., appt. and rev. depts., Jan., 1908; C.I.E., Jan., 1907; commr., July, 1909; census commr. for India, Nov., 1909; transf. to Bihar and Orissa as member, board of rev., April, 1912; C.S.I., June, 1912; member of exec. council, Aug., 1912; K.C.S.I., June, 1915; lieut.-govr., Bihar and Orissa, 20th Nov., 1915; ret'd., Dec., 1920; member of the Council of India, 20th June, 1922, to 19th June, 1927; author of 'Report on the Census of Assam, 1891, and other papers dealing with Assam govt., 'A History of Assam,' 'Bengal Census Report, 1901,' 'India Census Report, 1901,' and 'India Census Report, 1911.'

GALBRAITH, IAN WILLIAM, M.C., Maj., I.A., Indian Political Service (b. 10th Dec., 1898).—First commn., dated 30th Jan., 1917; joined the pol. dept., 24th Oct., 1922; asst. commr., Kohat, Jan., 1923; supy. asst. commr., Peshawar, Mar., 1924; under-sec. to Ch. Com. N.W.F.P., May, 1925; asst. commr., Charasadda, Feb., 1927; ditto, Ajmer-Merwara, Nov., 1927; commr., ditto, May, 1930, and again, Sept., 1934; pol. agent, Mahil Kantha, Feb., 1931; secty. to A.G.G., Baluchistan, June, 1935; pol. agent, Gilgit, Oct., 1937.

GALE, BERKELEY, Lt.-Col., I.M.S. (civil surg., Punjab) (b. 5th Feb., 1884).—First commn. dated 30th Jan., 1909; in mil. employ to 1st Jan., 1923; civil surg., Jan., 1923.

GALE, LUCY, India Office (hr. clerical officer) (b. 16th Jan., 1882).—Apptd. clerical officer, 1st June, 1921; actg. hr. ditto, July, 1935; confd., Jan., 1936.

GALE, U. MAUNG (C1), Indian Police (b. 5th May, 1889).—Apptd. dist. supt., Burma, on 7th April, 1925; awarded King's Police medal, Jan., 1927; awarded bar to ditto, Jan., 1932.

GALE, U. MAUNG (A8), B.A., late Burma commission (b. 15th June, 1880).—Previous service from July, 1904; dep. commr., March, 1924; offg. dep. sec. to govt., March, 1924; sec. to govt., local govt. dept., May, 1927; ditto, judl. dept., Aug., 1929; ret'd., June, 1935.

GALES, Sir Robert Richard, Kt., F.C.H., M.Inst.C.E., M.A.S.C.E., late Railway Dept.—Trained at R.I.E. Coll.; apptd. asst. engr., 24th Aug., 1886, and posted to state rlys.; exec. engr., Nov., 1896; suptg. engr., March, 1910; chief engr., March, 1915; knighted, June, 1915; chief engr. with the rly. board, July, 1915; offg. agent, N.W. Ry., May, 1917; temp. agent, ditto, July, 1917; ret'd., Oct., 1919.

GALLAGHER, William Norman, B.A., Indian C.S. (asst. collr., Bombay) (b. 12th Jan., 1913).—Educ. at Royal Academical Institution, Belfast, and Trinity College, Dublin; joined the service, 18th Sept., 1937; arrived in India, Oct., 1937, and served in Bombay as asst. collr.

GALLANT, MAURICE NAHUM, B.A., B.Sc., Indian Forest Service (dep. consvr., Burma) (b. 24th Mar., 1902).—Joined the service as asst. consvr., on 27th Dec., 1924; dep. consvr., Nov., 1928; for econ., Oct., 1934.

GALLETTI DI CADILHAC, A. M. A. C. (see Di Cadilhac, A. M. A. C. Galletti).

GALLETTI DI CADILHAC, R. (see Di Cadilhac, R.)

GALLOWAY, ARNOLD CRAWSHAW, Capt., I.A., Indian Political Service (b. 5th Jan., 1901).—First commn., 24th Dec., 1920; in milty. employ to June, 1928; under-secty. to A.G.G. in the states of Western India, Mar., 1932; vice-consul, Zahidan, Jan., 1933; under-secty. to pol. resdt. in the Persian Gulf, Feb., 1924; secty., ditto, June, 1934; and again, Apl., 1937; secty., British legn., Kabul, Nov., 1934; asst. pol. agent, Bahrein, Nov., 1937.

GALVIN, JOHN SMART, M.B., Lt.-Col., I.M.S., Medical Dept., Bombay (civil surg.) (b. 4th Mar., 1892).—First commn., 15th Mar., 1920; civil surg., Mar., 1931; offg. presdy. surg., Bombay, Feb., 1935.

GALVIN, Percy Harold, Indian State Rlys. (transpn. supt., E.I. rly.) (b. 6th Sept., 1890).—Joined the service, 27th Sept., 1913 as asst. supt., E.I. rly.; transpn. supt. (traffic comml. E.I. rly., May, 1934).

GANAPATI, CODANDA MADIAH, Major, I.M.S., Medical Dept., Madras (dir. of public health) (b. 1st Sept., 1886).—Date of first commn., 15th Mar., 1920; asst. dir.-genl., I.M.S., June, 1924; dep. ditto, May, 1933; dir. of public health, Madras, Oct., 1934.

GANAPATIPILLAI, NATARAJA, B.E., late Indian Service of Engrs. (b. Oct., 1879).—Apptd. 1st Jan., 1928; offg. exec. engr., Sept., 1928; confd., Jan., 1933; ret'd., Oct., 1934.

GANDASING CHEEMA, SARDAR, D.Sc., Indian Agric. Service (horticulturist to govt. of Bombay) (b. 2nd Aug., 1894).—Joined the service as horticulturist, 13th June, 1921.

GANDY, G. L., A.M.R.A.E.S., Civil Aviation Directorate (dep. dir.) (b. 30th June, 1900).—Apptd. dep. dir. of civil aviation in India, 2nd July, 1935; offg. dir., Apl., 1936.

GANGA NATH, BABU, B.A., LL.B., Judl. Dept., United Provs. (judge, high ct., Allahabad) (b. 11th Nov., 1882).—Joined the service, 19th May, 1908; sub judge, June, 1923; dist. and sess. judge, June, 1933; addl. judge, high ct., Allahabad, variously from Oct., 1934; judge, ditto, Apl., 1937.

GANGARAM KAULA (see Kaula, G.).

GANGARAM, V. (see Vadhumal Gangaram).

GANGULI, ANIL BEHARI, B.A., Indian C.S. (jt. mag. and dep. collr., Bengal) (b. 1st Dec., 1907).—Educ. at Calcutta Univ. and School of Oriental Studies, London; joined the service, 12th Oct., 1931; arrived in India, Dec., 1931, and served in Bengal as asst. mag. and collr.; jt. mag. and dep. collr., June, 1933.

GANGULI, JOGENDRA NATH, Rai Bahadur, B.A., late Indian Police Service (Bihar and Orissa) (b. 11th July, 1875).—Joined the service, 15th June, 1897, and held non-gazetted appt. prior to 27th Feb., 1913; inspr., Feb., 1913; dep. supt., July, 1921; apptd. to I.P.S., 1st June, 1928; supt., June, 1928; ret'd., Sept., 1930.

GANGULI, JYOTI PRAKASH, RAI BAHADUR, late Indian Stores Dept. (b. 20th Feb., 1882).—Held non-gazetted appt. from 1st Apl., 1900; offg. asst. secty., Indian munitions board, Jan., 1919; confd., Feb., 1919; off. under-secty., industries and labour dept., Aug., 1924; on spl. duty in posts and tels. dept., Nov., 1925; dep. dir. of admn. and intell., June, 1930; ret'd., June, 1937.

GANGULI, NIBARAN CHANDRA, Rai Bahadur, B.E., late Indian Service of Engrs. (Bihar and Orissa) (b. 13th Mar., 1875).—Joined the service, 7th Feb., 1901; asst. exec. engr., Sept., 1924; exec. engr., Nov., 1924; ret'd., Mar., 1930.

GANGULI, SANAT KUMAR, B.L., Bengal Judl. Service (b. 9th Nov., 1886).—Apptd. dist. and sess. judge, Feb., 1928.

GANGULI, SUSIL KUMAR, RAI BAHADUR, B.A., late Bengal C.S. (b. 16th July, 1884), Joined the service, 20th June, 1905; dep. mag. and dep. collr., Apl., 1910; asst. regr. co-op. societies, Oct., 1922; offg. asst. secty., agric. and industries dept., Bengal, Dec., 1926; asst. secty. to Govt., Bengal, irrig. dept., Feb., 1929; regr., co-op. societies, Bengal, Jan., 1931; ret'd., Oct., 1934.

GANPAT RAI, RAI BAHADUR, LALA, B.A. Indian Service of Engrs. (exec. engr., Punjab) (b. 16th Sept., 1891).—Joined the service, 24th Sept., 1915; asst. engr., Sept., 1916; asst. exec. engr., Jan., 1920; exec. engr., Oct., 1925; under-secty. to govt., irrigation bch., Punjab, Oct., 1931.

GANY, HAFIZ MUHAMMAD ABDUL (see Hafiz, M. A. G.).

GARBETT, COLIN CAMPBELL, C.S.I., C.M.G., C.I.E., B.A., LL.B., Indian C.S. (fin. commr., Punjab) (b. 22nd May, 1881).—Educ. at King William's Coll., Isle of Man, and Jesus Coll., Cambridge; apptd. after exam. of 1904; arrived, 28th Nov., 1905, and served in the Punjab as asst. commr.; settlt. offr., Mandi State, 1910; dist. judge, Simla, 1913; lent to mil. dept., 1915; asst. postal censor, Karachi, March, 1915; ditto, Bombay, Sept., 1916; on active service, Mesopotamia, 1916; revenue commr., Mesopotamia; Feb., 1917,

and administrator, agricl. development (mil.), June, 1917; twice mentd. in despatches. C.I.E., Aug., 1917; temply. empld. in pol. dept., India Office, July, 1919, to Aug., 1920; civil sec. to high commr., Mesopotamia, Sept., 1920, to May, 1922; dep. commr., Dec., 1921; C.M.G., June, 1922; ch. sec. to govt., Punjab, April, 1931; commr., Multan, Mar., 1935; C.S.I., June, 1935; finl. commr. (devel.), Dec., 1937.

GARDINER, REGINALD THOMAS, B.Sc., Indian Service of Engrs. (asst. exec. engr.) (b. 27th Apl., 1906).—Joined the service as proby. asst. exec. engr., Punjab, 1st Oct., 1929; confd., Nov., 1930.

GARDINER, WILLIAM NORMAN, Office of High Commr. for India (higher exec. officer, store dept.) (b. 1st June, 1882).—Apptd. clerk, 2nd class, store dept., 13th Nov., 1901; on mily. service, Nov., 1917, to Feb., 1919; transfed. to office of high commr. for India, Oct., 1920; higher clerical officer, April, 1922; higher exec. officer, March, 1926.

GARDNER, JAMES CLARK MOLESWORTH, Indian Forest Service (systematic entomologist, Dehra Dun) (b. 15th Mar., 1894).—Joined the service as asst. consvr. on 11th Dec., 1922; systematic entomologist, Aug., 1923; offg. forest entomologist, Feb., 1932.

GAREWAL, AJAB SINGH, M.B., Ch.B. (Edin.), Major, I.M.S. Jail Dept. (supr., Central Jail) (b. 13th Jan., 1894).—First commn., 24th Apl., 1920; in mil. employ up to 16th Feb., 1931; supt., Central Jail, Jubbulpore, Apl., 1931; ditte, Hazaribagh (B. & O.), Dec., 1931; ditto, Central Provs., Mar., 1933.

GAREWAL, SARDAR JAGDEO SINGH, M.R.C.V.S., I.V.S. (b. 18th Nov., 1894).—Joined the service as supt., C.V.D. (Punjab and N.W.F.P.), 30th Jan., 1918; confd., Jan., 1921.

GARLAND, EDWARD ANDREW, B.A., Indian Forest Service (dep. consvr., Bombay) (b. 10th May, 1898).—Joined the service, 11th Dec., 1922, as asst. consvr.; dep. consvr., Nov., 1925.

GARNIER, CHARLES NEWDIGATE, O.B.E., India Posts and Telegraphs (p.m.g., Lahore) (b. 5th Jan., 1883).—Trained at R.I.E. Coll.; joined the service as asst. supt., on 1st Oct., 1906; O.B.E., June, 1918; asst. divl. engr., Nov., 1920; divl. engr., March, 1925; offg. dir., tel. engrg., Punjab and N.W.F. circle, Mar., 1931; confd. as dir. tel. engrg., May, 1934; offg. P.M.G., Punjab, Apl., 1935; confd., Aug., 1936.

GARRAD, ERNEST WILLIAM, Indian Police (b. 9th Apl., 1897).—Joined the service as asst. supt., Punjab, on 12th Nov., 1923; offg. supt., July, 1929; addl. asst. inspr.-genl., Simla, June, 1935.

GARRARD, HAROLD SEPTIMUS, India Office (higher clerical officer, military dept.) (b. 21st Aug., 1886).—Apptd. clerical officer, 17th Oct., 1921; higher clerical officer, Jan., 1927.

GARRATT, RICHARD, Lt.-Col., I.A. (ret'd.) (b. 3rd Oct., 1874).—First commn. dated 15th May, 1895; in mil. emp. to July, 1901, when he was apptd. sanitary officer; supy. asst. commr., Punjab, Dec., 1902; pol. asst., June, 1903; pol. agent, Tochi, Feb. to Sept., 1910; asst. commr., Thal sub-dn., Jan., 1913; dep. commr., Kohat, Oct., 1914; attd. offr., Kurram

agency, Nov., 1914; pol. agent, Kurram, Dec., 1914; dep. commr., Kohat, Oct., 1917; pol. agent, Tochi, Dec., 1917; ditto, N. Waziristan, Sept., 1919; dist. judge, Peshawar, April, 1922; pol. agent, Khyber, March, 1925; offg. rev., commr., N.W.F.P., Feb., 1928; retd., Oct., 1929.

GARRETT, HERBERT LEONARD OFFLEY, C.I.E., M.A., late Indian Educl. Service (b. 16th June, 1881).—Educ. at Charterhouse and St. John's Coll., Cambridge; joined the service, 13th Dec., 1912; prof., govt. coll., Lahore, 5th Jan., 1913; on milty. duty from 1st Oct., 1917, to 31st Dec., 1918; sec., publicity dept., June to Oct., 1919; keeper of the records of the govt. of Punjab since 1924; member of the Indian Records Commn. since 1926; prinpl., govt. coll., Lahore, Sept., 1927; retd., June, 1936; C.I.E., Feb., 1937; supervisor of I.C.S. probationers at Cambridge University, Oct., 1937. Publications:—'Cunningham's History of the Sikhs' (new ed.), 'Muhammadan Period of Indian History,' and a number of historical textbooks; general editor of Punjab Govt. Record Office publications.

GARRETT, JOSEPH HUGH, C.S.I., B.A., Indian C.S. (commr., Ahmedabad) (b. 22nd June, 1880).—Educ. at Highgate Sch., and Caius Coll., Cambridge; apptd. after exam. of 1903; arrived, 25th Nov., 1904, and served in Bombay as asst. collr. and mag., and asst. settlt. offr., dep. commr. of salt and excise, northern divn., Dec., 1919; offg. collr. and dist. mag., and pol. agent, Jan., 1921; offg. collr. and Talukdari Settlement officer, June, 1923, and again, June, 1925; confd., Jan., 1926; offg. commr., March, 1925, Feb., 1926, Mar., 1929, and again, June, 1933; member legisl. council, July, 1929; again, Mar., 1931; C.S.I., Jan., 1931; offg. ch. secty. to govt., Bombay, pol. and reforms dept., Apl., 1935, and again, Apl., 1936; commr., Ahmedabad, Nov., 1935.

GARROD, EDGAR SAMUEL, D.S.O., M.C., D.C.M., Indian Police (asst. supt., N.W.F.P.) (b. 6th Mar., 1895).—Joined the service as asst. supt., on 12th Oct., 1923; asst. dist. officer, frontier constabulary, Jan., 1925; offg. supt., May, 1933.

GARROD, REV. WILLIAM FRANCIS, Indian Eccl. Estab. (junior chaplain) (b. 23rd Mar., 1893).—Apptd. junior chaplain, 10th Feb., 1930.

GARROD, WILLIAM HENRY EDWARD, A.M.I.C.E., Indian Service of Engrs. (exec. engr. Bombay) (b. 10th May, 1892).—Joined the service, 1st Oct., 1919; asst. exec. engr., March, 1920; exec. engr., Oct., 1924; cons. pub. health engr. to govt., Nov., 1937.

GARSON, WILLIAM AUBREY, M.A., late Indian Service of Engrs. (B. 3rd May, 1895) Joined the service as asst. exec. engr. on 1st Oct., 1921; exec. engr., Oct., 1927.

GARSTIN, WILLIAM ARTHUR MACDONNELL, C.B.E., Lt.-Col. I.A., retd., late Indian Political Service (b. 14th May, 1882).—In mily. employ from 28th July, 1900; asst. resdt. Western States, Rajputana, Aug., 1907; supy. asst. commr., N.W. Frontier Prov., Feb., 1908; asst. commr., ditto, July, 1909; presl. asst. to chief commr., N.W. Frontier Prov., Dec., 1910, to May, 1911, and from Sept., 1911, to May, 1914; district judge, Kohat, Dec., 1916; asst. sec. to chief commr.,

N.W. Frontier Prov., April, 1918; dep. commr., Bannu, Nov., 1920; O.B.E., Jan., 1923; divl. and sess. judge Peshawar, May, 1925; pol. agent, Khyber, Feb., 1928; C.B.E., Jan., 1930; pol. agent, southern states of Central India and in Malwa, Mar., 1932; resdt., Gwalior, Nov., 1932; resdt., Mewar, and pol. agent, S. Rajputana states, Mar., 1933; offg. resdt., and A.G.G., Madras states, Feb., 1935; retd., May, 1937.

GASH, REV. IVER JOHN, A.K.C., Indian Eccles. Estab. (junior chaplain) (b. 26th May, 1890).—Apptd. chaplain-on-probation, Bengal, 9th Oct., 1928; transferred to Bihar and Orissa, Oct., 1929; junr. chaplain, Oct., 1930; transf. to Central Provs., Oct., 1932.

GASKELL, WILLIAM, C.I.E., M.A., late Indian C.S. (b. 14th Jan., 1874).—Educ. at Loughborough and St. John's Coll., Cambridge; apptd. after exam. of 1897; arrived, 21st Nov., 1898, and served in N.W. Prov. and Oudh as asst. mag. and collr.; asst. supt., Dehra Dun, Dec., 1902; under sec. to govt., April, 1904; asst. settlmnt. offr., Oct., 1905; dep. commr. and jt. sec. to board of rev., April, 1912; tepmly. employed in finl. dept., India Office, from Jan. to Dec., 1916; mag. and collr., March, 1918; commr. of income tax, Dec., 1920; opium agent, Ghazipur, March, 1924; C.I.E. June, 1925; offg. member, central bd. of revenue, Oct., 1928; retd., July, 1932.

GASKING, REV. CLEMENT AUGUSTINE, Indian Eccl. Estab. (junr. chaplain, Punjab) (b. 6th June, 1894).—Apptd. chaplain on probation, 12th Oct., 1926; junr. chaplain, Oct., 1928.

GASSON, LIONEL BELL, Indian Police (supt., Madras) (b. 9th Aug., 1889).—Joined the service, 24th Nov., 1910; asst. supt., July, 1912; supt., Jan., 1926; dep. commr., March, 1927; awarded King's police medal, Jan., 1928; asst. inspr.-genl., Nov., 1930; awarded Indian police medal, Jan., 1934; supt. spl. branch, C.I.D., Apl., 1935.

GASTER, VIVIAN ISAAC, B.A., Office of High Commr. for India (educ. officer, principal exec. grade, education dept.) (b. 11th Sept., 1892).—Educ. at Univ. Coll. Sch. and Univ. Coll., London; on mil. service Nov., 1914, to April, 1917; asst. principal (temp.), Home Office, Oct., 1918, to Feb., 1920; asst. to local adviser to Indian students, London, Feb., 1920; transf. to Office of High Commr. for India as asst. to joint secs., Indian students dept., Oct., 1920; principal exec. officer, educ. dept., April, 1925.

GASTRELL, EVERARD HUDDLESTON, O.B.E., Major, I.A., Indian Political Service (b. 30th Apl., 1898).—First commn., dated 14th Nov., 1916; joined pol. dept., 8th March, 1922; Brit. Consul for Arabistan, July, 1922; vice-consul, Ahwaz, Nov., 1922; on settlement training in Punjab, Nov., 1926; services placed at disposal of Ch. Commr., N.W.F.P., Jan., 1927; asst. resdt., Kolhapur, March, 1927; vice-consul, Duzdap, Dec., 1930; secty. to pol. resdt. in the Persian Gulf, Dec., 1931; and again, Nov., 1933; pol. agent, Bahrein, July, 1933; consul-genl. for Khorasan, Mar., 1934; claims commr., Baluchistan, July, 1935; pol. agent, Quetta-Pishin, Mar., 1936; rev. and judl. commr., Baluchistan, Nov., 1936; pol. agent, Quetta-Pishin, Dec., 1936; pol. agent i/c. of the Bolan Pass and Chagai dist., Feb., 1937; O.B.E., May, 1937.

GATES, Sir FRANK CAMPBELL, K.C.I.E., C.S.I., Barr. (1891), late Indian C.S.—Educ. at Cheltenham Coll. and Balliol Coll., Oxford; apptd. after exam. of 1880; arrived, 13th Dec., 1882, and served in Bengal as asst. mag. and collr.; from Aug., 1884, served in Burma as asst. commr.; on special duty in Upper Burma, March, 1887; junr. sec. to chief commr., April, 1887; under sec., Oct., 1887; dep. commr., Aug., 1889; junr. sec., March, 1891; sec., March, 1893; rev. sec., Jan., 1895; sec. to govt., May, 1897; commr., Aug., 1901; chief sec. to govt., Sept., 1903; member of legisl. council, Burma, Sept., 1903, and Oct., 1905; finl. commr., April, 1907; C.S.I., Jan., 1906; K.C.I.E., Jan., 1913; ret'd., April, 1914.

GATFORD, HARRY, B.Sc., A.M.I.C.E., Indian State Rlys. (exec. engr., E.I. rly.) (b. 6th Apl., 1889).—Joined the service, 10th Apl., 1920; resdt. engr., E.I. rly., Apl., 1924; exec. engr. (junr.) E.I. rly., Apl., 1930; ditto (senr.), Nov., 1933.

GAUL, Rev. ARTHUR CECIL, M.A. (Oxon), late Indian Eccles. Estabt. (b. 20th Oct., 1880).—Apptd. chaplain on probation, 22nd April, 1918; junior chaplain, April, 1920; senr. chaplain, May, 1927; ret'd., Oct., 1935.

GAULD, WILLIAM, O.B.E., late India Office (b. 5th Mar., 1875).—Clerk, 2nd class, registry and record dept., 28th Dec., 1895; transid. to public works dept., Jan., 1902; resdt., clerk, March, 1914 to March, 1923; staff clerk, April, 1919; administrative officer, April, 1922; apptd. asst. govt. dir. of Indian rly. cos., Jan., 1925; O.B.E., Jan., 1934; ret'd., Jan., 1936.

GAUNT, CLIVE HERBERT, LL.P., late Burma Judl. dept. (b. 7th June, 1874).—Apptd. offg. asst. govt. advocate, 16th April, 1914; confd., April, 1921; ret'd., June, 1934.

GAUNTLETT, Sir MAGER FREDERIC, K.C.I.E., K.B.E., late Indian C.S. (b. 12th Oct., 1873).—Educ. at Dulwich Coll. and Emmanuel Coll., Cambridge; apptd. after exam. of 1891; arrived 17th Nov., 1893, and served in Bengal as asst. mag. and collr., and asst. sttlt. officer; asst. acct.-gen., Bengal, Dec., 1897; dep. acct.-gen., N.W.P. and Oudh, March, 1898; joint mag. and dep. collr., March, 1901; transfd. to fin. dept., govt. of India, as dep. acctnt.-gen., United Provs., April, 1903; offg. acctnt.-gen., ditto, Nov., 1904; ditto. Burma, April, 1906, to Nov., 1907; acctnt.-gen., Burma, and commr. of paper currency, Nov., 1908; acctnt.-gen., Bengal, Nov., 1909; ditto, Burma, April, 1906, to Nov., 1907; acctnt.-gen., Burma, and commr. of paper currency, Nov., 1908; acctnt.-gen., Bengal, Nov., 1909; ditto, rys., April, 1910; offg. compt. and auditor-gen., March, 1912 to Oct., 1914; C.I.E., Jan., 1915; on deputation under the Board of Admiralty, Aug., 1915 to Nov., 1917; again offg. compt. and auditor-gen., Jan., 1918; confd. July, 1918; C.B.E., Jan., 1918; K.B.E., Jan., 1923; K.C.I.E., June, 1929; ret'd., May, 1930.

GAURI, NATH, P.M.S. Civil Surgeon, United Provs. (b. 20th Sept., 1884).—Civil surg., Apl., 1927.

GAVAGHAN, EDWARD LAWRENCE, late Finance Dept., Govt. of India (b. 18th Dec., 1885).—Joined the service, Apl., 1908, as asst. examr., P.W. Accts.; on milty. service from 7th Jan., 1916, to 22nd July, 1920; asst. acct.-genl., Bombay, Jan., 1921; dep. ditto, Bengal, Feb., 1921; ditto, United Provs., Dec., 1921; ditto, Central Provs., April, 1923; dep. dir. of audit, U.P., Oct., 1926; offg. dep. acct.-genl., Punjab, March, 1927; ditto, posts and tels., Calcutta Nov., 1927; dep. acct.-genl., Bengal, Nov., 1929; offg. dep. auditor-genl., Dec., 1930; offg. compt., Assam, Feb., 1931; ch. auditor, E.B. rly., and govt. exr. of accts., Assamrly., Jan., 1934; offg. acct.-genl., Central Provs., Apl., 1934; ret'd., Aug., 1936.

GAWAN-TAYLOR, EDWARD, B.A., Indian C.S. (collr. and magte., Sind) (b. 29th Oct., 1889).—Educ. at Durham Sch., and Pembroke Coll., Oxford; apptd. after exam. of 1913; arrived, 28th Nov., 1914, and served in Bombay as asst. magte. and collr., supt. land records, Southern Div., Jan., 1921; on depn. as settlt. officer from Dec., 1921, to May, 1922; offg. collr. and dist. mag., Nov., 1925; ditto and Talukdari settlement offr., Feb., 1926; confd., Apl., 1931.

GAWNE, EWAN MOORE, Indian C.S. (collr. and dist. magte., Madras) (b. 26th March, 1889).—Educ. at Wellington, and Brasenose Coll., Oxford; apptd. after exam. of 1912; arrived, 8th Dec., 1913, and served in Madras as asst. collr. and mag.; sub. collr., and jt. mag., Oct., 1917; under sec. to govt., Jan., 1919, to Feb., 1921; temp. dep. sec. to govt. of Madras, April, 1924, to June, 1925; offg. collr. and dist. mag., July, 1925; confd., Feb., 1932.

GAWTHORNE, ARTHUR RICHARDSON, Indian State Rlys. (exec. engr.) (b. 12th Oct., 1894).—Joined the service as asst. engr., 15th Apl., 1920; exec. engr., Burma Rlys., July, 1933.

GEARING, ALFRED HENRY, late Indian Police (b. 23rd Mar., 1890).—Joined the service as asst. supt., Bombay, on 18th Mar., 1923; offg. supt., Feb., 1933; ret'd., Jan., 1935.

GEBBIE, Sir FREDERICK ST. JOHN, Kt., C.I.E., late Indian Service of Engrs.—Trained at R.I.E. Coll.; joined the service, 1st Oct., 1893, as asst. engr.; exec. engr., Oct., 1902; suptg. engr., Aug., 1912; acting chief engr. in Sind, and sec., Indus River Commission, April, 1915; chief engr., and actg. sec. to govt., Bombay, Dec., 1916; C.I.E., Jan., 1920; confd. as sec. to govt., Jan., 1920; offg. inspr.-gen. of irrign., govt. of India, July, 1921; consulting engr. to govt. of India, June, 1923; Kt., Jan., 1925; ret'd., Aug., 1926.

GEMMELL, GAVIN, late Indian Service of Engrs.—Joined the service, 6th Sept., 1905; asst. engr., Jan., 1911; exec. engr., Jan., 1915; ret'd., July, 1928.

GEMMELL, GEOFFREY WOODBURN, Major, I.A. Survey of India (supt.) (b. 26th Sept., 1897).—First commn., 15th Nov., 1915; asst. supt., geodetic bch., May, 1927; supt., Apl., 1930.

GENNINGS, JOHN FREDERICK, C.B.E., Barr. (b. 21st Sept., 1885) (dir. of information and commr. of lab., Bombay).—Joined the service as dir. of information (temp.), 26th Dec., 1920; confd., 10th Oct., 1924; member,

legisl. council, Bombay, 1932; commr. of labour, Bombay, Apl., 1933; delegate of Govt. of India to internatl. lab. conf., Geneva, 1933; presdt., appellate ct., Dec., 1933; C.B.E., June, 1933.

GENTLE, FREDERICK WILLIAM, Barr., Judl. Dept., Madras (judge, high ct.).—Apptd. judge, high ct., Madras, Jan., 1936.

GENTLE, JOSEPH ALFRED HECTOR ROBERTS, B.A., B.Sc., Indian Forest Service (dep. consvr., Central Provs.) (b. 26th Feb., 1900).—Joined the service as asst. consvr., 2nd Jan., 1925; dep. consvr., Jan., 1929.

GEORGE, FRANK, B.A., Indian C.S. (asst. commr., Burma) (b. 9th Feb., 1907).—Educ. at Priory School, Shrewsbury, and Downing Coll., Cambridge; joined the service, 13th Oct., 1930; arrived in India, Nov., 1930, and served in Burma as asst. commr.

GEORGE, FRANK SEAVAR SCOTNEY, late Indian Police Service (b. 16th Mar., 1876).—Joined the service as probr., Madras, 22nd Nov., 1896; asst. supt., July, 1898; supt., June, 1907; services lent to Travancore Durbar from Aug., 1910, to Aug., 1915; dep. inspr.-gen., July, 1919; awarded King's police medal; retd., May, 1930.

GEORGE, HUGH SHAW, B.A., Indian Forest Service (dep. consvr., Central Provinces) (b. 29th Jan., 1892).—Joined the service as asst. consvr., on 6th Dec., 1915; on mil. duty from 9th Aug., 1917, to 8th March, 1919; dep. consvr., Nov., 1921; sylviculturist, C.P., Oct., 1932.

GEORGE, JAMES, B.A. (Cantab.) Indian C.S. (jt. mag. and dep. collr., Bengal) (b. 17th May, 1905).—Educ. at Priory County School, Shrewsbury, Queen's Coll., Cambridge, and School of Oriental Studies, London; joined the service, 15th Oct., 1929; arrived, Dec., 1929, and served in Bengal as asst. mag. and collr.; jt. mag. and dep. collr., Apl., 1931; offg. under-secty. to govt., Bengal, pol. and appt. depts., Sept., 1933; confd., Jan., 1934; addl. dist. and sess. judge, Feb., 1936.

GEORGESON, WILLIAM WARES, Indian C.S. (jt. mag. and sub-collr., Madras) (b. 13th March, 1906).—Educ. at Aberdeen Grammar School, Aberdeen University, and Pembroke College, Oxford; joined the service, 4th Oct., 1930; arrived in India, Nov., 1930, and served in Madras as asst. mag. and collr.; sub-collr., Jan., 1933.

GERAGHTY, MICHAEL JOSEPH, Indian Service of Engrs.—Joined the service as asst. exec. engr. Burma, 29th Dec., 1929.

GERRARD, FREDERICK WERNHAM, C.I.E., late Indian Police (b. 25th Nov., 1887).—Joined the service, 10th Dec., 1908; serving in Army dept. as asst. censor, Oct., 1914, to April, 1915; I.A.R.O., April, 1915, to Jan., 1923; C.I.E., June, 1920; supt. of police, May, 1922; on foreign service in Shanghai, May, 1929 to Mar., 1931; retd., Dec., 1933.

GERRARD, Miss JESSIE MARGARET, M.A., Indian Educ. Service (prinpl., Lady Willingdon training coll., Madras) (b. 9th Jan., 1892).—Apptd. prof., Queen Mary's Coll., Madras, 8th Aug., 1921; prinpl., Lady Willingdon trng. coll., Dec., 1925; confd., Jan., 1927.

GHAFFUR, ABDUL (see Abdul Ghaffur).

GHANSHYAM, DAS, RAI BAHADUR, LALA, M.A., LL.B., Punjab C.S. (dist. and sess. judge) (b. 10th Oct., 1884).—Joined the service, 2nd Aug., 1906; munsif, Mar., 1910; extra asst. commr., Apl., 1913; sub-judge, Apl., 1916; dist. and sess. judge, confd., Jan., 1934.

GHARPUREY, H. G. (see Hari Ganpat Rao Gharpurey).

GHARPUREY, KHANDU GANPAT RAO, B.A. (Cal.), L.R.C.P. & S. (Edin.) L.R.F.P. & S. (Glas.), Lt.-Col., I.M.S., retd. (b. 20th July, 1880).—Joined, 1st Feb., 1906, and served in mil. dept. prior to 12th July, 1911; on milty. duty from 14th Aug., 1914; to 18th Jan., 1920; civil surg., Dec., 1922; offg. surg.-genl. with govt. of Bombay, Jan., 1934; retd., July, 1935.

GHATAK, NARENDRANATH, M.B.E., Barr., Judl. Dept., Bengal (b. Nov., 1887).—Joined the service as master and official referee, high ct., original side, Calcutta, 22nd Nov., 1922.

GHATE, BAL GANGADHAR, B.Sc., LL.B., Indian Police (b. 1st Oct., 1903).—Joined the service as asst. dist. supt., Central Provs., 27th April, 1928.

GHATE, NAMDEV SITARAMPANT, Indian Forest Service (dep. consvr., Madras) (b. 4th Aug., 1901).—Joined the service, 3rd Dec., 1926; dep. consvr., Nov., 1930.

GHAZANFAR ALI KHAN, C.I.E., O.B.E., B.A., late Indian C.S. (b. 31st Mar., 1875).—Educ. at Calcutta Univ., Christ's Coll., Cambridge, and Univ. Coll., London; apptd. after exam. of 1898; arrived, 8th Dec., 1899, and served in the Cent. Provs. as asst. commr.; dep. commr., Jan., 1915; O.B.E., June, 1918; excise commr., Nagpur, April, 1920; offg. commr., Jan., 1924, and Mar., 1925; confd., Aug., 1926; C.I.E., Jan., 1932; retd., July, 1934.

GHAZANFAR ALI NAQVI, M.A., LL.B., Indian Police (b. 17th Apl., 1902).—Joined the service as asst. supt., United Provs., 27th May, 1926; offg. supt., Mar., 1933.

GHOLAP, LAKSHMAN TRIMBAK, Indian C.S. (collr. and mag., Bombay) (b. 4th Oct., 1903).—Educ. at Univ. of Bombay, and School of Oriental Studies, London; joined the service, 18th Oct., 1927; arrived, Dec., 1927, and served in Bombay as asst. magte. and collr.; offg. pol. agent, Jawhar, Mar., 1933; collr. and dist. magte., Hyderabad.

GHOSAL, Sir JOSNA, Kt., C.S.I., C.I.E., late Indian C.S. (Bombay) (b. 13th June, 1871).—Educ. at Metropolitan Coll., Calcutta, Calcutta Univ., and Univ.
