Coll., Oxford; apptd. after exam. of 1894; arrived, 25th Nov., 1895, and served in Bombay as asst. collr.; junr. collr. and pol. agent, April, 1912; K.I.H. medal, 1st class, Jan., 1913; C.I.E., June, 1918; senr. collr., Jan., 1918; member of legisl. council, 1922, 1924; offg. commr. and commr. of excise, March, 1924; confd., Jan., 1926; commr., Jan., 1928; C.S.I., June, 1929; temp. member of govt.'s exec. council, Bombay, Mar., 1930; retd., Jan., 1931; knighted, Jan., 1936.

GHOSE, SARAT KUMAR, M.A. (Cantab), Indian C.S. (judge, high ct., Calcutta) (b. 3rd July, 1879).—Educ. at the Metro. Instn. and the Univ., Calcutta, and Trinity Coll., Cambridge; apptd. after exam. of 1902; arrived, 30th Nov., 1903, and served in Bengal as asst. mag. and collr.; jt. mag. and dep. collr., Nov., 1910; s.p.t., dist. and sess. judge, Oct., 1914; confd. as dist. and sessions judge, Jan., 1916; offg. judge, high ct., Calcutta, May—Sept., 1928, and again, May, 1929; confd., Sept., 1929.

GHOSH, AJOY KUMAR, B.Sc., Indian C.S. (asst. mag. and collr., Bengal) (b. 14th Apl., 1908).—Educated at King's College and School of Oriental Studies, London; joined the service, 10th Nov., 1932; arrived in India, Dec., 1932, and served in Bengal as asst. mag. and collr.

GHOSH CHAUDHURI, HARI SADHAN, B.Sc., Indian Police (asst. supt.) (b. 3rd Jan., 1907).—Joined the service as proby. asst. supt., Bengal, 11th Mar., 1929; confd., Oct., 1931.

GHOSH CHAUDHURI, TAPENDRA KUMAR, Rai Sahib, Indian Police (supt., Bengal) (b. 16th Jan., 1887).—Joined the service as proby. dep. supt., Jan., 1911; confd., Jan., 1913; addl. supt., Dec., 1918; offg. supt., April, 1919; confd., May, 1927.

GHOSH, E. M. B., Indian Service of Engrs. (b. 6th Feb., 1907).—Apptd. asst. exec. engr., Bengal, 5th Sept., 1932; transfed. to Central P.W.D., May, 1935; offg. exec. engr., May, 1936.

GHOSH, J. M., Barr. Judl. Dept., Bengal (regr. in insolvency, high court, Calcutta) (b. 16th Apl., 1884).—Joined the service 14th May, 1921; asst. regr., high ct., Calcutta, original side, Nov., 1921; dep., regr., ditto, Dec., 1931; offg. regr., in insolvency, original side, Nov., 1932.

GHOSH, MAHIM CHANDRA, M.A. (Cantab), B.A. (Calc.), Barr., Indian C.S. (judge, high ct., Calcutta) (b. 20th Jan., 1880).—Educ. at Santosh Jahnavi Sch., Bengal, Calcutta Univ., and St. John's Coll., Cambridge; apptd. after exam. of 1903; arrived, 12th Dec., 1904, and served in Bengal as asst. mag. and collr.; transfed. to E. Bengal and Assam, Oct., 1905; jt. mag., and dep. collr., June, 1911; retransfd. to Bengal, April, 1912; dist. and sess. judge, March, 1916; offg. sec. to govt., judl. dept., Nov., 1927; confd., March, 1928; offg. judge, Calcutta high ct., May, 1930; and again, Nov., 1931; confd., Nov., 1932.

GHOSH, NAGENDRA NATH, Inc. tax dept. (asst. commr.) (b. 8th July, 1885).—Joined the service, 25th Nov., 1912; inc. tax officer, Oct., 1927; asst. commr., Bihar, Nov., 1933.

GHOSH, NIBARAN CHANDRA, B.A., Indian State Ry's. (transptn. supt., E.I. rly.) (b. 21st Dec., 1890).—Apptd. to the service, 20th Mar., 1913; asst. supt. E.I. rly., Jan., 1916; supt., Apl., 1928; offg. divl. supt., E.I. rly., variously from Apl., 1934.

GHOSH, RADHAKANTA, B.L., late Bihar and Orissa Civil Service (b. 23rd July, 1874).—Joined the service, 4th March, 1902; munisif, April, 1904; sub-judge, April, 1918; dist. and sess. judge, Dec., 1926; ret'd., June, 1930.

GHOSH, SACHINDRA MOHAN, B.A., Indian police (b. 1st Apl., 1913).—Apptd. asst. supt., Bengal, on probn., 1st Mar., 1935.

GHOSH, SHOTTYENDRA KUMAR, Indian C.S. (mag. and collr., Bengal) (b. 21st May, 1896).—Educ. at Scottish Churches Coll., Calcutta; joined the service, 12th Dec., 1921; arrived, 3rd Jan., 1922, and served in Bengal as asst. mag. and collr.; addl. dist. and sess. judge, Nov., 1927; addl. dist. mag., April, 1928; offg. mag. and collr., April, 1928; confd., Dec., 1933.

GHOSHAL, UMESH KUMAR, B.A., Indian C.S. (asst. mag. and collr., Bengal) (b. 25th Aug., 1912).—Educ. at Hindu School, Calcutta, Calcutta Univ., and Lincoln College, Oxford; joined the service, 28th Sept., 1937; arrived in India, Oct., 1937, and served in Bengal as asst. mag. and collr.

GHULAM AHMAD, MIAN, M.A., Indian Police (supt.) (b. 12th Aug., 1901).—Joined the service as asst. supt., Punjab, on 29th Aug., 1923; offg. supt., June, 1928; and again, Oct., 1932; confd., Apl., 1934; asst. to D.I.G., C.I.D., variously from Feb., 1936.

GHULAM, AHMAD KHAN, B.Sc., M.B., B.S., Major, I.M.S., Jail Dept., Punjab (supt.) (b. 2nd Mar., 1892).—First commr., 1st Nov., 1920; supt., Central Jail, Oct., 1930.

GHULAM AHMED, MADANI, B.A., Indian C.S. (asss. mag. and collr., U.P.) (b. 2nd Mar., 1913).—Educ. at Faiz-I-Am High School, Meerut, and Aligarh Univ.; joined the service, 20th Sept., 1937; arrived in India, Oct., 1937, and served in the U.P. as asst. mag. and collr.

GHULAM, MUHAMMAD, M.A., LL.B., Indian Audit Dept. (finance officer, Communications) (b. 29th Aug., 1895).—Joined the service 7th March, 1920; asst. audit officer, O. and R. rly., Jan., 1921; govt. exr. of accts., B. & N.W. rly's., Jan., 1925; services placed at disposal of rly. board, Nov., 1925; on foreign service under Bhopal state, June, 1932 to Feb., 1934; dep. acct.-genl., posts and tels., Mar., 1934; offg. dep. dir.-genl., posts and tels., finance, May, 1934; confd., Jan., 1935; offg. finance officer, posts and tels., July, 1936; confd., Apl., 1937; fin. offcr., communications, Oct., 1937.

GHUZNAVI, SIR ABDEL KERIM, NAWAB BAHADUR, Kt.—Member of govr's council, Bengal, 22nd April, 1929, to Apl., 1934.

GIBLING, MORLEY THEODORE, B.A., Indian Service of Engrs. (exec. engr., Sind) (b. 12th Mar., 1899).—Joined the service as asst. exec. engr., on 1st Oct., 1922; offg. exec. engr., Apl., 1928; confd., Oct., 1931; secty., cent. bd. of irrig., Simla, May, 1935.

GIBSON, ALEXANDER JAMES, F.C.H., late Indian Forest Service (Bihar and Orissa) (b. 14th May, 1877).—Joined the service, 18th Nov., 1898, as asst. consr. of forests; instr., Imperial Forest Sch., Dehra Dun, April, 1905; dep. consr., July, 1906; Imperial forest economist, Dehra Dun, April to Oct., 1908; dep. consr. and pol. asst. to supt., Simla Hill States, March, 1909, to April, 1910; consr., July, 1921; offg. chief consr., Punjab, March, 1922; consr., Bihar and Orissa, Oct., 1925; ret'd., Nov., 1929.

GIBSON, EDMUND CURREY, C.I.E., M.A., Indian C.S., Indian Political Service (b. 6th July, 1886).—Educ. at Merchant Taylors' Sch., London, St. John's Coll., Oxford, and University Coll., London; apptd. after exam. of 19

arrived in India, 26th Nov., 1910, and served in the Central Provs. as asst. commr.; employed temply. under govt. of India, for. and pol. dept., from June, 1915; tutor and guardian to H.H. the Maharaja of Bharatpur; asst. pol. agent, E. Rajputana States (in addn.), Sept., 1917; addl. asst. master, Mayo Coll., March, 1918; asst. commr., Ajmer-Merwara, April, 1918; dep. commr., Oct., 1923; commr., Ajmer-Merwara, Jan., 1924; pol. agent, Eastern States of Rajputana, March, 1925; commr., Ajmer-Merwara, April, 1927; offg. resdt., and agent to govr.-genl., Eastern States, Apl., 1933, C.I.E., June, 1933; offg. resdt., Gwalior, Nov., 1934; resdt., cl. II, Aug., 1935; resdt. for States of W.I., Oct., 1937.

GIBSON, JOHN CARRINGTON, A.M.I.C.E., Indian State Rlys. (dep. ch. mechl. engr., E.I. Rly.) (b. 28th July, 1887).—Joined the service as asst. loco. supt., 8th March, 1912; dist. loco. supt., April, 1918; supt., E.I. Rly., July, 1925, offg. dep. ch. mechl. engr., Feb., 1934; confid., Jan., 1935; offg. dep. agent (organisation), E.I. rly., Jan., 1936.

GIBSON, JOHN PERCIVAL, M.A., India Office (principal, pol. dept.) (b. 2nd June, 1898).—Educ. at Royal Academ. Inst., Belfast and Trinity Coll., Dublin; apptd. asst. principal, mil. dept., 19th Oct., 1921; transfed. to commerce and revenue dept., June, 1923, and to pol. dept., April, 1924; resident clerk, Oct., 1924; asst. pte. sec. to sec. of state, Oct., 1927; pte. secty. to perm. under-sec. of state, Dec., 1928; principal Apl., 1930.

GIBSON, RAYMOND EVELYN, C.S.I., C.I.E., late Indian C.S. (b. 10th Oct., 1878).—Educ. at Winchester and New Coll., Oxford; apptd. after exam. of 1901; arrived, 22nd Dec., 1902, and served in Sind as asst. collr. and mag.; supt. of land records and registrn., Sind, Jan. to April, 1906, and from April, 1907; colonisation offr., Jamrao Canal, Dec., 1909, to Oct., 1910; asst. commr. in Sind, and Sindhi translator to govt., Jan., 1911; pte. sec. to govr., Bombay, Oct., 1912, to March, 1914; collr. and dist. mag., Nov., 1922; actg. commr. in Sind, April, 1923; C.I.E., Jan., 1924; member of legisl. council, 1924; commr., Jan., 1932; rev. commr. for Sind, Apl., 1936; C.S.I., June, 1936; ret'd., Dec., 1937.

GIDNEY, CLAUDE HENRY, C.S.I., C.I.E., B.A., Indian C.S. Indian Political Service (b. 23rd Nov., 1887).—Educ. at Haileybury, Hertford Coll., Oxford, and University Coll., London; apptd. after exam. of 1911; arrived, 23rd Nov., 1912, and served in the United Provs. as asst. mag. and collr.; held various temp. appts. under govt of India, for. and pol. dept., from Oct., 1914; pol. offr. (on probn.), and asst. pol. agent, Quetta, Jan., 1918; asst. for Mekran to pol. agent, Kalat, Sept., 1918; pol. agent, Sibi, Nov., 1919; second asst. to agent to govr.-genl. in Baluchistan, Feb., 1920; sec. to pol. resdt., Persian Gulf, April, 1921; dep. commr., Bannu, Dec., 1923; ditto, Dera Ismail Khan, Nov., 1926; secty. to ch. commr. and agent to govr.-genl., N.W.F.P., May, 1928; dep. commr., Bannu, May, 1930; secty. to ch. commr. and agent to govr.-genl., N.W.F.P., Nov., 1930; ch. secty. to govt., N.W.F.P., June, 1932; mem. exec. council of govr., N.W.F.P., Apl. and Nov., 1932; C.I.E., June, 1932;

commr., Ajmer-Merwara, Nov., 1934; member, govr.'s exec. council, N.W.F.P., May, 1936; resident, Hyderabad, Apl., 1937; C.S.I., May, 1937.

GIDWANI, VISHINO LAKHIMAL, B.A., B.Sc., Indian C.S. (asst. collr., Bombay) (b. 4th Feb., 1914).—Educ. at N.J. High School, Karachi and Bombay Univ.; joined the service, 20th Sept., 1937; arrived in India, Oct., 1937, and served in Bombay as asst. collr.

GILBERT, CHARLES FREDERICK, Indian State Rlys. (dep. agent, E.I. Rly.) (b. 25th Oct., 1885).—Joined the service as asst. engr., 1st Feb., 1923; offg. exec. engr., April, 1923; confd., Oct., 1923; offg. senr. supt., Nov., 1932; offg. dep. agent, E.I. rly., Mar., 1935; confid., Jan., 1936.

GILBERT, LEONARD BURGES, B.Sc., Indian Service of Engrs. (exec. engr., United Provs.) (b. 20th July, 1887).—Joined the service as asst. engr. on 1st Oct., 1910; exec. engr., Oct., 1918; offg. dep. ch. engr., Apl., 1933; offg. consulting engr. to the Govt. of India (roads), July, 1936.

GILBERT, LEONARD ERSKINE, C.I.E., M.D., B.S. (Lond.), D.P.H. (Cantab.), Lt.-Col. I.M.S. (ret'd.), late Burma Medical Dept.—Arrived 12th May, 1900, and served in mil. dept.; offg. civil surgn. Feb., 1903; civil surgn. Oct., 1905; offg. surgn. to H.E. the Govr. of Madras, Jan. to Dec., 1915; on mil. duty, Oct., 1914, to Aug., 1920; C.I.E., June, 1919; offg. insp.-genl., civil hospitals, March, 1927; ret'd., Jan. 1929.

GILCHRIST, ROBERT NIVEN, C.I.E., M.A., Indian Educl. Service (reforms commr., Bengal) (b. 13th Apl., 1888).—Apptd. profr. Presy. Coll., Calcutta, 1911; asst. dir. of public instrn., Bengal, 1914; principal, Krishnagar Coll., 1916; centr., labour bureau, govt. of India, 1921; labour intellige. officer, Bengal, and dep. sec., commerce dept., 1922; jt. secty. to govt., commerce and marine depts., July, 1930; labour commr., Bengal, Mar., 1932; C.I.E., June, 1934; reforms commr., Oct., 1934; author of 'Indian Nationality,' Principles of Political Science, 'Conciliation and Arbitration.'

GILDEA, ERIC PERCIVAL, Indian State Rlys. (divl. rolling stock officer, N.W. Rly.) (b. 9th Feb., 1886).—Joined the service, 22nd Apl., 1916; temp. asst. loco. supt., Sept., 1916-Oct., 1919; confd., Nov., 1919; asst. rolling stock officer, Oct., 1924; offg. variously as divl. rolling stock officer from Jan., 1925; confd., Nov., 1927.

GILES, ALEXANDER GEORGE, Indian Service of Engrs. (exec. engr., Punjab) (b. 13th May, 1900).—Joined the service as asst. exec. engr., Punjab, on 21st Oct., 1923; exec. engr., Oct., 1932.

GILL, CLIFFORD ALLCHIN, M.R.C.S., L.R.C.P., D.P.H., Col. I.M.S., ret'd. (b. 12th Aug., 1878).—First commn., 31st Aug., 1903; in mil. employ till Jan., 1906, when he was appointed asst. plague medical officer, Punjab; chief malaria medical officer, Punjab, Oct., 1913; on mil. service, Oct., 1914, to Dec., 1918; asst. dir. of public health, Punjab, Nov., 1921; offg. dir. of public health, Punjab, June, 1924; confd., Nov., 1927; offg. insp.-genl. of civil hosp., May, 1930; ditto, Burma, July, 1932; confd., Feb., 1933; ret'd., Aug., 1935.

GILL, FRANK FREDERICK PERCY, Indian Police (supvt. and asst. to I.G., Orissa) (b. 2nd Apl., 1906).—Joined the service as asst. sup., Bihar and Orissa, on 18th Nov., 1925.

GILL, GURDIAL SINGH, M.B., Ch.B., Major, I.M.S., Jail Dept., Madras (supvt.) (b. 21st Mar., 1896).—Date of first commn., 28th Aug., 1926; offg. sup., jail dept., Nov., 1931; confd., April, 1932.

GILL, HARRY ANDERSON CLIFFORD, Indian C.S. (under-secty., fin. dept., Govt. of India) (b. 12th Sept., 1906).—Educ. at Bradfield and Magdalene Coll., Cambridge; joined the service, 10th Oct., 1929; arrived, Nov., 1929, and served in the Punjab as asst. commr.; offg. dep. commr., Nov., 1935; under-secty., finance dept., Govt. of India, Apl., 1937.

GILLAN, GEORGE VAN BAERLE, C.I.E., Major, L.A., Indian Political Service (b. 31st Sept., 1890).—First commn. dated 19th Jan., 1912; in milty. employ to 31st Dec., 1918; under-sec. to govt. of India, for. and pol. dept., March, 1924; offg. dep. sec. to ditto, Nov., 1924; H.B.M.'s consulgenl., Khasgar, July, 1925; 1st asst. to resdt., Kashmir, Nov., 1928; pol. agent, Gilgit, Sept., 1931; C.I.E., June, 1934; secty. to A.G.G., Punjab states, Aug. 1935; resdt., W. Rajputana States, Apl., 1937; resdt., Jaipur (addtl. charge), Dec., 1937.

GILLAN, Sir ROBERT WOODBURN, K.C.S.I., B.A., LL.B., Barr. (1889), late Indian C.S.—Educ. at Ayr Acad. and Christ's Coll., Cambridge; apptd. after exam. of 1886; arrived 11th Nov., 1889, and served in the N.W. Prov. and Oudh as asst. mag. and collr.; joint mag., Aug., 1893; offg. city mag. and wasika officer, Lucknow, March, 1894; asst. commr., May, 1896; settlt. offr., Oct., 1896; dep. commr., July, 1899; mag. and collr., Dec., 1901; joint sec., board of rev., April, 1902; sec., board of rev., Jan., 1903; offg. sec. to govt., Sept., 1907; compt. and auditor-gen., and head commr. of paper currency, fin. dept., govt. of India, July, 1910; C.S.I., Dec., 1911; sec. to govt. of India, fin. dept., May, 1912; member, Royal commn. on Indian finance and currency, April, 1913; member, ry. board, April, 1914; temp. member of govr.-genl.'s council, April to Oct., 1914; presdt., ry. board, April, 1915; K.C.S.I., Jan., 1916; commr., United Provs., April, 1919; ret'd., April, 1920.

GILLIAT, ALGERNON EARLE, C.I.E., Indian C.S. (commr., Burma) (b. 1st Jan., 1884).—Educ. at Manchester Grammar Sch., and Wadham Coll., Oxf.; apptd. after exam. of 1907; arrived, 7th Dec., 1908, and served in Burma as asst. commr.; under-sec., rev. sec.'s dept., April, 1913; under-sec. to govt. of India, rev. and agric. dept., April, 1916; offg. dep. commr., April, 1921; offg. sec. to govt., fin. and rev. dep., May, 1923; dep. commr., Sept., 1924; ditto and addtl. sec., fin. and rev. dept., Dec., 1924; sec. to govt., fin. dept., May, 1927; C.I.E., June, 1928; offg. commr., May, 1932; confd., Jan., 1936.

GILLIES, JOHN ERNEST, M.A., late Indian C.S. (b. 30th April, 1889).—Educ. at Old Deer Public School, Aberdeenshire, Robert Gordon's Coll., Aberdeen, and Aberdeen University; apptd. after exam. of 1912; arrived, 9th Dec.,

1913, and served in Burma as asst. commr.; on mil. service, July, 1918, to Feb., 1919; under-sec. to govt. of Burma, July, 1919; offg. dist. and sessions judge April, 1924; confd., May, 1924; ret'd., Feb., 1931.

GILLIES, PETER, L.D.S. (asst. supvt. Bihar) (b. 22nd May, 1897).—Apptd. dental surg. and sup., Punjab dental hosp., Lahore on probn., 19th Mar., 1930; confd., Sept., 1932.

GILLIGAN, WILLIAM BOYD, Indian C.S. (backward cl. offcr.) (b. 3rd Apl., 1893).—Educ. at High School, Dublin, R.M.C. Sandhurst, and Trinity Coll., Dublin; joined the service, 20th Feb., 1922; arrived, 10th April, 1922, and served in Bombay as asst. collr. and mag.; offg. collr. and dist. magte., May, 1926; offg. criminal tribes settlement officer, Bombay Presdy., Nov., 1927; Labour officer, Bombay, Oct., 1934; collr. and mag., July, 1935; backward class officer, Nov., 1937.

GILMORE, EDWARD FITZGERALD GELSTON, B.Sc. (Hons.), M.I.Mech.E. (b. 25th June, 1890).—Joined the service, 12th Feb., 1924, as physicist, Govt. Test House, Alipore; sup. Govt. Test House (provl.), Nov., 1934.

GIMSON, CHRISTOPHER, B.A., Indian C.S. (dep. commr., Assam) (b. 24th Dec., 1886).—Educ. at Oundle, and Emmanuel Coll., Cambridge; apptd. after exam. of 1910; arrived in India, 8th Dec., 1911, and served in Eastern Bengal and Assam as asst. commr.; transf. to Assam, April, 1912; settlt. offr., July, 1914; on mil. duty (I.A.R.O.), Feb., 1918, to Jan., 1919; presdt., Manipur State Durbar, Feb., 1919, to Nov., 1921; offg. depy. commr., March, 1923; confd., Apl., 1928; pol. agent, Manipur, Feb., 1933.

GITA RAM, GARG, Lala.—Indian Service of Engrs. (exec. engr., Punjab) (b. 30th Sept., 1891).—Joined the service as asst. engr., 8th Sept., 1918; asst. exec. engr., Jan., 1920; offg. exec. engr. Apl., 1927; confd. Oct., 1928.

GLADDING, DONALD, C.I.E., B.A., Indian C.S. (secty. to govt., finance, commerce and marine depts., Bengal) (b. 22nd Aug., 1888).—Educ. at St. Olave's Gr. Sch., Southwark, and Brasenose Coll., Oxford; apptd. after exam. of 1912; arrived, 10th Dec., 1913, and served in Bengal as asst. mag. and collr.; on mil. duty, May to June, 1918; under-sec., govt. of Bengal, fin. dept., Oct., 1918; ditto, pol. and appt. dept., Dec., 1918; industrial intelligence officer Bengal, July, 1920; jt. mag. and dep. collr., Jan., 1923; labour intelligence officer, Bengal, Jan., 1925; offg. dep. sec., fin. dept., Nov., 1925; confd., Feb., 1927; offg. dep. secty. to govt. of India, fin. dept., June, 1928; magte. and collr., Bengal, July, 1928; dep. secty. to govt. of India, home dept., June, 1931; secty. to govt., finance, commerce and marine depts., Bengal, July, 1933; C.I.E., June, 1936.

GLAISBY, ARTHUR GEOFFREY TINDALL, Indian State Rlys. (liaison officer, G.I.P. rly.) (b. 12th Apl., 1890).—Joined the service, 12th Sept., 1914; asst. engr., G.I.P. rly., Apl. 1917; resdt. engr., Sept., 1921; liaison officer, G.I.P., rly., Jan., 1934; offg. dep. contr. of stores, Nov., 1935; offg. dist. contr. of stores, Mar., 1936.

GLANCY, Sir BERTRAND JAMES, K.C.I.E., C.S.I., B.A., Indian C.S., Indian Political Service (b. 31st Dec., 1882).—Educ. at Monmouth School and Exeter Coll., Oxford; apptd. after exam. of 1905; arrived, 26th Nov., 1906, and served in the Punjab as asst. commr. and pol. asst.; temp. under-sec. to govt. of India, for dept., March to Nov., 1913; asst. resident, Mewar, May, 1914; 1st asst. to the agent to the govr.-gen. in Rajputana, March, 1915; 1st asst. to resdt. in Kashmir, Dec., 1918; dep. sec. to Govt. of India, pol. dept., Oct., 1921, and again, April, 1927; employed under Kashmir durbar from Nov., 1921; C.I.E., Jan., 1924; offg. pol. secty. to govt. of India, June, 1928; offg. agent to gov. genl., Punjab states, Apr., 1929; in foreign service as president, council of state, Jaipur, Oct., 1929; offg. resdt., and A.G.G., Punjab states, Apr., 1932; offg. pol. secty. to govt. of India, for. and pol. dept., July, 1932, and again, July, 1933; confd., Nov., 1933; C.S.I., Jan., 1933; resdt. and A.G.G. in C.I., June, 1933; member, council of state, variously from July, 1933; secty., Chamber of Princes, Oct., 1934; K.C.I.E., Jan., 1935; secty. to H.E. the Crown Representative, Apr., 1937.

GLANCY, Sir REGINALD ISIDORE ROBERT, K.C.S.I., K.C.I.E., B.A., late Indian C.S. (adviser to the sec. of state for India) (b. 19th Sept., 1874).—Educ. at Clifton Coll., and Christ Church, Oxford; apptd. after exam. of 1896; arrived, 22nd Nov., 1897, and served in the Punjab as asst. commr.; asst. sec. to agent to govr.-gen. and chief commr., N.W. Frontier Prov., Nov., 1901, to Sept., 1903; settmt. offr., Bannu, Oct., 1903; pol. asst., Feb., 1906; pol. agent, Feb., 1907; first asst., resdt., Hyderabad, Dec., 1909; asst. minister of fin., H.H. the Nizam's govt., July, 1911; C.I.E., June, 1917; C.S.I., June, 1921; offg. agent to govr.-gen. in Central India, April, 1924; confd., Dec., 1924; K.C.I.E., June, 1928; ret'd. Apr., 1930; adviser to delegate for Hyderabad, Indian round table conf., London, Nov., 1930; Jan., 1931; member of council, of India, July, 1931; member, Indian States enquiry comee., Jan., 1932; K.C.S.I., Jan., 1936; adviser to the sec. of state for India, Apr., 1937.

GLASCOTT, JOHN RICHARD DONOVAN, C.I.E., late Indian State Rlys. (b. 10th June, 1877).—Joined the service as asst. engr. (temp.), 9th Jan., 1901; ditto (perm.), June, 1905; signal engr., Nov., 1908; dist. engr., Mar., 1915; dep. ch. engr., Sept., 1917; ch. engr., Nov., 1919; agent, Mar., 1920; C.I.E., Jan., 1926; ret'd., Apr., 1934.

GLASS, ERNEST LESLIE, late Indian Service of Engrs. (b. 2nd Oct., 1879).—Trained at T.C.E. Coll.; apptd. asst. engr., Punjab, Nov., 1902; on deputn. to Gwalior State, Oct., 1908, to April, 1914; exec. engr., Oct., 1910; transf'd. to Central Provs., Dec., 1914, and temply. to Bengal, Dec., 1917; suptg. engr., April, 1920; offg. ch. engr. and secty., irrgn. dept., Jan., 1928; confd., May, 1928; ret'd., Oct., 1934.

GLASS, FRANK CECIL, late Indian Service of Engrs. (b. 16th Oct., 1881).—Joined the service as tempy. engr., 1st Oct., 1906; asst. engr., Oct., 1925; apptd. to I.S.E. as asst. exec. engr., Jan., 1927; exec. engr., Jan., 1932; ret'd., Aug., 1934.

GLASS, GEORGE WILLIAM, Indian Audit Dept. (comptroller, p. and t. accts., Burma) (b. 29th Dec., 1887).—Held non-gazetted appt. from Aug., 1906 to Dec., 1918; offg. asst. accts. officer, posts and tels., Feb., 1919; confd., Dec., 1919; dep. acct. genrl., ditto, Aug., 1927; examr., outside audit dept., Bengal, Feb., 1935; dep. acct.-genl., Bengal, Apr., 1935; attd. officer, office of the dep. acct.-genl., posts and tels., Madras, Feb., 1937; on for. service as comp. of posts and tele. accts., Burma, Apr., 1937.

GLASS, HARRY LISTER, late Indian State Rlys. (b. 23rd Dec., 1886).—Joined the service as asst. engr. on 23rd Sept., 1909; on spl. duty, recruiting, 4th Dec., 1916, to 12th Feb., 1917; exec. engr., Feb., 1918; offg. dep. dir., projects, Rly. Bd., April, 1925; offg. dep. agent, April, 1928; offg. senr. ditto, Mar., 1930; confd., Aug., 1930; offg. divl. supt., Apr., 1931; offg. dep. ch. engr., Nov., 1932; ret'd., Feb., 1934.

GLASS, LESLIE CHARLES, B.A., Indian C.S. (asst. commr., Burma) (b. 28th May, 1911); educated at Bradfield, Trinity College, Oxford, and the School of Oriental Studies, London; apptd. after exam. of 1933; arrived in India, Dec., 1934, and served in Burma as asst. commr.; asst. warden, Burma oilfields, Sept., 1937.

GLASSON, ALFRED KIRBY, late Indian Forest Service (b. 11th Oct., 1889).—Joined the service as asst. consvr. on 7th Dec., 1912; dep. consvr., Dec., 1917; employed under Oxford Imperial Forestry Institute, Dec., 1927, to July, 1929; ch. forest officer, Andamans, Aug., 1931; offg. consvr., Apr., 1933; ret'd., July, 1935.

GLEDHILL, ALAN, B.A., Indian C.S. (dist. and sess. judge, Burma) (b. 26th Oct., 1895).—Educ. at Rugby School, and Corpus Christi Coll., Cambridge; joined the service, 26th Oct., 1920; arrived, 19th Feb., 1921, and served in Burma as asst. commr.; addl. dist. and sess. judge, Dec., 1926; offg. ditto, March, 1928; confd. June, 1928.

GLEESON, HENRY JOSEPH, A.M.I.C.E., late Indian Service of Engrs. (b. 14th Oct., 1878).—Joined the service, 1st Aug., 1902; asst. engr. (provl. service), Jan., 1919; asst. exec. engr., Aug., 1919; offg. under sec. to govt., P.W.D. (rds. and bldgs.), Jan., 1924; offg. exec. engr., Oct., 1924; confd., Jan., 1928; ret'd., Oct., 1933.

GLENN, HENRY JAMES, late Indian Service of Engrs. (Punjab) (b. 25th Sept., 1874).—Joined the service 4th July, 1897; asst. engr., Oct., 1906; exec. engr., Sept., 1914; awarded Kaisar-i-Hind medal, 1st class, Jan., 1916; ret'd., Sept., 1929.

GLENN, JOHN ARTHUR GOULD, Indian Police (b. 31st July, 1917).—Apptd. asst. supt., United Provinces, on probn., Dec., 1937.

GLENNIE, EDWARD AUBREY, D.S.O., Lt.-Col., R.E., Survey of India (supt.), (b. 18th July, 1889).—First commn., 23rd July, 1910; asst. supt. (proby.), 20th Dec., 1919; confd., Dec., 1921; supt., May, 1928; offg. dir., Nov., 1936.

GLOVER, HAROLD MATTHEW, B.A., Indian Forest Service (consvr., Punjab) (b. 29th Oct., 1885).—Joined the service, 18th Dec., 1908, as asst. consvr.; dep. consvr., April, 1912;

offg. consvr., Jan., 1925; confd., Nov., 1929; served in United Provs., Oct., 1932; posted as consvr., Punjab, June, 1933.

GLOVER, JOHN WILLIAM THOMSON, C.B.E., Lt.-Col., I.A., Indian Political Service (b. 30th July, 1887).—First commn., 29th Aug., 1906; apptd. to pol. dept., govt. of India, 10th April, 1914; asst. to resdt. in Kashmir for Leh, July, 1919; pol. agent, Wana, March, 1923; O.B.E., June, 1924; asst. commr., Thal, April, 1926; dep. commr., Kohat, Nov., 1926; pol. agent, Dir. Swat and Chitral, Oct., 1928; C.B.E., June, 1931; pol. agent, Khyber, Aug., 1931; consul-genl. at Kashgar, Nov., 1933; resdt., Kashmir, Oct., 1937.

GOBIND NARAYAN PAREEKH, B.A., Indian Police (b. 10th Jan., 1905).—Joined the service as asst. supt., United Provs., 21st Jan., 1929.

GOBIND SARUP MATHUR, BABU, B.A. (b. June, 1878).—Joined the service as offg. munsif, 23rd June, 1906; confd., May, 1908; sub-judge, Dec., 1919; offg. dist. and sess. judge, Apr., 1928; confd. Jan., 1932; ret'd., June, 1933.

GODBER, ALEXANDER RICHARD, V.D., late Burma Civil Service (b. 18th Apr., 1876).—Held various ministerial appts. from 1st April, 1902; asst. master, Rangoon coll., April, 1905; extra asst. commr., April, 1905; ret'd., Aug., 1929.

GODBER, Ven. JOHN, M.A., late Indian Eccles. Estabt. (b. 20th July, 1871).—Apptd. junior chaplain, 1st Dec., 1905; senior chaplain, April, 1922; archdeacon of Calcutta, April, 1923; ret'd., July, 1928.

GODBOLE, YESHWANT ANANT, B.A., LL.B., Indian C.S. (mag. and collr., Bihar) (b. 4th Jan., 1889).—Educ. at Bombay Univ. and at Cambridge; apptd. after exam. of 1912; arrived, 24th Nov., 1913, and served in Bihar and Orissa as asst. mag. and collr.; jt. mag., April, 1923; offg. mag. and collr., Oct., 1924; confd., Dec., 1928; regr. of co-op. societies, Bihar and Orissa, Mar., 1934; offg. dir. of industries in addn., Mar., 1935.

GODFREY, AUGUSTUS EDWARD, Comdr., R.N.R. (ret'd.).—Marine dept., Madras. Apptd. port officer, Tuticorin, Nov., 1925; ditto, Cochin, Nov., 1927; pers. asst. to presdy. port officer and agent for govt. consignments, April, 1928; ret'd., Dec., 1929.

GODFREY, REGINALD WILLIAM, Indian Police (supt., Assam) (b. 10th Mar., 1899).—Joined the service as proby. asst. supt., 15th March, 1921; confd., March, 1923; offg. supt., April, 1930; confd., June, 1930.

GODSON, CHARLES AUBREY, M.C., Lt.-Col., I.M.S., ret'd. (b. 15th Nov., 1880).—Joined the service, 1st Feb., 1906; in milty. employ up to 27th Dec., 1909; offg. civil surg., Jan., 1910; on mil. duty from Aug., 1914, to Jan., 1920; civil surg., Oct., 1921; ret'd., Nov., 1935.

GODWIN, CHARLES, Office of High Commr. for India (executive officer, store dept.) (b. 3rd Sept., 1884).—Apptd. 2nd class clerk, store depot, 2nd May, 1904; transf. to office of High Commr. for India, Oct., 1920; exec. officer, April, 1922.

GOFF, GEORGE HERBERT, Indian State Railways (dist. traffic supt., E.B. rly.) (b. 25th Apr., 1885).—Joined the service, 13th Sept., 1910; dist. traffic supt., E.B. rly., Mar., 1931.

GOGHARI, DULLABHAI WAGHJI, L.C.E., late Indian Service of Engrs. (b. 31st May, 1875).—Held non-gazetted appts. from 1st Feb., 1905, to 3rd Aug., 1915; sub-engr., Aug., 1915; asst. engr. (provl. service), Jan., 1917; asst. exec. engr., Aug., 1919; exec. engr., Jan., 1926; ret'd., May, 1930.

GOIL, DWARKA PRASAD, M.B., F.R.C.S.E., Maj.-Genl., I.M.S. (surg.-genl. with govr. of Bengal.) (b. 1st Aug., 1879).—Joined the service, 31st Jan., 1903; arrived, 21st July, 1903; in mil. employ to April, 1907; civil surgeon, Dec., 1908; offg. prin. medl. coll., Calcutta, Mar., 1930; inspr.-genl. of civil hosps., Punjab, Apr., 1932; surg.-genl. with govr. of Bengal, Dec., 1933.

GOKHALE, BHALCHANDRA KRISHNA, M.A., India C.S. (mag. and collr., Bihar) (b. 23rd July, 1892).—Educ. at Bombay and Cambridge; apptd. after exam. of 1914; arrived 15th Dec., 1915; and served in Bihar and Orissa as asst. mag. and collr.; ditto and asst. settlmt. officer, Nov., 1919; offg. dep. commr., April, 1927; secty. to govt., educn. and dev. dept., Mar., 1929; magte. and collr. (provl. sub.), May, 1930; confd., Dec., 1931.

GOKHALE, RAO BAHADUR GANESH HARI, B.A., LL.B., late Central Provs. C.S. (b. July, 1881).—Joined the service as munsif, 2nd Apr., 1906; sub-judge, Nov., 1923; offg. dist. and sess. judge, Mar., 1933; confd., Apr., 1933; ret'd., Oct., 1936.

GOKHALE, GANGADHAR NILKANTH, L.C.E., B.Sc., late Indian Service of Engrs.—Joined the service as apprentice engr., April, 1910; asst. engr., April, 1911; exec. engr., May, 1919; ret'd., April, 1928.

GOKHALE, VINAYAK GOPAL, L.Ag. (Bom.), Indian Agric. Service (princl., agric. coll., Poona) (b. 10th Mar., 1883).—Held non-gazetted appts. from 1st Feb., 1905, to 4th Sept., 1915; extra dep. dir. of agric., Sept., 1915; dep. dir. of agric., June, 1924; prof. of agric., agric. coll., Poona, Jan., 1929; acting princpl., ditto, Oct., 1932; confd., July, 1933.

GOKUL CHAND BADHWAR, B.A., LL.B., Barr. (1898), late Indian C.S. (b. 6th Mar., 1875).—Educ. at the Punjab Univ., and Christ's College, Cambridge; apptd. after exam. of 1898; arrived, 18th Nov., 1899, and served in the N.W. Provs. as asst. mag. and collr.; asst. sess. judge, Nov., 1905; jt. mag., Feb., 1910; asst. sess. judge, April, 1912, to Feb., 1913; sess. and sub. judge, March, 1916; dist. and sess. judge, Sept., 1916; ret'd., Nov., 1934.

GOLDING, GORDON HENRY ARCHIBALD, late Indian Forest Service (b. 6th Dec., 1887).—Arrived, 28th Dec., 1910, and served as asst. consvr.; dep. consvr., Dec., 1915; on mil. service, Feb., 1916, to March, 1920; ret'd., Dec., 1933.

GOLDNEY, ALFRED PEEL, late Indian State Rlys. (b. 17th Jan., 1878).—Joined the service, 14th Nov., 1901; asst. traffic supt., April, 1902; dist. ditto, Aug., 1909; dep. traffic manager, Nov., 1922; offg. ch. traffic manager, March, 1927; confd., Dec., 1929; ret'd., Jan., 1935.

GOLDSMITH, ARTHUR OLIVER, India Audit Office (higher executive officer) (b. 26th Mar., 1886).—Apptd. 2nd class clerk, 28th Dec., 1904; minor staff clerk, Jan., 1920; higher exec. officer, April, 1922.

GOLDSWORTHY, FRANK TRUSCOTT, India Office (exec. officer, acctnt.-gen.'s dept.) (b. 11th Sept., 1892).—Educ. at Bristol Grammar Sch.; apptd. 2nd class clerk, 25th Feb., 1913; on mil. service from May, 1915, to Feb., 1919; exec. officer, April, 1922.

GOLDSWORTHY, LEONARD JAMES, M.A., B.Sc., Indian Educl. Service (lecturer in chemistry, Univ. Coll., Rangoon) (b. 12th Dec., 1890).—Joined the service on 10th Oct., 1914; profrr., Nagpur, Dec., 1914; on mil. service from 23rd Oct., 1917, to 23rd March, 1920; inspr. of schools, Central Provs., March, 1920; lecturer in chemistry, Rangoon, June, 1923.

GOLLAN, DONALD WILLIAM, A.M.I.C.E., Indian Service of Engrs. (exec. engr., Madras) (b. 16th Sept., 1886).—Joined the service as asst. engr. on 1st Oct., 1910; exec. engr., Oct., 1918.

GOOD, JAMES FULLER, M.E., B.Ch., Col., I.M.S. (retd.), late Burma Medical Dept.—Arrived, 22nd Sept., 1900, and served in mil. dept.; offg. civil surgn., May, 1903; civil surgn., Dec., 1908; services placed at disposal of Govt. of India, Sept., 1914, to Oct., 1919; reverted to milty. employ. July, 1928; retd., Aug., 1931.

GOODCHILD, WILLIAM BADEN, Burma Office (asst. princpl.) (b. 19th May, 1900).—Apptd. clerical officer, India Office, 22nd Mar., 1920; higher clerical officer, May, 1928; resdt. clerk, Dec., 1928, to July, 1934; asst. secty., Indian delegations to Assembly of League of Nations, 1928 to 1934; asst. secty., Burma round table conf., London, Dec., 1931; actg. pte. secty. to perm. under-secty. of state, July to Oct., 1935; asst. princpl., Nov., 1935.

GOODMAN, REGINALD TERENCE WYNNE, Indian Service of Engrs. (exec. engr., N.W.F.P.) (b. 26th July, 1900).—Joined the service as asst. exec. engr., 20th Oct., 1922; offg. exec. engr., May, 1929; confd., Oct., 1931.

GOODWYN, HAROLD PETER, B.A., Indian C.S. (asst. mag. and collr., Bengal) (b. 19th July, 1913).—Educ. at Wellington College and Peterhouse, Cambridge; appointed after exam. of 1935; arrived in India, Nov., 1936, and served in Bengal as asst. mag. and collr.

GOPAL, NAND (see Nand Gopal).

GOPAL, RAM (see Ram Gopal).

GOPALASWAMI AYYANGAR, NARASIMHA, C.S.I., C.I.E., B.A., B.L., Diwan Bahadur, late Madras C.S. (b. 31st Mar., 1882).—Joined the service as dep. collr., 1st Apl., 1905; collr. and dist. magte., Dec., 1920; secty. to bd. of rev., land rev., April, 1923; secty. to govt., public works and lab., Madras; offg. second secty. to govt., Madras, Nov., 1932; 3rd member, bd. of rev., Madras, and offg. 2nd ditto, May, 1935; C.I.E., June, 1935; 2nd secty. to govt., Madras, Mar., 1936; offg. 1st member, bd. of rev., May, 1936; commr. of excise, Madras, Jan., 1937; retd., Mar., 1937; C.S.I., May, 1937.

GOPALAKRISHNA RAO, BELTHANGADI, B.A., Indian C.S. (jt. mag. and dep. collr. Bengal) (b. 1st June, 1909); educ. at Board High School, Puttur, University of Madras and St. Johns College, Cambridge; joined the service, 9th Oct., 1934; arrived in India, Nov., 1934, and served in Bengal as asst. mag. and collr.; jt. mag. and dep. collr., May, 1935.

GOPALAKRISHNAN, PANANGATTI ANANTHANARAYANAIYER, B.A., Indian C.S. (jt. mag., United Provinces) (b. 20th Dec., 1909).—Educated at Raja's High School, Kollengode, South Malabar, Madras University, University College, London, and the School of Oriental Studies, London; joined the service, 6th Oct., 1933; arrived in India, Nov., 1933 and served in the United Provinces as asst. mag. and collr.

GOPALASWAMI, RAMASWAMI AYYANGAR, Indian Civil Service (sub collr. and jt. magte., Madras) (b. 18th Aug., 1902).—Educ. at Univ. of Madras and Emmanuel Coll., Cambridge; joined the service, 14th Oct., 1927; arrived, Nov., 1927, and served in Madras as asst. coll. and magte.; sub. collr. and jt. mag., Mar., 1930.

GORDON, ALEXANDER, C.I.E., B.Sc., Indian Service of Engrs. (ch. engr. and secty. to govt., p.w.d., Sind) (b. 15th June, 1880).—Joined as asst. engr. on 1st Oct., 1909; exec. engr., Oct., 1917; suptg. engr. (subt. temp.), Jan., 1927; ditto (provl. perm.), Dec., 1934; confd., Sept., 1935; ch. engr. and secty. to govt. p.w.d., Sind, May, 1936; C.I.E., Jan., 1938.

GORDON, ARCHIBALD DOUGLAS, C.I.E., Indian Police (dep. inspr.-genl., Bengal) (b. 14th Apl., 1888).—Joined the service as asst. supt. on 5th Dec., 1907; supt., Oct., 1915; dep. commr. of police, Calcutta, Nov., 1927; awarded King's police medal, Jan., 1931; dep. inspr.-genl., Nov., 1931; C.I.E., June, 1934; offg. commr. of police, Calcutta, Apl., 1935.

GORDON, EYRE, C.S.I., C.I.E., B.A., Indian C.S. (chairman, fed. public services commn.) (b. 28th Feb., 1884).—Educ. at Rossal and Queen's Coll., Oxford; apptd. after exam. of 1907; arrived, 24th Nov., 1908, and served in the Central Provs. as asst. commr.; under-sec. to chief commr., Feb., 1912, to July, 1915; settlt. offrr., Jan., 1916; dep. commr. (provl.), May, 1921; ditto and rev. sec. to govt., Cnte. Provs., June, 1921; commr. of settlts., dir. of land records, I.G. of reg., regr.-genl. and sec. to govt., April, 1926; offg. commr., Jan., 1929; offg. ch. sec. to govt., Cent. Provs., Dec., 1929; confd., Aug., 1932; C.I.E., Jan., 1931; temp. member, govr.'s exec. council, Central Provs., May, 1933; confd., Nov., 1933; C.S.I., June, 1935; chairman, fed. public services commn., Oct., 1936.

GORDON, FRANCIS WALDEN, M.C., M.A., late Indian Forest Service (Burma) (b. 10th Oct., 1895).—Joined the service as asst. consvr. on 8th Feb., 1921; dep. consvr., Jan., 1925; retd., Oct., 1930.

GORDON, GERALD WATSON, B.Sc., Indian Service of Engrs. (asst. exec. engr., Assam) (b. 31st Oct., 1902).—Joined the service, 27th Oct., 1925, as asst. exec. engr.; under secty., p.w.d., Assam, June, 1937.

GORDON, JAMES, M.B.E., India Office (exec. officer, military dept.) (b. 27th Dec., 1898).—Apptd. clerical officer, 3rd Oct., 1921; M.B.E., June, 1934; actg. exec. officer, Nov., 1934; confd., Sept., 1935.

GORDON, JOHN DE LA HAY, C.I.E., O.B.E., M.C., Lt.-Col., I.A., Indian Political Service (b. 30th Mar., 1887).—First commn., 29th Aug., 1906; in mil. employ till Dec., 1911, when he was apptd. asst. pol. agent (on probn.); asst. resdt., Aden, April, 1913; mil. govr., Basra, Sept., 1916, to Sept., 1919; O.B.E. June, 1918; pol. agent, Eastern Kathiawar States, Jan., 1925; pol. agent, Mahi Kantha, Dec., 1927; 1st asst. resdt., Aden, Nov., 1931; pol. agent, eastern Kathiawar agency, Mar., 1932; offg. A.G.G., Deccan States, Aug., 1935; offg. A.G.G., states of W.I., June, 1936; dep. A.G.G., states of W.I., Nov., 1936; C.I.E., May, 1937; resdt., Mysore and chief commr., Coorg, Dec., 1937.

GORDON, NORMAN HORATIUS, B.A., late United Provs., C.S. (b. 27th Dec., 1832).—Joined the service, 10th June, 1907; dep. collr., Mar., 1911; offg. mag. and collr., June, 1925; confd., May, 1933; ret'd., Dec., 1937.

GORDON, ROLAND GRAHAM, C.I.E., B.A., late Indian C.S. (b. 30th Oct., 1880).—Educ. at Marlborough Coll., and Selwyn Coll., Cambridge; apptd. after exam. of 1903; arrived, 25th Nov., 1904, and served in Bombay as asst. collr. and mag.; compiled admn. report, 1906-07; supt. of land records and registrn., Jan., 1908, to Aug., 1912; talukdari settlt. offr., April, 1915; offg. collr., dist. mag. and pol. agent, April, 1920, and again, April, 1924; confd., Feb., 1925; on spl. duty in connection with talukdari estates, Dec., 1925, and again, Nov., 1926; ditto in rev. dept., March, 1926; pol. agent, Surgana, March, 1927; C.I.E., June, 1932; ret'd., Jan., 1934; author of a land survey manual.

GORDON, RONALD EAGLESON, M.C., Lt.-Col., R.E., Indian State Rlys. (dep. agent (organisation) N.W. Rly.) (b. 17th Jan., 1889).—First commn. dated 23rd Dec., 1900; asst. engr., March, 1921; exec. engr., June, 1921; offg. divl. supt. (senr.), N.W. rly., Dec., 1932; and again, Mar., 1935; offg. dep. agent (organisation), Nov., 1936.

GORDON, SIDNEY VICTOR LEOPOLD DE MONTMORENCY, late Indian Police (b. 9th Feb., 1884).—Joined the service, 16th Nov., 1903, and posted to United Provs., asst. supt. of police, Nov., 1906; supt., Dec., 1915; ditto and A.D.C. to lieut.-govr., March, 1916; on mil. duty from Sept., 1917, to Feb., 1919; principal, police training sch., Moradabad, April, 1923; offg. dep. inspr.-genl., Mar., 1929; confd., Apl., 1932; ret'd., Mar., 1934.

GORRIE, Rev. LESLIE MANIFOLD, Indian Eccles. Estab. (senr. chaplain, Punjab) (b. 21st Mar., 1887).—Apptd. chaplain on probation, 18th April, 1919; junr. chaplain, April, 1921; senr. chaplain, Apl., 1929.

GORRIE, ROBERT MACLAGAN, D.Sc., Indian Forest Service (dep. consvr., Punjab) (b. 26th June, 1897).—Joined the service as asst. consvr., 15th Dec., 1922 dep. consvr., Sept., 1930.

GORTON, WILLIAM HUGH, late Indian State Rlys. (b. 27th Oct., 1884).—Joined the service as asst. engr., 1st Oct., 1907; exec. engr., Oct., 1915; ret'd., April, 1932.

GORWALA, ASTAD DINSHAW, B.A., Indian C.S. (dep. secy. govt. of India, communications dept.) (b. 7th Dec., 1900).—Educ. at Bombay Univ. and Cambridge Univ. (Non-Coll.); joined the service, 24th Oct., 1924; arrived, 5th Dec., 1924, and served in Bombay as asst. magte. and collr.; offg. collr. and dist. magte., May, 1928; offg. asst. rev. officer, Lloyd barr. and C.S., Nov., 1931; dep. secy., govt. of India, communications dept., Nov., 1937.

GOSCHEN, Viscount, Rt. Hon. GEORGE JOACHIM GOSCHEN, OF HAWKHURST, P.C., G.C.S.I., G.C.I.E., C.B.E.—Educ. at Rugby and Balliol Coll., Oxford; M.P. for East Grinstead, 1895 to 1906; joint parly. sec. Board of Agriculture, 1918; govr. of Madras, 14th April, 1924, to 11th Nov., 1929; actg. govr. genl. of India; G.C.S.I., June, 1929; P.C., Feb., 1930.

GOSS, EDWARD SLADE, M.C., Lt.-Col. I.M.S. (b. 25th Sept., 1883).—First commn. dated 29th July, 1911; in military employ up to 31st Dec., 1920, and again from 6th Jan., 1923 to 30th Sept., 1929; offg. asst. dir.-genl., I.M.S. (stores), Jan., 1921, and again, Oct., 1929, confd., Nov., 1929; offg. dep. dir.-genl., I.M.S., Oct., 1932; officer in charge, medl. store depot, Lahore, Aug., 1933.

GOSS, JAMES SAMUEL, late Indian State Rlys. (N.W. Rly.) (b. 18th Apl., 1885).—Joined the service as asst. loco. supt., 25th March, 1912; dist. loco. supt. (provl.), April, 1919; ditto (perm.), March, 1922; asst. agent, N.W. Rly., Oct., 1926; divl. personnel officer; ret'd., Feb., 1932.

GOSSET, HUGH HENRY EYRE, M.C., Major R.E., Indian State Rlys. (exec. engr.) (b. 3rd Aug., 1888).—Joined the service, 8th Oct., 1912; asst. engr., July, 1913; on milty. duty from 21st Oct., 1914; exec. engr., Jan., 1921; offg. dep. ch. engr., Sept., 1932.

GOSWANI, UPENDRA LAL, B.A., Burma C.S. (Cl. I.) (asst. commr.) (b. 5th Jan., 1912).—Educ. at Rangoon Univ.; joined the service, 24th Sept., 1937; arrived in Burma, Nov., 1937, and served as asst. commr.

GOULD, ALFRED JOHN, B.Sc., Indian Educl. Service (b. 28th Feb., 1900).—Joined the service as lecturer in civil engrg., Univ. Coll., Rangoon, 29th Sept., 1924.

GOULD, BASIL JOHN, C.M.G., C.I.E., B.A., Indian C.S. Indian Political Service (b. 29th Dec., 1883).—Educ. at Winchester, and New Coll., Oxford; apptd. after exam. of 1906; arrived, 29th Nov., 1907, and served in the Punjab as asst. commr. and pol. asst.; asst. to agent to gov.-gen. in Central India, May, 1909; asst. sec. to govt. of India for dept., June, 1910; special duty in for. dept. govt. of India, Sept., 1910; under-sec. to govt. of India, for. dept., Oct., 1910; Br. trade agent, Gyantse, May, 1912; offg. pol. offr., Sikkim, Oct., 1913; asst. commr., Nowshera, Oct., 1914; ditto and asst. commr., Charsada, May, 1915; dist. judge, Peshawar, Sept., 1916; asst. commr., Mardan, May, 1917; asst. pte. sec. to Viceroy, Sept., 1917; on spl. duty under orders of H.B.M.'s Consul, Seistan, from May, 1918; H.B.M.'s Consul for Seistan and Kain, Nov.

1918; offg. dep. sec. to govt. of India, April, 1920; C.I.E., Sept., 1921; first asst. to resident in Kashmir, Dec., 1925; counsellor, British legation, Kabul, June, 1926; and again, June, 1928; chargé d'affaires, Kabul, March, 1927; C.M.G., June, 1929; pol. agent, Kurram, Novr., 1929; ditto, Dir, Swat and Chitral, Jan., 1931; offg. resdt., Waziristan, Oct., 1931; rev. and judl. commr. in Baluchistan, Oct., 1933; and again, Dec., 1934; offg. resdt. and A.G.G., Baluchistan, July, 1934; pol. officer, Sikkim, Dec., 1935.

GOULD, HERBERT Ross, C.I.E., B.A., late Indian C.S. (b. 17th April, 1887).—Educ. at Clifton, and Brasenose Coll., Oxford; apptd. after exam. of 1910; arrived in India, 23rd Nov., 1911, and served in Bombay as asst. collr.; on mil. duty, July, 1916, to Jan., 1919; depy. commr., U.S. Frontier, Jan., 1921; offg. collr., dist. mag. and pol. agent, Dec., 1924; member of legisl. council, Aug., 1929; pte. secty. to govr. of Bombay, Aug., 1930; and again, Dec., 1933; C.I.E., June, 1931; ret'd., Dec., 1936.

GOVINDA MENON, A. V., Rao Bahadur, Barr., late Madras Civil Service (Judicial Branch) (b. 31st Jan., 1882).—Apptd. addl. sess. judge, 10th July, 1924; dist. and sess. judge, Jan., 1926; ret'd., Jan., 1937.

GOVINDAN NAYAR, ULLATHIL, B.A., B.L., late Judl. dept., Madras (b. 9th Nov., 1877).—Joined the service as offg. dist. munsif, 6th July, 1907; confd., Feb., 1908; sub-judge, Dec., 1925; offg. dist. and sess. judge, Feb., 1932; ret'd., Nov., 1932.

GOVINDARAJA AYYANGAR, NARASIMAH, Rao Bahadur, B.A., B.E., Indian Service of Engrs. (dep. ch. engr., P.W.D., Madras) (b. 10th Dec., 1890).—Joined the service as appr. engr., 16th June, 1914; asst. engr., July, 1917; exec. engr., June, 1924; dep. ch. engr., bldgs. and roads, Dec., 1935.

GOVIND DINANATH MADGAONKAR (see Madgaonkar, G. D.).

GOVIND MAHADEV PANDIT (see PANDIT G. M.).

GOW, PETER FLEMING, Lt.-Col., I.M.S. (prof. midwifery, Med. Coll., Calcutta) (b. 28th June, 1885).—Joined the service, 27th Jan., 1912, apptd. prof. midwifery, Med. Coll., Calcutta, Oct., 1932.

GOWAN, SIR HYDE CLARENDON, K.C.S.I., C.I.E., V.D., LL.B. (Nagpur), B.A., late Indian C.S. (b. 4th July, 1878).—Educ. at Rugby and New Coll., Oxford; apptd. after exam. of 1901; arrived, 6th Dec., 1902, and served in the Central Provs. as asst. commr.; under-sec. to chief commr., March, 1905; offg. under-sec. to govt. of India, commerce and industry, July to Nov., 1908; settl. offr., Oct., 1913; dep. commr., and ditto, Jan., 1915; 2nd sec. to govt. of Central Provs., Aug., 1918, to Nov., 1921; fin. sec. to govt. of Central Provs., March, 1925; ch. sec. to ditto, March, 1927; C.I.E., Jan., 1928; temp. member govr.'s exec. council, Cent. Provs., May, 1931; and again, July, 1932; confd., Aug., 1932; K.C.S.I., July, 1933; govt. of Central Provs., Sept., 1933.

GOYDER, CECIL WILLIAM, B.Sc., A.C.G.I., All-India Radio (chief engineer) (b. 1st Apl., 1905).—Joined the service as chief engineer, ditto, 20th Aug., 1936.

GRABHAM, REGINALD GEORGE DENIS, Indian Police (supt.) (b. 11th May, 1903).—Joined the service as asst. supt., United Provs., 23rd Dec., 1922; offg. supt., March, 1926; asst. commr., Port Blair, Dec., 1930; supt., May, 1935; addtl. asst. to I.G., Feb., 1937.

GRACE, OLIVER GILBERT, O.B.E., Indian Police (supt., N.W.F.P.) (b. 10th Feb., 1896).—Joined the service, 1st Sept., 1920; asst. supt. Feb., 1922; supt., June, 1925; dist. officer, frontier constabulary, N.W.F.P., Jan., 1929; O.B.E., Jan., 1934.

GRACIE, CLEMENT FREDERICK, B.A., Indian C.S. (asst. commr., Burma) (b. 29th June, 1907).—Educ. at King Edward VII School, Sheffield, and Queen's College, Oxford; joined the service, 17th Oct., 1930; arrived in India, Nov., 1930, and served in Burma as asst. commr.; secty. to finl. commr., Burma, July, 1936 to Dec., 1936.

GRAHAM, CYRIL GEORGE, Indian State Railways (exec. engr., G.I.P. rly.) (b. 28th May, 1889).—Joined the service, 1st Oct., 1909; resdt. engr., G.I.P. rly., Nov., 1914; exec. engr., May, 1932; offg. dep. agent, G.I.P. rly., Apl., 1935; offg. dep. ch. engr., maintenance, G.I.P. rly., May, 1936; and again, Sept., 1937.

GRAHAM, DAVID LIVINGSTONE, O.B.E., Lt.-Col., I.M.S., ret'd. (b. 1st Sept., 1882).—First commn. dated 2nd Feb., 1907; O.B.E., Jan., 1919; in milty. employ to 26th Nov., 1921; offg. civil surg., 27th Nov., 1921; ret'd., Sept., 1937.

GRAHAM, FREDERICK ERNEST, Office of High Commr. for India (higher clerical officer, store dept.) (b. 29th Apl., 1892).—Apptd. 2nd class clerk, 8th Sept., 1914; on mil. service from Nov., 1915, to Sept., 1919; transf. to office of high commr. for India, Oct., 1920; exec. officer, April, 1922; higher clerical officer, April, 1926.

GRAHAM, HAROLD, C.I.E., Indian C.S. (secty. to govt., educn. dept., Bengal) (b. 26th April, 1889).—Educ. at Manchester Cr. Sch. and Merton Coll., Oxford; apptd. after exam. of 1912; arrived in Bengal, 10th Dec., 1913, and served in Bengal as an asst. mag. and collr.; I.A.R.O., June, 1915, to Jan., 1919; jt. mag. and dep. collr., Oct., 1919; offg. dist. and sess. judge, Oct., 1919; offg. mag. and collr., Sept., 1922; confd., Nov., 1926; pte. sec. to govr. of Bengal, April, 1927; offg. commr., Sept., 1931, and again, Jan., 1933; C.I.E., Jan., 1932; secty. to govt., educn. dept., Bengal, Mar., 1936.

GRAHAM, HAROLD JOHN, O.B.E., Indian State Rlys. (divl. transportation supt., G.I.P. Rly.) (b. 1st June, 1886).—Joined the service 30th June, 1905; asst. traffic supt., April, 1907; dist. ditto, Sept., 1911; in milty. employ from 4th Oct., 1914, to 14th Feb., 1920; supt. of claims, Nov., 1922; pers. asst. to ch. transportation supt., Oct., 1923; offg. dep. agent, March, 1926; adviser to Indian delegation to the internatl. labour conference, Geneva, May-June, 1928; divl. transportation supt., Nov., 1928.

GRAHAM, HUBERT, Indian C.S. (magt. and collr., Bengal) (b. 7th Nov., 1890).—Educ. at Manchester Gr. Sch. and Exeter Coll., Oxford; apptd. after exam. of 1913; arrived, 18th Dec., 1914, and served in Bengal as an asst. mag. and collr.; offg. addl. under-sec. to govt. of Bengal, political dept., April, 1919; offg. mag. and collr., Mar., 1923; confd., Mar., 1931; asst. commr., inc. tax, Bengal, Jan., 1927 commr. of inc. tax, B. & O. (temp.), Mar., 1929.

GRAHAM, SIR JAMES DRUMMOND, Kt., C.B., C.I.E., M.B., Major-Genl., I.M.S. ret'd. (b. 21st Jan., 1875).—First commn., 28th June, 1900; in mil. employ till April, 1908; offg. civil surgeon, United Provs., April, 1908; civil surgeon, June, 1911; on mil. service, Oct., 1914, to May, 1920; C.I.E., March, 1919; offg. public health commr. to govt. of India, Feb., 1924; confd., Sept., 1924; C.B., June, 1931; rep. of Govt. of India on Imp. council of agricl. research, Mar., 1933; ret'd., Feb., 1934; knighted, June, 1934.

GRAHAM, JOHN FULLER, late Indian C.S. (b. 4th Nov., 1872).—Educ. at Bruton Sch. and Univ. Coll., London; apptd. after exam. of 1895; arrived, 6th Dec., 1896, and served in Assam as asst. commr., and subdiv. offr.; asst. mag. and collr., Bengal, Jan., 1902; joint mag. and dep. collr., E. Bengal and Assam, Nov., 1906; dist. and sess. judge, March, 1909 transf. to Assam, April, 1912; re-transferred to Bengal, Feb., 1919; member of Bengal legisl. council, April, 1919; sec. to govt. of Bengal, legisl. dept., Nov., 1919; actg. judge, high ct., Calcutta, March to Aug., 1924, and from May, 1925; addl. judge, high ct., Calcutta, Feb., 1926; actg. judge, ditto, March to Aug., 1926; confd., March, 1927; ret'd., Dec., 1932.

GRAHAM, SIR LANCELOT, K.C.S.I., K.C.I.E., B.A., Barr., Indian C.S. (gouv. of Sind) (b. 18th April, 1880).—Educ. at St. Paul's Sch., and Balliol Coll., Oxford; apptd. after exam. of 1903; arrived, 25th Nov., 1904, and served in Bombay as asst. collr. and mag. and pol. agent; asst. judge and sess. judge, from Jan., 1909; asst. remember., legal affairs, and sec. for making laws and regns., March, 1911; judge and pol. agent and judl. asst. in Kathiawar from Aug., 1913; addl. jt. sec. to govt. of India, legislv. dept., Nov., 1921; C.I.E., Jan., 1924; actg. sec. to govt. of India, legislv. dept., April, 1923; confd., Dec., 1924; K.C.I.E., Jan., 1930; K.C.S.I., Feb., 1936; govr. of Sind, Apl., 1936.

GRAHAM-SMITH, CYRIL, O.B.E., A.C.G.I., A.M.I.C.E., Indian Educl. Service (prinpl. & prof. of engrg., Poona) (b. 2nd June, 1882).—Apptd. prof. of engrg., coll. of engrg., Poona, 10th Jan., 1916; prinpl. & prof. ditto, Nov., 1927; O.B.E., June, 1919.

GRANT, CHARLES FREDERICK, C.S.I., M.A., late Indian C.S. (b. 29th Sept., 1878).—Educ. at Dundee High Sch. and Edinburgh Univ.; apptd. after exam. of 1900; arrived, 2nd Dec., 1901, and served in Burma as asst. commr.; dep. commr., April, 1915; on mil. duty from May, 1915, to Oct., 1919; chief. sec. to govt. of Burma, Aug., 1922; commr. (s.p.t.), May, 1924; confd., April, 1926; offg. finl. commr., Jan., 1928; chairman, dev. trust, Rangoon, Jan., 1929; ret'd., Jan., 1937; C.S.I., May, 1937; chairman, public services commission, Burma, April, 1937.

GRANT, CHARLES WILLIAM, B.A., late Indian C.S. (b. 6th Feb., 1879).—Educ. at Dulwich and Exeter Coll., Oxford, apptd. after exam. of 1901; arrived, 1st Dec., 1902, and served in the United Provs. as asst. mag. and collr.; jt. mag., March, 1913; asst. supt., Dehra Dun, Oct., 1913, to Oct., 1915; mag. and collr., April, 1920; dep. commr. Nov., 1921; mag. and collr., Oct., 1924; offg. commr., June, 1928; confd., Dec., 1931; offg. member, bd. of rev., United Provs., May, 1935; presdt., bd. of public health, United Provs., May, 1935; ret'd., Dec., 1937.

GRANT, JOHN LESLIE, A.C.G.I., A.M.I.C.E., Indian Service of Engrs. (exec. engr., Sind) (b. 12th Oct., 1890).—Joined the service as asst. engr., 1st Oct., 1913; in milty. employ from 1st Aug., 1916, to 18th March, 1920; asst. exec. engr., March, 1920; exec. engr., Oct., 1922.

GRANT, JOHN WILLIAM, M.A., B.Sc., Indian Agricl. Service (b. 18th Aug., 1894).—Joined the service as dep. dir. of agric., Burma, on 14th Nov., 1922.

GRANT, LACHLAN COLVILLE PATRICK MAC-ALPINE, O.B.E., Indian Posts and Telegraphs (divl. engr.) (b. 12th Aug., 1902).—Arrived in India, 27th Mar., 1925; joined the service as asst. div. engr., 30th Mar., 1925; div. engr., Sept., 1927; p.a. to chief engr., July, 1935.

GRANTHAM, HENRY DONALD, late Indian Police Service.—Joined the service as asst. supt. on 8th Dec., 1907; dist. supt., Dec., 1917; awarded King's police medal, Jan., 1920; dep. commr. of police, Rangoon, Nov., 1925; offg. commr. of police, Rangoon, March, 1926; ret'd., Mar., 1929.

GRANTHAM, STANLEY GEORGE, B.A., late Indian C.S. (b. 26th Aug., 1882).—Educ. at City of London School, and Trinity Coll., Cambridge; apptd. after exam. of 1905; arrived, 26th Nov., 1906, and served in Burma as asst. commr., and as settlt. offr.; asst. sec. to finl. commr., May, 1911, to July, 1913; dep. commr., Dec., 1917; supt., census operations, Burma, May, 1920, to June, 1921; dir. of statistics and labour commr., Burma, June, 1931; ret'd., Feb., 1937.

GRASSBY, CYRIL GEORGE, Indian Police (supt., Bengal) (b. 15th Mar., 1899).—Joined the service on 29th Aug., 1921; offg. supt., Feb., 1925; confd., Aug., 1931.

GRAY, ALFRED GORDON, late Indian State Rlys. (b. 3rd Sept., 1878).—Joined the service as asst. engr., 9th Sept., 1904; resdt. engr., March, 1909; dist. engr., Jan., 1921; dep. ch. engr., July, 1926; ret'd., Dec., 1934.

GRAY, WILLIAM COWAN, Lt.-Col. I.M.S. (ret'd.) (b. 4th Oct., 1878).—Joined the service, 1st Sept., 1905; civil surg., Cochin, Dec., 1923; offg. prof., medl. coll., Madras, July, 1924; offg. D.M.O., Madras, Jan., 1927; confd., June, 1929; ret'd., Nov., 1931.

GREAVES, SIR WILLIAM EWART, Kt., Barr.—Apptd. temp. addl. judge, high court, Calcutta, 23rd Nov., 1914; judge, ditto, March, 1916; Kt., Jan., 1924; ret'd., Dec., 1927.

GREEN, SIR ALAN MICHAEL, Kt., C.I.E., M.A., late Indian C.S. (b. 11th Apl., 1885).—Educ. at St. Paul's Sch., and Lincoln Coll., Oxford; apptd. after exam. of 1908; arrived, 19th Nov., 1909; and served in Bombay as asst. collr.; compiled general administration report for 1912-13 and 1913-14; asst. censor, Poona and Bombay, Oct., 1914, to March, 1915; offg. asst. collr. of customs, Bombay, April, 1915; confd. as asst. collr. of customs, Oct., 1917; collr. of customs, Madras, Nov., 1921; collr. impl. customs service and collr. of customs, Bombay, Dec., 1922; actg. trade commr., London, Mar.-Nov., 1929; secty. to high commr. for India, Nov., 1929; dep. high commr. for India, Sept., 1930; C.I.E., June, 1933; knighted, June, 1935; ret'd., Nov., 1936.

GREEN, ALFRADE DALE, V.D., late Indian State Railways (N.W. Rly.) (b. 13th Nov., 1874).—Joined the service, 8th May, 1895; asst. traffic supt., June, 1907; dist. traffic supt., April, 1913; on mil. duty from May, 1917, to May, 1918; offg. dep. chief comml. manager, April, 1927; dep. ch. operating supt. (provl.), Oct., 1927; on spl. duty, rly. bd., Jan., 1928; ret'd., Nov., 1929.

GREEN, ARTHUR EATOUGH, O.B.E., M.C., M.Sc., A.M.I.C.E., Indian Service of Engrs. (exec. engr., Bihar) (b. 16th Dec., 1892).—Joined the service, 1st Oct., 1919; asst. engr., March, 1920; exec. engr., Oct., 1925; under sec., public works dept., Bihar and Orissa, Jan., 1926; O.B.E., May, 1937.

GREEN, CHARLES WILLIAM TANDY, A.M.I.C.E., Indian Service of Engrs. (suptg. engr., Bengal) (b. 21st May, 1887).—Joined the service, 1st Oct., 1909, as asst. engr.; I.A.R.O. from April, 1915, to Dec., 1919; exec. engr., Oct., 1917; offg. suptg. engr., June, 1926; pers. asst. to ch. engr. and secty., P.W. dept., March, 1928; offg. suptg. engr., Oct., 1929; confd., Dec., 1931.

GREEN, EDWARD WASTIE, B.A., late Indian Educl. Service (b. 10th Nov., 1877).—Apptd. addl. prof. of hist. presy., coll., Madras, 5th Feb., 1910; inspr. of schools, Jan., 1918; principl. govt. coll. for Muhammadans, June, 1921; ret'd., Nov., 1932.

GREEN, FRANCIS, A.M.I.C.E., Indian Service of Engrs. (exec. engr., Sind) (b. 5th June, 1887).—Joined the service as asst. engr., 1st Feb., 1922; in foreign service, Kashmir from Dec., 1927; apptd. to I.S.E. as asst. exec. engr. from 1st Jan., 1930; exec. engr., Feb., 1935; consltg. pub. health engr. to govt., Sind, in addn., June, 1936.

GREEN, HARRY DALE, V.D., late Indian State Rlys. (b. 19th July, 1876).—Joined the service, 9th Aug., 1899, as asst. engr., N.W. Rly.; exec. engr., March, 1908; suptg. engr., March, 1924; offg. chief engr., June, 1925; confd., Jan., 1928; offg. senr. govt. inspr. of rlys., Mar., 1929; ret'd., July, 1931.

GREEN, JOHN MOWBRAY, Indian Police (dist. supt., Madras) (b. 23rd Apl., 1897).—Joined the Service, 31st Jan., 1921; asst. supt., Sept., 1922; offg. supt., Apl., 1925; confd., Oct., 1930; comdt. of police, Aden, May, 1934; reverted, Oct., 1934.

GREEN, LESLIE BENTON, M.B.E., M.I.E. Ind.), Dept. of Industries, Madras (dir. of industries) (b. 11th June, 1892).—Apptd. offg. asst. dir., 16th Dec., 1915; M.B.E., Dec., 1919; dep. dir., Feb., 1922; dir., June, 1935.

GREEN, THOMAS CROTHERS, late Indian Police (b. 26th June, 1904).—Joined the service as asst. supt. Bihar and Orissa, on 22nd Nov., 1923; ret'd., Sept., 1933.

GREEN - ARMYTAGE, VIVIAN BARTLEY, M.D., Lt.-Col., I.M.S., ret'd., late Bengal Civil Medical Dept. (b. 14th Aug., 1882).—Joined the service, 27th July, 1907; in mil. employ to May, 1911; officiated as civil surgn., Jalpaigari, from June, 1911, and as resdt. surgn., Calcutta, from Aug., 1911; prof'r., Medical Coll., Calcutta, March, 1921; ret'd., July, 1935.

GREENE, HUBERT EUGENE, India Posts and Tels. (b. 10th June, 1883).—Held non-gazetted appts. from 27th Feb., 1901; on field service with Mesopotamia expedy. force; on foreign service with Iraq admin. from 1st Nov., 1919, to 23rd Mar., 1921; asst. supt., Aug., 1924; supt., Feb., 1926; dep. P.M.G., traffic, Bengal and Assam circle, Nov., 1930; ch. supt., Madras tel. office, Jan. 1932; ditto, Bombay, May, 1934.

GREENEP, DONALD PRESTWICH, B.A., Indian Forest Service (dep. consvr.) (b. 20th Aug., 1901).—Joined the service as asst. consvr., Burma, on 22nd Dec., 1925; dep. consvr., Nov., 1933.

GREENFIELD, HARRY, C.I.E., Impl. Customs Service (collr. of salt rev., Madras) (b. 2nd Oct., 1898).—Joined the Indian Police service as assist supt. on 27th Dec., 1919; dep. commr., Calcutta, Oct., 1925; asst. collr. impl. customs service, Bombay, 1927; ditto, Calcutta, Feb., 1930; collr. of salt rev., Madras, Nov., 1936; C.I.E., Jan., 1938.

GREENFIELD, HENRY CHALLEN, C.I.E., B.A., Indian C.S. (commr., Central Provs.) (b. 8th Dec., 1885).—Educ. at Lancing Coll., and Pembroke Coll., Oxford; apptd. after exam. of 1909; arrived in India, 26th Nov., 1910, and served in the Central Provs. as asst. commr.; under-sec. to chief commr., July to Sept., 1913; asst. commr., Ajmer-Merwara, May, 1915; commr. (temp.), ditto, Dec., 1916, to Jan., 1917; 2nd asst. to A.G.G. in Rajputana, July, 1918; dep. commr. (provl.), Feb., 1921; confd., Aug., 1923; rev. sec. to govt., April, 1927, and again, July, 1928; secty. in settlts. and land records, etc., depts., Jan., 1929; commr. of settlements and dir., land records, Mar., 1933; offg. commr., May, 1933; confd. (provl.), July, 1934; subst., May, 1936; C.I.E., June, 1934; temp. member, gov.'s exec. council, Central Provs., Mar., 1937.

GREENING, LAURENCE EDWIN, A.M.I.C.E., Indian Service of Engrs. (exec. engr., Bombay) (b. 19th Aug., 1891).—Joined the service, 1st Oct., 1919; asst. exec. engr., May, 1920; exec. engr., Oct., 1924; dep. secty. to govt., P.W.D. (roads and bldgs.), Bombay, Mar., 1934.

GREENWAY, CHARLES MERVYN, late Indian State Rlys. (b. 19th Apl., 1886).—Joined the service as 1st asst. supt. of stores, 30th Apl. 1913; dep. supt. of stores, June, 1921; supt. of stores, Oct., 1925; ret'd., Oct., 1935.

GREENWOOD, DAVID ABERNEATHY, Jail Dept., Madras (supt.) (b. 13th Mar., 1887).—Joined the service 14th Nov., 1919; supt. central jail, Vizagapatam, 1st Nov., 1920; awarded Kaiser-i-Hind medal (2nd class), Jan., 1924; offg. supt., Alipuram jail, Bellary, Dec., 1924.

GREENWOOD, EDWARD JOSEPH BERNAL, M.Sc., M.I.E.E., M.I.E. (Ind.) (elec. inspr.), Electricity Dept., Madras (b. 16th Sept., 1886).—Joined the service as elec. inspr. to Govt., 25th Feb., 1913; elec. inspr., Jan., 1933; p.a. to the chief engr. for electricity, Apr., 1937.

GREG, LIONEL HYDE, C.I.E., late Indian Service of Engrs. (b. 19th Jan., 1879).—Trained at R.I.E. Coll.; apptd. asst. engr., 1st Oct., 1900; exec. engr., Oct., 1908; offg. suptg. engr., March, 1926; confid., June, 1927; offg. ch. engr. (irrign.), Aug., 1928; confd., July, 1929; C.I.E., Jan., 1934; retd., Jan., 1934.

GREGORY, ALBERT EDWARD, India Office (hr. clerical officer Acct. Genl.'s dept.) (b. 12th Feb., 1900).—Apptd. clerical officer, 16th Apr., 1920; actg. higher ditto, July, 1935; confd., Aug., 1936.

GREGORY, JOHN ROGER, late Indian Police Service (b. 2nd Feb., 1893).—Joined the service, 15th Nov., 1912; on milty. duty from May, 1915, to March, 1921; supt. of police, July, 1924; retd., July, 1932.

GREGORY, MARCAR SHERIDAN, O.B.E., M.C., V.D., late Indian State Rlys. (b. 23rd June, 1888).—Joined the service as asst. engr., 1st Oct., 1910; I.A.R.O., 14th March, 1915; exec. engr., Oct., 1918; offg. dep. agent, N.W. Rly., Jan., 1926; confd., Dec., 1928; on spl. duty on E.B. Rly., Oct., 1929; ditto with rly. bd., May, 1930; ch. personnel officer, N.W. Rly., Apr., 1931; O.B.E., June, 1932; retd., May, 1935.

GREGORY, RALPH, Indian C.S. (jt. mag., United Provs.) (b. 11th Dec., 1908).—Educ. at Univ. Coll., London, and Keble Coll., Oxford; joined the service, 11th Oct., 1932; arrived in India, Nov., 1932, and served in the United Provs. as asst. mag. and collr.

GREGORY, WALTER GASPER, Barr., Judl. Dept., Bengal.—Acted as addl. judge, high ct., from 27th March, 1925, to 27th Aug., 1925, and from 9th Nov., 1925, to 11th March, 1926; offg. judge, high ct., March to Aug., 1926, Nov., 1926, to Aug., 1927, and again from Nov., 1927.

GREGORY MOSES, RALPH, Indian C.S. (b. 11th Dec., 1908).—Educ. at University College, London, and Keble College, Oxford; joined the service, 11th Oct., 1932; arrived in India, Nov., 1932, and served in the United Provs. as asst. mag. and collr.; name changed to Gregory, Ralph (q.v.).

GREGSON, EDWARD GELSON, C.M.G., C.I.E., Barr., late Indian Police Service (b. 10th June, 1877).—Joined the service, 14th Jan., 1899; supt. of police, N.W.F. Prov., Nov., 1906; C.I.E., Dec., 1911; spl. duty in connection with suppression of illicit arms traffic, Persian Gulf, Sept., 1914; commr. of police, Basra, April, 1916; C.M.G., June, 1917; pol. offr., Bushire Fd. Force, Oct., 1918, to April, 1919; reverted to police duties as supt., Punjab, May, 1919; offg. dep. inspr.-gen., March, 1925; confd., April, 1926; retd., May, 1931.

GREGSON, WILLIAM, N.D.A., Indian Agricl. Service (b. 28th July, 1892).—Joined the service as dep. dir. of agric., Burma, on 3rd April, 1923.

GREIG, ALEXANDER WILLIAM, M.R.C.S., L.R.C.P., B.A., Lt.-Col., I.M.S. (retd.).—First commn., 19th April, 1902; supt., central jail, Montgomery, Oct., 1913; ditto, Lahore, Dec., 1921; inspr.-gen. of prisons, Punjab, Feb. to April, 1924, and from Oct., 1924; retd., Jan., 1929.

GREWAL, BALBIR SINGH, B.A., Indian C.S. (asst. commr., Punjab) (b. 28th Aug., 1912).—Educated at Government High School, Ludhiana, the School of Oriental Studies, London, and Pembroke College, Cambridge; joined the service, 23rd Sept., 1936; arrived in India, Dec., 1936, and served in the Punjab as asst. commr.

GRIERSON, SIR GEORGE ABRAHAM, O.M., K.C.I.E., Ph.D. (Halle), Litt. D. (T.C.D.); LL.D. (Cantab.), D. Litt. (Oxon.), late Indian C.S. (Bengal).—Educ. at St. Bees, Shrewsbury, and Trinity Coll., Dublin; apptd. after exam. of 1871; arrived, 25th Oct., 1873; served in Bengal as asst. mag. and collr., joint mag. and dep. collr., and small cause court judge; on famine duty, 1874; on special duty in the rev. dept., Dec., 1882; joint mag. and dep. collr., Oct., 1884; mag. and collr., March, 1890; opium agent, Bihar, Oct., 1896; on special duty with Linguistic Survey of India, May, 1898; retd., Sept., 1903; C.I.E., May, 1894; hon. Ph.D. (Halle), Aug., 1894; Litt. D., Dub. 1902; hon. member, Asiatic Soc. of Bengal, Nagari Pracharini Sabha, Benares, American Oriental Soc., Société Finno-Ougrienne, Helsingfors; for assoc. member, Société Asiatique de Paris; corres. member, Königliche Gesellschaft der Wissenschaften zu Gottingen; awarded Prix Volney (1905) by the Institute de France; was vice-presdt., R. Asiatic Soc.; R. Asiatic Soc.'s gold medal (1909); K.C.I.E., June, 1912; O.M., June, 1928; now hon. sec., R. Asiatic Soc.; has publd. various works in connection with the Behari language and its dialects, translns., collections of folk songs, &c.; also "Curiosities of Indian Literature," 1885; "Vidyapati and his Contemporaries," 1885; "Modern Vernacular Literature of Hindustan," 1890; and "Inscriptions of Piyadasi," 1891.

GRIEVE, ROBERT GEORGE, C.I.E., M.A., late Indian Educl. Service (b. 18th Oct., 1881).—Apptd. vice-princl., Teachers' Coll., Saidapet, 31st Oct., 1906; inspr. of schools, Madras, Jan., 1910; dep. dir. of public instrn., Madras, Aug., 1912; princl., Teachers' Coll., July, 1920; offg. dir. of pub. instrn. variously from Aug., 1920; C.I.E., Jan., 1930; retd., Aug., 1934.

GRIEVE, WILLIAM, M.A., B.Sc., Indian Educl. Service (dir. of public instrn., Bombay) (b. 21st Mar., 1885).—Joined the service, 9th Nov., 1914; educ. inspr. in Sind, July, 1923; offg. inspr. of European schools, Bombay Presdy., April, 1924; offg. dep. dir. of public instrn., April, 1926; confd., Sept., 1929; offg. dir., ditto, Feb., 1932; confd., Aug., 1936.

GRIFFE-WILLIAMS, JOHN HENRY, late Indian Finance Dept. (b. 11th Aug., 1881).—Trained at R.I.E. Coll.; joined dept., 23rd Nov., 1901; asst. acct.-genl., post office and tels., Mar., 1911; dep. acct.-genl., ditto, Feb., 1913;

examiner of accts., Oudh and Rohilkhand Rly., Dec., 1915; dep. acct.-gen., Rangoon, Apr., 1920; ditto, Madras, March, 1924; ditto, Bombay, April, 1926; ditto, Burma, Nov., 1928; offg. dep. acct.-genl., Punjab (works), June, 1929; ret'd., Sept., 1934.

GRIFFIN, ARTHUR CECIL, O.B.E., B.Sc. (divl. supt. (senr.), N.W. Rly.) (b. 30th Mar., 1888).—Joined the service as asst. engr., 1st Oct., 1911; on mil. duty from 5th Nov., 1914; O.B.E., Jan., 1919; exec. engr., Oct., 1920; offg. dep., agent Apl., 1933; divl. supt. (sen.), Nov., 1934.

GRIFFIN, FREDERICK CHARLES, M.I.C.E., Engrg. Branch, Public Health Dept., Bengal (ch. engr.) (b. 20th Feb., 1883).—Joined the service as asst. sany. engr., Oct., 1913; offg. exec. engr., Oct. 1922; offg. ch. engr., Nov., 1924, and again, Jan., 1927; confd., Apl., 1927.

GRIFFIN, LANCELOT CECIL LEPEL, B.A., Indian C.S., Indian Political Service (b. 5th Jan., 1900).—Educ. at Wellington Coll., and Christ Church, Oxford; joined the service, 25th Oct., 1923; arrived, 17th Dec., 1923, and served in United Provs. as asst. mag. and collr.; asst. commr., Naini Tal, March, 1925; jt. mag., Nov., 1925; under-secty. to agent to govr.-genl. in C.I., Nov., 1927; asst. pte. secty. to Viceroy, Apl., 1929; secty. to A.G.G., Punjab States, Mar., 1932; commr., Ajmer-Merwana, Apl., 1936; addl. dep. secty., pol. dept., Apl., 1937.

GRIFFITH, ARTHUR LAWRENCE, Indian Forest Service (dep. consvr., Madras) (b. 24th May, 1903).—Joined the service, 17th Dec., 1926; dep. consvr., Nov., 1930.

GRIFFITH, SIR FRANCIS CHARLES, Kt., C.S.I., O.B.E., late Indian Police (b. 9th Nov., 1878).—Joined the service, 14th Nov., 1898; supt. of police, Bombay, June, 1906; ditto, and asst. to inspr.-gen., Bombay, Sept., 1909; ditto, and dep. commr. of police, C.I.D., Bombay, March, 1913; recd. King's police medal, Jan., 1916; O.B.E., June, 1919; offg. commr. of police, Bombay, Feb., 1919; confd., Nov., 1919; inspr.-genl., Sept., 1922; C.S.I., Jan., 1923; Kt., Jan., 1931; ret'd., July, 1933.

GRIFFITH, FRANCIS GORDON, O.B.E., Indian State Rlys. (divl. transpn. officer, N.W. Rly.) (b. 23rd Nov., 1889).—Joined the service as asst. traffic supt., 1st April, 1913; on spl. duty, rly. b.l., March, 1920; asst. dist. traffic supt., Jan., 1922; in milty. employ from July, 1924, to March, 1926; O.B.E., June, 1926; transportation officer, Aug., 1926; statistical officer, Apl., 1929.

GRIFFITH, SIR RALPH EDWIN HOTCHKIN, K.C.S.I., C.I.E., Lt.-Col. I.A., ret'd. (b. 4th Mar., 1882).—In mil. employ from 8th May, 1901, to 18th Nov., 1908, when apptd. to N.W. Frontier Prov. as supg. asst. commr.; asst. pol. agent, May, 1909; asst. to agent to govr.-gen. in Central India, May to Sept., 1909; asst.-commr., N.W. Frontier Prov., Sept., 1909; city mag., Peshawar, Jan., 1911; pol. asst., and ditto, April, 1911; asst. commr., Peshawar, Oct., 1911; ditto, Bannu, March, 1914; dep. commr., Bannu, June, 1914; C.I.E., Jan., 1917; asst. commr., Charsadda, March, 1917; ditto, Mardan, Jan., 1919; pol. agent, Tochi, May, 1920; dep. commr., Peshawar, March, 1924; offg. resdt. Waziristan, June, 1929; offg.

C.C. and A.G.G., N.W.F.P., Sept., 1931; Kt., Jan., 1932; K.C.S.I., Apl., 1932; govr., N.W.F.P., Apl., 1932; ret'd., Sept., 1937.

GRIFFITH, WILLIAM EDWARD, M.A. (Oxon.), late Indian Educ. Service (Bengal) (b. 13th Sept., 1874).—Joined the service, 21st Jan., 1907; prin., David Hare training coll., Feb., 1911; addl. dir. of public instrn., Nov., 1925; ret'd., July, 1931.

GRIFFITH, WILLIAM MAURICE, A.M.I.C.E., late Indian Service of Engrs. (United Provs.) (b. 7th May, 1875).—Joined the service as asst. engr., 3rd Jan., 1903; exec. engr., Jan., 1915; offg. suptg. engr., April, 1927; ret'd., Nov., 1930.

GRIFFITHS, CHRISTOPHER HOWARD, Indian State Railways (signal engr., E. B. rly.) (b. 11th Nov., 1883).—Joined the service, 13th Oct., 1922 as asst. signal engr., N.W. rly.; dist. signal engr., June, 1931; offg. signal engr., E.B. rly., Aug., 1935; confd., May, 1936.

GRIFFITHS, DAVID THOMAS, B.A., Indian Forest Service (dep. consvr., Burma) (b. 10th Oct., 1896).—Joined the service as asst. consvr., 1st Jan., 1923; offg. dep. consvr., Aug., 1924; confd., Feb., 1927.

GRIFFITHS, HARRY MELVILLE, A.M.I.C.E., late Indian Service of Engrs. (Punjab) (b. 16th July, 1881).—Trained at the R.I.E. Coll.; apptd. exec. engr., Punjab, 1st Oct., 1903; exec. engr., April, 1912; on mil. service, April, 1917, to Dec., 1919; offg. suptg. engr., Feb., 1920; ret'd., Aug., 1930.

GRIFFITHS, JOHN COLDWELL, B.A., Indian C.S. (asst. mag. and collr., Madras) (b. 30th Apl., 1913).—Educ. at Swansea Grammar School and Jesus College, Oxford; joined the service, 2nd Oct., 1937; arrived in India Nov., 1937, and served in Madras as asst. mag. and collr.

GRIFFITHS, PERCIVAL JOSEPH, B.A. (Cantab.), B.Sc. (Lond.), late Indian C.S. (dep. commr., Bengal) (b. 15th Jan., 1899).—Educ. at Cambridge (Peterhouse); joined the service, 30th Oct., 1922; arrived, 13th Dec., 1922, and served in Bengal as asst. mag. and collr.; offg. magte. and collr., May, 1928; and again, Sept., 1933; chief manager, Dacca nawab estate, Mar., 1929; mag. and collr., Apl., 1936; offg. dep. commr., May, 1935; confd., May, 1936; ret'd. Aug. 1937.

GRIFFITHS, VINCENT MAYOR, late Indian Service of Engrs.—Trained at the R.I.E. Coll.; apptd. asst. engr., 1st Oct., 1899; exec. engr., March, 1908; suptg. engr., March, 1921; offg. ch. engr. in Sind, Jan., 1926; chief engr., Dec., 1926; ret'd., Nov., 1928.

GRIFFITHS, WILLIAM THOMAS, Indian State Rlys. (dep. agent (staff) G.I.P. Rly.) (b. 20th Aug., 1888).—Joined the service, 1st May, 1916; 3rd asst. secty., May, 1921; offg. publicity supt., Apl., 1923; asst. traffic manager, Oct., 1923; divl. ditto, Feb., 1929; offg. dep. agent, G.I.P. rly., July, 1933; confd., Dec., 1935.

GRIGG, SIR PERCY JAMES, K.C.B., K.C.S.I., (member, govr. genl.'s exec. council) (b. 16th Dec., 1890).—Educ. at Bournemouth Sch. and St. John's Coll., Cambridge; apptd. to Treasury, 1913; served R.G.A., 1915-1918; prin. pte. secty. to successive chancellors of the exchequer, 1921-1930; Chairman, Bd. of

Customs and Excise, Nov., 1930; member, govt. genl.'s exec. council, May, 1934; K.C.S.I., Jan., 1936.

GRIGSON, WILFRED VERNON, B.A., Indian C.S. (dep. commr., Central Provs.) (b. 11th Oct., 1896).—Educ. at St. John's Sch., Leatherhead, and Christ Church, Oxford; joined the service, 25th Oct., 1920; arrived, 3rd Dec., 1920, and served in Central Provs. as asst. commr.; offg. dep. commr., March, 1925; and again, March, 1931; confd., Apl., 1934; on foreign service as adminr., Bastar State, March, 1927.

GRILLE, Sir FREDERICK LOUIS, M.A., Barr., Indian C.S. (judge, high ct., Nagpur) (b. 20th Feb., 1889).—Educ. at Harrow, and Jesus Coll., Cambridge; apptd. after exam. of 1912; arrived, 22nd Nov., 1913, and served in the Central Provs. as asst. commr.; serving under govt. of India, army dept., from Oct., 1917, to Oct., 1919; sub-judge, Central Provs., Oct., 1919; offg. dist. and sess. judge, Oct., 1920; confd., Dec., 1926; offg. legal membr. and secty. to govt., legal dept., Oct., 1927; offg. addl. judl. commr., July, 1931; confd., March, 1934; offg. judl. commr., June, 1933; confd., Jan., 1935; judge, high ct., Nagpur, Jan., 1936; knighted, May, 1937.

GRIMES, Ven. CECIL JOHN, late Indian Eccles. Estabt. (b. 6th Jan., 1881).—Joined the service, 26th Jan., 1915; junr. chaplain, Jan., 1918; offg. archdeacon of Calcutta, Dec., 1926; confd., July, 1928; senr. chaplain, Jan., 1928; retd., Jan., 1934.

GRINDAL, ALAN DOUGLAS, Punjab Civil Service (dep. secty., fin. dept., Punjab) (b. 14th Dec., 1881).—Joined the service, 16th Dec., 1899, and held non-gazetted appts. to 6th Dec., 1913; extra asst. commr., Jan., 1915; asst. regr., ch. ct., Lahore, April, 1918; asst. secty. to finl. commrs., Punjab, Dec., 1920; offg. under-secty. to govt., finl. dept., May, 1922; dep. secty., ditto, April, 1923; offg. secty., ditto, Apl., 1934; and again, Aug., 1936.

GRINDAL, ERIC BERTRAM WOOD, Indian Finance Dept. (b. 25th Oct., 1885).—Held non-gazetted appt. from 30th Mar., 1904; senior acct., Apl., 1920; accts. officer, central accts. office, July, 1929; dep. acct.-genl., central revs., Apl., 1932; offg. acct.-genl., ditto, Aug., 1934; offg. ch. auditor, rly. clearing accts., Mar., 1936; offg. compt. r., N.W.F.P., July, 1936; audit officer, Indian Stores dept., Sept., 1936.

GRIST, FREDERIC EDWIN, India Office (asst. secty., finl. dept.) (b. 9th Nov., 1883).—Educ. at King's Coll. Sch.; apptd. clerk, 2nd class, registry and record dept., 23rd Jan., 1902; attached to mil. dept., May to Oct., 1907; transf. to mil. dept., Nov., 1912; minor staff clerk, March, 1917; staff clerk, June, 1919; principal, Oct., 1921; actg. asst. secty., July, 1931; secty., capitation rate tribunal, Nov., 1932; asst. secty., finl. dept., Dec., 1934.

GROSE, FRANK SAMUEL, Burma Frontier Service (dep. commr.) (b. 15th Oct., 1881).—Held non-gazetted appts. from 7th Dec., 1904; extra asst. commr., Sept., 1906; asst. supt. (B.F.S.), Dec., 1922; dep. commr., June, 1927; offg. commr., Mar., 1933.

GROSSETT, GEORGE ANGUS, late Indian Service of Engrs. (b. 28th Feb., 1880).—Joined the service as asst. engr. on 1st Oct., 1903; exec. engr., Nov., 1911; retd., Feb., 1930.

GRUER, HAROLD GEORGE, M.A., Barr., Indian C.S. (judge, high ct., Nagpur) (b. 6th May, 1886).—Educ. at Robert Gordon's Coll., Aberdeen, Aberdeen Univ., and Christ Church, Oxford; apptd. after exam. of 1909; arrived in India, 26th Nov., 1910, and served in the Central Provs., as asst. commr.; addl. sr. sub-judge, July to Oct., 1915; sub-judge, June, 1917; on mil. service (I.A.R.O.), Nov., 1917, to March, 1919; offg. dist. and sess. judge, Feb., 1923; confd., Nov., 1925; offg. addl. judl. commr., June, 1934; and again, Sept., 1935; judge, high ct., Nagpur, Jan., 1936.

GRUNDY, ERIC EDMUND LEIGH, Indian State Rlys. (b. 6th Apl., 1888).—Joined the service as princl., technical school, Jamalpur, 29th Sept., 1924.

GUBBAY, HENRY ABRAHAM, C.I.E., A.M.I.C.E., late Indian Service of Engrs. (b. 26th Sept., 1883).—Trained at the R.I.E. Coll.; apptd. asst. engr., 1st Oct., 1904; R.I.E. Coll.; apptd. asst. engr., 1st Oct., 1904; asst. sany. engr., Bengal, Oct., 1908, to Jan., 1912; exec. engr., Oct., 1912; temp. suptg. engr., April, 1922; suptg. engr., Aug., 1924; offg. ch. engr. and sec. to govt., roads and bldgs. branch, April, 1926; confd., July, 1926; C.I.E., Jan., 1932; retd., Mar., 1936.

GUBBAY, MOSES MORDECAI SIMEON, C.S.I., C.I.E., late Indian C.S.—Educ. at Clifton, and Caius Coll., Cambridge; apptd. after exam. of 1898; arrived, 18th Dec., 1899, and served in Bombay as asst. collr. and mag., and asst. collr. of customs; compiled the gen. admn. report, 1901-02; sec. stores committee, 1906; offg. under sec. to govt. of India dept. of commerce and industry, June, 1906; confd., 1908, to Feb., 1910; coll. of customs, Bombay, Oct., 1910; on spl. duty in England as Indian trade commr. from Sept., 1914, to March, 1915; on spl. duty under govt. of India as wheat commr., April, 1915; dep. sec. to govt. of India, fin. dept., Oct., 1915; C.I.E., Jan., 1916; offg. controller of currency, July, 1916; confirmed, May, 1917; C.S.I., June, 1918; on deputn. as foodstuffs commr. with govt. of India, and jt. sec., dept. of rev. and agri., Nov., 1918, to April, 1919; member, commee. on Indian exchange and currency, 1919; sec. to govt. of India, finance dept., May, 1920; retd., Apl., 1921; apptd. commr. for I.C.S. Family Pension Fund, Oct., 1937.

GUGGALI, GURUSIDDAPPA HOOVAPPA, B.A., Indian C.S. (dist. and sess. judge, Bombay) (b. 18th Oct., 1897).—Educ. at Fergusson Coll., Poona, and Trinity Hall, Cambridge; joined the service, 12th Dec., 1921; arrived, 6th Jan., 1922, and served in Bombay as asst. collr.; offg. dist. and sess. judge, Feb., 1932; confd., Oct., 1933.

GUHA, BRAJA KANTA, B.A., Indian C.S. (jt. mag. and dep. collr., Bengal) (b. 3rd July, 1900).—Educ. at Calcutta Univ., and Brasenose Coll., Oxford; joined the service, 25th Oct., 1924; arrived, 8th Dec., 1924, and served in Bengal as asst. mag. and collr.; under-secty.

to govt. of Bengal, fin. commerce and marine depts., Mar., 1929; offg. dist. and sess. judge, Feb., 1931; K-i-H. medal, 1st class, Feb., 1937.

GUHA, SURENDRA KUMAR, B.E., Indian Service of Engrs. (exec. engr., Bengal) (b. 4th May, 1894).—Joined the service, 7th Jan., 1920; asst. exec. engr., April, 1923; exec. engr., Jan., 1930.

GUHA, SURENDRA NATH, RAI BAHADUR, M.A., B.L., late Bengal Judicial dept. (b. 11th Nov., 1876).—Joined the service as senr. govt. pleader, 1st Dec., 1927; addl. judge, high ct., Calcutta, Nov., 1929; judge, ditto, Nov., 1933; ret'd., Nov., 1937.

GUHA RAY, SAILENDRA NATH, B.A., Indian C.S. (jt. mag. and dep. collr., Bengal) (b. 14th Nov., 1899).—Educ. at Calcutta Univ., and Lincoln Coll., Oxford; joined the service, 25th Oct., 1924; arrived, 8th Dec., 1924, and served in Bengal as asst. mag. and collr.; offg. dist. and sess. judge, Apl., 1930.
