GUL MUHAMMAD KHAN, B.A., Indian Police (supt.) (b. 8th Feb., 1900).—Joined the service as asst. supt., N.W.F.P., on 30th Jan., 1923; offg. supt., Jan., 1931; confd., June, 1934.

GULAM HUSSAIN RAHIMTULLA KHAI-RAZ (see Khairaz, G.H.R.).

GUMBLETON, JOHN GILLUM MAXWELL, Indian Police (supt.) (b. 13th Sept., 1900).—Joined the service as asst. supt., Bombay, on 17th Nov., 1922; offg. supt., Jan., 1926; confd., Jan., 1934; dep. inspr.-genl., states of W.I., Oct., 1936.

GUMBRILL, ARTHUR, Indian State Rlys. (publicity officer, E.I. Rly.) (b. 15th June, 1887).—Joined the service as publicity officer (temp.) 15th Mar., 1927; confd., Dec., 1928; offg. central publicity officer, Indian State rlys., Apl., 1933, and again, Apl., 1937.

GUNDEVIA, YEZDEZARD DINSHAW, B.A., Indian C.S. (jt. mag., United Provs.) (b. 19th June, 1908).—Educ. at Bombay University and School of Oriental Studies, London; joined the service, 12th Oct., 1931; arrived in India, Nov., 1931, and served in United Provs. as asst. mag. and collr., asst. settlement officer, Oct., 1936.

GUNDIL, NARAYAN RAMKRISHNA, B.A., LL.B., Judl. Dept., Bombay (asst. judge) (b. 22nd Feb., 1885).—Joined the service, 20th Nov., 1913; asst. judge, Dec., 1933; offg. dist. and sess. judge variously from Dec., 1931.

GUNDY, ARTHUR ROBERT, late Indian State Railways (b. 15th Nov., 1881).—Served in Calcutta Mint from May, 1907, to May, 1910; joined rly. dept. as elec. engr., E. Ben. Rly., 16th May, 1910; dep. loco. supt. (electrical), Nov., 1926; ch. electrical engr., Jan., 1928; ret'd., Nov., 1936.

GUNJIKAR, KASHINATH RAMCHANDRA, M.A. (Cantab.), B.Sc. (Bom.), Indian Educl. Service (prof., R.I. of Science, Bombay) (b. 8th Mar., 1894).—Joined the service as prof. of maths., Elph. Coll., Bombay, 7th June, 1925; ditto, R.I. of Science, Bombay, Nov., 1926.

GUNN, JOHN PATERSON, Indian Service of Engrs. (exec. engr., Punjab) (b. 31st Oct., 1889).—Joined the service as asst. engr., Oct., 1913; I.A.R.O., from 7th Sept.,

1916, to 11th April, 1920; asst. exec. engr., Jan., 1920; exec. engr., Oct., 1922; pers. asst. to ch. engr., Nov., 1927; dir., irrign. research inst., Lahore, in addn., June, 1935, to Nov., 1936.

GUNNING, CARDWELL SINCLAIR, M.A., Indian C.S. (dep. commr., Assam) (b. 28th Jan., 1889).—Educ. at Bedford Gr. Sch., and Pembroke Coll., Cambridge; apptd. after exam. of 1911; arrived, 30th Nov., 1912, and served in Assam as asst. commr.; acting under-sec. to chief commr., Sept., 1916; confirmed, Dec., 1916; on mil. duty, I.A.R.O., Oct., 1918, to Jan., 1919; offg. dep. commr., May, 1923; confd., Sept., 1928.

GUNTER, CLARENCE PRESTON, C.I.E., O.B.E., Col., R.E. (ret'd.), late Survey of India.—First commn. dated 10th Feb., 1893; employed in mil. works dept. from April, 1895, to Dec., 1897, and from Oct., 1898; apptd. asst. supt. survey of India, 27th June, 1899; dep. supt., Jan., 1909; ditto, in charge of surveyor gen.'s office, Calcutta, Sept., 1914; reverted to mil. duty, June, 1916, to Aug., 1918; supt., April, 1923; O.B.E., June, 1918; offg. surveyor-genl. of India, March, 1926; dir. frontier circle, Nov., 1926; ret'd., May, 1928; C.I.E., June, 1928.

GUPTA, AJOY KUMAR, B.A., Indian State Railways (dist. traffic supt., E.B. Rly.) (b. 30th Apl., 1894).—Asst. traffic supt., 17th Nov., 1915; dist. ditto, Dec., 1936.

GUPTA, ARUN CHANDRA, Indian Forest Service (dep. consvr.) (b. 30th Oct., 1902).—Joined the service as asst. consvr., Bengal, 3rd Dec., 1926; dep. consvr., Nov., 1930.

GUPTA, ASHOKE CHANDRA, O.B.E., M.A., Indian Finance Dept. (acct.-genl., United Provs.) (b. 4th Nov., 1884).—Joined the service, 1st Apl., 1905; asst. acctnt.-gen., Jan., 1906; dep. contr. of war accts., Aug., 1917; O.B.E., June, 1917; auditor of govt. of India sanctions, April, 1921; dep. acctnt.-gen., March, 1923; offg. dep. auditor-genl., Sept., 1923; offg. acct.-genl., Bihar and Orissa, March, 1926; offg. dir. of audit, United Provs., Feb., 1927; offg. acct. genl. Punjab, Mar. 1929; dir. of army audit, Mar., 1930; acct.-genl., United Provs., Apl., 1933.

GUPTA, AVADH BEHARI (see Avadh Behari G.).

GUPTA, BINODE BIHARI, Indian Police (supt., Assam) (b. Apl., 1885).—Joined the service, 15th Apl., 1905; inspr., Dec., 1928; dep. supt., July, 1929; promoted to I.P., as addl. supt., July, 1932.

GUPTA, BIRENDRA CHANDRA, A.I.E.E., Indian Educl. Service (princl., Dacca engrg sch.) (b. 19th Dec., 1881).—Joined the service, 28th Jan., 1913; prof., civil engrg. coll., May, 1919; princpl., Dacca engng. sch., May, 1933.

GUPTA, D. C., B.Sc. (Har.), Industries Dept., Bihar and Orissa.—Dir. of industries, 13th Apl., 1926 to Apl., 1933.

GUPTA, HARISH CHANDRA, M.Sc., Indian C.S. (jt. mag. and dep. collr., Bengal) (b. 15th July, 1910).—Educated at Ewing Christian College, Allahabad, University of Allahabad and New College, Oxford; joined the service, 6th Sept., 1935; arrived in India, Oct., 1935, and served in Bengal as asst. mag. and collr.; jt. mag. and dep. collr., Nov., 1936.

GUPTA, HIRENDRA NATH, B.Sc., Indian Police (supt., Bengal) (b. 23rd Nov., 1899).—Joined the service as asst. supt., Bengal, on 16th Aug., 1924; offg. supt., Mar., 1931; confd., Apl., 1937.

GUPTA, IRESH CHANDRA, M.Sc., A.I.C., His Majesty's Mint, Bombay (b. 11th Mar., 1898).—Joined the service, 30th Aug., 1921, as dep. assay master; offg. assay master, April, 1922, March, 1923, and Sept., 1926.

GUPTA, JATINDRA CHANDRA, B.A., Barr., late Bengal Judl. Dept. (b. 23rd June, 1877).—Joined the service, 15th Sept., 1908, as regt. and chief. mins. offr., Calcutta small cause ct.; offg. addl. judge, Feb., 1913; judge, Calcutta small cause ct., Nov., 1916; offd. as chief judge variously from Feb., 1924; ret'd., June, 1932.

GUPTA, JNANENDRA NATH, C.I.E., M.B.E., M.A., late Indian C.S.—Educ. at Calcutta Univ., and Balliol Coll., Oxford; apptd. after exam. of 1892; arrived, 12th Dec., 1893, and served in Bengal as asst. mag. and collr.; joint mag. and dep. collr., July, 1900; transf. to E. Bengal and Assam, Oct., 1905; mag. and collr., Oct., 1905; re-transf. to Bengal, April, 1912; M.B.E., June, 1918; offg. commr., March, 1919; confd. as commr., Sept., 1919; delegate of govt. of India to internatl. labour conference, Geneva, 1921; chairman, Calcutta corpn., Jan. to May, 1922; offg. member, board of rev., Sept., 1925; C.I.E., June, 1927; ret'd., Dec., 1928.

GUPTA, JYOTISH CHANDRA, B.A., Income-tax Dept., Bengal (asst. commr.) (b. Dec., 1884).—Joined the service, Apl., 1910; held non-gaz. appts. prior to 8th Mar., 1916; sub-dy. collr., Burdwan, Mar., 1916; income-tax officer, Apl., 1922; confd. as asst. commr. of income-tax, May 1934.

GUPTA, KULADA CHARAN DAS (see Das Gupta, K.C.).

GUPTA, LAKSHMAN SARUP (see Lakshman, S. G.).

GUPTA, NAGENDRA NATH, Judl. Dept., Bengal M.A., Barr. (b. 9th April., 1884).—Joined the service as municipal mag., Calcutta, 16th Sept. 1926.

GUPTA, PANNA LAL, Indian State Railways (exec. engr., E.I. Rly.) (b. 16th Jan., 1896).—Asst. engr., 24th Sept., 1919; asst. exec. engr., Jan., 1920; exec. engr. (Prov.), 1937.

GUPTA, RADHA KRISHANA (see Radha, K.G.).

GUPTA, RAGHUVANSH LAL, M.Sc. (Allbd.), Indian C.S. (under secty., fin. dept., govt. of India) (b. 7th Aug., 1905).—Educ. at Allahabad University and London School of Oriental Studies; joined the service, 11th Oct., 1930, arrived in India, Nov., 1930, and served in Bihar and Orissa as asst. mag. and collr.; under secty. to govt. fin. dept., Bihar and Orissa, Apl., 1934; and again, Feb., 1936; offg. dep. secty., ditto, Oct., 1936; under-secty., fin. dept., govt. of India, Oct., 1937.

GUPTA, RANJIT, Indian C.S. (jt. mag. and dep. coll., Bengal) (b. 1st Feb., 1906).—Educ. at University Coll., Aberystwyth, and King's Coll., Cambridge; joined the service, 8th Oct., 1928; arrived, Nov., 1928, and served in Bengal as asst. mag. and collr.; jt. mag. and dep. collr., Nov., 1930; offg. dist. and sess. judge, Apl., 1935; offg. mag. and collr., Sept., 1935.

GUPTA, ROMESH CHANDRA, late Indian State Railways (b. 13th Dec., 1877).—Trained at R.I.E. Coll.; joined the service, 1st Oct., 1899; dist. traffic supt., Oct., 1913; offg. dep. traffic manager, March, 1924; ret'd., Dec., 1932.

GUPTA, SAMARENDRA NATH, Dept. of Industries, Punjab (princl., Mayo sch. of arts) (b. 29th Mar., 1887).—Joined the service as asst. princpl., Mayo sch. of arts, 2nd Jan., 1914; vice-princl., ditto, May, 1928; princpl., ditto, Nov., 1929.

GUPTA, SARAT KUMAR DUTTA, M.A., F.S.S. M.B.E., late Indian Finance Dept. (b. 17th June, 1881).—Held ministerial appt. from 15th Jan., 1902; asst. acct.-genl., Bengal, July, 1913; asst. contr., India treasuries, Sept., 1915; addl., asst. secty. to govt. of India, fin. dept., Nov., 1916; asst. secty., ditto, Nov., 1917; dep. contr. of war accts., munitions bch., May, 1918; contr. of army factory accts., Oct., 1928; ch. auditor, rly. clearing accts., Mar., 1932; offg. dep. dir. of rly. audit, Nov., 1932; ch. accts. officer, N.W. rly., Jan., 1933; ret'd., June, 1936.

GUPTA, SATISH CHANDRA, C.I.E., Barr. (b. 16th Sept., 1876).—Apptd. asst. secty., Bengal legisl. council (tem.), 22nd Dec., 1911; legal asst., legisl. dept., govt., of India, Sept., 1914; offg. addl. sec., ditto, April, 1923; addl. jt. secty. ditto, Oct., 1925; secty., legislative assembly dept. (temp.), Jan., 1929; confd., Apl., 1930; C.I.E., Jan., 1932; ret'd., Sept., 1933.

GUPTA, SATYENDRA NATH, C.I.E., B.A., Indian C.S. (jt. mag. and dep. collr., Bengal) (b. 29th July, 1895).—Educ. at St. Paul's Sch., London, and Trinity Hall, Cambridge; joined the service, 23rd Oct., 1918; arrived, 10th Jan., 1919, and posted to Bengal; jt. mag. and dep. collr., Jan., 1921; offg. asst. collr. of customs, Calcutta, 1922; offg. mag. and collr., Dec., 1925; confd., Aug., 1930; offg. dep. Indian trade commr. in London, July, 1928; offg. Indian trade commr., Nov., 1929; trade commr., Hamburg, Mar., 1931; C.I.E., June, 1935.

GUPTA, SEN (see Sen Gupta).

GUPTA, SHAIBAL KUMAR, B.A., Indian C.S. (jt. mag. and dep. collr., Bengal) (b. 26th Feb., 1902).—Educ. at Calcutta Univ., and Jesus Coll., Oxford; joined the service, 29th Oct., 1925; arrived, 7th Dec., 1925, and served in Bengal as asst. collr. offg. dist. and sess. judge, Mar., 1932; addl. ditto, 24-Parganas, Aug., 1935.

GUPTA, SHAMLAL, Indian Service of Engrs. (exec. engr., Central Provs.) (b. 15th Apl., 1897).—Joined the service, 21st Oct., 1919; asst. exec. engr., Oct., 1920; exec. engr., Oct., 1929.

GUPTA, SHORLINDRA CHANDRA, M.A. (Cantab.), Indian Finance Dept. (b. 20th Feb., 1892).—Joined the service, 20th May, 1916; currency officer, Cawnpore, Apl., 1922; under-secty., fin. dept. Govt. of India, Dec., 1925; offg. under-secty. for. and pol. dept., Aug., 1930; audit officer Indian stores dept., Nov., 1931; offg. dep. govt. exr., B.N. rly., Feb., 1933; exr., outside audit dept., Bengal, Dec., 1933; compt. Assam, Feb., 1935.

GUPTA, SUKUMAR, M.A., Indian Police (supt., Bengal) (b. 1st Jan., 1899).—Joined the service as asst. supt., Bengal, on 7th Dec., 1922; addl. supt., Mar., 1928; offg. supt., Mar., 1931; confd., Oct., 1932.

GUPTA, SUNNY, B.Sc., Indian Service of Engrs. (b. 6th Mar., 1905).—Appd. asst. exec. engr., Bengal, 14th Apl., 1931.

GUPTA, SURESH CHANDRA, RAI BAHADUR, M.A., late India Posts and Tels. (b. 10th Aug., 1881).—Joined the service as proby. supt., 16th Sept., 1903; supt., May, 1906; 1st asst. post-master, Calcutta, July, 1909; supt., rly. mail service, Apl., 1911; supt., Aug., 1913; supt., post offices, Oct., 1924; offg. dep., P.M.G., Bengal and Assam, Apl., 1926; confd., Dec., 1927; offg. dep. dir.-genl., postal services, Apl., 1933; p.m.g., United Provs., June, 1935; ret'd., Aug., 1936.

GURBACHAN SINGH, SARDAR, Indian Service of Engrs. (exec. engr., Punjab) (b. 15th Dec., 1902).—Joined the service as apprentice engr., 26th Oct., 1925; asst. exec. engr., Oct., 1926; exec. engr., Oct., 1935.

GURBAKSH SINGH, late India Posts and Tels. (b. 2nd Sept., 1877).—Joined the service as apprentice asst. supt., 5th June, 1899; asst. supt., June, 1900; supt., Sept., 1911; divl. engr., Aug., 1920; offg. dir., tel. engrg., June, 1926; confd., Oct., 1927; ret'd., Sept., 1932.

GURBAKSH SINGH, SARDAR BAHADUR, B.A., M.B.E., Indian Service of Engrs. (suptg. engr., Punjab) (b. 15th Jan., 1883).—Joined the service, 13th Sept., 1905; asst. engr., Sept., 1906; exec. engr., Oct., 1914; M.B.E., June, 1919; offg. suptg. engr., May, 1931; confd., July, 1932.

GURCHARN DAS, LALA, MEHTA, Rai Sahib, B.A., Indian Service of Engrs. (exec. engr., Punjab) (b. 11th Mar., 1887).—Joined the service, 18th Oct., 1909; asst. engr., Oct., 1910; exec. engr., Apl., 1920; offg. suptg. engr., June, 1933.

GURDIAL SINGH, Indian Police (b. 15th June, 1908).—Joined the service as asst. supt., Punjab, on probn., 3rd Feb., 1932; confd., Feb., 1934.

GURDIAL SUNDER SINGH, M.A., B.Sc., Indian Forest Service (dep. consvr., Bombay) (b. 22nd July, 1899).—Joined the service, 9th Nov., 1923; asst. consvr., Dec., 1923; dep. consvr., Nov., 1927.

GURJAR, NARAYAN PANDURANG, B.E., Indian Service of Engrs. (exec. engr., Bombay) (b. 12th Apl., 1895).—Joined the service as apprentice engr., 7th Oct., 1919; asst. engr., Oct., 1920; offg. exec. engr., Oct., 1928; confd., Oct., 1929.

GURMUKH SINGH, late Income Tax Dept., Bombay (b. 14th July, 1879).—Held temp. and permanent non-gazetted appts. from 12th Sept., 1900, to 3rd March, 1924; pers. asst. to commr. of income tax, March, 1924; asst. commr. of income tax, June, 1927; ret'd., Oct., 1934.

GURNER, CYRIL WALTER, Indian C.S. (chairman, Calcutta improvement trust) (b. 18th Jan., 1888).—Educ. at Merchant Taylors' Sch., London, Oriel Coll., Oxford, and Univ. Coll., London; apptd. after exam. of 1910; arrived in India, 4th Dec., 1911, and served in Eastern Bengal and Assam as asst. mag. and collr.; transf. to Bengal, April, 1912; under-secty. to govt. genl. dept., Sept., 1915; dep. chairman, Calcutta Corp., July, 1918; dep. sec. to govt., of Bengal, pol. and appt. dept., Sept., 1923;

mag. and collr., March, 1925; secty. govt. of Bengal, local self-govt. dept., Mar., 1929; offg. chairman, Calcutta improvement trust, Mar., 1933; confd., Dec., 1934.

GURNEY, ANGUS HUGH, B.A., Indian C.S. (dist. and sess. judge, United Provs.) (b. 24th Jan., 1889).—Educ. at Highgate School, and Queen's Coll., Oxford; apptd. after exam. of 1912; arrived, 22nd Nov., 1913; and served in the United Prov. as asst. mag. and collr.; jt. mag., March, 1918; on mil. duty (I.A.R.O.), Aug., 1918, to Jan., 1919; dep. acctnt.-gen., Punjab, May, 1922; offg. acctnt.-gen., Bihar and Orissa, April, 1923; ditto, Punjab, March, 1926; dep. acctnt.-gen., Punjab, Oct., 1926; offg. dir. of audit, United Provs., April, 1928; dep. acct.-genl., central revenues, Nov., 1928; offg. dir. audit, U.P., Nov., 1929; acct. genl., U.P., Nov., 1931; offg. addl. dist. and sess. judge, July, 1933; dist. and sess. judge, Oct., 1934.

GURNEY, WILFRED ERIC, V.D., A.M.I.C.E., Indian State Rlys. (divl. engr., G.I.P. Rly.) (b. 2nd Dec., 1887).—Joined the service as asst. engr., 16th Nov., 1911; on milty. service, 16th Nov., 1914, to 1st Oct., 1919; resdnt. engr., Oct., 1919; divl. engr., Oct., 1930; offg. dep. ch. engr. (bridges), July, 1936.

GUTHRIE, ALAN, M.B.E., late Dept. of Industries, Madras (b. 1st June, 1881).—Joined the service as leather expert, 1st Sept., 1911; on mil. duty from 30th June, 1915, to 29th May, 1917; on duty as dep. contr. of hides, Madras, under Indian Munitions Board from 6th June, 1917, to 31st July, 1919; M.B.E., June, 1919; principal, leather trades institute, Madras, Oct., 1923; officer on spl. duty, ditto, Feb., 1930; ret'd., Aug., 1935.

GWILLIM, JOHN DAVID LLEWELYN, Burma Police (Class I.) (b. 30th Oct., 1916).—Appd. asst. supt., Burma, on probn., Dec., 1937.

GWYER, SIR MAURICE LINFORD, K.C.B., K.C.S.I., K.C. (Chief Justice of India).—Member of Indian States Enquiry Committee, 1932, late First Parliamentary Counsel to the Treasury; apptd. first Chief Justice of India, Oct., 1937.

GWYN, AYLMER HAMOND, Lt.-Col., I.A., ret'd., late Survey of India (b. 13th Feb., 1883).—First commn. dated 27th Aug., 1902; apptd. asst. supt. trigl. survey, 17th Feb., 1908; reverted to milty. duty from Dec., 1914, to Aug., 1918; dep. supt., June, 1922; supt., Jan., 1926; offg. dir., Oct., 1931; ret'd., Oct., 1934.

GWYNNE, CLEMENT WANSBROUGH, C.S.I., C.I.E., O.B.E., B.A., Indian C.S. (ch. secty. to govt., United Provs.) (b. 3rd July, 1883).—Educ. at the High Sch., Newcastle, Staffs., and St. John's Coll., Oxford; apptd. after exam. of 1906; arrived, 25th Nov., 1907, and served in United Provs. as asst. mag. and collr.; city mag., Lucknow, Feb., 1913; pte. sec. to lieut.-govr., United Provs., and jt. mag., July, 1917; offg. asst. sec. to govt. of India, army dept., Feb., 1918-Mar., 1919; sec., Indian soldiers' bd., March, 1919; offg. dep. sec. to govt. of India, home dept., Oct., 1919; confd., Sept., 1920; O.B.E., Dec., 1919; on special duty in connection with retrenchment measures in the Andamans, 1922; sec., arms rules ctee., June, 1922; C.I.E., Jan.,

1924; mag. and collr., March, 1924; dep. commr., Nov., 1924; jt. secty. to govt. of India, home dept., Apl., 1929; ch. secty. to govt., United Provs., Apl., 1935; reforms commr., U.P. (temp.), Oct., 1935; offg. ch. secty. to govt., U.P., Oct., 1936; confd., Dec., 1936; C.S.I., May, 1937; author of "A Monograph on Wire and Tinsel."

GWYTHER, BERTRAM GRANVILLE, late Indian Service of Engrs. (b. 29th Nov., 1879).—Joined the service, 1st Oct., 1902; arrived 11th Nov., 1904, and apptd. asst. engr.; exec. engr., June, 1909; suptg. engr., March, 1919; ret'd., July, 1929.

GWYTHER, CYRIL EDRYK, B.A., F.S.S., late Indian Finance Dept. (b. 3rd March, 1882).—Educ. at Blundell's Sch., Tiverton, and Sidney Sussex Coll., Cambridge; joined the service, 18th Feb., 1907, as prob. fin. dept.; asst. acctnt.-genl., May, 1907; dep. contr. of paper currency, Calcutta, June, 1919; dep. acctnt.-gen., Madras, Oct., 1921; dep. contr. of currency, Calcutta, Oct., 1922; ditto, Delhi, Jan., 1924; dep. auditor-genl., May, 1929; acct.-genl., Punjab, Oct., 1931; ret'd., May, 1936.

GYAN NATH, RAI BAHADUR, Diwan, C.I.E., B.A., Indian Political Service (b. Feb., 1885).—Held non-gazetted appts. from 19th July, 1909, to 22nd Aug., 1913; extra asst. commr., Jan., 1916; under secty. to agent to govr.-genl., Punjab States, Jan., 1922; on foreign service to Nabha State, 1st May, 1928; presdt., council of regency, Nabha state, Mar., 1932; C.I.E., Jan., 1934.

GYAN SINGH, BHAI, B.A., Income Tax Dept., Punjab (asst. commr.) (b. 28th Aug., 1884).—Joined the service 6th May, 1911; income tax collr., Jan., 1923; extra asst. commr., Oct., 1924; offg. asst. commr., June, 1926; confd., Jan., 1931.

GYAW, U., B.A., B.Sc., Indian Service of Engrs. (exec. engr., Burma) (b. 6th Mar., 1891).—Temp. service as agric. engr. from 3rd Jan., 1919; agric. engr., Oct., 1920; asst. exec. engr., Oct., 1922; exec. engr., Oct., 1924; offg. suptg. engr., Mar., 1936.

GYAW, U ON, O.B.E., Indian Police (dist. supt., Burma) (b. 8th June, 1887).—Apptd. asst. dist. supt., April, 1920; dist. supt., Jan., 1924; O.B.E., June, 1932.

GYI, SIR JOSEPH AUGUSTUS MAUNG, Kt., Barr., Burma Judl. Dept. (b. 31st Dec., 1872).—Minister in charge of agric., excise and forest depts., Burma, Jan., 1923; apptd. judge, high ct., Rangoon, Nov., 1924; member of govr.'s exec. council, Burma, July, 1926; Kt., Jan., 1927; actg.-govr., Burma, Aug., 1930; minister, govt. of Burma, May, 1932; minister for transf. subjects, Jan., 1933; member, Burma legisl. council, Oct., 1932.

GYI, U THA DIN, Barr., Burma Judl. Service (b. 18th Oct., 1890).—Apptd. second judge, small cause courts, Rangoon, 21st April, 1925; offg. ch. judge, ditto, Nov., 1932.

GYI, U TIN, Burma Commn. (dep. commr., Burma) (b. 26th Feb., 1889).—Joined the service, 26th Jan., 1910; apptd. to Burma commn. as dep. commr., April, 1933; regr., co-op. soc., Burma, Feb., 1935.

HAAS, ADOLPH LOUIS, M.I.Mech.E., Office of High Commr. for India (mechanical engr., store dept.) (b. 25th Oct., 1878).—Apptd. sub-inspr. of stores, Dec., 1903; asst. ditto, Feb., 1909; asst. mech. engr., June, 1919; mech. engr., Nov., 1920.

HABIBULLAH, MUHAMMAD (see Muhammad H.).

HABIBUR, RAHMAN, Indian Police (supt.) (b. 10th May, 1894).—Joined the service, 2nd Jan., 1918, apptd. to I.P. as supt., Bengal, Apl., 1937.

HACKFORTH, PERCY, A.M.I.C.E., late Indian State Ryis. (b. 23rd June, 1880).—Joined the service as junr. engr., 27th March, 1903; resdt. engr., Oct., 1914; dist. engr., April, 1923; off. dep. ch. engr., Dec., 1926; confd., Feb., 1930; offg. ch. engr., Mar., 1932 and Apl., 1934; confd., July, 1934; ret'd., Feb., 1937.

HACKING, Rev. HENRY M.A., late Indian Eccl. Estabt. (Madras) (b. 8th Dec., 1877).—Joined 18th March, 1907; jun. chaplain, March, 1910; on mil. duty from 20th Dec., 1916, to 5th May, 1919; senr. chaplain, March, 1920; ret'd., Mar., 1930.

HACKNEY, NORMAN, B.Sc., Indian State Ryis. (chemist and metallurgist, N.W. rly.) (b. 18th Nov., 1891).—Apptd. asst. chemist and metallurgist, G.I.P. rly., 26th June, 1919; chemist and metallurgist, N.W. rly., Apl., 1927.

HADDOW, JOHN REID, B.Sc., M.R.C.V.S., D.V.S.M., Indian Vety. Service (vety. research officer) (b. 4th April, 1896).—Joined the service, 24th Aug., 1923; dep. dir., Impl. vety. serum inst., May, 1930; vety. research officer (serology), May, 1933.

HADOW, DOUGLAS SCOTT, late Indian Police Service (Punjab).—Joined the service, 15th Oct., 1897, as prob. asst. dist. supt. of police; asst. dist. supt., May, 1899; supt., Aug., 1905; senior supt. of police, Delhi, Aug., 1914; on mil. duty, Aug., 1918, to April, 1919; senr. supt., Simla, April, 1919; inspr.-gen. of police, Kashmir State, July, 1924; ret'd., May, 1929.

HADOW, SIR RAYMOND PATRICK, Kt., C.I.E., A.M.I.C.E., late Indian Service of Engrs. (b. 26th Mar., 1879).—Trained at the R.I.E. Coll.; joined the service, 1st Oct., 1900, as asst. engr., Punjab; exec. engr. (temp.), Aug., 1907; confirmed, Oct., 1908; under-sec. to govt., Punjab, irrig. branch, Dec., 1914; dep. sec. to govt. of India, P.W. dept., Nov., 1920, to Nov., 1922; suptg. engr., Jan., 1921; C.I.E., June, 1925; offg. ch. engr., and sec. to govt. P.W.D., irrigation branch, April, 1926; confd., July, 1927; knighted, Jan., 1934; ret'd., Mar., 1934.

HAEGERT, GOTTFRIED ERNEST JOHN, Public works dept., Punjab (asst. secty. to govt., P.W.D. bldgs. and roads bch.) (b. 8th Aug., 1883).—Held non-gazetted appt. from 7th Jan., 1901; offg. asst. secty. to govt., Punjab, P.W.D. bldgs., and roads bch., Apl., 1920; confd., May, 1921.

HAFAZAT HUSSAIN, Indian C.S. (jt. mag., United Provs.) (b. 28th Feb., 1902).—Educ. at Univ. of Patna and Allahabad, and Caius College, Cambridge; joined the service, 22nd Oct., 1926; arrived, Nov., 1926, and served in the United Provinces as asst. mag. and collr.; under-secty., industries and labour dept., govt. of India, June, 1930; offg. mag. and collr., Jan., 1934; dep. secty. to govt., United Provs., industries branch (temp.), Apl., 1935.

HAFIZUDDIN, A. K. MUHAMMAD, B.A., Indian Police (b. 15th Jan., 1909).—Joined the service as asst. supt. of police, Bengal, on probn., 21st Apl., 1933; confd., May, 1935.

HAFEEZ, MUHAMMAD ABDUL (see Abdul H. M.).

HAGGAR, HENRY JAMES, Marine Dept., Bengal (b. 17th Feb., 1880).—Apptd. senr. ship surveyor, Calcutta, 8th Feb., 1929.

HAIDAR, SHAMS-UD-DIN, KHAN BAHADUR, O.B.E., late Bihar and Orissa Civil Service (b. 16th July, 1879).—Joined the service as proby. dep. magte. and dep. collr., 7th Jan., 1902; confd., Nov., 1903; magte. and collr., Nov., 1929; O.B.E., June, 1931; secty., bd. of rev., Sept., 1932; retd., July, 1934.

HAIDER, AGHA (see Agha Haider).

HAIDER, SAHYD WAHID-UD-DIN, Khan Bahadur, P.M.S. (civil surgeon, United Provs.) (b. Oct., 1886).—Civil surg., Apl., 1929.

HAIG, GEOFFREY ALEXANDER, Indian C.S. (jt. magte., United Provs.) (b. 19th May, 1909).—Educ. at Winchester and Trinity Coll., Oxford; apptd. after exam. of 1930; arrived in India, Nov., 1931, and served in the United Provinces as asst. magte. and collr., asst. settlement offcr., Oct., 1936; on spec. duty, Dec., 1937.

HAIG, SIR HARRY GRAHAM, K.C.S.I., C.I.E., M.A., Indian C.S. (gouv., United Provs.) (b. 13th April, 1881).—Educ. at Winchester and New Coll., Oxford; apptd. after exam. of 1904; arrived, 2nd Dec., 1905, and served in the United Prov. as asst. mag. and collr., and asst. commr.; special duty in fin. dept. of govt. of India, April, 1910; under-sec. to govt., United Provs., June, 1910, to May, 1912; commr. of municipalities, Indore State, Dec., 1912, to May, 1915; apptd. to the I.A.R.O., May, 1915; reverted from milty. duty, April, 1919; dep. sec., fin. dept., govt. of India, Dec., 1919, to Nov., 1920; secty., Indian fiscal commn., 1921-1922; mag. and collr., Sept., 1922; C.I.E., June, 1923; on special duty with royal commn. on superior civil services in India, 1923-4; offg. pte. sec. to the Viceroy, Jan., 1925; offg. sec. to govt. of India, home dept., April, 1926; confd., Dec., 1926; C.S.I., Jan., 1930; temp. member, exec. council of govr. genl., Mar.-July, 1930; attended Indian round table conf., London, Nov., 1930-Jan., 1931; member, govr. genl.'s exec. council, Mar., 1932; K.C.S.I., Jan., 1933; govr., United Provs., Dec., 1934.

HAIG, SIR THOMAS WOLSELEY, K.C.I.E., C.S.I., C.M.G., Lieut.-Col., Indian Army (retd.).—Joined the service, 6th Feb., 1884; regt. duty to July, 1892; asst. commr., Hyderabad, July, 1892; pol. agent, Feb., 1906; asst. sec. to govt. of India, foreign dept., July

to Oct., 1907; pol. agent, Alwar, Nov., 1907; H.B.M.'s consul at Kerman, April, 1910; C.M.G., June, 1912; first asst. to agent, gov.-gen., and chief commr., Baluchistan, May, 1913; H.B.M.'s consul-gen., and agent to the govt. of India, in Khorasan, Dec., 1913; consul-gen. at Isphahan, 1916; C.S.I., June, 1919; retd., Aug., 1920; K.C.I.E., Jan., 1922.

HAIG, WOLSELEY DE HAGA, D.S.O., Lt.-Col. R.E., Indian Service of Engrs (ch. engr., United Provs.) (b. 25th Aug., 1884).—First commn., dated 15th July, 1903; arrived in India, 20th Nov., 1905; asst. engr., Mar., 1909; exec. engr., Feb., 1914; dep. ch. engr. (provl. perm.), Dec., 1926; confd., Apl., 1930; offg. ch. engr., Apl., 1936; confd., Feb., 1937.

HAIGH, FRANK FRASER, Indian Service of Engrs. (exec. engr., Punjab) (b. 4th Feb., 1891).—Joined the service as asst. engr., Oct., 1913; in military employ, 1916-19; exec. engr., Oct., 1922; under-sec., to govt. P.W.D. (irrigation), April, 1927.

HAIGH, PERCY BARNES, M.A., late Indian C.S. (Bombay).—Educ. at Leys Sch., and St. John's Coll., Cambridge; apptd. after exam. of 1901; arrived, 28th Nov., 1902, and served in Bombay as asst. collr. and mag.; chief inspector of factories, June, 1907, to Jan., 1912; supt. land records and regn., March to June, 1913; on mil. duty with I.A.R.O., 1916-19; collr., Nov., 1922; collr. and dist. mag., April, 1923; ditto, and pol. agent, April, 1925; retd., Dec., 1928.

HAILES, MARTIN HUNTER, Indian State Rlys. (dist. traffic supt., Burma Rlys.) (b. 19th Apl., 1885).—Joined the service as asst. traffic supt., 29th July 08 dist. traffic supt., Nov., 1928.

HAILEY, PHILIP COTES, Capt. I.A., Indian Political Service (b. 20th Jan., 1903).—First commn., 1st Feb., 1923; in milty. employ to Sept., 1927; under-secty. to A.G.G. and ch. commr. in Baluchistan, Dec., 1929; asst. to pol. agent, Kalat, Apl., 1930; asst. pol. agt., S. Waziristan, Oct., 1931; offg. under-secty., for. and pol. dept., June, 1933; Brit. trade agent, Gyantse and asst. to pol. officer in Sikkim, Dec., 1933; confd., Mar., 1937; offer on spec. duty, F. & P. dept., July, 1936; under-secty., f. and p. dept., Aug., 1936; under-secty., pol. dept., Apl., 1937.

HAILEY, Baron, WILLIAM MALCOLM HAILEY OF SHAHPUR AND NEWPORT PAGNELL, G.C.S.I., G.C.I.E., B.A., late Indian C.S. (b. 15th Feb., 1872).—Educ. at Merchant Taylors' Sch. and Corpus Christi Coll., Oxford; apptd. after exam. of 1894; arrived, 6th Dec., 1895, and served in the Punjab as asst. commr.; junr. sec. to finl. commr., April, 1898, to Oct., 1899; settlt. offr., Dec., 1899; colonization offr., Jhelum Canal, Nov., 1901, to Oct., 1904, and from Sept., 1905, to March, 1907; dep. commr., Oct., 1906; sec. to Punjab govt., Nov., 1907; offg. dep. sec. to govt. of India, fin. dept., July, 1908; confd., Nov., 1909; special duty in fin. dept. from March, 1911; C.I.E., Dec., 1911; 1st chief commr., Delhi, from 1st Oct., 1912; apptd. to Imperial Delhi Cttee., 1913; C.S.I., June, 1915; on deputn. with Reforms Commee. from Nov., 1918;

finance member, govr.-genl.'s council, Dec., 1919; K.C.S.I., Jan., 1922; govr of Punjab, 1st June, 1924; G.C.I.E., June, 1928; govr., United Provs., Aug., 1928, to Oct., 1930; Apl., 1931, to Apl., 1933; and again, Nov., 1933 to Dec., 1934; attended Indian round table conf., London, Nov., 1930-Jan., 1931; G.C.S.I., June, 1932; Baron, June, 1936.

HAINES, REGINALD, late Indian Service of Engrs. (b. 10th Mar., 1881).—Trained at the R.I.E. Coll.; apptd. asst. examr. of public wks. accts., 1st Oct., 1902; asst. engr., Bombay, Oct., 1906; exec. engr., Oct., 1911; offg. suptg. engr., April, 1927; confd., Dec., 1931; ret'd., Nov., 1932.

HAJARA, KARUNA KUMAR, M.A. (Cal.), Indian C.S. (jt. mag. and dep. coll., Bengal) (b. 22nd Aug., 1903).—Educ. at University of Calcutta and School of Oriental Studies, London; joined the service, 5th Oct., 1928; arrived, Nov., 1928, and served in Bengal as asst. mag. and collr.; jt. mag. and dep. collr., May, 1930; offg. mag. and collr., May, 1934; offg. dist. and sess. judge, June, 1935.

HAKEWILL, CHARLES THOMAS LANCELOT, Indian Police (b. 31st Oct. 1915).—Apptd. asst. supt., Central Provs., on probn., Oct., 1936.

HAKIM RAI, LALA, B.A., Indian Service of Engrs. (exec. engr., Punjab) (b. 2nd Sept., 1897).—Joined the service, 17th Oct., 1921; asst. exec. engr., Oct., 1922; exec. engr., Oct., 1931.

HAKSAR, SUNDAR NARAIN, M.A., B.Sc., Indian C.S. (asst. commr. Punjab) (b. 4th Dec., 1909).—Educated at D.N. High School, Delhi, Punjab University, Delhi University, and School of Oriental Studies, London; joined the service 6th October, 1933; arrived in India, November, 1933, and served in the Punjab as asst. commr.

HAKUMAT RAI, DIWAN, M.C., M.D., Lt.-Col., I.M.S. (prof. of clinical medicine, King Edward medl. coll., Lahore) (b. 15th May, 1883).—First commn., dated 27th July, 1907; in milty. employ to 21st Aug., 1921; offg. civil surg., Aug., 1921; confd., Nov., 1921; profrr., King Edward medl. coll., Oct., 1923; offg. inspr.-genl. of civil hosps., Punjab, Apl., 1935; offg. dep. dir.-genl., I.M.S., Nov., 1935.

HALDAR, BINAY RANJAN, B.E., late Indian Service of Engrs. (b. 27th Aug., 1883).—Joined the service, 28th Feb., 1909; asst. engr., May 1910; exec. engr., May, 1922; ret'd., Mar., 1934.

HALDAR, SUDHANSU KUMAR, B.Sc., Indian Civil Service (jt. mag. and dep. collr., Bengal) (b. 6th Dec., 1901).—Educ. at Calcutta Univ., and King's College, Cambridge; joined the service, 25th Oct., 1926; arrived, Dec., 1926, and served in Bengal as asst. mag. and collr.; jt. magte. and dep. collr., Dec., 1927; commr. of excise and salt, Oct., 1932; offg. dist. and sess. judge, Apl., 1933.

HALDAR, SUDHINDRA KUMAR, M.A., Indian Civil Service (secty. dept. of communications and works, Bengal) (b. 4th Oct., 1891).—Educ. at Calcutta, and London University; apptd. after exam. of 1914; arrived, 21st Nov., 1915, and served in Bombay as asst. collr.; asst. mag. and collr., Bengal, April, 1919; offg. mag. and collr., May, 1923; addl. dist. and sess. judge, Dec., 1926; magte.

and collr., July, 1928; dep. secty. to govt. of Bengal, fin. commerce and marine depts., July, 1929; sec. retrench. ctee., Bengal, May, 1932; commr. of excise and salt, Bengal, Oct., June, 1935; secty. dept. of communications and works, Bengal, Apl., 1937.

HALE, HARRY WILLIAM, B.Sc., Indian Police (b. 5th Feb., 1903).—Joined the service as asst. supt., N.W.F.P., on 20th Nov., 1923; dist. officer, frontier constaby., Mar., 1930; secty., frontier regns. ctee., May, 1931; on spl. duty under dir., intl. bureau, Nov., 1936; to Sept., 1937.

HALES, THOMAS CHRISTMAS, V.D., Indian State Rlys. (divl. supt. (senr.), N.W. Rly.) (b. 15th Dec., 1885).—Joined the service, 1st Jan., 1908, as asst. traffic supt.; dist. ditto, July 1917; offg. dep. ch. comml. manager, Jan., 1928; senr. asst., comml., Apl., 1929; and again, Apl., 1931; offg. divl. supt., Apl., 1929; offg. dep. ch. operating supt. (movements), Feb., 1932; offg. divl. supt. (senr.), N.W. rly., variously from Jan., 1933; dep. ch. comml. manager, N.W. rly., Aug., 1933; offg. ch., ditto, Apl., 1935; divl. supt. (senr.), July, 1936.

HALFHIDE, ROBERT ALFRED, M.B.E., Printing and Stationery Dept. (dep. contr., printing) (b. 5th Dec., 1886).—Held non-gazetted appt. from 8th Feb., 1904; supt., May, 1920; dep. contr., Mar., 1924; M.B.E., June, 1936.

HALFORD, DAVID REECE CHARLES, B.A., B.Sc., Indian C.S. (asst. collr., Bombay) (b. 14th Sept., 1910).—Educ. at St. Paul's School, Emmanuel College, Cambridge, and the School of Oriental Studies, London; apptd. after exam. of 1933; arrived in India, Nov., 1934 and served in Bombay as asst. collr.

HALIFAX, VISCOUNT, RT. HON. EDWARD FREDERICK LINDLEY WOOD, K.G., P.C., G.C.S.I., G.C.I.E.—Viceroy and governor-general of India, 3rd April, 1926, to 17th April, 1931.

HALIM, ABDUL, Munshi (see Abdul Halim).

HALL, ARTHUR GEORGE, Indian State Rlys. (exec. engr.) (b. 7th Nov., 1894).—Joined the service as asst. exec. engr., Jan., 1920; confd., Jan., 1922; exec. engr., June, 1929; govt. insp. rlys., Burma, Apl., 1937.

HALL, CLARENCE ESME, Indian Railway Accts. Service (accts. officer) (b. 16th Oct., 1891).—Joined the service as asst. auditor and accent., G.I.P. Rly., 15th Sept., 1919; services permanently placed at the disp. of finl. commr. rlys., Oct., 1929; confd. as accts. officr., Gr. I., Feb., 1936.

HALL, DANIEL GEORGE EDWARD, M.A., late Indian Educl. Service (b. 17th Nov., 1891).—Joined the service as lecturer in history, Univ. coll., Rangoon, May, 1921; ret'd., Mar., 1936.

HALL, FIELDING (see Fielding-Hall).

HALL, FREDERICK JOSEPH, M.B.E., late Printing and Stationery Dept. (b. 26th Apl., 1879).—Held non-gazetted appts. up to 24th April, 1916; dep. supt., April, 1916; offg. supt., April, 1916; M.B.E., Jan., 1919; dep. contr. (temp.), Jan., 1923; confd., Oct., 1923; manager, G.I. press, Oct., 1926; ret'd., Apl., 1931.

HALL, GEOFFREY FOWLER, C.I.E., M.C., Indian Service of Engineers (supgt. engr., Bihar (b. 9th March, 1888)).—Joined the service, 1st Oct., 1911; asst. engr., Nov., 1911; exec. engr., Oct., 1920; offg. supgt. engr., May, 1931, and again, Feb., 1933; confd., Mar., 1933; C.I.E., June, 1935; ch. engr. (temp.), Jan., 1936.

HALL, JOHN FREDERICK, C.S.I., C.I.E., O.B.E., Indian C.S. (member, bd. of rev., Madras) (b. 14th Apl., 1882).—Educ. at Hymer's Coll., Hull, and Clare Coll., Cambridge; apptd. after exam. of 1904; arrived, 11th Dec., 1905, and served in Madras as asst. collr. and mag.; sub. collr. and joint mag., Feb., 1913; offg. collr. and dist. mag., Feb., 1919; O.B.E., Dec., 1919; confd., April, 1925; sec. to govt. rev. dept., Mar., 1925; C.I.E., June, 1931; offg. 3rd member, bd. of rev., Madras, and commr. of land rev. and settlement, Feb., 1933; offg. 2nd member, ditto, July, 1933; 2nd member, ditto (provl.) and 1st member offg., May, 1935; C.S.I., Feb., 1937.

HALL, RIDLEY MARTIN, Indian Police (b. 25th Mar., 1898).—Joined the service as asst. dist. supgt., Burma, on 22nd Oct., 1923; offg. dist. supgt., Mar., 1931.

HALL, WILLIAM ÆNEAS, B.A., Indian Service of Engineers (exec. engr., Burma) (b. 26th Mar., 1889).—Joined the service as asst. engr. on 1st Oct., 1912; asst. exec. engr., Jan., 1921; offg. exec. engr., Aug., 1926; confd., April, 1928; offg. supgt. engr., June, 1932.

HALL, WILLIAM STIRLING, Indian Service of Engrs. (exec. engr., Punjab) (b. 26th Mar., 1899).—Joined the service as asst. exec. engr., on 20th Oct., 1922; exec. engr., Oct., 1931.

HALL, WILLIAM TELFORD, B.Sc., Indian Forest Service (dep. consvr., United Provs.) (b. 2nd June, 1895).—Joined the service as asst. consvr. on 27th Feb., 1921; dep. consvr., Oct., 1922; instr., Forest coll., Dehra Dun, Mar., 1935.

HALLAND, GORDON HERBERT RAMSAY, C.I.E., O.B.E., late Indian Police (b. 13th Apl., 1888).—Joined the service, 1st Jan., 1909; asst., supgt., Oct., 1909; on milty. duty from 30th June, 1915, to 30th Sept., 1919; O.B.E., June, 1918; supgt., April, 1920; princpl. police training school, Phillour, Jan., 1921; serving in intell. bureau, home dept., govt. of India, Feb., 1927, to Feb., 1930; C.I.E., Jan., 1931; ret'd., Sept., 1933; princpl., Metropolitan police training coll., July, 1933.

HALLETT, Sir MAURICE GARNIER, K.C.S.I., C.I.E., B.A., Indian C.S. (gouv. of Bihar) (b. 28th Oct., 1883).—Educ. at Winchester and New Coll., Oxford; apptd. after exam. of 1906; arrived, 25th Nov., 1907, and served in Bengal as asst. mag. and collr.; transf'd. to Bihar and Orissa, April, 1912; under-sec. to govt., pol., appt. and educn. depts., March, 1913; jt. mag. and dep. collr., April, 1915; under sec. to govt., pol., appt. and educn. depts., Nov., 1915, to Feb., 1916; mag. and collr., Nov., 1920; ditto and sec. to govt., local self-govt. dept., Sept., 1922; offg. commr., Patna divn., 16th Aug., 1925, to 15th Oct., 1925; offg. ch. sec. to govt., April, 1927, and again June, 1930; offg. commr., Bhagalpur, Mar., 1929; C.I.E., Jan., 1930; offg.

secty. govt. of India, home dept., July, 1932; confd., Mar., 1933; C.S.I., June, 1934; member, council of state, Mar., 1936; K.C.S.I., Feb., 1937; govr. of Bihar, Mar., 1937.

HALLIDAY, ROBERT STIRRAT, M.A., Indian C.S. (asst. collr., Bombay) (b. 11th February, 1909).—Educated at Greenock High School, Glasgow University and Wadham College, Oxford; apptd. after exam. of 1932; arrived in India, November, 1933, and served in Bombay as asst. collr.

HALLIDAY, Rev. SYDNEY LANG, Indian Eccles. Estabt. (junr. Chaplain).—Apptd. chaplain on probn., Bihar and Orissa, 12th Oct., 1932; confd., Oct., 1934.

HALLILAY, HERBERT, M.B., M.R.C.S., L.R.C.P., Lt.-Col. I.M.S. ret'd. (b. 27th May, 1876).—First commission, 25th July, 1902; in mil. employ till Jan., 1908, when he was apptd. asst. plague medical officer, Punjab; offg. civil surgeon, April 1908; on mil. service, Aug., 1914, to July, 1919; civil surgeon, Simla West, Aug., 1919; ret'd., Apl., 1931.

HALLOWES, BASIL JOHN KNIGHT, B.A., Indian C.S. (commr., Ajmer-Merwara) (b. 27th Aug., 1884).—Educ. at Mill Hill, Cais Coll., Cambridge, and University Coll., London; apptd. after exam. of 1908; arrived, 2nd Dec., 1909, and served in the United Prov. as asst. mag.; sec., municipal bd., Agra, Aug., 1913; under-sec. to govt., United Prov., Sept., 1916, to April, 1918; jt. mag., May, 1918; offg. as mag. and collr. from Nov., 1920; offg. dy. sec. to govt., industries dept., Oct., 1924; confd. as mag. and collr., Oct., 1925; dep. commr., Nov., 1927; offg. commr., variously from July, 1928; commr., Ajmer-Merwara, Apl., 1937.

HALLOWS, HENRY COLIN, Indian Police (b. 23rd May, 1911).—Joined the service as proby. asst. supgt., Punjab, 24th Nov., 1930; confd., Nov., 1932.

HALLOWS, RALPH INGHAM, Indian Police (asst. supgt.) (b. 4th May, 1913).—Joined the service as proby. asst. supgt., Punjab, Nov., 1932; confd., Nov., 1934.

HALLOWS, RICHARD MILNES, Indian police (b. 14th Feb., 1916).—Apptd. asst. supgt., Punjab, on probn., 10th Nov., 1935.

HALLOWS, WILLIAM, Factory Dept., Bombay (inspr.) (b. 28th July, 1891). Apptd. asst. inspr. of cotton excise dept., Ahmedabad, 7th July, 1923; inspr. of factories, Bombay, Apl., 1926.

HAMBLING, Rev. VICTOR FREDERICK, Indian Eccles. Estabt. (b. 27th Nov., 1896). Apptd. junr. chaplain, 23rd Oct., 1929.

HAMERTON, The Ven. FREDERICK WILLIAM MOUNTGARETT, M.A., late Indian Eccl. Estabt. (Bombay).—Joined, 3rd Nov., 1907; jun. chaplain, Nov., 1910; senr. chaplain, Nov., 1920; archdeacon, March, 1925; ret'd., Sept., 1928.

HAMID, ABUL FAZL ABDUL (see ABUL F. A. H.)

HAMID, AGHA ABDUL, M.A., Indian C.S. (asst. commr., Punjab) (b. 2nd Aug., 1912).—Educ. at Muslim High School, Lyallpur, and Punjab Univ.; joined the service, 20th Sept., 1937; arrived in India, Oct., 1937, and served in the Punjab as asst. commr.

HAMID ALI, MUHAMMAD, B.A., Indian C.S. (jt. mag. and dep. coll., Bengal) (b. 4th Sept., 1906).—Educ. at Lucknow Univ. and Peterhouse Coll., Cambridge; joined the service, 13th Oct., 1931; arrvd. in India 19th Nov., 1931, and served in Bengal as asst. mag. and coll.; jt. mag. and dep. collr., May, 1934.

HAMID ASGHAR, B.A., Indian C.S. (asst. commr., Punjab) (b. 27th Sept., 1897).—Educ. at Punjab Univ. and Christ's Coll., Cambridge; arrived, 9th Jan., 1922, and served in the Punjab as asst. commr.; offg. dist. and sess. judge, June, 1930; and again, Oct., 1932.

HAMID, MUHAMMAD (see Muhammad Hamid)

HAMID, MUHAMMAD ABDUL (see Muhammad Abdul H.)

HAMID MOIZUDIN ABDUL ALI (see Abdul Ali, H. M.)

HAMIL, HARRY, B.A., Indian Educational Service (secty., fed. public services commn.) (b. 3rd Aug., 1891).—Joined the service as prof. of English, Deccan Coll., Poona, 27th Sept., 1919; offg. prin., Gujarat Coll., Ahmedabad, June, 1924; prin., Elph. Coll., Bombay, June, 1926; secty., fed. public service commn., Sept., 1936.

HAMILTON, ARCHIBALD HENRY DE BURGH, B.A., Indian C.S. (judge, ch. ct., Oudh) (b. 4th July, 1886).—Educated at King's Sch., Canterbury, Corpus Christi Coll., Cambridge, and University Coll., London; apptd. after exam. of 1909; arrived in India, 28th Nov., 1910, and served in the United Provs. as asst. commr., and asst. mag. and collr., jt. mag., Oct., 1916; offg. dist. and sess. judge, Nov., 1920; sess. and sub-judge and ditto, Aug., 1924; dist. and sess. judge, Nov., 1925, and again, May, 1936; offg. legal membr. to govt., Apl., 1928; offg. judl. commr., N.W.F.P., Jan., 1936; offg. judge, high ct., Allahabad, July, 1937; judge, ch. ct., Oudh, Oct., 1937.

HAMILTON, ARTHUR FRANCIS, C.I.E., M.B., F.R.C.S., Lt.-Col. I.M.S., ret'd. (b. 13th May, 1880).—Joined, 1st Feb., 1905; in milty. dept. prior to June, 1910; civil surgeon, Nov., 1913; on milty. duty from 1st Oct., 1914, to 6th Oct., 1918; physician, Bai Motibai and Petit Hosps., and prof'r., Grant med. coll., Bombay, May, 1919; supt., ditto and prof. ditto, Nov., 1926; C.I.E., Jan., 1930; ret'd., Mar., 1935.

HAMILTON, ARTHUR PLUMPTRE FAUNCE, B.A., M.C., Indian Forest Service (dep. consvr., Punjab) (b. 7th Oct., 1895).—Joined the service as asst. consvr., 17th Dec., 1921; dep. consvr., Nov., 1925; instr., Rangers' coll., Dehra Dun, June, 1933.

HAMILTON, CHARLES GEORGE, late India Posts and Telegraphs (b. 11th Nov., 1873).—Joined the service, 20th Dec., 1890; supt., Feb., 1905; pers. asst. to dir.-genl., Oct., 1906; offg. asst. dir.-genl., Feb., 1914; on deputation to Iraq from Feb., 1919, to March, 1920; asst. sec. to govt. of India dept. of commerce (temp.), April, 1920; ditto, public wks. dept., Aug., 1921; asst. dir.-genl., Jan., 1923; ret'd. Nov., 1928.

HAMILTON, FREDERICK ARNOLD, C.I.E., late Indian Police Service (Madras) (b. 6th Oct., 1875).—Joined the service, 22nd Nov., 1896; asst. supt., Jan., 1899; supt., Sept.,

1907; ditto, and asst. inspr.-genl., Feb., 1914; awarded King's police medal; actg. commr. of police, Madras, Feb., 1925; dep. inspr.-genl., Sept., 1926; offg. inspr.-gen., Oct., 1926; confd., Jan., 1927; C.I.E., June, 1929; ret'd., Oct., 1930.

HAMILTON, JAMES, late Indo-European Telegraph Dept. (b. 29th Oct., 1874).—Held non-gazetted appt. from 30th March, 1899, to 28th Feb., 1918; asst. supt., March, 1918; supt., April, 1923; ret'd., Oct., 1929.

HAMILTON, JAMES DICK, late Indian Forest Service (Burma) (b. 5th Aug., 1876).—Joined the service, 24th Nov., 1898; dep. consvr., Dec., 1922; ret'd., Nov., 1930.

HAMILTON, KISMET LELAND BREWER, C.I.E., M.A., late Indian C.S. (b. 13th Aug., 1883).—Educ. at Tonbridge, and St. John's Coll., Cambridge; apptd. after exam. of 1906; arrived 27th Nov., 1907 and served in the Central Provs. as asst. commr.; under-sec. to chief commr., April, 1911, to July 1913; settlt. offr., Nov., 1915; dep. commr. (provl.), Feb., 1920; confd., June, 1920; pol. agent, Central Provs. feudatory states, March, 1925; offg. commr., Dec., 1928; confd., Aug., 1933; C.I.E., Jan., 1934; ret'd., May, 1936.

HAMILTON, SACKVILLE WILLIAM SACKVILLE, D.S.O., Col. R.E., late Survey of India (b. 3rd Nov., 1882).—First. commn. dated 23rd July, 1901; apptd. to survey dept., as asst. supt., 14th Jan., 1906; temp. reverted to milty. duty from Oct., 1914, to Nov., 1919; dep. supt., March, 1920; supt., Jan., 1926; offg. dir., March, 1928; confd., Aug., 1929; offg. surveyor-genl., June, 1935; ret'd., Nov., 1937.

HAMILTON, WILLIAM FRANCIS ALFRED, M.B.E., Indian Police (asst. supt., Madras) (b. 28th Dec., 1907).—Joined the service as proby. asst. supt., 3rd Dec., 1928; confd., Dec., 1930; M.B.E., June, 1931; asst. dir., intelligence bureau, Nov., 1934; awarded Indian police medal, Jan., 1934.

HAMMETT, ROY HESSELTINE, A.M.I.C.E., Indian Service of Engrs. (exec. engr., Bombay) (b. 17th Oct., 1892).—Joined the service as asst. exec. engr., Dec., 1919; exec. engr., Oct., 1925; dep. secty. to govt., Bombay, p.w.d. (rds. and bldgs.), Dec., 1937.

HAMMOND, SIR EGBERT LAURIE LUCAS, K.C.S.I., C.B.E., B.A., late Indian C.S. (b. 12th Jan., 1873).—Educ. at Newton Coll., S. Devon, and Keble Coll., Oxford; apptd. after exam. of 1895; arrived, 27th Nov., 1896, and served in Bengal as asst. mag. and collr.; supt., Cooch Behar State, Nov., 1903; mag. and collr., Bengal, Sept., 1910; transf. to Bihar and Orissa, April, 1912; regr. co-op. credit societies, April, 1912; offg. sec. to govt., fin. and muncl. depts., April, 1913; sec. to govt., fin. and muncl. depts., March, 1914; sec., provl. recruiting bd., July, 1917; also controller of munitions, Bihar and Orissa circle from April, 1918; C.B.E., June, 1919; chief sec. to govt. of Bihar and Orissa, July, 1924; C.S.I., Jan., 1925; temp. member of govr.'s exec. council, Bihar and Orissa, March, 1925; member of govr.'s exec. council, Dec., 1925; K.C.S.I., May, 1927; govr. of Assam, June, 1927; ret'd., May, 1932; chairman, Ind., delimitn. ctee., Aug., 1935.

HAMPTON, CYRIL HERBERT, Indian Police (asst. supt., Burma) (b. 7th Apl., 1897).—Joined the service as asst. dist. supt., Burma, on 29th Oct., 1921; offg. dist. supt. variously from Sept., 1924.

HAMPTON, HAROLD JOHN CHARLES, Office of the High Commr. for India (exec. officer, store dept.) (b. 23rd Jan., 1903).—Apptd. clerical officer, 11th Oct., 1920; exec. officer, 1st Nov., 1932.

HAMPTON, HENRY VERNER, M.A., Indian Educ. Service (princl. secondary trng., coll. Bombay) (b. 1st May, 1891).—Joined, 22nd Feb., 1914, as profr. of Logic and English, Gujerat Coll.; profr. of Logic and English, Feb., 1915; vice-princl., Karnatak Coll., Dharwar, June, 1920; actg. princpl., March, 1921, June, 1923, and Mar., 1924; confd., June, 1925; princpl. secondary trng., coll., Bombay, Nov., 1930.

HAN, MAUNG SAW, B.A., Indian C.S. (asst. commr., Burma) (b. 9th Oct., 1908).—Educated at Government High School, Rangoon, Rangoon University and Wadham College, Oxford; joined the service, 6th Oct., 1933; arrived in India, Nov., 1933, and served in Burma as asst. commr.

HANAFIN, JOHN BERMANS, C.I.E., F.R.C.I., Lt.-Col., I.M.S., ret'd. (b. 10th Aug., 1882).—First commn. dated 29th July, 1907; in milty. employ up to 18th Nov., 1928; asst. dir.-genl., I.M.S., Nov., 1928; offg. health officer, Port of Aden, Apl., 1932; and again, Apl., 1935; ret'd., Oct., 1936.

HANCE, JAMES BENNETT, O.B.E., M.D., Major, I.M.S. (resdy. surg., Mysore) (b. 2nd Apl., 1887).—First commn. dated 27th July, 1912; offg. civil surg., Quetta, Nov., 1919; O.B.E., Oct. 1920; ditto, D.I. Khan., March, 1923; M.O., S.W. Scouts, Aug., 1923; civil surg., D.I. Khan., Sept., 1924; on foreign service in Jodhpur State from 20th Aug., 1925; ch. medl. officer, W.I. States agency and resdy. surg., Rajkot, July, 1928; resdy. surg., Mysore, Oct., 1933.

HANCOCK, CYRIL PERCY, O.B.E., M.C., Major I.A., Indian Political Service (b. 18th Sept., 1896).—First commn. dated 16th Dec., 1914; in milty. dept. prior to 29th Mar., 1920; temp. asst. pte. sec. to govr., Bombay, Nov., 1922; asst. pte. sec. to Viceroy, Feb., 1923; sec. to agent to govr.-genl. in States of Western India, Mar., 1926; O.B.E., June, 1930; offg. dep. secty. to govt. of India, for. and pol. dept., July, 1930; secty. to agent to govr.-genl. in Rajputana, &c., Oct., 1930; presdt., council of state, Bharatpur, May, 1932; offg. pol. agent, E. Rajputana states, in addn. to his duties in Bharatpur, Feb., 1933, and Aug., 1935.

HANCOX, LESLIE PASCOE, Indian C.S. (jt. mag., United Provinces) (b. 18th May, 1906).—Educ. at St. Olave's Grammar School and Christ's Coll., Cambridge; joined the service, 9th Oct., 1929; arrived, 1st Dec., 1929, and served in the United Provinces as asst. mag. and coll.; jt. mag., Mar., 1931; city mgste., Lucknow, Apl., 1935; offg. mag. and collr., Feb., 1936.

HANDS, ARTHUR SHELDON, C.I.E., Indian Civil Service (dep. secty. to the govt. of India, home dept.) (b. 26th April, 1896).—Educ. at Epsom Coll., Middlesex Hosp. and Med. Sch., and Oxford Univ.; apptd. after exam. of 1920;

arrived, 3rd Jan., 1921, and served in Bengal as asst. mag. and collr., offg. mag. and collr., Feb., 1926; and again, Dec., 1931; confd., July, 1933; offg. dep. commr., Aug., 1927; C.I.E., July, 1933; dep. secty. to the govt. of India, home dept., May, 1935.

HANDS, PATRICK LANCELOT JOHN, Indian State Rlys. (dist. contr. of stores, E.I. rly.) (b. 13th Dec., 1889).—Apptd. dist. contr. of stores, on probation, 6th Dec., 1919; confd., Oct., 1920; offg. dep. contr., Aug., 1934.

HANLEY, PERCY JOSEPH, Indian Service of Engrs. (b. 20th Jan., 1904).—Joined the service as asst. exec. engr., Punjab, 16th Oct., 1927; transf. to Central P.W.D., May, 1935.

HANNA, CHARLES HUGH, India Audit Office (higher executive officer) (b. 23rd Feb., 1894).—Educ. at Alleyne's Sch., Dulwich; apptd. second class clerk, 15th July, 1912; higher exec. officer, April, 1922.

HANNA, GEORGE BOYLE, Lt.-Col., I.M.S. (b. 20th Nov., 1887).—First commn. dated 30th May, 1916; in milty. employ up to 31st Aug., 1925; supt., central jail, Dacca, Sept., 1925; supt., presdy. jail, Calcutta, Dec., 1928; reverted to milty. duty.

HANNAH, JOHN STEWART, M.C., late Indian Police Service (Bengal) (b. 22nd Apl., 1893).—Joined the service as proby. asst. supt. on 12th Nov., 1913; confd., Dec., 1915; offg. dep. commr., port police, Jan., 1923; addl. supt., Dec., 1926; supt., Dec., 1927; ret'd., Apl., 1929.

HANNAH, RICHARD GEORGE ALBERT, late Indian Forest Service (b. 1st Dec., 1873).—Joined the service, 1st Apl., 1894; dep. consvr., Nov., 1922; ret'd., Dec., 1928.

HANNAH, SIR HUGH AUGUSTUS MACNISH, Kt., late Indian State Rlys. (b. 14th May, 1878).—Joined the service as asst. supt., 20th Nov., 1906; dist. supt., Oct., 1914; dep. traffic manager, Oct., 1921; junr. dep. agent, Dec., 1921; offg. genl. traffic manager, June, 1923; confd., July, 1923; chief operating supt., Feb., 1925; member, rly. bd., Apl., 1929; offg. agent, E.B. rly., July, 1932; ditto, E.I. rly., Mar., 1933; confd., Apl., 1933; knighted, Jan., 1934; ret'd., Jan., 1936.

HANSEN, HENRY ERIC, late Indian Police (b. 18th Oct., 1892).—Joined the service as proby. asst. supt., 28th Nov., 1912; confd., May, 1914; addl. supt., Feb., 1925; dep. commr. of police, Calcutta, June, 1928; awarded King's police medal, Jan., 1933; ret'd., July, 1936.

HAPPELL, ALEXANDER JOHN, O.B.E., Indian Police (dep. inspr.-genl., Madras) (b. 13th June, 1887).—Joined the service, Nov., 1907; supt., Dec., 1917; dep. commr. of police, Madras City, Mar., 1921; awarded King's police medal, Jan., 1924; O.B.E., June, 1925; supt., govt. railway police, Oct., 1927; offg. dep. inspr.-genl., June, 1931, confd., Feb., 1936.

HAPPELL, ARTHUR COMYN, Indian C.S. (dist. and sess. judge, Madras) (b. 20th Oct., 1891).—Educ. at Tonbridge, and King's Coll., Cambridge; joined the service, 4th Oct., 1921; arrived, 1st Dec., 1921, and served in Madras as asst. collr. and mag.; sub-collr. and jt. mag., July, 1924; offg. dep. sec. to govt., finance dept., May, 1925; offg. under-sec. to govt., public

dept., July, 1925; ditto, dev. dept., April, 1926; registrar, high ct., Oct., 1926; offg. dist. and sess. judge, Dec., 1930; confd., Sept., 1932.

HAQ, S. M., B.A., LL.B., Punjab C.S. Judl. bch. (dist. and sess. judge) (b. 13th Aug., 1895).—Joined the service, 8th July, 1932, as offg. dist. and sess. judge; confd., Jan., 1934.

HAQUE, KAZI ANWARUL, B.A., Indian Police (b. 8th Feb., 1909).—Joined the service as asst. supt. of police, Bengal on probn., 20th Feb., 1933; confd., May, 1935.

HARBANS LAL, VEDHERA, LALA, Indian Service of Engrs. (exec. engr., Punjab) (b. 11th Jan., 1901).—Joined the service as apprentice engr., 23rd Oct., 1924; asst. exec. engr., Oct., 1925; exec. engr., Oct., 1934.

HARDIE, ROBERT, M.A., Indian Police (dep. insp.-genl., Burma) (b. 6th June, 1888).—Joined the service as asst. supt. on 4th Dec., 1909; dist. supt., Dec., 1917; offg. dep. insp.-gen., Nov., 1926; offg. commr. of police, Dec., 1932; confd., Sept., 1933; awarded King's police medal, Jan., 1934; dep. insp.-genl., Jan., 1937.

HARDING, GEORGE TREVOR HAMILTON, C.I.E., Indian Police (supt., Punjab) (b. 18th June, 1895).—Joined the service, 28th Nov., 1925; on milty. duty from 26th July, 1916, to 9th April, 1919; asst. supt., Dec., 1920; supt., Mar., 1925; C.I.E., Jan., 1931.

HARDING, Rev. JOHN AMBROSE, M.A., Indian Eccl. Estabt. (junr. chaplain, Bombay, (b. 12th Sept., 1894).—Apptd. chaplain on probation, 7th May, 1926; junr. chaplain, May, 1928.

HARDINGE, EUSTACE GILBERT, late Survey of India (b. 6th May, 1873).—Joined the service, 25th Nov., 1896; supt., July, 1925; retd., May, 1931.

HARDINGE OF PENSHURST, Baron, Right Hon. CHARLES HARDINGE, K.G., P.C., G.C.B., G.C.S.I., G.C.M.G., G.C.I.E., G.C.V.O., I.S.O.—Viceroy and govr.-gen. of India, 23rd Nov., 1910, to 3rd April, 1916.

HARDINGHAM, HAROLD, late Indian Audit Dept. (b. 27th May, 1880).—Joined the service, 2nd Jan., 1900; asst. acct. genl., Burma, Aug., 1929; retd., Nov., 1934.

HARDMAN, JAMES SNELSON, B.A., Indian C.S. (jt. mag. and dep. collr., Bihar) (b. 7th June, 1905).—Educ. at Manchester Grammar School, and Trinity College, Cambridge; joined the service, 10th Oct., 1929; arrived, 29th Nov., 1929, and served in Bihar and Orissa as asst. mag. and coll.; under-secty. to govt., pol. and appts. dept., Bihar and Orissa, April, 1934; offg. dist. and sess. judge, Oct., 1936.

HARDWICK, DONALD ROSS, Indian Police (supt., Bengal) (b. 26th Feb., 1895).—Joined the service as asst. supt. on 18th Dec., 1914; offg. supt., May, 1924; confd., Dec., 1925; asst. insp. genl., Jan., 1933.

HARDWICK, NOEL DE COURCY, O.B.E., V.D., late Indian State Rlys. (b. 1st Dec., 1877).—Joined the service as asst. traffic supt., 28th June, 1905; on milty. duty from 19th Dec., 1914, to 2nd June, 1920; O.B.E., June, 1919; dist. traffic supt., June, 1923; offg. dep. traffic manager, Dec., 1928; confd., July, 1931; retd., Sept., 1933.

HARDY, DONALD PEVERIL, B.A., Indian C.S. (asst. commr., U.P.) (b. 29th July, 1912).—Educated at High School, Middlesbrough, and Selwyn College, Cambridge; appointed after exam. of 1934; arrived in India, Nov., 1935, and served in the United Provinces as asst. mag. and collr.; asst. commr., Nov., 1937.

HARDY, JAMES DOUGLAS, B.A., Indian C.S. (asst. commr., Punjab) (b. 8th June, 1915).—Educ. at Penistone Grammar School and Caius College, Cambridge; joined the service, 23rd Sept., 1937; arrived in India, Nov., 1937, and served in the Punjab as asst. commr.

HARDY, REGINALD HERBERT, A.C.A., late Indian State Rlys. (b. 1st Oct., 1880).—Joined the service as 1st asst. auditor, 8th Apl., 1909; dep. ch. accts. offr., Dec., 1912; ch. accts. officer, Burma rlys., Nov., 1930; retd., Mar., 1934.

HARE, Rev. ARTHUR NEVILLE, B.A., Indian Eccles. Estabt. (United Provs.) (senr. chaplain) (b. 28th Feb., 1880).—Apptd. chaplain on probation, 12th April, 1918; junr. chaplain, Apl., 1920; senr. chaplain, Apl., 1930.

HARE, REGINALD ARTHUR PROCTOR, Indian Police (supt., Bihar) (b. 13th Feb., 1896).—Joined the service as asst. supt., on 20th Mar., 1921; supt., July, 1925.

HARE-DUKE, ARTHUR ROBERT AUBREY, M.A., A.M.I.C.E., Indian State Rlys. (dep. contr. of stores, E.I. Rly.) (b. 13th July, 1885).—Joined the service as dep. contr. of stores (temp.), 13th Dec., 1926; confd., Dec., 1928; offg. contr. of stores, variously from June, 1929.

HARGREAVE, HERBERT NEWTON, Indian Police (asst. supt., Bihar) (b. 21st Jan., 1905).—Joined the service as asst. supt., Bihar and Orissa, on 8th Dec., 1924; offg. supt., Feb., 1933.

HARGREAVES, HAROLD, late Archaeological survey dept. (b. 29th May, 1876).—Held non-gazetted appt. from 16th Apl., 1909; offg. supt., Nov., 1916; confd., Mar., 1914; offg. dir. genl. of archaeology, Oct., 1928; retd. May, 1931.

HARI CHAND, M.C., M.R.C.S., L.R.C.P., Lt.-Col., I.M.S. (civil surg., Punjab) (b. 26th Aug., 1886).—First commn. dated 31st Jan., 1914; in milty. employ to 30th Jan., 1924; civil surg., Jan., 1924.

HARICHAND, D.E., P.H.E., D.Litt. (Paris), Indian Educl. Service (prof., Patna Coll.) (b. 26th Apl., 1889).—Apptd. supt. of Sanskrit studies, Bihar and Orissa, 8th April, 1918; prof. of Sanskrit, Patna, April, 1929.

HARISH CHANDRA, M.Sc., Indian C.S. (dist. and sess. judge, United Provs.) (b. 15th Sept., 1894).—Educ. at Muir Central Coll., Allahabad, and Oxford Univ. (Non-Coll.); apptd. proby. dep. collr., 4th March, 1918; asst. mag. and collr. 1st Mar., 1922; jt. mag., Oct., 1924; offg. mag. and collr., May, 1925; offg. sess. and sub. judge, Dec., 1926; confd., June, 1929; dist. and sess. judge, Dec., 1929.

HARISH CHANDRA, KHANNA, B.A., Indian Forest Service (dep. consvr., Punjab) (b. 4th Nov., 1899).—Joined the service as asst. consvr., on 9th Nov., 1923; dep. consvr., Dec., 1928; on deptn. to forest research inst., Dehra Dun, Apl., 1929.

HARI SINGH, SARDAR BAHABUR, Sardar, B.A., Punjab C.S. (b. Dec., 1880).—Joined the service, 26th June, 1902, and held non-gazetted appts. to 3rd Jan., 1916; extra asst. commr., July, 1917; dep. commr. for criminal tribes, April, 1923; ditto, and reclamation officer and insp.-genl. of reformatories, Punjab, Feb., 1931.

HARIVANSH, LAL, M.A., Indian C.S. (asst. mag. and collr., B. and O.) (b. 6th Dec., 1912).—Educ. at D.A.V. High School, Lahore, Punjab Univ., and Jesus College, Oxford; joined the service, 15th Sept., 1937; arrived in India, Nov., 1937, and served in B. and O. as asst. mag. and collr.

HARLEY, ALEXANDER HAMILTON, M.A., late Indian Educ. Service (b. 11th June, 1882).—Joined the service, Jan., 1911, and apptd. prin. of Calcutta, Madrasah; ditto, Islamia Coll., Calcutta, July, 1926; ret'd., June, 1937.

HARLEY, THOMAS WILLIAM, C.I.E., M.B., Lieut.-Col., I.M.S. (ret'd.) (b. 10th Dec., 1876).—Joined the service, 29th Jan., 1902; D.M.O., supt. medl. school and medl. officer, dist. jail, Madura, Dec., 1923; C.I.E., June, 1926; ret'd., Dec., 1931.

HARLOW, CHRISTOPHER MILLWARD, B.Sc., Indian Forest Service (consrv., Cent. Provs.) (b. 5th Jan., 1889).—Joined the service as asst. consrv., 23rd Dec., 1911; on milty. duty from 30th Sept., 1916, to 1st May, 1917; dep. consrv., Dec., 1916; employed under Rewa Durbar, Nov., 1929; offg. consrv., May, 1931; confd., Aug., 1936.

HARNAM DAS BHANOT (see Bhanot, H. D.).

HARNETT, WALTER LIDWELL, C.I.E., M.B., F.R.C.S., Lt.-Col., I.M.S. ret'd., (b. 7th June, 1879).—Joined the service, 2nd Feb., 1907; in milty. employ up to 31st March, 1911, and again from 21st Feb., 1916, to 23rd Nov., 1921; civil surg., Oct., 1922; supt., Campbell medl. sch. and hosp., Calcutta, April, 1925; prof'r. of surg., medl. coll., Calcutta, Mar., 1930; C.I.E., June, 1933; ret'd., Dec., 1934.

HARPER, CUTHBERT SUTCLIFFE, M.A., late Indian Educl. Service (b. 17th Apl., 1888).—Joined the service as asst. master, Daly Coll., Indore, 5th Oct., 1914; on milty. duty from 11th Nov., 1916, to 11th March, 1919; supt. of educen., Baluchistan, and princpl., Sandeman high sch., Quetta, Feb., 1925; ret'd., Dec., 1931.

HARPER, GEORGE MILNE, M.A., Indian C.S. (commr., United Provs.) (b. 27th Aug., 1882).—Educ. at Royal High School, Edinburgh, Edinburgh Univ., and Oriel Coll., Oxford; apptd. after exam. of 1905; arrived 26th Nov., 1906, and served in the United Provs. as asst. mag. and collr.; jt. mag., May, 1915; ditto, and asst. settlt. offr., Oct., 1916; on mil. duty (I.A.R.O.), Sept., 1918, to Feb., 1919; mag. and collr., Dec., 1922; offg. commr. from Aug. to Oct., 1923, and from Aug., 1925; secty. to govt., rev. and public wks. dept., Mar., 1931; offg. commr., Nov., 1932; confd., Nov., 1936.

HARPER, KENNETH GORDON, B.C.L., M.A., (Oxon.), late Indian C.S. (United Provs.) (b. 2nd Jan., 1888).—Educ. at Dulwich Coll., and Wadham Coll., Oxford; apptd. after exam. of 1911; arrived, 23rd Nov., 1912, and served in the United Provs. as asst. mag. and

collr.; joint mag., Oct., 1917; on mil. duty (I.A.R.O.), Oct., 1918, to Jan., 1919; services placed at disposal of govt. of India, legisl. dept., April, 1925; sess. and sub-judge, Dec., 1926; offg. dist. and sess. judge, July, 1927; confd., Oct., 1928; ret'd., May, 1931.

HARPER, REGINALD KIRMAN, M.C., Indian C.S. (exr. local fund accts., Burma) (b. 31st Dec., 1895).—Educ. at King Edward VII Grammar School, Morpeth; joined the service, 29th Oct., 1923, and served in Burma as asst. commr.; offg. dep. commr., Dec., 1927; offg. regr., co-op. societies, Burma, Mar., 1932; exr., local fund accts., Burma, June, 1936.

HARPER-NELSON, JOHN JOSEPH, C.I.E., O.B.E., M.C., M.D., Lt.-Col., I.M.S., ret'd. (b. 12th Jan., 1882).—First commn., dated 1st Aug., 1908; O.B.E., Jan., 1919; prof'r., King Edward medl. coll., 24th Nov., 1919; princpl., ditto, Mar., 1930; C.I.E., June, 1936; ret'd., Jan., 1937.

HARRIES, ARTHUR TREVOR, Judl. Dept., United Provs. (judge, high ct., Allahabad).—Apptd. judge, high ct., Allahabad, July, 1934.

HARRIS, BRIAN ALFRED, late Indian Finance Dept. (b. 11th June, 1890).—Joined the service as asst. examr. on 30th July, 1908; asst. acct.-genl., Jan., 1915; dep. acct.-genl., U.P., April, 1924; ditto, Central revenues, Oct., 1925; ditto, Madras, March, 1927; audit officer, Cauvery Mettur project, Dec., 1927; offg. comptr., Assam, May, 1933; ret'd. Feb., 1934.

HARRIS, DIGBY GORDON, late Opium Dept., United Provs. (b. 2nd May, 1887).—Joined the service as proby. asst. opium agent, 2nd April, 1907; confd., April, 1909; 2nd asst. factory supt., April, 1913; 1st ditto, Dec., 1914; factory supt., Feb., 1924; ret'd., July, 1932.

HARRIS, DOUGLAS GORDON, C.S.I., C.I.E., M.I.E. (Ind.) late Dept. of Industries and Labour, Govt. of India (b. 19th Oct., 1883).—Joined the service, 1st Oct., 1907, as asst. engr., United Provs.; exec. engr., Oct., 1915; under sec. to govt. of Ind., public works dept., Oct., 1915; asst. inspr.-gen. of irrig. in India, Oct., 1920; sec., new capital construction cttee., Delhi, 1st Oct., 1922; dep. sec., public wks. branch, dept. of industries, etc., Nov., 1922; C.I.E., June, 1925; offg. consulting engr. to govt. of India, Aug., 1926; confd., Jan., 1927; C.S.I., June, 1932; ret'd., May, 1934; offl. member, exec. council of Palestine, Jan., 1936.

HARRIS, GEORGE JAMES, late Indian State Ryis. (E.I. Rly.) (b. 19th Mar., 1874).—Held non-gazetted appt. from 9th March, 1893, to 4th Sept., 1906; offg. asst. supt., Sept., 1906; and again, Jan., 1908; asst. traffic manager, Aug., 1907; asst. supt., April, 1910; offg. dist. supt., April, 1916; confd., April, 1919; supt., Feb., 1925; temp. dep. ch. operating supt., April, 1927; ret'd., Jan., 1931.

HARRIS, HENRY WILLIAM, Indian State Ryis. (works manager (manufactg.) E.I., rly.) (b. 30th May, 1883).—Joined the service, Sept., 1920; asst. works manager, E.I. rly., Apl., 1921; offg. works manager (manufactg.), Feb., 1924; confd., Mar., 1928.

HARRIS, HUBERT ARNOLD, Indian Service of Engrs. (exec. engr., Punjab) (b. 22nd July, 1896).—Joined the service, 1st Oct., 1920; asst. exec. engr., Dec., 1920; exec. engr., Oct., 1928; offg. under secty. to govt., Punjab, p.w.d., bldgs. and road beh., May, 1933; and again, Oct., 1936.

HARRIS, JOHN FREDERICK WILLOUGHBY, Indian Police (asst. supt.) (b. 19th June, 1912).—Joined the service as proby. asst. supt., United Provs., 1st Nov., 1931; confd., April, 1934; offg. supt., July, 1936.

HARRIS, JOHN RUSSELL, Indian Police (b. 19th Mar., 1907).—Joined the service as asst. supt., Bombay, 14th Nov., 1927; offg. supt., May, 1932.

HARRIS, RONALD MONTAGUE JOSEPH, B.A., Burma Office (asst. princpl.) (b. 6th May, 1913).—Educ. at Harrow and Trinity Coll., Oxford; apptd. asst. princpl., India Office, 16th Nov., 1936.

HARRIS, WILLIAM, M.R.C.V.S., late Indian Vety. Service, Assam (b. 4th Feb., 1879).—Joined the service, 25th April, 1906, or training in Bengal; supt., Assam, May, 1907; retd., Feb., 1934.

HARRISON, ALBERT FRANCIS LOUIS HELFRICH, A.M.I.C.E., late Indian Service of Engrs. (b. 22nd Dec., 1883).—Joined the service, 1st Oct., 1906, as asst. engr.; arrived, 22nd Oct., 1906; exec. engr., Oct., 1914; I.A.R.O., Oct., 1916, to Feb., 1919; offg. suptg. engr., Jan., 1926; confd., July, 1929; retd., Nov., 1934.

HARRISON, ARTHUR NEVILLE JOHN, C.I.E., B.A., Indian Finance Dept. (b. 15th Sept., 1881).—Joined the service, 1st Oct., 1904; asst. examr., Jan., 1905; dep. examr., April, 1909; dep. chief auditor, B.B. and C.I. Rly., April, 1924; chief auditor, ditto, Dec., 1925; C.I.E., June, 1936.

HARRISON, ARTHUR SIDNEY, B.Sc., late Indian Service of Engrs. (b. 14th Sept., 1886).—Joined the service as asst. engr., 1st Oct., 1909; I.A.R.O., from 19th June, 1918, to 5th Feb., 1919; exec. engr., March, 1919; retd., June, 1936.

HARRISON, SIR CHARLTON SCOTT CHOLMELEY, Kt., C.I.E., M.I.E., late Indian Service of Engrs. (b. 18th May, 1881).—Trained at the R.I.E. Coll.; apptd. asst. engr., 1st Oct., 1902; exec. engr., Oct., 1910; temp. suptg. engr., Feb., 1921; offg. chief engr., Lloyd Barrage, July, 1923; chief engr., March, 1927; C.I.E., June, 1928; Kt., Jan., 1932; retd., June, 1935.

HARRISON, CLIFFORD PEVENSEY MATTISON, M.I.C.E., Indian Service of Engrs. (ch. engr. Bengal) (b. 23rd Feb., 1884).—Trained at R.I.E. Coll.; joined the service, 1st Jan., 1907, as asst. engr.; arrived, 11th Jan., 1907; under-sec. to govt. of Bengal, public works dept. June, 1913; exec. engr., Jan., 1915; under-sec. to govt. of Bengal, public works dept., May, 1920; offg. suptg. engr., March, 1927; confd., Sept., 1929; ch. engr., Nov., 1935.

HARRISON, FRANCIS ANTHONY KITCHENER, B.A., India Office (asst. princpl., political dept.) (b. 28th Aug., 1914).—Educ. at Winchester and New College, Oxford; apptd. asst. princpl., 1st Nov., 1937.

HARRISON, GEORGE FRANCIS, Indian Police (asst. supt., Madras) (b. 3rd July, 1909).—Joined the service as proby. asst. supt., 3rd Dec., 1928; confd., Jan., 1931.

HARRISON, MISS GLADYS, B.A., late Indian Educl. Service (b. 27th Dec., 1887).—Joined the service as vice-principal, Queen Mary's Coll., Lahore, 15th Nov., 1919; offg. prinpl. ditto, April, 1921; confd., April, 1922; retd., June, 1936.

HARRISON, JAMES LEES, Indian Forest Engrg. Service (dep. consvr., Assam) (b. 3rd May, 1893).—Joined the service as forest engr., Coorg, 24th Nov., 1921; ditto, Assam, 4th Dec., 1927; dep. consvr., Oct., 1932.

HARRISON, JOSEPH RICHARD, F.G.S., Indian State Rlys (ch. mining engr., rly. bd.) (b. 25th Feb., 1888).—Joined the service as asst. coal supt., 13th March, 1916; colliery supt., Kargali colliery, G.I.P. Rly., Feb., 1923; supt. of collieries, Oct., 1925; offg. chief mining engr., rly. board, variously from Apl., 1927; confd., June, 1935.

HARRISON, ROBERT TULLIS, C.S.I., late Indian Service of Engrs. (Bombay) (b. 18th Sept., 1876).—Trained at the R.I.E. Coll.; joined the service, 1st Oct., 1899, as asst. engr.; exec. engr., March, 1908; ditto, and under sec. to govt., P.W.D., Bombay, May, 1910, to April, 1912; K.-I.-H. medal, 2nd class, Dec., 1911; suptg. engr., July, 1918; offg. chief engr. and joint sec. to govt., Dec., 1920; confd., July, 1921; sec. to govt., P.W.D., March, 1925; C.S.I., March, 1929; retd., July, 1931.

HARRISON, WILLIAM, Office of High Commr. for India (prinpl. executive officer, store dept.) (b. 22nd Jan., 1877).—Clerk, 2nd class, 21st Oct., 1897; supervisor, Sept., 1914; transf. to office of high commr. for India, Oct., 1920; higher exec. officer, April, 1922; prinpl. ditto, April, 1926.

HARROP, HENRY ROBERT, M.A. (Oxon.), late Indian Educl. Service (b. 19th Oct., 1882).—Joined the service, 31st Oct., 1910, as head-master, govt. high sch., Cawnpore; inspr. of schools, United Provs., March, 1914; on milty. service, June, 1915, to May, 1919; asst. dir. of public instrn., United Provs., Dec., 1921; offg. dep. dir., ditto, Oct., 1926; confd., Dec., 1926; offg. dir. of public instrn., U.P., and dep. secty., educn. dept., Apl., 1932; confd., Feb., 1935; retd., Oct., 1937.

HART, FRANK ANTHONY ALBERT, O.B.E., B.A., Indian Forest Service (asst. consvr., Orissa) (b. 30th Oct., 1897).—Joined the service as asst. consvr., Bihar and Orissa, on 29th Nov., 1921; agency forest officer, Nov., 1930; O.B.E., Jan., 1936.

HART, FREDERIC JOHN ALBERT, Indian Forest Service (dep. consvr., Bengal) (b. 2nd Jan., 1896).—Joined the service as asst. consvr. on 15th Nov., 1921; dep. consvr., Nov., 1922.

HART, VINCENT, C.S.I., A.M.I.C.E., late Indian Service of Engrs. (b. 23rd June, 1881).—Joined the service as asst. engr., 1st Oct., 1903, and posted to Madras; exec. engr., Oct., 1911; offg. suptg. engr., Sept., 1925; confd., July, 1929; engr.-in-chief, Cauvery (Mettur) project, Apl., 1930; ch. engr., Jan. 1934; C.S.I., Jan., 1935; retd., June, 1936.

HARTLEY, ARTHUR COULTON, B.A., Indian C.S. (jt. mag. and dep. collr., Bengal) (b. 24th Mar., 1906).—Educ. at University of Manchester and Balliol Coll., Oxford; joined the service, 18th Oct., 1929; arrived, November, 1929, and served in Bengal as asst. mag. and collr. jt. mag. and dep. collr., May, 1931.

HARTLEY, GEORGE EDWARD, B.A., Indian Service of Engrs. (b. 2nd Apl., 1907).—Apptd. asst. exec. engr., United Provs., 24th Oct., 1930.

HARTLEY, JOHN MATHEWS, late Indian Finance Dept. (b. 7th Mar., 1874).—Joined the service, 15th Jan., 1894, as asst. examr., P.W. accts.; dep. examr., Jan., 1898; examr., April, 1903; govt. examr. of accts., Aug., 1910; on depn. to mil. accts. dept., from Jan., 1918; offg. chief auditor, E.B. Rly., April, 1920; chief auditor, N.W. Rly., Oct., 1921; acctnt.-gen., rlys., Oct., 1925; retd., Mar., 1930.

HARTNOLL, ERIC STEWART, B.A., Indian Forest Service (dep. constr., Burma) (b. 5th Jan., 1892).—Joined the service as asst. constr. on 21st Dec., 1915; dep. constr., Feb., 1924; offg. consvr., Apr., 1936.

HARTOG, SIR PHILIP JOSEPH, K.B.E., C.I.E., M.A., B.Sc. (b. 2nd Mar., 1864).—Educ. University Coll. Sch., Owens Coll., Manchester, and Universities of Paris and Heidelberg; C.I.E. Jan., 1917; member of commn. on Univ. of Calcutta, 1917-19; vice-chancellor of Univ. of Dacca since 1920; Kt., Jan., 1926; chairman of public services commn., Oct., 1926, to Apl., 1930; on depn. as chairman to educn. commee. of Indian statutory commn., June, 1928; retd., Apl., 1930; K.B.E., June, 1930.

HARTSHORNE, JOHN FRANCIS BYNG, B.A., late Indian C.S. (b. 17th July, 1884).—Educ. at Eton and Magdalen Coll., Oxf.; apptd. after exam. of 1907; arrived, 20th Nov. 1908, and served in Bombay as asst. collr., asst. settlt. offr., and talukdari settlt. offr.; actg. collr. and dist. mag. and pol. agent, Dec., 1922; actg. dep. commr. of salt and excise, Dec., 1923; offg. collr. and dist. mag., March, 1924; pol. agent, Surat, May, 1927; collr., June, 1927; pres. civil and mily. examn. comm., April, 1931; retd., Nov., 1934.

HARTY, ARTHUR HENRY, M.R.C.S. (Eng.), L.R.C.P. (Lond.), Lt.-Col., I.M.S.—Apptd. offg. supt., central mental hosp., Yeravada, Aug., 1925; confd., Sept., 1926; offg. civil-surg., Dec., 1929.

HARVEY, ALEXANDER FORDYCE, F.C.H., Indian State Railways (agent, E. B. rly.) (b. 24th Mar., 1884).—Trained at R.I.E. Coll., joined the dept., Oct., 1905, as asst. engr.; exec. engr., Oct., 1913; dep. dir. of stores, Apl., 1924; technical officer, rly. board (temp.), Sept., 1925; offg. eng. in ch., surveys and construction, April, 1929; offg. dep. ch. engr., Jan., 1930; confd., Feb., 1932; offg. ch. engr., Mar., 1932; dir., rly. bd., Nov., 1932; offg. agent, E.B. rly., Mar., 1935; ch. engr., June, 1935; agent, E.B. rly., Oct., 1935.

HARVEY, CECIL WALTER LEWERY, O.B.E., M.C., Major, I.A., Indian Political Service (b. 27th Oct., 1897).—First commn. dated 12th Nov., 1914; in milty. employ to 30th Apl., 1924; asst. mag. and collr., Agra, May, 1924; on foreign service as adviser and pte. sec. to Raja of Poonch, Jan., 1925; offg. under-sec. to govt. of India, for. and pol. dept., Jan., 1927; under-sec. to A.G.G. in Rajputana and C.C. in Ajmer Merwara, March, 1927; under-sec. to resident at Hyderabad, April, 1927; ditto to agent to gov.-genl. in Rajputana, May, 1928; offg. under-sec. to govt. of India, for. and pol. dept., Mar., 1929; confd., Sept., 1929; on

foreign service in Manipur State, Dec., 1930; first asst. to resdt., Baroda, Feb., 1933; secty. to A.G.G., Gujarat states, Apl., 1933; pol. agent, W. Kathiawar agency, Oct., 1934; secty. to A.G.G., states of Western India, Mar., 1936; O.B.E., Jan., 1938.

HARVEY, Rev. Canon GEORGE FREDERICK, M.A., late Indian Eccl. Estabt. (b. 29th June, 1877).—Joined 23rd Apl., 1909; jun. chaplain, June, 1912; senr. chaplain, Apl., 1922; retd., Jan., 1934.

HARVEY, GODFREY ERIC, M.A., late Indian C.S. (b. 13th April, 1889).—Educ. at Exeter Sch., London Univ. (external student), and Exeter Coll., Oxford; apptd. after exam. of 1911; arrived, 2nd Dec., 1912, and served in Burma as asst. commr.; offg. under-sec. to govt., Sept., 1917; joint regr., co-operative societies dept., Sept., 1919; sub-divl. officer, March, 1925; offg. supt., N. Shan states, Oct., 1927; confd., Jan., 1929; on duty in connection with Wa states, Nov., 1931; offg. secty. local govt. dept., Burma, June, 1932; retd., Dec., 1934.

HARVEY, GUY JOSEPH, late Indian Police (b. 5th Mar., 1894).—Joined the service as asst. supt. on 29th Nov., 1913; dist. supt., May, 1918; awarded King's police medal, June, 1932; retd., Aug., 1937.

HARVEY, THOMAS, M.Sc., A.M.I.C.E., Indian Service of Engineers (exec. engr., Madras) (b. 13th Nov., 1885).—Joined 1st Oct., 1909, as asst. engr., Madras; exec. engr., Oct., 1919; suptg. engr. housing and suburban circle, Bombay, Oct., 1925; on spl. duty in connection with Back Bay Reclamation Scheme, Nov., 1926, to Feb., 1929.

HASAN, ABUL (see Abul, H.).

HASAN, ALI (see Ali Hasan).

HASAN, ASGHAR (see Asghar Hasan).

HASAN, CHAUDHRI SAJJID ABDUL (see Abdul, H. C. S.).

HASAN, MUNSHI MAHMUD (see Mahmud, H. M.).

HASAN, SAJJID WAZIR (see Wazir Hasan, S.).

HASAN, SHEIKH MAHMUD, M.Sc., Indian C.S. (sub. collr., Madras) (b. 14th June, 1908).—Educated at Government Jubilee High School, Lucknow, Lucknow University and Magdalen College, Cambridge; joined the service, 9th October, 1933; arrived in India November, 1933, and served in Madras as asst. mag. and collr.; sub.-collr., Feb., 1936.

HASAN, SYED SIDDIQUE, Indian C.S. (jt. mag., United Provinces) (b. 4th Feb., 1904).—Educ. at University of Lucknow and Exeter Coll., Oxford; joined the service, 14th Oct., 1929; arrived, 19th Nov., 1929, and served in the United Provinces as asst. mag. and collr.; offg. mag. and collr., Aug., 1933.

HASAN, ZAFAR (see Zafar Hasan).

HASAN ZAHEER, SAJJID, B.A., Indian C.S. (dep. secty. to govt., United Provs.) (b. 16th Nov., 1899).—Educ. at Allahabad Univ. and New Coll., Oxford; joined the service, 9th Nov., 1923; arrived, 14th Dec., 1923, and served in United Provs. as asst. mag. and collr.; asst. commr., March, 1925; jt. mag., April, 1926; offg. mag. and collr., Nov., 1928; and again, Sept., 1933; addl. dep. secty., genl. bch., United Provs. (temp.), Apl., 1935; dep. secty. to govt., U.P., Mar., 1937.

HASAN, ZIA-UL (see Zia-ul Hasan).

HASHIM RAZA, SAIYID, M.A., Indian C.S., (asst. collr., Bombay) (b. 16th Feb., 1910); educated at Government High School, Hardoi, Lucknow Christian College, University of Lucknow and Jesus College, Oxford; joined the Service, 9th Oct., 1934; arrived in India, Nov., 1934, and served in Bombay as asst. commr.

HASHMI, ZAMANSHAH MAHABUBSHAH, Khan Bahadur, late Indian Police (b. 1st July, 1881).—Joined the service, 2nd July, 1906; dep. supt., Dec., 1920; apptd. to I.P. as supt., Dec., 1934; ret'd., July, 1936.

HASLAM, ROY HAVELOCK, Indian Police (supt., Bombay) (b. 30th Oct., 1892).—Joined the service, 14th Nov., 1913; awarded King's police medal, Jan., 1918; supt. of police, April, 1925; ditto and dep. commr., Bombay, Dec., 1927; supt., Aden, Nov., 1928.

HASLEHUST, ARNOLD MAITLAND M.B.E., Indian Police (dep. inspr.-genl., Bombay) (b. 30th Nov., 1888).—Joined 10th Dec., 1908; on milty. duty from May, 1915, to Feb., 1919; M.B.E., June, 1919; supt. of police, June, 1921; awarded King's police medal, Jan., 1923; offg. dep. inspr.-gen., Mar., 1936; confd., May, 1936.

HASSAN, AHMAD, Khan Bahadur, M.A., Punjab C.S. (dep. commr.) (b. 20th Jan., 1885) Joined the service, 3rd Nov., 1908; extra asst. commr., July, 1912; dep. commr., subst. perm., July, 1933.

HASSAN, KHWAJA MUHAMMAD, B.A., Indian State Ry.s. (supervisor of rly. lab.) (b. 20th Mar., 1891).—Joined the service, 14th Oct., 1914; dep. dir., estabt., rly. bd., May, 1932; supervisor of rly. lab., May, 1937.

HASSAN, SYED FIDA, M.A., Indian C.S. (asst. commr., Punjab) (b. 4th Dec., 1908).—Educated at D.S. High School, Lahore, Punjab University, the School of Oriental Studies, London, and Oriel College, Oxford; joined the service, 5th Oct., 1933; arrived in India, Nov., 1933, and served in the Punjab as asst. commr.; sub-divl. offer., Apl., 1936.

HATCH, GEORGE WASHINGTON, C.I.F., late Indian C.S. (Bombay).—Educ. at St. Paul's Sch., and Balliol Coll., Oxford; apptd. after exam. of 1891; arrived, 20th Nov., 1893, and served in Bombay as asst. collr. and mag.; acting under sec., rev. and finl. depts., Jan. to March, 1899; 2nd asst., Aug., 1900; 1st asst., Oct., 1904; junr. collr. and collr. of Bombay, Oct., 1909; junr. collr. and pol. agent, Nov., 1910; senr. collr., Aug., 1915; dep. chairman, Bombay Port Trust, May, 1917; actg. chairman, ditto, Jan., 1918; chairman, ditto, July, 1918; commr., July, 1922; member of the legislv. council, 1923; C.I.E., Jan., 1927; temp. member, exec. council, Bombay, March to July, 1928; ret'd., Jan., 1929.

HATCH-BARNWELL, ANTHONY WILLIAM CHARLES, B.A., Indian C.S. (asst. mag. and collr., Bengal) (b. 6th March, 1912).—Educated at Sherborne and Worcester College, Oxford; apptd. after exam. of 1934; arrived in India, Dec., 1935, and served in Bengal as asst. mag. and collr.

HATCH-BARNWELL, STEPHEN BENEDICT, B.A., Indian C.S. (jt. mag. and dep. collr., Bengal) (b. 26th June, 1909).—Educated at Malvern College and Keble College, Oxford; apptd. after exam. of 1932; arrived in India, Nov., 1933, and served in Bengal as asst. mag. and collr.

HATFIELD, MORGAN BESLEY, B.Sc., Indian Service of Engineers (exec. engr., United Provs.) (b. 16th Jan., 1893).—Joined the service, 1st Oct., 1919; asst. engr., Jan., 1920; asst. exec. engr., March, 1921; exec. engr., Oct., 1925.

HATT-COOK, ROWLAND, late Indian State Ry.s. (b. 14th Sept., 1885).—Joined the service as asst. engr., 4th Nov., 1909; exec. engr., Oct., 1917; R.H.A. from 8th Mar., 1916, to 31st. Mar., 1919; offg. senr. govt. inspr. of ry.s., May, 1933; and again, June, 1934; ret'd., Jan., 1937.
