HATTIANGADI, SHRIDHAR SHIVARAO, B.A., (Bom.), Indian C.S. (jt. mag. and dep. collr., Bengal) (b. 24th Sept., 1904).—Educ. at University of Bombay and School of Oriental Studies, London; joined the service, 5th Oct., 1928; arrived, Nov., 1928, and served in Bengal as asst. mag. and collr.; jt. mag. and dep. collr., Dec., 1930; offg. dist. and sess. judge, Mar., 1935.

HATTYANGADI, SUMETRA ANANTAYYA, B.A., LL.B., late Bombay Civil Service (Judicial Branch) (b. 26th July, 1879).—Held non-gazetted appts. from 1st Apl., 1909, to 23rd July, 1912; sub-judge, July, 1912; ditto, class I., Nov., 1927; offg. judge, small causes ct., Poona; ret'd., July, 1934.

HAVELOCK, LIONEL ACTON; late Burma Excise Service (b. 5th May, 1878).—Held non-gazetted appts. from 13th Jan., 1898; supt., Sept., 1906; offg. ch. supt., Apl., 1929; confd., July, 1929; ret'd., May 1933.

HAWES, CHARLES GEORGE, M.C., B.Sc. (Lon.), A.C.G.I., A.M.I.C.E., M.R.San.I., Indian Service of Engrs. (exec. engr., Sind) (b. 7th Mar., 1890).—Joined the service as asst. engr., Oct., 1912; in milty. employ from 19th June, 1915, to 7th Feb., 1919; exec. engr., Dec., 1919.

HAWES, DERRICK GORDON HARINGTON, Capt. I.A., Indian Political Service (b. 22nd May, 1907).—First commn., 29th Jan., 1927; in mil. employ up to 23rd Mar., 1934; p.a. to Resident, Kashmir, Mar., 1934; under training in the Punjab, Oct., 1934; under-secty. to A.G.G. in C.I., Mar., 1936; pol. agent, Bhopal, Sept., 1936; under-secty. to the A.G.G. in C.I., Nov., 1936; offg. dep. Opium Agent in Malwa, in addn., Nov., 1936.

HAWES, RONALD NISBETT, Capt., R.E., ret'd., India Posts and Tels. (chief tele. commn. engr.) (b. 11th Nov., 1895).—Joined the service, 5th Jan., 1920; divl. engr., wireless, Oct., 1923; dir., Calcutta stn., Indian State broadcasting service, in addn., Apl. to Oct., 1933; chief tele. commn. engr., Burma, Apl., 1937.

HAWKER, REGINALD JAMES, Office of High Commr. for India (higher exec. officer, store dept.) (b. 9th Dec., 1884).—Apptd. 2nd class clerk, India store depôt, 21st Sept., 1903; transf. to office of high commr. for India, Oct., 1920; exec. officer, April, 1922; higher clerical officer, May, 1922; higher exec. officer, May, 1926.

HAWKES, FRANK ROXBURGH, O.B.E., Indian State Rlys. (ch. comml. manager, N.W. Rly.) (b. 9th Oct., 1885).—Joined the service, 22nd Oct., 1904; asst. traffic supt., Sept., 1906; dist. ditto, April, 1914; O.B.E., June, 1919; divl. comml. officer, Nov., 1924; offg. divl. supt., Nov., 1927; confd., Apl., 1930; offg. ch. comml. manager, Nov., 1932; confd., Dec., 1932.

HAWLEY, HERBERT, M.Sc., F.I.C., Medical Dept., Madras (b. 4th Nov., 1887).—Apptd. govt. analyst, King Inst., Guindy, 9th Jan., 1924.

HAWORTH, ARTHUR NOEL, M.R.C.S., L.R.C.P., Indian State Rlys. (dist. medl. officer, G.I.P. Rly.) (b. 22nd Dec., 1894).—Joined the service as dist. surg., 13th Jan., 1920; dist. medl. officer, Apl., 1929.

HAWORTH, REV. CYRIL EVINGTON, late Indian Eccl. Estabt. (b. 1st Feb., 1903).—Apptd. chaplain on probation, United Provs., 6th Nov., 1930; confd., Nov., 1932; ret'd., Feb., 1936.

HAWORTH, EDWIN, M.R.C.S., L.R.C.P., Indian State Rlys. (dist. medl. officer, N.W. rly.) (b. 14th Mar., 1899).—Joined the service, 13th Oct., 1923; dist. medl. officer, G.I.P. rly., July, 1929; ditto, N.W. rly., Dec., 1935.

HAWORTH, SIR LIONEL BERKELEY HOLT K.B.E., Lt.-Col., I.A. (ret'd.).—Joined the service, 23rd Dec., 1893; in mil. employ to May, 1901; pol. asst., March, 1902; asst. to a.g.-g. in Baluchistan, March, 1902; asst. pol. agent, Lower Zlob, March, 1903; asst. to a.g.-g. in Baluchistan, and asst. commr., Quetta-Pishin, March, 1904; consul, Kerman, May, 1905; ditto, Kermanshah, Oct., 1906; H.B.M.'s consul for Arabistan, and asst. to pol. resdt., Persian Gulf, Jan., 1911, to May, 1914; pol. agent, and H.B.M.'s consul, Muscat, Nov., 1916; pol. agent, southern states of Central India, Dec., 1920; H.B.M.'s consul general and agent to govt. of India in Khorasan, March, 1924; offg. pol.-resident, Persian Gulf, Jan., 1927; confd., May, 1927; K.B.E., June, 1928; ret'd., May, 1929.

HAWTHORN, GEORGE THOMAS, Office of the High Commr. for India (exec. officer, store dept.) (b. 20th June, 1892).—Apptd. to store dept., Nov., 1919; clerical officer, July, 1921; exec. officer, Jan., 1926.

HAY, ALLAN SELWOOD, Indian State Rlys. (exec. engr., N.W. Rly.) (b. 21st July, 1888).—Asst. engr., 1st Oct., 1916; asst. exec. engr., 18th Jan., 1921; exec. engr. (prov'l), Jan., 1936.

HAY, BROUGHTON, B.A., Indian C.S. (asst. mag. and collr., B. and O.) (b. 2nd Nov., 1914).—Educ. at Radley and New College, Oxford; joined the service, 21st Sept., 1937; arrived in India, Nov., 1937, and served in B. and O. as asst. mag. and collr.

HAY, NORMAN TREVOR CARLTON, B.E., Indian Service of Engrs.—Joined the service as asst. exec. engr., Burma, 8th Feb., 1931.

HAY, ROBERT, M.B., Lt.-Col., I.M.S. (civil surg.) (b. 8th Mar., 1889).—First commn. dated 23rd Jan., 1917; in milty. employ to 25th June, 1925; offg. agency surg., Bundelkhand, July, 1925; offg. medl. officer, Seistan consulate, Feb., 1926; agency surg., D. I. Khan, Nov., 1928; ditto, Kurram, April, 1930; resdy. surg., Kashmir, Apl., 1931; agency surgeon, Bundelkhand, Dec., 1931; civil surg., Quetta, Nov., 1933; ch. medl. officer, Baluchistan, Mar., 1935; resdy. surg., Mewar, Sept., 1935; ch. medl. officer, Rajputana, Dec., 1935.

HAY, WILLIAM RUPERT, C.I.E., Major, I.A., Indian Political Service (b. 16th Dec., 1893).—First commn. dated 3rd July, 1915; joined pol. dept., 25th May, 1920; asst. pol. agent, Chitral, August, 1921; asst. commr., Bannu, Dec., 1923; ditto, Tonk, March, 1924; pol. agent, S. Waziristan, Nov., 1925; asst. commr., Mardan, Dec., 1928; jt. dep. commr., Peshawar, Aug., 1930; spl. magte. in D.I. Khan dist., Aug., 1931; on special duty, Dokalim Boundary commn., June, 1932; pol. agent, Dir, Swat and Chitral, July, 1932; and again, Jan., 1933; offg. ch. secty. to govt., N.W.F.P., Nov., 1932; C.I.E., Jan., 1934; counsellor, Brit. legn., Kabul, Nov., 1934; offg. dep. secty., F. and P. dept., Govt. of India, Oct., 1936; confd., as dep. secty., External Affairs Dept., Apl., 1937.

HAYAT, SIR UMAR (see Umar Hayat Khan).

HAYDEN, ARTHUR BARTRUM, Lt., R.E., Indian Service of Engrs. (exec. engr., Burma) (b. 16th Nov., 1901).—First commn. dated 13th July, 1921; asst. exec. engr., Nov., 1925; exec. engr., Nov., 1933.

HAYES, ARTHUR ANDERSON, Indian Police (b. 26th July, 1894).—Joined the service as asst. dist. supt., Burma, on 26th Jan., 1920; offg. dist. supt., Nov., 1925; confd., May, 1926.

HAYES, CYRIL BEAUCHAMP, M.C., Indian State Rlys. (exec. engr., Burma rly.) (b. 30th Apl., 1894).—Joined the service as asst. engr., 12th May, 1920; exec. engr., Burma rly., June, 1929; offg. supt. of stores, Feb., 1937.

HAYES, SIDNEY NUTTALL, F.R.C.S. (E.), Lt.-Col., I.M.S. (prof'r., King Edward medl coll., Lahore, Punjab) (b. 19th July, 1891).—First commn. dated 30th July, 1921; in milty. employ to 1st Oct., 1922; civil surg., Oct., 1923; offg. prof'r. of midwifery, King Edward Medical Coll., Lahore, May, 1925; Lieut.-Col., Sept., 1937.

HAYFIELD, WILLIAM MONTAGUE, Indian Service of Engrs. (exec. engr., Burma) (b. 16th Feb., 1883).—Joined the service, 12th Oct., 1904; asst. engr., Oct., 1905; exec. engr., Dec., 1913; offg. suptg. engr., June, 1928.

HAYMAN, ALBERT MELVILLE, O.B.E., late Indian Finance Dept. (b. 21st Aug., 1882).—Joined the service, 20th March, 1900; dep. acctnt.-gen., April, 1919; O.B.E., Jan., 1920; services placed at the disposal of railway bd. from 4th July, 1923; dep. dir., finance, rly. bd., Oct., 1924; dir. of finance, rly. bd., Aug., 1925; member, rly. bd., April, 1929; ret'd., Jan., 1933.

HAYNE, PERCY ALFRED, M.R.C.S., L.R.C.P., late Indian State Rlys. (b. 2nd July, 1876).—Joined the service as dist. medl. officer, 25th Oct., 1906; ch. medl. officer, Apl., 1927; ret'd., May, 1933.

HAYWARD, SIR MAURICE HENRY WESTON, Kt., K.C.S.I., LL.B., Barr., late Indian C.S.—Educ. at Harrow and St. John's Coll., Cambridge; apptd. after exam. of 1886; arrived, 19th Nov., 1889, and served in Bombay as asst. collr. and mag.; under sec. to govt. of Bombay, pol., &c., depts., Aug., 1895; jud. asst. to pol. agent, Kathiawar, Sept., 1896; acting dist. judge, Karachi, Nov., 1899; confd., June, 1900; legal remembrancer, Bombay, Nov., 1905, and sec.

to govt. legal dept., July, 1907; extra addl. jud. commr. of Sind, March-April, 1908; dist. judge, Jan., 1909; addl. judl. commr., Sinda, Aug., 1910; judge, high ct., Bombay, May, 1918; member of govt.'s exec. council, Jan., 1921; Kt., June, 1923; K.C.S.I., June, 1925; retd., July, 1926.

HAYWARD, REV. WILLIAM GEORGE, B.A., Indian Eccles. Estab. (senr. chaplain) (b. 18th June, 1888).—Apptd. junr. chaplain, Madras, 5th March, 1926; senr. chaplain, Mar., 1936.

HAYWOOD, WILLIAM GERALD, Indian Police (asst. supt., United Provs.) (b. 5th Sept., 1908).—Joined the service as proby. asst. supt., 25th Nov., 1929; confid., Nov., 1931.

HEAD, EDGAR ALFRED, Office of High Commr. for India (higher clerical officer, store dept.) (b. 7th April, 1876).—Educ. at King Edward's Gr. Sch., Bath; clerk, 2nd class, 30th May, 1895; transfid. to office of high commr. for India, Oct., 1920; higher clerical officer, April, 1922.

HEAD, WILLIAM RALEIGH, late Burma Frontier Service (b. 13th June, 1880).—Joined the service as inspr. of police, Jan., 1901; extra asst. commr., Feb., 1906; asst. supt., B.F.S., Dec., 1922; offg. dep. commr., March, 1925; confid., Aug., 1925; retd., Oct., 1934.

HEADLEY, ROBERT HOLLOWELL, O.B.E., LL.B., late India Office (b. 9th Mar., 1876).—Educ. at King's Coll. Sch., and King's Coll., London; clerk, 2nd class, acctnt.-gen.'s dept., 9th Mar., 1896; LL.B. (honours), London Univ., 1901; transfid. to mil. dept., Nov., 1901; called to the bar (Middle Temple), 1903; apptd. member standing advisory cttee. on colours and distinctions, July, 1911; apptd. member, Navy and Army Ins. Fund Advisory Cttee., April, 1913; minor staff clerk, May, 1913; sec. to ordnance services cttee., 1914; resdt. clerk (temp.), Feb., 1915, to Aug., 1919; staff clerk (temp.), April, 1915; M.B.E., Jan., 1918; asst. to mil. sec. and to the staff offr., mil. dept., July, 1919; suptg. exec. officer, April, 1922; chief exec. officer, June, 1924; O.B.E., June, 1930; retd., Nov., 1937.

HEALD, SIR BENJAMIN HERBERT, Kt., V.D., M.A., late Indian C.S. (b. 16th Sept., 1874).—Educ. at Nottingham High Sch. and Queen's Coll., Oxford; apptd. after exam. of 1897; arrived, 3rd Dec., 1898, and served in Burma as asst. commr. and asst. settlmt. offr.; dist. judge, Dec., 1908; addl. judge, ct. of judl. commr., March, 1917; divl. judge, Nov., 1917; judge, chief ct., Lower Burma, June, 1921; judge, high ct., Rangoon, Dec., 1922; Kt., Jan., 1926; retd., Nov., 1933.

HEALE, ROBERT JOHN WINGFIELD, C.I.E., O.B.E., B.A., Lt.-Col., I.A. (retd.) (b. 24th Sept., 1876).—First commn., dated 23rd May, 1900; in mil. emp. to Jan., 1903; asst. commr., Punjab, Jan., 1903; pol. asst. N.W.F. Prov., Jan., 1904; asst. commr. and commtdt., B.M.P., Bannu, Oct., 1911; ditto, Peshawar, Nov., 1912; asst. commr., Charsadda, April, 1914; ditto, Thal sub-div., April, 1915; dep. commr., Kohat, Nov., 1915; ditto, D.I. Khan, Dec., 1915; on spl. duty as pol. offr. with Waziristan fd. force from May to July, 1917; pol. agent, Kurram, Oct., 1917; O.B.E., Dec., 1917;

commr., Ajmer-Merwara, March, 1925; offg. resident, Gwalior, March, 1928; offg. ch. commr. and agent to govt. genl. N.W.F. Prov., May, 1929; C.I.E., Jan., 1930; agent to govt. genl. in C.I., June, 1930; retd., Jan., 1932.

HEALY, DANIEL, C.I.E., Indian Police (inspr. genl., Sind) (b. 22nd Aug., 1884).—Joined the service, 19th Nov., 1904; supt. of police, Mar., 1914; awarded King's police medal, Jan., 1923; actg. commr. of police, July, 1923; offg. dep. inspr.-gen. of police, April, 1926; again, Nov., 1930; confid., May, 1931; offg. commr. of police, Bombay, March, 1927; again, Apr., 1930; offg. inspr.-genl., June, 1934; inspr.-genl., Sind, Apr., 1936; C.I.E., May, 1937.

HEALY, ROWLAND HENRY SAVAGE, B.A., Indian C.S. (asst. commr., Burma) (b. 13th Nov., 1905).—Educ. at Earlsfort House School, Dublin, and Trinity Coll., Dublin; joined the service, 19th Oct., 1929; arrived, Dec., 1929, and served in Burma as asst. commr.; dep. regr., high ct., Rangoon, Aug., 1932; asst. dist. judge, Nov., 1934; offg. dist. and sess. judge, Mar., 1936.

HEANEY, GEORGE FREDERICK, Capt., R.E. Survey of India (supt.) (b. 1st June, 1897).—First commn., dated 26th May, 1916; in milty. employ to 10th Aug., 1921; asst. supt., Aug., 1921; offg. supt. Mar., 1929; confid., Aug., 1929.

HEAPE, ROBERT H., A.M.I.C.E., Indian State Rlys. (exec. engr.) (b. 8th Aug., 1894).—Joined the service as resdt. engr., G.I.P. rly. 30th June, 1921; exec. engr., Apr., 1935.

HEARD, EDWARD SEPTIMUS, B.A., late Indian Service of Engrs. (Punjab) (b. 10th Sept., 1878).—Joined the service, 5th April, 1903; asst. engr., June, 1908; exec. engr., June, 1915; retd., Dec., 1929.

HEARN, GEORGE WILLIAM, India Office (suptg. executive officer, acctnt.-gen.'s dept.) (b. 23rd June, 1889).—Educ. at Central Foundation Sch., London; apptd. clerk, 2nd class, 4th Dec., 1907; minor staff clerk, Jan., 1920; acting staff clerk, April, 1921; higher exec. officer (acting), April, 1922; confid., Nov., 1922; also assessor of income tax; suptg. exec. officer, Sept., 1935.

HEARN, SIR GORDON RISLEY, Kt., C.I.E., D.S.O., A.M.I.C.E., Lt.-Col., R.E., late Railway Dept.—Apptd. asst. engr., N.W. Rly., 19th June, 1894; dep. consg. engr. for rlys., Madras, Oct., 1901, to Nov., 1902; exec. engr., Jan., 1903; suptg. engr., May, 1918; on mil. duty from Oct., 1914, to Dec., 1918; chief engr., March, 1924; C.I.E., June, 1924; Kt., July, 1926; retd., Sept., 1926.

HEARN, JOHN WHITCOMBE, B.A., LL.B., Indian C.S. (dep. commr., Punjab) (b. 27th Nov., 1885).—Educ. at Blundell's Sch., Tiverton Sidney Sussex Coll., Cambridge, and University Coll., London; apptd. after exam. of 1909; arrived in India, 28th Nov., 1910, and served in the Punjab as asst. commr.; under-sec. to govt. of India, rev. and agric. dept., Oct., 1920; dep. commr. (provl.), Oct., 1924; ditto (perm.), Oct., 1927; home secty. to govt., Punjab, Oct., 1930; offg. commr., Feb., 1936; addl. secty. to govt., Punjab, Feb., 1936.

HEATH, CLEMENT JOHN, Indian Police (dist. supt., Burma) (b. 10th June, 1890).—Joined the service as asst. supt., 5th Dec., 1910; dist. supt., Dec., 1917; dep. commr. of police, Rangoon, Nov., 1927; offg. dep. inspr.-genl., Jan., 1936.

HEATHCOTE, LAWRENCE HECTOR MARK, late Indian Service of Engrs. (b. 30th Oct., 1881).—Trained at the R.I.E. Coll.; apptd. asst. engr., Punjab, 1st Oct., 1904; exec. engr., Oct., 1922; offg. suptg. engr., Feb., 1930; and again, June, 1933; ret'd., Sept., 1936.

HECTOR, GEORGE PITENDRIGH, M.A., D.Sc., late Indian Agric. Service (b. 20th Oct., 1881).—Joined the service, 14th Jan., 1908, as supy. botanist; apptd. economic botanist on probn., July, 1909; confd., Jan., 1910; offg. dir. of agric., Bengal, July, 1925; offg. dir. of agric., Feb., 1929; confd., Aug., 1932; ret'd., Oct., 1935.

HECTOR, JOHN ALEXANDER ERNEST, B.Sc., Indian Service of Engrs. (exec. engr., Burma) (b. 11th Feb., 1901).—Joined the service on 1st Oct., 1923; exec. engr., Oct., 1932; ditto, temp. aviation divn., central p.w.d., Rangoon, Feb., 1935.

HEDDON, HAROLD, Office of High Commr. for India (higher clerical officer, store dept.) (b. 13th Oct., 1882).—Apptd. 2nd class clerk, registry and record dept., 16th Dec., 1902; transf. to store dept., Jan., 1917; transf. to office of high commr. for India, Oct., 1920; higher clerical officer, April, 1922.

HEENAN, JOHN LAWDER, Indian Police (b. 21st Apl., 1896) (dist. supt., Burma).—Joined the service as asst. dist. supt., on 7th Feb., 1921; offg. dist. supt., July, 1924; confd., May, 1929.

HEINIG, JOSEPH ERIS, B.Sc., Indian State Rlys. (asst. exec. engr., N.W. Rly.) (b. 27th Jan., 1892).—Joined the service as asst. exec. engr., 1st Oct., 1919; offg. exec. engr., Oct., 1925.

HEJMADI, VASUDEO SUBRAO, Indian C.S. (sub-collr., Madras) (b. 26th Jan., 1900).—Educ. at St. Xavier's High School, Bombay, and Caius Coll., Camb.; apptd. after exam. of 1923; arrived, 3rd Dec., 1923, and served in Madras as asst. collr. and mag.; sub-collr., Jan., 1925; offg. collr. and dist. magte., Feb., 1931.

HELLIWELL, JAMES WILFRED, late Indian State Rlys. (b. 25th Aug., 1881).—Joined the service, 13th Jan., 1909; asst. loco. supt., 1st Jan., 1915; on mil. duty from 24th Oct., 1915; asst. works manager, Mar., 1927; offg. works manager, Apl., 1927; confd., Oct., 1927; ret'd., May, 1933.

HELME, CHARLES GEORGE GORDON, M.A., late Indian C.S. (b. 19th March, 1885).—Educ. at Paisley Gr. Sch., Glasgow Univ., and Christ Church Coll., Oxford; apptd. after exam. of 1908; arrived, 9th Dec., 1909, and served in Eastern Bengal and Assam as asst. mag. and collr.; asst. commr., Aug., 1911; ditto, Assam, April, 1912; on mil. duty (I.A.R.O.), Aug., 1918, to Feb., 1919; offg. dep. commr., April, 1919; dep. commr. (provl.), March, 1926; ret'd., Feb., 1934.

HEMEON, CLARENCE REID, Indian C.S. (dist. and sess. judge, Central Provs.) (b. 30th Jan., 1897).—Educ. at Cork Gr. Sch., and Univ. Coll., Cork; apptd. by nomination; joined the service, 1st Nov., 1921; arrived, 12th April, 1922, and served in Central Provs. as asst. commr.; sub-judge, June, 1925; offg. dist. and sess. judge, March, 1927; confd., Jan., 1935; offg. legal membr. and sec. legal dept., Central Provs., June, 1932; confd., Jan., 1935.

HEMINGWAY, CHARLES ERIC MONTGOMERIE, B.A., India Office (asst. princpl., milty. dept.) (b. 20th Dec., 1912).—Educ. at Winchester and New Coll., Oxford; apptd. asst. princpl., 26th Oct., 1936.

HEMMADI, RAMRAO ANNAPA, Rai Bahadur, late Indian Forest Service.—Held non-gazetted appts. from 1st Oct., 1894, to 20th Feb., 1909; extra asst. consvr., Feb., 1909; dep. consvr., Sept., 1922; ret'd., May, 1928.

HEMMING, Rev. Canon CHARLES HENRY, late Indian Eccles. Estabt. (Punjab) (b. 27th July, 1875).—Apptd. junior chaplain on 21st Dec., 1912; on milty. duty from 20th June, 1916, to 22nd Aug., 1917; senr. chaplain, Dec., 1922; ret'd. Feb., 1931.

HEMSWORTH, THOMAS GARRETT FREDERICK, B.A., Indian Service of Engrs. (exec. engr., Burma) (b. 2nd Aug., 1890).—Joined the service as asst. exec. engr. on 1st Oct., 1920; exec. engr., Oct., 1925.

HENDERSON, ALAN GERALD RUSSELL, B.A., Indian C.S. (judge, high ct., Calcutta) (b. 22nd March, 1886).—Educ. at Westminster Sch., and Christ Church, Oxford; apptd. after exam. of 1909; arrived in India, 27th Nov., 1910, and served in E. Bengal and Assam as asst. mag. and collr.; transf. to Bengal, April, 1912; jt. mag. and dep. collr., Dec., 1915; I.A.R.O., Jan. to March, 1918; dist. and sessions judge, March, 1923; offg. secty., judicial dept., and supt. and membr. of legal affairs, Apl., 1932; confd., July, 1932; offg. addl. judge, high ct., Calcutta, variously from Apl., 1933; actg. judge, ditto, Apl., 1934; confd., Mar., 1937.

HENDERSON, CHARLES ALEXANDER, C.S.I., V.D., Indian C.S. (collr. and dist. mag., Madras) (b. 2nd May, 1882).—Educ. at Haileybury, and Exeter Coll., Oxford; apptd. after exam. of 1904; arrived, 11th Dec., 1905, and served in Madras as asst. collr. and mag. and spl. settl. offr.; sub-collr. and jt. mag., Sept., 1914; dir. of land records, June, 1916; on foreign service as Diwan, Jeypore Estate, April, 1920; offg. collr. and dist. mag., Oct., 1921; sub-collr., Aug., 1922; offg. collr. and dist. mag., Apl., 1926; offg. secty. to govt., dev. dept., Madras, May, 1933; offg. second secty. to govt., Madras, Jan., 1935; and again Mar., 1936; offg. 2nd member, bd. of rev., May, 1936; 3rd member, ditto, Mar., 1937; C.S.I., Jan., 1938.

HENDERSON, CHARLES JOSEPH, C.E. (Glas.), A.M.I.C.E., A.I.E.E., late Indian Educl. Service (b. 3rd July, 1878).—Joined the service as hd. master, sch. of engrg., Dacca, 11th Mar., 1910; prin., Dacca engrg. sch., Dec., 1923; ret'd., May, 1933.

HENDERSON, GEOFFRY STEELE, N.D.A., N.D.D., late Impl. Dept. of Agric. (b. 23rd Apl., 1882).—Joined the service, 18th Jan., 1907, as asst. to the dep. dir. of agric., Bombay; 2nd dep. dir. of agric., July, 1907; dep. dir. of agric., Feb.,

1909; offg. impl. agriculturist, agricl. research inst., Pusa, June, 1916; confirmed, March, 1918; on deputn. as controller (agricl. requirements, Mesopotamia), under Indian munitions bd., July, 1918, to June, 1919; dir. of agric., Bihar and Orissa, Nov., 1929; ret'd., July, 1934.

HENDERSON, JOHN WILMOT, Indian State Ry.s. (divl. transptn. officer, N.W. rly.) (b. 11th July, 1893).—Joined the service, 4th June, 1912; asst. transptn. traffic officer, Oct., 1924; offg. divl. personnel officer, N.W. rly., Mar., 1930; divl. transptn. officer, Mar., 1931.

HENDERSON, KENNETH HARVEY, Indian C.S. (asst. commr., Punjab) (b. 24th Dec., 1905).—Educ. at Mill Hill, New College, Oxford, and King's College, London; joined the service, 15th Oct., 1930; arrived in India, Nov., 1930, and served in the Punjab as asst. commr.; pol. asst. and comdt. border milty. police, June, 1933; offg. dep. commr., Mar., 1935.

HENDRY, DAVID, M.C., B.Sc., late Indian Agricl. Service (Burma) (b. 7th Jan., 1894).—Joined the service as dep. dir. of agric., on 19th Feb., 1921; ret'd., Nov. 1929.

HENNESSY, JOSEPH MARTIN REEVES L.R.C.P. & S. (Edin.), D.T.M. & H. (Lond.), Lt.-Col., I.M.S. Medical Dept., Central Provs. (civil surgeon) (b. 7th May, 1886).—First commn. 31st Jan., 1914; civil surgeon, Jubbulpore., Sept., 1931; offg. in pr.-genl. of civil hosps., C.P., May, 1935; and again, Feb., 1937.

HENNESSY, JOSSLEYN (dir. of Public Information, Home Dept., Govt. of India) (b. 17th Nov., 1902).—Educ. at Charterhouse and New College, Oxford; apptd. dir. of public information, Sept., 1937.

HENNIKER-GOTLEY, GEORGE RAINALD, B.A., D.S.O., Indian Forest Service (dep. consvr., Punjab) (b. 5th Sept., 1893).—Joined the service as asst. consvr., Punjab, 18th Dec., 1922; dep. consvr., Aug., 1926.

HENRY, COLIN, B.A., Indian C.S. (dist. and sess. judge, Punjab) (b. 23rd Feb., 1902).—Educ. at King Edward VII School, Sheffield, and Wadham College, Oxford; joined the service, 23rd Oct., 1926; arrived, Dec., 1926, and served in the Punjab as asst. commr.; offg. dist. and sess. judge, variously from June, 1931; confd., Nov., 1936.

HENSHAW, LEWIS, A.M.I.Mech.E., A.M.I.E.E., A.M.I.Struct.E., M.I.E. (Ind.) (supgt. engr., Electricity Dept., Madras) (b. 2nd May, 1888).—Joined the service as elec. inspr. to govt., on 8th Nov., 1920; elec. engr., Mar., 1921; confd., Apr., 1924; supgt. engr., offg., Aug., 1935; confd., Aug., 1937.

HERAPATH, WILLIAM LANE KYNASTON, Indian Police (suplt., Bombay) (b. 24th Nov., 1891).—Joined the service 15th Nov., 1912, as asst. suplt.; suplt. of police, Oct., 1924; awarded King's police medal, Jan., 1928; asst. to inspr.-genl., Nov., 1937.

HERBERT, CHARLES GORDON, B.A., Indian C.S. (secty. to A.G.G., in Rajputana) (b. 28th Feb., 1893).—Educ. at Merchant Taylors' Sch. and Wadham Coll., Oxford; joined the service, 14th Feb., 1920; arrived, 11th May, 1920, and served in Madras as asst. collr. and mag.; subcollr. and jt. mag., May, 1923; offg. collr. and dist. magte., March, 1928; ditto (provl. subs.), Jan., 1935; secty. to A.G.G., in Rajputana, Mar., 1936.

HERBERT, CHRISTOPHER ALFRED, B.A., Indian C.S. (asst. mag. and collr., U.P.) (b. 15th June, 1913).—Educ. at Mountjoy School, Dublin, and Trinity Coll., Dublin; joined the service, 22nd Sept., 1937; arrived in India, Nov., 1937, and served in the U.P. as asst. mag. and collr.

HERBERT, VAUGHAN AUGUSTUS, M.A., M.C., Indian Forest Service (consr., United Provs.) (b. 13th Sept., 1883).—Joined the service, 17th Dec., 1909, as asst. consr.; on mil. service from June, 1915, to June, 1919; dep. consr., June, 1919; offg. consvr., March, 1927; confd., June 1930.

HERCHENRODER, MARIE FERDINAND PHILIPPE, LL.B., Barr., late Indian C.S. (United Provs.) (b. 1st Jan., 1893).—Educ. at Royal Coll., Curepipe, Mauritius, and Christ Church, Oxford; apptd. after exam. of 1915; arrived, 4th Dec., 1916, and served in the United Prov. as asst. mag. and collr. and asst. commr.; asst. suplt., Dehra Dun, Aug., 1918; jt. mag., Nov., 1920; temp. addl. dep. sec. to govt., judl. dept., March, 1926; offg. dist. and sess. judge, Aug., 1926; confd., Oct. 1929; ret'd., July, 1931.

HERDON, GERALD CHARLES ROUTH, Indian Police (suplt., N.W.F.P.) (b. 30th July, 1897).—Joined the service as asst. suplt. on 27th Dec., 1919; offg. suplt., Mar., 1925; confd., May, 1926; awarded King's police medal, Jan., 1928.

HERON, ALEXANDER MACMILLAN, D.Sc., F.G.S., Geological Survey of India (dir.) (b. 31st July, 1884).—Joined the service as asst. suplt., 27th Dec., 1906; offg. suplt., Jan., 1920; suplt., Sept., 1929; offg. dir., Apr., 1932, Oct., 1933, and Sept., 1935; confd., Nov., 1935.

HERON, DAVIS, C.I.E., M.B., Lt.-Col. I.M.S. (ret'd.) (b. 8th Jan., 1878).—First commndated 31st Jan., 1903; in milty. employ. to 25th Oct., 1912; M.O., Seistan Consulate, Dec., 1912; C.I.E., June, 1918; resdy. surg., Mewar, Jan., 1927; ret'd., Aug., 1930.

HERON, FRANCIS ADENS THEODORE, O.B.E., late Indian Police Service (b. 14th June, 1890).—Joined the service 26th Nov., 1910; asst. suplt., May, 1912; O.B.E., April, 1921; suplt., April, 1922; ret'd., July, 1932.

HERVEY, ARTHUR CHARLES CONSTANTINE, M.A. (Cantab.), Indian Educl. Service (prinpl., Ludhiana Coll., Punjab) (b. 18th Dec., 1886).—Joined the service as prof., Khalsa Coll., Amritsar, on 7th Oct., 1918; prinpl., Ludhiana Coll., 26th Sept., 1925.

HESTERLOW, ALFRED MAURICE VICTOR, M.B., Lt.-Col., I.M.S. (asst. dir. of public health, Madras) (b. 4th May, 1887).—First commndated 1st Nov., 1920; in milty. employ. until 2nd Feb., 1924; asst. dir. of public health, Madras, Feb., 1924; prof., medl. coll., Madras, May, 1926; asst. dir. of public health, Madras, Aug., 1931; offg. dir., Mar., 1934; ditto, Bombay, Apr., 1935.

HEWETSON, CYRIL EDWARD, B.A., B.Sc., Indian Forest Service (dep. consvr. Cent. Provs.) (b. 18th June, 1903).—Joined the service as asst. consvr., 25th Dec., 1926; dep. consvr., Dec., 1930.

HEWETT, DECIMUS HENRY, Indian State Rly. (works manager, G.I.P. rly.) (b. 30th Jan., 1893).—Joined the service 12th Mar., 1926, as production engr., G.I.P. rly., Mar., 1926; offg. works manager, G.I.P. rly., May, 1928; confd., Oct., 1932.

HEWETT, DOUGLAS PEARSE, late Indian Forest Service (b. 29th Sept., 1885).—Arrived, 11th Nov., 1907, and served as asst. consr.; dep. consr., Dec., 1912; on mil. service, Nov., 1917, to Feb., 1919; offg. consvr., Aug. 1924; confd., Dec., 1926; ret'd., Sept., 1933.

HEWETT, Sir JOHN PRESCOTT, G.C.S.I., K.B.E., C.I.E., late Indian C.S.—Educ. at Winchester, and Balliol Coll., Oxford; apptd. after exam. of 1875; arrived, 17th Dec., 1877, and served in the N.W. Prov. and Oudh as asst. mag. and collr., and asst. commr.; in charge of provl. gazetteer, Aug., 1883; acted as asst. acctnt.-gen., Jan., 1884; junr. sec., board of rev., April, 1884; offg. under sec. to govt. of India, home dept., Oct., 1886; confd., Aug., 1887; offd. as private sec. to the viceroy in 1888 and 1892, and as sec. to govt. of India, home dept., March, 1890; offg. census commr. for India, July, 1890; dep. sec. to govt. of India, home dept., Dec., 1890; C.I.E., May, 1891; dep. commr., March, 1893; sec. of royal commn. on opium, Oct., 1893; mag. and collr., April, 1894; sec. to govt. of India, home dept., Dec., 1895; C.S.I., Dec., 1898; member of plague commn., Nov., 1898; offg. chief commr., Central Provs., Sept., 1902; confd., Nov., 1904; temp. member of govr.-gen.'s council, Aug. 1904; confd., Dec., 1904; lieut.-govr. of United Prov. (K.C.S.I.), Jan., 1907 to July, 1912; special duty in connection with Coronation Durbar from March, 1911; G.C.S.I., Dec., 1911; author of contributions to provl. gazetteer; knight of justice and bailiff of Egle of St. John of Jerusalem; ret'd., Sept., 1912; K.B.E., Dec., 1917; M.P. for Luton Divn., Nov., 1922, to Dec., 1923.

HEWITT, REV. GEORGE, late Indian Eccl. Estabt. (b. 22nd Nov., 1875).—Joined, 27th June, 1908; jun. chaplain, June, 1911; senr. chaplain, June, 1921; ret'd., Nov., 1931.

HEWITT, JOHN ARCHIBALD, Indian Service of Engineers (exec. engr., Burma) (b. 23rd Dec., 1898).—Joined the service, 20th Nov., 1921; asst. exec. engr., Nov., 1922; exec. engr., Nov., 1931.

HEWITT, RICHARD CLAUDE LIFFORD, B.A., Indian Service of Engineers (exec. engr., Bihar) (b. 10th Aug., 1889).—Joined the service as asst. engr., Oct., 1912; exec. engr., Oct., 1921; offg. suptg. engr., May, 1934, and again, Sept., 1936.

HEWLETT, GEORGE GORDON, India Office (higher exec. officer, acctnt.-gens'. dept.) (b. 6th Aug., 1894).—Apptd. second class clerk, India Office, 7th Jan., 1913, and att'd. to library; transf'd. to acctnt.-gensl's. dept., Mar., 1913; on mil. service from Dec., 1915, to Jan., 1919; exec. officer, April, 1922; asst. clk. to council, Feb., 1932 to Feb., 1935; act. hr exec. officer, Feb., 1934; confd., Dec., 1934.

HEY, NORMAN HEATHER, B.A., late Indian C.S.—Educ. at Hulme Grammar School, Manchester, Victoria Univ., Balliol Coll., Oxford, and University Coll., London; apptd. after exam., 1905; arrived, 10th Dec., 1906, and

served in Sind and Bombay as asst. collr. and mag.; served in fin. dept., govt. of India, July to Nov., 1909; asst. settl. offr., Bombay, Oct., 1914; on spl. duty as recruiting offr., July, 1918; rev. officer, Lloyd Barrage Project, Dec., 1923; offg. collr., dist. mag. and pol. agent, Jan., 1925; ret'd., April, 1928.

HEYCOCK, WILLIAM BISSILL, B.A., late Indian C.S. (Bihar and Orissa) (b. 26th April, 1873).—Educ. at Oakham Sch., and Sidney Sussex Coll., Cambridge; apptd. after exam. of 1895; arrived, 7th Dec., 1896, and served in Bengal as asst. mag. and collr.; joint mag. and dep. collr., Dec., 1903; mag. and collr., Nov., 1909; dir. of agric., April, 1911; transf'd. to Bihar and Orissa, as mag. and collr., and dir. of agric., April, 1912; mag. and collr., March, 1914; ditto and dep. commr., June, 1919; offg. commr., Jan., 1920; commr., Oct., 1923; ret'd., Dec., 1931.

HEYSHAM, ERIC HENRY BERKELEY, Indian State rlys. (transptn. supt.) (b. 2nd Mar., 1896).—Joined the service, 28th Nov., 1919 as asst. supt. E.I. rly., transptn. supt. (traffic comml.), May, 1935.

HEZLETT, JAMES, C.I.E., B.A., late Indian C.S. (b. 26th June, 1875).—Educ. at Coleman Academy, Queen's Coll., Galway, and Univ. Coll., London; apptd. after exam of 1898; arrived, 20th Nov., 1899, and served in Bengal as asst. mag. and collr.; transf'd. to E. Bengal and Assam, May, 1906; joint mag. and dep. coll., Sept., 1906; dep. commr., Assam, April, 1912; supt. Lushai Hills, Oct., 1912, to April, 1917; dep. commr., Sept., 1914; commr. of excise, April, 1921, to July, 1922; offg. commr., April, 1923, and again, May, 1926; confd., Oct., 1928. C.I.E., Jan., 1928; representative of govt. of Assam in legisl. assembly, Nov., 1932; ret'd., Nov., 1934; chairman, pub. serv. commn., Assam, Aprl., 1937.

HIBBERT, RICHARD OSWALD, B.A., Indian C.S. (asst. commr., Punjab) (b. 16th Dec., 1914).—Educ. at Shrewsbury and St. John's College, Cambridge; joined the service, 14th Sept., 1937; arrived in India, Nov., 1937, and served in the Punjab as asst. commr.

HICKEY, PATRICK FRANCIS BURKE, D.S.O., Indian Service of Engineers (suptg. engr., United Provs.) (b. 4th Dec., 1885).—Joined the service as asst. engr. on 1st Oct., 1909; in mil. employ from 18th June, 1915, to 5th May, 1919; exec. engr., Oct., 1917; offg. suptg. engr., Nov., 1930; confd., Nov., 1931; offg. ch. engr., variously from Mar., 1935.

HICKINBOTHAM, TOM, Capt., I.A., Indian Political Service (b. 27th Aprl., 1903).—First commn., 30th Aug., 1923; in military employ until 15th Mar., 1930; offg. under secty. to A.G.G., Punjab States, Dec., 1932; civil secty. to ch. commr., Aden, Dec., 1933; asst. pol. agent, Bahrain, Apr., 1936; offg. pol. agent, Bahrain, May, 1936; secty. to pol. resdt. in Persian Gulf, Nov., 1937.

HICKS, GEORGE AUGUSTUS, V.D., late Indian State Rlys. (b. 10th Dec., 1879).—Joined the service as asst. engr., 3rd Septl., 1903; dist. engr., April, 1913; dep. ch. engr., Mar., 1919; ch. engr., April, 1921; offg. agent, Burma rlys., May, 1933; ret'd., Dec., 1936.

HICKS, HENRY ARTHUR HUGH GERARD, Indian Forest Service (dep. consvr., Madras) (b. 31st Oct., 1898).—Joined the service as asst. consvr., on 11th Dec., 1922; dep. consvr., Nov., 1926.

HICKS, VERNON WILLIAM FARQUHARSON, O.B.E., Indian Police (supt., Bengal) (b. 30th Jan., 1897).—Joined the service as asst. supt., on 27th Dec., 1919; addl. supt., March, 1926; supt., Aug., 1929; C.O.E., Jan., 1935.

HIDAYATULLAH, KHAN BAHADUR SHAIKH, Sir GHULAM HUSAIN, K.C.S.I., B.A., LL.B.—Member of govt.'s exec. council, Bombay, June, 1928, to June, 1934; delegate to Indian round table conf., London, Nov., 1930—Jan., 1931; Second Session, Sept.-Dec., 1931; services placed at disposal of Govt. of India, Reforms Office, Oct., 1931; K.C.S.I., June, 1933.

HIDE, PERCY, C.I.E., M.A., late Indian Educ. Service (b. 12th April, 1874).—Asst. master (temp.), Mayo Coll., 17th July, 1904; vice-principal, Rajkumar Coll., Rajkote, Nov., 1904; principal, Daly Coll., Indore, July, 1906, on deputation to training school for Indian cadets, from Sept., 1918; C.I.E., Jan., 1926; retd., April, 1929.

HIGGINS, JOHN COMYN, C.I.E., Indian C.S. commr., Assam (b. 21st May, 1882).—Educ. at Bradfield and Brasenose Coll., Oxford; apptd. after exam. of 1905; arrived, 22nd Dec., 1906, and served in E. Bengal and Assam as asst. mag., and collr.; vice-presdnt., Manipur Durbar, May, 1910; asst. commr., Assam, and ditto, April, 1912; C.I.E., June, 1920; dep. commr., March, 1921; pol. agent, Manipur, April, 1924; offg. commr., Mar., 1934; ditto (provl. subs.), Feb., 1935; confd., Oct., 1935.

HIGGINS, RICHARD, Indian Police (supt., Bengal) (b. 19th April, 1898).—Joined the service as asst. supt. on 15th March, 1921; addl. supt., Feb., 1925, and again March, 1927; offg. supt., March, 1926; offg. dep. comm. of police, Calcutta, March, 1928; confd., Jan., 1931.

HIGGS, MERVYN ARMSTRONG, M.B.E., M.A., Indian Service of Engineers (b. 8th Oct., 1887).—Joined the service, 20th Sept., 1910; temp. engr., Sept. 1911; asst. engr., July, 1915; asst. exec. engr., Oct., 1921; exec. engr., July, 1924; M.B.E., Jan., 1935.

HIGHAM, BERNARD, C.I.E., M.B., M.R.C.S. (Eng.), L.R.C.P. (Lond.), Lt.-Col., I.M.S., retd. (b. 14th Dec., 1880).—First commn., dated 1st Feb., 1906; served in mil. dept. until Jan., 1909, when apptd. prob. in chemical examr.'s dept., Bombay; chemical analyser for Sind and health officer of the port of Karachi, June, 1912; on mil. duty from Nov., 1914; to Dec., 1920; chemical analyser to govt. of Bombay, Dec., 1920; C.I.E., June, 1935; retd., Dec., 1935.

HIGHAM, Rev. PHILIP, M.A., Indian Eccles. Estabt. (senr. chaplain) (b. 21st Sept., 1888).—Joined the service, 1st Dec., 1919; junr. chaplain, Nov., 1921; senr. chaplain, Nov., 1931.

HIGHET, JAMES COCHRANE, F.C.H., A.M.I.C.E., Indian State Railways (agent, N.W. Rly.) (b. 19th May, 1884).—Joined the service, 1st Oct., 1905, as asst. engr.; exec.

engr., Oct., 1913; asst. sec., rly. bd., June, 1915; offg. junr. dep. agent, Jan., 1923; offg. senr. ditto, July, 1924; offg. secty., rly. board, Nov., 1926; dep. agent, N.W. rly., April, 1927; offg. dir. of estabt., rly. bed., Jan., 1928; confd. Oct., 1930; offg. agent, N.W. Rly., May, 1931; confd., Feb., 1933; offg. member, rly. bd., Mar., 1936.

HIGMAN, BERTRAM FREDERICK, V.D.; late Indian State Rlys. (b. 27th Mar., 1877).—Joined the service, 14th Feb., 1895; traffic inspr., Jan., 1897; asst. dist. traffic supt., July, 1899; dist. ditto, Nov., 1909; divl. traffic manager, Oct., 1914; traffic manager, April, 1919, and again, April, 1921; chief supt., Jan., 1920; chief comml. manager, Feb., 1925; retd., Sept., 1933.

HILEY, ARTHUR CLIFFORD, Indian Forest Service (chief consvr.) (b. 11th Sept., 1887).—Joined the service, Dec., 1910, as asst. consvr.; dep. consvr., Dec., 1915; offg. consvr., July, 1927; confd., Aug., 1928; chief consvr., Bombay, Apl., 1937.

HILL, Rev. EDWARD EUSTACE, M.A., late Indian Eccl. Estabt. (Bombay) (b. 2nd July, 1876).—Joined 11th May, 1906; junr. chaplain, May, 1909; senr. chaplain, May, 1919; on military duty from 10th June, 1918, to 7th April, 1919; retd., Apl., 1930.

HILL, JAMES HERBERT, B.A., A.M.I.C.E., late Indian Service of Engrs. (Burma) (b. 31st Aug., 1885).—Joined the service as asst. engr. on 1st Oct., 1908; exec. engr., Oct., 1916; offg. supt. engr., July, 1926; retd., Nov., 1931.

HILL, KENNETH ARTHUR LULHAM, B.A. (Cantab.), Indian C.S. (magte. and collr., Bengal) (b. 22nd April, 1899).—Educ. at Dulwich Coll. and Emmanuel Coll., Cambridge; joined the service, 30th Oct., 1922; arrived, 13th Dec., 1922, and served in Bengal as asst. magte. and collr.; offg. mag. and collr., variously from Mar., 1932; confd., Feb., 1936.

HILL, NEVILLE CHARLES, B.A., Indian Police (asst. supt.) (b. 9th Sept., 1909).—Joined the service as prob. asst. supt., Burma, 26th Jan., 1933; confd., Jan., 1935.

HILL, REGINALD JOHN JAMES, M.A., Indian C.S. (asst. commr., Central Provs.) b. 1st July, 1905).—Educ. at Univ. of Edinburgh, and Emmanuel Coll., Cambridge; joined the service, 12th Oct., 1928; arrived, 12th November, 1928, and served in the Central Provinces as asst. commr.; under sec. to govt., Jan., 1931; offg. dep. commr., Apl., 1932.

HILL, ROBERT H., M.A., Indian Agricul. Service (dep. dir. of agric., Central Provs.) (b. 5th Nov., 1892).—Joined the service as asst. dir. of agric., Dec., 1920; economic botanist, Dec., 1925; dep. dir. of agric., Oct., 1926.

HILL, RONALD ARTHUR CHARLES, O.B.E., Indian Police (supt., Punjab) (b. 11th July, 1898).—Joined the service as asst. supt., 15th Oct., 1921; offg. supt., Feb., 1926; confd., July, 1927; awarded King's police medal, Jan., 1931; O.B.E., May, 1937.

HILL, ROWLAND ALFRED KIRKHAM, Indian Political Service (b. 26th Mar., 1884).—Held non-gazetted appts. from 16th Nov., 1903; supt. for. and pol. dept., Dec., 1927; asst. secty., ditto, Oct., 1931; offg. under-secty., ditto, Apl., 1935, to Nov., 1935; asst. secty. to govt. of India, external affairs dept., Apl., 1937.

HILSON, GEORGE ROBERT, B.Sc., late Indian Agric. Service (b. 19th Sept., 1882).—Joined the service, 26th Feb., 1910; dep. dir. of agric., Mar., 1910; cotton specialist, Coimbatore, June, 1920; offg. dir. of agric., Madras, April, 1925; ret'd., Oct., 1932.

HILTON, GEORGE GRIMMER, M.B.E., late India Office (b. 15th Sept., 1867).—Appointed clerical officer, 30th July, 1921; higher clerical officer, Jan., 1927; M.B.E., Jan., 1919; ret'd., Sept., 1932.

HILTON, GORDON CHARLES, B.A., Indian C.S. (dist. and sess. judge, Punjab) (b. 26th Oct., 1885).—Educ. at Winchester, and New Coll., Oxford; appt. after exam. of 1908; arrived, 29th Nov., 1909, and served in the Punjab as asst. commr.; ditto, and pol. asst., and comdt., B.M. Police, Oct., 1915, to Oct., 1916; offg. under-sec. to govt., finl. dept., May, 1918; ditto, rev. dept., June, 1918; under sec., rev. dept., Nov., 1918; dep. commr., Sept., 1923; provl. dist. and sess. judge, April, 1925; dist. and sess. judge, July, 1925; offg. legal membr. and sec. to govt., legisl. dept., May, 1927; offg. judge, high ct., Lahore, April, 1929; addl. judge, ditto, variously from Mar., 1931.

HINDE, REGINALD GRAHAM, Lt.-Col., I.A., Indian Political Service (b. 19th May, 1887).—Joined the service, 19th Jan., 1907; in milty. employ to June, 1920; pol. agent and consul, Muscat, Sept., 1923; 2nd asst. to Resident in Kashmir, Feb., 1925; 1st asst. to ditto, June, 1926; sec., to A.G.G., Punjab States, April, 1927; pol. agent, S. Rajputana States, Feb., 1929; secy., to agent to govr.-genl., C.I., Oct., 1929; pol. agent in Bhopal, Oct., 1930; and again, Apl., 1931; on spl. duty under agent to govr.-genl., C.I., Nov., 1930; pol. agent, Bundelkhand, Mar., 1931; pol. agent, Loralai, Dec., 1933; pol. agent, Chhattisgarh States, Nov., 1936.

HINDLEY, SIR CLEMENT DANIEL MAGGS, K.C.I.E., late Indian Railway Board.—Held appt. as agent, East Indian rly.; chairman, commrs. of the Port of Calcutta, 1st Nov., 1921; apptd. chief commr. of rlys., 1st Nov., 1922; Kt., June, 1925; K.C.I.E., March, 1929; ret'd., Sept., 1929.

HINDLEY, NOEL LANYON, B.A., Indian C.S. (dist. and sess. judge, Bengal) (b. 24th Dec., 1893).—Educ. at Marlborough Coll. and Cambridge Univ.; apptd., after exam. of 1920; arrived, 4th Dec., 1920, and served in Bengal as asst. mag. and collr.; addl. dist. and sess. judge, Dec., 1925; dist. and sess. judge, Jan., 1931; registrar, high ct., appellate side, Nov., 1931.

HINDLEY, OLIVER WALTER, B.A., A.M.I.C.E., late Indian Service of Engrs. (b. 12th Aug., 1877).—Joined the service, 1st Oct., 1902, as asst. engr.; exec. engr., April, 1911; offg. suptg. engr., April, 1923; confd., June, 1927; ret'd., Aug., 1932.

HINGSTON, CLAYTON ALEXANDER FRANCIS, C.I.E., O.B.E., Lt.-Col., I.M.S., ret'd. b. 6th May, 1877).—First commn. dated 1st Sept., 1902; apptd. persl. asst. to surgn.-gen. with the govt., Madras, Nov., 1910; profr. of midwifery, medl. coll., Madras, Nov., 1914; O.B.E., June, 1919; C.I.E.,

June, 1925; prinpl. ditto, Jan., 1928; offg. surg.-genl. with govt. of Madras, Aug., 1920; ret'd., May, 1932.

HIRA SINGH, SARDAR SAHIB, Punjab Med. Service (civil surg.) (b. Oct., 1882).—Joined the service, 26th Aug., 1905; variously apptd. as asst. surg., from Apl., 1907; civil surgeon (offg.), Nov., 1927; confd., Apl., 1932.

HIRST, REGINALD JOHN, C.I.E., B.A. (Cantab.) late Indian Police (b. 11th July, 1880).—Joined the service, 13th Nov., 1901, as asst. supt. of police; supt., Aug., 1907; on deputn. as inspr.-gen. of police, Baroda, from Oct., 1916; dep. inspr.-gen., Bihar and Orissa, Sept., 1922; offg. inspr.-gen., Mar., 1925; confd., Jan., 1931; C.I.E., June, 1927; ret'd., June, 1934.

HISTED, SYDNEY EDWARD, late Indian Police (b. 1st Feb., 1880).—Held non-gazetted appts. from 27th Nov., 1909, to 1st Feb., 1923; apptd. to I.P.S. as asst. supt., Feb., 1923; offg. supt., Sind Rlys., Mar., 1927; confd., April, 1932; ret'd., Feb., 1935.

HITCHCOCK, RONALD VICTOR, V.D., B.Sc., A.M.I.C.E., Indian State Rlys. (dep. ch. engr., G.I.P. Rly.) (b. 22nd June, 1885).—Joined the service as asst. engr., 5th Nov., 1910; resld. engr., July, 1913; on milty. service from 3rd April, 1916, to 2nd April, 1919; divl. engr., Dec., 1926; offg. dep. agent (Works), G.I.P. rly., Jan., 1933; offg. dep. ch. engr. (maintenance), G.I.P. rly., Apl., 1934; confd. (provl.), Feb., 1935.

HITCHCOCK, WILLIAM SURRIDGE, Indian Police (supt., Bihar) (b. 14th Nov., 1889).—Joined the service as asst. supt. on 30th Nov., 1910; supt., March, 1922; awarded King's police medal, Jan., 1927; on deputation as dept. supt., watch and wards, E.I. Rly., Feb., 1927, to April, 1928.

HNYIN, U THA, B.A., Barr., late Burma Commission (b. Oct., 1876).—Apptd. offg. dist. and sessions judge, March, 1923; confd., May, 1924; ret'd., Oct., 1931.

HOARE, CHARLES EDGAR, India Office (higher executive officer, mil. dept.) (b. 1st Jan., 1891).—Apptd. 2nd class clerk, 13th Nov., 1913; war services—European war, 1914-16; exec. officer, April, 1922; joint editor, India Office List, May, 1932, to Oct., 1937; actg. higher exec. officer, Mar., 1933; confd., Sept., 1935.

HOARE, THE RIGHT HON. SIR SAMUEL, Bt., P.C., G.C.S.I., G.B.E., C.M.G., Hon. LL.D. (Cantab.), M.P.—Secty. of State for India, Aug., 1931, to June, 1935.

HOBART, ROBERT CHARLES ARTHUR STANLEY, C.I.E., Indian C.S. (commr., United Provs.) (b. 27th July, 1881).—Educ. at Charterhouse Sch., and Trinity Coll., Oxford; apptd. after exam. of 1904; arrived, 29th Nov., 1905, and served in the United Provs. as asst. commr. and asst. mag. and collr.; jt. mag., Nov., 1914; mag. and collr., Sept., 1922; offg. commr., Apl., 1931; confd., Dec., 1934; C.I.E., Feb., 1937.

HOBHOUSE, Rt. Hon. SIR CHARLES EDWARD HENRY, Bart.—Educ. at Eton and Christ Church, Oxford, and at Royal Mil. Coll., Sandhurst; Lieut., 60th Rifles, 1884-1890; M.P. for E. Wilts, and private sec. to under-sec. of state for the colonies, 1892-1895; M.P. for E. Bristol, 1900-1918; member of Thames Conservancy

Board since 1904, and an Ecclesi. Commr. since 1906; partly under-sec. of state for India, Jan., 1907, to April, 1908; chairman of Royal Commn. on Indian decentralisation, 1907; finl. sec. to Treasury, April, 1908; chancellor, duchy of Lancaster, 1911; postmaster-genl., 1914-1915.

HOBSON, JAMES, Indian Police (supt., Bombay) (b. 24th Aug., 1895).—Joined the service, 16th Dec., 1922; offg. supt., Nov., 1925; confd., Nov., 1932

HODDER, WILFORD OWEN PENDEREL, Indian Police Service (supt., N.W.F.P.) (b. 10th May, 1903).—Joined the service as asst. supt. on 30th Nov., 1922; offg. dist. officer, front or constabulary, Mar., 1926; offg. supt., Sept., 1930; supt. (prov. subs.), June, 1935; offg. asst. dir., intel., Govt. of India, July, 1936.

HODDER, WILLIAM ST. LAWRENCE, Indian Police (b. 10th Aug., 1907).—Joined the service as asst. supt., Punjab, 16th Nov., 1927.

HODGE, EDWARD HUMFREY VERE, Lt.-Col., I.M.S. (prof. of medicine, Med. Coll., Calcutta) (b. 28th Aug., 1883).—Joined the service, 31st July, 1909; apptd. prof. of med., Med. Coll., Calcutta, Mar., 1934.

HODGE, JOHN DOUGLAS VERE, C.I.E., Indian C.S. (mag. and collr., Bengal) (b. 17th Oct., 1887).—Educated at Durham Sch., and Pembroke Coll., Cambridge; apptd. after exam. of 1911; arrived, 30th Nov., 1902 and served in Bengal as asst. mag. and collr.; temp. offg. addl. under sec., pol. dept., June, 1916; on spl. duty, pol. dept., Nov., to Dec., 1917; sec., sedition commee., Dec., 1917, to April, 1918; jt. mag. and collr., April, 1918; services placed at disposal of govt. of Bengal in pol. dept. from Oct., 1918; offg. under-sec. to govt. of India, home dept., April, 1919; addl. under sec., ditto, April, 1920; regr., high ct., Calcutta, Nov., 1923; offg. addl. dep. sec., home dept., govt. of India, Jan., 1925; offg. dep. sec. from Mar.-Sept., 1926; confd., Oct., 1926; mag. and collr., April, 1929; C.I.E., June, 1929; offg. commr., July, 1932; secty. to govt., Bengal, agric. and ind. depts., Aug., 1934.

HODGE, WILLIAM ERIC, Indian Forest Service (dep. consvr., Bengal) (b. 2nd April, 1895).—Joined the service as asst. consvr., 6th Oct., 1921; dep. consvr., Nov., 1923.

HODGSON, EDWARD MARSDEN, late Indian Forest Service (Bombay).—Joined the service, 16th Nov., 1896, as asst. consr.; dep. consr., Nov., 1902; also ex officio asst. pol. agent for the Dangs, from Sept., 1902, to June, 1910; K.-i.-H. medal, 1st class, Dec., 1911; on mil. duty from March, 1917, to Jan., 1918; consr., May, 1922; offg. ch. consvr., April, 1927; confd., Aug., 1927; retd., Aug., 1928.

HODGSON, ROBERT CECIL, late Indian Service of Engrs. (Bengal) (b. 14th Feb., 1876).—Trained at R.I.E. Coll.; joined the service, 1st Oct., 1900, and served in Bengal as asst. engr.; prof. in C.E. Coll., Sibpur, from June to Oct., 1901; exec. engr., Oct., 1908; and under-sec., govt. of Bengal, P.W. dept., Oct., 1910, to Jan., 1914; suptg. engr., Oct., 1919; war decoration, Sikkim-Tibet mission, 1903-4; offg. cn. engr. and sec., P.W. dept. govt. of Bengal, June, 1926; ch. engr., Oct. 1928; retd., Sept., 1929.

HODSON, ERIC, Indian Police (supt., Bengal) (b. 19th Mar., 1894).—Joined the service as prob. asst. supt. on 2nd Dec., 1913; confd., June, 1915; addl. supt., April, 1922; awarded King's police medal, 1924; supt., July, 1925; dep. commr. of police, Calcutta, Feb., 1934.

HOEY, GEORGE McCAUSLAND, B.A., B.E., M.I.E. (Ind.), late Indian Service of Engrs. (b. 17th Sept., 1885).—Joined the service, 1st Oct., 1907; as asst. engr.; exec. engr., Oct., 1915; suptg. engr., Nov., 1923.

HOGG, GILBERT PITCAIRN, C.S.I., C.I.E., M.A., Indian C.S. (ch. secty. to govt., Bengal) (b. 2nd Feb., 1884).—Educ. at Glasgow High Sch. and Glasgow Univ.; apptd. after exam. of 1907; arrived, 28th Nov., 1908, and served in E. Bengal and Assam as asst. mag. and collr.; transf. to Bengal, April, 1912; jt. mag. and dep. collr., Nov., 1914; vice-chairman, Chittagong Port Comms., July, 1915; on mil. duty, Oct., 1917, to Jan., 1918; offg. addl. dist. and sess. judge, Assam, May, 1918; on mil. duty, Aug., 1918, to Jan., 1919, and returned to Bengal; mag. and collr., April, 1921; commr. of excise and salt, Bengal, Sept., 1923; offg. sec., govt. of Bengal, agric. and industries dept., and director of industries, April, 1926; secty., govt. of Bengal, agric. and industries and P.W. depts., Nov., 1928; offg. commr., July, 1931; confd., Dec., 1931; C.I.E., June, 1932; addl. secty. to govt. of Bengal, pol. dept., Oct., 1932; offg. ch. secty. to govt., Bengal, Apr., 1933; confd., Jan. 1934; temp. member, govt's. exec. council, Bengal, Apr., 1936, and again, Feb., 1937; C.S.I., June, 1936.

HOGG, SIR MALCOLM NICHOLSON, Kt.—Dep. chairman, Bombay Chamber of Commerce, and member of Bombay legisl. council, 1915 to 1917; chairman, Bombay Chamber of Commerce, and member govrn.-gen.'s legve. council, 1917 to 1919; member of franchise cmtee., 1918-19; knighted, 1920; member of council of India, May, 1920, to April, 1925.

HOGG, WILLIAM PEAT, D.S.O., M.C., Lt.-Col., I.M.S. (resdy. surg., Mewar) (b. 29th Feb., 1888).—First commn., 13th Mar., 1916; in milty. employ. to 4th Oct., 1926; offg. civil surg., Quetta, Oct., 1926; offg. surg., British Legation, Kabul, Apr., 1927; agency surg., Bhopal, Feb., 1930; ditto, Gilgit, Oct., 1931; ditto, N. Waziristan, May, 1933; resdy. surg., Mewar, Nov., 1934; offg. resdt., Mewar, Feb., 1935.

HOGGARTH, ROBERT ANDREW, A.R.C.A., Indian Educ. Service (Bombay) (b. 28th June, 1886).—Apptd. inspr. of drawing and craft work, Bombay Presdy., 6th Nov., 1914; retd., Mar., 1931.

HOGSHAW, FRANCIS HENRY, Indian Service of Engrs. (exec. engr., Bengal) (b. 16th Dec., 1892).—Joined the service as asst. engr. on 10th Oct., 1915; exec. engr., Oct., 1924; offg. suptg. engr., Nov., 1934.

HOLBERTON, NELSON VAUGHAN, Indian Forest Service (consvr., Burma) (b. 27th May, 1885).—Arrived 29th Oct., 1906, and served as asst. consvr.; dep. consvr., Nov., 1911; employed under Indian munitions board, May, 1918, to Jan., 1920; asst. inspr.-gen. of forests, govt. of India, Feb., 1922; offg. consvr., March, 1926; confd., April, 1926.

HOLDING, SAMUEL HENRY, late India Posts and Tels. (b. 7th April, 1882).—Held non-gazetted appt. from 1st Dec., 1896, to 22nd March, 1917; with Mesopotamian field force from March, 1917, to Oct., 1920; asst. divl. engr., Aug., 1923; divl. engr., Oct., 1927; offg. dir., tels., Madras Circle, Apl., 1936; ret'd., Apl., 1937.

HOLDSWORTH, BENJAMIN GEORGE Indian C.S. (collr., Madras) (b. 31st July, 1892).—Educ. at Brasenose Coll., Oxford; apptd. after exam. of 1915; arrived, 6th Dec., 1916, and served in Madras as asst. collr. and mag.; on mil. duty from Nov., 1917, to May, 1920; sub-collr., Aug., 1920; special settl. officer, Oct., 1923; secty. to bd. of revenue, land revenue, Aug., 1927; jt. secty., Indian round table conf., London, Nov., 1930-Jan., 1931; offg. collr., Mar., 1934; confd., Jan., 1935.

HOLGATE, MAURICE JAMES, O.B.E., M.B., B.S. (Lon.), Lt.-Col., I.M.S. (civil surg. Karachi) (b. 15th May, 1884).—First commn. dated 31st July, 1909; offg. civil surg., June, 1924; confd., Dec., 1927.

HOLLAND, ARCHER SELWYN, Indian Police (dep. inspr.-genl., Bombay) (b. 23rd Oct., 1886).—Joined the service, 23rd Nov., 1907, as asst. supt.; supt. of police, April, 1920; offg. dep. inspr.-genl., Jan., 1932; confd., Jan., 1934.

HOLLAND, EDGAR WILLIAM, Indian C.S. (mag. and collr., Bengal) (b. 28th April, 1899).—Educ. at Rossall Sch., and Brasenose Coll., Oxford; joined the service, 26th Oct., 1923; arrived, 15th Dec., 1923, and served in Bengal as asst. mag. and collr.; offg. magte. and collr., Mar., 1929; confd., Dec., 1936.

HOLLAND, GEORGE REGINALD, late Salt Dept., Bombay (b. 30th Oct., 1880).—Held non-gazetted appts. (temp. and perm.), from 5th July, 1906, to 16th Sept., 1917; asst. collr. of salt and excise, Sept., 1917; on milty. duty from 20th March, 1917, to 9th Oct., 1919; supt. of salt and excise, Sept., 1920; asst. collr. of salt rev. (designation changed), Sept., 1926; ret'd., Aug., 1932.

HOLLAND, LAWRENCE BERTRAM, B.A., Indian Forest Service (consvr., Orissa) (b. 25th Feb., 1885).—Educ. at Tonbridge and Trinity coll., Oxford; joined the service, 23rd Dec., 1909, and served in the Punjab as asst. consvr.; dep. consvr., Dec., 1914; offg. consvr., April, 1926; confd., Jan., 1932; transfed. to Central Provs., Oct., 1934; transfed. to Orissa, Jan., 1937.

HOLLAND, SIR ROBERT ERSKINE, K.C.I.E., C.S.I., C.V.O., B.A., late Indian C.S. (b. 29th June, 1873).—Educ. at Winchester, and Oriel Coll., Oxford; apptd. after exam. of 1895; arrived, 10th Dec., 1896, and served in Madras as asst. collr. and mag.; transfed. to Bombay, July, 1900; asst. collr., March, 1901; retransfed. to Madras, Nov., 1902; dep. commr. of salt, &c., March, 1904; offg. asst. to the agent in Central India, June, 1904; offg. asst. sec. to govt. of India, foreign dept., Sept., 1904; offg. dep. sec., Jan., 1908; pol. agent, Maskat, Nov., 1908; 1st as t. to agent to govr.-gen. in Rajputana, and chief commr., Ajmer-Merwara, April, 1910; p.l. agent, Eastern Rajput na States, June, 1911; ditto, and asst. resdt., Mewar, May, 1913; offg.

dep. sec. for. and pol. dept., govt. of India, Feb., 1914; confirmed, April, 1914; on special duty, Persian Gulf, Sept., 1915, to March, 1916; spl. duty as jt. trade commr., Persian Gulf, Dec., 1916, to June, 1917; C.I.E., Jan., 1917; offg. pol. sec. to govt. of India, Feb., 1919; agent to govr.-gen. in Rajputana, Dec., 1919; C.S.I., June, 1921; C.V.O., June, 1922; K.C.I.E., Jan., 1925; ret'd. from I.C.S., April, 1925; member of Council of India, 21st April, 1925 to July, 1931; apptd. judl. adviser to govt. of Siam, June, 1933; Order of Crown of Siam (1st cl.), Aug., 1936.

HOLLAND, SIR THOMAS HENRY, K.C.S.I., K.C.I.E., D.Sc., F.R.S., late Berkeley Fellow of the Owens Coll., late Geol. survey of India.—Joined the service 10th July, 1890, as asst. supt. and curator, geol. museum and laboratory; dep. supt., July, 1894; dir., Feb., 1903, to 1909; ret'd., Nov., 1910; chairman of trustees, Indian Museum, Calcutta, 1906-08; K.C.I.E., June, 1908; profir. of geology, Manchester Univ., 1909; presdt., Indian industrial commn., 1916; presdt., bd. of munitions, India, from 1917; K.C.S.I., June, 1918; temp. member of Vice-roy's council, Feb., 1919; member, govr.-genl.'s Council, April, 1920, to Sept., 1921; rector, impl. coll. of science and technology, 1922; pres., British Association, 1929; principal and vice-chancellor of Edinburgh Univ., 1929; has contributed to the survey publications.

HOLLIDAY, GEORGE DARGUE, B.Sc., Indian Police (b. 14th May, 1906).—Joined the service as asst. supt., Punjab, 17th Nov., 1926.

HOLLINS, SAMUEL THOMAS, C.I.E., late Indian Police (b. 6th Oct., 1881).—Joined the service, 24th Nov., 1902, and posted to the United Provs.; asst. supt. of police, Oct., 1907; supt., Aug., 1914; ditto, Ajmer-Merwara, March, 1915; inspr.-gen., Tonk State, April, 1916, to Nov., 1918; awarded King's police medal, Jan., 1918; offg. dep. inspr.-gen., April, 1928; confd., Oct., 1930; offg. inspr.-genl., Apl., 1931; confd., July, 1933; C.I.E., June, 1931; ret'd., Oct., 1936.

HOLMAN, THOMAS GEORGE HOATHLEY, Indian Police (b. 10th July, 1905).—Joined the service as asst. supt., Bengal, on 1st Jan., 1926; offg. addl. supt., variously from Mar., 1932; offg. princpl., police training coll., Sardah, Apl., 1933; supt. (temp.), June, 1936.

HOLMAN-HUNT, HILARY LUSHINGTON, C.I.E., A.M.I.C.E., late Indian Service of Engrs. (b. 6th May, 1879).—Trained at the R.I.E. Coll.; joined the service, 1st Oct., 1901, and served in Burma as an asst. engr.; exec. engr., Oct., 1909; C.I.E., June, 1917; on mil. service, May, 1917, to Sept., 1919; suptg. engr., Dec., 1921; offg. chief engr. and sec. to govt., Nov., 1923; chief engr., Sept., 1926; ret'd., Feb., 1933.

HOLME, ALFRED DIEGFRIED, O.B.E., Lt.-Col., R.E., late Indian Service of Engrs. (Punjab) (b. 17th Mar., 1875).—First commn., dated 17th Aug., 1894; joined the public works dept., Feb., 1897, as asst. engr., and served in the Punjab; exec. engr., Oct., 1906; reverted to mil. duty from Nov., 1912, to March, 1913; under-sec. to govt., March, 1913; suptg. engr., Feb., 1918; temp. reverted to mil. duty, from Oct., 1914, to June, 1919; O.B.E., June 1919; ret'd., Mar., 1930.

HOLMES, ANDREW GILMOUR, M.A., Indian C.S. (asst. mag. and collr., Bengal) (b. 5th June, 1915).—Educ. at George Heriot's School, Edinburgh and Edinburgh Univ.; joined the Service 20th Sept., 1937; arrived in India, Oct., 1937 and served in Bengal as asst. mag. and collr.

HOLMES, ARTHUR CLEMENT, B.A., late Indian C.S. (b. 25th Nov., 1875).—Educ. at Christ's Hosp. and Corpus Christi Coll., Oxford; apptd. after exam. of 1898; arrived, 20th Nov., 1899, and served in the N.W. Prov. and Oudh as asst. mag. and collr.; joint mag., Nov., 1908; ditto, and settlt. offr., Jan., 1914; dep. commr. and settlt. offr., Dec., 1914; on mil. duty (I.A.R.O.), Oct., 1918, to Feb., 1919; mag. and collr. (s.p.t.), March, 1919; confd., Feb., 1922; offg. commr., May to Oct., 1924, and again, March, 1926; confd., Nov., 1928; offg. member, bd. of rev., United Provs., May, 1933; ret'd., Nov., 1934.

HOLMES, ARTHUR MALCOLM, Indian Police (b. 21st July, 1908).—Joined the service as asst. dist. supt., Central Provs., 24th Nov., 1928; A.D.C. to govt. C.P., Dec., 1933.

HOLMES, BERNARD WILLOUGHBY, M.R.C.S., L.R.C.P., V.D., late Indian State Rlys. (E.I. Rly.) (b. 13th Sept., 1872).—Joined the service as asst. medl. officer, 5th Aug., 1897; dist. medl. officer, March, 1901; ret'd., Aug., 1929.

HOLMES, HENRY CHRISTOPHER, Indian Forest Service (dep. consvr., Bombay) (b. 10th Sept., 1897).—Joined the service, as forest engr., Bombay, 24th Nov., 1921; dep. consvr., July, 1928.

HOLMES, JAMES EDWARD, office of High Commr. for India (exec. officer, store dept.) (b. 30th Aug., 1897).—Apptd. clerical officer, India Office, 2nd Feb., 1920; transferred to Office of High Commr. for India, Oct., 1920; actg. exec. officer, May, 1930; confd., July, 1935.

HOLROYD, Lt.-Col. GILBERT, M.B., I.M.S., ret'd., Jail Dept., United Provs. (b. 17th Sept., 1880).—First commn., 2nd Feb., 1907; in milty. employ to 4th May, 1911; again, Oct., 1914, to Sept., 1920; supt., Sept., 1920; offg. inspr.-genl., Apl., 1932; ret'd., Aug., 1935.

HOLT, EDWARD HARLAND, B.A., Indian C.S. (asst. collector, Bombay) (b. 20 April, 1907).—Educ. at Bradford Grammar School and Queen's College Oxford; apptd. after examination of 1930; arrived in India November, 1931, and served in Bombay as asst. collector.

HOMAN, ARTHUR KNOX, Indian State Rlys. (divl. supt. (senior) N.W. rly.) (b. 17th Oct., 1884).—Joined the service, 22nd Sept., 1908; asst. loco. supt., N.W. rly., Oct., 1908; dist. ditto, E.B. Rly., June, 1914; divl. supt., E.I. rly., July, 1925; divl. rolling stock officer, Nov., 1927; dep. ch. transptn. supt., E.I. rly., May, 1931; offg. divl. supt., senior, N.W. rly., Apl., 1934; divl. supt. (junr.), May, 1934; offg. divl. supt. (senior), Aug., 1933; confd., Sept., 1934.

HOMAN, LAWRENCE WILLIAM NICHOLSON, B.A., India Office (actg. prinpl., services and gen. dept.) (b. 22nd June, 1907).—Educ. at Winchester and Christ Church, Oxford; asst. principal, 27th Oct., 1930; actg. resdt. clerk, Jan.-Apl., 1932; apptd. resdt. clk., Sept., 1932; asst. pte. secty. to secty. of state, Sept., 1934; actg. prinpl., July, 1936.

HOMFRAY, CHARLES KENYON, Indian Forest Service (dep. consvr., Bengal) (b. 19th April, 1897).—Joined the service as asst. consvr. on 11th Dec., 1922; dep. consvr., Nov., 1924.

HOMFRAY, JESTON, late Indian Forest Service (b. 12th Dec., 1885).—Arrived 4th Nov., 1907, and served as asst. consvr.; dep. consvr., Nov., 1919; offg. consvr., April, 1924; confd., Mar., 1929; ret'd. June, 1933.

HOOD, HAROLD HAYNES, late Impl. Customs Service (b. 23rd Nov., 1875).—Joined the service, 19th Oct., 1907, and served as asst. collr. in Bengal; apptd. to Bombay, July, 1911; to Madras, May, 1913; and to Sind, March, 1915; Rangoon, April, 1918; collr., Madras, Aug., 1919; ditto, Karachi, 1921; ret'd., Nov., 1930.

HOOD, HUGH MEGGISON, C.I.E., Indian C.S. (collr., Madras) (b. 5th June, 1885).—Educ. at Middlesbrough High Sch., and Jesus Coll., Cambridge; appt. after exam. of 1908; arrived, 23rd Nov., 1909, and served in Madras as asst. collr. and mag.; sub-collr. and jt. mag. Sept., 1916; on mily. duty from Aug., 1916, to April, 1919; registrar, co-operative societies, April, 1923; and again, Nov., 1926; offg. collr. and dist. mag., March, 1924; confd., Aug., 1929; offg. sec. to govt., rev. dept., Dec., 1925; chairman, Madras provl. banking enquiry commee., Dec., 1929; offg. secty. to govt., finance dept., Apl., 1931; confd. Dec., 1931; C.I.E., June, 1934; offg. secty. to govt., Madras, law and educn. dept., Mar., 1936; secty to govt., home dept., Apl., 1936; offg. second secty. to govt., Apl., 1936; offg. ch. secty. to govt., Madras, June, 1936.

HOOD, JAMES REANEY, M.A., Barr., late Indian C.S. (b. 4th May, 1888).—Educ. at Woodbridge Sch., Pembroke Coll., Oxford, and Univ. Coll., London; apptd. after exam. of 1911; arrived, 21st Nov., 1912, and served in Bombay as asst. mag. and collr.; offg. collr. and dist. magte. and pol. agent, July, 1923; offg. secty. to govt. of Bombay, home and eccl. depts., Oct., 1931; ret'd., Dec., 1932.

HOOD, ROBERT HOOD, Indian Service of Engineers (exec. engr., United Provs.) (b. 18th Dec., 1891).—Joined the service, 1st Oct., 1919; asst. engr., Dec., 1919; asst. exec. engr., Sept., 1921; exec. engr., Oct., 1924.

HOOD, VISCOUNT, SAMUEL HOOD, B.A., India Office (asst. pte. secty. to secty. of state for India and for Burma) (b. Oct. 15, 1910).—Educ. at Eton and Trinity College, Cambridge; appointed constant principal, Feb. 19th, 1935; resdt. clk., Marl, 1936; asst. pte. secty. to secty. of state for India, Sept., 1936; ditto for Burma, Apl., 1937.

HOOD, WILLIAM, Indian State Rlys. (dep. ch. engr. (bridges), G.I.P. Rly.) (b. 5th Sept., 1893).—Joined the service as asst. bridge engr., 23rd Jan., 1925; senr. asst. ditto, Aug., 1927; offg. dep. ch. engr. (bridges), Nov., 1932; confd., Sept., 1934; offg. dep. ch. contr. of standardisation, July, 1936.

HOOLE, NORMAN PETER GLEDHILL, Indian Police (b. 9th May, 1916).—Apptd. assist. supt., Burma, on probn., 6th Dec., 1935.

HOOLEY, SAMUEL PROSPER, late Indian State Rlys. (b. 12th Mar., 1876).—Joined the as 2nd asst. auditor, 22nd April, 1914; dep. agent, Apl., 1927; retd., Mar., 1933.

HOON, PRAN NATH, Indian State Rlys. (dist. contr. of stores, E.B. rly.) (b. 5th Mar., 1889).—Joined the service, 12th Jan., 1915; asst. contr. of stores, Sept., 1923; dist. contr. of stores (junr.) E.B. rly., Sept., 1926; ditto (senr.), May, 1933.

HOOPER, ERNEST GEORGE GALE, Office of the High Commr. for India (exec. officer, store dept.) (b. 14th Dec., 1899).—Apptd. clerical officer, 3rd Feb., 1924 after service at India Office from 21st May, 1920; exec. officer, 1st Nov., 1932.

HOOPER, GEOFFREY GRANT, B.A., M.C., late Indian Civil Service (b. 7th Dec., 1891).—Educ. at St. Paul's School, and Christ Church, Oxford; apptd. under Reconstruction Scheme; arrived, 2nd May, 1921, and served in Bengal as asst. mag. and collr.; addl. dist. and sess. judge, Nov., 1925; services placed at disposal of govt. of India, legisl. dept., Jan., 1927; dist. and sess. of judge, Mar., 1928; offg. secty. to govt. of Bengal, legisl. dept., Oct., 1931, and again, Sept., 1932; confd. Oct., 1933; retd., Jan., 1938.

HOOPER, JOHN MICHAEL, late Local Fund Audit Dept., Madras (b. 24th Aug., 1873).—Held non-gazetted appt. from 6th Jan., 1896; asst. acct. offr., Nov., 1917; examr. of local fund accts., May, 1924; retd., Dec., 1928.

HOOTON, ALFRED, C.I.E., Major-Gen., I.M.S. (ret'd.) (b. 15th Mar., 1870).—First commn. dated 29th July, 1895; apptd. dep. sanitary commr., Bombay, April, 1903; civil surg., March, 1904; on mily. duty from Oct., 1918; offg. surg.-genl. with the govt. of Bombay, May, 1923; confd., Jan., 1924; C.I.E., Jan., 1923; offg. D.G., I.M.S., Apl., 1927; retd., Feb., 1930.

HOPE, ADRIAN JAMES ROBERT, C.I.E., late Indian Service of Engineers.—Trained at R.I.E. Coll.; joined the service, 1st Oct., 1897, as asst. engr., exec. engr., Oct., 1907; under sec. to govt. of India, P.W. dept., Nov., 1914; dep. sec. to ditto, April, 1916; suptg. engr., Dec., 1919; chief engr., Burma, Sept., 1921; C.I.E., Jan., 1922; retd., June, 1928.

HOPKINS, ALAN CECIL, B.A., Indian Forest Service (dep. consvr., Central Provs.) (b. 30th Oct., 1896).—Joined the service as asst. consvr., 5th Dec., 1924; dep. consvr., Sept., 1926.

HOPKINS, EDWARD ALFRED, Indian Police (supt., Punjab) (b. 4th Aug., 1901).—Joined the service as asst. supt., Punjab, on 29th Nov., 1921; offg. supt., May, 1928; awarded King's police medal, March, 1929.

HOPKINS, GEOFFREY MAYNE, Indian Forest Service (dep. consvr., United Provs.) (b. 5th July, 1898).—Joined the service as asst. consr., on 30th Nov., 1921; dep. consvr., Oct., 1926.

HOPKINS, GERALD ADAMSON, late India Posts and Telegraphs (b. 9th Sept., 1880).—Trained at the R.I.E. Coll.; apptd. asst. supt., telegraphs, 1st Oct., 1901; supt., March, 1913; divl. engr., tels., Oct., 1920; offg. dep. chief engr., tels., May, 1925; dir. tele. energ. (prov.), Jan., 1930; confd., Mar., 1930; offg. P.M.G., B. and O. circle, Dec., 1930; P.M.G., Burma, Oct., 1932; retd., July, 1934.

HOPKINSON, ARTHUR JOHN, C.I.E., Indian Civil Service, Indian Political Service. (b. 28th Nov., 1894).—Educ. at Marlborough, and Exeter Coll., Oxford; arrived, 10th May, 1920, and served in United Provs. as asst. mag. and collr.; services placed at disposal of govt. of India, for. and pol. dept., Feb., 1924; asst. commr., Ajmer-Merwara, March, 1924; British Trade Agent at Gyantse and asst. to pol. offr. in Sikkim, Jan., 1927; pol. agent, E. Kathiawar States, Nov., 1928; dep. commr., Hazara, Nov., 1930; secty. to govt., N.W.F.P., transferred depts., Apl., 1932; chief secty., N.W.F.P., Apl., 1934; C.I.E., Jan., 1938.

HOPKYNs, WILLIAM STENNING, C.I.E., O.B.E., M.A., late Indian C.S. (b. 3rd Feb., 1879).—Educ. at Westminster Sch., and Christ Church, Oxford; apptd. after exam. of 1902; arrived, 27th Nov., 1903, and served in Bengal as asst. mag. and collr.; jt. mag. and dep. collr., Oct., 1910; s.p.t. mag. and collr., Nov., 1914; confd., April, 1917; O.B.E., Jan., 1919; offg. sec. to govt. of Bengal, revenue dept., Dec., 1920, to April, 1922, and from April, 1924; confd. as sec. rev. dept., March, 1925; C.I.E., June, 1928; offg. ch. secty. to govt. of Bengal, June, 1928; confd., May, 1930; commr., June, 1932; retd., June, 1933.

HOPWOOD, STEPHEN FRANCIS, M.C., late Indian Forest Service (b. 23rd June, 1880).—Arrived, 28th Nov., 1903, and served as asst. consvr.; dep. consvr., Dec., 1909; on mil. duty, Jan., 1916, to March, 1919; consvr., April, 1920; off. ch. consvr., May, 1927; confd., Sept., 1930; retd., June, 1935.

HORMASJI, JIVANJI, C.I.E., I.S.O., M.A., LL.B., Barr., late Burma Judl. Dept. (b. 11th Feb., 1871).—Appointed asst. regr., chf. ct., Lower Burma, Nov., 1900; 2nd dep. regr., Feb., 1911; 1st dep. regr., Nov., 1916; regr., high ct., Rangoon, May, 1924; offg. administrator-gen. and offl. trustee, April, 1925; confd., May, 1926; C.I.E., June, 1929; retd., Feb., 1931.

HORMAZDIAR PHIROZ DASTUR (sec. Dastur, H. P.).

HORN, WILLIAM ROWATT, D.Sc., late Indian State Rlys. (E.I.Rly.) (b. 25th Aug., 1879).—Trained at the R.I.E. Coll.; joined the state rys. as asst. engr., 1st Oct., 1903; exec. engr. (junr. govt. inspr.), Madras, Feb., 1912; spl. duty, ry. board, April, 1915; asst. sec., ry. board, Aug., 1915, to May, 1919; offg. dep. agent, E.B. Rly., Nov., 1924; offg. divl. supt., E.I. Rly., Oct., 1926; offg. contr. stores, E.I. Rly., March, 1928; retd., Oct., 1931.

HORNELL, SIR WILLIAM WOODWARD, Kt., C.I.E., B.A.(Oxon), late Indian Educatl. Service.—Educ. at Radley Coll., and Trinity Coll., Oxford; joined the service, 9th Jan., 1902; profrr., Presy. Coll., Calcutta, Jan., 1902; inspr. of schools, Bengal, Sept., 1903; asst. dir. of public instn., Bengal, Oct., 1906; junr. examr., bd. of edcn., England, 1908; asst. dir., ditto, 1910; dir., public instn., Bengal, May, 1913 to 1924; addl. member, Bengal legisl. council; services placed at disposal of govt. of India, Nov., 1917; C.I.E., Jan., 1918; vice-chancellor, Hong Kong Univ., 1924; Kt., Jan., 1931.

HORSFIELD, HERBERT ELLIS, B.A., late Indian C.S. (b. 3rd Nov., 1887).—Educ. at Leeds Gr. School, and St. John's Coll.,

Oxford; apptd. after exam. of 1910; arrived in India, 8th Dec., 1911, and served in Bengal as asst. mag. and collr.; transf. to Bihar and Orissa, April, 1912; jt. mag. and dep. collr., Oct., 1918; serving under govt. of India, army dept., from Oct., 1916; persl. asst. to air., central intell., India, July to Oct., 1920; mag. and collr., May, 1925; sec., local self-govt. dept., July, 1925; commr., excise and salt, Oct., 1928; offg. commr., Nov., 1932; ret'd., Apl., 1937.

HORSFIELD, RODERICK, Indian State Rlys. (divl. trans. supt., G.I.P. Rly.) (b. 30th Apl., 1893).—Joined the service as asst. trans. supt., 18th April, 1924; offg. divl. trans. supt., Dec., 1926; confd., Sept., 1930.

HORTON, RALPH ALBERT, C.I.E., Indian Police (inspr.-genl., United Provs.) (b. 5th Oct., 1885).—Joined the service as asst. supt. on 25th Nov., 1906; supt., Oct., 1921; C.I.E., Jan., 1928; offg. dep. inspr.-genl., Apl., 1932; confd., May, 1936; offg. inspr.-genl., June, 1935; confd., Oct., 1936.

HORWILL, LIONEL CLIFFORD, Indian C.S. (dist. and sess. judge, Madras) (b. 19th Sept., 1890).—Educ. at Royal Coll. of Science and Univ. Coll., London; apptd. after exam. of 1914; arrived, 10th Dec., 1915, and served in Madras as asst. collr. and mag.; on mily. duty, July, 1916, to Dec., 1919; sub-collr., Sept., 1921; offg. dist. and sess. judge, March, 1925; confd., Feb., 1929; addl. judge, ct. of resdt., Hyderabad, Nov., 1935; offg. judge, high ct., Madras, variously from July, 1936.

HORWOOD, Rev. KENRIC COSENS, B.A., late Eccles. Dept., Central Provs. (b. 2nd Nov., 1880).—Apptd. chaplain on probation, 8th Dec., 1909; junr., chaplain, March, 1912; senr. chaplain, Mar., 1922; ret'd., Sept., 1932.

HOSALI, SATYENDRANATH, B.A., Indian Police (b. 4th July, 1913) Apptd. asst. supt. Bombay on probn., 3rd Feb., 1937.

HOSE, Sir JOHN WALTER, Kt., C.S.I., late Indian C.S.—Educ. at Dulwich, and Christ Church, Oxford; apptd. after exam. of 1884; arrived, 3rd Dec., 1886, and served in the N.W. Prov. and Oudh as asst. mag. and collr.; joint mag., May, 1891; under sec. to govt., April, 1893; registrar, high court, March, 1894; dep. commr., March, 1898; mag. and collr., June, 1899; member of legisl. council from Oct., 1905; finl. sec. to govt., March, 1906; chief sec., April, 1910; C.S.I., Dec., 1911; commr., April, 1912; ret'd., Jan., 1916; employed temply. in the jud. and public dept., India Office, from Jan., 1916 to Oct., 1926; acting asst. sec., judl. and public dept., Oct., 1917 to May, 1918, and from Dec., 1918. Kt., July, 1926.

HOTSON, Sir JOHN ERNEST BUTTERY, K.C.S.I., O.B.E., M.A. (Oxon.) late Indian C.S. (b. 17th Mar., 1877).—Educ. at Edinburgh Coll. Sch., Edinburgh Acad., and Magdalen Coll., Oxford; apptd. after exam. of 1899; arrived, 24th Nov., 1900, and served in Bombay as asst. collr. and mag.; supt., managed estates, Kathiawar, Nov., 1903; under sec. to govt., rev., fin., &c., dpts., Jan., 1908; ditto, pol., judl., &c., depts., March, 1908, to Jan., 1911; temp. dep. sec., ditto, Oct., 1914; on mil. duty (I.A.R.O.) from April, 1915; spl. duty

in Baluchistan from March, 1916; O.B.E., June, 1918; sec. to govt., pol. dept., Nov., 1922; C.S.I., Jan., 1926; member of govr.'s exec. council, Bombay, April, 1926, to May, 1931; K.C.S.I., June, 1930; actg. govr. of Bombay. Apl., 1931; ret'd., Feb., 1934.

HOULDEY, JOHN EMERIS, B.A., late Indian C.S. (Burma) (b. 19th June, 1879).—Educ. at Durham Sch. and Clare Coll., Cambridge; apptd. after exam. of 1902; arrived, 7th Dec., 1903, and served in Burma as asst. commr.; dep. commr., Jan., 1917; on mil. duty, Jan. to March, 1918, and July, 1918, to April, 1919; prest. Rangoon municipal commee., April, 1920; offg. chairman, dev. trust, April, 1922; offg. commr., Nov., 1923; again offg. chairman, dev. trust, March, 1924; confd., July, 1924; ret'd., Aug., 1930.

HOULDING, GEOFFREY WILLIAM, B.Sc., A.M.I.M.E., late Indian Forest Engrg. Service (b. 20th Sept., 1896).—Joined the service as forest engr. on 19th Oct., 1921; ret'd., Oct., 1932.

HOULTON, JOHN WARDLE, B.A. (Cantab.), Indian C.S. (secty. to govt., rev. dept., Bihar) (b. 28th Jan., 1892).—Educ. at Christ's Coll. Cambridge; joined the service, 5th Feb., 1920; arrived, 12th May, 1920, and served in Bihar and Orissa as asst. mag. and collr.; magte. and collr. (prov. sub.), Mar., 1932; offg. secty. to govt., rev. dept., Bihar and Orissa, Apl., 1933; magte. and collr., July, 1935.

HOWARD, Sir ALBERT, Kt., C.I.E., M.A., A.R.C.S., F.C.S., F.L.S., late Dept. of Agriculture.—Joined the service, 5th May, 1905, as biological botanist, Agric. Coll., Pusa; imperial economic botanist, May, 1906; C.I.E., June, 1924; on foreign service under Indore Durbar, Oct., 1924; ret'd., Dec., 1928; knighted, June, 1934.

HOWARD, ALBERT JOHN, India Audit Office (exec. officer) (b. 23rd Sept., 1888).—Apptd. clerical officer, 1st July, 1922; exec. officer, Aug., 1930.

HOWARD, Sir HENRY FRASER, K.C.I.E., C.S.I., late Indian C.S.—Educ. at Aldenham Sch., and Trinity Hall, Cambridge; apptd. after exam. of 1896; arrived, 9th Dec., 1897, and served in Bengal as asst. mag. and collr.; supt. of gaz. revision, March, 1904; under sec. to govt. of India, fin. dept., Feb., 1904; jt. mag. and dep. collr., E. Bengal and Assam, April, 1908; mag. and collr., March, 1909; asst. collr. of customs, Calcutta, July, 1909; collr., Dec., 1909; offg. sec., govt. of India, fin. dept., May, 1912; C.I.E., June, 1913; offg. contr. of currency, Jan., 1914; confirmed, April, 1914; offg. sec. to govt. of India, fin. dept., July, 1916; addl. member, govr.-genl.'s legis. council, April, 1916; offg. sec. to the govt. of India, comm. and industry dept., Dec., 1916; sec. to the govt. of India, finance dept., May, 1917; C.S.I., Jan., 1919; temp. member of govr.-gen.'s council, 29th May, 1919; on deputation as contr. of finance, India Office, 5th May, 1920, to 4th May, 1923; K.C.I.E., June, 1923; ret'd., Sept., 1925; author of 'Handbook of Criminal Procedure,' 'India and the Gold Standard,' and articles in Imperial Gazetteer.

HOWARD, HENRY GEORGE, Major, C.I.E., M.C., M.I.E.E., M.A.M.Soc.C.E., Electricity Dept., Madras, (b. 5th Oct., 1883).—Chief Engineer for Electricity on agreement from 6th Sept., 1927.

HOWARD, STANLEY HERBERT, B.A., Indian Forest Service (consr., United Provs.) (b. 17th Apl., 1888).—Joined the service 23rd Nov., 1912, as asst. consr.; dep. consr., Nov., 1916; silviculturist, forest research inst., Dehra Dun, Oct., 1919 to Jan., 1926; offg. consvr., Dec., 1929; and again, Apl., 1933; confd., Apl., 1936.

HOWE, HENRY, B.A., A.M.I.C.E., M.I.E. (Ind.), Indian State Rlys. (dep. ch. engr., E.I. Rly.) (b. 19th July, 1883).—Joined the service as junr. engr., 12th Nov., 1910; asst. engr., Jan., 1919; resdt. engr., Sept., 1919; sub-divl. engr., Aug., 1925; offg. supt. (way and works), Feb., 1926; offg. dep. ch. enr. (signals), March, 1927; Mar., 1933; and again, Mar., 1934; confd., July, 1934.

HOWELL, ALFRED EDWARD, Indian State Rlys. (production engr. N.W. rly.) (b. 31st Mar., 1890).—Joined the service as asst. works manager, N.W. rly., 6th April, 1927; production engr., N.W. rly., Oct. 1930.

HOWELL, DAVID ARNOLD, A.M.I.C.E., Public Works Dept., Punjab (suplg. engr.) (b. 28th June, 1890).—Joined the service as asst. sany. engr., 25th Dec., 1920; exec. sany. engr., Mar., 1921; offg. suptg. engr., Apl., 1932; confd., Dec., 1932.

HOWELL, SIR EVELYN BERKELEY, K.C.I.E., C.S.I., M.A., late Indian C.S. (b. 12th Feb., 1877).—Educ. at the Charterhouse, and Emmanuel Coll., Cambridge; apptd. after exam. of 1899; arrived, 8th Dec., 1900, and served in the Punjab and N.W. Frontier Prov., as asst. commr.; asst. sec. to chief commr., N.W. Frontier Prov., May, 1904, to Sept., 1906; pol. asst., 1st Feb., 1906; dep. commr., May, 1907; dist. judge, Nov., 1907; asst. sec. for dept. govt. of India, Nov., 1909; dep. commr., Kohart Oct., 1910; durbar sec. to chief commr., Peshawar, July, 1911; asst. commr., Mardan, Aug., 1912; ditto, Peshawar, Dec., 1912; censor of Indian mails, Indian Expdny. Force, France, Sept., 1914; C.I.E., Jan., 1916; pol. agent and H.B.M.'s consul at Muscat, June to Oct., 1916; dep. civil commr., Basrah, Oct., 1916; rev. sec., Mesopotamian administr., Dec., 1918; C.S.I., March, 1919; dep. sec. to govt. of India, foreign and pol. dept., Feb., 1922; offg. resdt. in Waziristan, May, 1924; for. sec. to govt. of India (temp.), Oct., 1926; resident, Kashmir, April, 1927; for. sec. to govt. of India, May, 1930; K.C.I.E., Jan., 1932; retd., Sept., 1934.

HOWLETT, CECIL GEORGE, M.B., Lt.-Col., I.M.S., retd., late Civil Medl. Dept., Bihar and Orissa (b. 30th May, 1882).—First commn. dated 29th Jan., 1910; in milty. service up to 16th Dec., 1922; civil surg., Dec., 1922; retd., Mar., 1934.

HOWLETT, RICHARD ROSS, B.A., late Office of High Commr. for India (b. 20th Nov., 1872).—Educ. at Westminster Sch., and Christ's Coll., Cambridge (scholar); junr. clerk, 16th April, 1895; senior clerk, April, 1909; dep. dir.-gen. of stores, Nov., 1915;

transfd. to office of high commr. for India, Oct., 1920; dir. of purchase, store dept., April, 1927; retd. Nov., 1932.

HOWSON, GEOFFREY, C.I.E., M.C., Lt.-Col., I.A., retd. For. and Pol. dept., govt. of India (asst. master, Mayo coll., Ajmer) (b. 14th Aug., 1883).—First commn. dated 21st Jan., 1903, C.I.E., June, 1919; in milty. employ. to 24th April, 1927; asst. master, Mayo coll., Ajmer, April, 1927.

HOYLE, ARTHUR LASCELLES, B.A., late Indian C.S. (b. 18th Jan., 1887).—Educ. at Rugby, St. John's Coll., Oxford, and Univ. Coll., London; apptd. after exam. of 1910; arrived in India, 25th Nov., 1911, and served in the Central Provs. as asst. commr.; on mil. service (I.A.R.O.), July, 1916, to Nov., 1918; temply. transfd. to Punjab, April to July, 1919; dep. commr. (prov.), Oct., 1923; confd., April, 1925; excise commr., Central Provs., March, 1926; dep. commr., Sept., 1926; commr. N.I. salt rev., May, 1928; retd., Mar., 1937.

HOYLE, LEONARD ARTHUR, Indian State Rlys. (asst. divl. supt. (traction), G.I.P. rly.) (b. 4th Feb., 1886).—Joined the service, 5th July, 1928, as traction engr., rolling stock, G.I.P. rly.; offg. depy. transport supt. (traction), June, 1930; asst. divl. supt. (traction), G.I.P. rly., May, 1933.

HTAY, MAUNG BA, B.A., Indian C.S. (asst. commr., Burma) (b. 3rd June, 1908).—Educated at the Wesleyan High School, Monywa, Rangoon University and the School of Oriental Studies, London; joined the service, 6th Oct., 1933; arrived in India, January, 1934, and served in Burma as asst. commr.

HUBAN, JOHN PATRICK, O.B.E., Lt.-Col., I.M.S. (ch. medl. officer, Jaipur) (b. 13th Feb., 1891).—First commn. dated 23rd Sept., 1914; in milty. employ. to 16th Oct., 1922; O.B.E., Jan., 1920; civil surg., Oct., 1922; ch. medl. officer, Jaipur, Jan., 1932.

HUBBACK, SIR JOHN AUSTEN, K.C.S.I., M.A., Indian C.S.
