(govr. of Orissa) (b. 28th Feb., 1878).—Educ. at Winchester and King's Coll., Cambridge; apptd. after exam. of 1901; arrived, 6th Dec., 1902; and served in Bengal as asst. mag. and collr., and asst. settlmt. offr.; settlmt. offr., May, 1909; jt. mag. and dep. collr., Feb., 1910; transfd. to Bihar and Orissa, April, 1912; special duty, bd. of rev., Dec., 1912; offg. sec., ditto, Sept., 1913, to May, 1914; temply. empd. in rev. and statistics dept., India Office, from May to Sept., 1915; mag. and collr., March, 1916; serving under govt. of India, army dept., from May to July, 1918; offg. sec. to govt., Bihar and Orissa, rev. and judl. depts., Feb., 1919; confirmed, April, 1919; dir. of land records and surveys, Bihar and Orissa, Feb., 1923; offg. commr., Feb., 1925; confd., Dec., 1928; offg. member, bd. of rev., May, 1932; C.S.I., Jan., 1933; temp. member, govr's exec. council, Bihar and Orissa, Aug., 1933, and again, Oct., 1934; confd., Feb., 1935; K.C.S.I., Feb., 1936; govr. of Orissa, Apl., 1936.

HUBBARD, ALEXANDER EGERTON, Indian State Railways (exec. engr., E.I. rly.) (b. 5th Nov., 1882).—Joined the service, 22nd May,

1920; resdt., engr., E.I. rly., May, 1922; exec. engr., Nov., 1931.

HUBBARD, PHILIP MAITLAND, B.A., Indian C.S. (asst. commr., Punjab) (b. 9th Nov., 1910).—Educated at Elizabeth College, Gurney and Jesus College, Oxford; apptd. after exam. of 1933; arrived in India, Nov., 1934 and served in Punjab as asst. commr.; asst. colon. officer, May, 1937.

HUDDLESTON, ERNEST WHITESIDE, C.I.E., C.B.E., Captain, R.I.N. (ret'd.), Office of High Commr. for India (shipping surveyor and adviser) (b. 18th Aug., 1874).—Entered R.I.M., 1895; served in Egyptian Campaign, 1895-6, China Expedition, 1901-2, and in Somaliland Expedition, 1902-4; staff officer, Bombay dockyard, 1911; senior marine transport officer, Bombay, 1914 to 1918; C.I.E., June, 1916; C.B.E., July, 1919; presidency port officer, Madras, 1920 to 1924; dep. director, R.I.M., from 1924; ret'd. from R.I.M., Dec., 1925; apptd. shipping surveyor and adviser, office of High Commr. for India, Nov., 1925.

HUDSON, SIR HAVELOCK, G.C.B., K.C.I.E., A.D.C., Gen., Indian Army (ret'd.) (b. 26th June, 1862).—First commission, 1881; Commandant, Cavalry Sch., Sangor, 1912; Brig.-Gen., General Staff, Northern Army, 1912-14; Adjutant-General, India, 1917-20; K.C.B., Jan., 1918; K.C.E., June, 1919; G.O.C.-in-chief, Eastern Army in India, 1920; A.D.C. General to H.M. the King, 1922; member of the Council of India, 1st Oct., 1924, to 30th Sept., 1929; vice-president, ditto, Nov., 1928; G.C.B., Jan., 1928; war services—Secnd Miranzai Expedition, 1901 (medal with clasp); N.W. Frontier, 1897 (medal and clasp); China, 1900 (despatches, medal, brevet of major); European War, 1914-18 (despatches, promoted Major-Gen.).

HUDSON, RICHARD JOHN HARRINGTON, B.Sc., A.M.I.C.E., late Indian Service of Engrs. (Bihar and Orissa) (b. 17th May, 1889).—Joined the service as asst. engr., Oct., 1912; exec. engr., Oct., 1921; ret'd., May, 1930.

HUDSON, SIR WALTER FRANK, K.C.I.E., B.A., late Indian C.S. (b. 22nd Aug., 1875).—Educ. at Dulwich Coll., Brasenose Coll., Oxford, and Univ. Coll., London; apptd. after exam. of 1898; arrived, 18th Dec., 1899, and served in Sind as asst. collr. and mag.; asst. commr. in Sind, and Sindhi transl. to govt., Feb., 1905; manager, Sind encumbered estates, Oct., 1907, to Oct., 1909; dep. commr., Thar and Parkar, Oct., 1911, to April, 1913; junr. collr. and collr. and pol. agent, Oct., 1916; collr. and dist. mag. and pol. agent, April, 1919; C.I.E., Jan., 1922; on depn. as member of legisl. assembly, Sept.-Oct., 1922, and May-June, 1924; collr. and supt. of stamps, Nov., 1923; member of legisl. council, 1924-26; offg. commr., Oct., 1926; confd., June, 1927; commr. in Sind, July, 1927; memler, govr.'s exec. council, Bombay, Aug., 1929; ret'd., Oct., 1933; K.C.I.E., Jan., 1933.

HUGGINS, HENRY WALTER, Indian State Rlys. (divl. mech. engr. N.W. Rly.) (b. 10th Oct., 1880).—Joined the service as asst. loco. supt., 25th Jan., 1914; dist. loco. supt., Sept., 1925; divl. rolling stock officer, Oct., 1927; offg. dep. ch. operatg. supt., Mar., 1936; offg. dep. ch. mechl. engr., Oct., 1936.

HUGHES, ARTHUR, B.A. (Man.) Indian C.S. (jt. mag. and dep. collr., Bengal) (b. 25th Jan., 1905).—Educ. at City and County School, Chester, Victoria Univ., Manchester, and Balliol College, Oxford; joined the service, 14th Oct., 1927; arrived, Dec., 1927, and served in Bengal as asst. mag. and collr.; offg. mag. and collr., Aug., 1933; offg. addl. dep. secty. to the Govt. of India, fin., comm. and marine depts., Nov., 1936.

HUGHES, DEDWYDD WILLIAM, B.A., Indian Forest Service (dep. consvr., Burma) (b. 7th Apl., 1894).—Joined the service as asst. consvr. on 19th Dec., 1912; dep. consvr., March, 1915.

HUGHES, HENRY, A.M.I.C.E., Indian Service of Engrs. (suptg. engr., Burma) (b. 18th June, 1885).—Joined the service as asst. engr. on 1st Oct., 1907; exec. engr., Oct., 1915; offg. suptg. engr., July, 1924; confd., June, 1928; offg. dep. ch. engr., June, 1932; offg. ch. engr., Apl., 1935.

HUGHES, IAN DAVID ERSKINE, A.M.I.C.E., late Indian Service of Engrs. (b. 10th Oct., 1881).—Trained at the R.I.E. Coll.; apptd. asst. engr., 1st Oct., 1904, and posted to the Punjab irrig. branch; exec. engr., Oct., 1912; offg. suptg. engr., April, 1924; confd., July, 1931; ret'd., Aug., 1934.

HUGHES, THOMAS LEWIS, Indian Civil Service (secty. to finl. commr., Burma) (b. 27th July, 1897).—Educ. at Birkenhead Sch.; on mil. service from Sept., 1915, to Dec., 1922; joined the service, 29th Oct., 1923, and served in Burma as asst. commr.; sub-divl. officer, Jan., 1925; offg. dep. commr., Dec., 1932; ditto (provs. subs.), Mar., 1935; secty. to finl. commr., Burma, Dec., 1936.

HUGHES, VIVIAN ARTHUR, India Posts and Tels. (b. 14th Nov., 1882).—Held non-gazetted appts. from 1st Dec., 1898; on field service from 7th April, to 1st Dec., 1917; supt., tele. traffic, Apl., 1921; dep. P.M.G., Lahore, Aug., 1925; ditto, Burma Circle, Oct., 1927; ch. supt., tele. traffic, Madras, Nov., 1930; contr., tele. traffic, Mar., 1931; ch. supt., Bombay, tele. office, Jan., 1932.

HUGHES-HUGHES, EDWARD ESSEX, Indian Police (b. 11th Aug., 1915).—Apptd. asst. supt. Assam on probn. Nov., 1936.

HUGHFF, REGINALD GEORGE, Indian State Rlys. (works manager, E.I. rly.) (b. 30th Oct., 1892).—Joined the service, 11th Feb., 1921; works manager E.I. rly., Sept., 1923; offg. pers. asst. to dep. ch. mechl. engr. senr. scale, June, 1925, and again, Feb., 1929; offg. dep. ch. mechl. engr., E.I. rly., Jan., 1936.

HUGO, EDWARD VICTOR, C.M.G., V.H.S., M.D., Lieut.-Col., I.M.S. (ret'd.) (b. 5th Jan., 1865).—First commn. dated 27th July, 1892; in mil. emp. until March, 1900, when he was apptd. to spl. plague duty, Punjab; offg. civil surgn., Punjab, Jan., 1901; confirmed as civil surgn., March, 1902; civil surgn., and prof. of midwifery, etc., Lahore medl. Coll., Aug. to Oct., 1906; prof. of surgery, Lahore medl. coll., June, 1909; on mil. duty from Dec., 1914; hon. surgn. to the Viceroy, May, 1916; ret'd., April, 1922; apptd. addl. member of medical board, India Office, June, 1925; member, ditto, Nov., 1928 to Jan., 1931.

HUKAM CHAND, RAI BAHADUR, LALA, late Indian Service of Engrs. (b. 22nd Feb., 1880).—Joined the service, 11th Nov., 1903; asst. engr., Nov., 1904; exec. engr., Dec., 1912; offg. suptg. engr., March, 1925, Sept., 1926, and again, Aug., 1932; ret'd., Feb., 1935.

HUKKOO, RAJ BAHADUR (see Raj Bahadur H.).

HUKUM SINGH, THAKUR, Rai Bahadur, B.A, United Provs. C.S. (mag. and collr.) (b. March, 1884).—Joined the service, 27th Sept., 1907; dep. collr., June, 1911; offg. mag. and collr., July, 1930; confd., Sept., 1934; Rai Bahadur, June, 1935.

HULKOTI, CHANAPPA CHANVIRAPPA, LL.B., Bombay Civil Service (Judl. Branch) (dist. and sess. judge) (b. 27th Dec., 1887).—Held various judicial appointments from 22nd June, 1928; judge and sess. judge 16th Jan., 1930; dist. and sess. judge, Dec., 1936.

HULL, GEORGE, Comdr., R.I.N., ret'd., late Marine Dept., Burma (b. 2nd July, 1879). 2nd engr. and ship surveyor, Bombay, Jan., 1920, to April, 1922; offg. engr. and ship surveyor, Karachi, Nov., 1924; confd., Feb., 1925; offg. prinpl. engr. and ship surveyor and suptg. engr. to govt. of Burma, Nov., 1928; offg. ch. engr. with govt. of India, dept. of commerce, Apl., 1933; ret'd., April, 1937.

HULLAND, WALTER GEORGE, B.A., Indian C.S. (asst. collector, Bombay) (b. 28th Jan., 1908).—Educ. at the Grammar School, Barnstaple, and Jesus College, Oxford; apptd. after examination of 1930; arrvd. in India Nov., 1931, and served in Bombay as asst. collector.

HUME, ANDREW PARKE, Indian Civil Service. (jt. mag., United Provs.) (b. 24th Nov., 1904).—Educ. at University School, Hastings, Lausanne, and King's Coll., Cambridge; joined the service, 21st Oct., 1927; arrived, Dec., 1927, and served in the United Provs. as asst. commr.; it. mag., Aug., 1929; offg. dep. commr., Mar., 1932; offg. addl. dep. secty. to the govt. of India, dept. of educn., health and lands, June, 1936; officer on spl. duty, ditto, Sept., 1936; chairman, Delhi improvement trust, Mar., 1937.

HUME, ROBERT, O.B.E., Indian Police (dep. commr. Madras) (b. 1st Jan., 1896).—Joined the service, 4th Dec., 1920; asst. supt., Aug., 1923; supt., May, 1930; awarded King's police medal, Jan., 1925; O.B.E., June, 1931; dep. commr., Feb., 1935.

HUMPHREY, CECIL ALBERT, Indian C.S. (asst. commr., Assam) (b. 17th Oct., 1904).—Educ. at Uppingham, and Balliol Coll., Oxford; joined the service, 8th Oct., 1928; arrived Dec., 1928, and served in Assam as asst. commr.; offg. dep. commr., Apl., 1936.

HUMPHREY, WILFRED, Indian Police (asst. supt., N.W.F.P.) (b. 11th June, 1905).—Joined the service as asst. supt., on probn., 5th Dec., 1924; confd., Oct., 1929; asst. dir. intell. G.I.R., Mar., 1937.

HUMPHRYS, SIR FRANCIS HENRY, G.C.M.G., G.C.V.O., K.B.E., C.I.E., Lt.-Col., I.A. (ret'd.) (b. 24th Apl., 1879).—First commn. dated 23rd May, 1900; in mil. emp. to Dec., 1903, when he was apptd. sup. asst. commr.; personal asst. to chief commr., N.W.F. Province, April,

1905; asst. commr., Hazara, Oct., 1907; asst. commr., Charsadda, April, 1910; dep. commr., Bannu, May, 1910; asst. commr., Charsadda, May, 1910; asst. commr., Thal, April, 1914; dep. commr., Kohat, Sept., 1914; asst. commr., Thal sub-divn., Oct., 1914; dep. commr., Bannu, April, 1915; pol. agent, Tochi, Nov., 1915; pol. offr. with Waziristan fd. force, in addn., from May to Nov., 1917; granted temp. commn., Royal Air Force, April, 1918; dep. commr., Bannu, April, 1919; pol. agent, Khyber, Oct., 1919; C.I.E., June, 1920; offg. dep. sec. to govt. of India, for. and pol. dept., April, 1921; H.M.'s Minister at Kabul, Jan., 1922; K.B.E., June, 1924; G.C.V.O., March, 1928; K.C.M.G., June, 1929; ret'd. from I.A., Nov., 1929; high commr. in Iraq, Nov., 1929; G.C.M.G., Jan., 1932.

HUNT, EDWARD, V.D., late Indian State Railways (N.W. Rly.) (b. 18th Feb., 1875).—Joined the service, March, 1904, as asst. loco. supt.; dist. loco. supt., April, 1911; dep. loco. supt., Feb., 1920; divl. rolling stock officer, Oct., 1924; offg. divl. supt., Jan., 1925; ret'd., Feb., 1930.

HUNT, EDWIN WILLIAM, Indian Police (b. 1st Feb., 1906).—Joined the service as asst. supt., United Provs., 19th Nov., 1925; offg. supt., Nov., 1932, and again, Mar., 1933; awarded Indian police medal.

HUNT, GEOFFREY HOWARD, B.Sc., M.C., late Indian Service of Engrs. (b. 7th Nov., 1891).—Joined the service as asst. engr. on 1st Oct., 1919; asst. exec. engr., Jan., 1920; exec. engr., Oct., 1924; under secty. to govt., p.w.d., Punjab, Sept., 1933; ret'd.

HUNT, HENRY CARTER, Indian Police (dep. inspr.-genl., Bengal) (b. 19th Sept., 1889).—Joined the service as asst. supt. on 26th Nov., 1909; supt., June, 1920; dep. commr. of police, Calcutta, Feb., 1925; offg. dep. inspr.-genl., Jan., 1933, and again, Oct., 1936; confd., July, 1937.

HUNTER, ALEXANDER MELEDORE, V.D., late Indian State Rlys. (b. 26th Apl., 1882).—Held non-gazetted appt. from 7th April, 1902, to 31st Dec., 1912; offg. asst. supt., Jan., 1913; confd., June, 1913; dist. supt., Feb., 1923; supt., Feb., 1925; offg. divl. supt., Sept., 1936; ret'd., Sept., 1937.

HUNTER, CHARLES FREDERICK, Indian Service of Engrs. (exec. engr., United Provs.) (b. 12th Feb., 1887).—Joined the service as asst. engr. on 1st Oct., 1909; in milty. employ from 28th July, 1918, to 31st Jan., 1919; exec. engr., May, 1919; offg. dep. ch. engr., Feb., 1933.

HUNTER, DOUGLAS CAMPBELL, B.A., Indian C.S. (dist. and sess. judge, United Provs.) (b. 30th Aug., 1886).—Educ. at Felsted Sch., and Jesus Coll., Oxford; apptd. after exam. of 1909; arrived in India, 28th Nov., 1910, and served in the United Provs. as asst. mag., and collr., and asst. settl. offr.; it. mag., Dec., 1916; on mil. duty, July, 1918, to March, 1919; offg. as dist. and sess. judge, Nov., 1921; sess. and sub-judge, May, 1925; dist. and sess. judge, Nov., 1925.

HUNTER, FREDERICK FRASER, D.S.O., Lt.-Col., I.A., late India Survey Dept. (b. 7th Aug., 1876).—First commn., 10th Aug., 1898; in mil. emp. to Aug., 1905, when he was apptd. asst.

supt., Survey of India; dep. supt., Sept., 1912; temply. reverted to mil. duty, Nov., 1914, to Nov., 1919; in charge of Mesopotamia survey party, Nov., 1919; offg. dir., map publ. office, June, 1927; dir., Dec., 1928; ret'd., Aug., 1931.

HUNTER, GUY GUNNING RATCLIFFE, M.A., Indian Educl. Service (profr., Morris coll., Nagpur) (b. 16th Apl., 1890).—Joined the service as profr., Jubbulpore coll., 28th Dec., 1912; profr., Morris coll., Nagpur, July, 1924; offg. princl., ditto, July, 1931.

HUNTER, JAMES DE GRAAFF, C.I.E., D.Sc., M.A., late Survey of India (b. 11th Sept., 1881).—Joined the service, 25th Oct., 1907, as mathematical expert on probn.; confirmed, Oct., 1910; mathematical adviser, trigl. survey office, Dehra Dun, April, 1912; depy. supt., May, 1920; supt., Jan., 1926; offg. dir. geodetic branch, July, 1927; dir. (tempy.), ditto, Aug., 1929; C.I.E., June, 1933; ret'd., Mar., 1935.

HUNTER, Lt.-Col. JOHN BROWN DALZELL, O.B.E., M.B., F.R.C.S.E., I.M.S. (ret'd.).—Joined, 8th Dec., and served in milty. dept. prior to 5th Jan., 1908; medl. offr. H.B.M.'s consulate for Seistan and Kain, Jan., 1908; agency surg., Khyber, Aug., 1914; ditto, Baghelkhand, Oct., 1914; O.B.E., Oct., 1920; legation surg., Nepal, May, 1922; residency surg., Rajkot, Dec., 1925; ret'd., Dec., 1928.

HUNTER, THOMAS, C.I.E., M.A., B.Sc., B.Ch., M.D., Lieut.-Col. I.M.S. (ret'd.).—First commn., 27th July, 1898; apptd. offg. civil surgn., United Prov., 28th May, 1902; confirmed as civil surgn., May, 1906; profr. King George's Med. Coll., Lucknow, March, 1921; C.I.E., June, 1923; ret'd., Aug., 1928.

HURLEY, THOMAS JOSEPH, M.R.C.V.S., Indian Veterinary Service (princl. profr. of medicine, Madras Vety. Coll.) (b. 4th May, 1895).—Apptd. supt., I Circle, Civil Vety. dept. Vizagapatam, March, 1922; offg. as princl., Madras vety coll., June, 1926; and again, Sept., 1928; profr. of surg., ditto, Aug., 1928; offg. dir. of vety. services, Madras, variously from June, 1929, to Oct., 1935.

HURN, Rev. EDWARD LIDDELL ARTHUR, Indian Eccles. Estabt. (United Provs.) (junr. chaplain) (b. 5th Sept., 1905).—Apptd. chaplain on probation, 11th Nov., 1935; junr. chaplain, Nov., 1937.

HURRELL, IAN MURRAY, Indian Police (assl. supt., United Provs.), Apptd. asst. supt., United Provs. on probn., 1st Nov., 1934; confd., Nov., 1936.

HURST, THOMAS ANDERSON, late Indian State Rlys. (E.B. Rly.) (b. 21st Jan., 1875).—Held non-gazetted appt. from 2nd April, 1897, to 31st Aug., 1917; asst. engr., Sept., 1917; exec. engr., Sept., 1922; ret'd., Jan., 1930.

HUSAIN, AKHTAR, Indian C.S. (dep. commr., Punjab) (b. 1st March, 1902).—Educ. at Allahabad Univ. and St. John's Coll., Cambridge; joined the service, 19th Oct., 1926; arrived, Nov., 1926, and served in the Punjab as asst. commr.; under secty. to govt. of India. dept. of educn., &c., Oct., 1930; offg. dep. commr., Apl., 1932; confd. (subs. provrl.), Mar., 1933; addl. dep. secty. to govt. of India, for. and pol.

dept., June, 1935; junr. secty. to finl. commrs. and dep. secty. to govt. revenue dept., May, 1937.

HUSAIN, AKHTAR, Indian C.S. (jt. mag., United Provinces) (b. 10th Jan., 1907).—Educated at the Universities of Allahabad and Agra and at Corpus Christi College, Cambridge; joined the service, 11th Oct., 1932; arrived in India, Nov., 1932, and served in the United Provinces as asst. mag. and collr.; asst. commr., Ajmer-Merwara, Mar., 1937.

HUSAIN, BADR-UD-DIN TYABJI (see Tyabji, H. B.).

HUSAIN, CHAUDHRI AKBAR (see Akbar Husain, C.).

HUSAIN, HAFAZAT (see Hafazat Husain).

HUSAIN, MAQBUL (see Maqbul Husain).

HUSAIN, MIAN MUHAMMAD TASDIQUE (see Mian M. T. H.).

HUSAIN, MUHAMMAD AZIM (see Muhammad Azim Hussain).

HUSAIN, NAJABAT (see Najabat Husain).

HUSAIN WAJAHAT (see Wajahat Husain).

HUSAIN, ZAHID (see Zahid Husain).

HUSAIN, ZAKIR (see Zakir Husain).

HUSBAND, JAMES, M.B., Col., I.M.S., ret'd. (b. 6th Aug., 1876).—First commn. dated 27th June, 1901; in milty. employ. to 24th Mar., 1903; offg. civil surg., D.I. Khan, March, 1913; ditto, Wano Agency, Sept., 1913; in milty. employ. from 27th Oct., 1914; Kaisar-i-Hind medal, 1st class, Jan., 1915; offg. supt., dist. jail, Peshawar, March, 1921; civil surg., Abbottabad, July, 1923; ditto, Kumaon military, Dec., 1923; resdy. surg., Panagar, March, 1924; legation surg., Nepal, Jan., 1928; resdy. surg. and C.M.O., Baluchistan, Apl., 1929; reverted to milty. employ., Sept., 1929; ret'd., Aug., 1933.

HUSSAIN, BUNYAD (see Bunyad Hussain).

HUSSAIN, HAFAZAT (see Hafazat Hussain).

HUSSAIN, IQBAL (see Iqbal Hussain).

HUSSAIN, MUHAMMAD AFZAL (see Muhammad. A. H.).

HUSSAIN, MUHAMMAD HADI, M.A., Indian C.S. (under secty. to govt. Assam) (b. 15th Jan., 1906).—Educ. at Punjab University and School of Oriental Studies, London; joined the service 12th Oct., 1931; arrived in India, Nov., 1931, and served in Assam as asst. commissioner; under secty., under chief secty., govt. of Assam, May, 1937.

HUSSAIN, MUZAFFAR (see Muzaffar Hussain).

HUSSAIN, SHAH SYED GHULAM (see Shah, S. G. H.).

HUSSEY, BERNARD EDWARD, I.C.S. (sub-collr., Madras) (b. 14th Jan., 1909).—Educated at King Edward VI. Sch., Stafford, and Downing Coll., Cambridge; appointed after exam. of 1931; arrived in India, Jan., 1933 and served in Madras as asst. mag. and collr.; sub-collr., Oct., 1935.

HUTCHESON, GEORGE, M.B., M.R.C.S., L.R.C.P., Col., I.M.S. (ret'd.), late Jail dept., Assam (b. 17th Dec., 1874).—First commn. dated 27th July, 1898; in mil. and temp. civil employ. until Feb., 1906, when he was apptd. civil surgn. and supt. dist. jail, United Provs.; offg. principal, med. sch., Agra, May, 1918, to Aug., 1919; offg. inspr.-gen.,

civil hosp., United Provs., April, 1925; inspr.-genl. of civil hospitals and prisons, Feb., 1927; retd., Feb., 1931; war services—China Expedn., 1900 (medal).

HUTCHINGS, ROBERT HOWELL, C.I.E., Indian Civil Service (mag. and collr., Bengal) (b. 17th Mar., 1897).—Educ. at St. Paul's Sch. and Trinity Coll., Oxford; apptd. after exam. of 1920; arrived, 4th Dec., 1920, and served in Bengal as asst. mag. and collr.; jt. mag. and collr., April, 1922; under-sec., govt. of Bengal, pol. and appt. dept., Oct., 1924; offg. supt., Central jail, Alipore, Oct., 1926; addl. magte. and collr., March, 1928; addl. dep. secty., pol. dept., govt. of Bengal, Nov., 1929; offg. pte. secty. to govr. of Bengal, Feb., 1930; offg. dep. secty. pol. dept., Bengal, April, 1931; member, mining board, Bengal, Nov., 1932; offg. dep. secty. to govt. of India, dept. of educn., health and lands, Apr., 1933; and again, June, 1934; offg. dep. commr., Apr., 1935; C.I.E., June, 1935; mag. and collr., May, 1935.

HUTCHINSON, FREDERICK HEAP, B.Sc., Indian Service of Engrs. (exec. engr., United Provs.) (b. 16th Oct., 1892).—Joined the service as asst. engr. on 1st Oct., 1914; asst. exec. engr., Sept., 1922; exec. engr., Oct., 1923; agric. engr., Sept., 1929; under-secy. to govt., p.w.d., United Provs., Dec., 1931.

HUTCHINSON, WILLIAM GORDON, O.B.E., Lt.-Col., I.A. (rettd.) (b. 15th June, 1876).—First commn. dated 5th Aug., 1896; in mil temp. to April, 1903; sup. asst. commr., Punjab, Oct., 1903; pol. asst., April, 1904; ditto and asst. to resdt. in Hyderabad, May, 1904; asst. commr., Merwara, Oct., 1906; asst. to agent to govr.-gen. in Rajputana, Oct., 1909; asst. resdt., W. Rajputana States, July, 1911; asst. commr., Ajmer-Merwara, May, 1912; asst. pol. agent, E. Rajputana States, June, 1912; second asst. to agent to govr.-gen., Baluchistan, Nov., 1913; asst. pol. agent and asst. commr., Quetta-Pishin, Nov., 1914; second asst. to chief commr., Baluchistan, Feb., 1915; pol. agent, Chagai, April, 1915; also pol. adviser on Sarhad affairs, to G.O.C., Seistan, from June, 1916; O.B.E., June, 1918; pol. agent, Kalat, April, 1921; pol. agent in Southern States of Central India, Nov., 1922, and pol. agent, Malwa, March, 1924; on foreign service in Cooch Behar as vice-pres. of the council of regency, Nov., 1926; retd., June, 1931.

HUTCHISON, ALLAN MOIR, M.A. (b. 20th Mar., 1878).—Joined the service as asst. sec., Bengal legisl. council (s.p.t.), 16th Jan., 1908; confd., April, 1908; asst. sec., legisl. dept., April, 1914; dep. sec., ditto (temp.), March, 1922; 1st asst. sec., legisl. dept., May, 1924; retd., Mar., 1933.

HUTCHISON, LIONEL PHILIP, late opium dept., United Provs. (b. 24th Nov., 1884).—Joined the service as asst. proby. asst. opium agent, 2nd April, 1904; confd., March, 1906; dist. opium officer (s.p.t.); confd., Dec., 1923; retd., Aug., 1932.

HUTTON, CHARLES INGLIS, late Indian State Rlys. (b. 31st Dec., 1879).—Joined the service as asst. loco. supt., 14th May, 1903; dist. loco. supt., Mar., 1910; engine shop manager, Dec., 1914; dep. carr. and wagon supt., Nov., 1921; offg. loco. and carr. supt.; confd., June, 1929; retd., Sept., 1935.

HUTTON, JOHN HENRY, C.I.E., B.A., Indian C.S. (dep. commr., Assam) (b. 27th June, 1885).—Educ. at Chigwell Sch., and Worcester Coll., Oxford; apptd. after exam. of 1908; arrived, 27th Nov., 1909, and served in E. Bengal and Assam as asst. mag. and collr.; asst. commr., Assam, April, 1912; offg. depy. commr., April, 1920; confd., July, 1926; C.I.E., June, 1920; services placed at the disposal of govt. of India as census commr., Sept., 1929; offg. ch. secty. to govt., Assam, Apr., 1935.

HYDARI, M. S. A. (see Muhammad Saleh Akbar Hydari).

HYDE, EDGAR STANLEY, Indian C.S. (asst. commr., Central Provs.) (b. 6th Feb., 1906).—Educ. at Sedbergh and St. John's College, Cambridge; joined the service, 10th Oct., 1928; arrived, 30th Nov., 1928, and served in the Central Provs., as asst. commr.; admr., Baster state, Mar., 1934.

HYDE, HENRY ARMROID, C.I.E., M.C., Indian Service of Engineers (ch. engr., and secty., P.W.D. (B. and R.), Central Provs.) (b. 17th Jan., 1885).—Trained at R.I.E. Coll.; joined the service as asst. engr., 1st Oct., 1906; exec. engr., Oct., 1914; offg. suptg. engr., Feb., 1924; confd., June, 1929; ch. engr., and secty. to govt., P.W.D., Central Provs., Nov., 1932; C.I.E., Jan., 1936.

HYDER, LODHI KARM, C.I.E., Ph.D. Formerly prof. of economics, Aligarh Univ.; member, Taxation Enquiry comee., 1924-5; member, Banking Enquiry comee., 1930-1; substitute delegate to League of Nations, 1931; member, public services commn., Jan., 1932 to Jan., 1937; C.I.E., June, 1932; offg. chairman, public services commn., Aug.-Oct., 1936.

IBBOTSON, ARCHIE WILLIAM, C.I.E., M.B.E., M.C., B.A., Indian C.S. (dep. commr., United Provs.) (b. 25th Jan., 1886).—Educ. at King Edward's Sch., Birmingham, and Pembroke Coll., Cambridge; apptd. after exam. of 1909; arrived in India, 28th Nov., 1910, and served in the United Provs. as asst. mag.; on mily. service (I.A.R.O.), from May, 1915 (Military Cross, Feb., 1917), to June, 1919; M.B.E., June, 1919; jt. mag., Oct., 1916; offg. dep. commr., May, 1920; settlt. officer, Nov., 1923, to May, 1924; offg. mag. and collr., May, 1924; offg. dep. commr., April, 1925; confd. as mag. and collr., Aug., 1926; offg. commr., May, 1929; special commr., Alwar durbar, Jan., 1933; C.I.E., June, 1934; dep. commr., Oct., 1935.

IBRAHIM, MUHAMMAD (see Muhammad Ibrahim).

IDDON, COLWELL, M.B.E., India Office (staff officer for services questions) (b. 1st Oct., 1893).—Apptd. clerical officer, 27th Mar., 1922; asst. to jt. editors, India Office List, 1927-29; acting higher clerical officer, June, 1930; confd., Sept., 1933; M.B.E., June, 1933; apptd. staff officer for services questions, Jan., 1936.

IDLE, JOSEPH BIRKENSHAW, M.R.C.V.S., late Indian Vety. Service (b. 31st Oct., 1884).—Joined the service as supt., stock breeding, Burma, on 19th Feb., 1921; supt., central circle, Burma, Nov., 1926; dep. dir., July, 1929; retd., June, 1933.

IENGAR, HARARRI VENKATANARASINGHA
VERADA RAJ, Indian Civil Service (secty. to govt., fin. dept., Bombay) (b. 23rd Aug., 1902).—Educ. at Mysore Univ. and Queen's Coll., Oxford; joined the service, 20th Oct., 1926; arrived, Nov., 1926, and served in Bombay as asst. collr.; offg. under sec. to govt., revenue dept., Bombay, March, 1931; confd., July, 1931; passport officer to govt., Bombay, Oct., 1933; dep. secty. to govt., fin. dept., Bombay, June, 1934, and again, Oct., 1936; secty., ditto, May, 1937.

IJAZ ALI, MAULVI SAIYID, Khan Bahadur, B.A., M.B.E., late United Provs. Civil Service (b. 30th Jan., 1881).—Joined the service as proby. dep. collr., 4th June, 1903; confd., June, 1905; M.B.E., Dec., 1919; offg. magte. and collr., Feb., 1929; confd., Aug., 1930; ret'd., Jan., 1936.

IKBAL, MUSHIR KIDWAI, SHAIKH, B.A., Indian C.S. (jt. mag., United Provs.) (b. 3rd March, 1897).—Educ. at Clifton and Cambridge (Fitzwilliam Hall); served in United Provs. as asst. mag. and collr., from 28th Feb., 1922; joint mag., Nov., 1924; asst. commr., Jan., 1927; offg. dist. and sess. judge, Apl., 1929; offg. dep. commr., Sept., 1935; offg. mag. and collr., Dec., 1935.

IKRAM, SHEIKH MOHAMAD, M.A., Indian C.S. (asst. collr., Bombay) (b. 10th September, 1908).—Educ. at Government High School, Lyallpur, the Punjab University, and Jesus College, Oxford; joined the service, 2nd October, 1933; arrived in India, November, 1933, and served in Bombay as asst. collr.; dep. reg. co-op. societies.

IKRAMULLAH, MOHAMMAD, B.A., Indian C.S. (dep. Indian trade commr.) (b. 15th Jan., 1903).—Educ. at Univ. of Allahabad and Nagpur, and Trinity Coll., Cambridge; joined the service, 18th Oct., 1927; arrived, 13th Dec., 1927, and served in the Central Provinces as asst. commr.; under secty. to govt., C.P., May, 1933; under secty. govt. of India, dept. of indus. and lab., Oct., 1933; offg. dep. secty., ditto, Jan., 1936; and again, July and Oct., 1936; dep. Indian trade commr., May, 1937.

ILAHI, NUR (see Nur Ilahi).

ILLIUS, HENRY WARWICK, C.I.E., F.R.C.S., L.R.C.P., Lt.-Col., I.M.S. (ret'd.) (b. 9th Oct., 1875).—First commn. 27th June, 1901; in mil. employ till Oct., 1907, when he was employed on plague duty; offg. civil surgeon and jail supt., April, 1909; on mil. service, Aug., 1914, till Sept., 1921; C.I.E., Feb., 1921; civil surgeon and jail supt., Sept., 1921; ret'd., Oct., 1930.

ILM-UD-DIN, MOHAMMAD, M.A., Imperial Customs Service (asst. collr.) (b. 14th Nov., 1895).—Joined the service, Mar., 1923. Asst. collr. of customs, Nov., 1935.

INAM-UL, MAJID (see Majid, Inam-ul).

INAYATULLAH KHAN, B.Sc., Indian C.S. (asst. commr., Punjab) (b. 16th Feb., 1907).—Educated at Allahabad University and Christ Church, Oxford; joined the service, 10th Oct., 1932; arrived in India, Nov., 1932, and served in the Punjab as asst. commr.

INDARJIT RAI, TANDAN, Indian Service of Engrs. (asst. exec. engr., Punjab) (b. 10th Nov., 1905).—Joined the service as apprentice engr., 19th Nov., 1927; proby. asst. exec. engr., Nov., 1928; confd., Oct., 1931.

INDER, ROBERT WILFRIE, B.A., Indian Forest Service (consvr., Bombay) (b. 7th June, 1885).—Joined the service, Feb., 1911, as asst. consvr.; dep. consvr., Feb., 1916; offg. consvr., July, 1927, and again, Feb., 1928; confd., Nov., 1929.

INDRANARAYAN BRIJMOHANLAL, LL.B., Bombay Judicial dept. (judge, small causes ct., Bombay) (b. 31st July, 1885).—Officiated as addl. judge and as judge, small causes ct., Bombay, from 9th Feb., 1925, to 30th Sept., 1928; confd., Oct., 1928; offg. ch. judge, June, 1935.

INDRA MOHAN LAL, B.A., LL.B., Indian C.S. (dist. and sess. judge, Punjab) (b. 26th June, 1896).—Educ. at Punjab Univ.; joined the service, 1st Sept., 1922; arrived, 2nd Oct., 1922, and served in the Punjab as asst. commr.; offg. dist. and sess. judge, June, 1929; ditto, Nov., 1932; confd., July, 1934.

INDRAVADAN NARAYANBHAI MEHTA (see Mehta, I. N.).

INDUBHUSAN MUNDLE (see Mundle, I. B.).

INGLIS, ARTHUR LOVEDAY, B.A., late Indian C.S.—Educ. at Dulwich Coll., and King's Coll., Cambridge; apptd. after exam. of 1904; arrived, 2nd Dec., 1905, and served in Bengal as asst. mag. and collr.; jt. mag. and dep. collr., Jan., 1912; transf. to Bihar and Orissa, April, 1912; mag. and collr., Feb., 1918; serving under govt. of India, army dept., from April to Aug., 1918; sec. to govt. of Bihar and Orissa, local self-govt. dept., Nov., 1923; offg. commr., April, 1925; ret'd., Jan., 1929.

INGLIS, CLAUDE CAVENDISH, C.I.E., B.A.I., (Trin. Coll., Dub.), Indian Service of Engrs. (suptg. engr., Bombay) (b. 3rd March, 1883).—Joined the service as asst. engr., 1st Oct., 1906; exec. engr., Oct., 1914; offg. suptg. engr., Dec., 1927; confd. Nov., 1934; C.I.E., Jan., 1936.

INGLIS, HUGH ARBUTHNOT, Indian Police (supt. United Provs.) (b. 28th June, 1890).—Joined the service as asst. supt., Nov., 1910; supt., July, 1923; prinpl., police training sch., Moradabad, Mar., 1931.

INGOLDBY, ERIC, Indian State Rlys. (dir., rly. bd.) (b. 7th Jan., 1892).—Joined the service, 14th Nov., 1921; asst. mech. engr., Nov., 1922; works manager, May, 1925; offg. dep. ch. mech. engr., March, 1925; works manager, June, 1928; offg. dir., mech. engrg., Nov., 1928; on spl. duty, rly. bd., Nov., 1929; offg. dep. ch. mech. engr., Mar., 1930; confd., April, 1933; dep. ch. contr. of standardisation (temp.), Dec., 1932; confd., Feb., 1935; offg. ch. ditto, Apl., 1935; offg. dir., rly. bd., Oct., 1935; confd., Jan., 1936.

INGRAM, Rev. JAMES WILLIAM, Indian Eccl. Estabt. (presy. senr. chaplain, ch. of Scotland) (b. 10th Apl., 1890).—Apptd. chaplain-on-probation, 21st Aug., 1919; junr. chaplain, Aug., 1921; senr. chaplain, Aug., 1929; offg. presdy. senr. chaplain, ch. of Scotland, Madras, Nov., 1930; and again, Feb., 1935; confd., Aug., 1937.

INGRAM, LESTER CHARLES, Indian Police (b. 7th Feb., 1914).—Joined the service as proby. asst. supt. of police, United Provinces, Nov., 1933; confd., Apl., 1936.

INKSON, NORMAN LEOPOLD, D.S.O., V.D., Indian State Ry's. (divl. transportation supt., G.I.P. Rly.) (b. 31st Oct., 1882).—Joined the service, 11th March, 1914, and employed on Barsi Light Rly. as loco. supt.; offg. dist. loco. supt., March, 1914; in milty. employ from 22nd Oct., 1914, to 1st Jan., 1919; dist. loco. supt., Jan., 1919; asst. trans. supt., Nov., 1922; divl. ditto, Aug., 1926.

INNES, CHARLES, O.B.E., B.Sc., late Indian Service of Engineers (b. 3rd Apl., 1882).—Joined the service, 1st Oct., 1904; arrived in India, Jan., 1905; exec. engr., Oct., 1912; O.B.E., June, 1919; suptg. engr., Sept., 1923; tempy. chief engr., July, 1926; offg. dep. ch. engr., Nov., 1929; ch. engr., Sept., 1931; retd., June, 1935.

INNES, Sir CHARLES ALEXANDER, K.C.S.I., C.I.E., late Indian C.S. (b. 27th Oct., 1874).—Educ. at Merchant Taylors' School, and St. John's Coll., Oxford; apptd. after exam. of 1897; arrived, 5th Dec., 1898, and served in Madras as asst. collr. and mag.; spl. asst. settlm. offr., March, 1901, to July, 1903; under-sec. to govt. of India, rev. and agric. dept., Feb., 1907, to March, 1910; head asst. collr. and mag., March, 1910; sub-collr. and joint mag., May, 1910; on spl. duty, dept. of industries, from Oct., 1916, to March, 1919; also controller of munitions, Madras circle, under Indian Munitions Board; collr. and dist. mag. (on spl. duty), July, 1917; jt. sec., rev. and agri. dept., govt. of India and foodstuffs commr., from April, 1919; C.I.E., June, 1919; sec. to govt. of India, commerce dept., March, 1920; C.S.I., June, 1921; member, govr.-gen.'s council, Sept., 1921; K.C.S.I., Jan., 1924; govr. of Burma, Dec., 1927, to Dec., 1932; attended Indian round table conf., London, Nov., 1930-Jan., 1931; retd., Aug., 1933.

INNES, FERGUS MUNRO, B.A., Indian C.S. (dep. commr., Punjab) (b. 12th May, 1903).—Educ. at Charterhouse Sch. and Brasenose Coll., Oxford; joined the service, 16th Oct., 1926; arrived, Nov., 1926, and served in the Punjab as asst. commr.; under-sec., pol. dept., Punjab, Dec., 1927; registrar, high ct., Lahore, Dec., 1930; dep. commr. (subs. provl.), Oct., 1932; confd., Oct., 1936.

INNES, HUBERT M.D., Lieut.-Col., I.M.S. (retd.).—First commn., 28th Jan., 1899; in mil. and temp. civil employ until 10th July, 1905, when apptd. civil surgn., Bengal; on mil. service from Dec., 1914, to April, 1920; offg. inspr.-gen. of civil hosps., Assam, July, 1925; retd., Aug., 1928.

IQBAL AHMAD, B.A., LL.B., Judl. dept., United Provs. (judge, high ct., Allahabad).—Appt. addl. judge, Allahabad high ct., Oct., 1932; judge, ditto, July, 1933.

IQBAL HUSSAIN, KHAN SAHIB, MIAN, B.Sc., Indian Service of Engineers (exec. engr., Punjab) (b. 9th Mar., 1889).—Joined the service as asst. engr. on 1st Oct., 1912; asst. exec. engr., Jan., 1920; exec. engr., Oct., 1921; offg. suptg. engr., Oct., 1933.

IRADAT-ULLAH, Barr., late Bengal Civil Service. —Joined the service, 23rd May, 1899; sub. judge, May, 1918; dist. and sess. judge, June, 1924; retd., May, 1928.

IRANI, MERWAN SORAB, L.R.C.P. (Edin.), L.R.C.S. (Edin.), L.F.P. and S. (Glas.), F.R.C.S. (Edin.), Lt.-Col., I.M.S., retd. (b. 17th Nov., 1878).—Joined 30th Jan., 1904, and served in mil. dept. prior to 31st May, 1909; civil surg., March, 1913; on milty. duty from 23rd Oct., 1914, to 30th March, 1922; retd., Nov., 1933.

IRANI, RUSTOMJEE HORMUSJI, F.C.H., late Indian State Railways (b. 3rd June, 1880).—Trained at the R.I.E. Coll.; apptd. asst. engr., 1st Oct., 1903; exec. engr., Oct., 1911; asst. dep. agent, N.W. Rly., Sept., 1918; asst. agent, ditto, June, 1919; offg. dep. ch. engr., Nov., 1923; offg. divl. supt., Jan., 1925; offg. dep. ch. engr., Nov., 1926; confd., Dec., 1927; offg. dir. of civil engrg., rly. bd., Sept., 1930; offg. ch. engr., and senr. govt. inspr. of rly's., May, 1932; confd., June, 1934; retd., June, 1935.

IRELAND, FRANK WILLIAM, B.Sc., A.M.I.C.E., late Indian Service of Engineers (b. 17th July, 1886).—Joined the service as asst. engr., on 1st Oct., 1910; exec. engr., Oct., 1918; retd., Nov., 1932.

IRVING, Sir MILES, Kt., C.I.E., O.B.E., B.A., late Indian C.S. (b. 1st Aug., 1876).—Educ. at Blundell's Sch., Tiverton, and Balliol Coll., Oxford; apptd. after exam. of 1898; arrived, 10th Nov., 1899, and served in the Punjab as asst. commr.; senior sec. to fin. commrs., Punjab, April, 1914; dep. commr., and ditto, Oct., 1914; on mil. duty from Feb., 1917, to Feb., 1919; O.B.E., June, 1919; fin. sec. to govt. of Punjab, April, 1922; C.I.E., July, 1926; offg. commr., May, 1927; addl. secty. to govt., Punjab, reforms dept., Feb., 1928; commr., Feb., 1930; offg. finl. commr. (rev.), Sept., 1931; confd., Nov., 1931; temp. member, govr.'s exec. council, Punjab, Feb. and June, 1934; knighted, June, 1934; retd., March, 1935.

IRWIN, BARON (see Halifax, Viscount).

IRWIN, Rev. Canon BENJAMIN CHRISTOPHER BULTEEL, M.A., late Indian Eccl. Estab. (United Provs.) (b. 7th Feb., 1874).—Apptd. chaplain on probation, 18th Feb., 1908; junr. chaplain, April, 1908; in milty. employ from 28th Oct., 1916, to 31st Aug., 1901; senr. chaplain, April, 1918; retd., Feb., 1930.

IRWIN, CYRIL JAMES, C.S.I., C.I.E., V.D., B.A., late Indian C.S. (b. 4th Jan., 1881).—Educ. at Stonyhurst Sch., and Wadham Coll., Oxford; apptd. after exam. of 1903; arrived, 25th Nov., 1904, and served in the Central Provs. as asst. commr.; under sec. to chief commr., Feb., 1909, to March, 1910; settlm. offr., Nov., 1913; dep. commr. (provl.), July, 1917; on mil. service, Jan., 1918, to March, 1919; dep. commr., Nagpur, Feb., 1920; C.I.E., June, 1922; rev. sec. to govt., Jan., 1924; offg. commr., May, 1925, and April, 1926; commr. (provl.), July, 1929; confd., Aug., 1932; C.S.I., Jan., 1930; retd., Aug., 1936.

IRWIN, HERBERT WILLIAM, B.A., Indian Service of Engrs. (b. 11th July, 1906).—Apptd. asst. exec. engr., Bengal, 18th Feb., 1930.

IRWIN, JOSEPH BOYD, D.S.O., M.C., B.A., Indian C.S. (secty. home and eccl. depts., Bombay) (b. 6th March, 1895).—Educ. at Trinity Coll., Dublin; joined the service, 23rd Oct., 1921; arrived, 2nd Dec., 1920, and served in Bombay as asst. collr.; persnl. asst. to commr. in Sind and Sindhi translator to govt., Dec., 1921; asst. commr. in Sind and ditto, March, 1927; offg. collr. and dist. magte., Feb., 1929; and again, Mar., 1932; confd., Nov. 1935; offg. secty., home and eccl. dpts., Bombay, Feb., 1936; confd., May, 1936

IRWIN, REGINALD DE VERE, Indian State Rlys. (dep. ch. mechl. engr., E.B. Rly.) (b. 5th April, 1893).—Joined the service as asst. loco. supt., 3rd March, 1915; dist. loco. supt., March, 1924; offg. dep. ch. mechl. engr., Oct., 1934; confd., Feb., 1937.

ISAAC, PALATHUNKAL VARKI, B.A., M.Sc., D.L.C., Indian Agricul. Service (2nd entomologist) (b. 6th June, 1891).—Joined the service as 2nd entomologist, 26th June, 1922.

ISEMONGER, FREDERICK CHARLES, C.I.E., C.B.E., late Indian Police Service (b. 5th July, 1876).—Joined the service, 11th Jan., 1898; dist. supt. of police, Oct., 1905; princpl. police training sch., Phillour, Feb., 1916; on mil. service with I.A.R.O., July, 1917 to April, 1919; chief civil intell. offr., Punjab, April, 1919; principal, police training sch., Phillour, July, 1919; C.B.E., Jan., 1921; dep. insp.-gen., Punjab, April, 1925; insp.-gen., N.W.F.P., Nov., 1927; offg. dir., intell. bureau, home dept., govt. of India, April, 1928; C.I.E., Jan., 1931; ret'd., July, 1931.

ISHAQUE, HAFIZ SAYED MUHAMMAD, B.A., Indian C.S. (jt. mag. and dep. collr., Bengal) (b. 6th August, 1906).—Educ. at Aligarh University, Lucknow University and School of Oriental Studies, London; joined the service, 14th October, 1931; arrived in India, Nov., 1931, and served in Bengal as asst. mag. and collr.; jt. mag. and dep. collr., Dec., 1933.

ISHWAR, DAVAI, M.A., Indian Audit Dept. (b. 16th Oct., 1895).—Joined the service, 18th Mar., 1921; asst. acct.-genl., Punjab, July, 1923; divl. audit officer, O. and R. rly., Mar., 1925; accts. officer, N.W. rly., Apl., 1929; asst. contr. of rly. accts., Apl., 1932; asst. auditor-genl. (personnel), Mar., 1933; audit officer, Indian Stores Dept., Mar., 1936, and again, Jan., 1937; dep. acct.-genl., posts and tels., Nov., 1936.

ISLAM AHMAD, Indian Police (b. 5th Feb., 1913).—Apptd. asst. supt., United Provs., on probn., 27th Jan., 1936.

ISLAM AHMAD KHAN, MUNSHI, B.A., late Indian Police Service (b. Oct., 1877).—Joined the service on 14th Jan., 1906; depy. supt., July, 1907; asst. supt., Feb., 1920; supt., April, 1920; ret'd., Oct., 1932.

ISMAIL, PARVEZ MUHAMMAD, B.Sc., Indian C.S. (asst. commr., Punjab) (b. 27th May, 1908).—Educ. at Central Model School, Lahore, Punjab Univ. and Selwyn Coll., Cambridge; joined the service, 6th October, 1933; arrived in India, November, 1933, and served in the Punjab as asst. commr.; sub.-divl. offcr., Nov., 1936.

ISMAIL, SHAH ABUL HASANAT MUHAMMAD see Shah, A. H. M. I.).

ISVARAN, VENGUAYYAR, B.A., Indian C.S. (asst. collr., Bombay) (b. 30th June, 1908).—Educated at Madras University and Balliol College, Oxford; joined the service, 14th Oct., 1932; arrived in India, Nov., 1932, and served in Bombay as asst. collr.

IVEY, RONALD CHURCHILL, Indian State Rlys. (dep. transpn. supt., G.I.P. rly.) (b. 18th June, 1887).—Joined the service as asst. traffic supt., G.I.P. Rly., 8th July, 1919; transpn. (traffic comml.) supt., May, 1929; offg. divl. transpn. supt., May, 1933; offg. dep. transpn. supt., Jan., 1935; confd., July, 1935.

IYENGAR, KOMBAR RAMASWAMI KRISHNASWAMI, M.B., Lt.-Col., I.M.S. (dir. pasteur inst., Coonor) (b. 5th July, 1883).—First commn. dated 4th July, 1920; bacteriological officer, central research institute, Kasauli, Nov., 1914; offg. dir., March, 1925; confd., July, 1926; dir. pasteur inst., Coonor, April, 1928.

IYENGAR, MANDAYAN ANNADHORI THIRUMALAI (see Thirumalai Iyengar, M.A.)

IYENGAR, SESHADRY AMARENDRA, B.A., Indian C.S. (asst. mag. and collr., Madras) (b. 12th Feb., 1911).—Educated at La Martinière College, Lucknow, Bishop Cotton's Boys' High School, University of Madras and the School of Oriental Studies, London; joined the service, 6th Sept., 1935; arrived in India, Nov., 1935, and served in Madras as asst. mag. and collr.

IYENGAR, VANKIPURAM RANGASWAMI, India Posts and Tels. (dir., tel. engrg.) (b. 29th Apl., 1884).—Held non-gazetted appts. from 15th Oct., 1900; asst. divl. engr., Dec., 1921; divl. engr., tels., May, 1930; offg. dir., tel. engrg., July, 1932; confd., June, 1937.

JACK, ALEXANDER PURVIS, Indian Police (dist. supt., Central Provs.) (b. 21st Dec., 1889).—Joined the service as asst. dist. supt., Nov., 1909; dist. supt., Jan., 1922; awarded King's police medal, Jan., 1932.

JACK, SIR ROBERT ERNEST, Indian C.S. (judge, high ct., Calcutta) (b. 20th Dec., 1878).—Educ. at Queen's Coll., Cork; apptd. after exam. of 1901; arrived, 28th Nov., 1902, and served in Bengal as asst. mag. and collr.; transf. to E. Bengal and Assam, Apl., 1908; jt. mag. and dep. collr., March, 1909; retransfd. to Bengal, April, 1912; s.p.t. dist. and sess. judge, Oct., 1914; confd., Nov. 1915; transf. to Assam, June, 1921; offg. judge, Calcutta high ct., April-Sept., 1928; confd., Nov., 1928; knighted May, 1937.

JACKETS, CECIL HECTOR, Indian Police (asst. supt., Madras) (b. 19th Mar., 1904).—Joined the service as asst. supt., 20th Nov., 1923; asst. commr. of police, Madras, July, 1930; offg. dist. supt., Apl., 1931.

JACKSON, EUGENE WENTWORTH DISNEY, O.B.E., M.C., B.A., B.A.I., A.M.I.C.E., Indian Service of Engrs. (exec. engr., Burma) (b. 1st July, 189).—Joined the service as asst. engr., Oct., 1919; asst. exec. engr., Jan., 1920; exec. engr., Oct., 1926; O.B.E., Feb., 1937.

JACKSON, Right Hon. SIR FRANCIS STANLEY, Lt.-Col., P.C., G.C.S.I., G.C.I.E. (b. 21st Nov., 1870).—Educ. at Harrow and Trinity Coll., Cambri[ge]; finl. secty. to War Office, 1922-3; chairman of Unionist Party, Mar., 1923; P.C., 1926; G.C.I.E., Feb., 1927; govr. of Bengal, Mar., 1927 to Mar., 1932. Knight of Grace of Order of St. John of Jerusalem, Dec., 1931; G.C.S.I., May, 1932.

JACKSON, Sir GILBERT HOLLINSHEAD BLOMFIELD, Kt., M.A., late Indian C.S. (b. 26th Jan., 1875).—Educ. at Marlborough Coll., Merton Coll., Oxford, and Univ. Coll., London; apptd. after exam. of 1898; arrived, 5th Dec., 1899, and served in Madras as asst. collr. and mag.; under-sec. to govt. of India, fin. dept., Mar., 1908; head asst. collr. and mag., Madras, Nov., 1909; sub-collr. and joint mag., May, 1910; dist. and sess. judge, Aug., 1917; offg. judge, high ct., Madras, Jan., 1924; ditto, June, 1926; confd., Dec., 1927; knighted, June, 1934; ret'd., Jan., 1935.

JACKSON, GORDON NOEL, Indian Police (asst. supt., Punjab) (b. 25th Dec., 1913).—Joined the service as proby. asst. supt. of police, Punjab, Nov., 1933; confd., Nov., 1935.

JACKSON, HARRY, late Indian State Ryls. (b. 19th Aug., 1881).—Joined the service, 8th Sept., 1909; asst. loco. supt., July, 1914; dist. ditto, Feb., 1916; on milty. duty from 4th Oct., 1917, to 1st June, 1919; sec. to commee. for standardisation of locomotive, Feb., 1924, to May, 1924; ditto, wagon and coaching stock, Dec., 1924; offg. dep. dir., stores, Oct., 1925; offg. dep. ch. mechl. engr., Nov., 1927, and again, Nov., 1932; confd. (provl.), May, 1934; offg. ch. ditto, Mar., 1933; ret'd., Feb., 1937.

JACKSON, HERBERT GUY, B.Sc., A.M.I.C.E., Indian Service of Engrs. (exec. engr., Madras) (b. 30th April, 1893).—Joined the service as asst. engr., Madras, 1st Oct., 1915; asst. exec. engr., July, 1922; offg. exec. engr., Nov., 1924; confd., Aug., 1926.

JACKSON, JAMES DUNCAN, Indian Service of Engrs. (suptg. engr., Punjab) (b. 25th Aug., 1883).—Trained at R.I.E. Coll.; joined the service as asst. engr., 1st Jan., 1907; on mil. duty from 5th Sept., 1916, to 3rd Feb., 1920; exec. engr., Jan., 1915; offg. suptg. engr., May, 1928, and again, Nov., 1932; confd., Jan., 1934.

JACKSON, JAMES WOTHERSPOON CLARKE, late Indian Police (b. 5th July, 1891).—Joined the service as asst. supt., on 29th Nov., 1912; supt., June, 1923; ret'd., Apl., 1931.

JACKSON, LAURENCE HUNGERFORD, Major, I.A., Survey of India (supt.) (b. 27th Sept., 1889).—First commn. dated 8th Sept., 1909; asst. supt. (proby.), 7th Sept., 1914; in milty. employ from 18th Sept., 1914, to 18th Dec., 1919; asst. supt., May, 1921; supt., Dec., 1926; offg. dir., June, 1934.

JACKSON, LAURENCE SEPTIMUS, M.A., Indian Eccles. Dept. (junr. chaplain)—Apptd. junr. chaplain, 11th Dec., 1933.

JACKSON, LESLIE FREDRICK, late Indian State Ryls. (b. 9th Nov., 1882).—Joined the service as asst. engr., 2nd Nov., 1904; exec.

engr., Nov., 1912; offg. suptg. engr. and engr.-in-chief, June, 1925; offg. dep. ch. engr., April, 1928; confd., Nov., 1929; engr.-in-ch., Bombay-Sind construction rlwys. survey, Sept., 1930; offg. ch. engr., E.B. rly., Nov., 1932; offg. senr. govt. inspr. of rlwys., Oct., 1934; ch. engr., May, 1935; ret'd., Nov., 1937.

JACKSON, ROBERT JOHN, B.A. (Oxon), Barr., late Indian C.S. (b. 17th Oct., 1883).—Educ. at Campbell Coll., Belfast, R.U.I., and Queen's Coll., Oxford; apptd. after exam. of 1906; arrived, 9th Dec., 1907, and served in the Central Provs. as asst. commr.; sub-judge, Aug., 1914; addl. dist. and sess. judge, June, 1915; regr., judl. commr.'s ct., Dec., 1915; serving under govt. of India, army dept., from Nov., 1918, to Feb., 1919; dist. and sessions judge, Dec., 1921; ditto and legal remembr. and sec. to govt., legal dept., Jan., 1923; addl. judl. commr., May, 1929; offg. judl. commr., June, 1931; ret'd., Mar., 1934.

JACOB, Sir CLAUD WILLIAM, G.C.B., G.C.S.I., K.C.M.G., Field-Marshal, I.A. (b. 21st Nov., 1863).—Educ. at Sherborne Sch. and Royal Mil. Coll., Sandhurst; first commn., 1882; G.S.O. 1st grade, 1912; brig.-gen. commdg. Dehra Dun Brigade, 1915; ditto, Meerut Divn., Sept., 1915; commdg. 2nd Army Corps, 1916-19; K.C.M.G., 1919; chief of the general staff, India, 1920-24; K.C.S.I., Jan., 1924; G.O.C.-in-chief, Northern Command, India, 1924-25; C.-in-C. in India, 1925; sec. mil. dept., India Office, April, 1926, to May, 1930. War Services.—Zhob Valley, 1890; N.W. Frontier, 1901-2; European War, 1914-18 (C.B., wounded, mentd. in despatches ten times, promoted major-gen., lieut.-gen.) Order of St. Vladimir with swords (Russia); Grand Officier de l'Ordre de la Couronne (Belgium); Grand Officier Legion d'Honneur, Croix de Guerre (France); G.C.B., July, 1926; Field Marshal, Nov., 1926; Col. 2/10th Baluch. regt., Dec., 1928; G.C.S.I., June, 1930.

JACOB, CLYDE WILLIAM, B.A., late Indian C.S. (Punjab).—Educ. at Dulwich College, Sidney, Sussex Coll., Cambridge, and University Coll., London; arrived, 4th Dec., 1906, and served in the Punjab as asst. commr.; transld. to Bengal as asst. mag. and collr., Jan., 1911; jt. mag. and dep. collr., Oct., 1912; asst. commr., Punjab, Oct., 1916; asst. wheat commr. for India, Feb., 1917; wheat commr. for India, Nov., 1917; dep. sec. to govt. of India, rev. and agric. dept. (temp.), Oct., 1919; dep. commr., July, 1922; ret'd., June, 1928.

JACOB, KORATHU GEORGE, M.A., Imp. Customs Service (asst. collr.) (b. 9th May, 1900).—Joined the service, 14th April, 1924, asst. collr., Bombay, April, 1926.

JACOB, MAZHUANCHARY CHAKKA, B.A., Indian Forest Service (b. 8th May, 1887).—Joined the service as asst. consvr., Assam, 9th Nov., 1930.

JACOB, WILLOUGHBY ROSS LE GRAND, late Indian Forest Service (Assam) b. 27th May, 1881).—Joined the service, 29th Nov., 1903, as asst. consvr. of forests, Assam; dep. consvr., Oct., 1913; offg. consvr., May, 1923; consvr., Nov., 1924; ret'd., Feb., 1931.

JACOBSON, ERIC JOHN HART, Indian State Rlys. (transpn. supt., E.I. rly.) (b. 23rd Mar., 1894).—Joined the service, 27th Sept., 1913; transpn. supt., E.I. rly., Mar., 1926.

JAFRI, S. N. A., Home Dept., Govt. of India (dep. dir. of public information).—Appdt. dep. dir. of public information, 8th Oct., 1932; offg. dir., Mar., 1935; addl. dir. temp., July, 1937.

JAGAN NATH BHAGAT, Rai Bahadur, M.A., LL.B., Punjab C.S., Judl. bch. (dist. and sess. judge) (b. 1st Sept., 1886).—Joined the service, 16th Jan., 1911; extra asst. commr., Nov., 1913; sub-judge, Sept., 1919; dist. and sess. judge, confd. July, 1934.

JAGAN NATH JUNEJA, LALA, A.M.I.E.(Ind.), Indian Service of Engrs. (exec. engr., Punjab) (b. 27th Oct., 1888).—Joined the service, 5th Oct., 1911; asst. engr., Oct., 1912; exec. engr., Oct., 1921.

JAGANNATHAN, SARRUKAI, B.Sc., Indian C.S. (asst. mag. and collr., Bihar) (b. 18th May, 1914).—Educ. at Christian College School, Madras, Madras University and School of Oriental Studies, London; joined the service, 21st Sept., 1936; arrived in India Oct., 1936, and served in Bihar as asst. mag. and collr.

JAGAN NATH KUKAR, B.Sc., late Indian Service of Engrs. (b. 7th Aug., 1892).—Joined the service, 22nd Oct., 1914; asst. engr., Oct., 1915; asst. exec. engr., Jan., 1920; retd., June, 1934.

JAGAT NARAYAN, Indian C.S. (jt. mag., United Provinces) (b. 14th Feb., 1911).—Educ. at Government High School, Sitapur, Queen's Intermediate Coll., Benares, Thomason Civil Engineering Coll., Roorkee, and the School of Oriental Studies, London; joined the service, 6th Oct., 1933; arrived in India, Nov., 1933, and served in the United Provinces as asst. mag. and collr.

JAGAT PRASAD, C.I.E., M.A., B.Sc., late Indian Finance Dept. (b. 16th May, 1879).—Appdt. to Indian Finance Dept., 16th June, 1902; asst. acct.-gen., Bombay, April, 1905; dep. comptr., Post Office, Nov., 1908; asst. acct.-gen., Bengal, July, 1909; asst. comptr.-gen., ditto, Oct., 1909; asst. acct.-gen., Hyderabad State, April, 1910, to April, 1911; dep. acct.-gen., posts and telegraphs, India, April, 1913; asst. comptr.-gen., June, 1914; again dep. acct.-gen., posts and telegraphs, Aug., 1914; dep. acct.-gen., Punjab, Oct., 1916; dep. acct.-gen., United Provs., June, 1917; ditto, Punjab, April, 1918; ditto, United Provs., May, 1918; ditto, Bombay, May, 1918; ditto, Punjab, July, 1919; asst. auditor-gen., June, 1922; dept. contr. of currency, Northern India (temp.), March, 1924; offg. acct.-gen., Calcutta, Oct., 1925; acct.-genl., posts and tels., Delhi, April, 1928; addl. dep. auditor-gen., Feb., 1933; offg. ditto, Apr., 1933; C.I.E., Jan., 1934; retd., May, 1934.

JAGDEO SINGH, GAREWAL, SARDAR, M.R.C.V.S., Indian Vety. Service (supt., civil vety. dept., Central Provs.) (b. 18th Nov., 1894).—Joined the service, 29th Jan., 1918; supt., May, 1919; offg. dir. of civil vety. dept., B. and O., Apr. to Nov., 1930; offg. princpl., Punjab vety. coll., Apr., 1935; offg. dir. vety. services, C.P. June, 1937.

JAGDISH PRASAD, KUNWAR, SIR, K.C.S.I., C.I.E., O.B.E., M.A., late Indian C.S. (member, govr.-genl.'s exec. council) (b. 17th June, 1880).—Educ. at Allahabad Univ. and Lincoln Coll., Oxford; apptd. after exam. of 1902; arrived, 11th Dec., 1903, and served in the United Provs. as asst. mag. and collr.; jt. mag., June, 1913; O.B.E., June, 1919; mag. and collr., Feb., 1921; and sec. to govt. (addl.); C.I.E., Jan., 1923; ch. secty. to govt., United Provs., July, 1927; confd., Jan., 1928; C.S.I., Jan., 1931; commr., June, 1932; retd., Apr., 1933; member, govr.'s exec. council, United Provs., Apr., 1933; member, govr.-genl.'s exec. council, Apr., 1935; member, council of state, Apr., 1935; knighted, June, 1935; K.C.S.I., May, 1937.

JAGDISHWAR NIGAM, B.Sc., Indian C.S. (jt. mag., United Provs.) (b. 7th April, 1902).—Educ. at Allahabad Univ. and Queen's Coll., Cambridge; joined the service, 24th Oct., 1925; arrived, 28th Nov., 1925, and served in the United Provs. as asst. magte.; offg. dep. commr., July, 1928, and again, Jan., 1934; offg. mag. and collr., Nov. 1932; dir. of indus., April, 1937.

JAGTAP, BALKRISHNA PIRAJIRAO, RAO BAHADUR, L.C.E., M.R.San.I. and C., Indian Service of Engrs. (exec. engr., Bombay) (b. 30th June, 1889).—Joined the service as asst. engr., 1st Feb., 1921; asst. exec. engr., Oct., 1925; offg. exec. engr., May, 1928; confd., Oct., 1933.

JAI LAL, RAI BAHADUR, B.A., LL.B., Judl. Dept., Punjab (judge, high ct., Lahore) (b. 17th Aug., 1878).—Joined the service as asst. legal remembrancer, 1st Jan., 1920; govt. advocate, Punjab, March, 1922; addl. judge, high ct., Nov., 1924, judge, ditto, Jan., 1930.

JAI RAJ SINGHA, B.Sc., Indian Forest Service (dep. consvr.) (b. 19th Feb., 1900).—Joined the service as asst. consvr., United Provs., on 5th Dec., 1924; dep. consvr., Nov., 1929.

JAI RAM, LALA, B.Sc., late Indian Service of Engrs. (b. 17th Oct., 1887).—Joined the service, 29th Jan., 1914; asst. engr., Dec., 1916; asst. exec. engr., Jan., 1920; retd., Jan., 1936.

JAIN, LAKSHMI CHANDRA, B.Sc., Indian C.S. (jt. mag., United Provs.) (b. 30th June, 1910).—Educ. at Christ Church College, Cawnpore, Allahabad Univ., Imperial College of Science, London, and the School of Oriental Studies, London; joined the service, 9th October, 1934; arrived in India, Nov., 1934, and served in the United Provinces as asst. mag. and collr.

JAKEMAN, ARTHUR REGINALD, Indian Police (b. 1st June, 1907).—Joined the service as asst. supt., Madras, 18th Nov., 1926; awarded King's police medal, Jan., 1932; commr. of police and cmdt. of state forces, Pudukottai, Aug., 1932; reverted to Brit. serv., May, 1933; offg. dist. supt., Mar., 1934; offg. asst. cmdt. of police, Aden, June, 1936; reverted, Dec., 1936.

JAKUB, SHAH, M.A., Indian Audit Dept. (b. 2nd Apr., 1897).—Joined the service, 23rd Feb., 1920; asst. auditor, O. and R. Rly., Oct., 1924;

divl. audit officer, N.W. rly., Jan., 1926; asst. acct.-genl., Burma, Jan., 1929; offg. ch. accts. officer, N.W. rly., Sept., 1932; dep. acct.-genl., Punjab, Nov., 1933.

JAMAL-UD-DIN, M.B., Ch.B., Lt.-Col., I.M.S. (civil surg., Punjab) (b. 22nd May, 1891).—First commn. dated 1st Aug., 1914; civil surg., May, 1925.

JAMES, ANTHONY SAMUEL, M.C., India Posts and Tels. (b. 28th Apr., 1891).—Joined the service as asst. supt., 18th Dec., 1919; asst. divl. engr., Feb., 1921; divl. engr., March, 1927; dir., posts and tels., Sind and Baluchistan circle, May, 1933; offg. dir. tel. engrg., Bombay, Mar., 1934; ditto, Punjab, Apr., 1935.

JAMES, ARTHUR HULBERT, Office of High Commr. for India (higher clerical officer, store dept.) (b. 12th Mar., 1893).—Apptd. second class clerk, India Office, 26th June, 1912, and attd. to stores dept.; on mil. service from Oct., 1915, to Nov., 1917; transfed. to office of high commr. for India, Oct., 1920; exec. officer, April, 1922; higher clerical officer, April, 1926.

JAMES, BASIL REGINALD, B.Sc., Indian Civil Service (jt. mag. United Provs.) (b. 1st Aug., 1903).—Educ. at Univ. of Allahabad and Selwyn Col., Cambridge; joined the service, 20th Oct., 1927; arrived, Dec., 1927, and served in the United Provs. as asst. mag. and collr.; offg. sess. and sub-judge, Jan., 1934; offg. dist. and sess. judge, Apr., 1936.

JAMES, CLAUDE HUBERT, Indian Fin. Dept. (b. 3rd Oct., 1882).—Joined the service as asst. examr., p.w. accts., 17th Aug., 1903; dep. examr., Jan., 1907; dept. acct.-gen., Punjab, Sept., 1914; asst. acct.-gen., ditto, Nov., 1916; on depn. to milty. accts. dept., April, 1917, to March, 1919; offg. audit offr., Nushki Extension Rly., Aug., 1919; govt. examiner of accts., G.I.P. and B.B. and C.I. Rly., March, 1923; dep. auditor, B.B. rly., Nov., 1927; offg. dep. acct.-genl., rlys. (inspection), Feb., 1928; offg. audit officer, Burma Rlys., Jan., 1929; offg. dep. ch. auditor, E.I. Rly., Apr., 1930; dep. govt. examr. of accts., B.N. Rly., Nov., 1930; offg. ch. auditor, G.I.P. Rly., Apr., 1931.

JAMES, EDMUND HENRY SALT, C.I.E., C.B.E., Lt.-Col., I.A. (ret'd.) (b. 26th June, 1874).—First commn. dated 12th Dec., 1884; in milty. employ to May, 1901; with Khyber Rifles and Border Mtd. Police to April, 1903; pol. asst., April, 1903; in charge Baluchistan camp, Delhi Coronation Durbar, Sept., 1911; asst. to resdt. in Kashmir, Dec., 1912; first asst. to resident, Mysore, and sec. to the chief commr. of Coorg, June, 1917, to March, 1919; dep. commr., Hazara, May, 1920; C.I.E., Jan., 1921; offg. rev. commr., N.W.F. Prov., April, 1922; pol. agent, Dir, Swat and Chitral, D.C., 1924; offg. rev. and judl. commr. in Baluchistan, Nov., 1925, and again Oct., 1926; offg. agent to govr.-gen. in ditto, July, 1926; C.B.E., July, 1926; offg. rev. and judl. commr., Baluchistan, Oct., 1926; ret'd., June, 1929.

JAMES, JOHN FRANCIS WILLIAM, M.A., (Oxon), Barr., Indian C.S. (judge, high ct., Patna) (b. 3rd June, 1879).—Educ.

at Christ Church, Oxford; apptd. after exam. of 1902; arrived, 30th Nov., 1903, and served in Bengal as asst. mag. and collr., and asst. settl. offr.; jt. mag. and dep. collr., Oct., 1910; transfed. to Bihar and Orissa, April, 1912; dist. and sess. judge, Jan., 1918; offg. regr., Patna high ct., Feb., 1920, to Feb., 1921; regr., high ct., Patna, April, 1924; offg. judge, high ct., May, 1927; addl. ditto, Dec., 1928; judge, ditto, Mar., 1931.

JAMES, JOSEPH FRAIN, M.B., Lt.-Col., I.M.S., ret'd. (b. 1st Nov., 1882).—First commn. dated 1st Sept., 1906; in mil. employ up to 5th Feb., 1910; offg. civil surg., Feb., 1910; confd., April, 1923; reverted to milty. employ, May, 1930; ret'd., Apr., 1937.

JAMES, Rev. RHYS, B.A., Indian Eccles. Estabt. (senr. chaplain, Madras) (b. 10th Oct., 1889).—Apptd. tempy. chaplain, 1st July, 1918; junr. chaplain, Jan., 1921; senr. chaplain, Jan., 1931.

JAMES, SIDNEY HAMILTON, Dept. of Industries, Burma (chief insp. of factories expl. and mines) (b. 14th Dec., 1891).—Apptd. asst. inspr. of factories, 19th Oct., 1920; inspr., Oct., 1924; offg. ch. inspr., variously from Feb., 1925; confd., April, 1936, chief inspr. fac. expl., and mines, Apr., 1937.

JAMESON, ALEXANDER KEITH, M.A., late Indian C.S. (Bengal) (b. 26th Dec., 1883).—Educ. at Edinburgh Univ., and Balliol Coll., Oxford; apptd. after exam. of 1905; arrived, 26th Nov., 1906, and served in Bengal as asst. mag. and coll.; jt. mag. and dep. collr., Aug., 1912; settl. offr., April, 1913; mag. and collr., April, 1918; sec. to board of rev.; Oct., 1921; dir. of land records, Bengal, Feb., 1923; offg. commr., Sept., 1927; ret'd., Aug., 1930.

JAMESON, GERALD GREY, Indian Police (supt., Punjab) (b. 4th July, 1898).—Joined the service, 19th July, 1916; in mil. employ from 19th July, 1916, to 14th Nov., 1921; asst. supt., Nov., 1921; offg. dist. offr., frontier constabulary, Jan., 1925; supt., Jan., 1927.

JAMESON, THOMAS BLANDFORD, M.C., Indian C.S. (dist. and sess. judge, Bengal) (b. 5th Jan., 1894).—Educ. at Royal Gram. Sch., Newcastle-on-Tyne, and Queens' Coll., Cambridge; joined the service, 1st Oct., 1921; arrived, 26th Nov., 1921, and served in Bengal as asst. mag. and collr.; addl. dist. and sess. judge, July, 1926; offg. ditto, May, 1928; confd., Feby., 1931; offg. presdt., special tribunal, Alipore, Aug., 1933; offg. commr. for workmen's compensation, Bengal, June, 1935.

JAMESON, WILLIAM JAMES, Indian Police (b. 29th Oct., 1915).—Apptd. asst. supt., Bengal, on probn., 21st Nov., 1935.

JANAKIRAM SINGH, BANAPHAR, M.A., Indian Forest Service (dep. consvr., Madras) (b. 15th Oct., 1899).—Joined the service, 5th Dec., 1924; dep. consvr., Nov., 1928.

JANARDHANA RAO, KARASAGOD PATNA-SHETTI, RAO BAHADUR, B.A., Indian Police (dep. commr., crimes branch) (b. 15th Mar., 1892).—Joined the service, 4th June, 1915; apptd. to I.P. as dist. supt., Madras, Aug., 1932; offg. asst. inspr.-genl., April, 1936; dep. commr., crimes branch, Nov., 1936.

JANES, RONALD ARTHUR, India Office (actg. asst. acct.-genl.) (b. 28th Sept., 1888).—Educ. at Thanet Coll., Margate, and Taunton Sch.; apptd., 2nd class clerk, 4th July, 1907; on mil. service, from Dec., 1915, to Feb., 1919; minor staff clerk, Jan., 1920; acting staff clerk, April, 1921; acting higher exec. officer, April, 1922; confd., July, 1922; suptg. exec. officer, Sept., 1932; actg. asst. acct. genl., Nov., 1937; assessor of inc. tax, Dec., 1937.

JANG, RANA LOKE SHAMSHER (see Rana, L. S. J.).

JANGALWALA, NANABHAI TEMULJI, LL.B., late Bombay Judicial Dept. (b. 24th March, 1879).—Apptd. presdy. magte., 15th Apl., 1929; retd., Mar., 1934.

JAKI NATH, ATAL, Pandit, late Punjab C.S. (b. 16th Sept., 1881).—Joined the service, 12th June, 1908; extra asst. commr., Dec., 1912; offg. dep. commr., Sept., 1928; confd., Dec., 1932; offg. dir., land records, Apl., 1931; retd., Sept., 1936.

JANVRIN, JULIAS VYVIAN BRES, Indian Police (supt., Bengal) (b. 16th July, 1903).—Joined the service as asst. supt., Bengal, on 7th Dec., 1922; offg. addl. supt., Mar., 1929; offg. supt., Jan., 1930; confd., Feb., 1934.

JACQUES, COLIN CAMPBELL, Indian Police (asst. supt., Assam) (b. 11th June, 1905).—Joined the service 23rd Nov., 1925; in training to Oct., 1927; proby. asst. supt. Oct., 1927; confd., Nov., 1927.

JARBO, WILLIAM GEORGE, late Survey of India (b. 10th Mar., 1876).—Joined the service as proby. sub-assst. supt., 17th Nov., 1897; confd., Nov., 1898; extra asst. supt., Aug., 1906; dep. supt., Aug., 1925; supt., Jan., 1926; retd., May, 1931.

JARDINE, LIONEL WESTROPP, Indian C.S. Indian Political Service (b. 15th Feb., 1895).—Educ. at Charterhouse and Wadham Coll., Oxford; joined the service, 30th Sept., 1921; arrived in India, 30th Nov., 1921, and served in United Prov. as asst. commr.; city mag., Nov., 1923; pol. officer on probn., Jan., 1925; asst. commr., Nowshera, Nov., 1926; ditto, Mardan, Aug., 1927; offg. dep. commr., Peshawar, May, 1928; pol. agent, Khyber, Nov., 1928; sec. to ch. commr., N.W.F.P., May, 1930; dep. commr., Dera Ismail Khan, Nov., 1930; 1st asst. to resdt. in Kashmir, May, 1931; minister, Kashmir, Feb., 1932; pol. agent, Bundelkhand, Apl., 1934; officr. on spec. duty, Peshawar, Feb., 1936; dep. commr., Peshawar, Mar., 1936; offg. rev., and divl. commr., Peshawar, Nov., 1936.

JASBIR SINGH, KUNWAR, Barr., United Provs. C.S. (mag. and collr.) (b. 16th June, 1889).—Joined the service, 1st. Aug., 1913; under secty. to govt., United Provs., Apl., 1921; mag. and collr., Oct., 1935.

JASWANT RAI, TANEJA, RAI BAHADUR, LALA, M.A., LL.B., late Punjab Civil Service (b. 10th Aug., 1878).—Held non-gazetted appts. from 28th Jan., 1900; munisf., May, 1911; extra asst.-commr., Feb., 1913; sub-judge, Jan., 1915; dist. and sess. judge, Aug., 1930; retd., Aug., 1933.

JATAR, K. S. (see Kashinath Shriram Jatar).

JATAR, NILKANTH SHIRAM, D.S.O., M.R.C.S., L.R.C.P., Lt.-Col., I.M.S., Jail Dept., Central Provs. (inspr.-genl.) (b. 26th May, 1887).—In milty. employ prior to 21st Jan., 1922; supt., central jail, Nagpur, Jan., 1922; offg. inspr.-genl. of prisons, Feb., 1930; confd., Feb., 1934.

JATHAR, GANESH BHASKAR, B.A. (Cantab.), M.A. (Edin.), Indian Educl. Service (profr., Karnataka coll.) (b. 10th July, 1887).—Joined the service, 21st June, 1920; prof. of history, Elph. coll., Bombay, June, 1920; ditto, Deccan coll., Dec., 1924; offg. princpl., Karnataka coll., Dharwar, June, 1931.

JAVAD HUSSAIN KHAN SAHID, Khan Bahadur, Madras C.S. (sub. collr.) (b. 11th Feb., 1893).—Joined the service, 8th Sept., 1916; dep. collr., Apl., 1929; sub. collr., Nov., 1930; offg. collr., Nov., 1932; commr. to Madras corp., Jan., 1934.

JAYAKAR, MUKUND RAMRAO, M.A., LL.B., Bar.-at-law, (judge, federal ct. of India).—Member Bombay legis. cl. 1923; member legis. assembly 1926; delegate Indian R.T.C. 1930-1933; judge, federal court of India 1937.

JAYAL, CHAKRA DHAR (see Chakra, D. J.).

JAYARAJAN, P. M. R. (see Paul, M. J. R.)

JAYARATANAM, THOMAS COOKE SAMUEL, B.A., B.Sc., Indian C.S. (dep. commr., Central Provs.) (b. 10th Oct., 1893).—Educ. at London Univ., and Clare Coll., Cambridge; joined the service, 23rd Oct., 1918, and served in Central Provs. as asst. commr. from 20th Feb., 1919; under-secy., govt. of Central Provs. in addition, July, 1923; under secy., govt. of India, home dept., June, 1924; dep. commr., Aug., 1927; jt. secty., Indian franchise comee., Jan., 1932; offg. dep. sec., dept. of industries and lab., Govt. of India, July, 1932, and again Apl., 1933; offg. commr. of settlements and dir. of land records, etc., Central Provs., Oct., 1933; confd., June, 1934.

JAYARAO, SIRUGURI, B.Sc., Indian C.S. (asst. commr., Central Provs.) (b. 15th Aug., 1905).—Educ. at Madras University and St. Catherine's College, Cambridge; joined the service, 11th Oct., 1930; arrived in India, Nov., 1930; and served in Central Provs. as asst. commr.

JAYAVANT, ANANT RAMKRISHNA, Indian Police (b. 20th June, 1910).—Joined the service as proby. asst. supt., Central Provs., 20th Feb., 1932; confd., Nov., 1933.

JAYKAR, MOTABHAI SUNDARRAO, C.I.E., M.A., Bombay C.S. (collr., Bombay) (b. 1st Dec., 1884).—Apptd. dep. collr., March, 1909; actg. collr. of income tax, Dec., 1914; personal asst. to collr., March, 1924; offg. collr. and dist. mag. and pol. agent, May, 1926; collr., Oct., 1930; C.I.E., June, 1936.

JEFFERIS, CHARLES EDWARD, A.M.I.C.E., Indian Service of Engrs. (exec. engr., Punjab) (b. 1st Dec., 1885).—Joined the service as asst. engr. on 1st Oct., 1909; exec. engr., Oct., 1917; under sec. to govt., P.W.D. (irrigation), Jan., 1926; offg. suptg. engr., variously from Mar., 1929; on depn. to Orissa, Apl., 1936, to Nov., 1937.

JEFFERYS, REGINALD CASTELMAN, Indian Police (supt., Punjab) (b. 15th Aug., 1922)—Joined the service, 15th Nov., 1912; asst. supt., May, 1914; on mil. duty from 6th Nov., 1917, to 2nd March, 1919; supt., Feb., 1924; awarded King's police medal, Jan., 1931; offg. dep. inspr.-genl., Mar., 1936.

JEHANGIR, SIR COWASJI, Bart., K.C.I.E., O.B.E.—Temp. member of govr.'s exec. council, 8th Dec., 1912, to 12th July, 1922; confd., 23rd June, 1923; O.B.E., Jan., 1918; K.C.I.E., June, 1927; ret'd., June, 1928; delegate to Indian round table conf., London, Nov., 1930; Jan., 1931; 2nd session, Sept.-Dec., 1931; 3rd session, Nov., 1932; member, legisl. assembly, Jan., 1931; member of Ind. delegation to monetary and economic conf., London, June, 1933; member, Ind. res. bank ctee., July, 1933.

JENKIN, JAMES LESLIE, Office of High Commissioner for India (principal exec. officer, store dept.) (b. 23rd Aug., 1888).—Educ. at King Edward VI.'s Grammar Sch., Grantham; apptd. clerk, 2nd class, 10th June, 1907 transf'd. to office of high commissioner for India, Oct., 1920; asst. controller, store dept., April, 1921; principal exec. officer, April, 1922.

JENKIN, REGINALD TREVOR, M.A., Indian Forest Service (dep. consvr., Central Provs.) (b. 25th Aug., 1887).—Joined the service as asst. consvr., Dec., 1912; asst. postal censor, Bombay, from 27th April, 1915; dep. consvr., Sept., 1919; offg. consvr., variously from May, 1931.

JENKIN, WILLIAM NORMAN PRENTICE, C.I.E., Indian Police (supt.) (b. 11th Aug., 1899).—Joined the service as asst. supt., Punjab, on 27th Dec., 1919; awarded King's police medal, Jan., 1925; offg. supt., April, 1927; confd. May, 1930; C.I.E., June, 1931; cent. intell. officer., Punjab, Delhi and Sind, Nov., 1935.

JENKINS, EVAN MEREDITH, C.I.E., Indian C.S. (ch. commr., Delhi) (b. 2nd Feb., 1896).—Educ. at Rugby and Balliol Coll., Oxford; joined the service, 25th Oct., 1920; arrived, 4th Dec., 1920, and served in the Pubjab as asst. commr.; offg. dep. commr., Nov., 1925; ditto (provl.), Oct., 1927; ditto (perm.), Jan., 1932; dep. secty. to the govt. of India, dept. of indus. and lab., Nov., 1933; offg. jt. secty., ditto, June, 1935, and again, Jan., 1936; C.I.E., June, 1936; ch. commr., Delhi, Mar., 1937.

JENKINS, JAMES BARROW, late Indian Police Service (Bombay) (b. 14th Nov., 1878).—Joined the service, 17th Nov., 1899; supt. of police, May, 1908; princpl., police training sch., Nasik, June, 1914, to Aug., 1916; asst. to inspr.-gen., April, 1918; offg. dep. inspr.-gen., April, 1921; confd., Oct., 1924; ret'd., June, 1931.

JENKINS, JOHN LEWIS, Indian Police (asst. inspr.-genl., Bengal) (b. 8th Sept., 1897).—Joined the service as asst. supt., on 27th Jan., 1920; addl. supt., April, 1926; supt., Nov., 1929; asst. inspr.-genl., Aug., 1934.

JENKINS, WALTER ALLEN, D.Sc. (Sheffield), Indian Educl. Service (prin., David Hare training coll.) (b. 1st Apl., 1891).—Joined the service as prof. of Dacca Coll. on 2nd March, 1916; services placed at disposal of Univ. of Dacca from July, 1921 to July, 1926; offg. inspr. of schools, Dec., 1926; confd., Jan., 1927;

offg. asst. dir. public instrn., Jany., 1931; prin., Rajshahi Coll., Mar., 1932; offg. special officer for primary educn., Mar., 1933; prin., David Hare training coll., Nov., 1934.

JENKINS, WILLIAM JOHN, M.A., B.Sc., Indian Agric. Service (dep. dir. of agric., Sind) (b. 27th Oct., 1892).—Joined the service as dep. dir. of agric., Dec., 1920; dep. secty., Indian Central Cotton Comtee.; offg. dir. of Institute of Plant Industry, Indore, May to Novr., 1927; dep. dir. of agric., Sind, June, 1930; offg. dep. dir., Bombay, Nov., 1936.

JENKS, ROBERT LEONARD, F.I.C., F.C.S., late Imperial Customs Service (Bengal) (b. 8th Mar., 1871).—Joined the service, 23rd Nov., 1904; chemical examr., Jan., 1912; ret'd., Jan., 1929.

JENKYNS, HUGH HOBHOUSE, B.A., late Indian C.S. (b. 8th Nov., 1879).—Educ. at Winchester Coll., and Balliol Coll., Oxford; asst. commr.; personal asst. to pol. agent, apptd. after exam. of 1902; arrived, 30th Nov., 1903, and served in the Punjab as Phulkian States, &c., April, 1906, to Aug., 1907; private sec. to lieut.-gov., Punjab, Dec., 1906, to March, 1907; under sec. to govt., Feb. to Sept., 1910; asst. settl. offr., Nov., 1912; judge, small cause ct., Simla, March, 1917; dep. commr., Nov., 1919; offg. commr., Dec., 1927; ret'd., July, 1929.

JENNER, ERIC JOHN, Indian Police (asst. supt.) (b. 11th Feb., 1910).—Joined the service as proby. asst. supt., Bombay 25th Nov., 1930; confd., July, 1932.

JENNETT, STANLEY, B.Sc., Indian Service of Engrs. (asst. exec. engr.) (b. 6th Aug., 1904).—Joined the service as proby. asst. exec. engr., Punjab, 1st Oct., 1928; confd., Sept., 1930.

JENNINGS, ALFRED KICKWEED, Office of High Commr. for India (higher clerical officer, store dept.) (b. 30th Mar., 1890).—Apptd. 2nd class clerk, 2nd Feb., 1914; on mil. service from June, 1915, to June, 1919; transf'd. to office of high commr. for India, Oct., 1920; exec. officer, April, 1922; higher clerical officer, May, 1926.

JENNINGS, EDGAR, Indian Police (dist. supt., Burma) (b. 18th Mar., 1895).—Joined the service as asst. dist. supt., Burma, on 19th Feb., 1921; dist. supt., Mar., 1929.

JEPHSON, MAURICE KEARLEY, India Office (higher executive officer, mil. dept.) (b. 2nd Apl., 1893).—Apptd. second class clerk, 26th Feb., 1912, and att'd. to registry and record dept.; transf'd. to rev. and statistics dept., Jan., 1917; on mily. service from March, 1917, to Sept., 1919; exec. officer, April, 1922; actg. higher exec. officer, June, 1924, to June, 1925; confd., Aug., 1927.

JERROM, JOHN HENLEY GRIMLEY, M.V.C.V.S., Indian Veterinary Service (dir. vety. services, Sind) (b. 6th Dec., 1886).—Joined the service as supt. civil veterinary dept., Sind, March, 1921; offg. dir., vety. services, Bombay, Apl., 1933; dir., vety. services, Sind, Apl., 1936.

JESSON, ARTHUR WILLIAM MOSLEY, A.M.I.C.E., Indian Service of Engrs. (supt. engr., Punjab) (b. 12th Aug., 1884).—Joined the

service, 20th Sept., 1905; asst. engr., Oct., 1906; exec. engr., Oct., 1914; offg. suptg. engr., June, 1927; confd., Apl., 1932.

JETLEY, BRIJ BHUSHAN SHARAN (see Brij, B. S. J.).

JEVONS, HERBERT STANLEY, M.A., B.Sc., late Indian Educl. Service (Burma).—Joined the service as lecturer in economics, Univ. Coll., Rangoon, 10th Aug., 1923; ret'd., Oct., 1931.

JHA, ADITYA NATH, M.A., Indian C.S. (asst. commr., U.P.) (b. 18th Aug., 1911).—Educ. at Jesus College, Oxford; joined the service, 16th Sept., 1936; arrived in India, Oct., 1936, and served in the United Provinces as asst. mag. and collr.; asst. commr., Oct., 1936.

JHA, BHOLA NATH, Indian C.S. ((jt. mag., United Provs.) (b. 23rd Feb., 1902).—Educ. at Allahabad Univ., and Exeter Coll., Oxford; joined the service, 22nd Oct., 1926; arrived, 27th Nov., 1926, and served in the United Provinces as asst. mag. and collr.; jt. mag., 27th Oct., 1929; secty. bd. of rev., Dec., 1937.

JHA, CHANDRA SHEKHAR, M.Sc., Indian C.S. (under secty., fin. dept., Bihar) (b. 20th Oct., 1909).—Educ. at the Watson High English School, Madhubani, Patna Univ. and the School of Oriental Studies, London; joined the service, 3rd Oct., 1933; arrived in India, Nov., 1933, and served in Bihar and Orissa as asst. mag. and collr.; under secty., fin. dept., Bihar, Oct., 1936.

JHA, GANGA NATH (see Ganga N., J.).

JHA, LAKSHMI KANT, B.A., Indian C.S. (asst. mag. and collr., B. & O.) (b. 22nd Nov., 1913).—Educ. at Barari H.E. School, Bhagalpur, Benares Univ. and Trinity College, Cambridge; joined the service, 20th Sept., 1937; arrived in India, Nov., 1937 and served in B. & O. as asst. mag. and collr.

JHANJI KANWARLAL, B.A., late Indian Service of Engrs. (b. 3rd Nov., 1885).—Joined the service, 26th Sept., 1908; asst. engr., July, 1910; exec. engr., Sept., 1917; ret'd., Apl., 1937.

JHAVERI, CHIMANLAL MANEKLAL, B.A., LL.B., late Judicial dept., Bombay (b. 17th Aug., 1878).—Joined the service, 1st Aug., 1908; sub-judge, June, 1910; judge, small cause ct., Ahmedabad, June, 1930; ret'd., Aug., 1933.

JHAVERI, KRISHNALAL MOHANLAL, M.A., LL.B., late Judl. Dept., Bombay.—Apptd. actg. judge, small cause ct., Bombay, 1st April, 1905; judge, ditto, Jan., 1907; chief judge, ditto, Oct., 1919; addl. judge, Bombay high ct., Sept., 1927; ret'd., Dec., 1928.

JHUNNA SINGH, B.Sc., Indian Forest Service (dep. consvr. Punjab) (b. 31st July, 1898).—Joined the service as asst. consvr. on 2nd Dec., 1922; dep. consvr., Nov., 1926.

JIA RAM, Indian Police (b. 21st Nov., 1912).—Apptd. asst. supt., United Provs., on probn., Feb., 1937.

JIVA NAND PANDE, B.A., Indian Police (supt., United Provs.) (b. 19th Dec., 1883).—Joined the service as dep. supt., Jan., 1908; supt., March, 1925.

JIWAN AMRIK SINGH, B.A., Dip. of Forestry, Indian Forest Service (dep. consvr., Bombay) (b. 10th July, 1901).—Joined the service as asst. consvr., Nov., 1923; dep. consvr., Nov., 1927.

JOG, DATTATRAYA PARASHURAM (see Parashuram Jog, D.).

JOHN, JORDAN CONSTANTINE, O.B.E., M.B., Major, I.M.S., Civil Medl. Dept., Bihar (civil surg.) (b. 22nd Sept., 1886).—First commn. dated 27th July, 1912; O.B.E., June, 1919; in mil. employ up to 30th April, 1923; civil surg., Sept., 1924.

JOHN, RICHARD STANLEY THOMAS, B.A., Indian C.S. (b. 2nd Feb., 1909).—Educ. at Haverfordwest Grammar School, University Coll., Cardiff, and Exeter Coll., Oxford; apptd. after exam. of 1931; arrived in India, December, 1932, and served in Bengal as asst. mag. and collr.; jt. mag. and dep. collr., Nov., 1934; asst. commr., Dera Ismail Khan, Dec., 1936; asst. pol. agent, Chitral, Feb., 1937.

JOHN, WILLIAM REES, late Indian Police (b. 21st Feb., 1892).—Joined the service, 16th Nov., 1912; asst. supt., Nov., 1913; service with mil. dept. from 24th Oct., 1915, to 7th March, 1916; I.A.R.O. from 20th June, 1917; supt. in charge of agency operations, Apl., 1923; offg. supt., Aug., 1924; prinpl., police training school, Vellore, June, 1928; pers. asst. to dep. inspr.-genl. of police (rlwys. and C.I.D.), Madras, Apl., 1930; awarded King's police medal; ret'd., July, 1933.

JOHNSON, FRANK, India Office (higher clerical officer, milty. dept.) (b. 10th Dec., 1883).—Apptd. clerical officer, 17th Oct., 1921; acting higher ditto, Sept., 1933; confd., Dec., 1934.

JOHNSON, JAMES RUSSELL, Indian Police (supt., Bengal) (b. 15th May, 1891).—Joined the service as proby. dep. collr., 22nd May, 1911; dep. mag. and dep. collr., May, 1913; proby. asst. supt. of police, March, 1920; confd., June, 1921; asst. supt., May, 1920; addl. supt., April, 1921; offg. supt., April, 1924; confd., Aug., 1926.

JOHNSON, SIR JOHN NESBITT GORDON, C.S.I., C.I.E., Indian C.S. (b. 25th Feb., 1885).—Educ. at Rossall, and Queen's Coll., Oxford; apptd. after exam. of 1908; arrived, 23rd Nov., 1909, and served in the United Provs. as asst. mag.; under-sec. to govt., May, 1915, to April, 1916; jt. mag., April, 1917; regr., high ct., Allahabad, March, 1918; on mil. service (I.A.R.O.), Aug., 1918, to Feb., 1919; again regr., high ct., Allahabad, March, 1919; mag. and collr., Dec., 1924; again regr., high ct., Allahabad, Nov., 1920; dep. commr., Delhi, Nov., 1924; offg. dep. sec., govt. of India, industries and labour dept., May, 1925; dep. commr., Delhi, Sept., 1926; C.I.E., Jan., 1928; offg. ch. commr., Delhi, March, 1928; confd., March, 1932; C.S.I., June, 1936; vacated appt. of chief commr., Mar., 1937; knighted, May, 1937.

JOHNSON, NORMAN, Indian State Rlys. (dep. carr. and wagon supt., Burmarlys.) (b. 31st July, 1894).—Joined the service, 1st Apl., 1920; asst. rolling stock officer, Dec., 1924; asst. works manager, Nov., 1927; asst. loco. supt., Nov., 1932; dist. ditto, Burma Rlys., Mar., 1933; offg. dep. carr. and wagon supt., Mar., 1933; confd., Dec., 1934.

JOHNSON, STANLEY MERRY, M.C., Indian State Rlys. (bridge engr.) (b. 5th Jan., 1896).—Joined the service, 3rd Dec., 1923; asst. bridge engr., July, 1928; exec. engr. (bridges), N.W. Rly., Mar., 1931; offg. dep. ch. engr. (bridges), N.W. rly., Mar., 1936.

JOHNSON, The Ven. WILFRID HARRY COWPER, M.A., late Indian Eccles. Estabt. (b. 24th Feb., 1879).—Apptd. chaplain on probation, 24th Feb., 1909; junr. chaplain, Feb., 1912; senr. chaplain, Feb., 1922; ret'd., Oct., 1932.

JOHNSON, ALEXANDER, India Office (higher clerical officer, acctnl.-gen.'s dept.) (b. 6th Dec., 1882).—Apptd. clerical officer, 17th Oct., 1921; higher clerical officer, 6th April, 1926.

JOHNSON, DAVID PERCIVAL, A.R.C.Sc.I., Indian Agricl. Service (asst. dir. of agric., Punjab) (b. 29th Jan., 1890).—Joined the service as dep. dir. of agric., 1st Dec., 1920; offg. prof. of agric., Punjab agricl. coll., July, 1927; confd., May, 1933; offg. asst. dir. of agric., Mar., 1930; confd., Oct., 1933.

JOHNSON, DAVID SETON, C.I.E., Lt.-Col., R.E., Indian Service of Engrs. (suptg. engr., Bombay) (b. 7th Aug., 1886).—Joined as asst. engr., 21st Sept., 1909; on milty. duty from 25th Oct., 1911, to 1st Sept., 1919; exec. engr., Feb., 1917; on deputation as chairman and ch. engr., Aden Port Trust, &c., Oct., 1924; C.I.E., Jan., 1933; chairman, Karachi Port Trust, May, 1934; suptg. engr., Dec., 1934.

JOHNSON, ERIC FURKER, Lt.-Col., R.E., late Indian State Rlys. (b. 9th Aug., 1891).—First comm., dated 20th July, 1911; joined Indian State Rlys., as asst. exec. engr., 18th Nov., 1920; exec. engr., Jan., 1923; ret'd., July, 1937.

JOHNSON, Sir FREDERICK WILLIAM, K.C.I.E., C.S.I., B.A., LL.B., late Indian C.S. (b. 2nd Nov., 1872).—Educ. at Glasgow Univ. and Trinity Hall, Cambridge; apptd. after exam. of 1895; arrived, 17th Dec., 1896, and served in the Punjab as asst. commr.; pol. agent, N.W.
