acctnt.-gen., Punjab, May to Nov., 1909; asst. commr., United Provs., Oct., 1910; jt. mag. and dep. collr., Jan., 1912; dep. commr. (s.p.t.), and settlt. offr., Oct., 1915; mag. and collr., Nov., 1920; C.I.E., June, 1924; commr., Dec., 1931; member, bd. of rev., United Provs., Dec., 1934; chairman, ditto, May, 1935; chairman, excise advisory bd., United Provs., Nov., 1935; retd., Nov., 1936.

KOCHAR, DAULAT RAM, B.Sc., Indian Service of Engrs. (exec. engr., Central Provs.) (b. 26th June, 1889).—Joined the service, 13th April, 1913; asst. engr., June, 1919; asst. exec. engr., Jan., 1920; offg. exec. engr., April, 1920; confd., June, 1928; under-secty., P.W. dept. (bldgs. and rds.), Sept., 1929.

KOHLI, DHARAMNATH PRASAD, M.Sc., Indian Police (asst. supt.) (b. 8th Feb., 1907).—Joined the service as proby. asst. supt., United Provs., 5th Jan., 1931; confd., Feb., 1933.

KOIMATTUR, SHRINIWAS SHESHO, RAI BAHADUR, late Bombay Civil Service (b. 25th Sept., 1874).—Held non-gazetted appts. from 2nd Apl., 1896, to 11th Jan., 1912; mamlatdar, Jan., 1912; dep. collr., June, 1913; dist. dep. collr., Jan., 1920; offg. collr. and dist. magte., Feb., 1929; retd., Sept., 1929.

KOLAND, VICTOR SAMUEL, B.E., Indian State Railways (dist. loco. supt., E.B. Rly.) (b. 20th May, 1894).—Asst. loco. supt. (junior scale), 5th May, 1922; dist. loco supt. (senr. scale), Nov., 1936.

KOLATKAR, DIGAMBAR KRISHNA, B.A., LL.B., late Central Provs. C.S. (b. Aug., 1874).—Joined the service, 2nd April, 1901; sub-judge, Jan., 1908; offg. dist. and sess. judge, Jan., 1926; confd., May, 1926; retd., Aug., 1929.

KOMAN, MALIKAYAL CHANDAPPA BALACHANDRA (see Balachandra Koman, M. C.).

KORDIK, OSCAR, M.M., India Office (higher executive officer, acctnt.-gen.'s dept.) (b. 19th May, 1893).—Appdtd. second class clerk, India Office, 30th Jan., 1913, and attd. to finl. dept.; on mil. service, from Sept., 1915, to Feb., 1919 (awarded the Military Medal); exec. officer, April, 1922; higher ditto, Aug., 1931.

KOREISHI, ABDULLAHMIA OOSMANMIA, M.A., late Bombay C.S. (b. 5th Jan., 1876).—Joined the service, 1st Feb., 1900; administrator, Radhanpur State, June, 1909, to May, 1910; collr. and mag., March, 1922; retd. Dec., 1931.

KORGAOKAR, HARISH CHANDRA KRISHNARAO, M.A., B.Sc., A.C.G.I., A.M.I.C.E., Indian State Rlys. (exec. engr., E.B. Rly.) (b. 16th Sept., 1885).—Asst. engr., 1st Oct., 1917; asst. exec. engr., Aug., 1919; exec. engr. (prov.), July, 1937.

KOTHAVAALA, HECTOR RATANJI, M.V.O., O.B.E., late Indian Police Service (b. 26th July, 1874).—Held non-gazetted appts. from 11th Oct., 1894, to Dec., 1907; dep. supt., Dec., 1907; awarded King's police medal, Jan., 1915; M.V.O., Feb., 1921; on spl. duty in connection with the tour of H.R.H. the Prince of Wales from Sept., 1921, to March, 1922; O.B.E., March, 1922; apptd. to the I.P.S. as supt. of police, Aug., 1922; retd., July, 1929.

KOTHAWALA, DARASHAH DHANJISHAH, Khan Bahadur, M.B.E. (supt., Bombay) (b. 22nd Feb., 1885).—Joined the service, 5th July, 1909; M.B.E., Dec., 1930; promoted to I.P. as supt., May, 1936.

KOTVAL, KHARSHEDIJ EDALJI, late Indian Forest Service (b. 9th Sept., 1874).—Held non-gazetted appts. from 28th Jan., 1896, to 20th Feb., 1909; extra asst. consvr., Feb., 1909; dep. consvr., Nov., 1923; retd., Sept., 1929.

KRISHEN, PREM, M.A., Indian C.S. (jt. mag., U.P.) (b. 20th Nov., 1911).—Educ. at St. Edward's High School, Simla, Delhi University and Christ Church, Oxford; joined the service, 4th Sept., 1935; arrived in India, Sept., 1935, and served in the United Provinces as asst. mag. and collr.; jt. magte., July, 1937.

KRISHNA AIYAR, NARNAMMALPURAM CHELLUM, M.A., late Indian Educl. Service (b. 31st Aug., 1879).—Joined the service as asst. lecturer in physics and mathematics, Rangoon, 21st July, 1908; lecturer ditto, Mar., 1910; prof. of physics, June, 1922; retd., Aug., 1934.

KRISHNA AIYAR, SAMBAVISA, B.A., B.L., Indian Audit Dept. (b. 10th July, 1884).—Joined the service, 27th Mar., 1907; asst. accts. officer, posts and tels., Madras, Apl., 1924; dep. acct.-genl., Madras, Sept., 1931; audit officer, C.M. project, May, 1933; attd. officer, office of acct.-genl., Bihar and Orissa, Sept., 1934; dep. acct.-genl., Madras, Nov., 1935; dep. acct.-genl., posts and tels., Madras, Apl., 1937.

KRISHNA, DAYA, KHANNA (see Daya Krishna, K.).

KRISHNA KANIYA LAL, B.Sc., late Indian Service of Engrs. (b. 22nd June, 1890).—Joined the service, 12th Oct., 1914; asst. engr., June, 1919; asst. exec. engr., Jan., 1920; retd., May, 1932.

KRISHNAMA ACHARYA, Sir VANGAL TIRUVENKATA, K.C.I.E., B.A., B.L., Rao Bahadur, late Madras Civil Service (b. 8th Feb., 1881).—Joined the service as dep. collr., 12th April, 1903; asst. secty. to bd. of rev., land rev., Dec., 1913; under-secty. to govt., local and municipal dept., Jan., 1916; trustee of Vizianagram estate, June, 1919; collr. and dist. magte., July, 1920; offg. secty. to govt., law dept., April, 1924; on foreign service as diwan of Baroda State, Feb., 1927; and member, bd. of rev., Madras, Sept., 1932; 1st member, ditto, Aug., 1933; Kt., Jan., 1933; member, Ind res. bank ctee., July, 1933; del. of India at Assembly of League of Nations, 1933 and 1934; 1st member, bd. of rev., Aug., 1935; K.C.I.E., Jan., 1936; retd., Feb., 1936.

KRISHNAMMA, NOBLE RATNAM, B.A. (Cantab.), Indian Educl. Service (prin., ceded dists., coll., Anantapur) (b. 1st June, 1887).—Joined the service, 2nd April, 1910; prin., govt. arts coll., Rajahmundry, July, 1921; ditto, Coimbatore, April, 1925; offg. dist. edul. officer, Dec., 1925; prin. ceded districts coll., Anantapur, April, 1926.

KRISHNAMOORTHY, ENNAPPADAM SUNDERA, Ayyar, B.A., Imperial Customs Service (asst. collr.) (b. 15th Nov., 1905).—Joined the service, Apl., 1930; asst. collr. of customs, Nov., 1931.

KRISHNA RAO, KARNAD, M.A., Indian Audit Dept. (b. 17th Nov., 1899).—Joined the service, 5th May, 1924; dep. dir. of rly. audit, Apl., 1934; dep. acct.-genl., Punjab, Apl., 1937.

KRISHNAMURTI AYYAR, VADAPURI, Indian Veterinary Service (b. Nov. 1885).—Apptd. prof. of pathology and bacteriology, Madras, 30th June, 1922; offg. prinpl., vety. coll., Madras, May, 1929, and again, Apl., 1933.

KRISHNAMURTI, VEPA, Indian Service of Engrs. (b. 13th Sept., 1910).—Joined the service as proby. asst. exec. engr., Madras, 30th April, 1933; confd., Jan., 1934.

KRISHNA NARAYAN KATHPALIA, B.A., Indian Service of Engrs. (exec. engr., United Provs.) (b. 19th Jan., 1902).—Joined the service as apprentice engr., 3rd Nov., 1924; asst. exec. engr., Nov., 1925; offg. exec. engr., Nov., 1929; confd., Oct., 1934.

KRISHNA PRASADA, B.A., Indian C.S. (jt. mag., United Provs.) (b. 4th Aug., 1894).—Educ. at New Coll., Oxford; joined the service, 12th Dec., 1921; arrived in India, 6th Jan., 1922, and served in United Provs. as asst. mag. and collr.; jt. mag., Nov., 1925; offg. mag. and collr., Mar., 1926; confd., Aug., 1935; p.m.g., central circle, Feb., 1935; ditto, Madras, June, 1937.

KRISHNA RAO, NANDALIKE DEVARAO, Indian C.S. (sub-collr. and jt. mag., Madras) (b. 19th July, 1904).—Educ. at University of Madras, School of Economics, London and Brase-nose Coll., Oxford; joined the service, 28th Sept., 1928; arrived, Nov., 1928, and served in Madras as asst. collr. and mag.; sub. collr. and jt. mag., Mar., 1930.

KRISHNAN NAYAR, DIWAN BAHADUR, Sir M., Kt.—Member of govt.'s exec. council, Madras, 25th Apl., 1928, to Apl., 1934; Kt., Jan., 1930.

KRISHNAN, HARIKESANALUR RAMASASTRI, M.Sc., Indian C.S. (asst. mag. and collr., Bihar.) (b. 9th Jan., 1910).—Educated at Tirthpathi High School, University of Madras, University of Benares and Sidney Sussex College, Cambridge; joined the service, 6th Sept., 1935; arrived in India, Oct., 1935, and served in B. & O. as asst. mag. and collr.

KRISHNAN, ODAYATH, B.A., Imperial Customs Service (asst. collr.) (b. 15th June, 1900).—Joined the service, Apl., 1925; asst. collr. of customs, Mar., 1927.

KRISHNAN, SIVARAMA (see Sivarama Krishnan).

KRISHNASWAMI AYYANGAR, KOMBUR RAMASWAMI Major, I.M.S. (b. 5th July, 1883).—Apptd. dir., Pasteur Institute, Coonoor, 11th Apl., 1928.

KRISHNASWAMI AYYAR, Diwan Bahadur, Sir ALLADI, Kt., B.A., B.L., Judl. Dept., Madras (b. 13th May, 1883).—Apptd. Advocate-genl. for presdy. of Madras, Jan., 1929.

KRISHNASWAMI, V. S., Indian Forest Service (dep. consvr.) (b. 12th Aug., 1903).—Joined the service as asst. consvr., Madras, 4th Oct., 1929; dep. consvr., Oct., 1934.

KRISHNASWAMY, RANGACHARI SRINI-VASADESIKA, B.A., Indian C.S. (jt. mag. and dep. collr., Bengal) (b. 2nd June, 1906).—Educ. at Madras University and London School of Oriental Studies; joined the service 10th Oct., 1930; arrived in India, Nov., 1930, and served in Bengal as asst. mag. and collr.; jt. mag. and dep. collr., May, 1933.

KRISHNASWAMY, SUCHEENDRUM YEGNA-NARAYANA, Indian C.S. (sub-collr., Madras) (b. 30th Sept., 1906).—Educ. at University of Madras and School of Oriental Studies, London; joined the service, 9th Oct., 1919; arrived, Nov., 1929, and served in Madras as asst. mag. and collr.; sub-collr., Aug., 1932.

KUDVA, V. NARAYANA (see Narayana Kudva, V.).

KUKAR, JAGAN NATH (see Jagan Nath, K.).

KULKARNI, LAKSMAN PANDURANG, Diwan Bahadur, M.A., late India Posts and Telegraphs (b. 12th June, 1880).—Joined the service as proby. supt., 16th May, 1902; confd., May, 1904; offg. asst. dir.-genl., April, 1920; confd., May, 1922; offg. dep. dir.-genl., Jan., 1930; offg. P.M.G., U.P., Apl., 1931; ditto, Bihar and Orissa, May, 1932; reid., June, 1935.

KUMAR ASANAND (see Asanand Kumar).

KUMTHEKAR, KONDO MAHADEV, B.A., LL.B., late Judl. Dept., Bombay (b. 8th Jan., 1882).—Joined the service, 16th June, 1910; asst. judge, Nov., 1933; offg. dist. and sess. judge, variously from Aug., 1931; reid., Jan., 1937.

KUNDAN LAL, India Posts and Tels. (dep. P.M.G.) (b. 12th April, 1883).—Joined the service as proby. supt., 1st Oct., 1906; confd., July, 1907; dep. P.M.G., central circle, Feb., 1930; ditto, United Provs., Feb., 1935; offg. p.m.g., United Provs., Aug., 1936.

KUNDAN LAL AGGARWAL, M.Sc., Indian Forest Service (dep. consvr., Punjab) (b. 21st Aug., 1900).—Joined the service as asst. consvr. on 8th Dec., 1924; dep. consvr., Nov., 1928.

KUNDAN LAL, LALA, B.A., A.M.I.E. (Ind.), Indian Service of Engrs. (exec. engr., Punjab) (b. 21st Aug., 1888).—Joined the service, 5th Oct., 1911; asst. engr., Oct., 1912; asst. exec. engr., Jan., 1920; exec. engr., Oct., 1921.

KUNHIRAMAN, CHURYA, B.A., B.L., Barr., Judl. Dept., Madras (ch. judge, ct. of small causes) (b. 1st Mar., 1888).—Apptd. offg. asst. prof., law coll., Madras, 10th July, 1911; confd., July, 1916; prof. ditto, July, 1921; 3rd judge, small cause ct., Feb., 1927; 2nd ditto, Feb., 1928; tempy. judge, city civil ct., Madras, June, 1932; ch. judge, small cause ct., July, 1933.

KUNHIRAMAN NAMBIYAR, AMANDA KALOTH, Indian Police (asst. supt., Madras) (b. 15th May, 1911).—Apptd. asst. supt., on probn., 13th Feb., 1935; confd., Feb., 1937.

KUNJ BHARILAL SETH (see Seth, K. B.).

KUNZRU, TRIBHUVAN NATH, Indian State Rlys. (asst. exec. engr., N.W. Rly.) (b. 15th Dec., 1890).—Joined the service as asst. engr., E.B. Rly., 10th Oct., 1915; asst. exec. engr., N.W. Rly., Jan., 1921; resdt. engr., G.I.P. Rly., Mar., 1924; offg. exec. engr., Apl., 1926.

KUPPUSWAMI AYYAR, C. N., B.A., B.L., Judl. Dept., Madras (dist. and sess. judge) (b. 1st Apl., 1887).—Joined the service, 21st Oct., 1908; sub judge, Apl., 1934; dist. and sess. judge, Dec., 1936.

KUPPUSWAMI RAMASWAMI AYYANGAR, Incoms Tax Dept., Madras (asst. commr.) (b. 28th Dec., 1887).—Held non-gaz. appts.; joined the service, Sept., 1918; incoms-tax officer, Oct., 1923; confd. as asst. commr. of incoms-tax, Mar., 1937.

KUPPUSWAMI SASTRI, S., M.A., late Indian Ednel. Service (b. 16th Dec., 1880).—Apptd. prof., presdy. coll., Madras, 23rd Feb., 1921; ret'd., Dec., 1935.

KUPPUSWAMI, VALAVANUR SUBRAHMANYA, Indian Forest Service (dep. consvr., Madras) (b. 1st July, 1899).—Joined the service, 5th Dec., 1924; dep. consvr., Nov., 1928.

KUPPUSWAMI AYYAR, NARAYANASWAMY, RAO BAHADUR, late Income tax dept., Burma (b. 23rd Jan., 1870).—Apptd. asst. commr., income tax, Mar., 1927; ret'd., Jan., 1930.

KURBAN ALI KHAN, KHAN, Indian Police (supt., Punjab) (b. 11th May, 1898).—Joined the service as asst. supt., Punjab, 8th Nov., 1921; offg. supt., Mar., 1927; confd., Apl., 1933.

KURVA, SHUJAUDDIN ESMAILJI, Barr., Bombay Judicial Dept. (b. 13th July, 1885).—Apptd. judge, small causes ct., Bombay, Oct., 1928.

KUTUB-UD-DIN AHMAD (see Qutub-ud-din Ahmed).

KYAW KHINE, Oo, B.Sc., Indian C.S. (asst. commr., Burma) (b. 9th Jan. 1904).—Educ. at University of Rangoon, and New Coll., Oxford; joined the service, 29th Sept., 1928; arrived, Nov., 1928, and served in Burma as asst. commr.

KYAW MIN, MAUNG (see Min, Maung Kyaw).

KYAW, U., late Indian Forest Service (b. 20th Sept., 1876).—Joined the service, Sept., 1912; dep. consvr., Oct., 1923; ret'd., Mar., 1932.

KYAW, U. (A.2), B.A., late Burma Civil Service (b. 10th Aug., 1877).—Apptd. extra asst. commr., May, 1910; dep. commr., Dec., 1926; ret'd., Aug., 1932.

KYIN MAUNG, M.Sc., Indian C.S. (regr., co-op. soc., Burma) (b. 25th April, 1904).—Educ. at University of Rangoon, and Oriel Coll., Oxford; joined the service, 28th Sept., 1928; arrived, Oct., 1928, and served in Burma as asst. commr.; under-secty., educn. dept., Aug., 1932; offg. dep. commr., Oct., 1934; jt. regr., co-op. soc., Dec., 1935; offg. regr., ditto, Mar., 1937.

KYIN, U Po, Indian Police (dist. supt.) (b. 1st June, 1885).—Joined the service as asst. dist. supt., Burma, 25th July, 1922; offg. dist. supt., May, 1930; confd., Sept., 1933.

KYLE, JOHN STEWART, late Indian Police Service (Punjab) (b. 8th Nov., 1898).—Joined the service as asst. supt., 21st March, 1921; offg. dist. officer, frontier constabulary, Dec., 1925; supt., March, 1928; ret'd., Oct., 1931.

LABERN, REGINALD ALBERT, Office of High Commr. for India (exec. officer, general dept.) (b. 29th Oct., 1897).—Apptd. temp. clerk, India Office, Oct., 1920; transfid. to H.C.'s Office, April, 1922; clerical officer, Jan., 1924; exec. ditto, June, 1927; office secty. to Indian del. to Indo-British trade negotiations, June, 1937.

LABHU RAM, RAI BAHADUR, Lala, M.A., LL.B., late Punjab Civil Service (b. 16th Nov., 1876).—Joined the service, 7th Jan., 1901, as extra asst. commr.; dep. commr., July, 1927; ret'd., Nov., 1931.

LABORDA, FELIX EDWARD RICARDO, Indian State Rlys. (dist. medl. officer, E.I. Rly.) (b. 1st Apl., 1890).—Joined the service as dist. medl. officer, 17th Oct., 1919; offg. C.M.O., E.I. rly., variously from May, 1933.

LABOUC HARDIERE, BASIL RICHARD EMILE, Indian Police.—Apptd. asst. supt., Bombay, on probn., 22nd Jan., 1936; confd., Oct., 1937.

LA BOUC HARDIERE, MAURICE PRINCE, Indian Police (supt., Bombay) (b. 3rd Sept., 1897).—Joined the service as asst. supt., 22nd March, 1920; offg. supt., April, 1923; confd., Nov., 1927.

LACAMP, ADRIAN, Survey of India (asst. supt.) (b. 19th Nov., 1893).—Joined the service, 19th Jan., 1923; asst. supt., mathematical instruments office, Mar., 1926.

LACEY, CHRISTOPHER WILLIAM, Indian Service of Engrs. (exec. engr.) (b. 28th Dec., 1889).—Joined the service as asst. engr., 1st Oct., 1913; in mil. employ from 28th Jan., 1918, to 23rd Mar., 1919; exec. engr., Oct., 1922; offg. suptg. engr., Mar., 1936, to Nov., 1936.

LACEY, GERALD, B.Sc., A.M.I.C.E., Indian Service of Engineers (suptg. engr., United Provs.) (b. 26th July, 1887).—Joined the service as asst. engr., 1st Oct., 1910; in mil. employ from 5th Oct., 1917, to 29th Aug., 1919; exec. engr., Oct., 1918; prof., T.C.E. Coll., Roorkee, Oct., 1928; offg. suptg. engr., Mar., 1934; confd., Aug., 1936.

LACEY, ONWARD BAYES, V.D., A.M.I.M.E., A.M.I.C.E., late Indian State Rlys. (b. 10th Feb., 1883).—Joined the service as asst. loco. supt. N.W. Rly., Dec., 1910; supt., July, 1925. E.I. Rly.; works manager, E.B. Rly., Nov., 1928; offg. dep. loco. and carr supt., Oct., 1929; offg. dep. ch. mechl. engr., E.B. rly., Feb. 1934; confd., July, 1935; ret'd., Feb., 1937.

LACEY, RICHARD HARRY DRACOTT, late Indian Finance Dept. (b. 11th Dec., 1886).—Joined the service, 26th Nov., 1908; asst. examr., Nov., 1909; asst. acctnt.-gen., April, 1915; dep. acctnt.-gen., Burma, July, 1923; ditto, Madras, Oct., 1927; audit officer, Cauvey-Metur project, Mar., 1930; dep. acct.-genl., Burma; ret'd., Nov., 1933.

LACEY, WALTER GRAHAM, Indian C.S. (addl. secty., pol. and appt. dept., Bihar) (b. 17th July, 1894).—Educ. at Norwich and Bedford Grammar Schs. and Balliol Coll., Oxford; joined the service, 24th Oct., 1919; arrived, 1st Dec., 1919, and served in Bihar and Orissa as asst. mag. and collr.; ditto and under-sec. to govt., finl. dept. May, 1922; offg. dep. commr., March, 1925, and March, 1926; addl.

judl. commr., Feb., 1926; offg. mag. and collr., May, 1926; offg. secy. finl. dept., April, 1930; offg. supt. of census, Nov., 1930; offg. secty. to govt. local self-govt. dept., Nov., 1933; magte. and collr. (provl. subs.), Aug., 1933; confd., Sept., 1935; offg. secty. to govt., fin. dept., May, 1935; secty. to govt., local self-govt. dept., Oct., 1935; addl. secty. to govt., pol. and appt. dept., Bihar, Apl., 1936.

LACHHMAN PRASAD BHARGAV, Indian Service of Engrs. (exec. engr.).—Joined the service as asst. exec. engr., United Provs., 15th Oct., 1927; confd., Oct., 1936.

LACHHMAN SITARAM KHER, RAO SAHIB PANDIT, B.A., late Income Tax Dept., United Provs. (b. 1st July, 1881).—Joined the service, 2nd Nov., 1903; dep. collr., Nov., 1905; asst. commr., Mar., 1925; offg. commr., Central Provs., Aug., 1933; confd., Oct., 1934; retd., July, 1936.

LAD, PURUSHOTTAM MANGESH, B.A., Indian C.S. (judge and sess. judge, Bombay) (b. 29th March, 1905).—Educ. at University of Bombay and Cairns Coll., Cambridge; joined the service, 11th Oct., 1928; arrived, Dec., 1928, and served in Bombay as asst. collr.; judge and sess. judge, Aug., 1936.

LADLI PERSHAD, RAI SAHIB, B.A., Commerce Dept., Govt. of India (asst. secty.) (b. 12th Oct., 1889).—Joined the service, 26th Aug., 1913; supt., dept. of commerce, June, 1925; asst. secty., ditto, May, 1932.

LAHIRI, BISHWA NATH (see Bishwa, N. L.).

LAHIRI, JATINDRA CHANDRA, late Bengal Civil Service (b. 13th July, 1875).—Joined the service, 24th Feb., 1902; munsif., Dec., 1903; sub-judge, Dec., 1920; dist. and sess. judge, July, 1928; retd., July, 1930.

LAINE, SIR ABRAHAM JAMES, K.C.I.E., B.A., late Indian C.S. (b. 26th Aug., 1876).—Educ. at Elizabeth Coll., Guernsey and Pembroke Coll., Oxford; apptd. after exam. of 1899; arrived, 3rd Dec., 1900, and served in Bengal as asst. mag. and collr.; transf. to Assam as asst. commr., Nov., 1904; joint mag. and dep. collr., E. Bengal and Assam, Jan., 1906; asst. commr., Assam, April, 1912; dep. commr., Oct., 1915; second sec. to govt. of Assam, April, 1922; C.I.E., Jan., 1930; member, govt.'s exec. council, Nov., 1930; actg. govt. of Assam, June to Oct., 1935; K.C.I.E., June, 1935; retd., Dec., 1935.

LAIQ SINGH, RAI SAHIB, late Indian Forest Service (b. 22nd June, 1880).—Held non-gazetted appts. from 17th June, 1901, to 30th June, 1911; proby. extra asst. consvr., July, 1911; confd., Sept., 1912; apptd. to I.F.S., 1st Sept., 1928; dep. consvr., 1928; retd., Feb., 1934.

LAIRD-MACGREGOR, EDWIN GEORGE LISTER, B.A., late Indian C.S. (Bombay) (b. 2nd July, 1874).—Educ. at Charterhouse, and Trinity Hall, Cambridge; apptd. after exam. of 1897; arrived, 3rd Dec., 1898, and served in Bombay as asst. collr. and mag., and forest settlt. offr.; on spl. duty under provl. supt., census operations, Bombay, Oct., 1911; acting collr. of customs, Calcutta, Nov., 1913; collr. of customs, Calcutta, April, 1914; junr. collr., and ditto, July, 1916; collr. and dist. mag.

and pol. agent, Oct., 1917; collr. of salt revenue, March, 1924; offg. commr. of excise, May, 1926; member, legisl. council, July, 1926; retd., Nov., 1930.

LAITHWAITE, JOHN GILBERT, C.S.I., C.I.E., M.A., India Office (secty. to govr.-genl. and pte. secty. to Viceroy) (b. 5th July, 1894).—Educ. at Clongowes and Trinity Coll., Oxford; served in France with 10th Lanc. Fus., May, 1917, to July, 1918; specially employed, War Office, March to Sept., 1919; apptd. junr. clerk, mil. dept., 16th July, 1920, after previous service as temp. junr. clerk since 1st Oct., 1919; asst. principal, April, 1921; pte. sec. to parly. under-sec. of state, March, 1922; on deputation to India with Lord Winterton, Aug. to Nov., 1922; asst. pte. sec. to sec. of state, Oct., 1923; principal, services and genl. dept., Sept., 1924; pol. dept., May, 1927; personally attached to Prime Minister for Indian round table conf., London, 2nd Session, Sept.-Dec., 1931; secty., Indian Franchise ctee., Jan.-June, 1932; C.I.E., Jan., 1935; secty., Ind. delimitn. ctee., Aug., 1935; pte. secty. to Viceroy, Apl., 1936; secty. to govt.-genl. (in addn.), Apl., 1937; C.S.I., Jan., 1938.

LAKHPAT, RAI, M.A., B.Sc., Indian Forest Service (dep. consvr., Central Provs.) (b. 8th July, 1899).—Joined the service as asst. consvr., 9th Nov., 1923; dep. consvr., Feb., 1928.

LAKSHMAN SARUP GUPTA, LALA, Indian Service of Engrs. (exec. engr., Punjab) (b. 20th Dec., 1902).—Joined the service as apprentice engr., 26th Oct., 1924; asst. exec. engr., Oct., 1926; exec. engr. Oct., 1935.

LAKSHMANA RAO, KASARAGOD PATANASHETTI, Madras Civil Service (Judicial Branch) (judge, high ct., Madras) (b. 15th Dec., 1887).—Joined the service as asst. profr., law coll., Madras, 28th Aug., 1916; profr., ditto, Oct., 1920; dist. and sess. judge, June, 1921; offg. judge high ct., Madras, variously from Aug., 1930, addl. ditto, Jan., 1934; confd., Sept., 1935.

LAL, BANWARI (see Banwari Lal).
LAL, BASANT (see Basant Lal).
LAL, BEHARI (see Behari Lal).
LAL, BRIJ (see Brij Lal).
LAL, BRAJ BHUSAN (see Braj Bhusan Lal).
LAL, BRIJ MOHAN (see Brij Mohan Lal).
LAL, BURTON CHANDU (see Chandu Lal, B.).
LAL, CHAMPA (see Champa Lal).
LAL, CHANDU (see Chandu Lal).
LAL, CHHOTE (see Chhotte Lal).
LAL, CHHUTTAN (see Chhuttan Lal).
LAL, CHIMAN (see Chiman Lal).
LAL, CHUNI, Handa (see Chuni Lal, Handa).
LAL, DONALD CHANDU (see Chandu Lal, D.).
LAL, DHAWAN PURUSHOTTAM (see Dhawan Purushottam Lal).
LAL, HARBANS (see Harbans Lal).
LAL, HARIVANSH (see Harivansh Lal).
LAL, INDRA MOHAN (see Indra Mohan Lal).
LAL, JAI (see Jai Lal).
LAL, KANHAIYA (see Kanhaiya Lal).
LAL, KRISHNA KANIYA (see Krishna Kaniya Lal).
LAL, KUNDAN (see Kundan Lal).
LAL, KUNJ BEHARI MOHAN (see Mohan Lal, K.B.).
LAL, MOHA, BATRA (see Mohan Lal, Batra).
LAL, MOTI (see Moti Lal).
LAL, MUNNA (see Munna Lal).

LAL, MUTSADDI (see Mutsaddi Lal).

LAL, PANNA (see Panna Lal).

LAL, RAM (see Ram Lal).

LAL, RANG BEHARI (see Rang Behari Lal).

LAL, RANGI (see Rangi Lal).

LAL, SANGHAM (see Sangham Lal).

LAL, SARDARI (see Sardari Lal).

LAL, SHADI (see Shadi Lal).

LAL, SHAM (see Sham Lal).

LAL, SHAMALDHARI (see Shamaldhari Lal).

LAL, SHAVAX ARDESHIR (see Shavax Ardeshir, Lal).

LAL SINGH, MUNSHI, B.A., Rai Bahadur, late Punjab Civil Service (b. 16th Dec., 1882).—Joined the service as proby. extra asst. commr., 15th Jan., 1907; confd., Oct., 1911; offg. dep. commr., Sept., 1927; confd., Sept., 1931; ret'd., Dec., 1937.

LAL, SUNDER (see Sunder Lal).

LALIT CHAND, THAKUR, B.A., Punjab Civil Service (b. 15th Dec., 1891).—Extra asst. commr., Punjab, Oct., 1914.

LALKAKA, FRAMROZ DHANJISHAH, late Impl. Customs Service (b. 21st Aug., 1874).—Joined the service, 6th Jan., 1898; and held non-gazetted appts. until June, 1908; appt. asst. collr. of customs, June, 1908; offg. collr. of customs, Bombay, Jan., 1921; offg. chief collr., Karachi, May, 1922; actg. collr. of customs, Karachi, Feb., 1925; asst. ditto, Bombay, Oct., 1925; offg. collr., Rangoon, Mar., 1929; ret'd., Nov., 1929.

LALKAKA, MINOCHAR DORABJI, B.A., LL.B., Barr., Judicial Dept., Bombay (b. 3rd Aug., 1894).—Apptd. judge, small cause ct., Bombay, 15th Apl., 1929.

LALL (see also under Lal).

LALL, ARTHUR SAMUEL, M.A., Indian C.S. (asst. commr., Punjab) (b. 14th July, 1911); educated at Central Model School, Lahore, University of the Punjab and Balliol College, Oxford; joined the service, 9th Oct., 1934; arrived in India, Nov., 1934 and served in Punjab as asst. commr.; sub-divl. offcr., May, 1937.

LALVANI, LALCHAND VARUMAL, B.Sc., Indian Service of Engrs. (exec. engr., Sind) (b. 14th Sept., 1897).—Joined the service as asst. exec. engr., 1st Oct., 1921; offg. exec. engr., May, 1926; confd., Oct., 1930.

LAMARQUE, WALTON GEOFFREY, B.A., Indian C.S. (asst. mag. and collr., Madras) (b. 12th Feb., 1913).—Educ. at Marlborough and Oriel Coll., Oxford; joined the service, 20th Sept., 1937; arrived in India, Dec., 1937, and served in Madras as asst. mag. and collr.

LAMB, GEORGE HAMISH, Indian State Rlys. (works manager, G.I.P. rly.) (b. 15th Mar., 1887).—Joined the service 23rd Sept., 1921; production engr., G.I.P. rly., Jan., 1929; asst. work manager, G.I.P. rly., Feb., 1931; works manager, Oct., 1932.

LAMBERT, ERIC THOMAS DRUMMOND, Indian Police (b. 3rd Nov., 1909).—Joined the service as asst. supt., Assam, 25th Nov., 1929.

LAMBERT, E. W., M.A., Barr., Burma Judl. Service (asst. govt. advocate) (b. 7th Apl., 1890).—Apptd. offg. asst. govt. advocate, Burma, 25th Mar., 1926; confd., June, 1934.

LAMBERT, SIR GEORGE BANCROFT, K.C.S.I., B.A., late Indian C.S. (b. 28th Oct., 1873).—Educ. at Magdalen Coll., Oxford, and Univ. Coll., London; apptd. after exam. of 1896; arrived, 6th Dec., 1897, and served in the N.W. Provs. as asst. mag. and collr. and asst. commr.; joint man., Dec., 1906; dep. commr., Sept., 1914; mag. and collr., March, 1916; dep. dir. of land records, and inspr.-gen. of registrn., Nov., 1917; offg. finl. sec. to govt., April, 1919; chief sec. to govt., United Provs., Feb., 1921; C.S.I., June, 1922; member, govr.'s exec. council, Jan., 1928, to Oct., 1930; reappointed ditto, Apl., 1931; offg. govr., United Provs., Dec., 1928; K.C.S.I., June, 1929; govr., United Provs., Oct., 1930 to Apl., 1931; ret'd., Dec., 1932.

LAMBERT, HENRY, M.A. (Cantab.), late Indian Educl. Service (b. 22nd Feb., 1881).—Joined the service, 19th Nov., 1907, and appt. inspr. of schools, Bengal; transf. to Bihar and Orissa, April, 1912; actg. principl., Ravenshaw Coll., Cuttack, July, 1915; confd., April, 1918; offg. dir. of public instrn., Bihar and Orissa, variously from Jan., 1924; on spl. duty, educn. dept., govt. of India, Dec., 1928; ret'd., Feb., 1936.

LAMBERT, OSCAR WILFRED, Office of the High Commr. for India (exec. officer, accts. dept.) (b. 2nd Mar., 1894).—Apptd. clerical officer, 8th July, 1923, after previous service from 7th Oct., 1919; acting exec. officer, June, 1931; confd., Apl., 1933.

LAMBERT, WILLIAM JAMES, B.A., B.Sc., late Indian Forest Service (b. 2nd Feb., 1891).—Joined the service as asst. consvr., 14th Dec., 1915; dep. consvr., Oct., 1922; instr., forest coll., Dehra Dun, May, 1923; consvr., Kashmir Nov., 1924, to Oct., 1929; ret'd., Jan., 1933.

LAMBRICK, HUGH TREVOR, Indian C.S. (collr. and mag., Bombay) (b. 20th Apl., 1904).—Educ. at Rossall School, and Oriel Coll., Oxford; joined the service, 28th Oct., 1927; arrived, Dec., 1927, and served in Bombay as asst. collr.; offg. asst. commr. in Sind, Apr., 1931, temp. dep. secty. to govt., pol. and reforms dept., Bombay, Mar., 1935; offg. collr., June, 1936.

LAMINGTON (BARON), RIGHT HON. CHARLES WALLACE ALEXANDER NAPIER COCHRANE-BAHLLIE, G.C.M.G., G.C.I.E.—Gouv. of Bombay 12th Dec., 1903, to 27th July, 1907.

LAMOON, ALBERT JOSEPH, India Office (higher clerical officer, acct.-genl.'s dept.) (b. 4th May, 1881).—Apptd. clerical officer, 29th Aug., 1921; actg. higher clerical officer, Jan., 1925; confd., Nov., 1926.

LANCASHIRE, JOHN KENNETH, M.A., Barr., late Indian C.S. (b. 20th Feb., 1878).—Educ. at Currie Sch., Folkestone, and Brasenose Coll., Oxford; apptd. after exam. of 1900; arrived, 2nd Dec., 1901, and served in Madras as asst. collr. and mag., and spl. settlt. offr.; sub-collr. and jt. mag., Sept., 1914; dist. and sessions judge, Sept., 1922; ret'd., Nov., 1936.

LANCASTER, HAROLD, Indian State Rlys. (colliery supt., E.I. Rly.) (b. 30th June, 1883).—Apptd. colliery supt., 1st July, 1922.

LANDER, ANTHONY JOHN MEADS, M.C., M.A., Indian C.S. (agric. commr., Burma) (b. 21st Aug., 1895).—Educ. at Epsom Coll., and Sidney Sussex Coll., Cambridge; joined the service, 26th Oct., 1920; arrived, 19th Feb., 1921, and served in Burma as asst. commr.; offg. dep. commr., May, 1925; confd., Jan., 1934.

LANDER, PERCY EDWARD, M.A. (Cantab.), D.Sc. (London), A.I.C., Dept. of Agriculture, Punjab (agricl. chemist to Govt.) (b. 8th May, 1889).—Dep. dir. of agric., 4th Dec., 1920; offg. agricl. chemist to govt., Mar., 1921; confd., Dec., 1923.

LANDON, CYRIL, O.B.E., M.C., late India Posts and Telegraphs (b. 23rd Sept., 1880).—Apptd. asst. supt., 1st Oct., 1902; asst. supt. engrg., April, 1914; on field service, from Oct., 1914, to July, 1917; supt., engrg., Nov., 1917; O.B.E., Jan., 1920; apptd. asst. to dir. in-chief, Indo-European Tel., Public Works Dept., India Office, Nov., 1924, to Nov., 1925; divl. engr., telegraphs, Bombay Div., March, 1926; ret'd., Sept., 1931; war decorations—South African Medal, 1890-1902, with clasps; Military Cross, Feb., 1917.

LANE, A. S., M.B.E., B.Sc., Dept. of Industries and Labour, Govt. of India (b. 22nd May, 1900).—Apptd. aircraft and engine inspr. on the staff of the dir. of civil aviation in India, 29th Mar., 1929; M.B.E., Jan., 1935; ret'd.

LANE, BASIL WILLIAM, Indian Police (b. 5th Aug., 1905).—Joined the service as asst. supt., Madras, 13th Jan., 1926; offg. dist. supt., Dec., 1932; and again, Mar., 1933.

LANE, CHARLES MACDONALD, C.S.I., late Indian Service of Engrs. (b. 26th Jan., 1882).—Trained at the R.I.E. Coll.; apptd. asst. engr., 1st Oct., 1903; exec. engr., Oct., 1911; offg. suptg. engr., Dec., 1924; suptg. engr. (provl. perm.), Mar., 1927; confd., Nov., 1928; offg. ch. engr., May, 1929; confd., July, 1931; member, legisl. council, Bombay, 1932; C.S.I., June, 1935; ret'd., Jan., 1937.

LANE, HERBERT ALLARDYCE, C.I.E., Indian C.S. (mag. and collr., United Provs.) (b. 13th Aug., 1883).—Educ. at Wellington Coll., and New Coll., Oxford; apptd. after exam. of 1907; arrvd., 23rd Nov., 1908, and served in the United Provs. as asst. mag.; jt. mag., July, 1916; ditto, and asst. settlt. offr., Oct., 1916; dep. commr. (provl.) and settlt. offr., Oct., 1918; addl. collr., Nov., 1921; mag. and collr., and settlt. officer, Aug., 1924; rev. and judl. sec. to govt., Oct., 1925; secty. to govt., rev. and public wks. dept., May, 1927; C.I.E., Jan., 1927; offg. settlement commr., Mar., 1929; secty. to govt., rev. and p.w.d. (b. and r.), Feb., 1932; offg. presdt., ct. of wards, Apl., 1934; confd., Nov., 1935.

LANE, HUGH THOMPSON, B.A., Indian C.S. (asst. mag. and collr., U.P.) (b. 26th Apl., 1914).—Educ. at Rossall Sch. and Trinity Hall, Cambridge; joined the service, 24th Sept., 1937; arrived in India, Nov., 1937, and served in U.P. as asst. mag. and collr.

LANE, LESLIE RICHARD, late Indian Customs Service (b. 28th Nov., 1893).—Joined the service, 21st March, 1920; asst. collr., March, 1922; ret'd., March, 1932.

LANE, PAUL CUTHBERT HORSBURGH, Capt., R.I.M., ret'd. (b. 7th Sept., 1884).—Apptd. port officer, Rassein, 8th Feb., 1924; dep. port conservator, Madras, Jan., 1927; divl. marine transport officer, Burma, Feb., 1929; prinpl. officer, mercantile marine dept., Burma, Feb., 1933; ret'd., June, 1934.

LANE, RICHARD GEORGE McLAREN, Indian Police (supt., Bihar) (b. 29th June, 1902).—Joined the service as asst. supt., Bihar and Orissa, 25th Nov., 1922; offg. supt., Feb., 1932, and again, Sept., 1933; confd., Apl., 1935.

LANG, LIONEL EDWARD, C.I.E., M.C., Lt.-Col., I.A., Indian Political Service (b. 1st Nov., 1885).—First commn., 18th Jan., 1905; apptd. asst. pol. agent, Mahi Kantha, March, 1909; adviser to H.H. the Nawab of Radhanpur, Feb., 1912, to May, 1913; asst. pol. agent, Palanpur, April, 1914; on mil. duty from Dec., 1914, to Nov., 1919; C.I.E., June, 1921; asst. resdt., Kolhapur, April, 1922; pol. agent, W.K. States, Mar., 1927; asst. pol. agent, S.M.C. States, Dec., 1927; resdt., Kolhapur, and pol. agent, S.M.C. States, Jan., 1928; pol. agent, Bhopal, Apl., 1933; resdt., Kashmir, Nov., 1933.

LANG, WILLIAM GORDON, Indian Police (b. 23rd Nov., 1916).—Apptd. asst. supt., Bombay, on probn., 27th Oct., 1936.

LANGDON, FREDERICK GUY, A.M.I.C.E., Indian State Rlys. (exec. engr., G.I.P. Rly.) (b. 5th Nov., 1887).—Joined the service as asst. engr., 9th June, 1911; offg. resdt. engr., Nov., 1914; confd., Sept., 1919; divl. engr., Dec., 1929; offg. dep. ch. engr., contrn., May, 1935; and variously from May, 1936.

LANGFORD, JOHN BRIAN, Indian C.S. (jt. mag., United Provs.) (b. 21st June, 1907).—Educ. at City and County School, Chester, and Clare College, Cambridge; joined the service, 16th Oct., 1930; arrived in India, Nov., 1930, and served in the United Provs. as asst. mag. and collr.; dep. secty. rev. dept., Provs. (temp.), Jan., 1936.

LANGHORNE, HERBERT YORKE, M.A., late Indian Educl. Service (Punjab) (b. 27th Jan. 1877).—Joined the service as prof., Khalsa coll., Amritsar, 28th Oct., 1914; prof., govt. coll., Lahore, Oct., 1921; ret'd. Jan., 1932.

LANGLEY, ALEXANDER, C.S.I., C.I.E., B.A., late Indian C.S. (Punjab) (b. 22nd May, 1871).—Educ. at Christ's Hosp., and Pembroke Coll., Oxford; apptd. after exam. of 1894; arrived, 10th Dec., 1895, and served in the Punjab as asst. commr.; dep. commr., Nov., 1909; regr. co-op. creditsocieties in the Punjab, April, 1909; regr. of joint stock companies, in addn., June, 1912, to April, 1916; C.I.E., Jan., 1918; commr. (provl.), Nov., 1923; confd., Nov., 1924; C.S.I., Jan., 1927; ret'd., Dec., 1930.

LANGLEY, GEORGE HARRY, M.A. (Lon.), late Indian Educl. Service (b. 14th July, 1881).—Joined the service, 13th Jan., 1913, as supy. prof., Presy. coll.; apptd. vice-chancellor, Dacca Univ., 1st Jan., 1926; ret'd., July, 1936.

LANKTREE, HENRY RICHARD, late Indian Police Service (b. 15th Feb., 1890).—Joined the service as asst. supt., 5th Dec., 1910; dist. supt., Dec., 1917; ret'd., July, 1932.

LAO HTIN WAH, Indian Forest Service (dep. consvr.) (b. 26th Nov., 1888).—Joined the service as asst. consvr., Burma, 30th Dec., 1925; dep. consvr., Mar., 1932.

LAPSLY, JAMES BURNE, M.C., M.B., Lt.-Col., I.M.S., ret'd. (b. 16th Aug., 1884).—First commn. dated 1st Feb., 1908; in milty. employ up to 15th Feb., 1925; offg. asst. dir.-genl., Indian midl. service (stores), Feb., 1925; confid., Nov., 1926; ret'd., Apl., 1935.

LARKIN, ALFRED SLOANE, Indian C.S. (mag. and collr., Bengal) (b. 11th June, 1894).—Educ. at High Sch., Dublin, and Trinity Coll., Dublin; apptd. under Reconstruction Scheme; arrived, 2nd May, 1921, and served in Bengal as asst. mag. and collr.; addl. mag. and collr., March, 1926; offg. ditto, Mar., 1928; confid., Nov., 1930; offg. commr. of excise and salt, May, 1936.

LARWILL, Rev. GUTHRIE JAMES, M.A., Indian Eccles. Estabt. (United Provs.) (junior chaplain) (b. 26th Dec., 1891).—Apptd. chaplain on probation, 24th Oct., 1926; junior chaplain, Oct., 1928.

LASCELLES, FELIX, B.Sc., A.M.I.C.E., late Indian Service of Engrs. (Bihar & Orissa).—Joined the service as asst. engr. on 1st Oct., 1909; exec. engr., Nov., 1921, to May, 1929; ret'd., May, 1929.

LASRADO, EDWARD ALOYSIUS, Indian Forest Service (dep. consvr., Madras) (b. 21st June, 1902).—Joined the service, 4th Dec., 1926; dep. consvr., Nov., 1930.

LASRADO, JOSEPH PATRICK, Indian C.S. (sub. collr. and jl. mag., Madras) (b. 29th Oct., 1907).—Educ. at Madras University and London, School of Oriental Studies; joined the service, 13th Oct., 1930; arrived in India, Nov., 1930, and served in Madras as asst. mag. and collr.; sub.-collr., Mar., 1933.

LATHAM, HUGH DALLAS, Indian Police (b. 5th Feb., 1906).—Joined the service as asst. supt., Madras, 5th Nov., 1925; offg. dist. supt., Nov., 1931.

LATHAM, TREVOR, late Indian Police Service.—Joined the service, 22nd Nov., 1893, as asst. dist. supt. of police; dist. supt., Nov., 1899; ditto, and principal, police offrs. training sch., Saurashtra, March, 1909, to March, 1912; supt., govt. ry. police, Central Provs., May, 1916; dep. inspr.-gen., Central Provs., Dec., 1918; offg. inspr.-gen., Feb., 1924; awarded King's police medal; ret'd., June, 1928.

LATIFI, ALMA, C.I.E., O.B.E., M.A., LL.M. (Cantab.); LL.D. (Dublin), Barr. (1902), late Indian C.S. (b. 12th Nov., 1879).—Educ. at Bombay (St. Xavier's Sch., and Coll.), Cambridge (St. John's Coll.), Paris and Heidelberg; apptd. after exam. of 1901; arrived, 8th Jan., 1903, and served in the Punjab as asst. commr., dist. judge, dep. commr., and commr.; on special duty to survey Punjab industries, 1909-10; serving under the govt. of India in connection with the press camps at the Coronation Durbar, Delhi, 1911-12; dir. of public instruction, Hyderabad state, Aug., 1913, to Aug., 1916; O.B.E., Dec., 1919; addl. secty. to govt., Punjab, Apl., 1921; sec. to govt. of Punjab, transfd. depts., July, 1921 to Oct., 1924; member, Punjab leg. council, 1921-24; dep. commr., Karnal, 1924-27; commr., Ambala, Nov., 1927, and member,

council of state, Nov., 1927 to Aug., 1928, and July, 1929 to Feb., 1930; member, Indian delegation to International law conference, Hague, Mar., 1930; substitute delegate and adviser, Indian delegation, International labour conference, Geneva, June, 1930; on duty with 1st, 2nd and 3rd round table conferences, London, Sept., 1930-Mar., 1931; Aug., 1931-Jan., 1932, and Oct., 1932-Jan., 1933 respectively, and with consultative ctee., I.R.T.C., Delhi, Jan. to Oct., 1932; commr., Multan, Mar., 1931; C.I.E., June, 1932; finl. commr. (rev.), Punjab, Mar., 1933, Feb., 1934, and again, Feb., 1936; ret'd., Jan., 1938; author of "Effects of War on Property," and of "The Industrial Punjab."

LATIF-UR-RAHMAN, NAWABZADA, A. S.M., M.A. (Cantab.), Barr., Bengal Judl. dept (chief judge, Calcutta small cause ct.).—Joined the service, 14th Nov., 1918; judge, Calcutta small cause ct., Oct., 1920; chief judge ditto, 1937.

LATIMER, SIR COURTENAY, K.C.I.E., C.S.I., B.A., Indian C.S., Indian Political Service (b. 22nd Sept., 1880).—Educ. at St. Paul's Sch., and Christ Church Oxford; apptd. after exam. of 1903; arrived, 29th Nov., 1904, and served in the Punjab as asst. commr.; pol. asst., Central India Agency, April, 1908; pol. agent, Baghelkhand, June, 1908; asst. sec. to govt. of India, for. dept., April, 1909; asst. commr., Peshawar, Nov., 1909; asst. sec. to chief commr., Feb., 1910; supt., census operations, N.W.F. Prov., Nov., 1910; asst. commr., Oghi, and commtdt., B.M. Pol., Hazara, April, 1912; 1st asst. sec. to chief commr., N.W. Frontier Prov., Nov., 1913, to Jan., 1915, and from April, 1915, to Oct., 1917; dep. commr., N.W.F. Prov., March, 1918; C.I.E., June, 1921; offg. dep. sec. to govt. of India, for. and pol. dept., Sept., 1923; dep. commr., Kohat, Oct., 1925; offg. rev. commr., N.W.F.P., Jan., 1927; pol. agent, Dir, Swat and Chitral, May, 1927; dep. ch. commr., N.W.F.P., Aug., 1930; C.S.I., Jan., 1931; rev. commr., N.W.F.P., June, 1931; resdt., Kashmir, Sept., 1931; offg. resdt. and agent to govt.-genl. in the states of Western India, Aug., 1932; confid., Feb., 1933; K.C.I.E., June, 1935; employed as spl. representative of the Viceroy, Oct., 1936; resdt. for States of W.I., Apl., 1937; addtl. secty., pol. dept., Oct., 1937.

LATIMER, COURTENAY ROBERT, B.A., Indian C.S. (asst. commr., Punjab) (b. 13th July, 1911).—Educated at Rugby and Christ Church, Oxford; apptd. after exam. of 1934; arrived in India, Oct., 1935, and served in the Punjab as asst. commr.

LA TOUCHE, JAMES FLEMING DIGGES, Indian Forest Service (dep. consvr., Burma) (b. 18th Dec., 1900).—Joined the service as asst. consvr., Burma, 27th Dec., 1924; dep. consvr., June, 1930.

LATTA, JOHN JOPP, M.A., late Indian Finance Dept. (b. 18th Aug., 1885).—Joined the service, 3rd Jan., 1910; asst. acctnt.-genl., May, 1911; currency officer, Calcutta, April, 1923; dep. acct.-genl., Bombay, Dec., 1927; ditto, Bengal, July, 1928; offg. dep.-dir. of Army Audit, N.C., April, 1929; ret'd., Feb., 1934.

LAUDDIE, ABDUR RAHMAN KHAN, M.D., Ch.B., Lt.-Col. I.M.S., ret'd. (b. 18th June, 1877).—First commn., 1st Sept., 1902; in mil. employ till Jan., 1906, when he

was apptd. asst. plague medical officer, Punjab; dist. plague medl. offr., Oct., 1907; on mil. service, Aug., 1914, to 1919; civil surgeon, Punjab, Dec., 1918; ret'd., June, 1932.

LAUGHTON, FREDERICK EASTFIELD, Major, I.A., Indian Political Service (b. 29th Apl., 1900).—First commn., 16th July, 1919; asst. resdt. Aden, Feb., 1929; under-secty. to A.G.G. in the states of W.I., Aug., 1931; asst. commr., Mansehra, Nov., 1931; ditto, Hangu, Nov., 1932; asst. comm., Charsadda, Feb., 1935; asst. commr., Mardan, May, 1936; offer on spec. duty, Peshawar, Oct., 1936; dep. commr., Bannu, Mar., 1937.

LAUGHTON, GEORGE CHRISTIAN, A.M.I.C.E., Indian State Rlys. (dep. ch. engr.) (b. 26th Jan., 1887).—Joined the service as asst. engr., 1st Oct., 1910; exec. engr., Oct., 1918; divl. engr., Oct., 1925; offg. divl. supt., Nov., 1926; offg. dep. dir., programme. rly. bd., Mar., 1929; offg. dir. civil engrg., Apl., 1931; offg. secty., rly. bd., June, 1932; offg. dep. dir., ditto, Oct., 1932; offg. dep. ch. engr., Nov., 1932; confd., Dec., 1935.

LAURENCE, Rev. GEORGE, M.A., B.D., Indian Eccles. Estabt. (senr. chaplain, Punjab) (b. 12th Nov., 1892).—Apptd. chaplain on probation, 26th March, 1926; junr. chaplain, Nov., 1927; senr. ditto, Nov., 1937.

LAURIE, ALBERT STEVENSON, late Indian Service of Engrs. (b. 29th Oct., 1876).—Trained at the R.I.E. Coll.; apptd. asst. engr., 1st Oct., 1899; exec. engr., March, 1908; on mil. duty from Nov., 1915, to Sept., 1916; offg. suptg. engr., Jan., 1926; confd., Sept., 1926; ret'd., Oct., 1931.

LAURIE, MALCOLM VVYYAN, Indian Forest Service (silviculturist, Dehra Dun) (b. 30th Aug., 1901).—Joined the service, 20th Nov., 1925; forest economist, April, 1926; dep. consvr., Nov., 1930; offg. silviculturist, forest research inst., Dehra Dun, Apl., 1935; confd., Oct., 1936.

LAW, HENRY DUNCAN GRAVES, C.I.E., M.A., late Indian C.S. (b. 13th Aug., 1883).—Educ. at Westminster School, Edinburgh Univ., and Trinity Coll., Cambridge; apptd. after exam. of 1905; arrived, 4th Dec., 1906, and served in Bengal as asst. mag. and collr.; transf. to Bihar and Orissa, April, 1912; 2nd asst. resdt., Hyderabad, June, 1912; asst. commr., Ajmer-Merwara, May, 1914; 2nd asst. to agent to govr.-gen. in Rajputana, March, 1915; asst. sec., for. and pol. dept., govt. of India, Oct., 1915; under-sec., ditto, April, 1916; spl. asst. to resident in Kashmir, Nov., 1916; first asst. agent of govr.-gen. in Baluchistan, Oct., 1919; H.M.'s consul for Kerman and Persian Baluchistan, Sept., 1924; C.I.E., Jan., 1927; pol. agent, Loralai, Nov., 1927; consul-genl. and agent to govr. genl. in Khorasan, April, 1929; ret'd., Sept., 1934.

LAW, HENRY RUSSEL MACKAY, B.A., Indian Forest Service (dep. consvr., Madras) (b. 16th Jan., 1896).—Joined the service as asst. consvr., 14th Nov., 1921; dep. consvr., Nov., 1924.

LAWIE, WILLIAM, Major, I.M.S., Civil Surgeon, United Provs. (b. 22nd Jan., 1895).—Date of first commn., 29th Nov., 1923; civil surgeon, 3rd July, 1933.

LAWLEY, WILFRED, Indian Service of Engrs. (exec. engr.) (b. 2nd June, 1902).—Joined the service as asst. exec. engr., Punjab, 25th Sept., 1925; exec. engr., Sept., 1934.

LAWRENCE, FREDERICK WILLIAM, Office of High Commr. for India (exec. officer, store dept.) (b. 8th June, 1900).—Apptd. asst. clerk, 27th Oct., 1919; clerical officer, Jan., 1920; transferred to office of high commr., Oct., 1920; actg. exec. officer, Feb., 1927; confd., June, 1929.

LAWRENCE, SIR HENRY STAVELEY, K.C.S.I., late Indian C.S.—Educ. at Haileybury Coll., and Magdalen Coll., Oxford; apptd. after exam. of 1888; arrived, 23rd Nov., 1890, and served in Bombay as asst. collr. and mag.; 2nd asst., Sept., 1896; under sec. to govt. rev. and finl. depts., May, 1898; 1st asst., Sept., 1900; K.-i-H. medal, 2nd class, May, 1900; offd. as sec. to govt., rev. and finl. depts., Nov., 1901, and as dir. of land records and agric. inspr.-genl. of registrn., &c., Oct., 1901, and March, 1902; confd. in latter appt., Oct., 1902; junr. collr., Dec., 1906; dir. of agric. and cc-operative credit societies, June, 1907 to April, 1908; supt. of stamps, &c., Oct., 1911; senr. collr., March, 1912; commr., May, 1916; commr. in Sind, July, 1916; C.S.I., June, 1918; member of gov.'s exec. council, Bombay, 17th April, 1921, to 19th March, 1926; K.C.S.I., Jan., 1926; actg. govr. of Bombay, 20th March, 1926; ret'd., Sept., 1926; member of Royal Commn. on Agric. in India, 1926-7.

LAWRENCE, JAMES TAYLOR, M.A., late Indian C.S. (b. 28th Jan., 1888).—Educ. at King Edward's Gr. Sch., Five Ways, Birmingham, Aberdeen Univ., Trinity Coll., Cambridge, and Univ. Coll., London; apptd. after exam. of 1911; arrived, 21st Nov., 1912, and served in Bombay as asst. collr. and magte., and asst. judge; actg. judge and sess. judge, May, 1922; addl. dep. sec. to govt., fin. dept. (temp.), Oct., 1924; on spl. duty in fin. dept., Dec., 1925, and again, Feb., 1926; addl. dep. sec. to govt., fin. dept., Jan., 1926; offg. judge and sess. judge, and agent for sardars in the Deccan, June, 1926; offg. asst. pol. resdt., Aden, and addl. sess. judge, Aden, April, 1927; judge and sess. judge doing duty as judl. asst. to pol. resdt., Aden, Sept., 1927; ret'd., June, 1932.

LAWRENCE, SIR WALTER ROPER, Bart., G.C.I.E., G.C.V.O., C.B., late Indian C.S.—Educ. at Cheltenham Coll., and Balliol Coll., Oxford; apptd. after exam. of 1877; arrived, 5th Dec., 1879, and served in the Punjab as asst. commr., and in Ajmer and Merwara as pol. asst. and mag.; under sec. to govt., Punjab, 1885 and 1886; under sec. to govt. of India, rev. and agricl. dept., Sept., 1886; offd. as sec. in 1888; settl. officer, Kashmir, April, 1889; C.I.E., Jan., 1891; ret'd., Mar., 1897; author of 'The Valley of Kashmir,' 1895; private sec. to the Viceroy (Lord Curzon), Jan., 1899, to Oct., 1903; K.C.I.E., Jan., 1903; G.C.I.E., May, 1906; Bart., July, 1906; member of the Council of India, Oct., 1907, to March, 1909; member of committee on Indian rly. fin. and administration, 1907-8; C.B., Jan., 1917; G.C.V.O., June, 1918.

LAWSON, REGINALD GEORGE BATEMAN, Indian Police (asst. inspr.-genl.) (b. 22nd Dec., 1894).—Joined the service as asst. supt., 15th Dec., 1914; dist. supt., May, 1918; asst. inspr.-genl., Rangoon, Mar., 1935, and again, Oct., 1936; offg. dep. inspr.-genl., Jan., to Oct., 1936; asst. inspr.-genl., Oct., 1936.

LAWTHER, BARRY CHARLES ALFRED, C.I.E., late Indian Police (b. 25th Aug., 1888).—Joined the service, 10th Dec., 1908; asst. supt., March, 1910; supt., Feb., 1919; offg. dep. inspr.-genl., C.I.D., Punjab, May, 1929; C.I.E., June, 1931; ret'd., Mar., 1934.

LAWTON, HAROLD, F.C.H., late Indian State Railways (b. 22nd Jan., 1879).—Trained at the R.I.E. Coll.; apptd. asst. engr., 1st Oct., 1900; exec. engr., Oct., 1908; asst. agent, E. Bengal Ry., Dec., 1913; suptg. engr., June, 1924; offg. chief engr., Nov., 1924; confd., Feb., 1927; ret'd., Jan., 1934.

LAYARD, AUSTEN HAVELOCK, C.I.E., M.A., Barr.-at-Law, Indian C.S. (dep. commr., Central Provs.) (b. 20th Feb., 1895).—Educ. at Rugby and King's Coll., Cambridge; joined the service, 25th Oct., 1920; arrived, 29th Dec., 1920, and served in Central Provs. as asst. commr.; offg. dep. commr., March, 1926; dep. commr., Delhi, Mar., 1932; C.I.E., Feb., 1937.

LAYTON, GEORGE EDWARD, Office of High Commr. for India (higher clerical officer, store dept.) (b. 12th Oct., 1879).—Apptd., 1st July, 1925, in connection with the G.I.P. Ry. Co.

LAZARUS, MISS THEODORA, B.A., Indian Educ. Service (inspectress of girls' schools, Madras) (b. 9th Oct., 1884).—Apptd. supt., govt. secondary sch. for women, Royapetta, 28th June, 1924; inspectress of girls' schools, May, 1926.

LEACH, SIR ALFRED HENRY LIONEL, Barr. (ch. justice, high ct., Madras).—Apptd. judge, high ct., Rangoon, 15th Feb., 1933; ch. justice, ditto, July, 1937; knighted, Jan., 1938.

LEACH, ARTHUR GORDON, C.I.E., late Indian C.S. (b. 16th March, 1885).—Educ. at Bradfield and New College, Oxford; apptd. after exam. of 1908; arrived, 24th Nov., 1909, and served in Madras as asst. collr. and mag. and spl. settl. offr.; sub-collr. and jt. mag., Oct., 1917; on mil. duty, 1917-19; offg. collr. and dist. mag., March, 1920; ditto, June, 1922; ditto, March, 1925; confd., Jan., 1930; offg. secty. to govt., P.W.D. and labour, April, 1929; C.I.E., June, 1933; ret'd., Oct., 1934.

LEACH, FRANK BURTON, C.I.E., B.A., late Indian C.S. (b. 31st August, 1881).—Educ. at Eton, and Magdalen Coll., Oxford; apptd. after exam. of 1904; arrived, 10th Dec., 1905, and served in Burma as asst. commr.; under-sec. to govt., Nov., 1911, to April, 1912, and from Sept., 1912, to April, 1913; dep. commr. s.p.t. and dep. commr., Port Blair, April, 1916; dep. commr., Dec., 1917; on mil. duty, Dec., 1917, to April, 1919; offg. commr., March, 1924; ch. secty. to govt., Burma, Aug., 1930; C.I.E., Jan., 1932; ret'd., Oct., 1934.

LEAKE, JONES INCHQUIN, Indian Police (asst. supt., N.W.F.P.) (b. 18th Mar., 1895).—Joined the service, 8th April, 1924; asst. supt., Jan., 1925.

LE BAILLY, WILLIAM FRANCIS GRAHAME, Indian C.S. (dep. commr., Punjab) (b. 17th Oct., 1904).—Educ. at Wellington and Brasenose Coll., Oxford; joined the service, 19th Oct., 1927; arrived, Dec., 1927, and served in the Punjab as asst. commr.; offg. dep. commr., Apl., 1932, and Oct., 1933; ditto (subs. provl.), Mar., 1935.

LE BROCO, EDWARD HENRY, Indian Police (supt., Bengal) (b. 25th May, 1903).—Joined the service as asst. supt., Bengal, 6th Jan., 1923; offg. supt., April, 1926; confd., Aug., 1934.

LECK, JOHN STEVENSON, Indian State Ry.s. (elec. and mechl. engr.).—Apptd. electrical and mechl. engr. (temp.), 16th Oct., 1926; ditto (perm.) Jan., 1930.

LECKY-THOMPSON, THOMAS EDWARD, B.Sc., Indian Police (b. 7th Nov., 1902).—Joined the service as asst. dist. supt., Burma, June, 1926; offg. dist. supt., June, 1934.

LECKY - THOMPSON, WILLIAM, Indian Police (dist. supt., Burma) (b. 4th Sept., 1900).—Joined the service as asst. dist. supt., Burma, 10th Sept., 1922; offg. dist. supt., Feb., 1930; confd., Feb., 1936.

LEDGARD, The Venerable RALPH GILBERT, M.A., late Indian Eccles. Estabt. (Central Provs.) (b. 15th Jan., 1877).—Joined the service, 12th Jan., 1910; apptd. chaplain on probation, 22nd Feb., 1910; junr. chaplain, May, 1910; senr. chaplain, May, 1920; offg. archdeacon, Nov., 1926; con'd., May, 1927; ret'd., Apl., 1930.

LEDGER, LLOYD KIRKWOOD, Lt.-Col., I.M.S. (civil and agency surg.) (b. 27th May, 1895).—First commn. dated 15th Mar., 1920; in milty. employ up to 30th June, 1925; offg. agency surg., Peshawar, July, 1925; offg. medl. officer, Seistan; agency surg., Bundelkhand, May, 1929; ditto, Gilgit, Oct., 1932; civil surg., Peshawar, Oct., 1936.

LEE, REV. ARTHUR OLDFIELD NORRIS, M.A., Indian Eccles. Estabt. (senr. chaplain) (b. 6th Aug., 1888).—Joined the service as junr. chaplain, 30th Nov., 1921; senr. chaplain, Nov., 1931.

LEE, DIONYS JOHN NORRIS, B.A., Indian Civil Service (dep. commr., Central Provs.) (b. 9th Oct., 1892).—Educ. at King's School, Canterbury, and Wadham, Coll. Oxford; on mil. service from April, 1915; joined the service, 3rd Oct., 1919, and served in the Central Provs. as asst. commr. from 2nd Dec., 1919; offg. dep. commr., March, 1924; dep. commr. (provl.), Dec., 1926; confd., March, 1927.

LEE, EDWARD OWEN, B.A., Indian C.S. (commr. of excise, etc., Bihar) (b. 3rd Nov., 1891).—Educ. at Malvern and King's Coll., Cambridge; apptd. after exam. of 1914; arrived, 3rd Dec., 1915, and served in Bihar and Orissa as asst. mag. and collr.; on mil. service from Aug., 1916, to Feb., 1919; asst. commr., Assam, April, 1922; services replaced at disposal of govt. of Bihar and Orissa, Oct., 1922; offg. dep. commr., Oct., 1924; offg. mag. and collr., Oct., 1925; mgte. and collr. (provl. sub.), Jan., 1929; confd., May, 1930; offg. dep. commr., Delhi, April, 1929; chairman, Assam labour bd., Apl., 1933; contr. of emigrant lab. in Assam, Oct., 1933; commr. of excise, etc., Bihar, Oct., 1936.

LEE, GEORGE HENRY, M.C., Indian Police (b. 19th Jan., 1898).—Joined the service as asst. dist. supt., Central Provs., 18th Oct., 1923; offg. dist. supt., Mar., 1931; off. dep. supt. Watch and Ward dept., E.I. ry., Apl., 1933.

LEE, JOSEPH STANDISH SEARCHFIELD, A.M.I.C.E., Indian Service of Engrs. (exec. engr., Unite! Provs.) (b. 8th Oct., 1883).—Joined the service as asst. engr., 1st Oct., 1907; exec. engr., Oct., 1916.

LEE, Rev. Canon PERCY ERSKINE, M.A., late Indian Eccles. Estabt. (b. 9th Feb., 1882).—Joined the service, 23rd Dec., 1919; junr. chaplain, Dec., 1921; senr. chaplain, Dec., 1927; ret'd., Aug., 1937.

LEE, REGINALD HENRY, M.B., Lt.-Col., I.M.S., ret'd., late Civil Medl. Dept., Bihar and Orissa (b. 19th May, 1880).—First commn. dated 1st Feb., 1906; civil surg., Oct., 1921; ret'd., May, 1935.

LEE, Rev. ROBERT EWING, M.A., B.D., M.C., late Indian Eccles. Estabt. (b. 26th Sept., 1883).—Apptd. chaplain on probation, 23rd Oct., 1923; on mil. service from 13th June, 1916, to 22nd Sept., 1919; junr. chaplain, Oct., 1916; senr. chaplain, Oct., 1925; services placed at disposal of Army Dept., from 28th March, 1924; ditto, Bombay Govt., from 10th Jan., 1927; offg. presy. senr. chaplain, church of Scotland, Bombay, Dec., 1927; ditto, Madras, Nov., 1928; confid., Feb., 1931; offg. presy. senr. chaplain, Bengal, Oct., 1933; confid., Mar., 1935; ret'd., Aug., 1937.

LEEPER, WILLIAM CLUFF, Major, I.A., Indian Political Service (b. 20th Nov., 1899).—First commn., 15th Apr., 1919; in milty. employ to 21st Aug., 1928; asst. pol. agent, S. Waziristan, Oct., 1929; pers. asst. to ch. commr. and A.G.G., N.W.F.P., May, 1930; asst. commr., Peshawar, May, 1931; ditto, Kohat, Mar., 1933; ditto, Nowshera, June, 1933; ditto, Charsadda, Oct., 1933; ditto, Mardan, Feb., 1935; asst. commr., Manshera, May, 1937; pol. agent, Kurram, Oct., 1937.

LEES, LEONARD MARTYN, Indian C.S. (dep. commr., Burma) (b. 2nd Dec., 1898).—Educ. at Winchester and New Coll., Oxford; joined the service, 1st Nov., 1921; arrived, 21st Dec., 1921, and served in Burma as asst. commr.; offg. under-sec. to govt. (Edn.), June, 1924; offg. dep. commr., Sept., 1927; dep. commr., p.s., Apr., 1937.

LEES, ROGER EDWARD ROWLEY, Indian Police (asst. supt., Bihar) (b. 14th Aug., 1914).—Joined the service as asst. supt., Bihar and Orissa, 22nd Nov., 1934.

LEETE, Capt. M.B.T.S., Civil Aviation Directorate (b. 8th Oct., 1898).—Joined the service as addl. technical officer (tempy.), Apr., 1936.

LEGGETT, STEPHEN, M.I.C.E., late Public Works Dept., Punjab (b. 4th Mar., 1878).—Joined the service as asst. supt., 30th Oct., 1907; supt., Aug., 1927; ret'd., Mar., 1932.

LEHNA SINGH, BHAI RAI BAHADUR, B.A., M.B.E., late Indian Political Dept (b. Sep., 1872).—Apptd. divl. and sess. judge, Derajat, 2nd Jan., 1924; M.B.E., June, 1919; ret'd., Sept., 1929.

LEIGH, Rev. ARTHUR GEORGE DAVIES, M.A., Indian Eccles. Estabt. (United Provs.) (junr. chaplain) (b. 3rd Apr., 1892).—Apptd. chaplain on probation, 26th Dec., 1926; junr. chaplain, Dec., 1928.

LEIGHTON, ERIC, Indian Police (b. 11th May, 1905).—Joined the service as asst. dist. supt., Central Provinces, 7th Dec., 1924; offg. dist. supt., Feb., 1928.

LE MESURIER, ALGERNON PAUL, B.A., Indian C.S. (asst. coll., Bombay) (b. 28th Feb., 1905).—Educ. at Rugby, Lausanne, and Oriel Coll., Oxford; joined the service, 15th Oct., 1928; arrived, Dec., 1928, and served in Bombay as asst. collr.; offg. collr., June, 1936.

LEMON, GUY TALBOT, O.B.E., V.D., B.A., A.M.I.C.E., late Indian State Riys. (b. 24th Jan., 1886).—Joined the service as junr. engr., 16th April, 1910; in milty. employ from 25th Nov., 1914, to 20th March, 1920; O.B.E., 1919; asst. engr., Jan., 1919; resdt. engr., Sept., 1919; offg. exec. engr., Jan., 1920; confid., Nov., 1926; pers. asst. to engr.-in.-ch., Nov., 1928; ret'd., Oct., 1933.

LEMON, ROBERT ERNEST, B.A.I., late Indian Service of Engrs. (United Provs.) (b. 2nd June, 1886).—Joined the service as asst. engr., 1st Oct., 1908; exec. engr., Oct., 1916; ret'd., Apr., 1930.

LENNAN, AIDAN WILLIAM, late Indian Police (b. 12th Aug., 1913).—Joined the service as proby. asst. supt. of police, Madras, 26th Nov., 1933; ret'd., Dec., 1937.

LENNOX, GILBERT, Capt. I.A., ret'd., late Survey of India (b. 19th Feb., 1894).—First commn. dated 22nd Jan., 1913; asst. supt. (proby.), June, 1920; confid., June, 1922; supt., Dec., 1928; ret'd., May, 1932.

LESLIE, ANDREW SMITH, Lt.-Col., I.M.S. ret'd. (b. 24th June, 1882).—Joined the service, 1st Sept., 1906; D.M.O., Kurnool, Dec., 1923; offg. prof. of medicine, medl. coll., Vizagapatam, Feb., 1926; offg. civil surg., Sept., 1926; confid., June, 1929; offg. surg. to govr. of Madras, June, 1929; ret'd., June, 1937.

LESLIE-JONES, FREDERICK ARCHIBALD, C.B.E., M.A., late Indian Educl. Service (b. 9th July, 1874).—Educ. at Hereford Cath. Sch., Bromsgrove Sch., and Lincoln Coll., Oxford; asst. master, Marlborough Coll., 1897-1901; apptd. princpl., Aitchison Coll., Lahore, 27th Sept., 1904; princpl., Mayo Coll., Ajmer, July, 1917; C.B.E., Dec., 1919; ret'd., July, 1929.

LESSLIE, DAVID FINDLAY, M.A., Indian Police (supt., Bengal) (b. 31st Mar., 1897).—Joined the service as asst. supt., 27th Jan., 1920; offg. supt., March, 1925; confid., Aug., 1929; asst. to dep. inspr.-genl., Oct., 1936.

LESTER-GARLAND, Rev. GEORGE HENRY, M.A., Indian Eccles. Estabt. (senr. chaplain, Punjab) (b. 9th May, 1895).—Apptd. chaplain on probation, N.W.F.P., 26th March, 1926; junr. chaplain, Apr., 1926; senr. chaplain, June, 1936.

L'ESTRANGE, ALBERT PERCIVAL PORTER, Land Records Dept., Burma (asst. dir.) (b. 13th Dec., 1882).—Joined the service as probnr., 22nd Sept., 1903; supt., Aug., 1907; asst. dir., Aug., 1930.

LETHBRIDGE, MONTAGU HENRY BOWATER, B.A., Indian C.S. (dist. and sess. judge, Bengal) (b. 22nd March, 1889).—Educ. at Westminster, and Christ Church, Oxford; apptd. after exam. of 1911; arrived, 30th Nov., 1912, and served in Bengal as asst. mag. and collr.; asst. settlt.

Offr., Oct., 1915; I.A.R.O., Aug., 1916, to Feb., 1919; jt. mag. and dep. collr., April, 1918; dist. and sess. judge and commr. for workmen's compensation, Nov., 1924; dist. and sess. judge, Assam, Nov., 1931; offg. secty. to govt., Assam, legisl. dept., Oct., 1933; confd., Dec., 1934; dist. and sess. judge, Bengal, Nov., 1935.

LETT, HARRY NELSON, D.F.C., M.A., Indian C.S. (asst. commr., Burma) (b. 21st Oct., 1896).—Educ. at Univ. Coll., Southampton, and Queen's Coll., Oxford; joined the service, 1st Nov., 1921; arrived, 21st Dec., 1921, and served in Burma as asst. commr.; offg. dep. commr., March, 1925; secty. to finl. commr., Rangoon, Aug., 1933.

LETTON, CHARLES THOMAS, C.I.E., M.B.E., late Printing and Stationery Dept. (b. 25th Mar., 1878).—Held non-gazetted appts. from 8th April, 1907; offg. supt. govt. printing, India, Calcutta, May, 1911; supt., govt. central branch press, Simla, April, 1914; M.B.E., Jan., 1919; offg. manager, govt. of India press, Calcutta, March, 1925; confd., Oct., 1926; offg. contr. of ptg., staty. and stamps, Feb., 1927; confd., Oct., 1930; retd., Mar., 1935; C.I.E., June, 1935.

LEVINGE, SIR EDWARD VERE, K.C.I.E., G.S.I., B.A., late Indian C.S.—Educ. at Cheltenham Coll., and Balliol Coll., Oxford; apptd. after exam. of 1885; arrived, 28th Nov., 1888, and served in Bengal as asst. mag. and collr.; under sec., jud., &c., depts., Nov., 1893; joint mag. and dep. collr., April, 1896; mag. and collr., July, 1898; temp. addl. commr., Feb., 1908; offg. chief sec. to govt., April, 1910; commr., April, 1911; C.S.I., Dec., 1911; transf. to Bihar and Orissa as member, bd. of rev., April, 1912; member, exec. council, Aug., 1912, to Nov., 1917; K.C.I.E., Jan., 1916; actg. lieut.-govr., Bihar and Orissa, from April, 1918; retd., Aug., 1918.

LEWIS, CLINTON GRESHAM, Brig., O.B.E., R.E., Survey of India (surveyor-genl.) (b. 25th Nov., 1885).—First commn. dated 21st Dec., 1904; apptd. asst. supt., trigl. survey, 7th Nov., 1907; in charge Miri mission survey detachment, Oct., 1911, to Apl., 1912; temp. reverted to mily. duty Oct., 1914, to July, 1918; dep. supt., Aug., 1922; on foreign service with Turco-Iraq frontier delimitation commn., from Feb., 1927; supt., April, 1928; O.B.E., June, 1928; offg. dir., Apl., 1933; confd., June, 1934; surveyor-genl., Sept., 1937.

LEWIS, ELWYN BYRON, Northern India Salt Rev. Dept. (chief mining engr., Khewra) (b. 3rd Jan., 1900).—Joined the service, 5th Nov., 1935; chief mining engr., Khewra, Nov., 1935.

LEWIS, LUCIEN ALEXANDER, M.I.E.E., Office of High Commr. for India (dep. dir. of inspection, store dept.) (b. 3rd May, 1882).—Educ. at Brighton Gr. Sch., and City and Guilds Inst., Finsbury Coll.; apptd. asst. inspr. of scientific supplies, Store Dept., 19th Nov., 1906; electrical engr., ditto, Aug., 1917; dep. dir. of inspection, store dept., July, 1936.

LEWIS, Rev. OLIVER GEORGE, M.A., Indian Eccl. Estab. (junr. chaplain) (b. 11th Aug., 1894).—Apptd. chaplain-on-probation, 5th Oct., 1929; junior chaplain, Oct., 1931.

LEWIS, SYDNEY ALLAN MAYBURY, M.R.San.I., Indian Service of Engrs. (supfg. engr., Sind) (b. 23rd Sept., 1886).—Joined the service as asst. engr., 1st Oct., 1910; exec. engr., Oct., 1918; offg. suptg. engr., June, 1933; suptg. engr. (prov. perm.), May, 1936.

LEWIS, WALTER ALAN SCOTT, B.A., Indian C.S. (jt. mag. and dep. collr., Bengal) (b. 1st July, 1907).—Educ. at Winchester and Lincoln College, Oxford; apptd. after examination of 1930; arrived in India, Nov., 1931, and served in Bengal as asst. mag. and collr.; jt. mag. and dep. collr., June, 1933.

LEWIS, SIR WILLIAM HAWTHORNE, K.C.I.E., C.S.I., B.A., Indian C.S. (reforms commr.) (b. 29th June, 1888).—Educ. at Oundle, and Caius Coll., Cambridge; apptd. after exam. of 1911; arrived, 29th Nov., 1912, and served in Bihar and Orissa as asst. mag. and collr.; on, censor duty, Bombay, from Apl., 1915, to March, 1916; under-sec. to govt., rev. and jud. depts., Bihar and Orissa, June, 1918; jt. mag., March, 1921; offg. dep. commr., March, 1923; offg. sec. to govt., rev. dept., Oct., 1925; confd. and magte. collr., Oct. 1926; on spl. duty, home dept., govt. of India, April, 1927; jt. secty., reforms office, May, 1930; jt. secty. to British delegation, Indian round table conf. London, Nov., 1930-Jan., 1931; 2nd Session, Sept.-Dec., 1931; C.I.E., Jan., 1931; jt. secty., reforms office, Jan., 1932; offg. reforms commr., Govt. of India, May, 1932; C.S.I., Jan., 1934; addl. secty., reforms, govt. of India, Feb., 1935; reforms commr., July, 1936; K.C.I.E., Jan., 1938.

LEWIS, WILLIAM HOWARD, Indian Police (b. 7th Nov., 1906).—Joined the service as asst. supt., Bengal, 14th Nov., 1927; offg. supt., Feb. 1934; offg. dep. commr., Calcutta, Feb., 1936.

LEWISOHN, LEON HORNBY, Indian Police (b. 24th Dec., 1912).—Joined the service as proby. asst. supt., Bengal, Nov., 1932; confd., Mar., 1935.

LEWTON-BRAIN, GERALD EVAN, Indian Police (b. 24th June, 1907).—Joined the service as asst. supt., N.W.F.P., 17th Nov., 1926.

LEWYS-LLOYD, IDRIS WYN, B.A., Indian Civil Service (jt. magte., United Provs.) (b. 7th Dec., 1905).—Educ. at Colwyn County Sch., Univ. of Wales and Paris; joined the service, 10th Dec., 1928; arrived, 12th Jan., 1929, and served in the United Provs. as asst. magte. and collr.; jt. magte., Mar., 1931; offg. mag. and collr., Sept., 1934; settlement offcr., Nov., 1937.

LEY, ARTHUR HERBERT, C.S.I., C.I.E., C.B.E., late Indian C.S. (b. 7th Nov., 1879).—Educ. at Winchester Coll. and New Coll, Oxford; apptd. after exam. of 1902; arrived, 4th Dec., 1903, and served in Bengal as asst. mag. and collr.; under-sec. to govt., rev., pol. and appt. dep. Jan., 1908; transf. to Bihar, April, 1912; under-sec. to govt. of India, dept. of commerce and industry, from Aug., 1909, to Nov., 1912; jt. mag. and dep. collr., Nov., 1912; dir.-gen. of commercial intelligence, govt. of India, July, 1914; dep. sec. to govt. of India, commerce and industry dept. (temp.), from Jan., 1916; C.I.E., Jan., 1918; temp. sec., commerce and industry dept., Nov., 1917; temp. jt. sec., ditto, Aug., 1918; temp. sec., ditto, Dec., 1918; sec. to govt. of India, dept. of industries

and labour, April, 1923; C.B.E., June, 1924; temp. member of govr.-gen.'s exec. council, 28th March to 26th June, 1925; C.S.I., Jan., 1926; member, public services commn., Oct., 1926-Sept., 1931; retd., Aug., 1933.

LIAQAT HAYAT KHAN, SIR, NAWAB, KHAN BAHADUR, KT., O.B.E., late Indian Police Service (Punjab) (b. 1st Feb., 1884).—Joined the service, 22nd May, 1909; asst. supt., March, 1919; supt., Dec., 1919; O.B.E., June, 1923; home sec., Patiala State, Oct., 1923; retd., June, 1931; delegate to Indian round table conf., London, 2nd Session, Sept.-Dec., 1931; 3rd Session, Nov., 1932; knighted, Jan., 1933.

LIDDELL, HENRY CRAWFORD, C.I.E., M.A., Barr., late Indian C.S. (Bengal), (b. 17th Dec., 1878).—Educ. at Geo. Watson's Coll., Edinburgh, and Edinburgh Univ.; apptd. after exam. of 1900; arrived, 5th Dec., 1901, and served in the Central Provs. as asst. commr.; asst. mag. and collr., Bengal, April, 1906; jt. mag. and dep. collr., Feb., 1910; dist. and sess. judge, Sept., 1914; ditto, Assam, Feb., 1916; re-transfd. to Bengal as dist. and sessions judge, Jan., 1922; supt. and legal remembr. and sec., judicial dept., govt. of Bengal, June, 1925; C.I.E., June, 1927; retd., Mar., 1930.

LIDIERTH, JAMES EUGENE, I.S.O., M.B.E. (offr. of pol. dept.) (b. 12th Oct., 1883).—Held non-gazetted appts. from 1st June, 1908, to 15th Oct., 1919; M.B.E., Dec., 1919; extra asst. commr., April, 1921; offg. under-sec. to agent to govr.-genl. in Baluchistan, Oct., 1922, and again, Aug., 1929; awarded Kaisar-i-Hind medal (2nd class) in 1925; registrar, A.G.G.'s office, Quetta, April, 1929; asst. secty. to A.G.G. and C.C. in Baluchistan, Aug., 1929; offg. under secty., ditto, Dec., 1932; asst. pol. agent, Quetta-Pishin, Apl., 1934; offg. under-secy. to A.G., Baluchistan; July, 1935; I.S.O., May, 1937.

LIGHTFOOT, GORDON SHELLEY, Indian Police (supt., Assam) (b. 18th Aug., 1897).—Served in the Indian Army from 12th May, 1915, to 18th May, 1923; proby. asst. supt., 19th May, 1923; confid., May, 1925; awarded King's police medal, Jan., 1930; supt., May, 1930; pol. officer, Balipara frontier tract, Oct., 1934.

LIGHTWLER, IVOR HARRISON, Indian State Rlys. (dist. contr. of stores) (b. 21st Dec., 1892).—Joined the service as asst. storekeeper, 20th May, 1912; asst. contr. of stores, April, 1919; dist. ditto, Mar., 1931.

LILEY, WALTER HEMMING, A.M.I.M.E., late Indian State Rlys. (b. 7th June, 1882).—Joined the service as asst. signal and interlocking engr., 31st May, 1907; signal and interlocking engr., June, 1917; dep. ch. engr., signals, G.I.P. rly., Aug., 1926; retd., Dec., 1935.

LILLEY, HUGH MICHAEL, Indian Police (asst. supt., Burma) (b. 2nd Mar., 1915).—Apptd. asst. supt., Burma, on probn., 5th Dec., 1934; confd., Dec., 1936.

LILLIE, CECIL JAMES WINGATE, Indian C.S. (dep. commr., Central Provs.) (b. 6th July, 1897).—Educ. at Clifton, and Brasenose Coll., Oxford; joined the service, 27th Oct., 1921; arrived, 1st Dec., 1921; and served in Central Provs. as asst. commr.; offg. dep. commr.,

March, 1925; under secty. to govt., Dec., 1931; offg. dep. commr., Mar., 1932; offg. commr. of settlements and dir. of land records, etc., May, 1933; offg. secty., rev. dept., Central Provs., Nov., 1936; dep. commr., Mar., 1937.

LILLIE, HORACE, Indian Police (b. 2nd Apl., 1886).—Joined the service, 22nd Nov., 1906; asst. supt., Nov., 1907; supt., Aug., 1914; comdt., frontier constaby., N.W.F.P., Sept., 1933.

LIMPUS, CHARLES GEORGE, late Indian State Rlys. (b. 20th Apl., 1871).—Joined the service 18th Jan., 1889; asst. dist. loco. supt., July, 1919; asst. trans. supt., Nov., 1922; divl. trans. supt., Aug., 1926; offg. dep. ditto, Feb., 1928; retd., May, 1932.

LINCKE, SYDNEY HOWARD PHIPPS, Indian State Rlys. (dep. contr. of stores, G.I.P. Rly.) (b. 7th May, 1891).—Joined the service as asst. storekeeper, 8th Nov., 1912; dist. contr. of stores, Nov., 1926; dep. ditto, Nov., 1928; offg. contr. of stores, variously from Nov., 1929.

LINCOLN, EDWARD HENNING, V.D., Punjab C.S. (dep. commr.) (b. 16th Mar., 1889).—Joined the service, 14th Aug., 1905; extra asst. commr., Mar., 1934; dep. commr., Nov., 1933; offg. secty., elec. and indus. depts., Punjab, Apl., to July, 1936.

LINCOLN, HENRY HENNING, M.B.E., late Dept. of Educn., Health and Lands, Govt. of India (b. 25th Mar., 1882).—Joined the service, 24th Oct., 1898; supt., dept. of educn., health and lands, Govt. of India, Apl., 1923; offg. regr., ditto, Oct., 1926; asst. secty., ditto, Jan., 1928; M.B.E., Jan., 1935; retd., Sept., 1937.

LINDLEY, EDWARD SEARLES, B.A., M.I.C.E., late Indian Service of Engrs.—Joined the service, 1st Oct., 1904, and posted as asst. engr. Central Provs.; transfd. to Punjab, Dec., 1905; exec. engr., Oct., 1912; offg. suptg. engr., May, 1924; retd., Oct., 1928.

LINDOP, KENNETH JAMES HULME, M.C., Indian C.S. (asst. commr., Burma) (b. 13th July, 1898).—Educ. at Denston Coll., Staffs, and Worcester Coll., Oxford; joined the service, 1st Nov., 1922; arrived, 26th Dec., 1922, and served in Burma as asst. commr.; offg. settl. officer, March, 1923; offg. dep. commr., Oct., 1928.

LINDSAY, SIR BENJAMIN, KT., late Indian C.S. (United Provs.).—Educ. at the Methodist Coll., Belfast, and Trinity Coll., Dublin; apptd. after exam. of 1887; arrived, 4th Nov., 1889, and served in the N.W. Prov. as asst. mag. and collr., and in Oudh as asst. commr.; asst. supt., and small cause court judge, Dehra Dun, April, 1894; joint mag., May, 1894; registrar, high court, March, 1898; dist. and sess. judge, Feb., 1901; member of legisl. council, April, 1906; legal remembrancer to govt., April, 1908; 2nd addl. judl. commr., Oudh, April, 1911; 1st ditto, Nov., 1911; judl. commr., Oudh, May, 1914; judge, high ct., Allahabad, Nov., 1921; actg. ch. justice, ditto, July, 1927; Kt., June, 1928; retd., Oct., 1928.

LINDSAY, SIR HARRY ALEXANDER FANSHAW, K.C.I.E., C.B.E., late Indian C.S. (b. 11th Mar., 1881).—Educ. at St. Paul's Sch., and Worcester Coll., Oxford; apptd. after exam. of

1904; arrived, 28th Nov., 1905, and served in Bengal as asst. mag. and collr.; under sec. to govt., rev. and genl. depts., Mar., 1910; transf. to Bihar, April, 1912; under sec. to govt., rev. dept., April, 1912; under sec. to govt. of India, comm. and ind. dept., from Nov., 1912; dir.-gen., comml. intelligence, India, from Jan. 1916; C.B.E., June, 1919; offg. sec. to govt. of India, dept. of commerce, Sept., 1921; Indian trade commr., London, from 1st Feb., 1923, to Sept., 1934; C.I.E., Jan., 1926; sec. and adviser to Indian del., Impl. econ. conf., May to Sept., 1932; adviser to Indian delegation, monetary and economic conf., June, 1933; deputed to Indian lac cess ctee., U.S.A., Nov., 1933; K.C.I.E., Jan., 1934; commr., Dec., 1935; ret'd., Feb., 1937.

LINDSAY, JAMES HAMILTON, M.A., late Indian C.S. (Bengal) (b. 13th March, 1882).—Educ. at Glasgow Acad. and Glasgow Univ.; apptd. after exam. of 1904; arrived 4th Dec., 1905, and served in Bengal as asst. mag. and collr.; jt. mag. and dep. collr., Dec., 1912; mag. and collr., April, 1918; sec. to govt. of Bengal, educn. dept., May, 1925; ret'd., Nov., 1930.

LINDSAY, REV. W. T., M.A., Ind. Eccles. Estabt. (b. 2nd Jan., 1897).—Apptd. junior chaplain, Bombay, on probn., 8th Nov., 1934; confd., Nov., 1936.

LINES, ALFRED F.C.H., late Indian State Railways (b. 15th Oct., 1875).—Trained at R.I.E. Coll.; joined the service, 1st Oct., 1897, as asst. engr.; exec. engr., March, 1908; suptg. engr., Aug., 1923; chief engr., May, 1925; offg. dir., civil engrg., rly. board, April, 1926; confd., Oct., 1926; ret'd., Dec., 1930.

LINES, ROY NICHOLAS, B.A., Indian C.S. (asst. mag. and collr., B. and O.) (b. 11th Sept., 1914).—Educ. at Royal Latin Sch., Buckingham, and Worcester Coll., Oxford; joined the service, 20th Sept., 1937; arrived in India, Nov., 1937, and served in B. and O. as asst. mag. and collr.

LINGWOOD, FREDERICK, Office of the High Commr. for India (exec. officer, accts. dept.) (b. 1st Mar., 1901).—Apptd. clerical officer, 3rd Feb., 1924; actg. exec. officer, Feb., 1929; confd., Feb., 1936.

LINLITHGOW, Most Hon. Marquess of, VICTOR ALEXANDER JOHN HOPE, P.C., K.T., G.M.S.I., G.M.I.E., O.B.E., T.D. (viceroys and gov.-genl. of India)—Educ. at Eton; served European War, 1914-1918 (despatches); civil Lord of the Admy., 1922-24; presdt. navy league, 1924-31; chairman, departl. ctee. on distribn. and prices of agricl. produce, 1923; chairman, Rl. commn. on Indian agriculture, 1926-28; chairman, jt. select committee on Indian constitl. reform, 1933; G.C.S.I., Apl., 1936; viceroy and gov.-genl. of India, Apl., 1936; Kt. of Grace of Order of St. John of Jerusalem, June, 1936.

LIST, JOHN NAPIER, M.C., M.A., A.M.I.C.E., late Indian Service of Engrs. (b. 19th Nov., 1884).—Joined the service as asst. engr., 1st Oct., 1907; on mil. service from 21st Nov., 1915, to 1st Nov., 1920; exec. engr., Nov., 1920; offg. suptg. engr., April, 1928; ret'd., Sept., 1932.

LISTER, Rev. JOHN GEORGE, M.A., Indian Eccles. Estabt. (senr. chaplain, Punjab) (b. 8th Mar., 1885).—Joined the service as junr. chaplain, 2nd Feb., 1914; senr. chaplain, Feb., 1927.

LISTER, TOM, C.I.E., B.A., late Indian C.S. (b. 10th May, 1887).—Educated at Wheelwright Gr. Schools, Dewsbury, and St. John's Coll., Cambridge; apptd. after exam. of 1910; arrived in India, 2nd Dec., 1911, and served in Burma as asst. commr.; priv. sec. to lieut.-govr., April, 1917; services placed temporarily at disposal of govt. of United Provs., Jan., 1918; priv. sec. to lieut.-govr., United Prov., Feb., 1918; depd. to Federated Malay States on special duty, Aug., 1919, to Jan., 1920; asst. commr. and sec. to devl. commr., Burma, Jan., 1921; offg. dep. commr., Nov., 1922; and sec. to finl. commr. (add.), Nov., 1924; dep. commr. (provl. sub.), Sept., 1927; offg. secty. to govt., rev. dept., May, 1929; confd., Sept., 1929; offg. secty. to govt., reforms office, June, 1930; delegate to Burma round table conf., London, Dec., 1931; C.I.E., June, 1932; ret'd., May, 1937.

LISTER-JACKSON, ARTHUR, Indian State Rly. (exec. engr., E.B. Rly.) (b. 18th Sept., 1891).—Joined the service as asst. exec. engr., 1st Oct., 1914; on milty. service from Sept., 1915, to Nov., 1919; exec. engr. (junr.), Oct., 1923; ditto (senr.), July, 1934.

LITT, E. EDWARD PYBUS, Indian Service of Engrs. (exec. engr., Bombay).—Joined the service as asst. exec. engr., Bombay, 28th Sept., 1925; exec. engr., Sept., 1935.

LITTLE, MALCOLM, A.M.I.C.E., late Indian Service of Engrs. (b. 9th Dec., 1877).—Joined the service, 21st Nov., 1905; asst. engr., Nov., 1908; exec. engr., Nov., 1914; offg. suptg. engr., Oct., 1926; confd., Sept., 1928; offg. secy., P.W.D., Assam, June, 1930; confd., Oct., 1932; ch. engr., Oct., 1931; ret'd., Dec., 1932.

LITTLEHAILES, RICHARD, C.I.E., M.A. (Oxon), late Indian Educl. Service (b. 14th Feb., 1878).—Joined the service, 13th July, 1903, as prof. of maths., Presdy. Coll., Madras; inspr. of schools, Oct., 1908; prof. of physics, Presdy. Coll., June, 1913; meteorologist and dep. dir., Madras, Observat. ry, June to Nov., 1913; prof. of mathematics, Presdy. Coll., Aug., 1913; dir. of public instrn., Madras, Feb., 1919; offg. educ. commr. with govt. of India, Feb., 1925, and again, Apl., 1927; C.I.E., June, 1927; ret'd., Feb., 1933.

LITTLEWOOD, ALFRED MALCOLM CECIL, late Indian Forest Service (b. 5th Oct., 1879).—Joined the service 22nd July, 1899; dep. consvr., Mar., 1920; actg. consvr., Apl., 1927; ret'd., Oct., 1934.

LITTLEWOOD, GLADYS ELIZABETH, M.B.E., Indian Educl. Service (inspectress of girls' schools, N.W.F.P.) (b. 1st Nov., 1886).—Joined the service, 16th June, 1918; inspectress of schools, Ambala, Nov., 1919; ditto, N.W.F.P., May, 1920; K.-i-H. Medal, 1st Cl., June, 1934.

LITTLEWOOD, ROWLAND WHITELAW, Indian Agric. Service (b. 19th June, 1888).—Apptd. dep. dir. of agric., Madras, Jan., 1921.

LIVINGSTONE-LEARMOUTH, ARTHUR PERCY, late Indian Service of Engrs. (Punjab) (b. 5th Oct., 1885).—Joined the service as asst. engr., 1st Oct., 1909; exec. engr., Oct., 1917; ret'd., May, 1929.

LLEWELLYN, JOHN LISTER, B.A. (Cantab.) Indian C.S. (jt. mag. and dep. collr., Bengal) (b. 10th April, 1906).—Educ. at Nottingham High School, and King's Coll., Cambridge; joined the service, 17th Oct., 1929; arrived, November, 1929, and served in Bengal as asst. mag. and collr.; jt. mag. and dep. collr., Nov., 1931; offig. mag. and collr., July, 1935.

LLOYD, ALAN HUBERT, C.S.I., C.I.E., B.A., Indian C.S., Burma, attd. Impl. Customs Service (member, central board of revenue) (b. 30th Aug., 1883).—Educ. at King William's Coll., Isle of Man, and Caius Coll., Cambridge; apptd. after exam. of 1906; arrived, 25th Nov., 1907; and served in Burma as asst. commr.; offig. asst. collr. of customs, Impl. Customs Service, Rangoon, March, 1910; confd., Nov., 1911; transfd. to Bengal as asst. collr. of customs, June, 1916; on deputn. under commercial intelligence dept., India, Dec., 1916, to June, 1917; offig. collr. of customs, June, 1917; confd., Dec., 1919; apptd. first member of central board of rev., govt. of India, Dec., 1923; C.I.E., Jan., 1928; temp. member, govr.-genl's. exec. council, June, 1933; C.S.I., Jan., 1934; member, council of state, Apl., 1935.

LLOYD, ARTHUR HENRY, M.C., B.A. late Indian Forest Service (b. 15th June, 1893).—Joined the service as asst. consvr., 24th Dec., 1921; dep. consvr., May, 1923; dir., Pyinmana forest school, Dec., 1925; retd., Aug., 1930.

LLOYD, CLAUDE PEMBERTON WILKINSON, M.A., late Indian Educational Service. —Joined the service, 29th March, 1910, as profr., Muir Central Coll., Allahabad; on mil. service from July, 1915, to Dec., 1918; profr., Allahabad Univ., Oct., 1923; retd., May, 1928.

LLOYD, BARON, Rt. Hon. GEORGE AMBERCESE LLOYD OF DOLOBRAN, P.C., G.C.S.I., G.C.I.E., D.S.O., F.R.G.S.—GOVR. of Bombay from 16th Dec., 1918, to 9th Dec., 1923.

LLOYD, GEORGE THOMAS, M.A., late Indian C.S. (Assam).—Educ. at Merchant Tailors' Sch. and Trinity Coll., Dublin; apptd. after exam. of 1900; arrived, 17th Dec., 1907; and served in E. Bengal and Assam as asst. mag. and collr.; asst. commr., Assam, April, 1912; on mil. duty (I.A.R.O.), Oct., 1916, to May, 1919; offig. supt., census operations, Apl., 1920; dep. commr., Aug., 1924; offig. 2nd sec. to govt. of Assam, Dec., 1924; confd., Oct., 1925; retd., Sept., 1928.

LLOYD, SIR IDWAL GEOFFREY, C.S.I., B.A., late Indian C.S. (b. 13th Jan., 1878).—Educ. at King William's Coll., Isle of Man, and Caius Coll., Cambridge; apptd. after exam. of 1901; arrived, 1st Dec., 1902, and served in Burma as asst. commr.; asst. sec. to govt., July-Aug., 1905; regr., chief court, Lower Burma, Dec., 1906; under sec. to govt. of India, Home Dept., Feb., 1910; sec. to finl. commr., Burma, Jan., 1913, to May, 1919; dep. commr., April, 1916; offig. commr., Oct., 1924; commr. (provl.), April, 1926; ch. secty. to govt., home and pol. dept., Nov., 1929; finl. commr. (reserved subjects), Aug., 1930; temp. member of govr.'s exec. council, Burma, Oct., 1930; confd., June, 1932; C.S.I., Jan., 1933; finl. commr., July, 1933; member govr.'s exec. council, Burma (temp.), Apl., 1935; confd., Aug., 1935; knighted, May, 1937; retd., Dec., 1937.

LLOYD, REGINALD BROUGHTON, M.B., Lt.-Col., I.M.S. retd.
