(b. 31st Aug., 1881).—First commn., dated 1st Aug., 1908; in milty. employ up to 25th Sept., 1912; probnr., chemical examr.'s dept., Calcutta, Sept., 1912; in milty. employ. from Oct., 1914, to April, 1919; offig. impl. serologist, May, 1919; confd., July, 1920; retd., Apl., 1936.

LLOYD, STEPHEN, late Indian C.S. (b. 5th Sept., 1906).—Educ. at Marlborough and Lincoln College, Oxford; joined the service, 14th October, 1930; arrived in India, November, 1930, and served in the United Provinces as asst. mag. and collr.; retd., May, 1935.

LLOYD-WILLIAMS, JAMES EVAN, M.C., late Indian Police Service (Bihar and Orissa) (b. 7th Apl., 1888).—Joined the service, 2nd Dec., 1907; asst. supt., Feb., 1911; supt., Nov., 1917; retd., Sept., 1929.

LOBB, JOHN CHALICE, Indian Police (supl. Punjab) (b. 1st June, 1897).—Joined the service, 27th Dec., 1919; supt., Nov., 1926; awarded King's police medal, Jan., 1934; offig. dep. insp.-genl., variously from Apl., 1935.

LOBO, C. M., LL.B., Judl. Dept., Bombay, (b. 11th Aug., 1884).—Apptd. Govt. pleader and public prosecutor for Sind, 1st Apl., 1929; offig. addl. commr. for Sind, July, 1929; and again, Jan., 1936; confd., Mar., 1936.

LOBO, PASCHAL CONSTANTINE, Diwan Bahadur, B.A. (Cantab.), Barr., late Madras Civil Service (Judicial Branch) (b. 17th May, 1878).—Joined the service as dist. munsif, 4th Aug., 1910; sub. judge, June, 1917; dist. and sess. judge, Oct., 1922; retd., May, 1933.

LOBO-PRABHU, JOSEPH MATHIAS, Indian C.S. (jt. mag., United Provs.) (b. 26th Feb., 1906).—Educ. at Univ. of Madras and Sch. of Oriental Studies, London; joined the service, 28th Sept., 1928; arrived, Nov., 1928, and served in the United Provs. as asst. coll. and mag.

LOCH, PERCY GORDON, C.I.E., Lt.-Col., I.A., Indian Political Service (b. 16th Jan., 1887).—First commn., 5th Aug., 1905; in mil. employ till Feb., 1913; apptd. pol. officer on probation, March, 1913, and employed as asst. to pol. resdt. in Persian Gulf; on mil. duty, Oct., 1914, to Oct., 1916; pol. agent, Bahrein, Nov., 1916; under-sec. to govt. of India, for. and pol. dept., Oct., 1921; pol. agent, Gilgit, Sept., 1924; secty. to resdt., Mysore, Feb., 1929; offig. resdt., Mysore and C.C. Coorg, May, 1930; pol. agent, Eastern Kathiawar Agency, Mar., 1931; on foreign service as presdt. of Rewa Council; offig. resdt., Persian Gulf, Apl., and July, 1933; and again, July, 1934; pol. agent, Bahrein, May, 1933; C.I.E., June, 1935; offig. resdt., Persian Gulf, July, 1935; pol. agent, Bahrein, Oct., 1936.

LOCKE, JAMES EDWIN, late India Posts and Tels. (b. 28th Sept., 1887).—Held non-gazetted appt. from 1st Feb., 1907; asst. supt., Madras, May, 1919; supt., Madras, July, 1919; ditto, Lahore, Sept., 1919; asst. to dep. dir.-genl., Nov., 1921; supt., C.T.O., Rangoon, Aug., 1924; asst. dir. genl. traffic, Dec., 1926; dep. P.M.G., traffic, Calcutta, Mar., 1927; ch. supt., Calcutta tel. office, June, 1930; dep. p.m.g. traffic, Burma, Oct., 1932; retd., Feb., 1934.

LOCKWOOD, HENRY FRANCIS, late Indian State Railways (b. 26th Sept., 1879).—Joined the service, 3rd Aug., 1907; asst. traffic supt., E. Ben. Rly., Aug., 1907; ditto, N.W. Rly., Feb., 1912; dist. traffic supt., ditto, Oct., 1912; offg. divl. supt., Oct., 1924; confd., Oct., 1925; offg. ch. operatg. supt., Feb., 1930; and again, Apl., 1931; confd., Aug., 1933; retd., Sept., 1934.

LODGE, RONALD FRANCIS, B.A. (Cantab.), Indian C.S. (dist. and sess. judge) (b. 8th April, 1889).—Educ. at Liverpool Institute High Sch., and King's Coll., Cambridge; apptd. after exam. of 1912; arrived, 10th Dec., 1913, and served in Bengal as asst. mag. and collr.; jt. mag. and dep. collr., Oct., 1921; dist. and sess. judge, Sept., 1925; offg. judge, high ct., Calcutta, Apl., 1935, and again, Aug., 1936; regr. high ct., 1937.

LOGAN, FRANCIS HUGH, Indian C.S. (jt. mag., United Provs.) (b. 1st July, 1904).—Educ. at Haileybury, Corpus Christi Coll., Cambridge, Sorbonne, and Sch. of Oriental Studies, London; joined the service, 15th Oct., 1928; arrived, Nov., 1928, and served in the United Provs. as asst. mag. and collr.; asst. commr., Mar., 1930; jt. magte., Nov., 1931.

LOGAN, ROBERT ANGEL, M.R.C.S. (Engl.), L.R.C.P. (Lond.) Lt.-Col., I.M.S., Medical Dept., Central Provs. (civil surgeon) (b. 13th Nov., 1889).—First commn., 10th Oct., 1919; civil surgeon, Apl., 1932.

LOKUR, NAKAYAN SWAMIRAO, B.A., LL.B., Bombay Civil Service (judge and sess. judge, Bombay) (b. 21st Apl., 1887).—Held various judl. appts. from 22nd Oct., 1918, to 31st Dec., 1925; judge and sess. judge, Bombay, Jan., 1926.

LONG, CECIL EUSTACE, Indian State Rlys. (supt. of stores, Burma Rlys.) (b. 28th Feb., 1882).—Held non-gazetted appt. from 11th Aug., 1902 to 18th Apl., 1907; 2nd asst. supt. of stores, Apl., 1907; 1st ditto, Oct., 1922; dep. supt. of stores, Oct., 1925; offg. supt. of stores, Mar., 1928, and again, Mar., 1932; confd., Oct., 1935.

LONGHURST, ALBERT HENRY, late Archaeological Survey dept. (b. 2nd Nov., 1876).—Joined the service as asst. supt., 17th Nov., 1906; supt., Sept., 1910; retd. Nov., 1931.

LONGHURST, HENRY RICHARD, Indian Police (b. 20th Dec., 1912).—Joined the service as proby. asst. supt., Madras, Nov., 1932; confd. July, 1935; asst. comdt., Malabar special police, Oct., 1936.

LONGRIGG, JOHN HEMSLEY, M.A., Indian Forest Service (dep. consvr., Madras) (b. 6th Apl., 1890).—Joined the service as asst. consvr., Dec., 1913; dep. consvr., Dec., 1918; offg. consvr., Aug., 1928; princp. for. coll., Coimbatore, Jan., 1937.

LONSDALE, CHRISTOPHER EDWIN, Indian Police (asst. supt., Madras) (b. 18th Aug., 1910).—Joined the service as proby. asst. supt., Madras, 19th Nov., 1931; confd., Jan., 1934.

LORD, HUGH PROBYN MALET, Lt.-Col., I.A., retd., late Burma Commn. (b. 26th Aug., 1878).—Arrived in India, March, 1898, and served in mil. dept.; asst. commr., Burma, May, 1906; on mil. duty, Dec., 1914, to Sept., 1917; dep. commr., Jan., 1922; retd., June, 1933.

LORIMER, ALEXANDER PATRICK GORDON, Lt.-Col., I.M.S., retd. (b. 9th Dec., 1880).—Joined the service, 1st Sept., 1906; D.M.O., Anantapur, Dec., 1923; offg. D.M.O. and supt., mental hosp., Calicut, Nov., 1924; surg., 1st dist., June, 1929; retd., Aug., 1935.

LORT-WILLIAMS, SIR JOHN ROLLESTON, Kt., K.C., Barr., Judicial dept., Bengal (judge, high ct., Calcutta) (b. 14th Sept., 1881).—Educ. at Merchant Taylors' Sch. and London Univ.; called to Bar, 1904; member of L.C.C. from 1907 to 1910; M.P. for Rotherhithe, 1918 to 1923; judge, high ct., Calcutta, 28th Dec., 1927; knighted, June, 1936.

LORY, FREDERICK BURTON PENDARVES, C.I.E., M.A. (Oxon.), late Indian Educatl. Service (b. 23rd Mar., 1875).—Educ. at Marlborough Sch. and Exeter Coll., Oxford; joined the service, 27th Sept., 1901, as princpl., Elphinstone High Sch.; tutor and guardian to young Chiefs of Jamkhandi, Jath, Miraj (Junior), and Mudhol, May, 1902; headmaster, Poona High Sch., April, 1903; asst. to dir. of public instr., Oct., 1903; educatl. inspr., April, 1906; on mily. duty from July, 1916; dir. of public instrn., Bombay, July, 1923; retd., May, 1930; C.I.E., May, 1930.

LOTHIAN, ARTHUR CUNNINGHAM, C.S.I., C.I.E., M.A., B.Sc., Indian C.S., Indian Political Service (b. 27th June, 1887).—Educ. at Aberdeen Gr. Sch., Aberdeen University, and Christ Church, Oxford; apptd. after exam. of 1910; arrived in India, 25th Nov., 1911, and served in Bengal as asst. mag. and collr.; asst. mag. and asst. settlt. offr., Oct., 1915; jt. mag. and dep. collr., Oct., 1917; temply. offg. as asst. to pol. agent, Bunkelkhand, from Nov., 1915; first asst. to resdt. in Kashmir, Dec., 1920; sec. to resdt. at Hyderabad, Apl., 1924, and again, Nov., 1927; dep. sec. govt. of India, for. and pol. dept., July, 1926; secty. to resdt., Mysore, May, 1928; resdt., Jaipur, Apl., 1929; on spl. duty, for. and pol. dept., Nov., 1931; resdt., Baroda, July, 1932; pol. agent, E. Rajputana states, Mar., 1933; resdt., Jaipur, June, 1933; C.I.E., Jan., 1934; offg. resdt. and A.G.G., Rajputana, Apl., 1934; addl. secty. to the govt. of India, f. and p. dept., Apl., 1935; employed as spl. representative of the Viceroy, Oct., 1936; addtl. secty. to G. of I., f. and p. dept., Feb., 1937; C.S.I., May, 1937; resdt. for Rajputana, and C.C. in Ajmer Merwara, Oct., 1937.

LOTHIAN, MARQUIS OF (cr. 1701), Most Hon. Philip Henry Kerr, C.H.—Parliamentary Under Secretary of State for India, Nov., 1931, to Sept., 1932.

LOVATT, ARTHUR WILLIAM, M.A., Indian C.S. (asst. collr., Bombay) (b. 1st Sept., 1912).—Educ. at Dunfermline High Sch., St. Andrew's Univ., Vienna Univ., and London School of Economics; joined the service, 20th Sept., 1937; arrived in India, Oct., 1937, and served in Bombay as asst. collr.

LOVELAND, FRANK, Indian State Rlys. (dist. traffic supt.) (b. 14th May, 1896).—Joined the service as asst. traffic supt., 23rd July, 1920; dist. ditto, Burma Rlys., Aug., 1932.

LOVELL, JAMES HENRY, F.C.H., late Railway Dept.—Trained at R.I.E. Coll.; joined the service, 1st Oct., 1895, as asst. engr.; exec. engr., Oct., 1906; sec., Ganges bridge

commn., 1907; deputy agent, N.W. Rly., 1914
17; attch. for. and pol. dept. for Gondal Por-
bander advisory tribunal, July and Aug., 1917;
retd., Jan., 1921; asst. govt. dir. of Indian rly.
cos., Jan., 1921 to Dec., 1924.

LOVELUCK, EDWARD BURTON, late Indian
Police Service (Madras) (b. 6th May, 1882).—
Joined the Madras police as prob., 30th Nov.,
1902; asst. supt., July, 1904; supt., May, 1911;
retd., Oct., 1930.

LOVETT, Sir HARRINGTON VERNEY,
K.C.S.I., late Indian C.S.—Educ. at Sher-
borne, and Balliol Coll., Oxford; apptd.
after exam. of 1882; arrived, 20th Nov.,
1884, and served in the N.W. Prov. and Oudh
as asst. commr.; joint mag., Dec., 1893; dep.
commr., Feb., 1896; mag. and collr., Dec.,
1896; K.-i-H. Medal, Nov., 1901; addl.
commr., Dec., 1906, to Feb., 1907; commr.,
Nov., 1908; C.S.I., Dec., 1911; member of the
Bengal District Administration Cttee., Nov.,
1913, to May, 1914, and part author of the
Cttee.'s report; K.C.S.I., Jan. 1916; an addl.
member, govr.-gen.'s legisl. council, Feb., 1917;
member, bd. of rev., United Provs., April, 1918;
retd., Oct., 1919.

LOW, ALASTAIR PETRIE, Indian C.S. (b. 30th
Jan., 1908).—Educ. at George Watson's College,
1908).—Educ. at George Watson's College,
Edinburgh University and Balliol College,
Oxford; joined the service, 14th Oct., 1930;
arrived in India, Nov., 1930, and served in the
Punjab as asst. commr.; under-secty. to govt.
pol. and home depts., Punjab, Mar., 1934;
under-secty. to A.G.G., states of W.I., Nov.,
1935; secty. to A.G.G., Gujerat states, May,
1936.

LOW, Sir CHARLES ERNEST, K.C.I.E., B.A.,
late Indian C.S.—Educ. at Rugby, and Wadham
Coll., Oxford; apptd. after exam. of 1892;
arrived, 13th Dec., 1893, and served in the
Central Provs. as asst. commr., and offg. under
sec.; settlt. offr., Oct., 1898; K.-i-H. medal, 2nd
class, Jan., 1901; dep. commr., Oct., 1902;
regr. of co-operative credit societies, Jan., 1905,
to Dec., 1905; also dir. of agric., July to Dec.,
1905; dir. of agric. and regr., co-operative credit
societies, Aug., 1907; on special duty, Nov.,
1907; C.I.E., Jan., 1909; dir. of agric., Oct.,
1910; dir. of agric. and industries, Oct., 1913;
member, district administration commee.,
Calcutta, Nov., 1913, to May, 1914; dir. of agric.
and industries, Central Provs., May, 1914; on
spl. duty in comm. and industry dept., govt.
of India, Jan., 1915; sec. to govt. of India, comm.
and industry dept., and addl. member, govr.-
genl.'s council, Feb., 1915; member, Indian
industrial commn., Nov., 1916, to May, 1917,
and from Nov., 1917; member, Indian munitions
bd., May, 1919; K.C.I.E., June, 1919; offg.
president, Indian munitions bd., June, 1919,
chief contr. surplus stores, Oct., 1920; commr.,
Dec., 1920; retd. Aug., 1922.

LOW, PETER DUNSTAN, O.B.E., A.M.I.E.E.,
Indian State Rlys. (b. 29th Oct., 1891).—Joined
the service as dist. elec. engr., E.I. Rly., 7th Apl.,
1927.

LOW, THOMAS RALPH, M.C., B.Sc., Indian
Agric. Service (dir., inst. of plant industry,
Indore) (b. 21st Dec., 1893).—Joined the service

as dep. dir. of agric., 4th Dec., 1920; principl.
agric. coll., Cawnpore, May, 1934; offg. dir. of
agric., United Provs., Oct., 1935; dir., inst. of
plant industry, Indore, July, 1936.

LOWE, Miss IRENE H., M.Sc., Indian Educl.
Service (dep. directress of public instrn., Madras)
(b. 4th May, 1890).—Apptd. inspectress of
schools, 29th Mar., 1922; dep. directress of
public instrn., Aug., 1926.

LOWIS, Ross HENRY DONALD, M.B.E.,
Indian Police (b. 5th Feb., 1905).—Joined the
service as asst. supt., 5th Dec., 1924; asst. pol.
agent, Sth. Waziristan, Feb., 1936; M.B.E.,
Jan., 1938.

LOWNDES, Sir GEORGE RIVERS, K.C.S.I.,
K.C., Barr. — Member of the govr.-genl.'s
council, 20th Dec., 1915, to Dec., 1920; was
formerly a leader at the Bombay Bar, and in
practice before the judl. cttee. of the Privy
Council; K.C.S.I., Jan., 1918; apptd. member
of judl. cttee. of Privy Council, March, 1929.

LOWNDS, EDWIN ALBERT, India Office
(higher clerical officer, acctnt.-gen.'s dept.)
(b. 24th Apl., 1878).—Clerk, 2nd class, 26th
June, 1899; higher clerical officer, April, 1922.

LOWRY, WILLIAM EDWARD, B.A., late
Indian C.S. (Burma).—Educ. at Leys Sch.,
Cambridge, London Univ., and Clare Coll.,
Cambridge; apptd. after exam. of 1894;
arrived, 30th Dec., 1895, and served in Burma as
settlt. offr.; dep. dir., land records, Oct., 1906;
dep. commr., June, 1910; commr., Nov., 1919;
commr. of settlts. and land records, Feb., 1920;
fin. commr., Jan. to Dec., 1921, and from Dec.,
1923, to March, 1925; retd., Aug., 1928.

LOWSLEY, CONRAD OFFA, late Indian
Service of Engrs. (b. 27th Feb., 1882).—
Trained in E.I.E. Coll.; apptd. asst. engr.,
1st Oct., 1904; exec. engr., Oct., 1912; offg.
supt. engr., April, 1925; supt. engr.,
(provl. perm.), Mar., 1931; confd., July, 1931;
offg. ch. engr., April, 1931; retd., Nov., 1934.

LOWTHER, ERNEST HERBERT NEWTON,
Indian State Rlys. (supt., E.I. Rly.) (b. 19th
May, 1890).—Joined the service as asst. supt.,
10th June, 1911; dist. supt. (s.p.t.), July,
1923; supt. (s.p.t.) Feb., 1925; confd., July,
1925; offg. dep. ch. operating supt., July, 1932;
offg. dep. agent, personnel, E.I. rly., Mar., 1936.

LUBY, THOMAS, M.A., V.D., Indian C.S.
(dist. and sess. judge, Bihar) (b. 12th Nov.,
1882).—Educ. at Clifton, and Hertford Coll.,
Oxford; apptd. after exam. of 1906; arrived
28th Nov., 1907, and served in Bengal as asst.
mag. and collr.; transf. to Bihar and Orissa,
April, 1912; jt. mag. and dep. collr., Sept.,
1913; dist. and sessions judge, Aug., 1921;
offg. judge, high ct., Patna, July, 1934; and
again, July, 1935.

LUCAS, HENRY CHARLES EDWIN, Indian
Police (b. 27th Jan., 1906).—Joined the service
as asst. dist. supt., Burma, 25th Nov., 1925;
offg. dist. supt., June, 1934.

LUCAS, LIONEL JOHN, B.A., Indian C.S. (jt.
mag. and dep. coll., Orissa) (b. 5th Feb., 1905).
—Educ. at St. Xavier's Coll., Calcutta, and
St. John's Coll., Oxford; joined the service,
8th Oct., 1928; arrived, 30th Nov., 1928, and
served in Bihar and Orissa as asst. mag. and collr.,
jt. mag., Dec., 1928; offg. dist. and sess. judge,
May, 1935.

LUCE, GORDON HANNINGTON, M.A. (Cantab.), Indian Educl. Service (prof., Univ. of Rangoon) (b. 20th Jan., 1889).—Joined the service as Prof., Rangoon Coll., 1st Nov., 1912; on mil. service from 24th July, 1918, to 9th Feb., 1919; lecturer in English, May, 1922; lecturer in Burmese and Far Eastern history, Sept., 1929.

LUCK, CHARLES PYNE, Indian Police (supt., United Provs.) (b. 22nd Jan., 1896).—Joined the service as asst. supt., 22nd Nov., 1915; I.A.R.O. from Aug., 1918, to Aug., 1919; offg. supt., March, 1920; pers. asst. to inspr.-gen. of railway police and police asst. to agent to govr.-gen., Rajputana, Oct., 1921; offg. supt., Nov., 1923; confd., Nov., 1926; supt., Ajmer-Merwara, Nov., 1928, to Oct., 1932.

LUCKMAN, Rev. SYDNEY, B.A., Indian Eccles. Estabt. (United Provs.) (senr. chaplain) (b. 3rd Nov., 1888).—Apptd. chaplain on probation, 1st May, 1921; junr. chaplain, May, 1923; senr. chaplain, May, 1933.

LUFFMAN, MAURICE LLEWELYN ALBERT, late Indian Police Service (Assam) (b. 1881).—Joined the service, 22nd Jan., 1902, as asst. supt.; supt., Oct., 1908; offg. inspr.-gen., Assam, April, 1926; ret.d., July, 1931.

LUMBY, ARTHUR FREDERICK RAWSON, C.I.E., O.B.E., Lt.-Col. I.A., ret.d. (pers. asst. to secty., milty. dept., India Office) (b. 13th Aug., 1890).—First commn., 5th Sept., 1911; in milty. employ until 17th Mar., 1928, when he was apptd. offg. asst. secy. to the Govt. of India, Army Dept.; confd., June, 1928; offg. dep. ditto, July, 1929; confd., Mar., 1934; member council of state, Apl., 1935; pers. asst. to secy., milty. dept., India Office, May, 1936.

LUMBY, ESMOND WALTER RAWSON, M.A., India Office (pte. secty. to parly.-under-secy. of state for India and for Burma) (b. 27th Sept., 1909).—Educ. at Blundell's Sch., Tiverton, and Magdalen Coll., Cambridge; Gladstone Student at St. Deniol's Library, Harwarden, 1931-2; assistant principal, Customs and Excise Department, 23rd Oct., 1933, to 14th Oct., 1934; assistant principal, India Office, 15th Oct., 1934; pte. secty. to parly.-under-secy. of state for India and for Burma, Oct., 1937.

LUMLEY, Sir (LAWRENCE) ROGER, Kt., G.C.I.E., J.P., D.L., (b. 27th July, 1896). (Governor of Bombay):—Appointed Governor of Bombay, Sept., 1937.

LUNHAM, JOHN LUMSDEN, M.B., B.Ch. (R.U.I.), D.T.M. (Cantab.), F.R.C.S.I., Lt.-Col., I.M.S. (ret.d.) (b. 10th Mar., 1879).—Joined 1st Sept., 1902, and served in mil. dept. prior to 5th Jan., 1910; civil surg., June, 1913; on milty. duty from 27th Sept., 1914, to 9th Nov., 1921; ret.d., Sept., 1931.

LWIN, MAUNG SAW, B.A., Indian C.S. (asst. commr., Burma) (b. 17th Dec., 1911).—Educ. at Christ Church, Oxford; joined the service 18th Sept., 1936; arrived in India, Oct., 1936, and served in Burma as asst. commr.

LYALL, JOHN HENRY, B.A., Indian Forest Service (dep. consvr.) (b. 16th Aug., 1885).—Joined the service, 16th Dec., 1910; asst. consvr., Dec., 1910; dep. consvr., Dec., 1915; offg. consvr., June, 1929; ditto, United Provs., May, 1930; ditto, Bengal, Mar., 1931; ditto, Punjab, Oct., 1933.

LYDALL, EDWARD FRANCIS, Indian C.S. (b. 4th Aug., 1907).—Educ. at Marlborough and St. John's Coll., Cambridge; apptd. after exam. of 1931; arrived in India, Nov., 1932, and served in Assam as asst. commr.; offg. under-secy. and pers. asst. to A.G.G., Baluchistan, Nov., 1935; asst. pol. agent, Loralai, May, 1936; offcr. on spec. duty, Kalat agency, Aug., 1936.

LYDE, LIONEL GILDEA, Indian C.S. (jt. mag., United Provinces) (b. 13th Oct., 1904).—Educ. at Rugby, Wadham Coll., Oxford, and School of Oriental Studies, London; joined the service, 16th Oct., 1929; arrived, 30th Dec., 1929, and served in the United Provinces as asst. mag. and collr.; jt. mag., Mar., 1932.

LYLE, ARTHUR NEVIN (late R.E.), O.B.E., B.A., B.A.I., A.M.I.C.E. (exec. engr., United Provs.) (b. 16th June, 1893).—Joined the service, 1st Oct., 1919; asst. exec. engr., March, 1920; offg. exec. engr., Oct., 1923; confd., Oct., 1925.

LYLE, THOMAS McELDERRY, C.I.E., B.E., Indian Service of Engrs. (ch. engr. and jt. secty. to govt. (irrgn. bch), United Provs.) (b. 24th May, 1886).—Joined the service as asst. engr., 1st Oct., 1909; exec. engr., Nov., 1917; C.I.E., Dec., 1928; offg. suptg. engr., Mar., 1931; confd., Nov., 1933; ch. engr. and jt. secty. to govt., U.P. irrgn. bch. (tempy.), Aug., 1936; confd., Nov., 1937.

LYNE, HOWARD WILLIAM, M.C., late Indian C.S. (Bengal) (b. 8th Nov., 1892).—Educ. at St. Olave's Gr. Sch., and Merton Coll., Oxford; on mil. duty from Sept., 1914; joined the service, 3rd Feb., 1920; arrived, 8th Feb., 1920, and served in Bengal as asst. mag. and collr.; addl. dist. mag., Feb., 1925; offg. mag. and collr., Aug., 1925, and again, Feb., 1927; addl. dist. and sess. judge, June, 1926; ret.d., June, 1931.

LYNN, GREGORY, Indian Service of Engrs. (exec. engr., Burma) (b. 13th Sept., 1899).—Joined the service, Nov., 1921; asst. exec. engr., Nov., 1922; offg. exec. engr., June, 1928; confd., Oct., 1931.

LYSTER, ANTHONY ST. GEORGE, B.A., A.M.I.C.E., Indian Service of Engrs. (exec. engr., Punjab) (b. 13th Mar., 1888).—Joined the service as asst. engr., 9th Oct., 1911; on mil. duty from 12th March, 1915, to 10th March, 1919; exec. engr., Oct., 1919; offg. suptg. engr., Mar., 1933; on depn. to Bihar, Apl., 1936.

LYTTON, EARL OF, Rt. Hon. VICTOR ALEXANDER GEORGE ROBERT LYTTON, K.G., G.C.S.I., G.C.I.E., P.C.—Parly. under-sec. of state for India, 1st Oct., 1920; govr. of Bengal from Mar., 1922, to Mar., 1927; viceroy and actg. govr.-gen. of India, 10th Apl. to 5th Aug., 1925.

MACALPINE, ROBERT IAN, Indian Forest Service (dep. consvr., Bengal) (b. 8th May, 1902).—Joined the service as asst. consvr., 5th March, 1924; dep. consvr., Nov., 1928.

MACANN, ARTHUR ERNEST HENRY, Major I.A., Indian Political Service (b. 19th Feb., 1898).—First commn. dated 18th June, 1917; in milty. employ prior to 24th Oct., 1924; asst. magte.

and collr., Moradabad, Oct., 1924; vice-consul, Duzdap, March, 1926; sec. British Legation, Kabul, Mar., 1930; secty. to govt., N.W.F.P., transf. depts., Apl., 1934; dir. of agric. and att. depts. (in addn.), Oct., 1935; H.M.'s charge d'affairs, Kabul, July, 1937; counsellor, British legation, Kabul, Oct., 1936; and again Nov., 1937.

MACARTHUR, DONALD FREDERICK SWAN, Indian service of Engrs. (exec. engr., Central Provs.) (b. 7th Oct., 1886).—Joined the service 1st May, 1913; on mil. service from 17th Oct., 1918, to 28th Feb., 1920; asst. engr., June, 1919; asst. exec. engr., June, 1921; offg. exec. engr., June, 1921; confd., June, 1928.

MACAULAY, DONALD, Indian State Rlys. (dist. loco. supt.) (b. 4th June, 1895).—Joined the service, 11th May, 1920, as asst. loco. supt.; offg. works manager, E.B. rly., Dec., 1930; dist. loco. supt., E.B. rly. (prov.), Aug., 1934.

MCBRIEN, CHARLES HUGH, late Opium Dept., United Provs. (b. 28th Apl., 1881).—Joined the service, 25th Mar., 1903; asst. opium agent, Oct., 1912; dist. opium officer, Feb., 1923; ret. d., Apl., 1936.

MCCALL, ANTHONY GILCHRIST, Indian C.S. (dep. commr., Assam) (b. 7th Jan., 1895).—Educ. at Cheltenham College, and R.M.A., Woolwich; joined the service, 5th Aug., 1921; arrived, 2nd Nov., 1921, and served in Bengal as asst. mag. and collr.; transf. to Assam, Dec., 1923; offg. dep. commr., Nov., 1930; dep. commr., Oct., 1935.

MCCALLUM, DUGALD LESLIE, B.A., Indian C.S. (asst. commr., Burma) (b. 13th July, 1910).—Educated at Oundle, Edinburgh University, Lincoln College, Oxford, and the School of Oriental Studies, London; appointed after exam. of 1933; arrived in India, Dec., 1934, and served in Burma as asst. commr.

MCCALLUM, JAMES LESLIE, late Indian C.S. (b. 14th May, 1879).—Educ. at Carlisle Gr. Sch. and Edinburgh Univ.; apptd. after exam. of 1902; arrived, 11th Dec., 1903, and served in Burma as asst. commr. and settl. offr.; dep. commr., April, 1916; offg. commr., May, 1925; confd., May, 1929; finl. commr. (transf. subjects), May, 1931; ret. d., July, 1933.

MCCARTHY, DENIS ARTHUR MACKAURA, Indian Police (b. 11th Dec., 1904).—Joined the service as asst. supt., United Provs., 5th Dec., 1924; offg. supt., April, 1928; awarded King's police medal, Jan., 1930; comdt. and dist. supt., Port Blair, Dec., 1936.

MCCARTHY, FREDERICK WILLIAM, late Indian Service of Engrs. (Punjab).—Joined the service, 10th Nov., 1900; asst. engr., Nov., 1901; exec. engr., Oct., 1910; ret. d., July, 1928.

MCCAUL, Rev. MATTHEW WILSON, B.A., late Indian Eccles. Estabt. (b. 19th Nov., 1882).—Joined the service, 30th Sept., 1914; junr. chaplain, Sept., 1917; senr. chaplain, Sept., 1926; ret. d., Aug., 1936.

MCCLELLAND, ALEXANDER ALLÉN, A.M.I.C.E., late Indian Service of Engrs. (b. 17th Mar., 1893).—Joined the service, 1st Oct., 1919; asst. exec. engr., Feb., 1920; exec. engr., Oct., 1925; ret. d., Jan., 1934.

MCCLENAGHAN, HERBERT ERIC ST. GEORGE, Indian C.S. (dep. commr., Central Provs.) (b. 5th Aug., 1896).—Educ. at Royal Sch., Armagh, and Trinity Coll., Dublin; joined the service, 14th Nov., 1922; arrived, 8th Jan., 1923, and served in the Central Provs. as asst. commr.; offg. dep. commr., April, 1926; Mar. 1931, and again, Apl., 1933; confd., Mar., 1934.

MCCOLL, ALEXANDER MALCOLM, Indian Police (supt., river divn.) (b. 28th Feb., 1895).—Joined the service as asst. dist. supt., 26th Jan., 1920; dist. supt., Nov., 1924; supt., river divn., Nov., 1936.

MCCOLL, HUGH HERBERT, M.C., Indian Forest Service (dep. consvr., Burma) (b. 7th Sept., 1893).—Joined the service as asst. consvr., 28th Nov., 1921; offg. dep. consvr., June, 1923; confd., April, 1927.

MCCONAGHY, CHRISTOPHER BIRDWOOD, M.B., Lt.-Col. I.M.S. (ret. d.) (b. 25th Mar., 1877).—First commn., 27th June, 1901; agency surgeon, Baghdad, June, 1904; ditto, Bushire, and residency surgeon, Persian Gulf, Nov., 1909; agency surgeon, Bhopal, Sept., 1914; on mil. duty, May, 1916, to May, 1918; agency surgeon, Eastern States of Rajputana, June, 1918; agency surgeon, Bhopal, Jan., 1919; legation surgeon, Nepal, March, 1926; ret. d., May, 1930.

MCCORKELL, DAVID ROBERT ANDREW, Indian Police (b. 22nd Apl., 1916).—Joined the service as asst. supt., Burma, on probn., Dec., 1936.

MCCRACKEN, ANDREW, M.A., Indian C.S. (dep. commr., Burma) (b. 21st Oct., 1890).—Educ. at Ayr Academy and Glasgow Univ.; apptd. after exam. of 1914; arrived in India, Dec., 1915, and served in Burma as asst. commr.; on mil. service from Aug., 1916, to Sept., 1919; services placed at disposal of govt. of India, rev. and agric. dept.; asst. rice commr., Rangoon, Feb., 1920; under sec. to govt., May, 1921; offg. dep. commr., March, 1924; confd. Aug., 1930; offg. dep. secty. to govt. of India, reforms office, June, 1930.

MCCREA, KENNETH IAN, Indian Police (b. 15th Dec., 1907).—Joined the service as asst. supt., N.W.F.P., 16th Nov., 1927; awarded King's police medal, Jan., 1932.

MCCULLOCH, RUPERT LEAROYD, late Indian Police Service (b. 4th Aug., 1880).—Joined the service, 30th Nov., 1900; supt. of police, July, 1908; recd. King's police medal, Jan., 1920; dep. inspr.-gen. of police, Oct., 1920; actg. commr. of police, April, 1921; actg. inspr.-gen., Bombay, Nov., 1923; ret. d., Apl., 1932.

MACDERMOT, PATRICK JOSEPH, India Office (higher clerical officer, telegraph and mails branch) (b. 2nd Feb., 1885).—Apptd. clerical officer, 23rd Aug., 1921; higher clerical officer, Oct., 1931.

MACDONALD, ALEXANDER, M.A. (Glas.), B.Sc., Indian Educl. Service (prinpl. Bengal engrg. coll.) (b. 13th Nov., 1883).—Joined the service as prof., Dacca Coll., 27th Jan., 1911; prin., B.E. Coll., Sibpur, March, 1924; asst. dir. of public instrn., Dec., 1926; prinpl. Bengal engrg coll., Nov., 1928.

MACDONALD, ALEXANDER MACLEOD, Indian Police (b. 10th Oct., 1915).—Apptd. asst. supt., Bombay, on probn., 11th Nov., 1935.

MACDONALD, ALEXANDER RANALD HUME, Indian C.S. (asst. commr., Assam) (b. 3rd Feb., 1900).—Educ. at Eton and King's Coll., Cambridge; apptd. after exam. of 1930; arrived in India, Nov., 1931, and served in Assam as asst. commr.; offg. under secty. to govt., Assam, Mar., 1934; confd., Aug., 1934; asst. pol. agent, N. Waziristan, Dec., 1934; ditto, S. Waziristan, Nov., 1935; presdt., Manipur State Darbar, Feb., 1937.

MACDONALD, Rev. ALISDAIR, Indian Eccles. Estabt. (junn. chaplain) (b. 12th June, 1900).—Apptd. chaplain on probn., Church of Scotland, 1933; junn. chaplain, Oct., 1935.

MACDONALD, ANGUS ALEXANDER, M.A., Indian C.S. (asst. commr., Punjab) (b. 12th Nov., 1904).—Educ. at University of Edinburgh, and Oriel Coll., Oxford; joined the service, 12th Oct., 1928, and arrived, Nov., 1928, and served in the Punjab as asst. commr.; offg. dep. commr., Sept., 1932.

MACDONALD, Rev. DONALD, M.A., B.D., Indian Eccl. Estabt. (senr. chaplain) (b. 22nd Sept., 1891).—Apptd. junn. chaplain, ch. of Scotland, 27th Feb., 1927; apptd. senr. chaplain, Feb., 1937.

MC DONALD, DONALD CHARLES, Indian Forest Service (dep. consvr., Central Provs.) (b. 28th Aug., 1891).—Joined the service as proby. extra asst. consvr., 7th May, 1912; confd., Oct., 1916; in milty. employ from 29th Mar., 1918, to 20th Feb., 1919; apptd. to I.F.S., 25th June, 1929; dep. consvr., June, 1929; employed under Rewa Durbar, Feb., 1933.

MACDONALD, RODERICK WILLIAM, C.I.E., D.S.O., Lt.-Col., I.A., late Indian Police Service (b. 22nd Apl., 1881).—Served in mil. dept. from 28th July, 1900; asst. commdt., Burma mil. police, Dec., 1904; asst. supt., Feb., 1908; dept. supt., Dec., 1910; on mil. service, Dec., 1914, to June, 1919; dep. inspr.-gen., Sept., 1921; offg. inspr.-gen., Aug., 1923; confd. as inspr.-gen., Oct., 1923; awarded King's police medal, Jan., 1927; C.I.E., June, 1927; ret'd., Aug., 1932.

MC DONALD, THOMAS, B.A. (Cantab).—Indian Forest Service (dep. consvr., central provs.) (b. 16th Jan., 1908).—Joined the service as asst. consvr., central provs., 11th Oct., 1929; dep. consvr., Nov., 1934.

MACDONELL, OLIVER STEPHEN, late Indian State Rlys. (b. 2nd May, 1878).—Joined the service as asst. engr., 18th Jan., 1906; dist. engr., Sept., 1917; exec. engr., Lashio-Muse survey, Oct., 1927; ret'd., Apl., 1935.

MC DOUGALL, JAMES CURRIE, M.A., B.Sc., Indian Agricl. Service (prinpl. agric. coll., Nagpur) (b. 5th Jan., 1890).—Joined the service as asst. dir. of agric., Dec., 1920; dep. dir. of agric., Dec., 1925; offg. prinpl. agric. coll., Nagpur, Nov., 1928; confd., Jan., 1934; offg. dir. of agric. C.P., Oct., 1935.

MAC DOUGALL, RAIBEART MACINTYRE, C.I.E., M.A., Indian C.S. (dep. commr., Burma) (b. 30th April, 1892).—Educ. at Greenock Academy and Glasgow University; apptd. after exam. of 1914, and posted to Burma; on mil. service, from Nov., 1915, to Feb., 1919; under sec. to govt., Sept., 1920; regr., high ct., Rangoon (offg.), March, 1923; confd., Nov., 1923; offg. dep. commr., Nov., 1926; confd., June, 1930; secty., local

govt. dept., Mar., 1931; secty. to govt., educn. dept., Burma, Mar., 1934; and again, Apl., 1936; C.I.E., June, 1935.

MC DOWALL, ROGER GORDON, C.I.E., M.A., Indian C.S. (secty. to govt., reforms office, Burma) (b. 9th Nov., 1886).—Educ. at Dumfries Academy, Glasgow, and Christ Church Coll., Oxford; apptd. after exam. of 1910; arrived in India, 2nd Dec., 1911, and served in Burma as asst. commr.; offg. asst. sec. to govt., Sept., 1916; regr., chief court, Lower Burma, July, 1920; regr., high ct., Rangoon, Dec., 1922; offg. dep. commr., from Nov., 1923; dep. commr. (provl. sub.), July, 1927; offg. secty., fin. dept., Mar., 1929; confd., Sept., 1929; secty., reforms office, Nov., 1932, and again, Nov., 1936; C.I.E., Jan., 1935.

MC DOWELL, ARNOT EDWARD JOSEPH CONNELL, C.I.E., Lt.-Col., I.A., Indian Police (inspr.-gen., Bihar) (b. 13th Mar., 1883).—Joined the service 8th May, 1901; in mil. employ until 21st March, 1908, when apptd. asst. supt. of police; supt., Nov., 1909; on mil. service, 1914-19; offg. dep. inspr.-gen., Dec., 1923; confd., July, 1925; offg. inspr.-gen., Apl., 1933; confd., June, 1934; C.I.E., May, 1937.

MC DOWELL, KENNETH MACLEAN, B.Sc., A.M.I.C.E., Indian Service of Engrs. (exec. engr., Burma) (b. 16th Feb., 1893).—Joined the service, 1st Oct., 1919; asst. exec. engr., Jan., 1920; exec. engr., Oct., 1925.

MC DOWELL, LENNOX MCLEOD, late Indian Police Service (b. 20th Aug., 1876).—Joined the service, 14th May, 1899; asst. dist. supt., Jan., 1901; asst. supt., April, 1906; supt., Jan., 1911; ret'd., Aug., 1931.

MAC EDWARD, Rev. LACHLAN, M.A., Indian Eccles. Estabt. (junn. chaplain, ch. of Scotland) (b. 25th Apl., 1891).—Apptd. chaplain on probation, 15th Feb., 1926; junn. chaplain, Feb., 1928.

MC ELHINNY, GEOFFREY WILLIAM, B.A., Indian C.S. (collr., Bombay) (b. 16th Dec., 1900).—Educ. at Rugby and Corpus Christi, Oxford; joined the service 24th Oct., 1924; arrived, 5th Dec., 1924, and served in Bombay as asst. magt. and collr.; offg. dep. commr., May, 1928; under secty. to govt., home, etc., depts., Sept., 1929; member, legisl. council, Feb., 1932; offg. collr., Sept., 1933; temp. addl. dep. secty., home dept., govt. of India. Oct., 1934; collr., Nov., 1937.

MC ELLIGOTT, FRANCIS, Indian Police (dist. supt., Burma) (b. 17th Jan., 1895).—Joined the service as asst. dist. supt., 7th Feb., 1920; dist. supt., Oct., 1924; awarded King's police medal, Jan., 1930.

MAC EWEN, ALEXANDER ROBERT, M.C., Indian C.S. (collr., Madras) (b. 8th Aug., 1894).—Educ. at Edinburgh Academy and Lincoln Coll., Oxford; joined the service, 31st Oct., 1919; arrived 3rd Jan., 1920, and served in Madras as asst. mag. and collr.; sub. collr. and jt. mag., Jan., 1922; offg. under-sec., rev. dept., June, 1924; spl. asst. settlt. offic., Aug., 1924; spl. settlt. offic., July, 1925; secty. to bd. of rev., Oct., 1930; collr. (provl. subs.), Jan., 1936.

MAC EWEN, ROBERT BEGG, late Indian C.S. (b. 2nd July, 1898).—Educ. at Rugby and Balliol Coll., Oxford; joined, 1st Nov., 1922; arrived, 13th Dec., 1922, and served in Madras as asst. mag. and collr.; sub-collr. and jt.

mag., July, 1924; under-secty. to govt., rev. dept., Madras, Oct., 1929; offg. collr. and dist. magte., Sept., 1932; and again, July, 1933; ret'd., Apl., 1937.

MACFARLANE, DONALD, C.I.E., M.I.C.E., Indian Service of Engrs. (b. 3rd Aug., 1882).—Trained at the R.I.E. Coll.; apptd. asst. engr., Punjab, Oct., 1903; exec. engr., Oct., 1911; ditto, and under-sec. to govt., Punjab, Oct., 1916; on rly. duty, Mesopotamia, April, 1917, to Aug., 1918; services placed at disposal of Govt. of Sudan from Nov., 1923; offg. suptg. engr., Feb., 1927; confd., Mar., 1930; secty. to govt., P.W.D. (bldgs. and roads bch.), and offg. ch. engr., July, 1932; confd., Apl., 1933; C.I.E., May, 1937; ret'd.

MACFARQUHAR, ALEXANDER, Indian C.S. (dep. commr., Punjab) (b. 6th Nov., 1903).—Educ. at Royal Academy, Inverness, Univ. of Aberdeen, and Emmanuel College, Cambridge; joined the service, 27th Oct., 1926; arrived, Dec., 1926, and served in the Punjab as asst. commr.; dep. commr. (subs. provl.) Feb., 1933, confd., Sept., 1936.

MCGILLIGAN, MICHAEL, late Indian C.S. (b. 29th Sept., 1887).—Educ. at Clongowes Wood Coll., Univ. Coll., Dublin, and Trinity Coll., Dublin; apptd. after exam. of 1911; arrived, 7th Dec., 1912, and served in Madras as asst. mag. and collr.; asst. censor, Madras, Oct., 1914; sub.-collr. and jt. mag., Oct., 1917; asst. collr., Nov., 1919; sub.-collr., Nov., 1921; offg. dist. and sess. judge, Nov., 1923; confd., March, 1927; ret'd., Jan., 1933.

MCGOWAN, PERCIVAL BRYAN, late Indian State Railways (b. 17th Mar., 1880).—Joined the service, Feb., 1903; dist. traffic supt., April, 1914; ret'd., May, 1933.

MCGREGOR, ANDREW DAWSON, F.R.C.V.S., Indian Vety. Service (prin., veterinary coll. Bengal) (b. 5th Dec., 1888).—Joined the service, 30th March, 1914; I.A.R.O. from 19th Feb., 1916; offg. supt., civil vety. dept., Jan., 1919; confd. July, 1921; princpl., vety. coll., Bengal, Apl., 1922.

M A C G R E G O R, ROBERT ANDERSON, M.I.Mech.E., M.I.S.I., M.I.E.I., F.R.S.A., M.M.G.I. (metallurg. inspr.) (b. 9th Aug., 1888).—Joined the service as officer on special duty, 25th Feb., 1937; offg. metallurgical inspr., Apl., 1937; confd., June, 1937.

MACGREGOR, ROBERT FORRESTER DOUGLAS, M.C., M.B., Lt.-Col., I.M.S. (resy. surg., Hyderabad) (b. 5th Jan., 1885).—First commn. dated 29th Jan., 1910; M.O., Seistan Consulate, Dec., 1920; offg. agency surg., Bhopal, Sept., 1925; offg. resdy. surg., Indore, Sept., 1926; resdy. surg., Bushire, May, 1927; agency surg., Gilgit, Oct., 1927; resdy. surg. and C.M.O., Baluchistan, Sept., 1929; ditto, in C.I., Apl., 1931; resdy. surg., Hyderabad, Mar., 1933.

MACGREGOR, ROBERT MENZIES, late Indian Service of Engrs.—Trained at the R.I.E. Coll.; joined the service as asst. engr., Punjab, 1st Oct., 1903; exec. engr., ditto, Oct., 1911; transf. to British Baluchistan for irrig. work, May, 1916; offg. dep. sec. to govt. of India, P.W. dept., May, 1922; on deputation to Sudan from 6th Jan., 1923; ret'd., Jan., 1929.

MACGREGOR, WILLIAM DUNCAN, C.I.E., M.I.E.E., late India Posts and Telegraphs (l. 20th Oct., 1878).—Apptd. 27th Sept., 1900, and served as asst. supt., telegraphs; supt., Oct., 1911; ditto, engrg., April, 1914; asst. chief engr., March, 1920; dep. chief engr., March, 1921; offg. postmr.-gen., Central Circle, April, 1922, to Sept., 1923; dep. chief engr., telephones, Oct., 1923; offg. postmr.-gen., Central Circle, Oct., 1924; offg. postmr.-gen., Burma, Feb., 1925; dir., tele. engrg., Feb., 1925; dep. dir. genl., tels. and telephones, Nov., 1927; offg. P.M.G. & dir. tele. engrg., June, 1928; P.M.G., Burma, Sept., 1928; offg. ch. engr., posts and tels., July, 1931; P.M.G., Bengal and Assam Circle, Mar., 1932; C.I.E., Jan., 1933; ret'd., Sept., 1933.

MCGREGOR, WILLIAM MALCOLM, Indian State Rlys. (contr. of stores, E.B. rly.) (b. 22nd Jan., 1889).—Joined the service 26th Oct., 1908; dist. contr. of stores, E.B. rly., Aug., 1919; offg. dep. ditto, Oct., 1928; offg. contr., Mar., 1929; confd., Oct., 1931.

MCGUIGAN, HUGH SUTHERLAND, Indian Police (b. 28th May, 1917).—Apptd. asst. supt., Bombay, on probn., 23rd Nov., 1936.

MCGUIRE, ROBERT ELY, B.A., Indian C.S. (asst. commr., Burma) (b. 22nd Aug., 1901).—Educ. at the High School, Dublin, and Trinity College, Dublin; joined the service, 22nd Oct., 1926; arrived, Dec., 1926; and served in Burma as asst. commr.; under-secty. to govt., fin. dep., Nov., 1930; warden, oil fields, Sept., 1932; offg. dep. commr., Nov., 1932.

MCINERNY, AUGUSTINE, late Indian State Rlys. (G.I.P. Rly.) (b. 29th Aug., 1875).—Joined the service as temp. engr., 28th March, 1918; resdt. engr., April, 1920; dist. engr., Oct., 1925; ret'd., Dec., 1930.

MCINERNY, EDWARD FOLLET, B.A., Indian C.S. (asst. mag. and collr., Bengal) (b. 8th May, 1910).—Educ. at Swansea Grammar School and Jesus Coll., Oxford; apptd. after exam. of 1933; arrived in India, Mar., 1935, and served in Bengal as asst. mag. and collr.

MCINTOSH, ERIC PRIDEAUX, M.C., late Indian Police Service (United Provs.) (b. 15th July, 1890).—Joined the service as asst. supt., Dec., 1911; supt., Dec., 1923; awarded King's police medal, Jan., 1927; ret'd., Apl., 1930.

MCINTOSH, ROBERT, M.B.E., B.Sc., Indian Service of Engrs. (exec. engr., Madras) (b. 23rd May, 1894).—Joined the service as asst. exec. engr., 1st Oct., 1920; offg. exec. engr., Feb., 1926; confd., Oct., 1926; M.B.E., Feb., 1937.

MACINTYRE, JOHN MACLACHLAN, Indian Service of Engrs. (b. 2nd Dec., 1905).—Joined the service as asst. exec. engr., Punjab, 20th Oct., 1928; asst. exec. engr., Baluchistan, Aug., 1935.

MCIVER, ARCHIBALD DUNCAN CAMPBELL, late Northern India Salt Revenue Dept. (b. 1st Jan., 1877).—Joined the service as offg. asst. supt., 16th March, 1897; proby. ditto, April, 1897; confd., Dec., 1898; supt., May, 1902; asst. commr., April, 1923; genl. manager, Rajputana salt sources, March, 1925; ret'd., Jan., 1932.

MCIVER, COLIN, M.R.C.S., L.R.C.P., Lt.-Col., I.M.S., ret'd. (b. 21st Oct., 1884).—First comman. dated 27th July, 1912; in mil. employ to 10th Feb., 1924; civil surg., Jan., 1926; ret'd., Oct., 1935.

MACIVER, LEWIS JAMES, M.A., Indian C.S. (collr., Madras) (b. 9th March, 1895).—Educ. at Edinburgh Univ.; joined the service, 4th Feb. 1920; arrived 11th May, 1920, and served in Madras as asst. collr. and mag.; sub. collr. and jt. mag., Jan., 1922; under secty. to govt., Madras, local self-govt. dept., Jan., 1923, dep. dir. of commercial intelligence, Calcutta; June, 1923; offg. dir. genl., ditto, Apl., 1924; Feb., 1926, and Jan., 1928; offg. Ind. trade commr., Aug., 1925; offg. collr. and dist. magte., July, 1930; commr. of Coorg, Mar., 1931; collr., Bangalore, Apl., 1934.

MACK, EDMUND ELMAR, Indian C.S. (dist. and sess. judge, Madras) (b. 2nd May, 1896).—Educ. at Wadham Coll., Oxford; joined the service, 26th Oct., 1920; arrived, 5th Jan., 1921, and served in Madras as asst. collr. and mag.; sub. collr. and jt. mag., July, 1922; offg. dist. and sess. judge, Sept., 1929; and again, Oct., 1932; confid., Jan., 1933.

MACKARNESS, CUTHBERT GEORGE MILFORD, Indian Forest Service (dep. consvr., Assam) (b. 15th April, 1890).—Joined the service as asst. consvr., 7th Dec., 1912; in mil. employ from 23rd Nov., 1915, to 14th March, 1919; dep. consvr., June, 1919; pers. asst. to consvr., Assam, Apl., 1932.

MACKAY, CHARLES GORDON, M.A., Barr., late Indian C.S. (b. 16th April, 1877).—Educ. at Watson's Coll., Edinburgh, and Edinburgh Univ.; apptd. after exam. of 1900; arrived, 2nd Dec., 1901, and served in Madras as asst. collr. and mag.; sub. collr. and joint mag., May, 1910; regr., high ct., Oct., 1913, to March, 1919; dist. and sess. judge, May, 1922; offg. judge, high ct., Madras, July, 1928; retd., Mar., 1935.

MACKAY, HENRY FISHER, late India Office (b. 18th Oct., 1875).—Educ. at Robert Gordon's Coll., Aberdeen; clerk, 2nd class, 2nd Oct., 1895; joint editor, India Office List, from Nov., 1914, to Nov., 1920; staff clerk, March, 1919; higher exec. officer, April, 1922; suptg. exec. offr., April, 1926; retd., Oct., 1936.

MACKAY, THOMAS IAN SUTHERLAND B.Sc., A.M.I.C.E., Indian Service of Engrs. (exec. engr., Madras) (b. 25th Dec., 1888).—Joined the service as asst. engr., 1st Oct., 1912; exec. engr., Oct., 1921.

McKEEVER, RONALD FRASER, Indian Police (b. 20th Aug., 1914).—Apptd. asst. supt., Central Provs., on probn., 14th Nov., 1935.

McKEITH, ALEXANDER ADAM, Indian Police (b. 19th Mar., 1918).—Apptd. asst. supt., Punjab, on probn., Dec., 1937.

McKELVIE, GEORGE MAURICE, B.Sc., Indian Service of Engrs. (exec. engr. Central Provs.) (b. 21st Feb., 1896).—Joined the service as asst. exec. engr., 8th Dec., 1920; offg. exec. engr. and under-secty. to govt., July 1926; exec. engr., Oct., 1928; under-secty., p.w.d., Apl., 1937; on spec. duty with govt. of India, Nov., 1937.

McKELVIE, Rev. ROBERT FRITZ STANLEY, M.A., D.D., late Indian Eccles. Estabt. (b. 14th Jan., 1882).—Apptd. chaplain on probation, 2nd Nov., 1915; junr. chaplain, Nov., 1916; senr. chaplain, Nov., 1926; retd., Jan., 1935.

McKENNA, CORNELIUS HUGH, Indian Service of Engrs. (exec. engr., Punjab) (b. 17th Feb., 1896).—Joined the service, 1st Oct., 1920; asst. exec. engr., Dec., 1920; exec. engr., Oct., 1928.

McKENNA, Sir JAMES, Kt., C.I.E., M.A., late Indian C.S. (Burma) (b. 15th Aug., 1872).—Educ. at Dumfries Acad., Edinburgh Univ., and Balliol Coll., Oxford; apptd. after exam. of 1894; arrived, 23rd Dec., 1895, and served in Burma as asst. commr., and settlt. officer; dir. of land records and agric., May, 1904; dep. dir., Jan., 1905; dir. of agric., Oct., 1906; ditto, and jt. regr., co-op. credit soc., Nov., 1909; offg. agric. adviser to govt. of India, and dir., agric. research inst., Pusa, March, 1913, to March, 1914; on special duty in dept. of rev. and agric., govt. of India, Jan., 1915; agricl. adviser to govt. of India, and dir., agricl. research inst., Pusa, April, 1916; C.I.E., June, 1917; commr., Burma, May, 1920; member of Burma legisl. council, June, 1920; temp. member of govr.'s exec. council, Burma, April, 1925; Kt., June, 1925; services placed at disposal of govt. of India, dept. of educn., etc., from 24th Sept., 1926; member of Royal Commn. on Agric. in India, 1926-8; retd., May, 1930.

McKENZIE, Rev. DONALD FRANCIS, M.A., Indian Eccles. Estabt. (presdy. senr. chaplain, Church of Scotland, Bengal) (b. 31st Mar., 1885).—Apptd. chaplain on probation, 30th Sept., 1919; junr. chaplain, Church of Scotland, Sept., 1921; senr. chaplain, Sept., 1927; presdy. senr. chaplain, ch. of Scotland, Bengal, Aug., 1937.

McKENZIE, Rev. DONALD STEWART, M.A., Indian Eccles. Estabt. (senior chaplain) (b. 2nd Mar., 1891).—Apptd. chaplain on probation, 24th April, 1919; junr. chaplain, April, 1921; senr. chaplain, Sept., 1930.

McKENZIE, Sir DUNCAN GEORGE, K.C.I.E., M.A., Indian C.S., Indian Political Service (b. 1st July, 1883).—Educ. at Winchester, and Brasenose Coll., Oxford; apptd. after exam. of 1906; arrived, 29th Nov., 1907, and served in Bombay as asst. collr. and mag.; asst. to agent to govr.-gen. in Central India, June, 1912; asst. commr., Ajmer, March, 1913; city mag., Peshawar, Nov., 1914; asst. commr. N.W.F. Prov., Dec., 1915; asst. pte. sec. to Viceroy, Nov., 1918; first asst. to resdt., Hyderabad, Nov., 1919; sec. to the agent to the govr.-gen., Punjab States, Nov., 1921; pol. agent, Southern Rajputana States, Nov., 1926; on foreign service in Bharatpur State, Feb., 1928; C.I.E., June, 1930; pol. agent in Bhopal, Nov., 1930; resdt., Jaipur, Apl., 1931; ditto and in Western States of Rajputana, Jan., 1932; pol. agent, E. Rajputana states, Mar., 1933; resdt., Hyderabad, July, 1933; K.C.I.E., Feb., 1937.

McKENZIE, HARRY MALCOLM, C.I.E., M.B., Col., I.M.S. (ret'd.) (b. 14th May, 1875).—Joined the service, 27th Jan., 1900; in mil. emp. to July, 1906; health offr., Simla, April, 1907; prof., medl. coll. hosp., Lahore, Nov., 1908; health offr. and dist. plague medl. offr., March, 1909; medl. coll. hosp., Calcutta, June, 1910; ditto, Lahore, Sept., 1911; medl. offr., Bahawalpur States, Jan., 1912; dep. sanitary commr., Punjab, Sept., 1914; serving in the mil. dept., from 1915; prof. of pathology and hygiene, King Edward Medl. Coll., Lahore, and dep. sany. commr., Punjab (in addn.), Aug., 1918; bacteriologist to govt. of Punjab, Oct., 1922; offg. inspr. genl. of civil hosp., Sept., 1928; C.I.E., June, 1932; retd., May, 1932.

MACKENZIE, JAMES MACAULAY, Indian Police (supt., Bengal) (b. 20th June, 1887).—Joined the service, 28th Dec., 1907; asst. supt., April, 1910; supt., Nov., 1915; offg. dep. inspr.-genl., Mar., 1929.

MACKENZIE, JOHN MITCHELL DOUGLAS, B.A., late Indian Forest Service (Burma) (b. 8th June, 1889).—Joined the service as asst. consvr., 15th Jan. 1912; dep. consvr., Jan., 1917; ret'd., Dec., 1930.

MACKENZIE, MALCOLM AYERS, M.B.E., late Indian Police Service (Bihar and Orissa) (b. 27th Sept., 1890).—Joined the service as asst. supt., 30th Nov., 1910; supt., May, 1919; M.B.E., June, 1919; awarded King's police medal, Jan., 1931; ret'd., Nov., 1932.

MACKENZIE, STEPHEN NAIRNE, late Indian C.S. (b. 17th May, 1880).—Educ. at Winchester Coll.; apptd. after exam. of 1902; arrived, 11th Dec., 1903; and served in Bengal as asst. mag. and collr.; transfed. to E. Bengal and Assam, Oct., 1905; settl. offr., Dec., 1910; jt. mag. and dep. collr., Nov., 1910; ditto and settl. offr., Dec., 1910; asst. commr. and settl. offr., Assam, April, 1912; offg. pol. offr., N.E. Frontier, Jan. to Oct., 1914; dep. commr., March, 1916; on mil. duty (I.A.R.O.), Oct., 1918, to Jan., 1919; commr. of excise, inspr.-gen. of registrn., &c., Jan., 1919; supt., Lushai Hills, April, 1921; offg. commr., March, 1925; ret'd., Mar., 1934.

MACKEOWN, JOHN AINSLIE, Indian C.S. (dep. secty., Govt. of India, dept. of indus. and lab.) (b. 27th Oct., 1902).—Educ. at Radley and Worcester Coll., Oxford; joined the service, 29th Oct., 1925; arrived, 6th Dec., 1925, and served in the Punjab as asst. commr.; deputed to Bahawalpur State, Nov., 1929; dep. commr. (subs. provl.), Oct., 1932; dep. secty., Govt. of India, dept. of indus. and lab., Feb., 1936; offg. jt. secty., ditto, July, 1936, and again, Oct., 1936; addl. dep. secty., legisl. dept., govt. of India, Mar., 1937.

McKERNAN, ROBERT LUCIAN, M.A., late Indian C.S. (b. 7th Jan., 1891).—Educ. at St. Colomb's Coll., Derry, and University Coll., Dublin; apptd. after exam. of 1913; arrived, 12th Dec., 1914, and served in Madras as asst. collr. and mag.; on mil. duty as asst. recruiting offr., from Feb., 1918, to Jan., 1919; sub-collr. and jt. mag., Jan., 1919; dep. acctnt.-gen., Calcutta, Nov., 1921; ditto, Bombay, Sept., 1922; offg. acctnt.-gen., Bombay, Sept., 1923; dep. acctnt.-gen., Calcutta, March, 1925; offg. acctnt.-gen., June, 1925; dep. acctnt.-gen., Bombay, Nov., 1925; offg. acctnt.-gen. and dep. controller of currency, Madras, April, 1926; dep. acctnt.-gen., Burma, Nov., 1926; ditto, Bombay, Nov., 1929; offg. acct.-genl., Bombay, March, 1928; on spl. duty, Nov., 1931; offg. collr. and dist. mag., Oct., 1932; ret'd., Nov., 1933.

McKERRAL, ANDREW, C.I.E., M.A., B.Sc., late Indian Agr. Service (b. 3rd Sept., 1876).—Arrived in India, May, 1907, and served as dep. dir. of agric.; asst. inspr.-gen. of agric. in India, June, 1910; dep. dir. of agric., Burma, June, 1911; offg. dir. of agric., Burma, May, 1922; confd., Nov., 1923; C.I.E., June, 1931; ret'd., March, 1936.

MACKERTICH, MERVYN, late Opium Dept., United Provs. (b. 15th Aug., 1877).—Joined the service, 1st April, 1899; dist. opium officer, April, 1926; ret'd., Aug., 1932.

MACKIE, ALFRED WILLIAM WHITE, C.I.E., M.A., late Indian C.S. (b. 20th Oct., 1877).—Educ. at Dundee High Sch., St. Andrew's Univ., and Corpus Christi Coll., Cambridge; apptd. after exam. of 1900; arrived, 29th Nov., 1901, and served in Bombay as asst. collr. and mag.; supt. of land records and registrn., Nov., 1905, to Jan., 1910; collr., Nov., 1920; addl. dep. sec. to govt., in. dept., Feb., 1921; collr. and pol. agent, Bhor, Jan., 1923; on spl. duty in rev. dept., Feb., 1925; offg. settl. commr., etc., April, 1925; offg. sec. to govt., dev. dept., dep. dir. of dev., and commr., Bom. suburban div., and land manager, dev. directorate, April, 1926; pol. agent, Surat, Nov., 1926; ditto, Rewa Kantha, March, 1928; member of legisl. council, 1929, 1931 and 1932; C.I.E., Jan., 1931; commr., Apl., 1931; ret'd., June, 1936.

MACKIE, FREDERICK PERCIVAL, C.S.I., O.B.E., M.D., Ch.B., M.Sc. (Bristol), F.R.C.P. (Lond.), F.R.C.S. (Eng.), Bt.-Col., I.M.S. (ret'd.) (b. 19th Feb., 1875).—Joined 1st Sept., 1902, and served in mil. dept. to 24th March, 1923; O.B.E., June, 1918; offg. dir. bacteriological laby. and consulting bacteriologist, G.M. Coll., Bombay, March, 1923; confd., Sept., 1924; dir., Haffkine Inst., Bombay, Nov., 1926; offg. public health comm. with govt. of India, March, 1928, to Jan., 1929; offg. dir., Pasteur Inst., Shillong, May, 1931; ret'd., Feb., 1932; C.S.I., June, 1932.

MACKIE, JAMES, A.M.I.C.E., Indian Service of Engrs. (exec. engr., Bengal) (b. 1st Jan., 1904).—Joined the service as asst. exec. engr., Bengal, 10th Feb., 1928; exec. engr., Feb., 1937.

MACKIE, RONALD HASTINGS, late Indian State Ry's. (b. 3rd Dec., 1881).—Joined the service as asst. loco. supt., 25th Apl., 1907; dist. loco. supt., Nov., 1921; offg. dep. carr. & wagon supt., Nov., 1928; confd., Feb., 1930; offg. loco. and carr. supt., Nov., 1932; and again, Mar., 1934; confd., Dec., 1934; ret'd., Nov., 1936.

McKIE, PERCY WILLIAM, late India Posts and Tels. (b. 4th May 1881).—Held non-gazetted appt. from 1st July, 1896; proby. supt., Dec., 1919; confd., May, 1920; dep. P.M.G., traffic, June, 1930; ch. supt., Bombay tel. office, July, 1930; ret'd., Aug., 1932.

MACKINNON, JOHN, late Indian State Ry's. (ch. engr., N.W. Rly.) (b. 30th Jan., 1886).—Joined the service as asst. engr., 1st. Oct., 1909; on mil. duty from 4th May, 1916, to 17th May, 1919; exec. engr., October, 1917; offg. dep. agent, N.W. Rly., May, 1926; offg. dep. ch. engr., Feb., 1929, and again, Oct., 1933; offg. dep. agent, Mar., 1931, and again, Oct., 1933; confd., Feb., 1934; offg. ch. engr., N.W. rly., May, 1935; dep. ch. engr. (provl.), June, 1935; confd., Oct., 1935; offg. senr. govt. inspr. of ry's., May, 1936; chief engr. (provl.), Nov., 1937.

MACKINTOSH, CHARLES CALDER, B.Sc., A.M.I.C.E., Indian Service of Engrs. (suptg. engr., Burma) (b. 28th May, 1886).—Joined the service, 1st Oct., 1908; exec. engr., Oct., 1916; offg. suptg. engr., Jan., 1927; confd., Oct., 1927; offg. ch. engr., Mar., 1936; addl. ch. engr., P.W.D. (irrign. beh.), Burma, Feb., 1937.

MACKINTOSH, Rev. KENNETH, M.A., Indian Eccl. Estabt. (b. 10th Jan., 1903) (junr. chaplain, ch. of Scotland).—Apptd. chaplain on probation, ch. of Scotland, 27th April, 1928; junr. chaplain, Apl., 1930.

McKINTY, FRANCIS PATRICK, late Indian Police (b. 1st Aug., 1889).—Joined the service as asst. supt., 30th Nov., 1910; supt., Dec., 1923; ret'd., Feb., 1936.

MACKLIN, ALBERT SORTAIN ROMER, B.A., Indian C.S. (b. 4th March, 1890).—Educ. at Westminster and Christ Church, Oxford; apptd. after exam. of 1912; arrived, 1st Dec., 1913, and served in Bombay as asst. collr. and mag.; judge and asst. sess. judge, April, 1922; asst. judge and addl. sess. judge, June, 1923; offg. judge and sess. judge, Nov., 1924; regr. high ct., appellate side, Nov., 1926; judge and sess. judge, Jan., 1929; judl. asst. and addl. sess. judge, Aden, June, 1929; offg. secy. to govt., legal dept., June, 1931; judl. commr. in the states of Western India, July, 1932; offg. judge, high ct., Bombay, June, 1934; confd., June, 1935.

MACKNEY, HERBERT HODDY, B.A., Indian C.S. (judge, high ct., Rangoon) (b. 31st May, 1886).—Educ. at Liverpool Institute High Sch., and Christ Church Coll., Oxford; apptd. after exam. of 1909; arrived in India, 6th Dec., 1910, and served in Burma as asst. commr.; on mil. service from June to July, 1918; dist. and sessions judge, Dec., 1922; acting judge, high ct., Rangoon, Nov., 1932; confd., Nov., 1933.

MACLACHLAN, DUNCAN, Indian C.S. (collr., Bombay) (b. 9th Nov., 1888).—Educ. at Mountjoy Sch., Dublin, and Univ. Coll., Galway; apptd. after exam. of 1912; arrived, 21st Nov., 1913, and served in Bombay as asst. collr. and magte.; on mil. duty from Aug., 1916, to Nov., 1919; asst. commr. in Sind, and Sindhi translator to govt., April, 1920; addl. dep. sec. to govt., fin. dept. (tempy.), May, 1924; offg. dep. commr., Nov., 1924; offg. collr. & dist. magte., Sept., 1928; confd., Jan., 1931; pol. agent, Bhor, April, 1932; offg. commr., Apl., 1935.

MACLACHLAN, ROBERT BOYD, C.I.E., B.A., (B.E., A.M.I.C.E.), late Indian Service of Engrs. (b. 5th Sept., 1880).—Joined 5th Sept., 1905; asst. engr., Aug., 1911; exec. engr., Jan., 1915; suptg. engr. (sub. tempy.), variously from Oct., 1923; offg. suptg. engr., Dec., 1931; C.I.E., Jan., 1935; ret'd., Sept., 1935.

MACLAGAN, Sir EDWARD DOUGLAS, K.C.S.I., K.C.I.E., M.A., late Indian C.S.—Educ. at Winchester, and New Coll., Oxford; apptd. after exam. of 1883; arrived, 18th Dec., 1886, and served in the Punjab as asst. commr. and offg. under sec.; supt. of census operations in the Punjab, April, 1890; offg. under sec. to govt. of India, rev. and agric. dept., Aug., 1890; confd., April, 1893; retransfd. to the Punjab, as asst. commr., March, 1895; dep. commr., Oct., 1896; settlement commr., Oct., 1896; offg. sec. to govt. of India, fin. and rev., April to Oct., 1902; offg. judl. and gen. sec., Punjab, Sept. to Oct., 1903; offg. chief sec., April to July, 1904, and again from Jan., 1906; confd., Dec., 1906; offg. sec. to govt. of India, rev. and agric. dept., Dec., 1906, to April, 1907; C.S.I., Jan., 1909; sec. to govt. of India, dept. of rev. and agric.,

July, 1910; K.C.I.E., Jan., 1913; temp. member of govr.-gen.'s council, April to Oct., 1913; chief sec. to govt., Punjab, June, 1914; president, cttee. on co-operation in India, Oct., 1914, to June, 1915; sec. to govt. of India, educn. dept., Dec., 1915; also addl. member, govr.-gen.'s legisl. council; lieut.-govr. of the Punjab, 26th May, 1919; K.C.S.I., Jan., 1921; govr. of Punjab, 3rd Jan., 1921, to May, 1924; ret'd., Dec., 1924.

MACLEAN, CHARLES ALLAN, M.B.E., M.C., M.A., B.Sc., Indian Agric. Service (dep. dir. of agric., Bihar) (b. 22nd May, 1892).—Joined the service as dep. dir. of agric., 5th Dec., 1920; offg. dir., Bihar and Orissa, Apl., 1935.

MACLEAN, EVAN VICTOR, Indian State Rlys. (transpn. supt.) (b. 6th Apl., 1887).—Joined the service, 6th Nov., 1919; asst. supt. July, 1925; offg. dep. dir., traffic and statistics, Dec., 1930; transpn. supt. (traffic comml.), E.I.Rly., Dec., 1934.

MCLEAN, ANGUS, B.Sc., Indian Agricl. Service (dep. dir., Burma) (b. 27th Apl., 1891).—Joined the service as dep. dir., 16th Dec., 1921.

MCLEAN, ARTHUR ERROL JOHN, M.A., Indian Educ. Service (inspr. of schools) (b. 28th Oct., 1890).—Joined the service as head master, govt. high school, Maymyo, 30th Jan., 1922; offg. asst. dir. of public instrn., Aug., 1933.

MCLEAN, Rev. LAUCHLAN, M.A., B.D., Indian Eccles. Estabt. (presdy. senr. chaplain, ch. of Scotland) (b. 18th Feb., 1886).—Served in the milty. dept. from 28th Feb., 1921, to 6th Sept., 1925; junr. chaplain, ch. of Scotland, Sept., 1925; served under govt. of India, army dept., from 15th Mar., 1929, to 5th Dec., 1929; and again from 1st Dec., 1930; senr. chaplain, Jan., 1931; offg. presy. senr. chaplain, Bengal, May, 1932; ditto, Madras, Oct., 1933; ditto, Bombay, confd., Mar., 1935.

MCLEAN, Sir ROBERT, Kt., B.Sc., late Indian State Rlys. (G.I.P. Rly.) (b. 3rd Feb., 1884).—Joined the service as asst. engr., 1st Oct., 1905; exec. engr., Oct., 1912; offg. sec., rly. board, May, 1919; dep. agent, March, 1920; agent, April, 1922; Kt., 1926; ret'd., July, 1929.

MCLELLAN, Rev. DUNCAN TAIT HUTCHISON, M.A., B.D., Indian Eccles. Estabt. (senr. chaplain, Bengal) (b. 29th Nov., 1893).—Apptd. chaplain on probation, church of Scotland, 11th Feb., 1922; junr. chaplain, with govt. of India, Army dept., from 8th Nov., 1927; senr. chaplain, Sept., 1933.

MACLEOD, ALLAN, C.I.E., B.A., late Indian C.S. (b. 19th Mar., 1887).—Educ. at Edinburgh Academy, and Merton Coll., Oxford; apptd. after exam. of 1910; arrived in India, 24th Nov., 1911, and served in the Central Provs. as asst. commr.; under-sec. to chief commr., Nov., 1916; under sec. to govt. of India, home dept., Dec., 1921; dep. commr. (s.p.t.), July, 1922; dep. sec. to govt. of India, fin. dept., Sept., 1922; member of frontier civil forces commee., March, 1926; offg. finl. adviser, milty. finance, July, 1926; addl. ditto (tempy.), Aug., 1928; finl. adviser, ditto, Aug., 1929; C.I.E., Jan., 1933; ret'd., July, 1937.

MACLEOD, Sir NORMAN CRANSTOUN, Kt., B.A., Barr., late Bombay Judicial Dept.—Official assignee, insolvent debtor's court, Bombay; apptd. addl. member of govr.-gen.'s legisl. council, 21st Oct., 1908; judge, Bombay High Ct., June, 1910; chief justice, ditto, June, 1919; knighted, June, 1919; retd., June, 1926.

MACLEOD, RODERICK DONALD WILLIAM DUNNING, B.A., late Indian C.S. (b. 24th May, 1886).—Educ. at Christ's Hospital Sch., and Jesus Coll., Oxford; apptd. after exam. of 1909; arrived in India, 26th Nov., 1910, and served in the United Prov. as asst. mag.; jt. mag., s.p.t., Sept., 1916; asst. commr., Dec., 1918, to April, 1919; confd., as jt. mag., Jan., 1923; offg. mag. and collr. from Jan., 1923; confd., July, 1926; dep. secty. to govt., U.P., general branch, Oct., 1928; retd., Nov., 1935.

MCLEOD WILLIAM NORMAN, B.Sc., Indian Service of Engrs. (suptg. engr., Punjab) (b. 15th June, 1885).—Joined the service as asst. engr., Oct., 1908; exec. engr., Oct., 1916; offg. suptg. engr., Jan., 1926; suptg. engr. (provl. perm.), July, 1935; confd. as suptg. engr., Oct., 1936.

MCINTIC, JOHN FRANCIS, Indian Police (b. 29th Aug., 1916).—Apptd. asst. supt., Punjab, on probn., Nov., 1936.

MCMAHON, Sir ARTHUR HENRY, G.C.M.G., G.C.V.O., K.C.I.E., C.S.I., Col. Indian Army, retd., late Foreign Dept., Govt. of India.—First commn., 10th March, 1883; in mil. emp. to June, 1887, when he was apptd. asst. commr., Punjab; employed in Zohb from Oct., 1890; dep. commr., Thal, and pol. agent, Loralai, April, 1892; pol. agent in Zohb, April, 1893; accompanied mission to Kabul, Aug., 1893; C.I.E., Jan., 1894; acting rev. commr., Baluchistan, Feb., 1894; British joint commr., Afghan-Baluch boundary commn., April, 1894, to June, 1895, and from January to June, 1896; C.S.I., June, 1897; pol. agent, Gilgit, Aug., 1897; asst. sec. to govt. of India, foreign dept., Nov., 1898; pol. agent, Dir. Swat and Chitral, Mar., 1899; rev. and judl. commr., Baluchistan, April, 1901; on depn. with the Seistan Mission from Nov., 1902; agent to govr.-genl. in Baluchistan, Jan., 1905; K.C.I.E., Jan., 1906; sec. to govt. of India, for. dept., Feb., 1911; addl. member of govr.-gen.'s council, Feb., 1911; G.C.V.O., Dec., 1911; on special duty as British Plenipotentiary, Tibet Conference, Sept., 1913, to July, 1914; apptd. H.M.'s High Commr., Egypt, Dec., 1914; retd. from Indian Army, March, 1915; G.C.M.G., 1916; author of works on the tribes and fauna of Gilgit, Chitral, &c., and on volcanic and other rocks on the Baluchistan Afghan frontier.

MACMICHAEL, NEIL, C.S.I., M.A., late Indian C.S. (Madras) (b. 23rd July, 1871).—Educ. at the Edinburgh High Sch. and Univ., and Balliol Coll., Oxford; apptd. after exam. of 1893; arrived, 2nd Dec., 1894, and served in Madras as asst. collr. and mag.; dep. commr., rev. settlt., July, 1900; spl. settlt. officer, May, 1901; head asst., June, 1904; sub. collr. and jt. mag., Dec., 1908; collr. and mag., March, 1912; offg. chief commr. of income tax, May, 1920; offg. member, board of rev., March, 1921; second member, board of rev., June, 1924;

offg. first member, board of rev., Oct., 1925; commr. of labour, Jan., 1926; C.S.I., July, 1926; temp. member, govr.'s exec. council, Madras, Sept., 1927; retd., Aug., 1929.

MACMILLAN, ARCHIBALD MORVEN, C.I.E., M.A., B.Sc., late Indian C.S. (b. 25th April, 1880).—Educ. at the High Sch. and Univ., Glasgow; apptd. after exam. of 1902; arrived, 21st Nov., 1903, and served in Bombay as asst. collr., mag., chief inspr. of factories, Mar., 1907, to Jan. 1908; manager, Sind encumbered estates, May, 1914, to April, 1917; collr., Feb., 1923; C.I.E., June, 1925; offg. commr. of excise, Bombay presy. and inspr.-genl. of registrn., Mar., 1932; retd., Feb., 1935.

MACMILLAN, JOHN McCALLUM ANDERSON, M.A., M.D., F.R.C.S., Lt.-Col., I.M.S. (rettd.) (b. 14th Dec., 1876).—First commn. 31st Aug., 1900; in mil. employ to Jan., 1907, when he was apptd. civil surgeon, Central Provs.; on mil. service, Oct., 1914, to March, 1921; civil surgeon, Simla East, March, 1925; retd. Sept., 1930.

MACMILLAN, NEIL BOYD (commercial officer, Electricity Brch., Punjab) (b. 14th Jan., 1880).—Joined the service, 23rd Apr., 1928, as sales manager; asst. comml. manager, Sept., 1931; comml. manager, Apr., 1933; comml. officer, Jan., 1934.

MCMILLAN, WILLIAM SINGER, late India Posts and Telegraphs (b. 2nd Oct., 1883).—Trained at R.I.E. Coll.; apptd. 1st Oct., 1906, as asst. supt.; divl. engr., tels., Sept., 1921; retd., Aug., 1928.

MACNABB, ARCHIBALD CORRIE, M.A. (OXON), Indian C.S. (dep. commr., Punjab) (b. 1st Dec., 1886).—Educ. at Eton and Balliol Coll., Oxford; apptd. after exam. of 1910; arrived in India, 25th Nov., 1911, and served in the Punjab as asst. commr.; city mag., Delhi, April, 1914; sec., municipal cttee, Delhi, Sept., 1915; again city mag., Delhi, Oct., 1916; spl. duty on staff of lieut.-govr., Punjab, from Oct., 1917, to March, 1918; offg. dep. commr., Dec., 1923; dep. commr. (provl.), Nov., 1924; senr. secty. to finl. commrs., Punjab, Nov., 1928; offg. dep. secty. to govt., Punjab, dev. dept., Apr., 1933; offg. commr., May, 1934; admn. Lahore municipality and chairman, Lahore impr. trust, Apr., 1937.

MCNAIR, ARTHUR WYNDHAM, C.S.I., O.B.E., late Indian C.S. (b. 23rd Aug., 1872).—Educ. at St. Paul's Sch., and Balliol Coll., Oxford; apptd. after exam. of 1891; arrived, 8th Nov., 1893, and served in the N.W. Prov. and Oudh as asst. mag. and collr.; dep. commr., Sept., 1898; joint mag., May, 1902; asst. commr., May, 1904; dep. commr., Nov., 1908; mag. and collr., April, 1911; O.B.E., Jan., 1919; commr., Jan., 1921; C.S.I., June, 1924; offg. member board of rev., April, 1925, Dec., 1925, and Dec., 1926; confd., Jan., 1927; retd., Nov., 1928.

MCNAIR, GEORGE DOUGLAS, Barr., Judl. Dept., Bengal (judge, high ct., Calcutta).—Apptd. acting judge, high ct., Calcutta, 28th April, 1933; confd., Apr. 1934.

MACNAIR, Sir ROBERT HILL, Kt., Barr., late Indian C.S. (b. 3rd Dec., 1877).—Educ. at George Watson's Coll., Edinburgh, and Edinburgh Univ.; apptd. after exam. of 1899; arrived, 4th Dec., 1900, and served in the Cent. Provs. as asst. commr. and dist.

and sess. judge; served in fin. dept., govt. of India, from May, 1908; asst. acctt.-gen., Bengal, Dec., 1908; ditto, Bombay, Oct., 1909; ditto, Bengal, Nov., 1909; dep. acctt.-genl., United Prov., May, 1910; asst. commr., Central Provs., Nov., 1910; ditto, and addl. dist. and sess. judge, Nov., 1912; dep. commr., and divl. and sess. judge, Jan., 1915; dist. and sess. judge, May, 1917; addl. judl. commr., Jan., 1927; offg. judl. commr., Feb., 1929; confd., Jan., 1932; Kt., Jan., 1932; ret'd., Jan., 1935.

MCNAMARA, FRANCIS STEUART, late Indian Police Service (Bihar and Orissa) (b. 21st April, 1876).—Joined the service, 19th July, 1901; supt. of police, Nov., 1910; offg. dep. inspr.-gen., March, 1923; confd., July, 1923; awarded King's police medal, Jan., 1931; ret'd., Apl., 1931.

MACNEE, EUSTACE ALBERIC, M.A. (Cantab.), late Indian Educl. Service (b. 11th Nov., 1885).—Joined the service as prof. of English Morris Coll., Nagpur, 25th Oct., 1908; inspr. of schools, Central Provs., Feb., 1912; principal, training coll., Jubbulpore, Mar., 1922; offg. dir. public instrn., Central Provs., Mar., 1928; and again, Apl., 1932; also offg. secty., educn. dept.; confd., July, 1934; ret'd., Apl., 1937.

MCNEIL, JOHN, B.Sc., Indian C.S. (judge) (b. 2nd Dec., 1906).—Educ. at Rangoon Univ. and London School of Oriental Studies; joined the service, 10th Oct., 1930; arrived in India, Nov., 1930, and served in Burma as asst. commr.; dep. regr., high ct., genl. dept., Rangoon, Apl., 1936, to Apl., 1937. Judge, Oct. 1937.

MCNEILL, KENNETH JOHN, V.D., Indian State Rlys. (divl. transpn. supt., G.I.P. Rly.) (b. 13th July, 1890).—Joined the service as traffic probationer, 17th Feb., 1911; asst. traffic supt., Feb., 1914; dist. traffic supt., June, 1918; divl. traffic manager, Nov., 1922; offg. supt., watch and ward, June, 1930; offg. dep. traffic manager, variously from May, 1933; divl. transpn. supt., Dec., 1934.

MACONACHIE, GORDON WILLIAM, Lt.-Col., I.M.S., ret'd., late Jail Dept., Madras (b. 13th Nov., 1881).—First commn., 31st Aug., 1903; in mil. employ. to Jan., 1912, when he was apptd. jail supt.; supt., central jail, Sept., 1914; on mil. service, Oct., 1914, to Dec., 1919; offg. inspr.-genl. of prisons, Madras, May, 1921; supt. of prisons, July, 1925; offg. inspr.-genl., Bihar and Orissa, Dec., 1927; offg. inspr.-genl., Madras, Mar., 1930; confd., Apl., 1931; ret'd., July, 1936.

MACONACHIE, Sir RICHARD ROY, K.B.E., C.I.E., B.A., late Indian C.S. (b. 3rd Sept., 1885).—Educ. at Tonbridge, and Univ. Coll., Oxford; apptd. after exam. of 1908; arrived, 29th Nov., 1909, and served in the Punjab as asst. commr.; asst. commr., Peshawar, April, 1914; personal assistant to chief commr., N.-W. Frontier Prov., May, 1914; asst. commr., Bannu, Feb., 1915; ditto, Dera Ismail Khan, Oct., 1916; under-sec. to govt. of India, foreign and pol. dept., March, 1917; on mily. service from Oct., 1917, to Oct., 1919; first asst. to agent to govr.-gen. in Rajputana, Nov., 1919; offg. dep. sec. to govt. of India, for. and pol. dept., Nov., 1921; Counsellor,

H.M.'s Legation at Kabul, Feb., 1922; offg. dep. sec. to govt. of India, for. and pol. dept., Dec., 1925; C.I.E., Jan., 1926; dep. commr., Hazara, April, 1926; on spl. duty in for. and pol. dept., 1927; pol. agent, Kurram, Jan., 1928; H.M.'s Minister at Kabul, Mar., 1930, to Mar., 1935; K.B.E., June, 1931; ret'd., July, 1937.

MACPHERSON, COLIN JESSE, B.A., Indian C.S. (asst. commr., Punjab) (b. 1st Sept., 1914).—Educ. at Stowe and Corpus Christi Coll., Cambridge; joined the service, 29th Sept., 1937; arrived in India, Nov., 1937, and served in Punjab as asst. commr.

MACPHERSON, DONALD, M.A., Indian C.S. (commr. of excise and salt, Bengal) (b. 22nd Mar., 1894).—Educ. at Edinburgh Rl. High Sch. and Edinburgh Univ.; apptd. after exam. of 1916; arrived, 19th Dec., 1917, and served in Bengal as asst. mag. and collr.; I.A.R.O., May, 1918, to Jan., 1919; addl. dist. mag., Nov., 1923; and settlt. officer, July, 1924; offg. mag. and collr., April, 1926; confd., Mar., 1929; offg. secty., bd. of rev., March, 1929; confd., Sept., 1930; offg. commr. of excise and salt, Bengal, June, 1935; confd., June, 1937.

MACPHERSON, Rev. GEORGE COOK, O.B.E., M.A., B.D., late Indian Eccles. Estab. (b. 11th Mar., 1880).—Junr. chaplain, ch. of Scotland, Sept., 1910; senr. chaplain, Sept., 1920; presy. ditto, Nov., 1926; ditto, Bengal, Feb., 1931; ret'd., Aug., 1933.

MCPHERSON, Sir HUGH, K.C.I.E., C.S.I., late Indian C.S.—Educ. at Paisley Gr. Sch., Glasgow Univ., London Univ., and Balliol Coll., Oxford; apptd. after exam. of 1889; arrived, 28th Nov., 1891, and served in Bengal as asst. mag. and collr., and asst. settlt. offr., joint mag. and dep. collr., June, 1897; mag. and collr., Nov., 1902; dir. of land records, July, 1907; transf. to Bihar and Orissa, April, 1912, and apptd. dir. of land records; sec., rev. dept., Nov., 1912; offg. chief sec. to govt., Feb. to Sept., 1914, and again from April, 1915; confd. as chief sec., Nov., 1915; C.S.I., Jan., 1919; offg. sec. to govt. of India, home dept., Dec., 1919, to Dec., 1920; member of exec. council, Bihar and Orissa, April, 1921, to Dec., 1925; K.C.I.E., June, 1924; actg. govr. of Bihar and Orissa from 27th March to 25th July, 1925; ret'd., Nov., 1926.

MCPHERSON, JAMES, C.I.E., M.A., M.B., F.R.C.S., M.R.C.P., Lt.-Col., I.M.S., ret'd. (b. 16th Oct., 1876).—First commn. dated 26th July, 1902; agency surg., Bushire, Dec., 1915; ditto, Eastern Rajputana States, July, 1920; civil medl. duties of Peshawar Dist. and Khyber agency, July, 1923; resdy. surg., Mysore, July, 1925; C.I.E., June, 1933; ret'd., Oct., 1933.

MCPHERSON, Rev. KENNETH CECIL, M.A., Indian Eccles. Estab. (junior chaplain) (b. 8th July, 1890).—Apptd. chaplain on probn., Bombay, 10th Oct., 1931; confd., July, 1932.

MCPHERSON, Sir THOMAS STEWART, Kt., C.I.E., M.A., Barr., late Indian C.S. (b. 21st Aug., 1876).—Educ. at Edinburgh Univ., and Trin. Coll., Oxford; apptd. after exam. of 1898; arrived, 20th Nov., 1899, and served in Bengal as asst. mag. and collr.; joint mag. and dep. collr., Nov., 1907; transf. to Bihar and Orissa, April, 1912; dist. and sess. judge, Nov., 1913

ditto, and judl. commr., Chota Nagpur, April, 1916; addl. supt. and membr. of legal affairs, Dec., 1916; supt. and membr. of legal affairs, and sec. to legisl. council, Oct., 1917; registrar, Patna high ct., Oct., 1920; C.I.E., Jan., 1922; offg. judge, high ct., Patna, March to August, 1923, and from June, 1924; actg. judge, Patna; high ct., March, 1926, and again, Nov., 1926; addl. judge, ditto, Jan., 1927; judge, Dec., 1927; Kt., Jan., 1933; ret'd., Aug., 1936.

MACQUEEN, PERCY, Indian C.S. (collr. and dist. mag., Madras) (b. 13th Nov., 1883).—Educ. at Malvern and Selwyn Coll., Cambridge; apptd. after exam. of 1906; arrived, 11th Dec., 1907, and served in Madras as asst. collr. and mag.; sub. collr. and jt. mag., July, 1915; offg. collr. and dist. mag., March, 1919; ditto, Jan., 1920; ditto, March, 1922; confd., Oct., 1927; curator, Madras record office, Apl., 1930.

M CRAE, HENRY WARREN, Office of High Commr. for India (higher exec. officer, store dept.) (b. 13th July, 1884).—Educ. at City of London Sch.; apptd. 2nd class clerk, India store depôt, 19th Aug., 1902; transfed. to office of high commr. for India, Oct., 1920; higher clerical officer, April, 1922; higher exec. officer, March, 1926.

MACRAE, IAN MACPHERSON, C.I.E., O.B.E., M.B., Major-Genl., I.M.S., Jail Dept., Bihar and Orissa (b. 2nd Oct., 1882).—Joined the service 1st Sept., 1904; in mil. employ. March, 1905, to July, 1908; jail supt., Dec., 1911; on mil. service, Oct., 1914, to Aug., 1920; O.B.E., June, 1919; supt., central prison, Bareilly, Sept., 1920; ditto, Lucknow, March, 1923; inspr.-gen. of prisons, Bihar and Orissa, Feb., 1926; C.I.E., Jan., 1931; asst. dir., medl. service, Bombay, Nov., 1933; apptd. King's hon. physician, Jan., 1936.

MACRAE, WILLIAM, Lt.-Col., R.E., late Indian State Railways (b. 28th May, 1880).—First commn. dated 9th Aug., 1899; apptd. asst. engr., state rlys., Feb., 1907; exec. engr., March, 1910; ditto, and junr. govt. inspr. of rlys., Sept., 1912; on mil. duty from Oct., 1914, to Feb., 1920; dep. chief engr., Nov., 1926; ch. engr., July, 1931; ret'd., May, 1935.

M CRAE, WILLIAM, C.I.E., M.A., D.Sc., F.L.S., late Indian Agricultural Service (b. 26th May, 1878).—Joined the service, 28th March, 1908, as supy. mycologist; agricrl. mycologist, Coimbatore, Jan., 1910; imperial mycologist, Pusa, Dec., 1921; offg. agricrl. expert, Impl. council of agricrl. research, Apl., 1934; C.I.E., June, 1934; ret'd., June, 1934.

M CROBERT, GEORGE REID, Lt.-Col., I.M.S., Medical Dept., Madras (profr. of medicine, medl. coll., Madras) (b. 21st Jan., 1895).—Date of first commn., 15th Mar., 1920; apptd. profr. of medicine, medl. coll., Madras, Oct., 1934.

MCSWEENY, DENIS LEON, M.C., Office of High Commr. for India (suptg. exec. officer, accounts dept.) (b. 26th Jan., 1883).—Apptd. 2nd class clerk, store depôt, 14th March, 1904; on mil. service from Aug., 1914, to May, 1919; transfed. to office of high commr. for India, Oct., 1920; higher exec. officer, Apl., 1922; suptg. exec. officer, Nov., 1934.

M TURK, JOHN, B.A., Burma C.S. (Cl. I.) (asst. commr.) (b. 26th July, 1914).—Educ. at Stowe and St. John's Coll., Cambridge; joined the service, 30th Sept., 1937; arrived in Burma, Nov., 1937, and served as asst. commr.

MACWATT, SIR ROBERT CHARLES, Kt., C.I.E., M.B., Major-Gen., late I.M.S.—First commn., 1st Oct., 1887; agency surgeon, Haraoiti and Tonk, Ajmer and Kotah and Jhallowar to June, 1907, when he was apptd. resdy.-surgen, Western Rajputana States; agency-surgn., Eastern Rajputana States, Nov., 1913; chief med. offr. in Rajputana, and civil surgn., Ajmer, Sept., 1914; C.I.E., Jan., 1916; inspr.-gen. of civil hospss., Punjab, and chief plague and malaria med. officer, in addn., Aug., 1918; dir.-gen. Indian Medical Service, Jan., 1923; Kt., Jan., 1925; ret'd., Sept., 1926.

M WATTERS, SIR ARTHUR CECIL, Kt., C.I.E., M.A., late Indian C.S. (United Provs.) (b. 13th Sept., 1880).—Educ. at Clifton and Trinity Coll., Oxford; apptd. after exam. of 1903; arrived, 28th Nov., 1904, and served in the United Prov. as asst. mag. and collr.; under sec. to govt., Oct., 1909; ditto, govt. of India, comm. and ind. dept., Feb., 1910, to March, 1913; jt. mag., Nov., 1913; asst. wheat commr. for India, March, 1915; wheat commr. for India, Oct., 1915; controller of hides and wool, Indian munitions board (in addn.), April, 1917; C.I.E., June, 1918; spl. duty in England, Jan. to March, 1919; sec. to secretariat procedure cmtee., Sept., 1919; employed in foreign and pol. dept., govt. of India, from Jan., 1920; offg. contr. of currency, Dec., 1920; confd., May, 1921; offg. sec. to govt. of India, finance dept., March, 1923; confd., Sept., 1923; sec. to govt. of India, dept. of industries and labour, Oct., 1926; tempy. member of gov.-gen.'s council, Oct., 1927; April, 1928, Oct.-Dec., 1929, and again, Sept., 1930; Kt., June, 1929; secty. to govt. of India, fin. dept., Oct., 1929; attended Indian round table conf., London, Nov., 1930-Jan., 1931; ret'd., Dec., 1931; apptd. commr. for I.C.S. Family Pensions Fund, Oct., 1937.

MACWATTERS, MATHew ROBERT CECIL, M.B., F.R.C.S., Lt.-Col., I.M.S. (ret'd.) (b. 4th Jan., 1878).—First commn. 1st Feb., 1905; in mil. employ. till July, 1908, and from Dec., 1908, to May, 1913; on mil. service, Aug., 1914, to Sept., 1919; profr. of surgery, King George's Medl. Coll., Lucknow, Sept., 1919; civil surgeon, United Provs., July, 1920; ret'd., Jan., 1933.

McWILLIAM, PETER NEISH, B.A., Indian C.S. (jt. mag. and dep. collr., Bengal) (b. 21st Oct., 1906).—Educ. at Tottenham County School and East London College and Downing College, Cambridge; joined the service, 11th Oct., 1930; arrived in India, Nov., 1930, and served in Bengal as asst. mag. and collr.; jt. mag. and dep. collr., Nov., 1931.

MADAN, FRANCIS FALCONER, B.A., Indian C.S. (dist. and sess. judge, Bihar) (b. 27th Oct., 1886).—Educ. at Eton and Corpus Christi Coll., Oxford; apptd. after exam. of 1910; arrived in India, 27th Nov., 1911, and served in Bengal as asst. mag. and collr.; transfed. to Bihar and Orissa, April, 1912; on mil. duty, Aug., 1918.

to May, 1919; jt. mag. and dep. collr., Nov., 1919; offg. dist. and sessions judge, Oct., 1923, and again, Aug., 1926; offg. registrar, high ct., March, 1926; addl. dist. and sess. judge, Oct., 1926; confd., April, 1928; addl. judge, high ct., Patna, July, 1936.

MADAN, FRAMROZ RUSTONJI, B.A., Indian Forest Service (consvr., Madras) (b. 3rd Mar., 1888).—Joined the service as asst. consvr., Dec., 1911; dep. consvr., Dec., 1916; offg. consvr., Apl., 1926; confd., Oct., 1931.

MADAN GOPAL SARDHANA, RAI BAHADUR, B.A., A.M.I.C.E., Indian Service of Engrs. (suptg. engr., United Provs.) (b. 29th Jan., 1886).—Joined the service, 14th Oct., 1908; asst. engr., Oct., 1909; exec engr., Oct., 1917; offg. suptg. engr., Apl., 1932; confd., Nov., 1934.

MADAN, JANARDAN ATMARAM, C.I.E., B.A., Indian C.S. (secty. to govt., Bombay, rev. dept.) (b. 12th Feb., 1885).—Educ. at Elphinstone High Sch., Bombay, Bombay Univ., and Oxford (Non-Collegiate); apptd. after exam. of 1908; arrived, 19th Nov., 1909, and served in Bombay as asst. collr. and asst. settlt. offr.; asst. regr., co-op. societies, Oct., 1919; actg. collr. and regr., co-op. societies, Jan., 1920; member of legl. council, 1925; jt. sec. Royal Commn. on Agric. in India, 1926-8; collr. and dist. mgt., Oct., 1927; C.I.E., March, 1929; offg. dir. of lab. intell. and regr. of trade unions, May, 1930; offg. secty. to govt. rev. dept., Bombay, Jan., 1934; confd., June, 1934; commr., Dec., 1936.

MADAN LAL, PURI, Lt.-Col., I.M.S. (civil surg., Punjab) (b. 13th Apl., 1885).—First commn., 1st Feb., 1908; in mil. employ to 11th Dec., 1921; civil surg., Oct., 1922.

MADAN MOHAN SINHA, RAI BAHADUR, M.B.E. (mag. and collr., United Provs.) (b. 3rd Jan., 1886).—Joined the service, 26th Apl., 1913, as dep. collr.; M.B.E., Dec., 1919; asst. sec. to govt., March, 1921; sec., U.P. Commee. for the British Empire Exhibition, Feb., 1923; dep. dir. of industries, Dec., 1925; offg. mag. and collr., Feb., 1933; confd., May, 1933.

MADANI, GHULAM AHMED (see Ghulam Ahmed Madani).

MADELEY, WILLIAM YORKE, B.A., Indian C.S. (dist. and sess. judge, United Provs.) (b. 26th Nov., 1887).—Educ. at Woodbridge Gr. Sch., and Jesus Coll., Oxford; apptd. after exam. of 1911; arrived, 23rd Nov., 1912, and served in the United Provs. as asst. mag. and collr. and asst. commr.; jt. mag., July, 1917; on mil. duty (I.A.R.O.), Aug., 1918, to Jan., 1919; actg. mag. and collr., May, 1920; offg. dist. and sess. judge, April, 1922; confd., Feb., 1927.

MADGAONKAR, SIR GOVIND DINANATH, Kt., B.A., late Indian C.S. (Bombay) (b. 21st May, 1871).—Educ. at St. Xavier's and Elphinstone Colls., Bombay, Bombay Univ., and Balliol Coll., Oxford; apptd. after exam. of 1890; arrived, 2nd Dec., 1892, and served in Burma as asst. commr.; transfd. to Bombay, Mar., 1897; 2nd asst., July, 1897; 1st asst., Dec., 1903; judge and sess. judge, Aug., 1905; actg. addl. judl. commr. of Sind, Aug., 1920; actg. judge, high ct., Bombay, June, 1925; judge, high ct., Bombay, Aug., 1925; offg. ch. justice, ditto, June, 1930; ret'd., April, 1931; Kt., June, 1931.

MADGE, DAVID BRISTO CULLEY, Indian Police (supt., United Provs.) (b. 2nd Jan., 1895).—Joined the service as asst. supt., 27th Dec., 1919; offg. supt., May, 1923; supt. (prov. sub.), Feb., 1929; confd., May, 1929.

MADHU SUDAN DAYAL, MITHAL, Lala, B.Sc., Indian Service of Engrs. (exec. engr., Punjab) (b. 17th Feb., 1899).—Joined the service, 16th Oct., 1922; asst. exec. engr., Oct., 1923; exec. engr., Mar., 1935.

MAFFEY, SIR JOHN LOADER, G.C.M.G., K.C.B., K.C.V.O., C.S.I., C.I.E., late Indian C.S.—Educ. at Rugby Sch., and Christ Church, Oxford; appt. after exam. of 1899; arrived 3rd Dec., 1900, and served in the N.W. Prov. and Oudh as asst. commr. and asst. mag. and collr.; transfd. to N.W.
