Frontier Prov. as asst. commr., Jan., 1905; pol. asst., Oct., 1906; asst. sec. to chief commr., Jan., 1907; asst. pol. officer, Mohmand Field Force, May to June, 1908; sec. to chief commr., Jan., 1909; pol. agent, Khyber, Jan., 1909, to April, 1912; asst. commr., Mardan, Nov., 1913; dep. commr., Peshawar, April, 1914; offg. dep. sec. to govt. of India, for. and pol. dept., Jan., 1915; pte. sec. to Viceroy, from April, 1916; C.I.E., June, 1916; on spl. duty as chief pol. offr., N.W. Frontier Force, from May, 1919; C.S.I., June, 1920; chief sec. to H.R.H. the Duke of Connaught for Indian tour, 1920-1921; K.C.V.O., Feb., 1921; chief commr. and agent to govr.-gen., N.W. Frontier Prov., March, 1921; ret'd., Nov., 1925; apptd. govr.-gen., Sudan, Oct., 1926; K.C.M.G., June, 1931; perm. under sec. of state, Colonial Office, Nov., 1933; K.C.B., June, 1934; G.C.M.G., June, 1935.

MAFLIN, ALFRED JOHN GOODWIN, M.I.C.E., M.R.San.I., late Indian Service of Engrs. (Bengal) (b. 8th June, 1885).—Joined the service, 1st Oct., 1908, as asst. engr.; arrived, 22nd Oct., 1908; exec. engr., Oct., 1916; on mil. service, May, 1917, to March, 1919; ret'd., May, 1930.

MAFLIN, PERCY HAMILTON, O.B.E., M.C., late Indian State Rlys. (b. 26th Nov., 1883).—Trained at R.I.E. Coll.; joined the service as asst. engr., Jan., 1907; exec. engr., Jan., 1915; on milty. duty from 1st April, 1916, to 2nd March, 1919, and again from 15th July, 1919, to 17th March, 1920; O.B.E., Aug., 1920; dep. dir. (projects), rly. board, April, 1924; offg. secty., rly. board, May, 1924; divl. supt., N.W. Rly., Oct., 1927; on spl. duty, rly. bd., Oct., 1927; offg. secty., rly. bd., Aug., 1928; offg. agent, E.B. rly., Apl., 1929; offg. member, rly. bd., Sept., 1930; ret'd., Apl., 1933.

MAGNIAC, SIR CHARLES LANE, Kt., C.M.G., C.B.E., Brig.-Gen., R.E. (ret'd.), late Railway Dept.—Apptd. asst. engr., famine relief works, 7th Feb., 1897; ditto, N.W. Rly., Sept., 1897; dist. traffic supt., July, 1903; dep. traffic manager, June, 1913; on mil. duty, 1914; asst. dir. of rly. transport, May, 1915; C.M.G., 1916; C.B.E., 1919; dir. of movements, May, 1919; liaison offr. with the Rly. Bd., June, 1919; ret'd., March, 1922; Kt., June, 1923.

MAGRATH, WILLIAM CHRISTOPHER McLEAN, Indian Police (supt., Bihar) (b. 25th July, 1898).—Joined the service as assi. supt., 12th Sept., 1921; offg. supt., Jan., 1924; confd., Apl., 1932.

MAGUIRE, ANGUS DENYS, Indian Police (asst. supt., Bihar) (b. 2nd Oct., 1907).—Joined the service as asst. supt., Bihar and Orissa, 17th Nov., 1927; offg. supt., June, 1934.

MAHABIR PRASAD, B.Sc., Indian Service of Engrs. (exec. engr., United Provs.) (b. 7th Nov., 1888).—Joined the service, 19th Sept., 1911; asst. engr., Sept., 1912; exec. engr., Sep., 1921; offg. dep. dir. of industries, Jan., 1931; prof. of civil engrg., Thomason Coll., Dec., 1934, to Apl. 1937.

MAHADEVA AYYAR, CANDAMANGALAM SITARAMA, B.A., B.L., late Judicial dept., Madras (b. 31st July, 1876).—Joined the service as offg. dist. munisf, 24th July, 1905; confd., Apl., 1906; sub-judge, Sept., 1923; dist. and sess. judge, Dec., 1928; ret'd., July, 1931.

MAHADEVAN, VENKATASUBBA, Lt.-Col., I.M.S. (dist. medl. officer, Madras) (b. 1st Feb., 1888).—First commn. dated 31st Jan., 1914; in milty. employ until 27th May, 1923; dist. medl. officer, May, 1923; surg., 1st dist., Nov., 1932.

MAHALANABIS, PRASANTA CHANDRA, M.A. (Cantab.), B.Sc., Indian Educl. Service (prof., presy. coll., Calcutta) (b. 29th June, 1893).—Joined the service, 1st Sept., 1917; prof., Dacca coll., May, 1919; prof. presy. coll., July, 1920.

MAHAMADI, RASHID AHMED, B.A., Indian C.S. (asst. collr., Bombay) (b. 24th July, 1909).—Educ. at Madras-I-Aliya, Hyderabad, Madras University and School of Oriental Studies, London; joined the service, 6th October, 1933; arrived in India, Nov., 1933, and served in Bombay as asst. collr.

MAHANTY, DIBAKAR (see Dibakar, M.).

MAHARAJ SINGH, KUNWAR, Sir, C.I.E., M.A., Barr., late United Provs., C.S. (b. 17th May, 1878).—Joined the service, 1st Dec., 1904, as dep. collr., United Provs.; asst. regr., co-op. credit societies, July, 1908; asst. sec., dept. of education, April, 1911; senr. asst. sec., ditto, April, 1915; C.I.E., June, 1915; offg. dep. commr., July, 1917; mag. and collr., Sept., 1919; dep. sec. to govt. of India, dept. of educn. and health, Nov., 1920, to March, 1923; on depn. to Mauritius and to B. Guiana to report on questions connected with Indian emigration to those colonies, 1925; offg. commr., May, 1927, and again March, 1928; confd., Apl., 1931; member, E. Africa deputation, Oct., 1927; on foreign service as vice-presdt. of Jodhpur State Council; agent of Govt. of India in the Union of South Africa, Aug., 1932; Kt., Jan., 1933; ret'd., May, 1933; member, govr.'s exec. council, United Provs., Mar., 1935, to Mar., 1937.

MAHBUB ALI KHAN, KHAN BAHADUR SHAIKH, O.B.E., Indian Political Service (b. Mar., 1894).—Joined the service, Mar., 1915; asst. cmmr., Peshawar, May, 1930; ditto, Mansehra, Mar., 1935; dep. cmmr., Dera Ismail Khan, May, 1936; asst. cmmr., Thali, Mar., 1937.

MAHBUB, ILAHI CHARAGH, SHAIKH, Indian Service of Engrs. (exec. engr., Punjab) (b. 14th Mar., 1904).—Joined the service as apprentice engr., 30th Oct., 1925; asst. exec. engr., Oct., 1926; exec. eng., Oct. 1935.

MAHBUB, MIAN IMAM BAKSH (see Kadri M. M. I. B.).

MAHER, JOHN EDWARD, Indian C.S. (sub.-collr., Madras) (b. 5th Sept., 1904).—Educ. at Cheltenham, and Keble Coll., Oxford; joined the service, 10th Oct., 1928; arrived, Nov., 1928, and served in Madras as asst. mag. and collr.; sub.-collr., Apl., 1931.

MAHIDA, UDAYSINH NARSENHBAVA, B.E., Indian Service of Engineers (b. 20th April, 1904).—Appointed assistant executive engineer, Bombay, 1st Oct., 1929.

MAHONY, FRANCIS VINCENT, Indian State Rlys. (dep. contr. of stores, N.W. rly.) (b. 2nd Mar., 1886).—Held non-gazetted appts. from 22nd Dec., 1902, to 6th June, 1909; dist. contr. of stores, N.W. rly., May, 1919; offg. dep. contr., May, 1933; and again, Feb., 1934; confd., May, 1934; offg. contr., Aug., 1936.

MAHONY, GEORGE HENRY, M.B., Lt.-Col., I.M.S. (prof. of midwifery, Patna) (b. 31st Dec., 1888).—First commn. dated 31st Jan., 1914; in mil. employ to 1st April, 1925; offg. civil surg., Central Provs., April, 1925; confd., May, 1928; reverted to milty. service, June, 1931; prof. of midwifery, Prince of Wales medl. coll., Patna, Oct., 1935.

MAHTAB, Sir BIJAY CHAND, see BURDWAN.

MAIN, THOMAS FORSTER, O.B.E., B.Sc., late Indian Agricl. Service (b. 23rd Jan., 1882).—Appdt. asst. insp'r.-gen. of agric., Central Provs., 18th Aug., 1905; dep. dir. of agric., Bombay, Sept., 1909; ditto, Sind, Sept., 1915; offg. dir. of agric., April, 1924; dep. dir. of agric., Poona, June, 1925; O.B.E., June, 1927; dir. of agric., Oct., 1927; ret'd., July, 1933.

MAINWARING, ALBERT JAMES, C.I.E., B.A. (Oxon), Indian C.S. (mag. and collr., Bihar) (b. 30th Nov., 1891).—Educ. at King's School, Worcester, and Pembroke Coll., Oxford; appdt. after exam. of 1914; arrived, 4th Dec., 1915, and served in Bihar and Orissa as asst. mag. and collr.; on mil. duty from Aug., 1916, to June, 1919; ditto, and asst. settlmt. officer, Nov., 1919; offg. under-sec. to govt., pol. and apptt. dept., Feb., 1923; offg. asst. pol. agent, Orissa Feudatory States, Nov., 1924; offg. pte. sec. to govr., March, 1926; confd., Oct., 1926; offg. magte. and collr., Oct., 1928; ditto (provl. sub.), Nov., 1930; confd., Aug., 1933; C.I.E., June, 1934; secty. to govr., Apl., 1937.

MAIR, ROBERT, B.Sc., A.M.I.C.E., A.M.I.E. (Ind.) (dep. chief engr.) (b. 16th July, 1890).—Joined the service as junr. engr., June, 1914; in milty. employ from 26th Dec., 1914, to 21st Jan., 1919; asst. engr., June, 1919; asst. engr., June, 1920; exec. engr., Nov., 1925; on deputation to Calcutta Chord Rly. construction from Jan., 1927; offg. dep. ch. engr., E.I. rly., June, 1934; offg. dep. agent (organisation), Oct., 1934; dep. ch. engr. (provl. perm.), Apl., 1936.

MAITLAND, VICTOR KENNARD, M.A., M.C., Indian Forest Service (dep. consvr., Central Provs.) (b. 12th June, 1897).—Joined the service as asst. consvr., 16th Dec., 1921; dep. consvr., Dec., 1924; ditto, and dir. of forest school, Balaghat, Mar., 1937.

MAITRA, SANKAR NATH, B.A., Indian C.S. (asst. commr., Assam) (b. 7th June, 1912).—Educated at Mitra Institution, Bhowanipur, Calcutta, University of Calcutta, King's College, London, and St. Catherine's College, Cambridge; joined the service, 9th Sept., 1935; arrived in India, Oct., 1935 and served in Assam as asst. commr.

MAITRA, SURENDRA NATH, M.A., late Indian Edul. Service (b. 19th Sept., 1877).—Apptd. prin., Rajshahi Coll., 4th May, 1925; prinpl., Decca inter. coll., Nov., 1926; retd., Sept., 1932.

MAJID, A. (see Abdul Majid).

MAJID, HAFIZ ABDUL, M.A., Indian C.S. (asst. commr., Punjab) (b. 17th Oct., 1907).—Educ. at Punjab University and Christ Church, Oxford; joined the service, 12th Oct., 1931; arrived in India, Nov., 1931, and served in the Punjab as asst. commr.

MAJID, INAM-UL, M.A., Barr., Indian C.S. (secty., public dept., office of high commr.) (b. 19th May, 1895).—Educ. at Perse Sch., and Christ's Coll., Cambridge; joined the service, 10th Dec., 1921; arrived, 5th Jan., 1922, and served in Assam as asst. commr., under-secty. to govt., Apl., 1929; offg. dir. of indus., etc., Nov., 1930; offg. dep. commr., Sept., 1935; secty., public dept., office of High Commr., Oct., 1937.

MAJIDULLAH, SARDAR, Indian Service of Engrs. (exec. engr., Punjab) (b. 14th Oct., 1899).—Joined the service as asst. exec. engr., Punjab, 1st Oct., 1923; exec. engr., Oct., 1932.

MAJOR, JOHN, India Office (hr. clerical officer) (b. 13th Mar., 1888).—Apptd. clerical officer, 28th Sept., 1921; actg. higher ditto, Jan., 1935; confd., July, 1935.

MAJUMDAR, DWIJENDRA LAL, M.A. (Cal.), B.A. (Cantab.), Indian C.S. (jt. mag. and dep. coll., Bengal) (b. 25th Jan., 1905).—Educ. at University of Calcutta and Jesus Coll., Cambridge; joined the service, 11th Oct., 1929; arrived, Nov., 1929, and served in Bengal as asst. mag. and collr.; jt. mag. and dep. collr., Nov., 1930; offg. addl. dist. and sess. judge, Feb., 1936.

MAJUMDAR, NRIPENDRA NATH, M.A., Indian Audit Dept. (b. Feb., 1897).—Joined the service, 1st Mar., 1922; asst. acct.-genl., Bengal, Sept., 1923; ditto, posts and tels., Nov., 1928; dep. acct.-genl., Bengal, Jan., 1931; asst. dir., army audit, W. Cmd., Jan., 1932; attd. officer, office of the ch. auditor, E.I. rly., Mar., 1936; dep. govt. exr., B.N. rly., Apl., 1936.

MAJUMDAR, SATIS CHANDRA, B.Sc., Indian Service of Engrs. (suptg. engr., Bengal) (b. 22nd May, 1887).—Joined the service, 1st Oct., 1910, as asst. engr.; exec. engr., Oct., 1918; offg. suptg. engr., April, 1931; confd., Sept., 1935.

MAKAND, SAMUEL NORMAN, M.B., Ch. B., Lt.-Col., I.M.S., Medical Dept., Central Provs. (dir. of public health) (b. 10th July, 1886).—Date of first commn., 25th Feb., 1921; in milty. employ until 20th Feb., 1930; medl. officer of health, Simla, Feb., 1931; offg. dir. of public health, central provs., Oct., 1934; confd., Jan., 1937.

MAL, DEBI DATTA (see Debi Datta Mal).

MALABARI, PHIROZSHAH BEHRAMJI, KHAN BAHADUR, Barr., late Bombay Judicial Dept. (b. 2nd Nov., 1876).—Joined the dept., April, 1903; prothonotary and regr., June, 1919; offg. offi. assignee, Apl., 1928; confd., Oct., 1928; retd., Dec., 1935.

MALCOLM, AUSTIN KENNETH, Indian Police (b. 5th Feb., 1917).—Apptd., asst. supt., Bengal, on probn., Dec., 1937.

MALCOLM, CHARLES ADOLPH, C.I.E., late Indian Forest Service (b. 27th Aug., 1879).—Joined the service, 22nd Nov., 1902, as asst. consvr. of forests; dep. consvr., Dec., 1908; served under Indian munitions bd., as offg. dep. controller (timber supplies), Bombay, June to Aug., 1918; offg. consvr., Oct., 1920; consvr., Oct., 1923; chief consvr., Apl., 1929; C.I.E., June, 1931; retd., Aug., 1934.

MALCOLM, HUGH WAUDBY, India Office (exec. officer, acct.-genl.'s dept.) (b. 21st May, 1900).—Apptd. clerical officer, 23rd Apl., 1920; actg. exec. officer, Feb., 1934; confd., Dec., 1934.

MALCOLM, KENNETH ROBERT, Indian C.S. (jt. mag., United Provinces) (b. 17th Dec., 1908).—Educ. at Eton and New College, Oxford; apptd. after exam. of 1931; arrived in India, Nov., 1932, and served in the United Provinces as asst. mag. and collr.; jt. magte., Apl., 1935.

MALCOLM, ROBERT CONACHER, Survey of India (asst. supt., mathl. instrument office) (b. 19th June, 1891).—Joined the service, 4th Apl., 1921; asst. supt., mathl. instrument office, Mar., 1926; offg. supt., ditto, May, 1929.

MALCOLMSON, GREER EDMUND, Lt.-Col., I.M.S., retd. (b. 30th Sept., 1878).—First commn. dated 1st Feb., 1906; resigned, Sept., 1913; re-admitted, Feb., 1915; in mil. employ until 7th Aug., 1920; dist. medl. and sanitary officer, Madras, Sept., 1920; prof., medl. coll., and physician gen. hosp., Madras, Dec., 1923; retd., Sept., 1933.

MALET, GERALD RION LILLINGTON, A.M.I.C.E., Indian State Rlys. (exec. engr., E.B. Rly.) (b. 14th Oct., 1889).—Joined the service as asst. engr., 1st Oct., 1913; on milty. duty from 21st May, 1916, to 24th Sept., 1919; exec. engr., Oct., 1922.

MALHAN, FAQIR CHAND, Rai Bahadur, Indian Railway Accts. Service (dep. chief accts. officer, N.W. Rly.) (b. 28th May, 1883).—Held non-gaz. appts. from 2nd May, 1901; served under agent, N.W. Rly.; apptd. compilation officer, statistics, June, 1923; asst. dir. of statistics, Rly. Board, Jan., 1925; offer. on spec. duty, Rly. Board, Dec., 1925; senr. asst. dir., rly. clg. accts. office, Dec., 1926; Rai Bahadur, Jan., 1927; accts. officer, gr. 1., Apl., 1929; offer. on spec. duty, rates register experiment, Jan., 1930; dep. chief accts. officer, N.W. Rly. (provl.), July, 1933.

MALIK, ABDULLA KHALID, M.A., Indian C.S. (asst. commr., Punjab) (b. 7th July, 1910).—Educ. at Government High School, Gujrat, Government Intermediate College, Gujrat, University of the Punjab and Magdalene College, Cambridge; joined the service, 9th Oct., 1934; arrived in India, Nov., 1934, and served in the Punjab as asst. commr.; sub-divl. officer, May, 1937.

MALIK, HARDIT SINGH, O.B.E., B.A., (Oxon.), Indian C.S. dep. commr., Punjab (b. 23rd Nov., 1894).—Educ. at Eastbourne Coll., and Balliol Coll., Oxford; arrived in India, 6th Jan., 1922; and served in the Punjab as asst. commr.; offg. dep. commr., Apl., 1926; dep. commr. (prov.), Nov., 1927; dep. trade commr., London, Jan., 1931, to May, 1934; actg. trade commr., May-Sept., 1932; and again, Nov., 1933; dep. secty. to govt. of India, commerce dept., June, 1934; O.B.E., Jan., 1938.

MALIK, JASVANTSINGH, B.Sc., A.M.I.C.E., Indian Service of Engrs. (exec. engr., Sind) (b. 13th Apl., 1890).—Joined the service as asst. exec. engr., 21st Jan., 1921; offg. exec. engr., July, 1923; confd., Oct., 1929; in foreign service as munpl. engr., Ahmedabad from Feb., 1930.

MALIK, TEJA SINGH, SARDAR, BAHADUR, C.I.E., B.Sc., Indian Service of Engrs. (exec. engr., Central P.W.D.) (b. 1st Sept., 1887).—Joined the service as asst. engr., 4th Nov., 1910; exec. engr., 1st Oct., 1918; C.I.E., Jan., 1930; exec. engr., central P.W.D., Apl. 1930; offg. supt. engr., Sept., 1931, and again, Jan., 1933.

MALLAM, GEORGE LESLIE, Barr., Major, I.A., Indian Political Service (b. 13th Dec., 1895).—First commn. dated 14th Nov., 1916; joined pol. dept., 14th March, 1921; asst. commr., Bannu, June, 1921; under-sec. to pol. agent, Persian Gulf, Jan., 1923; dist. judge, Abbottabad, Dec., 1926; asst. commr., Tonk, June, 1927; ditto, Mardan, May, 1928; under-secty. to A.G.G. and C.C., N.W.F.P., Jan., 1929; census supt., N.W.F.P., April, 1930; counsellor, Brit. legn., Kabul, Apl., 1930; dep. commr., Kohat, Oct., 1934.

MALLET, FREDERICK JOHN, late Public Works Dept., United Provs. (b. 21st Jan., 1878).—Joined the service as mechanical engr. for waterworks, 25th Dec., 1920; retd., Jan., 1933.

MALLIK, see MULLICK.

MALLIK, BHOLA NATH, B.A., Indian Police (asst. supt., Bihar) (b. 9th Apl., 1905).—Joined the service as asst. supt., Bihar and Orissa, 13th April, 1927; awarded King's police medal, Jan., 1932.

MALLIK, HARENDRA KRISTO, M.A., Indian Audit Dept. (b. 16th Mar., 1886).—Joined the service, 1st Mar., 1910; asst. dir. of comml. audit, Burma circle, May, 1930; ditto, Bombay circle, Nov., 1931; asst. acct.-genl., United Provs., June, 1932; dep. ditto, Aug., 1934; attd. offcr., office of comp., Orissa, Oct., 1936; dep. acct.-genl., Central revenues, Apl., 1937.

MALLIK, SATYENDRA CHANDRA, M.A., late Indian C.S. (b. 25th Feb., 1874).—Educ. at Calcutta Univ., and St. John's Coll., Cambridge; apptd. after exam. of 1896; arrived, 8th Dec., 1897, and served in Bengal as asst. mag. and collr.; joint mag. and dep. collr., May, 1906; dist. and sess. judge, May, 1911; presdt., spl. tribunal, Rungpur, Jan. to April, 1918; actg. judge, Calcutta high ct., July, 1926; ditto, Jan., 1927; ditto, April to Aug., 1927; ditto, Jan., 1928; confd., July, 1928; retd., May, 1934.

MALLYA, BANTWAL GANAPATHI, Lt.-Col., I.M.S., Civil Medl. Dept., Bengal (civil surgeon, Bengal) (b. 4th April, 1890).—Joined the

service, 26th Nov., 1914; in milty. employ to 22nd June, 1923; offg. supt., July, 1923; confd., Oct., 1925; offg. civil surgeon, May, 1933.

MAMA, JAMSHED BYRAMJI, D.P.H. (Oxon), D.T.M. (Liver.) late Public Health Dept., Bombay (b. 4th Jan., 1876).—Held non-gazetted appt. from 1st April, 1911, to 31st Aug., 1913; analyst to sany. board, Sept., 1913, to July, 1920; officer in charge, public health laby., Poona, Jan., 1921; dir., vaccine institute, Belgaum, April 1927; retd., Jan., 1931.

MAN MOHAN, RAI BAHADUR, M.A., Punjab Educl. Service (asst. dir., pub. instrn.) (b. 27th July, 1888).—Joined the service, 13th Sept., 1911; asst. prof. of econ., govt. coll., Lahore, Jan., 1913; lecturer, ditto, Oct., 1921; principal, Khalsa Coll., June, 1924; inspr. of schools, Jan., 1931; asst. dir., pub. instrn., Nov., 1936.

MAN SINGH, Indian Service of Engrs. (exec. engr., Bengal) (b. 9th Nov., 1898).—Joined the service as asst. engr. on probation, 7th Dec., 1922; asst. exec. engr., Nov., 1926; exec. engr., Oct., 1931.

MAN SINGH, RAI BAHADUR, C.B.E., B.A., Indian Police (member, pub. serv. commn., United Provs.) (b. 3rd July, 1883).—Joined the service, 1st July, 1906, as dept. supt.; supt., Sept., 1918; awarded King's police medal, Jan., 1920; C.B.E., June, 1932; offg. dep. inspr.-genl., June, 1933; retd., Apl., 1937; member, United Provs. public service commission, Apl., 1937.

MANAVEDAN RAJA, KISHAKKA COVILAGATH. Rao Bahadur, Madras C.S. (sub-collr.) (b. 1st Oct., 1884).—Joined the service 13th Jan., 1915; dep. collr., Oct., 1917; sub-collr., Nov., 1932; offg. collr., Nov., 1932.

MANDAL, see MUNDLE.

MANGALMURTI, KESHAO TUKARAM, B.Sc., LL.B., Indian C.S. (dist. and sess. judge, Central Prov s.) (b. 7th Jan., 1897).—Educ. at Allahabad Univ. and Emmanuel Coll., Cambridge; joined the service, 25th Oct., 1923; arrived, 4th Dec., 1923, and served in Central Provs. as asst. commr.; offg. dep. commr., May, 1927; offg. dist. and sess. judge, Feb., 1929; and again, June, 1932; confd., Jan., 1936.

MANGAL SEN, DHODY, RAI BAHADUR LALA, late Indian Service of Engrs. (b. 22nd July, 1876).—Joined the service, 23rd July, 1899; asst. engr., July, 1900; exec. engr., Oct., 1910; suptg. engr., Aug., 1922; retd., July, 1931.

MANGAT RAI, RAI BAHADUR, B.A., Income Tax Dept., Punjab (b. 13th Jan., 1877).—Joined the service, 26th Oct., 1898; extra asst. commr., Feb., 1922; offg. asst. commr. of inc. tax, N.W.F.P., Feb., 1922; offg. commr. of inc. tax, Punjab and N.W.F.P., May, 1927; and again, Mar., 1933; asst. commr., ditto, Punjab, July, 1927.

MANI, RANGIAH SUBRA, B.A., B.Sc., Indian C.S. (asst. collr., Bombay) (b. 14th August, 1907).—Educ. at Madras University, Imperial College of Science and School of Oriental Studies, London; joined the service, 6th Oct., 1932; arrived in India, Nov., 1932, and served in Bombay as asst. collr.; under-secty. to the Govt. of India, dept. of educn., health and lands, Aug., 1937.

MANI, TIRUCHENDURAI MUTHUKRISHNA SUBRA, M.A., Indian C.S. (under-secty., rev. dept., Madras) (b. 1st Aug., 1908).—Educ. at University College, London, and School of Oriental Studies, London; joined the service, 12th Oct., 1931, and served in Madras as asst. mag. and collr.; sub-collr. and jt. mag., Apl., 1933; under secty., rev. dept., Madras, Apl., 1936.

MANICO, EDWARD LESLIE, Indian State Rlys. (dep. ch. mechl. engr.) (b. 12th Nov., 1887).—Joined the service as assistant carriage and wagon supt., 25th Jan., 1911; dist. ditto, April, 1918; divl. rolling stock officer, Oct., 1927; offg. dep. carr. and wagon supt., Burma rlys., Oct., 1931; dist. loco. supt., N.W. rly., Nov., 1932; offg. dep. ch. mechl. engr., N.W. rly., Mar., 1933; offg. dep. ch. opertg. supt., N.W. rly., Oct., 1933; and again, June, 1934; dep. ch. mechl. engr., Oct., 1936.

MANINDRA LAL SEN GUPTA (see Sen Gupta, M. L.).

MANLEY, EDWARD LOVELL, M.I.C.E., Indian State Rlys. (ch. engr.) (b. 29th June, 1886).—Trained at R.I.E. Coll.; joined the service as asst. engr., 1st Oct., 1908; exec. engr., Oct., 1916; on milty. duty from 8th Feb., 1918, to 17th Dec., 1919; offg. govt. inspr. of rlys., Dec., 1923; offg. dep. ch. engr., Apl., 1929; confd., June, 1934; offg. dep. agent, organisation, E.B. rly., Mar., 1934; offg. agent, E.B. rly., Apl., 1935; and again, May, 1937; offg. ch. engr., E.B. rly., Oct., 1935; prov. confd., Dec., 1935; confd., Aug., 1937.

MANMATHA NATH MITRA, L.M.S. (Punj.), L.M. (Dublin), D.P.H. (London), Public Health Dept., United Provs. (b. 23rd May, 1885).—Joined the service as health officer on probn., 1st Mar., 1914; confd., Mar., 1915; asst. dir. of public health, 12th Mar., 1930.

MANNING, ARTHUR PITCHER, C.I.E., O.B.E., M.C., late Indian State Rlys. (b. 11th Nov., 1882).—Joined the telegraph dept., India Posts and Telegraphs, 1st Oct., 1905, as asst. supt.; ditto, engrg., April, 1914; deputed to fd. service, E. Africa, July, 1915 (awarded the Military Cross, June, 1917; mentioned in despatch); O.B.E., June, 1918; C.I.E., June, 1919; divl. engr., tels., Dec., 1920; tel. supt., N.W. Rly., Oct., 1927; retd., Nov., 1932.

MANNING, DONALD EDMUND BELFORD, Indian Forest Service (dep. consvr., Burma) (b. 9th Feb., 1900).—Joined the service as asst. consvr., 24th Dec., 1923; dep. consvr., Mar., 1928.

MANNOOCH, GEOFFREY HERBERT, Indian Police (supt., Bengal) (b. 23rd Aug., 1890).—Joined the service as asst. supt., 30th Nov., 1910; supt., Feb., 1920; offg. dep. inspr.-genl., variously from 1932.

MANOHAR DAS CHATURVEDI, B.Sc., Indian Forest Service (dep. consvr. United Provs.) (b. 9th Dec., 1897).—Joined the service as asst. consvr., 28th Dec., 1923; silviculturist, March, 1927; dep. consvr., Dec., 1931.

MANOHAR LAL SOOD, B.A., Indian Service of Engrs. (asst. exec. engr., United Provs.) (b. 28th Jan., 1905).—Joined the service as appr. engr., 19th Nov., 1927; engr. student, Nov., 1928; proby. asst. exec. engr., Nov., 1928; confd., Jan., 1930.

MANOHAR LAL ZUTSHI, PANDIT, M.A., late Indian Educl. Service (b. July, 1876).—Joined the service, 21st Sept., 1902; inspr. of schools, Feb., 1921; offg. asst. dir. of public instrn. and under-sec. to govt., Jan., 1923; prinpl., jubilee intermediate coll., Lucknow, April, 1924; ditto, govt. coll., Sept., 1927; retd., July, 1931.

MANSER, THOMAS JAMES STEDMAN, India Posts and Tels. (divl. engr., tels.) (b. 24th Oct., 1883).—Joined the service, 1st Sept., 1899; asst. engr., tels., Oct., 1924; asst. divl. engr., tels., May, 1927; divl. engr., tels., June, 1933.

MANSFIELD, PHILIP THEODORE, C.I.E., B.A. (Cantab.), Indian C.S. (ch. secty. to govt., Orissa) (b. 1st Feb., 1892).—Educ. at Charterhouse and Pembroke Coll., Cambridge; apptd. after exam. of 1914; arrived, 11th Dec., 1915, and served in Bihar and Orissa as asst. mag. and collr.; on mil. duty, Oct., 1918, to Jan., 1919; ditto, and addl. pol. agent, Feudatory States, Oct., 1919; jt. mag., Jan., 1923; offg. dir. of land records and surveys, March, 1928; confd., Jan., 1929; mag. and collr. (provl. sub.), Jan., 1929; confd., Nov., 1930; offg. secty. to govt., rev. dept., Apl., 1931; dir. of land records and surveys, Apl., 1933; secty. to govt., Bihar and Orissa, reforms dept., Jan., 1935; C.I.E., Jan., 1936; ch. secty. to govt., Orissa, Apl., 1936.

MANSON, FREDERICK PERCY, A.M.I.C.E., Indian State Rlys. (exec. engr., G.I.P. Rly.) (b. 4th Dec., 1883).—Joined the service as tempy. engr., 12th Sept., 1914; asst. engr., Mar., 1918; resdt. engr., Sept., 1919; exec. engr., Jan., 1929.

MANSON, JOHN ALEXANDER SHAND, Indian Service of Engrs. (exec. engr., Bombay) (b. 19th Aug., 1901).—Joined the service as asst. exec. engr. Bombay, 1st Oct., 1924; offg. exec. engr., May, 1929; confd., Oct., 1933.

MANSON, SAMUEL DUNCAN, late Indian State Rlys. (b. 10th Jan., 1885).—Joined the dept. as asst. traffic supt., 26th Oct., 1908; dist. traffic supt., April, 1915; dir. of traffic, Apl., 1924; offg. dir. of traffic, May, 1924; member, rly. rates advisory committee, April, 1926; offg. dep. ch. coml. manager, Oct., 1928; confd., Jan., 1929; retd., Aug., 1933.

MANT, SIR REGINALD ARTHUR, K.C.S.I., K.C.I.E., B.A., late Indian C.S. (b. 27th Sept., 1870).—Educ. at Trinity Coll., Oxford; apptd. after exam. of 1893; arrived, 10th Mar., 1895; and served in the Punjab as asst. commr.; under-sec. to govt., Jan., 1899; under-sec. to govt. of India, finl. dept., March, 1901; dep. ditto, April, 1904, to June, 1905; dep. commr., June, 1905; dept. offg. dep. sec. to govt. of India, finl. dept., Nov. 1907; offg. joint sec., mily. fin. branch, July, 1908; dep. commr., Punjab, 1909; finl. sec. to govt., Nov., 1910, to June, 1915; sec. to govt. of India, dept. of rev. and agric., Jan., 1916, to June, 1920; addl. member, govr.-genl.'s legisl. council, Jan., 1916; temp. member, govr.-genl.'s council, April, 1919; C.S.I., June, 1919; member of Indian Fiscal Commission, Oct., 1921, to July, 1922; commr., April, 1923; K.C.I.E., Jan., 1924; retd., Sept., 1924; member of council of India, 1st Oct., 1924, to Oct., 1934; member, Royal Commn. on Indian Currency and Finance, 1925; member, East

African Commn., Nov., 1927; K.C.S.I., Jan., 1933; member, Ind. staty. rly. bd. ctee., June, 1933; vice-chairman, Ind. res. bank ctee., July, 1933.

MAQBUL HUSAIN, SHAIKH, KHAN BAHADUR, C.I.E., B.A., Barr., late United Provs., C.S. (b. 26th Nov., 1876).—Joined the service, 1st Dec., 1903, as dep. collr.; served under govt. of India, home dept., as asst. dir. of criminal intelligence, from July, 1909, to Nov., 1919; rev. minister, Kashmir, Nov., 1909; C.I.E., Jan., 1916; jt. regr., co-op. credit societies, United Provs., Jan., 1918; temp. regr., ditto, June, 1918; confd., Sept., 1921; dep. commr., Nov., 1924; mag. and collr., Sept., 1921; retd., Nov., 1931.

MAQBUL SHAH, KHAN BAHADUR, SAYAD, B.A., late Indian Educl. Service (b. Feb., 1875).—Joined the service, 14th Dec., 1896; inspr. of schools, March, 1920; ditto of vernacular education, Feb., 1921; retd., Dec., 1931.

MAQSUD ALI KHAN, KHAN BAHADUR, Munshi, B.A., late United Provs. C.S. (b. 15th Jan., 1878).—Joined the service as proxy. dep. collr., 14th June, 1901; confd., Dec., 1903; magte. and collr., Dec., 1928; retd., Jan., 1933.

MARAR, KOTTIL WALAPPIL PARAMESWARA, Indian C.S. (asst. commr., Assam) (b. 7th Nov., 1903).—Educ. at Univ. of Madras and School of Oriental Studies, London; joined the service, 4th Oct., 1928; arrived, Nov., 1928, and served in Assam as asst. commr.; offg. dep. commr., Apl., 1933; offg. under secty. to govt., Assam, in depts. under ch. secty., Oct., 1933; awarded K.-i.H. gold medal, Jan., 1935.

MARCHINTON, WILLIAM FRANKLIN, Indian Service of Engineers (electrical inspr., Bihar) (b. 14th Oct., 1884).—Joined the service as dep. dir. of industries, Ranchi, 29th Oct., 1920; industrial engr., Nov., 1925; offg. electrical engr., April, 1926; confd., April, 1927; surveyor of inland steam vessels, Patna, Nov., 1932.

MARDHEKAR, VAMAN BHIVRAM, M.A., late Bombay C.S. (b. 16th Nov., 1880).—Joined the service, 18th June, 1905; dep. collr., Oct., 1907; actg. supt., land records, Aug., 1916; confd., Aug., 1917; offg. collr., Feb., 1922; confd., May, 1930; retd., Nov., 1935.

MARIETTE, ERNEST EDGAR, A.M.I.C.E., Indian Service of Engrs. (exec. engr., Madras) (b. 13th Oct., 1890).—Joined the service, 1st Oct., 1914; asst. engr., March, 1919; exec. engr., Oct., 1923.

MARJORIBANKS, ALAN GEORGE, Indian Police (b. 28th Oct., 1904).—Joined the service as asst. dist. supt., Central Provs., 11th Dec., 1924; offg. dist. supt., Dec., 1928, Jan., 1933, and July, 1933.

MARJORIBANKS, GEORGE ERSKINE, late Indian Forest Service (Bombay) (b. 6th Jan., 1877).—Joined the service, 17th Nov., 1899, as asst. consvr.; divnl. forest offr., Nov., 1903; dep. consvr., Jan., 1907; consr., June, 1922; retd., Nov., 1929.

MARJORIBANKS, Sir NORMAN EDWARD, K.C.S.I., K.C.I.E., late Indian C.S. (b. 16th Oct., 1872).—Educ. at Bishop Cotton's Sch. and Coll. Bangalore, Belfast Royal Acadl. Inst., Royal Univ. of Ireland, and Trinity Hall, Cambridge; apptd. after exam. of 1891; arrived, 29th Oct.,

1893, and served in Madras as asst. collr. and mag.; head asst., July, 1901; offg. under-sec. to govt., rev. dept., March, 1900; dep. dir. of land records, Nov., 1903; dir. of land records, Sept., 1906; collr. and mag., Nov., 1911; spl. duty in connection with Indian labour commn., Oct., 1916, to April, 1917; collr. of Madras, supt. of stamps and stationery, &c., Aug. to Oct., 1917, and from May to June, 1918; member, bd. of rev., Madras, June, 1919; C.I.E., June, 1919; sec. to govt., rev. dept., Aug., 1919; 3rd member, board of rev., Nov., 1921; C.S.I., Jan., 1922; 2nd member, board of rev., Nov., 1922; offg. 1st member, board of rev., Nov., 1923; actg. ch. sec. to govt., March, 1924; temp. member of govr.'s exec. council, Madras, April, 1924; ch. sec. to govt., June, 1924; apptd. member, govr.'s exec. council, Madras, Dec., 1924; K.C.I.E., Jan., 1928; actg. govr., Madras, June, 1929; retd., Jan., 1930; chairman, Madras services commn., Jan., 1930; K.C.S.I., Jan., 1931.

MARLOW, THOMAS, late Indian Forest Engrg. Service (b. 7th Jan., 1892).—Joined the service as timber asst., 17th April, 1919; forest engr., Dec., 1922; retd., Apl., 1933.

MARLOW, WILLIAM, Office of High Commr. for India (higher executive officer, general dept.) (b. 8th July, 1889).—Educ. at Brentwood Gr. Sch., Monoux Sch., Walthamstow, and East London Coll.; apptd. sec. div. clerk board of educ., 29th April, 1907; 2nd class clerk, India Office, 29th Oct., 1907; on mil. service from Dec., 1916; transf. to office of high commr. for India, Oct., 1920; attd. to Indian del. to League of Nations, 1st Assembly, 1920; personal asst. to high commr., April, 1921; higher exec. officer, June, 1927; on special duty in connection with the building of India House, Mar., 1929, to Aug., 1930; services lent to Indian del. Impl. Economic Conf., May to Sept., 1932 asst. sec., ditto, July, 1932.

MARR, ALEXANDER, C.I.E., M.A., B.Sc., late Indian C.S. (b. 17th April, 1876).—Educ. at Robert Gordon's Coll., Aberdeen, and Aberdeen Univ.; apptd. after exam. of 1899; arrived, 3rd Dec., 1900, and served in Bengal as asst. mag. and collr.; transf. to E. Bengal and Assam, Oct., 1905; joint mag. and dep. collr., Jan., 1908; mag. and collr., Nov., 1909; re-transfd. to Bengal, April, 1912; offg. sec., board of rev., Bengal, Oct., 1912, to Nov., 1914; temp. employed under War Office from July, 1916, to March, 1917; on deputn. to America on spl. duty from March, 1917; sec. to govt. of Bengal, fin. dept., Nov., 1919; C.I.E., June, 1922; chairman, Calcutta improvement trust, Nov., 1924; member, exec. council, govr. of Bengal, Oct., 1927; retd., Aug., 1934.

MARRIOTT, ERIC LLEWELLYN, C.I.E., Indian Police (dep. inspr.-genl., Bihar) (b. 28th May, 1888).—Joined the service, 4th Dec., 1907; asst. supt., Apl., 1912; supt., July, 1917; offg. dep. inspr.-genl., May, 1931, and again, Feb., 1933; confd., June, 1934; C.I.E., June, 1934.

MARRIOTT, ROBERT ECKLIN, B.Sc., Indian States Rlys. (dep. ch. engr.) (b. 15th Oct., 1887).—Joined the service as asst. engr., Oct., 1910; exec. engr., Dec., 1924; offg. divl. supt., April, 1929; offg. dep. ch. engr., Oct., 1932; ditto (provl. perm.), Apl., 1936; confd., Aug., 1937.

MARRIOTT, ROBIN GEORGE, B.A., Indian Forest Service (consvr.) (b. 3rd Feb., 1886).—Joined the service, 11th Dec., 1911, as asst. consvr.; dep. consvr., Dec., 1916; services placed at disposal of Railway Board, Simla, from April, 1925, to Oct., 1927; offg. consvr. variously from Feb., 1932; confd., Sept., 1936.

MARRIS, SIR WILLIAM SINCLAIR, K.C.S.I., K.C.I.E., B.A., late Indian C.S. (b. 9th Oct., 1873).—Educ. at Canterbury Coll., New Zealand, and Christ Church, Oxford; apptd. after exam. of 1895; arrived in India, 18th Dec., 1896, and served in the N.W. Prov. and Oudh as asst. mag. and collr.; under-sec. to govt., Oct., 1899, to July, 1901; under-sec. to govt. of India, Home dept., Nov., 1901; dep. ditto, Nov., 1904; lent to govt. of the Transvaal, Oct., 1906, to 1908; dep. commr., May, 1911; C.I.E., Jan., 1914; mag. and collr., May, 1914; temply. employed in judl. and public dept., India Office, July, 1915, and from Sept. to Dec., 1915; insp.-gen. of police (temp.), United Provs., April, 1916; jt. sec. (temp.), home dept., govt. of India, Oct., 1917; on deputn. to England with the sec. of state for India, April to Oct., 1918; K.C.I.E., Jan., 1919; sec. to govt. of India, home dept., April, 1919; on spl. duty as reforms commr., 1919; K.C.S.I., Jan., 1921; govt. of Assam, 3rd March, 1921; govt. of United Provs., 24th Dec., 1922; member of Council of India, May, 1928—Sept., 1929; author of a summary of Lord Curzon's administration in the Home dept., 1899-1905, and of the report of the commn. of inquiry into the organization and making of the Central South African Rlys., 1907-8 (in part).

MARSDEN, EUSTACE HOWARD, Capt., R.I.M., ret'd., late Marine dept., Madras (b. 27th Oct., 1882).—Apptd. offg. presdy. port officer, 19th Feb., 1929; confd., Apl., 1929; prinpl. port officer, Madras dist., May, 1930; ret'd., Apl., 1933.

MARSDEN, PERCY, B.A., Barr., Indian C.S. (dep. commr., Punjab), (b. 26th Nov., 1888).—Educ. at Cheltenham, and St. John's Coll., Oxford; apptd. after exam. of 1911; arrived, 23rd Nov., 1912, and served in Punjab as asst. commr.; personal asst. to pol. agent, Phulkian States, Dec., 1914; sec., municipal cttee., Amritsar, March, 1935, to May, 1917; on mil. duty from July, 1918, to April, 1919; dep. commr. (provl.), July, 1925; offg. sec. to govt. trans. depts., Punjab, Dec., 1930; confd., Oct., 1932; offg. commr., Oct., 1935.

MARSH, PERCY WILLIAM, C.I.E., B.A., Indian C.S. (commr., United Provs.) (b. 14th Oct., 1881).—Educ. at Wellington, and Wadham Coll., Oxford; apptd. after exam. of 1904; arrived, 26th Nov., 1905, and served in the United Provs. as asst. mag. and collr. and asst. commr.; jt. mag., Oct., 1914; temply. employed under War Office from April, 1916, to Dec., 1918; mag. and collr., Sept., 1922; C.I.E., June, 1929; offg. commr., Mar., 1931; commr. (provl. subs.), May, 1933; confd., Nov., 1934.

MARSH, WILFRED, Indian Police (b. 20th July, 1906).—Joined the service as asst. dist. supt., Burma, 25th Nov., 1925; offg. dist. supt., Apl., 1934.

MARSH-SMITH, REGINALD NORMAN, Indian Police (supt., United Provs.) (b. 24th Aug., 1891).—Joined the service as asst. supt., Nov., 1911; asst. dep. insp.-gen., C.I.D., Sept., 1920; supt., Aug., 1923; awarded King's police medal, Jan., 1926; insp.-genl., Gwalior Darbar, May, 1936.

MARSHALL, FREDERICK, B.Sc., Indian Service of Engrs. (suptg. engr., Burma) (b. 14th Nov., 1889).—Joined the service as asst. engr., 1st Oct., 1913; on mil. service from 19th Aug., 1916, to 21st Nov., 1919; exec. engr., Oct., 1922; offg. suptg. engr., Mar., 1931; ditto (provl. subs.), May, 1933; confd., July, 1933.

MARSHALL, GEORGE WARD, Indian State Rlys. (manager, coke plant colliery dept.) (b. 5th Sept., 1883).—Apptd. manager, coke plant colliery dept., E.I. rly., 7th Nov., 1911.

MARSHALL, JOHN BROWN, C.I.E., M.A., B.Sc., late Indian C.S. (b. 18th Sept., 1884).—Educ. at Perth Academy and Edinburgh Univ.; apptd. after exam. of 1907; arrived, 23rd Nov., 1908, and served in Burma as asst. commr.; on spl. duty, offg. registrar co-operative societies; C.I.E., June, 1920; dep. commr., March, 1925; offg. excise commr., Dec., 1925; confd., April, 1927; ret'd., Mar., 1934.

MARSHALL, SIR JOHN HUBERT, Kt., C.I.E., M.A., Litt.D., F.S.A., Hon. A.R.I.B.A. (b. 19th Mar., 1876).—Educ. at Dulwich Coll., and King's Coll., Cambridge (Porson prize), Prendergast and Craven fellowships; student of British Sch. at Athens, 1898-99; apptd. dir.-gen. of archaeology, 22nd Feb., 1902; C.I.E., June, 1910; knighted, Jan., 1915; on spl. duty, Sept., 1928; ret'd., Mar., 1931; offcr. on spl. duty, Mar., 1931, to Sept., 1936.

MARSHALL, Rev. NORMAN EDWYN, M.A., Indian Eccles. Estabt. (senr. chaplain) (b. 31st Mar., 1887).—Joined the service as junr. chaplain, Punjab, 2nd Jan., 1915; senr. chaplain, 6th June, 1927.

MARSLAND, HAROLD, late Indian Service of Engrs. (b. 26th Aug., 1881).—Trained in R.I.E. Coll.; joined the service, 1st Oct., 1904; arrived Nov., 1904; exec. engr., Oct., 1912; on mil. duty from April to Sept., 1918; offg. suptg. engr., July, 1924; ret'd., Jan., 1935.

MARTEN, SIR ALFRED AMBERSON BARRINGTON, Kt., M.A., LL.D., (Barr., 1895) (b. 8th Dec., 1870).—Educ. at Eton and Trinity Coll., Cambridge; apptd. judge, high court, Bombay, Dec., 1916; Kt., Jan., 1924; chief justice, Bombay high court, June, 1926; ret'd., June, 1930.

MARTEN, ERIC CHARLES, Indian C.S. (dist. and sess. judge, Punjab) (b. 14th Feb., 1899).—Educ. at Uppingham Sch. and R.M.C., Sandhurst; joined the service, 17th Sept., 1922; arrived, 21st Nov., 1923, and served in the Punjab as asst. commr.; judge, small cause court, Simla, April, 1927; offg. dist. and sess. judge, June, 1930; Oct., 1932, and again May, 1933; addl. ditto, Sept., 1933; confd. as dist. and sess. judge, Oct., 1935.

MARTEN, FRANK RICHARD, O.B.E., late India Office (b. 9th Oct., 1872).—Solicitor to the Supreme Court, 1895; apptd. clerk, legal adviser's dept., 13th Aug., 1900; asst. solicitor, Dec., 1913; apptd. a commr. for oaths, July, 1916; O.B.E., Jan., 1931; ret'd., Aug., 1933.

MARTIN, ARCHDALL CHERRY, Indian Police (swpt., Madras) (b. 1st Apl., 1893).—Joined the service, 15th Nov., 1913; asst. supt., May, 1915; offg. supt., May, 1921; confd., Sept., 1929.

MARTIN, CHARLES DE CARTERET, M.B., Lt.-Col., I.M.S. (civil surg.) (b. 3rd Jan., 1888).—First commn. dated 1st Aug., 1914; in milty. employ prior to July, 1925; asst. dir., Pasteur inst., July, 1925; offg. asst. dir., central research institute, Kasauli, Jan., 1929; pathologist, genl. hosp., Rangoon, June, 1930; civil surgeon (tempy.), May, 1933.

MARTIN, FREDERICK GEORGE STEPHEN, M.C., Indian State Rlys. (contr. of stores, E.I. Rly.) (b. 26th Aug., 1890).—Joined the service as asst. works manager, G.I.P. Rly., 14th Dec., 1923; offg. works manager, May, 1925; offg. dep. ch. mech. engr., Feb., 1926; on spl. duty under Railway Board, 19th March, 1927; dep. ch. mechl. engr., E.I. Rly., Feb., 1928; offg. contr. of stores, Sept., 1930; confd., Oct., 1931.

MARTIN, The Ven. FREDERICK WALTER, M.A., late Indian Eccles. Estabt. (Central Provs.) (b. 7th July, 1874).—Apptd. chaplain on probation, 15th Oct., 1903, as chaplain on Bengal eccles. estab.; junr. chaplain, Dec., 1905; senr. chaplain, Dec., 1915; archdeacon, Jan., 1919; retd., Sept., 1928.

MARTIN, The Ven. FREDERICK WILLIAM, M.A., Eccles. Dept., Central Provs. (archdeacon) (b. 16th Jan., 1886).—Apptd. chaplain on probation, 10th April, 1918; junr. chaplain, May, 1919; senr., chaplain, April, 1930; archdeacon, Sept., 1932.

MARTIN, GEOFFREY NOEL, late Indian C.S. (b. 11th Dec., 1893).—Educ. at Magdalen Coll., Oxford; joined the service, 5th Mar., 1921; arrived, 20th Apl., 1921, and served in Burma as asst. commr.; and sub-divl. officer, Dec., 1921; offg. dep. commr., Nov., 1925; offg. dep. secty., fin. dep., Burma, Aug., 1929; confd., May, 1931; secty. to govt., Burma, forest dept., Apl., 1934; retd., Apl., 1937.

MARTIN, GEORGE WILLIAM PERCY, India Office (b. 16th Dec., 1877).—Educ. at Alleyn's Sch., Dulwich; apptd. clerk, 2nd class, 30th Jan., 1899, and attached to the registry and record dept.; minor staff clerk, Dec., 1911; transferred to the finl. dept., Jan., 1917; staff clerk (temp.), Aug., 1918; sec. to Indian delegation to internl. fin. conference, Brussels, 1920; administrative officer, April, 1922; administrative officer for currency duties, Apl., 1933, retd., Dec., 1937.

MARTIN, HARRY WALTER, Office of High Commr. for India (dep. ch. accountg. officer) (b. 26th May, 1884).—Apptd. 2nd class clerk, acctnt.-gen.'s dept., 13th Sept., 1902; minor staff clerk, Oct., 1919; staff clerk (actg.), April, 1920; services lent to high commr. for India from April, 1921; higher exec. officer, 1st April, 1922; suptg. exec. officer, 21st April, 1922; asst. ch. acctg. officer, Nov., 1934; dep. ditto, Nov., 1937.

MARTIN, JAMES REA, C.I.E., B.A., Barr.—late Indian C.S. (Bombay) (b. 2nd Aug., 1877).—Educ. at Methodist Coll., and Queen's Coll., Belfast; apptd. after exam. of 1900; arrived, 29th Nov., 1901, and served in Bombay and Sind as asst. collr. and mag.; manager, Sind encumbered estates, Nov., 1909; dep. commr., Upper Sind Frontier, Sept., 1912; temp. dep. sec. to govt., Bombay, rev. and finl. depts., Dec., 1916; on spl. duty in sectt., Jan. to Sept., 1917; compiled genl. admn. report, 1916-17; collr., Feb., 1921; C.I.E., June, 1923; dep. director of development, 1923; offg. director of development, July, 1924; member of council of state, 1924; member of legisl. council, 1924; sec. to govt., pol. dept., Aug., 1926; chief sec. to ditto, Jan., 1927; tempy. member of exec. council, Bombay, June, 1928; member legisl. council, 1929; retd., Jan., 1932; member, Indian States enquiry comte., Jan., 1932.

MARTIN, OLAUS MACLEOD, C.I.E., Indian C.S. (secty. to govt., rev. dept., Bengal) (b. 1st Feb., 1890).—Educ. at Oban High School and Edinburgh Univ; apptd. after exam. of 1912; arrived, 10th Dec., 1913; and served in Bengal as asst. mag. and collr.; I.A.R.O., Aug., 1916, to March, 1919; employed in Assam from Aug., 1920; offg. dist. and sess. judge, Aug., 1920; offg. mag. and collr., Oct., 1923; offg. labour intell. offr., Bengal, Nov., 1925; addl. dept. secty. to govt. of Bengal, pol. and appt. depts., Nov., 1925; magte. and collr., Aug., 1927; dep. commr., Apl., 1930; offg. secty. to govt., rev. dept., Bengal, Dec., 1933; confd., May, 1934; C.I.E., Feb., 1937.

MARTIN, OSMOND KENNETH, late Income Tax dept., Bengal (b. 15th Apl., 1878).—Joined the service, 12th July, 1909, and held non-gazetted appt. prior to 1st Apl., 1922; inc. tax officer, Apl., 1922; asst. commr., Mar., 1929; retd., Apl., 1933.

MARTIN, REGINALD VICTOR, C.I.E., M.R.C.S., L.R.C.P., Lt.-Col., I.M.S. (inspr.-genl. prisons, Bombay) (b. 20th Mar., 1889).—First commn. dated 26th July, 1913; in milty. employ to July, 1924; supt. of prison, Mar., 1927; offg. inspr.-genl. of prisons, Apl., 1931; confd., Sept., 1937; C.I.E., Jan., 1938.

MARTINDALE, Sir ARTHUR HENRY TEMPLE, K.C.S.I., late Indian C.S. (Madras).—Educ. at Cheltenham Coll.; apptd. after exam. of 1873; arrived, 10th Nov., 1875; served in Madras as asst. collr. and mag., and in the foreign dept. of the govt. of India as attaché, and asst. and junr. under sec. to govt.; offg. asst. to rest. in Mysore and sec. to the chief commr. of Coorg, Feb., 1885; offg. rest., May-June, 1885; first asst. and sec. for Berar to the rest., Hyderabad, Nov., 1886; pol. agent, Quetta and Pishin, April, 1887; 1st asst. to govr.-gen.'s agent, Central India, Oct., 1888; pol. agent, Bhopal, July, 1890, and Jhalawar, Jan., 1892; on spl. duty in connection with the revision of Aitchison's "Treaties," Jan.-Apl., 1892; commr., Ajmer-Merwara, May, 1894; acting rest., W. states of Rajputana, June, 1895; agent to govr.-genl., Rajputana, and chief commr., Ajmer-Merwara, Mar., 1898; C.S.I., May, 1900; K.C.S.I., June, 1904; retd., Oct., 1905.

MARTINDALE, Ven. HENRY, M.A. (Oxon), late Indian Eccles. Estabt. (b. 22nd Mar., 1879).—Apptd. chaplain on probation, 13th April, 1915; junr. chaplain, May, 1916; senr. chaplain, May, 1926; offg. archdeacon, June, 1927; confd., Sept., 1928; retd., Oct., 1934.

MARTYN, PHILIP DOCTON, B.A. (Man.), Indian C.S. (jt. mag. and dep. collr., Bengal) (b. 7th April, 1904).—Educ. at Stockport Grammar School, Victoria Univ., Manchester, and Exeter College, Oxford; joined the service, 25th Oct., 1927; arrived, Dec., 1927, and served in Bengal as asst. mag. and collr.; offg. ch. manager, Dacca Nawab estate, Jan., 1933; offg. mag. and collr., July, 1934.

MARVIN, EDITH ANNA, M.B.E., B.A. (London), India Office (staff officer for establ. duties) (b. 20th Apr., 1893).—Educ. at Notting Hill High Sch. and Bedford Coll.; apptd. clerical officer, 11th July, 1921; higher clerical officer (acting), April, 1922; confd., Dec., 1924; staff officer for establ. duties (temp.), June, 1933; ditto (perm.), June, 1936; M.B.E., Jan., 1935.

MARWOOD, SIDNEY LIONEL, B.A. (Oxon), Indian C.S. (magte. and collr., Bihar) (b. 8th April, 1891).—Educ. at St. Pauls' Sch., and Hertford Coll., Oxford; apptd. after exam. of 1914; arrived, 15th Nov., 1915, and posted to Bihar and Orissa; on mil. duty with the I.A.R.O., Nov., 1915, to Oct., 1919; offg. mag. and collr., Nov., 1924; offg. dep. commr., Nov., 1925; offg. pol. agent, Orissa feudatory states, Oct., 1926; offg. magte. and collr., April, 1920; confd. (prov. sub.), Nov., 1929; confd., Feb., 1931; commr. of inc. tax, Nov., 1931; Ki-H. medal, 1st cl., June, 1934.

MASCARENHAS, WILLIAM ZAVIAR, B.E., Indian Service of Engrs. (exec. engr., Bombay)—Joined the service as asst. exec. engr., Bombay, 22nd Dec., 1926; offg. exec. engr., Apr., 1932; confd., Oct., 1935; dep. secty. to govt., Bombay (roads and bldgs., P.W.D.), Dec., 1935, to Dec., 1937.

MASIH, SAIYID MUHAMMAD (see Saiyid Muhammad Masih).

MASON, Rev. CHARLES DOUGLAS THOMAS, M.A. (Edin.), A.K.C., late Indian Eccles. Estabt. (b. 26th Feb., 1883).—Apptd. chaplain on probation, 16th Dec., 1910; junr. chaplain, Dec., 1913; senr. chaplain, Dec., 1923; retd., Sept., 1934.

MASON, JOHN EMLYN VICTOR, late Indian Police (b. 31st Dec., 1902).—Joined the service as asst. supt., Bombay, 25th Nov., 1922; offg. dep. commr. of police, Bombay, April, 1928; retd., Apr., 1933.

MASON, KENNETH, M.C., Major R.E., retd., late Survey of India (b. 10th Sept., 1887).—First commn. dated 29th Dec., 1906; apptd. asst. supt., trigl. survey, 29th Aug., 1909; temp. reverted to mil. duty from Oct., 1914, to March, 1919; dep. supt., June, 1923; supt. Jan., 1926; awarded Founder's Medal. R.G.S., March, 1927; asst. surveyor-genl., Oct., 1927; retd., Mar., 1934.

MASON, LAWRENCE, C.I.E., O.B.E., M.C., B.A., Indian Forest Service (consr., United Provs.) (b. 27th Aug., 1886).—Joined the service 18th Dec., 1910, as asst. consr.; dep. consr., Dec., 1915; on mil. service, Oct., 1914, to Oct.,

1919; ch. forest officer, Andamans, April, 1928 to Dec., 1929; C.I.E., June, 1930; offg. consvr. Central Provs., Apr., 1933; ditto, United Provs., Apr., 1934; confd., Aug., 1934.

MASON, PHILIP, M.A., Indian C.S. (commr., United Provs.) (b. 30th Nov., 1882).—Educ. at Norwich Sch., and Queen's Coll., Oxford; apptd. after exam. of 1906; arrived, 9th Dec., 1907, and served in United Provs. as asst. mag. and collr.; under-sec. to govt., Oct., 1914; jt. mag. Oct., 1916; mag. and collr., May, 1923; secty. to govt., local self govt. and pub. health depts., Nov., 1931; commr. (prov. subs.), Nov., 1936.

MASON, PHILIP, Indian C.S. (jt. mag., United Provs.) (b. 19th March, 1906).—Educ. at Sedburgh and Balliol College, Oxford; joined the service, 9th Oct., 1928; arrived, Dec., 1928, and served in the United Provs. as asst. collr. and mag.; under-secty. to govt. of India defence dept., Nov., 1933; offg. dep. secty., defence dept., Mar., 1936; offg. dep. commr., Sept., 1936.

MASSON, JAMES, M.B., Ch.B., M.A., B.Sc., Lt.-Col., I.M.S. (retd.), late Medical Dept., Bihar and Orissa.—First commn., 29th Jan., 1901; in mil. employ till June, 1903; civil surgeon, Sept., 1903; in mil. employ from June, 1915, to March, 1921; retd., Nov., 1928.

MASTER, ALFRED, C.I.E., B.A., late Indian C.S. (b. 12th Feb., 1883).—Educ. at Epsom Coll., and Brasenose Coll., Oxford; apptd. after exam. of 1905; arrived, 10th Dec., 1906, and served in Bombay as asst. collr. and mag., and asst. settlt. offr.; munpl. commr., Ahmedabad, Aug., 1917; on mil. duty, Sept., 1918, to Aug., 1919; settlement officer, Nov., 1922; jt. sec. to govt., gen. educ. and marine depts., July, 1925; offg. sec. to ditto, Sept., 1925; member of legisl. council, Oct., 1925, and again, Oct., 1932; offg. collr. and dist. mag. and pol. agent, April, 1926; confd., Oct., 1926; C.I.E., Jan., 1931; retd., Nov., 1934.

MASTER, JAL ARDESHIR, B.Sc., Indian Forest Service (dep. consvr., Madras) (b. 23rd Aug., 1892).—Joined the service as asst. consvr., 23rd Nov., 1915; dep. consvr., Nov., 1920; offg. consvr., Mar., 1936.

MASTER, NADIRSHAH NASARVANJI MERVENJI, B.A., LL.B., late Bombay Civil Service, Judl. Branch (b. 3rd Dec., 1879).—Joined the service, 5th Aug., 1904; offg. judge and sess. judge, June, 1928; confd., Dec., 1930; retd., Dec., 1934.

MASTERMAN, CHRISTOPHER HUGHES, Indian C.S. (secty. to govt., educn. and public health depts., Madras) (b. 7th Oct., 1889).—Educ. at Winchester and Trinity Coll., Oxford; apptd. after exam. of 1913; arrived, 12th Dec., 1914, and served in Madras as asst. collr. and mag.; on mil. duty from July, 1917, to Dec., 1919; sub-collr., March, 1922; collr. of salt revenue, April, 1924; offg. collr. and dist. mag., July, 1932; ditto (prov. subs.), July, 1933; confd., Feb., 1935; secty. to govt., educn. and public health depts., Madras, Aug., 1936.

MASWOOD, MUHAMMAD ADBUL HAI, B.Sc., Indian Police (b. 1st Oct., 1912).—Apptd. asst. supt., Bengal, on probn., 1st Mar., 1935; confd., 1937.

MATHER, WILLIAM McMENZIE, O.B.E., Office of High Commr. for India (estabt. officer) (b. 12th Mar., 1885).—Apptd. temp. pte. secty. to High Commr., Oct., 1920; confd., Apl., 1927; O.B.E., Feb., 1937; suptg. exec. offer., accts. dept., Nov., 1937; estabt. officer, Apl., 1938.

MATHESON, Rev. ROBERTUS WILLIAM, M.A., Indian Eccles. Estabt., Church of Scotland (junr. chaplain) (b. 21st Apl., 1904).—Apptd. chaplain on probn., 20th Jan., 1934; junr. chaplain, Feb., 1936.

MATHEW, PUTHENVEETIL CHANDAPILLA, B.A., Indian C.S. (asst. mag. and collr., Madras) (b. 30th June, 1913).—Educ. at Church Missionary Society High Sch., Mavelikara, Madras Univ., and Univ. College, London; joined the service, 16th Sept., 1937; arrived in India, Oct., 1937, and served in Madras as asst. mag. and collr.

MATHIAS, ALFRED ERNEST, C.I.E., B.A., late Indian C.S. (b. 24th Jan., 1880).—Educ. at Clifton Coll., and Oriel Coll., Oxford; apptd. after exam. of 1903; arrived, 28th Nov., 1904, and served in the Central Provs. as asst. commr. and settl. offr.; under-sec. to chief commr., Oct., 1908 to Nov., 1909; on spl. duty under regr., co-operative credit soc., from Dec., 1915; offg. regr., co-operative credit soc., April, 1917; dep. commr., Nov., 1917; fin. sec. to govt. of Central Provs., Nov., 1921; offg. chief sec. to govt. of Central Provs., March, 1923; fin. sec. to govt. of Central Provs., Nov., 1925; member of tariff board, June, 1926; offg. president, tariff board, April, 1927; confd., Mar., 1930; commr. C.P., Feb., 1930; C.I.E., Jan., 1932; retd., Aug., 1933.

MATHRA DAS, DIWAN BAHADUR, Indian State Rlys. (dir., rly. bd.) (b. 13th Nov., 1887).—Joined the service as asst. traffic supt., 9th July, 1910; dist. traffic supt., Oct., 1923; offg. dep. agent, Sept., 1931; dir. rly. bd., Feb., 1933.

MATHRANI, HARBHAGWANDAS PRIBHDAS, B.Sc., Indian Service of Engrs. (exec. engr.) (b. 12th July, 1901).—Joined the service, 1st Oct., 1923; offg. exec. engr., April, 1927; confd., Oct., 1932.

MATHRANI, KEWALRAM PRIBHDAS, B.Sc., Indian C.S. (asst. collr., Bombay) (b. 21st Aug., 1911).—Educ. at Government High Sch., Shikarpur, Edinburgh University and School of Oriental Studies, London; joined the service, 21st Sept., 1936; arrived in India, Nov., 1936, and served in Bombay as asst. collr.

MATHRANI, MITHARAM PRIBHDAS, O.B.E., B.E., Indian Service of Engrs. (exec. engr., Sind) (b. 10th Nov., 1895).—Joined the provl. service as apprentice engr., 3rd Aug., 1918; asst. engr. (provl. subst.), Aug., 1919; asst. exec. engr., Aug., 1919; offg. exec. engr., 13th Dec., 1923; confd., Aug., 1928; O.B.E., Jan., 1933.

MATHUR, DHATRI SARAN, Indian C.S. (asst. mag. and collr., United Provs.) (b. 13th Nov., 1912).—Educ. at Government Intermediate Coll., Etawah, Agra University, Thomason Civil Engineering College and Balliol Coll., Oxford; joined the service, 16th Sept., 1936; arrived in India, Oct., 1936, and served in the United Provinces as asst. mag. and collr.

MATHUR, GOBIND SARUP, BABU (see Gobind, S. M.).

MATHUR, KALKA PRASAD, M.R.C.S., L.R.C.P., D.P.H., Public Health Dept., United Provs. (dir. of public health) (b. 15th Nov., 1884).—Joined the service as health officer, 1st Mar., 1914; asst. dir. of public health, Nov., 1921; dir., ditto, Nov., 1936.

MATHUR, KRISHNA BEHARI LALL, Indian State Rlys. (exec. engr., E.I. Rly.) (b. 24th May, 1889).—Joined the service as asst. engr., 30th Sept., 1913; asst. exec. engr., July, 1922; exec. engr., Oct., 1922; offg. dep. ch. engr., June, 1935.

MATHUR, MAHESANDRA SHANKAR, B.Sc., LL.B., Indian Police (asst. supt.) (b. 17th Oct., 1906).—Joined the service as proxy. asst. supt., United Provs., 5th Jan., 1931; confd., Mar., 1933.

MATHUR, MANMOHAN SARUP, Indian Service of Engrs. (asst. exec. engr., Central p.w.d.) (b. 15th July, 1898).—Joined the service, 29th Oct., 1923; asst. exec. engr., Oct., 1924; offg. exec. engr., Mar., 1932; transf. to central p.w.d., Aug., 1933.

MATHUR, PRAHLAD NARAYAN, P.M.S. Civil Surgeon, United Provs. (b. 1st Jan., 1886).—Civil surgeon, May, 1927.

MATSON, HORACE SYDNEY, M.B., Lt.-Col., I.M.S. (ret'd.), late Burma Medl. Dept. (b. 18th Oct., 1879).—First commn. dated 1st Sept., 1904; spl. plague officer, Jan., 1910; offg. civil surg., April, 1910; on mil. service from 23rd Oct., 1914, to 28th April, 1922; civil surg., June, 1922; ret'd., Dec., 1931.

MATTHAL, GEORGE, M.A. (Cantab.) Indian Educl. Service (b. 13th Nov., 1887).—Apptd. prof. of zoology, govt. coll., Lahore, 16th Sept., 1918.

MATTHAI, JACOB, Indian Service of Engrs. (exec. engr., Madras) (b. 3rd Mar., 1884).—Joined the service, 17th Feb., 1916; asst. engr., March, 1918; asst. exec. engr., Jan., 1920; offg. exec. engr., July, 1925; confd., March, 1927.

MATTHAI, JOHN, C.I.E., B.A., B.L., D.Sc., B.Litt., Indian Educl. Service (dir.-genl. of comml. intel. and statistics) (b. 10th Jan., 1886).—Apptd. to the I.E.S. as prof., presdy. coll., Madras, 3rd April, 1922; member, Indian Tariff Board, 22nd June, 1925; offg. president, ditto, Apl., 1931; confd., Dec., 1931; C.I.E., June, 1934; dir.-genl. of comml. intel. and statistics. Jan., 1935.

MATTHEW, KANIANTHRA THOMAS, B.Sc., Indian Forest Service (dep. consvr., Madras) (b. 12th Dec., 1899).—Joined the service, 15th Sept., 1924; dep. consvr., Sept., 1929.

MATTHEWMAN, THOMAS HENRY, M.I.E.E. (b. 10th Oct., 1883).—Apptd. prof. of electricity, Maclagan engrg. coll., Lahore, 17th Aug., 1924.

MATTHEWS, HARRY, M.B.E., late Indian Police (b. 22nd Feb., 1882).—Joined the service, 23rd Jan., 1901; M.B.E., June, 1919; asst. supt., Feb., 1923; supt., April, 1926; retd., Sept., 1934.

MATTHEWS, VICTOR GEORGE, B.Sc., Indian C.S. (asst. commr.) (b. 3rd Mar., 1907).—Educ. at Latymer Secondary Sch., Edmonton, University College, London, and Clare College, Cambridge; apptd. after

examination of 1930; arrived in India, Nov., 1931, and served in the Central Provinces as asst. commr.; under-secty. to govt., Central Provs., Aug., 1935; and again, Aug., 1936; pte. secty. to govt., Central Provs., Mar., 1936; under-secty., dept. of e., h., and l., govt. of India, Oct., 1937.

MATTHEWS, WILLIAM, M.B.E., India Office (higher executive officer, acctnt.-gen'l's dept.) (b. 23rd Sept., 1878).—Apptd. clerk, 2nd class, 2nd Jan., 1899; minor staff clerk, July, 1919; staff clerk (acting), Jan., 1920; confd., April, 1921; higher exec. officer, April, 1922; also assessor of income tax; M.B.E., May, 1937.

MAUDE, SIR WALTER, K.C.I.E., C.S.I., late Indian C.S.—Educ. at Highgate Sch., and Balliol Coll., Oxford; apptd. after exam. of 1881; arrived, 17th Nov., 1883, and served in Bengal as asst. mag. and collr.; under sec. to govt. of Bengal, gen., rev., and statistical depts., Aug., 1890; settlt. officer, Orissa, Nov., 1892; joint mag. and dep. collr., March, 1893; mag. and collr., July, 1895; dep. commr., Oct., 1901; commr., Aug. 1905; transfed. to Bihar and Orissa as commr., April, 1912; member bd. of rev., Nov., 1912; temp. member, exec. council, Nov., 1913, to May, 1914; C.S.I., June, 1914; addl. member, govr.-gen.'s council, Dec., 1914; again temp. memb. of exec. council, Bihar and Orissa, Feb., 1916; member, ditto, April, 1917; K.C.I.E., Jan., 1920; presdt., Bihar and Orissa exec. council, 1921; ret'd., May., 1921.

MAUGER, WALTER KYLE, Indian Police (supt.) (b. 1st Dec., 1902).—Joined the service as asst. supt., Punjab, 25th Nov., 1922; offg. supt., Mar., 1929, and again, Apl., 1933; confd., Nov., 1934.

MAUNG, MAUNG HLA, B.A., Indian C.S. (asst. commr., Burma) (b. 11th Feb., 1911).—Educated at Government High School, Akyab, Rangoon University and Jesus College, Oxford; joined the service, 5th Sept., 1935; arrived in India, Oct., 1935, and served in Burma as asst. commr.

MAUNG, MAUNG, SAN, B.Sc., Indian C.S. (asst. commr., Burma) (b. 26th April, 1904).—Educ. at University of Rangoon and Queen's Coll., Oxford; joined the service, 9th Oct., 1929; arrived, Nov., 1929, and served in Burma as asst. commr.; dep. regr., high ct., Rangoon, Sept., 1933; offg. dist. and sess. judge, Apl., 1936.

MAUNG SET, C.I.E., B.A., Indian Finance Dept. (b. 24th Nov., 1884).—Apptd. to dept., 25th April, 1910; asst. acctnt.-gen., Burma, May, 1910; dep. ditto, Mar., 1927; on foreign service under municipal corporation of Rangoon, Jan., 1929; C.I.E., May, 1937.

MAUNG, U. AYE, B.A., Indian Service of Engrs. (suptg. engr., Burma) (b. 26th Dec., 1891).—Joined the service as asst. engr., 1st Oct., 1914; asst. exec. engr., June, 1920; exec. engr., Oct., 1923; offg. suptg. engr., Jan., 1935; confd., Feb., 1937.

MAUNG, U. AYE (2), B.Sc., Indian Service of Engrs. (exec. engr., Burma) (b. 31st May, 1896).—Joined the service, Oct., 1919; asst. engr., Dec., 1919; asst. exec. engr., March, 1921; exec. engr., Oct., 1928.

MAUNG, U. BA (C.5), B.A., Indian Police (b. 9th Nov., 1907).—Joined the service as asst. dist. supt., Burma, 27th May, 1929.

MAUNG, U. CHIT (A2), B.A., Burma Commn. (secty. to govt., judl. dept., Burma) (b. 25th Mar., 1895).—Joined the service, 22nd Jan., 1917; extra asst. commr., June, 1921; dep. commr., Mar., 1934; secty. to govt., judl. dept., Burma, Mar., 1936.

MAUNG, U. KIN, B.A., Indian Police (b. 23rd Feb., 1911).—Apptd. asst. supt., Burma, on probn., 7th Feb., 1935; confd., Feb., 1937.

MAUNG, U. MAUNG, B.A., Indian C.S. (dist. and sess. judge) (b. 19th Aug., 1899).—Educ. at Govt. High Sch., Rangoon, Calcutta Univ., and Queen's Coll., Oxford; joined the service on 25th Oct., 1924, and served in Burma as an asst. commr.; under-sec. to govt., local govt., dept., May, 1927; judge, sub-divl. courts, June, 1930; addl. dist. and sess. judge, May, 1932; dist. and sess. judge, June, 1934.

MAUNG, U. MAUNG (B.7), Barr. Burma Judl. Dept. (dist. and sess. judge) (b. 5th Oct., 1884).—Previous service from 7th April, 1906; dist. and sess. judge, Dec., 1922.

MAUNG, ZAW WIN, Burma Police (Class 1).—Apptd. asst. supt., Burma, on probn., Dec., 1937.

MAW, WILLIAM NEWTON, C.I.E., B.A., late Indian C.S. (Central Provs.).—Educ. at Wesley Coll., Sheffield, and St. John's Coll., Cambridge; apptd. after exam. of 1892; arrived, 31st Dec., 1893, and served in the Central Provs. as asst. commr.; dep. commr., Mar., 1902; 2nd sec. to chief commr., Feb., 1906; 3rd sec., Nov., 1909, to Nov., 1910, and from July, 1912, to Feb., 1913; C.I.E., June, 1916; commr., Nov., 1920; ret'd., Dec., 1928.

MAXTED, DENIS SYDNEY, Indian Police (b. 2nd Aug., 1912).—Joined the service as prob. asst. supt., Bengal, Nov., 1932; confd., Mar., 1935.

MAXWELL, REGINALD MAITLAND, C.S.I., C.I.E., M.A., Indian C.S. (secty., home dept., Govt. of India) (b. 24th Aug., 1882).—Educ. at Marlborough and Corpus Christi Coll., Oxford; apptd. after exam. of 1905; arrived, 26th Nov., 1906, and served in Bombay as asst. collr. and mag. and asst. settlt. offr.; compiler of genl. admn. report, 1908-9; on deputn. to Madras to study working of salt and excise depts., August, to October, 1910; offg. collr. of salt rev., Bombay, Nov., 1916; dep. commr. of salt and excise, Bombay, Sept., 1917; pte. sec. to H.E. the Govr., Sept., 1920; spl. duty, Bombay sect., Feb., 1921; sec., retrenchment cttee., Sept., 1921; C.I.E., June, 1923; offg. collr. and mag. and pol. agent, Jan., 1924; confd., Oct., 1926; offg. secty. to govt., genl., &c., depts., June, 1928; pte. secty. to govt. of Bombay, Mar., 1929; offg. secty. to govt. of Bombay, genl. and educ. depts., Oct., 1931; secty. to govt. of Bombay, home and ecclesi. depts., Apl., 1932; C.S.I., June, 1933; secty., home dept., Govt. of India, Oct., 1936; member, Council of State, Oct., 1936; commr., Dec., 1936; temp. member, govr.-gen'l's. exec. council, June, 1937; secty. home dept., Govt. of India, Oct., 1937.

MAXWELL, Sir WILLIAM, K.C.I.E., M.V.O., late Indian C.S.—Educ. at Belfast Methodist Coll., Royal Univ. of Ireland, and Trinity Coll., Dublin; apptd. after exam. of 1889; arrived, 3rd Dec., 1891, and served in Bengal as asst. mag. and collr.; offg. mag. and collr., April, 1897; postmr.-gen., Bombay, May, 1902; ditto, and dep. dir.-gen. of Post Office of India, March, 1903; postmr.-gen., Punjab and N.W.F., Oct., 1905; on dep. to tel. cttee., Sept., 1906; postmr.-gen., Punjab and N.W.F., April, 1907; ditto, and dep. dir.-gen. of Post Office of India, Feb. 1908; offg. dir.-gen. of Post Office of India, March, 1908; also presdt. of cttee. to examine working of new arrangement of hours of duty in tel. dep., April and May, 1908; sub. pro tem. sec. to govt. of India, fin. dep., Oct., 1908; offg. dir.-gen. of Post Office of India, Nov., 1908; C.I.E., Jan., 1909; postmr.-gen., Bombay, July, 1909; offg. sec. to govt. of India, dept. of comm. and ind., Dec., 1909; on special duty in the dept. of comm. and ind., March, 1910; sub. pro tem. sec. to govt. of India, dept. of comm. and ind., March to Nov., 1910; addl. memb. of gov.-gen.'s legisl. council, Jan. to Nov., 1910; postmr.-gen., Punjab and N.W.F., Dec., 1910; M.V.O., Dec., 1911; on special duty to assist dir.-gen. of posts and telegraphs in the administration of post office and telegraph dept., March, 1912; addl. member of gov.-gen.'s legisl. council from June, 1912; offg. dir.-gen. of posts and telegraphs, Feb., 1913; dir.-gen. of posts and telegraphs, Aug., 1913; K.C.I.E., June, 1915; ret'd., Sept., 1918.

MAY, FRANK THOMAS, India Office (higher executive officer, acctt.-gen'l's. dept.) (b. 4th Nov., 1890).—Educ. at King Edward VI's Sch., Guildford; apptd. second class clerk, India Office, 6th Dec., 1911; posted to rev. and statistics dept., 1914; on mil. service Nov., 1916, to April, 1919; apptd. minor staff officer, Ministry of Transport, Nov., 1919; retransfd. to India Office, June, 1921, and attached to finl. dept.; transferred to acct.-gen'l's. dept., May, 1922; higher executive officer, April, 1926; att'd. to pol. dept., May, 1936 to Dec., 1937; assessor of inc. tax, Dec., 1937.

MAY, PAUL ROBERT BUCHAN, B.A., Indian C.S. (dist. and sess. judge, Punjab) (b. 19th May, 1905).—Educ. at Westminster and Christ Church Coll., Oxford; joined the service, 16th Oct., 1928; arrived, Nov., 1928, and served in the Punjab as asst. commr.; offg. dist. and sess. judge, Feb., 1932; addl. ditto, Oct., 1932; dist. and sess. judge, Nov., 1936.

MAYA DAS, C. P. (see Das, C. P. M.).

MAYERS, STANLEY RAY, late Indian Police (b. 27th Oct., 1882).—Joined the service, 22nd Nov., 1902, and posted to United Provs.; asst. supt. of police, Jan., 1907; ditto, Port Blair, Feb., 1914; supt., Port Blair, Oct., 1914; ditto, Ajmer-Merwara, April, 1916, to Oct., 1918; ret'd., June, 1935.

MAYES, WILLIAM, C.I.E., F.C.H., late Indian Forest Service (b. 1st Mar., 1874).—Joined the service, 18th Nov., 1895, as asst. consvr. of forests; dep. consvr., Sept., 1901; personal asst. to consvr. of forests, Punjab, March, 1929, to March, 1911; consvr., Western circle, Feb., 1920;

consvr., Burma, April, 1920; offg. chief consvr. and presdt., Forest Research Institute, Dehra Dun, April, 1923; chief consvr., Punjab, Sept., 1924; C.I.E., March, 1929; ret'd., Feb., 1929.

MAYNARD, REV. BERTRAM MARTIN, late Indian Eccles. Estabt. (b. 10th Feb., 1881).—Apptd. chaplain on probation, 19th Oct., 1914; junr. chaplain, Oct., 1917; senr. chaplain, Oct., 1927; ret'd., June, 1935.

MAYNARD, Sir HERBERT JOHN, K.C.I.E., C.S.I., M.A., late Indian C.S.—Educ. at Merchant Taylors' Sch. and St. John's Coll., Oxford; apptd. after exam. of 1883; arrived, 4th Dec., 1886, and served in the Punjab as asst. commr.; under sec., rev. dept., Nov., 1891, to May, 1894; junr. sec. to govt., Dec., 1896; dep. commr., Jan., 1899; judl. and gen. sec. to govt., Jan. to Oct., 1899; commr. of excise, inspr.-gen. of registrn., supt. of stamps, &c., Nov., 1903, to Nov., 1906; commr., Nov., 1910; addl. member. gov.-gen.'s council, Jan. to April, 1915; C.S.I., June, 1915; offg. finl. commr., Punjab, June, 1916; confd. as finl. commr., July, 1916; K.C.I.E., June, 1920; member of gov.'s exec. council, Punjab, Jan., 1921; ret'd., Jan., 1927.

MAZUMDAR, AJIT KUMAR, B.Sc., Indian Police (b. 13th Sept., 1911).—Joined the service as asst. supt. of police, Bengal on probn., 12th Mar., 1933.

MAZUMDAR, BIDHAN PRASHAD, L.M.S., D.P.H., D.T.M., Public Health Dept., Bihar (asst. dir.) (b. 22nd Oct., 1887).—Joined the service, 15th Mar., 1915; asst. dir. of public health, May, 1925.

MAZUMDAR, KHAGENDRA NATH, M.A., Barr. (first asst. sec. to Govt. of Bengal, legisl. dept.).—Joined the service as asst. sec., Bengal legisl. council, 1st May, 1919; 2nd asst. sec., legisl. dept., May, 1924; offg. 1st ditto, April to Nov., 1930; confd., Sept., 1932; offg. secty., ditto, Nov., 1935.

MAZUMDAR, NALINI NATH, O.B.E., Rai Bahadur. Indian Police (supt., Bengal) (b. 1st July, 1884).—Joined the service, 1st May, 1905; asst. commr., Feb., 1925; apptd. to Indian Police as supt., Apl., 1933; O.B.E., Jan., 1938.

MAZUMDAR, SUDHENDRA NATH, B.Sc., Indian C.S. (dep. secty. govt., Orissa) (b. 9th Nov., 1896).—Educ. at Canning Coll., Lucknow, and Peterhouse, Cambridge; joined the service, 12th Dec., 1921; arrived, 13th Jan., 1922, and served in Bihar and Orissa as asst. mag. and collr.; under-secty., fin. dept., offg. dep. commr., Mar., 1932; dept. secty. to govt., Apl., 1936.

MEADE, GEORGE ALFRED, V.D., late Indian State Rlys. (Eastern Bengal) (b. 29th Dec., 1873).—Joined the service as asst. storekeeper, 25th Feb., 1903; storekeeper, April, 1911; dep. contr., Aug., 1917; dist. contr., June, 1919; contr. of stores, Sept., 1923; ret'd., Apl., 1929.

MEAKINS, HAROLD WHAYMAN, Indian State Rlys. (dist. traffic mgr., E.B. Rly.) (b. 9th Oct., 1887).—Joined the service as asst. traffic supt., on 18th July, 1908; dist. ditto, April, 1918; offg. dep. traffic manager, E.B. rly., variously from Oct., 1932; confd., Jan., 1937.

MEARES, JOHN WILLOUGHBY, C.I.E., M.I.C.E., M.I.E.E., F.R.A.S., late India Public Works Dept.—Educ. at Winchester, and Univ. Coll., London; electrical engr. and inspr.

of electricity to the govt. of Bengal from 1st April, 1898; electrical adviser to the govt. of India, April, 1905; C.I.E., Jan., 1922; retd., Dec., 1924; author of "The Law Relating to Electrical Energy in India," 3rd ed., 1930; and "Electrical Engineering Practice, 1923-1930."

MEARNS, WILLIAM ANDERSON, M.B., Ch.B., D.P.H., Lt.-Col., I.M.S., retd. (b. 15th Nov., 1879).—First commn. dated 1st Feb., 1906; in mil. employ to 21st Sept., 1913, and again from 26th Oct., 1914, to 31st Oct., 1920; dep. sany. commr., Nov., 1920; asst. dir. of public health, Nov., 1921; dir., Nov., 1932; retd., Nov., 1934.

MEARS, Sir EDWARD GRIMWOOD, K.C.I.E., Barr (b. 21st Jan., 1869).—Apptd. chief justice, Allahabad high court, 27th Oct., 1919; Kt., 1917; K.C.I.E., Jan., 1928; retd., March, 1932.

MEASURES, PHILIP HERBERT JOSEPH, C.B.E., Indian Police (supt., United Provs.) (b. 2nd Jan., 1893).—Joined the service, Nov., 1913, as asst. supt.; supt., March, 1925; C.B.E., Jan., 1931.

MEEHAN, ROBERT LYS WILLIAM, V.D., Indian State Rlys. (dep. ch. mech. engr., E.I. Rly.) (b. 30th Apr., 1889).—Joined the service as asst. loco. supt., 3rd Jan., 1914; dist. loco. supt., Dec., 1922; supt., mech. records, Apr., 1925; pers. asst. to dep. ch. mech. engr., Dec., 1926; offg. dep. ch. mech. engr., June, 1929; confd. May, 1931; offg. ch. mech. engr., E.I. rly., Jan., 1936.

MEEK, ARTHUR STANLEY, C.M.G., Lt.-Col., I.A., Indian Pol. Service (b. 7th Oct., 1883).—Served in mil. dept. until 16th Oct., 1907, when apptd. asst. pol. agent and asst. resdt., Aden; transf. to Kathiawar, Sept., 1911; pol. agent, Gohilvad Prant, Jan., 1912; on mil. duty from Dec., 1914, to April, 1917; on spl. duty under orders of pol. resdt., Persian Gulf, from Dec., 1914, to May, 1919; C.M.G., Dec., 1919; pol. agent, Kathiawar, Apr., 1921; pol. agent, Mahikantha, Nov., 1924; pol. agent, W. K. States, May, 1928; on foreign service in Jaipur state, Mar., 1929; pol. agent, Southern States of C.I., Oct., 1929; pol. agent in Bundelkhand and Baghelkhand, Mar., 1932; resdt., and A.G.G., Eastern States, Apr., 1934; confd., Oct., 1935.

MEEK, SIR DAVID BURNETT, C.I.E., O.B.E., M.A., D.Sc., Indian Educl. Service (Indian trade commr.) (b. 10th Mar., 1885).—Joined the service, 11th Feb., 1911, as prof., Dacca Coll.; asst. contr. of munitions, govt. of India, Oct., 1918, to April, 1919; dir. of industries, Bengal, Jan., 1920; O.B.E., June, 1924; dir.-gen. of comml. intell., April, 1926; C.I.E., June, 1933; Indian trade commr., Jan., 1935; representative of the Govt. of India to Commonwealth Statistical Conf., Ottawa, Sept., 1935; Knighted, May, 1937.

MEGAW, Sir JOHN WALLACE DICK, K.C.I.E., M.B., B.Ch., B.A., K.H.P., Major-Genl., I.M.S., retd. (presdt., medical bd., India Office) (b. 8th Feb., 1874).—First commn. dated 27th Jan., 1900; in mil. employ to June, 1903, when he was apptd. to officiate as civil surgeon in Bengal; first resdt. surg., Presdy. Genl. Hospl., Nov., 1909; civil surg., Bihar and Orissa, April, 1912; prof. of pathology, King George's Med. Coll., Lucknow, Oct., 1914; temp. princpl. (in addn.), Sept., 1916; dir. and prof., Sch. of

Tropical Medicine, Calcutta, Nov., 1921; C.I.E., Jan., 1926; inspr.-genl. of civil hosps., Punjab, Feb., 1928; surg.-genl. with govt. of Madras, Sept., 1928; dir.-genl., I.M.S., Mar., 1930; K.C.I.E., Jan., 1933; retd., Nov., 1933; presdt., medl. bd., India Office, Nov., 1933.

MEGGITT, FREDERICK JOSEPH, M.Sc., Ph.D., Indian Educl. Service (b. 10th Aug., 1890).—Joined the service as lecturer in biological science, Univ. Coll., Rangoon, 5th Feb., 1923.

MEHER, MANEKSHA RUSTAMJI, M.A., Indian C.S. (dist. and sess. judge, Bombay) (b. 10th Dec., 1903).—Educ. at Univ. of Bombay and School of Oriental Studies, London; joined the service, 15th Oct., 1928; arrived, Nov., 1928, and served in Bombay as asst. coll.; offg. dist. and sess. judge, Oct., 1935; confd., Aug., 1936.

MEHTA, Sir CHUNILAL VIJBHUKHANDAS, K.C.S.I., M.A., LL.B.—Apptd. member of govr.'s exec. council, 22nd June, 1923; Kt., June, 1925; K.C.S.I., Jan., 1928; retd., Sept., 1928.

MEHTA, DADIBA CURSETJEE, M.A., LL.B., Bombay Civil Service, Judl. Branch (addl. judl. commr. of Sind) (b. 27th Nov., 1878).—Held various judicial appts. from 22nd Oct., 1918, to 3rd June, 1924; judge and sess. judge, 4th June, 1924; offg. judl. commr. of Sind, June, 1931; addl. ditto, Apr., 1934.

MEHTA, DESRAJ, B.A., Indian Service of Engrs. (exec. engr., Bihar) (b. 21st Nov., 1902).—Joined the service as asst. exec. engr., Bihar and Orissa, 6th Nov., 1925; offg. exec. engr., Feb., 1933; confd., Oct., 1935.

MEHTA, GUR SARAN DAS, Rai Bahadur, Indian Audit Dept. (b. Oct., 1883).—Joined the service 19th Dec., 1904. asst. dir. of Army Audit, N.C., June, 1935; asst. dir. of Audit Defence Service, W.C., May, 1936.

MEHTA, INDRAVADAN NARAYANBHAI, B.A., LL.B., Barr., Bombay Judicial Dept. (presdy. mag.) (b. 20th Jan., 1886).—Joined the dept., 18th Feb., 1925; 6th presdy. mag., Feb., 1925; 5th ditto, March, 1925.

MEHTA, KANHAIYA LAL, B.A., Indian C.S. (asst. mag. and collr. U.P.) (b. 15th Mar., 1913).—Educ. at Maharana's Inter. Coll., Udaipur, Agra Univ., and London School of Economics; joined the service 23rd Sept., 1937; arrived in India, Oct., 1937, and served in U.P. as asst. mag. and collr.

MEHTA, MAGANLAL GHELABHAI, B.A., LL.B., late Bombay Judicial Dept. (b. 1st Mar., 1875).—Held non-gazetted appts. from 1st Oct., 1904, to 21st Jan., 1908; sub-judge, Jan., 1908; retd., Sept., 1930.

MEHTA, NANALAL CHAMANLAL, B.A., Barr., Indian C.S. (secty., Impl. council of agricl. research) (b. 17th Sept., 1891).—Educ. at Bombay, Cambridge and London; apptd. after exam. of 1914; arrived, 2nd Dec., 1915 and served in the United Provs. as asst. mag. and collr.; jt. mag., May, 1918; dir. of co-operative societies, Gwalior State, Jan., 1920; offg. mag. and collr., July, 1924; confd., Dec., 1931; offg. dep. commr., Nov., 1924; dir. of land records and inspr.-genl. of registrn., July, 1930; secty., Impl. council of agricl. research, Aug., 1935; offg. vice-chairman, ditto, Aug.-Dec., 1936.

MEHTA, PERSHOTAM LAL, Indian Police (asst. supt.) (b. 1st Mar., 1907).—Apptd. proby. asst. supt., Bengal, 18th Jan., 1930; confd., Mar., 1932.

MEHTA, SHIV CHARAN DAS, RAI BAHADUR, late Indian Police (b. 1st Aug., 1881).—Held non-gazetted appt. from 14th Nov., 1907, to 24th June, 1910; dep. supt., June, 1910; supt., May, 1921; ret'd., Aug., 1936.

MEHTA, SHREENATH, Indian C.S. (asst. commr., Central Provs.) (b. 28th Oct., 1904).—Educ. at University of Allahabad; University of Nagpur; and School of Oriental Studies, London; joined the service, 28th Sept., 1928; arrived, 25th Oct., 1928, and served in the Central Provs. as asst. commr.

MEHTA, SHRIMUKHRAO LAXMILAL, B.A., Indian C.S. (dir. of industries, Assam) (b. 1st Nov., 1892).—Educ. at Bombay and Clare Coll., Cambridge; apptd. to the I.C.S. by nomination, 15th Oct., 1917; arrived in India, 21st Dec., 1917, and served in Assam as asst. commr.; offg. dep. commr., Nov., 1929; again, Jan., 1930; dir. of industries, Assam, Feb., 1935.

MEHTA, VINAYAK NANDSHANKAR, R.A., Barr., Indian C.S. (commr., United Provs.) (b. 3rd June, 1883).—Educ. at Govt. High Sch., Surat, Bombay Univ., and King's Coll., Cambridge; apptd. after exam. of 1905; arrived, 2nd Dec., 1906, and served in the United Provs. as asst. mag. and collr.; joint mag., March, 1915; dir. of industries, Dec., 1920; dep. commr., Nov., 1923; offg. sec. to govt., educ. and industries depts., April, 1925; confd., Nov., 1928; offg. commr., June, 1931; minister, rev. and dev., Kashmir darbar, Aug., 1932; commr., United Provs., Apl., 1935; prime minister, Bikaner State, Dec., 1936.

MEIKLE, HENRY GEORGE WATSON, F.F.A. (b. 4th May, 1870).—Apptd. actuary to govt. of India, 23rd Dec., 1912; ret'd. May, 1930.

MEIKLEJOHN, WILLIAM, B.Sc., Indian Forest Service (consvr.) (b. 5th Dec., 1886).—Joined the service as asst. consvr., 23rd Dec., 1910; in mil. employ from 1st Jan., 1918, to 14th April, 1919; dep. consvr., Oct., 1920; offg. consvr., Apl., 1930; transf. N.W.F.P. as offg. consvr., Dec., 1930; ditto, United Provs., Nov., 1934; ditto, Bengal, Apl., 1935; consvr., Aug., 1935.

MELDRUM, ANDREW NORMAN, D.Sc., late Indian Educl. Service (Bombay) (b. 19th Mar., 1876).—Apptd. prof. of physics and chemistry, M.R. Science Inst., Ahmedabad, 3rd April, 1912; prof. of chem., Royal Inst. of Science, Bombay, Nov., 1922; offg. prin., Royal Institute of Science, Bombay, Oct., 1925; confd., Feb., 1927; ret'd., Sept., 1931; author of 'Avogadro and Dalton, the standing in Chemistry of their Hypothesis,' 'Papers in Translations of Chemical Society,' 'Chemical News,' etc.

MELDRUM, JAMES WHITE, M.A., Indian C.S. (asst. commr., Central Provinces) (b. 12th April, 1908).—Educ. at Ayr Academy, Edinburgh University and Balliol College, Oxford; apptd. after examination of 1930; arrived in India, Nov., 1931, and served in the Central Provinces as asst. comr.; under secty. to govt., Central Provs., Jan., 1935.

MELHUISH, HERBERT MICHAEL HENRY, D.S.O., D.P.H., Lt.-Col., I.M.S. (ret'd.) (b. 22nd Sept., 1876).—First commn., 1st Sept., 1902; in mil. employ generally until June, 1908; on plague duty until Aug., 1914; on mil. duty from Aug., 1914, to April, 1920; dep. sany. commr., Sind Regr. Dist., April, 1920; asst. dir. of pub. health, Aug., 1922; dir. vaccine inst., Belgaum, Oct., 1925; offg. dir. public health for govt. of Bombay, Feb., 1926; confd., Nov. 1926; ret'd., Sept., 1929.

MELLOR, RAYMOND GEORGE, Indian Police (asst. supt.) (b. 6th Apl., 1912).—Joined the service as proby. asst. supt., Punjab, 19th Nov., 1931; confd., Nov., 1933.

MENESSE, NORMAN HAROLD, O.B.E., Indian Service of Engrs. (exec. engr.) (b. 20th Sept., 1902).—Joined the service as asst. exec. engr., Bombay, 1st Oct., 1925; awarded Kaisar-i-Hind medal, 2nd class, June, 1928; offg. exec. engr., Oct., 1929; confd., Sept., 1934; in for. service as Admin. Shikarpur Municipality, Nov., 1934, to Mar., 1937; O.B.E., May, 1937.

MENON, ATCHUTH MADHAVA, B.A., LL.B., A.S.A.A., Office of High Commr. for India (higher exec. officer, accts. dept.) (b. 10th July, 1892).—Apptd. to office of high commr. for India, 15th Nov., 1920; higher clerical officer, April, 1922; higher exec. officer (temp.), May, 1925; confd., June, 1927.

MENON, A. V. GOVINDA (see Govinda Menon, A. V.).

MENON, KIZHAKKEPAT RAMUNNI, Indian C.S. (sub. collr. and jl. mag., Madras) (b. 2nd June, 1896).—Educ. at Madras Univ. and St. John's Coll., Cambridge; joined the service, 26th Oct., 1920; arrived, 27th Dec., 1920; and served in Madras as asst. collr. and mag.; sub-collr. and jt. mag., July, 1922; offg. under-sec. to govt., law dept., Nov., 1924; under-sec., govt. of India, home dept., April, 1927; addl. dep. secty., govt. of India, educ., etc., dept., Oct., 1930, and again, June, 1934; secty., public dept., office of H.C. for India, Apl., 1931, to Mar., 1934; adviser and secty. to govt. of India delegation to internatl. labour conf., Geneva, 1933.

MENON, KIZHAKKEPAT SANKARA (see Sankara Menon, K.).

MENON, KOMATTIL GOVINDA, M.Sc., B.A., Indian C.S. (sub-collr., Madras) (b. 19th Sept., 1909).—Educ. at B.M. High School, Palghat, Madras University, University College, London, and the School of Oriental Studies, London; joined the service, 2nd Oct., 1933; arrived in India, Nov., 1933, and served in Madras as asst. mag. and collr.; sub-collr., Jan., 1936.

MENON, KUMAR PADMA SIVASANKARA (see Sivasankara Menon, K. P.).

MENON, MANAKAT KRISHNA, Indian Police (asst. supt.) (b. 14th Dec., 1906).—Joined the service as proby. asst. supt., Madras, Jan., 1931; confd., Jan., 1933.

MENON, MULLASSERI MADHAVA, Local fund audit dept., Burma (b. 8th July, 1888).—Apptd. asst. examr., 19th Mar., 1927.
