MENON, PARAKAT ACHUTHA, Indian C.S. (sub-collr., Madras) (b. 2nd Jan., 1905).—Educ. at University of Madras and New Coll., Oxford; joined the service, 9th October, 1929; arrived, November, 1929, and served in Madras as asst. mag. and collr.; sub-collr., May, 1931.

MENON, PARAKAT MADHAVA, Indian C.S. (under secty. to govt. (p.w.d.), Madras) (b. 7th Jan., 1906).—Educ. at Madras University and New College, Oxford; joined the service, 11th Oct., 1930; arrived in India, Nov., 1930, and served in Madras as asst. mag. and collr.; sub-collr., Feb., 1932; under secty. to govt. (p.w.d.), Madras, Jan., 1937.

MENON, THARAVANAT ACHUTHA, M.A., Indian C.S. (asst. mag. and collr., Bengal) (b. 4th Jan., 1911).—Educ. at the School of Oriental Studies, London; joined the service, 23rd Nov., 1936; arrived in India, Dec., 1936, and served in Bengal as asst. mag. and collr.

MENON, TOTTAKAT NARAYARA, B.A., Office of the High Commr. for India (exec. officer, accts. dept.) (b. 6th Oct., 1896).—Apptd. to office of High Commr., for India, Aug., 1924; actg. exec. officer, April, 1926; confd., Oct., 1930.

MENON, VADAIKE KURAPATH ROMUNI, B.A., Indian C.S. (jt. magte. and dep. collr., Bihar) (b. 29th Aug., 1903).—Educ. at Univ. of Madras and Christ's College, Cambridge; joined the service, 19th Oct., 1926; arrived 22nd Nov., 1926, and served in Bihar and Orissa as asst. mag. and collr.; under-secty. to govt., fin. dept., B. and O., Oct., 1931; offg. secty. to govt., ditto, July, 1933; offg. mag. and collr., Apl., 1934; offg. secty. to govt., local self govt., dept., Apl., 1936.

MENON, VAPAL PANGUNNI, Rao Bahadur. (Dep. Secty. to the Govt. of India, Reforms Office) (b. 30th Sept., 1894).—Joined the service, 8th Sept., 1914; asst. secty., Govt. of India, Reforms Office, Jan., 1933; under secty., ditto, Sept., 1934; dep. secty., ditto, Sept., 1936.

MERCER, CHARLES VICTOR, late Indian State Rlys. (b. 10th Feb., 1887).—Joined the service as supt. of catering and advertising, Burma Rlys., 13th Mar., 1922; ret'd., Mar., 1935.

MERCHANT, FRAMROZ RUSTOMJI, F.S.A.A., Income Tax Dept., Bombay (asst. commr. of Income Tax) (b. 12th Nov., 1888).—Joined the service, 1st July, 1922, as income tax officer, Bombay; offg. asst. commr. of income tax, July, 1928; confd., Oct., 1929; offg. commr. of income tax, Dec., 1932, and again, May, 1934.

MEREDITH, HERBERT RIBTON, B.A., Indian C.S. (dist. and sess. judge, Bihar) (b. 8th April, 1890).—Educ. at Trinity Coll., Dublin; apptd. after exam. of 1913; arrived, 17th Dec., 1914, and served in Bihar and Orissa as asst. mag. and collr.; jt. mag., Sept., 1922; offg. dist. and sessions judge, Jan., 1923; addl. ditto, April, 1925; offg. judl. commr., Mar., 1929; dist. and sess. judge, Mar., 1931; offg. regr., high ct., Patna, Apl., 1932; offg. secty. to govt., judl. dept., Bihar and Orissa, Apl., 1934; regr., high ct., Patna, Aug., 1934.

MERRICK, HENRY WYBRANTS, late Indian Police Service. —Joined the service, 22nd Nov., 1893, and posted to Central Provs.; dist. supt., April, 1900; ret'd., Aug., 1928.

MERRIMAN, JOHN LONGSHAW, B.A., Indian C.S. (mag. and collr., Bihar) (b. 26th Dec., 1886).—Educ. at Marlborough, Worcester Coll., Oxford, and Univ. Coll., London; apptd. after exam. of 1910; arrived in India, 18th Nov., 1911, and served in Bengal as asst. mag. and collr.; transfed. to Bihar and Orissa, April,

1912; jt. mag. and dep. collr., Nov., 1919; mag. and collr., Feb., 1926; manager, Bettiah Wards estate, Nov., 1929; dep. commr., Ranchi, Oct., 1935.

MERRINGTON, HERBERT FAGENT, Indian Service of Engrs. (exec. engr.) (b. 5th May, 1904). —Joined the service as asst. exec. engr., Punjab, 17th Oct., 1927; exec. engr., Oct., 1936.

MERSH, CHARLES MELMOTH BAILEY (elec. adviser to govt., Central Provs.) (b. 25th Oct., 1884).—Apptd. electrical adviser to govt., 27th Sept., 1920.

MESTON, OF AGRA AND DUNOTTAR, Baron, JAMES SCORGIE MESTON, K.C.S.I., LL.D., V.D., late Indian C.S.—Educ. at Gr. Sch. and Univ. of Aberdeen, and at Balliol Coll., Oxford; apptd. after exam. of 1883; arrived, 23rd Nov., 1885, and served in the N.W. Prov. and Oudh as asst. mag. and collr., and asst. commr.; joint mag., May, 1890; settl. offr., April, 1892; dep. commr., Nov., 1896; dir. of land records and agriculture, April, 1897; sec. to govt., March, 1899, to June, 1903; mag. and coll., March, 1899; member, legisl. council, March, 1899, and March, 1901; lent to govt. of Cap of Good Hope from Jan., 1905; offg. sec. to govt. of India, fin. dept., July, 1906; joint sec. to govt. of India, fin. dept. (mil. fin. branch), April, 1907; C.S.I., June, 1908; temp. member of council of govt., fin. dept., July to Nov., 1908; sec. to govt. of India, fin. dept. (ord. branch), Nov., 1909; K.C.S.I., Dec., 1911; lt.-govr., United Provs., 15th Sept., 1912; on deputn. to assist the Secretary of State at the meetings of the Imperial War Cabinet, and member of the Imperial War Conference, Feb. to June, 1917; resigned office of lt.-govr., United Provs., 15th Feb., 1918; member of the Viceroy's council, in charge of finance dept., from 2nd Nov., 1918; ret'd., Nov., 1919; created Baron, Nov., 1919.

METCALFE, SIR HERBERT AUBREY FRANCIS, K.C.I.E., C.S.I., M.V.O., B.A., Indian C.S. Indian Political Service (b. 27th Sept., 1883). —Educ. at Charterhouse, and Christ Church, Oxford; apptd. after exam. of 1907; arrived, 24th Nov., 1908, and served in the Punjab as asst. commr.; personal asst. to pol. agent, Phulkian States and Bahawalpur, April, 1911; transfed. to Delhi as sec., munpl. cttee., Nov., 1912; asst. pte. sec. to Viceroy, March, 1914; and asst. sec. to the chief commr., N.-W. Frontier Prov. (temp.), Mar., 1917; city magte., Peshawar, May, 1917; asst. commr., Charsadda, Feb., 1919; pte. sec. to chief commr., N.-W. Frontier Prov., May, 1919; sec. to ditto, Dec., 1920; asst. commr., Thal, April, 1922; M.V.O., June, 1922; counsellor, Kabul legation Nov., 1924; pol. agent. Dir, Swat and Chitral, Nov., 1925; sec. to ch. commr. and agent to gov.-genl. in N.W.F.P., Mar., 1927; C.I.E., June, 1928; dep. commr., Peshawar, Nov., 1928; offg. dep. secty., for. and pol. dept., govt. of India, Nov., 1930; confd., May, 1931; offg. for. secty., ditto, May, 1932; confd., Jan., 1933; C.S.I., June, 1933; K.C.I.E., Jan., 1936; secty. external affairs dept., Govt. of India, Apl., 1937.

MEYER, ERNEST ALEXANDER, late Survey of India (b. 11th Oct., 1880).—Joined the service, 1st Feb., 1902; extra asst. supt., Aug., 1909; supt., May, 1921; ret'd., Oct., 1935.

MEYER, P. M. ROSE (see Rose Meyer, P. M.)

MEYLER, Rev. EDWARD MOWBRAY, B.A., late Indian Eccles. Estabt. (United Provs.) (b. 26th Nov., 1876).—Apptd. chaplain on probation, 26th Nov., 1908; junr. chaplain, Nov., 1911; senr. chaplain, Nov., 1921; ret'd., Jan., 1930.

MIAN, MUHAMMAD TASDIQUE HUSSAIN, M.Sc. (Punj.), Indian Forest Service (dep. consvr., Central Provs.) (b. 1st June, 1905).—Joined the service as asst. consvr., Central Provs., 5th Nov., 1929; dep. consvr., Nov., 1934.

MIDDLETON, ARTHUR PURLING, B.A., Indian C.S. (commr., Bihar) (b. 14th Oct., 1884).—Educ. at Eton, and Magdalen Coll., Oxford; apptd. after exam. of 1908; arrived, 25th Nov., 1909, and served in Bengal as asst. mag. and collr.; transf. to Bihar and Orissa, April, 1912; under-sec. to govt., rev. and judl. depts., Feb., 1916; jt. mag. and dep. collr., June, 1918; mag. and collr., Oct., 1922; commr. of income tax, Bihar and Orissa, April, 1923; offg. commr., Apl., 1929; and again, Jan., 1932; confd., Jan., 1933.

MIDDLETON, LEONARD, B.A., Indian C.S. (b. 13th March, 1884).—Educ. at Dulwich Coll., and Pembroke Coll., Cambridge; apptd. after exam. of 1907; arrived, 30th Nov., 1908, and served in the Punjab as asst. commr.; personal asst. to pol. agent, Phulkian States, Feb., 1914; guardian to H.H. the Raja of Faridkot, Dec., 1914; settl. offr., Aug., 1915, to Feb., 1919; supt. of census operations, Punjab, March, 1920; dist. and sess. judge (prov.), Feb., 1924; confd., April, 1924; settl. officer, Kashmir State, May, 1924; dist. and sess. judge, Delhi, Dec., 1928, to Jan., 1931; services placed at disposal of govt. of India, pol. dept., Nov., 1931; judl. commr., N.W.F.P., Mar., 1933, to Apl., 1937.

MIDDLETON, PERCIVAL A. H., late Indian State Rlys.—Employed as temp. engr. from 15th April, 1905, to 31st Dec., 1919; resdt. engr., Jan., 1920; ret'd., Nov., 1928.

MIDDLETON, WILLIAM EDWARD, B.A., Indian Police (asst. supt.) (b. 2nd Oct., 1907).—Joined the service as prob. asst. supt., Madras, 5th Mar., 1929; confd., Aug., 1931; offg. dist. supt., June, 1934.

MIDDLETON-WEST, STEPHEN HAROLD, M.C., M.B., Lt.-Col., I.M.S. (ret'd.), late Burma Medl. Dept.—First commn. dated 1st Feb., 1908; offg. civil surg., Feb., 1923; ret'd., Aug., 1928.

MIDGLEY, ERIC ATKINSON, B.A., Indian C.S. (asst. mag. and collr., U.P.) (b. 25th March, 1913); educ. at Christ's Hospital, Horsham, and Merton Coll., Oxford; joined the service 15th Sept., 1937; arrived in India, Oct., 1937, and served in the U.P. as asst. mag. and collr.

MIEVILLE, Sir ERIC CHARLES, K.C.I.E., C.S.I., C.M.G. (b. 31st Jan., 1896).—Apptd. addl. pte. secty. to Viceroy, 17th Apl., 1931; pte. secty. to Viceroy, 22nd May, 1931 to Apl., 1936; C.M.G., Jan., 1930; C.S.I., June, 1933; secty. to govr.-gen'l's exec. council, Nov., 1935; K.C.I.E., Jan., 1936; pte. secty. to the Duke of York, July, 1936.

MILLAR, GEORGE MCGREGOR, O.B.E., M.B., Lt.-Col., I.M.S., ret'd. (b. 12th Sept., 1880).—First commn. 1st Sept., 1906; O.B.E., Jan., 1919; in milty. employ to 12th Feb., 1922; offg.

legation surg., Kabul, Feb., 1922; agency surg., ditto, Oct., 1924; resdy. surg., Mewar, March, 1926; civil surg., Peshawar, Jan., 1927; C.M.O., N.W.F.P., March, 1927; civil surg., Ajmer and C.M.O., Rajputana, Apl., 1929; ret'd., Feb., 1933; K.-i-H. medal, 1st class, Feb., 1937.

MILLARD, CLIVE NEWCOME, B.A., Bombay Civil Service (dep. collr.) (b. 9th Aug., 1891).—Joined the service as dep. collr., 22nd Aug., 1916; offg. collr. and dist. magte., Apl., 1929.

MILLER, ALFRED, M.B., Lieut.-Col., I.M.S. (ret'd.) (b. 29th Apl., 1869).—First commn. dated 20th July, 1896; in mil. employ to Dec., 1901; held various offg. appts. in Madras to Oct., 1903, when he was apptd. dist. medl. and san. offr.; chemical examr., July, 1911, and prof. of chem., Madras Med. Coll.; examr. of medical stores, India store depot, Dec., 1920-May, 1932.

MILLER, ARTHUR CONGREVE, O.B.E., M.A., late Indian Educl. Service (b. 24th Jan., 1877).—Joined the service, 28th July, 1911; educl. inspr., March, 1922; O.B.E., June, 1924; offg. dep. dir. of public instrn., March, 1925; offg. inspr. of European schools, Bombay presdy., March, 1926; confd., Jan., 1927; educl. inspr. in Sind, Sept., 1929; prinpl., Raj Kumar coll., Rajkot, Nov., 1930; ret'd., Jan., 1932.

MILLER, HAROLD BELLETT, Indian Police (supt., Bengal) (b. 4th May, 1899).—Joined the service as asst. supt., Bengal, 28th Feb., 1921; offg. supt., rly. police, May, 1927; supt., Nov., 1931.

MILLER, Sir JOHN ONTARIO, K.C.S.I., late Indian C.S.—Educ. at King's Coll., Aberdeen; apptd. after exam. of 1877; arrived, 8th Dec., 1879, and served in the N.W. Prov. and Oudh as asst. commr., and asst. mag. and collr.; asst. sec. to govt., Jan., 1883; under sec., May, 1884; private sec. to lieut.-govr., 1886-87; settl. officer, Oct., 1888; dir. of land records and agric., March, 1892; dep. commr., Nov., 1895; mag. and collr., Dec., 1895; offd. as jud. sec. and as fin. sec. to govt. N.W.P. and O., from Aug., 1895; chief sec. to govt., Nov., 1898; C.S.I., Jan., 1901; sec. to govt. of India, rev. and agric. dept., Oct., 1902; private sec. to viceroy, Dec., 1903, to Nov., 1904; presdt., Indian Survey Committee, Nov., 1904; chief commr., Central Provs., May, 1905; member of the govr.-gen.'s council, P.W.D., March, 1907; ret'd., Sept., 1911; K.C.S.I., Jan., 1911; author of the Muzaffarnagar settl. report.

MILLER, Sir THOMAS FREDERIC DAWSON, Kt., K.C., Barr., late Judl. Dept., Bihar and Orissa.—Educ. at Durham Sch. and Trinity Coll., Oxford; called to the Bar (Inner Temple), 1891; K.C., 1912; apptd. chief justice, Patna high ct., Oct., 1917; Kt., Jan., 1918; ret'd., April, 1928.

MILLER-BROWNIE, THOMAS ARCHIBALD, C.E., M.I.W.E., M.I.M. and C.E., late Agric. Dept., Punjab (b. 24th June, 1879).—Joined the service as agric. engr., Feb., 1915; prin., Punjab agric. coll., May, 1925; agricl. engr. to govt. Punjab, July, 1929; ret'd., June, 1934.

MILLET, HUMPHREY JOHN CURNOW, B.A., Indian Forest Service (dep. consvr., Bombay) (b. 29th June, 1897).—Joined the service, 21st Jan., 1921, as asst. consvr.; dep. consvr., Jan., 1925.

MILLS, JAMES PHILIP, B.A., Indian C.S. (secty. to the govr., Assam) (b. 18th Feb., 1890).—Educ. at Winchester Coll., and Corpus Christi Coll., Oxford; apptd. after the exam. of 1912; arrived, 10th Dec., 1913, and served in Assam as asst. commr.; offg. dep. commr., Jan., 1924; and again, Mar., 1930; confd., Aug., 1932; officer on spl. duty, reforms officer, Nov., 1935; secty. to the govr., Apl., 1937.

MILLS, PERCY STRICKLAND, C.I.E., M.B., Col., I.M.S., Civil Med. Dept., Bihar (inspr.-genl. of civil hosp.) (b. 28th May, 1883).—First commn. dated 1st Feb., 1906; in mil. employ. to 6th June, 1911; plague medl. off., June, 1911; on mil. duty from Aug., 1914, to May, 1921; civil surg., March, 1922; offg. inspr.-genl. of civil hosp., Bihar and Orissa, May, 1935; confd., Aug., 1935; C.I.E., Jan., 1938.

MILLS, SEYMOUR HERBERT HATTEN, late Indian Police (b. 3rd Feb., 1894). Joined the service as asst. supt., Dec., 1914; awarded King's police medal, 1920; asst. to dir. of intell. bureau, home dept., govt. of India, Oct., 1925; dep. commr., Calcutta, Jan., 1927; on deputation to Nizam's govt., June, 1928, to Feb., 1930; ret'd., Feb., 1933.

MILNE, DAVID, C.I.E., B.Sc. (Aberd.), late Indian Agric. Service (b. 9th May, 1876). Joined the service as economic botanist, Punjab, 7th Nov., 1907; princpl., Punjab agric. coll., Feb., 1921; offg. dir. of agricl., Punjab, Oct., 1922; confd., May, 1925; C.I.E., Jan., 1927; offg. agric. expert to impl. council of agric. research, Apl., 1933; ret'd., May, 1933.

MILNE, ERIC IVAN, Indian State Railways (dist. traffic supt., Burma Rlys.) (b. 20th Mar., 1899).—Joined the service as asst. traffic supt., 2nd Jan., 1920; dist. traffic supt., Burma rlys., Dec., 1930; offg. dep. traffic manager, Aug., 1932.

MILNE, ROBERT BLAIR, M.A., late Indian C.S. (b. 7th March, 1882).—Educ. at Robert Gordon's Coll., and the Univ., Aberdeen; apptd. after exam. of 1904; arrived, 24th Nov., 1905, and served in Bombay and Sind as asst. collr. and asst. judge; offg. under-sec. to govt., pol., judl. and spl. depts., April to May, 1915; asst. remembr. of legal affairs, and ex-officio sec. to govr.'s legal dept. (temp.), May, 1919; offg. judge and sess. judge, Jan., 1923; offg. jt. sec., govt. of India, legisl. dept., Nov., 1924; dist. and sess. judge, June, 1925; offg. addl. judl. commr. of Sind, July, 1928; confd., Nov., 1930; ret'd., Apl., 1934.

MILNER, CHARLES EDWARD, Indian Forest Service (consvr., Burma) (b. 17th Sept., 1887).—Arrived, 18th Dec., 1909, and served as asst. commr.; dep. commr., Dec., 1914; on mil. service, May, 1915, to Feb., 1919; offg. consvr., April, 1926; confd., Sept., 1933.

MILNER-WHITE, RUDOLPH, M.A., late Indian C.S. (b. 13th Nov., 1885).—Educ. at Harrow Sch., and Pembroke Coll., Cambridge; apptd. after exam. of 1909; arrived in India, 28th Nov., 1910, and served in the United Provs. as asst. mag. and collr.; spl. manager, Awah estate, Aug., 1915, to Nov., 1917; chairman, improvement trust, Cawnpore, May, 1920; jt. mag., acting dep. commr., Jan., 1923; actg. dep. sec. to govt., U.P., Oct., 1923; mag. and collr. (provl. sub.), Aug., 1926; confd., Jan., 1927; ret'd., Oct., 1935.

MILSOM, ERNEST, B.A., late Indian C.S. (Bengal) (b. 5th April, 1885).—Educ. at Wakefield Gr. Sch., and Caius Coll., Cambridge; apptd. after exam. of 1908, arrived, 27th Nov., 1909, and served in Bengal as asst. mag. and collr.; under-sec. to govt. of Bengal, genl. dept., Nov., 1913; ditto, finl. dept., April, 1914; jt. mag. and dep. collr., Nov., 1915; dist. and sessions judge, March, 1923; ret'd., Feb., 1931.

MILTON, HENRY JOSEPH, Indian Police (supt., Bihar) (b. 21st Dec., 1901).—Joined the service as asst. supt., Bihar and Orissa, 28th Nov., 1922; offg. supt., Mar., 1930; confd., June, 1934.

MILTON, RALPH STANLEY, Indian Police (supt., Madras) (b. 13th Apl., 1889).—Joined the service, 23rd Nov., 1909; asst. supt., May, 1911; supt., July, 1922; comr. of police, Bangalore, Mar., 1935.

MIN, MAUNG KYAW, M.A., LL.B., Indian C.S. (secty. to govt., forest dept., Burma) (b. 15th Nov., 1899).—Educ. at St. Xavier's Coll., Calcutta, and Trinity Hall, Cambridge; joined the service, 12th Dec., 1923, and served in Burma as asst. commr.; under-sec. to govt., fin. dept., Oct., 1927; offg. dep. commr., May, 1931; dep. secty., educn. dept., Burma, June, 1936; secty., ditto, Oct., 1936; dep. secty. to govt., Burma, Dec., 1936; secty., forest dept., Burma, Jan., 1937.

MINCHIN, ALFRED ALYSON FENNELL, Indian Forest Service (consvr., Madras) (b. 25th Aug., 1887).—Joined the service as asst. consvr., Dec. 1908; dep. consvr., Jan. 1914; offg. consvr., May, 1925; confd., June, 1928.

MINHAJ-UD-DIN, KHAN BAHADUR, SHAIKH, B.A., A.M.I.E. (Ind.), Indian Service of Engrs. (exec. engr., Punjab) (b. 7th June, 1889).—Joined the service, 21st Sept., 1912; asst. engr., Sept., 1913; exec. engr., Oct., 1922; offg. suptg. engr., Oct., 1933.

MINISTER, CYRIL JOHN, Indian Police (supt., Bengal) (b. 31st Oct., 1897).—Joined the service as asst. supt., 15th March, 1921; offg. supt., June, 1924; confd., Sept., 1930.

MINNITT, G. C., A.M.I.C.E., M.I. Struct. E., Indian State Rlys. (sanitary engr., G.I.P. Rly.) (b. 10th May, 1887).—Joined the service as resdt. engr., G.I.P. Rly., 14th Mar., 1905; sanitary engr., May, 1932.

MINSHULL, WILLIAM TRAVERS, B.E., Indian Service of Engrs. (exec. engr., United Provs.) (b. 31st Dec., 1884).—Joined the service as asst. engr., 1st Oct., 1908; exec. engr., Oct., 1916; in mil. emp. from 5th Sept., 1918, to 23rd Jan., 1919; exec. engr., Feb., 1919; p.a. to chief engr. and under secty., I.B., Mar., 1934.

MINSON, HERBERT, M.B.E., B.A., late Indian C.S. (b. 14th Nov., 1886).—Educ. at City of London Sch., and Christ's Coll., Cambridge; apptd. after exam. of 1910; arrived in India, 25th Nov., 1911, and served in the United Prov. as asst. mag. and collr.; under-sec. to govt., Jan., 1919; M.B.E., June, 1919; jt. mag., July, 1920; services placed at disposal of govt. of India, fin. dept., from Oct., 1920; acctnt.-genl., Punjab, Aug.,

1921; ditto, Central Provs., Nov., 1921; services replaced at disposal of govt. of United Provs., Oct., 1923; actg. dep. commr., Oct., 1923; offg. mag. and collr., March, 1925; confd., May, 1928; retd., May, 1937.

MIR AMIR-UD-DIN, B.A., LL.B., Barr., Judicial dept., Madras (dist. and sess. judge) (b. 7th Dec., 1889).—Joined the service as offg. 2nd asst. registrar, high ct., origl. side, 20th Feb., 1917; confd., Apl., 1917; 1st asst. ditto, Apl., 1921; dep. registrar, appellate side, Aug., 1922; offg. dist. judge, Sept., 1926; dist. and sess. judge, Nov., 1928.

MIRAJKAR, VAMAN RAGHUNATH, O.B.E., F.R.C.S., M.R.C.P., Lt.-Col., I.M.S. (prof., King Edward medl. coll., Lahore) (b. 13th Oct., 1887).—First commn. dated 15th March, 1920; in milty. employ to 28th June, 1926; prof., King Edward Medl. Coll., Lahore, June, 1926; O.B.E., Nov., 1935.

MIRAJ-UD-DIN, KHAN BAHADUR, Mirza, Indian Police (supt. Punjab) (b. 8th Apr., 1884).—Joined the service, 14th Apl., 1906, as inspr. of police; apptd. to I.P. as supt., Sept., 1935; asst. to D.I.G., C.I.D., Sept., 1935.

MIRAMS, ARTHUR EDWARD, F.S.I., F.S.A.—(b. 1st June, 1875).—Apptd. consulting surveyor to govt., Bombay, 30th Jan., 1914; retd., June, 1930.

MIRCHANDANI, BHAGWANDAS DAYARAM, B.A., Indian C.S. (asst. coll., Bombay) (b. 25th Feb., 1906).—Educ. at University of Bombay and School of Oriental Studies, London; joined the service 11th Oct., 1929; arrived Nov., 1929, and served in Bombay as asst. coll.

MIRCHANDANI, TANUMAL SHEWAKRAM, B.Sc., Indian Service of Engrs. (exec. engr., Sind) (b. 3rd Mar., 1888).—Joined the service as asst. engr., 1st Oct., 1911; exec. engr., Oct., 1920; offg. suptg. engr., March, 1925; May, 1928, and June, 1933.

MIRCHANDANI, TOLARAM JETHANAND, M.Sc. (Lond.), A.M.I.E.E. (exec. engr.), Electricity Dept., Madras. (b. 1st Oct., 1896).—Engaged on contract as pers. asst. to the chief engr. for electricity from 1st Sept., 1931; confd., as exec. engr., Sept., 1933.

MIRCHANDANI, UDHARAM MANIRAM, Indian C.S. (collr., Sind) (b. 26th Sept., 1896).—Educ. at St. Patrick's High School, Karachi, and Trinity Coll., Oxford; joined the service, 14th Dec., 1921; arrived, 23rd Jan., 1922, and served in Bombay as asst. collr.; offg. collr. and dist. mag., Oct., 1927; offg. dep. sectv. to govt., finance dept., Bombay, Feb., 1932; collr., Mar., 1936.

MIR SAEED ALAM KHAN, NAWABZADA, Barr., Indian Political Service. (b. 28th Dec., 1892).—Joined the service, 15th Oct., 1923; asst. to A.G.G., Madras States, July, 1928; asst. commr., Peshawar, Nov., 1930; ditto, Bannu, Apl., 1931; offg. under-secty. to A.G.G., Punjab States, Apl., 1933; under secty. to A.G.G., States of W.I., Aug., 1934; addl. pol. agent, Sabar Kantha agency, Jan., 1936; under-secty. to A.G.G., states of W.I., May, 1936; guardian and comp. to heir apparent of H.H. the Maharaja Morvi, Nov., 1936.

MIRZA, SAHIBZADA HARUN QADIR SAIYID MUSA ALI, late Indian Service of Engrs. (b. 4th Nov., 1882).—Trained at R.I.E. Coll.; joined the service as asst. engr., 1st Oct., 1905; exec. engr., May, 1914; offg. suptg. engr., May, 1931; confd., Nov., 1934; retd., Nov., 1937.

MIRZA, SAHIBZADA SYED ISKANDAR ALI (see Syed Iskandar Ali Mirza, S.).

MIRZA, SYDNEY J., B.A., LL.B., Income Tax Dept., Bombay (asst. commr. of income tax, Sind) (b. 21st Apl., 1886).—Joined the service, 27th Nov., 1912; inc. tax officer, Feb., 1924; spl. inspecting officer, May, 1926; offg. asst. commr. of income tax, Sind, March, 1928; confd. Oct., 1929.

MISBAHUL OSMAN, SYED, KHAN BAHADUR, B.A., late Central Provs. C.S. (b. Nov., 1877).—Joined the service, 29th Jan., 1901; extra asst. commr., Jan., 1908; dep. registrar, co-op. societies, Sept., 1919; dep. commr. and senr. dep. registrar, ditto, May, 1927; retd., Nov., 1932.

MISRA, KRUPA SINDHU, Indian C.S. (jt. mag., United Provinces) (b. 22nd July, 1908).—Educated at the Patna University and the School of Oriental Studies, London; joined the service, 7th October, 1932; arrived in India, November, 1932, and served in the United Provinces as asst. mag. and collr.; jt. magte., Apl., 1935.

MISRA, KUNJ BEHARI LAL., B.A., LL.B. Rai Bahadur, late Central Provs. Civil Service (b. May, 1875).—Joined the service, 28th Oct., 1899; sub-judge, Jan., 1908; dist. and sessions judge, Sept., 1922; retd., Jan., 1930.

MISRA, LAKSHMIPATI, B.Sc., Indian State Ry's. (dep. chief engr.) (b. 4th July, 1888).—Joined the service as asst. engr., 9th Oct., 1912; offg. exec. engr., O. and R. Ry., Dec., 1918; services lent to Baroda State as engr.-in-chief, Baroda State Ry's., from April, 1924; exec. engr., E.I. Ry., April, 1928; offg. contr. of stores, Nov., 1930; offg. dep. agent., June, 1932; confd., Nov., 1932; offg. divl. supt., May, 1935; confd., Jan., 1936; dep. chief engr. (prov'l. perm.), Apl., 1936.

MISRA, SHARAD CHANDRA, M.A., Indian Police (b. 7th Apl., 1910).—Joined the service as proxy. asst. supt., United Provs., 25th Jan., 1933; confd., Jan., 1935.

MISRA, SHAM BIHARI (see Sham Bihari M.).
MISRA, TIKA RAM (see Tika, R. M.).

MITCHELL, ALAN ALEXANDER McCASKILL, C.I.E., B.A., Indian C.S. (commr., Punjab) (b. 24th Nov., 1882).—Educ. at St. Paul's Sch. and St. Peter's Coll., Cambridge; arrived, 26th Nov., 1906, and served in the Punjab as asst. commr.; manager, Bashahr State, March, 1914; supt., Simla Hill States, Aug., 1917; under-sect. to govt., Punjab, April to May, 1919; dep. commr., June, 1922; offg. commr. of income tax, Punjab, Nov., 1924; secty. to govt., Punjab, transferred depts., Dec., 1928; offg. commr., May, 1932; and again, Mar., 1933. C.I.E., Jan., 1936; confd., May, 1936.

MITCHELL, ALEXANDER, V.D., M.D., Indian State Ry's. (dist. medl. officer, E.I. Ry.) (b. 19th Jan., 1888).—Joined the service as dist. surg., 13th Jan., 1920; dist. medl. officer, Apl., 1929.

MITCHELL, ALFRED NORVAL, Indian C.S. Indian Pol. Service (b. 18th Dec., 1906).—Educ. at Shrewsbury School, and Balliol Zoll., Oxford ; joined the service, 8th Oct., 1930 ; arrived in India, Nov., 1930, and served in the Central Provs., as asst. commr. ; offg. under-secty. to govt., C.P., Oct., 1933 ; under-secty. to govt., N.W.F.P., Feb., 1936 ; offg. ch. secty., ditto, June, 1936 ; home secty. to govt., N.W.F.P., Apl., 1937.

MITCHELL, SIR DAVID GEORGE, K.C.I.E., C.S.I., V.D., B.Sc., late Indian C.S. (b. 31st Mar., 1879).—Educ. at George Heriot's Sch., Edin. and Edinburgh Univ. ; apptd. after exam. of 1902 ; arrived, 30th Nov. 1903, and served in the Central Provs. as asst. commr. ; dist. supt. of pol., July, 1909 ; asst. commr., and addl. dist. and sess. judge, April, 1913 ; dist. judge, Nov., 1916 ; dist. and sess. judge, May, 1917 ; legal rembr., sec. to legisl. council and sec. to govt., legal dept., March, 1919 ; C.I.E., June, 1923 ; addl. judl. commr., June, 1926 ; offg. jt. sec. and draftsman, legisl. dept., govt. of India, April, 1927 ; confd., Apl., 1930 ; C.S.I., June, 1932 ; offg. secty., govt. of India, legisl. dept., Dec., 1932 ; secty., govt. of India, dept. of industries and labour, Oct., 1933 ; offg. member, govr.-genl.'s exec. council, June, 1935 ; K.C.I.E. Jan., 1936 ; ret'd., July, 1937.

MITCHELL, DAVID THOMAS, Veterinary dept., Burma (dir. of vety. services) (b. 16th Mar., 1885).—Apptd. research officer, 10th Dec., 1927 ; dir. of vety. services, Jan., 1930.

MITCHELL, HAROLD CHARLES, Indian Police (supt., United Provs.) (b. 7th Mar., 1896).—Joined the service, 21st March, 1920 ; offg. supt., May, 1923 ; supt. (prov. sub.), July, 1930 ; confd., July, 1931 ; asst. to dep. inspr. genl., C.I.D., U.P., Oct., 1932 ; cent. intell. offr. U.P., and Ajmere, Jan., 1937.

MITCHELL, HAROLD CHARLES BARNES, late Bombay Judl. Dept. (b. 18th Oct., 1883).—Joined the dept., 11th Feb., 1919 ; admtr.-gen. and offl. trustee and regr. of companies, March, 1922 ; member, legis. council, Mar., 1932 ; ret'd., Sept., 1936.

MITCHELL, REV. JAMES DONALD, M.A., B.D., late Indian Eccles. Estabt. (b. 17th Aug., 1875).—Joined, 1st Aug., 1907 ; junr. chaplain, Feb., 1909 ; presdy. senr. chaplain, ch. of Scotland, March, 1925 ; ret'd., Feb., 1931.

MITCHELL, JOHN EBENEZER MILLER, Indian Forest Service (dep. consvr., Madras) (b. 27th Mar., 1897).—Joined the service as asst. consvr., 11th Dec., 1922 ; dep. consvr., Dec., 1924 ; chief for. offr., Coorg, Aug., 1933.

MITCHELL, JOHN FOWLER, C.I.E., B.Sc., late Indian C.S. (b. 30th Dec., 1886).—Educ. at Allan Glen's Schl., Glasgow, Royal Coll. of Science, London, Glasgow, and Merton Coll., Oxford ; apptd. after exam. of 1909 ; arrived in India, 28th Nov., 1910, and served in the Punjab as asst. commr. ; transf. to Delhi, Oct., 1912 ; reverted to the Punjab, Jan., 1914 ; under-sect. to govt. of Punjab, fin. dept. (s.p.t.), Oct., 1920 ; offg. dep. sec. to govt. of India, fin. dept., May, 1922 ; offg. acctnt.-gen., Madras, Jan., 1924 ; acct.-genl., Central Provs., Oct., 1926 ; dir. of audit, United Provs., Nov., 1928 ; offg. dir. of rly. audit, Dec., 1930 ; offg. dep. auditor-genl. in India, Apl., 1934 ; C.I.E., June, 1935 ; ret'd., Mar., 1937.

MITCHELL, KENNETH GRANT, C.I.E., A.M.I.C.E., Indian Service of Engrs. (consulting engr. to govt. of India (roads)) (b. 28th Aug., 1885).—Joined the service as asst. engr., 1st Oct., 1909 ; exec. engr., Oct., 1917 ; road engr. (temp.) to govt. of India, May, 1930 ; C.I.E., Jan., 1934 ; consulting engr. to govt. of India (roads), Mar., 1934 ; suptg. engr., May, 1935 ; member, council of State, Mar., 1937.

MITRA, AKHIL CHANDRA (see Akhil, C.M.).

MITRA, AMRITA NATH, Rai Bahadur, late Bihar and Orissa Civil Service (b. 17th July, 1874).—Joined the service, 30th May, 1903 ; munsi, Oct., 1905 ; dep. registrar, high ct., Patna, Feb., 1917 ; sub-judge, April, 1918 ; dist. and sess. judge, Jan., 1928 ; ret'd., Oct., 1929.

MITRA, ANADI NATH, late Indian Service of Engrs. (b. 18th Nov. 1883).—Trained at Thomason C.E. Coll. ; joined the service, 23rd Sept., 1906 ; asst. engr., Sept., 1907 ; under sec. to govt., Public Works (irrign. and trys.) dept., Jan., 1914, to Jan., 1917 ; exec. engr., Sept., 1915 ; ret'd., Nov., 1933.

MITRA, BIRENDRA MOHAN, B.Sc., Indian C.S. (dist. and sess. judge, Bengal) (b. 28th Aug., 1899).—Educ. at Calcutta Univ. and Magdalen Coll., Cambridge ; joined the service, 25th Oct., 1923 ; arrived, 4th Dec., 1923, and served in Bengal as asst. mag. and collr. ; offg. magte. and collr., Mar., 1929 ; offg. dist. and sess. judge, June, 1932 ; ditto, Assam, Oct., 1933 ; addl. ditto, Oct., 1932 ; confd. as dist. and sess. judge, Feb., 1936.

MITRA, BIRENDRA NATH, B.A., Indian Fin. Dept. (b. 29th Aug., 1883).—Joined the service as asst. examr. of accts., public wks. dept., 13th Aug., 1903 ; dep. examr., Jan., 1907 ; asst. auditor, E. Ben. Ry., June, 1918 ; govt. examiner, E.I. & B.N. Rys., Mar., 1920 ; dep. acctnt.-gen., posts and tels., Calcutta, Mar., 1922 ; offg. audit offr., Lloyd Barrage project, Karachi, Apl., 1926 ; offg. dep. auditor, N.W. Rly., Dec., 1926 ; on spl. duty with acct.-genl. (rlys.), Feb., 1928-July, 1929 ; offg. acct.-genl., Bihar and Orissa, Oct., 1929 ; offg. ch. auditor, E.B. Rly., Feb., 1930 ; ditto, E.I. Rly., Mar., 1931 ; confd., Nov., 1932 ; dir., rly. audit, Nov., 1933 ; and again, Oct., 1934 ; offg. contr., rly. accts., Apl., 1934 ; dir. of rly. audit, Oct., 1934.

MITRA, DHIRENDRA NATH, B.A., B.L., Legis. Dept., apptd. solicitor to the Govt. of India, April, 1937.

MITRA, HIMANSHU MOHAN, B.Sc., Dept. of Mines in India (b. 31st Mar., 1884).—Joined the service as electric inspr., 1st May, 1924.

MITRA, MANMATHA NATH (see Manmatha, N.M.)

MITRA, MONORANJAN, B.A., Indian C.S. (asst. commr., Assam) (b. 1st Aug., 1898).—Educ. at Calcutta Univ. Coll. and Balliol Coll., Oxford ; joined the service, 25th Oct., 1923 ; arrived, 8th Dec., 1923, and served in Assam as asst. commr. ; offg. addl. dist. magte., Oct., 1931 ; offg. dep. commr., Apl., 1933.

MITRA, MUNINDRA KUMAR, M.A., late Indian Fin. Dept. (b. 12th May, 1877).—Joined the service, 8th Aug., 1898, as asst. examr. ; dep. examr., Jan., 1902 ; examr., Aug., 1908 ; ditto, and asst. acctnt.-gen., Bengal, March, 1914 ; dep. acctnt.-gen., Bengal, March, 1915 ; asst. compt.-gen., May, 1918 ;

dep. acctnt.-gen., Dec., 1922; on special duty in finl. dept. as temp. acctnt.-gen. from Nov., 1923; offg. chief auditor, E.I. Rly., Dec., 1925; offg. acctnt.-gen., rlys., June, 1926; offg. chief auditor, E.I. Rly., Nov., 1926; confd. Oct., 1927; offg. acct.-genl (rlys.), Aug., 1928; ch. auditor, N.W. Rly., Apl., 1929; offg. contr. of rly. accts., Jan., 1930; ret'd., Nov., 1932.

MITRA, NARENDRA KUMAR, B.Sc., Indian State Rlys. (exec. engr., E.I. rly.) (b. 4th Sept., 1887).—Joined the service, 1st Apl., 1913; resdt. engr., Nov., 1921; exec. engr., Aug., 1927;

MITRA, PHANINDRA NATH, C.I.E., B.A., late India Posts and Telegraphs (b. 12th July, 1881).—Joined the service as apprentice asst. supt., 2nd Nov., 1903; asst. supt., Jan., 1905; asst. postmaster-genl. tel. traffic, U.P. circle, Oct., 1914; on deputation to Kashmir State as supt., State Tel. Dept., from July, 1914, to Apl., 1917; dep. postmaster-genl., April 1917; supt., Bombay C.T.O., Dec., 1920; asst. dir.-genl., traffic, April, 1921; on depn. to internl. tele. conference, Paris, Aug., 1925; ditto, to internl. radio conference, Washington, Aug., 1927; ditto to tele. conference, Brussels, Aug., 1928; contr. tele. traffic, May, 1929; offg. contr. of tele. stores, Feb., 1930; dir. tel. engrg., June, 1931; offg. p.m.g., central circle, Sept., 1932; and again, Apl., 1934; C.I.E., Jan., 1935; confd. as p.m.g., Sept., 1934; offg. ch. engr., posts and tels., Apl., 1935; and again, Feb., 1936; offg. dep. dir.-genl., tels., Dec., 1935; ret'd., Aug., 1936.

MITRA, PRAN KUMAR, Indian Police (asst. supt., Bihar) (b. 15th Oct., 1904).—Joined the service as asst. supt., Bihar and Orissa, 8th Dec., 1924; offg. supt., Apl., 1932; and again, Mar., 1933.

MITRA, RABINDRA KUMAR, B.A. (Cal.), Barr. Indian C.S. (jt. mag. and dep. collr., Bengal) (b. 15th Dec., 1904).—Educ. at University of Calcutta, University Coll., London, and Balliol Coll., Oxford; joined the service, 11th Oct., 1929; arrived, Nov., 1929, and served in Bengal as asst. mag. and coll.; jt. mag. and dep. collr., May, 1931; offg. addl. dist. and sess. judge, Feb., 1936.

MITRA, ROOPENDRA KUMAR, Judl. Dept., Bengal (judge, high ct.).—Apptd. offg. judge, high ct., Calcutta, 31st July, 1934; addl. judge, ditto, Nov., 1934 and Aug., 1936; high ct. judge, Bengal, Nov., 1937.

MITRA, SAMARENDRA LAL, M.B., B.Sc., D.T.M. & H., D.P.H., Lt.-Col., I.M.S., Public Health dept., Bihar (dir., public health) (b. 23rd Dec., 1887).—First commn. dated 1st Nov., 1920; in mil. employ prior to 5th Sept., 1925; offg. dir. of public health, Sept., 1925, offg. dir. of public health, Assam, Mar. to Nov., 1929; ditto, Bihar and Orissa, Oct., 1930; and again, Feb., 1933; dir. of public health, Bihar, Feb., 1937.

MITRA, SARAT CHANDRA, Indian Posts and Tels. (b. 23rd Oct., 1882).—Joined the service, 13th Oct., 1905; asst. supt., April, 1907; contr. tel. traffic, Dec., 1925; asst. dir.-genl., March, 1927; dep. postmaster-genl., traffic, central circle, Oct., 1927; offg. contr., tele. traffic, Feb., 1930; offg. dir. of tel. engrg., U.P. circle, Mar., 1931, and again, May, 1933; ditto, Madras, Jan., 1934; confd., Sept., 1934; offg. p.m.g., Madras, Mar., 1935; and again, Sept., 1936; dir., tels., Madras, Apl., 1935.

MITRA, SATIS CHANDRA, Judicial Dept., Bengal (b. 9th Sept., 1870).—Joined the service as asst. master and offl. referee, 16th Jan., 1903; offg. registrar in insolvency, May, 1921; confd., Nov., 1921; offg. regr., high ct., July, 1923, Nov., 1923, March, 1924, and again April, 1927.

MITRA, SATISH CHANDRA, Barr., late Judicial Dept., Bengal (b. 10th Jan., 1880).—Joined the service as asst. regr., high ct., origl. side, 20th Dec., 1909; registrar, Feb., 1920; judge, small cause ct., Oct., 1920; ret'd., Jan., 1935.

MITRA, S. K., M.S. (Cal.), Ph.D. (Ohio), Indian Agricl. Service (dir. of agric.) (b. 15th Feb., 1887).—Joined the service as economic botanist, Assam, 28th Feb., 1921; dir. of agric., Assam, Sep., 1937.

MITRA, SATYENDRA NATH, M.B., Ch.B., D.F.N. (asst. dir. of public health, Bengal) (b. 11th Nov., 1889).—Apptd. asst. dir. of public health, 13th Sept., 1920; confd., Sept., 1925.

MITRA, SHAILENDRA NATH, B.Sc., Indian Civil Service (jt. mag. and dep. collr., Bengal) (b. 17th Jan., 1909).—Educ. at Government High School, Partabgarh, Government Intermediate College, Fyzabad, Allahabad University, Imperial College of Science, London, and the School of Oriental Studies, London; joined the service 7th Oct., 1933; arrived in India, Nov., 1933, and served in Bengal as asst. collr. and mag.

MITRA, TINCORI, B.E., Indian Service of Engrs. (exec. engr., Bengal) (b. Jan., 1900).—Joined the service as apprentice engr., 19th Dec., 1923; asst. exec., engr., Dec., 1925; offg. exec. engr., June, 1929; confd., Dec., 1934.

MITTER, SIR BROJENDRA LAL, K.C.S.I., Barr.—Apptd. advocate-general, Bengal, Dec., 1925; Kt., Jan., 1928; member, govr.-gent.'s exec. council, Dec., 1928; K.C.S.I., Jan., 1932; delegate of India at Assembly of League of Nations, 1933; member, govr.'s exec. council, Bengal, May 1934.

MITTER, DWARKA NATH, late Judicial dept., Bengal.—Apptd. addl. judge, high ct., Nov., 1926; judge, ditto, Sept., 1929; ret'd., Mar., 1937.

MITTER, GANESH CHANDRA, M.Sc., M.I.M. Mint and Assay Dept. (ch. assayer, Bombay) (b. 1st Aug., 1897).—Joined the service as dep. assay master on probn., 28th Mar., 1922; confd., Feb., 1923; offg. assay master, Bombay, Aug., 1927; ch. assayer, Apl., 1932.

MITTER, MOHAN LALL, M.I.L.E., Indian State Railways (Transpn. (Power) Supt., senr. scale, E.I. Rly.) (b. 3rd May, 1890).—Asst. loco. supt., 15th Aug., 1924; Supt. (power) senr. scale, July, 1936.

MITTER, S. C., B.Sc., (Eng.) Lond. (dir. of indus., Bengal) (b. 10th Dec., 1900).—Joined the service as indus. engr. 9th Oct., 1925; dep. dir. of indus. Bengal, Aug., 1934; dir. ditto, May, 1937.

MOBBS, ERIC CHARLES, B.A., B.Sc., Indian Forest Service (dep. consvr., United Provs.) (b. 10th Sept., 1899).—Joined the service as asst. consvr., 11th Dec., 1922; services lent to Tehri Garhwal State for two years from May, 1925; dep. consvr., Nov., 1927.

MOBERLY, RONALD STUART, Indian State Rlys. (divl. traffic manager, G.I.P. Rly.).—Joined the service as traffic probnr., 17th Sept., 1909; asst. traffic supt., Nov., 1910;

on milty. service from 2nd Sept., 1916, to 10th Apl., 1919; dist. traffic supt., Apl., 1919; asst. traffic manager, Jan., 1924; divl. ditto, Jan., 1929.

MOCKETT, VERE, Barr., Judl. Dept., Madras (judge, high ct., Madras).—Apptd. judge, high ct., Madras, 21st July, 1934.

MODAK, BHASKAR LAKSHMAN, A.M.I.C.E., M.C.E., Indian Service of Engrs. (exec. engr., Bombay) (b. 29th Sept., 1884).—Held non-gazetted appts., from 1st March, 1908, to 18th Sept., 1916; 3rd gr. engr. (provl. service), Sept., 1916; asst. engr. (ditto), Feb., 1920; I.S.E., as asst. exec. engr., Jan., 1928; exec. engr., Oct., 1933.

MODAK, SATYENDRA NATH, M.A. (Cal.), B.A. (Cantab.), Barr., Indian C.S. (dist. and sess. judge, Bengal) (b. 24th Dec., 1891).—Educ. at Emmanuel Coll., Cambridge; apptd. by nomination; arrived, Dec., 1916, and served in Bengal as asst. mag. and collr.; offg. dist. and sess. judge, Aug., 1925; confd., May, 1927.

MODDER, EUGENE ASHLEY CLIVE, Indian Forest Service (dep. consvr., Bengal) (b. 3rd Apl., 1883).—Joined the service as dep. consvr., 12th March, 1920; offg. consvr., variously from July, 1928.

MODI, LONJI SHAHPURIJI, F.R.C.S.I., Lt.-Col. I.M.S., Jail Dept., Central Provs. (supt.) (b. 9th Mar., 1884).—First commn. dated 1st Nov., 1920; in milty. employ to 5th March, 1925; supt., March, 1924; spt., Central Jail, Nagpur, Feb., 1934.

MOHAN LAL, BATRA, B.A., Indian Service of Engrs. (exec. engr., Punjab) (b. 1st July, 1903).—Joined the service as apprentice engr., Oct., 1926; asst. exec. engr., Oct., 1927; exec. engr., May, 1937.

MOHAN LAL, KUNJ BEHARI, M.Sc., Indian Forest Service (b. 25th Jan., 1907).—Joined the service as asst. consvr., Assam, 14th Nov., 1930.

MOHAN, MAN (see Man Mohan).

MOHAN, NETER PARKASH (see Neter Parkash M.).

MOHAN RAO, PEJAVAR, M.A., Indian Finance Dept. (b. 25th May, 1887).—Joined the service, 28th Oct., 1910; asst. acctnt.-gen., May, 1911; dep. acctnt.-gen., posts and tels., Aug., 1918; ditto, Madras, Sept., 1921; ditto, Nagpur, Jan., 1922; asst. acct.-genl., U.P., Oct., 1922; ditto, Madras, Mar., 1923; ditto, Bengal, Jan., 1924; dep. acct.-genl., posts and tels., Madras, Apl., 1925; dep. acct.-genl., C.P., Apl., 1928; audit officer, Indian stores dept., Oct., 1928; offg. dep. ch. accts. officer, E.I. rlys., Nov., 1932; offg. ch. ditto, Apl., 1933; offg. acct.-genl., Bombay, May, 1934; govt. exr. of accts., M. & S.M. and S.I. rlys., Oct., 1934; offg. ch. accts. officer, E.I. rly., Feb., 1935; acct. genl., Bengal, Sept., 1936.

MOHAN SINGH, SARDAR BAHADUR (adviser to secty. of State for India).—Apptd. member of Council of India, Mar., 1935; adviser to sec. of state for India, Apl., 1937.

MOHAN, YADAVA (see Yadava Mohan).

MOHGAONKAR, NARHARI KASHINATH, B.A., B.L., late Central Provs., C.S. (Judl.) (b. 3rd Oct., 1876).—Joined the service as munisif, 20th Mar., 1903; extra asst. commr., Apl., 1907; sub-judge, Feb., 1912; dist. and sess. judge, Jan., 1930; ret'd., Nov., 1931.

MOLES, SAMUEL, B.A., Indian C.S. (asst. commr., Punjab).—Educ. at Royal Academical Institution, Belfast, and Belfast Univ.; joined the service, 22nd Sept., 1937; arrived in India, Oct., 1937, and served in the Punjab as asst. commr.

MOLESWORTH, WALTER GUY, ate Indian Service of Engrs. (Madras) (b. 18th Feb., 1874).—Trained at R.I.E. Coll.; joined the service, 1st Oct., 1896, as asst. engr.; exec. engr., Oct., 1906; under sec. to govt., Madras, Oct., 1911, to 1918; suptg. engr. (temp.), April, 1919; consg. archt. to govt., Jan., 1920; confd. as suptg. engr., May, 1921; offg. chief engr., Sept., 1925; confd., Sept., 1926; ret'd., Feb., 1929.

MOLLOY, PATRICK REGINALD HEMBROUGH, B.A., Burma C.S. (Cl. I) (asst. commr.) (b. 16th Oct., 1912).—Educ. at St. Joseph's College, Naini Tal, India, and Rangoon Univ.; joined the service 30th Sept., 1937; arrived in Burma, Nov., 1937, and served as asst. commr.

MONAHAN GEORGE JOHN, M.A., late Indian C.S. (b. 28th Dec., 1874).—Educ. at Stonyhurst Coll., and Trin. Coll., Dublin; apptd. after exam. of 1897; arrived, 25th Dec., 1898, and served in Bengal as asst. mag. and collr., and asst. settl. offr.; joint mag. and dep. collr., Aug., 1907; transf. to Bihar and Orissa, April, 1912; dist. and sess. judge, Aug., 1912; ret'd., Dec., 1932.

MONANI, MAHOMEDALI GULAMHUSAIN, B.A., Indian C.S. (asst. collr., Bombay) (b. 29th Oct., 1910).—Educ. at St. Xavier's High School, Bombay, St. Vincent's High School, Poona, Bombay University and the School of Oriental Studies, London; joined the service, 9th Oct., 1934; arrived in India, Nov., 1934, and served in Bombay as asst. collr.

MONDAL, GOPI BALLAV, Indian Service of Engrs. (exec. engr., Bengal) (b. 2nd Feb., 1899).—Joined the service as asst. exec. engr., Bengal, 7th Oct., 1922; exec. engr., Oct., 1931.

MONE, NARAYAN TRIMBAK, B.Sc., Indian C.S. (asst. commr., Central Provinces) (b. 16th Apl. 1906).—Educ. at University of Allahabad, University of Nagpur, and School of Oriental Studies, London; joined the service, 11th Oct., 1929; arrived, 16th Dec., 1929, and served in the Central Provinces as asst. commr.; offg. dep. commr., July, 1933.

MONE, TRIMBAK WASUDEO, M.A., late Indian Educl. Service (b. 13th Feb., 1880).—Apptd. prof., Robertson Coll., Jubbulpore, 11th July, 1908; ret'd., Feb., 1935.

MONK, JAMES EDMONDSON, B.Sc., late Indian State Rlys. (E.I. Rly.) (b. 6th Mar., 1874).—Joined the service as resdt. engr. (temp.), 9th June, 1914; dist. engr., Nov., 1924; offg. dep. ch. engr., Dec., 1925; confd., May, 1926; engr.-in-chief, May, 1927; offg. ch. engr., June, 1928; ret'd., Oct., 1929.

MONRO, ALEXANDER, C.I.E., Indian C.S. (dep. commr., United Provs.) (b. 17th Feb., 1890).—Educ. at Charterhouse, and Magdalen Coll., Oxford; apptd. after exam. of 1912; arrived, 26th Nov., 1913, and served in the United Provs. as asst. mag. and collr. and asst. commr.; asst. supt., Dehra Dun, May to June, 1914; jt. mag., Jan., 1918; offg. dy. commr., Jan., 1923; offg. mag. and collr., Jan., 1926; confd., Dec., 1929; dep. commr., Jan., 1930; C.I.E., June, 1931.

MONRO, WALTER COLIN, Indian Forest Service (dep. consvr., United Provs.) (b. 27th Sept., 1888).—Joined the service 10th July, 1915; extra asst. consvr., United Provs., July, 1917; promoted to I.F.S. as asst. consvr., May, 1932; dep. consvr., Nov., 1933.

MONROE, JAMES HARVEY, Barr., Judicial Dept., Punjab (judge, high ct., Lahore)—Apptd. addl. judge, high ct., Lahore, Mar., 1932; judge, ditto, Oct., 1933.

MONTAGU, AINSLEY MARSHALL RENDALL. A.M.I.C.E., A.M.I.M.E. Indian Service of Engrs. (exec. engr., Punjab) (b. 6th Nov., 1891).—Joined the service as asst. engr., Oct., 1914; on mil. duty from 24th March, 1915, to 11th May, 1920; asst. exec. engr., Jan., 1920; exec. engr., Oct., 1923; secty. (temp.), cent. board of irrig., May, 1931.

MONTEATH, DAVID TAYLOR, C.V.O., O.B.E., B.A., Burma Office (asst. under sec. of state) (b. 7th Apl., 1887).—Educ. at Clifton Coll. and Trinity Coll., Oxford; apptd. to Admiralty, Dec., 1910; junr. clerk, mil. dept., India Office, 5th Dec., 1911; transfed. to pol. dept., Nov., 1912; resdt. clerk, April, 1914, to Feb., 1915; on duty in mil. dept. from Oct., 1914; services temporarily lent to the Admiralty, Feb., 1915; on mil. service, 1918; O.B.E., Jan., 1918; pte. sec. to Lord Sinha, April, 1919; senr. clerk, pol. dept., Dec., 1919; principal, April, 1921; actg. dep. clerk of the council, June, 1926; pte. sec. to sec. of state, Dec., 1927; actg. asst. secty., public and judl. dept., Apl., 1931; asst. secty., finl. dept., July, 1931; C.V.O., June, 1931; secty. to Burma round table conf., London, Dec., 1931; joint secty., milty. dept., Sept., 1934; asst. under sec. of state for Burma, Apl., 1937.

MONTEATH, GEORGE, B.A., late Indian C.S. (Bombay).—Educ. at Clifton and Christ's Coll., Cambridge; apptd. after exam. of 1895; arrived 4th Dec., 1896, and served in Bombay as asst. collr. and mag. and asst. pol. agent; 2nd asst., and supt. of land records, Nov., 1904; under-sec. to govt., rev., finl., and separate depts., Sept., 1905; pte. sec. to the govr., March, 1907; again under-sec. to govt., May, 1907; 2nd asst., Jan., 1908; 1st asst., Nov. 1909; junr. collr., Dec., 1912; collr. and dist. mag., May, 1917; offg. commr., June, 1926; ret'd., Sept., 1929.

MONTEATH, JOHN, B.A., C.I.E., late Indian C.S. (b. 9th October, 1878).—Educ. at Clifton and King's Coll., Cambridge; apptd. after exam. of 1901; arrived, 29th Nov., 1902, and served in Bombay as asst. collr. and mag., and asst. to the collr. of Bombay and chief inspr. of factories; asst. pol. agent, Kathiawar, March, 1908; 1st asst., Nov., 1909; municipal commr., Ahmedabad,

April, 1915; offg. postmaster-gen., Punjab, Nov., 1915; ditto, Madras, June, 1916; confirmed, Sept., 1918; collr. and dist. mag., Dec., 1922; collr., dist. mag. and pol. agent, Nov., 1923, and again, July, 1928; sec. to govt., home and ecl. depts., Oct., 1926; member, legisl. council, 1927 and 1932; offg. commr., Apl., 1932; C.I.E., Feb., 1937.

MONTFORD, HORACE MOUTRIE, India Audit Office (supgt. executive officer) (b. 4th June, 1900).—Apptd. asst. clerk, 14th Aug., 1919, after previous service from 24th April, 1918; second class Clerk, 29th April, 1920; transfed. to India Audit Office, March, 1922; exec. officer, April, 1922; higher exec. officer, Aug., 1933; supgt. ditto, Mar., 1936.

MONTGOMERIE, ALEXANDER, C.I.E., M.A., late Indian C.S. (Bombay) (b. 27th Feb., 1879).—Educ. at the High Sch. and Univ., Glasgow; apptd. after exam. of 1902; arrived, 21st Nov., 1903, and served in Bombay as asst. collr. and mag., and asst. judge; offg. dep. sec. to govt., pol., judl., and spl. depts., April, 1919; sec. to govt., pol., &c., depts. (s.p.t.), April, 1920; C.I.E., June, 1921; asst. collr. and secy. to govt., home dept., Jan., 1923; judge and sess. judge, Sept., 1923; sec. to govt., home and eccles. depts., May, 1925; judge and sess. judge, Dec., 1926; ret'd., Nov., 1929.

MONTGOMERY, HENRY, B.A., late Indian C.S. (b. 1st May, 1884).—Educ. at Clifton, and Balliol Coll., Oxf.; apptd. after exam. of 1907; arrived, 26th Nov., 1908, and served in Bombay as asst. collr.; manager, Sind encumbered estates, Mar., 1917; on mil. duty as asst. recruiting offr., 1918; actg. collr. and dist. mag., Feb., 1920; confd., July, 1927; ret'd., Mar., 1935.

MONTGOMERY, ROBERT ERNEST, Office of High Commr. for India (secty., general dept.) (b. 9th July, 1878).—Educ. at King's Coll. Sch., London; apptd. second class clerk, acctnt. gen.'s dept., Mar., 1898; transfed. to military dept., Sept., 1902; apptd. locum tenens to reading clerk to council, Aug., 1911; dep. reading clerk, May, 1913, to Feb., 1920; staff clerk (temp.), April, 1915; junr. clerk, July, 1919; representative of India Office on Army and Navy National Health Insurance Advisory Cmtee., Jan., 1920; principal (actg.), April, 1921; transfed. to Office of High Commr. for India as asst. sec., April, 1922; actg. sec. to high commr., 20th Oct. to 5th Nov., 1924, and 11th Dec., 1924, to Jan., 1925; designated secty. genl. dept., Nov., 1930.

MOODIE, ADAM WILSON, O.B.E., M.A., B.Sc., Indian Forest Service (consvr., Burma) (b. 17th May, 1887).—Joined the service as asst. consvr., 16th Dec., 1911; dep. consvr., Dec., 1916; O.B.E., June, 1919; offg. consvr., Apl., 1933; confd., Nov., 1935.

MOODY, BASIL, M.A., V.D., Indian State Rlys. (divl. comml. officer, N.W. Rly.) (b. 13th Oct., 1889).—Joined the service as asst. traffic supt., 11th Jan., 1912; asst. dist. traffic supt., N.W. Rly., Jan., 1922; offg. dist. ditto, Nov., 1923; offg. dep. dir. of traffic and statistics, rly. bd., Feb., 1926; divl. comml. officer, N.W. Rly., Nov., 1929; offg. divl. supt., N.W. rly., Jan., 1934; confd., Nov., 1936; offg. secty. rly. bd., Mar., 1935, and again, Oct., 1936.

MOODY, ROBERT HECTOR, M.A., late Indian Educl. Service (United Provs.) (b. 15th Apl., 1883).—Joined the service as prof., Muir Central Coll., Allahabad, 17th March, 1913; prinpl. govt. inter. coll., Allahabad, July, 1924; offg. dep. dir. of public instr., May, 1926; offg. inspr. of schools, Oct., 1926; confd., Nov., 1927; retd., Mar., 1932.

MOON, EDWARD PENDEREL, B.A., Indian C.S. (dep. commr., Punjab) (b. 13th Nov., 1905).—Educ. at Winchester and New Coll., Oxford; fellow of All Souls, 1927; joined the service, 16th Oct., 1929; arrived, Nov., 1929, and served in the Punjab as asst. commr.; under secty. to govt., pol. and home depts., Punjab, Dec., 1932; offr. on spec. duty, Nov., 1937; dep. commr., Dec., 1937.

MOONEY, HERBERT FRANCIS, Indian Forest Service (asst. consvr., Orissa) (b. 6th Nov., 1897).—Joined the service as asst. consvr., March, 1921; agency forest officer, Nov., 1923.

MOORE, ARTHUR GEORGE, late India Posts and Tels. (b. 6th Sept., 1874).—Held non-gazetted appt. from 1st Dec., 1889; on spl. duty in Persia (Seistan) from 25th April, 1903, to 12th May, 1905; supt. of post offices (traffic), April, 1914; on field service from 28th Oct., 1917, and on foreign service with Irak admn. from 1st Nov., 1919; offg. dep. postmaster-genl. (traffic), Feb., 1922; confd., March, 1922; supt., Madras C.T.O., Dec., 1923; ditto. Calcutta, April, 1924; dep. postmaster-genl. (traffic), Burma Circle, April, 1925; ditto, U.P. Circle, Oct., 1927; retd., Sept., 1929.

MOORE, DENIS BENJAMIN, Indian Police (b. 21st Mar., 1917).—Apptd. asst. supt., Bihar, on probn., Dec., 1937.

MOORE, ERNEST ARTHUR, Indian Service of Engrs. (exec. engr.) (b. 21st Dec., 1900).—Joined the service as asst. exec. engr., Bengal, 4th Oct., 1924; exec. engr., Oct., 1933.

MOORE, FREDERICK THOMAS, Office of High Commr. for India (exec. officer, general dept.) (b. 17th Feb., 1897).—Apptd. temp. clerk, India Office, Jan., 1920; transf. to H.C.'s Office, Apl., 1922; clerical officer, Jan., 1924; exec. ditto, June, 1927.

MOORE, JOHN, late Indian Police Service (Madras) (b. 19th Dec., 1880).—Joined the service, 10th Dec., 1901, as probr.; asst. supt., July, 1903; supt., July, 1910; awarded King's police medal; personal asst. to dep. inspr.-gen. rlys. and C.I.D., March, 1919; offg. dep. inspr.-gen., Oct., 1925; retd., July, 1930.

MOORE, JOHN RICHARD, Office of High Commr. for India (executive officer, store dept.) (b. 16th Jan., 1888).—Apptd. 2nd class clerk, 4th April, 1908; transf. to office of high commr. for India, Oct., 1920; exec. officer, April, 1922.

MOORE, LEONARD ALFRED JOHN, Office of High Commr. for India (exec. officer, general dept.) (b. 11th Oct., 1902).—Apptd. clerical officer, India Office, 27th Apl., 1920, after previous service, from 25th Feb., 1918; transf. to office of High Commr. for India, April, 1922; exec. officer, April, 1924.

MOORE, PIERCE LANGRISH, C.I.E., late Indian C.S. (Madras) (b. 26th Nov., 1873).—Educ. at Cheltenham, and Christ Church, Oxford; apptd. after exam. of 1896; arrived, 21st Nov., 1897, and served in Madras as asst. collr. and mag.; dist. mag., Bangalore,

Feb., 1904; first asst. to resdt. in Mysore, and sec. to chief commr. of Coorg, July to Oct., 1906; head asst. collr. and mag., March, 1909; sub-collr. and joint mag., May, 1910; president, Madras corporation, Nov., 1910; C.I.E., June, 1912; inspr.-gen. of police, Aug., 1914; collr. and dist. mag., Nov., 1919; offg. sec. to govt., fin. dept., April, 1921; sec. to govt. of Madras, local self-govt. dept., March, 1923; second sec. to govt., April, 1926; offg. third member, board of rev., May, 1926; confd., Aug., 1926; offg. 2nd member, ditto, Aug., 1926; retd., Aug., 1930.

MOORE, THOMAS ARTHUR, A.I.C., Office of High Commr. for India (chemist, store dept.) (b. 1st June, 1876).—Apptd. asst. chemist, India Store Depot, 1910; actg. chief chemist, 1914 to 1919; chemist, Oct., 1923.

MOORE, WENTWORTH WILLIAM THOMPSON, M.A., late Indian Educl. Service (b. 8th Dec., 1889).—Joined the service as prof., Patna Coll., 23rd Feb., 1914; offg. prinpl., New Coll., Patna, Oct., 1925; retd., March, 1932.

MOORTHY RAO, SRINIVASARAO SRINIVASA, B.E. (Mech. Mysore), Indian Posts and Telegraphs (chief electrician, wireless) (b. 21st Oct., 1899).—Joined the service as asst. div. engr., wireless (temp.), 10th Nov., 1926; confd. div. engr. wireless, Apr., 1928; chief elec. wireless, Mar., 1937.

MOOS, PESONJI NANABHAI, M.A., LL.B., Barr., Indian C.S. (judge and sess. judge, Bombay) (b. 29th Aug., 1894).—Educ. at Elphinstone Coll., Bombay, and University Coll., London; joined the service, 12th Dec., 1921; arrived, 6th Jan., 1922, and served in Bombay as asst. collr.; doing duty as asst. judge, Aug., 1925; offg. judge and sess. judge, April, 1928; confd., Nov., 1929.

MOOS, SORAB NANABHOY, M.A., Indian Educl. Service (dep. dir. of public instrn., Poona) (b. 25th Sept., 1890).—Joined the service, 14th May, 1919; vice-prin., D. J. Sind Coll., May, 1919; inspr. of science teaching, Bombay Presdy., Dec., 1925; offg. asst. director of public instrn., April, 1926; and again, Sept., 1933; eduel. inspr., Jan., 1927; offg. dy. dir. of public instrn., Mar., 1932; offg. dir., Nov., 1936; dep. dir. public instrn., Poona, Dec., 1937.

MORAES, ANTHONY XAVIER, L.C.E., M.I.E. (Ind.), late Indian Service of Engrs. (Bombay) (b. 20th Mar., 1876).—Joined, 8th March, 1900; exec. engr., 1909; suptg. engr., March, 1925; retd. Mar., 1931.

MORAN, ARTHUR VIVIAN, Office of High Commr. for India (higher exec. officer, store dept.) (b. 30th Mar., 1879).—Apptd. 2nd class clerk, India Store Depot, 30th Sept., 1903; transf. to office of high commr. for India, Oct., 1920; supervisor, April, 1921; higher exec. officer, April, 1922.

MOREHEAD, FREDERICK THOMAS, O.B.E., B.Sc., Indian Forest Service (dep. consvr., Burma) (b. 30th June, 1895).—Joined the service as asst. consvr., 18th Dec., 1922; dep. consvr., July, 1925; O.B.E., June, 1932.

MORGAN, Rev. BASIL IVOR, Indian Eccles. Estabt. (senr. chaplain) (b. 9th Dec., 1887).—Apptd. junr. chaplain, 16th Jan., 1922; senr. chaplain, Jan., 1932.

MORGAN, CHARLES CAREY, Judl. Dept., Bengal (dep. admwr. gen. and offl. trustee, Bengal), (b. 21st Sept., 1880).—Was acting administrator gen., Calcutta, from 25th Feb., 1910, to Nov., 1910, from March, 1911, to Jan., 1913, and from Jan., 1913; administrator gen. s.p.f., April, 1914; offg. administrator-gen. and official trustee, Bengal, Oct., 1920, and again, May, 1930; custodian of enemy property in Bengal Presdy., March, 1922.

MORGAN, DAVID EVAN, India Office (exec. officer, Acct.-Genl.'s Dept.) (b. 4th Mar., 1917).—Apptd. exec. officer, 31st Aug., 1936.

MORGAN, DONALD LINDSEY, late Indian Police (b. 5th Nov., 1888).—Joined the service as asst. supt., 14th Dec., 1908; supt., Dec., 1922; ret'd., May, 1937.

MORGAN, FRANCIS DAVID PATTERSON, Indian Police (asst. supt., Madras) (b. 14th Aug., 1914).—Apptd. asst. supt. on probn., Madras 22nd Nov., 1934; confd., Jan., 1937.

MORGAN, FREDERICK ROBERT, late Indian State Rlys. (b. 8th Aug., 1882).—Joined the service as asst. engr., 13th Oct., 1905; exec. engr., Oct., 1913; offg. dep. ch. engr., Nov., 1925; confd., June, 1932; offg. ch. engr., N.W. rly., June, 1934; and again, Oct., 1935; ditto, E.B. rly., Mar., 1935; ch. engr., N.W. rly., Oct., 1935; ret'd., Aug., 1937.

MORGAN, HUGH CAREY.—Apptd. solicitor to the Govt. of Bengal, April, 1935.

MORGAN, JEFFERY JOHN EVAN, Indian Police (b. 5th Jan., 1918).—Apptd. asst. supt., Punjab, on probn., Dec., 1937.

MORGAN, MAURICE DAVID, Indian Police (dist. supt., Central Provs.) (b. 2nd Dec., 1889).—Joined the service as asst. dist. supt., Dec., 1909; dist. supt., Feb., 1921.

MORGAN, PAUL ROBERT JAMES, Indian Police (supt., Punjab) (b. 1st Feb., 1898).—Joined the service as asst. supt., 6th Dec., 1920; offg. supt., April, 1925; confd., Aug., 1926.

MORISON, JOHN, C.I.E., M.B., Ch.B. (Glas.), D.P.H. (Cantab.), Lt.-Col., I.M.S., ret'd. (b. 6th Nov., 1879).—Joined 1st Sept., 1906, and served in mil. dept. to 4th April, 1922; asst. dir., B. B. Laboratory, April, 1922; offg. dir., Haffkine Inst., Bombay, and consulting bacteriologist, G.M. Coll., Aug., 1925; offg. dir., Pasteur institute, Rangoon, Nov., 1926; dir., ditto, Shillong, Oct., 1927; C.I.E., Jan., 1932; offg. inspr.-genl. of civil hosps. and prisons, Assam, May, 1933; ret'd., Nov., 1934.

MORLEY, ALEXANDER FRANCIS, B.A., India Office (princl., Econ. and Overseas Dept.) (b. 6th Jan., 1908).—Educ. at Rugby and Queen's Coll., Oxford; apptd. asst. principal, 20th Oct., 1930; pte. secty. to parly, under sec. of state, Dec., 1933; actg. princpl., Mar., 1936, and posted to econ. and overseas dept., Aug., 1936; adviser and secty., Indian del. to 21st and 22nd Internatl. lab. conf., 1936; advr. ditto 23rd Internatl. lab. conf., June, 1937; confd. principal, Oct., 1937; secty. to Indian del. to Brussels nine-power confr. Nov., 1937.

MORLEY, GEORGE RIPLEY, B.A., late Indian C.S. (b. 10th July, 1902).—Educ. at Kingswood School, Bath, and Jesus College, Oxford; joined the service, 22nd Oct., 1926; arrived, Dec., 1926, and served in Burma as asst. commr.; ret'd., Apl., 1932.

MORONY, THOMAS HENRY, C.S.I., C.I.E., late Indian Police (b. 8th Apl., 1879).—Joined the service, 9th Dec., 1899, and posted to Central Provs.; dist. supt., Sept., 1907; inspr.-gen. of pol., Indore State, April, 1912, to March, 1917; awarded King's police medal, Jan., 1918; dep. inspr.-gen. (prov.), Cent. Provs., Feb., 1921; offg. inspr.-gen. Jan., 1922; confd., Jan., 1923; C.I.E., June, 1925; C.S.I., Jan., 1931; ret'd., June, 1933.

MORRIS, AUSTIN ROBERT, B.A., late Indian C.S. (b. 7th Dec., 1885).—Educ. at Nottingham High School, and Queen's Coll., Cambridge; apptd. after exam. of 1908; arrived, 4th Dec., 1909, and served in Burma as asst. commr.; dep. commr. (prov. sub.), July, 1924; offg. sec. to finl. commr. (in addn.), Nov., 1924; dep. commr., Feb., 1926; sec. to govt., forest dept., May, 1927; excise commr., Feb., 1933; addl. secty. to govt., forest dept., Burma, Apl., 1936; ret'd.

MORRIS, FREDERICK WILLIAM AUBREY, Indian C.S. (jt. mag. and sub. collr., Madras) (b. 26th Aug., 1907).—Educ. at Cardiff High School and Corpus Christi College, Cambridge; joined the service, 17th Oct., 1930; arrived in India, Nov., 1930; and served in Madras as asst. mag. and collr.; sub-collr., Aug., 1932.

MORRIS, JAMES ARCHIBALD, A.M.I.M.E., Indian State rlys. (works manager) (b. 26th Sept., 1888).—Joined the service, 1st Jan., 1924; asst. works manager, E.I. rly., Nov., 1932; works manager, Aug., 1934.

MORRIS, MICHAEL, M.B.E., late Indian Police (b. 10th Feb., 1880).—Held non-gazetted appt. prior to 27th Feb., 1915; dep. supt. (prov. service), Feb., 1915; joined I.P.S. as asst. supt., June, 1927; supt., Dec., 1927; ret'd., Feb., 1935; M.B.E., June, 1935.

MORRIS, RALPH CLARENCE, Indian Police (dep. inspr.-genl., Burma) (b. 10th Mar., 1889).—Joined the service as asst. supt., 6th Dec., 1908; dist. supt., Dec., 1917; offg. dep. inspr.-genl., April, 1928; asst. inspr.-genl., Nov., 1928; dep. ditto, Dec., 1931; offg. inspr.-genl., June, 1933, and again, Feb., 1937; awarded King's Police Medal, Jan., 1935.

MORRISON, ROBERT VICTOR, M.D., Lt.-Col., I.M.S., Burma Medl. Dept. (prof. of medicine, Rangoon Univ.) (b. 1st Sept., 1887).—First commn. dated 27th Jan., 1912; in milty. employ prior to 15th July, 1925; pathologist, July, 1925; offg. medl. supt., Mar., 1929; princpl., medl. coll., Rangoon, Mar., 1933; prof. of medicine, Rangoon Univ., Nov., 1935.

MORSE, HENRY MERRET RAVENSBURY, Indian State Rlys. (divl. mechl. engr., N.W. Rly) (b. 31st May, 1893).—Joined the service as asst. loco. supt., 5th Oct., 1915; divl. rolling stock officer, Dec., 1927; offg. supt., workshops, N.W. rly., Mar., 1936; and again, Mar., 1937.

MORTLOCK, Rev. ARTHUR GEORGE, Indian Eccles. Estabt. (junn. chaplain). Apptd. junior chaplain, Madras, 1st Nov., 1931.

MORTON, JOHN PERCIVAL, Indian Police (asst. supt.) (b. 15th May, 1911).—Joined the service as proby. asst. supt., Punjab, 24th Nov., 1930; confd., Nov., 1932.

MORTON, KENNETH VALENTINE FREELAND, Indian C.S. (asst. commr., Punjab) (b. 13th May, 1907).—Educ. at Edinburgh Academy and University College, Oxford; joined the service, 14th October, 1930; arrived in India, Nov., 1930, and served in the Punjab as asst. commr.; under-secty. to govt., Punjab, pol. and home depts., Nov., 1934, to Oct., 1936.

MORTON, VIVIAN WALTER, late Survey of India (b. 10th Dec., 1880).—Joined the service, 15th Nov., 1900; extra asst. supt., Jan., 1909; supt., Mar., 1930; ret'd., Dec., 1935.

MOSELY, ARCHIE GERARD, Indian C.S. (judge, high ct., Rangoon) (b. 6th March, 1883).—Educ. at Clifton, and Wadham Coll., Oxford; apptd. after exam. of 1906; arrived, 19th Dec., 1907, and served in Burma as asst. commr.; dep. commr. (provl.), June, 1918; dist. and sess. judge, Jan., 1923; offg. judge, high ct., Rangoon; July, 1929; confd., Feb., 1932.

MOSS, BENJAMIN HUGH, M.I.Struct.E., A.M.I.S.E., late Indian Service of Engrs. (b. 4th Feb., 1881).—Joined the service, 8th Aug., 1901; I.A.R.O., from 13th Aug., 1918, to 3rd April, 1919; asst. engr., April, 1919; asst. exec. engr., Jan., 1920; exec. engr., Nov., 1924; ret'd., Feb., 1936.

MOSS, CHARLES HENRY DE VERE, Indian Police (supt., Bombay) (b. 30th Oct., 1898).—Joined the service as asst. supt., Bombay, 16th June, 1923; offg. supt., Apl., 1929; confd., July, 1933; awarded King's police medal, Jan., 1931.

MOSS, ERIC DE VERE, Indian C.S. (mag. and collr., United Provs.) (b. 13th April, 1896).—Educ. at Dulwich Coll. and Victoria Coll., Jersey; joined the service, 15th Oct., 1923, and served in United Provs. as asst. mag. and collr.; offg. mag. and collr., Oct., 1925; asst. commr., April, 1926; offg. dep. commr., Oct., 1926; offg. secty., bd. of rev., U.P., Mar., 1932; mag. and collr., Dec., 1934.

MOSS, RICHARD CECIL, A.M.I.C.E., B.E., B.A., B.A.I., late Indian State Railways (b. 24th May, 1879).—Joined the service, 1st Oct., 1902, as asst. engr.; exec. engr., 1st Oct., 1912; ditto, and asst. agent, E. Bengal Rly., Oct., 1914; on mil. duty from July, 1917, to Sept., 1919; suptg. engr. (supv.), May, 1923; dep. chief engr. (provl.), E.B. Rly., May, 1925; confd., Feb., 1928; offg. secty., rly. bd., Apl., 1929; contr. of stores, N.W. Rly., Oct., 1929; ret'd., May, 1934.

MOSSE, ARTHUR HENRY EYRE, Lt.-Col., C.I.E., I.A., ret'd., late Pol. Dept., Govt. of India (b. 28th Sept., 1877).—First commn. dated 4th Aug., 1897; in mil. emp. to Dec., 1901; asst. pol. agent, Oct., 1903; asst. pol. agent, Aden, June, 1904; asst. to resdt. at Baroda, Oct., 1909, to Nov., 1911; asst. resdt., Aden, Feb., 1914; on special duty under pol. resdt., Persian Gulf, Dec., 1915, to Jan., 1918; asst. pol. agent, Mahi Kantha, March, 1918; pol. agent, Kathia-

war, Aug., 1918; pol. agent, Mahi Kantha, Feb., 1923; in foreign service as vice-president of admn., Bhavnagar, March, 1927; offg. A.G.G. in States of W.I., July, 1931; ret'd., Sept., 1932; C.I.E., Jan., 1933; author of "My Somali Book."

MOTI LAL, AGGARWAL, LALA, Indian Service of Engrs. (b. 7th Jan., 1906) (asst. exec. engr., Punjab).—Joined the service as apprentice engr., 19th Nov., 1927; proby. asst. exec. engr., Nov., 1928; confd., Nov., 1929.

MOTI RAM, B.Sc., Indian Service of Engrs. (exec. engr., United Provs.) (b. 16th Feb., 1897).—Joined the service, 19th Nov., 1920, as apprentice engr.; asst. exec. engr., Jan., 1922; offg. exec. engr., May, 1928; confd., Jan., 1931; p.a. to ch. engr. and under secty. to govt. (I.B.), July, 1937.

MOULD, ARTHUR E., V.D., A.M.I.C.E., late Indian State Rlys. (b. 15th Sept., 1879).—Joined the service as bridge draughtsman, 23rd Nov., 1906; asst. engr., Jan., 1908; in milty. employ from 1st Jan., 1916, to 1st Feb., 1920; bridge engr., June, 1920; dep. ch. engr. (bridges), July, 1930; ret'd., Nov., 1934.

MOULD, SAM CARTER, C.I.E., B.A. (Cantab.), late Indian Service of Engrs. (b. 27th Sept., 1880).—Joined the service as asst. engr., 1st Oct., 1904; exec. engr., Oct., 1912; offg. chief engr., Lloyd Barrage, March, 1925; offg. suptg. engr., Rohri Canals Circle, Oct., 1925; offg. ch. engr., Lloyd Barrage and C.C., April, 1928; suptg. engr. (provl. perm.), July, 1931; confd., Nov., 1931; offg. jt. ch. engr., Nov., 1931; C.I.E., June, 1932; ret'd., Sept., 1935.

MOULD, WILLIAM CRAFTNANT, V.D., late Indian State Rlys. (E.I. Rly.) (b. 8th Aug., 1875).—Joined the service as asst. carr. and wagon supt. 8th April, 1905; dist. ditto, Jan., 1919; offg. dist. supt., June, 1919; confd., Dec., 1920; pers. asst. to dep. ch. mech. engr., April, 1925; ditto to ch. mech. engr., Dec., 1926; dep. ch. mech. engr., March, 1927; ret'd., Sept., 1931.

MOWBRAY, ROBERT, M.A., India Office (rly. adviser and govt. dir. of Indian rly. companies) (b. 31st Mar., 1877).—Educ. at Manchester Gr. Sch., and Brasenose Coll., Oxford (scholar, 1896-1900; Green Prize, 1905); apptd. junr. clerk, acctnt.-gen.'s dept., India Office, 29th Oct., 1900; joint editor of India Office List, Nov., 1907, to July, 1913; assessor of income tax, March, 1910, to June, 1917; senr. clerk, July, 1913; clerk to the commrs. of income tax, Oct., 1920; principal, April, 1921; actg. dep. clerk of Council, 1st Dec., 1928; confd., Feb., 1931; rly. adviser and govt. dir. of Indian rly. companies, June, 1931; member, Indian staty. rly. bd. ctee, June, 1933.

MOWDUDUR RAHMAN, B.A., Barr., Bengal Judl. Dept.—Joined the service as judge, Calcutta small cause ct., 14th Jan., 1923.

MRA, MAUNG SHWE, B.A., Indian C.S. (asst. commr., Burma) (b. 18th Feb., 1910).—Educ. at National High School, Akyab, Rangoon Univ., and Christ Church, Oxford; joined the service, 9th Oct., 1934; arrived in India, Nov., 1934, and served in Burma as asst. commr.

MUAZZAM HUSSAIN KHAN, M.A., Indian Police (asst. supt., Bengal (b. 1st Sept., 1903).—Joined the service as proby. asst. supt., 1st July, 1928; confd., Feb., 1931; sup. (temp.), May, 1936.

MUDALIAR, SIR RAMASWAMI, Diwan Bahadur (Adviser to the secty. of State for India) (b. 14th Oct., 1887).—Member, Legislative Council, Madras, 1920-26; member, Council of State, 1930; member, Indian Legislative Assembly, 1931-34; member, Round Table Conference, all sessions, and Federal Structure Committee; member, Indian Franchise Committee; member, Special Textile Tariff Board; member, Council of India, July, 1936; Kt., Feb., 1937; advisor to Secty. of State for India, Apl., 1937.

MUDALIYAR, C. TADULINGA (see Tadulinga, M.C.).

MUDIE, ROBERT FRANCIS, C.I.E., O.B.E., Indian C.S. (mag. and collr., United Provs.) (b. 24th Aug., 1890).—Educ. at Fettes Coll., and King's Coll., Cambridge; apptd. after exam. of 1914; arrived, 1st Nov., 1915; on mil. duty with territories to Apl., 1919; asst. mag. and collr., United Provs., Apl., 1919; O.B.E., June, 1919; joint mag., Dec., 1920; dep. commr. of income tax, Dec., 1921; offg. dep. commr., March, 1922; special rent officer in addn., July, 1924; offg. mag. and collr., Oct., 1925; confd., May, 1932; offg. dep. sec. to govt., rev. dept., April, 1926; offg. settlement officer, Oct., 1926; jt. secty., Indian round table conf., London, Nov., 1930-Jan., 1931; C.I.E., June, 1935; on spl. duty with rly. bd., Apl., 1936; magte. and collr., Dec., 1937.

MUEENUDDIN, GHULAM, Indian C.S. (asst. commr., Punjab) (b. 6th May, 1906).—Educ. at Aitchison College, Lahore, Punjab University and Oxford University; joined the service, 13th Oct., 1930; arrived in India, Nov., 1930, and served in the Punjab as asst. commr.; offg. dep. commr., May, 1935, and again April, 1936; addtl. dist. and sess. judge, Nov., 1936, to Apl., 1937.

MUHAMMAD ABDUL AZIZ (see Abdul Aziz, M.).

MUHAMMAD ABDUL GHANI ANSARI, B.A., LL.B., Income Tax Dept., United Provs. (asst. commr.) (b. 19th Oct., 1890).—Joined the service as proby. dep. coll., 23rd May, 1913; confd., May, 1915; inc. tax officer, May, 1926; asst. commr., July, 1927.

MUHAMMAD ABDUL HAMID, SHAIKH, B.A., Indian Service of Engrs. (under secty. to govt. (South) Punjab) (b. 16th Sept., 1897).—Joined the service, 6th Nov., 1921; asst. engr., Nov., 1922; asst. exec. engr., Aug., 1923; on deputation to Bahawalpur State, Apl., 1930; exec. engr., Nov., 1931; under secty. to Govt., Apl., 1937.

MUHAMMAD ABDUL KHALIK, B.A., Indian Police (supt., Bengal) (b. 25th Jan., 1886).—Joined the service as proby. dep. supt., 23rd March, 1912; confd., Mar., 1914; supt., Dec., 1930.

MUHAMMAD ABDUL MAJID, KHAN SAHIB, Khwaja, M.B.E., B.A., Punjab C.S. (dep. commr.) (b. 24th Aug., 1884).—Joined the service, 18th Jan., 1909; extra asst. commr., Apl., 1913; offg. dir. of land records, Punjab, May, 1929; member Council of State, Feb., 1930; dep. commr., confd., May, 1936.

MUHAMMAD ABDUL MUMIN, C.I.E., B.A., Khan Bahadur, late Bengal C.S. (b. 28th June, 1876).—Joined the service, 25th May, 1897; sub. dep. collr., May, 1902; dep. mag., March, 1907; under-sec. to govt. of Bengal, rev. dept., Oct., 1916; mag. and collr., Oct., 1918; offg. dir. of land records, Bengal, March, 1925; offg. commr. Apl., 1929; confd., May, 1930; ret'd., June, 1931; C.I.E., Jan., 1935.

MUHAMMAD ABDULLAH, KHAN SAHIB HAFIZ, CHAUDHRI (dep. dir. of agric., Punjab) (b. Oct., 1885).—Joined the service, 15th April, 1907, and held non-gazetted appts. to 9th May, 1916; asst. dir. of agric., May, 1916; apptd. to I.A.S. from 29th Oct., 1921; dep. dir. of agric., Oct., 1921; asst. prof., Punjab agric. coll., April, 1924.

MUHAMMAD ABDUR RAHMAN, SIR, Kt., Advocate (judge, high ct., Rangoon).—Dean of Faculty of Law, Delhi Univ., 1927-1934; Vice Chancellor, Delhi Univ., 1930-1934; apptd., judge, high ct., Rangoon, June, 1937.

MUHAMMAD ABOO ABDULLAH, Indian Police (asst. supt.) (b. 1st Sept., 1905).—Apptd. proby. asst. supt., Bengal, 6th Feb., 1930; confd., Apl., 1932.

MUHAMMAD, AFZAL HUSSAIN, M.Sc. (Punjab), M.A. (Cantab.), Dept. of Agric., Punjab (entomologist to govt., Punjab) (b. 10th Mar., 1889).—Joined the service, 6th Jan., 1919; entomologist to govt., Punjab, Sept., 1919; ditto and princpl., Punjab Agric. Coll., Lyallpur, Apl., 1933.

MUHAMMAD AHMAD SAID KHAN (see Ahmad, S. K. M.).

MUHAMMAD AKRAM, AGHA, B.A., late Indian Police (b. 17th June, 1881).—Joined the service, Sept., 1906, as dep. supt.; dist. supt., Nov., 1919; ret'd., June, 1936.

MUHAMMAD AKRAM KHAN, KHWAJA, B.A., Indian Police (dist. supt., Central Provs.) (b. 15th May, 1884).—Joined the service as dep. supt., Feb., 1908; awarded King's police medal, Jan., 1918; on foreign service as inspr.-genl. of police, Bhopal State, 7th Nov., 1921; dist. supt., Sept., 1926.

MUHAMMAD ALI KHAN, Indian Police (supt., Orissa) (b. 1st Sept., 1900).—Joined the service as asst. supt., Bihar and Orissa, 15th June, 1924; offg. supt., Apl., 1932, and again, Feb., 1933; supt., Apl., 1937.

MUHAMMAD ALI, KHAN SAHIB, late Indian Service of Engrs. (United Provs.) (b. 15th Sept., 1874).—Joined the service, 11th April, 1898; asst. engr., Nov., 1918; offg. exec. engr., March, 1922; confd., Oct., 1924; ret'd., Sept., 1929.

MUHAMMAD ANWAR ALI, Indian Police (b. 1st Nov., 1906).—Joined the service as proby. asst. supt., Punjab, 14th Jan., 1930; confd., Jan., 1932.

MUHAMMAD AZAM, KHAN, Indian Service of Engrs. (exec. engr.) (b. 3rd Feb., 1902).—Joined the service as asst. exec. engr., N.W.F.P., 1st Oct., 1924; exec. engr., Oct., 1933.

MUHAMMAD AZIM HUSAIN, B.A., Indian C.S. (asst. commr., Punjab) (b. 5th Oct., 1913).—Educ. at Central Model School, Lahore, Punjab Univ. and Christ's College, Cambridge; joined the service, 25th Sept.; arrived in India, Dec., 1937, and served in the Punjab as asst. commr.

MUHAMMAD AZFAR, B.A., LL.B., Indian C.S. (jt. mag. and dep. collr., Bihar) (b. 1st April, 1909).—Educ. at the Queen's Intermediate College, Benares, Allahabad University and Jesus College, Oxford; joined the service, 6th Oct., 1933; arrived in India, Nov., 1933, and served in Bihar and Orissa as asst. mag. and collr.

MUHAMMAD BAZL-UL-LAH SAHIB, Sir Khan Bahadur, B.A., C.I.E., O.B.E., late Madras Civil Service (b. 31st Jan., 1875).—Joined the service, 9th April, 1895, and held non-gazetted appt. to 8th July, 1902; dep. coll., July, 1902; O.B.E., Dec., 1919; offg. ch. presdy. magte., March, 1920; collr. and dist. magte., July, 1920; C.I.E., June, 1923; offg. dir. of industries, Aug., 1926; retd., Jan., 1930; apptd. member, Madras public service commn., Jan., 1930; chairman, ditto, Oct., 1932; knighted, Jan., 1935; vacated office of chairman, 30th Jan., 1935.

MUHAMMAD EHSANUR RAHMAN, B.A., Impl. Customs Service (asst. collr.) (b. 15th Sept., 1896).—Joined the service, 17th Jan., 1917; asst. collr., Mar., 1923; offg. collr., Oct., 1934; and again, Apl., 1937.

MUHAMMAD FAKHAR-UD-DIN, MIAN, Indian Service of Engrs. (exec. engr., Punjab) (b. 28th July, 1889).—Joined Oct., 1913, as asst. engr.; asst. engr., Jan., 1920; exec. engr., Oct., 1922; under-secty. to govt., irrign. branch, April, 1928.

MUHAMMAD FAZLUL KARIM, KHAN BAHADUR, B.A., late Bengal C.S. (b. 23rd Oct., 1881).—Joined the service, 3rd Aug., 1904; dep. mag., Apl., 1906; ditto and dep. collr., July, 1929; offg. mag. and collr. Aug., 1928; and, again, Dec., 1934; offg. addl. ch. presdy. mag., Aug., 1931; retd., Oct., 1936.

MUHAMMAD, GHULAM, see Ghulam, M.

MUHAMMAD, GUL (see Gul Muhammad).

MUHAMMAD HABIBULLAH, Sir, Sahib Bahadur, K.C.S.I., K.C.I.E.—Apptd. member of exec. council, Madras, 17th Dec., 1920; knighted, June, 1922; member of royal commn. on the superior public services in India, 1923-24; K.C.I.E., June, 1924; member of govr.-gen.'s exec. council, 31st Dec., 1924, to 31st Mar., 1930; K.C.S.I., June, 1927.

MUHAMMAD HAMID, KHAN BAHADUR, B.A., late Bihar C.S. (b. 16th Sept., 1883).—Joined the service, 29th Oct., 1904; dep. mag. and dep. collr., May, 1906; offg. addl. dist. mag. in charge, Patna city municipality, Jan., 1931; offg. secty. to govt., Bihar and Orissa, local self-govt. dept., May, 1935; mag. and collr., Aug., 1935; retd. May, 1937.

MUHAMMAD HAYAT KHAN, NAWAB, Malik, C.S.I., late Punjab Civil Service (b. June, 1875).—Joined the service on 12th April, 1901, as extra asst. commr.; dep. commr., Oct., 1921; offg. commr., C.S.I., Jan., 1931; retd., Aug., 1931.

MUHAMMAD HUSAIN, KHWAJA, B.A., Indian Police (dist. supt., Central Provs.) (b. 18th Mar., 1897).—Joined the service as asst. dist. supt., 14th June, 1920; offg. dist. supt., Feb., 1924; confd., March, 1928.

MUHAMMAD IBRAHIM, Mian, B.A., Indian Service of Engrs. (exec. engr., Punjab) (b. 7th June, 1899).—Joined the service as apprentice engr., 15th Oct., 1923; asst. exec. engr., Oct., 1924; offg. exec. engr., Sept., 1933; confd., Oct., 1933.

MUHAMMAD, INAMULLAH KHAN, Indian Service of Engrs. (asst. exec. engr., Bihar) (b. 7th Aug., 1908).—Joined the service as asst. exec. engr., Bihar and Orissa, on probn., 4th Apl., 1932; confd., Aug., 1933.

MUHAMMAD INAMUR RAHIM (see Rahim, M. I.).

MUHAMMAD ISMAIL, B.Sc., A.M.I.E.S., Indian Service of Engrs. (asst. exec. engr., Punjab) (b. Nov., 1901).—Joined the service as temp. engr., 4th July, 1927; apptd. to I.S.E. as asst. exec. engr., Punjab, July, 1931.

MUHAMMAD ISMAIL, Khan Bahadur, Barr.-at-law (judge, high court, Allahabad).—Apptd., Aug., 1937.

MUHAMMAD KHAN, SAHIBZADA SARDAR, Bombay Civil Service (collr. and dist. magte.) (b. 22nd May, 1886).—Joined the service as proby. dep. collr., 28th Aug., 1910; confd., June, 1913; offg. dep. commr., Feb., 1929; offg. collr. and dist. magte., Apl., 1930; confd., Dec., 1931.

MUHAMMAD MOOSA KHAN, Lt.-Col., I.M.S., Jail Dept., Madras (supt.) (b. 3rd Oct., 1887).—First commn. dated 8th Dec., 1915; in milty. employ to 27th July, 1925; supt., central jail, Clannanore, June, 1928; ditto, Vellore, June, 1931; offg. inspr. genl. of prisons, Jan., 1934.

MUHAMMAD MUSANNA, KHAN BAHADUR SHAIKH, United Provs. C.S. (mag. and collr.) (b. Feb., 1883).—Joined the service, 16th Mar., 1906; dep. collr., May, 1908; mag. and collr., Jan., 1933.

MUHAMMAD NUR, Sir, KHWAJA, C.B.E., KHAN BAHADUR, Judl. dept., Bihar (judge, high ct., Patna).—Apptd. dep. president, legislative council, 8th Aug., 1922; president, ditto, 21st November, 1922; C.B.E., Mar., 1929; judge, high ct., Oct., 1930; knighted, Feb., 1937.

MUHAMMAD RAZA, KHAN BAHADUR SAHYID, late Judicial Dept., United Provs. (b. 28th Jan., 1874).—Joined the service as offg. munsif, 16th June, 1899; confd., Nov., 1902; sub-judge (s.p.t.), Jan., 1914; confd., April, 1914; dist. and sess. judge, Aug., 1920; judge, ch. ct. of Oudh, Lucknow, Nov., 1925; retd., Jan., 1934.

MUHAMMAD SAID, KHAN BAHADUR, Mian, Barr., late Indian Police (b. 29th Sept., 1880).—Joined the service as proby. dep. supt., 12th Dec., 1906; confd., Dec., 1907; supt., Jan., 1927; retd., Sept., 1935.

MUHAMMAD SAID, MIRZA, M.A., late Indian Educl. Service (b. 1st Aug., 1886).—Joined the service, 4th Oct., 1909, as asst. prof., govt. coll., Lahore; prof. ditto, Feb., 1921, and again Sept., 1925; asst. sec. to govt. of India, educ. and health dept., Oct., 1921; prinpl., Ludhiana Coll., April, 1924; prin., govt. inter. coll., Rohtak, May, 1927; retd., Sept., 1931.

MUHAMMAD SALEH AKBAR HYDARI, C.I.E., Indian C.S. (jt. secty., govt. of India, dept. of educ., health and lands) (b. 12th Oct., 1894).—Educ. at Bombay Univ. and Balliol Coll., Oxford; joined the service, 24th Oct., 1919; arrived, 9th Feb., 1920, and served in Madras as asst. collr. and mag.; sub-collr. and jt. mag., Jan., 1922; offg. under-sec. to govt., development dept., Madras, Oct., 1923; under-sec., govt. of India, dept. of educ., health and lands, June, 1924; offg. dep. sec. ditto, Nov., 1925; agent to govt. of India in Ceylon, Oct., 1927; secty. to impl. council of agricl. research, June, 1929; jt. secty., Indian States delegation to Indian round table conf., London, 2nd session, Sept.-Dec., 1931; dep. secty., govt. of India, dept. of educ., health and lands, Apl., 1932; adviser to delegate for Hyderabad, Indian round table conf., 3rd session, Nov.-Dec., 1932; offg. jt. secty. to govt. of India, dept. of educ., health and lands, June, 1934 confd., Oct., 1935; C.I.E., Jan., 1935; collr. (provl. subs.), Oct., 1935; confd., Oct., 1936.

MUHAMMAD SANA ULLAH, KHAN BAHADUR, B.A., M.Sc., F.C.S., Archaeological Survey Dept. (b. 16th Nov., 1886).—Apptd. archæological chemist in India, 29th June, 1917.

MUHAMMAD SHAFI, Sir MIAN (see Shafi, Sir Mian Muhammad).

MUHAMMAD SHAHAB-UD-DIN, B.A., B.L., Indian C.S. (dist. and sess. judge, Madras) (b. 13th May, 1895).—Educ. at Madras Christian Coll. and Law Coll.; joined the service, 12th Dec., 1921; arrived, 2nd Jan., 1922, and served in Madras as asst. collr. and mag.; sub-collr. and jt. mag., June, 1925; on judl. training at Salem, July, 1929; offg. dist. and sess. judge, June, 1931; confd., Jan., 1935.

MUHAMMAD SHARIFF, SHAIKH, Indian Service of Eners. (exec. engr., Punjab) (b. 23rd Nov., 1895).—Joined the service as asst. engr., 1st Oct., 1919; asst. exec., Jan., 1920; exec. engr., Oct., 1931.

MUHAMMAD, SHEIKH TAJ. (see Taj Muhammad, Sheikh).
