MUHAMMAD SIRAJUDDIN SAHIB, B.A., Indian Police (asst. supt.) (b. 7th June, 1904).—Joined the service as proby. asst. supt., Madras, 14th June, 1930; confd., June, 1932.

MUHAMMAD SULAIMAN, KHAN BAHADUR, B.A., Indian Service of Engrs. (exec. engr., Central P.W.D.) (b. 5th June, 1892).—Joined the service, 1st Sept., 1917; asst. exec. engr., Oct., 1920; offg. exec. engr., March, 1926; confd., Oct., 1929; offg. suptg. engr., Aug., 1936.

MUHAMMAD TAQI, KHAN BAHADUR, B.A., late Indian Police (b. 3rd June, 1882).—Joined the service as dep. supt., 1st July, 1906; supt., March, 1924; retd., Dec., 1932.

MUHAMMAD USMAN, Sir, Sahib Bahadur, Khan Bahadur, K.C.I.E.—Apptd. member of govt.'s exec. council, Madras, 30th Mar., 1925; Kt., June, 1928; K.C.I.E., Jan., 1933; actg. govt. of Madras, May to Aug., 1934.

MUHAMMAD, WALI (see Wali Muhammad).
MUHAMMAD ZAHURUDDIN AHMAD FARUQI (see Zahuruddin, A. F. M.).

MUHSIN ALI, B.A., M.Sc., Indian Service of Engrs. (exec. engr., United Provs.) (b. 10th Aug., 1888).—Joined the service, 1st Oct., 1912; asst. engr., Nov., 1912; exec. engr., Oct., 1921; prof. of civil engrg., Thomason Coll., Roorkee, Oct., 1928; offg. suptg. engr., Mar., 1935.

MUIR, DAVID IAN RENNIE, Indian Civil Service (jt. secty., bd. of rev., Madras) (b. 11th Feb., 1903).—Educ. at George Watson's College, Univ. of Edinburgh, and Exeter Coll., Oxford; joined the service, 24th Oct., 1927; arrived, Dec., 1927, and served in Madras as asst. mag. and collr.; sub-collr., July, 1932; jt. secty., bd. of rev., Madras, Apl., 1936.

MUIR, GEORGE BASIL FAIRLIE, late Indian C.S. (b. 29th Aug., 1882).—Educ. at Rugby, and Corpus Christi Coll., Oxford; apptd. after exam. of 1904; arrived, 26th Nov., 1905, and served in the United Provs. as asst. mag. and collr., and asst. commr.; joint mag., Nov., 1913; tutor and guardian to H.H. the Raja of Tehri from May, 1915, to Oct., 1919; rev. sec. to govt., March, 1921; mag. and collr. and sec. to govt., March, 1922; offg. commr. of inc. tax, U.P., Mar., 1930; commr., May, 1933; retd., Feb., 1938.

MUIR, WILLIAM ANGUS, M.A., M.C., late Indian Forest Service (Central Provs.) (b. 6th Oct., 1893).—Joined the service as asst. consvr., 10th Jan., 1922; dep. consvr., Aug., 1924; retd., 30th Jan., 1929.

MUIRHEAD, ALEXANDER KERSHAW, Indian State Rlys. (contr. of stores, N.W. Rly.) (b. 14th Aug., 1886).—Joined the service as storekeeper, 10th Sept., 1907; on mily. duty from 4th Jan., 1917; dist. contr. of stores, July, 1918; contr., bales, Calcutta, Jan.-June, 1922; offg. dep. agent, Apl., 1929; offg. ch. personnel officer, Apl., 1932; offg. ch. contr. of stores, N.W. rly., May, 1933; and again, Feb., 1934; confd., May, 1934.

MUKERJEE, AMULLYA CHARAN, Rai Bahadur, B.Sc., Indian Finance Dept. (b. 20th Feb., 1885).—Joined the service, 20th Feb., 1906; supt., July, 1920; asst. sec. to govt. of India, fin. dept., April, 1922; examr., outside acct., Calcutta, March, 1923; offg. dep. contr. of currency, Calcutta, Oct., 1926; currency officer, Calcutta, Feb., 1929; dep. contr. of the currency, Calcutta, Apl., 1929; ditto, Bombay, Aug., 1934; ch. acct., res. bank of India, Jan., 1935; ch. auditor, E.B. rly. and govt. exr. of accts., A.B. rly., Nov., 1935.

MUKERJEE, SUBODH CHANDRA, M.A., late Indian Finance Dept. (b. 19th Aug., 1891).—Joined the service, 20th April, 1914; asst. auditor, June, 1918; asst. acctnt.-gen., Bengal, Sept., 1921; examr. of local fin. accts., Bengal, June, 1922; govt. examr. of accts., B.N. Rly., Feb., 1927, attached officer, office of dir. rly. audit, Jan., 1930; with finl. commr., rlwys., Feb., 1930; dep. ch. auditor, N.W. rly., May, 1932; retd., April, 1934.

MUKERJI, BRAHMADEVA, M.A., Indian C.S. (asst. mag. and collr., B. and O.) (b. 30th Oct., 1912).—Educ. at Govt. Intermediate College, Allahabad, and Allahabad Univ., joined the service, 21st Sept., 1937; arrived in India, Oct., 1937, and served in B. & O. as asst. mag. and collr.

MUKERJI, JANAKI NATH, B.A., O.B.E., late India Posts and Telegraphs (b. 7th May, 1879).—Joined the service, 25th Oct., 1900; asst. supt., Oct., 1901; elec. engr.-in-chief, Dec., 1921; O.B.E., June, 1925; offg. dep. chief engr., 'phones, July, 1925; offg. dir., tele. engrg., Aug., 1927; offg. P.M.G., central circle, Apl., 1931; and again, Dec., 1932; confd., Sept., 1933; ret'd., May, 1934.

MUKERJI, PARESH NATH, Rai Bahadur C.B.E., M.A., India Posts and Tels. (p.m.g. (b. 22nd Dec., 1882).—Joined the service as proby. supt., 9th Sept., 1904; supt., June, 1907; on depn. to international postal congress, Stockholm, May, 1924; ditto, London, Mar.-Aug., 1929; asst. dir.-genl., June, 1927; offg. dep. dir.-genl., Jan., 1931, and again, Jan., 1932; dep. p.m.g., Oct., 1932; offg. p.m.g., Mar., 1933; confd., Mar., 1934.

MUKHARJI, ABHAYAPADA, B.A., Indian C.S. (jt. mag. and dep. collr., Bihar) (b. 1st Dec., 1899).—Educ. at Patna Univ. and Exeter Coll., Oxford; joined the service, 24th Oct., 1924; arrived, 16th Dec., 1924, and served in Bihar and Orissa as asst. mag. and collr.; addl. dist. and sess. judge, Mar., and again Nov., 1932.

MUKHARJI, ADITYA NATH, M.A., Ph.D., late Indian Educl. Service (b. Sept., 1875).—Apppt. prinpl., Sanskrit Coll., Calcutta, 16th Feb., 1924; ret'd., Apl., 1931.

MUKHARJI, AGHOR NATH, Babu, B.A., LL.B., late Judicial Dept., United Provs. (b. Oct., 1875).—Joined the service as offg. munisif; confd., Feb., 1908; sub-judge (s.p.t.), Sept., 1918; confd., April, 1919; 2nd addl. dist. and sess. judge, Feb., 1928; ret'd., Oct., 1930.

MUKHARJI, AMULYA CHANDRA, B.Sc., Indian Service of Engrs. (exec. engr.) (b. 5th Mar., 1898).—Joined the service, 29th Oct., 1921; offg. exec. engr., April, 1927; confd., Jan., 1932.

MUKHARJI, ASHUTOSH, M.A., Indian Educl. Service (principal, science coll., Patna) (b. 16th Apl., 1885).—Joined the service, 2nd July, 1906; prof., Patna Coll., May, 1919; princpl., science coll., Patna, Feb., 1935.

MUKHARJI, BHABANI CHARAN, B.A., Indian C.S. (jt. mag. and dep. collr., Bihar, (b. 1st August, 1901).—Educ. at Calcutta Univ. and Oxford (non-collegiate); joined the service, 28th Oct., 1925; arrived, 6th Dec., 1925, and served in Bihar and Orissa as asst. mag. and collr.; under-sec. to govt. finl. dept., Sept., 1929; addl. under-secty. to govt. of India, dept. of educn., health and lands, July, 1931; offg. dep. commr., May, 1932; offg. magte. and collr., Sept., 1932; and again, Mar., 1933; offg. dep. secty. to govt., pol. and appts. depts., July, 1935.

MUKHARJI, CHARU CHANDRA, O.B.E., B.A., Rai Bahadur, Bihar Civil Service (mag. and collr.) (b. 18th Nov., 1882).—Joined the service as dep. mag. and dep. collr. (s.p.t.), 10th Feb., 1903; confd., Nov., 1904; offg. mag., collr. and sec., board of revenue, July, 1926; confd., Nov., 1926; O.B.E., Dec., 1930; offg. commr., July, 1933.

MUKHARJI, DEBABRATA, B.L., late Judicial Dept., Bengal (b. 18th Feb., 1875).—Joined the service as munisif, 4th Aug., 1900; sub-judge, Oct., 1919; ret'd., Feb., 1930.

MUKHARJI, DEBENDRA KUMAR, Rai Bahadur, B.A., late Assam Civil Service (b. 24th Dec., 1877).—Joined the service as sub dep. collr., 27th May, 1899; extra asst. commr., April, 1912; offg. dep. commr., Feb., 1928; confd., June, 1928; ret'd., Jan., 1933.

MUKHARJI, KSHIROD LAL, Rai Bahadur, B.A., late Bengal C.S. (b. 31st Mar., 1881).—Joined the service 28th July, 1904; dep. magte. and dep. collr., Mar., 1906; magte. and collr., June, 1932; ret'd., Mar., 1936.

MUKHARJI, SIR, LAL GOPAL, Rai Bahadur, Kt., B.A., LL.B., late Judicial Dept., United Provs. (b. 29th July, 1874).—Joined the service, 6th May, 1902, as munisif; sub. judge, March, 1914; judge, small cause ct., Sept., 1919; dist. and sess. judge, June, 1921; offg. addl. judge, high ct., Allahabad, Dec., 1923; judge, high ct., Allahabad, March, 1926; Kt., June, 1932; offg. ch. justice, high ct., Allahabad, Oct., 1932; ret'd., July, 1934.

MUKHARJI, SIR MANMATHA NATH, Kt., M.A., B.L., late Judl. Dept., Bengal (b. 28th Oct., 1874).—Joined the service, 22nd Feb., 1901; judge, Calcutta high ct., Nov., 1926; actg. ch. justice, July, 1934, and Nov., 1935; knighted, Jan., 1935; ret'd., Oct., 1936.

MUKHARJI, NANI GOPAL (No. 1), late Bengal Civil Service (b. 10th Jan., 1876).—Joined the service, 20th Apl., 1902; munisif, July, 1904; sub-judge, Dec., 1922; dist. and sess. judge, Nov., 1928; ret'd., Jan., 1931.

MUKHARJI, NIKHIL RANJAN, B.A., late Income Tax Dept., Bengal (b. Nov., 1877).—Held non-gazetted appt. prior to 1st April, 1914; dep. magte. and dep. collr., March, 1916; inc. tax officer, April, 1922, offg. asst. commr., Nov., 1926; confd., June, 1928; ret'd., Dec., 1932.

MUKHARJI, PANCHU GOPAL, Rai Bahadur, M.A., B.L., late Assam Civil Service (b. 19th Jan., 1878).—Joined the service, 11th March, 1902, as extra asst. commr.; dep. mag. and dep. collr., April, 1906; dep. commr., May, 1925; ret'd., Jan., 1933.

MUKHARJI, SATISH CHANDRA, B.A., late Bihar Civil Service (b. 3rd Nov., 1881).—Joined the service as proby. dep. magte. and dep. collr., 12th Oct., 1903; confd., Nov., 1904; offg. secty. bd. of rev., Aug., 1928; confd., July, 1929; magte. and collr., July, 1929; ret'd., Mar., 1937.

MUKHARJI, SURENDRA NATH, Rai Bahadur, M.A., B.L., late Bihar and Orissa Civil Service (b. 11th Sept., 1877).—Joined the service, 16th Mar., 1906; munisif, Nov., 1907; sub-judge, May, 1922; dist. and sess. judge, June, 1930; ret'd., Sept., 1933.

MUKHARJI, TULSI DAS, B.P., late Bihar and Orissa Civil Service, Judicial Branch.—Joined the service, 11th May, 1902; sub-judge, Feb., 1917; ret'd., May, 1928.

MUKHARJI, UPANANDA, M.A., Indian Police (b. 21st Dec., 1909).—Joined the service as asst. supt. of police, Bengal, on probn., 12th Mar., 1934.

MUKHERJI, PRAMATHA NATH, M.A., Indian Audit Dept. (b. 18th Nov., 1880).—Joined the service, 26th May, 1902; asst. audit officer, Delhi Province, July, 1913; inspr. of local fund accts., Bengal, Apl., 1915; asst. contr. of war accts., June, 1919; contr. of surplus stores accts., Jan., 1922; dep. acct. genl., Burma, May, 1929; exr., outside audit. office of A.G., Bengal, Oct., 1932; offg. ch. auditor, E.I. Rly. and govt. exr. of accts., B.N. Rly., May, 1933; offg. ch. auditor, E. B. rly., Mar., 1934; offg. dep. ch. accts. officer, ditto, May, 1934; offg. ch. auditor, E.B. rly., Jan., 1935.

MUL CHAND BIJAWAT, RAI BAHADUR, B.A., Indian Service of Engineers (exec. engr., United Provs.) (b. 15th Jan., 1886).—Joined the service, 13th Oct., 1909; asst. engr., Oct., 1910; exec. engr., Oct., 1918; under-secty. to govt. p.w.d., United Provs., Feb., 1931; offg. suptg. engr., July, 1934.

MULES, SIR HORACE CHARLES, Kt., C.S.I., M.V.O., O.B.E., late Sind Commn.—Educ. at Wellington Coll.; joined the Sind Commn., 18th May, 1876, as supy. dep. collr.; designation changed to asst. collr. and mag., April, 1883; head asst. collr., Feb., 1894; dep. commr., Nov., 1895; collr. and dist. regr., Dec., 1900; dep. commr.; paper currency, Karachi, Sept., 1903; collr. and mag. and dist. regr., Nov., 1904; M.V.O., March, 1906; C.S.I., Dec., 1911; chairman, Karachi Port Trust, from Nov., 1909; O.B.E., Jan., 1919; Kt., June, 1919; ret'd.; May, 1920.

MULK RAJ, CHOPRA, LALA, Indian Service of Engineers (asst. exec. engr., Punjab) (b. 18th Aug., 1906).—Joined the service as apprentice engr., 24th July, 1930; apptd. to I.S.E. as asst. exec. engr., Punjab, July, 1931.

MULKANI, GOPALDAS GURBOMAL, M.A., LL.B., Judl. Dept., Bombay (asst. judge) (b. 20th Nov., 1889).—Joined the service, 1st Apl., 1918; asst. judge, Dec., 1934; offg. dist. and sess. judge, variously from May, 1934.

MULLA, T. N. (see Tej Narayan Mulla).

MULLAN, CHARLES SEYMOUR, M.A., Indian Civil Service (secty. educ. and self govt. depts. Assam) (b. 17th June, 1893).—Educ. at Trinity Coll., Dublin; on mil. service, from Nov., 1915; joined the service, 7th Oct., 1919; served in Assam as asst. commr., from 4th Dec., 1919; presdt., Manipur durbar, June, 1921, to Sept., 1922; offg. under-sect. to govt. of Assam, May, 1924; confd., Jan., 1925; offg. dep. commr., Apl., 1929; and again, Mar., 1933; provincial census supt., Assam, April, 1930; commr. of excise, etc., Assam, Nov., 1933; dep. commr., Apl., 1935; reform officer and addl. secty. to govt., Assam, Mar., 1935. secty. educ. and local self govt. depts., Apl., 1937.

MULLEN, NORMAN ARTHUR JOHN, Indian Police (asst. supt., Burma) (b. 15th Aug., 1909).—Joined the service as asst. dist. supt., Burma, 1st Dec., 1930; confd., June, 1933.

MULLINGS, SIR CLEMENT TUDWAY, C.S.I., late Indian Service of Engrs. (Madras) (b. 9th Apl., 1874).—Trained at R.I.E. Coll.; joined the service, 1st Oct., 1895, as asst. engr.; exec. engr., Oct., 1906; temp. suptg. engr., Nov., 1917; ditto (perm.), April, 1920; dep. sec. to govt., Aug., 1921; offg. chief engr., May, 1925; confd., April, 1926; consulting ch. engr., Cauvery (Mettur) project, May, 1927, to Feb., 1931; C.S.I., March, 1929; ret'd. from I.S.E., July, 1929; knighted, Aug., 1934.

MUMIN, MUHAMMAD ABDUL, see Muhammad Abdul Mumin.

MUNDLE, INDU BHUSAN, B.A., Indian C.S. (dist. and sess. judge, United Provs.) (b. 12th June, 1881).—Educ. at Bishop's Coll. Sch., Calcutta, and Calcutta Univ.; apptd., after exam. of 1904; arrived, 29th Dec., 1905, and served in the United Provs. as asst. mag. and collr.; jt. mag., Oct., 1914; sees. and sub-judge, May, 1918; dist. and sess. judge, Oct., 1922; ditto, 1st grade, Nov., 1934.

MUNGAVIN, GEORGE ALEXANDER GAVIN, O.B.E., late Indo-European Tel. dept. (b. 14th Feb., 1883).—Held non-gazetted appt. from 24th Jan., 1901, to 31st Dec., 1919; asst. supt., Jan., 1920; supt., Nov., 1925; asst. dir., Oct., 1929; ret'd. July, 1931.

MUNISHWAR CHAND, LALA, Rai Bahadur, B.A., Indian Service of Engrs. (exec. engr., Punjab) (b. 28th Sept., 1891).—Joined the service, 2nd Dec., 1916; asst. exec. engr., Oct., 1922; exec. engr., Oct., 1931; under-secty. to govt., irrign. bch., Punjab, Feb., 1935, to Mar., 1937.

MUNJE, HARI SHEORAM, RAO BAHADUR, M.A., LL.B., late Central Provs., C.S. (b. 27th Jan., 1878).—Joined the service as munsif, 12th Mar., 1903; sub-judge, Nov., 1909; dist. and sess. judge, Dec., 1928; ret'd., Jan., 1933.

MUNN, NAPIER (see Napier-Munn).

MUNNA LAL, RAI BAHADUR, LALA, B.A., LL.B., Judl. Dept., Punjab (b. 5th Aug., 1871).—Joined the service, 15th June, 1898; held non-gazetted appts. to Sept., 1912; extra asst. commr., Sept., 1912; sub-judge, Nov., 1914; member, Sikh Gurdwara tribunal, Nov., 1926.

MUNRO, ALEXANDER SINCLAIR, Indian C.S. (jt. mag. and collr., United Provinces) (b. 4th Sept., 1910).—Educ. at Edinburgh Academy and University College, Oxford; apptd. after exam. of 1933; arrived in India, Nov., 1934, and served in the United Provinces as asst. mag. and collr.; jt. magte., Mar., 1937.

MUNRO, CHARLES ALEXANDER, Public Works Dept., Punjab (b. 14th Aug., 1889).—Joined the service as inspr. of draglines, 29th March, 1928; exec. engr., dragline divn., Oct., 1933.

MUNRO, DONALD GEORGE, B.Sc., Indian Agric. Service (b. 4th Apl., 1895).—Apptd. dep. dir. of agric., Madras, 4th Dec., 1920.

MUNRO, GEORGE FRANCIS, M.A., late Indian Educl. Service (b. 12th Jan., 1875).—Apptd. to the I.E.S. as inspr. of schools, Burma, from 5th Feb., 1921; ret'd., Jan., 1931.

MUNRO, JOHN BENNET LORIMER, Indian C.S. (under secty. to govt., public dept., Madras) (b. 20th May, 1905).—Educ. at Univ. of Edinburgh and Corpus Christi Coll., Oxford; joined the service 15th Oct., 1928; arrived, Nov., 1928, and served in Madras as asst. collr. and mag.; sub collr. and jt. magte, July, 1930; under secty. to govt., public dept., Madras, Jan., 1935.

MUNSEY, SYDNEY THOMAS HENSMAN, B.Sc., A.M.I.C.E., Indian Service of Engrs. (exec. engr., United Provs.) (b. 12th Oct., 1889).—Joined the service as asst. engr., 11th Oct., 1911; in mil. employ from 14th Sept., 1915, to 11th Sept., 1919; exec. engr., Oct., 1920; offg. suptg. engr., Mar., 1935.

MURISON, CECIL CHARLES, F.R.C.S.E., D.P.H., D.T.M., Col., I.M.S. (ret'd.).—First commn., 27th Jan., 1900; in mil. employ to Oct., 1905, when he was transferred to Bombay Presdy. to officiate as civil surgeon, etc.; confd., Feb., 1909; on mil. service, Dec., 1914, to March, 1919; civil surg., Mar., 1919; reverted to milty. employ, June, 1927; ret'd., Jan., 1931.

MURISON, TAYLOR DAVID, Lt.-Col., I.M.S. ret'd. (b. 7th Nov., 1882).—First commission, 1st Aug., 1908; in mil. employ from Aug., 1908, to May, 1901; on plague duty in Assam, June, 1911; offg. plague officer, United Provs., Nov., 1914; asst. dir. of public health, United Provs., Nov., 1921; offg. dir. of public health, Assam, Dec., 1923; confd., Nov., 1924; offg. inspr.-genl. of civil hosp., Assam, Mar., 1937; ret'd., Nov., 1937.

MURPHY, AFLRED, O.B.E., late Indian Service of Engrs. (b. 28th Jan., 1880).—Trained at the R.I.E. Coll.; apptd. asst. engr., Punjab, 3rd Jan., 1904; exec. engr., June, 1912; on mily. service from Oct., 1915, to Oct., 1919; under-sec. to govt. irrign. branch, Nov., 1919; O.B.E., Jan., 1920; offg. suptg. engr., March, 1922; confd., Nov., 1925; offg. ch. engr. and secty. to govt., Punjab irrigation br., Dec., 1930; confd., July, 1932; ret'd., Jan., 1935.

MURPHY, GERALD PATRICK, Lieut.-Col., I.A., Indian Political Service (b. 8th May, 1888).—First commn. 25th Jan., 1908; in mil. employ till April, 1913, when he was apptd. asst. pol. agent (on probn.); on mil. service, Dec. 1914, to Dec., 1920; asst. resdt., Aden, Dec., 1920; asst. pol. agent, April, 1921; asst. resdt., Aden, April, 1923; pol. agent and British consul, Muscat, Sept., 1926; ditto, Banas Kantha, Mar., 1931; pol. agent, Sahar Kantha, Apl., 1933; ditto, Western Kathiawar, May, 1933; secty. to A.G.G., Eastern states and pol. agent, Sambalpur, Oct., 1935; A.G.G., Eastern States (in addn.), Mar., 1937; pol. agent, Sambalpur, Mar., 1937; resdt. for Kolhapur and Deccan States, Oct., 1937.

MURPHY, JOHN JAMES CHRISTOPHER, B.A., Indian C.S. (dist. and sess. judge, Burma) (b. 24th Dec., 1896).—Educ. at Sandhurst and Univ. Coll., Dublin; joined the service, 1st Nov., 1921; arrived, 22nd Dec., 1921, and served in Burma as asst. commr.; offg. dist. and sess. judge, April, 1925; confd., Feb., 1931; regr., high ct., Rangoon, July, 1933, to Nov., 1936.

MURPHY, PATRICK WILLIAM, B.A., late Indian C.S. (b. 13th July, 1877).—Educ. at St. Peter's Coll., Wexford, and Univ. Coll., Dublin; apptd. after exam. of 1899; arrived, 3rd Dec., 1900 and served in Bengal as asst. mag. and collr., asst. settlmt. offr. and settlmt. offr.; jt. mag. and dep. collr., Mar., 1910; transfid. to Bihar and Orissa, April, 1912; mag. and collr. and settlmt. offr., Sept., 1913; dir. of land records and surveys, from July, 1916; dep. commr., Oct., 1921; offg. commr., Oct., 1923; Aug., 1925, and again, May, 1927; confd., Dec., 1928; member, bd. of rev., Dec., 1931; ret'd., Dec., 1935.

MURPHY, SIR STEPHEN JAMES, Kt., late Indian C.S. (b. 13th June, 1875).—Educ. at Framlingham Coll., and Univ. Coll., London; apptd. after exam. of 1898; arrived, 24th Nov., 1899, and served in Bombay as asst. collr. and mag. and asst. judge; dist. and sess. judge, Nov., 1917; membr. of legal affairs, and sec. to govt., legal dept., Nov., 1922; judi. commr., states of Western India, Oct., 1924; dist. and sess. judge, May, 1928; offg., judge, high ct., Bombay, June, 1928; confd., April, 1929; actg. ch. justice, high ct., Bombay, Apl., 1934; ret'd., June, 1935; knighted, June, 1935.

MURPHY, TERENCE ARTHUR, Income Tax dept., Bombay (asst. commr. of inc. tax) (b. 29th Dec., 1888).—Appointed to income tax dept. of central board of revenue, 12th May, 1907; asst. commr. of income tax, Sind, Apr., 1924; ditto, Bombay city, Dec., 1932.

MURPHY, WILLIAM O'SULLIVAN, M.B., B.Ch., Col., I.M.S. (ret'd.).—First commn., dated 27th Jan., 1900; apptd. dep. sany. commr., Bombay, Dec., 1908; on mil. duty from Nov., 1914; sany. commr. for govt., Bombay, 1919; designation changed to dir. of public health, Oct., 1921; ret'd., Feb., 1929.

MURRAY, SIR ALEXANDER ROBERTSON, K.C.I.E., C.B.E. (presdt. spl. tariff bd) (b. 29th Nov., 1872).—Presdt. associated chambers of commerce of India and Ceylon, 1920; member, Rl. commn. on Indian currency and finance, 1925-1926; member, Rl. commn. on Indian labour, 1929-1931; presdt., spl. tariff bd., Oct., 1935; K.C.I.E., June, 1936.

MURRAY, COLIN ROBERT BAILLIE, Indian Police (supt., Bihar) (b. 12th Feb., 1892).—Joined the service as asst. supt., 17th Nov., 1911; supt., Feb., 1923; cent. intell. offr., B. and O., Feb., 1936.

MURRAY, HERBERT EDWARD, Lt.-Col. I.M.S. (surg. supt. P.G. hosp., Calcutta) (b. 19th May, 1889).—Joined the service, 8th Dec., 1915; apptd. surg. supt., P.G. Hospital, Calcutta, March, 1933.

MURRAY, SIR HUGH, C.I.E., C.B.E., late India Forest Dept.—Joined the service, 30th Nov., 1882, as asst. consr.; dep. consr., Dec., 1887; consr., July, 1905; ret'd., Jan., 1911; C.I.E., Dec., 1911; asst. forestry commr. of England and Wales; Kt., Jan. 1924.

MURRAY, PETER FRANK WILFRED, Indian Police (b. 18th June, 1917).—Apptd. asst. supt., Madras, on probn., Dec., 1937.

MURRAY, WALTER, late Indian Police (b. 21st Oct., 1880).—Held non-gazetted appt. from 5th July, 1899; inspr., July, 1906; apptd. to I.P.S. as supt., 1st May, 1930; retd., Oct., 1935.

MURRELL, WILLIAM LEE, B.C.E., A.M.I.C.E., Indian Service of Engrs. (exec. engr., Bihar) (b. 26th Feb., 1893).—Joined the service as asst. engr., 1st Oct., 1919; exec. engr., Oct., 1925.

MURSHED, KHONDKAR GHOLAM (see Khondkar Gholam Morshed).

MURTI, NORI GOPALAKRISHNA, B.E., Ind. Service of Engrs. (asst. exec. engr.) (b. 16th Feb., 1910).—Asst. exec. engr., Bombay, Aug. 1932.

MURTI, N. V. SHRINIVAS, B.A., B.E., Indian Service of Engrs. (exec. engr., Bombay) (b. 5th Nov., 1895).—Joined the service as apprentice engr., 21st July, 1920; asst. exec. engr., July, 1921; offg. exec. engr., Dec., 1925; confd., July, 1930.

MUSHRAN, SHAMNATH, Barr., Legislative Dept., Govt. of India (2nd solicitor) (b. Nov., 1883).—Joined the service as 2nd asst. ollicitor, 15th July, 1921; asst. solicitor, March, 1928; 2nd solicitor, Apl., 1933.

MUSHTAQ AHMAD, B.Sc., B.A., Indian Forest Service (dep. consvr., Punjab) (b. 14th Feb., 1902).—Joined the service as asst. consvr., Bengal, 4th Dec., 1925; transferred to Punjab from 10th Oct., 1920; dep. consvr., Dec., 1929.

MUSPRATT, SIR SYDNEY FREDERICK, K.C.B., C.S.I., C.I.E., D.S.O., Lieut.-General, I.A., (secty., milty. dept., India Office) (b. 11th Sept., 1878).—First commn., 22nd Jan., 1898; 12th Cav., 1st Apl., 1900, to 29th Aug., 1921; staff capt. (intell.) and G.S.O. 3, June, 1906-June, 1910; brigad-major, Dec., 1914-June, 1915; G.S.O. 2, June-Dec., 1915; A.A. and Q.M.G., Dec., 1915-Jan. 1918; G.S.O. 1, Jan. 1918, to Oct., 1920; dep. dir. (intell.), Nov.-Dec., 1920; A.D.C. to King, June, 1921, to June, 1929; G.S.O. 1, War Office, Feb., 1922, to July, 1925; D.D.M.O. and I., War Office, Aug.-Oct., 1925; brigad-comdr., Nov., 1925, to Oct., 1927; dir. milty. operations, Nov., 1927, to Oct., 1929; dep. ch. of general staff, Dec., 1929; secty., milty. dept., India Office, Sept., 1931, to Nov., 1933; and again, Oct., 1937; G.O.C., Peshawar Dist., Nov., 1933. War Services.—N.W. Frontier, 1908; Intell. officer, Zakka Khel Mohmand, medal, with clasp. War of 1914-21—France and Belgium, Nov., 1914, to June, 1918; 4 times mentioned in despatches. Brev. of Lt.-Col.; Legion of Honour, 5th class; Order of the Sacred Treasure, 3rd class; Order of the Crown of Siam, 3rd class; D.S.O.

MUSTAFA, GHULAM, KHAN SAHIB CHAUDHRI, B.A., Punjab C.S. (dep. commr.) (b. Apl., 1889).—Joined the service, 10th Feb., 1913; offg. dep. commr., Apl., 1929; confd., May, 1936.

MUSTAFA, SAIDYD GHULAM, A.M.I.C.E., Indian Service of Engrs. (exec. engr., Sindj.) (b. 14th July, 1895).—Joined the service as asst. engr., Oct., 1917; asst. exec. engr., Aug., 1919; exec. engr., Oct., 1926.

MUSTILLI, FREDERICK JOHN, B.A., Indian Forest Service (dep. consvr., Burma) (b. 6th Oct., 1901).—Joined the service as asst. consvr., 27th Dec., 1924; dep. consvr., Nov., 1928.

MUSTO, SIR ARNOLD ALBERT, Kt., C.I.E., M. Inst. C.E., late Indian Service of Engrs. (b. 4th Oct., 1883).—Joined as asst. engr., 1st Oct., 1907; exec. engr., Oct., 1915; C.I.E., Jan., 1923; suptg. engr. (sub-temp.), July, 1923; Kt., June, 1932; retd., Aug., 1934.

MUTKEKAR, SATWAJI GUNDONJI, B.Ag. (Bom.), M.Sc. (Mass.), Indian Agric. Service (dep. dir. of agric., Central Provs.) (b. 22nd Mar., 1886).—Joined the service as asst. dir. of agric., Dec., 1920; dep. dir. of agric., Dec., 1923.

MATSADDI, LAL, B.A., Indian C.S. (asst. mag. and collr., U.P.) (b. 6th Aug., 1913).—Educ. at Shambhoo Dayal High School, Ghaziabad, Agra Univ., Allahabad Univ., London School of Oriental Studies, and Fitzwilliam House, Cambridge; joined the service 20th Sept., 1937; arrived in India, Oct., 1937, and served in the U.P. as asst. mag. and collr.

MUZAFFAR HUSSAIN, KHAN BAHADUR, Indian State Rlys. (dep. ch. engr.) (b. 8th Nov., 1886).—Joined the service as asst. engr., 19th Oct., 1910; exec. engr., Oct., 1918; offg. dep. ch. engr., N.W. rly., variously from Apl., 1932; offg. divl. supt. (senr.), Apl., 1936; dep. ch. engr. (provl. perm.), Jan., 1936.

MUZAFFAR KHAN, NAWAB, Khan Bahadur, Khan, C.I.E., Punjab Civil Service (member, govr.'s exec. council, Punjab) (b. 2nd Jan., 1880).—Joined the service, 20th Oct., 1905; held non-gazetted appts. to July, 1912; extra asst. commr., July, 1912; dir. of information, Jan., 1925; elections commr. and jt. secty. to govt., transferred depts., Nov., 1925; C.I.E., June, 1931; reforms commr., Oct., 1932; member, govr.'s exec. council, Punjab, Feb., 1935.

MUZAFFAR, SHAIKH-UD-DIN, M.A., M.Sc. (Punjab), Ph.D. (Cantab.), Public Works Dept., Punjab (b. 24th Sept., 1896).—Apptd. prof. of science, Maclagan engrg. coll., 22nd June, 1924.

MYAING, U THAN, Indian Police.—Joined the service as proby. asst. supt., Burma, 26th Jan., 1933.

MYERS, Miss KATHLEEN MABEL, M.A., Indian Educ. Service (princl., Queen Mary's coll. Madras) (b. 14th Jan., 1891).—Apptd. addl. prof., Queen Mary's coll., Madras, 26th Mar., 1921; prof., ditto, Dec., 1925; principl., ditto, Mar., 1936.

MYSORE SUBBA, RAO, B.A. (Mysore), Indian C.S. (jt. magte. and dep. collr., Orissa) (b. 25th Oct., 1907).—Educ. at Govt. High School, Bangalore, Mysore Univ. and School of Oriental Studies, London; apptd. after exam. of 1930; joined the service, Oct., 1931, and served in B. and O. as asst. magte. and collr.

NADGIR, YESHWANT GOVIND, M.S. (b. 12th Sept., 1881).—Joined the service as asst. surgn., 20th May, 1906; prof. of anatomy, Grant Med. Coll., Bombay, June, 1913; retd., Sept., 1932.

NAG, BALWANT SINGH (see Balwant, N. S.).
NAG, KHAGENDRA CHANDRA, M.B.E., B.A. (Cal.), Barr., late Bengal Judicial Service (b. 10th July, 1881).—Apptd. dist. and sess. judge, June, 1921; addl. judge, high ct., Calcutta, Apl., 1933; retd., Nov., 1936.

NAGARATNAM AYYAR, GOPALASWAMI, B.A., B.E., Indian Educl. Service (princl., coll. of engrg., Guindy) (b. 16th May, 1881).—Apptd. addl. prof. of civil engrg., Madras, Dec., 1920; princl., coll. of engrg., Guindy, June, 1927.

NAGARKAR, CHANDRAKANT BALVANTRAO, B.A., Indian C.S. (judge and sess. judge, Bombay) (b. 28th Aug., 1894).—Educ. at Wilson Coll., Bombay, and New Coll. Oxford; joined the service, 12th Dec., 1921; arrived, 6th Jan., 1922, and served in Bombay as asst. collr. and magte.; judge and sess. judge, May, 1930; regr. high ct., Bombay, June, 1931.

NAGARKATTI, KRISHNA NARAYANA, B.Sc., Indian C.S. (asst. commr., Central Provinces) (b. 5th March, 1905).—Educ. at University of Mysore and Peterhouse Coll., Cambridge; joined the service 10th Oct., 1929; arrived 15th Nov., 1929, and served in the Central Provinces as asst. commr.; offg. dept. commr., June, 1933.

NAGARVALA, JAMSHID DORAB, B.A., Indian Police (b. 13th Nov., 1914).—Apptd. asst. supt., Bombay, on probn., 2nd Feb., 1937.

NAGINLAL VENILAL DESAI (see Desai, N. V.).

NAGLE, JAMES NICHOLAS EASTMAN (see Eastman Nagle).

NAIDU, G. NARASIMHULU (see Narasimhulu Naidu G.).

NAIDU, SIRAM LAKSHMINARASINGHA RAO, Indian State Railways (dist. traffic supt., E.B., rly.) (b. 7th Dec., 1890).—Joined the service, 3rd July, 1911; dist. traffic supt., Mar., 1931; statistical officer, E. B. rly., Mar., 1931.

NAIFF, JOHN CALVERLEY, B.A., Burma C.S. (Cl. I) (asst. commr.) (b. 31st Oct., 1913).—Educ. at Hanley High School, Stoke-on-Trent, and St. John's College, Cambridge; joined the service, 28th September, 1937; arrived in Burma, Nov., 1937, and served as asst. commr.

NAIK, SHRIDHAR ATMARAM, late Judl. Dept., Bombay (b. 9th Mar., 1875).—Held non-gazetted appts. from 18th May, 1903, to 31st Aug., 1906; sub-judge, Sept., 1906; judge, small cause ct., Poona, Nov., 1927; retd., Mar., 1930.

NAIR (see also Nayar).

NAIR, THEKATH KUMARAN, B.A. (Mad.), B.Sc. (B'ham), Indian State Rlys. (chemist and metallurgist, E.I. Rly.) (b. 16th Mar., 1889).—Joined the service as asst. chemist and metallurgist, 2nd Aug., 1915; offg. chemist and metallurgist, July, 1916; confd., July, 1917.

NAIR, UNNI KANNAN, Local Fund Audit dept., Burma (asst. examr.) (b. 15th Apl., 1886).—Previous service in non-gazetted appts. from 15th April, 1908; divl. auditor, Feb., 1925; offg. asst. examr., May, 1926; confd., Sept., 1926; offg. examr., June, 1931.

NAJABAT HUSAIN, KHAN BAHADUR, B.L., late Bihar and Orissa Civil Service (b. 24th July, 1879).—Joined the service, 24th May, 1907; offg. munisf., Nov., 1909; confd., June, 1910; sub-judge, Feb., 1922; dist. and sess. judge, Jan., 1931; retd., Oct., 1934.

NAMBIYAR, AMANDA KALOTH KUNHIRAMAN (see Kunhiraman Nambiyar, A. K.).

NAMJOSHI, VINAYAK KRISHNA, B.A., late Bombay C.S. (b. 5th Oct., 1878).—Joined the service, 18th May, 1900; administrator, Cambay State, from 18th April, 1918, to 10th June, 1929; collr. and dist. mag., March, 1922; retd., Oct., 1930.

NANAK SINGH, Sardar Sahib Sardar. Punjab Civil Service (dep. commr.) (b. 1st Mar., 1889).—Extra asst. commr., Sept., 1913; dep. commr., Sept., 1936.

NANAVATI, ERACH MANEKSHAH, B.A., late Indian C.S. (b. 12th July, 1877).—Educ. at Surat High Sch., Bombay Univ., and King's Coll., Cambridge; apptd. after exam. of 1900; arrived, 19th Dec., 1901, and served in the United Provs. as asst. commr. and asst. mag. and collr.; jt. mag., July, 1911; dist. and sess. judge, Oct., 1919; offg. judge, chief ct. of Oudh, March, 1928; offg. secty. to govt., legal dept.; judge, ch. ct., Oudh, Mar., 1930; retd., July, 1937.

NAND GOPAL, DIWAN, Indian Service of Engrs. (asst. exec. engr., central p.w.d.).—Joined the service as apprentice engr., 24th July, 1931; apptd. to I.S.E., as asst. exec. engr., Punjab, July, 1932; transf. to central p.w.d., Apl., 1935.

NAND GOPAL, LAIA, A.M.I.E. (Ind.), Indian Service of Engrs. (exec. engr., Punjab.) (b. 1st Mar., 1894).—Joined the service, 8th Oct., 1917; asst. engr., Oct., 1918; asst. exec. engr., Jan., 1920; exec. engr., Oct., 1927.

NAND KISHORE, RAI BAHADUR, LALA, B.A., late Indian Service of Engrs. (b. 14th Nov., 1883).—Joined the service, 29th Sept., 1906; asst. engr., Sept., 1907; exec. engr., Sept., 1915; offg. supt., April, 1926; retd., Jan., 1933.

NANJAPPA, VENKATASUBRAMANIER, B.Sc., Indian C.S. (asst. collr., Bombay) (b. 26th Jan., 1911).—Educ. at Government Collegiate High School, Bangalore, Mysore University, University College, London, and Jesus College, Oxford; joined the service, 6th Oct., 1934; arrived in India, Nov., 1934, and served in Bombay as asst. collr.

NAPIER, ALEXANDER, Capt., I.A. Indian Political Service (b. 7th Sept., 1904).—First commn., 26th Aug., 1924; in milty. employ to Nov., 1930; under-secty. to A.G.G., in C.I., Apl., 1932; asst. pol. agent, Chitral, Dec., 1933; asst. commr., Kohat, Dec., 1936; asst. commr. and city magte., Peshawar, Apl., 1937; under secty. to resdt., Hyderabad, Nov., 1937.

NAPIER, ALEXANDER HARPER, M.D., Lt.-Col., I.M.S., retd., late Civil Medl. Dept., Bihar and Orissa (b. 28th Sept., 1880).—First commn. dated 2nd Feb., 1907; in mil. employ up to 19th May, 1922; civil surg., May, 1922; retd., Mar., 1932.

NAPIER-MUNN, Rev. WILLIAM ERNEST, Indian Eccl. Estabt. (b. 25th Apl., 1886).—Apptd. junr. chaplain, United Provs., 21st Apl., 1929.

NAQVI, ABU TALIB, B.A., Indian C.S. (jt. mag., United Provinces) (b. 1st March, 1907).—Educ. at Allahabad University and Oriel College, Oxford; joined the service, 14th Oct., 1931; arrived in India, Nov., 1931, and served in the United Provinces as asst. mag. and collr.; on spec. duty at govt. h.q., Nov., 1937.

NAQVI, GHAZANFAR ALI (see Ghazanfar A.N.).

NARA SIMHAM, RAMASWAMY LAKSHMI, B.A., Indian C.S. (jt. mag. and dep. collr., Orissa) (b. 27th Sept., 1906).—Educ. at Madras University and New College, Oxford; joined the service, 12th Oct., 1931; arrived in India, Nov., 1931, and served in Bihar and Orissa as asst. mag. and collr.; jt. mag. and dep. collr., May, 1935.

NARAHARI RAO, VYAKARANA, C.I.E., M.A., Indian Audit Dept. (b. 6th July, 1893).—Joined the service, 5 Mar., 1917; dep. acct. genl. posts and tels., May, 1923, under secty., fin. dep., Govt. of India, Aug., 1924; offg. asst. secty., ditto, Nov., 1927; offg. under secty. govt. of India, for. and pol. dept., Apl., 1929; addl. dep. secty., ditto, Mar., 1933; temp. jt. secty., ditto, in addn., Aug., 1936; addtl. dep. secty., and Crown fin. offcr., pol. dept., Apl., 1937; C.I.E., May, 1937.

NARASIMHA AYYANGAR, RAJAGOPALA AYYANGAR, Diwan Bahadur, B.A., B.E., late Indian Service of Engrs. (b. Sept., 1881).—Joined the service, 10th Feb., 1904; asst. engr., Feb., 1908; exec. engr., Feb., 1915; offg. suptg. engr., Aug., 1928; confd., Apl., 1931; offg. ch. engr., Sept., 1933; confd., Jan., 1934; retd., Sept., 1936.

NARASIMHAN PANTULU, MANTHRIPRA-GADA, Rao Bahadur, Madras C.S. (member, Madras public services commn.) (b. 1882).—Joined the service, 1st Oct., 1894; dep. collr., Mar., 1919; offg. collr., May, 1932; member, Madras public services commn., Feb., 1935.

NARASIMHAN, CHAKRAVARTHI VIJIRAGHAVA, B.A., Indian C.S. (asst. mag. and collr., Madras) (b. 21st May, 1915).—Educ. at National High School, Trichinopoly, Madras Univ., and St. Catherine's Society, Oxford; joined the service, 23rd Sept., 1937; arrived in India, Oct., 1937, and served in Madras as asst. mag. and collr.

NARASIMHULU, NAIDU G., Rao Bahadur, B.A., B.L., Indian Posts and Telegraphs (dep. P.M.G.) (b. 9th Oct., 1887).—Joined the service as proby. supt., 22nd Dec., 1910; confd., 3rd. July, 1912; asst. dep. dir. genl., Sept., 1935; dep. P.M.G., July, 1937.

NARAYAN, APPADWADULA LAKSHMI, M.A., D.Sc., Meteorological Dept. (asst. director) (b. 15th July, 1890).—Joined service as meteorologist on probn., 18th Dec., 1928; confd., 18th Dec., 1930; asstt. dir., ditto, Jan., 1934; again, Jan., 1937.

NARAYAN, BRIJ (see Brij Narayan).

NARAYAN, JAGAT (see Jagat N.).

NARAYAN, PANDIT HARI (see Pandit Hari Narayan).

NARAYAN, PRAG (see Prag, N.).

NARAYAN, RAJ (see Raj Narayan).

NARAYAN, SHIV, M.A., B.Sc. (Punj.), B.E., M.Sc. (U.S.A.), Mem. A.I.E.E., A.M.I.E.E. (Lond.), A.M.I.E. (India), Indian Educl. Service (b. 12th Nov., 1883).—Apptd. prof. of physics and elec. engr., coll. of engrg., Poona, March, 1921; offg. prinpl., ditto, June, 1932.

NARAYAN, S. L. (see Lokur, Narayan Swamirao).

NARAYAN, SRI (see Sri Narayan).

NARAYANA AYYAR, RAJAPPAYA, Barr., late Indian C.S. (Madras) (b. 8th July, 1876).—Educ. at Madras University and King's Coll., Cambridge; apptd. after exam. of 1899; arrived, 25th Dec., 1900, and served in Madras as asst. collr. and mag.; sub. collr. and jt. mag., May, 1910; dist. and sessions judge, May, 1923; retd., Sept., 1930.

NARAYANA KUDVA, VENKATARAYA, B.A., Indian C.S. (sub-collr. and jt. mag., Madras) (b. 13th Nov., 1898).—Educ. at Madras Univ. and Clare Coll., Cambridge; joined the service, 1st Nov., 1922; arrived, 13th Dec., 1922, and served in Madras as asst. collr. and mag.; sub. collr. and jt. mag., Jan., 1925; on foreign service as estate collr., Sivadanga, Nov., 1930; offg. collr. and mag., July, 1933; supt. of stationery, Madras, Jan., 1936.

NARAYANA RAI, AJILIDAY, Indian Police (asst. supt., Madras) (b. 28th Jan., 1908).—Apptd. proby. asst. supt., Madras, 7th Mar., 1932; confd., Mar., 1934.

NARENDRA NATH SEN, B.A., B.Sc., Indian Forest Service (dep. consvr., United Provs.) (b. 1st Feb., 1900).—Joined the service as asst. consvr., 5th Dec., 1924; dep. consvr., April, 1929.

NARIMAN, RUSTOM KAIKHUSRO, A.M.I.C.E., late Indian Service of Engrs. (b. 25th May, 1877).—Apptd. asst. engr., Punjab, 1st Oct., 1899; exec. engr., Dec. 1908; offg. suptg. engr., May, 1921, and again Feb., 1925; retd., May, 1932.

NASH, GEORGE WILLIAM, Indian Service of Engrs. (exec. engr.) (b. 10th Jan., 1904).—Joined the service as asst. exec. engr., United Provs., 15th Oct., 1927; offg. exec. engr., June, 1930; confd. Oct., 1936.

NASH, PHILIP GEOFFREY ELWIN, Indian C.S. (asst. pte. secty. to Viceroy) (b. 20th Sept., 1906).—Educ. at St. Paul's School and Worcester College, Oxford; apptd. after exam. of 1930; arrived in India, Dec., 1931, and served in Burma as asst. commr.; under secty. to govt., home and pol. dept., Burma, Mar., 1934, to May, 1936; on spec. duty with govt., Burma, May, 1936 to Apl., 1937; secty. to Burma delegate Imperial Conf., Apl.-June, 1937; asst. pte. secty. to Viceroy, Aug., 1937.

NASIM ALI, SYED, M.A., B.L., Bengal Judl. Dept. (judge, high ct., Calcutta).—Apptd. addl. judge, high ct. Calcutta, 13th Nov., 1933; apptd. judge, ditto, Oct., 1936.

NATH, AJUDHIA (see Ajudhia Nath).

NATH, AMAR (see Amar Nath).

NATH, BABU KAMESHAR (see Kameshar, N. B.).

NATH, GAURI (see Gauri Nath).

NATH, GYAN (see Gyan Nath).

NATH, JAGAN, Juneja (see Jagan Nath, J.).

NATH, JAGAN KUKAR (see Jagan, Nath K.).

NATH, JOGENDRA CHANDRA, Indian Forest Service (dep. consvr., Bengal) (b. 22nd Sept., 1901).—Joined the service as asst. consvr., 30th Jan., 1925; dep. consvr., Jan., 1930.

NATH, PREM (see Prem Nath).

NATH, SURATH (see Surath Nath).

NATHA SINGH, BAWA, RAI BAHADUR, Indian Service of Engrs. (supt. engr., Punjab) (b. 5th July, 1882).—Joined the service, 13th Oct., 1905, as asst. engr.; exec. engr., Nov., 1913; offg. supt. engr., March, 1927; confd., May, 1932; offg. ch. engr. and secty. to govt., Punjab, p.v.d. irrign. bch., May, 1936.

NAUBAT RAI, Indian Service of Engrs. (exec. engr., United Provs.) (b. 7th Sept., 1888).—Joined the service as temp. engr., 1st April, 1922; asst. engr. (provl. service), April, 1926; asst. exec. engr., Jan., 1928; offg. exec. engr., May, 1930; confd., Jan., 1936.

NAUBAT RAI, RAI BAHADUR, late India Posts and Tels. (b. 4th Sept., 1875).—Held gazetted appts. from 23rd Aug., 1894; supt., Feb., 1904; dep. postmaster-genl., Punjab and N.W.F., June, 1924; ret'd., Oct., 1930.

NAVAKLAR, Miss RUBY, B.A., late Indian Educl. Service (Bombay) (b. 25th Nov., 1875).—Joined the service as lady supt., training coll. for women, Poona, 19th May, 1920; awarded K.-I.-H. medal, 2nd class, Jan., 1930; ret'd., Nov., 1930.

NAWAB SINGH, Indian C.S. (asst. commr., Punjab) b. 9th April, 1907).—Educ. at University of Nagpur and School of Oriental Studies, London; joined the service 9th Oct., 1929; arrived, Nov., 1929, and served in the Punjab as asst. commr.; offg. dep. commr., June, 1933; offg. dist. and sess. judge, Feb., 1935, to Apl., 1937.

NAYAK, PANGAL RAMANATHA, M.A., Indian C.S. (asst. collr., Bombay) (b. 29th March, 1911).—Educated at Christian High School, Udipi, University of Madras and The School of Oriental Studies, London; joined the service, 20th Oct., 1935; arrived in India, Nov., 1935, and served in Bombay as asst. collr.

NAYAR, CHETTUR, Govindan, Diwan Bahadur, B.A., B.L., Barr-at-law, Judl. dept., Madras (Law secty. to the Govt. of Orissa) (b. 23rd Dec., 1881).—Apptd. District Munsif, June, 1910; offg. dist. judge, June, 1933; confd., Aug., 1933; secty. govt. of Orissa, law and comm. dept., Apl., 1936.

NAYAR, CHETTUR MADHAVAN, Judl. Dept., Madras (judge, high ct., Madras) (b. Jan., 1879).—Apptd. asst. law reporter, Feb., 1915; prof., law coll., Aug., 1916; advocate-gen., Feb., 1923; offg. high ct. judge, March, 1924; confd., March, 1927; offg. ch. justice, Madras, Sept., 1935.

NAYAR, KRISHNAPILLAI KARUKAIL KARUNAKARAN, Indian C.S. (jt. mag., United Provs.) (b. 11th Sept., 1907).—Educ. at Madras University and London School of Oriental Studies; joined the service, 11th Oct., 1930, arrived in India, Nov., 1930, and served in the United Provs. as asst. mag. and collr.; judge, small causes ct., Dec., 1936.

NAYAR, MANNATH GOVINDAN, B.A., B.L., late Indian Police (b. Aug., 1881).—Joined the service, 27th Jan., 1907; supt., Nov., 1924; ret'd., Aug., 1936.

NAYAR, MANAKAMPAD KESAVANUNNI, B.A., Indian Forest Service (dep. consvr., Madras) (b. 25th June, 1899).—Joined the service as asst. consvr., 9th Nov., 1923; dep. consvr., Nov., 1927.

NAYAR, M. KRISHNAN (see Krishnan Nayar, M.).

NAYAR, PADINHARANKUNNATH THAZA-THAVIL RAMAN, B.A., B.L., Indian C.S. (sub. collr., Madras) (b. 14th Jan., 1910).—Educ. at St. Joseph's Boys' High School, Calicut, University of Madras and St. Catherine's College, Cambridge; joined the service, 9th Oct., 1934; arrived in India, Nov., 1934, and served in Madras as asst. mag. and collr.; sub.-collr., Jan., 1937.

NAYAR, U. GOVINDAN (see Govindan Nayar, U.).

NAYLOR, LEONARD BROWN, Burma Frontier Service (asst. supt.) (b. 19th Mar., 1887).—Apptd. extra asst. commr., Mar., 1914; asst. supt. (B.F.S.), July, 1923.

NAYUDU, BANDI VENKATA SRI HARI RAO (see Sri Hari, R. N. B. V.).

NAYUDU, BHASKARA RAO, T. (see Bhaskara Rao Nayudu, T.).

NAYUDU, BUNDY SRIRAMULU (see Sriramulu Nayudu B.).

NAYUDU, RAI Bahadur, Sir K. V. R. (see Venkata Reddi Nayudu).

NAYUDU, PAKALA VENKATARAMANA RAO, Rao Bahadur (see Venkataramana Rao Nayudu, Pakala, Rao Bahadur).

NAYUDU, POLISETTY RANGANAYAKULU (see Ranganayakulu Nayudu, P.).

NAYUDU, VENKATANARAYANA J. (see Venkatanarayana, N. J.).

NAZIM-UD-DIN, KHWAJA, Sir, K.C.I.E. (member govr.'s exec. council, Bengal).—Apptd. minister, govt. of Bengal, 18th Dec., 1929; member, govr.'s exec. council, Bengal, May, 1934; K.C.I.E., June, 1934.

NAZIR ALAM, SHAH, Indian Police (asst. supt., United Provs.) (b. 4th Mar., 1906).—Joined the service as dep. supt. (proby.), 2nd Jan., 1927; confd., Jan., 1929; apptd. to I.P.S. as asst. supt., 31st Aug., 1930; offg. supt., Apl., 1931; and again, May, 1933.

NAZIRUDDIN, KAMALUDDIN, B.E., Indian Service of Engrs. (exec. engr., Orissa) (b. 31st Oct., 1898).—Joined the service as appr. engr., 26th June, 1922; asst. exec. engr., June, 1923; offg. exec. engr., Jan., 1928; confd., Aug., 1932; on depn. to Orissa, April, 1936.

NAZR-UL-BAKAR, SHEIKH, B.A., Indian C.S. (asst. mag. and collr., Bengal) (b. 21st Dec., 1913).—Educ. at Denny's High School, Rawalpindi, Govt. High School, Murree, and Punjab Univ.; joined the service 16th Sept., 1937; arrived in India, Oct., 1937, and served in Bengal as asst. mag. and collr.

NEAL, REGINALD FRANCIS, Indian Police (b. 8th Sept., 1903).—Joined the service as asst. supt., Punjab, 5th Dec., 1924; offg. supt., Apl., 1933.

NEALE, WALTER GORDON, Lt.-Col., C.I.E., C.V.O., I.A. (ret'd.) Indin Office, (pol. A.D.C. to secty. of state) (b. 2nd Oct., 1880). First commn. dated 15th Feb., 1902; in mil. employ. until July, 1906, when he was apptd. prob. for pol. dept., N.W.F. Prov.; personal asst. to chief commr., April, 1907; dep. commr., temp., D.I., Khan, Dec., 1908; asst. commr., ditto, Jan., 1909; asst. pol. agent, Dir Swat and Chitral, Aug., 1909; asst. commr.,

Hazara, March, 1910; ditto, Oghi and commdt. B.M. Police, March, 1910; asst. sec. to chief commr., N.W.F. Prov., and supt. of census, Aug., 1910; asst. to agent to govr.-gen. in Rajputana, Nov., 1911; pol. offir., Deoli, April, 1912; asst. resdt., Western States of Rajputana, Nov., 1912; asst. pol. agent, Eastern States of Rajputana, May, 1913; asst. to agent to govr.-gen. in Rajputana, July, 1913; asst. pol. agent, Eastern Rajputana States, Nov., 1913; asst. to resdt. in Persian Gulf, June, 1914, to April, 1915; on spl. duty under chief pol. officer, Basra, 1916; second asst. to agent to govr.-gen. in Rajputana, Oct., 1916; asst. sec. to govt. of India, for. and pol. dept., Feb., 1917; pol. agent, Dir, Swat and Chitral, Dec., 1920; pol. agent, Eastern Rajputana States, April, 1924; first asst. to resdt. in Kashmir, Nov., 1924; pol. agent, Bhopal, Oct., 1926; offg. dep. secty. to govt. of India, for. and pol. dept., June, 1928; C.I.E., March, 1929; retd., July, 1930; pol. A.D.C. to secty. of state, Sept., 1933; C.V.O., May, 1937.

NEEDHAM, HERBERT SIDNEY, B.A. (Vic.), late Indian Police Service (Bombay) (b. 7th Nov., 1890).—Joined the service, 17th Nov., 1911, as asst. supt.; supt. of police, June, 1923; dep. commr. of police, Nov., 1924; retd., Feb., 1932.

NEEDHAM, SIR RICHARD ARTHUR, Kt., C.I.E., D.S.O., M.D., B.Sc., F.R.C.P., D.P.H., Col., I.M.S. (ret'd.) (b. 31st July, 1877).—First commn. dated 31st Jan., 1903; in mil. employ until 27th May, 1911, when apptd. offg. health officer, Simla; on special duty with Chinese troops returning from Tibet, 1912; health officer, Simla, Feb., 1914; reverted to mil. duty, Aug., 1914; asst. dir.-gen., I.M.S. (stores), Oct., 1916; asst. dir.-gen., I.M.S. (sany.), 1918; dep. dir.-gen., I.M.S., Oct., 1918; C.I.E., June, 1919; on special duty to Europe to attend Intl. Sanitary Conference, 1921; inspr. of medical education (in addition), 1922; on deputation to England to General Medical Council, 1923; officer on special duty, govt. of India, rly. dept., 1925; on special duty as inspr. of medl. educn., 1926-7; retd., Oct., 1930; Kt., June, 1932.

NEHPAL SINGH, B.A., M.R.A.S., Indian Educl. Service (inspr. of schools, United Provs.) (b. 14th Jan., 1893).—Joined the service as prinpl., jubilee inter. coll., Lucknow, 4th July, 1922; hd. master, govt. high school, Bareilly, April, 1924; prinpl., govt. inter. coll., Almora, Oct., 1927; offg. inspr. of schools, June, 1928; confd., July, 1932; asst. dir. of public instrn. variously from Apl., 1935.

NEHRU, BRAJ KUMAR, B.Sc., Indian C.S. (asst. commr., Punjab) (b. 4th Sept., 1909).—Educ. at the Government Intermediate College, Allahabad, University of Allahabad, the School of Economics, London and Balliol College, Oxford; joined the service, 25th Oct., 1934; arrived in India, Nov., 1934, and served in Punjab as asst. commr.; sub. divl. offcr., Apl., 1937.

NEHRU, BRIJLAL, M.A., Barr., Indian Finance Dept. (b. 5th May, 1884).—Joined the service, 16th Nov., 1898; asst. acctnt.-gen., June, 1910; dep. acctnt.-gen., Burma, Nov., 1920; asst. ditto, Bengal, Oct., 1922; dep. ditto, United Provs.,

Sept., 1923; dep. ditto, Punjab, Jan., 1926; offg. asst. auditor-genl., Dec., 1926; offg. dep. ditto, Mar., 1929; offg. dep. ch. auditor, N.W. Rly., Apl., 1930; ditto, E.I. Rly., Mar., 1931; dep. govt. exr., B.N. Rly., Nov., 1931; offg. ch. accts. officer, E. B. Rly. Nov., 1932; offg. ch. auditor, N.W. rly., Apl., 1933; offg. dir., rly. audit, July, 1935; offg. acct. genl., posts and tels., Oct., 1935; confd., June, 1936.

NEHRU, RATAN KUMAR, B.A., Indian C.S. Impl. Customs Service (asst. collr. (b. 10th Apl., 1902)).—Educ. at Allahabad Univ. and Exeter Coll., Oxford; joined the service, 21st Oct., 1925; arrived, 5th Dec., 1925, and served in the Central Provs. as asst. commr.; offg. dep. commr., Mar., 1929; Apl., 1932; and again, Mar., 1933; asst. collr. of customs, Bombay, Mar., 1934; ditto, Calcutta, Oct., 1935; offg. collr., June, 1936; first secty. to central bd. of rev., etc., Feb., 1937; dep. commr. (provl.), Nov., 1937.

NEHRU, SHRI SHRIDHAR, M.A., Ph.D., Indian C.S. (mag. and collr., United Provs.) (b. 17th Nov., 1888).—Educ. at Agra Coll. School, India, Allahabad; Magdalen Coll., Cambridge, and Heidelberg; apptd. after exam. of 1912; arrived, 1st Dec., 1913, and served in the United Provs. as asst. mag. and collr. and asst. commr.; jt. mag., Mar., 1918; under-sec. to govt., April to July, 1919; offg. mag. and collr., Oct., 1923; confd., June, 1930; secty. to govt., industries and educn. dept., June, 1931; dir. of publicity and reforms officer, Jan., 1932; member, Impl. council of agricl. research, June, 1933; magte. and collr. again from Dec., 1933.

NEIL, JOHN FREDERICK, Office of the High Commr. for India (exec. officer, store dept.) (b. 12th June, 1898).—Apptd. asst. clerk, store dept., India Office, Nov., 1919; clerical officer, Jan., 1920; transf. to Office of High Commr. for India, Oct., 1920; exec. officer, March, 1926.

NEILSON, ERIC FITZROY, late Indian Police Service (b. 5th Aug., 1888).—Joined the service as asst. dist. supt., Dec., 1908; dist. supt., April, 1920; awarded King's police medal, Jan., 1931; retd., Dec., 1931.

NEILSON, JAMES, late Indian State Railways (b. 21st Aug., 1882).—Trained at the R.I.E. Coll., joined the dept., 1st Oct., 1904, as asst. engr., state rlys.; exec. engr., Bengal Rly., Oct., 1912; on mil. duty on Mesopotamia rlys., April to Dec., 1917; dep. ch. engr. and senr. govt. inspr., Oct., 1920; retd., Feb., 1932.

NELSON, SIR ARTHUR EDWARD, K.C.I.E., O.B.E., M.A., late Indian C.S. (b. 17th Apl., 1875). Educ. at Newcastle High Sch., and Magdalen Coll., Oxford; apptd. after exam. of 1898; arrived, 16th Nov., 1899; served in the Cent. Provs. as asst. commr. and registrar, judt. commr.'s court; provl. supt., impl. gazetteer and supt. of ethnography, Aug., 1908; 3rd sec. to chief commr., July, 1909; served in fin. dept., govt. of India, Sept., 1910, to March, 1911; settlt. duty, July to Sept., 1910, and April, 1911, to June, 1912; asst. commr. and settlt. offr., June, 1913; dep. commr., Jan., 1915; commr. of excise, &c., March, 1916; O.B.E., Jan., 1919; chief sec. to

chief commr., March, 1920; C.I.E., Jan., 1923; temp. member of exec. council, Cent. Provs., March, 1927, and again Aug., 1927; confd., Dec., 1927; Kt., March, 1929; K.C.I.E., June, 1932; actg. govr. of Central Provs., July-Nov., 1932; ret'd., Nov., 1934.

NELSON, JAMES STEWART, late Indian Posts and Tels. (b. 11th Oct., 1877).—Held non-gazetted appts. from 20th July, 1898; asst. postmaster, Allahabad, Apl., 1903; supt., Feb., 1904; supt., post offices, Oct., 1923; offg. asst. dir.-genl., Oct., 1927; dep., P.M.G., U.P., Dec., 1927; ditto, Bengal and Assam, Dec., 1929; ret'd., Oct., 1932.

NELSON, JOHN JOSEPH HARPER, O.B.E., M.C., M.D., Lt.-Col. late I.M.S. (b. 12th Jan., 1882).—First commn. dated 1st Aug., 1908; O.B.E., Jan., 1919; profr., King Edward medl. coll., Lahore, 24th Nov., 1919; principal and prof. of medicine, ditto, Mar., 1930; ret'd., Jan., 1937.

NELSON, JOHN WOOD, M.A., late Indian C.S. (b. 14th Sept., 1884).—Educ. at George Watson's College, Edinburgh, and Edinburgh Univ.; apptd. after exam. of 1907; arrived, 23rd Nov., 1908, and served in E. Bengal and Assam as asst. mag. and collr., and asst. settlt. offr.; transf. to Bengal, April, 1912; personal asst. to dir. of central intelligence, Simla, Jan., 1914, to April, 1919; on special duty under dir., central intelligence, April to May, 1919; jt. mag. and dep. collr., June, 1919; mag. and collr., May, 1920; dist. and sess. judge, Jan., 1921; offg. secty., judl. dept. and supt. and membr. of legal affairs, Bengal, May, 1930; confd., Oct., 1930; ret'd., Feb., 1934.

NELSON, WILLIAM HENRY, C.S.I., M.A., Indian C.S. (commr., Bengal) (b. 15th June, 1880).—Educ. at Watson's Coll., and the Univ., Edinburgh; apptd. after exam. of 1903; arrived, 3rd Jan., 1905, and served in Bengal as asst. mag. and collr., and asst. settlt. offr.; jt. mag. and dep. collr. and settlt. offr., Sept., 1911; mag. and collr., Sept., 1917; on mil. service, Aug. to Oct., 1918; offg. dir. of land records, Bengal, Feb., 1920; offg. secty. of rev. and irrigation depts., Bengal, Feb., 1928; offg. commr., Dec., 1928; confd., May, 1930; offg. member, board of rev., Bengal, Feb., 1933; and again, Feb., 1936; C.S.I., Jan., 1938.

NEOGI, PANCHANAN, M.A., Ph.D., F.C.S. (Lond.), Indian Educl. Service (profr., presidency coll., Calcutta) (b. 4th Oct., 1883).—Held appts. in the provl. service from 11th Nov., 1907; profr. (I.E.S.), Feb., 1921; vice-princl., Dacca Coll., July, 1921; offg. profr., B.E. Coll., Nov., 1921; offg. profr., presdy. coll., July, 1925; confd., Nov., 1925.

NEOGI, PROBHA CHANDRA, Indian Service of Engrs. (exec. engr., Bengal) (b. 31st July, 1900).—Apptd. asst. exec. engr., Bengal, 22nd Jan., 1926; offg. exec. engr., May, 1929; confd., Jan., 1935.

NETER PARKASH MOHAN, M.A., B.Sc., Indian Forest Service (dep. consvr., Punjab) (b. 12th June, 1900).—Joined the service as asst. consvr., 14th Nov., 1923; dep. consvr., Nov., 1927.

NETHERSOLE, MICHAEL HENRY BRADDO, D.S.O., B.A., Indian Civil Service (mag. and collr., United Provs.) (b. 15th Sept., 1891).—Educ. at Wadham Coll., Oxford; arrived, 17th

Dec., 1919, and served in the United Provs. as asst. mag. and collr.; jt. mag., Nov., 1924; offg. mag. and collr., Nov., 1925; confd., Dec., 1931; offg. dep. commr., Mar., 1927, to Nov., 1933.

NEVILL, GEORGE AUGUSTUS, late Indian Police Service.—Joined the service as asst. supt., July, 1910; supt., April, 1914; ret'd., June, 1928.

NEVILL, HENRY RIVERS, C.I.E., O.B.E., B.A., late Indian C.S. (b. 24th May, 1876).—Educ. at the Charterhouse and Oriel Coll., Oxford; apptd. after exam. of 1898; arrived, 21st Nov., 1899, and served in the N.W. Provs. and Oudh as asst. mag. and collr. and asst. commr.; engaged in revision of district gazetteers from Aug., 1901, to Oct., 1909; jt. mag., April, 1911; on mil. service from Nov., 1917; dep. commr. (seconded), July, 1918; O.B.E., June, 1919; C.I.E., June, 1921; magte. and collr. and record officer, Nov., 1923; offg. commr. (addl.), March, 1925; commr. (provl. sub.), Apl., 1929; confd., July, 1930; author of a number of district gazetteers; ret'd., June, 1932.

NEVILLE, COLLIN MAXWELL WRIGHT, Indian Police (supt., Bihar) (b. 19th Feb., 1896).—Joined the service as asst. supt., 1st May, 1921; supt., April, 1926.

NEW, GEORGE ERNEST, late Indo-European Tel. Dept. (b. 26th Oct., 1874).—Held non-gazetted appts. from 1st Nov., 1892, to 21st Dec., 1907; general service clerk, 22nd Dec., 1907; asst. supt., July, 1911; supt., May, 1918; director, Persian section, June, 1921; ret'd., Oct., 1929.

NEWCOMB, CLIVE, C.I.E., M.D., Lt.-Col. I.M.S. (ret'd.); office of high commr. for India, (examiner of medl. stores) (b. 3rd Oct., 1882).—First commn. dated 30th July, 1910; in mil. employ to 23rd June, 1914, and again from 13th Aug., 1914, to 22nd Feb., 1920; offg. chemical examiner, Lahore, Feb. to Nov., 1920; prof. of chemistry, Madras, Dec., 1920; actg. prinpl., medl. coll., Madras, May, 1932; offg. surg.-genl. with the govt. of Madras, Oct., 1935; and again Apl. 1936; C.I.E., May, 1937; ret'd., Oct., 1937; apptd. examr. of medl. stores, office of high commr. for India, Oct., 1937.

NEWLAND, BRODERICK EDWARD MIDDLETON, L.R.C.P., L.R.C.S., Lt.-Col. I.M.S., ret'd. (b. 19th Aug., 1878).—First commn. dated 1st Sept., 1905; in mil. employ to 15th July, 1913; civil surg., Nov., 1920; ret'd., Aug., 1933.

NEWLAND, MISS MABEL KATE, late Indian Educl. Service.—Joined the service as lady supt., training coll. for women, Dharwar, 3rd April, 1922; offg. inspectress of girls' schools, April, 1925; confd., May, 1926; ret'd., Nov., 1928.

NEWMAN, CARL DAMIEN, M.B., B.S., Indian State Rlys. (b. 13th Feb., 1893).—Joined the service as dist. medl. officer, E.B. Rly., 13th Feb., 1893; offg. ch. medl. officer. variously from June, 1931.

NEWMAN, FELIX LAWRENCE, M.B.E., lat. Indian Police (b. 12th May, 1879).—Joined the service, 21st March, 1907, and held non-gazetted appts. up to 21st Oct., 1914; dep. supt., Oct., 1914; M.B.E., June, 1927; supt., July, 1927; ret'd., June, 1934.

NEWMAN, HAROLD LANCELOT, C.I.E., late Indian Forest Service (b. 5th Aug., 1878).—Joined the service, 13th Nov., 1901, as asst. consr. of forests, Bombay; dep. consr. of forests, Jan., 1907; consr.; June, 1922; offg. ch. consvr., Feb., 1928; confd., Aug., 1928; C.I.E., Jan., 1930; ret'd., Aug., 1933.

NEWSAM, WILLIAM OSWALD, Indian C.S. (dist. and sess. judge, Madras) (b. 13th March, 1892).—Educ. at Jesus Coll., Oxford; apptd. after exam. of 1914; arrived, 10th Dec., 1915, and served in Madras as asst. collr. and mag.; on mil. duty from Nov., 1917, to March, 1919; sub-collr., Oct., 1921; offg. dist. and sess. judge, Jan., 1926; offg. regr., high court, July, 1932; dist. and sess. judge, Chittoor, and addtl. judge, court of Resdt. and Judl. Commr. Coorg., Oct., 1936.

NEWTON, BERNARD ST. JOHN, B.Sc., Indian Service of Engrs. (exec. engr., Central Provs.) (b. 13th Apr., 1890).—Joined the service as asst. engr., Oct., 1913; exec. engr., Oct., 1922; ditto and under-sec. P.W.D., Nov., 1925; offg. suptg. engr., Apr., 1931.

NEWTON, EDWARD BERNARD, late Indo-European Tel. Dept. (b. 5th June, 1881).—Held non-gazetted appt. from 1st Sept., 1898, to 28th Feb., 1918; asst. supt., March, 1918; supt. April, 1923; asst. dir., Nov., 1925; offg. dir., Persian section, Dec. 1930; ret'd., Sept., 1931.

NIAMAT ULLAH, CHAUDHRI, late Judl. dept., United Provs.—Apptd. addl. judge, Allahabad high ct., Nov., 1928; judge, ditto, Aug., 1932; ret'd., Dec., 1937.

NIAZ MUHAMMAD KHAN, B.A., LL.B., Indian C.S. (jt. magte. and dep. collr., Bengal) (b. 12th June, 1907).—Educ. at Punjab Univ. and Sch. of Oriental Studies, London; joined the service 10th Oct., 1932; arrived in India, Nov., 1932, and served in Bengal as asst. magte. and collr.; jt. magte. and dep. collr., May, 1933; under secty. fin. dept., Apr., 1937; K.i.H. Medal, 1938.

NICHOLL, REV. ESMOND MCKILLOP, M.A., M.C., Indian Eccles. Estabt. (senr. chaplain, Punjab) (b. 16th Mar., 1891).—Apptd. chaplain on probation, 19th Nov., 1919; junr. chaplain, Nov., 1921; senr. chaplain, June, 1930.

NICHOLLS, WILLIAM HENRY, F.R.I.B.A., late Madras Public Wks. Dept. (b. 5th July, 1875).—Educ. at Rugby, and Hertford Coll., Oxford, and trained under Sir Thos. G. Jackson, Bart., R.A., architect; apptd. archaeological surveyor, United Prov. and Punjab circle, 1904; consulting architect to the govt. of Madras, July, 1907; on depu'tn. as architect member, Imperial Delhi Committee, Delhi Prov., May, 1913, to April, 1917; ret'd., July, 1930.

NICHOLS, HAROLD LACY, Indian C.S. (commr., Burma) (b. 2nd April, 1884).—Educ. at Manchester Grammar Sch., and Merton Coll., Oxford; apptd. after exam. of 1907; arrived, 7th Dec., 1908, and served in Burma as asst. commr.; under-sec. to govt., Dec., 1914; on mil. duty from Oct., 1917, to Oct., 1919; liquidator of hostile firms in Burma, May, 1921; offg. depy. commr., June, 1921; dep. commr., Jan., 1924; dep. sec., home and pol. dept., Dec., 1926; secty. to govt., judl. dept., May, 1927; secty., rev. dept., June, 1930; offg. ch. secty. to govt., Burma, May, 1932; offg. commr., June, 1933; confd., Dec., 1935.

NICHOLSON, JOHN PATRICK, United Provs., C.S. (mag. and collr.) (b. 14th Jan., 1888).—Joined the service, 22nd June, 1907; dep. collr., April, 1911; offg. mag. and collr., May, 1931; confd., Sept., 1934.

NICHOLSON, JOHN WILFRED, B.A., Indian Forest Service (dep. consvr., Orissa) (b. 13th Jan., 1893).—Joined the service as asst. consvr., 3rd March, 1916; provl. sylviculturist, Dec., 1921; on deputation to Kenya for 3 years from Sept., 1927; dep. consvr., Feb., 1931; offg. consvr. variously from Sept., 1931.

NICHOLSON, MARK ALLEYNE, M.B., Lt.-Col., I.M.S. (C.M.O., Central India) (b. 10th June, 1885).—First commn. dated 31st July, 1909; civil surg., Kurram, Oct., 1922; M.O., S.W. Scouts, Dec., 1923; offg. resdy. surg. in Persian Gulf, Sept., 1924; agency surg., Hazara dist., Oct., 1928; ditto, and C.M.O., Rajputana, Mar., 1931; C.M.O., Central India, and resdy. surgeon, Indore, Apr., 1932.

NICOL, CAMERON MACDONALD, M.A., M.D., D.P.H., Lt.-Col., I.M.S., Dept. of Public Health, Punjab (dir.) (b. 20th July, 1891).—First commn., 9th Feb., 1923; asst. dir. of public health, Rawalpindi, 18th Feb., 1934; dir. of public health, Aug., 1936.

NICOLAS, FRANCIS CARMINOWE, Lieut.-Col., I.A. (ret'd.) (b. 16th Nov., 1874).—First commission 30th Aug., 1893; in mil. employ to Oct., 1902, when he was apptd. asst. commr., Punjab; offg. dist. and sess. judge, Dec., 1915, and Nov., 1919; dist. and sessions judge, Jan., 1923; ret'd., Nov., 1929.

NICOLLS, REGINALD NOEL, O.B.E., late Indian State Rlys. (E.I. Rly.) (b. 29th Nov., 1889).—Joined the service as asst. traffic supt., 1st July, 1908; on milty. duty from 10th May, 1916; dist. traffic supt., N.W. Rly., April, 1918; on depn. to D.H. Rly., 31st Mar., 1928; ret'd., Sept., 1931.

NICOLSON, JOHN FRANCIS HUNTER, M.C., Indian Service of Engrs. (exec. engr., Burma) (b. 28th Sept., 1894).—Joined the service, 1st Oct., 1919; asst. exec. engr., April, 1920; offg. exec. engr., June, 1925; confd., Oct., 1927; ch. public works officer, F.S.S., Apr., 1935.

NIGAM, BISHAN NARAIN, M.A., Indian C.S. (jt. mag., United Provinces) (b. 4th Apr., 1906).—Educ. at Allahabad University and School of Oriental Studies, London; joined the service, 12th Oct., 1931; arrived in India, Nov., 1931, and served in the United Provinces as asst. mag. and collr.

NIGAM, JAGDISHWAR (see Jagdishwar Nigam).

NIGEL JONES, M. E. (see Jones, M. E. Nigel).

NIGHTINGALE, ARTHUR WINFIELD, Indian Service of Engrs. (suptg. engr., Madras) (b. 18th Nov., 1884).—Joined the service, 1st Oct., 1908; asst. engr.; on mil. duty from April, 1915, to Dec., 1920; exec. engr., Oct., 1918; suptg. engr., Sept., 1936.

NIHAL CHAND, RAI SAHIB, B.A., late Dept. of Industries and Labour (b. 11th Jan., 1882).—Joined the service, 10th June, 1902; supt., dept. of industries and lab., p.w. bch., May, 1927; asst. secty., ditto, Dec., 1931; ret'd., Oct., 1934.

NILAKANTA AYYAR, MAHADEVA, RAO BAHADUR, B.A., B.E., late Indian Service of Engrs. (b. 7th Dec., 1878).—Joined the service, 5th Feb., 1902; asst. engr., Feb., 1903; exec. engr., Feb., 1916; offg. supt. engr., May, 1926; confd., Apl., 1930; retd., Dec., 1933.

NILAKANTA MAHADEVA AYYAR, M.A. (Mad.), Indian C.S. (dep. secty., dept. of labour, govt. of India) (b. 14th May, 1899).—Educ. at Madras University and Cambridge; joined the service, 30th Oct., 1922; arrived in India, 2nd Jan., 1923, and served in Bengal as asst. mag. and collr.; and sub. divl. officer, June, 1924; at disposal of govt. of India, fin. dep. (cent. rev.), March, 1925, as offg. asst. collr. of customs, Calcutta; confd., Dec., 1925; asst. collr. of customs, Madras, June, 1926; offg. magte. and collr., Bengal, July, 1928; and again, Mar., 1932; addl. dist. and sess. judge, Assam, Nov., 1928; offg. ditto, Bengal, Oct., 1930; mag. and collr., July, 1935; addl. dep. secty., dept. of indus. and lab., govt. of India, Apl., 1936; dep. secty., dept. of labour, Nov., 1937.

NILKANTH SHRIRAM JATAR (see Jatar, N.S.).

NIL RATAN DHAR, M.Sc. (Cal.), D.Sc. (Lon.), F.I.C., F.C.S., Indian Educl. Service (b. 2nd Jan., 1892).—Joined the service as prof., Muir Central Coll., Allahabad, 19th July, 1919; with Allahabad Univ. from July, 1922.

NIND, WILLIAM WALKER, C.I.E., B.A., late Impl. Customs Service (b. 26th Sept., 1882).—Arrived, 29th Jan., 1906, and served as prob. and asst. collr. in Burma; ditto and supt. preventive service, May, 1913; member of the commission for Port of Chittagong, March, 1915; British delegate to Anglo-Afghan Trade Conference, Aug., 1922, to Feb., 1923; offg. collr. of customs, Calcutta, Feb., 1923; asst. collr., do., Oct., 1924; asst. collr. of customs, Bombay, Oct., 1925; offg. collr. of customs, Bombay, Feb., 1926; ditto, Calcutta, Oct., 1926; ditto, Rangoon, Nov., 1927; confd., Nov., 1929; collr., Bombay, Oct., 1932; temp. addl. member, central bd. of rev., Jan., 1934; offg. jt. secty. to govt. of India, fin. dept., June, 1934; offg. member, central bd. of rev., Apl., 1935; and again, June, 1936; C.I.E., Jan., 1936; retd., Sept., 1937.

NINNIS, ROBERT DOUGLAS KIALLMARK, Indian Police (sup., Orissa) (b. 30th Apl., 1897).—Joined the service as asst. supt., 20th Mar., 1921; offg. supt., March, 1926; confd., Sept., 1929; awarded King's police medal, Jan., 1931.

NIRANJAN DAS, GULHATI, LALA, Indian Service of Engrs. (exec. engr., Punjab) (b. 15th Nov., 1904).—Joined the service as apprentice engr., Oct., 1926; asst. exec. engr., Oct., 1927; exec. engr., Oct., 1936.

NIRINJAN PRASAD GOYAL, LALA, Indian Service of Engrs. (exec. engr., Punjab) (b. 30th Apl., 1903).—Joined the service as apprentice engr., 28th Oct., 1905; asst. exec. engr., Oct., 1926; exec. engr., Oct., 1935.

NIVAS, HARI KRISHNA, Indian Service of Engrs. (exec. engr., Bihar).—Joined the service as asst. exec. engr., Bihar and Orissa, 14th Nov., 1928; exec. engr., Oct., 1937.

NIX-JAMES, EDWARD WILLIAM, B.Sc., Indian Service of Engrs. (exec. engr., Central Provs.) (b. 22nd Mar., 1895).—Joined the service as asst. engr., 28th March, 1920; asst. exec. engr., March, 1921; exec. engr., Oct., 1927.

NIXON, ARTHUR BAMFYLDE, B.A., late Indian Forest Service (b. 6th Dec., 1885).—Arrived, 19th Dec., 1910, and served as asst. consvr.; dep. consvr., Dec., 1915; personal asst. to consvr., June, 1919; retd., June, 1933.

NIXON, JOHN CARSON, C.S.I., C.I.E., B.Sc., Indian C.S. (secty. fin. dept., Govt. of India) (b. 21st Sept., 1887).—Educ. at Technical Institute, Portsmouth, Royal Coll. of Science, and Univ. Coll., London; apptd. after exam. of 1911; arrived, 29th Nov., 1912, and served in Bengal as asst. mag. and collr.; services placed at disposal of Indian munitions board, March to Dec., 1918; under-sec., govt. of Bengal, fin. dept., Dec., 1918; under-sec., comm. and ind. dept., govt. of India, July, 1919; under-sec., finance dept., govt. of India, April, 1920; dep. acct.-gen., Bengal, April, 1921; dep. contr. of currency, Bombay, Oct., 1923; offg. dep. contr. of currency, Madras, April, 1925; acct.-genl. Madras, Jan., 1926; ditto, Bombay, Nov., 1928; on spl. duty, fin. dept., govt. of India, Dec., 1930; temp. jt. secty., ditto, July, 1931; C.I.E., June, 1932; dir., rly. audit, Nov., 1932; acct.-genl., Bengal, Nov., 1933; addl. secty. to govt. of India, fin. dept. (temp.), Mar., 1935; member, council of state, Apl., 1935; secty. to govt. of India, fin. dept., Oct., 1935; C.S.I., June, 1936; offg. member, govr.-genl's exec. council, Apl., 1937.

NIYOGI, MACHRAJ BHAWANI SHANKAR, C.I.E., M.A., LL.M., Judicial Dept., Central Provs. (judge, high ct., Nagpur) (b. 30th Aug., 1886).—Apptd. offg. judl. commr., June, 1930; confd., Sept., 1930; judge, high ct., Nagpur, Jan., 1936; C.I.E., June, 1936.

NIZAM-UD-DIN, KHAN BAHADUR, Indian State Railways (exec. engr., N.W.Rly.) (b. 3rd July, 1893).—Asst. engr., 17th Sept., 1916; asst. exec. engr., Sept., 1922; exec. engr., Aug., 1935.

NOAD, CHARLES HUMPHREY CARDEN, B.A., Barr.-at-Law, late Judl. Dept., Punjab (b. 25th Jan., 1880).—Appt. asst. legal remember., Mar., 1922; admr.-genl. and offl. trustee, Nov., 1922; govt. advocate (provl.), Oct., 1926; confd., Feb., 1930; retd., Oct., 1933.

NOBLE, REV. REGINALD HARRY, M.A., Indian Eccl. Estabt. (senr. chaplain, Punjab).—Apptd. junr. chaplain, 11th Nov., 1928; senr. chaplain, Nov., 1936.

NOEL, EDWARD WILLIAM CHARLES, C.I.E., D.S.O., Lt.-Col., I.A., Indian Political Service (b. 14th Apl., 1886).—First commn., 21st Dec., 1904; in mil. employ till Dec., 1912, when he was apptd. to pol. dept. on probation; H.M.'s Vice-Consul, Ahwaz, April, 1915; confd. as pol. officer, Feb., 1916; on special duty in the Caucasus, Jan. to Oct., 1918; C.I.E., Jan., 1918; served under High Commr. for Iraq, Nov., 1918, to Jan., 1920; pol. agent, Kurram, April, 1924; consul for Kerman and Persian Baluchistan, July, 1929; dep. commr., Dehra Ismail Khan, May, 1931; dir. of agric. and allied depts., N.W.F.P., Dec., 1933.

NOON, SIR FIROZ KHAN, K.C.I.E. (high commr. for India).—Educ. Chief's Coll., Lahore, and Wadham Coll., Oxford; advocate of Lahore high ct., and member Punjab legisl. council from 1921; minister for local self-govt., Punjab, 1927; educn. Minister, Punjab, 1930; high commr. for India, 1st July, 1936; K.C.I.E., May, 1937.

NORBURY, HARRY CARTER, J.P.M.Inst.T., Indian Railway Accts. Service (chief accts. officer). (b. 18th Oct., 1883).—Joined the service as asst. auditor and acct., G.I.P.Rly., 2nd Oct., 1913; dep. chief auditor, G.I.P.Rly., Mar., 1924; confid. as dep. agent, Oct., 1926; served under Finl. Commr. Rlys. from Oct., 1929; dep. chief accts. officer, G.I.P.Rly., Oct., 1929; confid. as chief accts. officer, G.I.P.Rly., Mar., 1930.

NORMAN, CHARLES ARCHIBALD KENSIT, B.A., India Office (asst. solicitor) (b. 12th Mar., 1884).—Educ. at Eton and Trinity Coll., Cambridge; solicitor, Supreme Court, 1909; served on the legal staff, Public Trustee's Office, and H.M. Customs and Excise, Jan., 1910, to Feb., 1914; apptd. clerk, legal adviser's dept., 2nd Feby., 1914; asst. solicitor, Aug., 1933.

NORMAN, DOUGLAS REAY, M.A., Indian C.S. (judl. commr., states of W.I.) (b. 30th Apl., 1890).—Educ. at Jesus Coll., Oxford; apptd. after exam. of 1913; arrived, 28th Nov., 1914, and served in Bombay as asst. collr. and magte.; on mil. duty from July, 1916, to Dec., 1918; asst. rembr. of legal affairs and sec. to Bombay legislative council, May, 1921; offg. judge and sess. judge, March, 1923; confid., April, 1929; judl. commr., Ajmer-Merwara, Dec., 1932; ditto, states of western India and Gujerat states, Apl., 1933; and again, Oct., 1935.

NORMAN, WILLIAM JAMES, M.C., Major R.E., Survey of India (supt.) (b. 18th Apl., 1894).—Joined the service, 18th July, 1913; in milty. employ to 10th Aug., 1921; asst. supt., 11th Aug., 1921; offg. dep. supt., Nov., 1924; offg. supt., Jan., 1926; confid., May, 1929.

NORMAN-WALKER, J. N. (see Walker, J. N.).

NORMAND, CHARLES WILLIAM BLYTH, C.I.E., M.A., D.Sc., Indian Meteorological Dept. (dir. genl. of observatories) (b. 10th Sept., 1889).—Joined the service, 23rd Oct., 1913, as impl. meteorologist; on mil. service (I.A.R.O.), Feb., 1916, to April, 1919; offg. dir.-gen. of observatories from June, 1924; confid., Mar., 1928; C.I.E., Jan., 1938.

NORONHA, MARK STANISLAUS, B.A., LL.B., Judicial dept., Bombay (b. 19th July, 1889).—Apptd. judge, small cause ct., Bombay, 30th Dec., 1928.

NORRIS, ERIC MORTIMER, late Indo-European Telegraph Dept. (b. 11th Dec., 1875).—Held non-gazetted appt. from 15th May, 1894, to 14th Sept., 1913; offg. asst. supt., Sept., 1913; confid., May, 1915; supt., Jan., 1919; asst. dir., Oct., 1924; dir., Persian section, Oct., 1929; ret'd., Dec., 1930.

NORRIS, JAMES CECIL, M.A., late Indian Forest Service (b. 6th Apl., 1902).—Joined the service as asst. consvr., Burma, 22nd Dec., 1925; dep. consvr., Apl., 1931; ret'd., June, 1933.

NORTHEY, HENRY STEWART, Indian Service of Engrs. (suptg. engr., Madras) (b. 24th July, 1884).—Joined the service, 1st Oct., 1906, as asst. engr.; on mil. duty, Sept., 1916, to Feb., 1920; exec. engr., March, 1920; offg. suptg. engr., May, 1929; confid., Dec., 1933.

NORTON, ERNEST LINGHAM, B.A., late Indian C.S. (b. 4th Sept., 1883).—Educ. at St. Paul's Sch., and Trinity Coll., Oxford; apptd. after exam. of 1907; arrived, 30th Nov., 1908, and served in the United Provs. as asst. commr. and asst. mag. and collr.; asst. censor, Bombay, Dec., 1914; jt. mag., April, 1917; serving under pol. resdt., Persian Gulf, from Sept., 1917, to March, 1919; on special duty in civil administrn. of Mesopotamia from March, 1920, to Jan., 1922; jt. mag., offg. first addl. dist. and sess. judge, Jan., 1922; offg. dist. and sess. judge, Jan., 1923; confirmed, Aug., 1924; offg. legal remembr. to govt., April, 1926; ret'd., Aug., 1933.

NOTTAGE, JOHN BOWTELL, B.Sc., late Indian Service of Engrs. (b. 4th Feb., 1891).—Joined the service as asst. engr., 1st Oct., 1913; on mil. service from 3rd Oct., 1917, to 7th Aug., 1920; asst. exec. engr., Aug., 1920; exec. engr., Oct., 1921; offg. supt. engr., May, 1932.

NOTT-BOWER, JOHN REGINALD HORNBY, C.V.O., late Indian Police (b. 16th Mar., 1892).—Joined the service as asst. supt., Nov., 1911; supt., Mar., 1924; awarded King's police medal, June, 1931; on deputation in Public and Judl. dept., India Office, Jan., 1932; ret'd., June, 1934; C.V.O., July, 1937.

NOYCE, SIR FRANK, K.C.S.I., C.B.E., late Indian C.S. (b. 4th June, 1878).—Educ. at Salisbury Sch., and St. Catharine's Coll., Cambridge; apptd. after exam. of 1901; arrived, 1st Dec., 1902, and served in Madras as asst. collr. and mag.; spl. asst. settl. offr., Oct., 1906, to March, 1908; asst. sec., March, 1909; under-sec., dept. of chief sec., May, 1910; sub-collr. and jt. mag., June, 1911; offg. under-sec. to govt. of India, dept. of rev. and agric., Dec., 1912; confirmed, March, 1913; offg. sec., dept. of rev. and agric., Oct. 1915, to Jan., 1916; temply. employed in store dept., India Office, from July to Aug., 1916; on spl. duty under govt. of India, dept. of rev. and agriculture, as sec. cotton commee, July, 1917; ditto under comm. and ind. dept. as controller of cotton cloth, Sept., 1918; ditto under rev. and agric. dept. as vice-presdt. and later presdt., Indian sugar cttee., Oct., 1919; C.B.E., Dec., 1919; ditto under govt. of Burma as member Burma Land Revenue Cttee., Dec., 1920; Indian trade commr. in England, July, 1922, to March, 1923; offg. sec. to govt. of Madras, development dept., May, 1923; C.S.I., June, 1924; presdt., Indian Coal Cttee., Oct., 1924, to March, 1925; offg. commr. of labour, Nov., 1925; offg. sec. to govt., local self-govt. dept., Jan., 1926; member, tariff board, govt. of India, commerce dept., April, 1926; on spl. duty, dept. of educn., govt. of India, Dec., 1928; Kt., June, 1929; secty., ditto, July, 1929; member, govr.-genl.'s exec. council, Apl., 1932; K.C.S.I., June, 1934; ret'd., Nov., 1937.

NUNAN, Dr. WILLIAM, B.A., M.D., B.Ch. (Dub.), T.C.D., late Medical dept., Bombay (b. 26th Jan., 1880).—Apptd. police surg., &c., and prof. of medl. jurisprudence, Bombay, 1st April, 1920; ret'd., Jan., 1935.

NUNN, ALBERT JAMES, Officer of High Commr. for India (higher clerical officer, store dept.) (b. 18th Mar., 1884).—Educ. at City of Westminster Sch.; served as 2nd div. clerk, bd. of educn., March, 1903, and bd. of trade, May, 1904; apptd. 2nd class clerk, India Office, 20th May, 1905; transf. to office of high commr. for India, Oct., 1920; higher clerical officer, April, 1922.

NUR ILAHI, KHAN BAHADUR, Shaikh, M.A., Indian Educl. Service (princl., Central Training Coll., Lahore) (b. 8th July, 1884).—Joined the service as asst. prof., govt. coll., Lahore, 4th Oct., 1905; offg. inspr. of schools, Feb., 1921; confd., Oct., 1925; asst. dir. of public instrn., Punjab, Aug., 1931; principl, Central Training Coll., Lahore, Oct., 1935.

NUR, KHWAJA MUHAMMAD (see Muhammad Nur, Khwaja).

NUR MUHAMMAD, KHAN BAHADUR Shaikh, M.A., Punjab C.S. (dep. commr.) (b. 26th April, 1886).—Joined the service as extra asst. commr. on probn., 31st Mar., 1910; confd., Apl., 1913; waterlogging asst. to finl. commrs., Punjab, Oct., 1928; offg. dep. commr., Dec., 1931; confd., July, 1933.

NUTT, HAROLD ROTHERY, M.D., F.R.C.S., L.R.C.P., Maj.-Gen., I.M.S., ret'd. (b. 28th Dec., 1876).—First commn. 27th June, 1901; in mil. employ till Sept., 1909, when he was apptd. dep. sanitary commr., United Provs.; civil surgeon, Dec., 1912; on mil. service from Oct., 1914, to Oct., 1915, and from Sept., 1915, to April, 1920; prof. and supt., King George's Hosp., Lucknow, Jan., 1924; inspr.-genl. of civil hosp., Apl., 1930; offg. surg.-genl. with govt. of Bombay, Mar., 1933; confd., July, 1933; hon. surg. to the King, Feb., 1934; ret'd., Aug., 1935.

NWE, U NAN, B.Sc., late Indian Forest Service (b. 29th Feb., 1904).—Joined the service as asst. consvr., Burma, 22nd Dec., 1926; ret'd., Jan., 1934.

NYUN, MAUNG, Indian C.S. (under secty. com. and indus. dept. govt. of Burma) (b. 30th Dec., 1909).—Educ. at A.V. High School, Thaton, Rangoon University and School of Oriental Studies, London; joined the service, 10th Oct., 1933; arrived in India, Dec., 1933, and served in Burma as asst. commr.; under secty. com. and indus. dept., Burma, Mar., 1937.

NYUN, MAUNG SOE, B.A., B.L., Indian C.S. (asst. commr., Burma) (b. 6th Mar., 1906).—Educ. at Calcutta University, Rangoon University and School of Oriental Studies, London; joined the service, 3rd Oct., 1931; arrived in India, Nov., 1931, and served in Burma as asst. commr.

OAK, VIDYADHAR GOVIND, B.Sc., Indian C.S. (jt. mag., United Provinces) (b. 18th May, 1909).—Educ. at Allahabad University and School of Oriental Studies, London; joined the service, 12th Oct., 1931; arrived in India, Nov., 1931, and served in the United Provinces as asst. magte. and collr.; jt. magte., Nov., 1934.

OAKDEN, SIR RALPH, Kt., C.S.I., O.B.E., B.A., late Indian C.S. (United Provs.) (b. 13th July, 1871).—Educ. at Cranbrook Gr. Sch., and at Hertford Coll., Oxford; apptd. after exam. of 1894; arrived, 16th Dec., 1895; and served in N.W.P. and Oudh as asst. mag. and collr., and asst. commr.; joint mag., March, 1904; supt., Dehra Dun, Nov., 1910; mag. and collr., Jan., 1914; O.B.E., Jan., 1919; commr., Nov., 1923; and pol. officer in charge of ex-Amir of Kabul (in addn.), April, 1924; C.S.I., Jan., 1925; offg. member, board of revenue, April, 1927; confd., Nov., 1928; ret'd. Dec., 1930; Kt., Jan., 1931.

OAKLEY FRANK STUART, Indian Police (b. 8th Sept., 1902).—Joined the service as asst. dist. supt., Central Provs., 22nd Nov., 1923; offg. dist. supt., 1927.

OATEN, EDWARD FARLEY, M.A., LL.B., late Indian Educl. Service (Bengal) (b. 24th Feb., 1884).—Joined the service, 20th Oct., 1909, as prof., Presy. Coll., Calcutta; on mil. service, Sept., 1916, to March, 1919; asst. dir. of public instrn., Bengal, Feb., 1921; offg. dir. of public instrn., Feb., 1924; confd., June, 1924; ret'd., July, 1930.

OBAIDULLAH, ABU ZAFAR, Indian Police (asst. supt.) (b. 28th Feb., 1911).—Apptd. proby. asst. supt., Bengal, 1st July, 1931; confd., Sept., 1933.

O'BRIEN, CHARLES RICHARD, M.B., Lt.-Col. I.M.S., ret'd. (b. 4th Apl., 1879).—Joined the service, 1st Feb., 1906; arrived, 26th Sept., 1906; in mil. employ to Oct., 1908; civil surg., Sept., 1910; ret'd., Apl., 1934.

O'BRIEN, J. A., Civil Aviation Directorate (chief inspector of aircraft) (1st Feb., 1881).—Joined the service as chief inspr. of aircraft (temp.), Nov., 1936.

O'BRIEN, JOSEPH, O.B.E., B.A., late Indian Fin. Dept. (b. 7th Apl., 1879).—Joined the service as asst. examr., p.w. accts., 5th Feb., 1903; dep. examr., p.w. accts., 5th Feb., 1903; dep. examr., Jan., 1906; asst. acctnt.-gen., E. Bengal and Assam, Oct., 1909; govt. examr. of accts., R. and K. Ry., Feb., 1911; audit offr., Delhi Prov., May, 1913; on mil. service, Sept., 1914, to April, 1920; O.B.E., June, 1919; dep. acctnt.-gen., Bihar and Orissa, April, 1921; audit officer, Lloyd barrage and canals construction, Oct., 1923; offg. dep. finl. adviser ditto, Dec., 1926; ret'd., Apl., 1934.

O'BRIEN, MICHAEL, C.I.E., A.M.I.C.E., Indian Service of Engrs. (chief. engr., Madras) (b. 1st May, 1883).—Joined the service, 1st Oct., 1906, as asst. engr.; exec. engr., Nov., 1914; offg. suptg. engr., May, 1930; confd., Jan., 1934; chief engr., June, 1936; C.I.E., Jan., 1938.

O'BRIEN-SMITH, EDWARD QUINTON, B.A., Indian Forest Service (dep. consvr., Burma) (b. 20th Aug., 1901).—Joined the service as asst. consvr., 13th Dec., 1923; dep. consvr., Nov., 1928.

O'CALLAGHAN, HARRY BIRCHELL HONNER, late Indian Finance Dept. (b. 9th Jan., 1874).—Joined the service, 26th May, 1896; asst. audit officer, June, 1918; asst. acctnt.-gen., rlys., Feb., 1919; dep. ditto, Oct., 1923; auditor, J.B. Rly., April, 1924; asst. auditor,

N.W. Rly., Feb., 1927; offg. dep. ch. accts. officer, E.I. Rly., March, 1927; offg. dep. auditor, N.W. Rly., May, 1928; dep. ch. accts. officer, Lahore, Apr., 1929; retd. Mar., 1930.

O'GALLAGHAN, TIMOTHY PATRICK
