MORIARTY, C.I.E., late Indian Police, Assam (b. 25th Nov., 1886.—Joined the service as asst. supt., 8th Dec., 1906; in mil. employ from 16th Apr., 1915, to 14th Mar., 1919; supt., Apr., 1917; inspr.-genl., Sept., 1929; C.I.E., June, 1933; retd., July, 1937.

O'CONNOR, ARCHIBALD JOHN, late Indian Police (b. 19th Mar., 1881).—Joined the service, 19th March, 1908; I.A.R.O., from 5th March, 1915, to 19th Jan., 1923; supt., May, 1925; awarded King's police medal, Jan., 1927; retd., Feb., 1936.

O'CONNOR, RODRICK AUGUSTUS, late India Fin. Dept. (b. 19th July, 1878).—Joined the service, 1st Aug., 1899, as asst. examr., P.W. accts.; dep. examr., Jan., 1905; asst. acctnt.-gen., post office and telegraphs, March, 1914; dep. acctnt.-gen., Bombay, April, 1914; on depn. to mil. accts. dept., May, 1915, to March, 1919; dep. auditor, E.B. Rly., March, 1919; dep. auditor, N.W. Rly., Oct., 1923; offg. chief auditor, O. & R. Rly., June, 1924; offg. dep. auditor-gen., rlys. (inspn.), Nov., 1925; offg. ch. auditor, G.I.P. Rly., Dec., 1926; ch. auditor, N.W. Rly.; retd., Jan., 1932.

O'CONNOR, Sir WILLIAM FREDERICK
TRAVERS, Kt., C.S.I., C.I.E., C.V.O., Lieut.-Col., Indian Army (retd.).—1st commn., dated 14th Feb., 1890; in mil. emp. to Aug., 1899, when he was apptd. special asst. to pol. agent, Gilgit; intell. offr., Tibet Frontier Commn., June, 1903; C.I.E., Dec., 1904; Br. trade agent, Gyantse, April, 1905; asst. sec. to govt. of India, for. dept., April, 1908; H.B.M.'s consul for Seistan and Kain, Sept., 1909; H.B.M.'s consul-gen. and agent to govt. of India in Khorasan, April, 1910; H.B.M.'s consul for Seistan and Kain, Dec., 1910; H.B.M.'s consul for Kerman and Persian Baluchistan, Oct., 1912; H.B.M.'s consul at Shiraz, Nov., 1912; resdt. in Nepal, Dec., 1918; pol. officer in Sikkim, Jan. 1921; Brit. envoy at court of Nepal, Oct., 1921; C.V.O., June, 1922; C.S.I., Jan. 1924; Kt., June, 1925; retd., July, 1925.

ODDIN-TAYLOR, HARRY WILLOUGHBY, C.B.E., A.C.G.I., B.Sc., A.M.I.C.E., Indian Service of Engrs. (excec. engr., Bombay) (b. 12th July, 1886).—Joined the service as asst. engr., 1st Oct., 1909; exec. engr., Oct., 1917; resigned the service from 26th Sept., 1920; re-apptd. as exec. engr., 7th Dec., 1926; offg. suptg. engr., Oct., 1931; O.B.E., Jan., 1933; jt. secty. to A.G.G., Baluchistan, in the public works dept., Jan., 1936; C.B.E., May, 1937.

ODELL, FRANCIS DAVID, M.A., Indian Agric. Service (b. 20th Feb., 1891).—Joined the service as dep. dir. of agric., Burma, 2nd Dec., 1922.

ODGERS, Sir CHARLES EDWIN, Kt., Barr., late Judl. Dept., Madras (b. 17th Jan., 1870).—Apptd. junr. prof'r., Law Coll., Madras, 26th Aug., 1902; admr.-gen. and official trustee, Madras, Oct., 1911; acting judge high ct., Madras, July, 1919; judge, high ct., Madras, March, 1921; Kt., Jan., 1930; retd., Jan., 1930.

O'DONNELL, Sir SAMUEL PERRY, K.C.S.I., K.C.I.E., late Indian C.S. (United Provs.) (b. 16th Dec., 1874).—Educ. at Repton, and Univ. Coll., London; apptd. after exam. of 1896; arrived, 22nd Nov., 1897, and served in the N.W. Prov. as asst. mag. and collr.; asst. supt., Dehra Dun, Dec., 1903; joint mag., Dec., 1906; under-sec. to govt., Nov., 1907; asst. commr., Nov., 1909; regr., high court, N.W. Prov., April, 1910; dep. commr. and sec. to govt., April, 1912; on special duty in fin. dept. sectt., govt. of India, from April to June, 1914; mag. and collr., and sec. to govt., May, 1915; actg.-chief sec. to govt., Nov., 1917; confd., Feb., 1918; C.I.E., Jan., 1919; sec. to govt. of India, reforms office, Jan., 1920; sec. to govt. of India, home dept., March, 1921; member of govt.'s exec. council, United Provs., 4th Jan., 1923; C.S.I., Jan., 1923; K.C.I.E., June, 1925; actg. govt., Aug., 1926; K.C.S.I., Jan., 1928; retd., May, 1930.

O'DRISCOLL, MICHAEL JAMES, late Indian Police Service (b. 6th Dec., 1885).—Joined the service as asst. supt., 25th Nov., 1906; supt., Oct., 1919; supt., watch and ward, G.I.P. Rly., Nov., 1925, to June, 1930; retd., Oct., 1932.

O'DWYER, Sir MICHAEL FRANCIS, G.C.I.E., K.C.S.I., B.A., late Indian C.S.—Educ. at St. Stanislaus Coll., Tullamore, and Balliol Coll., Oxford; apptd. after open exam. of 1882; arrived, 19th Nov., 1885, and was apptd. asst. commr. in the Punjab; settlt. collr., 1889-94; dir. of dept. of land records and agric., Punjab, Dec., 1896; settlt. officer for Ulwar and Bhurtpore, July, 1897; special duty in connection with the scheme of the new frontier, April, 1901; dep. commr., July, 1901; rev. commr., N.W. Frontier Prov., Nov., 1901; resident and ditto, Feb., 1906; resident, Hyderabad, May, 1908; C.S.I., June, 1908; resident, Indore, Aug., 1910; lieut.-govr., Punjab, 26th May, 1913; K.C.S.I., June, 1913; G.C.I.E., June, 1917; retd., 1st Oct., 1920; member of commce. on Indian Army administration and organization, 1919.

O'FLYNN, Eric Mortimer, Indian Police (asst. supt.) (b. 28th Jan., 1907).—Joined the service as proby. asst. supt., Bombay, 11th Apr., 1930; confd., Mar., 1933.

OGDEN, KENNETH, Indian Railway Accts. Service (senr. accts. offer.) (b. 7th June, 1898).—Joined the service as asst. auditor, E.I. Rly., Nov., 1926; offcr. on spec. duty, Rly. bd., 11th Mar., 1921; accts. offer, E.I. Rly., Nov., 1926; offcr. on spec. duty for verification and valuation of stores held by Burma Rly., June, 1928; senr. accts. offer., Jan., 1937.

OGILVIE, CHARLES MACIVOR GRANT, C.B.E., B.A., Indian C.S. (secty to govt. of India, defence dept.) (b. 6th May, 1891).—Educ. at Bedford Grammar Sch., and Exeter Coll., Oxford; apptd. after exam. of 1914; arrived, 4th Dec., 1915, and served in the Punjab as asst. commr.; offg. administrator, Nabha State, July, 1923; asst. ditto, Sept., 1923; offg. dep. commr., May, 1925; provl., ditto, June, 1926; senr. secty. to finl. commrs., April, 1928; C.B.E., June, 1928; home secty. to govt., Punjab, May, 1929; and again, June, 1933; addl. secty. to govt., Punjab, fin. dept., Sept., 1933; offg.

secty., ditto, Oct., 1933; confd., Oct., 1934; on depn. for course at Impl. Defence Coll., Jan., 1936; secty. to govt. of India, defence dept., Apl., 1937.

OGILVIE, SIR GEORGE DRUMMOND, K.C.I.E., C.S.I., Lt.-Col., I.A., ret'd., late Indian Political Service (b. 18th Feb., 1882).—First commn. dated 28th July, 1900; supy. apptd. asst. commr., Delhi, Feb., 1905; asst. pol. agent and asst. commr., Quetta-Pishin, Dec., 1905; vice-consul, Bam, June, 1906; asst. to agent to govr.-gen. in Central India, Jan., 1908; asst. pol. agent, Bundelkhand, Feb., 1908; first asst. to agent to govr.-gen. in Central India, Dec., 1910; asst. pol. agent, Bundelkhand, Jan. to April, 1911; boundary settlt. commr. in Central India, April, 1911, to June, 1912; second asst. to resdt., Hyderabad, Nov., 1913; asst. sec. to govt. of India, Army Dept., Jan., 1915; dep. sec. (political) to govt. of India, for. and pol. dept., Jan., 1919; sec. to agent to govr.-gen., Rajputana, Nov., 1921, and again in Nov., 1922; resident in Mewar, March, 1922; dep. sec. to govt. of India, for. and pol. dept., Feb., 1923; resident in Mewar, Nov., 1925; C.I.E., Jan., 1925; secty., Indian States comee., Jan., 1928; offg. resdt., Kashmir, Apl., 1929; offg. A.G.G. in C.I., Sept., 1931; C.S.I., Jan., 1932; A.G.G. in Rajputana and ch. commr. Ajmer-Merwara, Oct., 1932; K.C.I.E., Jan., 1936; resdt. for Rajputana, Apl., 1937; ret'd., Oct., 1937.

OGILVIE, GEORGE HAMILTON, M.C., B.Sc., late Indian Forest Service (b. 20th Feb., 1888).—Joined the service as asst. consvr., 2nd Dec., 1912; on mil. service from 1st Dec., 1914, to 7th May, 1920; dep. consvr., Dec., 1917; ret'd., Sept., 1933.

OGLE, JOHN WILFRED, Indian State Rlys. (divl. transpn. officer, N.W. Rly.) (b. 3rd Feb., 1889).—Joined the service as asst. traffic supt., 25th March, 1913; asst. dist. traffic supt., Jan., 1922; divl. transportation officer, July, 1925; publicity officer, Mar., 1931.

O'GORMAN, FREDERICK WILLIAM, Indian Police (supt., Bombay) (b. 8th July, 1891).—Joined the service, 2nd July, 1913, as asst. supt.; awarded King's police medal, Jan., 1924; supt. of police, Jan., 1925; awarded bar to King's police medal, Jan., 1930.

O'GORMAN, MICHAEL THOMAS, O.B.E., late Indian Police (b. 15th Jan., 1881).—Joined the service, 16th March, 1899; asst. supt., Oct., 1919; awarded King's police medal, Jan., 1923; offg. supt., July, 1923; confd., July, 1927; O.B.E., Jan., 1935; ret'd., Jan., 1936.

OHN, MAUNG, B.A., B.L., Indian C.S. (asst. commr., Burma) (b. 16th October, 1909).—Educ. at Government High Schools, Thaton and Rangoon, Rangoon University and the School of Oriental Studies, London; joined the service, 6th Oct., 1933; arrived in India, Jan., 1934, and served in Burma as asst. commr.

OKA, DATTATRAYA SITARAM, LL.B., Bombay Civil Service (Judicial Branch) (asst. judge) (b. 20th June, 1883).—Held non-gazetted temp. appt. from 17th Sept., 1923, to 8th Jan., 1925; asst. judge and asst. sess. judge, Jan., 1925; offg. judge and sess. judge, Apl., 1928.

OLIVER, ALFRED JAMES, late India Posts and Tels. (b. 16th Aug., 1873).—Held non-gazetted appt. from 3rd March, 1901; asst. supt., tel. workshops, Sept., 1920; supt., ditto, Sept., 1925; ret'd., Oct., 1928.

OLIVER, HENRY ARTHUR, Indian Police (b. 14th Apl., 1916).—Apptd. asst. supt. Punjab, on probn., Dec., 1937.

OLIVIER, OF RAMSDEN, 1st Baron, Rt. Hon. SYDNEY HALDANE OLIVIER, P.C., K.C.M.G., C.B., B.A., LL.D.—Secty. of State for India, 23rd Jan., to 6th Nov., 1924.

OLPHERTS-FORRESTER, SHIRLEY WYBRANDTS GEORGE, B.A., Indian C.S. (asst. commr., Central Provs.) (b. 2nd Sept., 1905).—Educ. at University of Allahabad; University of Nagpur; University Coll., London, and School of Oriental Studies, London; joined the service, 9th Oct., 1929; arrived, 15th Nov., 1929, and served in the Central Provinces as asst. commr.

OLVER, SIR ARTHUR, C.B., F.R.C.V.S., Col., R.A.V.C., ret'd., Impl. Council of Agricl. Res. search Dept.—Apptd. animal husbandry expert, 18th Apl., 1930; knighted, May, 1937.

OLVER, STEPHEN JOHN LINLEY, Indian Police (b. 16th June, 1916).—Apptd. asst. supt., United Provs., on probn., 1st Nov., 1935.

OMAR, ZAFAR (see Zafar Omar).

OMMANNEY, DOUGLAS GRÆME, late Indian Police Service (Bombay) (b. 19th April, 1874).—Apptd. police probr., 17th Aug., 1893; asst. supt., Oct., 1895; supt., May, 1903; recd. King's police medal, Dec., 1911; dep. inspr.-gen., May, 1922; ret'd., April, 1929.

OMMANNEY, FREDERICK ROY, Indian Police (supt.) (b. 12th Sept., 1898).—Joined the service as asst. supt., Bombay, 15th Dec., 1922; offg. supt., March, 1926; confd., Jan., 1933.

O'NEILL, JOHN CONNELL, A.M.I.C.E., Indian State Rlys. (exec. engr., N.W. rly.) (b. 27th May, 1888).—Joined the service as asst. engr., Oct., 1911; exec. engr., Jan., 1922; services lent to Burma Rlys., from 2nd Dec., 1922; offg. divl. supt., N.W. rly., July, 1934.

O'NEILL, PATRICK LAURENCE, C.I.E., Col., I.M.S., ret'd. (b. 28th Feb., 1876).—Joined the service, 27th June, 1901; C.I.E., June, 1919; civil surg., Dec., 1923; offg. D.M.O. and M.O., central jail, Salem, Nov., 1925; inspr.-genl. of civil hosps., Burma, June, 1930; ret'd., Feb., 1933.

O'NEIL SHAW, JOHN CHARLES LIDDELL, Indian Police (supt., N.W.F.P.) (b. 5th Mar., 1896).—Joined the service, 5th May, 1920; asst. supt., Feb., 1922; offg. supt., March, 1925; confd., Aug., 1926.

O'NEILL, Rev. WILFRED STEPHEN, M.A., Indian Eccl. Estabt. (senr. chaplain) (b. 23rd Nov., 1886).—Apptd. junr. chaplain, Lahore, 24th March, 1927; ditto, N.W.F.P., Apl., 1927; senr. chaplain, Mar., 1937.

OPPENHEIM, ERNEST FERDINAND, C.I.E., B.A., Barr. (1899), late Indian C.S. (b. 3rd July, 1875).—Educ. at Rugby Sch. and Balliol Coll., Oxford; apptd. after exam. of 1898; arrived, 5th Dec., 1899, and served in the N.W. Prov. and Oudh as asst. mag. and collr., and asst. commr.; jt. mag.,

May, 1908; dep. commr., Nov., 1914; mag. and collr., Oct., 1918; offg. commr., April, 1925; confd., Jan., 1928; member, bd. of rev., Dec., 1931; C.I.E., Jan., 1932; ret'd., Dec., 1934.

ORAM, ARTHUR, B.E., Indian Service of Engrs. (suptg. engr., N.W.F.P.) (b. 20th Oct., 1885).—Joined the service as asst. engr., Oct., 1908; exec. engr., Oct., 1916; under secty. to govt. P.W.D. (irrigation), May, 1926; offg. suptg. engr., Nov., 1927; suptg. engr. (prov'l. perm.), July, 1935.

ORANGE, Sir HUGH WILLIAM, K.B.E., C.B., C.I.E.—Educ. at Winchester Coll., and New Coll., Oxford; junior examr., Board of Educn., London, 1893-1902; apptd. director-gen. of education in India, 6th March, 1902; ret'd., 30th Nov., 1910; C.I.E., Jan., 1906; chief inspr. of elem. schools, Board of Educn., London, Dec., 1910; now acctnl.-gen., ditto; C.B., Jan., 1914; Kt., Jan., 1919; K.B.E., June, 1928; author of 'Review of the Progress of Education in India, 1902-7.'

ORDE, Percy LANCELOT, C.I.E., Indian Police (inspr. genl., Punjab) (b. 19th Jan., 1888).—Joined the service, 24th Nov., 1907; asst. supt., April, 1908; supt., July, 1916; C.I.E., June, 1927; offg. dep. inspr.-genl., May, 1928; confd. (prov'l. subst.), May, 1930; confd., July, 1932; offg. inspr.-genl., Apl., 1935; inspr.-genl., and jt. secty. to govt. Punjab, home dept., confd., Sept., 1936; King's Police Medal, Feb., 1937.

O'REILLY, JOSEPH JAMES, B.A., B.E., late Indian Service of Engrs. (Madras) (b. 12th Feb., 1875).—Joined the service, 25th Jan., 1903, as temp. engr.; asst. engr., June, 1915; exec. engr., Aug., 1916; ret'd., Aug., 1930.

O'REILLY, MICHAEL PATRICK, late Indo-European Telegraph Dept. (b. 25th Apl., 1877).—Held non-gazetted appt. from 1st March, 1894, to 27th July, 1917; offg. asst. supt., July, 1917; confd., Sept., 1918; supt., June, 1924; ret'd., Apl., 1931.

ORGILL, TYRREL CHURTON, C.I.E., M.A., F.R.G.S., Indian Educl. Service (b. 7th June, 1884).—Joined the service as hd. master, Zilla Sch., Ranchi, 1st May, 1913; asst. dir. public instrn., Oct., 1924; dep. ditto, Dec., 1926; inspr. of schools, May, 1921; offg. dir. of public instrn., N.W.F.P., April, 1928; prinpl., Ravenshaw Coll., Oct., 1929; services placed at disposal of admin. of N.W.F. province, Nov., 1931; dir. of public instrn., N.W.F.P., Aug., 1933; C.I.E., Feb., 1937.

O'RIORDAN, KEVIN MORAN, O.B.E., Indian Police (dep. inspr.-genl., United Provs.) (b. 6th Mar., 1887).—Joined the service as asst. supt., 25th Nov., 1906; supt., Nov., 1921; offg. dep. inspr.-genl., Apl., 1931; Oct., 1932; and again, Oct., 1934; confd., June, 1935; O.B.E., June, 1935.

ORMEROD, CHARLES MAURICE, Indian C.S. dist. and sess. judge, Punjab (b. 27th Aug., 1904).—Educ. at Rugby and King's Coll., Cambridge; joined the service, 20th Oct., 1927; arrived, Dec., 1927, and served in the Punjab as asst. commr.; offg. under-secty. to govt., Punjab, l.s.g. dept. Nov., 1930; addl. dist. and sess. judge, Apl., 1933; offg. dist. and sess. judge, Apl., 1934; confd., Nov., 1936.

ORMISTON, GEORGE ALFRED, V.D., late Indian State Rlys. (E.I.Rly.) (b. 2nd Sept., 1875).—Joined the service as asst. dist. loco. supt., 30th Jan., 1904; dist. ditto, Feb., 1910; dep. loco. supt., April, 1922; dep. ch. operating supt., Feb., 1925; ret'd., Dec., 1930.

ORR, ARCHIBALD, Indian State Rlys. (supt. of collieries) (b. 27th Nov., 1890).—Joined the service as asst. mining engr., 28th Apl., 1920; asst. colliery supt., June, 1929; offg. ch. mining engr., July, 1931; offg. manager, Jerangdih Colliery, Mar., 1933; confd. as supt. collieries, June, 1935.

ORR, CECIL BRUCE, Indian Police (dist. supt.) (b. 2nd Mar., 1902).—Joined the service as asst. dist. supt., Burma, 30th Nov., 1921; offg. dist. supt., May, 1929; confd., May, 1933.

ORR, HERBERT, late Indian Service of Engrs. (b. 13th Mar., 1895).—Joined the service, 1st Oct., 1920; asst. engr., Dec., 1920; asst. exec. engr., Feb., 1923; exec. engr., Oct., 1927; ret'd., July, 1934.

ORTCHESON, JOHN, Indian C.S. (asst. commr., Punjab) (b. 6th Dec., 1905).—Educ. at Perth Academy, Edinburgh University, and Christ's College, Cambridge; joined the service, 14th Oct., 1930; arrived in India, Nov., 1930, and served in the Punjab as asst. commr.; judge, small cause et., Mar., 1936.

OSMAN ALI, SHUJAT, B.A., Indian C.S. (asst. mag. and collr., Madras) (b. 1st July, 1912).—Educated at St. Joseph's College, Coonoor, Madras, University of Madras, and Brasenose College, Oxford; joined the service, 11th Sept., 1935; arrived in India, Nov., 1935, and served in Madras as asst. mag. and collr.

OSMASTON, ARTHUR EDWARD, late Indian Forest Service (b. 4th Mar., 1885).—Joined the service, 1st Nov., 1907, as asst. consr.; dep. consvr., Nov., 1912; offg. consvr., Sept., 1925; confd., Feb., 1929; principal and prof. of forestry (temp.), forest coll., Dehra Dun, Apl., 1930; ret'd., Aug., 1935.

OSMASTON, BERTRAM HUTCHINSON, Indian Forest Service (dep. consvr., United Provs.) (b. 30th Mar., 1895).—Joined the service as asst. consvr., United Provs., 9th Dec., 1921; dep. consvr., Nov., 1926; offg. asst. forest economist, Dehra Dun, May, 1928; pers. asst. to forest economist, Apl., 1931.

OSMASTON, FITZ WALTER COMPLYON, B.A., Indian Forest Service (dep. consvr., Bihar) (b. 3rd July, 1901).—Joined the service as asst. consvr., 9th Feb., 1923; instr., forest research institute, April, 1924; dep. consvr., Nov., 1927.

OSMASTON, GEORGE HUTCHINSON, M.C., Capt. R.E., Survey of India (supt.) (b. 26th Sept., 1898).—First commn., dated 26th May, 1916; in milty. employ to 30th June, 1921; proby. asst. supt., July, 1921; confd., Oct., 1925; supt., Aug., 1929.

OSS, HENRY WILLIAM, late Indian State Rlys. (b. 16th Nov., 1879).—Joined the service, 23rd Aug., 1897; offg. asst. traffic supt., Apl., 1920; confd., Jan., 1921; asst. traffic manager, Nov., 1922; transptn. supt., Oct., 1925; ret'd., Nov., 1936.

O'SULLIVAN, ALFRED ERNEST, late Indian Police Service (b. 28th April, 1875).—Joined the service, 28th Nov., 1895, and served in Bengal and Assam as asst. supt.; supt., Oct., 1905; and asst. to inspr.-gen. of police, crime and rlyss., Sept., 1914; dep. inspr.-gen., Dec., 1919; inspr.-gen., April, 1927; confd., Aug., 1929; retd., Apl., 1930.

O'SULLIVAN, HUGH PATRICK, Indian Police (supt.) (b. 26th Jan., 1898).—Joined the service as asst. supt., Bombay, 7th June, 1923; offg. supt., Mar., 1926; confd., Dec., 1933.

OTTEWILL, WILLIAM THOMAS, O.B.E., India Office (supt. of records) (b. 21st April, 1878).—Educ. at Rochester Mathematical School; clerk, 2nd class, 12th Sept., 1898; asst. to joint editors, India Office List, Oct., 1914; temp. resdt. clerk, from March, 1917, to Aug., 1920; staff clerk, Aug., 1918; joint editor, India Office List, Nov., 1921, to April, 1926; attached to Indian delegation to League of Nations, 2nd Assembly, 1921; parliamentary clerk, April, 1922; asst. sec. to Indian delegation to League of Nations, 3rd, 4th, 5th and 6th assemblies, 1922, 1923, 1924 and 1925; M.B.E., June, 1925; supt. of records, April, 1926; O.B.E., June, 1935.

OULSNAM, SAMUEL HARRISON YARDLEY, C.I.E., M.C., B.A. (Cantab.), Indian C.S. (dep. commr., Central Provs.) (b. 17th Jan., 1898).—Educ. at Newcastle-under-Lyme High Sch., and St. John's Coll., Cambridge; joined the service, 29th Oct., 1921; arrived, 2nd Dec., 1921, and served in Central Provs. as asst. commr.; under-sec. to govt., March, 1926; offg. dep. commr., March, 1928; Sept., 1932; and again, Apl., 1933; offg. reforms offcr., Jan., 1935; dep. commr., Oct., 1937; C.I.E., May, 1937.

OUNG, TUN HLA, Indian Police.—Joined the service as asst. dist. supt., Burma, 23rd May, 1927.

OVERBECK-WRIGHT, ALEXANDER WILLIAM, M.D., D.P.H., Lt.-Col., I.M.S., retd. (b. 30th May, 1879).—First commn., dated 29th Jan., 1902; in milty. employ. to 28th Jan., 1909; offg. supt., lunatic asylum, Agra, March, 1909; reverted to milty. employ. from 28th Nov., 1909, to 3rd March, 1910; supy. supt., central jail, Dacca, March, 1910; ditto, Agra, Dec., 1910; supt., lunatic asylum, Agra (s.p.t.), April, 1912, and again, March, 1919; in milty. employ. from 30th Dec., 1914, to 3rd March, 1919; supt., mental hosp., Agra, Oct., 1925; retd., May, 1934.

OWDEN, JOHN SCAMBLER, Indian Forest Service (consvr.) (b. 28th Feb., 1887).—Joined the service as asst. consvr., 18th Dec., 1909; in mil. employ. from 12th Nov., 1915, to 8th March, 1919; dep. consvr., March, 1919; offg. consvr., U.P., June, 1929; ditto, Assam, April, 1930; and again, June, 1932; ditto, Bihar and Orissa, Feb., 1933; confd., July, 1934.

OWEN, GODFREY ELWIN, B.A., late Indian C.S. (b. 2nd Feb., 1890).—Educ. at Harrow and Hertford Coll., Oxford; apptd. after exam. of 1912; arrived, 19th Nov., 1913, and served in Bihar and Orissa as asst. mag. and collr.; under-sec. to govt., rev., and judl. depts., March, 1920; offg. mag. and collr., April, 1922;

offg. secty. to govt., local self-govt. dept., Nov., 1927; magt. and collr., Dec., 1928; secty. to local self-govt. dept., Dec., 1928; retd., Sept., 1935.

OWEN, LEONARD, M.A., C.I.E., Indian C.S. (mag. and collr., United Provs.) (b. 6th Oct., 1890).—Educ. at Univ. Coll., Bangor; apptd. after exam. of 1914; on mil. duty to March, 1919; arrived, 27th March, 1919, and posted to the United Provs.; jt. mag., Nov., 1921; offg. depy. commr., Nov., 1922; offg. mag. and collr., April, 1924; confd., Dec., 1931; offg. settlement officer, Nov., 1926; repres. of U.P. govt. in central legis. assembly, Aug. to Sept., 1935; C.I.E., Jan., 1938.

OWEN, LLEWELLYN GORDON, M.A., Indian Educl. Service (prof., Univ. of Rangoon) (b. 5th Mar., 1885).—Joined the service as prof. of mathematics, 12th Feb., 1915; on mil. service from 29th Aug., 1916, to 26th Oct., 1919.

OWEN, MORRIS, M.Sc., F.Inst.P.H., Indian Educl. Service (dir. of public instrn., C.P.) (b. 16th Feb., 1885).—Joined the service as prof., Victoria Coll. of Science, Nagpur, 3rd Feb., 1912; princpl., Morris Coll., Nagpur, April, 1923, and again, Jan., 1926; princpl., Victoria Coll. of Science, Nov., 1924; offg. princpl., coll. of science, Nagpur, July, 1926; confd., May, 1928; offg. dir. of public instrn., C.P., Dec., 1934; confd., Apl., 1937.

OWENS, JOSEPH HUBERT, C.I.E., M.B.E., Indian Posts and Telegraphs (dep. P.M.G.) (b. 18th Feb., 1887).—Joined the service as proby. supt., 12th Jan., 1909; confd., Dec., 1910; on depn. to I.E.F. from Oct., 1914, to Apl., 1922; dep. p.m.g., June, 1935.

OWENS, TERENCE FRANCIS, Lt.-Col., I.M.S., retd., late Burma Med. Dept. (b. 8th Oct., 1878).—Rendered service in the mil. dept. and as probationer in chemical examr.'s office; offg. chemical examr., June, 1909; chemical examr. and bacteriologist, Dec., 1911; examr. of medical stores, India store dept., May, 1932; retd., Oct., 1933.

OXBURY, HAROLD FREDERICK, B.A., Indian C.S., Impl. Customs Service (asst. collr.) (b. 11th Nov., 1903).—Educ. at King Edward VI Sch., Norwich, and Trinity Coll., Cambridge; joined the service, 4th Oct., 1928; arrived, Nov., 1928, and served in Burma as asst. commr.; under-secy. to govt., rev. dept., Nov., 1931; ditto, local govt. dept., Mar., 1932; ditto, home and pol. dept., Mar., 1933; dep. secty., fin. dept., Burma, Dec., 1934; asst. collr., Impl. Customs Service, May, 1935.

OXLEY, HUMPHREY LESLIE MALCOLM, India Office (clerk, legal adviser's dept.) (b. 9th Oct., 1909).—Apptd. clerk, legal adviser's dept., 23rd Sept., 1933.

OXLEY, JAMES CHARLES STEWART, F.R.C.S., L.R.C.P., Lieut.-Col., I.M.S. (retd.) (b. 15th Nov., 1874).—First commn. 28th June, 1900; in mil. employ. to July, 1903, when he was apptd. civil surgn.; on mil. service from Jan., 1915, to Aug., 1920; retd., May, 1930.

OZANNE, ROBERT DE LA CONDOMINE, Indian Police (b. 26th June, 1899).—Joined the service as asst. dist. supt., Central Provs., 13th Oct., 1923; offg. dist. supt., April, 1926.

PACKMAN, KENNETH CHALMERS, Major, I.A., Indian Political Service (b. 14th May, 1899).—First commn. dated 15th April, 1919; in milty. employ to March, 1924; asst. for Mekran to pol. agent in Kalat, May, 1925; asst. pol. agent, S. Waziristan, Sept., 1928; 2nd addl. ditto, June, 1930; pol. agent, Dir, Swat, and Chitral, Oct., 1930; ditto, North Waziristan, Apl., 1931; secty. to A.G.G., Deccan states, Mar., 1935; A.G.G., Deccan States, and resdt. at Kolhapur (in addn.), May, 1936; consul-genl., Kashgar, Oct., 1936.

PADDON, Sir STANLEY SOMERSET WREFFORD, Kt., C.I.E., A.M.I.Mech.E., Lieut.-Col., I.A. (ret'd.); Office of High Commr. for India (dir.-gen., stores dept.) (b. 28th March, 1881).—First commn., 15th Aug., 1900; apptd. to I.A., March, 1905; ret'd., Feb., 1914; re-employed as temp. staff offr., directorate of mil. operations, War Office, Nov., 1914; ditto, mil. dept., India Office, July, 1917; C.I.E., Jan., 1919; supt., India store depot, March, 1919; transfed. to office of high commr. for India, Oct., 1920; dir.-gen., store dept., 3rd Oct., 1923; Kt., Jan., 1932.

PADDONROW, LINDSAY ARTHUR, Indian Police (b. 18th Feb., 1912).—Joined the service as prob. asst. supt., Bombay, Nov., 1932.

PADFIELD, Rev. GEORGE AUGUSTUS SELWYN, late Indian Eccles. Estabt. (United Provs.) (b. 7th July, 1876).—Apptd. a chaplain on probation, 22nd March, 1909; junr. chaplain, Aug., 1911; senr. chaplain, Aug., 1921; ret'd., Feb., 1930.

PADMANABHA AYYAR, NARAYANA, B.A., B.E., Indian Service of Engrs. (exec. engr., Madras) (b. 10th Aug., 1899).—Joined the service as appr. engr., 11th June, 1923; asst. exec. engr., July, 1924; offg. exec. engr., May, 1928; confd., July, 1933.

PADMANABHAN, DORAIWAMIER, B.A., Indian C.S. (jt. mag., United Provinces) (b. 15th March, 1909).—Educ. at Madras University and King's College, Cambridge; joined the service, 12th Oct., 1931; arrived in India, Nov., 1931, and served in the United Provs. as asst. mag. and collr.; jt. magte., Apl., 1934.

PAGE, Sir ARTHUR, Kt., K.C., Barr. (1901), late Judicial dept., Bengal (b. 9th Mar., 1876).—Addl. judge, high ct., Calcutta, from 1st Jan., 1923; apptd. judge, high ct., Calcutta, 18th Jan., 1924; ch. justice, high ct., Rangoon, Apl., 1930; Kt., June, 1930; ret'd., Apl., 1936.

PAGE, ARTHUR JOHN, B.A., late Indian C.S. (b. 2nd Nov., 1882).—Educ. at Cheltenham Gr. Sch., and Corpus Christi Coll., Oxford; apptd. after exam. of 1906; arrived, 25th Nov., 1907, and served in Burma as asst. commr. and settlt. offr.; dep. commr., May, 1918; offg. commr. of settlts. and land records, March, 1925; confd., July, 1925; offg. secty. to govt., Burma, judl. dept., Nov., 1928; dir. of statistics and lab. commr., Dec., 1929; offg. commr., Nov., 1931; ret'd., Mar., 1935.

PAGE, JAMES ALFRED, A.R.I.B.A., late Archaeological Survey dept. (b. 25th Feb., 1887).—Joined the service as asst. supt., 21st Mar., 1913; supt., Jan., 1921; dep. dir. genl. of archaeology, Apl., 1929; ret'd., Aug., 1934.

PAHLUMAL, KALUMAL (see Kalumal Pahlumal).

PAI, AMMEMBAL VITTAL, Indian C.S. (agent of the govt. of India in Ceylon) (b. 11th Oct., 1901).—Educ. at University of Madras and Wadhram College, Oxford; joined the service, 25th Oct., 1926; arrived, Dec., 1926, and served in Madras as asst. collr. and magte.; sub-collr., and jt. magte., March, 1928; under-secty. to govt., P.W. and labour dept., Feb., 1931; agent of the Govt. of India in Ceylon, Apl., 1936.

PAI, AMMEMBALA UPENDRA (see Upendra Pai, A.).

PAI, K. RAMA (see Rama Pai, K.).

PAI, MANGALORE PURUSHOTHAM, Indian C.S. (sub. coll. and jt. mag., Madras) (b. 1st Dec., 1906).—Educ. at University of Madras, King's Coll., London, and Corpus Christi Coll., Oxford; joined the service, 10th Oct., 1919; arrived, Nov., 1929, and served in Madras as asst. coll. and mag.; sub-collr. and jt. magte., Nov., 1930.

PAINE, CRISPIN BERNARD CHITTY, Indian C.S. (asst. commr., Assam) (b. 2nd Mar., 1906).—Educ. at St. Paul's School and Exeter Coll., Oxford; joined the service, 11th Oct., 1929; arrived, Nov., 1929, and served in Assam as asst. commr.; offg. under secty. to govt., Assam, Apl., 1933; offg. commr., Nov., 1935.

PAING, MAUNG, B.Sc., Indian C.S. (under secty. to govt., educn. dept., Burma) (b. 7th Dec., 1907).—Educ. at Rangoon University and School of Oriental Studies, London; joined the service, 12th Oct., 1931; arrived in India, Jan., 1932, and served in Burma as asst. commr.; under secty. to govt., educn. dept., Burma, June, 1936.

PAISLEY, JOHN FREDERICK, B.E., Indian Service of Engrs. (b. 18th June, 1906).—Apptd. asst. exec. engr., Bombay, Oct., 1928.

PAKENHAM-WALSH, ERNEST, late Indian C.S. (b. 19th June, 1875).—Educ. at Birkenhead Sch., and Trinity Coll., Dublin; apptd. after exam. of 1898; arrived, 26th Dec., 1899, and served in Madras as asst. collr. and mag.; sub-collr. and joint mag., May, 1910; temp. addl. sess. judge, July, 1917; dist. and sess. judge, May, 1919; offg. judge, high ct., Madras, July, 1928, Oct., 1928, Jan., 1929, July, 1930, July, 1931, and again, Feb.-May, 1932; apptd. judge, high ct., Madras, July, 1932; ret'd., June, 1935.

PAKENHAM-WALSH, WILFRED PAKENHAM, M.A., Indian C.S. (dist. and sess. judge) (b. 2nd June, 1898).—Educ. at Malvern Coll. and University Coll., Oxford; joined the service, 2nd Nov., 1922; arrived, 26th Dec., 1922, and served in Burma as asst. commr.; dep. regr., high ct., Rangoon, March, 1928; offg. dist. and sess. judge, July, 1929; registrar, high ct., Rangoon, Sept., 1931; dist. and sess. judge, July, 1933; transfed. to govt. of Punjab, 1938.

PAL, ASHUTOSH, M.A., B.L., late Bengal Civil Service.—Joined the service, 17th April, 1900; munsf., Sept., 1901; sub-judge, May, 1919; ret'd., Oct., 1928.

PAL, NIRANJAN, Indian Forest Service (dep. consvr., Bengal) (b. 26th Oct., 1900).—Joined the service as asst. consvr., Dec., 1924; dep. consvr., Nov., 1928.

PALIT, ANATH NATH, O.B.E., F.R.C.S.E., Lt.-Col., I.M.S., Civil Medl. Dept., Bihar (civil surg.) (b. 14th Oct., 1883).—First commn, dated 29th Jan., 1910; in mil. service up to 30th Sept., 1922; civil surg., Oct., 1922; O.B.E., June, 1936.

PALMER, CADWALLADER EDWARDS, M.A., M.B., Col., I.M.S. (b. 24th Dec., 1880).—Joined the service, 1st Sept., 1905; in mil. employ, Sept., 1905, to Oct., 1907, and from Sept., 1908, to Apl., 1909; offg. jail supt., April, 1909; on mil. service, Oct., 1914, to Nov., 1919; supt., central prison, Allahabad, Nov., 1919; offg. inspr.-gen. of prisons, April, 1926; confd., Feb., 1930; offg. inspr.-genl., Assam, Jan., 1935; confd., Jan., 1935.

PALMER, RT. REV. EDWIN JAMES, M.A., D.D. (b. 1869).—Fellow and tutor of Balliol Coll., Oxford; apptd. Bishop of Bombay, 30th July, 1908; resigned, Sept., 1929.

PALMER, FRANCIS EDWARD CHETWYND, Indian Forest Service (dep. consvr., United Provs.) (b. 6th May, 1887).—Joined the service, 1st July, 1911; extra asst. consvr., June, 1914; promoted to I.F.S. as dep. consvr., United Provs., Nov., 1931.

PALMER, REGINALD WILLIAM VAUGHAN, Indian Forest Service (dep. consvr., Burma) (b. 2nd May, 1894).—Joined the service as asst. consvr., 23rd Dec., 1921; dep. consvr., July, 1923.

PALMER, WILLIAM JOHN, B.A., Indian C.S. (asst. mag. and collr., Bengal) (b. 25th April, 1909).—Educated at Cheltenham Grammar School and Christ's College, Cambridge; apptd. after exam. of 1931; arrived in India, Nov., 1932, and served in Bengal as asst. mag. and collr.

PALMES, WILLIAM TEVERY, late Indian C.S. (b. 17th Aug., 1888).—Educ. at Bradford Sch., Polytechnikum, Brunswick, Trinity Coll., Dublin, and Trinity Coll., Cambridge; apptd. after exam. of 1912; arrived, 9th Dec., 1913, and served in Burma as asst. commr.; on mil. service from Aug., 1916, to May, 1919; offg. under sec. to govt. of Burma, April, 1920; offg. dep. commr., Sept., 1921, Sept. 1923, and again, Dec., 1926; confd., Nov., 1930; ret'd., Jan., 1934.

PALSULE, KESHAV BALKRISHNA, B.A., LL.B., late Central Provs. C.S. (Judl.) (b. 6th Oct., 1878).—Joined the service as munsf., 10th Mar., 1903; extra asst. commr., May, 1906; sub. judge, July, 1910; dist. and sess. judge, Nov., 1929; ret'd., Oct., 1933.

PAMPHILON, ALFRED HART, India Office (exec. officer, acctnt.-gen.'s dept.) (b. 3rd Dec., 1890).—Served as asst. clerk, customs house, from June, 1910, to July, 1912, and as sec. div. clerk, foreign office, from July to Nov., 1912; apptd. 2nd class clerk, India Office, 11th Nov., 1912; and att'd. to registry and record dept.; transf'd. to mil. dept., Jan., 1917; on service with Royal Navy from Nov., 1916, to Feb., 1919; exec. officer, April, 1922; transf'd. to acctnt.-gen.'s dept., Feb., 1923.

PANANDIKAR, Dr. SATTYASHRAYA GOPAL, M.A. (Bom.), D.Sc. (Lon.), Indian Educl. Service (b. 18th July, 1893).—Joined the service as prof. of history and pol. economy, Elph. Coll., Bombay, 24th Oct., 1924.

PANCHAPAGESAM, TIRUVALUR RAMASWAMI, M.A., Finance dept., govt. of India (b. 4th Nov., 1887).—Joined the service as asst. prof., presdy. coll., Madras, 31st July, 1911; probnr., finance dept., Apl., 1915; asst. acct. genl., Calcutta, July, 1916; ditto, Madras, Nov., 1916; ditto, Bombay, May, 1921; ditto, central revs., Oct., 1923; dep. auditor, N.W. Rly., Apl., 1924; divl. audit officer, N.W. Rly., Nov., 1924; asst. dir. army audit, southern comd., Mar., 1925; dep. acct. genl., Madras, Apl., 1927; ditto, Bombay, Feb., 1928; ditto, Delhi, Nov., 1929; ditto, central revs., July, 1930; acct. genl., ditto, May, 1931; dep. acct.-genl. (works), Punjab, May, 1932; ditto (civil), Nov., 1932; offg. ch. auditor, Burma rlys., Nov., 1933; offg. acct.-genl., Burma, May, 1935; offg. ch. auditor, Burmarlys., June, 1935; comptr., Orissa, Apl., 1936.

PANCHAPAKESA AYYAR, AIYLAM SUBRAHMANYA AYYAR, B.A., Indian C.S. (dist. and sess. judge, Madras) (b. 26th Jan., 1899).—Educ. at Madras Univ. and Oxford Univ., (non-collegiate); joined the service, 1st Nov., 1922; arrived, 5th Dec., 1922, and served in Madras as asst. collr. and mag.; sub.-collr. and jt. mag., Jan., 1924; offg. collr. and dist. mag., Aug., 1925; offg. dist. and sess. judge, July, 1933; confd., Mar., 1935.

PANCKRIDGE, HUGH RAHERE, Barr., Judicial dept., Bengal (judge, high ct., Calcutta) (b. 2nd Oct., 1885).—Joined the service as standing counsel, 13th Jan., 1926; offg. judge, high ct., Calcutta, May, 1929; addl. judge, ditto, Nov., 1929; judge, ditto, April, 1930.

PANDALAI, KRISHNAN, RAO BAHADUR, LL.D., Barr., late Judl. Dept., Madras (b. 6th April, 1874).—Joined the service as 3rd judge, ct. of small causes, July, 1919; 2nd judge, ditto, July, 1921; ch. presdy. magte., Feb., 1927; offg. ch. judge, court of small causes, Feb., 1928; judge, high ct., Madras, Dec., 1928; ret'd., Apl., 1934.

PANDALAI, KRISHNAN GOPINATH, M.B., Lt.-Col., late Indian Medical Service, (b. 20th May, 1885).—First commn. dated 28th Jan., 1911; in milty. employ till 23rd April, 1922; dist. medl. officer, Jan., 1924; 2nd surg., genl. hosp., Madras, June, 1929; ret'd.

PANDE, CHANDRA DUTT, RAI SAHIB, M.A., late India Posts and Tels. (b. 28th July, 1880).—Joined the service, 15th July, 1902, and held non-gazetted appts. to 27th Jan., 1907; supt., Jan., 1907; asst. dir. genl., post office, Nov., 1928; ret'd., May, 1934.

PANDE, GAURI DATT, B.Sc., Indian Service of Engrs. (exec. engr., Burma) (b. 10th Aug., 1892).—Joined the service, 23rd Oct., 1916; asst. engr., Oct., 1917; asst. exec. engr., Jan., 1920; exec. engr., Oct., 1928.

PANDE, JIVA NAND (see Jiva Nand Pande),

PANDE, PITAMBAR DUTT, M.A., Indian Audit Dept. (b. 15th Dec., 1896).—Joined the service, 22nd Mar., 1921; services placed at the disposal of Finl. Commr. Rlys., Dec., 1935; on for. service under govt. of Burma, Oct., 1937.

PANDIT, ANAND DATTATRAYA, B.Sc., Indian C.S. (jt. mag., United Provinces) (b. 1st April, 1909).—Educ. at Patwardhan High School, Nagpur, Universities of Nagpur and Allahabad and Balliol College, Oxford; joined the service 5th Oct., 1933; arrived in India, Nov., 1933, and served in the United Provinces as asst. magte. and collr.; jt. magte., Mar., 1936.

PANDIT, GOVIND NARAYAN, B.E., B.Sc., Indian Service of Engineers (b. 28th Mar., 1906).—Apptd. asst. exec. engr., Bombay, 11th Aug., 1931.

PANDURANG RAO, VOMBATHKERE, B.A., Barr., Indian C.S. (judge, high ct., Madras) (b. 11th June, 1881).—Educ. at Govt. Coll., Mangalore, Madras Univ., and St. John's Coll., Cambridge; apptd. after exam. of 1903; arrived, 19th Dec., 1904, and served in Madras as asst. collr. and mag.; sub. collr. and jt. mag., July, 1912; dist. and sess. judge, April, 1924; dep. sec., local self-govt. dept., Oct., 1924; collr. and dist. mag. and sec. to govt., devl. dept., Dec., 1925; dist. and sess. judge, April, 1928; offg. judge, high ct., Jan., 1934; confd., July, 1934.

PANDYA, ISHWARRAI NARBHERAM, Income-Tax Dept., Bombay (asst. commr.) (b. 21st Sept., 1883).—Joined the service, Apl., 1917; held non-gaz. appts., 1916-1924; confd. as income-tax officer, Bombay, June, 1925; confd. as asst. commr. of income-tax, July, 1936.

PANDYA, DEBI DAT (see Debi, D. P.).

PANIKAR, ITTEKOTE ACHUTA, M.A., Indian Audit Dept. (b. 11th July, 1900).—Joined the service, 21st Feb., 1923; dep. acctnt.-genl., Burma, Sept., 1935; on for. service under the govt. of Burma, Apl., 1937.

PANJABI, KEWALRAM LALCHAND, B.A., Indian C.S. (collr. and magte., Bombay) (b. 29th Dec., 1898).—Educ. at St. Patrick's School, Karachi, and Bombay and London Universities; apptd. after exam. of 1923; arrived in India, 30th Nov., 1923, and served in Bombay as asst. collr. and magte.; offg. collr. and pol. agent, Surat, March, 1927; offg. regr., co-op. societies, Poona, Nov., 1931; collr. and magte., Oct., 1936.

PANNA LAL, B.A. (Cantab.), B.Sc., LL.B., Barr., Indian C.S. (magte. and collr., United Provs.) (b. 23rd Nov., 1883).—Educ. at Agra Collegiate Sch., Allahabad, and Calcutta Univs., and St. John's Coll., Cambridge; apptd. after exam. of 1906; arrived, 31st Dec., 1907, and served in United Provs. as asst. magte. and collr.; jt. magte., Jan., 1916; under-secty. to govt., April, 1917, to Aug., 1918; forest settlt. offr., Dehra Dun, Oct., 1918; magte. and collr., Oct., 1923; spl. officer in addition, July, 1924; dep. secty. to govt., industries dept., April, 1927; secty. to govt., industries and educn. dept., July, 1927, and again, Feb., 1935; offg. commr., July, 1931; and again, Feb., 1933; author of reports on "Jaunsar-Bawar Forest Settlement," "Forest Settlement, Etawah District," and "Kumaun Local Customs."

PANT, PANDIT CHINTAMANI, Central Public Works Dept. (exec. engr.) (b. 11th May, 1885).—Joined the service, 17th July, 1908; asst. engr., Feb., 1920; transf. to central p.w.d., Sept., 1922; exec. engr., Apl., 1930.

PANT, PITAMBAR, Rai Bahadur, P.M.S. Civil Surgeon, United Provs. (b. 1st Oct., 1883).—Civil surgeon, Mar., 1925.

PAPWORTH, HAROLD CHARLES, M.A., Indian Educl. Service (princl., presdy. coll., Madras) (b. 16th Dec., 1888).—Joined the service as prof. of English, presdy. coll., Madras, on 29th Jan., 1916; offg. princpl., govt. arts coll., June, 1926; offg. princpl., Victoria Coll., Palghat, Jan., 1928; ditto, Govt. Muhammad Coll., Madras, Jan., 1933; princpl. presdy. coll., Madras, Feb., 1937.

PAPWORTH, WALTER FRANCIS, B.A., late Indian Educl. Service (b. 12th May, 1886).—Joined the service, 2nd Apl., 1915; inspr. of schools, Aug., 1921; ret'd., July, 1934.

PARAMESWARAM PILLAI, NARAYANA PILLAI, Rao Bahadur, B.A., B.E., Indian Service of Engineers (suptg. engr., Madras) (b. 15th Nov., 1883).—Joined the service as apprentice engr., 15th Feb., 1907; asst. engr., Feb., 1908; exec. engr., Feb., 1918; suptg. engr., Apl., 1936.

PARANJPÉ, DHUNDIRAJ VISHWANATH, B.A., LL.B., Judl. Dept., Central Provs. (dist. and sess. judge) (b. 31st Dec., 1884).—Joined the service, 19th Feb., 1910; sub-judge, Nov., 1923; dist. and sess. judge, Oct., 1933.

PARANJPÉ, GOPAL RAMCHANDRA, M.Sc., Indian Educl. Service (b. 30th Jan., 1891).—Apptd. prof. of physics, Royal Inst. of Science, Bombay, 26th Oct., 1920; offg. princpl. ditto, June, 1933.

PARANJPÉ, RAGHUNATH PURUSHOTTAM, M.A. (Cantab.), B.Sc. (Bom.), D.Sc. (Cal.) (b. 16th Feb., 1876).—Educ. Maratha H.S., Bombay; Fergusson Coll., Poona; St. John's Coll., Cambridge (Fell); Indian Govt. scholar; princ. and prof. of math., Fergusson Coll., Poona, 1902-26; Fellow of the Univ. of Bombay, 1904-27; sometime vice-chancellor of the Indian Women's Univ., Poona; member, Bombay legis. council, 1913; awarded Kaisar-i-Hind medal, 1st class, 1916; minister, Bombay govt., 1921-23, 1927; member of council of India, April, 1927 to Apl., 1932; member, Indian taxation inquiry ctee., 1924-25; member, reforms inquiry ctee., 1924; delegate of the govt. of India to internatl. labour conferences, Geneva, 1927-1930; member, Int. conf. on treatment of foreigners, 1929; Int. conf. for formation of Int. Relief Union, 1927; Int. conf. on limitation of manufacture of narcotic drugs, 1931; author of lives of Gopal Krishna Gokhale and Dhondo Keshar Karve, and "The Crux of the Indian Problem."

PARASHURAM JOG, DATTATRAYA, Indian Service of Engineers (b. 9th Nov., 1906).—Joined the service as proby. asst. exec. engr., Madras, 1st Oct., 1931; confd., June, 1933.

PAREEKH, GOBIND NARAYAN. (see Gobind N.P.)

PARIJA, PRANKRISHNA, B.Sc., M.A., Indian Educl. Service (b. 1st Apl., 1891).—Apptd. prof. of botany, Ravenshaw Coll., Cuttack, 4th Aug., 1921.

PARIKH, HIMATLAL BAPALAL, M.I.E. (Ind.), L.C.E., Indian Service of Engrs (exec. engr., Sind) (b. 24th May, 1888).—Joined the provl. service as asst. engr., 29th March, 1911; exec. engr., March, 1921; spl. road engr., Bombay, Nov., 1935; ditto, Sind, Apl., 1936.

PARISH, FREDERICK FRANK, Indian Railway Accts. Service (accts. offcr.) (b. 5th May, 1892).—Joined the service as asst. auditor and acctnt., G.I.P. Rly., 1st May, 1919; services permanently placed at the disposal of finl. commr. rlys., Oct., 1929; confd. as accts. offcr., gr. I., May, 1935.

PARISH, JAMES EDWARD, Office of the High Commr. for India (asst. engr., mechanical transport, store dept.) (b. 21st Mar., 1887).—Apptd. to Office of H.C. for I., July, 1921; asst. inspr. of stores, Sept., 1922; asst. engr., mechanical transport, March, 1926.

PARK, Rev. WILLIAM ROBERT, C.I.E., O.B.E., M.A., late Indian Eccles. Estabt. (b. 12th Jan., 1880).—Apptd. chaplain on probn., Rangoon, on 26th Mar., 1912; junr. chaplain, Mar., 1915; C.I.E., Aug., 1917; O.B.E., June, 1919; senr. chaplain, Oct., 1925; offg. archdeacon of Rangoon, May, 1931; rettd., Sept., 1937.

PARKER, FREDERICK ROWLAND, Office of High Commr., for India (higher clerical officer, accts. dept.) (b. 29th June, 1884).—Apptd. to acctnt.-gen.'s dept., India Office, 12th Jan., 1920; transfed. to office of high commr. for India, 1st April, 1921; clerical officer, Feb., 1922; higher clerical officer, April, 1922.

PARKER, HARDING NEVILLE, B.A., Indian State Rlys. (dist. traffic supt., E.B. Rly.) (b. 20th July, 1890).—Joined the service as asst. traffic supt., 2nd Dec., 1912; in milty. employ from 12th May, 1919, to 14th Aug., 1920; dist. traffic supt., Dec., 1921; offg. asst. agent, E.B. Rly., Jan., 1928; dep. traffic supt., Oct., 1930; offg. dep. traffic manager, comml., E. B. rly., Mar., 1933; ditto, transpn., July, 1935; offg. dep. agent personnel, E.B. rly., July, 1936.

PARKER, HERBERT, M.A., Indian C.S. (dist. and sess. judge) (b. 17th Apl., 1889).—Educ. at Nottingham High School, and St. John's Coll., Cambridge; apptd. after exam. of 1911; arrived, 10th Dec., 1912, and served in Burma as asst. commr.; services placed at disposal of govt. of India, rev. and agric. dept.; asst. rice commr., Rangoon, Feb. to Aug., 1920; offg. dist. and sess. judge, Feb., 1924; confd., May, 1924; offg. judge, high ct., Rangoon, Apl., 1936, to Nov., 1937.

PARKER, RICHARD HENRY, Indian C.S. (dist. and sess. judge, Bengal) (b. 21st July, 1898).—Educ. at St. Paul's Sch., and St. John's Coll., Oxford; joined the service, 31st Oct., 1922; arrived, 15th Dec., 1922, and served in Bengal as asst. mag. and collr.; jt. mag. and dep. collr.; services placed at disposal of govt. of India, for, dept., Feb., 1926; under-secty. to A. to G.G. in Rajputana, and C.C., Ajmer-Merwara, March, 1926; ditto, to resdt., Hyderabad; reverted to Bengal govt., 5th Sept., 1930; offg. dist. and sess. judge, Nov., 1930; and again, May, 1932; confd., Mar., 1934; commr. for Workmen's Compensation, Feb., 1934.

PARKER, RICHARD NEVILLE, F.C.H., Indian Forest Service (ch. consvr., Punjab) (b. 4th Dec., 1884).—Joined the service, 10th Nov., 1905, as asst. consr.; dep. consr., Dec., 1910; offg. consr., Dec., 1912; confd., Nov., 1926; on depn. to forest research inst., Dehra Dun, Sept., 1924; offg. ch. consvr., May, 1933; confd., Aug., 1934.

PARKES, RODERICK WALLIS, B.A., Indian C.S., Indian Political Service (b. 2nd Apl., 1909).—Educ. at St. Paul's Sch., Magdalen Coll., Oxford, and the Sch. of Oriental Studies, London; apptd. after the exam. of 1931; arrived in India, Dec., 1932, and served in the Punjab as asst. commr.; addl. asst. pol. agent, S. Waziristan, Oct., 1935; asst. commr., Peshawar, Nov., 1935; secty., British legation, Kabul, Aug., 1936.

PARKIN, GEORGE DEVEREUX, Indian Police (supt., United Provs.) (b. 30th Aug., 1901).—Joined the service 29th Nov., 1921; offg. supt., March, 1925; supt. of police, Benares State, Apl., 1931; supt., Nov., 1932; on depn. as dep. inspr.-genl., Rampur State, Dec., 1934, to Nov., 1937.

PARKINSON, JOSEPH ERNEST, M.A., Indian Educl. Service (educ. commr. with the govt. of India) (b. 13th April, 1883).—Joined the service as vice-princl., C.T. Coll., Lahore, 10th March, 1913; inspr. of schools, Sept., 1922; offg. asst., dir. of pub. instrn., Apl., 1925; inspr. of training institutions, Oct., 1925; prin., C.T. Coll., Lahore, Oct., 1926; offg. dir., public instrn., and under-secty. to govt., Punjab, July, 1933; confd., Oct., 1935; educ. commr., with the govt. of India, Nov., 1936.

PARMANAND B.Sc., Indian C.S. (dep. commr., Central Provs.) (b. 7th Feb., 1901).—Educ. at Allahabad Univ. and Cambridge Univ. (non-coll.); joined the service, 30th Oct., 1924; arrived, 10th Feb., 1925, and served in the Central Provs. as asst. commr.; offg. dep. commr., variously from Jan., 1928; dep. commr., provl., Aug., 1937.

PARNELL, RALPH, late Indian Forest Service (b. 29th Jan., 1884).—Joined the service, 10th Nov., 1905, as asst. consvr.; dep. consvr., Dec., 1910; offg. consvr., Mar., 1922; confd., Nov., 1927; offg. ch. consvr., May, 1930; rettd., Aug., 1934.

PARPIA, YUSUF RAHIM, B.A., Indian C.S. (asst. coll., Bombay) (b. 25th Jan., 1905).—Educ. at University of Bombay and School of Oriental Studies, London; joined the service, 9th Oct., 1929; arrived, December, 1929, and served in Bombay as asst. coll.

PARR, ALFRED ERNEST, B.Sc., Ph.D., late Indian Agricultural Service (United Provs.) (b. 12th Nov., 1884).—Joined the service, 23rd Oct., 1907, as dep. dir. of agriculture; on mil. service, Sept., 1915, to Oct., 1919; offg. dir. of agric., April, 1926; rettd., Aug., 1931.

PARR, CLIFFORD HEATLEY, B.Sc., Indian Agric. Service (dep. dir. of agric., United Provs.) (b. 15th July, 1892).—Joined the service as dep. dir. of agric., 1st Jan., 1920.

PARRY, CLAUDE FREDERICK, Indian Police (dist. supt., Central Provs.) (b. 9th Mar. 1896).—Joined the service as asst. dist. s pt., Dec., 1919; dist. supt., Oct., 1925; princpl., pol. training sch., Saugor, Feb., 1933.

PARRY, NEVILL EDWARD, late Indian C.S. (Assam) (b. 3rd July, 1884).—Educ. at Rugby; apptd. after exam. of 1906; arrived, 10th Dec., 1907, and served in E. Bengal and Assam as asst. mag. and collr.; asst. commr., Assam, April, 1912; under-sect. to chief. commr., Assam, Dec., 1913; on mil. duty (I.A.R.O.), Aug., 1916, to April, 1919; dep. commr., June, 1923; supt., Lushai Hills, March, 1924; rettd., Aug., 1932.

PARSONS, Sir ALFRED ALAN LETHBRIDGE, K.C.I.E., B.A., late Indian C.S. (adviser to the sec. of state for India) (b. 22nd Oct., 1882).—Educ. at Bradfield Sch., and Univ. Coll., Oxford; apptd. after exam. of 1906; arrived, 18th Nov., 1907, and served in the Punjab as asst. commr.; under-sec., fin. dept., Punjab, July, 1912; under-sec. to govt. of India, fin. dept., June, 1916; addl. dep. finl. adviser, mil. fin., govt. of India, Dec., 1918; offg. acctnt.-gen., Bengal, Oct., 1919; confirmed, Jan., 1920; addl. finl. adviser, mil. fin. branch, Nov., 1920; offg. depy. controller of currency, Bombay, Jan., 1922; offg. fin. commr., rly. board, Jan., 1924; C.I.E., Jan., 1925; offg. sec. to govt. of India, dept. of industries and labour, March, 1925; offg. finl. commr., rly. board, April, 1926; confd., Jan., 1927; Kt., Jan., 1932; secty. to govt. of India, finl. dept., Apl., 1932; temp. member, govr.-genl.'s exec. council, June, 1932; member, Ind. staty. rly. bd. ctee., June, 1933; ret'd. from I.C.S., Nov., 1934; member of council of India, Nov., 1934; K.C.I.E., Jan., 1935; adviser to the sec. of state for India, Apl., 1937.

PARSONS, Arthur Edward BROADBENT, C.B.E., D.S.O., Lt.-Col., I.A., Indian Political Service (b. 5th Aug., 1884).—First commn., 4th Apl., 1906; pol. officer on probn., May, 1919; confd., Aug., 1921; O.B.E., Jan., 1920; pol. agent, Tochi, Dec., 1923; apptd. dep. sec., govt. of India, for. and pol. dept., 23rd Oct., 1924; C.B.E., June, 1927; dep. commr., Bannu, Oct., 1927; counsellor, H.M. Legation, Kabul, March, 1930; on spl. duty in Khyber agency, Oct., 1930; pol. agent, Khyber, Jan., 1931; dep. commr., Hazara, Aug., 1932; resdt., Waziristan, June, 1933; dep. agent to govr.-genl. in Baluchistan, June, 1935; ret'd., cl. II, Aug., 1935; offg. resdt. and A.G.G., Baluchistan, Apl., 1936; chairman, frontier ctee., Mar., 1936; ditto, frontier watch and ward ctee., Nov., 1936; offg. resdt. and A.G.G., Baluchistan, Dec., 1936; offg. secty. to govt. of India, ext. affairs dept., June, 1937; offg. resdt. and A.G.G., Baluchistan, Oct.—Nov., 1937.

PARSONS, CHARLES WULSTAN, late Indian State Rlys. (b. 17th Aug., 1878).—Joined the service as asst. to signal engr., 21st Oct., 1905; resdt. engr. Jan., 1909; offg. dep. ch. engr., signals, Feb., 1921; confd., Nov., 1922; ret'd., June, 1934.

PARSONS, CYRIL GORDON, M.C., Indian Police (supt., United Provs.) (b. 7th Aug., 1894).—Joined the service as asst. supt., 27th Dec., 1919; offg. supt., March, 1923; confd., April, 1927.

PARSONS, FRANK THOMAS, Indian Police (b. 9th Sept., 1916).—Apptd. asst. supt., Bihar and Orissa, on probn., Nov., 1936.

PARSONS, RUDOLPH ELLIOT REBSCH, Indian Police (asst. supt., Assam) (b. 17th Jan., 1910).—Joined the service as proby. asst. supt., Assam, 14th Oct., 1930; confd., and asst. to pol. officer, Saduja frontier tract, May, 1933.

PARTAB SINGH, BABU, B.A., LL.B., late United Provs. Civil Service.—Joined the service, 22nd Feb., 1899; sub-judge, Feb., 1915; dist. and sess. judge, July, 1921; ret'd., Feb., 1929.

PARTAB SINGH, B.A., Indian C.S. (dep. commr., Punjab) (b. 1st June, 1896).—Educ. at Khalsa Coll., Amritsar, and Punjab Univ.; arrived, 11th Jan., 1922, and served in the Punjab as asst. commr.; dep. commr. (provl.), August, 1928; ditto (perm.), Apl., 1932.

PARTAP SINGH, B.Sc., Indian Forest Service (b. 10th Jan., 1901).—Joined the service as asst. consvr., Punjab, on 9th Nov., 1923; transf. N.W.F.P., Mar., 1932.

PARTAP RAM (see Ram Partap).

PARTHASARATHI AYYANGAR, SRINIVASA AYYANGAR, B.A., Indian Police (b. 1st May, 1903).—Joined the service as asst. supt., Madras, 1st May, 1927; offg. supt., Apl., 1931.

PARTRIDGE, EDWARD HENRY WILLIAM, Office of the High Commr. for India (engr., mechanical transport, store dept.) (b. 22nd April, 1879).—Apptd. asst. engr., mechanical transport, Oct., 1924; engr., ditto, March, 1926.

PASCOE, Sir EDWIN HALL, Kt., M.A., D.Sc., F.G.S., late Geological Survey of India (b. 17th Feb., 1878).—Joined the service, 2nd March, 1905, as proby. asst. supt., geological survey of India; confirmed, May, 1907; apptd. to the I.A.R.O., May, 1915 to April, 1917; supt., geological survey, April, 1917; dir. geol. survey of India, June, 1921; Kt., Jan., 1928; ret'd., June, 1932.

PASRICHA, MUKUND LAL, C.I.E., late India Posts and Telegraphs (b. 25th July, 1879).—Trained at R.I.E. Coll.; joined the telegraph service, 1st Oct., 1901, as asst. supt.; supt., Nov., 1912; offg. postmr.-gen., central circle, May, 1923; offg. dep. dir.-gen., tel. traffic, 1st March, 1926; confd., July, 1928; C.I.E., Jan., 1931; offg. ch. engr., posts and tels., May, 1931; p.m.g., central circle, Nov., 1931; ditto, Bengal and Assam, Dec., 1932; chairman, postal enquiry ctee., Apl., 1934; ret'd., May, 1935.

PATCH, CHARLES JAMES LODGE, M.C., Lt.-Col., I.M.S. (medl. supt., Punjab mental hosp.) (b. 31st Oct., 1887).—First commn. dated 15th Dec., 1919; in milty. employ. to 5th Nov., 1922; supt., Punjab lunatic asylum, Nov., 1922; supt., Punjab mental hosp., July, 1924.

PATE, HENRY REGINALD, C.I.E., B.A., late Indian C.S. (b. 10th Aug., 1880).—Educ. at Clifton Coll., and King's Coll., Cambridge; apptd. after exam. of 1903; arrived, 30th Nov., 1904, and served in Madras as asst. collr. and mag.; spl. asst. settl. offr., Nov., 1906, to Jan., 1908; sub-collr. and jt. mag., July, 1914; sec., board of rev., June, 1916; dep. sec. to govt. of India, army dept., Aug., 1921; confd., Jan., 1922; collr. and dist. mag., Madras, Nov., 1925; secty. to govt., rev. dept., Madras, Nov., 1928; C.I.E., June, 1931; offg. 2nd secty. to govt. of Madras, Apl., 1929; ret'd., Mar., 1934.

PATEL, BHAGVATPRASAD RAOJIBHAI, M.A., Indian C.S. (asst. collr., Bombay) (b. 24th Aug., 1912).—Educ. at Proprietary High School, Ahmedabad, Bombay University, and School of Oriental Studies, London; joined the service, 21st Sept., 1936; arrived in India, Oct., 1936, and served in Bombay as asst. collr.

PATEL, BHAILAL KHUSHALDAS, B.A., Indian C.S. (asst. coll., Bombay) (b. 12th April, 1906).—Educ. at Univ. of Bombay, and Christ's Coll., Cambridge; joined the service, 11th Oct., 1929; arrived, Nov., 1929, and served in Bombay as asst. coll.

PATEL, BHAILAL SHANKARBHAI, B.Ag., N.D.A., N.D.D., C.D.A.D., Indian Agric. Service (dep. dir. of agric., Gujarat) (b. 29th Sept., 1887).—Joined the service as dep. dir. of agric., June, 1921; prof. of agric., April, 1924; dep. dir. of agric., Jan., 1929; offg. dir., Feb., 1935; livestock expert up to Dec., 1937.

PATEL, BHOGILAL PRABHUDAS, B.Sc., Indian C.S. (asst. collr., Bombay) (b. 16th Oct., 1912).—Educ. at Shree-Sayaji High Sch., Baroda, and Bombay Univ.; joined the service, 15th Sept., 1937; arrived in India, Oct., 1937, and served in Bombay as asst. collr.

PATEL, HIRALAL MULJIBHAI, Indian C.S. (Indian trade commr., Hamburg) (b. 27th Aug., 1904).—Educ. at Oxford (non-collegiate); joined the service, 14th Oct., 1927; arrived, Nov., 1927, and served in Bombay as asst. collr. and magte.; offg. collr., Jan. and Dec., 1932; attd. Central bd. of rev., June, 1934; offg. dep. secty. to govt., Bombay, fin. dept., Mar., 1936; Indian trade commr., Hamburg, July, 1937.

PATEL, RANCHHODBHAI BHAIBABHAI, M.A., LL.B., Barr., Judl. Dept., Bombay (prothonotary and senior master, high ct.) (b. 18th Dec., 1880).—Joined the dept., 3rd Feb., 1905; asst. prothonotary, Sept., 1921; offg. prothonotary, June, 1926; confd., Oct., 1928.

PATERSON, HUGH JAMES, B.Sc., Indian Service of Engrs. (asst. exec. engr., Central p.w.d.) (b. 18th Jan., 1905).—Joined the service as proby. asst. exec. engr., 18th Dec., 1928; confd., June, 1930; transferred to central p.w.d., Jan., 1934; offg. exec. engr., Jan., 1935.

PATERSON, NOEL KENNEDY, M.A., Indian C.S. (under-secty., fin. dept., Govt. of India) (b. 25th Dec., 1905).—Educ. at George Heriot's School, Edinburgh, University of Edinburgh, and St. John's Coll., Cambridge; joined the service, 8th Oct., 1929; arrived, 25th Nov., 1929, and served in the Central Provinces as asst. commr.; under secty. to govt., Central Provs., Mar., 1934; under-secty., fin. dept., govt. of India, May, 1937.

PATERSON, ROBERT HAND, Indian State Rlys. (dist. contr. of stores, N.W. Rly.) (b. 17th June, 1890).—Joined the service, 21st June, 1912; asst. contr. of stores, N.W. rly., Apl., 1917; dist. contr. of stores, Mar., 1931; offg. dep. contr. of stores, Aug., 1936.

PATHAK, CHAUBE SALIG RAM (see Salig Ram P.C.).

PATHAK, VINAYANAND (see Vinayanand, P.).

PATIL, PANDURANG CHIMNAJI, Rai Bahadur, L.Ag., M.Sc., late Indian Agric. Service (b. 19th June, 1877).—Held non-gazetted appts. from 1st Feb., 1906, to 31st March, 1909; divl. inspr. of agric., April, 1909; dep. dir. of agric., March, 1918; offg. profir. of agricl. economics, July, 1927; offg. princpl. agricl. coll., Poona, Mar., 1932; retd., Oct., 1932.

PATIL, RAMRAO KRISHNA RAO, B.Sc., LL.B., Indian C.S. (asst. commr. Central Provs.) (b. 13th Dec., 1907).—Educ. at Benares University, Nagpur University and Cambridge (Non-Collegiate); joined the service, 14th Oct., 1931; arrived in India, Dec., 1931, and served in the Central Provs. as asst. commr.

PATKAR, SHANTARAM KRISHNA, B.A., LL.B., late Bombay Civil Service, Judicial Branch (b. 26th Nov., 1878).—Held non-gazetted appts. from 21st Feb., 1907, to 17th Dec., 1914; sub-judge, Dec., 1914; offg. asst. judge, Sept., 1927; confd., May, 1930; offg. judge and sess. judge, June, 1930; retd., Nov., 1933.

PATKAR, SITARAM SUNDERRAO, B.A., LL.B., late Judicial Dept., Bombay (b. 16th May, 1873).—Apptd. addl. judge, high ct., Bombay, July, 1926; judge, ditto Nov., 1926; offg. ch. justice, June, 1931; retd., May, 1933.

PATON, WILLIAM CALDER, M.C., M.B., F.R.C.S., Lt.-Col., I.M.S. (chief medl. offcr., Delhi) (b. 27th Jan., 1886).—First commn. dated 27th July, 1912; resdt. medl. officer, gen. hosp., Madras, Jan., 1924; offg. civil surg., Coonoor, July, 1924; offg. D.M.O., Sept., 1927; confd., June, 1929; profir., medl. coll., Madras, May, 1932; chief medl. offcr. and civil surg., Delhi, Jan., 1934.

PATRICK, Rev. ALEXANDER, M.A., Indian Eccles. Estabt. (United Provs.) (senr. chaplain) (b. 11th Nov., 1887).—Apptd. chaplain on probation, 24th Aug., 1922; junr. chaplain, Oct., 1922; senr. chaplain, Oct., 1932.

PATRICK, PAUL JOSEPH, C.S.I., M.A., India Office (asst. secty., pol. (internal) dept.) (b. 6th Oct., 1888).—Educ. at Rugby and Corpus Christi Coll., Oxford; apptd. to sec.'s office, G.P.O., Jan. to Sept., 1913; apptd. junr. clerk, India Office, 1st Oct., 1913, and attd. to mil. dept.; apptd. to the I.A.R.O., March, 1916; temp. asst. sec., army dept., govt. of India, Sept., 1917, to May, 1918; served with 4/11th Gurkha Rifles in Palestine, May, 1918, to Jan., 1919; resumed duty at India Office, Feb., 1919; private sec. to parly. under-sec. of state, July, 1920, to Sept., 1920; addl. pte. sec. to govr. of Bihar and Orissa, Dec., 1920, to April, 1921; senior clerk, March, 1921; principal, April, 1921; establishment officer, Feb., 1922; delegate of govt. of India to Permt. Cttee. of Intl. Bureau of Public Health, Paris, 1922; administrative adviser to Indian delegation, International Sanitary Conference, Paris, 1926; sec. to Indian delegns. to 5th, 6th and 7th Assemblies of League of Nations, 1924, 1925 and 1926; actg. secty., pol. (internal) dept., Apl., 1930; jt. secty. to British delegation, Indian round table conf., London, 2nd session, Sept.-Dec., 1931, 3rd session, Nov., Dec., 1932; jt. secty., Indian States Enquiry comtee., Jan.-June, 1932; asst. secty., pol. (internal) dept., Dec., 1933; C.S.I., June, 1934.

PATRO, ANNEPU VASUDEVA, B.A., Indian Police (asst. supt.) (b. 3rd Aug., 1907).—Joined the service as proby. asst. supt., Madras, 11th June, 1930; confd., June, 1932.

PATTENSON, ARTHUR ERIC TYLDEN, Indian State Rlys. (member, rly. bd.) (b. 15th Nov., 1888).—Joined the service, 20th Nov., 1908; asst. traffic supt., May, 1910; dist. ditto, June, 1916; asst. trans. supt., Nov., 1922; asst. traffic manager, Dec., 1922; dep. ditto, Oct., 1924; offg. ch. traffic manager, Dec., 1923, Oct., 1924, and again, Nov., 1926; offg. ch. publicity officer, March, 1927; confd., Jan., 1929; offg. ch. transpn. supt., Apr., 1930; agent, July, 1932; offg. member, rly. bd., Nov., 1934; confd., Feb., 1935.

PATTERSON, BRIAN WALTER, Indian State Rlys. (dist. traffic supt., E.B. Rly.) (b. 24th Jan., 1899).—Asst. traffic supt., Apr., 1918; dist. ditto (prov.), Aug., 1936.

PATTERSON, DAVID CLARKE, B.A., Indian C.S. (judge, high ct., Calcutta) (b. 5th May, 1879).—Educ. at Oundle Sch. and Christ's Coll., Cambridge; apptd. after exam. of 1902; arrived, 10th Dec., 1903, and served in Bengal as asst. mag. and collr.; jt. mag. and dep. collr., April, 1911; s.p.t. mag. and collr., Sept., 1915; dist. and sess. judge, Feb., 1916; on mil. service, Sept., 1918, to Feb., 1919; offg. supt. and membr. of legal affairs, Bengal, March, 1926; offg. judge, high ct., Calcutta, June, 1929; Apr., 1930; and again Mar.-Sept., 1931; confd., Nov., 1931.

PATTERSON, SIR STEWART BLAKELY AGNEW, K.C.V.O., C.S.I., C.I.E., Lt.-Col., I.A. (ret'd.), late India Office (b. 18th Mar., 1872).—First commn. dated 13th Aug., 1892; in mil. employ to June, 1898; asst. pol. agent for Dir and Swat, Nov., 1898; commdt. B. mil. police, Sept., 1899; asst. to resdt. in Mewar, May, 1900; asst. to a.g.g. in Rajputana, Oct., 1902; asst. resdt. in Mewar, April, 1903; asst. to resdy. in Kashmir, Feb., 1904; asst. commr., Dera Ismail Khan, Nov., 1908; pol. agent, Wana, Dec., 1908; asst. commr., Ajmer, Oct., 1909; asst. sec. to govt. of India, for. dept., Nov., 1910; finl. member, Marwar state council, July, 1913; attd. to adjt.gen.'s branch, hd. qrs. of army, April, 1917; commr., Ajmer-Merwara, Nov., 1918; C.I.E., Jan., 1922; res. lt. at Jaipur, Feb., 1922; offg. sec., pol. dept., govt. of India, Dec., 1924; member of council of state, Jan.-Aug., 1925; agent to govr.-gen. in Rajputana, Aug., 1925; C.S.I., Jan., 1927; ret'd., Mar., 1927; apptd. pol. A.D.C. to secty. of state, Sept., 1928; K.C.V.O., June, 1933; ret'd., Sept., 1933.

PATTIPATE HANIMIREDDIGARI RAMA REDDI (see Rama Reddi, P. H.).

PATTLE, ERNEST GODFREY, B.A., Indian C.S. (commr., Burma) (b. 9th Aug., 1883).—Educ. at Merchant Taylors' Sch., London; open scholar, St. John's Coll., Oxford; apptd. after exam. of 1907; arrived, 23rd Nov., 1908, and served in Burma as asst. commr. and asst. scottl. offr.; regr., chf. ct., Lower Burma, Jan., 1917; warden of the Yenang-Yaung, Minbu, and Singu oilfields, Dec., 1917, to March, 1919; contr. of rents, city of Rangoon, April, 1920; dep. commr., Oct., 1923; services placed at disposal of central board of revenue as commr. of income tax, April, 1925; offg. sec. to govt., educn.,

local govt. and public health dept., Burma, Dec., 1925; registr., co-op. societies, Nov., 1926; senr. warden, oilfields, June, 1927; offg. commr., Sept., 1933; commr. (prov.), Jan., 1936.

PATTON, ARNOLD GORDON, B.A., Indian C.S. (secty. fin. and rev. dept., Assam) (b. 2nd Dec., 1892).—Educ. at City of London Sch. and St. John's Coll., Cambridge; apptd. after exam. of 1915; arrived in India, 6th Dec., 1916, and served in Assam as asst. commr.; on mil. duty (I.A.R.O.), Jan., 1918, to March, 1919; offg. dep. commr., Apr., 1925; and again, Feb., 1932; confd., Feb., 1934; secty., fin. and rev. dept., Apr., 1937.

PATUCK (see Patak).

PATWARDHAN, NARHAR MAHADEO, B.A., LL.B., Barr., late Bombay Civil Service—Appt. commr. for workmen's compensation, Bombay, June, 1924; offg. judge and sess. judge, April, 1928; confd., Aug., 1932; ret'd., Dec., 1933.

PATWARDHAN, RAMKRISHNA PURUSHOTAM, B.A., Indian Educl. Service (prof., Elph. coll., Bombay) (b. 8th Feb., 1892).—Joined the service, 5th Feb., 1921; prof. o Indian history and economics, Deccan Coll., Poona, Feb., 1921; prof'r., Elph. Coll., Bombay, July, 1926, and again, Oct., 1937; prof'r. of history, Deccan coll., Poona, June, 1932.

PAUL, CHAVUR JOHN, Rao Bahadur, Madras C.S. (sub-collr.) (b. 6th July, 1887).—Supt., rev. sectt., Jan., 1919; asst. secty., dev. dept., July, 1921; dep. collr., Aug., 1931; sub-collr., Apr., 1936; secty. to govt., dev. dept., Madras, Apr., 1936.

PAUL, MARCUS JAYARAJAN ROLAND, B.A., Indian C.S. (asst. mag. and collr., Madras) (b. 17th Oct., 1910).—Educ. at Royal College, Colombo and Emmanuel College, Cambridge; joined the service, 6th Oct., 1933; arrived in India, Nov., 1933, and served in Madras as asst. mag. and collr.

PAVRY, FAREDUN CURSETJI, C.I.E., F.C.H., late Indian State Rlys. (b. 23rd June, 1877).—Trained at the R.I.E. Coll.; apptd. asst. engr., 1st Oct., 1900; exec. engr., Oct., 1908; offg. suptg. engr., March, 1921; suptg. engr. (prov.), April, 1924; offg. ch. engr., June, 1925; suptg. engr., Sept., 1925; chief engr., Jan., 1928; C.I.E., June, 1930; ret'd., June, 1932.

PAW, MAUNG SAN, Indian State Rlys. (dist. traffic supt.) (b. 6th Sept., 1895).—Joined the service as asst. traffic supt., 6th Mar., 1920; dist. ditto, Burma Rlys., Dec. 1932.

PAWSEY, CHARLES RIDLEY, M.C., B.A. (Oxon), Indian Civil Service (dep. commr., Assam) (b. 14th July, 1894).—Educ. at Berkhamsted Sch., and Wadham Coll., Oxford; joined the service, 24th Oct., 1919; served in Assam as asst. commr. from 4th Dec., 1919; offg. dir. of land records, inspr.-genl. of registrn., etc., Assam, Sept., 1932; and again, Mai., 1934; dep. commr. (prov. subs.), Apr., 1935; confd., Oct., 1935.

PAYMASTER, BARJOR BEHRAMJI, B.Sc., Indian C.S. (asst. collr., Bombay) (b. 10th Jan., 1912).—Educated at Parsi High School, Panchgani, University of Bombay, University College, London, and The School of Oriental Studies, London; joined the service, 29th Oct., 1935; arrived in India, Nov., 1935, and served in Bombay as asst. collr.

PAYMASTER, BEHRAMJI BURJORJI, L.R.C.P. & S. (Edin.), L.F.P.S. (Glas.), Lt.-Col. I.M.S. (ret'd.).—Joined 26th July, 1902, and served in mil. dept. prior to 8th March, 1909; civil surg., April, 1911; on milty. duty from 1st Nov., 1914, to 13th Dec., 1921; ret'd., Jan., 1929.

PAYNE, Miss HELENA ELIZABETH, B.A. (Bom.), late Indian Educl. Service (Bombay) (b. 9th June, 1876).—Joined the service, 3rd April, 1922; inspectress of girls' schools, C.D. and Bom. Div., April, 1923; lady supt., training coll. for women, Ahmedabad, May, 1926; offg. inspectress of girls' schools July, 1926; ret'd., June, 1931.

PAYTON, WILFRED HUGH, B.A., Indian C.S. (secty., home dept., Burma) (b. 7th April, 1892).—Educ. at King Edward's Schl., Birmingham, and Trinity Coll., Cambridge; app't. after exam. of 1914; arrived in India, Dec., 1915, and served in Burma as asst. commr.; on mil. service from Aug., 1916, to Sept., 1919; asst. supt., S. Shan States, April, 1921; under sec. to govt., Feb., 1922; offg. dep. commr., Sept., 1925; confid., June, 1930; offg. secty. to govt., educn. dept., April, 1928; dep. secty. to govt., fin. dept.; addl. secty. to govt., fin. dept., Oct., 1930; secty., ditto, Mar., 1932; lab. commr., Burma, Apl., 1935; secty., home dept., Apl., 1937.

PE, MAUNG WIN, B.A., Indian C.S. (asst. commr., Burma) (b. 5th May, 1912).—Educ. at New College, Oxford; joined the service, 21st Sept., 1936; arrived in India, Oct., 1936, and served in Burma as asst. commr.

PE, U. ZAW, C.I.E., B.A., F.R.C.I., late Indian Educl. Service. (b. 11th Nov., 1879).—Apptd. to the I.E.S., as inspr. of schools, Burma, 23rd Nov., 1922; C.I.E., June, 1931; ret'd., Nov., 1934.

PEACE, GORDON, Dept. of Explosives (ch. inspr.) (b. 4th Aug., 1890).—Apptd. ch. inspr. of explosives in India, 10th Oct., 1934.

PEACOCK, DAVID HENRY, M.A., D.Sc., F.I.C., F.C.S., Indian Educl. Service (prof. of chemistry, Univ. of Rangoon) (b. 9th Dec., 1889).—Joined the service as lecturer in chemistry, univ. coll., Rangoon, 24th Oct., 1922; prof. of chemistry, Rangoon univ., Nov., 1935.

PEARCE, CHARLES FREDERICK BRYDE, B.A., Indian C.S. (secty. to govt., fin. dept., Burma) (b. 13th June, 1892).—Educ. at Marlborough Coll. and Balliol Coll., Oxford; joined the service, 30th Aug., 1920; arrived, 19th Feb., 1921, and served in Burma as asst. commr.; offg. under sec. to govt., June, 1923; offg. dep. sec., ditto, Dec., 1924; asst. settl. officer, March, 1925; settl. offr., July, 1927; dep. commr., Oct., 1930; dep. secty. to govt., fin. dept., Burma, June, 1934; secty. ditto, Nov., 1934; and again, Feb., 1936; apptd. representative of the Govt. of Burma, applien. ctee. for the Indo-Burma finl. award, Jan., 1936.

PEARCE, CYRIL, A.M.I.C.E., A.M.I.M.E., Indian State Rlys. (transpn. supt.) (b. 26th Sept., 1892).—Joined the service, 23rd Nov., 1923, as asst. dist. loco. supt., E.I. rly.; asst. supt., Dec., 1929; transpn. supt. (power), Aug., 1934.

PEARCE, FREDERICK SPENCER, late Indian State Rlys. (E.I. Rly.) (b. 26th June, 1879).—Joined the service as asst. supt., 6th Jan., 1899; dist. supt., Aug., 1908; supt., Feb., 1925; ret'd., April, 1930.

PEARCE, GEORGE ALFRED, Indian Police (supt., United Provs.) (b. 28th Oct., 1894).—Joined the service as asst. supt., 18th Oct., 1920; offg. supt., May, 1923; addl. supt., Dec., 1926; supt., Aug., 1928.

PEARCE, NATHANIEL, late Indian State Railways (b. 20th June, 1879).—Trained at the R.I.E. Coll.; apptd. asst. engr., rlys., 1st Oct., 1901; exec. engr., Oct., 1909; offg. agent, E.B. Rly., Feb., 1926; confid., Sept., 1926; ret'd., June, 1934.

PEARCE, WILLIAM ROBERT, late Income Tax Dept., Punjab (b. 18th Oct., 1879).—Joined the service, 7th Feb., 1898; extra asst. commr., Apl., 1916; asst. commr. of income tax, Punjab, Dec., 1923; awarded Kaisar-i-Hind medal, 2nd class, 2nd June, 1923; offd. variously as commr. of income tax since Oct., 1927; commr. of income tax, N.W.F.P., Oct., 1932; ret'd., Oct., 1934.

PEARMAN, JAMES EDGAR, O.B.E., Indian Police (supt., Orissa) (b. 18th May, 1896).—Joined the service as asst. supt., 10th Oct., 1921; offg. supt., Jan., 1924; confid., June, 1926; awarded King's police medal; O.B.E., June, 1935; asst. to I.G. and supt., C.I.D., May, 1937.

PEARSE, ARTHUR ERNEST, V.D., late Indian State Railways (E.I. Rly.) (b. 25th May, 1876).—Joined the service, 3rd Oct., 1898, as asst. loco. supt.; dist. loco. supt., April, 1906; dep. loco. and carr. supt., April, 1913; carr. and wagon supt., N.W.Rly., Oct., 1922; offg. chief mechanical engr., E.I. Rly., March, 1925; ret'd., May, 1931.

PEARSON, Rev. Canon CYRIL GREENWOOD, M.A., late Indian Eccles. Estabt. (b. 3rd Nov., 1881).—Joined the service, 13th Nov., 1922; junr. chaplain, Nov., 1925; senr. chaplain, Dec., 1932; ret'd., Nov., 1936.

PEARSON, Sir HERBERT GRAYHURST, Kt., Barr., India Office (legal adviser and solicitor to the sec. of state) (b. 5th Aug., 1878).—Apptd. acting addl. judge, Calcutta high ct. Nov., 1920; judge, ditto, Feb., 1922; Kt. Jan., 1931; legal adviser and solicitor to the sec. of state, June, 1933.

PEARSON, Sir RALPH SNEYD, Kt., C.I.E., F.L.S., late Indian Forest Service.—Joined the Service 4th Nov., 1898, as asst. consvr. of forests; dep. consvr., July, 1905; imperial forest economist, forest research inst. and coll., Dehra Dun, from May, 1909; C.I.E., June, 1920; consvr., May, 1922; ret'd., Aug., 1927; Knighted, June, 1933.

PEART, CHARLES LUBÉ, C.I.E., Col., I.A. (ret'd.) (b. 2nd April, 1876).—First commn. dated 22nd Jan., 1896; in mil. emp. to April, 1912, when he was apptd. sec., board of examrs., Calcutta; temply. reverted to mil. duty; C.I.E. March, 1919; ret'd., Aug., 1930.

PECK, LEONARD JASPER, M.A., M.C., late Indian Finance Dept. (b. 2nd May, 1884).—Joined the service, 28th Mar., 1908; asst. acctnt.-genl., Aug., 1910; on military duty from 5th Mar., 1915 to 30th Mar., 1921; dep. acctnt.-genl., June, 1921; dep. comptr., Assam, Oct., 1922; dep. acct.-gen., central rev., Nov., 1925; offg. dep., auditor-gen., March, 1927; confd., Mar., 1929; offg. acct.-genl., central revenues, April, 1930; ditto, Punjab, April, 1931; comptr., N.W.F.P., Nov., 1931; offg. acct.-genl., U.P., Nov., 1932; dir. of army audit, Apl., 1933; ret'd., July, 1937.

PECK, NORMAN FREDERICK, C.B.E., B.A., Indian C.S. (commr., Bihar) (b. 12th May, 1886).—Educ. at Latymer Upper Sch., Hammersmith, and Jesus Coll., Cambridge; apptd. after exam. of 1908; arrived, 2nd Dec., 1909, and served in E. Bengal and Assam as asst. mag. and collr.; transfed. to Bihar and Orissa, April, 1912; jt. mag. and dep. collr., March, 1916; offg. supt., Mayurbhanj State, April, 1917; confirmed, Dec., 1918; mag. and collr. and asst. pol. agent, Orissa Feudatory States, Nov., 1922; commr. excise and salt, Mar., 1932; C.B.E., June, 1932; offg. member, bd. of rev., Apl., 1936; commr. (prov. subs.), Apl., 1936.

PEDDIE, CROFTON, late Indian Service of Engrs. (Madras) (b. 2nd Nov., 1875).—Trained at R.I.E. Coll.; joined the service, 1st Oct., 1898, as asst. engr.; exec. engr., March, 1908; offg. suptg. engr., April, 1921; confd., April, 1926; ret'd., Nov., 1930.

PEDDIE, KENNETH, M.I.E. (Ind.), Indian State Rlys. (dep. ch. engr., E.I. Rly.) (b. 17th Feb., 1884).—Joined the service as junr. engr., 20th July, 1907; asst. engr., Jan., 1919; resdt. engr., Sept., 1919; exec. engr., July, 1925; offg. supt., way and works, Nov., 1925; offg. dep. ch. engr., variously from Sept., 1930; confd., Aug., 1935.

PEDLEY, JOHN EDWARD, M.C., B.A., Indian C.S. (mag. and collr., United Provs.) (b. 4th Dec., 1891).—Educ. at Repton and Trinity Coll., Oxford; joined the service, 2nd Aug., 1920; arrived, 3rd Dec., 1920, and served in United Provs. as asst. mag. and collr.; offg. regr., high ct., Allahabad, March, 1924; offg. dep. commr., Nov., 1928; offg. mag. and collr., Mar., 1930; confd., Jan., 1932.

PEEBLES, ALEXANDER SPALDING MACKIE, M.D., Lt.-Col., I.M.S. (ret'd.), late Medical Dept., Bihar and Orissa (b. 20th Nov., 1876).—First commn. 1st Sept., 1904; in mil. employ till May, 1911; supt., lunatic asylum, Berhampur, July, 1911; civil surgeon, Jan., 1923; ret'd., Nov., 1931.

PEEK, THOMAS ARTHUR, Office of High Commr. for India (higher clerical officer, general dept.) (b. 14th Aug., 1884).—Apptd. to India Office, 27th June, 1919; transfed. to office of High Commr. for India, April, 1922; higher clerical officer, Jan., 1924.

PEEL, EARDLEY GARFORTH BRYAN, C.I.E., Indian Police (dep. insp genl., United Provs.) (b. 12th Mar., 1888).—Joined the service as asst. supt., 20th Dec., 1907; supt., Sept., 1920; C.I.E., June, 1923; asst. to D.I.G., C.I.D., Feb., 1926; inspr.-genl., Kashmir durbar, Jan., 1933; asst. to D.I.G., C.I.D., Mar., 1936; asst. inspr.-genl., rly. police, Apl., 1936; dep. inspr.-genl., Sept., 1937.

PEEL, ROLAND TENNYSON M.C., B.A., India Office (asst. secty. public and judl. dept.) (b. 9th Oct., 1892).—Educ. at Bedford School, and Hertford Coll., Oxford; apptd. junr. clerk, correspondence dept., 28th July, 1919; war service—served in France with 6th Bn. Seaforth Highls. (T.F.), May, 1915, to Sept., 1917 (M.C., Jan., 1918); asst. principal, April, 1921; resident clerk, April, 1921, to March, 1922; asst. pte. sec. of sec. of state, March, 1922; pte. sec. to parly. under-sec. of state, Oct., 1923; principal, Aug., 1924; actg. asst. secty., public and judl. dept., Nov., 1930, to Apl., 1931; and again, Sept., 1931; confd., Oct., 1937.

PEERS, ELWIN VICTOR AUSTIN, Indian Police (dist. supt., Central Provs.) (b. 13th Nov., 1898).—Joined the service as asst. dist. supt., 17th March, 1921; offg. dist. supt., Oct., 1924; confd., June, 1928.

PEGGE, CYRIL CUTHBERT COLBECK, late Indian Police (b. 26th Dec., 1881).—Held non-gazetted appts. from 1st Nov., 1901, to 25th Jan., 1916; dep. supt., Jan., 1916; apptd. to I.P.S. as asst. supt., Oct., 1920; offg. supt., June, 1923; confd., Sept., 1930; ret'd., Sept., 1933.

PEJAVAR MOHAN RAO (see Mohan Rao Pejavar).

PELLY, CORNELIUS JAMES, Indian C.S. (asst. commr., Punjab) (b. 8th April, 1908).—Educ. at Clongowes Wood College, Co. Kildare, and Trinity College, Dublin; apptd. after examination of 1930; arrived in India, Nov., 1931, and served in the Punjab as asst. commr.

PELLY, HUTCHISON RAYMOND, Lt.-Col., I.A. (dep. commr., Burma) (b. 22nd Sept., 1887).—First commn. Aug. 1906; apptd. to I.A., Nov., 1909; seconded as asst. commr., Burma, Jan., 1914; reverted to mil. employment, Dec., 1914, to Jan., 1920; asst. commr., Namtu, Feb., 1920; offg. dep. commr., March, 1923; transfed. to supply list, Jan., 1924; offg. dep. commr., July, 1926; confd., Apl., 1930.

PEMBERTON, FREDERICK, B.A., Indian Service of Engrs. (exec. engr., Burma) (b. 7th Sept., 1890).—Apptd. asst. exec. engr., 18th Dec., 1929; offg. exec. engr., Mar., 1931; confd., June, 1931.

PENDHARKAR, GANPAT MAHADEO, B.A., B.L., late Central Provs., C.S. (b. Feb., 1879).—Joined the service as munsi, 30th July, 1904; sub-judge, June, 1922; offg. dist. and sess. judge, Oct., 1932; confd., Apl., 1933; ret'd., Feb., 1934.

PENMAN, DAVID, C.I.E., D.Sc., Dept. of Mines in India (chief. inspr.) (b. 9th Apl., 1883).—Apptd. principal, Indian School of Mines, Dhanbad, 18th March, 1921; chief inspr., Oct., 1932; member, mining board, Bengal, Nov., 1932; C.I.E., Feb., 1937.

PENNELL, KENNETH EUSTACE LEE, B.A., M.C., Indian Service of Engrs. (suplg. engr., Assam) (b. 26th July, 1890).—Joined the service as asst. engr., Oct., 1912; in mil. employ from 6th Dec., 1915, to 16th July, 1920; asst. exec. engr., March, 1922; exec. engr., Dec., 1925; offg. under-secty., P.W.D., Assam, Jan., 1928; asst. ch. engr. and under secty. to govt. (p.w.d.), Assam, Nov., 1934; offg. suptg. engr., Mar., 1936; confd., Nov., 1936.

PENNELLS, SIDNEY GILBERT, India Office (exec. officer, acct.-genl.'s dept.) (b. 4th May, 1896).—Apptd. clerical officer, 20th Oct., 1921; executive officer, May, 1927.

PENNY, JAMES DOWNING, C.I.E., B.A., Indian C.S. (chief secty. to govt. of Punjab) (b. 25th May, 1886).—Educ. at Marlborough Sch., and Magdalen Coll., Oxford; apptd. after exam. of 1909; arrived in India, 26th Nov., 1910, and served in the Punjab as asst. commr., asst. registrar, co-operative credit societies, Punjab, Mar., 1914; pol. asst. and commdt., B.M. Police, July, 1916; under-sec. to govt., rev. dept., Punjab, Oct., 1917; on mil. duty, June, 1918, to March, 1919; supt., Simla Hill States, April to May, 1919; dep. commr. (prov.), Nov., 1923; confd., Oct., 1933; offg. dep. sec., govt. of India, commerce dept., June, 1926; ditto, fin. dept., Dec., 1926; confd., March, 1927; sec., fin. dept., Punjab, Nov., 1927; offg. commr., variously from June, 1932; on special duty, Punjab civil secty., Aug., 1933; chief secty. to govt., and C.I.E., May, 1937.

PENNEY, THOMAS MAXWELL MONCRIEFF, Major, R.E., Survey of India (supt.) (b. 28th Mar., 1893).—First commn. dated 19th July, 1912; asst. supt. (proby.), 13th Oct., 1920; confd., Oct., 1922; supt., Dec., 1928.

PERCIVAL, PHILIP EDWARD, C.I.E., B.A., Barr., late Indian C.S. (Bombay) (b. 11th Nov., 1872).—Educ. at the Charterhouse, and Balliol Coll., Oxford; apptd. after exam. of 1894; arrived, 25th Nov., 1895, and served in Bombay as asst. collr. and mag., and joint addl. sess. judge; under-sec. to govt., pol., judl. and legisl. depts., Oct., 1903, to Aug., 1905; regr., high court, appellate side, April, 1909; judge and sess. judge, Sept., 1910; membr. of legal affairs and sec. to govt., legal dept., July, 1911, to Aug., 1915; judge and sess. judge and agent for Sardars in Deccan, Nov., 1915; C.I.E., June, 1924; addl. judl. commr. of Sind, Sept., 1924; actg. judge, Bombay high ct., 1925; member of legisl. council, 1925–26; remembrancer of legal affairs and sec. to govt., legal dept., Bombay; actg. judge, Bombay high ct., June, 1926; offg. judl. commr. of Sind, Oct., 1926; confd., Dec., 1926; chairman, Bombay riots enquiry comee., June, 1929; ret'd., Nov., 1930.

PERKIN, EMIL ATHOL OWEN, C.I.E., Indian Police (inspr.-genl., Orissa) (b. 25th Sept., 1889).—Joined the service as asst. supt., 26th Nov., 1909; supt., July, 1920; offg. dep. inspr.-genl., Mar., 1934; deputed as inspr.-genl., Orissa, Apl., 1936; inspr.-genl., July, 1936; C.I.E., May, 1937.

PERROTT, ARTHUR FINCH, Indian Police (supt., Punjab) (b. 13th Nov., 1892).—Joined the service, 15th Nov., 1912; asst. supt., May, 1914; awarded King's police medal, Jan., 1919; supt., Mar., 1924; awarded bar to King's police medal, Jan., 1925; dist. offr., frontier constabulary, Dec., 1925; offg. commdt., frontier constab., April, 1926; offg. dep.-inspr.-genl., Feb., 1934.
