PERRY, ARTHUR STANLEY HARRIS, Indian Service of Engrs. (supt. engr., Punjab) (b. 29th June, 1884).—Trained at R.I.E. Coll.; joined the service, 1st Oct., 1906; asst. engr., Nov., 1906; exec. engr., Oct., 1917; offg. suptg. engr., April, 1928; confd., Aug., 1932.

PERRY, EDWARD WILLIAM, C.I.E., B.A., Indian C.S. (collr. and magte., Bombay) (b. 12th June, 1891).—Educ. at St. Paul's Sch. and Caius Coll., Cambridge; apptd. after exam. of 1914; arrived, 9th Dec., 1915, and served in Bombay as asst. collr. and magte.; on mil. duty, Nov., 1917, to Feb., 1919; dep. sec. to govt., genl., etc., dept. (temp.), Oct., 1922; addl. dep. sec. to govt., fin. dept. (temp.), June, 1923; offg. collr., Bombay, sub. dist., and Salsette dev. officer (in addn.), May, 1924; offg. collr. and supt. of stamps, April, 1926; member legisl. council, Feb., 1927; pte. sec. to govr., April, 1927; asst. sec., Indian Statutory Commission, Nov., 1927; C.I.E., June, 1930; secty., Re-organisation Ctte.; collr., Jan., 1933; offg. dir. of information and commr. of labour, Bombay, May, 1933; offg. jt. secty. to govt. of India, dept. of indus. and lab., Apl., 1934.

PERRY, WILLIAM ERIC, M.C., Lieut.-Col., R.E., late Survey of India (b. 2nd Jan., 1884).—First commn. dated 16th Mar., 1905; proby. asst. supt., 19th April, 1910; confd., April, 1912; on milty. duty from 1st Sept., 1915, to 5th Aug., 1918; dep. supt., Sept., 1924; supt., Jan., 1926; asst. surveyor-genl., April, 1927; dep. master, security printing, India (on probation), Dec., 1928; confd., Oct., 1930; ret'd., Oct., 1935.

PERSHAD, Ladli (see Ladli, P.).

PESTONJI SORABJI PATAK (see Patak, P. S.).

PETCH, DONALD BURMAN, M.C., B.A., Indian C.S. (secty. to govt. of Burma, com. and ind. dept.) (b. 16th Jan., 1897).—Educ. at Merchant Taylors' School, and Caius Coll., Cambridge; joined the service, 30th Oct., 1920; arrived, 19th Feb., 1921, and served in Burma as asst. commr.; offg. under-sec. to govt., Oct., 1923; offg. dep. commr. and settlt. officer, July, 1924; under-sec. to govt., educn., local govt. and public health dept., Nov., 1925; offg. dep. commr., March, 1927; ditto, (prov. subs.), Dec., 1934; secty. to finl. commr., Burma, Nov., 1934; addl. secty., rev. dept., Burma, July, 1936; secty., com. and indus. dept., May, 1937.

PETERS, CHARLES ROBERT, Indian Police (supt., United Provs.) (b. 26th Sept., 1886).—Joined the service, 23rd Oct., 1907; asst. supt., Nov., 1907; supt., Apl., 1921; asst. I.G. rly. police, Feb., 1937.

PETERS, FELIX THEODORE, late India Posts and Tels. (b. 12th Aug., 1882).—Joined the service as proby. supt., 9th Mar., 1905; confd., Oct., 1906; offg. dep. P.M.G., Bihar and Orissa, Jan., 1929; dep. P.M.G., Madras, Nov., 1929; offg. dep. dir.-genl., postal services, Mar., 1936; ret'd., Aug., 1937.

PETERSON, ERIC CUTHBERT JAMES, Indian Finance Dept. (dep. compr., Assam) (b. 2nd Oct., 1886).—Joined the service as asst. accts. officer, 26th July, 1905; asst. examr., June, 1926; examr., local fund accts., Bihar and Orissa, July, 1927; dep. acct. genl., ditto, Apl., 1931; dep. compr., Assam, June, 1934.

PETERSON, IVAN EMILLE KERR, Northern India Salt Rev. Dept. (suptg. mech. and elec. engr.) (b. 13th Sept., 1885).—Joined the service, 28th Nov., 1923; elec. and mech. engr., Khewra Mines (temp.), Nov., 1923; suptg. ditto, Oct., 1928.

PETIGARA, KAVASJI JAMSHEDJI, KHAN BAHADUR, C.I.E., O.B.E., I.S.O., late Indian Police (b. 24th Nov., 1878).—Held non-gazetted appts. from 2nd Nov., 1903, to 31st Mar., 1919; supt., Bombay city police, C.I.D., Apl., 1919; Kaisar-i-Hind medal, 1st class, Jan., 1923; I.S.O., July, 1926; promoted to I.P.S. as supt. and dep. commr. of police, Bombay, Oct., 1928; O.B.E., Jan., 1931; C.I.E., June, 1933; retd., Apl., 1937.

PETRIE, Sir DAVID, Kt., C.I.E., C.V.O., C.B.E., M.A., late Indian Police (b. 9th Sept., 1879).—Educ. at Aberdeen Univ.; apptd. asst. dist. supt. of police, Dec., 1900; adjutant, Samana Rifles, Oct., 1907; supt. of police and ditto, Aug., 1908; asst. to dep. inspr.-gen., C.I. Dept. and Rly., April, 1909; asst. dir., criminal intelligence, India, March, 1911; special duty, Delhi, Dec., 1912; dep. inspr.-gen. of police, May, 1915; C.I.E., June, 1915; spl. duty under dir., central intelligence, from March, 1916; O.B.E., Jan., 1918; C.B.E., Jan., 1919; M.V.O., Feb., 1921; dep. inspr.-gen. intelligence bureau, March, 1921; C.V.O., June, 1922; member of royal commn. on the superior civil services in India, 1923-24; dir., intelligence bureau, Oct., 1924; Kt., June, 1929; member, public services commn., Oct., 1931; chairman, ditto, Aug., 1932.

PETRIE, HARRY, Office of the High Commr. for India (exec. officer, store dept.) (b. 4th Apl., 1898).—Apptd. clerical officer, store dept., India Office, March, 1920; transfed. to office of High Commr. for I., Oct., 1920; exec. officer, Jan., 1926.

PETTY, JOHN, M.C., Indian Forest Service (dep. consvr., Bombay) (b. 24th Sept., 1895).—Joined the service, 11th Dec., 1921, as asst. consvr.; dep. consvr., Dec., 1923.

PHARASKHANEWALA, BALKRISHNA SHAMRAO, B.A., LL.B., Judl. dept., Central Provs. (dist. and sess. judge) (b. 10th July, 1884).—Joined the service, 2nd Nov., 1909; sub-judge, Nov., 1923; dist. and sess. judge, June, 1935.

PHELIPS, DENIS CHARLES PRESCOT, B.A., Indian C.S. (asst. commr., Burma) (b. 14th Dec., 1899).—Educ. at Gresham's Sch., Holt, Norfolk, and New Coll., Oxford; joined the service, 24th Dec., 1923, and served in Burma as asst. commr.; asst. settlt. officer, Dec., 1924; warden, oilfields, May, 1926, and again, Mar., 1932; offg. dep. commr., variously from June, 1929.

PHEROZE MERWAN KHAREGAT, C.I.E., B.A. Indian C.S. (secty. to govt., industries and educn., United Provs.) (b. 29th May, 1890).—Educ. at Bombay and Clare Coll., Cambridge; apptd. after exam. of 1913; arrived in India, 9th Dec., 1914, and served in the United Provs. as asst. mag. and collr.; jt. mag., June, 1922; offg. under-sec. to govt. of India, June to Nov., 1922; offg. mag. and collr., April, 1923; confd., Apl., 1931; offg. registrar, co-op. societies, Dec., 1926; secty. to govt.; U.P. dept. of industries and educn., Mar., 1932; and again, Sept., 1935; C.I.E., June, 1935.

PHIBBS, ALFRED, late Office of High Commr. for India (b. 5th Dec., 1873).—Educ. at King Edward VI.'s Gr. Sch., Stourbridge; apptd. clerk, 2nd class, mil.

dep., India Office, 16th April, 1894; joint editor, India Office List, from Oct., 1907, to Nov., 1914; minor staff clerk, Dec., 1911; apptd. member, standing cttee. of enquiry into working of Indian transport service, Jan., 1912; staff clerk, Nov., 1914; junior clerk, store dept., April, 1917; transfed. to office of high commr. for India, Oct., 1920; principal exec. officer, April, 1922; retd., June, 1932.

PHILBY, RALPH MONTAGUE, Capt., R.I.N., (naautical adviser to the govt. of India) (b. 1st Apl., 1884).—Apptd. principal officer, Mercantile Marine Dept., and offg. nautical adviser to the govt. of India, May, 1935.

PHILIP, CHARLES LYALL, C.I.E., M.A., late Indian C.S. (b. 10th Dec., 1881).—Educ. at Watson's Coll., Edinburgh, and Edinburgh Univ.; apptd. after exam. of 1903; arrived, 12th Dec., 1904, and served in Bengal as asst. mag. and collr., and asst. settlement offr.; jt. mag. and dep. collr., Sept., 1911; transfed. to Bihar and Orissa, April, 1921; supt., Mayurbhanj State, Oct., 1912, to May, 1916; offg. pol. agent, Feudatory States of Orissa, April, 1917; mag. and collr., and ditto, May, 1917; on mil. service (I.A.R.O.), Aug., 1918, to Jan., 1919; addl. pol. agent, Orissa Feudatory States, Jan., 1919; commr., May, 1930; C.I.E., Jan., 1933; offg. member, bd. of rev., May, 1934; confd., Dec., 1935; retd., Oct., 1937.

PHILIP, GEORGE MORRISON, D.S.O., M.C., B.Sc., late Indian Service of Engrs. (b. 29th Mar., 1892).—Joined the service, 10th Oct., 1919; asst. exec. engr., Feb., 1920; exec. engr., Oct., 1924; retd., Dec., 1937.

PHILIP, MODEIL MANI (jt. mag. and dep. collr., Bihar) (b. 13th Feb., 1904).—Educ. at Univ. of Madras and Selwyn Coll., Cambridge; joined the service, 27th Sept., 1928; arrived, 20th Oct., 1928, and served in Bihar and Orissa as asst. mag. and collr.; jt. mag., Nov., 1928; offg. mag. and collr., June, 1935; offg. addl. dist. and sess. judge, Apl., 1936.

PHILLIP, CHARLES HENRY, late Indian Forest Service (b. 12th Oct., 1881).—Arrived in India, 14th Nov., 1904; dep. consvr., Dec., 1909; offg. consvr., May, 1922; confd., Apl., 1925; retd., Oct., 1936.

PHILLIMORE, REGINALD HENRY, Col., D.S.O., R.E., retd., late Survey of India (b. 19th June, 1879).—First commn. dated 23rd June, 1898; joined survey dept., June, 1903, as asst. supt.; dep. supt., Aug., 1911; temp. reverted to mil. duty, Oct., 1914, to April, 1919; supt., Jan., 1926; offg. dir. March, 1926; dir., geodetic branch, May, 1928; dir., frontier circle, Dec., 1928; dir., map publ., Apl., 1930; retd., June, 1934.

PHILLIPPE, WILLIAM AUGUSTINE, Income Tax Dept., Bengal (asst. commr.) (b. 17th Nov., 1886).—Joined the service, 11th July, 1904; asst. commr., Aug., 1934.

PHILLIPS, ALAN ANDREW, B.A., Indian State Rlys. (divl. comml. officer, N.W. Rly.) (b. 18th Aug., 1889).—Joined the service as asst. traffic supt., 18th Nov., 1912; dist. traffic supt., 2nd July, 1924; divl. comml. officer, N.W. Rly., Feb., 1927; offg. comml. instructor, rly. staff coll., Dehra Dun, Nov., 1929.

PHILLIPS, ARTHUR GORDON, C.I.E., Indian Police (dep. inspr. genl., United Provs.) (b. 20th Oct., 1885).—Joined the service, 25th Nov., 1906, as asst. supt.; supt., Aug., 1918; offg. dep. inspr.-genl., Sept., 1930; confd., Sept., 1933; inspr.-genl., Ajmer-Merwara, Nov., 1937; C.I.E., Jan., 1938.

PHILLIPS, E. H. (addl. dep. dir. of public information, home dept., Govt. of India).—Appd. addl. dep. dir. of public inform., Jan., 1936.

PHILLIPS, HENRY FRANCIS TRAVERS, late Indian Police Service (Madras) (b. 15th Feb., 1876).—Joined the service, 15th Feb., 1899, and posted as asst. supt., Madras; supt., March, 1908; off. dep. inspr.-gen., Oct., 1920; confd., Sept., 1921; commr. of police, town of Madras, Oct., 1925; ret'd., Aug., 1930.

PHILLIPS, JAMES ROLAND, B.Sc., Indian Service of Engrs. (exec. engr. Bombay) (b. 1st Nov., 1901).—Joined the service as asst. exec. engr., Bombay, 1st Oct., 1924; offg. exec. engr., March, 1927; confd., Sept., 1933; dep. secty. to govt., Bombay, P.W.D. (irrig. and rlys.), Nov., 1933.

PHILLIPS, JOHN, India Office (exec. officer, acct. genl's dept.) (b. 8th Feb., 1895).—Apptd. clerical officer, 18th July, 1921; exec. officer, May, 1927.

PHILLIPS, JOHN ALFRED STEELE, C.I.E., M.B., C.H.B., D.P.H., Col., I.M.S. (inspr.-genl. of civil hosp., United Provs.) (b. 6th Oct., 1882).—First commn. dated 2nd Feb., 1907; in milty. employ to 15th May, 1921; spl. malaria officer, United Provs., May, 1921; asst. dir. of public health U.P., Oct., 1924; offg. dir. of public health, Bihar and Orissa, Apl., 1926; confd., Apl., 1930; offg. inspr.-genl. of civil hosp., B. and O., variously from Oct., 1933; C.I.E., June, 1934; inspr.-genl. of civil hosp., United Provs., Mar., 1937.

PHILLIPS, KEITH DAVID, Indian Police (b. 5th Aug., 1917).—Apptd. asst. supt., Bengal, on probn., Dec., 1937.

PHILLIPS, PERCY PHILIP, Ph.D., F.I.C., late Indian Educl. Service (b. 22nd Mar., 1877).—Joined the service, 7th Oct., 1904, as prof. of chemistry, Thomason Coll., Roorkee; off. variously as prinpl., ditto, from Oct., 1923; principal, Thomason Coll., Roorkee, Aug., 1931; ret'd., Sept., 1932; author of various works on chemical subjects.

PHILLIPS, WILLIAM HENRY, Factory Dept., Bombay (inspr.) (b. 22nd May, 1892).—Apptd. inspr. of factories, Bombay, 15th Oct., 1923, after non-gazetted service from 12th May, 1922; offg. ch. inspr., May, 1930.

PHILPOT, HAMLET CUNNINGHAM VACHELL, B.A., late Indian C.S. (b. 21st Oct., 1887).—Educ. at Repton, and Wadham Coll., Oxford; apptd. after exam. of 1911; arrived, 30th Nov., 1912; and served in Bengal as asst. mag. and collr.; jt. mag. and collr., April, 1918; I.A.R.O., April to June, 1918, and Sept., 1918, to Jan., 1919; offg. sec., board of rev., June, 1924; offg. mag. and collr., Jan., 1926; confd. Nov., 1926; offg. secty. to govt., rev. and irrigation depts., Dec., 1928; confd., Feb., 1931; ret'd., Apl., 1936.

PHIPPS, ALBERT HENRY, Indian Police (dist. supt., Burma) (b. 22nd Feb., 1899).—Joined the service as asst. dist. supt., Burma, 14th Jan., 1922; offg. dist. supt., March, 1928; confd., July, 1932.

PHIPSON, EDWARD SELBY, C.I.E., D.S.O., M.D., B.S., D.P.H., D.T.M. & H., M.R.C.S., L.R.C.P., Lt.-Col., I.M.S. (health officer, Aden) (b. 10th Mar., 1884).—First commn., dated 1st Aug., 1908; in mil. employ pr or to March, 1914; reverted to mil. duty, Oct., 1914, to April, 1917; on deputn., 1st Jan., to 25th Feb., 1922, in connection with H.R.H. Prince of Wales' visit; health offr., Simla, Oct., 1915; offg. health offr., Port of Aden, July, 1923; confd., Nov., 1925; C.I.E., Jan., 1935.

PHUKAN, RADHA NATH, Rai Bahadur, M.A., late Assam Civil Service (b. Dec., 1876).—Joined the service, 1st April, 1903, as proby. extra asst. commr.; confd., April, 1905; addl. dist. and sess. judge, Feb., 1927; dep. commr., March, 1927; dir. of agric. and ind., Feb., 1928; addl. dist. and sess. judge, Oct., 1930; ret'd., Dec., 1931.

PIARE LAL RASTOGI, BABU, B.A., LL.B., late Judicial Dept., United Provs. (b. 22nd Feb., 1877).—Joined the service as offg. munsif, 24th April, 1906; confd., May, 1909; sub-judge (s.p.l.), Dec., 1919; confd., Jan., 1921; temp. addl. dist. and sess. judge, March, 1927; ret'd., Feb., 1932.

PICKETT, WILLIAM DAVID, late Indian State Rlys. (b. 15th Oct., 1880).—Joined the service, 13th Dec., 1902; asst. secy. to agent, N.W. Rly., Sept., 1925; ret'd., Oct., 1935.

PIGGOTT, SIR THEODORE CARO, Kt., B.A., late Indian C.S.—Educ. at Kingswood Sch., Bath, and Christ Church, Oxford; apptd. after exam. of 1886; arrived, 30th Nov., 1888, and served in the N.W. Prov. and Oudh as asst. commr., and asst. mag. and collr.; joint mag., Nov., 1893; dep. commr., July, 1900; dist. and sess. judge, Aug., 1900; addl. sess. judge, March, 1909; offg. 2nd addl. judl. commr., Oudh, April, 1909; 1st addl. judl. commr., April, 1911; judl. commr., Nov., 1911; judge, Allahabad high court, May, 1914; member, criminal procedure code committee, Sept. to Nov., 1916; Kt., June, 1924; ret'd., Oct., 1925.

PILDITCH, DENYS, Indian Police (supt., United Provs.) (b. 6th Oct., 1891).—Joined the service as asst. supt., Nov., 1912; supt., July, 1924; awarded King's police medal, Jan., 1933, addl. dep. dir., intell. bureau, Nov., 1933.

PILGRIM, HENRY GUY ELLOCK, D.Sc., F.G.S., late Geological Survey of India (b. 24th Dec., 1875).—Apptd. asst. supt., geological survey of India, 20th Oct., 1902; palæontologist, ditto, June, 1912; to mil. duty, June, 1915; on depn. to Persian Gulf and Persia, Oct., 1917, to March, 1918; supt., June, 1920; palæontologist, Nov., 1920; ret'd., Dec., 1930.

PILLAI, ARON APPADURAI, DIWAN BAHADUR, I.S.O., late Madras Civil Service (b. 13th June, 1876).—Joined the service, 15th April, 1898; dep. collr., Oct., 1914; asst. secty., bd. of rev., land rev., Feb., 1923; offg. collr. and dist. magte., Sept., 1927; sub-collr., Nov., 1928; offg. dir. of industries, July, 1929; I.S.O., June, 1931; ret'd., June, 1931.

PILLAI, A. SAVARINATHA (asst. commr., inc. tax, Madras) (b. 7th May, 1882).—Apptd. to income tax dept., central board of revenue, 1st March, 1923; asst. commr., northern range, Madras, 2nd March, 1925; ditto, western range, Coimbatore, July, 1931.

PILLAI, GOBIND KRISHNA, Public Works Dept., United Provs. (b. 10th Oct., 1887).—Joined the service as asst. sany. engr., 25th Dec., 1920; exec. engr., July, 1922; on dept. to Allahabad municipal bd., July, 1925.

PILLAI, NARAYANA P. P. (see Parameswaram Pillai, N.P.).

PILLAI, NARAYANA RAGHAVAN, C.B.E., B.A. (Mad.) M.A., LL.B., Indian C.S. (collr. of customs, Karachi) (b. 24th July, 1898).—Educ. at Madras Univ. and Trinity Hall, Cambridge; joined the service, 20th Nov., 1922; arrived, 16th Jan., 1923, and served in Central Provs. as asst. commr.; offg. dep. commr., March, 1927; asst. collr. customs, Madras, Dec., 1927; dep. dir. of comml. intell., Calcutta, May, 1929; dep. sec. to govt. of India, commerce dept., Mar., 1932; temp. jt. secty., ditto, June, 1934; offg. ditto, Feb., 1936; collr., Karachi, Apl., 1936; C.B.E., Feb., 1937; offer on specl. duty, govt. of India, commerce dept., Apl., 1937; dep. commr., confd. July, 1937.

PILLAI, NATARAJA GANAPATI (see Ganapati Pillai, N.).

PILLAI, T. G. P. S. (see Sanjivi Pillai, T. G. P.).

PILLAI, VELLAYAMBALAM KRISHNA BALA-KRISHNA (see Balakrishna Pillai, V. K.).

PILLAY, THILLAINAYAKAM SIVARAMA SETU, M.A., B.L., Indian Audit Dept. (b. 14th Apl., 1899).—Joined the service, 7th Feb., 1923; attd. offcr., office of auditor-genl., Feb., 1936; asst. auditor-genl. (personnel), Mar., 1936.

PIM, SIR ALAN WILLIAM, K.C.I.E., C.S.I., M.A., Indian C.S. (United Provs.) (b. 17th May, 1871).—Educ. at Leipzig Univ., and Trinity Coll., Dublin; apptd. after exam. of 1894; arrived, 4th Dec., 1895, and served in the N.W.P. and Oudh as asst. mag. and collr.; pte. sec. to lieut.-govr., May, 1898; asst. settle. offr., Nov., 1898; joint mag., Dec., 1903; settl. offr., Dec., 1903, to Dec., 1906; dep. commr., May, 1910; joint sec., board of rev., Oct., 1910; mag. and collr., and ditto, Nov., 1911; sec. to govt., April, 1912, to Aug., 1917; C.I.E., June, 1918; commr., May, 1921; member, board of revenue, April, 1925; C.S.I., Jan., 1926; temp. member, exec. council, United Prov., Dec., 1928; K.C.I.E., June, 1929; retd., July, 1930; author of a monograph on woolen fabrics.

PIMPLE, PRABHAKAR MAHIPAT, Indian Police (dist. supt.) (b. 15th May, 1896).—Joined the service as dep. supt. of Police, Cent. Provs., 1st Sept., 1919; confd., July, 1921; dist. supt., Apl., 1937.

PINHEY, LOUIS ALEXANDER GORDON, Capt. I.A., Indian Political Service (b. 28th Oct., 1901).—First commn., 14th July, 1921; in milty. employ to Nov., 1925; under-secty. to A.G.G., and ch. commr. in Baluchistan, Apl., 1929; asst. pol. agent, Quetta-Pishin, Aug., 1929;

ditto, Sibi, Dec., 1929; ditto, Quetta-Pishin, July, 1930; vice-consul, Zahidan, Nov., 1931; asst. commr., Ajmer-Merwara, Feb., 1933; secty. to A.G.G. in Rajputana, Oct., 1934; asst. commr., Ajmer-Merwara, Nov., 1934; addl. pol. agent, Quetta-Pishin, Apl., 1936; offg. pol. agent, ditto, in addn., Nov., 1936; secty. to A.G.G., resdt. and C.C., Baluchistan, Sept., 1937.

PINNELL, LEONARD GEORGE, C.I.E., Indian C.S. (secty. to govr., Bengal) (b. 10th June, 1896).—Educ. at City of London Sch., and Balliol Coll., Oxford; apptd. after exam. of 1920; arrived, 4th Dec., 1920, and served in Bengal as asst. mag. and collr.; manager, Dacca Nawab Estate, Nov., 1925; addl. depy. secty., fin., commerce and marine depts., Feb., 1930; offg. mag. and collr., Nov., 1930; confd., July, 1933; offg. lab. commr., Bengal, Apl., 1934; pte. secty. to govr. of Bengal, Aug., 1935; secty. to the Governor, Apl., 1937; C.I.E., Jan., 1938.

PINTO, LIGUORI SIMON, late Indus. Dept., Madras (b. 19th Nov., 1890).—Joined the service as asst. dir. of indus., Madras, 3rd Aug., 1920; indus. engr., May, 1922; retd., Sept., 1937.

PIPE, THOMAS SYLVANUS, B.Sc., A.M.I.C.E., late Indian Service of Engrs. (b. 20th May, 1883).—Joined the service as asst. engr., 1st Oct., 1906; exec. engr., Oct., 1914; offg. supt. engr., May, 1920; retd., Aug., 1937.

PIR, AHSAN-UD-DIN, B.A., Indian C.S. (asst. commr., Punjab) (b. 7th Sept., 1909).—Educ. at Government High School, Ludhiana, Ismalia High School, Amritsar, Punjab University and Queen's College, Cambridge; joined the service, 4th October, 1933; arrived in India, Nov., 1933, and served in the Punjab as asst. commr.; name changed, Nov., 1934, to AHSAN-UD-DIN (q.v.).

PITAMBAR PANT (see Pant Pitambar).

PITAMBERDAS LAVJI THACKER (see Thacker, P. L.).

PITAM CHAND AGRAWAL, RAI BAHADUR, B.Sc., Indian Service of Engrs. (exec. engr., United Provs.) (b. Jan., 1896).—Joined the service, 1st Oct., 1919; asst. exec. engr., Oct., 1920; exec. engr., Oct., 1929; Rai Bahadur, Jan., 1931.

PITKEATHLY, SIR JAMES STOTT, Kt., C.M.G., C.I.E., C.V.O., C.B.E., D.S.O., Indian Stores Dept. (chief contr. of stores) (b. 10th Nov., 1882).—Joined the service, 1st Oct., 1909, as electrical inspr.; electrical engr., April, 1911; C.V.O., Dec., 1911; on mil. service, Aug., 1916, to Sept., 1911; asst. dir. of works, electrical and mechanical sections, Mesopotamia, from Aug., 1916; C.I.E., Jan., 1920; chief contr. of stores, Indian Stores Dept., 1st Jan., 1922; on foreign service under Ceylon govt., June, 1928; C.M.G., Jan., 1930; Knighted, June, 1933; on spl. duty under Govt. of India, dept. of educn., health and lands, Nov., 1936, to Jan., 1937.

PITT CHARLES HARRY, M.C., M.Sc., Northern late India Salt Revenue Dept. (b. 11th Mar., 1893).—Joined the dept., 20th Feb., 1921; offg. gen. manager, salt mines, Punjab, Sept., 1925; retd., Nov., 1936.

PITT, WILLIAM HENRY, late Indian Police Service (Madras) (b. 29th Aprl., 1885).—Joined the service, 19th Dec., 1904, as asst. supt.; supt., Sept., 1914; on foreign service as commr. of police, Travancore, 26th March, 1921; retd., Aug., 1930.

PLATT, ARTHUR JAMES, Indian C.S. (sub-collr., Madras) (b. 13th Aug., 1908).—Educated at Liverpool Collegiate School and Trinity Hall, Cambridge; apptd. after exam. of 1931; arrived in India, Nov., 1932, and served in Madras as asst. mag. and collr.; sub-collr., Mar., 1934.

PLATT, SYDNEY ERNEST, O.B.E., B.Sc., Public Health Dept. (engrg. bch.) Bihar (suplg. engr.).—Apptd. suptg. engr., public health dept., 3rd Feb., 1925; O.B.E., Jan., 1938.

PLATTS, MATHEW GEORGE, O.B.E., M.C., B.Sc., M.I.C.E., Indian Service of Engrs. (exec. engr., Madras) (b. 17th Dec., 1886).—Joined the service as asst. engr., 1st Oct., 1910; exec. engr., Oct., 1918.

PLEW, SIDNEY FERDINAND, Indian Police (b. 8th May, 1917).—Apptd. asst. supt., United Provs., on probn., Nov., 1936.

PLOWDEN, CHARLES TERENCE CHICHELE, C.I.E., Lt.-Col., I.A., Indian Political Service (b. 6th Feb., 1883).—In milty. employ from 27th Aug., 1902; apptd. probnr., pol. dept. and supy. asst. commr., N.W. Frontier Prov., 25th Jan., 1908; pol. asst., May, 1909; asst. commr., D.I. Khan, Oct., 1909; asst. to agent to govr.-gen. in Central India, June, 1910; on spl. duty, Bundelkhand, April, 1911, to Feb., 1912; on spl. duty under agent to govr.-gen. in Central India, Feb. to Nov., 1912; asst. pol. agent, E. Rajputana States, May, 1914; temply. reverted to mily. duty, Oct., 1914; 1st asst. to resdt. in Mysore and sec. to chief commr., Coorg, April, 1919; vice-presdt., Council of Regency, Cooch Behar State, Nov., 1923; secty. to agent to govr.-genl. and ch. commr., Baluchistan, March, 1928; pol. agent in Kalat, Nov., 1920; C.I.E., Jan., 1933; resdt., Mysore and ch. commr., Coorg, May, 1933.

PLOWDEN, PHILIP PETER MEREDYTH CHICHELE, B.A. (dist. and sess. judge, United Provs.), Indian C.S. (b. 28th Oct., 1888).—Educ. at Eltham Coll., and Balliol Coll., Oxford; apptd. after exam. of 1911; arrived, 13th Dec., 1913, and served in the United Provs. as asst. mag. and collr.; under-sec. to govt. of India, rev. and agric. dept., Aug., 1919, to Nov., 1919; joint mag., April, 1921; addl. dist. and sess. judge, March, 1922; sess. and sub-judge, Feb., 1927; dist. and sess. judge, Oct., 1927; confd., June, 1929; offg. judl. secty. to govt., United Provs., May, 1933, to Oct., 1934.

PLOWMAN, ALBERT CHARLES, India Office (higher clerical officer, tel. and mails bch.) (b. 16th June, 1883).—Educ. at Bedford Modern Sch.; apptd. clerk, 2nd class, registry and record dept., 12th June, 1901; transfid. to finl. dept., Jan., 1917; minor staff clerk, Jan., 1919; higher clerical officer, April, 1922.

PLUMB, WALTER CYRIL, Indian Police (supt., N.W.F.P.) (b. 14th Dec., 1895).—Joined the service, 26th Dec., 1915; asst. supt., June, 1919; asst. dist. officer, Oct., 1921; awarded King's police medal, Jan., 1924; supt., Oct., 1924; dist. officer, frontier constabulary, Sept., 1925, to Mar., 1937.

PLUMMER, JOHN COATES, late Indian Police Service (b. 26th Oct., 1907).—Joined the service as asst. dist. supt., Burma, 6th Dec., 1927; retd., July, 1932.

PLUMPTRE, CYRIL MACDONALD, M.R.C.S. (Eng.) L.R.C.P. (Lond.), Lt.-Col., I.M.S. (prof., medl. coll., Madras) (b. 27th Dec., 1884).—First commn. dated 29th July, 1911; in milty. employ to 19th March, 1923; medl. offr., Kathiawar pol. agency, April, 1923; offg. civil surg., Nov., 1924; confd., April, 1926; offg. presy. surgeon, Bombay, Aprl., 1932; profir., medl. coll., Madras, Mar., 1934.

PLUNKETT, PERCY EDWARD, late Indian Forest Service. —Joined the service, 1st Nov., 1898; dep. consvr., March, 1920; retd., Mar., 1929.

PLYMEN, FRANCIS JOSEPH, C.I.E., A.C.G.I., late Indian Agricl. Service (b. 7th Feb., 1879).—Apptd. agricl. chemist, Central Provs., 5th Aprl., 1906; principal, agricl. coll., Nagpur, in addn., from April to Sept., 1914; served under govt. of India, army dept., from June, 1917; dep. dir. of agric., Central Provs., July, 1918; offg. dir. of agric., June, 1923; confd., Sept., 1924; C.I.E., June, 1929; retd., Feb., 1934.

POLLARD, ARMELL RICHARD, B.A. (Cantab.), late Indian Service of Engrs. (b. 20th Oct., 1880).—Joined the service 1st Oct., 1905; as asst. engr.; exec. engr., Oct., 1913; offg. suptg. engr., Dec., 1920; confd., Nov., 1924; retd., Mar., 1932.

POLLARD, RICHARD CHARLES, Indian Police (asst. supt.) (b. 3rd Sept., 1908).—Apptd. proby. asst. supt., Bengal, 26th Novr., 1929; confd., Jan., 1933.

POLLOCK, AUBRAY KEATINGE HALLIDAY, M.R.C.S., L.R.C.P., D.P.H., V.D., late Indian State Rlys. (b. 8th Feb., 1878).—Joined the service as asst. medl. officer, 16th March, 1906; dist. medl. officer, April, 1908; ch. medl. officer, Aug., 1924; hony. surg. to Viceroy, Jan., 1927; retd., Dec., 1932.

POLLOCK, JOHN DOUGLAS ALEXANDER, Indian Police (b. 28th June, 1913).—Joined the service as proby. asst. supt. of police, United Provs., Nov., 1933; confd., Nov., 1935.

POLLOCK, RONALD EVELYN, B.A., Indian C.S. (judge, high ct., Nagpur) (b. 17th April, 1891).—Educ. at Harrow and Pembroke Coll., Cambridge; apptd. after exam. of 1914; arrived, 11th Dec., 1915, and served in the Central Provs. as asst. commr.; ditto, and under-sec. to chief commr., Jan., 1920; sub-judge, Nov., 1921; offg. dist. and sess. judge, April, 1924; confd., Dec., 1928; secty., legal dept., Central Provs., March, 1930; offg. addl. judl. commr., June, 1932, and again, May, 1933; confd., Jan., 1935; judge, high ct., Nagpur, Jan., 1936.

PONT, LEON VICTOR, Indian State Rlys (dist. signal engr., N.W. Rly.) (b. 7th Dec., 1883).—Joined the service as asst. signal engr., 23rd March, 1909; I.A.R.O., 20th Feb., 1916; dist. signal engr., March, 1924; offg. dep. ch. engr. (signals), N.W. rly., Mar., 1936.

POOLEY, CHARLES BLOIS, C.I.E., late Indian Service of Engrs. (Bombay) (b. 28th Jan., 1881).—Trained at the R.I.E. Coll.; apptd. asst. engr., Bombay, 3rd Oct., 1903; exec. engr., Nov., 1911; offg. suptg. engr., March, 1922; confd., Dec., 1926; C.I.E., Oct., 1928; retd., Dec., 1931.

POPE, ALFRED HENRY GEORGE, M.B.E., India Office (higher clerical officer, pol. dept.) (b. 24th Mar., 1904).—Apptd. clerical officr., 14th Oct., 1921; actg. higher ditto, Nov., 1932; confd., Dec., 1934; M.B.E., May, 1937.

POPE, JAMES ALISTER, C.I.E., M.A., late Indian C.S. (b. 23rd June, 1883).—Educ. at Eton and Corpus Christi Coll., Oxford; apptd. after exam. of 1906; arrived, 22nd Nov., 1907, and served in Bombay as asst. collr. and mag.; compiler, genl. admn. report, 1910-11; under sec. to govt., rev. and finl. depts., Bombay, Aug., 1915; on mil. duty, 1916-1918; excise commr. for Central India, June, 1923; collr. and mag., June, 1927; C.I.E., June, 1931; retd., Mar., 1934.

POREOUS, JOHN BRIAN, India Office (exec. offr., acct. genl.'s dept.) (b. 29th Aug., 1898).—Apptd. asst. clerk, 13th Oct., 1919; clerical officer, Jan., 1920; exec. offr., Dec., 1926.

PORTER, ALFRED ERNEST, Indian C.S. (jt. secty., agric. and indus. dept., Bengal) (b. 2nd Nov., 1896).—Educ. at Manchester Grammar Sch. and Corpus Christi Coll., Oxford; joined the service, 28th Oct., 1922; arrived, 14th Dec., 1922, and served in Bengal as asst. mag. and collr.; offg. dep. dir. of comml. intell. dept., govt. of India, April, 1925; offg. under-sec., govt. of Bengal, pol. and appt. dept.; confid., March, 1926; offg. dist. and sess. judge, Nov., 1927; supt., census operations, Bengal, Apr., 1930; offg. mag. and collr., Sept., 1933; confd., June, 1933; jt. secty., agric. and indus. dept., Bengal, July, 1935.

PORTER, ARTHUR WILLIAM, Burma Frontier Service (asst. supt.) (b. 23rd Dec., 1887).—Apptd. extra asst. commr., Jan., 1911; asst. supt. (B.F.S.), Dec., 1922.

PORTER, Rev. JOHN, Indian Eccles. Estabt. (United Provs.) (senr. chaplain) (b. 7th Apr., 1889).—Apptd. chaplain on probation, 2nd Oct., 1921; junr. chaplain, Oct., 1923; senr. chaplain, Oct., 1931.

PORTER, MALCOLM TINDAL, M.C., Lt.-Col., R.E., late Indian State Rlys. (b. 14th Nov., 1882).—First commn., dated 4th Feb., 1901; apptd. asst. engr., state rlys., 12th Oct., 1908; exec. engr., Sept., 1911; supt. of works, Lower Burma Rlys. Survey, April, 1912, to Sept., 1913; on milty. duty from Oct., 1914, to Sept., 1919; offg. suptg. engr., Nov., 1923; employed on Burma-Siam rly. survey from 12th July, 1925; dep. ch. engr., N.W. Rly., April, 1927; retd., July, 1934.

PORTER, NOEL TINDAL, late Indian C.S. (b. 9th Nov., 1894).—Educ. at St. Edmund's Sch., Canterbury, and Corpus Christi Coll., Oxford; joined the service, 20th Nov., 1919; served in the Central Provs. as asst. commr. from 25th March, 1920; offg. dep. commr., May, 1924; dep. commr., (provl.) July, 1929; retd., Aug., 1932.

PORTER, RICHARD REGINALD MAITLAND, M.C., M.B., Major, I.M.S. (ret'd.), late Bihar and Orissa Civil Medl. dept.—First commn. dated 25th Jan., 1913; in milty. employ prior to 7th Feb., 1927; profir. of surgery, Patna medl. coll., Feb., 1927; retd., Aug., 1931.

POTTER, ARTHUR KINGSCOTE, B.A., Indian C.S. (asst. commr., Burma) (b. 7th Apr., 1905).—Educ. at Charterhouse and New Coll., Oxford; joined the service, 9th Oct., 1928; arrived, Dec., 1928; and served in Burma as asst. commr.; under secty., home and pol. dept., Aug., 1931; dep. secty., fin. dept., Burma, Mar., 1934; offg. dep. commr., June, 1934.

POULTON, HENRY MORTIMER, Major, I.A., Indian Political Service (b. 23rd Apr., 1898).—First commn., dated 18th June, 1917; joined the pol. dept., 16th May, 1922; supy. asst. commr., Bannu, March, 1924; asst. commr., ditto, April, 1924; asst. commr., Kohat, March, 1925; asst. pol. agent, Zhob, Dec., 1927; pol. agent, Chagai, and dep. commr., Shorand, Aug., 1928; pol. agent, Zhob, Nov., 1928; ditto, Kalat, June, 1929; ditto, Chagai, Aug., 1929; asst. pol. agent and asst. commr., Sibi, Aug., 1930; pol. agent, Kalat, Nov., 1931; ditto Quetta-Pishin, May, 1932; ditto, Haroati and Tonk, Oct., 1932; ditto E. Rajputana States, Sept., 1934; pol. agent, E. Kathiawar agency, Aug., 1935.

POUND, GRAHAM TREVOR, B.Sc., late Indian Service of Engrs. (Punjab) (b. 23rd Jan., 1890).—Joined the service as asst. engr., Oct., 1913; on mil. service from 4th Oct., 1915, to 17th May, 1919; asst. exec. engr., Jan., 1920; exec. engr., Oct., 1922; retd., May, 1931.

POUNDE, HERBERT, late India Posts and Tels. (b. 27th May, 1881).—Held non-gazetted appt. from June, 1898; dep. supt., Apr., 1918; supt. of post offices, Sept., 1919; dep. P.M.G., traffic, Patna, Mar., 1926; ditto, Madras, Nov., 1927; ditto, Punjab and N.W.F., Mar., 1930; retd., Apr., 1932.

POUNDE, HERBERT RICHARD GILDER, Office of High Commr. for India (exec. officer, store dept.) (b. 31st Jan., 1905).—Apptd. exec. officer, 10th Dec., 1935, after previous unestabd. service from 8th Aug., 1927.

POWELL, EDWIN VALENTINE MURRAY, Indian State Rlys. (transpn. officer, Burma Rlys.) (b. 14th Feb., 1892).—Joined the service as asst. loco. supt., 23rd July, 1920; offg. variously as dist. loco. supt., from Mar., 1921; transpn. officer, Apr., 1929; offg. dep. carr. and wagon supt., Oct., 1936.

POWELL, JOHN GLYNN, Indian Service of Engineers (ch. engr., Bihar) (b. 27th Sept., 1884).—Trained at R.I.E. Coll.; joined the service as asst. engr., 1st Jan., 1907; exec. engr., Jan., 1915; offg. suptg. engr., Dec., 1925; confd., July, 1926; ch. engr. and secty. to govt., p.w.d., buildings and roads bch., Bihar and Orissa, Nov., 1933.

POWELL, RICHARD, Indian Police (supt., United Provs.) (b. 4th Apr., 1889).—Joined the service as asst. supt., 5th Dec., 1908; in milty. employ from 19th Apr., 1915

to 4th June, 1916; asst. to inspr.-genl., C.I.D. Aug., 1918; supt., May, 1922; prinpl., P.T. school, Moradabad, Dec., 1925; asst. to inspr. genl., Jan., 1937.

POWELL, WILLIAM JACKSON, C.I.E., B.A., M.D., B.Ch., Col., I.M.S., Jail Dept., Central Provs. (b. 17th Dec., 1881).—In mil. employ to Aug., 1920; apptd. supt., central jail, Jubbulpore, 18th Aug., 1920; offg. inspr.-gen. of prisons, Cent. Provs., Jan., 1921; confd., Feb., 1923; C.I.E., June, 1930; offg. inspr.-genl., civil hosp., Central Provs., Mar., 1933; services placed at disposal of Govt. of India, Army Dept., Dec., 1934; hon. surg. to the King, Feb., 1937.

POWELL-PRICE, JOHN CADWAGAN, M.A., Indian Educl. Service (dep. dir. of public instrn., United Provs.) (b. 11th June, 1888).—Joined the service, 9th July, 1920; head master, govt. high school, Allahabad, Dec., 1920; prinpl., govt. inter. coll., Almora, April, 1921; offg. inspr. of schools, April, 1926; confd., Jan., 1929; asst. dir. of public instrn., United Provs., Mar., 1934; offg. dep. dir., ditto, Mar., 1936, and again, Oct., 1936; confd., Oct., 1937.

POWER, GERVASE BUSHE, C.I.E., M.C., late India Posts and Telegraphs (b. 17th Aug., 1883).—Trained at R.I.E. Coll.; apptd. to telegraph dept. as asst. supt., 1st Oct., 1905; ditto, engrg., Apl., 1914; deputed to field service, Mesopotamia, Sept., 1915 (three times mentioned in despatches), and to field service (Afghan War), May, 1919; C.I.E., Dec., 1916; on deputation with N.W. Rly., Nov., 1920; divl. engr., tels., Lahore, Jan., 1927; dir., posts and tels., Sind and Baluchistan, Aug., 1929; dir. tel. engrg., June, 1932; offg. p.m.g., Madras, Sept., 1934; p.m.g., Burma, May, 1935; retd., Oct., 1936.

POWER, ROBIN TURENNE, Indian State Rlys. (dep. traffic manager, Burma Rlys.) (b. 9th Oct., 1883).—Joined the service as asst. traffic supt., 29th July, 1908; dist. traffic supt., July, 1924; offg. dep. traffic manager, June, 1932; confd., Aug., 1932; offg. traffic manager, Mar., 1933.

POWIS, HARRY BADAN, late Indian Police (b. 4th Nov., 1880).—Joined the service, 1st Nov., 1902; dep. supt., Apl., 1923; appt. to I.P. as supt., Feb., 1935; retd., Sept., 1935.

POWIS, LEONARD JOHN PEARSON, M.I.St.E., Indian State Rlys. (exec. engr., E.I. Rly.) (b. 9th Dec., 1888).—Joined the service as asst. engr., 1st June, 1915; asst. exec. engr., Dec., 1922; exec. engr., June, 1924.

POYAMANI NAGASWAMI NATESH AYYAR, L.C.E., late Indian Service of Engrs. (Bombay) (b. 16th May, 1874).—Joined the service, 10th June, 1896; asst. engr., June, 1897; exec. engr., June, 1908; offg. sany. engr., Nov., 1919, May, 1923, and April, 1926; offg. mechnl. engr. to govt., April, 1926; offg. sany. engr., May, 1928; retd., May, 1929.

PRABH SINGH, SARDAR BAHADUR, Sardar, late Indian Service of Engrs. (b. 6th July, 1883).—Joined the service, 17th Sept., 1906; asst. engr., Sept., 1907; exec. engr., Apl., 1916; offg. suptg. engr., Apl., 1928, and again, Apl., 1932; confd., May, 1935; retd., May, 1937; member pub. serv. com., Punjab and N.W.F. Prov., May, 1937.

PRABHU DATTA SHASTRI, M.A., B.Sc., Ph.D. (Kiel), Indian Educl. Service (vice-prinpl., Rajshahi Coll.) (b. 24th Mar., 1885).—Held non-gazetted appt. from 14th Oct., 1905, to 30th Nov., 1911; prof., presy. coll., Aug., 1912; vice-prinpl., Hooghly Coll., July, 1926; offg. prof., presy. coll., Calcutta, July, 1928; vice-prinpl., Rajshahi coll., Jan., 1929; offg. prinpl., ditto, May, 1933; posted from Bombay presy.

PRADHAN, DWARKANATH RAMCHANDRA B.A., B.Sc., Indian C.S. (asst. collr., Bombay) (b. 8th June, 1906).—Educ. at Bombay University and Fitzwilliam House, Cambridge; joined the service, 13th Oct., 1930; arrived in India, December, 1930, and served in Bombay as asst. collr.

PRADHAN, SIR GOVIND BALVANT, Kt., B.A., LL.B., Apptd. member, govt.'s exec. council, Bombay, Sept., 1928; Kt., June, 1931.

PRAGER, MISS MILDRED F., B.A., late Indian Educl. Service (b. 17th July, 1882).—Apptd. offg. inspectress of girls' schools, 2nd Feb., 1920; confd., Jan., 1926; retd., July, 1937.

PRALL, SAMUEL REGINALD, M.D., B.C., M.R.C.S., Lt.-Col., I.M.S., Medical Dept., Bombay (supt., St. George's hosp.) (b. 18th Feb., 1891).—Date of first commn., 25th Feb., 1921; in milty. employ until 23rd June, 1930; civil surg., June, 1932; offg. supt., St. George's hosp., Mar., 1934; confd., July, 1934.

PRAMANIK, SUSHIL KUMAR, M.Sc., Ph.D., Meteorological Dept. (meteorologist) (b. 7th Jan., 1899).—Joined the service as meteorologist on probn., 25th Feb., 1927; confd., Feb., 1929.

PRANCE, BASIL CAMDEN, C.I.E., O.B.E., late Indian C.S.—Educ. at Rugby and Christ Church, Oxford; apptd. after exam. of 1906; arrived, 17th Dec., 1907, and served in E. Bengal and Assam as asst. mag. and collr., and asst. settl. offr.; transfd. to Bengal, April, 1912; jt. mag. and dep. collr., Jan., 1913; I.A.R.O., June, 1917, to May, 1919; O.B.E., (milty.), Jan., 1919; mag. and collr., Jan., 1920; retd., July, 1924; apptd. actg. magte. and collr., Dacca, Aug., 1932; apptd. actg. commr., Mar., 1934; C.I.E., Jan., 1935; retd., May, 1935.

PRANCE, GEOFFREY BERNARD SANDEMAN, Indian Police (supt., N.W.F.P.) (b. 28th July, 1896).—Joined the service, 29th April, 1919; asst. supt., May, 1921; addl. supt., April, 1925; supt., Aug., 1926; awarded King's police medal, Jan., 1927; awarded bar to ditto, Jan., 1928; dist. officer, frontier constaby., N.W.F.P., Dec., 1932.

PRASAD, AMBIKA (see Ambika P.).

PRASAD, BISHNATH (see Bishnath Prasad).

PRASAD, JAGAT (see Jagat Prasad).

PRASAD, KUNWAR JAGDISH (see Jagdish Prasad, Kunwar).

PRASAD, JWALA (see Jwala Prasad).

PRASAD, KANCHI (see Kanchi Prasad).

PRASAD, KRISHNA (see Krishna Prasad).

PRASAD, MAHABIR (see Mahabir Prasad).

PRASAD, NIRINJAN, GOYAL, LALA (see Nirinjan, P. G. L.).

PRASAD, RAGHUNATH (see Raghunath Prasad).

PRASAD, RAM (see Ram Prasad).

PRASAD, SHYAM SUNDER (see Shyam Sunder Prasad).

PRASADA, SHANKAR, Indian C.S. (jt. mag. United Provs.) (b. 11th March, 1905).—Educ. at Allahabad University and Magdalen College, Cambridge; joined the service, 11th Oct., 1930; arrived in India, Nov., 1930, and served in the United Provs., as asst. mag. and collr.; jt. magte., Apl., 1935.

PRASAD, SHANTI (see Shanti Prasad).

PRASAD, SITAL (see Sital Prasad).

PRASANNA KUMAR ACHARYA, M.A., D.Litt., Ph.D., Indian Eduel. Service (prof. Muir central coll., Allahabad) (b. 21st Apl., 1890).—Joined the service as prof., Muir central coll., Allahabad, 1st Dec., 1920; with Allahabad Univ. from 1st July, 1922.

PRASHAD, BAINI (see Baini Prashad).

PRASHAD, DURGA (see Durga Prashad).

PRASHAD, KAMTA (see Kamta Prashad).

PRATAP BIKRAM SHAH, KUNWAR, late Indian C.S. (United Provs.) (b. 24th Oct., 1894).—Educ. at Mayo Coll., and Oriel Coll., Oxford; joined the service, 22nd Oct., 1914, as proby. dep. collr.; asst. commr., 10th Jan., 1922; asst. supt., Dehra Dun, March, 1923, to Oct., 1924, and from March, 1925; asst. commr., Nov., 1925; ret'd., Aug., 1931.

PRATAP, RAM (see Ram Pratap).

PRATT, EDWARD BROUGHTON, Indian Police (b. 22nd Jan., 1907).—Joined the service as asst. dist. supt., Central Provs., 26th Nov., 1926; offg. comdt. of police, Aden, Apl., 1933.

PRATT, SIR HENRY SHELDON, Kt., M.A., late Indian C.S. (Burma) (b. 26th Jan., 1873).—Educ. at King's Grammar School, Warwick, and Lincoln Coll., Oxford; apptd. after exam. of 1895; arrived, 30th Nov., 1896, and served in Burma as asst. commr.; dist. judge, May, 1905; divl. judge, April, 1914; addl. judge, chief court of Lower Burma, Nov., 1917; judge, chief court of Upper Burma, May, 1919; judge, high ct., Rangoon, Dec., 1922; Kt., June, 1928; ret'd., July, 1930.

PRATT, WALTER PHILIP R., V.D., late Indian State Rlys. (b. 11th May, 1879).—Joined the service as asst. traffic supt., 13th June, 1902; dist. traffic supt., May, 1914; offg. dep. traffic manager variously from Apl., 1919; ret'd., Feb., 1932.

PREM CHAND, PURI, Lala, B.A., Indian Service of Engrs. (exec. engr., Punjab) (b. 20th Jan., 1898).—Joined the service, 24th Oct., 1921; asst. exec. engr., Oct., 1922; exec. engr., Oct., 1933.

PREM NATH, LALA, Indian Forest Service (dep. consvr., Punjab) (b. 1st Jan., 1884).—Apptd. asst. consvr., Punjab, 19th July, 1922; on deputation to Chamba State from 19th April, 1926, to Jan., 1931; dep. consvr., Jan., 1931.

PRESCOTT, HENRY CECIL, C.M.G., C.I.E., Lt.-Col., I.A. (ret'd.), late Indian Police Service.—First commn., April, 1902; in mil. employ to 10th Jan., 1908; asst. supt., Jan., 1908; dist. supt., Dec., 1910; on mil. service from 11th Dec., 1914; under civil admn., Iraq, from 6th June, 1917, as inspr.-gen. of police; C.I.E., March, 1919; C.M.G., June, 1926; ret'd., Aug., 1928.

PRESCOTT, RICHARD GORDON BATHGATE, Indian Police (depy. commr. of police) (b. 30th May, 1896).—Joined the service as asst. supt., 21st Dec., 1915; dist. supt., Oct., 1923; awarded King's police medal, June, 1932; dep. commr., Rangoon police, May, 1937.

PRESSWELL, HENRY EDWARD, late Indian State Rlys. (b. 1st Jan., 1879).—Joined the service, 1st Jan., 1895; asst. supt., Jan., 1913; dist. supt., April, 1920; divl. traffic manager, April, 1920; asst. comml. manager, March, 1926; supt., Oct., 1926; offg. dep. ch. operating supt., March, 1927; confd., Mar., 1930; offg. dep. agent, Apl., 1932; ret'd., Nov., 1935.

PRESSWELL, THOMAS GEORGE, late Indian State Rlys. (E.I. Rly.) (b. 12th Mar., 1877).—Joined the service as dist. supt., 15th March, 1920; dep. ch. comml. manager (s.p.t.), May, 1925; offg. junr. dep. agent, May, 1925; confd., March, 1927; senr. dep. agent, May, 1928; ret'd. Nov., 1931.

PRIA DAS, TANDAN, Rai Sahib, Lala, Indian Service of Engrs. (exec. engr., Punjab) (b. 6th Dec., 1884).—Joined the service as qualified student, 19th Sept., 1908; temp. engr., Sept., 1909; asst. engr., 1st Jan., 1923; apptd. to the I.S.E. as asst. exec. engr., Jan., 1927; exec. engr., Jan., 1932.

PRICE, ARCHIBALD MILNER, Dept. of indus. and labour, Govt. of India (asst. secty., dept. of labour) (b. 18th Jan., 1887).—Joined the service, 8th Dec., 1906; supt., dept. of indus. and lab., July, 1927; offg. asst. secty., ditto, public works bch., June, 1932; confd., Oct., 1934; asst. secty., dept. of lab., Nov., 1937.

PRICE, EARDLEY EDWARD CARNAC, A.C.A., Indian Finance Dept. (b. 11th Mar., 1892).—Joined the service, 11th June, 1910; asst. acctnt.-gen., Bihar and Orissa, Nov., 1920; asst. dir. of comml. audit, Aug., 1925; asst. acct.-genl., Bombay, Nov., 1928; asst. dir. comml. audit, Bombay, Nov., 1928; dep. acct.-genl., Bombay, May, 1932; services lent to Jodhpur Darbar, Jan., 1933.

PRICE, ERNEST, M.B.E., Office of High Commr. for India (prinpl. exec. officer, store dept.) (b. 14th Dec., 1879).—Apptd. 2nd class clerk, India store dep't, 9th March, 1898; supervisor, April, 1915; M.B.E., Jan., 1918; transf'd. to office of high commr. for India, Oct., 1920; higher exec. officer, April, 1922; prinpl. exec. officer, May, 1926.

PRICE, GLADYS MAUD, India Audit Office, (exec. officer) (b. 22nd May, 1898).—Apptd., clerical officer, 20th Jan., 1922; exec. officer, Oct., 1926.

PRICE, MURRAY ROKEBY, Indian Police (supt.) (b. 29th Oct., 1894).—Joined the service as asst. supt., Bombay, 23rd May, 1924; offg. supt., May, 1929; confd., Feb., 1934.

PRICE, WILLIAM ALAN BOURNE, Indian Police (supt., Bengal) (b. 28th April, 1896).—Joined the service as asst. supt., 15th March, 1921; offg. supt., April, 1925; confd., May, 1929.

PRICHARD, HENRY MONTAGUE, B.A., Indian C.S. (dep. commr., Assam) (b. 29th May 1887).—Educ. at Marlborough, Brasenose Coll., Oxford, and Univ. Coll., London; apptd. after exam. of 1910; arrived in India, 4th Dec., 1911, and served in E. Bengal and Assam as asst.,

commr.; asst. commr., Assam, April, 1912; on mil. duty (I.A.R.O.), Oct., 1918, to Jan., 1919; offg. dep. commr., Aug., 1922; offg. sec., fin. and rev. depts., June, 1927; confd. and dep. commr., Oct., 1927; finl. secty. to govt., Assam, Nov., 1934.

PRIDAY, LESLIE MORRIS, late Indian Service of Engrs. (b. 31st May, 1883).—Trained at R.I.E. Coll.; joined the service, 1st Oct., 1906, as asst. engr.; exec. engr., Oct., 1916; offg. suptg. engr., Aug., 1920; suptg. engr., Jan., 1925; ret'd., May, 1934.

PRIDEAUX, EDWIN TERREY, B.A. (Cantab.), Indian C.S. (jt. mag. and dep. collr., Bihar) (b. 16th Nov., 1906).—Educ. at Latymer Upper School, Hammersmith, and Sidney Sussex College, Cambridge; joined the service, 10th Oct., 1930; arrived in India, Nov., 1930, and served in Bihar and Orissa as asst. collr. and mag.

PRIDEAUX, FRANCIS WINCKWORTH AUSTICE, O.B.E., F.R.G.S., Barr., late Judicial dept., Central Provs. (b. 9th May, 1869).—Joined the service, 19th Aug., 1889; asst. commr., Berar, June, 1890; K.-i.-H. medal, 2nd class, Nov., 1901; dep. commr., July, 1904; civil and sess. judge, Nov., 1904; dist. and sess. judge, Sept., 1905; divl. and sess. judge, Nov., 1912; temp. addl. commr., judl. commr.'s ct., Sept., 1913; addl. judl. commr. (provl.), May, 1915; O.B.E., June, 1918; dist. and sessions judge, Jan., 1921; addl. judl. commr., Dec., 1921; ret'd., May, 1929.

PRIDMORE, WILLIAM HENRY, Indian C.S. (jt. mag. and collr., United Provinces) (b. 17th Aug., 1910).—Educ. at Rugby and Queen's College, Oxford; apptd. after exam. of 1933; arrived in India, Nov., 1934, and served in the United Provs. as asst. mag. and collr.; jt. magte., Nov., 1936.

PRIESTLEY, GERALD WILLIAM, Indian C.S. (commr. of labour) (b. 12th Nov., 1888).—Educ. at West Monmouthshire Sch., Pontypool, and Trinity Coll., Cambridge; apptd. after exam. of 1911; arrived, 29th Nov., 1912, and served in Madras as asst. collr. and mag.; supt., Pudukkottai State, Aug. to Sept., 1915; spl. asst. settlt. offr., Nov., 1916; sub-collr. and jt. mag. and ditto, Oct., 1917; special settlt. officer, July, 1919, to March, 1925; offg. collr. and dist. mag., March, 1927; confd., Aug., 1930; commr. of Coorg, Nov., 1937; commr. of labour, Nov., 1936.

PRING, NOEL GRATTAN, B.A., Indian Forest Service (dep. consvr., Punjab) (b. 30th Dec., 1896).—Joined the service as asst. consvr., Punjab, 6th Dec., 1922; dep. consvr., Dec., 1926.

PRINGLE, IVAN TERENCE ST. CLAIR, M.A., Indian State Rlys. (dep. agent (personnel), E. B. Rly.) (b. 20th Feb., 1887).—Joined the service as asst. traffic supt., 29th Dec., 1911; dist. traffic supt., Dec., 1920; offg. asst. agent, E.B. Rly., May, 1926; offg. dep. secty., rly. bd., Feb., 1928; offg. dep. agent, E.B. Rly., Dec., 1929; offg. dep. agent (personnel), E.B. rly., Jan., 1933; confd., Apl., 1934.

PRINGLE, JOHN MACKAY, B.A., Barr., late Indian C.S. (Bengal) (b. 27th April, 1888).—Educ. at Fettes Coll., Edinburgh, Merton Coll., Oxford, and Univ. Coll., London; apptd. after exam. of 1911; arrived, 29th Nov., 1912, and served in Bengal as asst. mag. and

collr.; jt. mag. and dep. collr., April, 1918; I.A.R.O., Aug. to Oct., 1918; jt. mag. and dep. collr., April, 1919; addl. dist. and sess. judge, June, 1924; dist. and sess. judge, May, 1925; ret'd., June, 1931.

PRINGLE, REGINALD JAMES, B.A., Indian C.S. (jt. mag. and dep. collr., Bengal) (b. 23rd Mar., 1910).—Educ. at Felsted School and Emmanuel College, Cambridge; apptd. after exam. of 1932; arrived in India, Nov., 1933, and served in Bengal as asst. mag. and collr.; offg. pte. secty. to govr., Bengal, Feb., 1936.

PRIOR, CHARLES GEOFFREY, C.I.E., Major, I.A., Indian Political Service (b. 9th Dec., 1896).—First commn. dated 14th July, 1915; joined the pol. dept., 24th Aug., 1923; under-sec. to A.G.G., Rajputana, Oct., 1924; under-sec. to pol. resident in Persian Gulf, Aug., 1926; sec. to ditto, April, 1927; under-secy. to A.G.G., Punjab States, Mar., 1929; pol. agent, Bahrein, Apl., 1929; offg. dep. secty., for, and pol. dept., June, 1933; confd., May, 1934; offg. jt. secty., ditto, May, 1934, and again, Apl., 1935; C.I.E., June, 1936; prime minister, Alwar State, Nov., 1936.

PRIOR, HENRY CARLOS, C.I.E., B.A., Indian C.S. (secty. to govt., fin. dept., Bihar) (b. 6th Jan., 1890).—Educ. at Eton, and King's Coll., Cambridge; apptd. after exam. of 1913; arrived, 30th Nov., 1914; and served in Bihar and Orissa as asst. mag. and collr.; temply. to army dept., Oct., 1915 (commtdt., 3rd (Naga Hills) Bn., Assam Rifles) to Oct., 1919; offg. mag. and collr., April, 1921; confd., Jan., 1929; manager, Bettiah wards estate, June, 1926; addl. secty. to govt., rev. dept., July, 1929; secty., ditto, Oct., 1931; secty., fin. dept., B. and O., Apl., 1933; C.I.E., June, 1936.

PRISTON, W. H. F.R.C.V.S., late Indian Veterinary Service (United Provs.) (b. 26th Dec., 1887).—Joined the service as third supt., 1st Dec., 1920; offg. veterinary adviser to govt., March, 1925; supt., Nov., 1925; offg. dir. vety. services, Cent. Provs., Mar., 1930; ret'd., July, 1931.

PRITAM SINGH, BRAR, Indian Police (supt.) (b. 17th Nov., 1900).—Joined the service as asst. supt., Punjab, 7th Aug., 1921; offg. supt., Feb., 1928; confd., Nov., 1933.

PRITCHARD, HUGH ROBERT NORMAN, C.I.E., O.B.E., Lt.-Col., I.A. ret'd. (b. 11th Apl., 1879).—First commn. dated 20th May, 1899; in mil. emp. to June, 1904, when he was apptd. sup. asst. commr.; pol. asst., Feb., 1906; asst. to agent. govr.-gen., Rajputana, and chief commr., Ajmer-Merwara, April, 1913; pol. agent, Bhopawar, July, 1913; pol. offr., Bundi, Oct., 1913; second asst. to agent to govr.-gen. in Rajputana, Nov., 1913; reverted temply. to mil. duty, March, 1915; asst. pol. agent, Loralai, Dec., 1917; 1st asst. to the agent to the govr.-gen. in Baluchistan, May, 1919; O.B.E., Jan., 1920; pol. agent, Bhopal, April, 1921; resdt. in Mewar, April, 1924; pol. agent, Haraoti and Tonk, Oct., 1926; pol. agent, Eastern Rajputana States, March, 1927; to govr.-agent genl. in C.I., Mar., 1929; C.I.E., June, 1929; agent to govr. genl., Madras States, Oct., 1930; ret'd., Apl., 1933.

PRITCHARD, JOSEPH WILLIAM, Indian C.S. (sub-collr., Madras) (b. 5th Aug., 1903).—Educ. at Newport (Salop) Grammar School, and Trinity Coll., Cambridge; joined the service, 25th Oct., 1927; arrived, Dec., 1927; and served in Madras as asst. magte. and collr.; sub-collr., July, 1932; offg. mag. and collr., May, 1936.

PROCTOR, ALFRED HENRY, D.S.O., M.D., Col., I.M.S., ret'd. (b. 21st Jan., 1879).—Joined the service, 1st Sept., 1904; arrived, 9th June, 1905; in mil. employ. to Feb., 1911; services placed at disposal of govt. of India, home dept., from Aug., 1914; offg. resdt. surgn., presidency gen. hosp., Calcutta, June, 1920; offg. surg. supt., ditto, Mar., 1925; confd., July, 1927; offg. surg.-genl. with govt. of Bengal, Mar., 1933; inspr.-genl. of civil hospss., United Provs., Dec., 1933; ret'd., Aug., 1935.

PROCTOR, ERNEST, M.C., B.Sc., A.M.I.C.E., Indian State Rlys. (exec. engr., Burma Rlys.) (b. 3rd July, 1894).—Joined the service, 15th Apl., 1920, as asst. engr.; exec., engr., Burma rlys., July, 1933.

PROMODE KUMAR DEY (see Dey, P. K.).

PROPHULLA CHANDRA KANJILAL, B.Sc., Indian Forest Service (dep. consvr., United Provs.) (b. 2nd Mar., 1886).—Joined the service, 1st July, 1911; dep. consvr., Oct., 1923; with govt. of Assam from Sept., 1929-Oct., 1931.

PROTHERO, EDWIN LYNDON, Indian Service of Engrs. (under-secty. to govt. (estab.), Punjab) (b. 20th June, 1893).—Joined the service, 1st Oct., 1919; asst. engr., Dec., 1919; asst. exec. engr., Jan., 1920; exec. engr., Oct., 1925; addl. secty. to govt., rev. dept., July, 1929; under-secty. to govt. (estab.), Punjab, Feb., 1935.

PROUDLOCK, ROBIN, Indian State Rlys. (divl. comml. officer, N.W. Rly.) (b. 12th June, 1892).—Joined the service as asst. traffic supt., 8th Nov., 1914; asst. dist. do., Jan., 1922; offg. dist. do., Oct., 1923 (lent to Q.M.Q.'s branch, Jan., 1927); divl. transpn. officer, July, 1927; divl. comml. officer, Oct., 1930.

PRU, U SAW HLA, B.A., B.L., Burma Civil Service (excise commr., Burma) (b. 24th Nov., 1886).—Apptd. extra asst. commr., 22nd Feb., 1915; offg. dep. commr., Apl., 1928; confd., Oct., 1930; secty. to govt., judl. dept., Burma, Oct., 1933; excise commr., Burma, Mar., 1936.

PRUCE, THOMAS HERBERT, late Indian Finance Dept.—Joined the service, 1st Sept., 1891; asst. acctnt.-gen., Punjab, July, 1914; ditto, Bombay, April, 1915; ditto, Central Provs., Dec., 1919; dep. acctnt.-gen., United Provs., Nov., 1923; ditto, Bengal, April, 1924; ditto, Central Provs., Jan., 1927; ret'd., Oct., 1928.

PRYDE, ARTHUR WALTER, Indian Police (supt., Bombay) (b. 3rd Nov., 1896).—Joined the service, 26th Aug., 1923; offg. supt., Jan., 1926; confd., Oct., 1932; lab. offcr., Bombay, Nov., 1935.

PUCKLE, FREDERICK HALE, C.S.I., C.I.E., B.A., Indian C.S. (ch. secty. to govt., Punjab) (b. 8th June, 1889).—Educ. at Uppingham, and King's Coll., Cambridge; apptd. after exam. of 1912; arrived, 22nd Nov., 1913, and served in the Punjab as asst. commr.; on mil. duty from May, 1915, to April, 1919; dep. commr. (provl.),

Oct., 1925; C.I.E., June, 1930; fin. secty. to govt., Punjab, Oct., 1930; offg. ch. secty. to govt., Punjab, Oct., 1933; confd., Feb., 1935; employed on spl. duty in the Home Dept., Govt. of India, May, 1937; C.S.I., Jan., 1938.

PUDDEN, HEDLEY HERBERT CRAWFORD, B.A., Indian Forest Service (dep. consvr., Burma) (b. 1st Feb., 1900).—Joined the service as asst. consvr., 24th Dec., 1923; dep. consvr., Oct., 1928.

PUGH, MAURICE DOUGLAS, Indian Police (asst. supt., N.W.F.P.) (b. 9th Nov., 1903).—Joined the service as asst. supt., Punjab, 12th Dec., 1923; asst. dist. officer, frontier constaby., Nov., 1929; offg. supt., Sept., 1933.

PULLA REDDI, OBULREDDIGARI, Indian C.S. (sub-collr., Madras) (b. 11th July, 1902).—Educ. at Univ. of Madras and Oriel Coll., Oxford; joined the service, 13th Oct., 1927; arrived, Nov., 1927, and served in Madras as asst. collr. and magte.; under-secty. to govt., dev. dept., Mar., 1931.

PULLAN, AYRTON GEORGE POPPLEWELL, M.A., late Indian C.S. (b. 6th June, 1879).—Educ. at Malvern Sch., and Trinity Coll., Oxford; apptd. after exam. of 1902; arrived, 30th Nov., 1903, and served in the United Provs. as asst. mag. and collr., and asst. commr.; asst. supt., Dehra Dun, May, 1908, to July, 1909; jt. mag., April, 1913; sess. and sub-judge, Dec., 1916; dist. and sess. judge, May, 1918; legal remembr. to govt. of United Provs., Oct., 1923; addl. judl. commr. of Oudh, April, 1924; dist. and sess. judge, Aug., 1924; actg. judge, Allahabad high ct., May to Oct., 1926, from Oct., 1926, again and from Mar., 1929; Jan., 1931; confd., Jan., 1931; offg. judge, chief ct., Oudh, May, 1927; confd., Oct., 1929; ret'd., July, 1933.

PULLEYNE, GEOFFREY BERNARD, Indian Police (dist. supt., Central Provs.) (b. 12th May, 1890).—Joined the service as asst. dist. supt., Dec., 1910; dist. supt., March, 1924; supt. rly. police, Hyderabad State, Oct., 1929.

PURDY, JOSEPH WILLIAM, Indian Police (b. 29th Nov., 1912).—Joined the service as proby. asst. supt. of police, Bengal, Nov., 1933.

PURI, B. D., RAI BAHADUR, Indian Railway Accts. Service (dep. chief accts. offer., N.W. Rly.) (b. 23rd June, 1883).—Held non-gaz. appts. from 1st May, 1906; asst. dir. of fin., Oct., 1924; Rai Bahadur, June, 1926; on for service to Baroda State, May, 1927, and to Porbunder Durbar, Aug., 1927; offer. on spec. duty, Rly. Bd., July, 1927; accts. offer., gr. I., N.W. rly., Apl., 1929; confd. as dep. chief accts. offer., N.W. rly., Apl., 1930.

PURI, BALWANT SINGH, B.A., Indian Service of Engrs. (exec. engr., Burma) (b. 16th July, 1894).—Joined the service, 7th Oct., 1918; asst. engr., Oct., 1919; asst. exec. engr., Jan., 1920; offg. exec. engr., Apl., 1928; confd., Oct., 1929; services placed at disposal of central p.w.d. (temp.), Mar., 1935.

PURI, INDRA SINGH, M.A., Indian Audit Dept. (b. 17th May, 1895).—Joined the service, 1st Mar., 1919; dep. dir. rly. audit, Apl., 1929; offg. contr., rly. accts., Mar., 1931; accts. officer, rly. clearing accts., Feb., 1933; offg. dep. dir., ditto, Mar., 1933; accts. officer,

N.W. rly., Nov., 1933; offg. dep. ch. accts. offer., N.W. rly., July, 1934, and again, June, 1937; offg. dep. dir., rly. clearing accounts office, Oct., 1935.

PURI, MADAN LAL (see Madan Lal, Puri).

PURI, NEHCHAL DAS, M.B., Lt.-Col., I.M.S., Jail Dept., Punjab (dep. inspr. genl. of prisons) (b. 5th Dec., 1888).—First commn. dated 31st Jan., 1914; supt., central jail, Jan., 1924; dep. inspr. genl. of prisons, Feb., 1927; offg. inspr. genl. of prisons, Mar., 1932; June, 1933; and again, Feb., 1935.

PURI, PREM CHAND (see Prem Chand Puri).

PURKIS, FREDERIC CHARLES, late Indian Forest Service (b. 27th Mar., 1877).—Joined the service, 4th Feb., 1903; dep. consvr., March, 1926; ret'd., Apl., 1929.

PURSELL, RICHARD STANLEY, C.I.E., O.B.E., India Posts and Tels. (ch. engr., posts and tels. (b. 5th July, 1882)).—Joined the telegraph service, 1st Oct., 1904, as asst. supt.; ditto, and persl. asst. to the dir. gen. of posts and telegraphs, April, 1910; O.B.E., June, 1918; divl. engr., Feb., 1921; asst. to chief engr., tels., May, 1923; divl. engr., tels., Dec., 1923; offg. dir., tele. engrg., U.P. circle, Nov., 1929; ditto, Bengal and Assam circle, Apl., 1930; dep. dir. genl., tels., May, 1931; C.I.E., June, 1933; offg. ch. engr., posts and tels., May, 1934; confd., Dec., 1934.

PUTNAM, JAMES, O.B.E., late India Office (b. 27th Mar., 1873).—Clerk, 2nd class, registry and record dept., India Office, 26th Feb., 1892; transf. to pol. dept., Dec., 1895; went to India as confidential clerk on the staff of the Viceroy, Nov., 1897; returned to India Office, July, 1905; proceeded to India in the suite of the Prince of Wales, Oct., 1905; actg. asst. pte. sec. to Viceroy, 1904-5; personal clk. to Sir W. Lawrence during Prince of Wales' tour in India, Oct., 1905, to April, 1906; resumed duty in pol. dept., April, 1906; apptd. clerk to the Royal Commn. on Indian Decentralisation, Nov., 1907; clerk to educatl. adviser to Indian students, April, 1909, to Nov., 1912; asst. sec. to Royal Commn. on Public Services in India, Dec., 1912; services lent to War Office, Dec., 1914; temply. attached to mil. dep., Jan., 1916; staff clerk, Jan., 1917; suptg. exec. officer, April, 1922; ret'd., Mar., 1933; O.B.E., June, 1933.

PUTTICK, HAROLD WALTER, M.I.M.E., Indian State Rlys. (asst. elect. engr., E.I. Rly.) (b. 17th June, 1890).—Joined the service as asst. elect. engr., 4th Oct., 1921; offg. dist. elect. engr., Oct., 1928.

PYNE, WILLIAM HENRY JOHN, Indian State Rlys. (transptn. supt.) (b. 30th June, 1882).—Joined the service, 3rd Jan., 1913; asst. transptn. supt., Oct., 1923; transptn. (traffic comml.) supt., G.I.P. Rly., Dec., 1933.

PYPER, JOHN CLARK, O.B.E., M.B., Lt.-Col. I.M.S. (medl. officer, Zabul) (b. 18th Mar. 1889).—First commn. dated 30th July, 1921; in milty. employ. to Oct., 1928; agency surg., Gilgit, 19th Oct., 1928; resdy. surg., Kashmir, May, 1933; medl. officer, Zabul, Nov., 1934.

PYU, MAUNG KHIN MAUNG, B.Sc., Indian C.S. (asst. commr., Burma) (b. 19th March, 1905).—Educ. at University of Rangoon and School of Oriental Studies, London; joined the service, 9th Oct., 1929; arrived Dec., 1929, and served in Burma as asst. commr.

QADIR, ABDUL (see Abdul Qadir).

QAIYUM, A. (see Abdul Quaiyum).

QUARRY, PHILLIPPE STOWELL, late Indian Police (b. 19th Mar., 1883).—Joined the service as proby. asst. opium agent, 10th April, 1905; confd., March, 1907; asst. supt. Sept., 1909; supt., Sept., 1916; ret'd., Oct., 1932.

QUAYLE, THOMAS, C.I.E., D.Litt., M.A., Office of High Commr. for India (sec., education dept.) (b. 13th Sept., 1884).—Apptd. jt. sec. for Indian students, July 1921; joint sec., education dept., April, 1925; asst. ditto, Aug., 1926; designation change to secty., ditto, Oct., 1930; C.I.E., June, 1936.

QUILTER, CHARLES FREDERICK GRANT, India Posts and Tels. (dep. dir.-genl., Burma) (b. 13th Dec., 1883).—Held non-gaz. appt. from 1st March, 1906; clerk, Dec., 1906; supt., Sept., 1907; I.A.R.O. from 21st Nov., 1915, to 9th Aug., 1920; offg. asst. dir. genl., May, 1928; confd., Oct., 1930; dep. P.M.G., Burma, Oct., 1930; dep. dir.-genl., Apl., 1937.

QUIN, THOMAS MICHAEL, B.A., Indian C.S. (asst. commr., Punjab) (b. 21st Sept., 1911).—Educated at Stonyhurst, New College, Oxford, and the School of Oriental Studies, London; apptd. after exam. of 1934; arrived in India, Dec., 1935, and served in the Punjab as asst. commr.

QUINLAN, DAVID, M.R.C.V.S., late Indian Civil Vety. Dept. (b. 6th July, 1880).—Joined the service, 28th Jan., 1906; supt. Bengal, April, 1908; vety. adviser to govt. of Bihar and Orissa, Oct., 1920; ret'd., June, 1930.

QUINLAN, PATRICK BAGOT, B.A. Indian Educl. Service (dir. of public instrn., Burma) (b. 12th Oct., 1884).—Joined the service as prinpl. of govt. high and normal school, Moulmein, 16th Feb., 1915; on mil. service from 1st Sept., 1916, to 2nd March, 1919; inspr. of schools, Aug., 1919; offg. asst. dir. of public instrn., Jan., 1933; confd., Jan., 1934; dir., ditto, Feb., 1935.

QUINN-YOUNG, FREDERICK CHARLES STEPHEN, Indian Police (b. 22nd Dec., 1902).—Joined the service as asst. supt., Central Prov., 27th Nov., 1922; offg. dist. supt., June, 1925, Feb., 1926, and again, Oct., 1932.

QUINTON, HAROLD, late Indian C.S. (b. 10th Nov., 1890).—Educ. at Berkhampstead Sch., and Brasenose College, Oxford; apptd. after exam. of 1913; arrived, 18th Dec., 1914, and served in Bengal as asst. mag. and collr.; on special duty, govt. of Bengal, gen. dept., Dec., 1919; ditto, finl. dept., Jan., 1920; jt. mag. and dy. collr., Feb., 1922; offg. mag. and collr., June, 1922; confd., Nov., 1927; ret'd., July 1935.

QURAISHI, ABUL HASAN, B.A., Indian C.S. (jt. mag. and dep. collr., Bengal) (b. 1st July, 1908)—Educ. at Government High School, Ballia, Queen's Intermediate College, Benares, Allahabad University and the School of Oriental Studies, London; joined the service 5th Oct., in Bengal as asst. mag. and collr.; jt. mag. and sub-divl. offcr., July, 1935.

QURBAN ALI KHAN, MIR, Indian Police (supt., Punjab) (b. 11th May, 1898).—Joined the service, 8th Nov., 1921; asst. supt., Nov., 1923; supt., July, 1932; dep. insp.-genl., in addn., May, 1935.

QURESHI, ABDUL KARIM NABI BUX, Indian State Rlys. (dist. traffic supt., E.B. Rly.) (b. 19th May, 1895).—Asst. traffic supt., 1919; dist. ditto (prov.), Dec., 1936.

QUTB, SYED NIAZ, KHAN BAHADUR, M.A., LL.B., late India Posts and Tels. (b. 1st July, 1877).—Joined the service as proby. supt., 6th Jan., 1900; supt., March, 1902; dep. postmaster-genl., April, 1920; offg. P.M.G., United Provs., April, 1928; dep. dir.-genl., postal services and offg. P.M.G., central circle, Mar., 1930; confd., Feb., 1931; offg. P.M.G., Bengal and Assam, Mar., 1931; ret'd., July, 1932.

QUTUB-UD-DIN AHMED, C.I.E., KHAN BAHADUR,—Apptd. member of govr.'s exec. council, Assam, 1st March, 1924; C.I.E., June, 1929; ret'd., Mar., 1929.

RAB, SAHYID MUJIBUR (see Saiyid Mujibur, R.).

RACHHPAL SINGH, Rai Bahadur THAKUR, Barr., United Provs. Civil Service (judge, high ct., Allahabad) (b. 20th Sept., 1880).—Joined the service as offg. munisf. 1st Dec., 1905; confd., Nov., 1907; sub-judge, Jan., 1917; offg. dist. and sess. judge, Jan., 1930; confd., Apl., 1931; acting judge high ct., Allahabad, Nov., 1932; actg. adtl. ditto, Feb., 1933; and again, July, 1933; judge. ch. ct., Oudh, Jan., 1934; judge, high ct., Allahabad, July, 1934.

RACHPAL SINGH, Indian Police (dist. supt., Central Provs.) (b. 9th June, 1887).—Apptd. asst. dist. supt., Central Provs., 26th July, 1922; offg. dist. supt., May, 1924; confd., Jan., 1936.

RACKSTRAW, GEORGE, late Indian State Rlys.—Joined the service as asst. electrical engr., June, 1922; resdt. ditto, Jan., 1923; dist. electrical engr., April, 1923; offg. dep. loco. supt., E.B. Rly., Jan., 1928; ret'd., Aug., 1928.

RADHA KISHAN, BABU, Rai Bahadur, United Provs. C.S. (dist. and sess. judge) (b. 8th Mar., 1883).—Joined the service, 1st July, 1909; sub-judge, Jan., 1925; dist. and sess. judge, Nov., 1934; Rai Bahadur, May, 1937.

RADHA KISHEN, RAI BAHADUR LALA, M.A., late Punjab Civil Service (b. July, 1878).—Joined the service as proby. extra asst. commr., 7th Feb., 1904; confd., Nov., 1908; dep. commr., Jan., 1930; ret'd., July, 1933.

RADHA KRISHNA AIYAR, TARURAI SESHUIYAR, B.A., Indian Audit Dept. (b. 15th Feb., 1886).—Joined the service, 19th Oct., 1907; asst. act.-genl., posts and tels., Aug., 1925; ditto, United Provs., Oct., 1925; currency officer, Cawnpore, June, 1926; dep. acct.-genl., Madras, Feb., 1930; ditto, posts and tels., Madras, May, 1930; govt. exr. of accts. M. and S.M. and S.I. Rlys., May, 1936; dep. ch. auditor, E.I. rly., Jan., 1937; dep. acct.-genl., posts and tels., Calcutta, Jan., 1937.

RADHA KRISHNA GUPTA, LALA, B.A., Indian Service of Engrs. (exec. engr., Punjab) (b. 18th Feb., 1900).—Joined the service as apprentice engr., 30th Oct., 1923; asst. exec. engr., Nov., 1924; exec. engr., Oct., 1933.

RADHAKRISHNAN, KOMARALINGAM, B.A. Indian C.S. (asst. commr. Central Provinces) (b. 15th Oct., 1909).—Educ. at the P.S. High School, Mylapore, Madras, the Madras University, the School of Economics, London, and the School of Oriental Studies, London; joined the service, 6th Oct., 1933; arrived in India, Nov., 1933, and served in the Central Provinces as asst. commr.; under-secty. to govt. C.P., Jan., 1937.

RADHAKRISHNAN, SIR SARVEPALLI, Kt., M.A., late Indian Educl. Service (b. 5th Sept., 1888).—Apptd. princpl., govt. Victoria Coll., Polghat, 21st June, 1927; dist. educl. offcr., Apl., 1929; profrr. of philosophy, Univ. of Calcutta, until 28th Feb., 1931; vice-chancellor, Andhra Univ., May, 1931; knighted, June, 1935; ret'd., Aug., 1937.

RADICE, PETER WOLFE, Indian C.S. (jt. mag., United Provs.) (b. 18th June, 1908).—Educ. at Blundell's School, Tiverton, and Exeter College, Oxford; apptd. after examination of 1930; arrived in India, Nov., 1931, and served in the United Provinces as asst. mag. and collr.; jt. magte., Oct., 1933.

RAE, BERTRAM LANGFORD DENIS, Indian Police (b. 28th Sept., 1903).—Joined the service as asst. dist. supt., Burma, 12th Dec., 1924; offg. dist. supt., May, 1933.

RAE, CECIL DOUGLAS, C.I.E., O.B.E., India Posts and Tels. (b. 29th June, 1882).—Joined the service, 1st Dec., 1899; dep. postmaster, Calcutta, Aug., 1910; postmaster, Simla, July, 1915; ditto, Lahore, Feb., 1918; presdy. postmaster, Madras, Jan., 1919; ditto, Calcutta, May, 1921; O.B.E., June, 1927; offg. dep. dir.-genl., April, 1928; confd., Aug., 1928; P.M.G., United Provs., Apl., 1930; ditto, Madras, Feb., 1932; ditto, Bombay, Apl., 1933; C.I.E., Jan., 1936; senr. dep. dir.-genl., posts and tels., Mar., 1936.

RAE, CECIL JAMES, M. Eng., B.E., Indian Service of Engrs. (exec. engr., Bombay) (b. 23rd June, 1899).—Joined the service as asst. exec. engr., 1st Oct., 1923; offg. exec. engr., Feb., 1928; confd., Oct., 1931.

RAE, MAITLAND EASTON, Lieut.-Col., I.A., ret'd. (b. 16th Mar., 1880).—First commn. dated 17th Jan., 1900; in mil. employ until 9th March, 1907, when apptd. supy. asst. commr., D.I. Khan; asst. to resdt., Persian Gulf, May, 1908; asst. resdt. and H.B.M.'s consul at Bunder Abbas, May, 1909; pol. asst., Oghi, and commdt., B.M. Police, Hazara, Aug., 1911; asst. pol. agent, Chitral, May, 1912; city mag., Peshawar, July, 1913; dist. judge, ditto, July, 1914; city mag., ditto, Sept., 1914; temply.

reverted to mil. duty, Oct., 1914; 1st asst. to pol. resdt., Persian Gulf, Aug., 1915, to April, 1919; dep. commr., Kohat, April, 1920; sec. to chief commr., N.W.F. Prov., Mar., 1924; dep. commr., Hazara, Nov., 1927; rev. commr., N.W.F.P., March, 1929; dep. commr., Hazara, May, 1930; offg. rev. commr., N.W.F.P., Oct., 1931; offg. residt. and rev. and judl. commr., Baluchistan, May, 1932; retd., Sept., 1933.

RAFI, MIAN MUHAMMAD, B.A. (Oxon.), Barr. (secty., legislative assembly dept.) (b. 1st Sept., 1889).—Apptd. dep. secty., legisl. assembly dept., 1st Aug., 1931; secty., ditto, Sept., 1933.

RAGHAVA AYYANGAR, SRINIVASA, B.A., B.L., late Madras Civil Service (Judicial Branch) (b. 1st June, 1874).—Joined the service as dist. munsif, 1st Feb., 1902; sub-judge, March, 1919; dist. and sess. judge, Feb., 1928; retd., June, 1929.

RAGHAVACHARI, NEMAM ECHAMBADI SRINIVASA, B.A., Indian C.S. (asst. mag. and collr., Madras) (b. 10th June, 1912).—Educated at Town High School, Guntur, Andhra University, Madras University and School of Oriental Studies, London; joined the service, 17th Sept., 1936; arrived in India, October, 1936, and served in Madras as asst. mag. and collr.

RAGHAVAN, NARASINHA VIJAYA, B.A., late Indian Finance Dept. (b. 27th Mar., 1879).—Apptd. probr., fin. dept., 13th May, 1901; asst. acctnt.-gen., Madras, May, 1902; asst. compr., India treasuries, Sept., 1903; asst. acctnt.-gen., Madras, Dec., 1903; asst. compr.-gen., outside audits, Calcutta, June, 1904; asst. acctnt.-gen., Madras, Jan., 1905; dep. compr., P.O., Calcutta, March, 1908; cffg. compr., Central Prov., Sept., 1908; supt., check office, Indian tel. dept., Sept., 1909; asst. acctnt.-gen., Bengal, Oct., 1909; dep. compr., P.O., Dec., 1909; dep. acctnt.-gen., ditto, April, 1910; asst. acctnt.-gen., posts and telegraphs, Sept., 1910; dep. acctnt.-gen., ditto, Aug., 1911; addl. asst. sec., fin. dept., Nov., 1913; dep. acctnt.-gen., Burma, April, 1914; addl. asst. sec. to govt. of India, fin. dept., Nov., 1914; dep. acctnt.-gen., United Provs., May, 1915; dep. acctnt.-gen., posts and telegraphs, Delhi, Sept., 1917; ditto, Nagpur, Nov., 1918; ditto, Calcutta, Oct., 1922; offg. acctnt.-gen., central revs., April, 1924; offg. finl. adviser, posts and tels., Oct., 1924; acctnt.-gen., Bombay, May, 1925; ditto, Madras, March, 1928; retd., Jan., 1932.

RAGHAVAN, TANDALAM NARASIMHACHARI SRINIVASA, B.A., Indian C.S. (sub-collr., Madras) (b. 1st Oct., 1901).—Educ. at Madras Univ. and St. John's Coll., Cambridge; joined the service, 30th November, 1925, and served in Madras as asst. collr. and mag.; sub-collr., Aug., 1927; under-secty. to govt., rev. dept., Madras, July, 1931; offg. mag. and collr., Apl., 1936; secty. to commr. of excise, Madras, July, 1936.

RAGHAVAYYA, THODLA, Diwan Bahadar, C.S.I., B.A., late Madras Civil Service.—Joined the service, 19th July, 1893, as dep. collr.; rev. officer, corporation of Madras, Jan., 1909; asst. sec. to board of rev., March, 1912; sec. to commr. of rev. settlt., survey, &c., Dec., 1913; collr. and dist. mag., Oct., 1918; on foreign

service as Diwan of Travancore, July, 1920; C.S.I., Jan., 1924; offg. third member, board of rev., May, 1925; spl. sec. to govt., development dept., Oct., 1926; offg. sec., law and educn. dept., Feb., 1927; chairman, Malabar tenancy comee., Aug., 1927; retd., July, 1928.

RAGHAVENDRA RAO, E., Barr., Apptd. member, govr's exec. council, Central Provs., 23rd Jan., 1931, after officiating from 8th Oct., 1930; actg. govr., Central Provs., May to Sept., 1936.

RAGHAVENDRA RAO, PATTADAKAL VENKANNA, M.Sc., Indian C.S. (asst. collr., Bombay) (b. 19th Sept., 1909).—Educ. at Maharaja's High School, Mysore, Universities of Mysore and Calcutta, and the School of Oriental Studies, London; joined the service, 6th Oct., 1933; arrived in India, Nov., 1933, and served in Bombay as asst. collr.

RAGHAVENDRA RAU, SIR PANAMBUR, M.A., Indian Finance dept. (b. 24th May, 1889).—Joined the service, 1st June, 1912; asst. acctnt.-gen., Nov., 1912; asst. contr. of currency, March, 1919; dep. ditto, April, 1920; asst. sec. to govt. of India, finance dept., April, 1921; under-sec. to ditto, Jan., 1922; dep. dir. of finance, rly. board, Aug., 1925; offg. dir. of finance, rly. board, March, 1926; confd., Oct., 1928; offg. finl. commr., March, 1929; confd., Apl., 1932; chairman, standing fin. cttee. for rlys., May, 1936. knighted, Feb., 1937; acct.-genl., Bombay, Aug., 1937.

RAGHUBAR DAYAL SHUKUL, PANDIT, B.A., LL.B., late United Provs. Civil Service (b. 15th Oct., 1878).—Joined the service as offg. munsif, 3rd Feb., 1903; confd., Feb., 1904; sub. judge, Oct., 1914; addl. dist. and sess. judge, Aug., 1927; confd., Oct., 1927; retd., Oct., 1933.

RAGHUNATH PRASAD, RAI BHADUR BABU, B.A., LL.B. (b. Nov., 1876).—Joined the service, 23rd May, 1905; sub. judge, Oct., 1917; offg. dist. and sess. judge, March, 1926; confd., March, 1929; retd., Nov., 1931.

RAGHUNATH RAI, HANNA, Lala, Indian Service of Engrs. (exec. engr., Punjab) (b. 9th Nov., 1899).—Joined the service, 11th Oct., 1922; asst. exec. engr., Sept., 1923; offg. exec. engr., Apl., 1932; confd., Oct., 1932; offg. under secty. local self govt. dept., Punjab Jan., 1933.

RAGHUNATHA RAO, S., B.A., Barr., Indian Forest Service (dep. consvr., Madras) (b. 10th May, 1899).—Joined the service as asst. consvr., 14th Feb., 1924; dep. consvr., Feb., 1928.

RAHA, HEMANTA KUMAR, C.I.E., M.A., Rai Bahadur, late India Posts and Telegraph Dept.—Joined the service, 22nd Aug., 1895, as supt.; dep. postmr.-gen., May, 1915; asst. dir.-gen., post office, Oct., 1918; dep. dir.-gen., Calcutta, May, 1922; C.I.E., July, 1926; retd., Sept., 1928.

RAHA, SHARAT KUMAR, RAI BHADUR, late Excise and Salt Dept., Bengal (b. 3rd Jan., 1877).—Apptd. supt. of excise and salt, June, 1915; dep. commr., ditto, Feb., 1921; offg. commr., ditto, April, 1926; confd., Apl., 1929; retd., Jan., 1932.

RAHIM BAKHSH, KHAN BAHADUR, SHAIKH, O.B.E., M.A., late Punjab Civil Service (b. 21st Sept., 1874).—Joined the service, 27th Feb., 1899, as extra asst. commr.; sub-judge, Oct., 1909; offg. dist. and sess. judge, July, 1922; confd., Oct., 1926; ret'd. Sept., 1929; O.B.E., June, 1930.

RAHIM, JALLALUDDIN ABDUR, Indian C.S. (asst. collr. of Customs, Calcutta) (b. 27th July, 1906).—Educ. at Trinity College, Cambridge and Munich University; joined the service, 19th Oct., 1931; arrived in India, Dec., 1931, and served in Madras as asst. mag. and collr.; sub-collr., Mar., 1935; asst. collr. of customs, Calcutta, Nov., 1936.

RAHIM, KHWAJA ABDUR, B.A., Indian C.S. (asst. commr., Punjab) (b. 14th Sept., 1908).—Educated at Punjab University and Peterhouse, Cambridge; joined the service, 12th Oct., 1932; arrived in India, Dec., 1932, and served in the Punjab as asst. commr.; name changed to Abdur Rahim (q.v.).

RAHIM, MUHAMMAD INAMUR, B.A., Indian C.S. (dep. commr., Central Provs.) (b. 1st Jan., 1908).—Educ. at Morris Coll., Nagpur, and Pembroke Coll., Cambridge; apptd. by nomination in India; joined the service, 12th Dec., 1921; arrived, 6th Jan., 1922, and served in Central Provs. as asst. commr.; offg. dep. commr., variously from March, 1927; under-sec., govt. of India, dept. of eductn., &c., June, 1927, to Oct., 1930; dep. commr. (confd.) Mar., 1937.

RAHIMTOOLA, FAZAL IBRAHIM, Commerce dept., Govt. of India.—Apptd. member, tariff board, 2nd Nov., 1930; and again, Oct., 1934; offg. presdt., ditto, June, 1935; member, spl. tariff bd., Oct., 1935.

RAHIMTULLA, SIR IBRAHIM, G.B.E., K.C.S.I., C.I.E.—Member of govt.'s exec. council, Bombay, from 1st March, 1918, to 17th March, 1923; K.C.S.I., June, 1923; presdt., Bombay legislative council, 1923; member of R.C. on Labour in India, July, 1929; presdt., legisl. assembly, Jan., 1931; G.B.E., June, 1935.

RAHIMUDDIN AHMAD, B.A., Indian Police (supt., Assam) (b. 22nd Oct., 1884).—Joined the service, 16th Sept., 1909; supt., July, 1925.

RAHMAN, A.F., B.A. (Oxon.), L.L.D., (member, federal pub. services commission).—Prof. of history, M.A.O. Coll., Aligarh, and later Muslim Univ., Aligarh, 1914-1921; Provost, Muslim Hall, Univ. of Dacca, 1921-27; secty., Aligarh Univ. Enquiry Ctte., 1927; member, Punjab Univ. Enquiry Ctte., 1932-1933; vice-chancellor, Univ. of Dacca, and chairman, Bd. of Inter. and Secondary Educ., Dacca, 1934-1936; member, Federal Public Service, Commn., 1937.

RAHMAN, ABDUR (see Abdur Rahman).

RAHMAN, ABUL FAẒE MUJIBOR (see Abul FaẒe Mujibor Rahman).

RAHMAN, ATAUR, Khan Bahadur (see Ataur Rahman).

RAHMAN, AZIZUR (see Azizur Rahman).

RAHMAN, MOWDUDUR (see Mowdudur Rahman).

RAHMAN, MUHAMMAD ABDUR, Lt.-Col. I.M.S. ret'd. (b. 22nd Oct., 1881).—First commd. dated 31st July, 1909; in mil. employ to 5th Aug., 1921; civil surg. and offg. prinpl., medl. school, Agra, Aug., 1921; ret'd., Oct., 1936.

RAHMAN, MUHAMMAD EHSANUR (see Muhammad E. R.).

RAHMAN, SHAIKH ABDUR, M.A., Indian C.S. (asst. commr., Punjab) (b. 4th June, 1903).—Educ. at University of the Punjab and Exeter Coll., Oxford; joined the service, 29th Sept., 1928; arrived, Oct., 1928, and served in the Punjab as asst. commr.; judge, small cause ct., Apl., 1933; offg. dist. and sess. judge, Apl., 1935.

RAHMAT-ULLAH, MUHAMMAD, Shaikh, Khan Bahadur, late Indian Forest Service (b. 6th May, 1877).—Joined the service, 1st April, 1900; I.F.S., Mar., 1924; dep. consvr., March, 1925; ret'd., May, 1932.

RAHMATULLAH, SHAHABUDDIN, B.A., Indian C.S. (asst. mag. and collr., Bengal). (b. 9th June, 1913).—Educ. at Arrah Zillah Sch., Arrah, and Patna Univ.; joined the Service 1st Dec., 1937; arrived in India, Dec., 1937, and served in Bengal as asst. mag. and collr.

RAI, AJILIDAY NARAYANA (see Narayana Rai, A.).

RAI, DIWAN GANPAT, Lt.-Col., I.M.S. (ret'd.) (b. 11th Nov., 1875).—Joined the service, 29th Jan., 1902; civil surg., Madras, Dec., 1923; ret'd., Nov., 1930.

RAI, GANPAT, LALA (see Ganpat Rai, L.).

RAI, HAKIM (see Hakim Rai).

RAI, HAKUMAT (see Hakumat R.).

RAI, INDARJIT (see Indarjit Rai).

RAI, LAKHPAT (see Lakhpat Rai).

RAI, MANGAT (see Mangat Rai).

RAI, NAUBAT (see Naubat Rai).

RAIKES, BERTIE, late Public Works Dept., Burma.—Apptd. electrical inspr. to govt., 17th July, 1905; services placed at disposal of govt. of India for employment under Indian munitions bd., Feb. to May, 1918; dep. controller of munitions, Burma, May, 1918, to April, 1919; on special duty in connection with Rangoon water supply scheme, from 25th Oct., 1926; ret'd., Feb., 1929.

RAINY, SIR GEORGE, K.C.S.I., K.C.I.E., B.A., late Indian C.S. (b. 11th Feb., 1875).—Educ. at Edinburgh Academy, and Merton Coll., Oxford; apptd. after exam. of 1898; arrived, 23rd Nov., 1899, and served in Bengal as asst. mag. and collr.; under-sec. to govt., finl. and muncl. depts., July, 1904; under-sec. to Govt. of India dept. of commerce and industry, March, 1906; jt. mag. and dep. collr., Bengal, August, 1909; transf. to Bihar and Orissa, April, 1912; mag. and collr., April, 1912; offg. sec. to govt., fin. and muncl. depts., March, 1914; finl. member, Imperial Delhi Committee, April, 1914; on spl. duty in Bihar and Orissa sectt., May, 1916; offg. dep. sec. to govt. of India, fin. dept., July, 1916; confirmed, May, 1917; C.I.E., June, 1918; spl. duty as representative of govt. of India with reforms ctte., Nov., 1918; offg. chief sec. to govt., Bihar and Orissa, April, 1919; confd., Oct., 1919; C.S.I., Jan. 1921 on depn. to govt. of

India, dept. of commerce, June, 1923; president, tariff board, 5th July, 1923; offg. sec. to govt. of India, dept. of commerce; K.C.I.E., Jan., 1925; member, gov.-genl.'s council, April, 1927; K.C.S.I., June, 1930; delegate of India to monetary and economic conf., London, June, 1933; retd., Feb., 1935.

RAISMAN, ABRAHAM JEREMY, C.S.I., C.I.E., B.A., Indian C.S. (member, central bd. of rev.)
