(b. 19th Mar., 1892).—Educ. at Leeds Central High Sch., and Pembroke Coll., Oxford; apptd. after examination of 1915; arrived, 5th Dec., 1916, and served in Bihar and Orissa as asst. mag. and collr.; under-sec. to govt., finl. dept., Oct., 1920; asst. collr. of customs, Bombay, Oct., 1922; ditto, Calcutta, June, 1926; commr. of inc. tax, Punjab and N.W.F. Provs., Feb., 1928, and again, Sept., 1929; on spl. duty, central bd. of rev., Aug., 1929; temp. dep. secty., govt. of India, commerce dept., Feb., 1931; offg. jt. secty., ditto, May, 1931; confd., Mar., 1932; C.I.E., Jan., 1934; offg. member, central bd. of rev., June, 1934; confd., Jan., 1935, addl. secty. to govt. of India, fin. dept. (temp.), June, 1936; offg. secty., ditto, Apl., 1937; Director, Reserve Bank of India, Oct., 1937; C.S.I., Jan., 1938.

RAJ BAHADUR HUKKOO, late Indian Service of Engrs. (b. 27th Aug., 1889).—Joined the service as temp. engr., 23rd March, 1914; asst. engr. (provl. service), June, 1919; asst. exec. engr., April, 1921; offg. exec. engr., May, 1926; confd., June, 1928; retd., June, 1934.

RAJ NARAYAN, Rai Bahadur, M.B.E., B.A., M.I.E.(Ind.), late Indian Service of Engrs. (b. 4th Nov., 1879).—Trained at T.C.E. Coll., and apptd. in India; asst. engr., Sept., 1903; exec. engr., Sept., 1911; M.B.E., June, 1919; suptg. engr., June, 1921; retd., Nov., 1934.

RAJ NARAYAN BRAHMAWAR, B.A., B.Sc., Indian Forest Service (dep. consvr. United Provs.) (b. 2nd Aug., 1898).—Joined the service as asst. consvr., 9th Nov., 1923; divl. forest officer, April, 1926; dep. consvr., Oct., 1931.

RAJ NARAYAN SINGH, B.A., B.Sc., Indian Forest Service (dep. consvr., United Provs.) (b. 15th Jan., 1899).—Joined the service as asst. consvr., 9th Nov., 1923; dep. consvr., May, 1926.

RAJA AYYAR, ABHIRAMAPURAM KRISHNAMURTI, B.A., M.B.E., Diwan Bahadur, Indian Police (dist. supt., Madras) (b. Dec. 1881).—Joined the service, 17th Feb., 1908; dep. supt., July, 1910; awarded King's police medal; M.B.E., June, 1925; supt., Sept., 1926; dist. supt., govt. rly. police, Madras, June, 1930; hon. A.D.C. to govr., Sept., 1932.

RAJADHYAKSHA, GANPAT SAKHARAM, M.A., Barr., Indian C.S. (dist. and sess. judge, Bombay) (b. 9th Sept., 1896).—Educ. at Bombay Univ. and St. Catherine's Coll., Cambridge; joined the service, 28th Oct., 1920; arrived, 18th Jan., 1921, and served in Bihar and Orissa as asst. magte. and collr.; transf. to Bombay, Aug., 1921; doing duty as asst. judge, April, 1924; offg. judge and sess. judge, June, 1926; offg. dep. secty. to govt., legal dept., Jan., 1928; confd., May, 1929; jt. secty., Indian round

table conf., London, Nov., 1930-Jan., 1931; offg. secty. to govt., legal dept., Bombay, July, 1935 and again Apl., 1936.

RAJAGOPAL, POORAEETI NADAMUNI, M.A., Indian Audit Dept. (b. 19th Apl., 1896).—Joined the service, 2nd Mar., 1920; asst. acct.-genl., Madras, Jan., 1922; currency officer, Madras, May, 1924; exr., local fund accts., Punjab, June, 1928; dep. acct.-genl., Bombay, Apl., 1931; currency officer, Bombay, Sept., 1931; asst. dir., army audit, northern cmd., Jan., 1932; dep. acct.-genl., posts and tels., Nagpur, Apl., 1935, and again, Mar., 1937; offg. dep. dir.-genl., fin., posts and tels., June, 1936; attd. officer, office of comp., Orissa, Nov., 1937.

RAJAGOPALA AYYANGAR NARASIMHA AYYANGAR (see Narasimha Ayyangar, R. A.).

RAJAGOPALA AYYAR, R., Income-tax dept., Madras (asst. commr. of inc. tax) (b. 1st July, 1888).—Joined the service as income tax officer, 5th Apl., 1922; offg. asst. commr., Mar., 1925; confd., Nov., 1929; asst. commr., northern range, Bezwada, Aug., 1931; ditto, southern range, Madras, Dec., 1933.

RAJAGOPALA THAMBAN, VENGANAD KOVILAGAM, Indian Police (b. 10th Oct., 1891) (dist. supt., Madras).—Joined the service as asst. supt., 10th Oct., 1918; offg. supt., Feb., 1924; confd., Aug., 1930; awarded Indian police medal, Jan., 1936.

RAJAGOPALAN, KUMMAYA MANAKADAN, B.A., Indian C.S. (asst. mag. and collr., Madras) (b. 26th Sept., 1912).—Educ. at Christian College Sch., Georgetown, Madras, and Madras Univ.; joined the service, 22nd Sept., 1937; arrived in India, Oct., 1937, and served in Madras as asst. mag. and collr.

RAJAGOPALAN, PUNDI, B.A., Indian C.S. (dist. and sess. judge, Madras) (b. 29th April, 1901).—Educ. at Madras Univ. and Queen's Coll., Cambridge; joined the service, 24th Oct., 1924; arrived, 15th Nov., 1924, and served in Madras as asst. collr. and mag.; sub-collr., Feb., 1926; offg. dist. and sess. judge, June, 1933; confd., July, 1936.

RAJAGOPALAM, TANJORE KRISHNA, B.A., Indian Finance Dept. (b. 10th Oct., 1885).—Joined the service, 10th Feb., 1906, as probr., finance dept.; asst. comptr.-gen., Delhi, Oct., 1916; dep. acct.-gen., Madras, April, 1920; ditto, Bombay, March, 1922; on special duty in finl. dept., govt. of India, from Nov., 1923; offg. ch. auditor, G.I.P. Rly., May, 1930; ditto, and govt. examr. of accts., B.B. and C.I. Rly., May, 1931; ditto, G.I.P. rly., Apl., 1932; offg. ch. accts. officer, G.I.P. rly., Sept., 1933; dir. of rly. audit, Apl., 1934; acct.-genl., Madras, Oct., 1934; acct.-genl., Central Revs., Apl., 1936.

RAJAN, ARUNACHALA TYAGA, M.A., Indian C.S. (dist. and sess. judge, Burma) (b. 6th Oct., 1883).—Educ. at Amalapuram High Sch., Madras Univ., and Trinity Coll., Cambridge; apptd. after exam. of 1906; arrived, 1st Jan., 1908, and served in Burma as asst. commr., dist. judge, July, 1921; dist. and sess. judge, Dec., 1922.

RAJAN, SRINIVASA, B.A., Indian C.S. (asst. commr., Central Provs.) (b. 10th Jan., 1911).—Educated at American College, Madura, University of Madras and the School of Oriental Studies, London; joined the service 10th Oct., 1934; arrived in India, Nov., 1934, and served in the Central Provinces as asst. commr.

RAJAN, VISWANATHA NATA, M.Sc., B.A., Indian C.S. (jt. mag. and dep. collr., Bengal) (b. 22nd Oct., 1907).—Educ. at the School of Economics and School of Oriental Studies, London; joined the service, 12th Oct., 1931; arrived in India, Nov., 1931, and served in Bengal as asst. mag. and collr.; jt. mag. and dep. collr., Nov., 1932.

RAJESHWAR DAYAL, M.A., Indian C.S. (jt. mag. and collr., United Provinces) (b. 12th Aug., 1909).—Educ. at Kayastha Pathahala Intermediate College, Allahabad, Allahabad University and New College, Oxford; joined the service, 7th Oct., 1933; arrived in India, Nov., 1933, and served in the United Provinces as asst. mag. and collr.; jt. magte., Oct., 1936.

RAJU, MANTHENA TIMMA, B.A., Indian C.S. (asst. mag. and collr., Madras) (b. 5th Oct., 1911).—Educated at P.S. High School, Madras, Govt. Model Secondary School, Benares Hindu University, University College, London, and the School of Oriental Studies, London; joined the service, 21st Sept., 1936; arrived in India, Oct., 1936, and served in Madras as asst. mag. and collr.

RAJU, VADREVVU BHADIR, B.A., Indian C.S. (asst. collr., Bombay) (b. 28th Oct., 1907).—Educated at Madras University and School of Oriental Studies, London; joined the service, 6th Oct., 1932; arrived in India, Nov., 1932, and served in Bombay as asst. collr.

RAJU, PUTTAYYA R. V. (see Venkataramana Raju, P. R.)

RAJWADE, LAKSHMANRAO GANPATRAO, B.A., Indian C.S. (asst. collr., Bombay) (b. 14th Sept., 1912).—Educ. at V.C. High Sch., Gwalior, and Bombay Univ.; joined the service, 20th Sept., 1937; arrived in India, Nov., 1937, and served in Bombay as asst. collr.

RAKE, BEAVEN CRAWFORD, late Indian Police (b. 18th April, 1885).—Joined the service as asst. supt., Nov., 1905; dist. supt., Dec., 1914; dep. inspr.-gen., Feb., 1924; retd., June, 1934.

RAKHA, RAM (see Ram Rakha).

RAM, AMOLAK (see Amolak Ram).

RAM, ATMA (see Atma Ram).

RAM, BASHI (see Bashi Ram).

RAM BATALIA (see Batalia, R.).

RAM CHAND SAKSENA, BABU, B.A., LL.B., late United Provs. Civil Service (b. 9th March, 1875).—Joined the service, 29th May, 1902; sub. judge, March, 1915; dist. and sess. judge, March, 1926; retd., Mar., 1930.

RAM CHANDRA, B.A., A.M.I.E. (Ind.), late Indian Service of Engrs. (b. 13th June, 1890).—Joined the service, Sept., 1913; asst. engr., Sept., 1914; asst. exec. engr., Jan., 1920; exec. engr., Oct., 1923; retd., Apl., 1935.

RAM CHANDRA, C.I.E., M.B.E., M.A., Indian C.S. (secty. to govt. fin. dept., Punjab) (b. 1st Mar., 1889).—Educ. at Central Model School, Lahore, Punjab, and Trinity Coll., Cambridge; apptd. after exam. of 1912; arrived, 17th Nov., 1913, and served in the Punjab as asst. commr.; coln. officer, Upper Chenab Canal, Nov., 1915, to Jan., 1917; M.B.E., June, 1919; temp. under-sec. to govt., Punjab, June, 1919; dep. commr. (prov.), Dec., 1925; sec. to govt., transferred depts., Punjab, Jan., 1926; dep. secty. to govt. of India, educn. dept., May, 1928; offg. jt. secty., ditto, Aug., 1930; confd., Oct., 1932; C.I.E., Jan., 1933; offg. secty. to the Govt. of India, educn. dept., Sept., 1935; secty., fin. dept., Punjab, Jan., 1936.

RAMCHANDRA KESHAV BAL (see Bal, R. K.).

RAM CHANDRA SAHAI, KUNWAR, B.Sc., Indian Service of Engrs. (exec. engr., United Provs.) (b. 19th March, 1900).—Joined the service, 15th Oct., 1923; apprentice engr., May, 1924; asst. exec. engr., Oct., 1924; offg. exec. engr., July, 1927; confd., Oct., 1933.

RAM DAS, RAI SAHIB LALA, late Indian Service of Engrs. (Punjab) (b. 7th Nov., 1876).—Joined the service, 9th Aug., 1899; asst. exec. engr., Jan., 1920; on deputation to Bahawalpur State from 9th Dec., 1921; exec. engr., Sept., 1926; retd., Nov., 1931.

RAM, GITA (see Gita, R.).

RAM GOPAL, M.Sc., LL.B., Indian Audit Dept. (b. Dec., 1894).—Joined the service, 21st Mar., 1921; dep. acct.-genl., U.P., Oct., 1936.

RAM, JAI (see Jai Ram).

RAM KISHAN, RAI SAHIB, Indian State Rlys. (dep. ch. engr.) (b. 1st May, 1884).—Joined the service as asst. engr., 1st Oct., 1908; exec. engr., Oct., 1916; divl. engr., O. and R. Rly., April, 1925; on loan to Baroda durbar; dep. ch. engr., July, 1934.

RAM LABHU (see Labhu, R.)

RAM LAL, BATRA, Lala, Indian Service of Engrs. (exec. engr., Punjab) (b. 20th July, 1884).—Joined the service, 11th Sept., 1907; asst. engr., Sept., 1908; exec. engr., Oct., 1918; offg. supt. engr., Apl., 1933.

RAM LAL, DIWAN, B.A., Barr., Judl. Dept., Punjab (advocate genl.).—Apptd. offg. asst. legal remembrancer 17th May, 1925; asst. legal remembrancer (conveyancing), Feb., 1930; admr. genl. and offl. trustee, June, 1931; govt. advocate, Oct., 1933; offg. secty. to govt., Punjab, legisl. dept., Oct., 1936; advocate-genl., Apl., 1937.

RAM LAL, KHANNA, B.A., Indian Service of Engrs. (asst. exec. engr., Punjab) (b. 11th Dec., 1905).—Joined the service as apprentice engr., 16th Jan., 1929; asst. exec. engr., Jan., 1930.

RAM LAL, LALA RAI BAHADUR, M.B.E., Dept. of Industries, Punjab (dir. of industries) (b. 18th Aug., 1888).—Held non-gazetted appts. from 2nd May, 1912; extra asst. commr., Nov., 1918; M.B.E., Jan., 1921; under-secty. to govt., rev. dept., Punjab, Jan., 1927; dir. of industries, Aug., 1930.

RAM LAL SETHI (see Sethi, R. L.).

RAM LAL, SONDHI, B.A., Indian Service of Engrs. (asst. to consulting engr., p.w. bch., Govt. of India) (b. 17th Oct., 1895).—Apptd. asst. exec. engr., Burma, 10th Feb., 1921; transferred to Punjab, July, 1929; exec. engr., Feb., 1930; asst. to consulting engr., dept. of indus. and lab., Govt. of India (p.w. bch.), Jan., 1935.

RAM, MOTI (see Moti Ram).

RAM NATH, RAI BAHADUR PANDIT, B.A., (second secty., central bd. of rev.) (b. 14th Sept., 1883).—Joined the service, 20th Oct., 1904; secty., central bd. of rev., June, 1931; second secty., ditto, Nov., 1934.

RAM PRASAD, BURMAN, LALA, B.Sc., Indian Service of Engrs. (exec. engr., Punjab) (b. 18th Jan., 1900).—Joined the service, 22nd Oct., 1923; asst. exec. engr., May, 1925; exec. engr., Apl., 1935.

RAM PRATAP, VASISHTH, Lala, Indian Service of Engrs. (exec. engr., Punjab) (b. 21st Oct., 1899).—Joined the service as apprentice engr., 25th Oct., 1923; asst. exec. engr., Oct., 1924; exec. engr., Oct., 1933.

RAM, RALA (see Rala Ram).

RAM RAKHA, RAI BAHADUR LALA, Indian Service of Engrs. (supvtg. engr., Punjab) (b. 10th June, 1883).—Joined the service, 17th Sept., 1906; asst. engr., Sept., 1907; exec. engr., Oct., 1915; offg. supvtg. engr., July, 1930; confd., July, 1935.

RAM, SADHU (see Sadhu Ram).

RAM, SALIG (see Salig Ram).

RAM, SANT (see Sant Ram).

RAM, SHEO (see Sheo Ram).

RAM SAHGAL, BASHI (see Bashi Ram, S.).

RAM SINGH, SARDAR BAHADUR, Indian State Rlys. (exec. engr., E.B. Rly.) (b. 17th Dec., 1886).—Joined the service, 16th Sept., 1910; asst., engr., Sept., 1911; exec. engr., April, 1921; offg. dep. ch. engr., variously from Sept., 1929.

RAM SINGH, THAKUR, B.Sc., Income Tax Dept., United Provs. (asst. commr. of inc. tax) (b. 4th Feb., 1889).—Joined the service, 16th Mar., 1920; asst. commr. of inc. tax, Benares, Oct., 1929.

RAMA AYYAR, KOLLINGODE, RANGA IYER, M.A., Indian Audit Dept. (b. 15th Jan., 1896).—Joined the service, 28th Feb., 1920; asst. acct.-genl., Burma, Nov., 1921; asst. dir. of audit, United Provs., Apl., 1926; dep. dir., ditto, Sept., 1926; dep. acct.-genl., Bengal, June, 1929; ditto, Madras, Nov., 1932; ditto, posts and tels., Madras, May, 1935; ditto, Bombay, Apl., 1937.

RAMA PAI, KRISHNA, M.A., Patents and Designs (contr.) (b. 15th Jan., 1893).—Joined the service as proby. asst. examr., 3rd Sept., 1920; confd., Sept., 1922; contr., Feb., 1926.

RAMA RAO, UDYAVAR, Rai Bahadur, late Madras Civil Service (b. 9th July, 1877).—Joined the service as dep. collr., 11th April, 1900; collr. and dist. magte., Sept., 1928; secty. to govt., law and educn. dept., July, 1930; retd., Aug., 1932.

RAMA RAU, BENEGAL, C.I.E., Indian C.S. (dep. high commr. for India) (b. 10th Jan., 1889).—Educ. at Madras Univ. and King's Coll., Cambridge; apptd. after exam. of 1912; arrived, 17th Nov., 1913, and served in Madras as asst. collr. and mag.; sub-collr., Nov., 1917; sub-collr. & jt. mag., Dec., 1918; offg. under-sec. to govt., development dept., July, 1920; ditto, local self-govt. dept., Sept., 1920; confd., July, 1921; tempy. dep. sec. to govt., local self-govt., Oct., 1922; offg. dep. sec. to govt., finl. dept., April, 1926; offg. dep. sec. to govt. of India, finance dept., June, 1926; confd., March, 1927; C.I.E., June, 1930; on depn. to Indian Statutory Comm., 1929-30; offg. jt. secty., dept. of indus. and labour, govt. of India, Oct., 1930; jt. secty., British Indian delegation to Indian round table conf., 2nd session, Sept.-Dec., 1931; 3rd session, Nov.-Dec., 1932; collr., Sept., 1932; secty. to British Indian delegation to jt. ctee. on Indian Constitutional Reform, May-November, 1933; dep. high commr. for India, Dec., 1934.

RAMA REDDI, PATTIPATE HANIMIREDDI-GARI, M.A., B.Sc., Indian Agric. Service (b. 15th Feb., 1888). (vice-prinpl., agricl. coll., Coimbatore).—Apptd. dep. dir. of agric., Madras, 17th Jan., 1919; vice-prinpl., agricl. coll., Coimbatore, June, 1928; offg. prinpl., ditto, Sept., 1929; secty., Indian Central cotton ctee., Dec., 1932.

RAMABHADRAN, TIRUVALYANGUDI, B.A., Indian C.S., (jt. mag. and collr., United Provs.) (b. 25th Aug., 1910).—Educated at Hindu High School, Triplicane, Delhi University and Balliol College, Oxford; joined the service, 9th Oct., 1934; arrived in India, Nov., 1934, and served in the United Provinces as asst. mag. and collr.; jt. magte., Oct., 1936.

RAMACHANDRA AYYAR, VENKATARAMA, Rao Bahadur, M.A., Indian Police (supl., Madras) (b. 13th Jan., 1883).—Joined the service, 4th Mar., 1907; dep. supt., Apl., 1909; supt., Apl., 1925; Rao Bahadur, June, 1932.

RAMACHANDRAN, THIAGARAJA SADASIVA, Indian C.S. (sub. collr. and jt. magte., Madras) (b. 12th Aug., 1902).—Educ. at Univ. of Madras, London School of Economics, and Balliol Coll., Oxford; joined the service, 19th Oct., 1926; arrived, Nov., 1926, and served in Madras as asst. collr. and magte.; sub. collr. and jt. magte., July, 1928; offg. collr. and dist. magte., June, 1930; offg. dist. and sess. judge, Apl., 1936.

RAMACHANDRAN, TRICHINOPOLY LAK-MANAPERUMAL (see Chandran, T. L. R.).

RAMADHYANI, RANGASWAMY, KALKUNTE, B.Sc., Indian C.S. (asst. commr., Central Provs.) (b. 20th Feb., 1908).—Educ. at Mysore University, University College, London, and London School of Oriental Studies; joined the service, 10th Oct., 1930; arrived in India, Nov., 1930; and served in the Central Provs. as asst., commr.; offg. dep. commr., Sept., 1934.

RAMAKRISHNA AYYAR, PERUMALAY-YAR, Indian C.S. (asst. mag. and collr., Madras) (b. 5th Feb., 1908).—Educated at Madras University and Sidney Sussex College, Cambridge; joined the service, 10th Oct., 1932; arrived in India, Nov., 1932, and served in Madras, as asst. mag. and collr.

RAMAKRISHNAN, CHITTUR ANANTA-KRISHNA, M.A., B.Sc., Indian C.S. (sub collr., Madras) (b. 9th Nov., 1910).—Educ. at P.S. High School, Mylapore, University of Madras, University College, London and Trinity College, Dublin; joined the service, 10th Oct., 1934; arrived in India, Nov., 1934, and served in Madras as asst. mag. and collr.; sub collr., Jan., 1937.

RAMAKRISHNA, VELGAPUDI, M.A., B.Sc., Indian C.S. (sub-collr., Madras) (b. 4th April, 1896).—Educ. at Edinburgh Univ.; joined the service, 12th Dec., 1921; arrived 10th Jan., 1922, and served in Madras as asst. collr. and mag.; sub-collr. and jt. mag., Jan., 1923; offg. under-sec. to govt., rev. dept., Nov., 1924; offg. collr. and dist. mag. and sec. to govt., rev. dept., June, 1925; dewan to Maharaja of Jeypore, Nov., 1925; dir. of industries, Madras, Nov., 1931; offg. dep. secty., dept. of indus. and lab., Govt. of India, June, 1935.

RAMALINGAM, ALAMPADI SUBBARAYA, B.Sc., Indian Service of Engrs. (exec. engr., Madras) (b. 26th Aug., 1891).—Joined the service as asst. engr., 6th Feb., 1919; exec. engr., Oct., 1924.

RAMALINGAM, PONNAMBALAM, Indian C.S. (dist. and sess. judge, Madras) (b. 1st July, 1897).—Educ. at Royal Coll., Colombo, and Christ Church, Oxford; joined the service, 3rd Nov., 1921; arrived, 3rd Jan., 1922, and served in Madras as asst. collr. and mag.; sub-collr. and jt. mag., July, 1923; offg. dist. and sess. judge, June, 1931, and again, Mar., 1933; addl. dist. and sess. judge, Feb., 1932; dist. and sess. judge, Apl., 1935.

RAMAMURTI, SONTI VENKATA, B.A., Indian C.S. (collr. and dist. magte., Madras) (b. 1st Aug., 1888).—Educ. at London Mission High Sch., Vizagapatam, Madras Univ. and Trinity Coll., Cambridge; apptd. after exam. of 1911; arrived, 23rd Nov., 1912, and served in Madras as asst. collr. and mag.; sub. collr. and jt. mag., Oct., 1917; offg. collr. and dist. mag., April, 1924; confd., Aug., 1930; offg. secty. to govt. dev. dept., July, 1929; dir. of agriculture, Madras, Feb., 1932.

RAMAN, A. SUNDARA, Local Fund Audit Dept., Burma (asst. exr.) (b. 14th Aug., 1890).—Joined the service, 26th Nov., 1913; asst. exr., Dec., 1933.

RAMAN, AGNESWARA VENKATA, Public Works dept., Madras (dep. sany. engr.) (b. 12th Sept., 1891).—Joined the service as offg. asst. sany. engr., 22nd Jan., 1921; confd., Mar., 1922; dep. sany. engr., Apl., 1927.

RAMAN, KODAGANALLUR SRINIVASA VENKATA, Indian C.S. (jt. mag. and dep. collr., Orissa) (b. 15th Dec., 1907).—Educ. at Madras University and School of Oriental Studies, London; joined the service, 7th Oct., 1932; arrived in India, Nov., 1932, and served in Bihar and Orissa as asst. mag. and collr.; under-secty., govt. of Orissa, home and rev. dept., Mar., 1937.

RAMAN, KRISHNASWAMI, B.Sc., Indian C.S. (asst. mag. and collr., Orissa) (b. 28th Oct., 1911).—Educated at Sidney Sussex College, Cambridge; joined the service, 21st Sept., 1936; arrived in India, Oct., 1936, and served in Orissa as asst. mag. and collr.

RAMANA RAO, KOTY VENKATA, Major, I.M.S. (D.M.O., Madras) (b. 14th Jan., 1888).—First commn. dated 31st Jan., 1914; in milty. employ until 10th July, 1925; offg. variously in civil appts., from July, 1925 to June, 1929; D.M.O., June, 1929.

RAMANAN, COIMBATORE SUBRAHMANYA VENKATA, M.B., Lt.-Col., I.M.S., Jail Dept., Madras (supt.).—First commn., 11th Jan., 1921; in milty. employ to 22nd June, 1931; jail supt., Madras, July, 1933.

RAMANATHAN, KALPATHI RAMA KRISHNA, M.A., D.Sc., Meteorological Dept. (meteorologist) (b. 28th Feb., 1893).—Joined the service as temp. meteorologist, 2nd Oct., 1925; meteorologist on probn., Apl., 1926; confd., Apl., 1927; offg. dir., Kodaikanal observatory, Mar., 1932.

RAMANATHAN, VENKATARAMA, M.A., B.L., Indian C.S. (asst. mag. and collr., Bihar) (b. 4th Sept., 1911).—Educated at Board High School, Namakal, University of Madras and The School of Oriental Studies, London; joined the service, 5th Sept., 1935; arrived in India, Oct., 1935, and served in B. and O., as asst. mag. and collr.

RAMANUJA AYYANGAR, NUGGEHALLI, Diwan Bahadur, B.A., Indian Police (supt., Madras) (b. 9th Jan., 1884).—Joined the service, 2nd Jan., 1907; dep. supt., Apl., 1909; asst. supt., June, 1920; supt., Nov., 1924; awarded King's police medal, Jan., 1925; commr. of police, Travancore, Nov., 1933; offg. dep. insp.-genl., Sept., 1935; Diwan Bahadur, May, 1937.

RAMAPPA, UCHIL, B.A., B.L. Judl. Dept., Madras (judge, city civil ct., Madras) (b. 6th July, 1882).—Joined the service, 24th July, 1912; sub. judge, Apl., 1929; offg. judge, city civil ct., Madras, July, 1933; confd., Oct., 1933.

RAMASUNDARAM, UNJALUR SUBRAHMANYA AYYAR, B.E., Indian Service of Engrs. (exec. engr., Madras) (b. 28th Dec., 1901).—Joined the service as appr. engr., 2nd July, 1924; asst. exec. engr., Oct., 1925; offg. exec. engr., May, 1931, and again, Jan., 1933; confd., Oct., 1934.

RAMASIVAMI AYYAR, RANGASWAMI IYER, B.A., Indian Audit Dept. (b. 12th Apl., 1897).—Joined the service, 24th Aug., 1920; dep. govt. ex. of accts., S.I. rly., Apl., 1936; dep. acctnt.-genl., P. and T., Nagpur, Oct., 1937.

RAMASWAMI AYYANGAR, GANDHARVAKOTA RANGASWAMI, B.A., late Indian Service of Engrs. (b. 1880).—Joined the service as appr. engr., 16th Feb., 1903; asst. engr., Nov., 1903; asst. exec. engr., Sept., 1920; offg. exec. engr., Sept., 1920; confd., March, 1925; retd., June, 1933.

RAMASWAMI AYYANGAR, K. R., M.A., late Indian Educl. Service (Madras) (b. June, 1876).—Apptd. prof., coll of engrg., 23rd Feb., 1921; retd., June, 1931.

RAMASWAMI AYYAR, BATLAGUNDU SITARAMA, B.A., B.E., Indian Service of Engrs. (exec. engr., Madras) (b. 18th April, 1889).—Joined the service as appr. engr., 26th June, 1911; asst. engr., June, 1912; exec. engr., June, 1921.

RAMASWAMI AYYAR, Sir CHETPAT PATTABHERAMA, K.C.I.E.—Apptd. advocate-general, Madras, Jan., 1921; C.I.E., Jan., 1923; member of govr.'s exec. council, Madras, Feb., 1923, to April, 1928; K.C.I.E., June, 1925; on deputation to League of Nations from 8th Aug. to 9th Oct., 1926; delegate to Indian round table conf., London, Nov., 1930-Jan., 1931, 2nd session Sept.-Dec., 1931; temp. member of govr.-genl.'s council, Aug., 1931; and again, June, 1932; delegate of India to monetary and economic conf., London, June, 1933; member, govt. of India Cttee. on secretariat procedure, 1935-1936.

RAMASWAMI AYYAR, GOPALA AYYAR, B.A., late Indian Service of Engrs. (b. 3rd Feb., 1881).—Joined the service, 31st Jan., 1905; asst. engr., March, 1908; exec. engr., March, 1917; offg. suptg. engr., March, 1929; confd., Oct., 1933; retd., Feb., 1936.

RAMASWAMI AYYAR, PALAMANERI NARAYANASWAMI, B.A., Indian C.S. (sub-collr. and mag., Madras) (b. 17th May, 1900).—Educ. at Madras Univ. and St. John's Coll., Cambridge; joined the service, 24th Oct., 1924; arrived, 8th Dec., 1924, and served in Madras as asst. collr. and mag.; sub-collr., Feb., 1926; offg. dist. and sess. judge, Sept., 1935.

RAMASWAMI MUDALIAR, ARCOT (see Mudaliar, Sir Ramaswami).

RAMASWAMI SASTRI, KADAMBI SUNDARARAMA, B.A., B.L. (b. Aug., 1878).—Joined the service as offg. dist. munsif, 5th July, 1907; confd., Mar., 1908; sub-judge, Dec., 1925; dist. and sess. judge, July, 1931; retd., Aug., 1933.

RAMASWAMI, VAIDYANATHIER, M.Sc. (Allbd.) Indian C.S. (jt. mag. and dep. coll., Bihar) (b. 30th Oct., 1904).—Educ. at University of Madras, University of Allahabad; Imperial Coll., and School of Oriental Studies, London; joined the service, 9th Oct., 1929; arrived, 29th Nov., 1929 and served in Bihar and Orissa as asst. mag. and coll.; actg. addl. dist. and sess. judge, Feb., 1936.

RAMCHANDANI, RANJIT SINGH V., Indian Railways Accounts Service (acct. offer.) (b. 19th June, 1888).—Held non-gaz. appts. from June, 1911; confd. as asst. contr. of stores, E.I. rly., June, 1926; confd. as asst. audit officer, May, 1927; asst. ditto, A.G. rlys., Aug., 1927; transfed. to Dehra Dun Coll., Nov., 1929; accts. offer., gr. I. (provl.), July, 1933.

RAMESAM, Sir VEPA, Kt., B.A., B.L. (b. 27th July, 1875).—Joined the service, 13th July, 1916, as govt. pleader; judge, high ct., Madras, Nov., 1920; Kt., March, 1929; offg. ch. justice, high ct., Madras, July, 1931, and again, July, 1933; retd., July, 1935.

RAMESHWAR, SAHAI, Indian Forest Service (dep. consvr., Orissa) (b. 13th Jan., 1905).—Joined the service as asst. consvr., Madras, 11th Nov., 1929; offg. dist. forest offer., Aug., 1933; dep. consvr., Nov., 1934; transfed. to Orissa prov., Apr., 1936.

RAMPLING, HAROLD, Indian Police (dist. supt., Burma) (b. 18th April, 1895).—Joined the service as asst. dist. supt., 26th Jan., 1920; dist. supt., June, 1927.

RAMRAO ANNAPI HEMMADI (see Hemmadi, R. A.)

RAMSAY, Sir JOHN, K.C.I.E., C.S.I., Lieut.-Col., Indian Army (ret'd.), late Foreign Dept., Govt. of India.—First commn., 9th Sept., 1882; from July, 1886, served at Ajmir and Indore as acting asst. agent; confd. in latter post, Jan., 1889; pol. agent, Haraoti and Tonk, Dec., 1891; 1st asst., Indore, May, 1892; in addn., asst. to gen. supt., Thagi and Dakaiti, Nov., 1892; pol. agent in Bundelkhand, April, 1893; C.I.E., Jan., 1898; pol. agent and dep. commr., Quetta-Pishin, Nov., 1898; offg. rev. and judl. commr., Baluchistan, from Nov., 1902, to April, 1905; pol. resdt. in Turkish Arabia, March, 1906; and H.B.M.'s consul gen. at Baghdad, Oct., 1907; resdt. in Kashmir, May, 1910; agent to govr.-gen., and chief commr., Baluchistan, April, 1911; C.S.I., Dec., 1911; K.C.I.E., June, 1915; ret'd., Dec., 1917.

RAMSAY, NORMAN, Capt., I.A., Indian Political Service (b. 11th Nov., 1907).—First commn., 1st Sept., 1927; in milty. employ prior to 20th Nov., 1933; 2nd-in-command and adjt., Kolhapur Inf., June, 1933; P.A. to Resdt., Hyderabad, Nov., 1933; under training in the Punjab, July, 1934; under-secty. to A.G.G., Rajputana, Apr., 1935; again under training in the Punjab, Jan., 1936; asst. pol. agent, Punjab Hill States, Oct., 1936; under-secty. to the resdt., Hyderabad, Nov., 1936.

RAMS BOTHAM, RICHARD BURY, M.B.E., M.A. (Oxon.), B.Litt. (Oxon.), F.R.Hist.S., late Indian Educl. Service (b. 27th Apr., 1880).—Joined the service, 3rd June, 1908, as inspr. of schools; profr., Dacca col., July, 1908; I.A.R.O., Aug., 1916, to April, 1919; M.B.E., June, 1919; principal, Hooghly coll., July, 1920; offg. prinpl., presy. coll., Calcutta, June, 1928; ditto, Chittagong coll., July, 1929; confd., July, 1930; on dep. to Muslim univ., from Feb., 1931; retd., Sept., 1935.

RAMSDEN, GEOFFREY CHARLES FRESCHVILLE, M.A. (Cantab.), Indian C.S. (dep. commr., Central Provs.) (b. 21st April, 1893).—Educ. at Haileybury and Sidney Sussex Coll., Cambridge; joined the service, 1st Sept., 1920; arrived, 4th Dec., 1920, and served in the Central Provs. as asst. commr.; services placed at disposal of govt. of India, dept. of commerce, Nov., 1923; offg. dep. commr., March, 1926; confd., July, 1927; excise commr. and supt. of stamps, Jan., 1929; offg. commr., Feb., 1936.

RAMZAN, ALI, Khan Bahadur, India Posts and Tels. (P.M.G.) (b. 14th Aug., 1883).—Joined the service as proby. supt., 7th June, 1905; confd., Oct., 1906; offg. dep. P.M.G., Bombay, Apr., 1928; confd., Oct., 1928; dep. P.M.G., Punjab and N.W.F.P., Feb., 1930; offg. dep. dir.-genl., posts and tels., Sept., 1934; P.M.G., Bihar and Orissa, Mar., 1936; P.M.G., Dec., 1936.

RANA LOKE SHAMSHER JANG, Indian Police (dist. supt., Central Provs.) (b. 4th Oct., 1887).—Joined the service, 11th July, 1910; offg. dist. supt., Sept., 1920; confd., Jan., 1930.

RANA, UDAYASINHJI HARMIRSINHJI, Indian Police (supt., Bombay) (b. 3rd Nov., 1899).—Joined the service as asst. supt., Bombay, 20th Aug., 1923; offg. supt., Feb., 1932.

RANADE, DATTATRAYA DAMODAR, Rai Bahadur, Indian Audit Dept. (b. 1st Jan., 1884).—Joined the service, 4th Jan., 1906; asst. acct.-genl., United Provs., Nov., 1930; dep. acct.-genl., Central Provs., Nov., 1931, and again, Feb., 1935; ditto, Bombay, Jan., 1933; ditto, U.P., Oct., 1936.

RANDHAWA, MOHINDAR SINGH, M.Sc., Indian C.S. (jt. mag. and collr., United Provs.) (b. 2nd Feb., 1909).—Educated at Khalsa High School, Huktsar, University of the Punjab and the School of Oriental Studies, London; joined the service, 6th Oct., 1934; arrived in India, Nov., 1934, and served in the United Provinces as asst. magte. and collr.; jt. magte., Oct., 1936.

RANDLE, HERBERT NIEL, Ph.D., M.A. (Oxon.), India Office (librarian and keeper of oriental printed books and M.S.S.), late Indian Educl. Service (b. 19th Oct., 1880).—Joined the service, 31st July, 1905, and officiated as prof. of philosophy, Queen's Coll., Benares; inspr. of schools, April, 1906; prof., Muir Central Coll., Allahabad, Nov., 1906; ditto, Queen's Coll., Benares, April, 1909; ditto, Muir Central Coll., Sept., 1918; actg. prinpl., Queen's Coll., April, 1919; Allahabad Univ. from July, 1925; asst. librarian, India Office, Sept., 1927; librarian and keeper of oriental printed books and M.S.S., Sept., 1933.

RANDOLPH, Rev. THOMAS BERKELEY, M.A., Indian Eccles. Estabt. (junr. chaplain) (b. 15th Mar., 1904).—Apptd. chaplain on probn., Bengal; junr. chaplain, Jan., 1934.

RANG BEHARI LAL, M.A., Finance dept., govt. of India (b. 19th Mar., 1889).—Joined the service, 5th Mar., 1914; probnr., fin. dept., Feb., 1915; asst. acct. genl., U.P., May, 1916; ditto, posts and tels., Aug., 1916; ditto, Punjab, May, 1917; asst. acct. genl., posts and tels. (tel. branch), Apl., 1919; asst. auditor, E.B. Rly., Sept., 1920; ditto, O. and R. Rly., Nov., 1921; and again, Jan., 1924; asst. acct. genl., posts and tels. (postal branch), Sept., 1923; asst. auditor, N.W. Rly., Oct., 1924; divl. audit officer, N.W. Rly., Nov., 1924; examr. of accts., C.I.S. coalfields rly. construction, Feb., 1926; audit officer, Indian Stores dept., Aug., 1926; dep. acct. genl., posts and tels., Nov., 1926; govt. examr., M. and S.M. Rly., Mar., 1928; dep. auditor, G.I.P. Rly., Oct., 1929; offg. ch. pay and accts. officer, U.P., Feb., 1930; offg. dep. ch. acctg. officer, U.P., Apl., 1931; dep. ch. auditor, Burma, rlys., Apl., 1932; dep. acct.-genl., Burma, Nov., 1932; offg. dep. ch. auditor, Burma rlys., Jan., 1933; offg. audit officer, Lloyd Barr. and canals constrn., Feb., 1933; offg. ch. auditor, rly. clearing accts., Nov., 1933; offg. dep. contr. of rly. accts., Jan., 1935, and again, Dec., 1935.

RANGANATHAN, CONDORMANICKAM RAGUNATHA, M.A., Indian Forest Service (dep. consvr., Madras) (b. 15th Aug., 1899).—Joined the service as asst. consvr., 30th Nov., 1923; dep. consvr., Nov., 1927.

RANGANATHAN, MAYAVARAM KRISHNASWAMI, B.A., B.E., A.M.I.C.E., Indian Service of Engrs. (exec. engr., Madras) (b. 16th Sep., 1892).—Joined the service as appr. engr., on 10th June, 1916; asst. engr., June, 1917; asst. exec. engr., July, 1922; exec. engr., June, 1926.

RANGANATHAN, SAMUEL E., M.A., late Indian Educl. Service (b. 30th Dec., 1877).—Apptd. to I.E.S., 23rd Feb., 1921; addl. prof., presdy. coll., Madras, July, 1922; on foreign service as vice-chancellor, Annamalai Univ., May, 1929; retd., Dec., 1932.

RANGANATHAN, SAURIRAJAN, Indian C.S. (jt. mag., United Provs.) (b. 12th Nov., 1908).—Educated at Madras University, School of Oriental Studies, London, and Oriel College, Oxford; joined the service, 11th Oct., 1932; arrived in India, Nov., 1932, and served in the United Provs. as asst. magte. and collr.; jt. magte., Feb., 1935; asst. settlement officer, Nov., 1936.

RANGANATHAN, SUBRAHMANYA, O.B.E., B.A., Indian C.S. (collr. and mag., Madras) (b. 2nd Feb., 1893).—Educ. at Madras Univ. and Queen's Coll., Oxford; apptd. after exam. of 1916; arrived, 1st Jan., 1918, and served in Madras as asst. collr. and mag.; sub. collr. and jt. mag., Dec., 1919; sub. collr., Dec., 1922; emigration agent, Ceylon, Sept., 1923; offg. dep. secty. to govt., local self govt. dept., April, 1928; O.B.E., Jan., 1930; offg. collr. and dist. magte., Aug., 1930; ditto (provl. subs.), Dec., 1934; confd., Sept., 1935.

RANGANAYAKULU NAYUDU, POLISSETTY Rao Sahib, B.C.E., late Indian Service of Engrs.—Joined the service, 21st May, 1895; asst. engr., March, 1908; exec. engr., March, 1918; retd., May, 1928.

RANGASWAMI AYYANGAR, GIYARPURAM NADATHUR, Indian Agricultural Service (millett specialist, Madras) (b. 19th May, 1887).—Apptd. asst. economic botanist, June, 1920; millet specialist, Oct., 1924.

RANGASWAMI AYYANGAR, S., B.A., B.L., Judl. dept., Madras (b. 21st July, 1889).—Apptd. administrator-genl. and offil. trustee, 16th July, 1929.

RANGASWAMI IYER, ALAGIAMANAVALAM, RAO BAHADUR, B.A., late Indian Audit Dept. (b. 4th Mar., 1881).—Joined the service, 7th Nov., 1900; asst. dir. of comml. audit, Apl., 1926; dep. acct. genl., Madras, Apl., 1932; offg. dep. govt. exr. of accts., Bengal Nagpur rly., July, 1934; dep. acct.-genl., Madras, Nov., 1934; retd., Mar., 1936.

RANGI LAL, RAI BAHADUR, LALA, M.A., late Punjab Civil Service (b. 1st Mar., 1882).—Joined the service as proby. extra asst. commr., 15th Jan., 1906; confd., Sept., 1910; sub-judge, Aug., 1912; dist. and sess. judge, Sept., 1929; addl. judge, high ct., Lahore, Oct., 1933; and again, Oct., 1934; offg. judl. commr., Quetta, Apl., 1936; retd., Mar., 1937.

RANGNEKAR, SAJBA SHANKAR, B.A., LL.B., Barr., Bombay Judicial Dept. (judge, high ct., Bombay) (b. 20th Dec., 1878).—Joined the dept., 29th Sept., 1924; chief presdy. mag., Sept., 1924; actg. judge, high ct., Bombay, Aug. 1926, June, 1927, and again, June, 1928; confd. April, 1929; offg. ch. justice, high ct., Bombay, June, 1936.

RANKIN, Sir GEORGE CLAUS, Kt., Barr., late Bengal Judicial Dept. (b. 12th Aug., 1877).—Apptd. judge, high ct., Calcutta, 14th Nov., 1918; Kt., Jan., 1925; chief justice, high ct., Nov., 1926; retd., Nov., 1934.

RANSFORD, ALISTER JOHN, Lt.-Col., C.I.E., M.A. (mint master, Bombay) (b. 5th Jan., 1895).—employ up to Jan., 1924; dep. mint master, Feb., 1926; offg. mint master, May, 1927; again, May, 1929; confd., Sept., 1931; C.I.E., June, 1936.

RANSFORD, CHARLES DE ST. CLEATHER, M.A., late Indian Educl. Service (b. 16th Sept., 1877).—Joined the service, 18th Jan., 1910, as head master, govt. high school; inspr. of schools, Aug., 1912; dist. edul. offr., Bellary, Feb., 1923; inspr. of European schools, Madras, Feb., 1930; retd., Sept., 1932.

RAO, BELTHANGADI GOPALAKRISHNA (see Gopalakrishna, R. B.)

RAO, CHIKBALLAPUR VENKATA SRINIVASA (see Srinivasa Rao, C.V.).

RAO, DHANVADA ANANDA (see Ananda Rao, D.).

RAO, EKANATHA RAJARAM (see Ekanatha Rajaram Rao).

RAO, GHANTA VENKATA (see Venkata Rao, G.).

RAO, KABAD APPAJI (see Appaji Rao, K.).

RAO, KASARAGOD PATANASHETTI LAKSHMANA (see Lakshmana Rao, K.P.).

RAO, KAIWAR SETU (see Setu Rao, K.).

RAO, KOTY VENKATA RAMANA (see Ramana Rao, K.V.).

RAO, KRISHNASWAMI ANANDA (see Ananda, R.K.).

RAO, KRISHNA KARNAD (see Krishna Rao).

RAO, MUTTA VENKATASUBBA (see Venkata-subba Rao).

RAO, MYSORE SUBBA (see Mysore Subba Rao).

RAO, NANDOLIKE DEVARAO KRISHNA (see Krishna Rao N.D.).

RAO, NEELAMANGALA KESHAVA (see Keshava Rao, N.).

RAO, PEJAVAR MOHAN (see Mohan Rao, P.).

RAO, R. (see Ramachandra Rao).

RAO, BENEGAL SANJIVA (see Sanjiva Rao, B.).

RAO, P. H. SESHAGIRI (see Seshagiri Rao).

RAO, S. RAGHUNATHA (see Raghunatha Rao, S.).

RAO, UDIYANANDA (see Ananda R.U.).

RAO, UDYAVAR RAMA (see Rama Rao, U.).

RAO, VYAKARANA NARAHARI (see Narahari Rao, V.).

RAO, V. P. (see Pandurang Rao, V.).

RAO, V. SURYAPRAKASA (see Suryaprakasa Rao, V.).

RAOOOF, ABDUL (see Abdul Raooof).

RAPER, JOHN HUGH FRANCIS, Indian State Rlys. (ch. traffic manager, G.I.P. Rly.) (b. 23rd Sept., 1889).—Joined the service, 28th June, 1912; asst. traffic supt., June, 1915; dist. ditto, March, 1920; asst. transportation supt., Nov., 1922; asst. traffic manager, Nov., 1926; offg. dep. ditto, Nov., 1926; confd., July, 1929; offg. ch. traffic manager, June, 1931; and again, Jan., 1933; confd., Mar., 1933; offg. agent, G.I.P. Rly., May, 1936.

RASHEED KHAN, Abdul, B.A., Indian C.S. (asst. collr., Bombay) (b. 28th Jan., 1905).—Educ. at Lucknow University and Exeter College, Oxford; joined the service, 10th Oct., 1930; arrived in India, Nov., 1930, and served in Bombay as asst. collr.

RASHID, ABDUL (see Abdul R.).

RASHID-UZ-ZAMAN, SAYYID, B.A., Indian C.S. (magte. and collr., Bihar) (b. 28th Mar., 1895).—Educ. at Patna Coll., Presidency Coll., Calcutta, and London Univ.; joined the service, 14th Dec., 1921; arrived, 19th Jan., 1922, and served in Bihar and Orissa as asst. mag., and collr.; offg. under-sec. to govt., Aug., 1925; confd., Jan., 1926; offg. mag. and collr., Apl., 1929; offg. addl. dep. secty. to the govt. of India, dept. of health, educn. and lands, Apl., 1933; secty., public dept., office of High Commr. for India, Mar., 1934; actg. dep. trade commr., June, 1934; adviser to Govt. of India delegation to Internatl. labour conf., Geneva, June, 1935; reverted to Bihar as magte. and collr., Nov., 1937.

RASTOGI, PIARE LAL, Babu (see Piare Lal Rastogi).

RATCLIFF, GEORGE MASSEY, B.A., Indian C.S. (jt. mag. and dep. collr., Bengal) (b. 8th Aug., 1906).—Educ. at King Edward VII School, Lytham, Lancs., and Emmanuel College, Cambridge; apptd. after examination of 1930; arrived in India, Nov., 1931, and served in Bengal as asst. mag. and collr.; jt. mag. and dep. collr., Dec., 1933.

RATNAGAR, RATAN DHANJIBHOY, B.A., B.Sc., Indian Service of Engineers (exec. engr., Central Prov.) (b. 14th Oct., 1890).—Joined the service as asst. engr., Oct., 1914; exec. engr., Oct., 1923; offg. supt. engr., Oct., 1932.

RATNAGAR, SHAPURJI NASARVANJI JAMSHEDJI, C.I.E., M.A., late Indian Forest Service (b. 28th Sept., 1882).—Joined the service, 11th July, 1911, as proby. extra asst. consvr.; extra asst. consvr., Oct., 1913; in foreign service, Junagadh State, from 1st June, 1915; dep. consvr., Dec., 1922; offg. consvr., Aug., 1934; retd. Sept., 1937; C.I.E., Jan., 1938.

RATNASWAMI, M., C.I.E., Barr. (member, Madras services commn.) (b. 15th Aug., 1885).—Apptd. offg. princpl., law coll., Madras, 3rd Jan., 1928; confd., June, 1928; member, Madras services commn., Jan., 1930; offg. chairman, ditto, June, 1935.

RAU, BANTWAL GOPALAKRISHNA, B.Sc., Indian C.S. (asst. mag. and collr., U.P.) (b. 10th Nov., 1912).—Educated at Canara High School, Mangalore, Madras University, City and Guilds (Engineering) College, London, and the School of Oriental Studies, London; joined the service, 5th Sept., 1935; arrived in India, Oct., 1935, and served in the United Provinces as asst. mag. and collr.; on spl. duty, reforms office, govt. of India, Sept., 1935.

RAU, BENEGAL NARSINGA, C.I.E., B.A. (Camb.), Indian C.S. (addl. jt. secty., legisl. dept., govt. of India) (b. 26th Feb., 1887).—Educ. at Canara High Sch., Mangalore, Madras and Trinity Coll., Cambridge; apptd. after exam. of 1909; arrived in India, 28th Nov., 1910, and served in Bengal as asst. mag. and collr.; jt. mag. and dep. collr., Jan., 1916; ditto, Assam, May, 1920; offg. dist. and sess. judge, April, 1922; offg. sec., legisl. dept., supt. and membr., legal affairs, March, 1925; confd., Dec., 1925; C.I.E., Jan., 1934; addl. jt. secty., legisl. dept., govt. of India, Dec., 1934; offg. jt. secty., ditto, Jan., 1935; offg. judge, high ct., Calcutta, Mar., 1935.

RAU, B. RAMA (see Rama Rau, Benegal).

RAU, HANUMANTA BHIMASENA (see Bhimasena Rau, Hanumanta).

RAU, HATTIANGADI SHANKAR, C.I.E., B.A., late Indian Finance Dept (b. 29th Sept., 1887).—Joined the service, 22nd Aug., 1907, and held non-gazetted appts.; offg. supt., fin. dept., Nov., 1922; pers. asst. to contr. of currency, May, 1923; supt., fin. dept., Aug., 1923; asst. secty., fin. dept. (temp.), March, 1924; confd., Aug., 1924; offg. under-secty., fin. dept., July, 1925; confd., Nov., 1926, and again, April, 1928; budget officer, Dec., 1926, and again Nov., 1928; offg. dep. contr. of the currency, Bombay, Apl., 1931; C.I.E., June, 1931; member and secty., Indian federal finance expert comee., Jan., 1932; offg. contr., of the currency, Apl., 1935; retd., Sept., 1937.

RAU, KALLE RAMA RAU SADASHIVA (see Sadashiva Rau, K. R. R.).

RAU, LAJMI LAKSHMAN, late India Posts and Tels. (b. 18th Sept., 1880).—Joined the service as apprentice asst. supt., 15th Oct., 1904; asst. supt., June, 1906; supt., July, 1916; dep. postmaster-genl., traffic, Bombay Circle, Oct., 1923; offg. dir., tele. engrg., Lucknow, April, 1930; ditto, Madras, Mar., 1931; retd., June, 1932.

RAU, P. RAGHAVENDRA (see Raghavendra, R. P.).

RAU, PENDYALA SATIANARAINA, B.A., Indian C.S. (dep. commr., Central Provs.) (b. 15th Nov., 1894).—Educ. at Madras Univ., and New Coll., Oxford; joined the service, 23rd Oct., 1918; served in Central Provs. as asst. commr. from 27th Jan., 1919; offg. dep. commr., Jan., 1924; confd., Dec., 1927; offg. commr. of settlements and dir. of land records, etc., C.P., Feb., 1936; offg. finl. secty. to govt., C.P., Mar., 1936.

RAWLINS, ERIC FORD, late Indian Police Service (b. 2nd July, 1887).—Joined the service 3rd Dec., 1907; asst. supt., Feb., 1911; supt., Dec., 1917; retd., Aug., 1932.

RAWLINSON, HUGH GEORGE, C.I.E., M.A., (Cantab.), late Indian Educl. Service (b. 12th May, 1880).—Joined the service, 7th Jan., 1908, as prof. of English literature, Deccan Coll., Poona; principle and prof. of English and history, Karnatak Coll., Dharwar, Sept., 1919; offg. principal and prof., Deccan Coll., Poona, June, 1923; confd., Dec., 1924; C.I.E., Jan., 1933; retd., May, 1935.

RAWLEY, RATAN CHAND, M.A., M.Sc., D.Sc. (Lon.), late Dept. of Industries, Punjab.—Apptd. dir. of industries, 7th Nov., 1927; adviser and secty. to the Indian delegation to the Internatl. labour conference, Geneva, May-June, 1928.

RAY (see also Roy).

RAY, ANNADA SANKAR, Indian C.S. (jt. mag. and dep. coll., Bengal) (b. 14th March, 1904).—Educ. at University of Patna and School of Oriental Studies, London; joined the service, 9th Oct., 1929; arrived Nov., 1929, and served in Bengal as asst. mag. and coll.; jt. mag. and dep. collr., May, 1931; offg. mag. and collr., Aug., 1936.

RAY, SIR ASOKE KUMAR, Bengal Judl. Dept., (advocate genl., Bengal) (b. 9th Sept., 1886).—Joined the service as standing councl, Bengal

(offg.), 20th Dec., 1929; confd., Apl., 1930; offg. judge, high ct., Calcutta, Apl., 1932, and again, Nov., 1933; advocate genl., Bengal, Mar., 1934; knighted, Feb., 1937.

RAY, BIMALENDU, B.A., Indian Police (asst. supt., Bihar) (b. 11th Oct., 1903).—Joined the service as asst. supt., Bihar and Orissa, 15th April, 1926; offg. supt., Oct., 1936.

RAY, CHUNI LAL, Rai Bahadur, B.A., late Excise and Salt Dept., Bihar and Orissa (b. 3rd May, 1881).—Joined the service, 23rd Sept., 1902; dep. mag. and dep. collr., Nov., 1903; supt. of excise and salt, Oct., 1916; dep. commr., Sept., 1923; offg. commr., March, 1925; retd., Oct., 1932.

RAY, GERALD GEORGE, Indian Police (supt., Bombay) (b. 14th June, 1895).—Joined the service, 6th Dec., 1915, as asst. supt.; on mil. duty from 20th June, 1917, to 17th Feb., 1919; offg. supt., April, 1922; confd., June, 1926; awarded King's police medal, Jan., 1932.

RAY, GORDON MEREDITH, B.A., Indian C.S. (asst. mag. and collr., B. and O.) (b. 2nd Aug., 1912).—Educ. at Caterham Sch. and Hertford Coll., Oxford; joined the service, 16th Sept., 1937; arrived in India, Dec., 1937, and served in B. and O. as asst. mag. and collr.

RAY, JITENDRA NATH, B.A., Indian Police (supt., Bengal) (b. 23rd August, 1892).—Joined the service, 2nd January, 1917; dep. supt., April, 1928; apptd. to I.P. as supt., June, 1934.

RAY, JNANENDRA NATH, I.S.O., M.A., B.L., Rai Bahadur, late Registration dept., Bengal (b. 17th May, 1876).—Joined the service as proby. dep. collr., 24th July, 1899; dep. magte. and dep. collr., July, 1901; inspr.-genl. of registration, Oct., 1926; I.S.O., June, 1929; retd., June, 1933.

RAY, KARUNAMAY, B.A., Indian Police (supt.) (b. 10th Nov., 1885).—Joined the service, 21st Oct., 1910; apptd. to I.P. as supt., Bengal, Dec., 1935.

RAY, KIRAN CHANDRA, M.A., B.Sc., Indian Forest Service (dep. consvr., Central Provs.) (b. 27th Feb., 1900).—Joined the service as asst., consvr., 2nd Nov., 1923; dep. consvr., Nov. 1927.

RAY, KUMUD BHUSON, Indian State Ryls. (exec. engr., E.B. Rly.) (b. 30th Jan., 1891).—Joined the service as apprentice engr., 15th Jan., 1915; asst. engr., Jan., 1916; asst. exec. engr., Jan., 1925; exec. engr., July, 1937.

RAY, LALIT KUMAR, L.M.S. (Punj.) D.P.H. (Cantab.) Public Health Dept., United Provs. (Asst. Dir. of Public Health) (b. Feb., 1887).—Joined the service as health officer, 1st Mar., 1914, Asst. Dir. of Public Health Nov., 15th, 1934.

RAY, NAGENDRA NATH, Bihar C.S. (mag. and collr.) (b. 1st July, 1885).—Joined the service, 16th Mar., 1905; dep. mag. and dep. collr., Oct., 1907; mag. and collr., May, 1937.

RAY, PRABHAT CHANDRA, B.E., Indian Service of Engrs. (exec. engr., Bengal) (b. 23rd Feb., 1888).—Joined the service, 15th Feb., 1913; asst. engr., Feb., 1914; exec. eng., June, 1926.

RAY, PRAN KRISHTO, RAI BAHADUR, B.A., late Judl. dept., United Provs. (b. 9th Aug., 1875).—Joined the service, 10th July, 1905; sub. judge, Dec., 1917; offg. dist. and sess. judge, April, 1926; confd., Nov., 1929; retd., Nov., 1930.

RAY, RAJENDRA CHANDRA, L.M.S., D.P.H., Public Health dept., Bengal (b. 9th Dec., 1877). Apptd. asst. dir. of public health, Mar., 1921.

RAY, RAJESVAR PRASHAD, B.A., Indian Police (supt., Bihar) (b. 15th Dec., 1895).—Joined the service, 20th June, 1920; transferred to I.P., as supt., May, 1934.

RAY, RANAJIT KUMAR, B.A., Indian C.S. (jt. mag. and dep. collr., Bengal) (b. 14th Oct., 1906).—Educ. at Calcutta University and King's College, Cambridge; joined the service, 12th Oct., 1931; arrived in India, Nov., 1931, and served in Bengal as asst. mag. and collr.; jt. mag. and dep. collr., May, 1933.

RAY, REGINALD EDWARD ANTHONY, Indian Police (supt., Bengal) (b. 15th May, 1891).—Joined the service as proby. asst. supt., 16th Dec., 1910; confd., June, 1912; addl. supt., Nov., 1924; spl. supt., Aug., 1925; offg. dep. inspr.-genl., Mar., 1934.

RAY, RICHARD LIONEL, V.D., M.I.M.E., late Indian State Rlys. (b. 19th Feb. 1880).—Joined the service as asst. loco. supt., 4th Nov., 1905; dist. loco. supt. 1st July, 1912; offg. dep. loco. supt., Dec., 1922; confd., June, 1923; dep. ch. mech. engr., 1st April, 1925; offg. ch. mech. engr., June, 1929; confd., May, 1931; retd., Jan., 1937.

RAY, SAILENDRA NATH GUHA (see Guha Ray, S. N.).

RAY, SANAT KUMAR, B.A., B.E., Indian Service of Engrs. (suptg. engr., Bihar) (b. 6th Sept., 1887).—Joined the service, 24th Jan., 1910; asst. engr., Feb., 1911; exec. engr., Jan., 1921; suptg. engr., Mar., 1934.

RAY, SATISH CHANDRA, M.A., Indian Educl. Service (b. 24th May, 1888).—Apptd. inspr. of schools, Assam, 12th Nov., 1923; offg. princpl. Cotton coll., Ganhati, July, 1933.

RAY, SATYENDRA NATH, Indian C.S. mag. and collr., Bengal (b. 25th June, 1902).—Educ. at Calcutta Univ. and King's Coll., Cambridge; joined the service, 2nd April, 1925; arrived, 4th May, 1925, and served in Bengal as asst. mag. and collr.; offg. mag. and collr., Apl., 1934; and again, Feb., 1936; confd., Mar., 1937.

RAY, TARAK CHANDRA, late Bengal Civil Service (b. 1880).—Joined the service, as proby. dep. magte. and dep. collr., 4th Aug., 1902; confd., May, 1903; magte. and collr., Sept., 1929; retd., July, 1935.

RAY CHAUDHURI, NIRMALKANTI, B.Sc., Indian C.S. (jt. mag. and dy. collr., Bengal) (b. 16th Jan., 1906).—Educ. at Calcutta University, and London School of Oriental Studies; joined the service, 10th Oct., 1930; arrived in India, Nov., 1930, and served in Bengal as asst. mag. and collr.; jt. mag. and dep. collr., Nov., 1931.

RAYNEAU, CYRIL ALEXANDER, Indian State Rlys. (coal supt.). (b. 8th Dec., 1889).—Joined the service as asst. coal supt., 1st May, 1916; offg. coal supt., April, 1928; confd., Apl., 1930.

RAYNER, ADRIEN BOOKER, Indian Service of Engrs. (exec. engr., Punjab) (b. 15th Jan., 1893).—Joined the service, Oct., 1919, as asst. engr.; asst. exec. engr., Jan., 1920; exec. engr., Oct., 1925.

RAYNER, WILLIAM RUFFELL, India Office (admin. officer for statistical questions, economic and overseas dept.) (b. 16th Mar., 1890).—Educ. at King Charles' School, Tunbridge Wells; apptd. second class clerk, India Office, 25th Jan., 1909, and attached to acctnt.-gen.'s dept.; transf. to pol. dept., March, 1912; services lent to Colonial Office from March, 1921, to Jan., 1922; transf. to judl. and public dept., Jan., 1922; higher clerical officer, April, 1922; deputy reading clerk to Council, Feb., 1923; asst. princpl., Aug., 1924; jt. editor, India Office list, Oct., 1932, to May, 1934; admin. officer for statistical questions, economic and overseas dept., May, 1934.

RAYNES, CHARLES HENRY, M.C., Indian Police (dist. supt., Burma) (b. 10th Aug., 1898).—Joined the service as asst. dist. supt., Burma, 19th Feb., 1921; offg. dist. supt., May, 1927; confd., May, 1933; crimes asst. to D.I.G. Rlys., and C.I.D., June, 1937.

RAYNOR, ERIC WALTER, B.A., Indian Forest Service (dep. consvr., United Provs.) (b. 20th Sept., 1896).—Joined the service as asst. consvr., on 12th Dec., 1923; dep. consvr., Sept., 1925.

RAYNOR, FRANK HENRY CHARLES, Indian Police (supt., Orissa) (b. 18th Jan., 1902).—Joined the service, 6th Dec., 1921; offg. dist. supt., Oct., 1924; confd., Oct., 1932.

RAYNOR, VERE OWEN, Indian State Rlys. (dep. chief oper. supt., N.W. Rly.) (b. 20th Jan., 1888).—Joined the service as asst. traffic supt., 29th June, 1908; dist. ditto, April, 1918; offg. dep. ch. operating supt. (movement), N.W. rly., Jan., 1933; confd., June, 1936; offg. dep. ch. comml. mangr., Nov., 1936.

RAZA ALI, SYED, Sir, Kt., C.B.E., B.A., LL.B. (agent-genl. for Govt. of India in Sth. Africa) (b. 29th Apl., 1882).—Educ. at high sch., Moradabad, and Muhammad Coll., Aligarh; member, U.P. legl. council, 1912-20; member, council of state, 1921-26; member, frontier enquiry comee., 1922; on deputation to S. Africa in connection with Indian question, 1925; C.B.E., July, 1926; member, public services commn., Oct., 1926, to Nov., 1931; agent-genl. for govt. of India in Sth. Africa, Jan., 1935; knighted, June, 1935.

RAZA, KAZIM (see Kazim Raza).

RAZA, MUHAMMAD (see Muhammad Raza).

RAZA, SAIYID ALAY (see Alay Raza Saiyid).

RAZA, SAIYID HASHIM (see Hashim Raza, S.).

READ, JAMES, Indian C.S. (dep. commr., Punjab) (b. 11th Jan., 1898).—Educ. at Winchester and Wellington Mil. Coll., India; on mil. service, May, 1916, to April, 1921; joined the service, 26th Oct., 1923; arrived, 22nd Dec., 1923, and served in the Punjab as asst. commr.; judge, small cause ct., Simla, March, 1926; offg. dist. and sess. judge, Mar., 1930; dep. commr. (subs. provl.), Oct., 1931; confd., Oct., 1936.

REAKS, FRANK HUBERT, late Indian State Railways (E.I. Rly.). (b. 8th Sept., 1876).—Joined the service, 1st Jan., 1897, as traffic candidate; asst. traffic supt., Jan., 1899; dist. supt., April, 1907; dep. traffic manager, April, 1918; offg. traffic manager, Feb., 1924; dep. chief operating supt., July, 1925; offg. ch. comml. manager, Nov., 1927; retd., Sept., 1931.

REAKS, REGINALD RUPERT, M.B.E., late Indian Stores Dept. (b. 10th May, 1875).—Held non-gazetted appts. from 1st Nov., 1893; supt., June, 1912; with Indian Munitions Board from 30th April, 1917; second asst. ch. contr., Nov., 1920; asst. ditto, April, 1921; offg. contr. of purchase, Aug., 1925; retd., May, 1930.

REAY, ROBERT WILLIAM, late Office of High Commr. for India (b. 14th June, 1875).—Clerk, 2nd class, 23rd Oct., 1894; staff clerk, June, 1917; services lent to high commr. for India, from Oct., 1920; suptg. exec. officer, April, 1922; asst. ch. acctg. officer, Oct., 1930; dep. ditto, and clerk to commrs. of inc. tax, Nov., 1934; retd., Nov., 1937.

REBELLO, ALBIN RICHARD, O.B.E., F.S.S., F.R.E.S., Indian Audit Dept. (b. 3rd Apr., 1886).—Joined the service, 25th Mar., 1908; supt., fin. dept., Mar., 1922; asst. secty., taxation enquiry ctee., Nov., 1925; asst. acct. genl., Punjab, Mar., 1927; asst. dir., army audit, May, 1927; dep. acct. genl., Bengal, Mar., 1933; ch. auditor, Burma rlys., Nov., 1935.

REDCLIFT, S. W. (electrical adviser and ch. electric inspr., Bengal) (b. 9th Oct., 1887).—Apptd. electric adviser and electric inspector, Bengal and Assam (on probn.), 1st July, 1926; confd., July, 1928; electrical adviser and ch. electrical inspr., Bengal, Dec., 1928.

REDDI, OBULREDDIGARI PULLA (see Pulla Reddi, O.).

REDDI, P. H. RAMA (see Rama Reddi, P. H.).

REDHA, KANTA SARAN, Income-tax Dept., Bihar and Orissa (asst. commr.) (b. 29th June, 1886).—Asst. commr., Sept., 1921.

REDMOND, LIONEL, M.I.C.E., V.D., late Indian State Rlys. (b. 11th Dec., 1878).—Joined the service as asst. engr. (tempy.), 14th Jan., 1908; confd., April, 1912; resdt. engr., Aug., 1915; in milty. employ from 8th Jan., 1918, to 16th Sept., 1919; exec. engr., July, 1925; retd., Dec., 1935.

REES, OWEN MEREDITH, M.A., late Indian C.S.—Educ. at Bolton Grammar Sch., and Queen's Coll., Oxford; apptd. after exam. of 1908; arrived, 4th Dec., 1909, and served in Burma as asst. commr.; on nil. service from April, 1916, to March, 1919; dep. commr. (prov. sub.), Sept., 1924; offg. commr. of settl. land records from July, 1923; retd. July, 1927; apptd. second addl. magte., 24 Parganas, Jan., 1933 to Mar., 1935; K-i-H. medal, 1st cl., June, 1935.

REES, STANLEY VERNON LLOYD, Indian Police (asst. supt., Assam) (b. 1st May, 1907).—Joined the service, 17th Nov., 1926; in training to May, 1928; proby. asst. supt., May, 1928; confd., Nov., 1928; offg. supt., rly. police, Mar., 1935.

REEVE, GILBERT AMOS, late Indian Police (b. 7th Dec., 1894).—Joined the service as asst. supt., 2nd Dec., 1920; offg. supt., Jan., 1926; asst. to A.G.G. in Central India, Oct., 1927; supt. (prov. sub.), Dec., 1928; confd., Feb., 1929; retd., July, 1934.

REGE, DATTATRAYA VAMAN, B.A., Indian C.S. (ep. commr., Central Provs.) (b. 18th Sept., 1897).—Educ. at Bombay Univ., and Cambridge (non-Collegiate); joined the service, 14th Nov., 1921; arrived, 7th Jan., 1922, and served in Central Provs. as asst. commr.; offg. dep. commr., April, 1925; and again, May, 1931; confd. (prov.), July, 1934; confd. May, 1936.

REGE, SHANTARAM BALKRISHN., B.A., B.E., Indian Service of Engrs. (exec. engr., Bombay) (b. 24th Oct., 1890).—Joined the provl. service as apprentice engr., 3th March 1915; asst. exec. engr., Aug., 1919; exec. engr., March, 1925.

REHMAN, MUHAMMAD BAZLUR, M.A., Ph.D., Bombay Educl. Service (prinpl., Ismail Coll.) (b. 11th Feb., 1896).—Joined the service as prinpl. Ismail Coll., Andhari, on probn., 7th Apr., 1930; confd., Aprl., 1932.

REID, ARTHUR BEATSON, C.I.E., B.A., Indian C.S. (secty. to govt., local self govt. and public health depts., United Provs.) (b. 16th March, 1888).—Educ. at Cheltenham Coll. and St. John's Coll., Oxford; apptd. after exam. of 1911; arrived, 23rd Nov., 1912, and served in the United Provs. as asst. mag. and collr. and asst. commr., under-sec. to govt., Oct., 1919; dep. sec. to govt., March, 1921; offg. dep. commr., Nov., 1925; addl. dep. secty. to govt. of India, educn., etc., dept., April, 1927; jt. secty., ditto, Dec., 1927; dep. secty. to govt. of India, dept. of educn., etc., Aprl., 1930; C.I.E., Jan., 1932; offg. secty. to govt. of India, dept. of educn., etc., Aprl., 1932; secty. to govt., United Provs., local self-govt. and public health depts., Mar., 1935.

REID, CECIL HUNTER, B.A. (Cantab.), late Indian C.S.—Educ. at Christ's Hospital, and Caius Coll., Cambridge; apptd. after exam. of 1900; arrived, 20th Dec., 1901, and served in Bengal as asst. mag. and collr.; jt. mag. and dep. collr., Feb., 1910; transfd. to Bihar and Orissa, April, 1912; dist. and sess. judge, March, 1916; ditto, and judl. commr., Chota Nagpur, Dec., 1916; retd., April, 1928.

REID, DOUGLAS FERNIE PATON, Indian Police (comdt., Aden) (b. 4th March, 1896).—Joined the service as asst. supt., Bombay, 15th Oct., 1923; offg. supt., May, 1930; offg. dep.-insp.-genl., W.I. States Agency, Mar., 1932; supt., Sept., 1935; comdt., Aden, July, 1936; under Col. govt., Aden, Aprl., 1937.

REID, FREDERICK DAVID, late Northern India Salt Revenue Dept., (b. 27th Sept., 1876).—Held non-gazetted appts. from 27th Sept., 1894; offg. asst. supt., June, 1897; confd. Oct., 1898; supt., Nov., 1902; Kaisar-i-Hind medal (1st class), Jan., 1905; asst. commr., Feb., 1914; genl. manager of the salt mines (tempy.), Feb., 1917; confd., Jan., 1924; dep. commr., Sept., 1925; retd., Sept., 1931.

REID, JAMES EDWARD, Indian Police (supt., Assam) (b. 5th Oct., 1894).—Joined the service as asst. supt., on 27th Dec., 1919; offg. supt., Nov., 1927; confd., June, 1928; personal asst. to inspr.-genl., Sept., 1929.

REID, Rev. JAMES POTTER, M.A., K.I.H., Indian Eccles. Estabt. (senr. chaplain) (b. 8th July, 1885).—Apptd. junr. chaplain, ch. of Scotland, 1st Jan., 1923; senr. chaplain, Jan., 1935; K.I.H. Medal.

REID, JOHN STUART, B.Sc., Indian Service of Engrs. (exec. engr., Bombay) (b. 30th Aug., 1904).—Joined the service as asst. exec. engr., Bombay, 1st Oct., 1927; offg. exec. engr., May, 1931; confd., Oct., 1936.

REID, Sir ROBERT NEIL, K.C.S.I., K.C.I.E., Indian C.S. (govr. of Assam) (b. 15th July, 1883).—Educ. at Malvern and Brasenose Coll., Oxford; apptd. after exam. of 1906; arrived, 25th Nov., 1907, and served in Bengal as asst. magte. and collr.; under-sect. to govt., rev. and gen. depts., Jan., 1912; ditto, and jt. mag. and dep. collr., and on special duty as sec., Calcutta port facilities cttee., Nov., 1913; I.A.R.O., Aug., 1916, to Feb., 1919; mag. and collr., Dec., 1919; K.i-H. medal, Jan., 1924; offg. secty., govt. of Bengal, agric., &c., dept., April, 1927; confd., Aug., 1927; offg. commr., Mar., 1930; C.I.E., June, 1930; offg. ch. secty. to govt., Bengal, Sept., 1930; confd., June, 1932; temp. member, exec. council, Bengal, June, 1932; and again, Dec., 1933; confd., Jan., 1934; C.S.I., Jan., 1934; K.C.I.E., Jan., 1936; K.C.S.I., Feb., 1937; govr. of Assam, Mar., 1937.

REID, Sir WILLIAM JAMES, K.C.I.E., C.S.I., late Indian C.S.—Educ. at Glasgow High Sch., and Emmanuel Coll., Cambridge; apptd. after exam. of 1889; arrived, 25th Nov., 1891, and served in Assam as asst. commr. and sub. divl. officer; personal asst. to chief commr., Nov., 1898, to March, 1899; dep. commr., Nov., 1900; mag. and collr., Jan., 1906; offg. sec., govt. of E. Bengal and Assam, fin. and municipal dept., April, 1907, to Feb., 1908; confd., March, 1911; chief sec. to chief commr., Assam, April, 1912; offg. commr., Surma Valley and Hill Dists., May, 1914; C.S.I., June, 1914; commr., Surma Valley and Hill Dists., Sept., 1914; addl. member of govr.-gen.'s legisl. council from Aug., 1916; commr., Assam Valley Dists., May, 1919; member of govr.'s exec. council, Assam, Jan., 1921, to Dec. 1925; K.C.I.E., June, 1924; offg. govr. of Assam, 11th April to 5th Aug., 1925, and from 11th Aug. to 8th Dec., 1925; ret., Nov., 1926.

REILLY, Sir BERNARD RAWDON, K.C.M.G., C.I.E., O.B.E., Lt.-Col., I.A., ret., late Indian Political Service (b. 25th Mar., 1882).—Served in mil. dep. until 17th April, 1908, when apptd. asst. pol. agent, Kolhapur; on deputn. as tutor and guardian to minor chief of Sangli, June, 1909; spl. duty, pol. dept., Bombay sectt., June, 1910; tutor and companion to Mir. of Khairpur, Jan. to July, 1911; asst. to resdt., Aden, April, 1912; O.B.E., June, 1918; actg. pol. resdt., Aden, May, 1925, and again June, 1926; C.I.E., Jan., 1926; pol. agent, Banas Kantha, Feb., 1928; 1st asst. resdt., Aden, Nov., 1928; offg. resdt. and C.-in-C., Aden, May, 1930; confd., Mar., 1931; ch. commr., Aden, Apl., 1932; K.C.M.G., June, 1934; ret., Mar., 1937.

REILLY, CALLCOTT, late Indian Finance Dept. (b. 1st June, 1875).—Joined the service, 21st March, 1900; asst. acctnt.-genl., April, 1915; examr. of customs accts., Bombay, Dec.,

1920; dep. acct.-genl., Bombay, Feb., 1927; ditto, Madras, Dec., 1927; ditto, posts and tels., Jan., 1929; ret., June, 1930.

REILLY, Sir HENRY D'ARCY CORNELIUS, late Indian C.S. (b. 15th Jan., 1876).—Educ. at Merchant Taylors' Sch., and Corpus Christi Coll., Oxford; apptd. after exam. of 1898; arrived, 20th Nov., 1899, and served in Madras as asst. collr. and mag.; head asst. ditto, Aug., 1909; regr., Madras High Court, July, 1910, to July, 1913; dist. and sess. judge, Dec., 1916; offg. judge, high ct., Madras, July, 1924; ditto, July and August, 1925; ditto, June, 1926; actg. addl. judge, ditto, May, 1927; judge, ditto, Dec., 1928; ret., July, 1934; chief justice in Mysore State; knighted, Jan., 1938.

REINHOLD, CARL HENRY, M.C., F.R.C.S.E., Col., late I.M.S. (b. 4th Dec., 1880).—First commn., 1st Sept., 1905; in milty. employ till Sept., 1908, and from Apl., 1909, to Apl., 1913; on mily. service, Aug., 1914, to Feb., 1921; civil surgeon, United Provs., Oct., 1920; ditto and jail supt., Feb., 1921; ch. medl. officr., Delhi, Apl., 1928; inspr.-genl. of civil hosp., Punjab, Dec., 1933; ret., Nov., 1937.

REMFY, CHARLES OLIVER, B.A., LL.B., Barr. (chief judge, Calcutta small cause ct.) (b. 19th Sept., 1877).—Apptd. offg. chief judge, small cause court, Calcutta, 1st Mar., 1925; confd., March, 1926; offg. judge, high ct., Calcutta, variously from June, 1928.

REMFY, MAURICE, late Judicial Dept., Bengal (b. 15th Nov., 1873).—Joined the service as dep. regr., 11th Dec., 1902; offg. regr. of insolvency, high ct., origl. side, June, 1915; confd., Feb., 1916; offg. regr., high ct., origl. side, May, 1921; confd., Nov., 1921; ret., Jan., 1933.

REMINGTON, JAME BOILEAU, V.D., Indian State Rlys. (divl. transportation supt.) (b. 12th Feb., 1891).—Joined the service, 14th Oct., 1910; asst. traffic supt., Oct., 1913; dist. ditto, June, 1917; asst. transportation supt., Nov., 1922; offg. divl. trans. supt., Jan., 1925; confd., April, 1926; offg. ch. trans. supt., May, 1933.

RENDELE, HUGH BAINBRIDGE, I.S.O., late Excise Dept., Madras.—Joined the service, 27th Aug., 1895; asst. commr., Sept., 1914; I.S.O. June, 1925; ret., July, 1928.

RENNICK, DENIS KINGSTON, M.B.E., Survey of India (supt.) (b. 25th Apl., 1884).—Joined the service, 4th Jan., 1904; extra asst. supt., Feb., 1912; supt., Oct., 1932.

RENNIE, Rev. JOHN YULE, M.A., B.D., B.Litt., Indian Eccles. Estab. (presdy. senr. chaplain, Bombay) (b. 10th Dec., 1892).—Apptd. chaplain on probn., 1st Dec., 1920; junr. chaplain, ch. of Scotland, Dec., 1922; senr. ditto, Dec., 1932; presdy. senr. ditto, Oct., 1937.

RENNISON, Rev. ERIC DAVID ROBERT, M.A., Indian Eccles. Estabt. (senr. chaplain) (b. 7th Oct., 1888).—Apptd. chaplain on probation, 14th Oct., 1922; junior chaplain, March, 1923 senr. ditto, Mar., 1929.

RENWICK, HENRY PERCIVAL, Indian State Rlys. (dep. trans. supt., G.I.P. Rly.) (b. 6th Jan., 1889).—Joined the service as asst. loco. supt., Nizam's G.S. Rly., 3rd Aug., 1912; dist. loco. and carr. supt., June, 1918; dist. loco. supt.

G.I.P. RLY., Aug., 1920; asst. trans. supt., Dec., 1922; supt., Feb., 1929; offg. divl. transportn. supt., Mar., 1933; offg. dep. transportn. supt., Oct., 1933; confd., Oct., 1936.

REUBEN, DAVID EZRA, Indian C.S. (dist. and sess. judge, Bihar) (b. 3rd Sept., 1893).—Educ. at Bombay and St. John's Coll., Cambridge; joined the service, 23rd Oct., 1918; arrived in India, 23rd Jan., 1919, and served in Bihar and Orissa as asst. mag. and collr.; offg. mag. and collr., July, 1925; offg. settlement officer, Feb., 1926; offg. dist. and sess. judge, Nov., 1928; confd., Oct., 1932.

REYNELL, CHARLES ARTHUR, late Indian Police (b. 30th May, 1886).—Joined the service as asst. supt., 26th Nov., 1905; dist. supt., May, 1915; King's police medal, Jan., 1918; dep. inspr.-gen., June, 1924; ret'd., Sept., 1933.

REYNELL, DOUGLAS, C.I.E., M.A., B.C.L. (Oxon.) late Indian Educl. Service (b. 23rd Nov., 1877).—Joined the service as inspr. of schools, 22nd April, 1919; asst. dir. of public instrn., Punjab, March, 1922; secty., public services commn., Aug., 1931, to Aug., 1936; offg. member, ditto, Aug.-Oct., 1936; C.I.E., June, 1936; ret'd., May, 1937.

REYNOLDS, CECIL HOWARD, Indian Police (supt., Bombay) (b. 22nd May, 1895).—Joined the service as asst. supt., Bombay, on 28th Nov., 1923; offg. supt., April, 1927; confd., Feb., 1935; on spec. duty, indus. dept., Mar., 1937.

REYNOLDS, FRANK, late Indian Police Service (United Provs.) (b. 10th May, 1877).—Joined the service, 21st April, 1899, and posted to United Prov.; asst. supt. of police, April, 1906; supt., Nov., 1912; ret'd., June, 1930.

REYNOLDS, FRANK ARMSTRONG ABBOTT, Indian Police (supt.) (b. 30th May, 1913).—Joined the service as prob. asst. supt., Burma, Nov., 1932; confd., Nov., 1934; supt., Jan. 1936.

REYNOLDS, HENRY FORSYTH, Indian Police (commr. of police) (b. 11th Nov., 1889).—Joined the service as asst. supt., 4th Dec., 1909; dist. supt., Dec., 1917; awarded King's police medal, Jan., 1930; offg. commr. of police, Rangoon city, Mar., 1932, and again, Dec., 1935; offg. dep. inspr.-genl., Dec., 1932; commr. of police, Rangoon, Dec., 1936.

REYNOLDS, HENRY OSBORNE, B.A., Indian C.S. (commr., Burma) (b. 11th Oct., 1883).—Educ. at Sedbergh Gr. Sch., Owen's Coll., Manchester, and Emmanuel Coll., Cambridge; apptd. after exam. of 1906; arrived, 25th Nov., 1907, and served in Burma as asst. commr.; asst. sec. to govt., Nov., 1911; asst. sec. to govt., rev. dept., Nov., 1911, to Nov., 1912; under sec., chief sec.'s dept., Feb., 1913, to Nov., 1914; dep. commr., March, 1918; offg. secy. to Govt., reforms office, Jan., 1922; secy. to govt. and dep. commr., Jan., 1923; confd., Aug., 1923; offg. sec. to govt., agric., excise and forest dept., Nov., 1925; confd., March, 1926; commr. of settlements and land records, Nov., 1926; offg. addl. secty. home and pol. dept., Oct., 1931; offg. commr., Apl., 1932; confd., Aug., 1935; finl. commr., Burma, Apl., 1935.

REYNOLDS, SIR LEONARD WILLIAM, K.C.I.E., C.S.I., M.C., late Indian C.S. (b. 26th Feb., 1874).—Educ. at Bradfield and Exeter Coll., Oxford; apptd. after exam. of 1897; arrived, 6th Dec., 1898, and served in the N.W. Prov. as asst. mag. and collr.; offg. asst. to govr.-gen.'s agent in Central India, May, 1902; offg. 1st asst. to ditto, April, 1904; confd., Feb., 1906; K-i-H. medal, 1st class, Jan., 1905; temp. asst. sec. to govt. of India, for. dept., June, 1908; confirmed, Nov., 1909; dep. sec., ditto, Nov., 1910, to Feb., 1914; C.I.E., Dec., 1911; press censor, War Office, Oct., 1914, to June, 1916; on spl. duty under resdt., Kashmir, Oct., 1916; commr., Ajmer-Merwara, Jan., 1917; spl. duty, Rajputana, Nov., 1918; resdt., W. Rajputana States, March, 1919; services lent to Jalpur Durbar from April, 1924; agent to govr.-gen. in Rajputana and ch. commr., Ajmer Merwara, March, 1927; C.S.I., June, 1928; attended Indian round table conf., London, Nov., 1930-Jan., 1931; K.C.I.E., June, 1931; ret'd., Dec., 1933.

RHIND, DONALD, B.Sc., Indian Agric. Service (economic botanist, Burma) (b. 26th Sept., 1899).—Joined the service as mycologist, Burma, 21st March, 1923; economic botanist, Dec., 1927.

RHODES, SIR CAMPBELL WARD, K.C.I.E., C.B.E. (b. 17th Jan., 1874).—C.B.E., Dec., 1919; Presdt., Bengal Chamber of Commerce and Presdt., Associated Chambers of Commerce of India and Ceylon, 1922; member of Indian fiscal commission; Kt., Jan., 1923; member of Council of India, 12th May, 1925 to May, 1935; vice-chairman, Ind. staty. rly. bd. ctee., June, 1933; member, Ind. res. bank ctee., July, 1933; K.C.I.E., June, 1935.

RHODES, CHARLES KENNETH, C.I.E., B.A., Indian C.S. (chief secty., govt. of Assam) (b. 5th May, 1889).—Educ. at Charterhouse, and Brasenose Coll., Oxford; apptd. after the exam. of 1912; arrived, 10th Dec., 1913, and served in Assam as asst. commr.; on mil. duty (I.A.R.O.), May, 1915, to July, 1919; under sec. to govt., Assam, Nov., 1920; asst. commr. and settl. officer, Nov., 1923; offg. secty. to govt. of Assam, fin. and rev. dept., Apl., 1929; confd. Dec., 1930; dep. commr., Dec., 1930; member of ctee. on public accts., Assam, 1933-34; offg., dep. secty., fin. dept., govt. of India, Apl., 1934; jt. secty., reforms office, govt. of India, Oct. 1934; C.I.E., Jan., 1936; chief secty., govt. of Assam, May, 1937.

RIAZ AHMAD, MIAN, B.Sc., Indian Forest Service (b. 24th Dec., 1903).—Joined the service as asst. consvr., Punjab, 22nd Jan., 1929.

RIAZUDDIN AHMAD KHAN, MUNSHI, B.A., Indian Police (supt., United Provs.) (b. 16th Aug., 1889).—Joined the service as dep. supt., 8th July, 1913; apptd. to I.P. as supt., Dec., 1934.

RICE, PERCIVAL RYAN, late Imperial Customs Service (b. 29th July, 1877).—Held non-gazetted appts. from 24th June, 1897; curator, comml. museum, comml. intell. dept. (temp.), July, 1915; confd., June, 1918; apptd. to office of Indian Trade commr. from Sept., 1919, to March, 1923; officer in charge, comml. intell. sec., British Empire exhib., April-Oct., 1924; offg. asst. collr. of customs, Calcutta,

Feb., 1925, and again, July, 1926; offg. collr. ditto, Chittagong, June, 1925; asst. collr., Calcutta, Jan., 1927; ditto, Bombay, Oct., 1930; ret'd., July, 1932.

RICH, WILLIAM HAROLD ADRIAN, Indian Police.—Joined the service as asst. supt., Punjab, 20th Nov., 1928.

RICHARDS, CECIL JOHN, Indian C.S. (dep. commr., Burma) (b. 8th March, 1894).—Educ. at Haileybury Coll., and Emmanuel Coll., Cambridge; joined the service, 28th Aug., 1920; arrived, 5th Jan., 1921, and served in Burma as asst. commr.; offg. settlt. officer, Dec., 1923; offg. dep. commr., Dec., 1927; confd., Apl., 1931.

RICHARDS, REV. GEORGE HENRY, Indian Eccles. Estabt. (junr. chaplain) (b. 24th Sept., 1892).—Apptd. junr. chaplain, 26th Mar., 1930.

RICHARDS, PHILIP BERNARD, A.R.C.S., F.E.S., Indian Agric. Service (entomologist to govt., United Provs.) (b. 31st May, 1886).—Joined the service as entomologist to govt. on 15th March, 1921; offg. dir. of agric., United Provs., May, 1934, and again May, 1936.

RICHARDS, ROBERT.—Educ. at the University Coll. of Wales, Aberystwyth and St. John's Coll. Cambridge; Lecturer at Glasgow Univ., and Head of Dept. of Economics and Extension Lecturer at Bangor Univ., M.P. for Wrexham division, Nov., 1922, to Oct., 1924; parly. under sec. of state for India, Jan. to Nov., 1924.

RICHARDSON, ALAN, Indian State Ry.s. (ch. mech. engr., G.I.P. Ry.) (b. 9th Nov., 1882).—Joined the service, 30th Nov., 1906; asst. carr. and wagon supt., Sept., 1910; dist. ditto, July, 1914; dep. transportation supt., April, 1925; offg. dep. ch. mech. engr. (C. & W.), March, 1927; dep. ditto (prov'l), Dec., 1927; offg. ch. mech. engr., Mar., 1933; confd., Apl., 1933.

RICHARDSON, ALEXANDER ARMITAGE, late Indian State Ry.s. (b. 8th Dec., 1885).—Trained at R.I.E. Coll.; joined the service as asst. engr., Jan., 1907; exec. engr., N.W. Ry., Jan., 1918; ditto, E.B. Ry., April, 1921; ret'd., April, 1933.

RICHARDSON, CHARLES RULAND SUGDEN, B.Sc., Indian Service of Engrs. (b. 29th Oct., 1889).—Joined the service as asst. engr., Oct., 1913; exec. engr., 1st Oct., 1922.

RICHARDSON, EDWARD ALBERT, M.A. (Oxon), late Indian Educl. Service (United Provs.) (b. 19th Nov., 1876).—Joined the service, 21st July, 1907, and apptd. inspr. of schs., United Provs.; asst. dir. of public instrn., ditto, July, 1912; offg. dir. of publi. instrn., ditto, May, 1919, to May, 1920; ret'd., Nov., 1931.

RICHARDSON, FRANCIS ALBERT ILFORD, Impl. Customs Service (asst. collr.) (b. 26th Aug., 1896).—Joined the service, 17th Mar., 1921; asst. collr., Mar., 1923.

RICHARDSON, HARRY MILLARD, late India Posts and Tels. (b. 1st Feb., 1878).—Held non-gazetted appt. from 7th Dec., 1896; offg. asst. supt., Aug., 1899; confd., Jan., 1900; with Govt. of Bombay on famine work, Apl., 1900; offg. supt., Sept., 1901; confd., Feb., 1904; postmaster, Oct., 1907; presy. postmaster, May, 1921; offg. P.M.G., Madras, Mar., 1931; presy. postmr., Bombay, July, 1931; ret'd., Mar., 1933.

RICHARDSON, HUGH EDWARD, B.A., Indian C.S. (b. 22nd Dec., 1905).—Educ. at Glenalmond and Keble Coll., Oxford; joined the service, 17th Oct., 1930; arrived in India, Nov., 1930, and served in Bengal as asst. mag. and collr.; jt. mag. and dep. collr., May, 1932; offg. asst. pol. agent, Loralai, Nov., 1934; British trade agent, Gyanse, July, 1936; pol. offcr., Sikkim, in addn., from May, 1937, to Nov., 1937.

RICHARDSON, MAURICE ROBERT, C.I.E., B.A., B.A.I., Indian Service of Engrs. (secty. to govt., P.W.D., irrign. bch., United Provs.) (b. 12th Dec., 1884).—Joined the service as asst. engr., 1st Oct., 1908; exec. engr., Oct., 1916; offg. suptg. engr., Aug., 1926; confd., Oct., 1931; offg. ch. engr., and jt. secty. to govt. p.w.d. irrign. bch., Nov., 1933; and again, Nov., 1934; confd., Aug., 1936; C.I.E., Jan., 1935.

RICHARDSON, SIR THOMAS WILLIAM, Kt., late Indian C.S., Barr. (1905).—Educ. at Brighton Coll., Cheltenham Coll., and New Coll., Oxford; apptd. after exam. of 1884; arrived, 8th Dec., 1886, and served in Bengal as asst. mag. and collr.; under-sec., gen., rev., and statistical depts., March, 1893; joint mag. and dep. collr., April, 1895; registrar, high court, appellate side, Dec., 1895; dist. and sess. judge, Mar., 1899; sec. to govt. of Bengal, judl. and gen. depts., Dec., 1906; offg. sec. to govt. of India, legisl. dept., March to Dec., 1907; acting judge, Calcutta High Court, Jan., 1909; dist. and sessions judge and supdt. and remembrancer of legal affairs, Sept., 1909; addl. judge, Calcutta high court, March, 1912; confirmed, Jan., 1913; Kt., Jan., 1922; ret'd., Nov., 1924.

RICHARDSON, WILLIAM NORMAN, O.B.E., late Bombay C.S.—Held non-gazetted appts. from 14th Feb., 1895, to 7th May, 1907; dep. coll., May, 1907; controller, hostile trading firms in Sind, from Dec, 1914; also custodian of enemy property; city mag., Karachi, Dec., 1919; ret'd., April, 1928; M.B.E., June, 1928; O.B.E., June, 1934.

RICHMOND, SIR ROBERT DANIEL, Kt., C.I.E., late Indian Forest Service (b. 29th Oct., 1878).—Apptd. asst. consvr. of forests, 16th Nov., 1901, and posted to Madras; dist. forest officer, March, 1907; principal, Madras forest coll., Coimbatore, Aug., 1913, to May, 1914; asst. inspr.-gen. of forests, India, Jan., 1919; consr., March, 1923; offg. ch. consvr., April, 1927; confd., June, 1928; C.I.E., June, 1932; ret'd., Nov., 1932; member, Madras public services commission, Nov., 1932; chairman, Jan. 1935; knighted, June, 1936.

RICKERBY, LEONARD WILLIAM ROBERT, Office of High Commr. for India (exec. officer, accts. dept.) (b. 1st Mar., 1901).—Apptd. clerical officer, 1st Mar., 1920, after previous service from 23rd Apl., 1919; exec. officer, Nov., 1934.

RICKETTS, ALFRED ARTHUR HICKMAN, M.I.E. (Ind.), Indian Service of Engrs. (suptg. engr., Burma) (b. 5th Jan., 1885).—Joined the service, 6th Oct., 1906; asst. engr., Oct., 1907; exec. engr., Oct., 1915; offg. supt. engr., May, 1930; confd., Sept., 1934.

RICKETTS, THOMAS CHARLES DONALD, M.C. (dep. consvr., Burma) (b. 3rd July, 1896).—Joined the service as asst. consvr., 17th Dec., 1923; dep. consvr., Dec., 1934.

RICKIE, JAMES HEPBURN, Indian State Rlys. (b. 25th July, 1882).—Joined the service as asst. engr. (temp.), 1st Nov., 1906; ditto (perm.), July, 1908; dist. engr., Sept., 1920; offg. dep. ch. engr., variously from May, 1926; confd., Mar., 1935; offg. dep. agent, Apl., 1932; ch. engr., Oct., 1935.

RIDLEY, SIDNEY, B.A., Indian C.S. (secty. to Agent Genl., for India in Sth. Africa) (b. 26th March, 1902).—Educ. at Royal Grammar School, Lancaster, and Sidney Sussex Coll., Cambridge; joined the service, 27th Oct., 1926; arrived, Dec., 1926, and served in Bombay as asst. collr., offg. collr. and dist. mag., Aug., 1929; offg. dep. commr., U.S. frontier, Oct., 1931; offg. dep. secty. to govt., Bombay, fin. dept., Jan., 1935; secty. to govt., fin. dept., Sind, Apl., 1936; secty. to Agent Genl. for India in South Africa, Oct., 1936.

RIDSDALE, Rev. ARTHUR CYRIL, D.D., late Indian Eccles. Estabt. (Bengal) (b. 24th March, 1877).—Joined the service, 11th Sept., 1905; junr. chaplain, Sept., 1908; senr. chaplain, Sept., 1918; retd., Feb., 1930.

RIEU, Sir JEAN LOUIS, K.C.S.I., late Indian C.S. (Bombay) (b. 23rd Nov., 1872).—Educ. at Univ. Coll. Sch., London, and Balliol Coll., Oxford; appt. after exam. of 1891; arrived, 20th Nov., 1893, and served in Sind as asst. collr. and mag.; 2nd asst., June, 1900; ditto, and manager, encumbered estates in Sind, Feb., 1903, to May, 1906; 1st asst., Dec., 1904; ditto, Bombay, March, 1905; junr. collr., Nov., 1909; sec. to govt., gen., educatl., &c., depts., Nov., 1911; senr. collr., and ditto, Dec., 1915, to Feb., 1916; senr. collr. and supt. of stamps, Feb., 1917; actg. sec. to govt., rev. and finl. depts., Feb., 1918; temp. addl. sec. to govt., ditto, July, 1918; C.S.I., June, 1920; commr., Feb., 1923; member, govt.'s exec. council, Bombay, Jan., 1926; K.C.S.I., June, 1929; retd., Oct., 1929.

RIGG, Rev. ARTHUR CECIL PIETRONI, M.A., Eccles. Dept., United Provs. (senr. chaplain) (b. 27th Feb., 1889).—Apptd. chaplain on probation, 16th April, 1917; junr. chaplain, April, 1919; senr. chaplain, April, 1929.

RIGG-STANSFIELD, Rev. HUBERT, Indian Eccles. Estabt. (b. 22nd Sept., 1897).—Apptd. junr. chaplain, 25th Dec., 1931.

RIJHWANI, JETHANAND MANDHANDAS, B.E., Indian Service of Engrs. (exec. engr.) (b. 4th June, 1903).—Apptd. asst. exec. engr., Bombay, 21st Apl., 1928; offg. exec. engr., Mar., 1933; confd., Apl., 1937.

RIKH, LIONEL JAIPAL, Indian Police (supt., United Provs.) (b. 21st Jan., 1892).—Joined the service as asst. supt., 26th Feb., 1921; offg. supt., March, 1924; confd., Nov., 1929.

RILEY, PERCY BROWN, M.R.C.V.S., Indian Veterinary Service (dir. vety. services, Bihar) (b. 13th Aug., 1887).—Joined the service as 2nd supt., civil vety. dept., Bihar and Orissa, 27th March, 1920; dep. dir., Dec., 1925; offg. dir., April, 1926, and again, Feb., 1928; confd., Nov., 1930; dir. vety. services, Bihar and Orissa, Nov., 1932.

RIPDAMAN, SINGH, Ind. Service of Engrs., (b. 6th Oct., 1908).—Apptd. assl. exec. engr., Bombay, 1st May, 1933.

RIPPON, STEWART ROSS, M.R.C.V.S., Indian Vety. Service (dep. dir., Burma) (b. 29th July, 1895).—Joined the service as supt., Burma, 18th Feb., 1924; dep. dir., Dec., 1929; offg. dir., July, 1932.

RISHWORTH, HENRY RICHARD, M.R.C.S., L.R.C.P., Indian State Rlys. (dist. medl. officer, G.I.P. Rly.) (b. 8th Feb., 1891).—Joined the service as dist. surg., G.I.P. Rly., 22nd June, 1923; dist. medl. officer, Apl., 1929.

RITCHIE, ADAM ALEXANDER, M.A., B.Sc., late Indian Educl. Service (b. 5th Jan., 1890).—Joined the service as asst. princpl., Aitchison Coll., Lahore, 21st Mar., 1915; asst. master, Mayo coll., Dec., 1927; vice-princpl., ditto, Jan., 1932; retd., Sept., 1937.

RITCHIE, CHARLES STEWART, late Indian State Rlys (E.B. Rly.) (b. 31st May, 1880).—Joined the service, 6th Dec., 1900; asst. traffic supt., May, 1907; dist. ditto, April, 1915; offg. dep. traffic manager, Nov., 1927; offg. dep. agent, Aug., 1929; retd., May, 1931.

RITCHIE, JOHN HALL, M.A., B.Sc., late Indian Agric. Service (b. 22nd Jan., 1891).—Joined the service as asst. dir. of agric., Oct., 1913; dep. ditto, Jan., 1915; offg. secty., Indian central cotton commee. Bombay, Feb., 1928; offg. princpl., agricl. coll., Nagpur, Apl., 1933; dir. of agric., Central Provs., Feb., 1934; ditto, United Provs., Nov., 1935.
