(b. 31st Oct., 1882).—Joined the service, 1st Oct., 1905, as asst. engr.; exec. engr., Oct., 1913; profr., civil engrg. coll., Roorkee, Nov., 1920, to Oct., 1924; offg. suptg. engr., Feb., 1925; confd., Apl., 1926; K-i-H. medal, 1st Class, Jan., 1926; offg. ch. engr. and jt. sec. to govt., P.W. dept., I.B., Apl., 1927; and again, Mar., 1931; confd., Nov., 1931; C.I.E., Jan., 1931; Knighted, Jan., 1935; ret'd. Oct. 1937.

STAMPER, THOMAS HENRY GILBORN, F.S.I., M.C. (consulting surveyor to govt., Bombay) (b. 27th Oct., 1884).—Apptd. asst. consulting surveyor to govt., Bombay, April, 1920; offg. consulting surveyor, Feb., 1926; confd., June, 1930; member, legisl. council, Oct., 1932.

STANBURY, GILBERT VIVIAN, late Indian Police Service (Madras) (b. 16th Sept., 1891).—Joined the service, 22nd Nov., 1911; asst. supt., Nov., 1912; offg. supt., May, 1919; offg. princpl., police training school, Vellore, July, 1925; supt. (provl. perm.), June, 1927; ret'd., Dec., 1928.

STANDEN, SIR BERTRAM PRIOR, K.C.I.E., C.S.I., late Indian C.S.—Educ. at Uppingham, and Trinity Coll., Cambridge; apptd. after exam. of 1886; arrived, 5th Dec., 1888, and served in Burma and the Central Provs. as asst. commr.; registrar, jud. commr.'s court, Nagpur, March, 1891, to March, 1892; settlt. officer, Dec., 1894; dep. commr., Aug., 1897; C.I.E., Jan., 1901; commr. of settlmt. and agric., Sept., 1904; dir. of agric., and regr., co-operative credit societies, April, 1908; chief sec. to chief commr., Nov., 1908, to Jan., 1911; commr., April, 1918; C.S.I., June, 1920; member of exec. council of govr. of Central Provs., Dec., 1920, to Dec., 1925; K.C.I.E., June, 1925; ret'd., June, 1926.

STANFORD, JOHN KEITH, O.B.E., M.A., M.C., Indian C.S. (b. 29th Apl., 1892).—Educ. at Rugby, and St. John's Coll., Oxford; on mil. service from Nov., 1914; apptd., 24th Oct., 1919; arrived, 24th Dec., 1919, and served in Burma; dep. dir. of comml. intellg., Calcutta, Jan., 1922; services replaced at disposal of govt. of Burma, June, 1923; under-sec., home and pol. dept., Dec., 1923; offg. dep. commr., June, 1924; regr., high ct., Rangoon, June, 1925; offg. dep. commr., June, 1927; confd., Sept., 1930; O.B.E., June, 1932; secty., rev. dept., Burma, June, 1932.

STANGER-LEATHES, HUGH ELLIS, Col., I.M.S., ret'd. (b. 1st Nov., 1878).—First commn. dated 30th Jan., 1904; in milty. employ up to 28th Feb., 1922; health officer, Delhi, March, 1922; dep. dir., public health, New Delhi, April, 1926; offg. dep. dir.-genl., I.M.S., March, 1928; confd., July, 1929; hon. surg. to the King, Sept., 1932; A.D.M.S., Madras Dist., Aug., 1933; ret'd., Aug., 1935.

STANIFORTH, WALTER HARLAND, India Office (suptg. executive officer, acclnt.-gen.'s dept.) (b. 21st June, 1886).—Apptd. second class clerk, India Office, 15th Aug., 1907; on mil. service from Nov., 1915, to Feb., 1919; minor staff clerk, Jan., 1920; actg. staff clerk, April, 1921; acting higher exec. officer, April, 1922; confd., Oct., 1925; also assessor of income tax; suptg. exec. officer Oct., 1936.

STANLEY, LORD, EDWARD MONTAGU CAVENDISH STANLEY, P.C., M.C., M.P. (partly under-secy. of state for India and for Burma) (b. 9th July, 1894).—Parl under-secy. of state for India and for Burma, May, 1937.

STANLEY, Lt.-Col. The Right Hon. Sir GEORGE FREDERICK, P.C., G.C.S.I., G.C.I.E., C.M.G. (b. 14th Oct., 1872).—Educated at Wellington and Woolwich; entered R.H.A. 1893; C.M.G., 1916; contr. of H.M.'s household, 1919; finl. secty. to War Office, 1921-22; M.P., Preston, 1910-1922; partly under-secy., Home Office, 1922-3; partly-secy., Ministry of Pensions, Nov., 1924 to June, 1929; P.C., 1927; G.C.I.E., Aug., 1929; govr. of Madras, 12th Nov., 1929, to Nov. 1934; actg. govr.-genl. of India, May to Aug., 1934; G.C.S.I. June, 1934.

STANSFIELD, Rev. HERBERT RIGG, Indian Eccles. Estabt. (b. 22nd Sept., 1897).—Apptd. junior chaplain, 25th Dec., 1931; name changed by deed poll to RIGG-STANSFIELD (q.v.).

STANTIALL, WILLIAM, C.I.E., late India Office (b. 13th June, 1865).—Educ. at the City of London Sch.; clerk, 2nd class, 25th Sept., 1882; junior clerk, July, 1898; senior clerk, Oct., 1903; sec., cttee. on Indian railway finance and administration, 1907-08; asst. sec. P.W.D., May, 1911; sec., ditto, Nov., 1919; C.I.E., Jan., 1923; rly. adviser and govt. dir., Indian rly. cos., Oct., 1926; ret'd., June, 1931.

STANTON, WILLIAM THOMAS, Indian State Rlys. (colliery manager) (b. 16th May, 1887).—Joined the service as asst. colliery manager, 15th Aug., 1919; colliery manager, Sept., 1931.

STAPLES, FRANCIS HAMMOND, M.A. (Oxon). Barr., late Indian C.S. (b. 1st July, 1884).—Educ. at Downside Coll., Bath, and St. John's Coll., Oxford; apptd. after exam. of 1906; arrived, 25th Nov., 1907, and served in the Central Provs. as asst. commr.; sub-judge, July, 1914; dist. judge, March, 1916; addl. dist. and sess. judge, Dec., 1916; dist. and sess. judge, June, 1919; addl. judl. commr., Dec., 1928; ret'd., July, 1936.

STAPLETON, HENRY ERNEST, D.Litt., B.Sc. (Oxon), late Indian Educatl. Service (b. 3rd May, 1878).—Joined the service, 16th Nov., 1900, as prof., presdy. coll., Calcutta; inspr. of schools, Oct., 1905; on mil. duty from May, 1915, to Dec., 1919; principal, Presy. Coll., Calcutta, July, 1924; offg. dir. of public instrn., Bengal, June, 1926; confd., July, 1930; ret'd., May, 1933.

STAPYLTON, JOHN MILES, late Indian C.S. (b. 13th Nov., 1896).—Educ. at Lancing Coll.; joined the service, 5th Sept., 1923; arrived, 16th Nov., 1923, and served in Central Provs. as asst. commr.; offg. dep. commr., Feb., 1927; dep. commr., etc., Port Blair, Apl., 1932; dep. commr. (subst.), Aug., 1936; ret'd.,

STARTE, HORACE WILDERSPIN, B.A., Indian Forest Service (consvr., Bombay) (b. 13th Mar., 1887).—Joined the service as asst. consvr., 1st Dec., 1911; dep. consvr., Dec., 1916; offg. consvr., Feb., 1929, and again, Sept., 1932; confd., May, 1935.

STARTE, OLIVER HAROLD BAPTIST, C.B.E., B.A., late Indian C.S. (b. 23rd Jan., 1882).—Educ. at Perse Grammar School, Cambridge, and Clare Coll., Cambridge; apptd. after exam. of 1905; arrived, 23rd Nov., 1906, and served in Bombay as asst. collr. and mag.; on spl. duty in connection with settlt. of criminal tribes in the Bijapur dist., from Nov., 1909; K.-i.-h. medal, 2nd class Dec., 1911; member, jail commn., 1919-1920; criminal tribes settlement officer, June, 1920; collr., Aug., 1926; C.B.E., June, 1932; ret'd., Apl., 1936.

STATHAM, RANDULPH MEVEREL, C.I.E., B.A. (Hons.), Indian Educl. Service (dir. pub. instrn., Madras) (b. 4th Sept., 1890).—Joined the service, 13th July, 1913; dep. dir. of public instrn., Madras, May, 1923; C.I.E., June, 1930; dist. eduel. officer, Sept., 1930; offg. princpl., teachers' coll., Saidapet, Sept., 1930; princpl., govt. training coll., Rajahmandry, Apl., 1932;

princpl., govt. Muhammadan coll., Madras, Oct., 1933; offg. educ. commr. with the Govt. of India, May, 1935; dir. of public instrn., April 1936.

STEAD, Sir CHARLES, Kt., C.B.E., M.V.O., late Indian Police Service (b. 30th Aug., 1877).—Joined the service, 17th Nov., 1898; supt. of police, July, 1906; M.V.O., Jan., 1912; awarded King's Police medal, Jan., 1915; personal asst. to inspr.-gen., Sept., 1915; O.B.E., Jan., 1921; asst. inspr.-gen., govt. rly. police, Punjab, March, 1922; C.B.E., June, 1922; offg. dep. inspr.-gen., April 1924; confd., Nov., 1925; offg. inspr.-genl. of police, N.W.F.P., May, 1927; ditto, Punjab, Feb., 1928; confd., May, 1930; Kt., Jan., 1931; retd., Aug., 1932.

STEADMAN, JOHN WILLIAM, Office of High Commr. for India (asst. chief acctg. officer.) (b. 3rd July, 1885).—Clerk to Rl. commn. on Indian decentralisation, June, 1908, to March, 1909; apptd. 2nd class clerk, India Office, 28th March, 1909, and attached to acctnt.-gen.'s dept.; clerk to royal commn. on public services, May, 1913, to Sept., 1915; asst. sec. to Jails Commee., from July, 1919, to Sept., 1920; minor staff clerk, Nov., 1920; services lent to high commr. for India from April, 1921; higher exec. officer, April, 1922; suptg. exec. offcr., June, 1927; asst. chief acctg. officer, Nov., 1937.

STEAVERSON, FREDERICK HENRY, late Indian C.S. (b. 19th Dec., 1885).—Educ. at Malvern, and Brasenose Coll., Oxford; apptd. after exam. of 1908; arrived, 18th Dec., 1909, and served in Burma as asst. commr.; asst. sec., rev. sec.'s dept., April, 1913, to Dec., 1916; jt. regr., co-op. societies, Nov., 1920; dep. commr., and ditto, March, 1925; offg. secty. to govt., forest dept., May, 1928; offg. dep. secty., home and pol. dept., Dec., 1928; ditto, edn. dept., Mar., 1929; offg. commr., July, 1933; retd., Nov., 1936.

STEEL, RICHARD FRANCIS, F.R.C.S., M.B., B.Ch., Lt.-Col., I.M.S., retd. (b. 28th Apl., 1878).—First commn., 31st Aug., 1903; apptd. civil surgn., Bombay, June, 1909; obstet. phys., J. J. Hospl., prof. of midwifry., G. M. Coll., April, 1923; supt., B. J. Med. School, Poona, Oct., 1925; retd., Apl., 1933.

STEELE, GROFFREY LANYON, late Salt Dept., Bombay (b. 23rd Dec., 1880).—Held non-gazetted appts., salt and customs depts., Bombay, from 15th Feb., 1900; asst. collr. of salt rev., Bombay, May, 1910; supt. of salt rev., Mar., 1924; asst. collr., Sept., 1926; retd., Oct., 1934.

STEEN, HUGH BARKLEY, M.D., Lt.-Col., I.M.S. (retd.) (addl. member, medl. bd., India Office) (b. 23rd Nov., 1875).—Joined the service, 20th Jan., 1902; arrived, 19th Apl., 1902; in mil. employ to Jan., 1906, when employed as civil surgn., Bengal; on mil. service, Sept., 1914, to Dec., 1915; prof., medical coll., Calcutta, Dec., 1916; retd., Nov., 1930; apptd. examr. of surgical instruments, India Store Dept., and addl. member, medl. bd., India Office, May, 1934.

STEIN, ARCHIBALD HYNDMAN, B.Sc., Indian Forest Service (dep. consvr., Central Provs.) (b. 17th Jan., 1903).—Joined the service as asst. consvr., 21st Feb., 1924; dep. consvr., Feb., 1928.

STEIN, Sir (MARC) AUREL, K.C.I.E., Ph.D., D.Litt., D.Sc., F.B.A., late Indian Archaeological Survey (b. 26th Nov., 1872).—Educ. at Budapest and Univs. of Vienna and Tubingen; principal, Oriental Coll., Lahore, and regr. of the Punjab Univ. from 1888 to May, 1899, when he was apptd. principal of the Calcutta Madrasah; proceeded on archl. tour with Buner Field Force, 1898; in 1900-01 deputed on special duty for archl. exploration in E. Turkestan; inspr. of schools, Punjab, Nov., 1901; inspr.-genl. of educn. and archl. surveyor, N.W. Frontier Prov. and Baluchistan, Jan., 1904; proceeded on a second archaeological and geographical expdn., Turkestan and West China, April, 1906; supt., archaeological survey, Eastern Circle, Oct., 1909; transferred to Frontier Circle, May, 1910; proceeded on spl. duty for explorations in E. Turkestan, W. China, Pamirs and Persia, Aug., 1913, to Feb., 1916; C.I.E., June, 1910; K.C.I.E., June, 1912; retd. June, 1929; author of 'Kalhanas Chronicle of Kashmir', ed. and translated (1892-1901); 'Sand-buried Ruins of Khotan' (1903); 'Ancient Khotan' (1907); 'Ruins of Desert Cathay' (1912); 'Serindia' (1922); various reports on his other explorations, &c.

STEIN, GEORGE LESLIE HAMILTON, Indian Police (supt., Bengal) (b. 4th March, 1890).—Joined the service as asst. supt., 26th Nov., 1909; supt., Oct., 1920.

STEIN, JOHN ALAN, A.M.I.C.E., Indian Service of Engrs. (suplg. engr., Bengal) (b. 31st Oct., 1888).—Joined the service as asst. engr., 1st Oct., 1912; exec. engr., Oct., 1921; offg. suptg. engr., Mar., 1931; and again, Apl., 1933; codfd., Mar., 1934.

STENT, PERCY JOHN HODSOLI, C.I.E., B.A., Indian C.S. (commr., Central Provs. and Berar) (b. 24th Nov., 1888).—Educ. at King's Coll. School, Wimbledon, and Pembroke Coll., Oxford; apptd. after exam. of 1912; arrived, 28th Nov., 1913, and served in the Central Provs. as asst. commr.; under-sec. to chief commr., May, 1916; serving under govt. of India, army dept., from Sept., 1916, to Aug., 1919; under-sec. to govt., Cent. Provs., Dec., 1920; offg. dep. commr. and dep. regr., co-operative societies, March, 1922; dep. commr., Dec., 1925; rev. secty. to govt., United Provs., Oct., 1933; offg. commr., Dec., 1933, confd., Mar., 1937; C.I.E., Jan., 1938.

STEPHEN, Sir HARRY LUSHINGTON, Bt., Barr. (1885).—Apptd. judge, high court, Calcutta, 18th Nov., 1901; knighted June, 1913; retd., Nov., 1914.

STEPHENS, CHARLES VIVIAN, A.M.I.C.E., A.M.I.M.E., Indian Service of Engrs. (exec. engr., Madras) (b. 28th Apl., 1887).—Joined the service as temp. engr., 25th June, 1910; perm. in provl. service, 1st Feb., 1920; asst. exec. engr. in I.S.E., 1st Oct., 1925; offg. exec. engr., May, 1926; confd., Nov., 1931.

STEPHENS, IAN MELVILLE, C.I.E., Dep. dir. of public information, govt. of India, Mar. 1930; dir., ditto, Aug., 1932 to Aug., 1937.

STEPHENS, JAMES, B.A., Indian Forest Service (dep. consvr., United Provs.) (b. 6th Sept., 1895).—Joined the service as asst. consvr., 30th Nov., 1923; dep. consvr., Nov., 1924.

STEPHENS, JOHN, Indian State Rlys. (transpn. supt., G.I.P. Rly.) (b. 22nd July, 1887).—Joined the service as asst. carr. and wagon supt., 3rd Apl., 1920; asst. transpn. supt., Apl., 1923; transpn. supt., July, 1930.

STEPHENS, STANLEY DONALD, Indian Police (asst. supt.) (b. 31st Aug., 1910).—Apptd. proby. asst. supt., Bengal, 3rd Dec., 1931; confd., Jan., 1934.

STEPHENSON, Rev. Canon HENRY STANLEY, M.A., late Eccles. Dept., Punjab.—Apptd. chaplain on probation, 14th Dec., 1906; junr. chaplain, June, 1907; senr. chaplain, June, 1917; ret'd., July, 1927.

STEPHENSON, Sir HUGH LANSDOWN, G.C.I.E., K.C.S.I., B.A., late Indian C.S. (adviser to sec. of state for Burma) (b. 8th April, 1871).—Educ. at Westminster, and Christ Church, Oxford; apptd. after exam. of 1894; arrived, 14th Dec., 1895, and served in Bengal as asst. mag. and collr.; under-sec. to govt. of Bengal, judl., etc., dept., Feb., 1901, to Nov., 1903; joint mag. and dep. coll., May, 1904; pte. sec. to lieut.-govr., Nov., 1903, to May, 1904, and from Nov., 1904, to April, 1905; mag. and collr., Nov., 1907; junr. sec., board of rev., Oct., 1910; sec., ditto, depts., Jan., 1912; duty with public services commn., Nov., 1912, to Feb., 1913; sec. to govt., finl. dept., Feb., 1913; C.I.E., June, 1913; services temply. lent to the War Office, 1916; services placed at disposal of govt. of India in the finl. dept., April, 1917; addl. sec., govt. of Bengal, Oct., 1917; on depn. to England in connection with Reforms Committee, Nov., 1918; C.S.I., June, 1919; chief sec., govt. of Bengal, May, 1920; member of exec. council of govr. of Bengal, March, 1922; K.C.I.E., Jan., 1924; actg. govr. of Bengal, June to Oct., 1926, and again, June-Sept., 1930; K.C.S.I., March, 1927; govr. of Bihar and Orissa, Apl., 1927 to Apl., 1932; govr. of Burma, Dec., 1932, to May, 1936; G.C.I.E., Jan., 1936; adviser to sec. of state for Burma, April, 1937.

STEPHENSON, HUGH SOUTHERN, Indian C.S. (under secty., home dept., Govt. of India) (b. 29th Nov., 1906).—Educ. at Winchester and Christ Church, Oxford; apptd. after exam. of 1930; arrived in India, Nov., 1931, and served in the U.P. as asst. magte. and collr; addl. under secty., fin. dept., Govt. of India, (temp.), Jan., 1935; confd., Oct., 1935; asst. secty. Niemeyer finl. enquiry, Jan., 1936; addl. under secty., home dept., Govt. of India, Nov., 1936; officer on spec. duty, ditto, Dec., 1936; under secty., ditto, April, 1937.

STEPHENSON, Rev. WILLIAM, B.A., Indian Eccl. Estabt. (b. 4th Mar., 1898) (junr. chaplain, Punjab).—Apptd. chaplain on probation, 15th Oct., 1927; junr. chaplain, Oct., 1929.

STERLING, THOMAS SMITH, M.B.E., M.A., late Indian Educl. Service.—Joined the service, 16th Oct., 1909, as profr., Presy. Coll., Calcutta; M.B.E., June, 1919; ret'd., Sept., 1928.

STEVENS, HAROLD SAMUEL EATON, M.A., Indian C.S. (secty. to govt., agric, etc., depts., Bengal) (b. 29th Nov., 1892).—Educ. at George Heriot's School, Edinburgh, and Edinburgh Univ.; joined the service, 23rd Aug., 1920; arrived, 7th Dec., 1920, and served in Bengal as asst. mag. and collr.; addl. dist. mag., Nov., 1925; offg. mag. and collr., Aug., 1926; dep. secty. to govt. of Bengal, pol. and apptt. depts., Mar., 1930; mag. and collr., June, 1931; offg. secty. to govt., Bengal, agric and indus., P.W. and irrig. depts., July, 1935; confd., Apl., 1936.

STEVENS, WILLIAM, Office of High Commr. for India (higher executive officer, accts. dept.) (b. 13th Mar., 1898).—Apptd. asst. clerk, 11th Nov., 1919, after previous service from 22nd Oct., 1915; on milty. service from Mar., 1915 to Mar. 1919; second class clerk, 29th April, 1920; services lent to high commr for India, from Oct. 1920; exec. officer, April 1922; acted as hr. exec. officer, 1st April to 10th July, 1926; confd., Oct., 1930.

STEVENSON, EDWARD THOMAS HOWARD, late Indian Police (b. 7th May, 1879).—Joined the service, 11th Dec., 1899, as probir.; asst. supt., July, 1902; supt., Dec., 1908; offg. dep. inspr.-gen., Feb., 1920; dep. inspr.-gen., Apl., 1925; ret'd., Aug., 1933.

STEVENSON, FREDERICK, M.B., Lt.-Col., I.M.S. (resdy. surg., Kashmir) (b. 1st June, 1884).—First commn. dated 1st Feb., 1908; offg. civil surg., D.I. Khan, Sept., 1920; agency surg., Gilgit, Nov., 1924; resy. surg., Kashmir, Oct., 1927; legation surg., Nepal, Oct., 1930; resy. surg., Kashmir, Apl., 1932; ditto, and C.M.O., Baluchistan, Feb., 1933; legn. surg., Nepal, Nov., 1935; resdy. surg., Kashmir, Apl., 1937.

STEVENSON, GEORGE, B.A., late Indian Service of Engrs. (Bihar and Orissa) (b. 2nd July, 1892).—Joined the service as asst. engr., 1st Oct., 1914; exec. engr., Oct., 1923; ret'd., Nov., 1929.

STEVENSON, Rev. GEORGE, M.A., Ind. Eccles. Estabt. (junr. chaplain).—Apptd. junr. chaplain, Burma, Dec., 1932.

STEVENSON, HENRY LOUIS, B.A., late Indian C.S. (b. 5th Dec., 1874).—Educ. at St. Paul's Sch., London, and Queen's Coll., Oxford; apptd. after exam. of 1897; arrived, 3rd Dec., 1898, and served in Burma as asst. commr., and asst. settlt. offr.; dep. commr., Dec., 1913; commr., Jan., 1923; offg. dev. commr. and addl. finl. commr. from June to Oct., 1924; chairman, Rangoon dev. trust, June, 1930; ret'd., Dec., 1933.

STEVENSON-MOORE, Sir CHARLES JAMES, K.C.I.E., C.V.O., late Indian C.S. (Bengal).—Educ. at Felsted Sch., and Emmanuel Coll., Cambridge; apptd. after exam. of 1885; arrived, 10th Dec., 1887, and served in Bengal as asst. mag. and collr.; joint mag. and dep. collr., Aug., 1894; also asst. settlt. officer, Dec., 1894; settlt. officer, Feb., 1896; mag. and collr., Jan., 1898; inspr.-genl. of police, Oct.,

1904; offg. dir. crimi. intell., June, 1907; chief sec. to govt., Bengal, Nov., 1910; C.V.O., Jan., 1912; member, board of rev., Jan., 1914; K.C.I.E., Jan., 1920; temp. member of govr.'s council, May, 1920; ret'd. Sept., 1921.

STEWART, ANDREW CHARLES, CAPT. I. A., Indian Political Service (b. 22nd Apl., 1907).—First commn., 29th Jan., 1927; in mil. employ prior to 6th Apl., 1933; Under Secty. to Pol. Resdt. in Persian Gulf, Apl., 1933; Offg. secty. to pol. resdt. in Persian Gulf, July, 1933; under secty. to the pol. resdt. in Persian Gulf, Nov., 1933; under training in the Punjab Jan., 1934; vice-consul, Meshed, Aug., 1935; under-secty. to pol. resdt. in Persian Gulf, Oct., 1936; asst. pol. agent, Bahrain (temp.), Dec., 1936; pol. officer, Kuwait, Apl., 1937; vice-consul, Khorramshahr, May—Oct., 1937.

STEWART, ALEXANDER DRON, C.I.E., M.B., F.R.C.S.E., Lt.-Col., I.M.S. (b. 22nd June, 1883).—Joined the service, 1st Sept., 1906; in mil. employ from 1st March, 1907, to 31st Jan., 1914; health offr., Simla, from 1st Feb. to 31st Oct., 1914; in mil. employ from Nov., 1914, to Nov., 1919; health offr., Delhi, Nov., 1919; dir. of public health laboratory, Calcutta, Dec., 1921; prof., ditto, Nov., 1926; offg. prof. of hygiene, sch. of tropical medicine, Calcutta, Jan., 1927; dir., public health institute, Calcutta, Oct., 1929; C.I.E., Jan., 1934.

STEWART, ALFRED JOSEPH, late India Office (b. 21st Feb., 1874).—Clerk, 2nd class, pol. dept., 27th Sept., 1892; transf. to registry and record dept., Jan., 1896; higher clerical officer, April, 1922; transf. to acctnt.-gen.'s dept., May, 1922; joint editor, 'India Office List,' May, 1926 to Apl., 1932; ret'd., Jan., 1935.

STEWART, CLYNE GARDEN, O.B.E., M.A., Indian Police Service (dir. defence bureau) (b. 6th July, 1888).—Joined the service as asst. supt., 4th Dec., 1909; dist. supt., Dec., 1917; awarded King's police medal, Jan., 1926; offg. dep. insp.-genl., Mar., 1932; O.B.E., June, 1932; offg. asst. insp.-genl., Feb., 1933, to Mar., 1936; dir., defence bureau, govt. of Burma, Apl., 1937.

STEWART, DAVID MACFARLANE, C.I.E., M.A., late Indian C.S.—Educ. at Hutcheson's Grammar Sch., Glasgow, Glasgow Univ., and Magdalen Coll., Oxford; apptd. after exam. of 1901; arrived, 3rd Jan., 1903, and served in the United Provs. as asst. mag. and collr.; under-sec. to govt., April, 1907, to Oct., 1908; jt. mag. and dep. collr., April, 1912; settlt. offr., Oct., 1914; dep. commr. (provl.), and ditto, Dec., 1914, to Aug., 1918; mag. and collr., Dec., 1919; commr. of income tax and opium agent (Ghazipur) in addn., March, 1925; C.I.E., June, 1927; ret'd., Feb., 1929.

STEWART, DONALD, O.B.E., M.A., B.Sc., Indian Forest Service (dep. consvr., United Provs.) (b. 14th March, 1894).—Joined the service as asst. consvr., 28th Dec., 1921; dep. consvr., Apl., 1925; O.B.E., May, 1937.

STEWART, SIR DOUGLAS LAW, Bart., late Indian Police Service (b. 1st June, 1878).—Joined the service as proby. asst. supt., 15th Sept., 1913; confd., Sept., 1915; offg. supt., Jan., 1919; confd., April, 1927; ret'd., Nov., 1931.

STEWART, ERNEST HENRY JAMES, M.C., B.Sc., Indian Service of Engrs. (exec. engr., Madras) (b. 5th June, 1893).—Joined the service as asst. exec. engr., 1st Oct., 1919; offg. exec. engr., March, 1925; confd., Oct., 1925.

STEWART, FRANCIS WILLIAM, C.I.E., M.C., Indian C.S. (collr. and dist. mag., Madras) (b. 26th Feb., 1885).—Educ. at Christ's Hosp. and Trinity Coll., Cambridge; apptd. after exam. of 1908; arrived, 24th Nov., 1909, and served in Madras as asst. collr. and mag.; sub-collr. and jt. mag., Sept., 1916; on mil. duty from Jan., 1916, to Nov., 1919; sub-collr. and asst. commr., Oriya agency, May, 1922; offg. variously as collr. and dist. mag., April, 1924, to April, 1928; collr. and dist. magte. (provl.), Oct., 1928; confd., Aug., 1929; C.I.E., Jan., 1931.

STEWART, GEORGE HERBERT, O.B.E., M.B., B.C.H. (Edin.), D.T.M. and H. (Edin.), D.P.H. (Cantab.), Lieut.-Col., I.M.S. (ret'd.), late Burma, Medical Dept. (b. 8th Feb., 1875).—Arrived, 7th Oct., 1899, and served in mil. dept.; offg. civil surg., May, 1903; civil surg., Dec., 1907; services placed at disposal of govt. of India, April, 1915, to Jan., 1920; O.B.E., June, 1919; ret'd., Sept., 1930.

STEWART, GERALD PAKENHAM, Indian C.S. (asst. commr., Assam) (b. 5th March, 1906).—Educ. at Portora Royal School, Enniskillen, and Trinity College, Dublin; joined the service, 13th Oct., 1930; arrived in India, Nov., 1930, and served in Assam as asst. commr.

STEWART, HERBERT RAY, F.R.C.S.E., D.I.C., N.D.A., Dept. of Agric., Punjab (dir. of agric.) (b. 10th July, 1890).—Joined the service as dep. dir. of agric., Dec., 1920; prof. of agric., Punjab Agric. Coll., Dec., 1923; actg. prin., ditto, March, 1927; offg. dir. of agric., May, 1927; on spl. duty, dept. of agric., Dec., 1927; asst. dir. of agric., Nov., 1928; offg. dir. of agric., April, 1931; confd., Dec., 1932.

STEWART, JOHN ALEXANDER, C.I.E., M.C., M.A., late Indian C.S. (b. 19th Jan., 1882).—Educ. at the Grammar Sch. and Univ., Aberdeen; apptd. after exam. of 1904; arrived, 10th Dec., 1905, and served in Burma as asst. commr.; on mil. service from May, 1915, to April, 1919, and from June, 1919, to Oct., 1919; dep. commr., Dec., 1917; offg. commr., March, 1927; confd., Nov., 1931; C.I.E., Jan., 1934; ret'd., Dec., 1935.

STEWART, PETER MCARTHUR, Barr., Indian Police (supt., Bombay) (b. 21st Oct., 1887).—Joined the service, 23rd Nov., 1907, as asst. supt.; supt. of police, Oct., 1920; offg. dep. insp.-genl., May, 1933; awarded King's police medal, Jan., 1934.

STEWART, SIR SAMUEL FINDLATER, G.C.I.E., K.C.B., C.S.I., LL.D., India Office and Burma Office (perm. under-secty. of state for India and for Burma) (b. 22nd Dec., 1879).—Educ. at Edinburgh Univ.; apptd. junior clerk, 19th Oct., 1903, and served in mil. dept. to Jan., 1910; reading clerk to Council, May, 1906; pte. sec. to permanent under-sec. of state, Jan., 1910; senr. clerk, fin. dept., May, 1911; on duty in mil. dept. from Nov., 1911, to Feb., 1912, and from Nov., 1914; asst. sec., mil. dept., June, 1918; C.I.E., June, 1919; dep. sec., mil. dept.,

April, 1919; joint sec., mil. dept., July, 1920; sec. to royal commission on the public services in India, May, 1923, to March, 1924; C.S.I., June, 1924; asst. under-sec. of state and clerk of the council, Sept., 1924; sec., Indian Statutory Commission, Nov., 1927; K.C.I.E., June, 1930; perm. under-secy. of state for India, 11th July, 1930; K.C.B., Jan., 1932; G.C.I.E., June, 1935; perm. under-secy. of state for Burma, Apl., 1937.

STEWART, SIR THOMAS ALEXANDER, K.C.I.E., C.S.I., M.A., B.Sc., Indian C.S. (member, govr.-genl.'s. exec. council) (b. 26th Feb., 1888).—Educ. at George Heriot's Sch., Edinburgh; Edinburgh Univ., and Univ. Coll., London; apptd. after exam. of 1911; arrived, 23rd Nov., 1912, and served in the United Prov. as asst. mag. and collr. and asst. commr.; on mil. service from July, 1918; actg. asst. collr., Imperial customs service, Jan., 1919; dep. commr. of rice, Rangoon, Jan., 1920; rice commr., July, 1921; offg. collr. of customs, Rangoon, Dec., 1923; collr. of customs, Madras, Oct., 1925; ditto, Bombay, Nov., 1928; collr. of salt revs., May, 1932; offg. addl. secy., commerce dept., Oct., 1932; offg. secy. to govt. of India, dept. of commerce, Mar., 1933; confd., Mar., 1934; C.S.I., June, 1935; member, council of state, Apl., 1936; K.C.I.E., Feb., 1937; member, govr.-genl.'s. exec. council, Apl., 1937; chairman, cent., adv. cl. for rlys., Nov., 1937.

STEWART - WILSON, Sir CHARLES, K.C.I.E., B.A., Barr. (1886), late Indian C.S.—Educ. at Edinburgh High Sch., and Univ. Coll., London; apptd. after exam. of 1883; arrived, 23rd Dec., 1886, and served in the N.W. Prov. and Oudh as asst. mag. and collr.; joint mag., Dec., 1890; under sec. to govt., Aug., 1891; from April, 1892, served as offg. postmr.-gen., N.W. Prov. and Oudh, Bengal (1893-94), and Bombay (1894); offg. dep. dir. of post office, Oct., 1894, and again April, 1897; postmr.-gen., Calcutta, April, 1896; ditto, Punjab, Jan., 1899; offg. dir. gen. of post office, Sept. to Dec., 1903, and from April, 1906; confd., Oct., 1906; dir.-gen., posts and telegraphs, April, 1912; K.C.I.E., Dec., 1911; retd., Aug., 1913.

STIBBS, JAMES CHARLES, O.B.E., India Office (dep. acctnt.-gen., acctnt.-gen.'s. dept.) (b. 5th Sept., 1878).—Educ. at Plymouth Corp. Gr. Sch., and Penzance Coll. Sch.; clerk, 2nd class, 5th Nov., 1897; staff clerk, March, 1919; junior clerk, April, 1921; suptg. exec. officer (acting), April, 1922; confd., Nov., 1922; also assessor of income tax; asst. acctnt.-genl., March, 1927; O.B.E., Jan., 1933; dep. acct.-genl., and clk. to the commrs. of inc. tax, Dec., 1934.

STIFFE, NORMAN CECIL, O.B.E., B.A., late Indian C.S. (b. 2nd Nov., 1872).—Apptd. after exam. of 1895; arrived, 16th Dec., 1896, and served in the N.W. Prov. and Oudh as asst. mag. and collr.; city mag., Lucknow, Dec., 1903; joint mag., June, 1906; asst. commr., July, 1908; forest settlt. offr., Oct., 1911; dep. commr., and ditto, Nov., 1913; mag. and collr., and ditto, Nov., 1914; mag. and collr., Oct., 1915; O.B.E., June, 1919; chairman, Cawnpore Improvement Trust, Dec., 1919; commr., Aug., 1924; retd. Dec., 1931.

STILEMAN, DONALD FENWICK, B.A., late Indian Forest Service (Madras) (b. 19th Aug., 1894).—Joined the service as asst. consvr., 15th March, 1921; dep. consvr., March, 1922; retd., March, 1930.

STIRLING, ALEXANDER GILCHRIST, India Office (exec. officer, milty. dept.) (b. 19th May, 1916).—Apptd. exec. officer, 10th Feb., 1936.

STIRLING, REV. JAMES CLERK PAUL, M.A., B.D., Indian Eccles. Estabt. Apptd. junr. chaplain Church of Scotland, May, 1931.

STOCK, THOMAS DAVID, D.I.C., A.R.C.S., B.Sc., late Indian Agric. Service (b. 19th June, 1891).—Joined the service as economic botanist, 24th Dec., 1919; dep. dir., Sept., 1923; offg. dir., Apl., 1926, and again, July, 1932; retd., Jan., 1936.

STOCKBRIDGE, Mrs. FRANCIS LUCIE, late Indian Educl. Service.—Joined the service as inspectress of girls' schools, Bombay and N.D., 1st Oct., 1922; retd., April, 1928.

STOCKER, CHARLES JAMES, M.C., M.D., Lt.-Col., I.M.S. (retd.) (b. 24th June, 1886).—First commn. dated 29th July, 1911; offg. agency surg., Mohmand frontier constabulary, March, 1923; agency surg., Meshed, April, 1926; offg. civil surg., Central Provs., Nov., 1927; retd., Aug., 1931.

STOCKWELL, FREDERICK RANDOLPH, Indian Police (asst. supt., United Provs.).—Apptd asst. supt., United Provs. on probn., 1st Nov., 1934; confd., Nov., 1936.

STODART, JAMES CARLYLE, B.A., Indian C.S. (judge, high ct., Madras) (b. 18th Feb., 1880).—Educ. at Campbell Coll., Belfast, Brasenose Coll., Oxford, and Trinity Coll., Dublin; apptd. after exam. of 1903; arrived, 30th Nov., 1904, and served in Madras as asst. collr. and mag.; sub-collr. and jt. mag., Nov., 1912; offg. dist. and sess. judge, Oct., 1919; dist. and sess. judge, July, 1924; addl. judge, ct. of resdt. of Hyderabad, Nov., 1932; offg. judge, high ct., Madras July, 1935; confd., July, 1936.

STOKES, Sir HOPETOUN GABRIEL, K.C.I.E., C.S.I., B.A., late Indian C.S. (b. 1st Aug., 1873).—Educ. at Clifton and Oriel Coll., Oxford; apptd. after exam. of 1895; arrived, 7th Dec., 1896, and served in Madras as asst. collr. and mag.; offg. under-sec. to govt. of India, Home Dept., July, 1903; confd., Jan., 1904; reverted to Madras, July, 1906; head asst., Nov., 1906; dep. sec. to govt. of India, Home Dept., Oct., 1909; ditto, fin. dept., May, 1911; C.I.E., Dec., 1911; fin. member, Impl. Delhi cttee., April, 1913, to July, 1914; acting private sec. to govr., Madras, March to Nov., 1915; collr. and dist. mag., Nov., 1915; sec. to Lord Hunter's cttee., 1919; offg. sec. to govt. of Madras, development dept., March, 1921, to Nov., 1922; confd. as third member, board of rev., Madras, June, 1924; second member, ditto, Aug., 1926; offg. as 1st. member, ditto, and ch. secy. to govt. variously from Aug., 1927; C.S.I., June, 1928; member, govr.'s. exec. council, Madras, March, 1930; K.C.I.E., Jan., 1934; retd., Mar., 1935.

STOKES, ISAAC WESTLEY, V.D., M.I.M.E., M.I.S.E., late Indian State Rlys. (G.I.P. Rly.) (b. 25th Mar., 1874).—Joined the service as signal and interlocking engr., 16th Jan., 1903;

asst. dep. traffic manager, June, 1917; dep. traffic manager, June, 1918; dep. transportation supt., Nov., 1922; ret'd., Mar., 1929.

STOKES, RICHARD CROSBY, Indian Police (asst. supt.) Bihar (b. 20th April, 1910).—Joined the service as, proxy. asst. supt., Bihar and Orissa, 26th Nov., 1929; confd., Oct., 1931; offg. supt., Sept., 1933.

STONE, BERNARD SIDNEY, Office of High Commr. for India (executive officer, store dept.) (b. 23rd Jan., 1898).—Apptd. asst. clerk, 30th July, 1919, after previous service from 22nd Oct., 1915; second class clerk, 29th April, 1920; transf'd. to office of high commr. for India, Oct., 1920; exec. officer, April, 1922.

STONE, Sir GILBERT, Kt., Barr. (ch. justice, high ct., Nagpur) (b. 6th June, 1886).—Apptd. judge, high ct., Madras, 30th Nov., 1930; ch. justice, high ct., Nagpur, Jan., 1936; Kt., Jan., 1936.

STONE, JULAND CHETWYND TALBOT WATKINS, Indian Service of Engrs. (exec. engr., Central Provs.) (b. 17th June, 1886).—Joined the service, 5th June, 1911; asst. engr., Nov., 1915; exec. engr., July, 1924.

STONE, WILFRED JOHN, Indian State Railways (bridge engr., Burma rlys.) (b. 5th May, 1884).—Joined the service, 28th Aug., 1925; bridge engr., Jan., 1928.

STONEY, RICHARD FRANCIS, late Indian Service of Engrs. (Madras) (b. 17th Apl., 1876).—Trained at R.I.E. Coll.; joined the service, 1st Oct., 1897, as asst. engr.; exec. engr., Oct., 1907; suptg. engr. (offg.), April, 1919; suptg. engr., March, 1924; offg. ch. engr., April, 1925; confd., Feb., 1929; ret'd., Apl., 1931.

STOREY, CHARLES AMBROSE, M.A., late India Office (b. 21st Aug., 1888).—Educ. at Rossall and Trinity Coll., Cam ridge (Porson Prize, Tyrwhitt Schol., Allen Schol.); prof. of Arabic, Muhammadan Coll., Aligarh, 1914, to 1919; asst. librarian, India Office, 1st June, 1919; libraia, ditto, Sept., 1927; has edited 'The Fakhir of al-Mufaddal'; ret'd., Aug., 1933.

STORK, HERBERT CECIL, B.A., Indian C.S. (dist. and sess. judge, Bengal) (b. 28th June, 1890).—Educ. at Merchant Taylors' Sch., and Queen's Coll., Oxford; apptd. after exam. of 1913; arrived, 18th Dec., 1914, and served in Bengal as asst. mag. and collr.; I.A.R.O., Aug., 1916, to Feb., 1919; regr., high ct., Calcutta, appellate side, Jan., 1925; offg. first land acquis. officer, Calcutta, Nov., 1930; dist. and sess. judge, Assam Valleys Districts, Mar., 1937.

STORR, NORMAN, Indian C.S. (jt. mag., United Provs.) (b. 9th Dec., 1907).—Educ. at Holgate Grammar School, Barnsley, and Keble College, Oxford; joined the service, 14th Oct., 1930; arrived in India, November, 1930, and served in the United Provs. as asst. mag. and collr.

STORRS-FOX, Rev. EDWIN AUBREY, M.A., Indian Eccles. Estabt. (senr. chaplain, Punjab) (b. 27th Jan., 1888).—Apptd. chaplain on probation, 18th March, 1916; junr. chaplain, Oct., 1919; senr. ditto, Oct., 1920.

STOTT, HUGH, O.B.E., M.D., Lt.-Col., I.M.S. (insp. genl. civil hosp., Bihar) (b. 18th July, 1884).—First commn., dated 1st Aug., 1908; apptd. surg. to H.E. the Govr. of Madras, 5th Feb., 1913; on mil. duty from Nov., 1914; O.B.E., June, 1918; prof., King George's medical coll., Lucknow, Nov., 1922; offg. prinpl., ditto, Aug., 1925, and again, Aug., 1927; employed under Lucknow Univ. from Nov., 1927; insp. genl. civil hosp., Bihar, Nov., 1937.

STOTT, RAYMOND, Home dept., govt. of India (govt. examr. of questioned documents) (b. 26th Aug., 1890).—Joined the service, 31st May, 1915; on milty. duty up to 17th Sept., 1920; asst. examr. of questioned documents, Sept., 1920; offg. govt. examr. of questioned documents, Mar., 1923; confd., June, 1926.

STOW, VINCENT AUBREY STEWART, C.I.E., M.A., Indian Educ. Service (principal, Mayo Coll.) (b. 27th July, 1883).—Educ. at Winchester, and Exeter Coll., Oxford; asst. master, Marlborough Coll., 1906; asst. master, Daly Coll., Indore, Feb., 1907; services placed at the disposal of the Ch. Commr., C.P., from 24th Aug., 1912; principal, Rajkumar Coll., Raipur, Aug., 1912; on milty. service, 1918-19; prinpl., Mayo Coll., Ajmer, July, 1931; C.I.E., June, 1934.

STOWELL, ARTHUR TERENCE, C.I.E., V.D., late Indian State Rlys. (N.W. Rly.).—Joined the service, 5th Feb., 1895; asst. traffic supt., April, 1897; dist. traffic supt., April, 1906; asst. sec., rly. board, Oct., 1914; dep. traffic manager, and ditto, April, 1915; dep. controller of traffic, rly. bd., Jan., 1918; controller of despatches, Sept., 1918; dep. controller of rly. transportn., May, 1919; traffic manager (tempy.), Oct., 1919; chief operating supt., Oct., 1924; C.I.E., Jan., 1928; ret'd., May, 1928.

STRACEY, HERBERT JAMES, late Indian State Rlys. —Joined the service as senr. dep. agent, E.I. Rly., 13th Aug., 1920; ret'd., Dec., 1928.

STRACEY, HUGH AUBREY ST. CLAIR, Indian Police (asst. supt.).—Apptd. asst. supt., United Provs., 27th Jan., 1934.

STRACEY, PATRICK DONALD, Indian Forest Service (b. 31st Jan., 1906).—Joined the service as asst. consvr., Assam, 9th Nov., 1930.

STRACEY, RALPH AUGUSTINE ST. CLAIR, B.A., Indian C.S. (jt. mag. and dep. collr., Bengal) (b. 30th Aug., 1909).—Educated at St. Joseph's College, Bangalore, Madras University, and the School of Oriental Studies, London; joined the service, 5th October, 1933; arrived in India, November, 1933, and served in Bengal as asst. mag. and collr.; asst. pte. secty. to govt., Bengal, Feb. 1936.

STRAKOSCH, SIR HENRY, G.B.E., India Office (adviser to the sec. of state for India) (b. 9th May, 1871). Member of R.C. on Indian currency and finance, 1925; G.B.E., 1927; member of Council of India, Oct., 1930; member of Ind. del. at Imp. Econ. conf., July, 1932; ditto, monetary and economic conf., London, June, 1933; ditto, Ind. res. bank ctee., July, 1933; adviser to the sec. of state for India, Apl., 1937.

STRAND-JONES, Rev. JOHN, B.A., late Indian Eccles. Estabt. (Punjab) (b. 2nd Dec., 1877).—Apptd. chaplain on probation, 21st Nov., 1909; junr. chaplain, Nov., 1912; senr. chaplain, Nov., 1922; ret'd., Apl., 1931.

STRANGE, FRANK HORACE LUMSDEN, Indian State Rlys. (dep. ch. operating supt., N.W. Rly.) (b. 12th Aug., 1884).—Joined the service as asst. loco. supt., 26th Dec., 1910; on milty. duty, 20th Jan., 1916; dist. loco. supt., April, 1918; divl. rolling stock officer, Oct., 1924; pers. asst. to C.M.E., N.W. Rly., Feb., 1929; offg. dep. ch. mechl. engr., Feb., 1932; confd., Oct., 1936; offg. ch. mech. engr., Aug., 1936; confd., Nov., 1936; dep. ch. operating supt., Nov., 1936.

STRANGE, WILLIAM HOOD, late Indian State Rlys. (b. 24th Nov., 1878).—Held non-gazetted appt. from 14th Feb., 1914, to 19th Aug., 1918; offg. dist. cart. and wagon supt., Aug., 1918; confd., Oct., 1920; supt., Feb., 1925; ret'd., Jan., 1932.

STRANGMAN, Sir THOMAS JOSEPH, Kt., B.A., LL.B., Barr.—Apptd. advocate-genl., Bombay, 23rd Oct., 1908; addl. member of govr.'s legisl. council, Oct., 1908; resigned, April, 1915; reappointed advocate-genl., Dec., 1916; knighted, Jan., 1920; ret'd., May, 1922.

STRATFORD, Miss LENNA, B.A., M.B.E., late Indian Educl. Service (b. 9th Aug., 1877).—Joined the service, 1st June, 1903; senr. inspectress, July, 1910; chief inspectress, Oct., 1913; M.B.E., June, 1919; dep. directress of public instrn., Punjab, Oct., 1923; awarded Kaisar-i-Hind medal, 1st class, Jan., 1927; ret'd., Aug., 1933.

STRATHIE, DAVID NORMAN, M.A., Indian C.S. (inspr. of municipal cls.) (b. 31st Oct., 1886).—Educ. at Glasgow Academy, and Balliol Coll., Oxford; apptd. after exam. of 1910; arrived in India, 22nd Jan., 1911, and served in Madras as asst. collr. and mag.; under-sec. to govt., July, 1917; under-sec. to govt. of India, comm. and ind. dept., 1918; sub-collr. and jt. mag., Madras, May, 1919; commr. of income tax, Madras, June, 1923; offg. collr. and secty. to govt., rev. dept., June, 1928; offg. regr., co-operative societies, Dec., 1929; collr., Mar., 1930; confd., Oct., 1931; inspr. mun. cls. and local boards, May, 1937.

STRATTON, Rev. BASIL, M.A., Indian Eccles. Estabt. (United Provs.) (Junr. Chaplain) (b. 27th Apl., 1906).—Apptd. chaplain on probation, 15th Sept., 1935; junr. chaplain, Sept., 1937.

STREATFEILD, Rev. SIDNEY FREMLYN, B.A., Eccles. Dept., Central Provs. (junr. chaplain) (b. 11th Oct., 1887).—Apptd. garrison chaplain on probation, 25th June, 1930; junr. chaplain, June, 1932.

STREATHFIELD, TERENCE ERNEST, B.A., Indian C.S. (asst. collr., Bombay) (b. 18th Feb., 1906).—Educ. at Sutton Valence School, and St. John's Coll., Cambridge; joined the service 12th Oct., 1928; arrived, Nov., 1928, and served in Bombay as asst. collr.; offg. under secty. to govt., rev. dept., Bombay, Jan., 1934; confd., June, 1934; offg. collr., June, 1936.

STREET, WALTER, O.B.E., late Burma Commn. (b. 19th May, 1905).—Held various appts. in India prior to appt. as asst. commr. on 12th April, 1907; on mil. service, Nov., 1917, to Feb., 1919; O.B.E., July, 1920; depy. commr., Sept., 1921; ret'd., May, 1931.

STRELLEY, RICHARD COLLIN, Indian Police (b. 30th June, 1905).—Joined the service as asst. supt., Bengal, 16th Nov., 1925; offg. addl. supt., Apl., 1932, and again, May, 1933.

STRICKLAND, CLAUDE FRANCIS, C.I.E., B.A., late Indian C.S. (Punjab) (b. 19th Dec., 1881).—Educ. at Winchester and New Coll., Oxford; apptd. after exam. of 1904; arrived, 27th Nov., 1905, and served in the Punjab as asst. commr.; supt., Mandi State, Dec., 1913, to Feb., 1914; dep. commr., Dec., 1919; offg., commr., N.I. salt rev., April, 1921; dep. regr., co-op. societies, Punjab, Nov., 1922; regr., ditto, June, 1926; with govt. of F.M.S., from Jan., 1929; ret'd., June, 1930; C.I.E., Jan., 1931; lecturer on "Indian Social Welfare" to I.C.S. probationers at Oxford Univ., Nov., 1937.

STRINGER, JOHN ALFRED, late India Audit Office (b. 1st Nov., 1877).—Educ. at Macclesfield Gr. Sch.; clerk, second class, 26th March, 1897; staff clerk, Oct., 1915; junior clerk, Feb., 1922; higher exec. officer, April 1922; suptg. exec. officer, April, 1922; ret'd., Aug., 1933.

STRONACH, JOHN LINDSAY, late Indian Police Service (United Provs.) (b. 15th Mar., 1886).—Joined the service as asst. supt., 1st Dec., 1906; supt., May, 1920; ret'd., Dec., 1929.

STRONG, FREDERICK WILLIAM, B.A., late Indian C.S. (b. 12th Sept., 1873).—Educ. at Bedford Gr. Sch., Selwyn Coll., Cambridge, and Univ. Coll., London; apptd. after exam. of 1896; arrived, 9th Dec., 1897, and served in Bengal as asst. mag. and coll.; transfed. to Assam as asst. commr. and sub-divl. offr., May, 1899; under-sec. to chief commr., April, 1904, to Nov., 1905; joint mag. and dep. collr., E. Bengal and Assam, Jan., 1906; mag. and collr., March, 1909; re-transfed. to Bengal, April, 1912; mag. and dep. commr., Dec., 1916; offg. commr. of income tax, Bengal, March, 1926; confd., Nov., 1926; ret'd., Sept., 1932.

STUART, DONALD NEIL, Lt.-Col., I.A., (ret'd.), late Burma Commn. (b. 8th Oct., 1881).—Arrived in India, Nov., 1902, and served in mil. dept.; asst. commr., May, 1906; on mil. service from Dec., 1914, to Feb., 1922; dep. commr., Oct., 1921; offg. commr., March, 1927; ret'd., Aug., 1930.

STUART, Miss HELEN GORDON, M.A., O.B.E., late Indian Educl. Service (b. 1st Sept., 1877).—Joined the service as chief inspectress of girls' schools, 26th Oct., 1908; O.B.E., June, 1925; ret'd., Sept., 1931.

STUART, JAMES DOUGLAS, A.M.I.C.E., M.I.E. (Ind.), late Indian Service of Engrs. (Burma) (b. 3rd Oct., 1875).—Trained at R.I.E. Coll.; joined the service, 1st Oct., 1896, and served in the Punjab as asst. engr.; transfed. to Burma, Nov., 1903; exec. engr., July, 1907; asst. to chf. engr. and under-sec. to govt. P.W.D., Burma, Oct., 1914; suptg. engr., Oct., 1919; offg. chief engr. and sec. to govt., May, 1923; confd., Jan., 1926; ret'd., Oct., 1930.

STUART, JOHN MATTHEW BLACKWOOD, C.I.E., B.A., B.A.I., T.C.D., A.M.I.C.E. (b. 14th Mar., 1880).—Joined the service as asst. engr., 1st Oct., 1905; exec. engr., Oct., 1913; on milty. service, May, 1913, to March, 1920; asst. to ch. engr., irrig. branch, and under secty. to govt. of Burma, Nov., 1920; offg. supt. engr., Nov., 1923; confd., Oct., 1927; offg. ch. engr., Apl., 1930; confd., Sept., 1930; C.I.E., June, 1934; ret'd., Mar., 1935.

STUART, Sir Louis, Kt., C.I.E., late Indian C.S. (United Provs.) (b. 12th March, 1870).—Educ. at the Charterhouse, and Balliol Coll., Oxford; apptd. after exam. of 1889; arrived, 17th Dec., 1891, and served in the N.W. Prov. and Oudh as asst. mag. and collr., and asst. commr.; joint mag., March, 1897; asst. supt. and judge, small cause court, April, 1898; joint mag., Feb., 1901; dist. and sessions judge, Jan., 1906; mag. and collr., June, 1910; sec. to govt., June, 1910; dist. and sess. judge, April, 1912; first addl. judl. commr., Oudh, May, 1914; offd. as judge, high ct., Allahabad, Oct. to Nov., 1916, April to Aug., 1919, and from Feb., 1921; C.I.E., June, 1919; apptd. judge, high ct., Allahabad, Oct., 1922; chief judge, Oudh chief ct., Nov., 1925; Kt., Jan., 1926; ret'd., Mar., 1930.

STUART, MALCOLM MONCRIEFF, Indian C.S. (jt. mag. and collr., Bengal) (b. 21st May, 1903).—Educ. at Sedbergh, St. John's Coll., Cambridge, and Queen's Coll., Oxford; joined the service, 21st Jan., 1927; arrived, Feb., 1927, and served in Bengal as asst. mag. and collr.; offg. mag. and collr., variously from April, 1932.

STUART, Sir Robert Charles Ochil-tree, K.C.S.I., Major-Gen., R.A. (ret'd.).—First commn., 19th May, 1880; served in Burma, 1885-87; ordce. dept. in India from Nov., 1889; asst. dir.-genl. of ordce. in India, Jan., 1898; dep. dir.-genl., June, 1904; ordce. consg. offr. for India, India Office, 2nd July, 1906; dir.-gen. of ordce. in India, May, 1911; C.S.I., June, 1915; K.C.S.I., Jan., 1917; member of Indian munitions bd., 1917-19; ret'd., Aug., 1919.

STUART, Ulick Charles (b. 25th Feb., 1882).—Held non-gazetted appts. from 4th March, 1901; offg. supt., govt. of India, home dept., 30th April, 1917, to April, 1918, and from June, 1918, to Oct., 1919; confd., Oct., 1919; asst. sec., home dept., Oct., 1920; supt., ditto, April, 1921; asst. sec., ditto, March, 1923; ret'd., Dec., 1933.

STUART-HORNER, BERNARD, Capt., I.A., ret'd., late Indian Political Dept. (b. 5th Dec., 1889).—First commn. dated 5th May, 1915; on mil. duty up to 13th April, 1920; asst. pol. agent, Loralai, Nov., 1920; first asst. to resdt., Baroda, March, 1921; under-sec. to agent to govr.-gen., Rajputana, April, 1923; sec. to pol. resdt. in Persian Gulf, Nov., 1924; supy. asst. commr., Hazara, Nov., 1928; dep. commr., ditto, Mar., 1929; divl. and sess. judge, Deraj at Dec., 1929; ret'd., Dec., 1932.

STUBBS, JOHN LAWRENCE COMMERELL, Indian C.S. (jt. mag., United Provs.) (b. 23rd May, 1908).—Educ. at Radley and New College, Oxford; joined the service, 9th Oct., 1930; arrived in India, Nov., 1930, and served in the United Provs. as asst. mag. and collr.

STUBBS, LAWRENCE MORLEY, C.S.I., C.I.E., M.A., late Indian C.S. (b. 23rd March, 1874).—Educ. at Winchester, and New Coll., Oxford; apptd. after exam. of 1897; arrived, 6th Dec., 1898, and served in the N.W. Prov. as asst. mag. and collr.; joint mag., July, 1907; asst. commr., Oct., 1911; joint mag., April, 1913; dep. commr., Oct., 1914; mag. and collr., May, 1918; C.I.E., June, 1922; offg. commr., March to Nov., 1922, and from April, 1924; confd., Aug., 1926; C.S.I., June, 1931; ret'd., Dec., 1933; author of a monograph on ivory-carving.

STUBBS, SHIRLEY GRAHAM, O.B.E., Indian Service of Engrs. (chief engr. and secty., p.w.d. (b.r.), Punjab) (b. 14th Sept., 1884).—Joined the service as asst. engr., 1st Oct., 1906; exec. engr., June, 1917; on mil. duty from 27th July, 1918, to 19th Apl., 1919; O.B.E., June, 1919; offg. supt. engr., May, 1932; confd., Apl., 1933; offg. ch. engr., and secty. to govy. (blds. and roads bch.), Punjab, Apl., 1935; confd., Apl., 1937.

STUDDERT-KENNEDY, REV. HENRY GERALD, M.A., Indian Eccles. Estabt. (junr. chaplain).—Apptd. chaplain on probn. Punjab, 7th Mar., 1932; junr. chaplain, Mar., 1934.

STUDDY, HENRY, Indian Police (supt., Bihar) (b. 20th Apl., 1894).—Joined the service as asst. supt., 22nd Dec., 1914; supt., Oct., 1923; persl. asst. to inspr.-gen., Mar., 1925, to Oct., 1926.

STURDY, WILLIAM ARTHUR, C.B.E., late India Audit Office (b. 6th Mar., 1877).—Educ. at Leeds Gr. Sch.; clerk, 2nd class, 27th Dec., 1894; junr. clerk, July, 1905; senior clerk, Nov., 1913; deputed to India in connection with compilation of war accounts, March to June, 1915; asst. to auditor, Oct., 1915; auditor, Dec., 1921; C.B.E., June, 1928; ret'd., Mar., 1934.

STURGIS, GERALD CLARE, Indian Police (b. 4th Sept., 1892).—Joined the service as proby. asst. supt., 28th Nov., 1912; confd., July, 1917; supt., May, 1925; prinpl., police training coll., Sardah, Mar., 1929.

SUBBA RAO, MYSORE, B.A., Indian C.S. (jt. mag. and dep. collr., Orissa) (b. 25th Oct., 1907).—Educ. at School of Economics, London, and School of Oriental Studies, London; joined the service, 13th Oct., 1931; arrived in India, Nov., 1931, and served in Bihar and Orissa as asst. mag. and collr.

SUBBA RAO, VELLAMURI VENKATA, B.A., B.L., Judl. Dept., Madras (dist. and sess. judge) (b. 20th Oct., 1885).—Joined the service, 2nd Aug. 1906; sub-judge, Aug., 1931; dist. and sess. judge, Oct., 1935.

SUBBARAYAN, VIRARAGHAVA, Indian Police (b. 28th Nov., 1902).—Joined the service as asst. supt., Madras, 12th April, 1926; offg. dist. supt., April, 1931.

SUBBAYYA NAYUDU, RAMISETTI, Rao Bahadur, Madras C.S. (sub. collr.) (b. Mar., 1885).—Joined the service, 30th Apl., 1905; dep. collr., Oct., 1918; sub.-collr., Aug., 1932; offg. collr., June, 1931; joint secty., local self. govt. dept., Madras, Sept., 1934.

SUBERWAL, BANKE LAL, B.Sc., Indian Service of Engrs. (suptg. engr., Bengal) (b. 14th Aug., 1889).—Joined the service, 1st Oct., 1913; asst. engr., Dec., 1913; exec. engr., Oct., 1922; offg. suptg. engr., June, 1933; confd., Jan., 1936.

SUBHEDAR, GANPAT LAXMAN, B.A., Barr., Judicial Dept., Central Provs. (b. 26th Apr., 1876).—Apptd. offg. judl. commr., Feb., 1929; confd., May, 1930.

SUBHEDAR, LAKSHMIKANT SADASHEO, B.A., LL.B., Income-tax Dept., Central Prov. (Asst. Commr.) (b. 12th Dec., 1889).—Joined the service, Mar., 1913, E.A.C., 1913-1920, Income-tax officer, June, 1922; confd. as asst. commr. of income-tax, July, 1936.

SUBHEDAR, TRIMBAK RAGHUNATH, Indian Police (asst. supt.) (b. 22nd Nov., 1907).—Joined the service as proby. asst. supt., Central Provs., 29th Jan., 1931; confd., Apr., 1933.

SUBRAMANYA AYYAR, TRIPUNITTURA SUBRAMANI, M.A., Indian Audit Dept. (b. 11th June, 1897).—Joined the service, 23rd Feb., 1922; asst. acct.-genl., Punjab, Oct., 1926; ditto, Bihar and Orissa, Mar., 1927; dep. ch. auditor, E.B. rly., June, 1930; asst. ch. auditor, E.I. rly., Nov., 1930; dep. govt. exr., B. and N.W. and R.K. rlys., July, 1933; ditto, S.I. rly., Oct., 1937; dep. acct.-genl., Madras, Apr., 1936.

SUBRAHMANYA AYYAR, CHANDRASEKHARA, B.A., Indian Finance Dept. (b. 8th Oct., 1885).—Joined the service as asst. examr., 10th Aug., 1907; govt. examr. of accts., June, 1918; dept. auditor, E.B. Rly., July, 1921; dep. acct.-genl., Madras, Oct., 1921; senr. ditto, Madras, June, 1923; dep. auditor, N.W. Rly., March, 1925; ditto, E.B. Rly., July, 1926; ditto, E.I. Rly., May, 1928; offg. dep. dir. of rly. audit (insp.), Ap., 1929; offg. govt. examr. of accts., M. & S.M. Rly., Apr., 1930; ch. auditor, E.B. and Burmarlys. and govt. exr. of accts., Assam Bengal rly., Feb., 1932; ch. auditor, G.I.P. rly., and govt. exr. of accts., B.B. and C.I. rly., Jan., 1935.

SUBRAHMANYAM, NARAYANASWAMY, B.Sc., Indian C.S. (asst. mag. and collr., Madras) (b. 20th Sept., 1912).—Educ. at Municipal High School, Mayavaram, and Madras Univ.; joined the service 15th Sept., 1937; arrived in India, Oct., 1937, and served in Madras as asst. mag. and collr.

SUBRAHMANYAN, MANGUDY, B.A., Indian Finance Dept. (b. 5th Aug., 1889).—Joined the service 1st Nov., 1910; asst. acctnt.-gen., May, 1911; dep. ditto, July, 1924; offg. dep. auditor-genl., Mar., 1929; acct.-genl., Mar., 1929; acct.-genl., Bihar and Orissa, Apr., 1932; ditto, Bengal, Mar., 1933.

SUBRAMANIAN, KAVASSERI NARAYANA IYER, M.A., Indian C.S. (asst. commr., Central Provinces) (b. 4th June, 1909).—Educated at Madras University and School of Oriental Studies, London; joined the service, 11th October, 1932, arrived in India, November, 1932, and served in the Central Provinces as asst. commr.

SUBRAMANYAN, MINAKHISUNDARAM VENKATA, B.A., Indian C.S. (sub. collr., Madras) (b. 8th July, 1900). Educ. at Madras Univ., and Christ's Coll., Cambridge; joined the service, 24th Oct., 1924; arrived, 8th Dec., 1924, and served in Madras as asst. collr. and mag.; sub. collr., Aug., 1926; offg. collr. and dist. mag., July, 1933.

SUBRAHMANYAN, NATESA, B.A., Indian C.S. (asst. mag. and collr., Madras) (b. 11th July, 1913).—Educ. at Banadurai High School, Kumbakonam, Madras Univ., and Univ. Coll., London; joined the service 20th Sept., 1937; arrived in India, Oct., 1937, and served in Madras as asst. mag. and collr.

SUD, AMAR NATH, B.Sc., Indian State Rlys. (exec. engr., N.W. Rly.) (b. 17th July, 1890).—Joined the service as apprentice engr., 9th Sept., 1913; asst. engr., March, 1915; exec. engr., 9th March, 1924.

SUDHANSU KUMAR HALDRA (see Haldar S. K.).

SUDHINDRA KUMAR HALDAR (see Haldar, S. K.).

SUFI, MUHAMMAD IBRAHIM (see Muhammad, I. S.).

SUHRAWARDY, SIR HASSAN, Kt., O.B.E., M.D., Indian State Rlys. (b. 4th Jan., 1884).—Served for 7 years 5½ months as gazetted medl. officer under govt. of Bengal; apptd. as dist. medl. officer, E.I. Rly., 4th April, 1916; O.B.E., June, 1927; offg. C.M.O., E.B. Rly., Mar., 1928; Kaiser-i-Hind medal, 1st class, June, 1930; vice-chancellor, Calcutta Univ., Kt., Feb., 1932; offg. ch. medl. officer, E.I. rly., Apr., 1932; confd., Dec., 1932; member, public services commn. Bengal, Apr., 1937.

SUHRAWARDY, SIR ZAHHADUR RAHIM ZAHID, Kt., Barr.-at-law, late Judicial dept., Bengal (b. 27th Nov., 1870).—Joined the service, 8th Jan., 1917, and apptd. judge, Calcutta small cause court; judge, high ct., Calcutta, 1921; Kt., June, 1927; ret'd., Nov., 1931; presdt., rly. rates advisory ctee., May, 1933.

SUKH, DAYAL, Punjab Med. Service (civil surg.) (b. 16th Oct., 1882).—Joined the service, 4th Feb., 1908; apptd. asst. surgeon, Feb., 1910; civil surgeon, Aug., 1934.

SUKHDEO PRASAD, TANDON, B.Sc., Indian Service of Engrs. (exec. engr., United Provs.) (b. 10th Aug., 1890).—Joined the service, 23rd Sept., 1914; asst. engr., Jan., 1916; asst. exec. engr., Jan., 1922; offg. exec. engr., Jan., 1923; confd., Sept., 1924.

SUKTHANKAR, YESHWANT NARAYAN, B.A., LL.D., Barr., Indian C.S. (dep. secty., commerce dept. govt. of India) (b. 24th Aug., 1897).—Educ. at Bombay Univ. and at Cambridge Univ. (non-collegiate); joined the service, 30th Oct., 1922; arrived, 16th Dec., 1922, and served in Central Provs. as asst. commr.; offg. dep. commr., April, 1927; under secty. to govt., C.P., Oct., 1932; offg. rev. secty. to

govt., C.P., June, 1933; dep. India trade commr., July, 1934; actg. Ind. trade commr., Oct., 1934; end again, Sept., 1935; dep. secty., commerce dept., Govt. of India (temp.), July, 1937; dep. commr. (confd.), Aug., 1937.

SULAIMAN, MUHAMMAD (see Muhammad Sulaiman).

SULAIMAN, SIR SHAH MUHAMMAD (see Shah Muhammad Sulaiman).

SULE, RAMCHANDRA GOVIND, L.C.E., Indian Service of Engrs. (exec. engr., Bombay) (b. 17th May, 1888).—Joined the provl. service as apprentice engr., 1st April, 1912; asst. engr., April, 1913; asst. exec. engr., Aug., 1919; exec. engr., April, 1922.

SULLEN, ARTHUR ERNEST BUCKNER, O.B.E., late Indian State Railways (b. 10th June, 1878).—Joined the service, 29th June, 1907; asst. engr., N.W. Rly., March, 1908; exec. engr., ditto, Jan., 1909; O.B.E., June, 1921; dep. ch. engr., March, 1927; offg. dep. agent, E.B. Rly., Nov., 1930; ret'd., June, 1933.

SULLIVAN, BASIL MARTIN, C.I.E., O.B.E., F.R.I.B.A., A.M.T.P.I. (suptg. architect, Punjab) (b. 30th Aug., 1882).—Apptd. consulting architect to the Punjab govt., 19th Jan., 1914; O.B.E., June, 1924; suptg. architect, Aug., 1928; C.I.E., May, 1937.

SULLIVAN, EDMOND HERVEY, late Indian Police (b. 21st Nov., 1886).—Joined the service, Dec., 1906; asst. supt., Sept., 1907; supt., Aug., 1915; awarded King's police medal; commr. of police, Bangalore, Oct., 1931; ret'd., July, 1937.

SULLIVAN, JOHN HUSSEY, A.M.I.C.E., late Indian Service of Engrs., (Punjab) (b. 12th Dec., 1885).—Trained at R.I.E. Coll.; joined the service as asst. engr., 1st Jan., 1907; exec. engr., Jan., 1915; ret'd., Oct., 1929.

SULLIVAN, MICHAEL JOHN KENT, B.A., Indian C.S. (asst. commr., C.P.) (b. 22nd Mar., 1913).—Educ. at Shrewsbury and Jesus Coll., Cambridge; joined the service, 20th Sept., 1937; arrived in India, Oct., 1937, and served in the C.P. as asst. commr.

SULTAN ALI, NOON, MALIK, Indian Agricl. Service (dep. dir. of agric., Punjab) (b. 23rd Mar., 1895).—Joined the service as supy. dep. dir. of agric., 2nd Sept., 1918; dep. dir. of agric., June, 1924.

SUMER CHAND, B.A., Indian Service of Engrs. (exec. engr., United Provs.) (b. 14th Feb., 1895).—Joined the service as temp. engr., 9th Sept., 1918; asst. engr. (provl. service), Feb., 1920; asst. exec. engr., March, 1923; offg. exec. engr., May, 1926; confd., Oct., 1930.

SUNDAR DAS, KHANGAR, Lala, B.A., Indian Service of Engrs. (exec. engr., Punjab) (b. 24th Nov., 1897).—Joined the service, 19th Nov., 1920; asst. exec. engr., Nov., 1921; exec. engr., Oct., 1930.

SUNDAR SINGH, BABA, M.I.E.E., late India Posts and Telegraphs (b. 12th Mar., 1876).—Trained at R.I.E. Coll.; joined the service as asst. supt., 1st Oct., 1898; supt., April, 1910; divl. engr., Nov., 1920; dir. tel.

engrg., Dec., 1921; dep. ch. engr., March, 1923; dir., tele. engrg., U.P. circle, June, 1928; ditto, Lahore, July, 1929; offg. P.M.G., B. & O., Mar., 1930; ret'd., Mar., 1931.

SUNDAR SINGH, SIR, SARDAR BAHADUR SARDAR, MAJITHIA, Kt., C.I.E.—Apptd. member of govr.'s exec. council, Punjab, 3rd Jan., 1921; Kt., Jan., 1926; C.I.E., Jan., 1920; member, Indian franchise comee., Jan., 1932; minister of revenue, Aprl., 1937.

SUNDAR SINGH, B.A., Indian Forest Service (dep. consvr., Punjab) (b. 7th Mar., 1898).—Joined the service as asst. consvr., Punjab, 8th Dec., 1923; dep. consvr., Aprl., 1932.

SUNDARA RAJ, B., M.A., Ph.D. (b. 25th Aug., 1888).—Joined the service, 21st Dec., 1912, as piscicultural asst., ditto expert, 12th Aprl., 1917; asst. dir. of Fisheries (sub. problem), 7th Feb., 1918; asst. dir. of Fisheries, inland, 1st Jan., 1919; actg. dir. of Fisheries, 7th Aug., 1923; Dir. of Fisheries (perm.), 1st Nov., 1924.

SUNDARACHARI, RANGASWAMI, Dewan Bahadur, B.A., Indian Finance Dept. (b. 7th Aprl., 1884).—Joined the service as asst. examr., 29th Aug., 1906; govt. exam. of accts., M. & S.M. & S.I. Rlys., Feb., 1925; dep. auditor, E.B. Rly., May, 1925; offg. dep. acctnt.-gen., rlys. (inspn.), Aprl, 1926; offg. dep. auditor, E.I. Rly., Oct., 1928; dep. dir., rly. clearing accts. office, Aprl., 1929; dep. ch. accts. officer, E.I. Rly., Aprl., 1929; offg. dep. ch. auditor, N.W. Rly., Sept., 1929; ditto, Burma Rlys., Aprl., 1930; ch. auditor, E.I. rly., and govt. exr. of accts., B.N. rly., Aprl., 1932; offg. ch. auditor, rly. clearing accts., Nov., 1932; ditto, E.I. rly., and govt. examr. of accts., Bengal-Nagpur rly., Mar., 1933.

SUNDARAM CHETTI, KRISHNAMA CHETTIYAR, DIWAN BAHADUR, B.A., B.L., late Judl. dept., Madras (b. 18th Nov., 1875).—Joined the service as offg. dist. munisf, 2nd Aug., 1902; confd., Jan., 1903; sub. judge, Mar., 1919; sess. judge, Sept., 1926; dist. and sess. judge, Aug., 1929; offg. judge, high ct., Madras, Sept., 1926, Aug., 1929, and again, Aprl., 1930; confd., July, 1930; ret'd., Sept., 1935.

SUNDARAM, RAO SAHIB GANGADARA, M.A., A.M.I.E.E. (exec. engr., Electricity Dept., Madras) (b. 14th Nov., 1896).—Joined the service, 4th Jan., 1926, as electrical asst.; confd. as exec. engr., Aprl., 1933.

SUNDARAM, KUTHUR VAIDYANATHA KALYANA (see Kalyana Sundaram, K. V.).

SUNDARAM, RAMASWAMY MEENAKSHI, Indian C.S. (sub. collr. and jt. mag., Madras) (b. 22nd May, 1905).—Educ. at University of Madras and Oxford (non-collegiate); joined the service, 28th Sept., 1928; arrived, Nov., 1928, and served in Madras as asst. collr. and mag.; sub-collr. and jt. magte., Feb., 1931.

SUNDARAM, VADUVUR SHRINIVAS, B.A., Indian Finance Dept. (b. 24th Nov., 1888).—Joined the service as probnr., 21st Nov., 1908; asst. acct.-genl., May, 1909; asst. comptr., India Treasuries, Aprl, 1915; dep., ditto, March, 1916; examr., local fund accts.

Nov., 1917; dep. acct.-genl., April, 1922; sec., central board of revenue, and under sec. to govt. of India, fin. dept., March, 1924; offg. commr. of inc. tax, Bihar and Orissa, Apl., 1930; dep. contr. of rly. accts., Nov., 1930; offg. ch. accts. officer, N.W. rly., Nov., 1932; offg. dir. of finance, rly. bd., Jan., 1933; offg. ch. accts. officer, E.I. rly., Sept., 1933; contr. of rly. accts., Jan., 1935; jt. secty., Niemeyer finl. enquiry, Oct., 1935.

SUNDARARAMAN, SUBRAHMANYA, M.A., late Indian Agric. Service (b. 7th Nov., 1881).—Apptd. asst. mycologist, June, 1920; offg. govt. mycologist, July, 1920; confd., Oct., 1924; retd., Nov., 1936.

SUNDARESAN, KUPPUSWAMY, B.A., Indian C.S. (jt. mag. and collr., United Provs.) (b. 6th Jan., 1910).—Educ. at Hindu High School, Triplicane, Univ. of Madras and the School of Oriental Studies, London; joined the service, 10th Oct., 1934; arrived in India, Nov., 1934, and served in the United Provinces as asst. mag. and collr.; jt. magte., Nov., 1936.

SUNDER LAL, RAI BAHADUR, late Indian Service of Engrs. (b. 16th Nov., 1882).—Joined the service, 22nd Sept., 1905; asst. engr., Sept., 1906; exec. engr., Sept., 1914; suptg. engr., Feb., 1925; retd., Nov., 1937.

SUR, HRISHIKESH, O.B.E., B.A., India Posts and Tels. (dir., tel. engrg.) (b. 2nd Jan., 1885).—Joined the service as apprentice asst. supt., 4th Oct., 1906; asst. supt., Dec., 1907; supt., April, 1917; asst. post-master-genl., July, 1918; dep. ditto, April, 1920; divl. engr., Oct., 1921; dir., posts and tels., Sind and Baluchistan, Mar., 1931; offg. dep. dir.-genl., tels., June, 1933; offg. dir. tel. engrg., Bengal and Assam, Mar., 1934; dir. tel. engrg., Dec., 1934; elec. engr. in ch., Dec., 1935; O.B.E., May, 1937.

SUR, NOLINI KANTA, D.Sc. Meteorological, Dept. (meteorologist) (b. June, 1891).—Joined the service as meteorologist on probn., 31st Oct., 1927; confd., Oct., 1929.

SUR, SACHINDRA NATH, M.B., D.P.H., Dept. of Public Health, Bengal (asst. dir. of public health) (b. 4th Sept., 1889).—Joined the service as offg. dep. sany. commr., Dec., 1916; asst. dir. of public health, Dec., 1918.

SURATH NATH, CHANDHURI, RAI SAHIB, Indian Service of Engrs. (exec. engr., Bihar) (b. 30th Aug., 1888).—Joined the service 5th Feb., 1910, and held non-gazetted appt. prior to 12th Apl., 1924; asst. engr., Apl., 1924; apptd. to I.S.E., 1st Jan., 1930, as asst. exec. engr.; offg. exec. engr., 1st Jan., 1930; confd., Apl., 1935.

SURENDRA NATH SEN, M.A., LL.D., Judicial Dept., United Provs. (judge, high ct., Rangoon).—Apptd. offg. judge, high ct., June, 1927; addl. judge, ditto, March, 1928; confd., Oct., 1930.

SURENDRA NATH SEN, Barr., late Judicial Dept., Burma.—Apptd. judge, high ct., Rangoon, 25th Oct., 1931; retd., Jan., 1938.

SURENDRA SINGH BHAGAT, B.Sc., Indian Service of Engrs. (exec. engr., United Provs.) (b. 19th Aug., 1889).—Joined the service as asst. engr., 1st Oct., 1913; exec. engr., Oct., 1922.

SURYAPRAKASA RAO, V., Indian Forest Service (b. 10th March, 1906).—Joined the service as asst. consvr., Bengal, 8th Nov., 1930.

SUTHERLAND, JOHN WILLIAM ROSS, B.Sc., Indian Forest Service (dep. consvr., Burma) (b. 11th Jan., 1895).—Joined the service as asst. consvr., 17th Dec., 1922; lecturer in forestry, Univ. Coll., Rangoon, Nov., 1924, to April, 1926; dep. consvr., April, 1926.

SUTHERLAND, WILLIAM, C.S.I., V.D., M.I.E.E., late India Posts and Tel. Dept.—(b. 16th Dec., 1870).—Trained at R.I.E. Coll. joined the service as asst. supt., 1st Oct., 1891; supt., Aug., 1904; dir. of tel., Oct., 1913; postmr.-gen. and dir., telegraph engrg., Burma, Oct., 1914; dep. dir.-gen., telegraph traffic, India, Dec., 1916; on mil. service, June, 1918, to March, 1919; dir. of telegraphs and offg. postmr.-gen., United Provs., April, 1919; offg. chief engr. telegraphs, March, 1920; confd. as chief engr., May, 1922; C.S.I., June, 1924; asst. to dir.-in-chief, Indo-European Tel., Public Works dept., India Office, Dec., 1925, to Mar., 1931.

SUTTON, ERNEST WILLIAM, late India Office (b. 30th Sept., 1871).—Second divn. clerk, census office, 7th April, 1891; 2nd class clerk, India Office, 1st Sept., 1891; shorthand writer to sec. of state, Sept., 1896, to Nov., 1910; staff clerk, Dec., 1911; apptd. to organise and take charge of Central tel. section, July, 1920; supt., tel. and mails branch, Nov., 1923; retd., Sept., 1931.

SUTTON, NOEL DUDLEY TAYLER, Indian Police (asst. supt., Punjab) (b. 20th Dec., 1913).—Joined the service as proby. asst. supt. of police, Punjab, Nov., 1933; confd., Nov., 1935.

SUTTON, STANLEY CECIL, B.Sc. (Econ.) India Office (sub-librarian) (b. 23rd Sept., 1907).—Educ. at Tottenham County Sch., London Sch. of Econ. (Graham Wallas prize-man), and Univ. Coll., London (Sir John Macalister Medallist); senr. asst., Brit. lib. of pol. and econ. science, 1931-1935; sub-librarian, India Office, 17th June, 1935.

SUTTON, WARWICK EDWARD late Indian Police (b. 21st Mar., 1882).—Joined the service, 1st Sept., 1907; dep. supt., Jan., 1919; promoted to I.P. as supt., from 26th July, 1929; retd., Mar., 1937.

SVARUP, BISHUN (see Bishun Svarup).

SWAIN, LEO HARRY, Indian State Rlys. (dep. contr. of standardisation) (b. 31st Dec., 1888).—Joined the service as bridge engr., 28th March, 1920; asst. contr. of standardisation, July, 1932; offg. dep. ch. contr., central standards office, Apl., 1934; confd., Apl., 1935; dep. ch. engr. (bdgs.), Aug., 1935.

SWAIN, WALTER, C.I.E., late Indian Police Service (Bihar and Orissa) (b. 17th Jan., 1876).—Joined the service, 30th Nov., 1895, as asst. supt. of police; dist. supt., Oct., 1905; addl. dep. commr. of police, Calcutta, Dec., 1905, to Jan., 1906; princpl., pol. training coll., April, 1913; dep. inspr.-gen., Aug., 1919; offg. inspr.-gen., Nov., 1920; C.I.E., June, 1922; inspr.-gen., Bihar and Orissa, Feb., 1923; has recd. King's Police Medal; retd., Jan., 1931.

SWAMINATHA AYYAR, NARAYANA, DIWAN BAHADUR, B.A., B.E., late Indian Service of Engrs. (b. Oct., 1878).—Joined the service, 10th April, 1901; asst. engr., April, 1902; exec. engr., Jan., 1915; offg. suptg. engr., May, 1925; confd., Sept., 1928; offg. ch. engr., Mar., 1929; confd., July, 1929; ret'd., Mar., 1934.

SWAMINATHAN, THINNIAM SUBRAHMANYA, Indian C.S. (sub. collr. and jt. mag., Madras) (b. 23rd Feb., 1906).—Educ. at University of Madras and Balliol Coll., Oxford; joined the service, 28th Sept., 1928; arrived, Nov., 1928, and served in Madras as asst. collr. and mag.; sub. collr. and jt. mag., Feb., 1931.

SWAMINATHAN, THIRUMALRAYA, B.A., Indian C.S. (asst. mag. and collr., United Provs., (b. 18th June, 1912).—Educ. at Pachaiyappa's High School, Chidambaran, Madras University, University College, London, and the School of Oriental Studies, London; joined the service, 21st Sept., 1936; arrived in India, October, 1936, and served in the United Provinces as asst. mag. and collr.

SWAN, JAMES GRAHAM GOODENOUGH, C.I.E., B.A., M.B., Ch.B., Lieut.-Col., I.M.S. (ret'd.) (b. 20th Oct., 1875).—First commn., 29th Jan., 1901; in mil. employ till June, 1905, when he was apptd. asst. plague medical officer, Punjab; civil surgeon, April, 1911; on mil. service. Aug., 1914, to Dec., 1919; C.I.E., Jan., 1918; ret'd., Oct., 1930.

SWAN, JOHN ARTHUR LAING, C.S.I., C.I.E., late Indian C.S. (b. 7th Nov., 1877).—Educ. at Glasgow Univ.; apptd. after exam. of 1900; arrived, 2nd Dec., 1901, and served in Bengal as asst. mag. and collr.; under-sec. to govt., judl. and genl. depts., March, 1906; pte. sec. to lieut.-govt., May to Oct., 1906; under-sec. to govt., Nov., 1908, to March, 1910; jt. mag. and dep. collr., Oct., 1910; mag. and collr., April, 1913; offg. sec. to board of revenue, Jan., 1916; confd., July, 1916; dep. commr., Oct., 1917; offg. sec. to govt. of Bengal, fin. dep., Aug. to Oct., 1918; mag. and collr. and dep. commr., Bengal, Oct., 1918; on special duty, pol. dept., govt. of Bengal, Feb., 1920; sec. to govt. of Bengal, dept. of agric. and industries, Feb., 1921; ditto, educn. dept., Oct., 1923; C.I.E., Jan., 1925; offg. commr. and pol. agent, Cooch Behar State, May, 1925; offg. commr., Chittagong divn., Nov., 1925; ditto, Burdwan divn., March, 1927; chairman, improvement trust, Calcutta, Oct., 1927; commr., Dec., 1928; chairman, retrenchment cttee., Bengal, May, 1932; C.S.I., Jan., 1933; temp. member, exec. council, govt. of Bengal, Apl., 1934; ret'd., Apl., 1936.

SWANZY, ROBERT EDWARD, B.A. (Oxon), Indian C.S. (mag. and collr., Bihar) (b. 17th July, 1888).—Educ. at Rugby, and Univ. Coll., Oxford; appt. after exam. of 1911; arrived, 30th Nov., 1912, and served in Bihar and Orissa as asst. mag. and collr.; on mil. service, Oct., 1915, to April, 1919; offg. mag. and collr., April, 1924; confd., Nov., 1927; offg. commr., Mar., 1936.

SWEENEY, JAMES AUGUSTINE, C.I.E., B.A., Barr., late Indian C.S. (b. 25th May, 1883).—Educ. at French Coll., Blackrock, and

Univ. Coll., Blackrock, R.U.I.; apptd. after exam. of 1906; arrived, 10th Dec., 1907, and served in Bengal as asst. mag. and collr. and asst. settlt. offr.; transfld. to Bihar, April, 1912; settlt. offr., North Bihar, Sept., 1913; jt. mag. and dep. collr. and ditto, Nov., 1913; sec., bd. of rev., Bihar and Orissa, from May, 1918; dist. and sess. judge, Feb., 1924; offg. supt. and membr. of legal affairs and sec. to govt., judl. dept., April, 1926; confd., Decr., 1928; C.I.E., Jan., 1931; ret'd., Apl., 1934.

SWEET, JACK McMURDO, B.A., Indian Forest Service (dep. consvr., Madras) (b. 20th Dec., 1895).—Joined the service as asst. consvr., 15th March, 1921; dep. consvr., March, 1923.

SWIFT, GERALD ARTHUR, Indian Police (b. 7th July, 1895) (supt., United Provs.).—Joined the service as asst. supt., 20th Nov., 1920; offg. supt., Sept., 1923; confd., June, 1930.

SWINNERTON, ALBERT RICHARD, India Office (exec. officer, milty. dept.) (b. 6th Apl., 1896).—Apptd. clerical officer, 3rd Aug., 1921; exec. officer, Jan., 1929.

SWINSTEAD, NORMAN HILLYARD, M.A. (Cantab.), M.B.E. (Mil. div.), India Posts and Tels. (divl. engr., wireless) (b. 21st Mar., 1891).—Joined the service, 5th Jan., 1920; divl. engr., wireless, 3rd Oct., 1921; asst. dir.-genl., wireless, May, 1930; offg. dir., Aug., 1932; asst. dir.-genl., Oct., 1935; div. engr., Feb., 1936.

SWITHINBANK, BERNARD WINTROP, C.B.E., M.A., Indian C.S. (dep. commr., Burma) (b. 4th Oct., 1884).—Educ. at Eton, and Balliol Coll., Oxford; apptd. after exam. of 1908; arrived, 29th Nov., 1909, and served in Burma as asst. commr. and settlt. offr.; dep. commr. (prov. subs.), Mar., 1924; confd., Nov., 1925; offg. secty., local govt. dept., Aug., 1929; offg. commr., Apl., 1933; C.B.E., Jan., 1938.

SYED ABDUL VAHID, B.A., B.Sc., Indian Forest Service (dep. consvr., Central Provs.) (b. 20th Jan., 1898).—Joined the service as asst. consvr., 11th Dec., 1922; dep. consvr. and sylviculturist, Jan., 1927.

SYED AHMAD, KHAN SAHIB, Indian Police (dist. supt.) (b. 20th Jan., 1894).—Held non-gaz. appts., Jan., 1906, to Oct., 1918; dep. supt., Jan., 1921; dist. supt., June, 1936.

SYED FIDA HASSAN (see Hassan, Syed Fida.)

SYED ISKANDER ALI MIRZA, SAHIBZADA, Capt., I.A., Indian Political Service. (b. 13th Nov., 1899).—First commn., 16th July, 1920; asst. mag. and collr., Aligarh, Aug., 1926; supy. asst. commr., Hazara, Mar., 1927; asst. commr., Dera Ismail Khan, Apl., 1928; ditto, Tonk, May, 1928; ditto, Bannu, Apl., 1930; ditto, Nowshera, Apl., 1931; dep. commr., Hazara, May 1933; asst. commr., Marden, Oct., 1936; dep. commr., ditto, Jan., 1937.

SYKES, The Right Hon. SIR FREDERICK HUGH, P.C. G.C.S.I., G.C.I.E., G.B.E., K.C.B., C.M.G., Major-Gen. (ret'd.) (b. 23rd July, 1877).—First commn., 1901; intell. branch, India, 1905-6; passed staff coll., 1908-9; C.M.G., 1916; G.B.E., 1919; K.C.B., 1919; member, Imperial war cabinet; chief of British

air section, peace conference, Paris; M.P., Hallam divn. of Sheffield, since 1922; G.C.I.E., Nov., 1928; govr. of Bombay from 8th Dec., 1928, to Dec., 1933; G.C.S.I., Feb., 1934.

SYKES, JOHN FRANCIS GASKELL, B.A., Indian C.S. (asst. commr., Punjab) (b. 21st Aug., 1909).—Educ. at Wellington College and Oriel College, Oxford; apptd. after exam. of 1933; arrived in India, Nov., 1934, and served in Punjab as asst. commr.; pol. asst. and comdt., Border Mily. Police, Apl., 1936; sub. div. offer., June, 1937.

SYKES, Sir PERCY MOLESWORTH, K.C.I.E., C.B., C.M.G., F.R.G.S., Lieut.-Col., temp. Brig.-Gen., Indian Army, (ret'd.).—Joined the service, 29th Feb., 1888; regtl. duty and staff duties in the intelligence dept., army headquarters, June, 1893, to May, 1901; pol. asst., Nov., 1902; pol. agent, Feb., 1906; H.B.M.'s consul-gen. and agent to govr.-gen. in Khorasan, Dec., 1906, to Nov., 1913; C.I.E., Dec., 1911; employed as interpreter, headquarters, 3rd (Lahore) divn., in France, Sept. to Oct., 1914; offg. consul-gen. at Kashgar, April, 1915; on spl. duty, South Persia Rifles, Feb., 1916, to Dec., 1918; K.C.I.E., June, 1915; C.B., June, 1919; ret'd., Sept., 1920; served in S. African war with Imperial Yeomanry, slightly wounded, mentioned in despatches; author of 'Ten Thousand Miles in Persia,' 'A History of Persia,' and of papers read before various learned societies.

SYLVESTER, HAROLD FRASER, Indian Service of Engrs. (exec. eng., Punjab) (b. 15th Dec., 1885).—Joined the service as temp. engr., 12th Jan. 1909; asst. engr., Jan., 1923; apptd. to I.S.E. as asst. exec. engr., 1st Jan., 1928; exec. engr., Jan., 1933.

SYMES, Sir GEORGE STEWART, K.C.M.G., K.B.E., D.S.O., Lt.-Col. (b. 29th July, 1882).—Educ. at Malvern Coll. and Sandhurst; first commn., 1900; A.D.C. to Sirdar and govr.-genl. of Sudan, 1906-07; asst. dir. of intell., 1908-12; pte. secty. and secty., govr.-genl.'s council, Sudan, 1913-16; C.M.G., 1917; attd. staff of high commr., Egypt., 1917-19; attd. miny. of int., Egypt., 1919-20; dist. govr., N.D. Palestine, 1920-4; ch. secty., Palestine govt., 1925-28; K.B.E., June, 1928; resident and comdr.-in-ch., Aden, July, 1928; govr. and comdr.-in-ch., Tanganyika territory, Jan., 1931; K.C.M.G., June, 1932.

SYMINGTON, DAVID, B.A., Indian C.S. (backward cl. offer.) (b. 4th July, 1904).—Educ. at Cheltenham and Oriel Coll., Oxford; joined the service, 28th Oct., 1926; arrived, Dec., 1926, and served in Bombay as asst. collr.; offg. collr. and dist. mag. and supt. of stamps, May, 1929; offg. collr., Jan., 1933; backward class offer., Bombay presdy., Nov., 1937.

SYMNS, JOHN MONTFORT, M.A., late Indian Educl. Service (b. 11th Jan., 1879).—Joined the service, 1st Sept., 1908, as inspr. of schools; offg. asst. dir. of public instrn., Jan., 1921, to Sept., 1922, and again, May, 1926; on depn. in connection with British Empire Exhibition, 1924; offg. dir. of public instrn., Jan., 1933; confd., Jan., 1934; ret'd., Feb., 1935.

SYMON, ALEXANDER COLIN BURLINGTON, India Office (asst. princpl., pol. dept.) (b. 13th May, 1902).—Apptd. clerical officer, 22nd Apl., 1920; exec. officer, Mar., 1933; asst. princpl., Nov., 1934.

SYMONS, HENRY JOHN HUGH, M.C., Lt.-Col. I.M.S. (b. 14th Oct., 1889).—First commn. dated 1st Nov., 1920; agency surg., Kurram, Nov., 1924; civil surg., Dera Ismail Khan, May, 1928; resy. surg., Bushire, Nov., 1929; vice-consul, ditto, Nov., 1929; civil surg., Kurram, Apl., 1933; ditto, Dera Ismail Khan, Oct., 1933.

SYMONS, NOEL VICTOR HOUSMAN, M.C., Indian C.S. (mag. and collr., Bengal) (b. 27th Nov., 1894).—Educ. at King Edward VI School, Bath; joined the service, 26th Aug., 1920; arrived, 4th Dec., 1920, and served in Bengal as asst. mag. and collr.; addl. dist. mag., March, 1926; offg. magte. and collr., April, 1927; offg. dep. commr., March, 1929; offg. secty. to bd. of rev., Bengal; confd., May, 1932; offg. pte. secty. to govr. of Bengal, Sept., 1934; mag. and collr., July, 1935.

SYMONS, RONALD STUART, Indian C.S. (jt. mag., United Provs.) (b. 24th July, 1904).—Educ. at Christ's Hospital and King's Coll., Cambridge; joined the service, 19th Oct., 1927; arrived, Dec., 1927, and served in the United Provs. as asst. mag. and collr.; jt. mag., Oct., 1930; offg. mag. and collr., Feb., 1933; services placed at disposal of govt. of India fin. dept., Dec., 1937.

SYMONS, RUDOLPH VICTOR, O.B.E., late Indian State Rlys. (G.I.P. Rly.) (b. 18th Jan., 1873).—Joined the service, 5th March, 1897; asst. engr., May, 1900; resdt. engr., May, 1903; dist. engr. (temp.), Nov., 1911; supt. engr., July, 1915; O.B.E., Jan., 1919; chief engr., July, 1919; ret'd., Jan., 1930.

SYMONS, Sir THOMAS HENRY, K.B.E., C.S.I., K.H.S., Major-Gen., I.M.S. (ret'd.) (b. 17th May, 1872).—First commn., dated 29th Jan., 1896; in mil. employ to May, 1903, when he was apptd. physician, Madras gen. hosp.; ditto, and prof. medl. coll., Nov., 1906; on mil. duty from Nov., 1914, to Feb., 1919; O.B.E., Dec., 1917; senr. medl. offr. and second surg., gen. hosp., Madras, also surg., 3rd dist.; offg. surg.-gen. with govt. of Madras, May, 1923; confd., Jan., 1924; C.S.I., June, 1925; dir.-gen., ind. medl. service, April, 1926; K.B.E., June, 1929; ret'd., Sept., 1930.

SYNGE, GEORGE BERNARD, Indian C.S. (jt. mag. and dy. collr., Bengal) (b. 11th Feb., 1903).—Educ. at Shrewsbury, and Keble Coll., Oxford; joined the service, 22nd Oct., 1926; arrived, Dec., 1926; and served in Bengal as asst. mag. and collr.; offg. under secty. to govt. of Bengal, pol. and apptt. depts., Jan., 1930; confd. Mar., 1930; offg. addl. dist. and sess. judge, Oct., 1932; offg. mag. and collr., Aug., 1933.

SYNNOTT, NEVILLE WILLIAM, Indian State Rlys. (supt., E.I. Rly.) (b. 31st July, 1889).—Joined the service as asst. traffic supt., 14th July, 1909; supt., July, 1925; offg. dep. ch. opertg. supt. (movement), Mar., 1935.

TADULINGA MUDALIYAR, C., late Indian Agric. Service (b. 24th Oct., 1878).—Joined the service, 1st April, 1901; asst. in systematic botany, May, 1917; govt. lecturing botanist, Sept., 1923; principal, agric. coll., Coimbatore, May, 1927; ret'd., Aug., 1933.

TAILYOUR, DOUGLAS STUART, M.C., late Indian Forest Engrg. Service (b. 30th Aug., 1886).—Joined the service as forest engr., Burma, 30th Nov., 1921; retd., Apl., 1934.

TAIT, DOUGLAS WALTER, India Office (supgt. exec. offcr., acctnt.-gen.'s dept.) (b. 12th Aug., 1889).—Served as 2nd divn. clerk (customs), from Nov., 1907; apptd. 2nd class clerk, India Office, 6th July, 1909, and attached to acctnt.-gen.'s dept.; transf. to library, March, 1913; on mily. service from March, 1917, to Nov., 1919; higher clerical officer, April, 1922; higher executive officer, Apl., 1926; assessor of income tax, Oct., 1936; actg. suptg. exec. offcr., Nov., 1937; confd., Jan., 1938.

TAJ MUHAMMAD, SHEIKH, M.A., M.Sc., Indian Finance Dept. (b. 13th Nov., 1887).—Joined the service, 17th Oct., 1907; asst. acct.-gen., Nov., 1911; dep. acct.-gen., central revenues, June, 1926; asst. acct.-gen., Punjab, Aug., 1928; examr., local fund accts., Punjab, Dec., 1928; offg. dep. dir. of army audit, E.C., March, 1929; comptr., N.W.F.P., Apl., 1932.

TAJAL ISLAM MUHAMMAD NURANABI CHAUDHURI, Indian C.S. (mag. and collr., Bengal) (b. 8th Feb., 1895).—Educ. at Presidency Coll., Calcutta; joined the service, 12th Dec., 1921; arrived, 6th Jan., 1922, and served in Bengal as asst. mag. and collr.; offg. dist. and sess. judge, Feb., 1927; offg. mag. and collr., Oct., 1931; confd., Nov., 1933.

TAJAMMUL ALI, KHAN BAHADUR, B.A., late Assam Civil Service (b. July, 1878).—Joined the service 22nd July, 1901; sub. dep. collr., Feb., 1904; dep. magte. and dep. collr., Jan., 1908; extra asst. commr., Apl., 1912; offg. dep. commr., July, 1930; confd., Feb., 1932; retd., July, 1933.

TAKAKHAV, NILKANTH SADASHIV M.A., late Indian Educl. Service (b. 13th Aug., 1881).—Joined the service as prof. of English, Karnatak Coll., Dharwar, Mar., 1922; retd., Aug., 1936.

TAKLE, GANESH GOVIND, B.A., B.Sc., Indian Forest Service (dep. consvr., Central Provs.) (b. 14th Sept., 1901).—Joined the service as asst. consvr., 4th Dec., 1925; dep. consvr., Feby., 1930.

TALBOT, Rev. ALFRED DIXON, late Indian Eccles. Estabt. (b. 28th Dec., 1881).—Apptd. chaplain on probation, 18th Oct., 1914; junr. chaplain, Oct., 1917; senr. chaplain, Oct., 1927; retd., Apl., 1935.

TALBOT, WALTER BLACQUIRE, late Opium Dept., United Provs. (b. 5th Oct., 1874).—Joined the service, 14th March, 1895; dist. opium officer, Oct., 1925; retd., Oct., 1929.

TALEYARKHAN, KAIKHUSRO MANEKSHAH, Barr., Judl. Dept., Bombay (Ct. Receiver and Liquidator).—Joined the service as offg. ct. receiver and liquidator, 26th Nov., 1934; confd., Mar., 1935.
