(retd.).—Educ. at St. John's Coll., Cambridge; apptd. after exam. of 1894; arrived, 30th Dec., 1895, and served in Burma as asst. commr., asst. settlmt. officer, and settlmt. offr.; dep. commr., Feb., 1910; offg. sec. to govt. from July, 1914; confd., Sept., 1915; member of lieut.-govr.'s council, Nov., 1915; offg. chief sec., Sept. to Dec., 1917; chief sec., April, 1919, to April, 1920; member of Burma legisl. council Nov., 1919; commr., Feb., 1920; C.I.E., Jan., 1921; chairman, Development Trust, Feb., 1921; Kt., Jan., 1924; retd., Feb., 1926.

WEBB, HORACE OSCAR, Engr.-Comdr., R.I.M., Marine Dept., Bombay (b. 17th Nov., 1881).—Apptd. 2nd engr. and ship surveyor, Mar., 1927; prinpl. engr. and ship surveyor, Bombay, Feb., 1929; engr. and ship surveyor, Karachi, Aug., 1931.

WEBB, JOHN ROBERT DOUGLAS, O.B.E., M.R.C.S., L.R.C.P., D.P.H., Lieut.-Col., I.M.S. (retd. (b. 4th Jan., 1888).—First commn. dated 25th Jan., 1913; O.B.E., June, 1919; in mil. employ prior to 17th July, 1923; medl. officer of health, Simla, July, 1923; asst. dir., public health, Delhi, March, 1928; offg. ditto, Madras, Aug., 1931; retd., July, 1936.

WEBB, MAURICE, C.I.E., B.A. (Oxon), late Indian C.S. (Bombay) (b. 23rd April, 1880).—Educ. at St. Paul's Sch., and Corpus Christi Coll., Oxford; apptd. after exam. of 1903; arrived, 25th Nov., 1904, and served in Bombay and Sind as asst. collr. and mag.; manager, Sind encumb. estates, Sept., 1912, to June, 1913; dep. sec. to govt., rev. dept., Sept., 1920; offg. sec. to govt., rev. dept., Feb., 1921; settl. officer (temp.), Nov., 1921; offg. collr. and mag., Nov., 1922; ditto, May, 1924; offg. pte. sec. to H.E. the govr. of Bombay, March, 1925; offg. collr. and dist. mag. and pol. agent, Sept., 1925; temp. dep. sec. to govt., pol. dept., Nov., 1925; offg. sec. to govt., pol. dept., Jan., 1926; collr., dist. mag., and pol. agent, June, 1927; C.I.E., Jan., 1930; retd., June, 1930.

WEBB, Miss M. V., L.R.C.P. & S.E. (b. 15th Sept., 1878).—Prinpl., women's medl. school, Agra, 9th Nov., 1921, to Feb., 1932.

WEBB, WILFRED FRANCIS, Major, I.A., Indian Political Service (b. 20th Feb., 1897).—Joined the dept. as asst. commr., Ajmer Merwara, 6th May, 1921; on duty under High Commr., Bagdad, from Dec. 1918; on foreign service in Tonk State from Oct., 1924; under secty. to resdt., Hyderabad, Oct., 1929; secty. to ditto, Mar., 1930; on foreign service in Rewa; diwan, Bundi state, Feb., 1932; pol. agent, Orissa States, Sambalpur, Oct., 1937.

WEBB-JOHNSON, STANLEY, O.B.E., Indian Legive. Dept. (2nd solicitor) (b. 1st Mar., 1888).—Apptd. asst. solicitor to govt. of India, Feb., 1920; 2nd solicitor, Nov., 1927; O.B.E., Dec., 1930; offg. solicitor, variously from Apl., 1933.

WEBSTER, GEORGE EVELYN EDEN, B.A., Indian C.S. (asst. commr., Burma) (b. 21st April, 1905).—Educ. at Calabar High School, Kingston, Jamaica, and Trinity Coll., Oxford; joined the service, 28th Oct., 1929; arrived Dec., 1929, and served in Burma as asst. commr.; under-secty. to govt., fin. dept., Sept., 1932, July, 1934, and again, Sept., 1935; ditto home and pol. dept., Feb., 1935.

WEIGHTMAN, HUGH, B.A. (Cantab), Indian C.S., Indian Political Service (b. 29th Nov., 1898).—Educ. at Hymer's Coll., and Corpus Christi Coll., Cambridge; joined the service, 9th Jan., 1922; arrived, 17th Feb., 1922, and served in Assam as asst. commr.; presdt., Manipur State Durbar, June, 1925, to Feb., 1926; offg. under-secty. to govt., Assam, Oct., 1927; confd., March, 1928; under secty. to A.G.G. in C.I., Apl., 1929; asst. pol. agent and asst. commr. in Quetta-Pishin, Nov., 1930; secty. to A.G.G. and ch. commr., Baluchistan, Apl., 1932; pol. agent, Kalat, June, 1935; ditto, Balrain, Oct., 1937.

WEIR, JAMES LESLIE ROSE, C.I.E., Lt.-Col., I.A., Indian Political Service (b. 29th Jan., 1883).—In mily. employ from 21st Dec., 1900; famine offr. under pol. agent, Bundelkhand, Jan., 1908; asst. to resdt. Dwalior, Feb., 1908; supt. asst. commt., Punjab, Dec., 1908; pol. asst., Nov., 1909; British trade agent, Gyantse, Dec., 1909; 2nd asst. to agent to govr.-gen. in Baluchistan, Mar., 1912; asst. pol. agent and asst. commr., Sibi, Dec., 1912; temp. reverted to mil. duty, Oct., 1914; on spl. duty, Persian Gulf,

Aprl., 1918; sec. to agent to govr.-gen. and chief commr. in Baluchistan, Nov., 1923; pol. agent and dep. commr., Quetta, April, 1925; 1st asst. to resident in Kashmir, Oct., 1926; offg. resident in Kashmir, Jan., 1927; pol. officer in Sikkim, Oct., 1928; resdt., Baroda, Jan., 1933; agent to the govr.-genl., Gujerat states, Aprl., 1933; C.I.E., June, 1933; resident, Gujerat States and Baroda, Aprl., 1937.

WEIR, RALPH SOMERVILLE, M.A., B.Sc., Indian Educl. Service (dir. of public instrn. United Provs.) (b. 10th Sept., 1884).—Joined the service as head master, govt. high school, Allahabad, 2nd March, 1917; vice-princl., training coll., Allahabad, June, 1920; prinpl. govt. inter. coll., Etawah, April, 1926; inspr. of schools, Aug., 1928; asst. dir. of public instrn., Nov., 1929; offg. dep. dir. of public instrn., U.P., and under-secty. to govt., educn. dept., Aprl., 1932; confd., Feb., 1935; offg. dir., Oct., 1936; confd., Oct., 1937.

WELLBORNE, CYRIL DE MONTFORT, C.I.E., O.B.E., Lt.-Col., I.A., Indian Police (inspr.-gen., Burma) (b. 2nd July, 1884).—First commn. 21st Jan., 1903; apptd. asst. supt. of police, Burma, Aug., 1909; dist. supt., April, 1913; on mil. servic., Dec., 1915, to April, 1919; O.B.E., June, 1919; dep. inspr.-g n., Aug., 1922; offg. inspr.-gen., March, 1927; awarded King's police medal, Jan., 1928; offg. inspr.-genl., Mar., 1931; confd., Aug., 1932; C.I.E., June, 1936.

WELLS, ARTHUR GEOFFREY, B.A. (Oxon), Indian C.S. (judge and sess. judge, Bombay) (b. 10th Aprl., 1903).—Educ. at King's School, Canterbury, and Worcester Coll., Oxford; joined the service, 20th Oct., 1927; arrived Dec., 1927, and served in Bombay as asst. magte. and collr.; asst. judge and sess. judge, Feb., 1933; judge and sess. judge, Aug., 1935; dist. and sess. judge, Aden, Oct., 1935.

WELLS, DOUGLAS THURBURN, A.M.I.C.E., late Indian Service of Engrs. (b. 12th Oct. 1885).—Trained at R.I.E. coll.; joined the service as asst. ngr., 1st Jan., 1907; exec. engr., Jan., 1915; offg. suptg. engr., Oct., 1926; confd., Mar., 1928; offg. dep. ch. engr., June, 1929; ret'd., Jan., 1934.

WELLS, HARRY LESLIE, B.Sc., Indian Service of Engrs. (exec. engr., Punjab) (b. 25th July, 1903).—Joined the service as asst. exec. engr., Punjab, 1st Oct., 1926; exec. engr., Oct., 1935.

WELLS, ROBERT TAIT, M.A., M.B., Ch.B., M.D., Lt.-Col., I.M.S., ret'd. (b. 24th Aprl., 1878).—First commn., 1st Sept., 1904; in mil. employ till July, 1908; asst. to dir., research institute Kasauli, Oct., 1908; plague medl. offr., Punjab, Oct., 1911; on mil. duty, Aug., 1914, to May, 1920; civil surgn., Feb., 1921; ret'd., Aprl., 1933.

WELLS, WILLIAM GEORGE, late India Posts and Tels. (b. 24th Aprl., 1875).—Held non-gazetted appts. from 1st Mar., 1891; dep. supt., Aprl., 1914; on field ervice from 22nd Aprl., 1917, to 22nd Nov., 1918; asst. to dep. dir.-genl., traffic, Feb., 1920; supt., tele. traffic, Mar., 1921; supt., Agra tele. office, May, 1927; ret'd., Aprl., 1930.

WELSH, JOHN ALBERT VICTOR, Indian Service of Engrs. (exec. engr., Burma) (b. 24th Oct., 1893).—Joined the service as forest engr., Burma, 30th Nov., 1921; trans. to I.S.E. as asst. exec. engr., Jan., 1929; exec. engr., Oct., 1931.

WEMYSS, FREDERICK FLEMING, Indian Police (dist. supt., Burma) (b. 7th Dec., 1897).—Joined the service as asst. dist. supt., Burma, 6th Sept., 1921; asst. commr. of police, Ran- goon, Feb., 1927; offg. dist. supt., Feb., 1930; confd., Dec., 1930.

WENDT, REGINALD ERNEST GEOFFREY, B.A., late Indian Finance Dept. (b. 15th Sept., 1889).—Joined the service, 24th Dec., 1913; asst. acct.-gen., Mar., 1916; ditto, posts and tels., May, 1918; dep. contr., Assam, Mar., 1925; examr., outside audit, Bengal, Oct., 1926; asst. acctnt.-gen., Central revenues, Nov., 1927; dep. ditto, July, 1928; offg. dir. rly. audit, Aprl., 1930; ret'd., July, 1934.

WERNHAM, JOHN WILLIAM KENCH, M.C., B.A., late Indian Forest Service (Madras) (b. 8th Jan., 1895).—Joined the service as asst. consvt., Madras, 11th Dec., 1921; ret'd., Jan., 1931.

WEST, EDWARD JOHN WRIGHT, Indian Posts and Tels. (divl. engr.) (b. 4th Feb., 1884).—Joined the service, 18th Mar., 1904; supt. engrg. branch, 2nd div., Mar., 1919; asst. engrg. Aprl., 1921; divl. engr., May, 1935.

WEST, MICHAEL PHILIP, M.A. (Oxon), D.Ph. (Oxon), late Indian Educl. Service (b. 26th Sept., 1888).—Joined the service, 30th Nov., 1912, as prof., David Hare training coll., Calcutta; vice-princl., Dacca training coll., Sept., 1913; prinpl., ditto, Dec., 1920; ret'd., July, 1933.

WEST, STEWART ELLES LAWRENCE, O.B.E., Indian State Rlys. (divl. supt., (junr.), N.W. rly.) (b. 2nd Mar., 1890).—Joined the service as asst. traffic supt., 1st March, 1909; on milty. duty, 1st May, 1916; O.B.E., Feb., 1919; dist. traffic supt., Jan., 1922; offg. dep. ch. comml. manager, Nov., 1925; transpn. supt., Nov., 1930; offg. dep. ch. comml. mgr., Feb., 1932; and again, Jan., 1934; offg. dep. ch. operating supt., N.W. rly., May, 1933; offg. divl. supt. junior, N.W. rly., Mar., 1934; confd., Sept., 1934; ditto (senior), May, 1937.

WEST, WILLIAM FREDERICK, Office of High Commr. for India (controller, store dept.) (b. 13th June, 1882).—Apptd. clerk, 2nd class store dept., 22nd Nov., 1900; junior clerk, April, 1909; senr. clerk, Nov., 1915; transfed. to office of high commr. for India, Oct., 1920; contr., April, 1922.

WESTCOTT, The Most Rev. Foss, M.A., D.D., Indian Eccl. Estabt. (Bishop of Calcutta and Metropolitan of India and Ceylon) (b. 23rd Oct., 1863).—Educ. at Cheltenham and Peterhouse, Cambridge; Bishop in Chota Nagpore, 1905-1919; Bishop of Calcutta, Aug., 1919.

WESTERDALE, JOSEPH STANLEY, B.Sc., M.I.C.E., Indian Service of Engrs. (exec. eng., Madras) (b. 10th Aprl., 1886).—Joined the service as asst. engr., 1st Oct., 1909; exec. engr., Ap il, 1921; offg. sanitary engr., April, 1922; confd., Oct., 1925; on foreign service under Travancore durbar, Aug., 1930, to Aug., 1933.

WESTLAKE, ALAN ROBERT CECIL, Indian C.S. (collr., Madras) (b. 18th July, 1894).—Educ. at Univ. Coll. Sch., Frognal, and Brasenose Coll., Oxford; joined the service, 7th Aug., 1921; arrived, 7th Oct., 1921, and served in Madras as asst. collr. and mag.; sub-collr. and jt. mag., July, 1923; offg. collr., Feb., 1927; offg. collr. and dist. mag., May, 1931; ditto (probr. subs.), Sept., 1935; secty., bd. of rev., Madras, Aug., 1933.

WESTOBY EDWARD HAROLD, Office of High Commr. for India (exec. officer) (b. 22nd Apl., 1900).—Apptd. clerical officer, 6th Feb., 1920; actg. exec. offr., June, 1927; confd., Nov., 1929.

WESTON, A. T., M.Sc., A.M.I.C.E., Deptt. of Industries, Bengal (dir. of industries, Bengal) (b. 10th May, 1882).—Joined the service 4th March, 1909; temp. works manager, Cossipore, Dec., 1915; dep. dir. of industries, Bengal, Nov., 1920; offg. dir. ditto, April, 1925; confd., May, 1927.

WESTON, DONALD, late Indian C.S. (Bihar and Orissa) (b. 21st Feb., 1872).—Educ. at Trinity Coll., Stratford-on-Avon, and Univ. Coll., London; apptd. after exam. of 1893; arrived, 26th Dec., 1894, and served in Bengal as asst. mag. and collr.; joint mag., Dec., 1902; chief presy. mag., Calcutta, Oct., 1903, to Aug., 1904; mag. and collr., April, 1907; transf. to Bihar and Orissa, April, 1912; on special duty in the Bengal sectt., Oct., 1911, to Sept., 1912; commr. of excise and salt, inspr.-gen. of registrn., &c., Bihar and Orissa, from July, 1918; commr., Oct., 1922; offg. member, bd. of revenue, April, 1928; confd., Dec., 1928; retd., Dec., 1929.

WESTON, ERIC, B.A., Indian C.S. (judl. commr., Ajmer-Merwara) (b. 8th Dec., 1892).—Educ. at St. John's Coll., Cambridge; apptd. after exam. of 1915; arrived, 3rd Dec., 1916, and served in Bombay as asst. collr. and magte.; asst. judge, Sept., 1921; asst. judge and asst. sess. judge, Jan., 1924; judge and sess. judge, April, 1929; judl. asst., and addl. sess. judge, Aden; dist. and sess. judge, Aden, Oct., 1933; judl. commr., states of W.I., Sept., 1934; judl. commr., Ajmer-Merwara, June, 1937.

WETHERILL, HENRY BUSWELL, C.I.E., late Indian Educl. Service (United Provs.) (b. 21st Nov., 1876).—Joined the service, 23rd Dec., 1913; head master, Allahabad, Jan., 1914; inspr. of schools, Jan., 1917; C.I.E., June, 1931; retd., Nov., 1931.

WEYMOUTH, JAMES CHARLES, Indian Police (dep. inspr.-genl., United Provs.) (b. 5th Sept., 1887).—Joined the service as asst. supt., 30th Dec., 1907; supt., Aug., 1921; asst. inspr.-genl., rly. police, June, 1933; dep. inspr.-genl., Oct., 1937.

WHARTON, JOHN, late Indian State Rlys. (Burma Rlys.) (b. 6th May, 1884).—Joined the service as asst. loco. carr. supt., 9th Apl., 1908; on milty. duty from 29th May, 1917, to 2nd Jan., 1920; dist. loco. supt., Nov., 1921; retd., June, 1930.

WHAYMAN, EDWARD, late Marine deptt. Bengal (b. 10th May, 1874).—Joined the service as offg. asst. shipping master, 4th Dec., 1910; confd. Feb., 1921; dep. shipping master, Apl., 1927; retd., May, 1931.

WHEATLEY, WILLIAM GEOFFREY, Indian Service of Engrs. (exec. engr.) (b. 6th June, 1901).—Joined the service as asst. exec. engr., Punjab, 26th Sept., 1924; exec. engr., Apl., 1934.

WHEELER, Rev. CHARLES ERNEST RUAPHEU, Indian Eccles. Estabt. (senr. chaplain, Madras) (b. 13th Oct., 1887).—Apptd. chaplain on probation, 20th Dec., 1920; junr., chaplain, Dec., 1924; senr. chaplain, Dec., 1930.

WHEELER, EDWARD OLIVER, M.C., Lt.-Col. R.E., Survey of India (supt.) (b. 18th Apl., 1890).—Joined the service, 23rd June, 1910; in milty. employ to 8th Dec., 1919; asst. supt., 9th Dec., 1919; offg. dy. supt., Oct., 1920; on depn. with expedition for ascent of Mount Everest from 15th April to 20th Oct., 1921; offg. supt., March, 1926; confd., Oct., 1927.

WHEELER, Sir HENRY, K.C.S.I., K.C.I.E., late Indian C.S. (b. 2nd June, 1870).—Educ. at Christ's Coll., Cambridge; apptd. after exam. of 1889; arrived, 14th Dec., 1891; and served in Bengal as asst. mag. and colr.; joint mag. and dep. collr., Feb., 1898; under sec. to govt., various depts., July, 1898, to May, 1899; junr. sec., board of rev., April, 1902; member of salt committee, 1903-04, and drainage commn., 1906-07; mag. and collr., Sept., 1906; sec. to Royal Commn. on Ind an Decen raisal on, Nov., 1907, to April, 1909; sec. to govt., finl. and munipl. depts., July, 1909; C.I.E., Jan., 1910; sec. to govt. of Ind a Home Dept., Feb., 1912 to 1916; addl. member, govr.-gen.'s council; C.S.I., Jan., 1914; K.C.I.E., Jan., 1917; member, Bengal exc. council, April, 1917; K.C.S.I., June, 1921; govr. of Bihar and Orissa, 12th April, 1922; retd., Oct., 1927; member of council of India, Oct., 1927; vice-presdt., ditto, Aug., 1932; delegate to disarmament conf., Geneva, Feb., 1932.

WHEELER, THE VEN. HUGH TREVOR, M.A., late Indian Eccl. Estabt. (Punjab) (b. 27th Sept., 1874).—Apptd. proby. chaplain, 23rd Oct., 1902; junr. chaplain, Dec., 1904; senr. chaplain, Dec., 1914; archdeacon of Lahore, Jan., 1919; retd., Apl., 1930.

WHELAN, ERNEST CARNEGIE, A.M.I.C.E., Indian State Rlys. (exec. engr., N.W. Rly.) (b. 2nd Nov., 1889).—Joined the service as asst. engr., 1st Oct., 1912; exec. engr., Oct., 1921; govt. inspr. of rlys., Burma, Apl., 1932.

WHELAN, JOHN PRATT, Indian Police (b. 18th Dec., 1903).—Joined the service as asst. dist. supt., Central Provs., 22nd Nov., 1923; offg. dist. supt., Nov., 1927.

WHISH, ERIC VIPAN, O.B.E., Capt., R.I.M., retd. (b. 31st Aug., 1878).—Apptd. principal officer, Bombay Dist., 1st Feb., 1930; retd., July, 1931; nautical adviser to the govt. of India until May, 1935.

WHITAKER, CECIL CHARLES, Indian Police (asst. supt., Bihar) (b. 6th Nov., 1911).—Joined the service as proby. asst. supt., Bihar and Orissa, Nov., 1932; confd., Nov., 1934.

WHITAMORE, VERNON NORTHWOOD, O.B.E., F.R.C.S., Lt.-Col., I.M.S. retd. (b. 7th June, 1879).—Firs commn., 1st Sept., 1905; O.B.E., June, 1919; apptd. civil surgn., Punjab, May, 1923; retd., June, 1934.

WHITCOMBE, CHARLES EDWARD, A.M.I.C.E., late Indian Service of Engrs. (b. 30th Dec., 1887).—Joined the service, 6th Oct., 1911; asst. engr., Oct., 1912; on mil. service from 6th Oct., 1917, to 5th Oct., 1920; asst. exec. engr., Oct., 1920; exec. engr., Oct., 1921; ret'd., Jan., 1935.

WHITE, ARTHUR DENHAM, M.B., Lt.-Col., I.M.S., ret'd. (b. 26th Feb., 1879).—Joined the service 1st Feb., 1905; arrived 18th Dec., 1905; in mil. employ to March, 1910; services placed at the disposal of chief commr., Assam, April to Oct., 1914; services placed at disposal of govt. of India, home dept., Oct., 1914, to Sept., 1917; offg. p.o.r. med. coll. hosp., Calcutta, Oct., 1917; offg. civil surg., April, 1919; ditto (permanent), Jan., 1921; offg. protector of emigrants, Calcutta, Oct., 1924; ret'd., Feb., 1934.

WHITE, ARTHUR JOHN STANLEY, O.B.E., B.A., Indian C.S. (b. 28th Aug., 1896).—Educ. at Marlborough Coll., and Clare Coll., Cambridge; joined the service, 2nd Nov., 1922; arrived, 26th Dec., 1922, and served in Burma as asst. commr.; offg. under secty. to govt., home and pol. dept., Nov. 1924; offg. dep. commr., Oct., 1928; confd., Dec., 1934; O.B.E., June, 1932; secty. to finl. commr., Burma, Nov., 1933; secty. to govt., rev. dept., Burma, Dec., 1934; secty. educn. dept., Burma, Mar., 1935; and again, Dec., 1936.

WHITE, CHARLES FITZWILLIAM, Indian State Rlys. (works manager, G.I.P. Rly.) (b. 3rd Dec., 1885).—Joined the service as asst. works manager, 7th Jan., 1925; works manager, Feb., 1929; offg. dep. ch. mechl. engr. G.I.P. Rly., Nov., 1933.

WHITE, GERALD SEBASTIAN, O.B.E., late Judl. Dept., Madras (b. 12th Feb., 1878).—Apptd. asst. regr., April, 1911; dep. regr., Feb., 1915; offg. admin. -gen. and offl. trustee, April, 1926; offg. regr., high ct., July, 1927; offg. master, ditto, Sept., 1927; confd., Sept., 1929; offg. registrar, Nov., 1929; confd., July, 1930; O.B.E., Jan., 1938; ret'd., Mar., 1938.

WHITE, REV. JACK, Indian Eccl. Estabt. (junr. chaplain) (b. 12th Mar., 1903).—Apptd. 1st Apl., 1930; junr. chaplain, Mar., 1932.

WHITE, JOHN GROVE, A.M.I.C.E., late Indian State Rlys. (b. 30th Aug., 1882).—Joined the service as asst. dist. loco. supt., 23rd Dec., 1911; dist. loco. supt., June, 1917; pers. asst. to dep. mech. engr., July, 1925; works manager, Sept., 1928; ret'd., Sept., 1935.

WHITE, LESLIE STUART, Indian C.S. (judl. secty. to govt., United Provs.) (b. 30th March, 1888).—Educ. at Marlborough, and University Coll., Oxford; apptd. after exam. of 1910; arrived in India, 27th Nov., 1911, and served in the United Provs. as asst. mag. and collr. and asst. commr.; asst. supt. and judge, small cause ct., Dehra Dun, April, 1917, to May, 1918; jt. mag., April, 1917; on mil. duty, Aug. to Dec., 1918; dep. secy. to govt., and dep. leg. membr., Oct., 1921; addl. dist. and sess. judge, Oct., 1923; offg. ditto, Jan., 1924; confd., Nov., 1926; judl. secty. to govt., United Provs., July, 1934.

WHITE, M. H. GLEESON, late Indian Forest Service (b. 13th Jan., 1905).—Joined the service as asst. consvr., Madras, 20th Oct., 1930; ret'd., Dec., 1931.

WHITE, NICHOLAS, C.B.E., late Indian Service of Engrs. (Punjab) (b. 7th May, 1875).—Trained at R.I.E. Coll.; joined the service 1st Oct., 1897, as asst. examr., P.W. accts.; employed in Sibpur Eng. Coll., March to Aug., 1898; asst. engr., Nov., 1898; exec. engr., March, 1908; suptg. engr. (temp.), July, 1916; confirmed, Jan., 1919; chief engr. and sec. to govt., P.W.D. irrigation branch, March, 1925; C.B.E., June, 1929; ret'd., May, 1930.

WHITE, SYDNEY WILFRED, Indian State Rlys. (bridge engr., E.I. rly.) (b. 22nd June, 1884).—Joined the service, 7th Nov., 1921; resdt. engr., Feb., 1924; exec. engr. (junr.), E.I. rly., July, 1927.

WHITE, WILLIAM PURCELL, late Indian Police Service (b. 31st July, 1877).—Joined the service, 4th Nov., 1898, and posted to Central Prov.; dist. supt., Jan., 1907; offg. dep. inspr. gen., Oct., 1920; dep. inspr. gen., Jan., 1923; ret'd., Jan., 1929.

WHITEHEAD, GEORGE SYDNEY, B.Sc. (Econ.), India Office (exec. officr., milty. dept.) (b. 15th Nov., 1915).—Apptd. exec. officr., 12th Dec., 1934.

WHITEHEAD, GEORGE UNSWORTH, Indian C.S. (dist. and sess. judge, Punjab) (b. 5th Nov., 1903).—Educ. at Bury Grammar Sch. and Emmanuel Coll., Cambridge; joined the service 21st Oct., 1927; arrived, Dec., 1927, and served in the Punjab as asst. commr.; judge, small cause ct., Jan., 1932; addl. dist. and sess. judge, Apl., 1933; dist. and sess. judge, Nov., 1936.

WHITEHEAD, THOMAS ALEC, Indian Forest Service (chief consvr., forests, Madras) (b. 8th May, 1886).—Joined the service, 30th Oct., 1907, as asst. consvr.; dep. consvr., Nov., 1912; offg. consvr., June, 1923; confd., Feb., 1926; chief consvr., June, 1937.

WHITEHOUSE, GEORGE EDWARD, Indian Police (asst. supt., United Provs.) (b. 22nd July, 1911).—Joined the service, 26th Nov., 1930; asst. supt., Nov., 1932; offg. A.D.C. to govt., U.P., Dec., 1933; offg. supt., June, 1936.

WHITEHOUSE, RICHARD HENRY, M.Sc., D.Sc., late Indian Educl. Service.—Joined the service, 16th Sept., 1917; vice-prinpl., C.T. Coll., Lahore, Sept., 1922; offg. prinpl., Sept., 1924; ret'd., May, 1929.

WHITEHOUSE, VICTOR, Indian Police (b. 31st May, 1915).—Apptd. asst. supt., United Provs. on probn., 1st Nov., 1935.

WHITEHURST, SQUIRE, O.B.E., late Indian State Rlys. (G.I.P. Rly.) (b. 21st Sept., 1878).—Joined the service, 31st Oct., 1913; asst. works manager, Feb., 1920; offg. dep. ch. mech. engr., May, 1925; works manager, Dec., 1927; offg. dep. ch. mech. engr., Feb., 1929; ret'd., Nov., 1931.

WHITLEY, ROGER, B.A., late Indian State Rlys. (b. 21st June, 1879).—Joined the service as asst. engr., 16th Feb., 1906; resdt. engr., Sept., 1912; dist. engr., Feb., 1925; ret'd., Mar., 1934.

WHITLOCK, PERCY ODDIE, M.A., late Indian Educl. Service (b. 15th Mar., 1884).—Joined the service as prof., 23rd Dec., 1914; inspr. of schools, Bihar and Orissa, Feb., 1925; prof. of English, Ravenshaw Coll., Cuttack, Feb., 1926; dep. dir. public instrn., Dec., 1929; offg. prinpl., Patna Coll., Feb., 1932; prof. of English, ditto, Aug., 1933; retd., Apl., 1935.

WHITMORE, FRANK BELWORTHY, M.A., late Indian Educl. Service (b. 6th Oct., 1885).—Joined the service as head master Patna collegiate school, 7th Dec., 1915; prinpl., training coll., Cuttack, June, 1923; retd., Mar., 1933.

WHITNEY, ALBERT, Indian State Railways (dist. signal engr., G.I.P. rly.) (b. 9th Mar., 1886).—Joined the service, 16th Jan., 1915; dist. signal engr., G.I.P. rly., Mar., 1931; offg. dep. ch. engr., Apl., 1936.

WHITNEY, WILLIAM WELCH, A.M.I.C.E., Indian State Ry. (transpn. supt., E.I. rly.) (b. 11th May, 1888).—Joined the service, 25th Feb., 1921, as asst. dist. loco. supt.; asst. supt., E.I. rly., Feb., 1925; transpn. supt., E.I. rly., May, 1934.

WHITTAKER, ARNOLD, C.I.E., B.Sc., Indian C.S. (mag. and collr., Bengal) (b. 27th July, 1900).—Educ. at London Sch. of Economics, and Christ Church, Oxford; joined the service, 27th Oct., 1924; arrived, 9th Dec., 1924, and served in Bihar and Orissa as asst. mag. and collr.; under-secty. to govt. appt. and pol. depts., Dec., 1927; ditto home dept., govt. of India, Apl., 1930; served in Bengal from Feb., 1934; C.I.E., Jan., 1938.

WHITTAKER, HARRY, B.Sc., A.M.I.C.E., A.M.I.M.E., A.M.I.E.E. (b. 23rd Feb., 1879).—Prinpl. of technical and mechanical engrg. coll., Lahore, 22nd April, 1923, to Oct., 1934.

WHITTAKER, HERBERT, B.Sc., Indian C.S. (jt. mag. and dep. coll., Bihar) (b. 26th June, 1903).—Educ. at Colne Secondary School, School of Economics, London, and Christ Church, Oxford; joined the service, 19th Oct., 1927; arrived, Dec., 1927, and served in Bihar and Orissa as asst. mag. and collr.; offg. judl., commr., Chota Nagpur, Mar., 1934; offg. mag. and collr., Oct., 1934; offg. dist. and sess. judge, Mar., 1936.

WHITTLE, GEORGE WILLIAM MURDOCH, Indian C.S. (jt. mag., United Provs.) (b. 7th April, 1907).—Educ. at Secondary School, Washington Village, Durham, Durham University, and King's College, Cambridge; joined the service, 16th Oct., 1930; arrived in India, December, 1930, and served in the United Provs., as asst. mag. and collr.

WHITTY, SIR JOHN TARLTON, Kt., C.S.I., C.I.E., late Indian C.S. (b. 1st Feb., 1876).—Educ. at Clifton Coll., New Coll., Oxford, and Univ. Coll., London; apptd. after exam. of 1898; arrived, 29th Dec., 1899, and served in Bengal as asst. mag. and collr.; jt. mag. and dept. collr., Dec., 1908; transfld. to Bihar and Orissa, April, 1912; mag. and collr., April, 1912; C.I.E., Jan., 1916; manager of the Bettiah Wards estate, Apr., 1916; offg. commr., April, 1925; confd., Nov., 1928; temp. member of council, B. & O., April, 1929; confd., Dec., 1931; C.S.I., Jan., 1932; acting govr., Bihar and Orissa, Oct., 1934; retd., Feb., 1935; knighted, June, 1935.

WHITWORTH, CHARLES STANLEY, C.I.E., late Indian State Ry. (b. 14th June, 1880).—Joined the service, 24th Oct., 1909, as asst. mining manager; senr. asst. coal supt., Dec., 1911; chief mining engr., April, 1922; C.I.E., June, 1927; retd., June, 1935.

WHITWORTH, HUGH HOPE ASTON, B.A., Indian C.S. (asst. collr., Bombay) (b. 21st May, 1914).—Educ. at Bromsgrove Sch. and Pembroke Coll., Cambridge; joined the service, 25th Sept., 1937; arrived in India, Nov., 1937, and served in Bombay as asst. collr.

WHURR, LEONARD ERNEST, Indian Police (b. 1st Mar., 1905).—Joined the service as asst. dist. supt., Central Provs., 7th Dec., 1924; offg. dist. supt., Mar., 1928.

WHYTE, SIR ALEXANDER FREDERICK, K.C.S.I., M.A.—Lecteur adjoint at the Sorbonne, 1905-6; special commr. on industrial insurance, Vienna and Budapest, 1908; Lieut., R.N.V.R., on special duty, 1914-17; M.P. for Perth City, 1910-18; president, Indian legve. assembly, 1920 to 1925; Kt., Jan., 1922; K.C.S.I., June, 1925; author of the Old Quadrangle (part), 1907, The Practice of Diplomacy from the French of François de Callières.

WHYTE, ALEXANDER HORNBLY, late Indian Service of Engrs. (b. 3rd Dec., 1879).—Trained at the R.I.E. Coll.; joined the service, 1st Oct., 1901, as asst. engr.; exec. engr., Oct., 1911; offg. suptg. engr., Feb., 1924; suptg. engr. (provl. perm.) Nov., 1928; confd., July, 1930; retd., Dec., 1934.

WHYTE, QUINTIN ALEXANDER, M.A., late Indian C.S. (b. 7th Mar., 1909).—Educated at Morgan Academy, Dundee, St. Andrew's University and Downing College, Cambridge; apptd. after exam. of 1932; arrived in India, Nov., 1933; and served in the United Provinces as asst. mag. and collector; retd., Oct., 1935.

WICKENDEN, THOMAS DOUGLAS, B.A., Indian C.S. (dist. and sess. judge, Central Provs.) (b. 2nd Mar., 1903).—Educ. at St. Catharine's Coll., Cambridge; joined the service, 28th Oct., 1925; arrived, 5th Dec., 1925, and served in the Central Provs. as asst. commr.; offg. dep. commr., June, 1929; sub-judge, Oct., 1931; offg. dist. and sess. judge, June, 1932, May, 1933, and again, May, 1934; confd. Jan., 1936.

WICKHAM, EDWARD THOMAS RUSCOMBE, M.V.O., Lt.-Col., I.A., retd., late Indian Political Dept. (b. 4th May, 1890).—Joined the service, 29th Jan., 1910; in mil. employ to 31st Dec., 1918; on special duty in Europe with H.I.M. the Shah of Persia, Aug.-Dec., 1919; asst. to consul, Ahwaz, Dec., 1921; sec., Kabul Legation, Feb., 1922; offg. H.B.M.'s consul and agent of govt. of India in Khorasan, March, 1925; pol. agent and dep. commr., Quetta Pishin, March, 1926; sec. to agent to gov. genl. in Baluchistan, Dec., 1926; counselor, Kabul legation, March, 1927; M.V.O., March, 1928; pol. agent, Chagai, Nov., 1928; on spl. duty under A.G.G. and ch. commr. in Baluchistan, May, 1929; sec. to agent to govr.-genl., Baluchistan, Nov., 1929; pol. agent and dep. commr. in Quetta-Pishin, Oct., 1930; offg. dep. secty. to the govt. of India, for. and pol. dept., May, 1932; retd., Jan., 1935.

WICKHAM, HARRY TEMPLE, late India Police Dept.—Joined the service, 10th Nov., 1904; supt. of police, Dec., 1912; dist. offr., frontier constabulary, Dec., 1913, to Oct., 1917; comdt. mil. police, N.W.F. Prov., Sept., 1919; ret'd., June, 1925; apptd. commr. for the Superior Services (India) Family Pension Fund, Oct., 1937.

WICKRAMASINGHE, PERCIVAL HERBERT, B.A., B.Sc., late Indian C.S. (b. 24th Dec., 1909).—Educated at Royal College, Colombo, University of Ceylon and Trinity College, Cambridge; joined the service 9th Oct., 1934; arrived in India, Nov., 1934, and served in Bihar and Orissa as asst. mag. and collr.; ret'd., Dec., 1934.

WIGHTWICK, HERBERT MILNER, Lt.-Col. I.A., Indian Political Service (b. 17th Apl., 1885).—First commn., 16th Aug., 1905; apptd. asst. pol. agent and asst., Aden, Sept., 1909; controller, hostile trading concerns, 1917; 1st asst. to resdt., Baroda, Oct., 1925; pol. agent, Banas Kuntha, March, 1926; 1st asst. resdt., Aden, Mar., 1931; resdt., Kolhapur and pol. agent, S. Maratha country states, Oct., 1932; pol. agent, Sabar Kantha, May, 1933; offg. ch. commr. and C. in C., Aden, Dec., 1933; pol. agent, Haraoti and Tonk, Feb., 1934; resdt., W. Rajputana states, Aug., 1934; offg. resdt., Jaipur, in addn., June, 1936; offg. resdt., Jaipur, in addn., Sept., 1936, to Feb., 1937.

WILBERFORCE-BELL, Sir HAROLD, K.C.I.E., Lt.-Col. I.A., Indian Political Service (b. 17th Nov., 1885).—First commn., 29th Nov., 1905; asst. pol. agent, Kolhapur, Jan., 1910; on special duty in connection with the Delhi Durbar, 1911; pol. agent, Sorath (Kathiewar) Nov., 1912; tutor and guardian to H.H. Minor Nawab of Junagadh, Apl. to Dec., 1914; on mil. duty, 26th Dec., 1914, to 9th Dec., 1919; asst. mil. sec. to H.E. the C.-in-C. in India, May, 1918, to March, 1919; asst. resdt., Kolhapur, Feb., 1920; dep. sec., pol. dept., Bombay, 1920; asst. resdt., Aden, March, 1922; pol. agent, Kathiawar, Jan., 1924; adviser to H.H. the Raja of Chamba, 1925; sec. to agent to gov.-genl. in C.I., Nov., 1926; pol. agent in Bhopal, May, 1927; ditto in Southern States of C.I. and in Malwa, July, 1927; offg. dep. secty. govt. of India, for. and pol. dept., Nov., 1928; confd., May, 1929; offg. pol. secty., ditto, July, 1930; C.I.E., Jan., 1931; pol. agent, Southern Maratha country states, Apl., 1932; on special duty under for. and pol. dept., Oct., 1932; A.G.G. Deccan states and resdt., Kolhapur, Apl., 1933; offg. resdt. and A.G.G., Punjab states, Oct., 1934; resdt., 1st class, confd., Mar., 1937; resdt. for Punjab States, Apl., 1937; K.C.I.E., Jan., 1938; author of "The History of Kathiawar," and other works.

WILBY, FREDERICK WILLIAM, Indian Finance Dept. (b. 22nd May, 1882).—Joined the service, 1st March, 1909; asst. auditor, O. & R. Rly., June, 1918; auditor, J.B. Rly., March, 1921; govt. examr. of accts., B. & N.W. Rly., June, 1922; dep. auditor, O. & R. Rly., March, 1923; dep. acctnt.-gen., rlys., Sept., 1925; asst. auditor, N.W. Rly., Dec., 1926; services

placed at disposal of Jodhpur Durbar, Jan., 1927, to Feb., 1930; with finl. commr. for rlyws., Nov., 1930; with Burma Rlys., Feb., 1931; offg. ch. accts. officer, Burma rlys., June, 1933; addl. dep. dir., rly. clearing accts., (temp.), Oct., 1934; offg. ch. auditor, N.W. rly., June, 1935.

WILCOCK, JOHN STEWART, B.A. (Cantab.), Indian C.S. (secty. to govr., Orissa) (b. 6th Feb., 1905).—Educ. at Manchester Grammar Sch., and Pembroke Coll., Cambridge; joined the service, 5th Oct., 1928; arrived, 30th Nov., 1928, and served in Bihar and Orissa as asst. mag. and collr.; under secty. to govt., pol. and appts. dept., Mar., 1932; offg. secty. to A.G.G., Eastern States, Mar., 1935; offg. mag. and collr., Dec., 1935; pte. secty. to govr., Orissa, Apl., 1936; secty., ditto, Apl., 1937.

WILCOX, NEVILLE CECIL, late Opium Dept., United Provs. (b. 2nd Feb., 1876).—Joined the service, 31st March, 1896; joint opium officer, Malwa, March, 1924; ret'd., Feb., 1931.

WILD, ARTHUR CLEMENT, B.A., late Indian C.S. (b. 19th June, 1875).—Educ. at Highgate Sch., and Corpus Christi Coll., Cambridge; apptd. after exam. of 1898; arrived, 24th Nov., 1899, and served in Bombay as asst. collr. and mag. and asst. judge; asst. rembr. of legal affairs, and sec. to council of govr., March, 1908, to Feb., 1909; dist. and sess. judge, April, 1914; judge and rembr. of legal affairs and secy. to govt., legal dept., Oct., 1919; agent for Sardars in the Deccan, Oct., 1923; addl. judl. commr. of Sind, Jan., 1929; offg. judge, high ct., Bombay, June, 1929; judl. commr. of Sind, Nov., 1930; ret'd., Sept., 1933.

WILES, Sir GILBERT, K.C.I.E., C.S.I., M.A., Indian C.S. (collr., Bombay) (b. 25th Mar., 1880).—Educ. at Perse Gr. Sch., and St. Catharine's Coll., Cambridge; apptd. after exam. of 1903; arrived, 25th Nov., 1904, and served in Bombay as asst. collr. and mag.; supt. of land records and regn., Nov., 1910; supt. of land records, Aug., 1915, to May, 1916; temp. dep. dir. of civil supplies, June, 1918; dep. sec. to govt., home dept., Nov., 1921; asst. collr. and sec. to govt., finance dept., Dec., 1923; collr. and ditto, Oct., 1925; member legisl. council, 1924, 1927, 1930, 1931, and 1932; also finl. adviser to govt., dev. and P.W. depts., April, 1928; C.I.E., Jan., 1926; C.S.I., Jan., 1931; member, tariff bd., Sept., 1933; presdt., ditto, Oct., 1934; chairman, Bombay Port Trust, June, 1935; K.C.I.E., Jan., 1938.

WILKES, JOHN SEBASTIAN, Indian Police dep. inspr.-genl., Madras) (b. 31st Jan., 1888).—Joined the service, 24th Nov., 1908; asst. supt., May, 1910; supt., Nov., 1922; dep. commr., Apl., 1929; offg. dep. inspr.-genl., Mar., 1934; confd., June, 1936.

WILKIE, HUGH GRAHAM, B.A., Indian C.S. (secty., govt. of Burma lands and rev. dept.), b. 13th Mar., 1893).—Educ. at Uppingham and Brasenose Coll., Oxford; on mil. service from Aug., 1914, to March, 1919; apptd., 7th Oct., 1919; arrived, 9th Dec., 1919, and served in Burma; colonization officer, Jan., 1922; headqrs. asst., May,

1923; offg. dep. commr., Mar., 1924; confd., May, 1931; administrator of govt. estates, Nov., 1926; land officer, Rangoon dev. trust, Mar., 1928; offg. secty. to govt., educn. dept., Burma, Oct., 1930; secty., lands and rev. dept., May, 1937.

WILKIE, RALPH SCUDAMORE, B.A., Indian C.S. (asst. commr., Burma) (b. 13th March, 1896).—Educ. at Uppingham, and Emmanuel Coll., Cambridge; joined the service, 1st Nov., 1921; arrived, 20th Dec., 1921, and served in Burma as asst. commr.; under-secty. to govt., home and pol. dept., June, 1924; asst. settl. officer, Nov., 1924; offg. dep. commr., Dec., 1928; offg. settlement officer, Mandalay, Nov., 1930.

WILKINS, PAUL BURGMAN, M.C., D.F.C., Indian Police (supt., Bombay) (b. 11th Aug., 1896).—Joined the service as asst. supt., Bombay, 11th Nov., 1923; offg. supt., May, 1929; awarded King's police medal, Jan., 1931; offg. dep. commr., Jan., 1933; supt., Jan., 1936.

WILKINSON, Rev. ERNEST ROLAND, M.A., Indian Eccles. Estabt. (senr. chaplain) (b. 24th July, 1882).—Joined the service, 5th Nov., 1913; junr. chaplain, Nov., 1916; senr. chaplain, Nov., 1926.

WILKINSON, HECTOR RUSSELL, C.I.E., Indian C.S. (mag. and collr., Bengal) (b. 11th March, 1888).—Educ. at Clifton and Queen's Coll., Oxford; apptd. after exam. of 1911; arrived, 30th Nov., 1912, and served in Bengal as asst. mag. and collr.; addl. under-sec. to govt. of Bengal, Nov., 1917; pte. sec. to H.E. the govr. of Burma in addn., Feb., 1918; under-sec. to govt. of Bengal, judl., pol. and appt. depts., April, 1918; pte. sec. to the govr. of Bengal, Nov., 1918, to Dec., 1919, and from March, 1922, to April, 1927; C.I.E., Jan., 1927; mag. and collr., Feb., 1927; offg. secty. to govt. of Bengal, dept. of educn., Oct., 1931; confd., Apl., 1932.

WILKINSON, WALTER HUGH JOHN, C.S.I., C.I.E., B.A., late Indian C.S. (b. 31st May, 1874).—Educ. at Winchester, and University Coll., Oxford; apptd. after exam. of 1896; arrived, 29th Nov., 1897, and served in Bombay as asst. collr. and mag.; asst. collr. of customs, &c., and chief inspr. of factories, March, 1902; asst. to resdt., Hyderabad, March, 1903; tutor to Maharaja of Kishengarh, May, 1904; offg. asst. sec. and subsequently offg. under-sec. to govt. of India, foreign dept., June to Dec., 1906; pol. asst., Ajmer, Nov., 1907, and offg. principal, Mayo College; 1st asst. to agent to gov.-gen., Rajputana, Jan., 1909; pol. agent and ditto, Oct., 1909; tutor and guardian to second surviving son of H.H. the Gaekwar of Baroda, March, 1910, to May, 1911; pol. offr. with H.H. the Maharaja of Patiala for the coronation, May to July, 1911; 1st asst. to agent to govr.-gen., Rajputana, and chief commr., Ajmer-Merwara, Sept., 1911, to May, 1912; offg. dep. sec. to govt. of India, for. dept., May to Sept., 1912; 1st asst. to agent to govr.-gen., Rajputana, and chief commr., Ajmer-Merwara, Sept., 1912, to April, 1913; offg. dep. sec. to govt. of India, for. dept., April to Dec., 1913; served under the War Office in France from Sept., 1914, to Dec., 1918

(four times mentioned in despatches; chevalier of the Order of Leopold I of Belgium, June, 1917; chevalier of the Légion d'Honneur, July, 1917; C.I.E., June, 1919); junr. collr. (seconded), Aug., 1915; 1st asst. to the resdt. in Mysore, and sec. to the chief commr., Coorg, March, 1919; offg. resdt., Baroda, May, 1919; resdt. in Mewar and pol. agent, Kotah and Jhalawar, June, 1920; British Envoy at Court of Nepal, April, 1924; C.S.I., Jan., 1930; ret'd., Mar., 1932; name changed by Deed Poll to Wilkinson-Guillemand, Feb., 1934.

WILKINSON-GUILLEMARD, WALTER HUGH JOHN, (see Wilkinson, W.H.J.)

WILL, WILLIAM MILLER, M.B., Ch.B., Lt.-Col., I.M.S. (asst. dir.-genl., I.M.S.) (b. 18th Feb., 1888).—First commn. dated 29th Jan., 1918; in milty. employ prior to 21st Apl., 1925, when he joined medl. store dept., asst. dir.-genl., I.M.S., Oct., 1935.

WILLCOCKS, HAROLD, B.Sc., Indian Service of Engrs. (exec. engr., central P.W.D.) (b. 11th May, 1895).—Joined the service as asst. engr., 1st Oct., 1919; asst. exec. engr., Aug., 1921; exec. engr., Mar., 1927; offg. supt. exec. engr., variously from Apl., 1932.

WILLFORD, GEORGE MARK, Indian Forest Service (dep. consvr., Central Prov.) (b. 20th Nov., 1892).—Joined the service as extra asst. consvr., July, 1915; dep. consvr., July, 1930.

WILLIAMS, ALEXANDER THOMAS, India Office (princ., finl. dept.), (b. 27th Feb., 1886).—Apptd. second class clerk, India Office, 22nd Mar., 1907; asst. editor, 'India Office List,' Oct., 1918; minor staff clerk, Jan., 1919; asst. principal, Apl., 1921; accts. dept., June, 1921; finl. dept., Feb., 1926; joint editor, 'India Office List,' Apl., 1926, to Aug., 1927; reading clerk to Council, Aug., 1927; pte. secty. to perm. under-secty. of state, Sept., 1930, seconded to finl. dept., Jan. to May, 1932; actg. princ., finl. dept., May, 1933; secty., Ind. staty. rly. bd. ctee., June, 1933; secty. Ind. Res. Bank ctee., July, 1933; princ., Mar., 1936; apptd. to undertake duties of asst. dir. of Indian rly. companies, Jan., 1936.

WILLIAMS, ALFRED ARTHUR, B.A., Indian Civil Service (asst. commr., Punjab) (b. 16th May, 1910).—Educated at Merchant Taylor's School, Crosby, and Selwyn College, Cambridge; apptd. after the exam. of 1931; arrived in India, Nov., 1932, and served in Punjab as asst. commr.; sub divl. officer., Oct., 1937.

WILLIAMS, ALFRED EDWARD, O.B.E., V.D., M.I.M.E., Indian State Rlys. (b. 13th Mar., 1878).—Joined the service, 3rd May, 1907; asst. works manager, Nov., 1912; works manager, Nov., 1918; dist. loco. supt., April, 1919; dist. mech. engr., Nov., 1922; offg. dep. ch. mech. engr., April, 1923; confd., May, 1925; offg. ch. mech. engr., Oct., 1926; and again, Dec., 1931; confd. (prov.), Mar., 1932; O.B.E., June, 1927.

WILLIAMS, ARTHUR DE COETLOGAN, Indian C.S. (addl. jl. secty. to the Govt. of India, legisl. dept.) (b. 27th Sept., 1890).—Educ. at Marlborough, and Balliol Coll., Oxford; apptd. after exam. of 1913; arrived, 7th May, 1915,

and served in Bengal as asst. mag. and collr.; cont. mech. transport stores depot, Rawal Pindi, Dec., 1916, to March, 1919; on mil. duty, May to Sept., 1919; services lent temporarily to govt. of Assam as offg. dist. and sess. judge, April, 1925; offg. sec., govt. of Bengal, legl. dept., and sec., legis. council, Feb., 1926; dist. and sess. judge, May, 1926; addl. dep. secty., legis. dept., govt. of India, Dec., 1927; offg. dep. secty., ditto, Apl., 1929; on spl. duty, home dept., govt. of India, Nov., 1931; sec. to govt., Bengal, legl. dept., Feb., 1934; supt. and remembrancer of legal affairs, July, 1934; offg. jt. secty. to Govt. of India, legl. dept., Mar., 1935; addl. dep. secty., ditto, Oct., 1935; secty., council of state, Dec., 1935; dep. secty. to govt. of India, legl. dept., Apl., 1936; addl. jt. secty., ditto, Nov., 1936; offg. secty. to the govt.-genl.'s exec. council in addn., Nov., 1936; confid., Mar., 1937.

WILLIAMS, CHARLES FREDERICK VICTOR, Indian C.S. (collr. and dep. magte.) (b. 21st May, 1898).—Educ. at West Ham and Pembroke Coll., Oxford; apptd. after exam. of 1923; arrived in India, 12th Dec., 1923. and served in Madras as asst. collr. and mag.; sub-collr. and jt. magte., Feb., 1928; under-secty. to govt., ch. secretariat, Madras, July, 1929; jt. secty., Indian round-table conf., London, Nov.-Dec., 1932; under secty. to govt. of India, home dept., Apl., 1933; offg. dep. secty., ditto, Apl., 1934; secty. to agent of the govt. of India in South Africa, Sept., 1934; collr. and dep. magte., Aug., 1937.

WILLIAMS, CHARLES ROBERT FARMER, M.C., B.A., Indian Forest Service (dep. consvr., Madras) (b. 19th June, 1893).—Joined the service as asst. consvr., 21st March, 1921; dep. consvr., March, 1922; prinpl., Madras forest coll., Coimbatore, July, 1924, to July, 1931.

WILLIAMS, CUTHBERT ARTHUR, late Indian Stores Dept. (b. 20th Jan., 1887).—Joined the service as asst. signal engr., Indian State Rlys., 9th Oct., 1910; with Indian Munitions Board from 13th Oct., 1917, to 24th Feb., 1920; contr. of sales, Calcutta, Oct., 1920; offg. contr. of stores, O. & R. Rly., May, 1921; dep. dir. of purchase, June, 1923; offg. dir. of purchase, Mar., 1931; ret'd., July, 1935.

WILLIAMS, DAVID, V.D., Indian State Rlys. (transportation supt., G.I.P., Rly.) (b. 12th Dec., 1889).—Joined the service as dist. loco. supt., 25th March, 1920; asst. trans. supt., 1st Nov., 1922; supt., May, 1926; offg. divl. trans. supt., variously from Aug., 1926.

WILLIAMS, EDWIN OWEN, Indian Forest Engrg. Service (b. 22nd Jan., 1894).—Joined the service as forest engr., Punjab, 11th Nov., 1921.

WILLIAMS, EMILY MATILDA, India Office (higher clerical officer, acct.-genl.'s dept.) (b. 21st Feb., 1894).—Appointed clerical officer, 5th Sept., 1921; higher clerical officer, Sept., 1932.

WILLIAMS, FRANK ROBERT, India Office (higher clerical officer, fin. dept.) (b. 26th Nov., 1887).—Apptd. clerical officer, 15th Aug., 1921; actg. higher clerical officer, 1st Nov., 1926; confid., June, 1930.

WILLIAMS, GEOFFREY BAYNTON, M.C., Major, I.A., Indian Political Service (b. 19th Aug., 1896).—First commn. dated 13th Oct., 1914; in milty. employ prior to 27th July, 1925; asst. collr. and magte., Bareilly, July, 1925; under-secty. to agent to govr.-genl. in C.I., March, 1926; ditto, in States of W.I., Dec., 1926; guardian and tutor to heir-apparent of Nawab of Junagadh, Dec., 1928; secty. to A.G.G., in the states of W.I., Nov., 1932; under-secty. ditto, Oct., 1933; pol. agent., Sabar Kantha, Dec., 1933; secty. to A.G.G. Eastern states, Dec., 1935; pol. agent, Bhopal, Mar., 1936.

WILLIAMS, HAROLD VINITH, late Indian Service of Engrs. (b. 9th June, 1898).—Joined the service as asst. engr., 13th Oct., 1931; exec. engr., Oct., 1930; ret'd., Feb., 1935.

WILLIAMS, HARRY EDWIN, Indian State Rlys. (E.B. Rly.) (b. 15th Apl., 1888).—Joined the service as asst. traffic supt., 23rd July, 1909; dist. ditto, Aug., 1922; ret'd., Dec., 1930.

WILLIAMS, Rev. Henry FRANK FULFORD, M.A., Indian Eccles. Estabt. (senr. chaplain) (b. 11th Nov., 1885).—Joined the service, 20th Feb., 1913; junr. chaplain, Feb., 1916; senr. chaplain, Feb., 1926.

WILLIAMS, HERBERT ARMSTRONG, B.A., M.B., B.Ch., D.S.O., Lt.-Col., I.M.S. (ret'd.) (b. 11th Feb., 1875).—First commn. dated 27th Jan., 1900; in mil. emp. to June, 1903, when he was apptd. dep. sany. commr., Burma; resdt. medl. offr., Rangoon genl. hosp., Jan., 1905; plague medl. offr., Burma, Sept., 1906; supt., Rangoon lunatic asylum, Jan., 1907; resdt. medl. offr., Rangoon genl. hosp., Dec., 1907; D.S.O., Nov., 1900; resdt. medl. offr., Mandalay, Nov., 1912; services placed at disposal of govt. of India, April, 1915, to Jan., 1920; offg. inspr.-genl. of civil hosp., Apl., 1929; ret'd., Jan., 1930.

WILLIAMS, HUBERT WATSON, B.A., Barr., late Indian C.S. (b. 10th Sept., 1884).—Educ. at Richmond (Yorks) Gr. Sch., and Queen's Coll., Oxford; apptd. after exam. of 1908; arrived, 27th Nov., 1909, and served in Bengal as asst. mag. and collr.; transfd. to Bihar and Orissa, April, 1912; jt. mag. and dep. collr., Aug., 1916; offg. dist. and sess. judge, Dec., 1924; confid. Jan., 1927; registrar, high ct., Patna, March, 1926; ret'd., Sept., 1929.

WILLIAMS, Rev. JAMES ERNEST HARRIS, M.A., late Indian Eccles. Estabt.—Apptd. chaplain on probation, 16th March, 1906; junr. chaplain, March, 1909; senr. chaplain, March, 1919; ret'd., Mar., 1929.

WILLIAMS, JAMES HAROLD, late Survey of India (b. 14th Oct., 1879).—Joined the service as proby. sub-assst. supt., 20th Nov., 1899; confid., Nov., 1900; extra asst. supt., Feb., 1908; on milty. duty from 1st Oct., 1915, to 12th Aug., 1921; supt., Oct., 1929; ret'd., Apl., 1932.

WILLIAMS, JOHN HOWARD FRY, Indian Police (b. 6th Oct., 1914).—Apptd. asst. supt., Assam, on probn., 10th Nov., 1935; asst. to pol. offcr., Sadiya, May, 1937.

WILLIAMS, RICHARD AELWYN ELLIS, B.A., Indian C.S. (jt. mag. and dep. collr., Bihar) (b. 5th Dec., 1901).—Educ. at Univ. of Wales and Lincoln Coll., Oxford; joined the service, 28th Oct., 1925; arrived, 7th Dec., 1925, and served in Bihar and Orissa as asst. mag. and collr.; under-sec. to govt. pol. dept., March, 1930; offg. asst. pol. agent, Orissa feudatory states, Mar., 1932; offg. mag. and collr., Feb., 1933.

WILLIAMS, RUSHBROOK—see RUSHBROOK WILLIAMS.

WILLIAMS, SEVERN, O.B.E., Lt.-Col., I.A., Indian Political Service (b. 14th Feb., 1884).—First commn., 27th Jan., 1904; in mil. employ to July, 1910; prob., pol. dept. and asst. pol. agent, Mekram, July, 1910; pol. agent, Tochi, Nov., 1920; city mag., Peshawar, May, 1921; under-sec. to resdt. at Hyderabad, Oct., 1923; pol. agent and dep. commr., Sibi, March, 1925; pol. agent, Zhob, Dec., 1927; ditto, Chagai and dep. commr., Shorarud, Nov., 1929; pol. agent, Loralai, Apl., 1931; ditto, Quetta-Pishin, Apl., 1935; O.B.E., Nov., 1935; asst. to resdt., Kashmir, Apl., 1936.

WILLIAMS, THOMAS TALIESIN, M.A., B.Sc., F.S.S., late Indian Educl. Service (Bengal) (b. 30th Aug., 1876).—Joined the service, 30th Dec., 1910; on mil. duty, Aug., 1918, to May, 1919; on special duty in office of collr. of income tax, Calcutta, May, 1919; collr. of income tax, Calcutta, Aug., 1919; asst. commr. of income tax, April, 1922; prinpl., Rajshahi Coll., Nov., 1926; ret'd., Aug., 1931.

WILLIAMS, Rev. WILLIAM PERCIVAL, B.A., Indian Eccl. Estabt. (junr. chaplain).—Apptd. chaplain-on-probation, 10th Mar., 1929; junr. chaplain, Mar., 1931; offg., archdeacon, Feb., 1936.

WILLIAMSON, ALEC, M.A., Indian C.S. (dep. commr., Burma) (b. 7th Nov., 1886).—Educ. at Kirkcudbright Academy, Edinburgh, and Balliol Coll., Oxford; apptd. after exam. of 1909; arrived in India, 6th Dec., 1910, and served in Burma as asst. commr. and asst. settlt. officer; on mil. service, Jan., 1918, to April, 1919; dep. chairman, bd. of trustees for development of Rangoon, March, 1925; dep. commr. (provl. sub.), Feb., 1926; offg. land officer, Rangoon Development Trust, March, 1927; officer i/c., Mandalay municipal commee., Oct., 1929; offg. chairman, Rangoon dev. trust, July, 1933; excise commr., Mar., 1934; commr. of settlements and land records, Burma, Mar., 1936.

WILLIAMSON, HAROLD, O.B.E., M.D., F.R.C.S., Lt.-Col., I.M.S. (civil surg.) (b. 26th Feb., 1891).—First commn., 3rd Dec., 1921; offg. agency surg., Quetta, Apl., 1931; confd., Feb., 1933; resdy. surg., Hyderabad, Apl., 1935; civil surg., Sibi and Loralai, Oct., 1935; offg. resdy. surg., Mysore, Mar., 1937.

WILLIAMSON, HERBERT STANSFIELD, M.A., late Indian C.S.—Educ. at Leeds Grammar Sch., and Christ Church, Oxford; apptd. after exam. of 1900; arrived, 10th Dec., 1901, and served in the Punjab as asst. commr.; asst. coln.

offr., Chenab Canal Colony, July, 1906, to Dec., 1907; junior sec. to finl. commrs., Punjab, April, 1909; on sol. duty in office of finl. commr., Oct., 1910; junr. sec. to finl. commrs., Feb. to April, 1912; offg. dep. commr., April, 1912; settlt. offr., Oct., 1912; employed in finl. dept., India Office, from Jan. to Aug., 1917; on mil. duty from May to Aug., 1918; dep. commr., Feb. 1918; senior sec. to fin. commrs., Punjab, March 1920; ret'd., Oct., 1922; apptd. sec., Univ. Delegacy for Oriental Students, April, 1923.

WILLIAMSON, Sir HORACE, Kt., C.I.E., M.B.E., late Indian Police (adviser to sec. of state for India) (b. 16th July, 1880).—Joined the service, 8th Dec., 1900, and posted to United Provs.; asst. supt., April, 1906; supt., March, 1913; asst. to inspr.-gen. of police, Mar., 1917; M.B.E., Jan., 1919; temply. employed in judl. and public depts., India Office, from Dec., 1920, to April, 1921; C.I.E., Jan., 1922; offg. dep. inspr.-genl., Oct., 1923; confd., July, 1925; offg. inspr.-genl., May, 1928; awarded King's police medal, Jan., 1931; dir., intell. bureau, home dept., govt. of India, Apl., 1931; Knighted, Jan., 1934; ret'd., Sept., 1936; member, Council of India, Dec., 1936; adviser to sec. of state for India, Apl., 1937.

WILLIAMSON, RICHARD HARCOURT, C.I.E., B.A., Indian C.S. (b. 1st Dec., 1879).—Educ. at St. Paul's Sch., and Exeter Coll., Oxford; apptd. after exam. of 1902; arrived, 30th Nov., 1903, and served in the United Provs. as asst. mag. and collr.; jt. mag., Sept., 1913; mag. and collr., March, 1921; C.I.E., Jan., 1931; offg. commr., Sept., 1931; confd., Oct., 1932.

WILLINGDON, Most Hon. MARQUESS OF, FREEMAN FREEMAN-THOMAS, P.C., G.C.S.I., G.C.M.G., G.C.I.E., G.B.E., LL.D.—Govr. of Madras, 10th Apl., 1910, to Apl., 1924; Viceroy and govr.-genl. of India, 19th Apl., 1931, to Apl., 1936.

WILLIS, ALFRED CHARLES, M.B.E., late Indian Service of Engrs. (b. 15th Sept., 1886).—Joined the service, 17th Oct., 1908; asst. engr., Oct., 1909; on mil. service from 16th Aug., 1917, to 22nd March, 1920; exec. engr., Oct., 1917; offg. suptg. engr., June, 1930; M.B.E., Jan., 1931; ret'd., Jan., 1935.

WILLIS, Sir GEORGE HENRY, Kt., C.I.E., M.V.O., M.I.Mech.E., M.I.E. (Ind.), Col., R.E., ret'd. (b. 21st Oct., 1875).—First commn. dated 3rd Aug., 1895; apptd. to mint dept., June, 1907; dep. mint master, Calcutta, Oct., 1909; M.V.O., Dec., 1911; dep. mint master, Bombay, July, 1912; offg. mint master, Calcutta, Jan., 1913, to Feb., 1915; junr. mint master, Bombay, Oct., 1915; C.I.E., June, 1918; mint master, Bombay, March 1921; senior mint master, May, 1923; master, security printing, India, Feb., 1926; Kt., June, 1928; ret'd., Oct., 1934.

WILLIS, HAROLD MITCHELL, B.A., Indian C.S. (collr., Bombay) (b. 26th Sept., 1890).—Educ. at St. Paul's School, and Christ Church, Oxford; apptd. after exam. of 1914; arrived 9th Dec., 1915, and served in Bombay as asst. collr.; offg. asst. collr. of customs, Oct., 1919; asst. collr. and personal asst.

to collr., Nov., 1922; offg. collr. and dist. magte. and Talukdari settlt. officer; offg. dist. magte. and collr., May, 1926, and again, Apl., 1933; confd., Apl., 1935.

WILLMORE, WILLIAM SOUTHWICK, M.D., Col., I.M.S. (ret'd.) (b. 7th Aug., 1872).—First commn., 28th Jan., 1899; apptd. temply. to civil employ, United Provs., May, 1903; civil sürgn., ditto, March, 1907; ditto and supt., dist. jail, April, 1909; princpl., med. sch., Agra, May to Oct., 1914; reverted to mil. duty, Oct., 1914, to Jan., 1920; prof., King George's Med. Coll., Lucknow, Oct., 1922, to Jan., 1923; supt., dist. jail, Cawnpore, July, 1923; inspr.-genl. of civil hospitals, May, 1927; ret'd., Aug., 1929.

WILLMOTT, HENRY CLAUDE, B.A., Indian C.S. (asst. mag. and collr., U.P.) (b. 18th May, 1914).—Educ. at Charterhouse and Queen's Coll., Cambridge; joined the service, 23rd Sept., 1937; arrived in India, Nov., 1937, and served in the U.P. as asst. mag. and collr.

WILLMOTT, HERBERT, India Audit Office (higher exec. officer) (b. 9th June, 1887).—Apptd. clerical officer, 9th Nov., 1921; exec. officer, April 1922; hr. exec. officer, Mar. 1936.

WILLOUGHBY, VERE WILLIAM DIGBY, Capt., I.A., Indian Political Service (b. 18th Aug., 1901).—First commn., 23rd Dec., 1921; in milty. employ to 20th June, Dec., 1921; in milty. employ to 20th June, 1927; vice-consul, Ahwaz, Dec., 1928; asst. pol. agent, N. Waziristan Nov., 1931; under secty. to resdt., Hyderabad, Mar., 1932; secty. to ditto (temp.), July, 1934; asst. pol. agent, Quetta-Pishin, Aug., 1935; ditto, Zhob, Oct., 1935.

WILLS, CECIL UPTON, C.I.E., B.A., late Indian C.S. (Central Provs.).—Educ. at Tiverton Sch. and Balliol Coll., Oxford; apptd. after exam. of 1900; arrived, 16th Nov., 1901, and served in the Central Provs. as asst. commr., and regr., judl. commr.'s court; settlmt. offr., Jan., 1906; regr., co-op. credit soc., Dec., 1910; under-sec. to chief commr., May, 1911, to Feb., 1912; commr. of settlts. and dir. of land records, July, 1912; dir. of agric. and industries, Feb., 1913; dep. commr., April, 1915; spl. duty as member of the village Panchayats commee., Jan., 1918; 2nd sec. to chief commr., March, 1918; chief sec. to chief commr., Aug., 1918; spl. duty in sectt., Oct., 1918; dep. dir. of civil supplies, Jan. to April, 1919; excise commr., Cent. Provs., Dec., 1920; supt. of stamps (in addn.), Feb., 1922; offg. commr., April, 1925; confd., Aug., 1927; C.I.E., Jan., 1927; ret'd., July, 1929.

WILSDON, BERNARD HOWELL, M.A., B.Sc., late Indian Educl. Service (Punjab) (b. 11th Dec., 1888).—Joined the service as prof. of chemistry, govt. coll., Lahore, 17th March, 1913; agric. chemist, March, 1917; prof., Amritsar, April, 1922; inspr. of schools, Jan., 1923; scientific research officer, Aug., 1924; ret'd., Aug., 1929.

WILSON, ANDREW, M.A., B.Sc., Indian Agric. Service (dep. dir. agric., Ootacamund) (b. 27th Aug., 1886).—Joined the service as asst. economic botanist, United Provs., 27th Jan., 1912; dep. dir. of agric., I.A.S., on probn., June, 1916; confd., Mar., 1920; spec. officer., Impl. Cl. of Agric. Research, Oct., 1937.

WILSON, SIR ARNOLD TALBOT, K.C.I.E., C.S.I., C.M.G., D.S.O., M.P., Bt. Lieut.-Col. I.A. (ret'd.).—First commn. dated 19th Aug., 1903; was in milty. employ until Dec., 1907, when apptd. to special duty in S. Persia; H.B.M.'s consul for Arabistan, July, 1909; on special duty under pol. resdt., Persian Gulf, Jan., 1911; C.M.G., June, 1912; 2nd asst. to ditto, July, 1912; addl. asst. to ditto for special duty in Khuristan, March, 1913; on special duty as dep. British commr., and later as British commr., Turko-Persian Frontier Boundary Commn., from Aug., 1913; on spl. duty, Persian Gulf, Jan., 1915; dep. chief pol. offr. Basrah, from Jan., 1916; dep. civil commr., Baghdad, from Oct., 1917; offg. civil commr., Baghdad, from March, 1918; C.S.I., June, 1919; K.C.I.E., Dec., 1919; pol. resdt. in Persian Gulf, Oct., 1920; ret'd., Aug., 1921; M.P., Hitchin, June, 1933.

WILSON, ARTHUR NORMAN, Indian Service of Engrs. (exec. engr., Punjab) (b. 4th May, 1902).—Joined the service as asst. exec. engr., Punjab, 25th Sept., 1925; exec. engr., Sept., 1935; vice-princpl., govt. school of engrg., Rasul, Sept., 1936.

WILSON, CECIL CLAUDE, B.A., Indian Forest Service (consr., Madras) (b. 11th July, 1885).—Joined the service, 21st Dec., 1908, as asst. consvr., dep. consvr., 1914; on milty. service, Oct., 1916, to Mar., 1919; actg. consr., Sept., 1924; confd., March, 1926; forest economist, Dehra Dun, Nov., 1925.

WILSON, CECIL HENRY ERSKINE, late Salt Dept., Bombay (b. 7th Dec., 1883).—Held non-gazetted appts. from 15th Aug., 1907, to 17th Aug., 1915; asst. collr. of salt rev., Aug., 1915; on milty. duty, 22nd Nov., 1914, to 20th Sept., 1919; supt. of salt and excise, Sept., 1920; asst. collr. of salt rev. (designation changed), Sept., 1926; on depn. to Jannagara State, April, 1928 to Apl., 1931; ret'd., June, 1933.

WILSON, CHARLES WATERHOUSE M.R.C.V.S., late Indian Vety. Service (b. 5th Dec., 1874).—Joined the service, 23rd April, 1906; supt., vety. dept., Aug., 1916; vety. adviser to govt., Central Provs., May, 1921; ret'd., Dec., 1929.

WILSON, DAVID HUTCHISON, A.M.I.C.E., late Indian State Railways (N.W. Riy.) (b. 15th Apl., 1875).—Joined the service as asst. engr., 24th Feb., 1905; exec. engr., March, 1912; ret'd., Mar., 1929.

WILSON, DENIS GREY, Lt.-Col., I.A., ret'd. (b. 6th Jan., 1880).—First commn. dated 12th Aug., 1899; in mil. employ until July, 1906, when he was apptd. asst. commr., N.W. Frontier Prov.; asst. to resdt., Hyderabad, Aug., 1907; asst. pol. agent, N.W. Frontier Prov., Nov. 1907; asst. commr., Oct., 1908; ditto, and commdr., B.M. Police, May, 1909; city mag., Peshawar, Oct., 1912; asst. pol. agent, Dir and Swat, July, 1913; ditto Chitral, Aug., 1913; temply. to mil. duty, Oct., 1914; asst. commr., Peshawar, Oct., 1915; ditto, Thal sub.-divn., Nov., 1915; dep. commr., Hazara, March, 1919; asst. commr., Thal sub.-divn.,

May, 1916; dist. judge, Peshawar, Nov. 1917; first asst. resdt. at Hyderabad, Dec. 1920. pol. agent, Bundelkhand, June, 1923; pol. agent in Bhopal, May, 1928; resdt. Baroda, Nov. 1929; offg. resdt., Gwalior, Apl., 1931; offg. resdt., and A.G.G. in C.I., Apl., 1932; ret'd., Jan., 1935.

WILSON, FRANCIS ARCHIBALD, late Excise and Salt Dept., Bengal (b. 17th May, 1875).—Held non-gazetted appt. prior to 1st April, 1915; supt., April, 1915; offg. dep. commr., April, 1924, July, 1925, and again, April, 1926; ret'd., May, 1930.

WILSON, FREDERICK ERNEST, M.B., Col., I.M.S., ret'd., late Medl. Dept., Cent. Provs. (b. 21st Apl., 1879).—First commn. dated 26th Jan., 1902; in milty. employ to 5th Dec., 1907; offg. civil surg., Miranshah, Dec., 1907; ditto, Peshawar, Oct., 1909; offg. agency surg., Meshed, Dec., 1910; ditto, Sibi, April, 1914; offg. medl. offr., Mewar Bhil Corps, Sept., 1914; dir., medl. dept., Bikaner, March, 1915; in milty. employ from Aug. 1917, to Aug., 1921; resdy, surg. and C.M.O., in Baluchistan, Aug., 1921; civil surg., Quetta, Oct., 1923; resdy, surg. and C.M.O., Baluchistan, Jan., 1928; ditto, W.I. States, Mar., 1930; inspr.-genl., civil hospitals, Cent. Provs., Feb., 1931; ret'd., Nov., 1933.

WILSON, REV. GEORGE ALBERT, M.A., Indian Eccles. Estabt. (junior chaplain) (b. 24th Apl., 1892).—Appointed chaplain on probation, Madras, 29th March, 1926; junr. chaplain, Mar., 1928.

WILSON, GEORGE, late Indian State Rlys. (b. 28th Sept., 1877).—Held non-gazetted appt. from 6th Jan., 1896, to 16th July, 1913, and again from 14th Nov., 1913, to 30th June, 1914; offg. dist. loco. supt., July, 1913; confd., July, 1914; dist. loco. supt., June, 1923; supt., Feb., 1925; ret'd., Mar., 1934.

WILSON, GERALD SIDNEY, C.S.I., late Indian Police (b. 29th Oct., 1880).—Joined the service, 23rd Nov., 1901; supt. of police, Sept., 1911; ditto, and dep. commr. of police for Port of Bombay, June, 1915; awarded King's Police Medal, Jan., 1918; offg. dep. inspr.-gen., Oct., 1921; confd., April, 1929; offg. inspr.-genl., Oct., 1928, and again, May, 1932; offg. commr. of police, Oct., 1930; C.S.I., June, 1931; inspr.-genl. of police, July, 1933; ret'd., Jan., 1934.

WILSON, Rt. Hon. Sir GUY DOUGLAS ARTHUR FLEETWOOD, P.C., G.C.I.E., K.C.B., K.C.M.G.—Clerk in paymaster-gen.'s office, May, 1870; in Egypt as private sec. to Sir C. Rivers-Wilson, Feb. to June, 1876; apptd. to 2nd class on re-organization, and examr. and assessor of Income Tax, May, 1883; transf. to War Office as clerk, higher div., Nov., 1883; senior clerk, April, 1891; resdt. clerk, Dec., 1885, to Aug., 1886; acted as private sec. to various secs. of state to Aug., 1893, when he was apptd. dir. of clothing; asst. under sec. of state, March, 1898, and dir. of army finance, June, 1904; dir.-gen. of army finance, Jan., 1907; sec. to special committee on army re-organization, June, 1887;

commr. of income duty for army services, Oct., 1898; finl. adviser to Lord Kitchener in S. Africa, Jan. to Aug., 1901 (medal); C.B., May, 1891; knighted, June, 1902; K.G.B., June, 1905; member of govr.-gen.'s council, Finl. Dept., 9th Nov., 1908, to June, 1913; discharged functions on behalf of the govr.-genl. after the attempt on the latter's life in Dec., 1912; K.C.M.G., 1909; G.C.I.E., Dec., 1911; P.C., 1914.

WILSON, JAMES LEITCH, M.A., Edin., Punjab Educl. Service (supt. of educ., Baluchistan) (b. 31st Mar., 1892).—Joined the service as offg. inspr. of schools, 27th Mar., 1925; confd., Mar., 1930; vice-princl., Central Training Coll., Lahore, Nov., 1935; supt. of educ., Baluchistan, May, 1937.

WILSON, JOHN ANDREW MUIR, B.Sc., late Indian Finance Dept. (b. 15th Sept., 1889).—Joined the service, 22nd Oct., 1915; asst. acctnt.-gen., Bihar and Orissa, Oct., 1917; examr., local fund acct., ditto, Nov., 1923; divl. audit officer, E.I. Ely., Aug., 1927; dep. acct.-genl., Bombay, Apl., 1929; ditto, Madras, Jan., 1931; ret'd., Apl., 1933.

WILSON, JOHN CYRIL, Indian Police (supt., Bombay) (b. 24th May, 1902).—Joined the service, 30th Nov., 1921; offg. supt., March, 1924; confd., Nov., 1930; asst. to inspr.-genl.

WILSON, JOSEPH ARNOLD, B.A., Indian Forest Services (dep. consvr., Madras) (b. 12th Dec., 1898).—Joined the service as asst. consvr., 11th Dec., 1922; dep. consvr., Nov., 1926.

WILSON, LEONARD, B.E., Indian State Rlys. (agent, G.I.P. Rly.) (b. 12th Mar., 1888).—Joined the service as asst. engr. (temp.), 24th April, 1911; confd., June, 1915; resdt. engr., Sept., 1919; offg. divl. trans. supt., Sept., 1925; confd., Feb., 1928; offg. dep. trans. supt., Feb., 1928; offg. dep. agent, Dec., 1929; offg. ch. engr., Apl., 1930; confd., Mar., 1933; offg. agent, G.I.P. rly., May, 1934; confd., Feb., 1935.

WILSON, Rt. Hon. Sir LESLIE ORME, P.C., G.C.S.I., G.C.I.E., C.M.G., D.S.O., Lt.-Col., R.M.L.I. (ret'd.).—Educ. at St. Michaels, Westgate, and St. Paul's Sch.; 2nd lieut., R.M.L.I., 1895; capt., 1901; A.D.C. to Govt. of New South Wales, 1903 to 1909; C.M.G., 1916; parly. asst. sec. to the War Cabinet, 1918; chairman National Maritime Board, 1919; parly. sec. to Ministry of Shipping, 1919; jt. parly. sec. to Treasury and Chief Unionist Whip, 1921; P.C., 1922; govr. of Bombay from 10th Dec., 1923, to 8th Dec., 1928; G.C.I.E., Oct., 1923; G.C.S.I., March, 1929; served in S. Africa, 1899-1901 (mentd. in despatches, D.S.O.); served through operations in Gallipoli, 1914-15 (mentd. in despatches, C.M.G.), and in France, 1915-16; govr. of Queensland, Mar., 1932.

WILSON, LESLIE TALBOT, Indian Police (asst. inspr.-genl., Burma) (b. 11th Aug., 1899).—Joined the service as asst. dist. supt., Burma, 5th Apl., 1921; offg. dist. supt. and asst. commr. of police, Rangoon, Mar., 1926; confd., Mar., 1932; asst. inspr.-genl., Jan., 1936.

WILSON, NORMAN METHUEN, O.B.E., Col., I.M.S. (inspr.-genl. of civil hosp., Central Provs.) (b. 23rd Feb., 1881).—First commn., 1st Feb., 1905; in mil. employ till June, 1912; plague med. offr., Punjab, Nov., 1912; on mil. service, Aug., 1914, to Sept., 1921; O.B.E., Aug., 1920; civil surgn., Punjab, Sept., 1921; war services, Waziristan operations, 1919-21 (mentioned in despatches); civil surg. and C.M.O., Delhi, Apl., 1932; inspr.-genl. of civil hosp., Central Provs., Jan., 1934; offg. dir. of public health, Central Provs., Mar., 1936.

WILSON, PERCY, A.R.I.B.A., Indian State Ry's. (architect, G.I.P. Ry.) (b. 4th Nov., 1891).—Joined the service as asst. architect, 22nd July, 1921; architect, Sept., 1930.

WILSON, RALPH ALEXANDER, M.A., late Indian C.S.—Educ. at High Sch., Dundee, and Edinburgh Univ.; apptd. after exam. of 1901; arrived, 17th Nov., 1902, and served in the Central Provs. as asst. commr. and regr., judl. commr.'s court; dep. commr., Dec., 1915; offg. chief sec. to govt., Nov., 1925; commr., Dec., 1927; ret'd., July, 1929.

WILSON, Sir ROGER COCHRANE, K.C.B., D.S.O., M.C., Lieut.-General, I.A. (b. 26th Dec., 1882).—First commn., 8th May, 1901; Indian Army, 1904 (114th Mahrattas); Brig. Major, India, Dec., 1914—April, 1915; DAA.G., Feb., 1916—May, 1916; A.A.G., Mesopotamian E.F. and Iraq E.F., Sept., 1917—Nov., 1921; Asst. Director, Auxiliary and Territorial Forces, India, Dec., 1921—Feb., 1922; G.S.O. I, India, Feb., 1922—Feb., 1926; Brig. Commander, India, Feb., 1926—Feb., 1930; C.B., June, 1930; Comdt., Staff College, Quetta, Sept., 1931—Oct., 1934; Commander, Rawalpindi District, Oct., 1934—May, 1936; Milty. Secty., India Office, May, 1936; K.C.B., May, 1937; Adj.-Genl., India, Oct., 1937; Colonel, 5th Royal Battalion, 5th Mahratta Light Infantry; War Services, European War, 1914-18, brevet of Lieut.-Col.; D.S.O., M.C.; six times mentioned in despatches.

WILSON, RONALD PATERSON, late Indian Police (b. 31st Oct., 1897).—Joined the service as asst. supt., 1st Feb., 1920; offg. supt., Nov., 1923; confd., Apl., 1931; ret'd., Apl., 1935.

WILTON DAVIES, PERCY WILLIAM, V.D., Indian State Railways (exec. engr., N.W. rly.) (b. 14th Jan., 1890).—Joined the service, 3rd Dec., 1912; asst. engr., Dec., 1913; exec. engr., Mar., 1931.

WIMBERLEY, Capt. E. A., R.E. (deputy mint master).—Under milty dept. up to 14th Apl., 1937; deputy mint master (on probn.), Apl., 1937.

WIMBUSH, ANTHONY, late Indian Forest Service (b. 19th May, 1886).—Joined the service, 30th Oct., 1907, as asst. consvr.; dep. consvr., Nov., 1912; acting consvr., Aug., 1922; confd., July, 1925; offg. ch. consvr., Aug., 1928; confd., Nov., 1932; ret'd., June, 1937.

WINDLE, OLIVER ETHELBERT, O.B.E., Indian Police (dep. inspr. genl., Madras) (b. 20th July, 1884).—Joined the service as asst. supt., 17th Dec., 1905; supt., April, 1915; awarded King's police medal, Jan., 1920; O.B.E., Jan., 1931; offg. dep. inspr.-genl., Aug.,

1930; confd., Oct., 1930; commr. of police, Madras, Apl., 1933; dep. inspr.-genl., Apl., 1937.

WINGATE, RONALD EVELYN LESLIE, C.I.E., B.A., Indian C.S., Indian Political Service (b. 30th Sept., 1889).—Educ. at Bradfield, and Balliol Coll., Oxford; apptd. after exam. of 1912; arrived, 8th Dec., 1913, and served in the Punjab as asst. commr., transf. to Delhi as city mag., April to Sept., 1916; spl. duty on staff of lieut.-govr., Punjab, from April, 1917; spl. duty under civil commr. of occupied territories, Mesopotamia, from Oct., 1917; pol. agent and H.M.'s consul at Maskat, Oct., 1919; special asst. to resdt. in Kashmir, Nov., 1921; pol. agent and H.M.'s Consul, Maskat, March, 1923; sec. to agent to govr.-gen. in Rajputana, Sept., 1924; ditto, Baluchistan, March, 1927; pol. agent and dep. commr., Quetta-Pishin, March, 1928; C.I.E., Jan., 1931; pol. agent. Sibi, Oct., 1931; dep. secty. to the govt. of India, foreign and pol. dept., Mar., 1932; offg. secty., ditto, Oct., 1932, and May, 1934; jt. secty., ditto, June, 1933; temp. addl. ditto, Mar., 1935; rev. and judl. commr., Baluchistan, Dec., 1935; offg. A.G.G., and ch. commr., Baluchistan, Nov., 1936; rev. and judl. commr., ditto, Dec., 1936; offg. A.G.G., Resdt. C.C., ditto, June, 1937.

WINGFIELD, DESMOND ERNEST, Indian Police (asst. supt.) (b. 17th Nov., 1909).—Joined the service as proby. asst. supt., United Provs., 26th Nov., 1930; confd., Nov., 1932; offg. supt., Nov., 1935.

WINTERTON, EARL, RIGHT HON. EDWARD TURNOUR, P.C., M.P., J.P., D.L.—Parliamentary under-sec. of state for India, Mar., 1922, to Jan., 1924, and Nov., 1924, to June, 1929; delegate to Burma round table conf., London, Dec., 1931; delegate to Indian round table conf., Ldn., Nov.-Dec., 1932.

WISE, JOHN HUMPHREY, Indian C.S. (b. 11th Mar., 1890) (dep. commr. Burma).—Educ. at Christ's Hosp., and Oxford (Univ.); apptd. after exam. of 1913; joined the service, 19th Oct., 1914, and served in Burma as asst. commr.; on mil. duty from April, 1915, to July, 1919; under-sec. to govt., Feb., 1921, to Aug., 1923; offg. sec. to finl. commr., Aug., 1923; services placed at disposal of govt. of India, health dept., from Nov., 1923, to Feb., 1924; offg. dep. commr., Dec., 1924; secy., public service commn., Oct., 1926; dep. commr., Feb., 1930; secty. rev. dept., Burma, Nov., 1932, and again, Apl., 1934; secty., commerce and indus. dep., Mar., 1937.

WITHERS, FREDERICK WALTER, late Indian Forest Service (b. 16th June, 1896).—Joined the service as asst. consvr., 12th Dec., 1922; dep. consvr., Aug., 1923; ret'd., Jan., 1933.

WITHINSHAW, LESLIE, late Indian Police (b. 27th Feb., 1886).—Joined the service, 3rd Dec., 1906; asst. supt., July, 1907; supt., Oct., 1915; awarded King's police medal, 1916; on milty. duty from 12th Aug., 1918, to 28th Feb., 1919; dep. commr., March, 1919; on duty in central intell. office, Simla, Jan.-July, 1920; offg. dep. dir. Central intelligence, Madras, Nov., 1921; asst. inspr.-gen., Jan., 1923; services placed at disposal of govt. of India, rly. bd., for appt. as supt., watch and ward dept., E.I. Rly., from 4th April, 1924; ret'd., Aug., 1935.

WITT, DAVID OTTO, late Indian Forest Service (Central Provs.) (b. 29th Sept., 1874).—Joined the service, 1st Dec., 1896, as asst. consr. of forests; dep. consr., Dec., 1903; consr. (prov.), Sept., 1920; confd., Dec., 1920; chief consvr., Oct., 1926; ret'd., Apl., 1929.

WOLFENDEN, RALPH, M.B.E., M.Sc., M.I.M.E., M.I.E. (Ind.), Barr., Indian Educl. Service (princl., Bengal Engrg. Coll.) (b. 18th Feb., 1884).—Joined the service as prof., civil engrg. coll., 16th Feb., 1921; M.B.E., Jan., 1918; asst. dir. of public instrn., Bengal, Nov., 1928; inspr. of European sch., Dec. 1934; princpl., Bengal Engrg. Coll., Nov., 1936.

WOOD, ANTHONY ST. JOHN, Indian Police, (asst. supt.) (b. 19th Jan., 1915).—Apptd. asst. supt., Punjab, 24th Nov., 1934; confd., Nov., 1936.

WOOD, ARTHUR EDWARD FRANCIS, Indian Police (supt., Bengal) (b. 18th June, 1889).—Joined the service as asst. supt., 1st Dec., 1910; supt., Nov., 1925.

WOOD, ARTHUR WESTMORELAND, Barr., late Indian Finance Dept. (b. 1st Aug., 1883).—Joined the service as asst. examr., Oct., 1908; on naval service from 11th Aug., 1914, to 22nd April, 1919; field examr. of mil. works accts., Aug., 1919; dep. finl. adviser, May, 1920; dir. of army audit., Western Command, May, 1925; offg. ditto, N.C., March, 1926; ditto, E.C., May, 1927; with finl. commr., rlys., Apl., 1929; on foreign service under Jodhpur State from Feb., 1930; ch. auditor, E.B. rly. and Govt. exr. of accts., Assam Bengal rly., Oct., 1933; ret'd., Mar., 1935.

WOOD, BERNARD READER, Indian Forest Service (b. 20th Feb., 1890).—Joined the service as asst. consvr., 30th Nov., 1914; dep. consvr., Oct., 1924; transferred to for. service as consvr. of forests in British Guiana, from 10th Oct., 1925.

WOOD, CHRISTOPHER FRANCIS, India C.S. (mag. and collr., United Provs.) (b. 26th Aug., 1884).—Educ. at Grocers' Company's Sch., London, and Christ's Coll., Cambridge; appointed after exam. of 1908; arrived, 3rd Dec., 1909, and served in the United Provs. as asst. mag. and asst. commr.; on mil. duty, I.A.R.O., Sept., 1916, to Feb., 1919; jt. mag., March, 1918; offg. mag. and collr., Dec., 1920; jt. sec. to board of rev., Feb., 1921; sec., ditto, April, 1923; offg. mag. and collr., Nov., 1924; confd., July, 1925; offg. commr., Dec., 1925.

WOOD, ERIC RAWLINSON, M.C., B.A., Indian C.S. (secty., govt. of Orissa educ. dept.) (b. 24th Mar., 1893).—Educ. at Denstone Coll., and St. Catherine's Coll., Cambridge; joined the service, 8th Oct., 1919; arrived, 1st Dec., 1919, and served in Madras as asst. collr. and mag.; sub-collr. and jt. mag., Jan., 1922; offg. collr. and dist. mag., Nov., 1931, and again, Jan., 1933; ditto (prov. subs.), Mar., 1935; collr., Mar., 1936; served temp. with govt. of Orissa from Apl., 1936; secty. to govt. of Orissa, Educ. Dept., Mar., 1937.

WOOD, ERNEST CLEMENT, Indian C.S. (collr., Madras) (b. 12th Sept., 1890).—Educ. at Cheltenham and New Coll., Oxford; apptd. after exam. of 1914; arrived, 10th Dec., 1915, and served in Madras as asst. collr. and mag.; on mil. duty from Oct., 1916, to May, 1919; asst. to resdt., Travancore and Cochin, June, 1919; sub-collr., March, 1922; offg. commr. of Coorg, May, 1926; offg. dep. sec. to govt., fin. dept., Sept., 1926; confd., March, 1927; offg. collr. and dist. mag., Oct., 1930; collr. (prov. subs.), May, 1934; confd., Jan., 1935.

WOOD, GEORGE HERBERT ANDERSON, Indian Finance Dept. (b. 31st Aug., 1894).—Joined the service, 14th June, 1919; asst. acctnt.-gen., Nov., 1920; currency officer, Madras, Oct., 1922; ditto, Lahore, May, 1925; ditto, Calcutta, Oct., 1926; dep. ch. auditor, E.B. Rly., June, 1932; services placed at disposal of finl. commr. of rlys., Feb., 1933; offg. dep. dir., rly. clearing accts., June, 1935; dep. ch. accts. officer, E.B. rly., May, 1936; on for. service under Jodhpur State, Jan., 1937.

WOOD, JOHN LUTHER, Indian C.S. (sub. collr., Madras) (b. 25th Sept., 1908).—Educ. at Burnley Grammar School and Exeter College, Oxford; apptd. after exam. of 1930; arrived in India, Nov., 1931 and served in Madras as asst. mag. and collr.; sub-collr., Mar., 1934.

WOOD, LESLIE DERING, Indian Police (b. 5th Jan., 1909).—Joined the service as asst. supt., United Provs., 24th Nov., 1928; offg. supt., April, 1935.

WOOD, PERCY, Indian Railway Accts. Service (dep. chief accts. offcr., G.I.P. Rly.) (b. 17th Apl., 1890).—Joined the service as asst. auditor and acctnt., G.I.P. rly., 7th Nov., 1913; confd. as asst. auditor, G.I.P. rly., Dec., 1920; on dept. to Agency Dept. as supt. of statistics, G.I.P. rly., Feb., 1924, to Mar., 1929; served under finl. commr., rlys., Oct., 1929, and again Dec., 1935; dep. chief accts. officer, G.I.P. rly., Oct., 1929; served under agent, G.I.P. rly., July, 1932; confd. as deputy agent, Jan., 1933; dep. chief accts. officer., Nov., 1936.

WOOD, WILLIAM GEORGE, Office of the High Commr. for India (exec. officer, store dept.) (b. 27th July, 1897).—Apptd. clerical officer, 1st Apl., 1927, after previous service from Apl., 1921; exec. officer, 1st Nov., 1932.

WOOD, WILLIAM OUTERSON, O.B.E., Indian Police (b. 11th Dec., 1903).—Joined the service as asst. supt., N.W.F.P., 20th Nov., 1923; awarded King's police medal, Mar., 1929; offg. supt., Jan., 1926; O.B.E., May, 1936.

WOODHEAD, SIR JOHN ACKROYD, K.C.S.I., C.I.E., B.A., Indian C.S. (member, exec. council govr. of Bengal) (b. 19th June, 1881).—Educ. at Bradford Gr. Sch., and Clare Coll., Cambridge; apptd. after exam. of 1903; arrived, 12th Dec., 1904, and served in Bengal as asst. mag. and collr.; transf. to E. Bengal and Assam, Oct., 1905; jt. mag. and dep. collr., Feb., 1911; re-transf. to Bengal, April, 1912; jt. mag. and collr., Jan., 1913; dist. and sess. judge, s.p.t., Jan., 1917; confd., March, 1917; 1st land acquisition offr., June, 1918; sec. to govt. of Bengal, fin., commerce and marine depts., Nov., 1924; jt.

sec., dept. of commerce, govt. of India, Nov., 1927; sec., ditto, Mar., 1929; C.I.E., Jan., 1931; temp. member, govr.-genl.'s council, May, 1931; delegate to Burma round table conf., London, Dec., 1931; member, exec. council govr. of Bengal, Aprl., 1932; actg. govr. of Bengal, Aug., 1934 to Dec., 1934; K.C.S.I., Aug., 1934.

WOODHOUSE, ALBERT CYRIL, C.I.E., B.A., Madras Civil Service (sub. collr.) (b. 5th Nov., 1887).—Joined the service as dep. collr., 30th Nov., 1912; sub collr. (provl. perm.), Feb., 1927; C.I.E., Feb., 1937.

WOODHOUSE, ARTHUR ALDERSON, late Indian Police Service (United Provs.) (b. 23rd Oct., 1874).—Joined the service, 16th April, 1898, and posted to United Provs.; asst. supt. of police, April, 1906; supt., June, 1912; ret'd., Jan., 1930; author of 'New Revolver Manual of Police and Infantry Forces.'

WOODHOUSE, HAROLD LISTER, M.C., Col., R.E., Indian State Rlys. (chief engr.) (b. 30th Jan., 1887).—First commn. dated 25th July, 1906; asst. engr., 23rd March, 1911; on mil. service from 20th Sept., 1914, to Oct., 1922; exec. engr., Jan., 1917; divl. supt., Sept., 1928; offg. dep. ch. engr., Oct., 1933; confd., May, 1935; offg. dir., rly. bd., July, 1934; confd., June, 1935; chief engr., Nov., 1937.

WOODHOUSE, SIDNEY, Survey of India supt., mathl. instrument office) (b. 9th Nov., 1882).—Joined the service, 9th June, 1905 and held non-gazetted appts. to 25th Oct., 1919; officer in charge mathematical instrument office, Feb., 1923; supt., ditto, Mar., 1926.

WOODS, DOUGLAS LESLIE, Indian Police (b. 12th Oct., 1909).—Joined the service as proby. asst. supt., Punjab, 22nd Nov., 1929; confd., Feb., 1932; transf'd. to N.W.F.P., Jan., 1933; asst. dist. officr., frontier constab., Mar., 1937.

WOODS, GEORGE WILLIAM MURRAY, O.B.E., B.Sc., late Indian Forest Service (b. 3rd June, 1899).—Joined the service as asst. consvr., 13th Dec., 1923; dep. consvr., Burma, Aprl., 1928; O.B.E., June, 1932; ret'd., Sept., 1935.

WOODS, PHILLIP DION, late Indian State Rlys. (N.W. Rly.) (b. 19th Nov., 1886).—Joined the service as asst. storekeeper, 21st Oct. 1908; services placed at disposal of Indian Munitions Board, 6th Oct., 1917; dist. contr. of stores, Aug., 1919; ret'd., Nov., 1931.

WOODS-BALLARD, BASIL, M.B.E., Capt., I.A., Indian Political Service (b. 28th Sept., 1900).—First commn. dated 29th Jan., 1920; in milty. employ prior to 1st Jan., 1925; probnr. in pol. dept., May, 1925; asst. commr., D.I. Khan, Dec., 1926; city magte., Peshawar, Jan., 1928; supy. asst. commr., Hazara, June, 1929; asst. pol. agent, Chitral, July, 1919; under-secty. to A.G.G. in Rajputana, Aprl., 1931; asst. pol. agent, Quetta, Pishin, Oct., 1934; M.B.E., Nov., 1935; pol. agent, Loralai, Feb., 1936.

WOODTHORPE, Mrs. MARIA VAUGHAN, M.A., late Indian Educl. Service (b. 19th Aprl., 1897).—Joined the service as inspectress of girl's schools, 20th Nov., 1915; ret'd., Aprl., 1934.

WOODWARD, FRANK JAMES, B.A., late Indian C.S. (b. 11th Sept., 1884).—Educ. at Dulwich Coll., and Caius Coll., Cambridge; apptd. after exam. of 1908; arrived, 27th Nov., 1909, and served in the Central Provs. as asst. commr.; addl. dist. and sess. judge, April, 1914; sub-judge (provl.), June, 1919; confd., Dec., 1919; dist. and sess. judge, Sept., 1923; ret'd., June, 1934.

WOODYARD, CHRISTOPHER RICHARD, India Office (exec. officer, acct.-genl.'s dept.) (b. 10th Aprl., 1901).—Apptd. clerical officer, 1st Jan., 1920, after previous service from Oct., 1918; actg. exec. officer, Sept., 1935; confd., Feb., 1936.

WOOLDRIDGE, LOVELL WILLIAM, Capt., I.A., Indian Political Service (b. 9th Dec., 1901).—First commn., 23rd Dec., 1921; in milty. employ to May, 1927; asst. pol. agent at Chilas, Nov., 1930; under secty. to resdt., Hyderabad, Dec., 1934; asst. commr., Tank, Feb., 1937; dep. commr., Dera Ismail, Khan, Nov., 1937.

WOOLLER, ARTHUR, B.A., Indian C.S. (jl. mag. and sub div. officr., Bengal) (b. 23rd May, 1912).—Educ. at Bradford Grammar Sch., and Corpus Christi College, Oxford; apptd. after exam. of 1934; arrived in India, Dec., 1935 and served in Bengal as asst. mag. and collr.; jl. mag. and sub divl. officr., Aprl., 1937.
