WORGAN, TREVOR HARTLAND, late Indian Finance Dept.—Joined the fin. dept., 19th Oct., 1901; asst. acct.-gen., United Provs., Nov., 1903; asst. comptr.-gen., forests, Jan., 1904; asst. acct.-gen., Bengal, April, 1905; asst. comptr.-gen. in charge paper currency, Calcutta, Nov., 1905; asst. acct.-gen., United Provs., Nov., 1907; ditto, Bombay, Dec., 1908; examr., local fund accts., Bombay, May, 1912; examr., outside audits, Calcutta, Sept., 1916; offg. comptr., Assam, May, 1920; dep. acctnt.-gen., Bombay, Nov., 1920; ditto, Madras, Oct., 1922; dep. auditor-gen., India inspection (civil), Aprl., 1924; on spec. duty, Feb., 1926; ret'd., Aug., 1928.

WORMALD, Rev. ROBERT LEONARD, M.B.E., M.A., late Indian Eccles. Estab. (b. 17th April, 1882).—Apptd. chaplain on probation, 30th Oct., 1914; junr. chaplain Oct. 1917; senr. chaplain, Oct., 1927; ret'd., Sept., 1935.

WORT, ALFRED WILLIAM EWART, Barr., Judl. dept., Bihar (b. 17th Mar., 1883).—Apptd. judge, high ct., Patna, Feb., 1927, offg. ch. justice, July, 1933; and again, Aprl., 1936.

WORTH, MEREDITH, B.A. (Cantab.), Indian C.S., Indian Political Service (b. Dec., 1905).—Educ. at Repton, Grenoble, and Corpus Christi Coll., Cambridge; joined the service, 5th Oct., 1928; arrived, Nov., 1928, and served in Bengal as asst. mag. and collr.; Brit. trade agent, Gyantse, and asst. to pol. officer in Sikkim, Aprl., 1933; offg. Brit. trade agent, Yatung, Aprl., 1933; asst. pol. agent, Bahrain, (temp.), May, 1934; asst. pol. agent, Sibol, Oct., 1935; offg. pol. agent in addn., Mar., 1936; senior sub judge, Hazara, Dec., 1936; asst. commr., Hazara, Jan., 1937; ditto, in addn., Manshera, Aprl., 1937; Counsellor, Brit. Legation, Kabul, July, 1937; asst. commr., Hazara, Dec., 1937.

WORTHY, GEORGE TOYNBEE, India Office (exec. officer, military dept.) (b. 9th Apl., 1882).—Educ. at Royal Gr. Sch., Newcastle-on-Tyne; apptd. 2nd div. clerk, War Office, 14th March, 1901; apptd. 2nd class clerk, acctnt.-genl.'s dept., 13th Nov., 1905; transfld. to registry and record dept., April, 1906, and to military dept., Jan., 1917; on mil. service, July, 1918, to Feb., 1919; exec. officer, April, 1922; actg. higher clerical officer, April, 1922, to Sept., 1923.

WRENCH, JOHN CECIL, B.A., Indian Forest Service (dep. consvr., Madras) (b. 13th Dec., 1895).—Joined the service as asst. consvr., Madras, 31st Jan., 1922; dep. consvr., Jan., 1925; offg. princpl., Madras forest coll., Jan., 1930, to Apl., 1934.

WRENCH, JOHN MERVYN DALLAS, C.I.E., Indian State Railways (ch. contr. of standardisation, Rly. Bd.) (b. 15th Sept., 1883).—Joined the service as asst. loco. supt., 13th Aug., 1906; dist. loco. supt., April, 1913; supt. of state workshops, Gwalior, Dec., 1914, to April, 1915; spl. duty with controller of stores, mil. rys., from Nov., 1916 (mentd. in despatch); chief mechanical engr., G.I.P. rly.; offg. dir. mechl. engrg., rly. dept., March, 1925; on spl. duty, Rly. Board, Oct., 1926; ch. mech. engr., G.I.P. Rly., June, 1928; C.I.E., March, 1929; on spl. duty with Rly. Bd., Feb., 1929; offg. ch. contr. of standards, Rly. Bd., March, 1930; dir., mechl. engrg. in addn., Nov. 1932; ch. contr. of standardisation, Apl., 1935.

WRENICK, BERNARD CHARLES, late India Posts and Tels. (Madras) (b. 24th Nov., 1875).—Trained at R.I.E. Coll.; joined the service as asst. supt., 1st Oct., 1897; supt., April, 1910; div. engr., tels., Oct., 1920; dir., S.C., Nov., 1920; dep. dir.-genl., Nov., 1926; dir., telegraph engrg., Eastern circle, Nov., 1927; ditto, Northern circle, Dec., 1928; P.M.G., Madras, Jan., 1930; retd., Aug., 1930.

WRIGHT, ALBERT ERNEST, B.A. (Oxon.), Imperial Customs Service (asst. collr.) (b. 30th June, 1902).—Joined the service, Nov., 1926; asst. collr. of customs, Nov., 1926.

WRIGHT, FRANCIS GERALD GRIBBLE, Indian Police (dist. supt., Central Prov.) (b. 2nd June, 1902).—Joined the service as asst. dist. supt., 4th Oct., 1921; offg. dist. supt., March, 1925; confd., Jan., 1929.

WRIGHT, REV. GEORGE AUGUSTUS ARTHUR, M.A., late Indian Eccl. Estabt. (Madras).—Joined the service, 1st Nov., 1904; junr. chaplain, Nov., 1907; senr. chaplain, Nov., 1917; retd., Sept., 1928.

WRIGHT, MISS GERTRUDE MARIAN, late Indian Educl. Service.—Apptd. princpl., Bethune coll., Calcutta, 5th Feb., 1919; retd., Feb., 1929.

WRIGHT, HERBERT LAWRENCE, B.A., Indian Forest Service (consvr., Punjab).—(b. 24th Jan., 1887) Joined the service, 24th Dec., 1909; served in the Punjab as asst. consvr.; dep. consvr., Dec., 1914; on deputation to Kashmir as consvr. of forests from Jan., 1923; chief consvr., Kashmir, Aug., 1924; consvr., N.W.F.P., Apl., 1932; apptd. offcr. on special duty in Gilgit Agency, Aug. to Oct., 1937.

WRIGHT, JOHN MONCRIEFF, C.B.E., B.A., late Indian C.S. (Burma) (b. 31st July, 1884).—Educ. at Dulwich Coll., and Jesus Coll., Camb.; apptd. after exam. of 1907; arrived, 23rd Nov., 1908, and served in Burma as asst. commr.; supt., Chin Hills, Aug., 1913; C.B.E., July, 1920; dep. commr., June, 1922; offg. chief commr., Andaman and Nicobar Islands, June, 1924, and again, Nov., 1927; spl. duty, Rangoon, as dep. commr., Oct., 1924; ch. commr., Andaman and Nicobar Islands, Nov., 1927; retd., Mar., 1931.

WRIGHT, PHILIP VICTOR, Indian Police (asst. supt., Bengal) (b. 23rd Sept., 1904).—Joined the service as asst. supt., Bengal, on probn., 16th Dec., 1934; confd., Jan., 1937.

WRIGHT, RICHARD GANE, B.A., late Indian Educl. Service (Punjab) (b. 1st Jan., 1877).—Apptd. asst. princpl., Aitchison Coll., Lahore, 15th Sept., 1915; offg. princpl., ditto, April, 1926; retd., Jan., 1932.

WRIGHT, ROBERT ERNEST, C.I.E., M.D., Lt.-Col., I.M.S., retd. (b. 2nd Oct., 1884).—First commn. dated 27th July, 1907; in mil. employ to Nov., 1911; offg. asst. dir., Pasteur institute, Nov., 1911; offg. as prof., medl. coll., Madras, Apl., 1914; reverted to mil. duty from 20th Aug., 1914, to 9th Feb., 1920; offg. prof., medl. coll., Madras, May, 1920; confd., Jan., 1922; C.I.E., Mar., 1929; offg. surg.-genl. with Govt. of Madras, Oct., 1933. retd.

WRIGHT, ROBERT MARTIN, Indian Police (dep. inspr.-genl., Bengal) (b. 8th May, 1889).—Joined the service as asst. supt., 27th Nov., 1908; supt., March, 1917; awarded King's police medal, 1918; princpl. police training coll., Bengal, Feb., 1928; offg. dep. inspr.-genl., June, 1929; confd., Feb., 1932.

WRIGHT, ROBERT WALLACE, M.B.E., India Office (asst. supt. of records) (b. 20th Sept., 1892).—Apptd. second class clerk, India Office, 2nd July, 1912, and attd. to acctnt.-genl.'s dept.; transfld. to registry and record dept., July, 1912, and to store dept., Jan., 1917; on mil. service from Nov., 1915; retransferred to registry and record dept., June, 1919; transfld. to comm. and rev. dept., Feb., 1922; higher clerical officer, April, 1922; resident clerk, March, 1923; parly. clerk, April, 1926; asst. sec. Indian delegation to 7th and 8th assemblies of League of Nations, 1926 and 1927; M.B.E., June, 1932; asst. supt. of records, Nov., 1937.

WRIGHT, SYDNEY WILLIAM WALTER, Indian Police (b. 29th Sept., 1903).—Joined the service as asst. supt., Madras, 20th Nov., 1923; offg. dist. supt., Sept., 1931; awarded Indian police medal, Jan., 1933.

WRIGHT, WALTER HENRY, Indian Police (supt., Madras) (b. 13th Sept., 1891).—Joined 22nd Nov., 1911; asst. supt., Nov., 1914; offg. supt., July, 1919, May, 1921, and Oct., 1923; awarded King's police medal, Jan., 1918; supt. (provl. perm.), Jan., 1927; confd., Dec. 1928.

WRIGHT, WILFRID THOMAS MERMOND, C.I.E., M.A., late Indian C.S. (United Provs.) (b. 10th Dec., 1882).—Educ. at Dulwich Coll., and Wadham Coll., Oxford; apptd. after exam. of 1905, arrived, 28th Nov., 1906, and served in United Provs. as asst. mag. and collr.; asst. acctnt.-genl., United Provs., July, 1911; dep. acctnt.-genl., ditto, March, 1912; jt. mag., May, 1915; dep. sec. to govt. of India, legisl. dept., Nov., 1918; jt. sec., ditto, May, 1920; dep. sec., ditto, July, 1920; temp. joint sec., ditto, Jan., 1921; confd., Nov., 1921; C.I.E., June, 1924; ret'd., Jan., 1932.

WRIGHT, WILLIAM ALAN, A.F.C., Indian C.S. (dist. and sess. judge, Burma) (b. 27th Nov., 1895).—Educ. at Oundle, and Caius Coll., Cambridge; joined the service, 28th Sept., 1921; arrived, 30th Nov., 1921, and served in Burma as asst. commr.; offg. dist. and sess. judge, Dec., 1924; confd., Mar., 1929.

WRIGHT, WILLIAM DUNDAS, M.B., Lt.-Col., I.M.S. (ret'd.) (b. 9th Nov., 1880).—First commn. dated 1st Sept., 1905; in mil. employ to 23rd Dec., 1907, and again from 8th May to 14th Sept., 1908; on plague duty, Sept., 1908; civil surg., United Provs., March, 1921; ret'd., Dec., 1931.

WRIGHT, WILLIAM MCKENDREE, Indian Police (asst. supt.) (b. 17th June, 1912).—Joined the service as prob. asst. supt., United Provinces, Nov., 1932; confd., Nov., 1934.

WRINCH, ROBERT CORNICK, India Posts and Tels. (dir. tels.) (b. 26th Nov., 1890).—Joined the service, 1st Oct., 1919; asst. divl. engr., Mar., 1921; divl. engr., tels., Aug., 1928; dir., posts and tels., Sind and Baluchistan, Mar., 1934; offg. dir., tels., Apl., 1936; confd., Dec., 1936.

WUNNAM, ESWARA VENKATA SITAPATI RAO, LL.B. (London Univ.), Office of High Commr. for India (higher clerical officer, accts. dept.) (b. 20th June, 1893).—Apptd. to India Office, acctnt.-gen.'s dept., 19th April, 1920; transferred to office of High Commr. for India, 1st Oct., 1920; clerical officer, Oct., 1921; exec. officer, 1st April, 1923; higher clerical officer (actg.), April, 1927; confd., June, 1927.

WYLIE, FRANCIS VERNER, C.I.E., B.A., Indian C.S., Indian Political Service (gouv., Central Provs. and Berar) (b. 9th Aug., 1891).—Educ. at Royal Sch., Dungannon, and Trinity Coll., Dublin; apptd. after exam. of 1914; arrived, 4th Dec., 1915, and served in the Punjab as asst. commr.; on mil. duty from July, 1916, to March, 1919; temp. offg. as asst. commr., Charsadda, from May, 1919; pol. agent, Tochi, May, 1921; settl. officer, Peshawar, Sept., 1923; C.I.E., June, 1929; offg. dep. sec. for and pol. dept., Dec., 1929 to June, 1930; offg. rev. commr., N.W.F.P., Aug., 1930; dep. secty. to govt. of India, for and pol. dept., Oct., 1930; prime minister, Alwar, Mar., 1933; dep. agent to Govr.-Genl. in Baluchistan, Dec., 1935; jt. secty. to the govt. of India, for and pol. dept., Apl., 1936; resdt., Jaipur, Aug., 1936; on spl. duty in for. and pol. dept., Sept., 1936; resdt., Jaipur, Feb. to Dec., 1937; govr., Central Provs., and Bérar, May, 1938.

WYNNE, MARTIN GOYMOUR, Indian Police (asst. supt.) (b. 26th May, 1910).—Joined the service as prob. asst. supt., Central Provs., 29th Nov., 1929; confd., Oct., 1931.

WYNNE, Sir TREVREDYN RASHLEIGH, K.C.S.I., K.C.I.E., V.D., M.I.C.E., Col., late India Public Works Dept.—Trained at R.I.E. Coll.; apptd. asst. engr., 18th Nov., 1874; transf. to Bombay, 1877 to 1882; exec. engr., May, 1883; resigned, April, 1887, and became agent and chief engr., Bengal Nagpur rly. co.; C.I.E., Jan., 1903; apptd. member of rly. board, Calcutta, Feb., 1905; presdt., May, 1908; K.C.I.E., 1st Jan., 1909; K.C.S.I., Dec., 1911; ret'd., May, 1914; apptd. govt. dir., Indian rly. cos., India Office, May, 1914; ret'd. from India Office, May, 1915.

YA (A), U TUN, late Burma Commission (b. 27th Oct., 1877).—Previous service from April, 1897; dep. commr., May, 1924; secty. to govt., judl. dept., Mar., 1930; ret'd., Mar., 1934.

YADAVA MOHAN, Indian Service of Engrs. (asst. exec. engr., United Provs.) (b. 10th Jan., 1906).—Joined the service as engr. student, 10th Oct., 1928; asst. exec. engr., Oct., 1929.

YAKUB SHAH, Indian Audit Dept. (b. 2nd Apl., 1897).—Joined the service, 23rd Feb., 1920; asst. acct.-genl., Burma, Jan., 1929; asst. contr., rly. accts., Mar., 1930; offg. dep. ch. accts. officer, N.W. rly., Sept., 1932; dep. acct.-genl., Nov., 1933; exr., local fund accts., Punjab, May, 1935; offg. asst. secty., finance dept., Govt. of India, Nov., 1935; dep. dir., rly. bd. (temp.), May, 1936; offg. dep. dir., finance, rly. bd., Oct., 1936; confd., Apl., 1937.

YARNOLD, FREDERICK HERBERT, B.A., Indian C.S. (under-secty., home dept., Burma) (b. 18th Nov., 1911).—Educ. at the King's Sch., Worcester, and University Coll., Oxford; apptd. after exam. of 1934; arrived in India, Dec., 1935, and served in Burma as asst. commr.; under-secty., home dept., Nov., 1937.

YATE, Sir CHARLES EDWARD, Bart., C.S.I., C.M.G., D.L., Col., Indian Army (ret'd.), late Foreign Dept., Govt. of India.—First commn., 9th Nov., 1867; arrived, 27th Feb., 1868; from Aug., 1871, served under the foreign dept.; on special duty, Sind border, 1872 and 1873; boundary settl. officer, Mewar-Marwar border, Sept., 1874; and Mewar-Tonk border, Dec., 1875; offd. for various periods as pol. asst., asst. to supt. for suppression of thagi and dakaiti, &c.; supt. of operations for control of Moghias, Sept., 1879; served in Afghanistan, 1880-81 (medal and clasp, and bronze star); subsequently served in the pol. dept. in Bhopal, Central India, and Bhopawar; attaché with Afghan boundary commn., July, 1884; on duty at Herat in connection with the fortifications, 1885; employed on various special services in connection with the Afghan boundary commn.; pol. agent, Thal Chotiali, March, 1890; offg. rev. commr., Baluchistan, Oct., 1892; pol. agent, Zhorb, March, 1893; on special duty on Russo-Afghan frontier, April, 1893; acting govr.

gen.'s agent and consul-gen., Khorassan and Seistan, Sept., 1893; confid. 1897; rest., Mewar, June, 1898; offg. rest., and agent to govr.-gen., Rajputana, Aug. to Oct., 1898; offg. resdt., Western States, Rajputana, April, 1899; agent to gov.-gen. and ch. commr. in Baluchistan, Dec., 1900; ret'd., Oct., 1904; C.S.I., Feb., 1887; C.M.G., June, 1888; M.P. for Melton Dn., Leicestershire, from Dec., 1910, to Oct., 1924; Baronet, Jan., 1921; knight of justice of St. John of Jerusalem; member of Afghan Order of Hurmat; author of gazetteers of Mewar, Banswara and Partabgarh, 'Northern Afghanistan,' 'Khurasan and Seistan,' &c.

YATES, CLEMENT MERVYN SCOTT, Indian Police (b. 7th June, 1904).—Joined the service as asst. supt., Bombay, 7th Dec., 1924; offg. supt., June, 1932, and again Feb., 1933; A.D.C. to govr., Bombay, Feb., 1936.

YATES, JAMES ALFRED, M.A., late Indian Educl. Service (b. 17th Apr., 1874).—Joined the service, 20th March, 1906, and apptd. inspr. of schools, Madras; principal, govt. coll., Kumbakonam, Aug., 1912, to Oct., 1916; ret'd., Apr., 1929.

YEAMAN, WILLIAM GEORGE, B.Sc., late Indian Service of Engrs. (b. 9th Mar., 1883).—Joined the service as asst. engr., 1st Oct., 1906; exec. engr., Oct., 1914; offg. suptg. engr., Oct., 1925, June, 1926, and again, Feb., 1932; ret'd., May, 1934.

YEATTS, WILLIAM WALTER MURRAY, C.I.E., M.A., Indian C.S. (dep. secty. to the Govt. of India, dept. of educn., health and lands) (b. 30th Sept., 1894).—Educ. at Edinburgh Univ.; joined the service, 25th Oct., 1920; arrived, 28th Dec., 1920, and served in Madras as asst. coll. and mag.; sub-collr. and jt. mag., July, 1922; under-sec. to govt., chief sectt., April, 1926; census supt., Madras, Apr., 1930; dep. secty., reforms office, June, 1932; addl. dep. secty., to Govt. of India, dept. of educn., health and lands, Apr., 1935; offg. dep. secty. ditto, June, 1935; confid., Oct., 1935; offg. jt. secty., ditto, variously from Sept., 1935; and again, Apr., 1937; C.I.E., Jan., 1938.

YELD, PERCIVAL HARLEY, Indian State Rlys. (dist. traffic supt., E.B. Rly.) (b. 14th Sept., 1884).—Joined the service as asst. traffic supt., 29th Oct., 1909; dist. ditto, April, 1918; on milty. duty from Sept., 1915, to Feb., 1919; offg. dep. traffic manager, May, 1928; ditto (comm'l.), E.B. rly., Oct., 1932; offg. traffic manager, Mar., 1933; offg. dep. ch. manager, transptn., E.B. rly., Sept., 1935.

YENNEMADI, DATTATRAYA VENKATRAO, B.A., LL.B., Bombay Civil Service, Judicial Branch (dist. and sess. judge) (b. 23rd Ap., 1883).—Held non-gazetted appts. from 1st Apr., 1908, to 31st Mar., 1914; sub-judge, Apr., 1914; offg. asst. judge, May 1927; confid., March, 1930; offg. judge and sess. judge, Jan., 1929; confid., Dec., 1934.

YIN, KHIN MAUNG, B.Sc., Barr., Indian C.S. (asst. commr., Burma) (b. 19th Feb., 1897).—Educ. at Calcutta Univ., and Hertford Coll., Oxford; joined the service, 24th Dec., and served in Burma as asst. commr.; under-sec. to govt., educn. dept., May, 1927; offg. dep. commr., Apr., 1931.

YO, U SEIN, late Burma Civil Service (b. 11th Feb., 1873).—Held various ministerial appts. from 1st Oct., 1893; extra asst. commr., May, 1910; dep. commr., Dec., 1926; ret'd., Apr., 1929.

YORKE, ROBERT LANGDON, B.A., Indian C.S. (judge, Oudh Chief Court) (b. 2nd Feb., 1887).—Educ. at Haileybury, and Lincoln Coll., Oxford; apptd. after exam. of 1910; arrived in India, 24th Nov., 1911, and served in the United Provs. as asst. mag. and collr.; jt. mag., June, 1917; apptd. to the I.A.R.O., Oct., 1917; reverted to civil duty, Jan., 1919; actg. dist. and sess. judge from July, 1919; offg. dep. sec. to govt., judl. branch, March, 1926; confid. as dist. and sess. judge, Nov., 1926; judl. sec. to govt., May, 1927; apptd. judge, Oudh chief ct., Jan., 1938.

YOUNG, Rev. ERNEST JOSEPH, B.A., Indian Eccles. Estabt. (archdeacon of Calcutta) (b. 26th Oct., 1888).—Joined the service, 6th April, 1918; junr. chaplain, April, 1918; senr. chaplain, Apr., 1928; offg. archdeacon of Calcutta, Mar., 1935; confid., Sept., 1936.

YOUNG, FREDERICK, C.I.E., Indian Police (supt., United Provs.) (b. 16th May, 1890). Joined the service as asst. supt., 16th Dec., 1909; supt., June, 1923; C.I.E., June, 1924; inspr.-genl. of police, Jaipur State, May, 1931.

YOUNG, GEORGE EDGAR, late India Office (b. 26th Aug., 1868).—Educ. at Warminster Gr. Sch., and King's Coll. Sch., London; apptd. 2nd class clerk, registry and record dept., 22nd May, 1888; transf. to finl. dept., 14th May, 1901; resdt. clerk, Oct., 1907, to March, 1914; staff clerk, March, 1914; higher exec. officer, April, 1922; transferred to acct.-genl's. dept., May, 1922; suptg. exec. officer, July, 1922; ret'd., Jan., 1932.

YOUNG, GERARD MACKWORTH, C.I.E., M.A., F.R.G.S., late Indian C.S. (b. 7th April, 1884).—Educ. at Eton, and King's Coll., Cambridge; apptd. after exam. of 1907; arrived, 4th Dec., 1908, and served in the Punjab as asst. commr.; under sec. to govt., April, 1913; under sec. to govt. of India, home dept., July, 1916; to March, 1919; dep. sec., ditto, Feb., 1919; employed in mil. dept., India Office, from July, 1919, to Sept., 1920; dep. commr., Jan., 1921, to May, 1924; dep. sec. to govt. of India, army dept., Nov., 1924; offg. jt. sec. to govt. of India, home dept., April, 1926; jt. sec. army dept., Sept., 1926; sec. to govt. of India, army dept., Nov., 1926; C.I.E., Mar., 1929; ret'd., Aug., 1934.

YOUNG, JAMES, Major, M.B.E., Indian Stores Dept. (dep. dir. of purchase) (b. 4th July, 1885).—In milty. employ from 20th May, 1915, to 15th September, 1924; M.B.E., Jan., 1920; asst. dir. of purchase, Sept., 1924; offg. dep. dir., ditto, May, 1925; confd., Feb., 1926.

YOUNG, JAMES WOLSTON, O.B.E., Indian Finance Dept. (b. 18th Sept., 1885).—Joined the service, 26th Jan., 1909; asst. acctnt.-gen., Sept., 1909; O.B.E., Dec., 1919; employed by Jodhpur Durbar from 10th March, 1923; delegate to Indian round table conf., London, 2nd Session, Sept.-Dec., 1931.

YOUNG, Sir JOHN DOUGLAS, Kt., Barr., Judl. dept., United Provs. (ch. justice, high ct., Lahore) (b. 7th Apl., 1883).—Apptd. judge, high ct., Allahabad, 15th Jan., 1929; ch. justice, high ct., Lahore, May, 1934; knighted, Jan., 1935.

YOUNG, Rev. PHILIP NORTON FRUSHARD, M.A., Indian Eccles. Estabt. (junr. chaplain). Apptd. chaplain on probation, Punjab, 2nd Nov., 1930; junr. chaplain, Nov., 1932.

YOUNG, THOMAS CHARLES McCOMBIE, M.D., D.P.H., Lt.-Col., I.M.S. (ret'd.).—First commn. dated 1st September, 1902; in mil. employ to Oct., 1909; dep. sanitary commr., Eastern Bengal and Assam, Nov., 1909; ditto, Assam, April, 1912; sanitary commr., Assam, July, 1915; offg. health officer, Port of Bombay, April, 1926; on foreign service under Indian Research Fund Assocn., Nov., 1926; ret'd., Sept., 1930.

YOUNG, WILLIAM HOARE HATCHELL, M.C., Indian State Rlys. (ch. operating supt., E.I. rly.) (b. 13th Aug., 1886).—Joined the service 28th April, 1904; asst. traffic supt., N.W. Rly., Jan., 1909; I.A.R.O., March, 1915; dist. traffic supt., Aug., 1917; offg. dep. chief operating supt., July, 1925; offg. divl. supt., Nov., 1926; confd., Jan., 1929; offg. ch. operating supt., E.I. rly., Aug., 1935; ch. comml. manager, E.I. rly., Nov., 1935; ch. operating supt., E.I. rly., Apl., 1937.

YOUNGHUSBAND, Sir FRANCIS EDWARD, K.C.S.I. K.C.I.E., LL.D. (Hon.), D.Sc. (Hon.) Lieut.-Col., Indian Army (ret'd.), late Foreign Dept., Govt. of India.—First commn., 10th May, 1882; appd. to the foreign dept., July, 1889; asst. to resdnt. in Kashmir, April, 1892; asst. pol. agent, Chitral, Oct., 1892; asst. to govr.-gen.'s agent, Rajputana, Nov., 1897; pol. agent in Haraot and Tonk Sept., 1898; K.-i.-H. medal, Jan., 1901; offg. resdnt., Indore, Feb., 1902; on depn. with Tibet Frontier commn. from June, 1903, to Nov., 1904; K.C.I.E., Dec., 1904; resdnt. in, Kashmir, June, 1906; ret'd., May, 1910; K.C.S.I., Jan., 1917; presdnt., R. Geographical Society, 1919; author of 'The Relief of Chitral,' 'The Heart of a Continent,' and 'S. Africa of To-Day'; holds gold medal of R. Geographical Soc., and R. Scottish ditto, and silver medal of the R. Soc. of Arts.

YOUNGMAN, WILLIAM, B.Sc., Ph.D., late Indian Agric. Service (Central Provs.) (b. 6th Aug., 1880).—Joined the service as asst. economic botanist, 5th Aug., 1918; economic botanist, May, 1920; ret'd., Feb., 1930; dir. of agric., Ceylon, Mar., 1930; ret'd., Nov., 1935.

YOUNIE, JOHN, M.A., Indian C.S. (dist. and sess. judge, Bengal) (b. 5th Apl., 1893).—Educ. at Elgin Acad. and Edinburgh University; apptd. after exam. of 1915; arrived in India, Dec., 1916, and served in Bengal as asst. mag. and collr.; I.A.R.O., Nov., 1918, to Jan., 1919; offg. mag. and collr., Dec., 1923; addl. dist. and sess. judge, Jan., 1925; offg. ditto, April, 1926; confd., Mar., 1927.

YOUSUF ALI SAYEED, Indian Police (asst. supt., Madras) (b. 20th May, 1911).—Apptd. proby. asst. supt., Madras, 23rd Jan., 1933; confd., Jan., 1935.

YULE, HARRY HOWARD, V.D., late Indian State Rlys. (b. 30th Nov., 1880).—Joined the service as asst. supt., 30th Jan., 1901; dist. supt., March, 1914; supt., Feb., 1915; divl. supt., Jan., 1927; offg. dep. ch. operating supt., Nov., 1927; confd., Mar., 1932; offg. divl. supt., Mar., 1932; confd., June, 1934; offg. ch. comml. manager, E.I. rly., Feb., 1935; ret'd., July, 1937.

YUSAF, SALAHUDDIN AHMED (see Salahuddin A. Y.).

YUSUF ALI, A. (see Abdullah Ibn Yusuf Ali).

YUSUF ALI KHAN, AGHA, Indian Agricl. Service (b. Jan., 1886).—Joined the service, 15th Apl., 1907, and held non-gazetted appts. to 31st Mar., 1919; extra asst. dir. of agric., Apl., 1919; offg. dep. dir. of agric., Mar., 1928.

ZACHARIAH, KURUVILA, M.A. (Oxon), Indian Educ. Service (prinpl. Hooghly Coll., Calcutta) (b. 24th Dec., 1890).—Joined the service as prof., presdy. coll., 10th Jan., 1916; prinpl. Hooghly coll., July, 1930.

ZAFAR, MUHAMMAD ABU, Indian Police.—Apptd. asst. supt., United Provs., on probn., 11th Feb., 1935; confd., Feb., 1937.

ZAFAR ALI, Sir, KHAN BAHADUR, MIRZA, Kt., B.A., late Punjab Judl. Dept. (b. 20th Feb., 1870).—Joined the service, 4th Feb., 1893; extra asst. commr., Nov., 1899; extra judl. asst. commr., Sept., 1907; dist. and sess. judge, March, 1918; offg. addl. judge, Punjab high ct., Oct., 1922, to March, 1923, and from Oct., 1923; judge, ditto, Oct., 1926; ret'd., 1930; Kt., Jan., 1931.

ZAFAR HASAN, KHAN BAHADUR, MAULVI, B.A., Archaeological Survey Dept. (supt. northern circle) (b. 17th July, 1886).—Joined the service, 17th June, 1913; supt., Feb., 1925; offg. dep. dir. genl., Feb., 1931; confd., Apl., 1932; supt., northern circle, Oct., 1935.

ZAFAR OMAR, B.A., Indian Police (supt., United Provs.) (b. 27th Apl., 1883).—Joined the service as dep. supt., 7th Jan., 1908; supt., Sept., 1924.

ZAFRULLAH KHAN, CHAUDHRI, Sir MUHAMMAD, K.C.S.I. (member govr.-genl.'s exec. council).—Apptd. temp. member, govr.-genl.'s exec. council, Apl., 1935; confd., May, 1935; Knighted, June, 1935; chairman, central advisory council of rlys., July, 1935, to Nov., 1937; K.C.S.I., May, 1937.

ZAHEER, SAIYID HASAN (see Hasan Zaheer, Saiyid).

ZAHHADUR, RAHIM ZAHID SUHRAWARDY (see Suhrawardy, Sir Z. R. Z.).

ZAHID HASAN KHAN, Indian Service of Engrs. (asst. exec. engr., Central Provs.) (b. 9th Dec., 1902).—Joined the service as appr. engr., Nov. 1926; asst. exec. engr., Nov., 1927.

ZAHID HUSAIN, M.A., Indian Audit Dept. (b. 6th Jan., 1895).—Joined the service, 14th Mar., 1918; asst. auditor, N.W. rly., Apl., 1921; ditto, E.B. rly., Nov., 1923, ditto, E.I. rly., Dec., 1925; dir. of army audit, westn. circle, Feb., 1929; dep. contr., N.W.F.P., Nov., 1931; asst. auditor-genl., Apl., 1932; offg. ch. auditor, rly. clearing accts. office, Mar., 1933; dep. dir.-genl., posts and tels. (finance), Jan., 1934; offg. compt., N.W.F.P., Feb., 1935.

ZAHURUDDIN AHMAD FARUQI, MUHAMMAD, KHAN BAHADUR, Indian Service of Engrs. (exec. engr.) (b. 9th Nov., 1890).—Joined the service, 8th Sept., 1913; asst. engr., Sept., 1914; asst. exec. engr., Dec., 1920; exec. engr., Oct., 1923; transfed. to central p.w.d., Feb., 1933; offg. suptg. engr., May, 1934, and again, Apl., 1935.

ZAIMAN, BARNET ABRAHAM, B.A., late Indian C.S.—Educ. at Sheffield Central Secondary Sch., and Pembroke Coll., Cambridge; apptd. after exam. of 1915; arrived, Dec., 1916, and served in Burma as asst. commr.; on mil. service from Oct., 1917, to Oct., 1919; offg. dist. and sess. judge, Nov. 1925; ret'd., Dec. 1928.

ZAIN-UD-DIN, KHAN BAHADUR, Saiyid M.A., late United Provs. Civil Service.—Joined the service, 6th April, 1896, as dep. collr., mag. and collr., March, 1926; ret'd., Sept., 1928.

ZAKIR HUSAIN, Indian Police (supt., Bengal) (b. 2nd Nov., 1898).—Joined the service as asst. supt., Bengal, 26th Sept., 1923; offg. supt., Sept., 1930; confd., Aug., 1936.

ZAMAN MEHDI KHAN, KHAN BAHADUR, Malik, B.A., late Punjab Civil Service (b. 1879).—Joined the service, 9th Feb., 1901; extra asst. commr., May, 1904; dep. commr., July, 1929; ret'd., June, 1934.

ZAW PE, U. (see Pe, U Zaw).

ZETLAND (MARQUESS OF), MOST HON. LAWRENCE JOHN LUMLEY DUNDAS, P.C., G.C.S.I., G.C.I.E. (sec. of state for India and for Burma).—Educ. at Harrow and Trinity Coll., Cambridge; A.D.C., Viceroy's staff, 1900; late captain 1st Bn. N. Riding of Yorks. Vol. Art., and late major, 4th Bn. Prince of Wales' Yorks. Regt.; M.P. for Hornsey divn. of Middlesex, 1907-16; member of the Royal Commn. on the public services in India, 1912-15; G.C.I.E., Feb., 1917; govr. of Bengal from 26th March, 1917, to 20th March, 1922; P.C., 1922; delegate to Indian round table conf., London, Nov., 1930-Jan., 1931; 2nd Session, Sept.-Dec., 1931; sec. of state for India, June, 1935; sec. of state for Burma, Apl., 1937; author of 'Sport and Politics under an Eastern Sky,' 'On the Outskirts of Empire In Asia,' 'A Wandering Student in the Far East,' 'An Eastern Miscellany.'

ZIA-UD-DIN AFZAL, LESLIE, Indian Police—Joined the service as asst. supt., United Provs., 16th July, 1928; offg. supt., May, 1934.

ZIA-UD-DIN AHMAD (see Ahmad, Z.).

ZIA-UL HASAN, KHAN BAHADUR, MUNSHI, B.A., Judl. Dept., United Provs. (judge, ch. ct., Oudh) (b. Dec., 1880).—Joined the service, 26th June, 1907; sub. judge, Jan., 1922; dist. and sess. judge, Mar., 1932; judge, ch. ct., Oudh, July, 1934.

ZIAUL HAQ, MIAN MUHAMMAD, Indian Forest Service (dep. consvr.) (b. 10th Sept., 1892).—Joined the service, 20th July, 1915; extra asst. consvr., July, 1917; promoted to I.F.S., United Provs., Apl., 1931; dep. consr., Oct., 1931.

ZIAULLAH KHAN, MUHAMMAD, M.A., Indian C.S. (jt. mag. and dep. collr., Bihar) (b. 4th Dec., 1906).—Educ. at Lucknow Univ. and Magdalene College, Cambridge; joined the Service, 12th Oct., 1931; arrvd. in India, Nov., 1931, and served in Bihar and Orissa as asst. mag. and collr.

ZUBERI, MASARRAT HUSAIN, M.A., Indian C.S. (asst. commr., Punjab) (b. 25th June, 1911).—Educated at St. Catherine's College, Cambridge; joined the service, 24th Sept., 1936; arrived in India, Oct., 1936, and served in the Punjab as asst. commr.

ZUTSHI, MANOHAR LAL (see Manohar Lal Z.).

CASUALTIES.

RETIREMENTS.

Name. Service or Appointment. Date.
Abdul Ali, Hamid Moizudin ... Indian Civil Service, Bombay ... 13 Feb., 1937.
Adam, J. H., C.I.E., O.B.E. ... Indian Police, Punjab ... 1 June, 1937.
Ananda Rao, R.B. D. ... Indian Agricultural Service, Madras ... 16 Nov., 1937.
Anderson, F., C.S.I., C.I.E. ... Indian Service of Engineers, United Provinces ... 1 Jan., 1938.
Ayyangar, D.B. N.N. ... Indian Service of Engineers, Bombay ... 12 Sept., 1936.
Bacon, E. G. ... Bengal Pilot Service ... 16 Nov., 1936.
Bagchi, R.B. Banamali ... Bengal Civil Service ... 24 Sept., 1936.
Balwant Singh, Shihn, Sardar ... Indian Service of Engineers, Punjab ... 30 Oct., 1936.
Banarji, Amrita Lal ... Bengal Civil Service ... 29 Jan., 1937.
Banarji, R.B. Bhupendra Nath ... Indian Police, Bengal ... 14 Oct., 1937.
Banarji, Raman Chandra ... Bengal Civil Service ... 16 Aug., 1936.
Barlee, Sir K. W. ... Indian Civil Service, Bombay ... 29 Nov., 1937.
Barretto, W. L., O.B.E. ... Burma Commission... ... 5 Aug., 1937.
Bartley, F. D., C.B.E. ... Indian Police, Bengal ... 13 Aug., 1936.

Basu, Panna Lal ... Bengal Civil Service ... 7 Oct., 1936.
Basu, R.B. Suresh Chandra ... Bengal Civil Service ... 20 Nov., 1937.
Bavin, L. N. ... Indian Police, Bengal ... 7 April, 1937.
Beasley, Sir H. O. C. ... Madras Judicial Department ... 2 July, 1937.
Bedford, Vice-Adm. A. E. F., C.B., C.S.I. ... Flag Officer Commanding, R.I.N. ... 11 Dec., 1937.
Bell, Lt. C. H. ... Indian Posts and Telegraphs Service, Bombay ... 20 Aug., 1936.
Bell, Sir R. D., K.C.S.I., C.I.E. ... Indian Civil Service, Bombay ... 12 Dec., 1937.
Belvalkar, S. K. ... Indian Educational Service, Bombay ... 11 Dec., 1936.
Betterton, F. A. ... Indian Service of Engineers, Bihar ... 14 Jan., 1938.
Bhandarkar, Gopal P., M.B.E. ... Indian Service of Engineers, Bombay ... 23 Oct., 1937.
Bhaumik, H. P., O.B.E. ... Indian Posts and Telegraphs Department ... 10 June, 1937.
Blake, A. G. ... Indian Civil Service, Madras ... 28 Nov., 1937.
Blakiston, J. F., C.I.E. ... Archaeological Survey Department ... Sept., 1937.
Blinkworth, Major K. W. ... Indian Medical Department ... 30 Sept., 1936.
Bose, B. K. ... Indian Service of Engineers, Bombay ... 16 Sept., 1937.
Bown, J. E. ... Indian Police, Bihar ... 31 Dec., 1937.
Boyd, Sir D. J., K.C.I.E. ... Indian Civil Service, Punjab ... 1 April, 1937.
Braja Nandan Singh, R.B. ... Bihar Excise and Salt Department ... 6 Nov., 1936.
Bull, W. ... Indian State Railways ... 8 Feb., 1937.
Burke, Lt.-Col. G. T. ... Indian Medical Service, United Provinces ... 31 May, 1937.
Burnand, W. H. ... Indian State Railways ... April, 1937.
Burne, Lt.-Col. L. E. L., C.I.E., C.B.E. ... Burma Commission... ... Dec., 1937.
Burton, L. H. ... Indian Police, Bengal ... 24 Mar., 1937.
Butler, F. G. ... Indian Civil Service, Madras ... 23 Nov., 1937.
Butler, S. G. ... Indian Service of Engineers, Assam ... 20 Aug., 1936.
Calder, N. D. ... Indian State Railways ... 13 Jan., 1937.
Canning, F., C.I.E. ... Indian Forest Service, United Provinces ... 31 Dec., 1937.
Carr, J. ... Indian Forest Service, Central Provinces ... 1 Dec., 1936.
Chakrabatti, J. N. ... Indian Agricultural Service, Assam ... 13 Nov., 1937.
Chalk, W. O. ... Indian State Railways ... 28 Nov., 1936.
Chapman, A. F. ... Indian Service of Engineers, Burma ... 20 April, 1937.
Chatarji, Gopendra Bhusan, Babu ... United Provinces Civil Service ... 18 June, 1937.
Chaubal, D. T. ... Bombay Civil Service ... 1 Oct., 1936.
Chaudhuri, Niraja Kant ... Indian Police, Central Provinces ... 7 Oct., 1937.
Chetan Anand, Lala ... Punjab Educational Service ... 11 Sept., 1936.
Chhuttan Lal, R.B. ... Indian Service of Engineers, United Provinces ... 11 Feb., 1937.
Chitre, A. A. ... Bombay Judicial Department ... 17 May, 1937.
RETIREMENTS—continued.
Name. Service or Appointment. Date.
Clague, J., C.I.E. .... Indian Civil Service, Burma .... 31 Mar., 1937.
Clift, F. C. L. .... Indian Service of Engineers, Madras .... 6 Jan., 1937.
Cohu, Rev. Canon, C. J. .... Indian Ecclesiastical Establishment, United Provinces 12 Feb., 1937.
Coldstream, J. .... Indian Civil Service, Punjab .... 23 Dec., 1937.
Commissariat, K.B. M. S. .... Indian Educational Service, Bombay .... 11 Dec., 1936.
Comrie, M. C. .... Indian Civil Service, Central Provinces .... 25 Oct., 1937.
Connor, Maj.-Gen. Sir F. P., D.S.O. .... Indian Medical Service, Madras .... 15 Sept., 1937.
Cooke, T. F., O.B.E. .... Indian Police, Punjab .... 14 June, 1937.
Cooper, Capt. M. P. .... Royal Indian Navy .... 22 Aug., 1937.
Cooper, R. F. .... Indian Educational Service, Bihar .... 13 Aug., 1937.
Corban-Lucas, A. J. L. .... Indian Police, Burma .... 4 Dec., 1936.
Cornish, H. D. .... Madras Judicial Department .... Aug., 1937.
Corser, Capt. C. H., C.I.E. .... Royal Indian Navy .... 11 Aug., 1937.
Couchman, Sir H. J., D.S.O., M.C. .... Survey of India .... Sept., 1937.
Couper, Sir T., C.S.I. .... Indian Civil Service, Burma .... 1 Dec., 1937.
Craig, T. J. A., C.I.E. .... Indian Police, Bengal .... 3 Dec., 1936.
Crichton, Ven. W. R. .... Indian Ecclesiastical Establishment, Madras Nov., 1937.
Cunliffe, Sir J. R. E. .... Bengal Judicial Department .... 8 Nov., 1937.
Cunningham, Sir C. B., C.S.I. .... Indian Police, Madras .... 2 Jan., 1938.
Dallas-Smith, Lt.-Col. E. D. .... Commandant, Eastern Frontier Rifles, Bengal Battalion. 21 April, 1937.
Darwin, J. H., C.I.E. .... Indian Civil Service, United Provinces .... 2 Nov., 1937.
Das Gupta, S. K., M.B.E. .... Indian Civil Service, Bengal .... 16 Nov., 1936.
Davies, Rev. C. R. .... Indian Ecclesiastical Establishment, United Provinces 11 Oct., 1936.
Dawkins, G. C. E. .... Indian Forest Service, Burma .... 5 Nov., 1936.
Day, Rev. E. R. .... Indian Ecclesiastical Department, Central Provinces 20 Aug., 1937.
De, Makhanlal .... Indian Educational Service, Central Provinces 4 Dec., 1937.
Dillon, W. .... Indian Civil Service, Bombay .... 11 June, 1937.
Divatia, V. B. .... Indian Educational Service, Bombay .... 24 April, 1937.
Dix, A. G. .... Indian Educational Service, Central Provinces. 5 Aug., 1937.
Drake, D. H. C., C.I.E. .... Indian Civil Service, Central Provinces .... 10 Aug., 1937.
Dunbar, A. .... Indian Police, Burma .... 8 Feb., 1937.
